sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।ततो द्रोणे हते राजन्दुर्योधनमुखा नृपाः ।भृशमुद्विग्नमनसो द्रोणपुत्रमुपागमन् ॥१॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,tato droṇe hate rājanduryodhanamukhā nṛpāḥ ,bhṛśamudvignamanaso droṇaputramupāgaman.
english: Vaiśaṃpāyana said: Then, O King, after Droṇa had been slain, the kings, led by Duryodhana and with greatly agitated minds, approached Droṇa's son.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 1 Verse 1. Vaiśaṃpāyana said: Then, O King, after Droṇa had been slain, the kings, led by Duryodhana and with greatly agitated minds, approached Droṇa's son.

sanskrit: ते द्रोणमुपशोचन्तः कश्मलाभिहतौजसः ।पर्युपासन्त शोकार्तास्ततः शारद्वतीसुतम् ॥२॥
transliteration: te droṇamupaśocantaḥ kaśmalābhihataujasaḥ ,paryupāsanta śokārtāstataḥ śāradvatīsutam.
english: They, lamenting Droṇa, with their energy overwhelmed by despair and distressed by sorrow, then gathered around the son of Śāradvatī (Aśvatthāman).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 1 Verse 2. They, lamenting Droṇa, with their energy overwhelmed by despair and distressed by sorrow, then gathered around the son of Śāradvatī (Aśvatthāman).

sanskrit: मुहूर्तं ते समाश्वास्य हेतुभिः शास्त्रसंमितैः ।रात्र्यागमे महीपालाः स्वानि वेश्मानि भेजिरे ॥३॥
transliteration: muhūrtaṁ te samāśvāsya hetubhiḥ śāstrasaṁmitaiḥ ,rātryāgame mahīpālāḥ svāni veśmāni bhejire.
english: After they had consoled themselves for a moment with arguments supported by the scriptures, the kings returned to their own camps as night fell.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 1 Verse 3. After they had consoled themselves for a moment with arguments supported by the scriptures, the kings returned to their own camps as night fell.

sanskrit: विशेषतः सूतपुत्रो राजा चैव सुयोधनः ।दुःशासनोऽथ शकुनिर्न निद्रामुपलेभिरे ॥४॥
transliteration: viśeṣataḥ sūtaputro rājā caiva suyodhanaḥ ,duḥśāsano'tha śakunirna nidrāmupalebhire.
english: Especially Karna, along with King Suyodhana, Duḥśāsana, and Śakuni, could not get any sleep.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 1 Verse 4. Especially Karna, along with King Suyodhana, Duḥśāsana, and Śakuni, could not get any sleep.

sanskrit: ते वेश्मस्वपि कौरव्य पृथ्वीशा नाप्नुवन्सुखम् ।चिन्तयन्तः क्षयं तीव्रं निद्रां नैवोपलेभिरे ॥५॥
transliteration: te veśmasvapi kauravya pṛthvīśā nāpnuvansukham ,cintayantaḥ kṣayaṁ tīvraṁ nidrāṁ naivopalebhire.
english: Even in their palaces, those lords of the earth did not find comfort. Pondering over the intense destruction, they could not obtain sleep.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 1 Verse 5. Even in their palaces, those lords of the earth did not find comfort. Pondering over the intense destruction, they could not obtain sleep.

sanskrit: सहितास्ते निशायां तु दुर्योधननिवेशने ।अतिप्रचण्डाद्विद्वेषात्पाण्डवानां महात्मनाम् ॥६॥
transliteration: sahitāste niśāyāṁ tu duryodhananiveśane ,atipracaṇḍādvidveṣātpāṇḍavānāṁ mahātmanām.
english: They gathered at night in Duryodhana's dwelling, driven by their exceedingly fierce hatred for the great-souled (mahātman) Pāṇḍavas.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 1 Verse 6. They gathered at night in Duryodhana's dwelling, driven by their exceedingly fierce hatred for the great-souled (mahātman) Pāṇḍavas.

sanskrit: यत्तद्द्यूतपरिक्लिष्टां कृष्णामानिन्यिरे सभाम् ।तत्स्मरन्तोऽन्वतप्यन्त भृशमुद्विग्नचेतसः ॥७॥
transliteration: yattaddyūtaparikliṣṭāṁ kṛṣṇāmāninyire sabhām ,tatsmaranto'nvatapyanta bhṛśamudvignacetasaḥ.
english: Remembering how they had brought Kṛṣṇā (Draupadī), who was distressed by the dice game, to the assembly hall, they, with exceedingly agitated minds, greatly repented.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 1 Verse 7. Remembering how they had brought Kṛṣṇā (Draupadī), who was distressed by the dice game, to the assembly hall, they, with exceedingly agitated minds, greatly repented.

sanskrit: चिन्तयन्तश्च पार्थानां तान्क्लेशान्द्यूतकारितान् ।कृच्छ्रेण क्षणदां राजन्निन्युरब्दशतोपमाम् ॥८॥
transliteration: cintayantaśca pārthānāṁ tānkleśāndyūtakāritān ,kṛcchreṇa kṣaṇadāṁ rājanninyurabdaśatopamām.
english: O King, contemplating those afflictions of the Pāṇḍavas caused by gambling, they spent the night with great difficulty, finding it to be like a hundred years.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 1 Verse 8. O King, contemplating those afflictions of the Pāṇḍavas caused by gambling, they spent the night with great difficulty, finding it to be like a hundred years.

sanskrit: ततः प्रभाते विमले स्थिता दिष्टस्य शासने ।चक्रुरावश्यकं सर्वे विधिदृष्टेन कर्मणा ॥९॥
transliteration: tataḥ prabhāte vimale sthitā diṣṭasya śāsane ,cakrurāvaśyakaṁ sarve vidhidṛṣṭena karmaṇā.
english: Then, in the clear morning, accepting the decree of destiny, they all performed the necessary rites according to what is prescribed by sacred law (dharma).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 1 Verse 9. Then, in the clear morning, accepting the decree of destiny, they all performed the necessary rites according to what is prescribed by sacred law (dharma).

sanskrit: ते कृत्वावश्यकार्याणि समाश्वस्य च भारत ।योगमाज्ञापयामासुर्युद्धाय च विनिर्ययुः ॥१०॥
transliteration: te kṛtvāvaśyakāryāṇi samāśvasya ca bhārata ,yogamājñāpayāmāsuryuddhāya ca viniryayuḥ.
english: O Bhārata, having performed their essential duties and having gathered their resolve, they ordered the battle formation (yoga) and then departed for battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 1 Verse 10. O Bhārata, having performed their essential duties and having gathered their resolve, they ordered the battle formation (yoga) and then departed for battle.

sanskrit: कर्णं सेनापतिं कृत्वा कृतकौतुकमङ्गलाः ।वाचयित्वा द्विजश्रेष्ठान्दधिपात्रघृताक्षतैः ॥११॥
transliteration: karṇaṁ senāpatiṁ kṛtvā kṛtakautukamaṅgalāḥ ,vācayitvā dvijaśreṣṭhāndadhipātraghṛtākṣataiḥ.
english: Having appointed Karṇa as commander and performed the auspicious rituals for good fortune, they caused the best of the brāhmaṇas (dvija) to recite blessings with curds, vessels, ghee, and unbroken rice grains.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 1 Verse 11. Having appointed Karṇa as commander and performed the auspicious rituals for good fortune, they caused the best of the brāhmaṇas (dvija) to recite blessings with curds, vessels, ghee, and unbroken rice grains.

sanskrit: निष्कैर्गोभिर्हिरण्येन वासोभिश्च महाधनैः ।वर्ध्यमाना जयाशीर्भिः सूतमागधबन्दिभिः ॥१२॥
transliteration: niṣkairgobhirhiraṇyena vāsobhiśca mahādhanaiḥ ,vardhyamānā jayāśīrbhiḥ sūtamāgadhabandibhiḥ.
english: Being honored with gold coins, cows, gold, and very costly garments, and with blessings of victory by the charioteers, bards, and panegyrists.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 1 Verse 12. Being honored with gold coins, cows, gold, and very costly garments, and with blessings of victory by the charioteers, bards, and panegyrists.

sanskrit: तथैव पाण्डवा राजन्कृतसर्वाह्णिकक्रियाः ।शिबिरान्निर्ययू राजन्युद्धाय कृतनिश्चयाः ॥१३॥
transliteration: tathaiva pāṇḍavā rājankṛtasarvāhṇikakriyāḥ ,śibirānniryayū rājanyuddhāya kṛtaniścayāḥ.
english: Likewise, O king, the Pandavas, having completed all their daily rituals, set forth from the camp, determined for battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 1 Verse 13. Likewise, O king, the Pandavas, having completed all their daily rituals, set forth from the camp, determined for battle.

sanskrit: ततः प्रववृते युद्धं तुमुलं रोमहर्षणम् ।कुरूणां पाण्डवानां च परस्परवधैषिणाम् ॥१४॥
transliteration: tataḥ pravavṛte yuddhaṁ tumulaṁ romaharṣaṇam ,kurūṇāṁ pāṇḍavānāṁ ca parasparavadhaiṣiṇām.
english: Then the tumultuous, hair-raising battle commenced between the Kurus and the Pandavas, who desired to kill one another.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 1 Verse 14. Then the tumultuous, hair-raising battle commenced between the Kurus and the Pandavas, who desired to kill one another.

sanskrit: तयोर्द्वे दिवसे युद्धं कुरुपाण्डवसेनयोः ।कर्णे सेनापतौ राजन्नभूदद्भुतदर्शनम् ॥१५॥
transliteration: tayordve divase yuddhaṁ kurupāṇḍavasenayoḥ ,karṇe senāpatau rājannabhūdadbhutadarśanam.
english: O king, for two days, the battle between the armies of the Kurus and Pandavas was a marvelous sight, with Karna serving as commander-in-chief.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 1 Verse 15. O king, for two days, the battle between the armies of the Kurus and Pandavas was a marvelous sight, with Karna serving as commander-in-chief.

sanskrit: ततः शत्रुक्षयं कृत्वा सुमहान्तं रणे वृषः ।पश्यतां धार्तराष्ट्राणां फल्गुनेन निपातितः ॥१६॥
transliteration: tataḥ śatrukṣayaṁ kṛtvā sumahāntaṁ raṇe vṛṣaḥ ,paśyatāṁ dhārtarāṣṭrāṇāṁ phalgunena nipātitaḥ.
english: Then, having inflicted a very great destruction of enemies in battle, Vṛṣa (Karṇa) was struck down by Arjuna, as the sons of Dhṛtarāṣṭra watched.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 1 Verse 16. Then, having inflicted a very great destruction of enemies in battle, Vṛṣa (Karṇa) was struck down by Arjuna, as the sons of Dhṛtarāṣṭra watched.

sanskrit: ततस्तत्संजयः सर्वं गत्वा नागाह्वयं पुरम् ।आचख्यौ धृतराष्ट्राय यद्वृत्तं कुरुजाङ्गले ॥१७॥
transliteration: tatastatsaṁjayaḥ sarvaṁ gatvā nāgāhvayaṁ puram ,ācakhyau dhṛtarāṣṭrāya yadvṛttaṁ kurujāṅgale.
english: Then, Sañjaya, having gone to the city named Nāgāhvaya (Hastinapura), recounted everything that had occurred in Kurujaṅgala to Dhṛtarāṣṭra.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 1 Verse 17. Then, Sañjaya, having gone to the city named Nāgāhvaya (Hastinapura), recounted everything that had occurred in Kurujaṅgala to Dhṛtarāṣṭra.

sanskrit: जनमेजय उवाच ।आपगेयं हतं श्रुत्वा द्रोणं च समरे परैः ।यो जगाम परामार्तिं वृद्धो राजाम्बिकासुतः ॥१८॥
transliteration: janamejaya uvāca ,āpageyaṁ hataṁ śrutvā droṇaṁ ca samare paraiḥ ,yo jagāma parāmārtiṁ vṛddho rājāmbikāsutaḥ.
english: Janamejaya said: "Having heard that Bhīṣma (Āpageya) and Droṇa were killed in battle by enemies, the aged King, son of Ambikā (Dhṛtarāṣṭra), who experienced supreme sorrow..."
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 1 Verse 18. Janamejaya said: "Having heard that Bhīṣma (Āpageya) and Droṇa were killed in battle by enemies, the aged King, son of Ambikā (Dhṛtarāṣṭra), who experienced supreme sorrow..."

sanskrit: स श्रुत्वा निहतं कर्णं दुर्योधनहितैषिणम् ।कथं द्विजवर प्राणानधारयत दुःखितः ॥१९॥
transliteration: sa śrutvā nihataṁ karṇaṁ duryodhanahitaiṣiṇam ,kathaṁ dvijavara prāṇānadhārayata duḥkhitaḥ.
english: How, O best among the twice-born (dvija), did that distressed king, having heard that Karṇa, the well-wisher of Duryodhana, had been killed, manage to sustain his life (prāṇa)?
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 1 Verse 19. How, O best among the twice-born (dvija), did that distressed king, having heard that Karṇa, the well-wisher of Duryodhana, had been killed, manage to sustain his life (prāṇa)?

sanskrit: यस्मिञ्जयाशां पुत्राणाममन्यत स पार्थिवः ।तस्मिन्हते स कौरव्यः कथं प्राणानधारयत् ॥२०॥
transliteration: yasmiñjayāśāṁ putrāṇāmamanyata sa pārthivaḥ ,tasminhate sa kauravyaḥ kathaṁ prāṇānadhārayat.
english: How did that king (Duryodhana), who had placed his hope for his sons' victory in him (Karṇa), sustain his life after he (Karṇa) was killed?
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 1 Verse 20. How did that king (Duryodhana), who had placed his hope for his sons' victory in him (Karṇa), sustain his life after he (Karṇa) was killed?

sanskrit: दुर्मरं बत मन्येऽहं नृणां कृच्छ्रेऽपि वर्तताम् ।यत्र कर्णं हतं श्रुत्वा नात्यजज्जीवितं नृपः ॥२१॥
transliteration: durmaraṁ bata manye'haṁ nṛṇāṁ kṛcchre'pi vartatām ,yatra karṇaṁ hataṁ śrutvā nātyajajjīvitaṁ nṛpaḥ.
english: Alas, I consider it truly difficult for people to die, even when they are in distress, given that the king (Duryodhana), upon hearing that Karṇa was slain, did not give up his life.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 1 Verse 21. Alas, I consider it truly difficult for people to die, even when they are in distress, given that the king (Duryodhana), upon hearing that Karṇa was slain, did not give up his life.

sanskrit: तथा शांतनवं वृद्धं ब्रह्मन्बाह्लिकमेव च ।द्रोणं च सोमदत्तं च भूरिश्रवसमेव च ॥२२॥
transliteration: tathā śāṁtanavaṁ vṛddhaṁ brahmanbāhlikameva ca ,droṇaṁ ca somadattaṁ ca bhūriśravasameva ca.
english: And, O Brahmin, similarly the aged son of Śantanu (Bhīṣma), and Bahlika, and Droṇa, and Somadatta, and Bhūriśravas too.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 1 Verse 22. And, O Brahmin, similarly the aged son of Śantanu (Bhīṣma), and Bahlika, and Droṇa, and Somadatta, and Bhūriśravas too.

sanskrit: तथैव चान्यान्सुहृदः पुत्रपौत्रांश्च पातितान् ।श्रुत्वा यन्नाजहात्प्राणांस्तन्मन्ये दुष्करं द्विज ॥२३॥
transliteration: tathaiva cānyānsuhṛdaḥ putrapautrāṁśca pātitān ,śrutvā yannājahātprāṇāṁstanmanye duṣkaraṁ dvija.
english: And similarly, O Brahmin (dvija), having heard that other friends, sons, and grandsons had fallen, the fact that he (Duryodhana) did not give up his life – that I consider extremely difficult (duṣkaram).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 1 Verse 23. And similarly, O Brahmin (dvija), having heard that other friends, sons, and grandsons had fallen, the fact that he (Duryodhana) did not give up his life – that I consider extremely difficult (duṣkaram).

sanskrit: एतन्मे सर्वमाचक्ष्व विस्तरेण तपोधन ।न हि तृप्यामि पूर्वेषां शृण्वानश्चरितं महत् ॥२४॥
transliteration: etanme sarvamācakṣva vistareṇa tapodhana ,na hi tṛpyāmi pūrveṣāṁ śṛṇvānaścaritaṁ mahat.
english: O ascetic (tapodhana), explain all this to me in detail. Indeed, I am not satisfied hearing the great deeds of the ancestors.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 1 Verse 24. O ascetic (tapodhana), explain all this to me in detail. Indeed, I am not satisfied hearing the great deeds of the ancestors.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।हते कर्णे महाराज निशि गावल्गणिस्तदा ।दीनो ययौ नागपुरमश्वैर्वातसमैर्जवे ॥२५॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,hate karṇe mahārāja niśi gāvalgaṇistadā ,dīno yayau nāgapuramaśvairvātasamairjave.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: O great king, then, with Karṇa having been slain (hate karṇe) that night, Saṃjaya, son of Gavalgala (gāvalgaṇi), distressed, went to Hastinapura on horses as swift as the wind.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 1 Verse 25. Vaiśampāyana said: O great king, then, with Karṇa having been slain (hate karṇe) that night, Saṃjaya, son of Gavalgala (gāvalgaṇi), distressed, went to Hastinapura on horses as swift as the wind.

sanskrit: स हास्तिनपुरं गत्वा भृशमुद्विग्नमानसः ।जगाम धृतराष्ट्रस्य क्षयं प्रक्षीणबान्धवम् ॥२६॥
transliteration: sa hāstinapuraṁ gatvā bhṛśamudvignamānasaḥ ,jagāma dhṛtarāṣṭrasya kṣayaṁ prakṣīṇabāndhavam.
english: Having reached Hastinapura, he (Saṃjaya), with his mind greatly agitated, went to the abode of Dhṛtarāṣṭra, whose relatives were completely diminished.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 1 Verse 26. Having reached Hastinapura, he (Saṃjaya), with his mind greatly agitated, went to the abode of Dhṛtarāṣṭra, whose relatives were completely diminished.

sanskrit: स समुद्वीक्ष्य राजानं कश्मलाभिहतौजसम् ।ववन्दे प्राञ्जलिर्भूत्वा मूर्ध्ना पादौ नृपस्य ह ॥२७॥
transliteration: sa samudvīkṣya rājānaṁ kaśmalābhihataujasam ,vavande prāñjalirbhūtvā mūrdhnā pādau nṛpasya ha.
english: Having thoroughly observed the king (Dhṛtarāṣṭra), whose vital energy had been struck down by a swoon, he (Saṃjaya), with folded hands, bowed his head to the king's two feet.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 1 Verse 27. Having thoroughly observed the king (Dhṛtarāṣṭra), whose vital energy had been struck down by a swoon, he (Saṃjaya), with folded hands, bowed his head to the king's two feet.

sanskrit: संपूज्य च यथान्यायं धृतराष्ट्रं महीपतिम् ।हा कष्टमिति चोक्त्वा स ततो वचनमाददे ॥२८॥
transliteration: saṁpūjya ca yathānyāyaṁ dhṛtarāṣṭraṁ mahīpatim ,hā kaṣṭamiti coktvā sa tato vacanamādade.
english: After properly honoring King Dhritarashtra, and exclaiming, "Alas, what a misfortune!", he then spoke these words.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 1 Verse 28. After properly honoring King Dhritarashtra, and exclaiming, "Alas, what a misfortune!", he then spoke these words.

sanskrit: संजयोऽहं क्षितिपते कच्चिदास्ते सुखं भवान् ।स्वदोषेणापदं प्राप्य कच्चिन्नाद्य विमुह्यसि ॥२९॥
transliteration: saṁjayo'haṁ kṣitipate kaccidāste sukhaṁ bhavān ,svadoṣeṇāpadaṁ prāpya kaccinnādya vimuhyasi.
english: "I am Sanjaya, O King. I hope you are well. Having incurred calamity through your own fault, I trust you are not now bewildered."
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 1 Verse 29. "I am Sanjaya, O King. I hope you are well. Having incurred calamity through your own fault, I trust you are not now bewildered."

sanskrit: हितान्युक्तानि विदुरद्रोणगाङ्गेयकेशवैः ।अगृहीतान्यनुस्मृत्य कच्चिन्न कुरुषे व्यथाम् ॥३०॥
transliteration: hitānyuktāni viduradroṇagāṅgeyakeśavaiḥ ,agṛhītānyanusmṛtya kaccinna kuruṣe vyathām.
english: Remembering the beneficial advice offered by Vidura, Drona, Bhishma (Gāṅgeya), and Krishna (Keśava) that you did not accept, I hope you are not causing yourself distress.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 1 Verse 30. Remembering the beneficial advice offered by Vidura, Drona, Bhishma (Gāṅgeya), and Krishna (Keśava) that you did not accept, I hope you are not causing yourself distress.

sanskrit: रामनारदकण्वैश्च हितमुक्तं सभातले ।नगृहीतमनुस्मृत्य कच्चिन्न कुरुषे व्यथाम् ॥३१॥
transliteration: rāmanāradakaṇvaiśca hitamuktaṁ sabhātale ,nagṛhītamanusmṛtya kaccinna kuruṣe vyathām.
english: And remembering the beneficial advice spoken by Rama, Narada, and Kanva in the assembly hall, which you did not accept, I trust you are not causing yourself distress.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 1 Verse 31. And remembering the beneficial advice spoken by Rama, Narada, and Kanva in the assembly hall, which you did not accept, I trust you are not causing yourself distress.

sanskrit: सुहृदस्त्वद्धिते युक्तान्भीष्मद्रोणमुखान्परैः ।निहतान्युधि संस्मृत्य कच्चिन्न कुरुषे व्यथाम् ॥३२॥
transliteration: suhṛdastvaddhite yuktānbhīṣmadroṇamukhānparaiḥ ,nihatānyudhi saṁsmṛtya kaccinna kuruṣe vyathām.
english: Remembering your devoted friends, led by Bhīṣma and Droṇa, who were engaged in your welfare and killed in battle by your enemies, surely you are not experiencing anguish?
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 1 Verse 32. Remembering your devoted friends, led by Bhīṣma and Droṇa, who were engaged in your welfare and killed in battle by your enemies, surely you are not experiencing anguish?

sanskrit: तमेवंवादिनं राजा सूतपुत्रं कृताञ्जलिम् ।सुदीर्घमभिनिःश्वस्य दुःखार्त इदमब्रवीत् ॥३३॥
transliteration: tamevaṁvādinaṁ rājā sūtaputraṁ kṛtāñjalim ,sudīrghamabhiniḥśvasya duḥkhārta idamabravīt.
english: The king, afflicted by sorrow (duḥkha), sighed very deeply and then spoke this to the son of Sūta (Sanjaya), who was speaking thus and had made an obeisance (kṛtāñjali).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 1 Verse 33. The king, afflicted by sorrow (duḥkha), sighed very deeply and then spoke this to the son of Sūta (Sanjaya), who was speaking thus and had made an obeisance (kṛtāñjali).

sanskrit: गाङ्गेये निहते शूरे दिव्यास्त्रवति संजय ।द्रोणे च परमेष्वासे भृशं मे व्यथितं मनः ॥३४॥
transliteration: gāṅgeye nihate śūre divyāstravati saṁjaya ,droṇe ca parameṣvāse bhṛśaṁ me vyathitaṁ manaḥ.
english: O Sanjaya, when the heroic son of Gaṅgā (Bhīṣma), who possessed divine weapons, was slain, and also when Droṇa, the supreme archer (parameṣvāsa), was killed, my mind was greatly distressed.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 1 Verse 34. O Sanjaya, when the heroic son of Gaṅgā (Bhīṣma), who possessed divine weapons, was slain, and also when Droṇa, the supreme archer (parameṣvāsa), was killed, my mind was greatly distressed.

sanskrit: यो रथानां सहस्राणि दंशितानां दशैव हि ।अहन्यहनि तेजस्वी निजघ्ने वसुसंभवः ॥३५॥
transliteration: yo rathānāṁ sahasrāṇi daṁśitānāṁ daśaiva hi ,ahanyahani tejasvī nijaghne vasusaṁbhavaḥ.
english: Who, born of the Vasu (Bhīṣma), radiant and powerful, indeed slew ten thousands of armored chariots day after day.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 1 Verse 35. Who, born of the Vasu (Bhīṣma), radiant and powerful, indeed slew ten thousands of armored chariots day after day.

sanskrit: स हतो यज्ञसेनस्य पुत्रेणेह शिखण्डिना ।पाण्डवेयाभिगुप्तेन भृशं मे व्यथितं मनः ॥३६॥
transliteration: sa hato yajñasenasya putreṇeha śikhaṇḍinā ,pāṇḍaveyābhiguptena bhṛśaṁ me vyathitaṁ manaḥ.
english: My mind is greatly distressed because he was killed here by Shikhaṇḍin, the son of Drupada (Yajñasena), who was protected by the Pāṇḍavas.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 1 Verse 36. My mind is greatly distressed because he was killed here by Shikhaṇḍin, the son of Drupada (Yajñasena), who was protected by the Pāṇḍavas.

sanskrit: भार्गवः प्रददौ यस्मै परमास्त्रं महात्मने ।साक्षाद्रामेण यो बाल्ये धनुर्वेद उपाकृतः ॥३७॥
transliteration: bhārgavaḥ pradadau yasmai paramāstraṁ mahātmane ,sākṣādrāmeṇa yo bālye dhanurveda upākṛtaḥ.
english: To that great soul (mahātman), Bhārgava (Paraśurāma) gave the supreme weapon (paramāstra), and to whom the science of archery (dhanurveda) was directly taught by Rāma in his childhood.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 1 Verse 37. To that great soul (mahātman), Bhārgava (Paraśurāma) gave the supreme weapon (paramāstra), and to whom the science of archery (dhanurveda) was directly taught by Rāma in his childhood.

sanskrit: यस्य प्रसादात्कौन्तेया राजपुत्रा महाबलाः ।महारथत्वं संप्राप्तास्तथान्ये वसुधाधिपाः ॥३८॥
transliteration: yasya prasādātkaunteyā rājaputrā mahābalāḥ ,mahārathatvaṁ saṁprāptāstathānye vasudhādhipāḥ.
english: By whose grace the Kaunteyas, those greatly powerful princes, attained the state of being great charioteers (mahāratha), and similarly, other kings (vasudhādhipa) too.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 1 Verse 38. By whose grace the Kaunteyas, those greatly powerful princes, attained the state of being great charioteers (mahāratha), and similarly, other kings (vasudhādhipa) too.

sanskrit: तं द्रोणं निहतं श्रुत्वा धृष्टद्युम्नेन संयुगे ।सत्यसंधं महेष्वासं भृशं मे व्यथितं मनः ॥३९॥
transliteration: taṁ droṇaṁ nihataṁ śrutvā dhṛṣṭadyumnena saṁyuge ,satyasaṁdhaṁ maheṣvāsaṁ bhṛśaṁ me vyathitaṁ manaḥ.
english: Having heard that Droṇa, who was truthful (satyasaṃdha) and a great archer, was slain in battle by Dhṛṣṭadyumna, my mind is greatly distressed.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 1 Verse 39. Having heard that Droṇa, who was truthful (satyasaṃdha) and a great archer, was slain in battle by Dhṛṣṭadyumna, my mind is greatly distressed.

sanskrit: त्रैलोक्ये यस्य शास्त्रेषु न पुमान्विद्यते समः ।तं द्रोणं निहतं श्रुत्वा किमकुर्वत मामकाः ॥४०॥
transliteration: trailokye yasya śāstreṣu na pumānvidyate samaḥ ,taṁ droṇaṁ nihataṁ śrutvā kimakurvata māmakāḥ.
english: In all three worlds, no man is found to be his equal in the sciences. Having heard that Drona was slain, what did my people do?
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 1 Verse 40. In all three worlds, no man is found to be his equal in the sciences. Having heard that Drona was slain, what did my people do?

sanskrit: संशप्तकानां च बले पाण्डवेन महात्मना ।धनंजयेन विक्रम्य गमिते यमसादनम् ॥४१॥
transliteration: saṁśaptakānāṁ ca bale pāṇḍavena mahātmanā ,dhanaṁjayena vikramya gamite yamasādanam.
english: And when the army of the Saṃśaptakas had been sent to the abode of Yama by the great-souled (mahātman) Pāṇḍava, Dhanañjaya, who displayed valor.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 1 Verse 41. And when the army of the Saṃśaptakas had been sent to the abode of Yama by the great-souled (mahātman) Pāṇḍava, Dhanañjaya, who displayed valor.

sanskrit: नारायणास्त्रे निहते द्रोणपुत्रस्य धीमतः ।हतशेषेष्वनीकेषु किमकुर्वत मामकाः ॥४२॥
transliteration: nārāyaṇāstre nihate droṇaputrasya dhīmataḥ ,hataśeṣeṣvanīkeṣu kimakurvata māmakāḥ.
english: When the Nārāyaṇāstra of the intelligent son of Drona (Aśvatthāman) was rendered ineffective, what did my people do amidst the armies that remained after the slaughter?
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 1 Verse 42. When the Nārāyaṇāstra of the intelligent son of Drona (Aśvatthāman) was rendered ineffective, what did my people do amidst the armies that remained after the slaughter?

sanskrit: विप्रद्रुतानहं मन्ये निमग्नः शोकसागरे ।प्लवमानान्हते द्रोणे सन्ननौकानिवार्णवे ॥४३॥
transliteration: vipradrutānahaṁ manye nimagnaḥ śokasāgare ,plavamānānhate droṇe sannanaukānivārṇave.
english: I myself am submerged in an ocean of sorrow. I imagine my people, scattered and adrift when Drona was slain, like foundering ships in the ocean.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 1 Verse 43. I myself am submerged in an ocean of sorrow. I imagine my people, scattered and adrift when Drona was slain, like foundering ships in the ocean.

sanskrit: दुर्योधनस्य कर्णस्य भोजस्य कृतवर्मणः ।मद्रराजस्य शल्यस्य द्रौणेश्चैव कृपस्य च ॥४४॥
transliteration: duryodhanasya karṇasya bhojasya kṛtavarmaṇaḥ ,madrarājasya śalyasya drauṇeścaiva kṛpasya ca.
english: Regarding Duryodhana, Karṇa, Bhoja, Kṛtavarman, Śalya (the king of Madra), Drauṇi, and Kṛpa.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 1 Verse 44. Regarding Duryodhana, Karṇa, Bhoja, Kṛtavarman, Śalya (the king of Madra), Drauṇi, and Kṛpa.

sanskrit: मत्पुत्रशेषस्य तथा तथान्येषां च संजय ।विप्रकीर्णेष्वनीकेषु मुखवर्णोऽभवत्कथम् ॥४५॥
transliteration: matputraśeṣasya tathā tathānyeṣāṁ ca saṁjaya ,viprakīrṇeṣvanīkeṣu mukhavarṇo'bhavatkatham.
english: And how, O Sañjaya, was the complexion of my surviving sons and of others in the scattered ranks of the armies?
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 1 Verse 45. And how, O Sañjaya, was the complexion of my surviving sons and of others in the scattered ranks of the armies?

sanskrit: एतत्सर्वं यथा वृत्तं तत्त्वं गावल्गणे रणे ।आचक्ष्व पाण्डवेयानां मामकानां च सर्वशः ॥४६॥
transliteration: etatsarvaṁ yathā vṛttaṁ tattvaṁ gāvalgaṇe raṇe ,ācakṣva pāṇḍaveyānāṁ māmakānāṁ ca sarvaśaḥ.
english: O Gāvalgaṇi (Sañjaya), relate to me fully the entire truth of all that transpired in the battle concerning both the Pāṇḍavas and my own sons.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 1 Verse 46. O Gāvalgaṇi (Sañjaya), relate to me fully the entire truth of all that transpired in the battle concerning both the Pāṇḍavas and my own sons.

sanskrit: संजय उवाच ।पाण्डवेयैर्हि यद्वृत्तं कौरवेयेषु मारिष ।तच्छ्रुत्वा मा व्यथां कार्षीर्दिष्टे न व्यथते मनः ॥४७॥
transliteration: saṁjaya uvāca ,pāṇḍaveyairhi yadvṛttaṁ kauraveyeṣu māriṣa ,tacchrutvā mā vyathāṁ kārṣīrdiṣṭe na vyathate manaḥ.
english: Sañjaya said: "O respected one, having heard what indeed transpired concerning the Kauravas at the hands of the Pāṇḍavas, do not be distressed, for the mind does not grieve over what is destined."
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 1 Verse 47. Sañjaya said: "O respected one, having heard what indeed transpired concerning the Kauravas at the hands of the Pāṇḍavas, do not be distressed, for the mind does not grieve over what is destined."

sanskrit: यस्मादभावी भावी वा भवेदर्थो नरं प्रति ।अप्राप्तौ तस्य वा प्राप्तौ न कश्चिद्व्यथते बुधः ॥४८॥
transliteration: yasmādabhāvī bhāvī vā bhavedartho naraṁ prati ,aprāptau tasya vā prāptau na kaścidvyathate budhaḥ.
english: A discerning person (budha) is never agitated or distressed regarding a matter, whether it concerns something that is destined not to be or something that is destined to be, nor does he become distressed over its non-attainment or attainment.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 1 Verse 48. A discerning person (budha) is never agitated or distressed regarding a matter, whether it concerns something that is destined not to be or something that is destined to be, nor does he become distressed over its non-attainment or attainment.

sanskrit: धृतराष्ट्र उवाच ।न व्यथा शृण्वतः काचिद्विद्यते मम संजय ।दिष्टमेतत्पुरा मन्ये कथयस्व यथेच्छकम् ॥४९॥
transliteration: dhṛtarāṣṭra uvāca ,na vyathā śṛṇvataḥ kācidvidyate mama saṁjaya ,diṣṭametatpurā manye kathayasva yathecchakam.
english: Dhritarashtra said: "O Sanjaya, I feel no distress (vyathā) while listening. I believe that this was all ordained by destiny (diṣṭa) long ago. Therefore, please narrate whatever you wish."
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 1 Verse 49. Dhritarashtra said: "O Sanjaya, I feel no distress (vyathā) while listening. I believe that this was all ordained by destiny (diṣṭa) long ago. Therefore, please narrate whatever you wish."

sanskrit: संजय उवाच ।हते द्रोणे महेष्वासे तव पुत्रा महारथाः ।बभूवुराश्वस्तमुखा विषण्णा गतचेतसः ॥१॥
transliteration: saṁjaya uvāca ,hate droṇe maheṣvāse tava putrā mahārathāḥ ,babhūvurāśvastamukhā viṣaṇṇā gatacetasaḥ.
english: Sanjaya said: "When Drona, the great archer, was killed, your sons, those great warriors (mahārathāḥ), became disheartened, dejected, and utterly bewildered, with their faces downcast."
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 2 Verse 1. Sanjaya said: "When Drona, the great archer, was killed, your sons, those great warriors (mahārathāḥ), became disheartened, dejected, and utterly bewildered, with their faces downcast."

sanskrit: अवाङ्मुखाः शस्त्रभृतः सर्व एव विशां पते ।अप्रेक्षमाणाः शोकार्ता नाभ्यभाषन्परस्परम् ॥२॥
transliteration: avāṅmukhāḥ śastrabhṛtaḥ sarva eva viśāṁ pate ,aprekṣamāṇāḥ śokārtā nābhyabhāṣanparasparam.
english: O Lord of the People (viśāṃ pate), all of them, bearing their weapons, stood with their faces downcast, overwhelmed by grief, not looking at each other, and not speaking to one another.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 2 Verse 2. O Lord of the People (viśāṃ pate), all of them, bearing their weapons, stood with their faces downcast, overwhelmed by grief, not looking at each other, and not speaking to one another.

sanskrit: तान्दृष्ट्वा व्यथिताकारान्सैन्यानि तव भारत ।ऊर्ध्वमेवाभ्यवेक्षन्त दुःखत्रस्तान्यनेकशः ॥३॥
transliteration: tāndṛṣṭvā vyathitākārānsainyāni tava bhārata ,ūrdhvamevābhyavekṣanta duḥkhatrastānyanekaśaḥ.
english: O Bhārata, seeing those (soldiers) with distressed appearances, your armies, repeatedly tormented by sorrow, indeed looked upwards.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 2 Verse 3. O Bhārata, seeing those (soldiers) with distressed appearances, your armies, repeatedly tormented by sorrow, indeed looked upwards.

sanskrit: शस्त्राण्येषां च राजेन्द्र शोणिताक्तान्यशेषतः ।प्राभ्रश्यन्त कराग्रेभ्यो दृष्ट्वा द्रोणं निपातितम् ॥४॥
transliteration: śastrāṇyeṣāṁ ca rājendra śoṇitāktānyaśeṣataḥ ,prābhraśyanta karāgrebhyo dṛṣṭvā droṇaṁ nipātitam.
english: And their weapons, O king of kings, completely smeared with blood, fell from their fingertips upon seeing Droṇa struck down.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 2 Verse 4. And their weapons, O king of kings, completely smeared with blood, fell from their fingertips upon seeing Droṇa struck down.

sanskrit: तानि बद्धान्यनिष्टानि लम्बमानानि भारत ।अदृश्यन्त महाराज नक्षत्राणि यथा दिवि ॥५॥
transliteration: tāni baddhānyaniṣṭāni lambamānāni bhārata ,adṛśyanta mahārāja nakṣatrāṇi yathā divi.
english: O Bhārata, O great king, those weapons, formerly held but now undesirable, were seen lying scattered, appearing like stars in the sky.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 2 Verse 5. O Bhārata, O great king, those weapons, formerly held but now undesirable, were seen lying scattered, appearing like stars in the sky.

sanskrit: तथार्तं स्तिमितं दृष्ट्वा गतसत्त्वमिव स्थितम् ।स्वं बलं तन्महाराज राजा दुर्योधनोऽब्रवीत् ॥६॥
transliteration: tathārtaṁ stimitaṁ dṛṣṭvā gatasattvamiva sthitam ,svaṁ balaṁ tanmahārāja rājā duryodhano'bravīt.
english: Thus, O great king, seeing his own army distressed, stunned, and standing as if devoid of vitality, King Duryodhana then spoke.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 2 Verse 6. Thus, O great king, seeing his own army distressed, stunned, and standing as if devoid of vitality, King Duryodhana then spoke.

sanskrit: भवतां बाहुवीर्यं हि समाश्रित्य मया युधि ।पाण्डवेयाः समाहूता युद्धं चेदं प्रवर्तितम् ॥७॥
transliteration: bhavatāṁ bāhuvīryaṁ hi samāśritya mayā yudhi ,pāṇḍaveyāḥ samāhūtā yuddhaṁ cedaṁ pravartitam.
english: Indeed, it was by relying on your valor and strength in battle that I challenged the Pāṇḍavas, and this war was initiated.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 2 Verse 7. Indeed, it was by relying on your valor and strength in battle that I challenged the Pāṇḍavas, and this war was initiated.

sanskrit: तदिदं निहते द्रोणे विषण्णमिव लक्ष्यते ।युध्यमानाश्च समरे योधा वध्यन्ति सर्वतः ॥८॥
transliteration: tadidaṁ nihate droṇe viṣaṇṇamiva lakṣyate ,yudhyamānāśca samare yodhā vadhyanti sarvataḥ.
english: Now that Droṇa has been killed, this (army) appears dejected. And warriors fighting in battle are being slain everywhere.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 2 Verse 8. Now that Droṇa has been killed, this (army) appears dejected. And warriors fighting in battle are being slain everywhere.

sanskrit: जयो वापि वधो वापि युध्यमानस्य संयुगे ।भवेत्किमत्र चित्रं वै युध्यध्वं सर्वतोमुखाः ॥९॥
transliteration: jayo vāpi vadho vāpi yudhyamānasya saṁyuge ,bhavetkimatra citraṁ vai yudhyadhvaṁ sarvatomukhāḥ.
english: For one fighting in battle, either victory or death may occur; what wonder is there in that? Indeed, fight fiercely, facing all directions!
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 2 Verse 9. For one fighting in battle, either victory or death may occur; what wonder is there in that? Indeed, fight fiercely, facing all directions!

sanskrit: पश्यध्वं च महात्मानं कर्णं वैकर्तनं युधि ।प्रचरन्तं महेष्वासं दिव्यैरस्त्रैर्महाबलम् ॥१०॥
transliteration: paśyadhvaṁ ca mahātmānaṁ karṇaṁ vaikartanaṁ yudhi ,pracarantaṁ maheṣvāsaṁ divyairastrairmahābalam.
english: And behold the great-souled Karṇa, son of Vīkarta, fighting in battle, the mighty archer, immensely powerful with divine weapons!
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 2 Verse 10. And behold the great-souled Karṇa, son of Vīkarta, fighting in battle, the mighty archer, immensely powerful with divine weapons!

sanskrit: यस्य वै युधि संत्रासात्कुन्तीपुत्रो धनंजयः ।निवर्तते सदामर्षात्सिंहात्क्षुद्रमृगो यथा ॥११॥
transliteration: yasya vai yudhi saṁtrāsātkuntīputro dhanaṁjayaḥ ,nivartate sadāmarṣātsiṁhātkṣudramṛgo yathā.
english: Indeed, from the great fear of whom in battle, Arjuna (Kuntīputra), the conqueror of wealth (Dhanañjaya), turns back, filled with constant indignation, just as a small deer turns from a lion.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 2 Verse 11. Indeed, from the great fear of whom in battle, Arjuna (Kuntīputra), the conqueror of wealth (Dhanañjaya), turns back, filled with constant indignation, just as a small deer turns from a lion.

sanskrit: येन नागायुतप्राणो भीमसेनो महाबलः ।मानुषेणैव युद्धेन तामवस्थां प्रवेशितः ॥१२॥
transliteration: yena nāgāyutaprāṇo bhīmaseno mahābalaḥ ,mānuṣeṇaiva yuddhena tāmavasthāṁ praveśitaḥ.
english: By whom Bhīmasena, who possessed the strength of ten thousand elephants (nāgāyutaprāṇa) and was exceedingly mighty (mahābala), was driven to that condition by a purely human combat.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 2 Verse 12. By whom Bhīmasena, who possessed the strength of ten thousand elephants (nāgāyutaprāṇa) and was exceedingly mighty (mahābala), was driven to that condition by a purely human combat.

sanskrit: येन दिव्यास्त्रविच्छूरो मायावी स घटोत्कचः ।अमोघया रणे शक्त्या निहतो भैरवं नदन् ॥१३॥
transliteration: yena divyāstravicchūro māyāvī sa ghaṭotkacaḥ ,amoghayā raṇe śaktyā nihato bhairavaṁ nadan.
english: By whom that heroic Ghaṭotkaca, who was skilled in divine weapons (divyāstravit) and an illusionist (māyāvī), was killed in battle by an unfailing weapon (śakti), even as he roared terribly.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 2 Verse 13. By whom that heroic Ghaṭotkaca, who was skilled in divine weapons (divyāstravit) and an illusionist (māyāvī), was killed in battle by an unfailing weapon (śakti), even as he roared terribly.

sanskrit: तस्य दुष्पारवीर्यस्य सत्यसंधस्य धीमतः ।बाह्वोर्द्रविणमक्षय्यमद्य द्रक्ष्यथ संयुगे ॥१४॥
transliteration: tasya duṣpāravīryasya satyasaṁdhasya dhīmataḥ ,bāhvordraviṇamakṣayyamadya drakṣyatha saṁyuge.
english: Today, in battle (saṃyuga), you will witness the inexhaustible wealth (draviṇa) in the arms of that wise (dhīmat) man, whose valor (vīrya) is insurmountable and who is firm in his truth (satyasaṃdha).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 2 Verse 14. Today, in battle (saṃyuga), you will witness the inexhaustible wealth (draviṇa) in the arms of that wise (dhīmat) man, whose valor (vīrya) is insurmountable and who is firm in his truth (satyasaṃdha).

sanskrit: द्रोणपुत्रस्य विक्रान्तं राधेयस्यैव चोभयोः ।पाण्डुपाञ्चालसैन्येषु द्रक्ष्यथापि महात्मनोः ॥१५॥
transliteration: droṇaputrasya vikrāntaṁ rādheyasyaiva cobhayoḥ ,pāṇḍupāñcālasainyeṣu drakṣyathāpi mahātmanoḥ.
english: You will also see the prowess of both Drona's son (Aśvatthāmā) and Karna, the son of Radha (Rādheya), these great souls (mahātman), among the armies of the Pandavas and Panchalas.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 2 Verse 15. You will also see the prowess of both Drona's son (Aśvatthāmā) and Karna, the son of Radha (Rādheya), these great souls (mahātman), among the armies of the Pandavas and Panchalas.

sanskrit: सर्व एव भवन्तश्च शूराः प्राज्ञाः कुलोद्गताः ।शीलवन्तः कृतास्त्राश्च द्रक्ष्यथाद्य परस्परम् ॥१६॥
transliteration: sarva eva bhavantaśca śūrāḥ prājñāḥ kulodgatāḥ ,śīlavantaḥ kṛtāstrāśca drakṣyathādya parasparam.
english: Indeed, all of you  -  who are brave, wise, of noble birth, virtuous, and skilled in weaponry  -  will witness each other today.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 2 Verse 16. Indeed, all of you  -  who are brave, wise, of noble birth, virtuous, and skilled in weaponry  -  will witness each other today.

sanskrit: एवमुक्ते महाराज कर्णो वैकर्तनो नृपः ।सिंहनादं विनद्योच्चैः प्रायुध्यत महाबलः ॥१७॥
transliteration: evamukte mahārāja karṇo vaikartano nṛpaḥ ,siṁhanādaṁ vinadyoccaiḥ prāyudhyata mahābalaḥ.
english: O great king, when this was said, Karna, the son of the Sun god (Vikartana), who was a mighty warrior and king, let out a loud lion's roar and began to fight.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 2 Verse 17. O great king, when this was said, Karna, the son of the Sun god (Vikartana), who was a mighty warrior and king, let out a loud lion's roar and began to fight.

sanskrit: स सृञ्जयानां सर्वेषां पाञ्चालानां च पश्यताम् ।केकयानां विदेहानामकरोत्कदनं महत् ॥१८॥
transliteration: sa sṛñjayānāṁ sarveṣāṁ pāñcālānāṁ ca paśyatām ,kekayānāṁ videhānāmakarotkadanaṁ mahat.
english: While all of the Srinjayas, Panchalas, Kekayas, and Videhas watched, he (Karna) wrought great slaughter upon them.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 2 Verse 18. While all of the Srinjayas, Panchalas, Kekayas, and Videhas watched, he (Karna) wrought great slaughter upon them.

sanskrit: तस्येषुधाराः शतशः प्रादुरासञ्शरासनात् ।अग्रे पुङ्खे च संसक्ता यथा भ्रमरपङ्क्तयः ॥१९॥
transliteration: tasyeṣudhārāḥ śataśaḥ prādurāsañśarāsanāt ,agre puṅkhe ca saṁsaktā yathā bhramarapaṅktayaḥ.
english: Hundreds of streams of arrows appeared from his bow, connecting at the front and at the feather-end, just like rows of bees.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 2 Verse 19. Hundreds of streams of arrows appeared from his bow, connecting at the front and at the feather-end, just like rows of bees.

sanskrit: स पीडयित्वा पाञ्चालान्पाण्डवांश्च तरस्विनः ।हत्वा सहस्रशो योधानर्जुनेन निपातितः ॥२०॥
transliteration: sa pīḍayitvā pāñcālānpāṇḍavāṁśca tarasvinaḥ ,hatvā sahasraśo yodhānarjunena nipātitaḥ.
english: Having tormented the mighty Pāñcālas and Pāṇḍavas, and having slain thousands of warriors, he was felled by Arjuna.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 2 Verse 20. Having tormented the mighty Pāñcālas and Pāṇḍavas, and having slain thousands of warriors, he was felled by Arjuna.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।एतच्छ्रुत्वा महाराज धृतराष्ट्रोऽम्बिकासुतः ।शोकस्यान्तमपश्यन्वै हतं मत्वा सुयोधनम् ।विह्वलः पतितो भूमौ नष्टचेता इव द्विपः ॥१॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,etacchrutvā mahārāja dhṛtarāṣṭro'mbikāsutaḥ ,śokasyāntamapaśyanvai hataṁ matvā suyodhanam ,vihvalaḥ patito bhūmau naṣṭacetā iva dvipaḥ.
english: Vaiśaṃpāyana said: "O great king, upon hearing this, Dhṛtarāṣṭra, the son of Ambikā, found no end to his grief (śoka), having considered Suyodhana slain. Distraught, he collapsed to the ground, like an elephant whose mind was lost, as if unconscious."
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 3 Verse 1. Vaiśaṃpāyana said: "O great king, upon hearing this, Dhṛtarāṣṭra, the son of Ambikā, found no end to his grief (śoka), having considered Suyodhana slain. Distraught, he collapsed to the ground, like an elephant whose mind was lost, as if unconscious."

sanskrit: तस्मिन्निपतिते भूमौ विह्वले राजसत्तमे ।आर्तनादो महानासीत्स्त्रीणां भरतसत्तम ॥२॥
transliteration: tasminnipatite bhūmau vihvale rājasattame ,ārtanādo mahānāsītstrīṇāṁ bharatasattama.
english: O best among the Bharatas, when that distraught, most excellent of kings (Dhṛtarāṣṭra) fell to the ground, there arose a great cry of lamentation from the women.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 3 Verse 2. O best among the Bharatas, when that distraught, most excellent of kings (Dhṛtarāṣṭra) fell to the ground, there arose a great cry of lamentation from the women.

sanskrit: स शब्दः पृथिवीं सर्वां पूरयामास सर्वशः ।शोकार्णवे महाघोरे निमग्ना भरतस्त्रियः ॥३॥
transliteration: sa śabdaḥ pṛthivīṁ sarvāṁ pūrayāmāsa sarvaśaḥ ,śokārṇave mahāghore nimagnā bharatastriyaḥ.
english: That sound completely filled the entire earth. The women of the Bharata lineage were submerged in a dreadful ocean of sorrow.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 3 Verse 3. That sound completely filled the entire earth. The women of the Bharata lineage were submerged in a dreadful ocean of sorrow.

sanskrit: राजानं च समासाद्य गान्धारी भरतर्षभ ।निःसंज्ञा पतिता भूमौ सर्वाण्यन्तःपुराणि च ॥४॥
transliteration: rājānaṁ ca samāsādya gāndhārī bharatarṣabha ,niḥsaṁjñā patitā bhūmau sarvāṇyantaḥpurāṇi ca.
english: O bull among the Bhāratas, having approached the king, Gāndhārī fell unconscious on the ground, and all the ladies of the inner apartments also collapsed.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 3 Verse 4. O bull among the Bhāratas, having approached the king, Gāndhārī fell unconscious on the ground, and all the ladies of the inner apartments also collapsed.

sanskrit: ततस्ताः संजयो राजन्समाश्वासयदातुराः ।मुह्यमानाः सुबहुशो मुञ्चन्त्यो वारि नेत्रजम् ॥५॥
transliteration: tatastāḥ saṁjayo rājansamāśvāsayadāturāḥ ,muhyamānāḥ subahuśo muñcantyo vāri netrajam.
english: O King, then Saṃjaya consoled those distressed women, who were repeatedly fainting and shedding tears from their eyes.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 3 Verse 5. O King, then Saṃjaya consoled those distressed women, who were repeatedly fainting and shedding tears from their eyes.

sanskrit: समाश्वस्ताः स्त्रियस्तास्तु वेपमाना मुहुर्मुहुः ।कदल्य इव वातेन धूयमानाः समन्ततः ॥६॥
transliteration: samāśvastāḥ striyastāstu vepamānā muhurmuhuḥ ,kadalya iva vātena dhūyamānāḥ samantataḥ.
english: But even when comforted, those women were repeatedly trembling and being shaken from all sides by the wind, just like plantain trees.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 3 Verse 6. But even when comforted, those women were repeatedly trembling and being shaken from all sides by the wind, just like plantain trees.

sanskrit: राजानं विदुरश्चापि प्रज्ञाचक्षुषमीश्वरम् ।आश्वासयामास तदा सिञ्चंस्तोयेन कौरवम् ॥७॥
transliteration: rājānaṁ viduraścāpi prajñācakṣuṣamīśvaram ,āśvāsayāmāsa tadā siñcaṁstoyena kauravam.
english: Then Vidura also consoled the King (Dhṛtarāṣṭra), the ruler who possessed the eye of wisdom, sprinkling the Kuru with water.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 3 Verse 7. Then Vidura also consoled the King (Dhṛtarāṣṭra), the ruler who possessed the eye of wisdom, sprinkling the Kuru with water.

sanskrit: स लब्ध्वा शनकैः संज्ञां ताश्च दृष्ट्वा स्त्रियो नृप ।उन्मत्त इव राजा स स्थितस्तूष्णीं विशां पते ॥८॥
transliteration: sa labdhvā śanakaiḥ saṁjñāṁ tāśca dṛṣṭvā striyo nṛpa ,unmatta iva rājā sa sthitastūṣṇīṁ viśāṁ pate.
english: O King, O Lord of the people, after slowly regaining consciousness and seeing those women, that king remained silent as if he were mad.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 3 Verse 8. O King, O Lord of the people, after slowly regaining consciousness and seeing those women, that king remained silent as if he were mad.

sanskrit: ततो ध्यात्वा चिरं कालं निःश्वसंश्च पुनः पुनः ।स्वान्पुत्रान्गर्हयामास बहु मेने च पाण्डवान् ॥९॥
transliteration: tato dhyātvā ciraṁ kālaṁ niḥśvasaṁśca punaḥ punaḥ ,svānputrāngarhayāmāsa bahu mene ca pāṇḍavān.
english: Then, after pondering (dhyāna) for a long time and sighing repeatedly, he condemned his own sons and held the Pāṇḍavas in high esteem.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 3 Verse 9. Then, after pondering (dhyāna) for a long time and sighing repeatedly, he condemned his own sons and held the Pāṇḍavas in high esteem.

sanskrit: गर्हयित्वात्मनो बुद्धिं शकुनेः सौबलस्य च ।ध्यात्वा च सुचिरं कालं वेपमानो मुहुर्मुहुः ॥१०॥
transliteration: garhayitvātmano buddhiṁ śakuneḥ saubalasya ca ,dhyātvā ca suciraṁ kālaṁ vepamāno muhurmuhuḥ.
english: Having condemned his own judgment (buddhi) and that of Śakuni, the son of Subala, and having pondered (dhyāna) for a very long time, he trembled repeatedly.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 3 Verse 10. Having condemned his own judgment (buddhi) and that of Śakuni, the son of Subala, and having pondered (dhyāna) for a very long time, he trembled repeatedly.

sanskrit: संस्तभ्य च मनो भूयो राजा धैर्यसमन्वितः ।पुनर्गावल्गणिं सूतं पर्यपृच्छत संजयम् ॥११॥
transliteration: saṁstabhya ca mano bhūyo rājā dhairyasamanvitaḥ ,punargāvalgaṇiṁ sūtaṁ paryapṛcchata saṁjayam.
english: The king, endowed with fortitude, again controlled his mind and questioned Saṃjaya, the charioteer (son of Gavalgaṇa), once more.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 3 Verse 11. The king, endowed with fortitude, again controlled his mind and questioned Saṃjaya, the charioteer (son of Gavalgaṇa), once more.

sanskrit: यत्त्वया कथितं वाक्यं श्रुतं संजय तन्मया ।कच्चिद्दुर्योधनः सूत न गतो वै यमक्षयम् ।ब्रूहि संजय तत्त्वेन पुनरुक्तां कथामिमाम् ॥१२॥
transliteration: yattvayā kathitaṁ vākyaṁ śrutaṁ saṁjaya tanmayā ,kaccidduryodhanaḥ sūta na gato vai yamakṣayam ,brūhi saṁjaya tattvena punaruktāṁ kathāmimām.
english: O Saṃjaya, I have heard the words you spoke. Tell me, charioteer, has Duryodhana indeed not gone to the abode of Yama? O Saṃjaya, recount this story to me truthfully.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 3 Verse 12. O Saṃjaya, I have heard the words you spoke. Tell me, charioteer, has Duryodhana indeed not gone to the abode of Yama? O Saṃjaya, recount this story to me truthfully.

sanskrit: एवमुक्तोऽब्रवीत्सूतो राजानं जनमेजय ।हतो वैकर्तनो राजन्सह पुत्रैर्महारथैः ।भ्रातृभिश्च महेष्वासैः सूतपुत्रैस्तनुत्यजैः ॥१३॥
transliteration: evamukto'bravītsūto rājānaṁ janamejaya ,hato vaikartano rājansaha putrairmahārathaiḥ ,bhrātṛbhiśca maheṣvāsaiḥ sūtaputraistanutyajaiḥ.
english: The charioteer, thus addressed, spoke to the king. 'O King, Karṇa (the son of Vikartana) has been slain along with his great warrior sons, and with his brothers, the mighty archers who sacrificed their lives.'
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 3 Verse 13. The charioteer, thus addressed, spoke to the king. 'O King, Karṇa (the son of Vikartana) has been slain along with his great warrior sons, and with his brothers, the mighty archers who sacrificed their lives.'

sanskrit: दुःशासनश्च निहतः पाण्डवेन यशस्विना ।पीतं च रुधिरं कोपाद्भीमसेनेन संयुगे ॥१४॥
transliteration: duḥśāsanaśca nihataḥ pāṇḍavena yaśasvinā ,pītaṁ ca rudhiraṁ kopādbhīmasenena saṁyuge.
english: And Duḥśāsana was slain by the glorious Pāṇḍava. And his blood was drunk by Bhīmasena in battle out of rage.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 3 Verse 14. And Duḥśāsana was slain by the glorious Pāṇḍava. And his blood was drunk by Bhīmasena in battle out of rage.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।एतच्छ्रुत्वा महाराज धृतराष्ट्रोऽम्बिकासुतः ।अब्रवीत्संजयं सूतं शोकव्याकुलचेतनः ॥१॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,etacchrutvā mahārāja dhṛtarāṣṭro'mbikāsutaḥ ,abravītsaṁjayaṁ sūtaṁ śokavyākulacetanaḥ.
english: Vaiśaṃpāyana said: "Having heard this, O great king, Dhṛtarāṣṭra, the son of Ambikā, whose mind was bewildered by sorrow, spoke to Sañjaya, the charioteer."
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 4 Verse 1. Vaiśaṃpāyana said: "Having heard this, O great king, Dhṛtarāṣṭra, the son of Ambikā, whose mind was bewildered by sorrow, spoke to Sañjaya, the charioteer."

sanskrit: दुष्प्रणीतेन मे तात मनसाभिप्लुतात्मनः ।हतं वैकर्तनं श्रुत्वा शोको मर्माणि कृन्तति ॥२॥
transliteration: duṣpraṇītena me tāta manasābhiplutātmanaḥ ,hataṁ vaikartanaṁ śrutvā śoko marmāṇi kṛntati.
english: O dear one (tāta), for me, whose being (ātman) is overwhelmed by an ill-guided mind, upon hearing that Vikartana's son (Karṇa) has been slain, sorrow pierces my vital parts.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 4 Verse 2. O dear one (tāta), for me, whose being (ātman) is overwhelmed by an ill-guided mind, upon hearing that Vikartana's son (Karṇa) has been slain, sorrow pierces my vital parts.

sanskrit: कृतास्त्रपरमाः शल्ये दुःखपारं तितीर्षवः ।कुरूणां सृञ्जयानां च के नु जीवन्ति के मृताः ॥३॥
transliteration: kṛtāstraparamāḥ śalye duḥkhapāraṁ titīrṣavaḥ ,kurūṇāṁ sṛñjayānāṁ ca ke nu jīvanti ke mṛtāḥ.
english: Among the Kurus and the Sṛñjayas – those who held Śalya as their supreme weapon and wished to cross beyond the ocean of suffering – who among them are now alive, and who are dead?
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 4 Verse 3. Among the Kurus and the Sṛñjayas – those who held Śalya as their supreme weapon and wished to cross beyond the ocean of suffering – who among them are now alive, and who are dead?

sanskrit: संजय उवाच ।हतः शांतनवो राजन्दुराधर्षः प्रतापवान् ।हत्वा पाण्डवयोधानामर्बुदं दशभिर्दिनैः ॥४॥
transliteration: saṁjaya uvāca ,hataḥ śāṁtanavo rājandurādharṣaḥ pratāpavān ,hatvā pāṇḍavayodhānāmarbudaṁ daśabhirdinaiḥ.
english: Sañjaya said: "O King, the son of Śāntanu (Bhīṣma), that invincible and glorious warrior, has been slain, but only after he had killed ten crores (an arbuda) of Pāṇḍava warriors within ten days."
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 4 Verse 4. Sañjaya said: "O King, the son of Śāntanu (Bhīṣma), that invincible and glorious warrior, has been slain, but only after he had killed ten crores (an arbuda) of Pāṇḍava warriors within ten days."

sanskrit: ततो द्रोणो महेष्वासः पाञ्चालानां रथव्रजान् ।निहत्य युधि दुर्धर्षः पश्चाद्रुक्मरथो हतः ॥५॥
transliteration: tato droṇo maheṣvāsaḥ pāñcālānāṁ rathavrajān ,nihatya yudhi durdharṣaḥ paścādrukmaratho hataḥ.
english: Then Drona, the great archer (maheṣvāsaḥ) and formidable (durdharṣaḥ) in battle, having destroyed the throngs of chariots belonging to the Pancalas, Rukmaratha was subsequently killed.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 4 Verse 5. Then Drona, the great archer (maheṣvāsaḥ) and formidable (durdharṣaḥ) in battle, having destroyed the throngs of chariots belonging to the Pancalas, Rukmaratha was subsequently killed.

sanskrit: हतशिष्टस्य भीष्मेण द्रोणेन च महात्मना ।अर्धं निहत्य सैन्यस्य कर्णो वैकर्तनो हतः ॥६॥
transliteration: hataśiṣṭasya bhīṣmeṇa droṇena ca mahātmanā ,ardhaṁ nihatya sainyasya karṇo vaikartano hataḥ.
english: After a portion of the army had been slain by Bhishma and the great-souled (mahātmanā) Drona, Karna, the son of Vikartana (vaikartano), having destroyed half of the remaining forces, was himself killed.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 4 Verse 6. After a portion of the army had been slain by Bhishma and the great-souled (mahātmanā) Drona, Karna, the son of Vikartana (vaikartano), having destroyed half of the remaining forces, was himself killed.

sanskrit: विविंशतिर्महाराज राजपुत्रो महाबलः ।आनर्तयोधाञ्शतशो निहत्य निहतो रणे ॥७॥
transliteration: viviṁśatirmahārāja rājaputro mahābalaḥ ,ānartayodhāñśataśo nihatya nihato raṇe.
english: O great king (mahārāja), prince (rājaputraḥ) Vivimshati, who was very powerful (mahābalaḥ), having killed hundreds of Anarta warriors (ānartayodhān), was himself killed in battle (raṇe).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 4 Verse 7. O great king (mahārāja), prince (rājaputraḥ) Vivimshati, who was very powerful (mahābalaḥ), having killed hundreds of Anarta warriors (ānartayodhān), was himself killed in battle (raṇe).

sanskrit: अथ पुत्रो विकर्णस्ते क्षत्रव्रतमनुस्मरन् ।क्षीणवाहायुधः शूरः स्थितोऽभिमुखतः परान् ॥८॥
transliteration: atha putro vikarṇaste kṣatravratamanusmaran ,kṣīṇavāhāyudhaḥ śūraḥ sthito'bhimukhataḥ parān.
english: Now, your son (putraḥ) Vikarna, remembering the warrior's code of conduct (kṣatravrata) (dharma), stood bravely (śūraḥ) facing (abhimukhataḥ) the enemies (parān), despite his steeds and weapons being exhausted (kṣīṇavāhāyudhaḥ).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 4 Verse 8. Now, your son (putraḥ) Vikarna, remembering the warrior's code of conduct (kṣatravrata) (dharma), stood bravely (śūraḥ) facing (abhimukhataḥ) the enemies (parān), despite his steeds and weapons being exhausted (kṣīṇavāhāyudhaḥ).

sanskrit: घोररूपान्परिक्लेशान्दुर्योधनकृतान्बहून् ।प्रतिज्ञां स्मरता चैव भीमसेनेन पातितः ॥९॥
transliteration: ghorarūpānparikleśānduryodhanakṛtānbahūn ,pratijñāṁ smaratā caiva bhīmasenena pātitaḥ.
english: Indeed, because of the many dreadful troubles of ghastly nature caused by Duryodhana, he was struck down by Bhimasena, who remembered his vow.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 4 Verse 9. Indeed, because of the many dreadful troubles of ghastly nature caused by Duryodhana, he was struck down by Bhimasena, who remembered his vow.

sanskrit: विन्दानुविन्दावावन्त्यौ राजपुत्रौ महाबलौ ।कृत्वा नसुकरं कर्म गतौ वैवस्वतक्षयम् ॥१०॥
transliteration: vindānuvindāvāvantyau rājaputrau mahābalau ,kṛtvā nasukaraṁ karma gatau vaivasvatakṣayam.
english: The two immensely powerful princes from Avanti, Vinda and Anuvinda, having accomplished a task that was not easy, both departed to the abode of Yama (vaivasvata).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 4 Verse 10. The two immensely powerful princes from Avanti, Vinda and Anuvinda, having accomplished a task that was not easy, both departed to the abode of Yama (vaivasvata).

sanskrit: सिन्धुराष्ट्रमुखानीह दश राष्ट्राणि यस्य वै ।वशे तिष्ठन्ति वीरस्य यः स्थितस्तव शासने ॥११॥
transliteration: sindhurāṣṭramukhānīha daśa rāṣṭrāṇi yasya vai ,vaśe tiṣṭhanti vīrasya yaḥ sthitastava śāsane.
english: Here, indeed, ten kingdoms, headed by the Sindhu region, are under the control of that hero who is steadfast in your command.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 4 Verse 11. Here, indeed, ten kingdoms, headed by the Sindhu region, are under the control of that hero who is steadfast in your command.

sanskrit: अक्षौहिणीर्दशैकां च निर्जित्य निशितैः शरैः ।अर्जुनेन हतो राजन्महावीर्यो जयद्रथः ॥१२॥
transliteration: akṣauhiṇīrdaśaikāṁ ca nirjitya niśitaiḥ śaraiḥ ,arjunena hato rājanmahāvīryo jayadrathaḥ.
english: O King, having conquered eleven Akṣauhiṇīs with sharp arrows, the immensely powerful Jayadratha was killed by Arjuna.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 4 Verse 12. O King, having conquered eleven Akṣauhiṇīs with sharp arrows, the immensely powerful Jayadratha was killed by Arjuna.

sanskrit: तथा दुर्योधनसुतस्तरस्वी युद्धदुर्मदः ।वर्तमानः पितुः शास्त्रे सौभद्रेण निपातितः ॥१३॥
transliteration: tathā duryodhanasutastarasvī yuddhadurmadaḥ ,vartamānaḥ pituḥ śāstre saubhadreṇa nipātitaḥ.
english: Similarly, Duryodhana's mighty son, who was infatuated with battle and acting according to his father's instructions, was struck down by Abhimanyu (the son of Subhadrā).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 4 Verse 13. Similarly, Duryodhana's mighty son, who was infatuated with battle and acting according to his father's instructions, was struck down by Abhimanyu (the son of Subhadrā).

sanskrit: तथा दौःशासनिर्वीरो बाहुशाली रणोत्कटः ।द्रौपदेयेन विक्रम्य गमितो यमसादनम् ॥१४॥
transliteration: tathā dauḥśāsanirvīro bāhuśālī raṇotkaṭaḥ ,draupadeyena vikramya gamito yamasādanam.
english: Similarly, Duḥśāsana's heroic son, mighty-armed and fierce in battle, was valorously sent to Yama's abode by Draupadī's son.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 4 Verse 14. Similarly, Duḥśāsana's heroic son, mighty-armed and fierce in battle, was valorously sent to Yama's abode by Draupadī's son.

sanskrit: किरातानामधिपतिः सागरानूपवासिनाम् ।देवराजस्य धर्मात्मा प्रियो बहुमतः सखा ॥१५॥
transliteration: kirātānāmadhipatiḥ sāgarānūpavāsinām ,devarājasya dharmātmā priyo bahumataḥ sakhā.
english: The chief of the Kirātas, who dwell near the seashore, (he was) righteous (dharmātmā), beloved, and a highly respected friend of the king of gods (Indra).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 4 Verse 15. The chief of the Kirātas, who dwell near the seashore, (he was) righteous (dharmātmā), beloved, and a highly respected friend of the king of gods (Indra).

sanskrit: भगदत्तो महीपालः क्षत्रधर्मरतः सदा ।धनंजयेन विक्रम्य गमितो यमसादनम् ॥१६॥
transliteration: bhagadatto mahīpālaḥ kṣatradharmarataḥ sadā ,dhanaṁjayena vikramya gamito yamasādanam.
english: King Bhagadatta, who was always devoted to the warrior's (kṣatra) intrinsic nature (dharma), was valorously sent to Yama's abode by Dhanaṃjaya (Arjuna).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 4 Verse 16. King Bhagadatta, who was always devoted to the warrior's (kṣatra) intrinsic nature (dharma), was valorously sent to Yama's abode by Dhanaṃjaya (Arjuna).

sanskrit: तथा कौरवदायादः सौमदत्तिर्महायशाः ।हतो भूरिश्रवा राजञ्शूरः सात्यकिना युधि ॥१७॥
transliteration: tathā kauravadāyādaḥ saumadattirmahāyaśāḥ ,hato bhūriśravā rājañśūraḥ sātyakinā yudhi.
english: O King, similarly, Bhurishrava, the renowned son of Somadatta and an heir of the Kauravas, a brave warrior, was killed in battle by Satyaki.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 4 Verse 17. O King, similarly, Bhurishrava, the renowned son of Somadatta and an heir of the Kauravas, a brave warrior, was killed in battle by Satyaki.

sanskrit: श्रुतायुरपि चाम्बष्ठः क्षत्रियाणां धनुर्धरः ।चरन्नभीतवत्संख्ये निहतः सव्यसाचिना ॥१८॥
transliteration: śrutāyurapi cāmbaṣṭhaḥ kṣatriyāṇāṁ dhanurdharaḥ ,carannabhītavatsaṁkhye nihataḥ savyasācinā.
english: And Shrutayu, who was also the Ambaṣṭha king and a renowned archer among the kṣatriyas, was killed fearlessly moving in battle by Savyasācin (Arjuna).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 4 Verse 18. And Shrutayu, who was also the Ambaṣṭha king and a renowned archer among the kṣatriyas, was killed fearlessly moving in battle by Savyasācin (Arjuna).

sanskrit: तव पुत्रः सदा संख्ये कृतास्त्रो युद्धदुर्मदः ।दुःशासनो महाराज भीमसेनेन पातितः ॥१९॥
transliteration: tava putraḥ sadā saṁkhye kṛtāstro yuddhadurmadaḥ ,duḥśāsano mahārāja bhīmasenena pātitaḥ.
english: O great king, your son Duḥśāsana, who was always skilled in weaponry and fierce in battle (yuddha-durmada), was struck down by Bhīmasena.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 4 Verse 19. O great king, your son Duḥśāsana, who was always skilled in weaponry and fierce in battle (yuddha-durmada), was struck down by Bhīmasena.

sanskrit: यस्य राजन्गजानीकं बहुसाहस्रमद्भुतम् ।सुदक्षिणः स संग्रामे निहतः सव्यसाचिना ॥२०॥
transliteration: yasya rājangajānīkaṁ bahusāhasramadbhutam ,sudakṣiṇaḥ sa saṁgrāme nihataḥ savyasācinā.
english: O King, that same Sudakṣiṇa, whose amazing elephant army numbered many thousands, was killed in battle by Savyasācin (Arjuna).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 4 Verse 20. O King, that same Sudakṣiṇa, whose amazing elephant army numbered many thousands, was killed in battle by Savyasācin (Arjuna).

sanskrit: कोसलानामधिपतिर्हत्वा बहुशतान्परान् ।सौभद्रेण हि विक्रम्य गमितो यमसादनम् ॥२१॥
transliteration: kosalānāmadhipatirhatvā bahuśatānparān ,saubhadreṇa hi vikramya gamito yamasādanam.
english: The lord of the Kosalas, having killed many hundreds of enemies, was indeed sent to the abode of Yama by the son of Subhadrā (Abhimanyu) after he displayed his prowess.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 4 Verse 21. The lord of the Kosalas, having killed many hundreds of enemies, was indeed sent to the abode of Yama by the son of Subhadrā (Abhimanyu) after he displayed his prowess.

sanskrit: बहुशो योधयित्वा च भीमसेनं महारथः ।चित्रसेनस्तव सुतो भीमसेनेन पातितः ॥२२॥
transliteration: bahuśo yodhayitvā ca bhīmasenaṁ mahārathaḥ ,citrasenastava suto bhīmasenena pātitaḥ.
english: And your son, Citrasena, a great warrior, after fighting Bhīmasena many times, was struck down by Bhīmasena.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 4 Verse 22. And your son, Citrasena, a great warrior, after fighting Bhīmasena many times, was struck down by Bhīmasena.

sanskrit: मद्रराजात्मजः शूरः परेषां भयवर्धनः ।असिचर्मधरः श्रीमान्सौभद्रेण निपातितः ॥२३॥
transliteration: madrarājātmajaḥ śūraḥ pareṣāṁ bhayavardhanaḥ ,asicarmadharaḥ śrīmānsaubhadreṇa nipātitaḥ.
english: The glorious son of the Madra king, a brave warrior who inspired fear in enemies, bearing a sword and shield, was struck down by the son of Subhadrā (Abhimanyu).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 4 Verse 23. The glorious son of the Madra king, a brave warrior who inspired fear in enemies, bearing a sword and shield, was struck down by the son of Subhadrā (Abhimanyu).

sanskrit: समः कर्णस्य समरे यः स कर्णस्य पश्यतः ।वृषसेनो महातेजाः शीघ्रास्त्रः कृतनिश्चयः ॥२४॥
transliteration: samaḥ karṇasya samare yaḥ sa karṇasya paśyataḥ ,vṛṣaseno mahātejāḥ śīghrāstraḥ kṛtaniścayaḥ.
english: Vṛṣasena, who was equal to Karṇa in battle, greatly powerful, skilled in swift weapons, and resolute, stood there as Karṇa watched.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 4 Verse 24. Vṛṣasena, who was equal to Karṇa in battle, greatly powerful, skilled in swift weapons, and resolute, stood there as Karṇa watched.

sanskrit: अभिमन्योर्वधं स्मृत्वा प्रतिज्ञामपि चात्मनः ।धनंजयेन विक्रम्य गमितो यमसादनम् ॥२५॥
transliteration: abhimanyorvadhaṁ smṛtvā pratijñāmapi cātmanaḥ ,dhanaṁjayena vikramya gamito yamasādanam.
english: Remembering Abhimanyu's death and his own (ātman) vow, (Jayadratha) was sent to the abode of Yama (the god of death) by Dhananjaya (Arjuna), who displayed great valor.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 4 Verse 25. Remembering Abhimanyu's death and his own (ātman) vow, (Jayadratha) was sent to the abode of Yama (the god of death) by Dhananjaya (Arjuna), who displayed great valor.

sanskrit: नित्यप्रसक्तवैरो यः पाण्डवैः पृथिवीपतिः ।विश्राव्य वैरं पार्थेन श्रुतायुः स निपातितः ॥२६॥
transliteration: nityaprasaktavairo yaḥ pāṇḍavaiḥ pṛthivīpatiḥ ,viśrāvya vairaṁ pārthena śrutāyuḥ sa nipātitaḥ.
english: Shrutayus, the king who was constantly engaged in hostility with the Pandavas, after proclaiming his enmity, was struck down by Partha (Arjuna).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 4 Verse 26. Shrutayus, the king who was constantly engaged in hostility with the Pandavas, after proclaiming his enmity, was struck down by Partha (Arjuna).

sanskrit: शल्यपुत्रस्तु विक्रान्तः सहदेवेन मारिष ।हतो रुक्मरथो राजन्भ्राता मातुलजो युधि ॥२७॥
transliteration: śalyaputrastu vikrāntaḥ sahadevena māriṣa ,hato rukmaratho rājanbhrātā mātulajo yudhi.
english: O respected one, O King, Shalya's valiant son Rukmaratha, who was his maternal cousin, was killed by Sahadeva in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 4 Verse 27. O respected one, O King, Shalya's valiant son Rukmaratha, who was his maternal cousin, was killed by Sahadeva in battle.

sanskrit: राजा भगीरथो वृद्धो बृहत्क्षत्रश्च केकयः ।पराक्रमन्तौ विक्रान्तौ निहतौ वीर्यवत्तरौ ॥२८॥
transliteration: rājā bhagīratho vṛddho bṛhatkṣatraśca kekayaḥ ,parākramantau vikrāntau nihatau vīryavattarau.
english: The elder King Bhagiratha and Brihatkṣatra, the Kekaya, both valiant and extremely powerful, were killed while displaying their prowess.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 4 Verse 28. The elder King Bhagiratha and Brihatkṣatra, the Kekaya, both valiant and extremely powerful, were killed while displaying their prowess.

sanskrit: भगदत्तसुतो राजन्कृतप्रज्ञो महाबलः ।श्येनवच्चरता संख्ये नकुलेन निपातितः ॥२९॥
transliteration: bhagadattasuto rājankṛtaprajño mahābalaḥ ,śyenavaccaratā saṁkhye nakulena nipātitaḥ.
english: O King, Bhagadatta's mighty and discerning son was struck down by Nakula in battle, just as he was moving like a hawk.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 4 Verse 29. O King, Bhagadatta's mighty and discerning son was struck down by Nakula in battle, just as he was moving like a hawk.

sanskrit: पितामहस्तव तथा बाह्लिकः सह बाह्लिकैः ।भीमसेनेन विक्रम्य गमितो यमसादनम् ॥३०॥
transliteration: pitāmahastava tathā bāhlikaḥ saha bāhlikaiḥ ,bhīmasenena vikramya gamito yamasādanam.
english: And similarly, your paternal grandfather Bahlika, along with the Bahlikas, was sent to the abode of Yama by Bhimasena, who acted with great valor.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 4 Verse 30. And similarly, your paternal grandfather Bahlika, along with the Bahlikas, was sent to the abode of Yama by Bhimasena, who acted with great valor.

sanskrit: जयत्सेनस्तथा राजञ्जारासंधिर्महाबलः ।मागधो निहतः संख्ये सौभद्रेण महात्मना ॥३१॥
transliteration: jayatsenastathā rājañjārāsaṁdhirmahābalaḥ ,māgadho nihataḥ saṁkhye saubhadreṇa mahātmanā.
english: And similarly, O King, Jayatsena, the mighty Magadhan son of Jarasandha, was slain in battle by the great-souled son of Subhadra (Abhimanyu).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 4 Verse 31. And similarly, O King, Jayatsena, the mighty Magadhan son of Jarasandha, was slain in battle by the great-souled son of Subhadra (Abhimanyu).

sanskrit: पुत्रस्ते दुर्मुखो राजन्दुःसहश्च महारथः ।गदया भीमसेनेन निहतौ शूरमानिनौ ॥३२॥
transliteration: putraste durmukho rājanduḥsahaśca mahārathaḥ ,gadayā bhīmasenena nihatau śūramāninau.
english: O King, your two sons Durmukha and Dussaha, both great chariot warriors who considered themselves brave, were struck down by Bhimasena with a mace.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 4 Verse 32. O King, your two sons Durmukha and Dussaha, both great chariot warriors who considered themselves brave, were struck down by Bhimasena with a mace.

sanskrit: दुर्मर्षणो दुर्विषहो दुर्जयश्च महारथः ।कृत्वा नसुकरं कर्म गता वैवस्वतक्षयम् ॥३३॥
transliteration: durmarṣaṇo durviṣaho durjayaśca mahārathaḥ ,kṛtvā nasukaraṁ karma gatā vaivasvatakṣayam.
english: Those great chariot-fighters, who were difficult to tolerate, hard to overcome, and unconquerable, after performing deeds not easy to accomplish (karma), have gone to the abode of Yama.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 4 Verse 33. Those great chariot-fighters, who were difficult to tolerate, hard to overcome, and unconquerable, after performing deeds not easy to accomplish (karma), have gone to the abode of Yama.

sanskrit: सचिवो वृषवर्मा ते सूतः परमवीर्यवान् ।भीमसेनेन विक्रम्य गमितो यमसादनम् ॥३४॥
transliteration: sacivo vṛṣavarmā te sūtaḥ paramavīryavān ,bhīmasenena vikramya gamito yamasādanam.
english: Your minister Vrishavarma, the supremely valorous charioteer, was sent to Yama's abode after Bhimasena attacked him forcefully.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 4 Verse 34. Your minister Vrishavarma, the supremely valorous charioteer, was sent to Yama's abode after Bhimasena attacked him forcefully.

sanskrit: नागायुतबलो राजा नागायुतबलो महान् ।सगणः पाण्डुपुत्रेण निहतः सव्यसाचिना ॥३५॥
transliteration: nāgāyutabalo rājā nāgāyutabalo mahān ,sagaṇaḥ pāṇḍuputreṇa nihataḥ savyasācinā.
english: The great king, who possessed the strength of ten thousand elephants, along with his retinue, was killed by the son of Pandu (Pāṇḍuputreṇa), Savyasacin (Arjuna).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 4 Verse 35. The great king, who possessed the strength of ten thousand elephants, along with his retinue, was killed by the son of Pandu (Pāṇḍuputreṇa), Savyasacin (Arjuna).

sanskrit: वसातयो महाराज द्विसाहस्राः प्रहारिणः ।शूरसेनाश्च विक्रान्ताः सर्वे युधि निपातिताः ॥३६॥
transliteration: vasātayo mahārāja dvisāhasrāḥ prahāriṇaḥ ,śūrasenāśca vikrāntāḥ sarve yudhi nipātitāḥ.
english: O great king (mahārāja), the two thousand Vasatis, who were formidable warriors, and all the valiant Shurasenas, were struck down in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 4 Verse 36. O great king (mahārāja), the two thousand Vasatis, who were formidable warriors, and all the valiant Shurasenas, were struck down in battle.

sanskrit: अभीषाहाः कवचिनः प्रहरन्तो मदोत्कटाः ।शिबयश्च रथोदाराः कलिङ्गसहिता हताः ॥३७॥
transliteration: abhīṣāhāḥ kavacinaḥ praharanto madotkaṭāḥ ,śibayaśca rathodārāḥ kaliṅgasahitā hatāḥ.
english: The armored attackers, those furious with pride, as well as the Sibis, renowned for their chariots, along with the Kalingas, have been slain.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 4 Verse 37. The armored attackers, those furious with pride, as well as the Sibis, renowned for their chariots, along with the Kalingas, have been slain.

sanskrit: गोकुले नित्यसंवृद्धा युद्धे परमकोविदाः ।श्रेणयो बहुसाहस्राः संशप्तकगणाश्च ये ।ते सर्वे पार्थमासाद्य गता वैवस्वतक्षयम् ॥३८॥
transliteration: gokule nityasaṁvṛddhā yuddhe paramakovidāḥ ,śreṇayo bahusāhasrāḥ saṁśaptakagaṇāśca ye ,te sarve pārthamāsādya gatā vaivasvatakṣayam.
english: All those multitudes, numbering many thousands, who were ever thriving in Gokula and supremely skilled in battle, as well as the troops of the Saṃśaptakas - they all, upon encountering Pārtha (Arjuna), have gone to the abode of Yama (Vaivasvata).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 4 Verse 38. All those multitudes, numbering many thousands, who were ever thriving in Gokula and supremely skilled in battle, as well as the troops of the Saṃśaptakas - they all, upon encountering Pārtha (Arjuna), have gone to the abode of Yama (Vaivasvata).

sanskrit: स्यालौ तव महाराज राजानौ वृषकाचलौ ।त्वदर्थे संपराक्रान्तौ निहतौ सव्यसाचिना ॥३९॥
transliteration: syālau tava mahārāja rājānau vṛṣakācalau ,tvadarthe saṁparākrāntau nihatau savyasācinā.
english: O great king, your two brothers-in-law, the kings Vṛṣaka and Acala, who fought valiantly for your sake, have been slain by Savyasācin (Arjuna).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 4 Verse 39. O great king, your two brothers-in-law, the kings Vṛṣaka and Acala, who fought valiantly for your sake, have been slain by Savyasācin (Arjuna).

sanskrit: उग्रकर्मा महेष्वासो नामतः कर्मतस्तथा ।शाल्वराजो महाराज भीमसेनेन पातितः ॥४०॥
transliteration: ugrakarmā maheṣvāso nāmataḥ karmatastathā ,śālvarājo mahārāja bhīmasenena pātitaḥ.
english: O great king, the king of Śālva, who was known for his fierce deeds and was a great archer, both by reputation and by his actions, has been felled by Bhīmasena.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 4 Verse 40. O great king, the king of Śālva, who was known for his fierce deeds and was a great archer, both by reputation and by his actions, has been felled by Bhīmasena.

sanskrit: ओघवांश्च महाराज बृहन्तः सहितो रणे ।पराक्रमन्तौ मित्रार्थे गतौ वैवस्वतक्षयम् ॥४१॥
transliteration: oghavāṁśca mahārāja bṛhantaḥ sahito raṇe ,parākramantau mitrārthe gatau vaivasvatakṣayam.
english: And Oghavan, O great king, along with Brihanta, displaying great valor in battle for the sake of a friend, went to Yama's abode.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 4 Verse 41. And Oghavan, O great king, along with Brihanta, displaying great valor in battle for the sake of a friend, went to Yama's abode.

sanskrit: तथैव रथिनां श्रेष्ठः क्षेमधूर्तिर्विशां पते ।निहतो गदया राजन्भीमसेनेन संयुगे ॥४२॥
transliteration: tathaiva rathināṁ śreṣṭhaḥ kṣemadhūrtirviśāṁ pate ,nihato gadayā rājanbhīmasenena saṁyuge.
english: Similarly, O lord of the people, O king, Kshemadhurti, who was the foremost among charioteers, was killed in battle by Bhimasena with his mace.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 4 Verse 42. Similarly, O lord of the people, O king, Kshemadhurti, who was the foremost among charioteers, was killed in battle by Bhimasena with his mace.

sanskrit: तथा राजा महेष्वासो जलसंधो महाबलः ।सुमहत्कदनं कृत्वा हतः सात्यकिना रणे ॥४३॥
transliteration: tathā rājā maheṣvāso jalasaṁdho mahābalaḥ ,sumahatkadanaṁ kṛtvā hataḥ sātyakinā raṇe.
english: Similarly, King Jalasandha, a great archer and exceedingly powerful, after causing significant destruction, was killed in battle by Satyaki.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 4 Verse 43. Similarly, King Jalasandha, a great archer and exceedingly powerful, after causing significant destruction, was killed in battle by Satyaki.

sanskrit: अलायुधो राक्षसेन्द्रः खरबन्धुरयानगः ।घटोत्कचेन विक्रम्य गमितो यमसादनम् ॥४४॥
transliteration: alāyudho rākṣasendraḥ kharabandhurayānagaḥ ,ghaṭotkacena vikramya gamito yamasādanam.
english: Alayudha, the chief of Rākṣasas, related to Khara and swift-moving, was sent to Yama's abode by Ghatotkacha, after he displayed great valor.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 4 Verse 44. Alayudha, the chief of Rākṣasas, related to Khara and swift-moving, was sent to Yama's abode by Ghatotkacha, after he displayed great valor.

sanskrit: राधेयाः सूतपुत्राश्च भ्रातरश्च महारथाः ।केकयाः सर्वशश्चापि निहताः सव्यसाचिना ॥४५॥
transliteration: rādheyāḥ sūtaputrāśca bhrātaraśca mahārathāḥ ,kekayāḥ sarvaśaścāpi nihatāḥ savyasācinā.
english: The sons of Rādhā, the sons of the charioteer, and the great warrior brothers, along with all the Kekayas, were slain by Savyasācin (Arjuna).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 4 Verse 45. The sons of Rādhā, the sons of the charioteer, and the great warrior brothers, along with all the Kekayas, were slain by Savyasācin (Arjuna).

sanskrit: मालवा मद्रकाश्चैव द्रविडाश्चोग्रविक्रमाः ।यौधेयाश्च ललित्थाश्च क्षुद्रकाश्चाप्युशीनराः ॥४६॥
transliteration: mālavā madrakāścaiva draviḍāścogravikramāḥ ,yaudheyāśca lalitthāśca kṣudrakāścāpyuśīnarāḥ.
english: The Mālavās, the Madrakas, and indeed the Draviḍas of fierce valor; and the Yaudheyas, the Lalitthas, and also the Kṣudrakas and the Uśīnarās (were all present or slain).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 4 Verse 46. The Mālavās, the Madrakas, and indeed the Draviḍas of fierce valor; and the Yaudheyas, the Lalitthas, and also the Kṣudrakas and the Uśīnarās (were all present or slain).

sanskrit: मावेल्लकास्तुण्डिकेराः सावित्रीपुत्रकाञ्चलाः ।प्राच्योदीच्याः प्रतीच्याश्च दाक्षिणात्याश्च मारिष ॥४७॥
transliteration: māvellakāstuṇḍikerāḥ sāvitrīputrakāñcalāḥ ,prācyodīcyāḥ pratīcyāśca dākṣiṇātyāśca māriṣa.
english: O revered one, the Māvellakas, the Tuṇḍikerās, the Sāvitrīputrakāñcalas, the Easterners, the Northerners, the Westerners, and the Southerners (were also present or slain).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 4 Verse 47. O revered one, the Māvellakas, the Tuṇḍikerās, the Sāvitrīputrakāñcalas, the Easterners, the Northerners, the Westerners, and the Southerners (were also present or slain).

sanskrit: पत्तीनां निहताः संघा हयानामयुतानि च ।रथव्रजाश्च निहता हताश्च वरवारणाः ॥४८॥
transliteration: pattīnāṁ nihatāḥ saṁghā hayānāmayutāni ca ,rathavrajāśca nihatā hatāśca varavāraṇāḥ.
english: Multitudes of infantry were slain, and tens of thousands of horses; troops of chariots were destroyed, and excellent elephants were killed.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 4 Verse 48. Multitudes of infantry were slain, and tens of thousands of horses; troops of chariots were destroyed, and excellent elephants were killed.

sanskrit: सध्वजाः सायुधाः शूराः सवर्माम्बरभूषणाः ।कालेन महता यत्ताः कुले ये च विवर्धिताः ॥४९॥
transliteration: sadhvajāḥ sāyudhāḥ śūrāḥ savarmāmbarabhūṣaṇāḥ ,kālena mahatā yattāḥ kule ye ca vivardhitāḥ.
english: Those heroes, bearing banners and weapons, adorned with armor, garments, and ornaments, who had been meticulously prepared over a long period and raised in distinguished families...
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 4 Verse 49. Those heroes, bearing banners and weapons, adorned with armor, garments, and ornaments, who had been meticulously prepared over a long period and raised in distinguished families...

sanskrit: ते हताः समरे राजन्पार्थेनाक्लिष्टकर्मणा ।अन्ये तथामितबलाः परस्परवधैषिणः ॥५०॥
transliteration: te hatāḥ samare rājanpārthenākliṣṭakarmaṇā ,anye tathāmitabalāḥ parasparavadhaiṣiṇaḥ.
english: O King, they were slain in battle by Pārtha (Arjuna), whose actions (karma) are unwearied. Similarly, other warriors of immeasurable strength, desiring to kill each other, also fell.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 4 Verse 50. O King, they were slain in battle by Pārtha (Arjuna), whose actions (karma) are unwearied. Similarly, other warriors of immeasurable strength, desiring to kill each other, also fell.

sanskrit: एते चान्ये च बहवो राजानः सगणा रणे ।हताः सहस्रशो राजन्यन्मां त्वं परिपृच्छसि ।एवमेष क्षयो वृत्तः कर्णार्जुनसमागमे ॥५१॥
transliteration: ete cānye ca bahavo rājānaḥ sagaṇā raṇe ,hatāḥ sahasraśo rājanyanmāṁ tvaṁ paripṛcchasi ,evameṣa kṣayo vṛttaḥ karṇārjunasamāgame.
english: O King, these and many other kings, along with their troops, about whom you ask me, were slain by thousands in battle. Thus, this devastation occurred during the encounter between Karṇa and Arjuna.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 4 Verse 51. O King, these and many other kings, along with their troops, about whom you ask me, were slain by thousands in battle. Thus, this devastation occurred during the encounter between Karṇa and Arjuna.

sanskrit: महेन्द्रेण यथा वृत्रो यथा रामेण रावणः ।यथा कृष्णेन निहतो मुरो रणनिपातितः ।कार्तवीर्यश्च रामेण भार्गवेण हतो यथा ॥५२॥
transliteration: mahendreṇa yathā vṛtro yathā rāmeṇa rāvaṇaḥ ,yathā kṛṣṇena nihato muro raṇanipātitaḥ ,kārtavīryaśca rāmeṇa bhārgaveṇa hato yathā.
english: Just as Vṛtra was slain by Mahendra (Indra), and Rāvaṇa by Rāma; and just as the demon Mura, who was brought down in battle, was killed by Kṛṣṇa; similarly, Kārtavīrya was slain by Rāma, the descendant of Bhṛgu.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 4 Verse 52. Just as Vṛtra was slain by Mahendra (Indra), and Rāvaṇa by Rāma; and just as the demon Mura, who was brought down in battle, was killed by Kṛṣṇa; similarly, Kārtavīrya was slain by Rāma, the descendant of Bhṛgu.

sanskrit: सज्ञातिबान्धवः शूरः समरे युद्धदुर्मदः ।रणे कृत्वा महायुद्धं घोरं त्रैलोक्यविश्रुतम् ॥५३॥
transliteration: sajñātibāndhavaḥ śūraḥ samare yuddhadurmadaḥ ,raṇe kṛtvā mahāyuddhaṁ ghoraṁ trailokyaviśrutam.
english: He was a hero, accompanied by his kinsmen and relatives, furious in war. Having fought a great and terrible battle on the field, he was renowned throughout the three worlds.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 4 Verse 53. He was a hero, accompanied by his kinsmen and relatives, furious in war. Having fought a great and terrible battle on the field, he was renowned throughout the three worlds.

sanskrit: तथार्जुनेन निहतो द्वैरथे युद्धदुर्मदः ।सामात्यबान्धवो राजन्कर्णः प्रहरतां वरः ॥५४॥
transliteration: tathārjunena nihato dvairathe yuddhadurmadaḥ ,sāmātyabāndhavo rājankarṇaḥ praharatāṁ varaḥ.
english: Thus, Karna, the best of warriors, who was furious in battle and accompanied by his ministers and relatives, was killed by Arjuna in a chariot duel, O king.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 4 Verse 54. Thus, Karna, the best of warriors, who was furious in battle and accompanied by his ministers and relatives, was killed by Arjuna in a chariot duel, O king.

sanskrit: जयाशा धार्तराष्ट्राणां वैरस्य च मुखं यतः ।तीर्णं तत्पाण्डवै राजन्यत्पुरा नावबुध्यसे ॥५५॥
