transliteration: jayāśā dhārtarāṣṭrāṇāṁ vairasya ca mukhaṁ yataḥ ,tīrṇaṁ tatpāṇḍavai rājanyatpurā nāvabudhyase.
english: O king, that which was the hope for victory for the Dhritarashtras (Kauravas), and the very origin of their enmity, has now been overcome by the Pandavas; this you did not grasp previously.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 4 Verse 55. O king, that which was the hope for victory for the Dhritarashtras (Kauravas), and the very origin of their enmity, has now been overcome by the Pandavas; this you did not grasp previously.

sanskrit: उच्यमानो महाराज बन्धुभिर्हितकाङ्क्षिभिः ।तदिदं समनुप्राप्तं व्यसनं त्वां महात्ययम् ॥५६॥
transliteration: ucyamāno mahārāja bandhubhirhitakāṅkṣibhiḥ ,tadidaṁ samanuprāptaṁ vyasanaṁ tvāṁ mahātyayam.
english: O great king, this immense calamity, about which you were repeatedly warned by your well-wishing relatives, has now truly come upon you.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 4 Verse 56. O great king, this immense calamity, about which you were repeatedly warned by your well-wishing relatives, has now truly come upon you.

sanskrit: पुत्राणां राज्यकामानां त्वया राजन्हितैषिणा ।अहितानीव चीर्णानि तेषां ते फलमागतम् ॥५७॥
transliteration: putrāṇāṁ rājyakāmānāṁ tvayā rājanhitaiṣiṇā ,ahitānīva cīrṇāni teṣāṁ te phalamāgatam.
english: O King, the harmful actions that you, desiring the welfare of your sons who longed for the kingdom, carried out against them, have now returned to you as their consequence.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 4 Verse 57. O King, the harmful actions that you, desiring the welfare of your sons who longed for the kingdom, carried out against them, have now returned to you as their consequence.

sanskrit: धृतराष्ट्र उवाच ।आख्याता मामकास्तात निहता युधि पाण्डवैः ।निहतान्पाण्डवेयानां मामकैर्ब्रूहि संजय ॥५८॥
transliteration: dhṛtarāṣṭra uvāca ,ākhyātā māmakāstāta nihatā yudhi pāṇḍavaiḥ ,nihatānpāṇḍaveyānāṁ māmakairbrūhi saṁjaya.
english: Dhritarashtra said: "My dear Sanjaya, you have recounted that my sons were killed in battle by the Pandavas. Now, please tell me about those sons of Pandu who were killed by my people."
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 4 Verse 58. Dhritarashtra said: "My dear Sanjaya, you have recounted that my sons were killed in battle by the Pandavas. Now, please tell me about those sons of Pandu who were killed by my people."

sanskrit: संजय उवाच ।कुन्तयो युधि विक्रान्ता महासत्त्वा महाबलाः ।सानुबन्धाः सहामात्या भीष्मेण युधि पातिताः ॥५९॥
transliteration: saṁjaya uvāca ,kuntayo yudhi vikrāntā mahāsattvā mahābalāḥ ,sānubandhāḥ sahāmātyā bhīṣmeṇa yudhi pātitāḥ.
english: Sanjaya said: "The courageous and immensely strong sons of Kunti (the Pandavas), along with their followers and companions, were felled in battle by Bhishma."
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 4 Verse 59. Sanjaya said: "The courageous and immensely strong sons of Kunti (the Pandavas), along with their followers and companions, were felled in battle by Bhishma."

sanskrit: समः किरीटिना संख्ये वीर्येण च बलेन च ।सत्यजित्सत्यसंधेन द्रोणेन निहतो रणे ॥६०॥
transliteration: samaḥ kirīṭinā saṁkhye vīryeṇa ca balena ca ,satyajitsatyasaṁdhena droṇena nihato raṇe.
english: Satyajit, who was equal to Arjuna (kirīṭin) in battle, both in valor and strength, was killed in combat by Drona, who was always true to his word (satyasaṃdha).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 4 Verse 60. Satyajit, who was equal to Arjuna (kirīṭin) in battle, both in valor and strength, was killed in combat by Drona, who was always true to his word (satyasaṃdha).

sanskrit: तथा विराटद्रुपदौ वृद्धौ सहसुतौ नृपौ ।पराक्रमन्तौ मित्रार्थे द्रोणेन निहतौ रणे ॥६१॥
transliteration: tathā virāṭadrupadau vṛddhau sahasutau nṛpau ,parākramantau mitrārthe droṇena nihatau raṇe.
english: Likewise, the aged kings Virata and Drupada, along with their sons, though fighting valiantly for their ally, were slain by Drona in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 4 Verse 61. Likewise, the aged kings Virata and Drupada, along with their sons, though fighting valiantly for their ally, were slain by Drona in battle.

sanskrit: यो बाल एव समरे संमितः सव्यसाचिना ।केशवेन च दुर्धर्षो बलदेवेन चाभिभूः ॥६२॥
transliteration: yo bāla eva samare saṁmitaḥ savyasācinā ,keśavena ca durdharṣo baladevena cābhibhūḥ.
english: Who, even as a mere boy, was deemed equal in battle to Arjuna (savyasācin), unassailable even by Krishna (Keśava) and Baladeva, and truly a conqueror.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 4 Verse 62. Who, even as a mere boy, was deemed equal in battle to Arjuna (savyasācin), unassailable even by Krishna (Keśava) and Baladeva, and truly a conqueror.

sanskrit: स एष कदनं कृत्वा महद्रणविशारदः ।परिवार्य महामात्रैः षड्भिः परमकै रथैः ।अशक्नुवद्भिर्बीभत्सुमभिमन्युर्निपातितः ॥६३॥
transliteration: sa eṣa kadanaṁ kṛtvā mahadraṇaviśāradaḥ ,parivārya mahāmātraiḥ ṣaḍbhiḥ paramakai rathaiḥ ,aśaknuvadbhirbībhatsumabhimanyurnipātitaḥ.
english: This very Abhimanyu, an expert in battle, having caused great destruction, was surrounded by six supreme chariot-warriors (mahāmātra)  -  warriors who were themselves unable to defeat Arjuna (Bībhatsu)  -  and was then struck down.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 4 Verse 63. This very Abhimanyu, an expert in battle, having caused great destruction, was surrounded by six supreme chariot-warriors (mahāmātra)  -  warriors who were themselves unable to defeat Arjuna (Bībhatsu)  -  and was then struck down.

sanskrit: तं कृतं विरथं वीरं क्षत्रधर्मे व्यवस्थितम् ।दौःशासनिर्महाराज सौभद्रं हतवान्रणे ॥६४॥
transliteration: taṁ kṛtaṁ virathaṁ vīraṁ kṣatradharme vyavasthitam ,dauḥśāsanirmahārāja saubhadraṁ hatavānraṇe.
english: O great king, Dushasana's son (Dauḥśāsani) killed that hero, Abhimanyu (Saubhadra), who had been rendered without a chariot and was upholding the warrior's code (kṣatradharma) in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 4 Verse 64. O great king, Dushasana's son (Dauḥśāsani) killed that hero, Abhimanyu (Saubhadra), who had been rendered without a chariot and was upholding the warrior's code (kṣatradharma) in battle.

sanskrit: बृहन्तस्तु महेष्वासः कृतास्त्रो युद्धदुर्मदः ।दुःशासनेन विक्रम्य गमितो यमसादनम् ॥६५॥
transliteration: bṛhantastu maheṣvāsaḥ kṛtāstro yuddhadurmadaḥ ,duḥśāsanena vikramya gamito yamasādanam.
english: But Brihanta, the great archer, who was skilled in weapons and furious in battle, was valiantly sent to the abode of Yama by Duḥśāsana.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 4 Verse 65. But Brihanta, the great archer, who was skilled in weapons and furious in battle, was valiantly sent to the abode of Yama by Duḥśāsana.

sanskrit: मणिमान्दण्डधारश्च राजानौ युद्धदुर्मदौ ।पराक्रमन्तौ मित्रार्थे द्रोणेन विनिपातितौ ॥६६॥
transliteration: maṇimāndaṇḍadhāraśca rājānau yuddhadurmadau ,parākramantau mitrārthe droṇena vinipātitau.
english: Maṇimān and Daṇḍadhāra, two kings who were furious in battle, were struck down by Droṇa while valiantly fighting for the sake of a friend.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 4 Verse 66. Maṇimān and Daṇḍadhāra, two kings who were furious in battle, were struck down by Droṇa while valiantly fighting for the sake of a friend.

sanskrit: अंशुमान्भोजराजस्तु सहसैन्यो महारथः ।भारद्वाजेन विक्रम्य गमितो यमसादनम् ॥६७॥
transliteration: aṁśumānbhojarājastu sahasainyo mahārathaḥ ,bhāradvājena vikramya gamito yamasādanam.
english: But Aṃśumān, the Bhoja king and a great warrior who was accompanied by his army, was valiantly sent to the abode of Yama by Bhāradvāja (Droṇa).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 4 Verse 67. But Aṃśumān, the Bhoja king and a great warrior who was accompanied by his army, was valiantly sent to the abode of Yama by Bhāradvāja (Droṇa).

sanskrit: चित्रायुधश्चित्रयोधी कृत्वा तौ कदनं महत् ।चित्रमार्गेण विक्रम्य कर्णेन निहतौ युधि ॥६८॥
transliteration: citrāyudhaścitrayodhī kṛtvā tau kadanaṁ mahat ,citramārgeṇa vikramya karṇena nihatau yudhi.
english: Citrāyudha and Citrayodhī, after inflicting great slaughter, were valiantly slain in battle by Karṇa through extraordinary means.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 4 Verse 68. Citrāyudha and Citrayodhī, after inflicting great slaughter, were valiantly slain in battle by Karṇa through extraordinary means.

sanskrit: वृकोदरसमो युद्धे दृढः केकयजो युधि ।केकयेनैव विक्रम्य भ्रात्रा भ्राता निपातितः ॥६९॥
transliteration: vṛkodarasamo yuddhe dṛḍhaḥ kekayajo yudhi ,kekayenaiva vikramya bhrātrā bhrātā nipātitaḥ.
english: A strong prince of the Kekaya kingdom, who was like Vṛkodara (Bhīma) in battle, was struck down in combat by his own brother, another Kekaya prince, after the latter had displayed great prowess.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 4 Verse 69. A strong prince of the Kekaya kingdom, who was like Vṛkodara (Bhīma) in battle, was struck down in combat by his own brother, another Kekaya prince, after the latter had displayed great prowess.

sanskrit: जनमेजयो गदायोधी पार्वतीयः प्रतापवान् ।दुर्मुखेन महाराज तव पुत्रेण पातितः ॥७०॥
transliteration: janamejayo gadāyodhī pārvatīyaḥ pratāpavān ,durmukhena mahārāja tava putreṇa pātitaḥ.
english: O great king, the powerful mace-wielder Janamejaya, who hailed from the mountains, was struck down by your son, Durmukha.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 4 Verse 70. O great king, the powerful mace-wielder Janamejaya, who hailed from the mountains, was struck down by your son, Durmukha.

sanskrit: रोचमानौ नरव्याघ्रौ रोचमानौ ग्रहाविव ।द्रोणेन युगपद्राजन्दिवं संप्रेषितौ शरैः ॥७१॥
transliteration: rocamānau naravyāghrau rocamānau grahāviva ,droṇena yugapadrājandivaṁ saṁpreṣitau śaraiḥ.
english: O king, those two glorious, tiger-like men, shining like a pair of planets, were simultaneously sent to heaven by Droṇa's arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 4 Verse 71. O king, those two glorious, tiger-like men, shining like a pair of planets, were simultaneously sent to heaven by Droṇa's arrows.

sanskrit: नृपाश्च प्रतियुध्यन्तः पराक्रान्ता विशां पते ।कृत्वा नसुकरं कर्म गता वैवस्वतक्षयम् ॥७२॥
transliteration: nṛpāśca pratiyudhyantaḥ parākrāntā viśāṁ pate ,kṛtvā nasukaraṁ karma gatā vaivasvatakṣayam.
english: O lord of the people, the mighty kings, fighting in resistance, went to the abode of Yama (vaivasvatakṣayam) after performing a difficult feat (karma).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 4 Verse 72. O lord of the people, the mighty kings, fighting in resistance, went to the abode of Yama (vaivasvatakṣayam) after performing a difficult feat (karma).

sanskrit: पुरुजित्कुन्तिभोजश्च मातुलः सव्यसाचिनः ।संग्रामनिर्जिताँल्लोकान्गमितो द्रोणसायकैः ॥७३॥
transliteration: purujitkuntibhojaśca mātulaḥ savyasācinaḥ ,saṁgrāmanirjitāँllokāngamito droṇasāyakaiḥ.
english: Purujit and Kuntibhoja, the maternal uncle of Arjuna (savyasācin), were sent by Drona's arrows to the realms they had previously conquered in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 4 Verse 73. Purujit and Kuntibhoja, the maternal uncle of Arjuna (savyasācin), were sent by Drona's arrows to the realms they had previously conquered in battle.

sanskrit: अभिभूः काशिराजश्च काशिकैर्बहुभिर्वृतः ।वसुदानस्य पुत्रेण न्यासितो देहमाहवे ॥७४॥
transliteration: abhibhūḥ kāśirājaśca kāśikairbahubhirvṛtaḥ ,vasudānasya putreṇa nyāsito dehamāhave.
english: Abhibhu and the king of Kashi, who was surrounded by many Kāśi warriors, had their bodies laid down (killed) in battle by Vasudana's son.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 4 Verse 74. Abhibhu and the king of Kashi, who was surrounded by many Kāśi warriors, had their bodies laid down (killed) in battle by Vasudana's son.

sanskrit: अमितौजा युधामन्युरुत्तमौजाश्च वीर्यवान् ।निहत्य शतशः शूरान्परैर्विनिहतौ रणे ॥७५॥
transliteration: amitaujā yudhāmanyuruttamaujāśca vīryavān ,nihatya śataśaḥ śūrānparairvinihatau raṇe.
english: Amitaujas and the valiant Yudhamanyu and Uttamaujas, after killing hundreds of brave warriors, were themselves slain in battle by the enemies.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 4 Verse 75. Amitaujas and the valiant Yudhamanyu and Uttamaujas, after killing hundreds of brave warriors, were themselves slain in battle by the enemies.

sanskrit: क्षत्रधर्मा च पाञ्चाल्यः क्षत्रवर्मा च मारिष ।द्रोणेन परमेष्वासौ गमितौ यमसादनम् ॥७६॥
transliteration: kṣatradharmā ca pāñcālyaḥ kṣatravarmā ca māriṣa ,droṇena parameṣvāsau gamitau yamasādanam.
english: O venerable one (māriṣa), Kṣatradharmā and Pañcālya, along with Kṣatravarmā, both supreme archers, were sent to Yama's abode (killed) by Drona.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 4 Verse 76. O venerable one (māriṣa), Kṣatradharmā and Pañcālya, along with Kṣatravarmā, both supreme archers, were sent to Yama's abode (killed) by Drona.

sanskrit: शिखण्डितनयो युद्धे क्षत्रदेवो युधां पतिः ।लक्ष्मणेन हतो राजंस्तव पौत्रेण भारत ॥७७॥
transliteration: śikhaṇḍitanayo yuddhe kṣatradevo yudhāṁ patiḥ ,lakṣmaṇena hato rājaṁstava pautreṇa bhārata.
english: O King (rājan), O Bhārata (bhārata), Kṣatradeva, the son of Śikhaṇḍin and a lord of battles, was killed in battle by Lakṣmaṇa, your grandson.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 4 Verse 77. O King (rājan), O Bhārata (bhārata), Kṣatradeva, the son of Śikhaṇḍin and a lord of battles, was killed in battle by Lakṣmaṇa, your grandson.

sanskrit: सुचित्रश्चित्रधर्मा च पितापुत्रौ महारथौ ।प्रचरन्तौ महावीर्यौ द्रोणेन निहतौ रणे ॥७८॥
transliteration: sucitraścitradharmā ca pitāputrau mahārathau ,pracarantau mahāvīryau droṇena nihatau raṇe.
english: Sucitra and Citradharmā, father and son, both great warriors (mahārathau) and exceedingly powerful, were killed in battle by Droṇa while they were fighting.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 4 Verse 78. Sucitra and Citradharmā, father and son, both great warriors (mahārathau) and exceedingly powerful, were killed in battle by Droṇa while they were fighting.

sanskrit: वार्धक्षेमिर्महाराज कृत्वा कदनमाहवे ।बाह्लिकेन महाराज कौरवेण निपातितः ॥७९॥
transliteration: vārdhakṣemirmahārāja kṛtvā kadanamāhave ,bāhlikena mahārāja kauraveṇa nipātitaḥ.
english: O great king (mahārāja), Vārdhakṣemi, after causing great slaughter in battle, was struck down by the Kaurava Bāhlika, O great king (mahārāja).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 4 Verse 79. O great king (mahārāja), Vārdhakṣemi, after causing great slaughter in battle, was struck down by the Kaurava Bāhlika, O great king (mahārāja).

sanskrit: धृष्टकेतुर्महाराज चेदीनां प्रवरो रथः ।कृत्वा नसुकरं कर्म गतो वैवस्वतक्षयम् ॥८०॥
transliteration: dhṛṣṭaketurmahārāja cedīnāṁ pravaro rathaḥ ,kṛtvā nasukaraṁ karma gato vaivasvatakṣayam.
english: O great king (mahārāja), Dhṛṣṭaketu, the foremost warrior (rathaḥ) of the Cedis, after performing a difficult action (karma), went to the abode of Yama (Vaivasvata), that is, he met his death.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 4 Verse 80. O great king (mahārāja), Dhṛṣṭaketu, the foremost warrior (rathaḥ) of the Cedis, after performing a difficult action (karma), went to the abode of Yama (Vaivasvata), that is, he met his death.

sanskrit: तथा सत्यधृतिस्तात कृत्वा कदनमाहवे ।पाण्डवार्थे पराक्रान्तो गमितो यमसादनम् ॥८१॥
transliteration: tathā satyadhṛtistāta kṛtvā kadanamāhave ,pāṇḍavārthe parākrānto gamito yamasādanam.
english: Likewise, O sire, Satyadhriti, having wrought great destruction in battle, fought valiantly for the sake of the Pāṇḍavas and was dispatched to the abode of Yama.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 4 Verse 81. Likewise, O sire, Satyadhriti, having wrought great destruction in battle, fought valiantly for the sake of the Pāṇḍavas and was dispatched to the abode of Yama.

sanskrit: पुत्रस्तु शिशुपालस्य सुकेतुः पृथिवीपते ।निहत्य शात्रवान्संख्ये द्रोणेन निहतो युधि ॥८२॥
transliteration: putrastu śiśupālasya suketuḥ pṛthivīpate ,nihatya śātravānsaṁkhye droṇena nihato yudhi.
english: And, O lord of the earth, Suketu, the son of Śiśupāla, after having killed enemies in battle, was himself slain by Droṇa in the fight.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 4 Verse 82. And, O lord of the earth, Suketu, the son of Śiśupāla, after having killed enemies in battle, was himself slain by Droṇa in the fight.

sanskrit: तथा सत्यधृतिर्वीरो मदिराश्वश्च वीर्यवान् ।सूर्यदत्तश्च विक्रान्तो निहतो द्रोणसायकैः ॥८३॥
transliteration: tathā satyadhṛtirvīro madirāśvaśca vīryavān ,sūryadattaśca vikrānto nihato droṇasāyakaiḥ.
english: Similarly, the heroic Satyadhriti, the mighty Madirāśva, and the valiant Sūryadatta were all slain by Droṇa's arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 4 Verse 83. Similarly, the heroic Satyadhriti, the mighty Madirāśva, and the valiant Sūryadatta were all slain by Droṇa's arrows.

sanskrit: श्रेणिमांश्च महाराज युध्यमानः पराक्रमी ।कृत्वा नसुकरं कर्म गतो वैवस्वतक्षयम् ॥८४॥
transliteration: śreṇimāṁśca mahārāja yudhyamānaḥ parākramī ,kṛtvā nasukaraṁ karma gato vaivasvatakṣayam.
english: And O great king, the valiant Śreṇimān, while fighting, performed a deed not easily done and departed to the realm of Vaivasvata (Yama).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 4 Verse 84. And O great king, the valiant Śreṇimān, while fighting, performed a deed not easily done and departed to the realm of Vaivasvata (Yama).

sanskrit: तथैव युधि विक्रान्तो मागधः परवीरहा ।भीष्मेण निहतो राजन्युध्यमानः पराक्रमी ॥८५॥
transliteration: tathaiva yudhi vikrānto māgadhaḥ paravīrahā ,bhīṣmeṇa nihato rājanyudhyamānaḥ parākramī.
english: Similarly, O king, the valorous Magadhan, the slayer of enemy heroes, who was fighting powerfully, was slain by Bhishma.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 4 Verse 85. Similarly, O king, the valorous Magadhan, the slayer of enemy heroes, who was fighting powerfully, was slain by Bhishma.

sanskrit: वसुदानश्च कदनं कुर्वाणोऽतीव संयुगे ।भारद्वाजेन विक्रम्य गमितो यमसादनम् ॥८६॥
transliteration: vasudānaśca kadanaṁ kurvāṇo'tīva saṁyuge ,bhāradvājena vikramya gamito yamasādanam.
english: And Vasudana, causing great destruction in battle, was sent to the abode of Yama (god of death) by Drona (Bhāradvāja), who fought with valor.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 4 Verse 86. And Vasudana, causing great destruction in battle, was sent to the abode of Yama (god of death) by Drona (Bhāradvāja), who fought with valor.

sanskrit: एते चान्ये च बहवः पाण्डवानां महारथाः ।हता द्रोणेन विक्रम्य यन्मां त्वं परिपृच्छसि ॥८७॥
transliteration: ete cānye ca bahavaḥ pāṇḍavānāṁ mahārathāḥ ,hatā droṇena vikramya yanmāṁ tvaṁ paripṛcchasi.
english: And these, along with many other great charioteers (mahārathas) of the Pandavas, were slain by Drona, who fought valiantly, regarding whom you question me.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 4 Verse 87. And these, along with many other great charioteers (mahārathas) of the Pandavas, were slain by Drona, who fought valiantly, regarding whom you question me.

sanskrit: धृतराष्ट्र उवाच ।हतप्रवीरे सैन्येऽस्मिन्मामके वदतां वर ।अहताञ्शंस मे सूत येऽत्र जीवन्ति केचन ॥८८॥
transliteration: dhṛtarāṣṭra uvāca ,hatapravīre sainye'sminmāmake vadatāṁ vara ,ahatāñśaṁsa me sūta ye'tra jīvanti kecana.
english: Dhritarashtra said: 'O best among speakers (vara), O charioteer (sūta), tell me about those who are unslain and still live here in this army of mine, in which the chief warriors are slain.'
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 4 Verse 88. Dhritarashtra said: 'O best among speakers (vara), O charioteer (sūta), tell me about those who are unslain and still live here in this army of mine, in which the chief warriors are slain.'

sanskrit: एतेषु निहतेष्वद्य ये त्वया परिकीर्तिताः ।अहतान्मन्यसे यांस्त्वं तेऽपि स्वर्गजितो मताः ॥८९॥
transliteration: eteṣu nihateṣvadya ye tvayā parikīrtitāḥ ,ahatānmanyase yāṁstvaṁ te'pi svargajito matāḥ.
english: Among those who have been slain today, whom you have praised and whom you still consider unslain, they too are regarded as having conquered heaven.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 4 Verse 89. Among those who have been slain today, whom you have praised and whom you still consider unslain, they too are regarded as having conquered heaven.

sanskrit: संजय उवाच ।यस्मिन्महास्त्राणि समर्पितानि चित्राणि शुभ्राणि चतुर्विधानि ।दिव्यानि राजन्निहितानि चैव द्रोणेन वीरद्विजसत्तमेन ॥९०॥
transliteration: saṁjaya uvāca ,yasminmahāstrāṇi samarpitāni; citrāṇi śubhrāṇi caturvidhāni ,divyāni rājannihitāni caiva; droṇena vīradvijasattamena.
english: Saṃjaya said: "O King, in him, the best of heroic brahmins, Droṇa had deposited all the great, wonderful, brilliant, four-fold, and divine weapons (astra)."
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 4 Verse 90. Saṃjaya said: "O King, in him, the best of heroic brahmins, Droṇa had deposited all the great, wonderful, brilliant, four-fold, and divine weapons (astra)."

sanskrit: महारथः कृतिमान्क्षिप्रहस्तो दृढायुधो दृढमुष्टिर्दृढेषुः ।स वीर्यवान्द्रोणपुत्रस्तरस्वी व्यवस्थितो योद्धुकामस्त्वदर्थे ॥९१॥
transliteration: mahārathaḥ kṛtimānkṣiprahasto; dṛḍhāyudho dṛḍhamuṣṭirdṛḍheṣuḥ ,sa vīryavāndroṇaputrastarasvī; vyavasthito yoddhukāmastvadarthe.
english: That son of Droṇa - a great charioteer, skilled, swift-handed, firm with his weapons, firm-fisted, and with powerful arrows - he, valorous and energetic, stands ready, eager to fight for your sake.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 4 Verse 91. That son of Droṇa - a great charioteer, skilled, swift-handed, firm with his weapons, firm-fisted, and with powerful arrows - he, valorous and energetic, stands ready, eager to fight for your sake.

sanskrit: आनर्तवासी हृदिकात्मजोऽसौ महारथः सात्वतानां वरिष्ठः ।स्वयं भोजः कृतवर्मा कृतास्त्रो व्यवस्थितो योद्धुकामस्त्वदर्थे ॥९२॥
transliteration: ānartavāsī hṛdikātmajo'sau; mahārathaḥ sātvatānāṁ variṣṭhaḥ ,svayaṁ bhojaḥ kṛtavarmā kṛtāstro; vyavasthito yoddhukāmastvadarthe.
english: That son of Hṛdika, a resident of Ānarta, and the best among the Sātvatas - Kṛtavarman himself, the Bhoja chief, skilled in weapons - he stands ready, eager to fight for your sake.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 4 Verse 92. That son of Hṛdika, a resident of Ānarta, and the best among the Sātvatas - Kṛtavarman himself, the Bhoja chief, skilled in weapons - he stands ready, eager to fight for your sake.

sanskrit: शारद्वतो गौतमश्चापि राजन्महाबलो बहुचित्रास्त्रयोधी ।धनुश्चित्रं सुमहद्भारसाहं व्यवस्थितो योत्स्यमानः प्रगृह्य ॥९३॥
transliteration: śāradvato gautamaścāpi rāja;nmahābalo bahucitrāstrayodhī ,dhanuścitraṁ sumahadbhārasāhaṁ; vyavasthito yotsyamānaḥ pragṛhya.
english: O King, Shāradvata (Kṛpa), the son of Gautama, of great strength, skilled in wielding many wondrous weapons, stands ready to fight, having taken up his beautiful, very mighty, burden-bearing bow.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 4 Verse 93. O King, Shāradvata (Kṛpa), the son of Gautama, of great strength, skilled in wielding many wondrous weapons, stands ready to fight, having taken up his beautiful, very mighty, burden-bearing bow.

sanskrit: आर्तायनिः समरे दुष्प्रकम्प्यः सेनाग्रणीः प्रथमस्तावकानाम् ।स्वस्रेयांस्तान्पाण्डवेयान्विसृज्य सत्यां वाचं तां चिकीर्षुस्तरस्वी ॥९४॥
transliteration: ārtāyaniḥ samare duṣprakampyaḥ; senāgraṇīḥ prathamastāvakānām ,svasreyāṁstānpāṇḍaveyānvisṛjya; satyāṁ vācaṁ tāṁ cikīrṣustarasvī.
english: The powerful Ārtāyani (Kṛtavarman), unshakeable in battle, the foremost general of your forces, is desirous of fulfilling that true promise, having abandoned his Pāṇḍava nephews.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 4 Verse 94. The powerful Ārtāyani (Kṛtavarman), unshakeable in battle, the foremost general of your forces, is desirous of fulfilling that true promise, having abandoned his Pāṇḍava nephews.

sanskrit: तेजोवधं सूतपुत्रस्य संख्ये प्रतिश्रुत्वाजातशत्रोः पुरस्तात् ।दुराधर्षः शक्रसमानवीर्यः शल्यः स्थितो योद्धुकामस्त्वदर्थे ॥९५॥
transliteration: tejovadhaṁ sūtaputrasya saṁkhye; pratiśrutvājātaśatroḥ purastāt ,durādharṣaḥ śakrasamānavīryaḥ; śalyaḥ sthito yoddhukāmastvadarthe.
english: Having promised before Ajātaśatru (Yudhiṣṭhira) the destruction of the Sūtaputra's (Karṇa's) glory in battle, the unconquerable Śalya, whose valor is equal to Indra's, stands ready, desiring to fight for your sake.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 4 Verse 95. Having promised before Ajātaśatru (Yudhiṣṭhira) the destruction of the Sūtaputra's (Karṇa's) glory in battle, the unconquerable Śalya, whose valor is equal to Indra's, stands ready, desiring to fight for your sake.

sanskrit: आजानेयैः सैन्धवैः पार्वतीयैर्नदीजकाम्बोजवनायुबाह्लिकैः ।गान्धारराजः स्वबलेन युक्तो व्यवस्थितो योद्धुकामस्त्वदर्थे ॥९६॥
transliteration: ājāneyaiḥ saindhavaiḥ pārvatīyai;rnadījakāmbojavanāyubāhlikaiḥ ,gāndhārarājaḥ svabalena yukto; vyavasthito yoddhukāmastvadarthe.
english: The King of Gandhāra (Śakuni), accompanied by his own army consisting of noble horses from Sindhu, the mountains, riverine regions, Kambojas, Vanāyus, and Bāhlīkas, stands ready, desiring to fight for your sake.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 4 Verse 96. The King of Gandhāra (Śakuni), accompanied by his own army consisting of noble horses from Sindhu, the mountains, riverine regions, Kambojas, Vanāyus, and Bāhlīkas, stands ready, desiring to fight for your sake.

sanskrit: तथा सुतस्ते ज्वलनार्कवर्णं रथं समास्थाय कुरुप्रवीर ।व्यवस्थितः कुरुमित्रो नरेन्द्र व्यभ्रे सूर्यो भ्राजमानो यथा वै ॥९७॥
transliteration: tathā sutaste jvalanārkavarṇaṁ; rathaṁ samāsthāya kurupravīra ,vyavasthitaḥ kurumitro narendra; vyabhre sūryo bhrājamāno yathā vai.
english: Similarly, O best among the Kurus, O king, your son, having mounted his chariot, which was the color of blazing fire and the sun, stood positioned like the radiant sun in a cloudless sky, truly a friend of the Kurus.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 4 Verse 97. Similarly, O best among the Kurus, O king, your son, having mounted his chariot, which was the color of blazing fire and the sun, stood positioned like the radiant sun in a cloudless sky, truly a friend of the Kurus.

sanskrit: दुर्योधनो नागकुलस्य मध्ये महावीर्यः सह सैन्यप्रवीरैः ।रथेन जाम्बूनदभूषणेन व्यवस्थितः समरे योद्धुकामः ॥९८॥
transliteration: duryodhano nāgakulasya madhye; mahāvīryaḥ saha sainyapravīraiḥ ,rathena jāmbūnadabhūṣaṇena; vyavasthitaḥ samare yoddhukāmaḥ.
english: Duryodhana, immensely powerful, stood amidst the elephant battalions along with his chief military commanders. Positioned in his chariot, adorned with gold, he was eager to fight in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 4 Verse 98. Duryodhana, immensely powerful, stood amidst the elephant battalions along with his chief military commanders. Positioned in his chariot, adorned with gold, he was eager to fight in battle.

sanskrit: स राजमध्ये पुरुषप्रवीरो रराज जाम्बूनदचित्रवर्मा ।पद्मप्रभो वह्निरिवाल्पधूमो मेघान्तरे सूर्य इव प्रकाशः ॥९९॥
transliteration: sa rājamadhye puruṣapravīro; rarāja jāmbūnadacitravarmā ,padmaprabho vahnirivālpadhūmo; meghāntare sūrya iva prakāśaḥ.
english: That hero among men, with his splendid gold-adorned armor, shone brightly amidst the kings. He was resplendent like a lotus-radiant fire with little smoke, and like the sun shining through a break in the clouds.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 4 Verse 99. That hero among men, with his splendid gold-adorned armor, shone brightly amidst the kings. He was resplendent like a lotus-radiant fire with little smoke, and like the sun shining through a break in the clouds.

sanskrit: तथा सुषेणोऽप्यसिचर्मपाणिस्तवात्मजः सत्यसेनश्च वीरः ।व्यवस्थितौ चित्रसेनेन सार्धं हृष्टात्मानौ समरे योद्धुकामौ ॥१००॥
transliteration: tathā suṣeṇo'pyasicarmapāṇi;stavātmajaḥ satyasenaśca vīraḥ ,vyavasthitau citrasenena sārdhaṁ; hṛṣṭātmānau samare yoddhukāmau.
english: Similarly, your son Suṣeṇa, holding a sword and shield in hand, and the heroic Satyasena, both stood ready with Chitrasena. With joyful spirits (ātman), they were eager to fight in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 4 Verse 100. Similarly, your son Suṣeṇa, holding a sword and shield in hand, and the heroic Satyasena, both stood ready with Chitrasena. With joyful spirits (ātman), they were eager to fight in battle.

sanskrit: ह्रीनिषेधा भरता राजपुत्राश्चित्रायुधः श्रुतकर्मा जयश्च ।शलश्च सत्यव्रतदुःशलौ च व्यवस्थिता बलिनो योद्धुकामाः ॥१०१॥
transliteration: hrīniṣedhā bharatā rājaputrā;ścitrāyudhaḥ śrutakarmā jayaśca ,śalaśca satyavrataduḥśalau ca; vyavasthitā balino yoddhukāmāḥ.
english: The fearless Bharatas and princes, along with Citrāyudha, Śrutakarmā, Jaya, Śala, and Satyavrata and Duśśala, are all positioned here, mighty and eager to fight.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 4 Verse 101. The fearless Bharatas and princes, along with Citrāyudha, Śrutakarmā, Jaya, Śala, and Satyavrata and Duśśala, are all positioned here, mighty and eager to fight.

sanskrit: कैतव्यानामधिपः शूरमानी रणे रणे शत्रुहा राजपुत्रः ।पत्री हयी नागरथप्रयायी व्यवस्थितो योद्धुकामस्त्वदर्थे ॥१०२॥
transliteration: kaitavyānāmadhipaḥ śūramānī; raṇe raṇe śatruhā rājaputraḥ ,patrī hayī nāgarathaprayāyī; vyavasthito yoddhukāmastvadarthe.
english: The lord of the Kaitavyas, who is proud of his valor, a slayer of enemies in every battle, a prince, skilled with arrows and horses, and a rider of elephant-chariots, is positioned for your sake, eager to fight.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 4 Verse 102. The lord of the Kaitavyas, who is proud of his valor, a slayer of enemies in every battle, a prince, skilled with arrows and horses, and a rider of elephant-chariots, is positioned for your sake, eager to fight.

sanskrit: वीरः श्रुतायुश्च श्रुतायुधश्च चित्राङ्गदश्चित्रवर्मा स वीरः ।व्यवस्थिता ये तु सैन्ये नराग्र्याः प्रहारिणो मानिनः सत्यसंधाः ॥१०३॥
transliteration: vīraḥ śrutāyuśca śrutāyudhaśca; citrāṅgadaścitravarmā sa vīraḥ ,vyavasthitā ye tu sainye narāgryāḥ; prahāriṇo māninaḥ satyasaṁdhāḥ.
english: The brave Śrutāyu, Śrutāyudha, Citrāṅgada, and the valorous Citravarmā – and indeed those other foremost men in the army who are powerful assailants, honorable, and firm in their pledges – are all positioned.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 4 Verse 103. The brave Śrutāyu, Śrutāyudha, Citrāṅgada, and the valorous Citravarmā – and indeed those other foremost men in the army who are powerful assailants, honorable, and firm in their pledges – are all positioned.

sanskrit: कर्णात्मजः सत्यसेनो महात्मा व्यवस्थितः समरे योद्धुकामः ।अथापरौ कर्णसुतौ वरार्हौ व्यवस्थितौ लघुहस्तौ नरेन्द्र ।बलं महद्दुर्भिदमल्पधैर्यैः समाश्रितौ योत्स्यमानौ त्वदर्थे ॥१०४॥
transliteration: karṇātmajaḥ satyaseno mahātmā; vyavasthitaḥ samare yoddhukāmaḥ ,athāparau karṇasutau varārhau; vyavasthitau laghuhastau narendra ,balaṁ mahaddurbhidamalpadhairyaiḥ; samāśritau yotsyamānau tvadarthe.
english: The great-souled Satyāśena, son of Karṇa, is positioned in battle, eager to fight. And then, O King, two other excellent sons of Karṇa, quick-handed, are positioned. Relying on a great strength that is difficult for those of little courage to break, they are about to fight for your sake.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 4 Verse 104. The great-souled Satyāśena, son of Karṇa, is positioned in battle, eager to fight. And then, O King, two other excellent sons of Karṇa, quick-handed, are positioned. Relying on a great strength that is difficult for those of little courage to break, they are about to fight for your sake.

sanskrit: एतैश्च मुख्यैरपरैश्च राजन्योधप्रवीरैरमितप्रभावैः ।व्यवस्थितो नागकुलस्य मध्ये यथा महेन्द्रः कुरुराजो जयाय ॥१०५॥
transliteration: etaiśca mukhyairaparaiśca rāja;nyodhapravīrairamitaprabhāvaiḥ ,vyavasthito nāgakulasya madhye; yathā mahendraḥ kururājo jayāya.
english: And by these, O King, along with other principal and mighty warriors of immeasurable prowess, the Kuru king (Duryodhana) stood arrayed amidst a host of elephants, just like the great Indra, for victory.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 4 Verse 105. And by these, O King, along with other principal and mighty warriors of immeasurable prowess, the Kuru king (Duryodhana) stood arrayed amidst a host of elephants, just like the great Indra, for victory.

sanskrit: धृतराष्ट्र उवाच ।आख्याता जीवमाना ये परेभ्योऽन्ये यथातथम् ।इतीदमभिगच्छामि व्यक्तमर्थाभिपत्तितः ॥१०६॥
transliteration: dhṛtarāṣṭra uvāca ,ākhyātā jīvamānā ye parebhyo'nye yathātatham ,itīdamabhigacchāmi vyaktamarthābhipattitaḥ.
english: Dhṛtarāṣṭra said: 'I now clearly understand this, from the unfolding of the circumstances, that those of our own men who are still alive, and similarly those of the enemies, have been described accurately.'
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 4 Verse 106. Dhṛtarāṣṭra said: 'I now clearly understand this, from the unfolding of the circumstances, that those of our own men who are still alive, and similarly those of the enemies, have been described accurately.'

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।एवं ब्रुवन्नेव तदा धृतराष्ट्रोऽम्बिकासुतः ।हतप्रवीरं विध्वस्तं किंचिच्छेषं स्वकं बलम् ।श्रुत्वा व्यामोहमगमच्छोकव्याकुलितेन्द्रियः ॥१०७॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,evaṁ bruvanneva tadā dhṛtarāṣṭro'mbikāsutaḥ ,hatapravīraṁ vidhvastaṁ kiṁciccheṣaṁ svakaṁ balam ,śrutvā vyāmohamagamacchokavyākulitendriyaḥ.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: Just as Dhṛtarāṣṭra, the son of Ambikā, was speaking thus, upon hearing that his own army was shattered, its chief warriors slain, and only a small part of it remained, he fell into a state of utter confusion, his senses agitated by grief.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 4 Verse 107. Vaiśampāyana said: Just as Dhṛtarāṣṭra, the son of Ambikā, was speaking thus, upon hearing that his own army was shattered, its chief warriors slain, and only a small part of it remained, he fell into a state of utter confusion, his senses agitated by grief.

sanskrit: मुह्यमानोऽब्रवीच्चापि मुहूर्तं तिष्ठ संजय ।व्याकुलं मे मनस्तात श्रुत्वा सुमहदप्रियम् ।नष्टचित्तस्ततः सोऽथ बभूव जगतीपतिः ॥१०८॥
transliteration: muhyamāno'bravīccāpi muhūrtaṁ tiṣṭha saṁjaya ,vyākulaṁ me manastāta śrutvā sumahadapriyam ,naṣṭacittastataḥ so'tha babhūva jagatīpatiḥ.
english: And being utterly bewildered, he also said, 'Wait for a moment, Saṃjaya! My mind, dear one, is greatly agitated after hearing such extremely unpleasant news.' Then, with his consciousness lost, that lord of the earth (Dhṛtarāṣṭra) became completely distraught.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 4 Verse 108. And being utterly bewildered, he also said, 'Wait for a moment, Saṃjaya! My mind, dear one, is greatly agitated after hearing such extremely unpleasant news.' Then, with his consciousness lost, that lord of the earth (Dhṛtarāṣṭra) became completely distraught.

sanskrit: जनमेजय उवाच ।श्रुत्वा कर्णं हतं युद्धे पुत्रांश्चैवापलायिनः ।नरेन्द्रः किंचिदाश्वस्तो द्विजश्रेष्ठ किमब्रवीत् ॥१॥
transliteration: janamejaya uvāca ,śrutvā karṇaṁ hataṁ yuddhe putrāṁścaivāpalāyinaḥ ,narendraḥ kiṁcidāśvasto dvijaśreṣṭha kimabravīt.
english: Janamejaya asked: "O best among the twice-born (dvija), after hearing that Karna was killed in battle and that his sons did not flee, what did that king, somewhat consoled, then say?"
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 5 Verse 1. Janamejaya asked: "O best among the twice-born (dvija), after hearing that Karna was killed in battle and that his sons did not flee, what did that king, somewhat consoled, then say?"

sanskrit: प्राप्तवान्परमं दुःखं पुत्रव्यसनजं महत् ।तस्मिन्यदुक्तवान्काले तन्ममाचक्ष्व पृच्छतः ॥२॥
transliteration: prāptavānparamaṁ duḥkhaṁ putravyasanajaṁ mahat ,tasminyaduktavānkāle tanmamācakṣva pṛcchataḥ.
english: (He, Dhṛtarāṣṭra) had experienced supreme sorrow, a great distress born from the misfortune of his sons. Please tell me, who am asking, what he said at that particular time.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 5 Verse 2. (He, Dhṛtarāṣṭra) had experienced supreme sorrow, a great distress born from the misfortune of his sons. Please tell me, who am asking, what he said at that particular time.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।श्रुत्वा कर्णस्य निधनमश्रद्धेयमिवाद्भुतम् ।भूतसंमोहनं भीमं मेरोः पर्यसनं यथा ॥३॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,śrutvā karṇasya nidhanamaśraddheyamivādbhutam ,bhūtasaṁmohanaṁ bhīmaṁ meroḥ paryasanaṁ yathā.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: "Upon hearing of Karna's death – which was astonishing, almost unbelievable, terrifying, like a great delusion falling upon all beings, or indeed, like the overturning of Mount Meru itself – (Dhṛtarāṣṭra reacted in a certain way)."
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 5 Verse 3. Vaiśampāyana said: "Upon hearing of Karna's death – which was astonishing, almost unbelievable, terrifying, like a great delusion falling upon all beings, or indeed, like the overturning of Mount Meru itself – (Dhṛtarāṣṭra reacted in a certain way)."

sanskrit: चित्तमोहमिवायुक्तं भार्गवस्य महामतेः ।पराजयमिवेन्द्रस्य द्विषद्भ्यो भीमकर्मणः ॥४॥
transliteration: cittamohamivāyuktaṁ bhārgavasya mahāmateḥ ,parājayamivendrasya dviṣadbhyo bhīmakarmaṇaḥ.
english: (It was like) an inexplicable bewilderment of mind for the great-minded (mahāmate) Bhārgava (Paraśurāma), or like the defeat of Indra at the hands of his formidable enemies (dviṣat) who perform terrible deeds (bhīmakarman).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 5 Verse 4. (It was like) an inexplicable bewilderment of mind for the great-minded (mahāmate) Bhārgava (Paraśurāma), or like the defeat of Indra at the hands of his formidable enemies (dviṣat) who perform terrible deeds (bhīmakarman).

sanskrit: दिवः प्रपतनं भानोरुर्व्यामिव महाद्युतेः ।संशोषणमिवाचिन्त्यं समुद्रस्याक्षयाम्भसः ॥५॥
transliteration: divaḥ prapatanaṁ bhānorurvyāmiva mahādyuteḥ ,saṁśoṣaṇamivācintyaṁ samudrasyākṣayāmbhasaḥ.
english: Like the falling of the greatly radiant sun from the sky onto the earth; or like the inconceivable complete drying up of the ocean whose waters are inexhaustible.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 5 Verse 5. Like the falling of the greatly radiant sun from the sky onto the earth; or like the inconceivable complete drying up of the ocean whose waters are inexhaustible.

sanskrit: महीवियद्दिगीशानां सर्वनाशमिवाद्भुतम् ।कर्मणोरिव वैफल्यमुभयोः पुण्यपापयोः ॥६॥
transliteration: mahīviyaddigīśānāṁ sarvanāśamivādbhutam ,karmaṇoriva vaiphalyamubhayoḥ puṇyapāpayoḥ.
english: Or like the astounding complete destruction of the earth, sky, and the guardians of the directions; or like the utter fruitlessness of both merit (puṇya) and demerit (pāpa).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 5 Verse 6. Or like the astounding complete destruction of the earth, sky, and the guardians of the directions; or like the utter fruitlessness of both merit (puṇya) and demerit (pāpa).

sanskrit: संचिन्त्य निपुणं बुद्ध्या धृतराष्ट्रो जनेश्वरः ।नेदमस्तीति संचिन्त्य कर्णस्य निधनं प्रति ॥७॥
transliteration: saṁcintya nipuṇaṁ buddhyā dhṛtarāṣṭro janeśvaraḥ ,nedamastīti saṁcintya karṇasya nidhanaṁ prati.
english: King Dhṛtarāṣṭra, the ruler of men, having thoroughly reflected with his intellect (buddhi), and having considered that 'this cannot be' regarding Karṇa's death...
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 5 Verse 7. King Dhṛtarāṣṭra, the ruler of men, having thoroughly reflected with his intellect (buddhi), and having considered that 'this cannot be' regarding Karṇa's death...

sanskrit: प्राणिनामेतदात्मत्वात्स्यादपीति विनाशनम् ।शोकाग्निना दह्यमानो धम्यमान इवाशयः ॥८॥
transliteration: prāṇināmetadātmatvātsyādapīti vināśanam ,śokāgninā dahyamāno dhamyamāna ivāśayaḥ.
english: This destruction could even be (understood as) the destruction of the very essence (ātman) of living beings. His inner being (āśaya), scorched by the fire of grief, was as if being fanned (to intensify the flames).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 5 Verse 8. This destruction could even be (understood as) the destruction of the very essence (ātman) of living beings. His inner being (āśaya), scorched by the fire of grief, was as if being fanned (to intensify the flames).

sanskrit: विध्वस्तात्मा श्वसन्दीनो हा हेत्युक्त्वा सुदुःखितः ।विललाप महाराज धृतराष्ट्रोऽम्बिकासुतः ॥९॥
transliteration: vidhvastātmā śvasandīno hā hetyuktvā suduḥkhitaḥ ,vilalāpa mahārāja dhṛtarāṣṭro'mbikāsutaḥ.
english: O great king, Dhṛtarāṣṭra, the son of Ambikā, whose self (ātman) was shattered, sighing, wretched, and extremely sorrowful, lamented, having exclaimed "Alas, alas!".
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 5 Verse 9. O great king, Dhṛtarāṣṭra, the son of Ambikā, whose self (ātman) was shattered, sighing, wretched, and extremely sorrowful, lamented, having exclaimed "Alas, alas!".

sanskrit: धृतराष्ट्र उवाच ।संजयाधिरथो वीरः सिंहद्विरदविक्रमः ।वृषमप्रतिमस्कन्धो वृषभाक्षगतिस्वनः ॥१०॥
transliteration: dhṛtarāṣṭra uvāca ,saṁjayādhiratho vīraḥ siṁhadviradavikramaḥ ,vṛṣamapratimaskandho vṛṣabhākṣagatisvanaḥ.
english: Dhṛtarāṣṭra said: "O Sañjaya, the heroic Karna, who possesses the valor of a lion and an elephant, whose shoulders are unequalled like a bull's, and whose gaze, gait, and roar are like that of a bull - "
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 5 Verse 10. Dhṛtarāṣṭra said: "O Sañjaya, the heroic Karna, who possesses the valor of a lion and an elephant, whose shoulders are unequalled like a bull's, and whose gaze, gait, and roar are like that of a bull - "

sanskrit: वृषभो वृषभस्येव यो युद्धे न निवर्तते ।शत्रोरपि महेन्द्रस्य वज्रसंहननो युवा ॥११॥
transliteration: vṛṣabho vṛṣabhasyeva yo yuddhe na nivartate ,śatrorapi mahendrasya vajrasaṁhanano yuvā.
english: ...who, like a bull among bulls, never retreats in battle, even from a powerful enemy like Mahendra (Indra), a young man whose body (saṃhanana) is as firm as a thunderbolt.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 5 Verse 11. ...who, like a bull among bulls, never retreats in battle, even from a powerful enemy like Mahendra (Indra), a young man whose body (saṃhanana) is as firm as a thunderbolt.

sanskrit: यस्य ज्यातलशब्देन शरवृष्टिरवेण च ।रथाश्वनरमातङ्गा नावतिष्ठन्ति संयुगे ॥१२॥
transliteration: yasya jyātalaśabdena śaravṛṣṭiraveṇa ca ,rathāśvanaramātaṅgā nāvatiṣṭhanti saṁyuge.
english: ...due to whose bowstring's twang and palm-guard's sound, and the roar of his shower of arrows, chariots, horses, men, and elephants do not stand firm in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 5 Verse 12. ...due to whose bowstring's twang and palm-guard's sound, and the roar of his shower of arrows, chariots, horses, men, and elephants do not stand firm in battle.

sanskrit: यमाश्रित्य महाबाहुं द्विषत्संघघ्नमच्युतम् ।दुर्योधनोऽकरोद्वैरं पाण्डुपुत्रैर्महाबलैः ॥१३॥
transliteration: yamāśritya mahābāhuṁ dviṣatsaṁghaghnamacyutam ,duryodhano'karodvairaṁ pāṇḍuputrairmahābalaiḥ.
english: Relying on that mighty-armed (ally), the unfailing one, who was a slayer of enemy hosts, Duryodhana waged war against the immensely powerful sons of Pāṇḍu.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 5 Verse 13. Relying on that mighty-armed (ally), the unfailing one, who was a slayer of enemy hosts, Duryodhana waged war against the immensely powerful sons of Pāṇḍu.

sanskrit: स कथं रथिनां श्रेष्ठः कर्णः पार्थेन संयुगे ।निहतः पुरुषव्याघ्रः प्रसह्यासह्यविक्रमः ॥१४॥
transliteration: sa kathaṁ rathināṁ śreṣṭhaḥ karṇaḥ pārthena saṁyuge ,nihataḥ puruṣavyāghraḥ prasahyāsahyavikramaḥ.
english: How could that Karna, the foremost among charioteers, a tiger among men, whose prowess was irresistibly forceful, be slain in battle by Arjuna (Pārtha)?
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 5 Verse 14. How could that Karna, the foremost among charioteers, a tiger among men, whose prowess was irresistibly forceful, be slain in battle by Arjuna (Pārtha)?

sanskrit: यो नामन्यत वै नित्यमच्युतं न धनंजयम् ।न वृष्णीनपि तानन्यान्स्वबाहुबलमाश्रितः ॥१५॥
transliteration: yo nāmanyata vai nityamacyutaṁ na dhanaṁjayam ,na vṛṣṇīnapi tānanyānsvabāhubalamāśritaḥ.
english: He who, always relying solely on the strength of his own arms, never considered Kṛṣṇa (acyuta) (to be powerful), nor Arjuna (Dhananjaya), nor even those other members of the Vṛṣṇi clan.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 5 Verse 15. He who, always relying solely on the strength of his own arms, never considered Kṛṣṇa (acyuta) (to be powerful), nor Arjuna (Dhananjaya), nor even those other members of the Vṛṣṇi clan.

sanskrit: शार्ङ्गगाण्डीवधन्वानौ सहितावपराजितौ ।अहं दिव्याद्रथादेकः पातयिष्यामि संयुगे ॥१६॥
transliteration: śārṅgagāṇḍīvadhanvānau sahitāvaparājitau ,ahaṁ divyādrathādekaḥ pātayiṣyāmi saṁyuge.
english: I alone will fell from their divine chariot both the unconquered wielder of the Śārṅga bow (Kṛṣṇa) and the wielder of the Gāṇḍīva bow (Arjuna), even when they are united in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 5 Verse 16. I alone will fell from their divine chariot both the unconquered wielder of the Śārṅga bow (Kṛṣṇa) and the wielder of the Gāṇḍīva bow (Arjuna), even when they are united in battle.

sanskrit: इति यः सततं मन्दमवोचल्लोभमोहितम् ।दुर्योधनमपादीनं राज्यकामुकमातुरम् ॥१७॥
transliteration: iti yaḥ satataṁ mandamavocallobhamohitam ,duryodhanamapādīnaṁ rājyakāmukamāturam.
english: Thus, he who constantly spoke to foolish Duryodhana, who was infatuated by greed, helpless, desirous of the kingdom, and distressed.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 5 Verse 17. Thus, he who constantly spoke to foolish Duryodhana, who was infatuated by greed, helpless, desirous of the kingdom, and distressed.

sanskrit: यश्चाजैषीदतिबलानमित्रानपि दुर्जयान् ।गान्धारान्मद्रकान्मत्स्यांस्त्रिगर्तांस्तङ्गणाञ्शकान् ॥१८॥
transliteration: yaścājaiṣīdatibalānamitrānapi durjayān ,gāndhārānmadrakānmatsyāṁstrigartāṁstaṅgaṇāñśakān.
english: And he who conquered even the extremely powerful, unconquerable enemies: the Gandharas, Madras, Matsyas, Trigartas, Tanganas, and Shakas.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 5 Verse 18. And he who conquered even the extremely powerful, unconquerable enemies: the Gandharas, Madras, Matsyas, Trigartas, Tanganas, and Shakas.

sanskrit: पाञ्चालांश्च विदेहांश्च कुणिन्दान्काशिकोसलान् ।सुह्मानङ्गांश्च पुण्ड्रांश्च निषादान्वङ्गकीचकान् ॥१९॥
transliteration: pāñcālāṁśca videhāṁśca kuṇindānkāśikosalān ,suhmānaṅgāṁśca puṇḍrāṁśca niṣādānvaṅgakīcakān.
english: the Panchalas, and the Videhas, the Kunindas, the Kashi-Kusalas, the Suhmas, and the Angas, and the Pundras, the Nishadas, and the Vanga-Kichakas.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 5 Verse 19. the Panchalas, and the Videhas, the Kunindas, the Kashi-Kusalas, the Suhmas, and the Angas, and the Pundras, the Nishadas, and the Vanga-Kichakas.

sanskrit: वत्सान्कलिङ्गांस्तरलानश्मकानृषिकांस्तथा ।यो जित्वा समरे वीरश्चक्रे बलिभृतः पुरा ॥२०॥
transliteration: vatsānkaliṅgāṁstaralānaśmakānṛṣikāṁstathā ,yo jitvā samare vīraścakre balibhṛtaḥ purā.
english: and the Vatsas, Kalingas, Taralas, Ashmakas, and Rishikas. That hero, having conquered them in battle, formerly made them tribute-bearers.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 5 Verse 20. and the Vatsas, Kalingas, Taralas, Ashmakas, and Rishikas. That hero, having conquered them in battle, formerly made them tribute-bearers.

sanskrit: उच्चैःश्रवा वरोऽश्वानां राज्ञां वैश्रवणो वरः ।वरो महेन्द्रो देवानां कर्णः प्रहरतां वरः ॥२१॥
transliteration: uccaiḥśravā varo'śvānāṁ rājñāṁ vaiśravaṇo varaḥ ,varo mahendro devānāṁ karṇaḥ praharatāṁ varaḥ.
english: Uccaiḥśravas is the foremost of horses, Vaiśravaṇa (Kubera) is the foremost of kings, Mahendra (Indra) is the foremost of the gods, and Karṇa is the foremost among warriors.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 5 Verse 21. Uccaiḥśravas is the foremost of horses, Vaiśravaṇa (Kubera) is the foremost of kings, Mahendra (Indra) is the foremost of the gods, and Karṇa is the foremost among warriors.

sanskrit: यं लब्ध्वा मागधो राजा सान्त्वमानार्थगौरवैः ।अरौत्सीत्पार्थिवं क्षत्रमृते कौरवयादवान् ॥२२॥
transliteration: yaṁ labdhvā māgadho rājā sāntvamānārthagauravaiḥ ,arautsītpārthivaṁ kṣatramṛte kauravayādavān.
english: Having obtained him (Karṇa) with conciliatory words, honor, and generous gifts (artha-gauravaiḥ), the king of Magadha (Jarāsandha) had confined the royal warrior class, with the sole exception of the Kauravas and Yadavas.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 5 Verse 22. Having obtained him (Karṇa) with conciliatory words, honor, and generous gifts (artha-gauravaiḥ), the king of Magadha (Jarāsandha) had confined the royal warrior class, with the sole exception of the Kauravas and Yadavas.

sanskrit: तं श्रुत्वा निहतं कर्णं द्वैरथे सव्यसाचिना ।शोकार्णवे निमग्नोऽहमप्लवः सागरे यथा ॥२३॥
transliteration: taṁ śrutvā nihataṁ karṇaṁ dvairathe savyasācinā ,śokārṇave nimagno'hamaplavaḥ sāgare yathā.
english: Upon hearing that Karṇa was killed in a duel (dvairathe) by Savyasācin (Arjuna), I became immersed in an ocean of grief, like someone without a boat in the vast sea.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 5 Verse 23. Upon hearing that Karṇa was killed in a duel (dvairathe) by Savyasācin (Arjuna), I became immersed in an ocean of grief, like someone without a boat in the vast sea.

sanskrit: ईदृशैर्यद्यहं दुःखैर्न विनश्यामि संजय ।वज्राद्दृढतरं मन्ये हृदयं मम दुर्भिदम् ॥२४॥
transliteration: īdṛśairyadyahaṁ duḥkhairna vinaśyāmi saṁjaya ,vajrāddṛḍhataraṁ manye hṛdayaṁ mama durbhidam.
english: O Sañjaya, if I am not destroyed by such sorrows, then I consider my heart to be harder than a thunderbolt and indeed unbreakable.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 5 Verse 24. O Sañjaya, if I am not destroyed by such sorrows, then I consider my heart to be harder than a thunderbolt and indeed unbreakable.

sanskrit: ज्ञातिसंबन्धिमित्राणामिमं श्रुत्वा पराजयम् ।को मदन्यः पुमाँल्लोके न जह्यात्सूत जीवितम् ॥२५॥
transliteration: jñātisaṁbandhimitrāṇāmimaṁ śrutvā parājayam ,ko madanyaḥ pumāँlloke na jahyātsūta jīvitam.
english: O charioteer, having heard of this defeat of my kinsmen, relatives, and friends, what man in the world, other than myself, would not give up his life?
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 5 Verse 25. O charioteer, having heard of this defeat of my kinsmen, relatives, and friends, what man in the world, other than myself, would not give up his life?

sanskrit: विषमग्निं प्रपातं वा पर्वताग्रादहं वृणे ।न हि शक्ष्यामि दुःखानि सोढुं कष्टानि संजय ॥२६॥
transliteration: viṣamagniṁ prapātaṁ vā parvatāgrādahaṁ vṛṇe ,na hi śakṣyāmi duḥkhāni soḍhuṁ kaṣṭāni saṁjaya.
english: O Saṃjaya, I would choose poison, fire, or a fall from a mountain peak. Indeed, I will not be able to endure these grievous sufferings.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 5 Verse 26. O Saṃjaya, I would choose poison, fire, or a fall from a mountain peak. Indeed, I will not be able to endure these grievous sufferings.

sanskrit: संजय उवाच ।श्रिया कुलेन यशसा तपसा च श्रुतेन च ।त्वामद्य सन्तो मन्यन्ते ययातिमिव नाहुषम् ॥२७॥
transliteration: saṁjaya uvāca ,śriyā kulena yaśasā tapasā ca śrutena ca ,tvāmadya santo manyante yayātimiva nāhuṣam.
english: Saṃjaya said: 'Today, the virtuous consider you, by your splendor, lineage, fame, spiritual austerity (tapas), and sacred knowledge, to be like Yayāti, the son of Nahuṣa.'
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 5 Verse 27. Saṃjaya said: 'Today, the virtuous consider you, by your splendor, lineage, fame, spiritual austerity (tapas), and sacred knowledge, to be like Yayāti, the son of Nahuṣa.'

sanskrit: श्रुते महर्षिप्रतिमः कृतकृत्योऽसि पार्थिव ।पर्यवस्थापयात्मानं मा विषादे मनः कृथाः ॥२८॥
transliteration: śrute maharṣipratimaḥ kṛtakṛtyo'si pārthiva ,paryavasthāpayātmānaṁ mā viṣāde manaḥ kṛthāḥ.
english: O King, you are like a great sage in sacred knowledge, and you have accomplished your duty. Therefore, compose your self (ātman) and do not give your mind over to despair.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 5 Verse 28. O King, you are like a great sage in sacred knowledge, and you have accomplished your duty. Therefore, compose your self (ātman) and do not give your mind over to despair.

sanskrit: धृतराष्ट्र उवाच ।दैवमेव परं मन्ये धिक्पौरुषमनर्थकम् ।यत्र रामप्रतीकाशः कर्णोऽहन्यत संयुगे ॥२९॥
transliteration: dhṛtarāṣṭra uvāca ,daivameva paraṁ manye dhikpauruṣamanarthakam ,yatra rāmapratīkāśaḥ karṇo'hanyata saṁyuge.
english: Dhritarashtra said: "I consider destiny (daivam) alone to be supreme; shame on futile human effort (pauruṣam)! For it was in battle that Karna, who was like Rama, was killed."
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 5 Verse 29. Dhritarashtra said: "I consider destiny (daivam) alone to be supreme; shame on futile human effort (pauruṣam)! For it was in battle that Karna, who was like Rama, was killed."

sanskrit: हत्वा युधिष्ठिरानीकं पाञ्चालानां रथव्रजान् ।प्रताप्य शरवर्षेण दिशः सर्वा महारथः ॥३०॥
transliteration: hatvā yudhiṣṭhirānīkaṁ pāñcālānāṁ rathavrajān ,pratāpya śaravarṣeṇa diśaḥ sarvā mahārathaḥ.
english: Having destroyed Yudhishthira's army and the hosts of chariots of the Panchalas, that great charioteer (Karna), having scorched all directions with a shower of arrows...
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 5 Verse 30. Having destroyed Yudhishthira's army and the hosts of chariots of the Panchalas, that great charioteer (Karna), having scorched all directions with a shower of arrows...

sanskrit: मोहयित्वा रणे पार्थान्वज्रहस्त इवासुरान् ।स कथं निहतः शेते वातरुग्ण इव द्रुमः ॥३१॥
transliteration: mohayitvā raṇe pārthānvajrahasta ivāsurān ,sa kathaṁ nihataḥ śete vātarugṇa iva drumaḥ.
english: ...having bewildered the Pandavas in battle, just as Indra, holding the thunderbolt (vajrahasta), bewilders the demons (asurās) – how then does he lie slain, like a tree broken by the wind?
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 5 Verse 31. ...having bewildered the Pandavas in battle, just as Indra, holding the thunderbolt (vajrahasta), bewilders the demons (asurās) – how then does he lie slain, like a tree broken by the wind?

sanskrit: शोकस्यान्तं न पश्यामि समुद्रस्येव विप्लुकाः ।चिन्ता मे वर्धते तीव्रा मुमूर्षा चापि जायते ॥३२॥
transliteration: śokasyāntaṁ na paśyāmi samudrasyeva viplukāḥ ,cintā me vardhate tīvrā mumūrṣā cāpi jāyate.
english: I do not see an end to this sorrow, just as small fish (viplukāḥ) do not see the end of the ocean. My intense anxiety (cintā) grows, and indeed, a desire to die (mumūrṣā) arises in me.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 5 Verse 32. I do not see an end to this sorrow, just as small fish (viplukāḥ) do not see the end of the ocean. My intense anxiety (cintā) grows, and indeed, a desire to die (mumūrṣā) arises in me.

sanskrit: कर्णस्य निधनं श्रुत्वा विजयं फल्गुनस्य च ।अश्रद्धेयमहं मन्ये वधं कर्णस्य संजय ॥३३॥
transliteration: karṇasya nidhanaṁ śrutvā vijayaṁ phalgunasya ca ,aśraddheyamahaṁ manye vadhaṁ karṇasya saṁjaya.
english: O Sañjaya, having heard of Karṇa's death and Arjuna's victory, I consider Karṇa's demise unbelievable.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 5 Verse 33. O Sañjaya, having heard of Karṇa's death and Arjuna's victory, I consider Karṇa's demise unbelievable.

sanskrit: वज्रसारमयं नूनं हृदयं सुदृढं मम ।यच्छ्रुत्वा पुरुषव्याघ्रं हतं कर्णं न दीर्यते ॥३४॥
transliteration: vajrasāramayaṁ nūnaṁ hṛdayaṁ sudṛḍhaṁ mama ,yacchrutvā puruṣavyāghraṁ hataṁ karṇaṁ na dīryate.
english: Surely, my heart must be exceedingly strong, made of adamantine essence, since it does not break even after hearing that Karṇa, that tiger among men, has been slain.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 5 Verse 34. Surely, my heart must be exceedingly strong, made of adamantine essence, since it does not break even after hearing that Karṇa, that tiger among men, has been slain.

sanskrit: आयुर्नूनं सुदीर्घं मे विहितं दैवतैः पुरा ।यत्र कर्णं हतं श्रुत्वा जीवामीह सुदुःखितः ॥३५॥
transliteration: āyurnūnaṁ sudīrghaṁ me vihitaṁ daivataiḥ purā ,yatra karṇaṁ hataṁ śrutvā jīvāmīha suduḥkhitaḥ.
english: Surely, a very long life was ordained for me by the gods previously, since, having heard of Karṇa's death, I still live here, greatly distressed.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 5 Verse 35. Surely, a very long life was ordained for me by the gods previously, since, having heard of Karṇa's death, I still live here, greatly distressed.

sanskrit: धिग्जीवितमिदं मेऽद्य सुहृद्धीनस्य संजय ।अद्य चाहं दशामेतां गतः संजय गर्हिताम् ।कृपणं वर्तयिष्यामि शोच्यः सर्वस्य मन्दधीः ॥३६॥
transliteration: dhigjīvitamidaṁ me'dya suhṛddhīnasya saṁjaya ,adya cāhaṁ daśāmetāṁ gataḥ saṁjaya garhitām ,kṛpaṇaṁ vartayiṣyāmi śocyaḥ sarvasya mandadhīḥ.
english: O Sañjaya, shame on this life of mine today, deprived of my dear friend! And today, O Sañjaya, I have reached this despicable state. I shall live miserably, a dull-witted man pitiable to all.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 5 Verse 36. O Sañjaya, shame on this life of mine today, deprived of my dear friend! And today, O Sañjaya, I have reached this despicable state. I shall live miserably, a dull-witted man pitiable to all.

sanskrit: अहमेव पुरा भूत्वा सर्वलोकस्य सत्कृतः ।परिभूतः कथं सूत पुनः शक्ष्यामि जीवितुम् ।दुःखात्सुदुःखं व्यसनं प्राप्तवानस्मि संजय ॥३७॥
transliteration: ahameva purā bhūtvā sarvalokasya satkṛtaḥ ,paribhūtaḥ kathaṁ sūta punaḥ śakṣyāmi jīvitum ,duḥkhātsuduḥkhaṁ vyasanaṁ prāptavānasmi saṁjaya.
english: Having formerly been honored by all people, how, O charioteer, can I live again now that I have been disgraced? O Sañjaya, I have fallen into a calamity that is a very profound sorrow, arising from my previous grief.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 5 Verse 37. Having formerly been honored by all people, how, O charioteer, can I live again now that I have been disgraced? O Sañjaya, I have fallen into a calamity that is a very profound sorrow, arising from my previous grief.

sanskrit: तस्माद्भीष्मवधे चैव द्रोणस्य च महात्मनः ।नात्र शेषं प्रपश्यामि सूतपुत्रे हते युधि ॥३८॥
transliteration: tasmādbhīṣmavadhe caiva droṇasya ca mahātmanaḥ ,nātra śeṣaṁ prapaśyāmi sūtaputre hate yudhi.
english: Therefore, with the slaying of Bhīṣma and also of the great-souled Droṇa, and with the charioteer's son (Karṇa) killed in battle, I see no remainder (hope) here.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 5 Verse 38. Therefore, with the slaying of Bhīṣma and also of the great-souled Droṇa, and with the charioteer's son (Karṇa) killed in battle, I see no remainder (hope) here.

sanskrit: स हि पारं महानासीत्पुत्राणां मम संजय ।युद्धे विनिहतः शूरो विसृजन्सायकान्बहून् ॥३९॥
transliteration: sa hi pāraṁ mahānāsītputrāṇāṁ mama saṁjaya ,yuddhe vinihataḥ śūro visṛjansāyakānbahūn.
english: Indeed, O Sañjaya, he was a great support for my sons. That hero, discharging many arrows, was slain in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 5 Verse 39. Indeed, O Sañjaya, he was a great support for my sons. That hero, discharging many arrows, was slain in battle.

sanskrit: को हि मे जीवितेनार्थस्तमृते पुरुषर्षभम् ।रथादतिरथो नूनमपतत्सायकार्दितः ॥४०॥
transliteration: ko hi me jīvitenārthastamṛte puruṣarṣabham ,rathādatiratho nūnamapatatsāyakārditaḥ.
english: Indeed, what is the purpose of life for me without that best of men (puruṣarṣabha)? Surely, that supreme warrior (atiratha), afflicted by arrows, fell from his chariot.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 5 Verse 40. Indeed, what is the purpose of life for me without that best of men (puruṣarṣabha)? Surely, that supreme warrior (atiratha), afflicted by arrows, fell from his chariot.

sanskrit: पर्वतस्येव शिखरं वज्रपातविदारितम् ।शयीत पृथिवीं नूनं शोभयन्रुधिरोक्षितः ।मातङ्ग इव मत्तेन मातङ्गेन निपातितः ॥४१॥
transliteration: parvatasyeva śikharaṁ vajrapātavidāritam ,śayīta pṛthivīṁ nūnaṁ śobhayanrudhirokṣitaḥ ,mātaṅga iva mattena mātaṅgena nipātitaḥ.
english: Certainly, he lies on the earth, like a mountain peak split open by a thunderbolt, adorning the ground while drenched in blood. He has been struck down like an elephant by a rutting elephant.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 5 Verse 41. Certainly, he lies on the earth, like a mountain peak split open by a thunderbolt, adorning the ground while drenched in blood. He has been struck down like an elephant by a rutting elephant.

sanskrit: यद्बलं धार्तराष्ट्राणां पाण्डवानां यतो भयम् ।सोऽर्जुनेन हतः कर्णः प्रतिमानं धनुष्मताम् ॥४२॥
transliteration: yadbalaṁ dhārtarāṣṭrāṇāṁ pāṇḍavānāṁ yato bhayam ,so'rjunena hataḥ karṇaḥ pratimānaṁ dhanuṣmatām.
english: Karna, who was the very strength of the Dhritarashtras and from whom the Pandavas felt fear, has been slain by Arjuna. He was the standard among archers.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 5 Verse 42. Karna, who was the very strength of the Dhritarashtras and from whom the Pandavas felt fear, has been slain by Arjuna. He was the standard among archers.

sanskrit: स हि वीरो महेष्वासः पुत्राणामभयंकरः ।शेते विनिहतो वीरः शक्रेणेव यथा बलः ॥४३॥
transliteration: sa hi vīro maheṣvāsaḥ putrāṇāmabhayaṁkaraḥ ,śete vinihato vīraḥ śakreṇeva yathā balaḥ.
english: Indeed, that hero, a great archer and protector of his sons, lies completely slain, just as the hero Bala was struck down by Indra.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 5 Verse 43. Indeed, that hero, a great archer and protector of his sons, lies completely slain, just as the hero Bala was struck down by Indra.

sanskrit: पङ्गोरिवाध्वगमनं दरिद्रस्येव कामितम् ।दुर्योधनस्य चाकूतं तृषितस्येव पिप्लुकाः ॥४४॥
transliteration: paṅgorivādhvagamanaṁ daridrasyeva kāmitam ,duryodhanasya cākūtaṁ tṛṣitasyeva piplukāḥ.
english: Duryodhana's desire is like a journey for a cripple, or a cherished wish for a poor person; it is like mere drops of water for a thirsty man.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 5 Verse 44. Duryodhana's desire is like a journey for a cripple, or a cherished wish for a poor person; it is like mere drops of water for a thirsty man.

sanskrit: अन्यथा चिन्तितं कार्यमन्यथा तत्तु जायते ।अहो नु बलवद्दैवं कालश्च दुरतिक्रमः ॥४५॥
transliteration: anyathā cintitaṁ kāryamanyathā tattu jāyate ,aho nu balavaddaivaṁ kālaśca duratikramaḥ.
english: A matter conceived in one way indeed turns out completely differently. Alas, destiny (daivam) is powerful, and time (kāla) is insurmountable.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 5 Verse 45. A matter conceived in one way indeed turns out completely differently. Alas, destiny (daivam) is powerful, and time (kāla) is insurmountable.

sanskrit: पलायमानः कृपणं दीनात्मा दीनपौरुषः ।कच्चिन्न निहतः सूत पुत्रो दुःशासनो मम ॥४६॥
transliteration: palāyamānaḥ kṛpaṇaṁ dīnātmā dīnapauruṣaḥ ,kaccinna nihataḥ sūta putro duḥśāsano mama.
english: O charioteer, I hope that my son Duḥśāsana, who was fleeing miserably, with a dejected spirit and diminished valor, was not killed.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 5 Verse 46. O charioteer, I hope that my son Duḥśāsana, who was fleeing miserably, with a dejected spirit and diminished valor, was not killed.

sanskrit: कच्चिन्न नीचाचरितं कृतवांस्तात संयुगे ।कच्चिन्न निहतः शूरो यथा न क्षत्रिया हताः ॥४७॥
transliteration: kaccinna nīcācaritaṁ kṛtavāṁstāta saṁyuge ,kaccinna nihataḥ śūro yathā na kṣatriyā hatāḥ.
english: O dear one, I hope he did not perform a cowardly act in battle. I hope the hero was not killed, lest Kṣatriyas (as a whole) be annihilated.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 5 Verse 47. O dear one, I hope he did not perform a cowardly act in battle. I hope the hero was not killed, lest Kṣatriyas (as a whole) be annihilated.

sanskrit: युधिष्ठिरस्य वचनं मा युद्धमिति सर्वदा ।दुर्योधनो नाभ्यगृह्णान्मूढः पथ्यमिवौषधम् ॥४८॥
transliteration: yudhiṣṭhirasya vacanaṁ mā yuddhamiti sarvadā ,duryodhano nābhyagṛhṇānmūḍhaḥ pathyamivauṣadham.
english: The foolish Duryodhana never accepted Yudhiṣṭhira's advice, which was "Do not wage war," just as one does not accept beneficial medicine.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 5 Verse 48. The foolish Duryodhana never accepted Yudhiṣṭhira's advice, which was "Do not wage war," just as one does not accept beneficial medicine.

sanskrit: शरतल्पे शयानेन भीष्मेण सुमहात्मना ।पानीयं याचितः पार्थः सोऽविध्यन्मेदिनीतलम् ॥४९॥
transliteration: śaratalpe śayānena bhīṣmeṇa sumahātmanā ,pānīyaṁ yācitaḥ pārthaḥ so'vidhyanmedinītalam.
english: While lying on a bed of arrows, the immensely noble-hearted Bhishma requested water. Partha (Arjuna) then pierced the surface of the earth.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 5 Verse 49. While lying on a bed of arrows, the immensely noble-hearted Bhishma requested water. Partha (Arjuna) then pierced the surface of the earth.

sanskrit: जलस्य धारां विहितां दृष्ट्वा तां पाण्डवेन ह ।अब्रवीत्स महाबाहुस्तात संशाम्य पाण्डवैः ॥५०॥
transliteration: jalasya dhārāṁ vihitāṁ dṛṣṭvā tāṁ pāṇḍavena ha ,abravītsa mahābāhustāta saṁśāmya pāṇḍavaiḥ.
english: Seeing that stream of water made by the son of Pandu (Arjuna), the great-armed one (Bhishma) said, "My dear son, make peace with the Pandavas."
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 5 Verse 50. Seeing that stream of water made by the son of Pandu (Arjuna), the great-armed one (Bhishma) said, "My dear son, make peace with the Pandavas."

sanskrit: प्रशमाद्धि भवेच्छान्तिर्मदन्तं युद्धमस्तु च ।भ्रातृभावेन पृथिवीं भुङ्क्ष्व पाण्डुसुतैः सह ॥५१॥
transliteration: praśamāddhi bhavecchāntirmadantaṁ yuddhamastu ca ,bhrātṛbhāvena pṛthivīṁ bhuṅkṣva pāṇḍusutaiḥ saha.
english: Indeed, peace arises from cessation; let this war end with me. Enjoy the earth together with the sons of Pandu, in a spirit of brotherhood.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 5 Verse 51. Indeed, peace arises from cessation; let this war end with me. Enjoy the earth together with the sons of Pandu, in a spirit of brotherhood.

sanskrit: अकुर्वन्वचनं तस्य नूनं शोचति मे सुतः ।तदिदं समनुप्राप्तं वचनं दीर्घदर्शिनः ॥५२॥
transliteration: akurvanvacanaṁ tasya nūnaṁ śocati me sutaḥ ,tadidaṁ samanuprāptaṁ vacanaṁ dīrghadarśinaḥ.
english: My son, certainly grieves for not following his (Bhishma's) instruction. Indeed, this very word of the far-sighted one has now come to pass.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 5 Verse 52. My son, certainly grieves for not following his (Bhishma's) instruction. Indeed, this very word of the far-sighted one has now come to pass.

sanskrit: अहं तु निहतामात्यो हतपुत्रश्च संजय ।द्यूततः कृच्छ्रमापन्नो लूनपक्ष इव द्विजः ॥५३॥
transliteration: ahaṁ tu nihatāmātyo hataputraśca saṁjaya ,dyūtataḥ kṛcchramāpanno lūnapakṣa iva dvijaḥ.
english: But I, whose ministers have been slain and whose sons have been killed, O Sañjaya, have fallen into great distress because of gambling, just like a bird (dvija) with its wings clipped.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 5 Verse 53. But I, whose ministers have been slain and whose sons have been killed, O Sañjaya, have fallen into great distress because of gambling, just like a bird (dvija) with its wings clipped.

sanskrit: यथा हि शकुनिं गृह्य छित्त्वा पक्षौ च संजय ।विसर्जयन्ति संहृष्टाः क्रीडमानाः कुमारकाः ॥५४॥
transliteration: yathā hi śakuniṁ gṛhya chittvā pakṣau ca saṁjaya ,visarjayanti saṁhṛṣṭāḥ krīḍamānāḥ kumārakāḥ.
english: O Sañjaya, just as gleeful boys, after catching a bird and clipping its wings, then release it while playing.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 5 Verse 54. O Sañjaya, just as gleeful boys, after catching a bird and clipping its wings, then release it while playing.

sanskrit: छिन्नपक्षतया तस्य गमनं नोपपद्यते ।तथाहमपि संप्राप्तो लूनपक्ष इव द्विजः ॥५५॥
transliteration: chinnapakṣatayā tasya gamanaṁ nopapadyate ,tathāhamapi saṁprāpto lūnapakṣa iva dvijaḥ.
english: Because its wings are clipped, it is unable to fly. Similarly, I too have become like a bird (dvija) with clipped wings.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 5 Verse 55. Because its wings are clipped, it is unable to fly. Similarly, I too have become like a bird (dvija) with clipped wings.

sanskrit: क्षीणः सर्वार्थहीनश्च निर्बन्धुर्ज्ञातिवर्जितः ।कां दिशं प्रतिपत्स्यामि दीनः शत्रुवशं गतः ॥५६॥
transliteration: kṣīṇaḥ sarvārthahīnaśca nirbandhurjñātivarjitaḥ ,kāṁ diśaṁ pratipatsyāmi dīnaḥ śatruvaśaṁ gataḥ.
english: Weakened, deprived of all resources, without kinsmen and abandoned by my relatives, to which direction shall I go, wretched and fallen under the power of my enemies?
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 5 Verse 56. Weakened, deprived of all resources, without kinsmen and abandoned by my relatives, to which direction shall I go, wretched and fallen under the power of my enemies?

sanskrit: दुर्योधनस्य वृद्ध्यर्थं पृथिवीं योऽजयत्प्रभुः ।स जितः पाण्डवैः शूरैः समर्थैर्वीर्यशालिभिः ॥५७॥
transliteration: duryodhanasya vṛddhyarthaṁ pṛthivīṁ yo'jayatprabhuḥ ,sa jitaḥ pāṇḍavaiḥ śūraiḥ samarthairvīryaśālibhiḥ.
english: The powerful lord who conquered the earth for the prosperity of Duryodhana, he was vanquished by the heroic, capable, and valorous Pāṇḍavas.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 5 Verse 57. The powerful lord who conquered the earth for the prosperity of Duryodhana, he was vanquished by the heroic, capable, and valorous Pāṇḍavas.

sanskrit: तस्मिन्हते महेष्वासे कर्णे युधि किरीटिना ।के वीराः पर्यवर्तन्त तन्ममाचक्ष्व संजय ॥५८॥
transliteration: tasminhate maheṣvāse karṇe yudhi kirīṭinā ,ke vīrāḥ paryavartanta tanmamācakṣva saṁjaya.
english: O Sañjaya, tell me, when that great archer Karṇa was slain in battle by the crowned one (Arjuna), which warriors then continued to fight?
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 5 Verse 58. O Sañjaya, tell me, when that great archer Karṇa was slain in battle by the crowned one (Arjuna), which warriors then continued to fight?

sanskrit: कच्चिन्नैकः परित्यक्तः पाण्डवैर्निहतो रणे ।उक्तं त्वया पुरा वीर यथा वीरा निपातिताः ॥५९॥
transliteration: kaccinnaikaḥ parityaktaḥ pāṇḍavairnihato raṇe ,uktaṁ tvayā purā vīra yathā vīrā nipātitāḥ.
english: O hero (Sañjaya), I hope that not a single one (of our warriors), having been killed in battle by the Pāṇḍavas, was abandoned. You previously described how the warriors were struck down.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 5 Verse 59. O hero (Sañjaya), I hope that not a single one (of our warriors), having been killed in battle by the Pāṇḍavas, was abandoned. You previously described how the warriors were struck down.

sanskrit: भीष्ममप्रतियुध्यन्तं शिखण्डी सायकोत्तमैः ।पातयामास समरे सर्वशस्त्रभृतां वरम् ॥६०॥
transliteration: bhīṣmamapratiyudhyantaṁ śikhaṇḍī sāyakottamaiḥ ,pātayāmāsa samare sarvaśastrabhṛtāṁ varam.
english: With excellent arrows, Śikhaṇḍī felled Bhīṣma, who was not fighting back, in battle; Bhīṣma, the foremost among all weapon-bearers.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 5 Verse 60. With excellent arrows, Śikhaṇḍī felled Bhīṣma, who was not fighting back, in battle; Bhīṣma, the foremost among all weapon-bearers.

sanskrit: तथा द्रौपदिना द्रोणो न्यस्तसर्वायुधो युधि ।युक्तयोगो महेष्वासः शरैर्बहुभिराचितः ।निहतः खड्गमुद्यम्य धृष्टद्युम्नेन संजय ॥६१॥
transliteration: tathā draupadinā droṇo nyastasarvāyudho yudhi ,yuktayogo maheṣvāsaḥ śarairbahubhirācitaḥ ,nihataḥ khaḍgamudyamya dhṛṣṭadyumnena saṁjaya.
english: O Sañjaya, similarly, Drona, the great archer, who had laid down all his weapons in battle and was absorbed in deep spiritual contemplation (yoga), and who was pierced by many arrows, was killed by Drupada's son; Dhṛṣṭadyumna killed him, raising his sword.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 5 Verse 61. O Sañjaya, similarly, Drona, the great archer, who had laid down all his weapons in battle and was absorbed in deep spiritual contemplation (yoga), and who was pierced by many arrows, was killed by Drupada's son; Dhṛṣṭadyumna killed him, raising his sword.

sanskrit: अन्तरेण हतावेतौ छलेन च विशेषतः ।अश्रौषमहमेतद्वै भीष्मद्रोणौ निपातितौ ॥६२॥
transliteration: antareṇa hatāvetau chalena ca viśeṣataḥ ,aśrauṣamahametadvai bhīṣmadroṇau nipātitau.
english: These two were killed unfairly, by deceit, and particularly so. I indeed heard this, that Bhishma and Drona were felled.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 5 Verse 62. These two were killed unfairly, by deceit, and particularly so. I indeed heard this, that Bhishma and Drona were felled.

sanskrit: भीष्मद्रोणौ हि समरे न हन्याद्वज्रभृत्स्वयम् ।न्यायेन युध्यमानौ हि तद्वै सत्यं ब्रवीमि ते ॥६३॥
transliteration: bhīṣmadroṇau hi samare na hanyādvajrabhṛtsvayam ,nyāyena yudhyamānau hi tadvai satyaṁ bravīmi te.
english: Indeed, even the thunderbolt-wielder (Indra) himself would not kill Bhishma and Drona in battle if they were fighting justly. That truth I indeed tell to you.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 5 Verse 63. Indeed, even the thunderbolt-wielder (Indra) himself would not kill Bhishma and Drona in battle if they were fighting justly. That truth I indeed tell to you.

sanskrit: कर्णं त्वस्यन्तमस्त्राणि दिव्यानि च बहूनि च ।कथमिन्द्रोपमं वीरं मृत्युर्युद्धे समस्पृशत् ॥६४॥
transliteration: karṇaṁ tvasyantamastrāṇi divyāni ca bahūni ca ,kathamindropamaṁ vīraṁ mṛtyuryuddhe samaspṛśat.
english: But how did Death (mṛtyu) touch Karṇa, that hero comparable to Indra, who was hurling many divine weapons and missiles in battle?
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 5 Verse 64. But how did Death (mṛtyu) touch Karṇa, that hero comparable to Indra, who was hurling many divine weapons and missiles in battle?

sanskrit: यस्य विद्युत्प्रभां शक्तिं दिव्यां कनकभूषणाम् ।प्रायच्छद्द्विषतां हन्त्रीं कुण्डलाभ्यां पुरंदरः ॥६५॥
transliteration: yasya vidyutprabhāṁ śaktiṁ divyāṁ kanakabhūṣaṇām ,prāyacchaddviṣatāṁ hantrīṁ kuṇḍalābhyāṁ puraṁdaraḥ.
english: To whom Indra (Purandara) gave a divine power (śakti) that possessed the radiance of lightning, was adorned with gold, and was a slayer of enemies, taking it in exchange for his two earrings.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 5 Verse 65. To whom Indra (Purandara) gave a divine power (śakti) that possessed the radiance of lightning, was adorned with gold, and was a slayer of enemies, taking it in exchange for his two earrings.

sanskrit: यस्य सर्पमुखो दिव्यः शरः कनकभूषणः ।अशेत निहतः पत्री चन्दनेष्वरिसूदनः ॥६६॥
transliteration: yasya sarpamukho divyaḥ śaraḥ kanakabhūṣaṇaḥ ,aśeta nihataḥ patrī candaneṣvarisūdanaḥ.
english: Whose divine, serpent-mouthed arrow, adorned with gold, feathered, and a destroyer of enemies, lay struck down among the sandalwood trees.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 5 Verse 66. Whose divine, serpent-mouthed arrow, adorned with gold, feathered, and a destroyer of enemies, lay struck down among the sandalwood trees.

sanskrit: भीष्मद्रोणमुखान्वीरान्योऽवमन्य महारथान् ।जामदग्न्यान्महाघोरं ब्राह्ममस्त्रमशिक्षत ॥६७॥
transliteration: bhīṣmadroṇamukhānvīrānyo'vamanya mahārathān ,jāmadagnyānmahāghoraṁ brāhmamastramaśikṣata.
english: Who, having disregarded great charioteers and heroes such as Bhishma and Drona, learned the exceedingly terrible divine weapon, the brahma-astra (brāhmam astra), from the son of Jamadagni (Paraśurāma).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 5 Verse 67. Who, having disregarded great charioteers and heroes such as Bhishma and Drona, learned the exceedingly terrible divine weapon, the brahma-astra (brāhmam astra), from the son of Jamadagni (Paraśurāma).

sanskrit: यश्च द्रोणमुखान्दृष्ट्वा विमुखानर्दिताञ्शरैः ।सौभद्रस्य महाबाहुर्व्यधमत्कार्मुकं शरैः ॥६८॥
transliteration: yaśca droṇamukhāndṛṣṭvā vimukhānarditāñśaraiḥ ,saubhadrasya mahābāhurvyadhamatkārmukaṁ śaraiḥ.
english: And who, the mighty-armed one, having seen warriors headed by Drona retreating and wounded by (Abhimanyu's) arrows, then shattered the bow of Saubhadra (Abhimanyu) with his own arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 5 Verse 68. And who, the mighty-armed one, having seen warriors headed by Drona retreating and wounded by (Abhimanyu's) arrows, then shattered the bow of Saubhadra (Abhimanyu) with his own arrows.

sanskrit: यश्च नागायुतप्राणं वातरंहसमच्युतम् ।विरथं भ्रातरं कृत्वा भीमसेनमुपाहसत् ॥६९॥
transliteration: yaśca nāgāyutaprāṇaṁ vātaraṁhasamacyutam ,virathaṁ bhrātaraṁ kṛtvā bhīmasenamupāhasat.
english: And he who, having made his brother Bhīmasena  -  who possessed the strength of ten thousand elephants, the speed of the wind, and was unwavering  -  dismounted from his chariot, then ridiculed him.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 5 Verse 69. And he who, having made his brother Bhīmasena  -  who possessed the strength of ten thousand elephants, the speed of the wind, and was unwavering  -  dismounted from his chariot, then ridiculed him.

sanskrit: सहदेवं च निर्जित्य शरैः संनतपर्वभिः ।कृपया विरथं कृत्वा नाहनद्धर्मवित्तया ॥७०॥
transliteration: sahadevaṁ ca nirjitya śaraiḥ saṁnataparvabhiḥ ,kṛpayā virathaṁ kṛtvā nāhanaddharmavittayā.
english: And having defeated Sahadeva with straight-shafted arrows, he did not slay him, but out of compassion and due to his understanding of (dharma), he merely caused him to be dismounted from his chariot.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 5 Verse 70. And having defeated Sahadeva with straight-shafted arrows, he did not slay him, but out of compassion and due to his understanding of (dharma), he merely caused him to be dismounted from his chariot.

sanskrit: यश्च मायासहस्राणि ध्वंसयित्वा रणोत्कटम् ।घटोत्कचं राक्षसेन्द्रं शक्रशक्त्याभिजघ्निवान् ॥७१॥
transliteration: yaśca māyāsahasrāṇi dhvaṁsayitvā raṇotkaṭam ,ghaṭotkacaṁ rākṣasendraṁ śakraśaktyābhijaghnivān.
english: And he who, after destroying thousands of magical illusions (māyā), killed Ghatotkaca, the fierce chief of the rākṣasas, with Indra's (śakti) weapon.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 5 Verse 71. And he who, after destroying thousands of magical illusions (māyā), killed Ghatotkaca, the fierce chief of the rākṣasas, with Indra's (śakti) weapon.

sanskrit: एतानि दिवसान्यस्य युद्धे भीतो धनंजयः ।नागमद्द्वैरथं वीरः स कथं निहतो रणे ॥७२॥
transliteration: etāni divasānyasya yuddhe bhīto dhanaṁjayaḥ ,nāgamaddvairathaṁ vīraḥ sa kathaṁ nihato raṇe.
english: For all these days, the hero Dhananjaya (Arjuna), being afraid of him in battle, did not engage in single chariot combat. How then could such a one (Karṇa) have been killed in battle?
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 5 Verse 72. For all these days, the hero Dhananjaya (Arjuna), being afraid of him in battle, did not engage in single chariot combat. How then could such a one (Karṇa) have been killed in battle?

sanskrit: रथसङ्गो न चेत्तस्य धनुर्वा न व्यशीर्यत ।न चेदस्त्राणि निर्णेशुः स कथं निहतः परैः ॥७३॥
transliteration: rathasaṅgo na cettasya dhanurvā na vyaśīryata ,na cedastrāṇi nirṇeśuḥ sa kathaṁ nihataḥ paraiḥ.
english: If his chariot had not been stuck, or his bow had not been shattered, and if his weapons had not been destroyed, then how could he have been killed by his enemies?
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 5 Verse 73. If his chariot had not been stuck, or his bow had not been shattered, and if his weapons had not been destroyed, then how could he have been killed by his enemies?

sanskrit: को हि शक्तो रणे कर्णं विधुन्वानं महद्धनुः ।विमुञ्चन्तं शरान्घोरान्दिव्यान्यस्त्राणि चाहवे ।जेतुं पुरुषशार्दूलं शार्दूलमिव वेगितम् ॥७४॥
transliteration: ko hi śakto raṇe karṇaṁ vidhunvānaṁ mahaddhanuḥ ,vimuñcantaṁ śarānghorāndivyānyastrāṇi cāhave ,jetuṁ puruṣaśārdūlaṁ śārdūlamiva vegitam.
english: Who indeed could be capable of defeating Karṇa in battle, as he brandishes his mighty bow, releasing terrible arrows and divine weapons in combat? Who could conquer that best of persons (puruṣa), who is like an impetuous tiger?
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 5 Verse 74. Who indeed could be capable of defeating Karṇa in battle, as he brandishes his mighty bow, releasing terrible arrows and divine weapons in combat? Who could conquer that best of persons (puruṣa), who is like an impetuous tiger?

sanskrit: ध्रुवं तस्य धनुश्छिन्नं रथो वापि गतो महीम् ।अस्त्राणि वा प्रनष्टानि यथा शंससि मे हतम् ।न ह्यन्यदनुपश्यामि कारणं तस्य नाशने ॥७५॥
