transliteration: dhruvaṁ tasya dhanuśchinnaṁ ratho vāpi gato mahīm ,astrāṇi vā pranaṣṭāni yathā śaṁsasi me hatam ,na hyanyadanupaśyāmi kāraṇaṁ tasya nāśane.
english: Surely, his bow must have been broken, or his chariot must have been overthrown, or his weapons must have been lost, since you report his death to me. Indeed, I perceive no other reason for his destruction.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 5 Verse 75. Surely, his bow must have been broken, or his chariot must have been overthrown, or his weapons must have been lost, since you report his death to me. Indeed, I perceive no other reason for his destruction.

sanskrit: न हन्यामर्जुनं यावत्तावत्पादौ न धावये ।इति यस्य महाघोरं व्रतमासीन्महात्मनः ॥७६॥
transliteration: na hanyāmarjunaṁ yāvattāvatpādau na dhāvaye ,iti yasya mahāghoraṁ vratamāsīnmahātmanaḥ.
english: "As long as I have not killed Arjuna, I shall not wash my feet." Such was the extremely dreadful vow of that great-souled one (ātman).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 5 Verse 76. "As long as I have not killed Arjuna, I shall not wash my feet." Such was the extremely dreadful vow of that great-souled one (ātman).

sanskrit: यस्य भीतो वने नित्यं धर्मराजो युधिष्ठिरः ।त्रयोदश समा निद्रां न लेभे पुरुषर्षभः ॥७७॥
transliteration: yasya bhīto vane nityaṁ dharmarājo yudhiṣṭhiraḥ ,trayodaśa samā nidrāṁ na lebhe puruṣarṣabhaḥ.
english: Yudhishthira, the king of righteousness (dharma), perpetually feared that best among men (puruṣarṣabhaḥ) (Duryodhana) while in the forest, and thus did not obtain sleep for thirteen years.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 5 Verse 77. Yudhishthira, the king of righteousness (dharma), perpetually feared that best among men (puruṣarṣabhaḥ) (Duryodhana) while in the forest, and thus did not obtain sleep for thirteen years.

sanskrit: यस्य वीर्यवतो वीर्यं समाश्रित्य महात्मनः ।मम पुत्रः सभां भार्यां पाण्डूनां नीतवान्बलात् ॥७८॥
transliteration: yasya vīryavato vīryaṁ samāśritya mahātmanaḥ ,mama putraḥ sabhāṁ bhāryāṁ pāṇḍūnāṁ nītavānbalāt.
english: My son, relying on the valor of that powerful, great-souled one (mahātman) (Duryodhana), forcibly brought the wife of the Pandavas into the assembly.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 5 Verse 78. My son, relying on the valor of that powerful, great-souled one (mahātman) (Duryodhana), forcibly brought the wife of the Pandavas into the assembly.

sanskrit: तत्र चापि सभामध्ये पाण्डवानां च पश्यताम् ।दासभार्येति पाञ्चालीमब्रवीत्कुरुसंसदि ॥७९॥
transliteration: tatra cāpi sabhāmadhye pāṇḍavānāṁ ca paśyatām ,dāsabhāryeti pāñcālīmabravītkurusaṁsadi.
english: And there, in the midst of that assembly, within the Kuru assembly, and while the Pandavas were watching, he addressed Panchali as 'slave-wife'.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 5 Verse 79. And there, in the midst of that assembly, within the Kuru assembly, and while the Pandavas were watching, he addressed Panchali as 'slave-wife'.

sanskrit: यश्च गाण्डीवमुक्तानां स्पर्शमुग्रमचिन्तयन् ।अपतिर्ह्यसि कृष्णेति ब्रुवन्पार्थानवैक्षत ॥८०॥
transliteration: yaśca gāṇḍīvamuktānāṁ sparśamugramacintayan ,apatirhyasi kṛṣṇeti bruvanpārthānavaikṣata.
english: And he (Duryodhana), not considering the fierce impact of arrows released from the Gaṇḍīva bow, looked at the sons of Pritha (Pandavas), saying, 'Indeed, O Krishna (Draupadi), you are without a husband!'
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 5 Verse 80. And he (Duryodhana), not considering the fierce impact of arrows released from the Gaṇḍīva bow, looked at the sons of Pritha (Pandavas), saying, 'Indeed, O Krishna (Draupadi), you are without a husband!'

sanskrit: यस्य नासीद्भयं पार्थैः सपुत्रैः सजनार्दनैः ।स्वबाहुबलमाश्रित्य मुहूर्तमपि संजय ॥८१॥
transliteration: yasya nāsīdbhayaṁ pārthaiḥ saputraiḥ sajanārdanaiḥ ,svabāhubalamāśritya muhūrtamapi saṁjaya.
english: O Sañjaya, he (Karṇa) who, relying on the strength of his own arms, never experienced fear, not even for a moment, from the Pāṇḍavas, their sons, and Janārdana (Kṛṣṇa).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 5 Verse 81. O Sañjaya, he (Karṇa) who, relying on the strength of his own arms, never experienced fear, not even for a moment, from the Pāṇḍavas, their sons, and Janārdana (Kṛṣṇa).

sanskrit: तस्य नाहं वधं मन्ये देवैरपि सवासवैः ।प्रतीपमुपधावद्भिः किं पुनस्तात पाण्डवैः ॥८२॥
transliteration: tasya nāhaṁ vadhaṁ manye devairapi savāsavaiḥ ,pratīpamupadhāvadbhiḥ kiṁ punastāta pāṇḍavaiḥ.
english: I (Dhṛtarāṣṭra) do not believe that he (Karṇa) can be killed even by the gods, accompanied by Indra, if they were to rush hostilely against him. How much less likely is it then, O dear one (Sañjaya), that he could be killed by the Pāṇḍavas?
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 5 Verse 82. I (Dhṛtarāṣṭra) do not believe that he (Karṇa) can be killed even by the gods, accompanied by Indra, if they were to rush hostilely against him. How much less likely is it then, O dear one (Sañjaya), that he could be killed by the Pāṇḍavas?

sanskrit: न हि ज्यां स्पृशमानस्य तलत्रे चापि गृह्णतः ।पुमानाधिरथेः कश्चित्प्रमुखे स्थातुमर्हति ॥८३॥
transliteration: na hi jyāṁ spṛśamānasya talatre cāpi gṛhṇataḥ ,pumānādhiratheḥ kaścitpramukhe sthātumarhati.
english: Indeed, no man is able to stand in the presence of Adhiratha's son (Karṇa) while he is touching the bowstring and donning his arm-guards.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 5 Verse 83. Indeed, no man is able to stand in the presence of Adhiratha's son (Karṇa) while he is touching the bowstring and donning his arm-guards.

sanskrit: अपि स्यान्मेदिनी हीना सोमसूर्यप्रभांशुभिः ।न वधः पुरुषेन्द्रस्य समरेष्वपलायिनः ॥८४॥
transliteration: api syānmedinī hīnā somasūryaprabhāṁśubhiḥ ,na vadhaḥ puruṣendrasya samareṣvapalāyinaḥ.
english: Even if the earth were to lose the radiant beams of the moon and sun, still there would be no slaying for that lord of men (Karṇa) who never flees in battles.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 5 Verse 84. Even if the earth were to lose the radiant beams of the moon and sun, still there would be no slaying for that lord of men (Karṇa) who never flees in battles.

sanskrit: यदि मन्दः सहायेन भ्रात्रा दुःशासनेन च ।वासुदेवस्य दुर्बुद्धिः प्रत्याख्यानमरोचयत् ॥८५॥
transliteration: yadi mandaḥ sahāyena bhrātrā duḥśāsanena ca ,vāsudevasya durbuddhiḥ pratyākhyānamarocayat.
english: If the foolish and wicked-minded Duryodhana, aided by his brother Duśāsana, chose to reject the proposal of Vāsudeva (Kṛṣṇa).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 5 Verse 85. If the foolish and wicked-minded Duryodhana, aided by his brother Duśāsana, chose to reject the proposal of Vāsudeva (Kṛṣṇa).

sanskrit: स नूनमृषभस्कन्धं दृष्ट्वा कर्णं निपातितम् ।दुःशासनं च निहतं मन्ये शोचति पुत्रकः ॥८६॥
transliteration: sa nūnamṛṣabhaskandhaṁ dṛṣṭvā karṇaṁ nipātitam ,duḥśāsanaṁ ca nihataṁ manye śocati putrakaḥ.
english: I believe that poor son (Duryodhana) must certainly be grieving now, having seen Karṇa, who had bull-like shoulders, struck down, and Duśāsana killed.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 5 Verse 86. I believe that poor son (Duryodhana) must certainly be grieving now, having seen Karṇa, who had bull-like shoulders, struck down, and Duśāsana killed.

sanskrit: हतं वैकर्तनं श्रुत्वा द्वैरथे सव्यसाचिना ।जयतः पाण्डवान्दृष्ट्वा किं स्विद्दुर्योधनोऽब्रवीत् ॥८७॥
transliteration: hataṁ vaikartanaṁ śrutvā dvairathe savyasācinā ,jayataḥ pāṇḍavāndṛṣṭvā kiṁ svidduryodhano'bravīt.
english: What did Duryodhana then say, after hearing that Vaikartana (Karṇa) had been slain by Savyaśācin (Arjuna) in a duel, and seeing the Pāṇḍavas victorious?
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 5 Verse 87. What did Duryodhana then say, after hearing that Vaikartana (Karṇa) had been slain by Savyaśācin (Arjuna) in a duel, and seeing the Pāṇḍavas victorious?

sanskrit: दुर्मर्षणं हतं श्रुत्वा वृषसेनं च संयुगे ।प्रभग्नं च बलं दृष्ट्वा वध्यमानं महारथैः ॥८८॥
transliteration: durmarṣaṇaṁ hataṁ śrutvā vṛṣasenaṁ ca saṁyuge ,prabhagnaṁ ca balaṁ dṛṣṭvā vadhyamānaṁ mahārathaiḥ.
english: (Having heard) that Durmarṣaṇa and Vṛṣasena were slain in battle, and (having seen) the army routed and being slaughtered by the great warriors.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 5 Verse 88. (Having heard) that Durmarṣaṇa and Vṛṣasena were slain in battle, and (having seen) the army routed and being slaughtered by the great warriors.

sanskrit: पराङ्मुखांस्तथा राज्ञः पलायनपरायणान् ।विद्रुतान्रथिनो दृष्ट्वा मन्ये शोचति पुत्रकः ॥८९॥
transliteration: parāṅmukhāṁstathā rājñaḥ palāyanaparāyaṇān ,vidrutānrathino dṛṣṭvā manye śocati putrakaḥ.
english: I believe your son (Duryodhana) is now grieving, having seen the kings thus turned away and intent on fleeing, and also the routed charioteers.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 5 Verse 89. I believe your son (Duryodhana) is now grieving, having seen the kings thus turned away and intent on fleeing, and also the routed charioteers.

sanskrit: अनेयश्चाभिमानेन बालबुद्धिरमर्षणः ।हतोत्साहं बलं दृष्ट्वा किं स्विद्दुर्योधनोऽब्रवीत् ॥९०॥
transliteration: aneyaścābhimānena bālabuddhiramarṣaṇaḥ ,hatotsāhaṁ balaṁ dṛṣṭvā kiṁ svidduryodhano'bravīt.
english: What indeed did Duryodhana say – that stubborn, childish-minded, and resentful man, driven by pride – after seeing his army lose its enthusiasm?
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 5 Verse 90. What indeed did Duryodhana say – that stubborn, childish-minded, and resentful man, driven by pride – after seeing his army lose its enthusiasm?

sanskrit: भ्रातरं निहतं दृष्ट्वा भीमसेनेन संयुगे ।रुधिरं पीयमानेन किं स्विद्दुर्योधनोऽब्रवीत् ॥९१॥
transliteration: bhrātaraṁ nihataṁ dṛṣṭvā bhīmasenena saṁyuge ,rudhiraṁ pīyamānena kiṁ svidduryodhano'bravīt.
english: What indeed did Duryodhana say after seeing his brother killed in battle by Bhimasena, who was (then) drinking his blood?
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 5 Verse 91. What indeed did Duryodhana say after seeing his brother killed in battle by Bhimasena, who was (then) drinking his blood?

sanskrit: सह गान्धारराजेन सभायां यदभाषत ।कर्णोऽर्जुनं रणे हन्ता हते तस्मिन्किमब्रवीत् ॥९२॥
transliteration: saha gāndhārarājena sabhāyāṁ yadabhāṣata ,karṇo'rjunaṁ raṇe hantā hate tasminkimabravīt.
english: What did (Duryodhana) say when Karna, who had declared in the assembly along with the king of Gandhara (Shakuni) that he would slay Arjuna in battle, was himself slain?
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 5 Verse 92. What did (Duryodhana) say when Karna, who had declared in the assembly along with the king of Gandhara (Shakuni) that he would slay Arjuna in battle, was himself slain?

sanskrit: द्यूतं कृत्वा पुरा हृष्टो वञ्चयित्वा च पाण्डवान् ।शकुनिः सौबलस्तात हते कर्णे किमब्रवीत् ॥९३॥
transliteration: dyūtaṁ kṛtvā purā hṛṣṭo vañcayitvā ca pāṇḍavān ,śakuniḥ saubalastāta hate karṇe kimabravīt.
english: O dear one (tāta), what did Shakuni, son of Subala, say after Karna was killed, Shakuni who was previously delighted after having deceived the Pandavas through gambling?
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 5 Verse 93. O dear one (tāta), what did Shakuni, son of Subala, say after Karna was killed, Shakuni who was previously delighted after having deceived the Pandavas through gambling?

sanskrit: कृतवर्मा महेष्वासः सात्वतानां महारथः ।कर्णं विनिहतं दृष्ट्वा हार्दिक्यः किमभाषत ॥९४॥
transliteration: kṛtavarmā maheṣvāsaḥ sātvatānāṁ mahārathaḥ ,karṇaṁ vinihataṁ dṛṣṭvā hārdikyaḥ kimabhāṣata.
english: What did Kritavarma, the great archer and a great warrior (mahāratha) among the Satvatas, also known as Hardikya, say upon seeing Karna slain?
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 5 Verse 94. What did Kritavarma, the great archer and a great warrior (mahāratha) among the Satvatas, also known as Hardikya, say upon seeing Karna slain?

sanskrit: ब्राह्मणाः क्षत्रिया वैश्या यस्य शिक्षामुपासते ।धनुर्वेदं चिकीर्षन्तो द्रोणपुत्रस्य धीमतः ॥९५॥
transliteration: brāhmaṇāḥ kṣatriyā vaiśyā yasya śikṣāmupāsate ,dhanurvedaṁ cikīrṣanto droṇaputrasya dhīmataḥ.
english: Brahmins, Kshatriyas, and Vaishyas, desiring to master the science of archery, attend the instruction of the intelligent son of Drona (Aśvatthāmā).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 5 Verse 95. Brahmins, Kshatriyas, and Vaishyas, desiring to master the science of archery, attend the instruction of the intelligent son of Drona (Aśvatthāmā).

sanskrit: युवा रूपेण संपन्नो दर्शनीयो महायशाः ।अश्वत्थामा हते कर्णे किमभाषत संजय ॥९६॥
transliteration: yuvā rūpeṇa saṁpanno darśanīyo mahāyaśāḥ ,aśvatthāmā hate karṇe kimabhāṣata saṁjaya.
english: O Sañjaya, what did Aśvatthāmā, who is youthful, endowed with a beautiful form, and greatly renowned, say when Karna was slain?
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 5 Verse 96. O Sañjaya, what did Aśvatthāmā, who is youthful, endowed with a beautiful form, and greatly renowned, say when Karna was slain?

sanskrit: आचार्यत्वं धनुर्वेदे गतः परमतत्त्ववित् ।कृपः शारद्वतस्तात हते कर्णे किमब्रवीत् ॥९७॥
transliteration: ācāryatvaṁ dhanurvede gataḥ paramatattvavit ,kṛpaḥ śāradvatastāta hate karṇe kimabravīt.
english: O father, what did Kṛpa, the son of Śaradvan, who had attained mastery in archery and was a knower of the highest truth, say when Karṇa was killed?
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 5 Verse 97. O father, what did Kṛpa, the son of Śaradvan, who had attained mastery in archery and was a knower of the highest truth, say when Karṇa was killed?

sanskrit: मद्रराजो महेष्वासः शल्यः समितिशोभनः ।दिष्टं तेन हि तत्सर्वं यथा कर्णो निपातितः ॥९८॥
transliteration: madrarājo maheṣvāsaḥ śalyaḥ samitiśobhanaḥ ,diṣṭaṁ tena hi tatsarvaṁ yathā karṇo nipātitaḥ.
english: The King of Madras, Śalya, a great archer and an ornament of the assembly – indeed, everything, just as Karṇa was struck down, was foretold by him.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 5 Verse 98. The King of Madras, Śalya, a great archer and an ornament of the assembly – indeed, everything, just as Karṇa was struck down, was foretold by him.

sanskrit: ये च केचन राजानः पृथिव्यां योद्धुमागताः ।वैकर्तनं हतं दृष्ट्वा किमभाषन्त संजय ॥९९॥
transliteration: ye ca kecana rājānaḥ pṛthivyāṁ yoddhumāgatāḥ ,vaikartanaṁ hataṁ dṛṣṭvā kimabhāṣanta saṁjaya.
english: And what did all those kings who had come to fight on earth say, upon seeing Vaikartana (Karṇa) slain, O Saṃjaya?
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 5 Verse 99. And what did all those kings who had come to fight on earth say, upon seeing Vaikartana (Karṇa) slain, O Saṃjaya?

sanskrit: कर्णे तु निहते वीरे रथव्याघ्रे नरर्षभे ।किं वो मुखमनीकानामासीत्संजय भागशः ॥१००॥
transliteration: karṇe tu nihate vīre rathavyāghre nararṣabhe ,kiṁ vo mukhamanīkānāmāsītsaṁjaya bhāgaśaḥ.
english: But when the heroic Karṇa, that tiger among charioteers and bull among men, was slain, O Saṃjaya, what was the countenance of your armies, division by division?
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 5 Verse 100. But when the heroic Karṇa, that tiger among charioteers and bull among men, was slain, O Saṃjaya, what was the countenance of your armies, division by division?

sanskrit: मद्रराजः कथं शल्यो नियुक्तो रथिनां वरः ।वैकर्तनस्य सारथ्ये तन्ममाचक्ष्व संजय ॥१०१॥
transliteration: madrarājaḥ kathaṁ śalyo niyukto rathināṁ varaḥ ,vaikartanasya sārathye tanmamācakṣva saṁjaya.
english: O Sañjaya, explain this to me: How was Shalya, the king of Madra and the foremost among charioteers, appointed to act as the charioteer for Vaikartana (Karna)?
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 5 Verse 101. O Sañjaya, explain this to me: How was Shalya, the king of Madra and the foremost among charioteers, appointed to act as the charioteer for Vaikartana (Karna)?

sanskrit: केऽरक्षन्दक्षिणं चक्रं सूतपुत्रस्य संयुगे ।वामं चक्रं ररक्षुर्वा के वा वीरस्य पृष्ठतः ॥१०२॥
transliteration: ke'rakṣandakṣiṇaṁ cakraṁ sūtaputrasya saṁyuge ,vāmaṁ cakraṁ rarakṣurvā ke vā vīrasya pṛṣṭhataḥ.
english: Who protected the right wheel of the son of Sūta (Karna) in battle? And who protected his left wheel? Or who stood behind the hero (Karna)?
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 5 Verse 102. Who protected the right wheel of the son of Sūta (Karna) in battle? And who protected his left wheel? Or who stood behind the hero (Karna)?

sanskrit: के कर्णं वाजहुः शूराः के क्षुद्राः प्राद्रवन्भयात् ।कथं च वः समेतानां हतः कर्णो महारथः ॥१०३॥
transliteration: ke karṇaṁ vājahuḥ śūrāḥ ke kṣudrāḥ prādravanbhayāt ,kathaṁ ca vaḥ sametānāṁ hataḥ karṇo mahārathaḥ.
english: Which brave warriors stood by Karna? Which cowardly ones fled in fear? And how was Karna, the great charioteer, killed while all of you were assembled?
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 5 Verse 103. Which brave warriors stood by Karna? Which cowardly ones fled in fear? And how was Karna, the great charioteer, killed while all of you were assembled?

sanskrit: पाण्डवाश्च कथं शूराः प्रत्युदीयुर्महारथम् ।सृजन्तं शरवर्षाणि वारिधारा इवाम्बुदम् ॥१०४॥
transliteration: pāṇḍavāśca kathaṁ śūrāḥ pratyudīyurmahāratham ,sṛjantaṁ śaravarṣāṇi vāridhārā ivāmbudam.
english: And how did the brave Pāṇḍavas advance to confront the great charioteer (Karna), who was showering torrents of arrows just as a cloud pours down streams of rain?
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 5 Verse 104. And how did the brave Pāṇḍavas advance to confront the great charioteer (Karna), who was showering torrents of arrows just as a cloud pours down streams of rain?

sanskrit: स च सर्पमुखो दिव्यो महेषुप्रवरस्तदा ।व्यर्थः कथं समभवत्तन्ममाचक्ष्व संजय ॥१०५॥
transliteration: sa ca sarpamukho divyo maheṣupravarastadā ,vyarthaḥ kathaṁ samabhavattanmamācakṣva saṁjaya.
english: Sañjaya, tell me how that divine, serpent-mouthed, chief of great arrows became futile at that time.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 5 Verse 105. Sañjaya, tell me how that divine, serpent-mouthed, chief of great arrows became futile at that time.

sanskrit: मामकस्यास्य सैन्यस्य हृतोत्सेधस्य संजय ।अवशेषं न पश्यामि ककुदे मृदिते सति ॥१०६॥
transliteration: māmakasyāsya sainyasya hṛtotsedhasya saṁjaya ,avaśeṣaṁ na paśyāmi kakude mṛdite sati.
english: Sañjaya, when the chief part has been crushed, I see no remnant of this army of mine, whose elevation and pride have been destroyed.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 5 Verse 106. Sañjaya, when the chief part has been crushed, I see no remnant of this army of mine, whose elevation and pride have been destroyed.

sanskrit: तौ हि वीरौ महेष्वासौ मदर्थे कुरुसत्तमौ ।भीष्मद्रोणौ हतौ श्रुत्वा को न्वर्थो जीवितेन मे ॥१०७॥
transliteration: tau hi vīrau maheṣvāsau madarthe kurusattamau ,bhīṣmadroṇau hatau śrutvā ko nvartho jīvitena me.
english: Indeed, after hearing that those two heroes, the great archers and best among the Kurus - Bhīṣma and Droṇa - have been slain for my sake, what purpose does life hold for me now?
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 5 Verse 107. Indeed, after hearing that those two heroes, the great archers and best among the Kurus - Bhīṣma and Droṇa - have been slain for my sake, what purpose does life hold for me now?

sanskrit: न मृष्यामि च राधेयं हतमाहवशोभिनम् ।यस्य बाह्वोर्बलं तुल्यं कुञ्जराणां शतं शतम् ॥१०८॥
transliteration: na mṛṣyāmi ca rādheyaṁ hatamāhavaśobhinam ,yasya bāhvorbalaṁ tulyaṁ kuñjarāṇāṁ śataṁ śatam.
english: And I cannot bear that Rādhya (Karṇa), who shone brilliantly in battle, has been killed. His arms possessed strength equal to hundreds upon hundreds of elephants.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 5 Verse 108. And I cannot bear that Rādhya (Karṇa), who shone brilliantly in battle, has been killed. His arms possessed strength equal to hundreds upon hundreds of elephants.

sanskrit: द्रोणे हते च यद्वृत्तं कौरवाणां परैः सह ।संग्रामे नरवीराणां तन्ममाचक्ष्व संजय ॥१०९॥
transliteration: droṇe hate ca yadvṛttaṁ kauravāṇāṁ paraiḥ saha ,saṁgrāme naravīrāṇāṁ tanmamācakṣva saṁjaya.
english: O Sañjaya, recount to me what transpired for the Kauravas and their heroic warriors in battle against their enemies after Droṇa was slain.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 5 Verse 109. O Sañjaya, recount to me what transpired for the Kauravas and their heroic warriors in battle against their enemies after Droṇa was slain.

sanskrit: यथा च कर्णः कौन्तेयैः सह युद्धमयोजयत् ।यथा च द्विषतां हन्ता रणे शान्तस्तदुच्यताम् ॥११०॥
transliteration: yathā ca karṇaḥ kaunteyaiḥ saha yuddhamayojayat ,yathā ca dviṣatāṁ hantā raṇe śāntastaducyatām.
english: And relate how Karṇa waged war with the sons of Kuntī, and how the slayer of foes became still (śāntaḥ) in that conflict; that should be told.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 5 Verse 110. And relate how Karṇa waged war with the sons of Kuntī, and how the slayer of foes became still (śāntaḥ) in that conflict; that should be told.

sanskrit: संजय उवाच ।हते द्रोणे महेष्वासे तस्मिन्नहनि भारत ।कृते च मोघसंकल्पे द्रोणपुत्रे महारथे ॥१॥
transliteration: saṁjaya uvāca ,hate droṇe maheṣvāse tasminnahani bhārata ,kṛte ca moghasaṁkalpe droṇaputre mahārathe.
english: Sañjaya said: 'O descendant of Bharata, on that very day, when the great archer Droṇa was slain, and the great warrior, Droṇa's son (Aśvatthāman), had his determination rendered fruitless...'
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 6 Verse 1. Sañjaya said: 'O descendant of Bharata, on that very day, when the great archer Droṇa was slain, and the great warrior, Droṇa's son (Aśvatthāman), had his determination rendered fruitless...'

sanskrit: द्रवमाणे महाराज कौरवाणां बले तथा ।व्यूह्य पार्थः स्वकं सैन्यमतिष्ठद्भ्रातृभिः सह ॥२॥
transliteration: dravamāṇe mahārāja kauravāṇāṁ bale tathā ,vyūhya pārthaḥ svakaṁ sainyamatiṣṭhadbhrātṛbhiḥ saha.
english: O great king, while the Kaurava army was fleeing, Pārtha (Arjuna), after forming his own battle array, stood firm with his brothers.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 6 Verse 2. O great king, while the Kaurava army was fleeing, Pārtha (Arjuna), after forming his own battle array, stood firm with his brothers.

sanskrit: तमवस्थितमाज्ञाय पुत्रस्ते भरतर्षभ ।द्रवच्च स्वबलं दृष्ट्वा पौरुषेण न्यवारयत् ॥३॥
transliteration: tamavasthitamājñāya putraste bharatarṣabha ,dravacca svabalaṁ dṛṣṭvā pauruṣeṇa nyavārayat.
english: O best of Bharatas, realizing that he (Abhimanyu) stood firm, and seeing his own army fleeing, your son checked its retreat with his prowess.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 6 Verse 3. O best of Bharatas, realizing that he (Abhimanyu) stood firm, and seeing his own army fleeing, your son checked its retreat with his prowess.

sanskrit: स्वमनीकमवस्थाप्य बाहुवीर्ये व्यवस्थितः ।युद्ध्वा च सुचिरं कालं पाण्डवैः सह भारत ॥४॥
transliteration: svamanīkamavasthāpya bāhuvīrye vyavasthitaḥ ,yuddhvā ca suciraṁ kālaṁ pāṇḍavaiḥ saha bhārata.
english: O Bhārata, having arrayed his own army and stood firm through the prowess of his arms, he fought with the Pāṇḍavas for a very long time.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 6 Verse 4. O Bhārata, having arrayed his own army and stood firm through the prowess of his arms, he fought with the Pāṇḍavas for a very long time.

sanskrit: लब्धलक्षैः परैर्हृष्टैर्व्यायच्छद्भिश्चिरं तदा ।संध्याकालं समासाद्य प्रत्याहारमकारयत् ॥५॥
transliteration: labdhalakṣaiḥ parairhṛṣṭairvyāyacchadbhiściraṁ tadā ,saṁdhyākālaṁ samāsādya pratyāhāramakārayat.
english: Then, facing enemies who had achieved their objective, were jubilant, and had been fighting hard for a long time, he ordered a retreat upon reaching the time of dusk.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 6 Verse 5. Then, facing enemies who had achieved their objective, were jubilant, and had been fighting hard for a long time, he ordered a retreat upon reaching the time of dusk.

sanskrit: कृत्वावहारं सैन्यानां प्रविश्य शिबिरं स्वकम् ।कुरवोऽऽत्महितं मन्त्रं मन्त्रयां चक्रिरे तदा ॥६॥
transliteration: kṛtvāvahāraṁ sainyānāṁ praviśya śibiraṁ svakam ,kuravo''tmahitaṁ mantraṁ mantrayāṁ cakrire tadā.
english: Then, after withdrawing their armies and entering their own camp, the Kurus deliberated on a plan for their own welfare.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 6 Verse 6. Then, after withdrawing their armies and entering their own camp, the Kurus deliberated on a plan for their own welfare.

sanskrit: पर्यङ्केषु परार्ध्येषु स्पर्ध्यास्तरणवत्सु च ।वरासनेषूपविष्टाः सुखशय्यास्विवामराः ॥७॥
transliteration: paryaṅkeṣu parārdhyeṣu spardhyāstaraṇavatsu ca ,varāsaneṣūpaviṣṭāḥ sukhaśayyāsvivāmarāḥ.
english: They were seated on magnificent couches adorned with enviable coverings, and on excellent seats, like immortals resting on comfortable beds.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 6 Verse 7. They were seated on magnificent couches adorned with enviable coverings, and on excellent seats, like immortals resting on comfortable beds.

sanskrit: ततो दुर्योधनो राजा साम्ना परमवल्गुना ।तानाभाष्य महेष्वासान्प्राप्तकालमभाषत ॥८॥
transliteration: tato duryodhano rājā sāmnā paramavalgunā ,tānābhāṣya maheṣvāsānprāptakālamabhāṣata.
english: Then King Duryodhana, having addressed those great archers with exceedingly gentle and pleasing words of conciliation, spoke appropriately for the occasion.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 6 Verse 8. Then King Duryodhana, having addressed those great archers with exceedingly gentle and pleasing words of conciliation, spoke appropriately for the occasion.

sanskrit: मतिं मतिमतां श्रेष्ठाः सर्वे प्रब्रूत माचिरम् ।एवं गते तु यत्कार्यं भवेत्कार्यकरं नृपाः ॥९॥
transliteration: matiṁ matimatāṁ śreṣṭhāḥ sarve prabrūta māciram ,evaṁ gate tu yatkāryaṁ bhavetkāryakaraṁ nṛpāḥ.
english: O kings, O best among the wise, all of you, declare your counsel without delay. For when things have come to such a state, whatever action might be effective should be undertaken.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 6 Verse 9. O kings, O best among the wise, all of you, declare your counsel without delay. For when things have come to such a state, whatever action might be effective should be undertaken.

sanskrit: एवमुक्ते नरेन्द्रेण नरसिंहा युयुत्सवः ।चक्रुर्नानाविधाश्चेष्टाः सिंहासनगतास्तदा ॥१०॥
transliteration: evamukte narendreṇa narasiṁhā yuyutsavaḥ ,cakrurnānāvidhāśceṣṭāḥ siṁhāsanagatāstadā.
english: When the king had spoken thus, those lion-like men, eager for battle and seated on their thrones, then performed various actions.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 6 Verse 10. When the king had spoken thus, those lion-like men, eager for battle and seated on their thrones, then performed various actions.

sanskrit: तेषां निशम्येङ्गितानि युद्धे प्राणाञ्जुहूषताम् ।समुद्वीक्ष्य मुखं राज्ञो बालार्कसमवर्चसः ।आचार्यपुत्रो मेधावी वाक्यज्ञो वाक्यमाददे ॥११॥
transliteration: teṣāṁ niśamyeṅgitāni yuddhe prāṇāñjuhūṣatām ,samudvīkṣya mukhaṁ rājño bālārkasamavarcasaḥ ,ācāryaputro medhāvī vākyajño vākyamādade.
english: Hearing their intentions, those who desired to sacrifice their lives in battle, and observing the face of the king, whose radiance was like the rising sun, the intelligent preceptor's son, skilled in speech, then spoke words.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 6 Verse 11. Hearing their intentions, those who desired to sacrifice their lives in battle, and observing the face of the king, whose radiance was like the rising sun, the intelligent preceptor's son, skilled in speech, then spoke words.

sanskrit: रागो योगस्तथा दाक्ष्यं नयश्चेत्यर्थसाधकाः ।उपायाः पण्डितैः प्रोक्ताः सर्वे दैवसमाश्रिताः ॥१२॥
transliteration: rāgo yogastathā dākṣyaṁ nayaścetyarthasādhakāḥ ,upāyāḥ paṇḍitaiḥ proktāḥ sarve daivasamāśritāḥ.
english: Enthusiasm (rāga), diligent application (yoga), skill, and policy - these are declared by the wise as the means for attaining objectives. All of them are supported by destiny.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 6 Verse 12. Enthusiasm (rāga), diligent application (yoga), skill, and policy - these are declared by the wise as the means for attaining objectives. All of them are supported by destiny.

sanskrit: लोकप्रवीरा येऽस्माकं देवकल्पा महारथाः ।नीतिमन्तस्तथा युक्ता दक्षा रक्ताश्च ते हताः ॥१३॥
transliteration: lokapravīrā ye'smākaṁ devakalpā mahārathāḥ ,nītimantastathā yuktā dakṣā raktāśca te hatāḥ.
english: Those who were the foremost warriors in the world among us, like unto gods, great charioteers, prudent in policy, committed, skillful, and devoted - they have been slain.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 6 Verse 13. Those who were the foremost warriors in the world among us, like unto gods, great charioteers, prudent in policy, committed, skillful, and devoted - they have been slain.

sanskrit: न त्वेव कार्यं नैराश्यमस्माभिर्विजयं प्रति ।सुनीतैरिह सर्वार्थैर्दैवमप्यनुलोम्यते ॥१४॥
transliteration: na tveva kāryaṁ nairāśyamasmābhirvijayaṁ prati ,sunītairiha sarvārthairdaivamapyanulomyate.
english: However, despair should not be entertained by us concerning victory. For here, through well-executed policies and all (other) means, even destiny is made favorable.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 6 Verse 14. However, despair should not be entertained by us concerning victory. For here, through well-executed policies and all (other) means, even destiny is made favorable.

sanskrit: ते वयं प्रवरं नॄणां सर्वैर्गुणगणैर्युतम् ।कर्णं सेनापतिं कृत्वा प्रमथिष्यामहे रिपून् ॥१५॥
transliteration: te vayaṁ pravaraṁ nṝṇāṁ sarvairguṇagaṇairyutam ,karṇaṁ senāpatiṁ kṛtvā pramathiṣyāmahe ripūn.
english: We, indeed, will crush our enemies by making Karna - who is the foremost among men and endowed with all good qualities - our commander.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 6 Verse 15. We, indeed, will crush our enemies by making Karna - who is the foremost among men and endowed with all good qualities - our commander.

sanskrit: ततो दुर्योधनः प्रीतः प्रियं श्रुत्वा वचस्तदा ।प्रीतिसंस्कारसंयुक्तं तथ्यमात्महितं शुभम् ॥१६॥
transliteration: tato duryodhanaḥ prītaḥ priyaṁ śrutvā vacastadā ,prītisaṁskārasaṁyuktaṁ tathyamātmahitaṁ śubham.
english: Then Duryodhana, pleased, having heard that dear speech - which was endowed with the essence of affection, truthful, beneficial for himself, and auspicious - ...
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 6 Verse 16. Then Duryodhana, pleased, having heard that dear speech - which was endowed with the essence of affection, truthful, beneficial for himself, and auspicious - ...

sanskrit: स्वं मनः समवस्थाप्य बाहुवीर्यमुपाश्रितः ।दुर्योधनो महाराज राधेयमिदमब्रवीत् ॥१७॥
transliteration: svaṁ manaḥ samavasthāpya bāhuvīryamupāśritaḥ ,duryodhano mahārāja rādheyamidamabravīt.
english: Having brought his own mind to a state of composure, and relying on the strength of his arms, King Duryodhana spoke these words to Radheya (Karna).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 6 Verse 17. Having brought his own mind to a state of composure, and relying on the strength of his arms, King Duryodhana spoke these words to Radheya (Karna).

sanskrit: कर्ण जानामि ते वीर्यं सौहृदं च परं मयि ।तथापि त्वां महाबाहो प्रवक्ष्यामि हितं वचः ॥१८॥
transliteration: karṇa jānāmi te vīryaṁ sauhṛdaṁ ca paraṁ mayi ,tathāpi tvāṁ mahābāho pravakṣyāmi hitaṁ vacaḥ.
english: O Karna, I am aware of your valor and your profound friendship towards me. Nevertheless, O mighty-armed one, I will speak beneficial words to you.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 6 Verse 18. O Karna, I am aware of your valor and your profound friendship towards me. Nevertheless, O mighty-armed one, I will speak beneficial words to you.

sanskrit: श्रुत्वा यथेष्टं च कुरु वीर यत्तव रोचते ।भवान्प्राज्ञतमो नित्यं मम चैव परा गतिः ॥१९॥
transliteration: śrutvā yatheṣṭaṁ ca kuru vīra yattava rocate ,bhavānprājñatamo nityaṁ mama caiva parā gatiḥ.
english: O hero, having heard, and whatever pleases you, do as you wish. You are always the most discerning, and indeed my ultimate recourse.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 6 Verse 19. O hero, having heard, and whatever pleases you, do as you wish. You are always the most discerning, and indeed my ultimate recourse.

sanskrit: भीष्मद्रोणावतिरथौ हतौ सेनापती मम ।सेनापतिर्भवानस्तु ताभ्यां द्रविणवत्तरः ॥२०॥
transliteration: bhīṣmadroṇāvatirathau hatau senāpatī mama ,senāpatirbhavānastu tābhyāṁ draviṇavattaraḥ.
english: My two great chariot-warriors (atiratha), Bhishma and Drona, who were my commanders, have been slain. May Your Honor become the commander, more powerful than those two.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 6 Verse 20. My two great chariot-warriors (atiratha), Bhishma and Drona, who were my commanders, have been slain. May Your Honor become the commander, more powerful than those two.

sanskrit: वृद्धौ च तौ महेष्वासौ सापेक्षौ च धनंजये ।मानितौ च मया वीरौ राधेय वचनात्तव ॥२१॥
transliteration: vṛddhau ca tau maheṣvāsau sāpekṣau ca dhanaṁjaye ,mānitau ca mayā vīrau rādheya vacanāttava.
english: O Radheya, those two great archers were aged and also favorably inclined towards Dhananjaya (Arjuna). Moreover, those heroes were honored by me on your instruction.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 6 Verse 21. O Radheya, those two great archers were aged and also favorably inclined towards Dhananjaya (Arjuna). Moreover, those heroes were honored by me on your instruction.

sanskrit: पितामहत्वं संप्रेक्ष्य पाण्डुपुत्रा महारणे ।रक्षितास्तात भीष्मेण दिवसानि दशैव ह ॥२२॥
transliteration: pitāmahatvaṁ saṁprekṣya pāṇḍuputrā mahāraṇe ,rakṣitāstāta bhīṣmeṇa divasāni daśaiva ha.
english: O dear one, having deeply considered his status as their grandfather, Bhishma protected the sons of Pandu in the great battle for ten days only, indeed.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 6 Verse 22. O dear one, having deeply considered his status as their grandfather, Bhishma protected the sons of Pandu in the great battle for ten days only, indeed.

sanskrit: न्यस्तशस्त्रे च भवति हतो भीष्मः पितामहः ।शिखण्डिनं पुरस्कृत्य फल्गुनेन महाहवे ॥२३॥
transliteration: nyastaśastre ca bhavati hato bhīṣmaḥ pitāmahaḥ ,śikhaṇḍinaṁ puraskṛtya phalgunena mahāhave.
english: And so, in that great battle, the grandfather Bhishma, having laid down his weapons, was killed by Arjuna, who had placed Shikhandin in front.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 6 Verse 23. And so, in that great battle, the grandfather Bhishma, having laid down his weapons, was killed by Arjuna, who had placed Shikhandin in front.

sanskrit: हते तस्मिन्महाभागे शरतल्पगते तदा ।त्वयोक्ते पुरुषव्याघ्र द्रोणो ह्यासीत्पुरःसरः ॥२४॥
transliteration: hate tasminmahābhāge śaratalpagate tadā ,tvayokte puruṣavyāghra droṇo hyāsītpuraḥsaraḥ.
english: Then, O tiger among men, when that greatly fortunate one, having fallen upon a bed of arrows, was killed, Drona indeed became the commander, as you had stated.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 6 Verse 24. Then, O tiger among men, when that greatly fortunate one, having fallen upon a bed of arrows, was killed, Drona indeed became the commander, as you had stated.

sanskrit: तेनापि रक्षिताः पार्थाः शिष्यत्वादिह संयुगे ।स चापि निहतो वृद्धो धृष्टद्युम्नेन सत्वरम् ॥२५॥
transliteration: tenāpi rakṣitāḥ pārthāḥ śiṣyatvādiha saṁyuge ,sa cāpi nihato vṛddho dhṛṣṭadyumnena satvaram.
english: And by him (Drona), the sons of Pritha (pārthāḥ) were protected in this battle due to their status as his students. But he, though aged, was also swiftly killed by Dhrishtadyumna.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 6 Verse 25. And by him (Drona), the sons of Pritha (pārthāḥ) were protected in this battle due to their status as his students. But he, though aged, was also swiftly killed by Dhrishtadyumna.

sanskrit: निहताभ्यां प्रधानाभ्यां ताभ्याममितविक्रम ।त्वत्समं समरे योधं नान्यं पश्यामि चिन्तयन् ॥२६॥
transliteration: nihatābhyāṁ pradhānābhyāṁ tābhyāmamitavikrama ,tvatsamaṁ samare yodhaṁ nānyaṁ paśyāmi cintayan.
english: O You of immeasurable valor, considering the fall of those two foremost warriors, I do not see any other fighter in battle who is equal to you, even after much thought.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 6 Verse 26. O You of immeasurable valor, considering the fall of those two foremost warriors, I do not see any other fighter in battle who is equal to you, even after much thought.

sanskrit: भवानेव तु नः शक्तो विजयाय न संशयः ।पूर्वं मध्ये च पश्चाच्च तवैव विदितं हि तत् ॥२७॥
transliteration: bhavāneva tu naḥ śakto vijayāya na saṁśayaḥ ,pūrvaṁ madhye ca paścācca tavaiva viditaṁ hi tat.
english: You alone are certainly capable of securing victory for us; there is no doubt. Indeed, that outcome is known to you alone, whether in the beginning, the middle, or the end.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 6 Verse 27. You alone are certainly capable of securing victory for us; there is no doubt. Indeed, that outcome is known to you alone, whether in the beginning, the middle, or the end.

sanskrit: स भवान्धुर्यवत्संख्ये धुरमुद्वोढुमर्हसि ।अभिषेचय सेनान्ये स्वयमात्मानमात्मना ॥२८॥
transliteration: sa bhavāndhuryavatsaṁkhye dhuramudvoḍhumarhasi ,abhiṣecaya senānye svayamātmānamātmanā.
english: Therefore, like a beast of burden, you are fit to carry the main responsibility in battle. Anoint yourself as the commander (senānī) by your own self (ātman).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 6 Verse 28. Therefore, like a beast of burden, you are fit to carry the main responsibility in battle. Anoint yourself as the commander (senānī) by your own self (ātman).

sanskrit: देवतानां यथा स्कन्दः सेनानीः प्रभुरव्ययः ।तथा भवानिमां सेनां धार्तराष्ट्रीं बिभर्तु मे ।जहि शत्रुगणान्सर्वान्महेन्द्र इव दानवान् ॥२९॥
transliteration: devatānāṁ yathā skandaḥ senānīḥ prabhuravyayaḥ ,tathā bhavānimāṁ senāṁ dhārtarāṣṭrīṁ bibhartu me ,jahi śatrugaṇānsarvānmahendra iva dānavān.
english: Just as Skanda is the imperishable commander (senānī) and lord of the gods, so may you lead this army of Dhṛtarāṣṭra's sons for me. Destroy all the hosts of enemies, just as Mahendra (Indra) destroyed the Dānavas.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 6 Verse 29. Just as Skanda is the imperishable commander (senānī) and lord of the gods, so may you lead this army of Dhṛtarāṣṭra's sons for me. Destroy all the hosts of enemies, just as Mahendra (Indra) destroyed the Dānavas.

sanskrit: अवस्थितं रणे ज्ञात्वा पाण्डवास्त्वां महारथम् ।द्रविष्यन्ति सपाञ्चाला विष्णुं दृष्ट्वेव दानवाः ।तस्मात्त्वं पुरुषव्याघ्र प्रकर्षेथा महाचमूम् ॥३०॥
transliteration: avasthitaṁ raṇe jñātvā pāṇḍavāstvāṁ mahāratham ,draviṣyanti sapāñcālā viṣṇuṁ dṛṣṭveva dānavāḥ ,tasmāttvaṁ puruṣavyāghra prakarṣethā mahācamūm.
english: Knowing you, the great warrior (mahāratha), to be present on the battlefield, the Pāṇḍavas, along with the Pāñcālas, will flee, just as the Dānavas flee upon seeing Viṣṇu. Therefore, O tiger among men (puruṣavyāghra), you should lead this great army forward.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 6 Verse 30. Knowing you, the great warrior (mahāratha), to be present on the battlefield, the Pāṇḍavas, along with the Pāñcālas, will flee, just as the Dānavas flee upon seeing Viṣṇu. Therefore, O tiger among men (puruṣavyāghra), you should lead this great army forward.

sanskrit: भवत्यवस्थिते यत्ते पाण्डवा गतचेतसः ।भविष्यन्ति सहामात्याः पाञ्चालैः सृञ्जयैः सह ॥३१॥
transliteration: bhavatyavasthite yatte pāṇḍavā gatacetasaḥ ,bhaviṣyanti sahāmātyāḥ pāñcālaiḥ sṛñjayaiḥ saha.
english: When it is established that your Pandavas are bewildered, they will be so along with their ministers, the Panchalas, and the Srinjayas.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 6 Verse 31. When it is established that your Pandavas are bewildered, they will be so along with their ministers, the Panchalas, and the Srinjayas.

sanskrit: यथा ह्यभ्युदितः सूर्यः प्रतपन्स्वेन तेजसा ।व्यपोहति तमस्तीव्रं तथा शत्रून्व्यपोह नः ॥३२॥
transliteration: yathā hyabhyuditaḥ sūryaḥ pratapansvena tejasā ,vyapohati tamastīvraṁ tathā śatrūnvyapoha naḥ.
english: Indeed, just as the risen sun, shining with its own splendor, dispels intense darkness, so too dispel our enemies.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 6 Verse 32. Indeed, just as the risen sun, shining with its own splendor, dispels intense darkness, so too dispel our enemies.

sanskrit: कर्ण उवाच ।उक्तमेतन्मया पूर्वं गान्धारे तव संनिधौ ।जेष्यामि पाण्डवान्राजन्सपुत्रान्सजनार्दनान् ॥३३॥
transliteration: karṇa uvāca ,uktametanmayā pūrvaṁ gāndhāre tava saṁnidhau ,jeṣyāmi pāṇḍavānrājansaputrānsajanārdanān.
english: Karṇa said: "This was indeed said by me previously, O Gāndhāra, in your presence, O King: 'I shall conquer the Pandavas, along with their sons and Janārdana (Kṛṣṇa).'"
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 6 Verse 33. Karṇa said: "This was indeed said by me previously, O Gāndhāra, in your presence, O King: 'I shall conquer the Pandavas, along with their sons and Janārdana (Kṛṣṇa).'"

sanskrit: सेनापतिर्भविष्यामि तवाहं नात्र संशयः ।स्थिरो भव महाराज जितान्विद्धि च पाण्डवान् ॥३४॥
transliteration: senāpatirbhaviṣyāmi tavāhaṁ nātra saṁśayaḥ ,sthiro bhava mahārāja jitānviddhi ca pāṇḍavān.
english: I shall be your commander-in-chief; there is no doubt about this. O great king, be firm, and know the Pandavas to be vanquished.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 6 Verse 34. I shall be your commander-in-chief; there is no doubt about this. O great king, be firm, and know the Pandavas to be vanquished.

sanskrit: संजय उवाच ।एवमुक्तो महातेजास्ततो दुर्योधनो नृपः ।उत्तस्थौ राजभिः सार्धं देवैरिव शतक्रतुः ।सेनापत्येन सत्कर्तुं कर्णं स्कन्दमिवामराः ॥३५॥
transliteration: saṁjaya uvāca ,evamukto mahātejāstato duryodhano nṛpaḥ ,uttasthau rājabhiḥ sārdhaṁ devairiva śatakratuḥ ,senāpatyena satkartuṁ karṇaṁ skandamivāmarāḥ.
english: Sañjaya said: Thus addressed, the mighty-splendored King Duryodhana then rose up with the kings, just as Indra (śatakratu) would rise with the deities. (He did this) to honor Karṇa with the position of commander-in-chief, just as the immortals (amarāḥ) would honor Skanda.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 6 Verse 35. Sañjaya said: Thus addressed, the mighty-splendored King Duryodhana then rose up with the kings, just as Indra (śatakratu) would rise with the deities. (He did this) to honor Karṇa with the position of commander-in-chief, just as the immortals (amarāḥ) would honor Skanda.

sanskrit: ततोऽभिषिषिचुस्तूर्णं विधिदृष्टेन कर्मणा ।दुर्योधनमुखा राजन्राजानो विजयैषिणः ।शातकौम्भमयैः कुम्भैर्माहेयैश्चाभिमन्त्रितैः ॥३६॥
transliteration: tato'bhiṣiṣicustūrṇaṁ vidhidṛṣṭena karmaṇā ,duryodhanamukhā rājanrājāno vijayaiṣiṇaḥ ,śātakaumbhamayaiḥ kumbhairmāheyaiścābhimantritaiḥ.
english: Then, O King, the kings, led by Duryodhana and desiring victory, quickly performed the anointing ceremony with rites (karma) prescribed by sacred rules, using pitchers made of pure gold and also earthen pitchers consecrated by sacred syllables (mantra).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 6 Verse 36. Then, O King, the kings, led by Duryodhana and desiring victory, quickly performed the anointing ceremony with rites (karma) prescribed by sacred rules, using pitchers made of pure gold and also earthen pitchers consecrated by sacred syllables (mantra).

sanskrit: तोयपूर्णैर्विषाणैश्च द्वीपिखड्गमहर्षभैः ।मणिमुक्तामयैश्चान्यैः पुण्यगन्धैस्तथौषधैः ॥३७॥
transliteration: toyapūrṇairviṣāṇaiśca dvīpikhaḍgamaharṣabhaiḥ ,maṇimuktāmayaiścānyaiḥ puṇyagandhaistathauṣadhaiḥ.
english: (They anointed him) with horns filled with water, including those of tigers (dvīpi), rhinoceroses (khaḍga), and great bulls (maharṣabha), and with other (vessels) made of jewels and pearls, and with sacred fragrant substances, and with medicinal herbs.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 6 Verse 37. (They anointed him) with horns filled with water, including those of tigers (dvīpi), rhinoceroses (khaḍga), and great bulls (maharṣabha), and with other (vessels) made of jewels and pearls, and with sacred fragrant substances, and with medicinal herbs.

sanskrit: औदुम्बरे समासीनमासने क्षौमसंवृतम् ।शास्त्रदृष्टेन विधिना संभारैश्च सुसंभृतैः ॥३८॥
transliteration: audumbare samāsīnamāsane kṣaumasaṁvṛtam ,śāstradṛṣṭena vidhinā saṁbhāraiśca susaṁbhṛtaiḥ.
english: (They anointed him) while he was seated on a fig-wood (audumbara) throne, covered with silk cloth, using methods prescribed by the scriptures (śāstra) and with well-prepared materials.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 6 Verse 38. (They anointed him) while he was seated on a fig-wood (audumbara) throne, covered with silk cloth, using methods prescribed by the scriptures (śāstra) and with well-prepared materials.

sanskrit: जय पार्थान्सगोविन्दान्सानुगांस्त्वं महाहवे ।इति तं बन्दिनः प्राहुर्द्विजाश्च भरतर्षभ ॥३९॥
transliteration: jaya pārthānsagovindānsānugāṁstvaṁ mahāhave ,iti taṁ bandinaḥ prāhurdvijāśca bharatarṣabha.
english: O bull among the Bhāratas, the bards and Brahmins (dvijāḥ) said to him, 'You, conquer the Pārthas, along with Govinda (Krishna) and their followers, in this great battle!'
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 6 Verse 39. O bull among the Bhāratas, the bards and Brahmins (dvijāḥ) said to him, 'You, conquer the Pārthas, along with Govinda (Krishna) and their followers, in this great battle!'

sanskrit: जहि पार्थान्सपाञ्चालान्राधेय विजयाय नः ।उद्यन्निव सदा भानुस्तमांस्युग्रैर्गभस्तिभिः ॥४०॥
transliteration: jahi pārthānsapāñcālānrādheya vijayāya naḥ ,udyanniva sadā bhānustamāṁsyugrairgabhastibhiḥ.
english: O son of Rādhā (Rādheya), destroy the Pārthas and the Pāñcālas for our victory, just as the rising sun always dispels darkness with its fierce rays.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 6 Verse 40. O son of Rādhā (Rādheya), destroy the Pārthas and the Pāñcālas for our victory, just as the rising sun always dispels darkness with its fierce rays.

sanskrit: न ह्यलं त्वद्विसृष्टानां शराणां ते सकेशवाः ।कृतघ्नाः सूर्यरश्मीनां ज्वलतामिव दर्शने ॥४१॥
transliteration: na hyalaṁ tvadvisṛṣṭānāṁ śarāṇāṁ te sakeśavāḥ ,kṛtaghnāḥ sūryaraśmīnāṁ jvalatāmiva darśane.
english: Indeed, those ungrateful ones (kṛtaghnāḥ) along with Keśava (Krishna) are not capable of withstanding your released arrows, just as one cannot endure the sight of blazing sun's rays.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 6 Verse 41. Indeed, those ungrateful ones (kṛtaghnāḥ) along with Keśava (Krishna) are not capable of withstanding your released arrows, just as one cannot endure the sight of blazing sun's rays.

sanskrit: न हि पार्थाः सपाञ्चालाः स्थातुं शक्तास्तवाग्रतः ।आत्तशस्त्रस्य समरे महेन्द्रस्येव दानवाः ॥४२॥
transliteration: na hi pārthāḥ sapāñcālāḥ sthātuṁ śaktāstavāgrataḥ ,āttaśastrasya samare mahendrasyeva dānavāḥ.
english: Indeed, the Pārthas along with the Pāñcālas are not capable of standing before you, just as the Dānavas (demons) are not capable of standing before Mahendra (Indra) who has taken up his weapon in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 6 Verse 42. Indeed, the Pārthas along with the Pāñcālas are not capable of standing before you, just as the Dānavas (demons) are not capable of standing before Mahendra (Indra) who has taken up his weapon in battle.

sanskrit: अभिषिक्तस्तु राधेयः प्रभया सोऽमितप्रभः ।व्यत्यरिच्यत रूपेण दिवाकर इवापरः ॥४३॥
transliteration: abhiṣiktastu rādheyaḥ prabhayā so'mitaprabhaḥ ,vyatyaricyata rūpeṇa divākara ivāparaḥ.
english: Indeed, the son of Rādhā (Rādheya), once consecrated, being already of immeasurable splendor, surpassed others in beauty, like another sun.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 6 Verse 43. Indeed, the son of Rādhā (Rādheya), once consecrated, being already of immeasurable splendor, surpassed others in beauty, like another sun.

sanskrit: सेनापत्येन राधेयमभिषिच्य सुतस्तव ।अमन्यत तदात्मानं कृतार्थं कालचोदितः ॥४४॥
transliteration: senāpatyena rādheyamabhiṣicya sutastava ,amanyata tadātmānaṁ kṛtārthaṁ kālacoditaḥ.
english: Having consecrated the son of Rādhā (Rādheya) to the position of commander-in-chief, your son (Duryodhana) then, impelled by time or fate, considered himself to have fulfilled his objective (kṛtārtham).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 6 Verse 44. Having consecrated the son of Rādhā (Rādheya) to the position of commander-in-chief, your son (Duryodhana) then, impelled by time or fate, considered himself to have fulfilled his objective (kṛtārtham).

sanskrit: कर्णोऽपि राजन्संप्राप्य सेनापत्यमरिंदमः ।योगमाज्ञापयामास सूर्यस्योदयनं प्रति ॥४५॥
transliteration: karṇo'pi rājansaṁprāpya senāpatyamariṁdamaḥ ,yogamājñāpayāmāsa sūryasyodayanaṁ prati.
english: O king, Karna, the subduer of enemies, having also obtained the position of commander-in-chief, commanded the engagement (yoga) to commence at sunrise.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 6 Verse 45. O king, Karna, the subduer of enemies, having also obtained the position of commander-in-chief, commanded the engagement (yoga) to commence at sunrise.

sanskrit: तव पुत्रैर्वृतः कर्णः शुशुभे तत्र भारत ।देवैरिव यथा स्कन्दः संग्रामे तारकामये ॥४६॥
transliteration: tava putrairvṛtaḥ karṇaḥ śuśubhe tatra bhārata ,devairiva yathā skandaḥ saṁgrāme tārakāmaye.
english: O Bhārata (Dhṛtarāṣṭra), Karna, surrounded by your sons, shone there just like Skanda, accompanied by the gods, in the war against Tārakā (tārakāmaya).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 6 Verse 46. O Bhārata (Dhṛtarāṣṭra), Karna, surrounded by your sons, shone there just like Skanda, accompanied by the gods, in the war against Tārakā (tārakāmaya).

sanskrit: धृतराष्ट्र उवाच ।सेनापत्यं तु संप्राप्य कर्णो वैकर्तनस्तदा ।तथोक्तश्च स्वयं राज्ञा स्निग्धं भ्रातृसमं वचः ॥१॥
transliteration: dhṛtarāṣṭra uvāca ,senāpatyaṁ tu saṁprāpya karṇo vaikartanastadā ,tathoktaśca svayaṁ rājñā snigdhaṁ bhrātṛsamaṁ vacaḥ.
english: Dhritarashtra said: "But then, having obtained the command of the army (senāpatyam), and having been addressed by the king himself with affectionate words like those of a brother, Karna, son of Vikartana... (what did he do?)"
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 7 Verse 1. Dhritarashtra said: "But then, having obtained the command of the army (senāpatyam), and having been addressed by the king himself with affectionate words like those of a brother, Karna, son of Vikartana... (what did he do?)"

sanskrit: योगमाज्ञाप्य सेनाया आदित्येऽभ्युदिते तदा ।अकरोत्किं महाप्राज्ञस्तन्ममाचक्ष्व संजय ॥२॥
transliteration: yogamājñāpya senāyā āditye'bhyudite tadā ,akarotkiṁ mahāprājñastanmamācakṣva saṁjaya.
english: What did that greatly wise Karna do, Sanjaya, after commanding the deployment (yoga) of the army when the sun had risen? Tell me that.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 7 Verse 2. What did that greatly wise Karna do, Sanjaya, after commanding the deployment (yoga) of the army when the sun had risen? Tell me that.

sanskrit: संजय उवाच ।कर्णस्य मतमाज्ञाय पुत्रस्ते भरतर्षभ ।योगमाज्ञापयामास नान्दीतूर्यपुरःसरम् ॥३॥
transliteration: saṁjaya uvāca ,karṇasya matamājñāya putraste bharatarṣabha ,yogamājñāpayāmāsa nāndītūryapuraḥsaram.
english: Sanjaya said: "O bull among Bharatas (Dhritarashtra), your son, having understood Karna's opinion, commanded the deployment (yoga) of the army, preceded by auspicious trumpets."
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 7 Verse 3. Sanjaya said: "O bull among Bharatas (Dhritarashtra), your son, having understood Karna's opinion, commanded the deployment (yoga) of the army, preceded by auspicious trumpets."

sanskrit: महत्यपररात्रे तु तव पुत्रस्य मारिष ।योगो योगेति सहसा प्रादुरासीन्महास्वनः ॥४॥
transliteration: mahatyapararātre tu tava putrasya māriṣa ,yogo yogeti sahasā prādurāsīnmahāsvanaḥ.
english: But, O respected one, in the deep last watch of the night, a great clamor of 'Deployment! Deployment!' (yoga) suddenly arose at your son's command.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 7 Verse 4. But, O respected one, in the deep last watch of the night, a great clamor of 'Deployment! Deployment!' (yoga) suddenly arose at your son's command.

sanskrit: नागानां कल्पमानानां रथानां च वरूथिनाम् ।संनह्यतां पदातीनां वाजिनां च विशां पते ॥५॥
transliteration: nāgānāṁ kalpamānānāṁ rathānāṁ ca varūthinām ,saṁnahyatāṁ padātīnāṁ vājināṁ ca viśāṁ pate.
english: O lord of the people, from the sounds of elephants being caparisoned, of chariots with their protectors, of foot-soldiers arming themselves, and of horses -
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 7 Verse 5. O lord of the people, from the sounds of elephants being caparisoned, of chariots with their protectors, of foot-soldiers arming themselves, and of horses -

sanskrit: क्रोशतां चापि योधानां त्वरितानां परस्परम् ।बभूव तुमुलः शब्दो दिवस्पृक्सुमहांस्तदा ॥६॥
transliteration: krośatāṁ cāpi yodhānāṁ tvaritānāṁ parasparam ,babhūva tumulaḥ śabdo divaspṛksumahāṁstadā.
english: And then, from the warriors who were shouting and rushing towards each other, there arose a very great, sky-touching, tumultuous sound.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 7 Verse 6. And then, from the warriors who were shouting and rushing towards each other, there arose a very great, sky-touching, tumultuous sound.

sanskrit: ततः श्वेतपताकेन बालार्काकारवाजिना ।हेमपृष्ठेन धनुषा हस्तिकक्ष्येण केतुना ॥७॥
transliteration: tataḥ śvetapatākena bālārkākāravājinā ,hemapṛṣṭhena dhanuṣā hastikakṣyeṇa ketunā.
english: Then, [a warrior appeared] with a white banner, with horses resembling the rising sun, with a bow having a golden back, and with a standard marked by an elephant's girth.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 7 Verse 7. Then, [a warrior appeared] with a white banner, with horses resembling the rising sun, with a bow having a golden back, and with a standard marked by an elephant's girth.

sanskrit: तूणेन शरपूर्णेन साङ्गदेन वरूथिना ।शतघ्नीकिङ्किणीशक्तिशूलतोमरधारिणा ॥८॥
transliteration: tūṇena śarapūrṇena sāṅgadena varūthinā ,śataghnīkiṅkiṇīśaktiśūlatomaradhāriṇā.
english: - with a quiver full of arrows, with arm-protectors, with protective gear, and bearing a hundred-killer weapon (śataghnī), small bells, a spear (śakti), a trident (śūla), and a lance (tomara).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 7 Verse 8. - with a quiver full of arrows, with arm-protectors, with protective gear, and bearing a hundred-killer weapon (śataghnī), small bells, a spear (śakti), a trident (śūla), and a lance (tomara).

sanskrit: कार्मुकेणोपपन्नेन विमलादित्यवर्चसा ।रथेनातिपताकेन सूतपुत्रो व्यदृश्यत ॥९॥
transliteration: kārmukeṇopapannena vimalādityavarcasā ,rathenātipatākena sūtaputro vyadṛśyata.
english: Karna, the son of the charioteer, became visible, equipped with his bow, radiant with the splendor of a spotless sun, and seated in a chariot bearing a mighty banner.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 7 Verse 9. Karna, the son of the charioteer, became visible, equipped with his bow, radiant with the splendor of a spotless sun, and seated in a chariot bearing a mighty banner.

sanskrit: धमन्तं वारिजं तात हेमजालविभूषितम् ।विधुन्वानं महच्चापं कार्तस्वरविभूषितम् ॥१०॥
transliteration: dhamantaṁ vārijaṁ tāta hemajālavibhūṣitam ,vidhunvānaṁ mahaccāpaṁ kārtasvaravibhūṣitam.
english: O dear one, he was blowing a conch (vārija) adorned with a network of gold, and brandishing his great bow, which was also embellished with gold.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 7 Verse 10. O dear one, he was blowing a conch (vārija) adorned with a network of gold, and brandishing his great bow, which was also embellished with gold.

sanskrit: दृष्ट्वा कर्णं महेष्वासं रथस्थं रथिनां वरम् ।भानुमन्तमिवोद्यन्तं तमो घ्नन्तं सहस्रशः ॥११॥
transliteration: dṛṣṭvā karṇaṁ maheṣvāsaṁ rathasthaṁ rathināṁ varam ,bhānumantamivodyantaṁ tamo ghnantaṁ sahasraśaḥ.
english: Upon seeing Karna, that great archer, seated in his chariot, the foremost among charioteers, who, like the rising sun, dispelled darkness in countless ways.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 7 Verse 11. Upon seeing Karna, that great archer, seated in his chariot, the foremost among charioteers, who, like the rising sun, dispelled darkness in countless ways.

sanskrit: न भीष्मव्यसनं केचिन्नापि द्रोणस्य मारिष ।नान्येषां पुरुषव्याघ्र मेनिरे तत्र कौरवाः ॥१२॥
transliteration: na bhīṣmavyasanaṁ kecinnāpi droṇasya māriṣa ,nānyeṣāṁ puruṣavyāghra menire tatra kauravāḥ.
english: O venerable one (māriṣa), O tiger among men (puruṣavyāghra), the Kauravas present there did not consider the misfortune of Bhishma, nor Drona's, nor that of any others.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 7 Verse 12. O venerable one (māriṣa), O tiger among men (puruṣavyāghra), the Kauravas present there did not consider the misfortune of Bhishma, nor Drona's, nor that of any others.

sanskrit: ततस्तु त्वरयन्योधाञ्शङ्खशब्देन मारिष ।कर्णो निष्कासयामास कौरवाणां वरूथिनीम् ॥१३॥
transliteration: tatastu tvarayanyodhāñśaṅkhaśabdena māriṣa ,karṇo niṣkāsayāmāsa kauravāṇāṁ varūthinīm.
english: Then, O respected one, Karna, with the sound of his conch, spurred the warriors into action and led out the army of the Kauravas.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 7 Verse 13. Then, O respected one, Karna, with the sound of his conch, spurred the warriors into action and led out the army of the Kauravas.

sanskrit: व्यूहं व्यूह्य महेष्वासो माकरं शत्रुतापनः ।प्रत्युद्ययौ तदा कर्णः पाण्डवान्विजिगीषया ॥१४॥
transliteration: vyūhaṁ vyūhya maheṣvāso mākaraṁ śatrutāpanaḥ ,pratyudyayau tadā karṇaḥ pāṇḍavānvijigīṣayā.
english: Then, Karna, the great archer and tormentor of foes, having arranged the Makara (crocodile) battle formation, advanced against the Pandavas with the desire for conquest.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 7 Verse 14. Then, Karna, the great archer and tormentor of foes, having arranged the Makara (crocodile) battle formation, advanced against the Pandavas with the desire for conquest.

sanskrit: मकरस्य तु तुण्डे वै कर्णो राजन्व्यवस्थितः ।नेत्राभ्यां शकुनिः शूर उलूकश्च महारथः ॥१५॥
transliteration: makarasya tu tuṇḍe vai karṇo rājanvyavasthitaḥ ,netrābhyāṁ śakuniḥ śūra ulūkaśca mahārathaḥ.
english: O King, Karna was indeed positioned in the snout of the Makara (crocodile) formation. In its two eyes were the heroic Shakuni and the great chariot-warrior Uluka.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 7 Verse 15. O King, Karna was indeed positioned in the snout of the Makara (crocodile) formation. In its two eyes were the heroic Shakuni and the great chariot-warrior Uluka.

sanskrit: द्रोणपुत्रस्तु शिरसि ग्रीवायां सर्वसोदराः ।मध्ये दुर्योधनो राजा बलेन महता वृतः ॥१६॥
transliteration: droṇaputrastu śirasi grīvāyāṁ sarvasodarāḥ ,madhye duryodhano rājā balena mahatā vṛtaḥ.
english: But Drona's son (Ashwatthama) was at the head [of the formation], and all the brothers (Kauravas) were in its neck. In the middle was King Duryodhana, surrounded by a great army.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 7 Verse 16. But Drona's son (Ashwatthama) was at the head [of the formation], and all the brothers (Kauravas) were in its neck. In the middle was King Duryodhana, surrounded by a great army.

sanskrit: वामे पादे तु राजेन्द्र कृतवर्मा व्यवस्थितः ।नारायणबलैर्युक्तो गोपालैर्युद्धदुर्मदः ॥१७॥
transliteration: vāme pāde tu rājendra kṛtavarmā vyavasthitaḥ ,nārāyaṇabalairyukto gopālairyuddhadurmadaḥ.
english: O King, Kritavarma was positioned on the left flank, accompanied by the Narayana forces and the cowherds, fiercely resolute in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 7 Verse 17. O King, Kritavarma was positioned on the left flank, accompanied by the Narayana forces and the cowherds, fiercely resolute in battle.

sanskrit: पादे तु दक्षिणे राजन्गौतमः सत्यविक्रमः ।त्रिगर्तैश्च महेष्वासैर्दाक्षिणात्यैश्च संवृतः ॥१८॥
transliteration: pāde tu dakṣiṇe rājangautamaḥ satyavikramaḥ ,trigartaiśca maheṣvāsairdākṣiṇātyaiśca saṁvṛtaḥ.
english: O King, on the right flank was Gautama, known for his true valor, surrounded by the Trigartas, who were great archers, and also by the southerners.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 7 Verse 18. O King, on the right flank was Gautama, known for his true valor, surrounded by the Trigartas, who were great archers, and also by the southerners.

sanskrit: अनुपादस्तु यो वामस्तत्र शल्यो व्यवस्थितः ।महत्या सेनया सार्धं मद्रदेशसमुत्थया ॥१९॥
transliteration: anupādastu yo vāmastatra śalyo vyavasthitaḥ ,mahatyā senayā sārdhaṁ madradeśasamutthayā.
english: Furthermore, on the secondary left flank, Shalya was positioned with a large army originating from the Madra country.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 7 Verse 19. Furthermore, on the secondary left flank, Shalya was positioned with a large army originating from the Madra country.

sanskrit: दक्षिणे तु महाराज सुषेणः सत्यसंगरः ।वृतो रथसहस्रैश्च दन्तिनां च शतैस्तथा ॥२०॥
transliteration: dakṣiṇe tu mahārāja suṣeṇaḥ satyasaṁgaraḥ ,vṛto rathasahasraiśca dantināṁ ca śataistathā.
english: O great King, on the right flank was Sushena, true to his word in battle, surrounded by thousands of chariots and also by hundreds of elephants.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 7 Verse 20. O great King, on the right flank was Sushena, true to his word in battle, surrounded by thousands of chariots and also by hundreds of elephants.

sanskrit: पुच्छे आस्तां महावीरौ भ्रातरौ पार्थिवौ तदा ।चित्रसेनश्च चित्रश्च महत्या सेनया वृतौ ॥२१॥
transliteration: pucche āstāṁ mahāvīrau bhrātarau pārthivau tadā ,citrasenaśca citraśca mahatyā senayā vṛtau.
english: Then, at the very rear, were the two great warrior brothers, the kings Citrasena and Citra, encompassed by a vast army.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 7 Verse 21. Then, at the very rear, were the two great warrior brothers, the kings Citrasena and Citra, encompassed by a vast army.

sanskrit: ततः प्रयाते राजेन्द्र कर्णे नरवरोत्तमे ।धनंजयमभिप्रेक्ष्य धर्मराजोऽब्रवीदिदम् ॥२२॥
transliteration: tataḥ prayāte rājendra karṇe naravarottame ,dhanaṁjayamabhiprekṣya dharmarājo'bravīdidam.
english: Then, O king, after Karṇa, that foremost among men, had departed, the king of righteousness (Dharmarāja), looking at Dhanañjaya, spoke these words.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 7 Verse 22. Then, O king, after Karṇa, that foremost among men, had departed, the king of righteousness (Dharmarāja), looking at Dhanañjaya, spoke these words.

sanskrit: पश्य पार्थ महासेनां धार्तराष्ट्रस्य संयुगे ।कर्णेन निर्मितां वीर गुप्तां वीरैर्महारथैः ॥२३॥
transliteration: paśya pārtha mahāsenāṁ dhārtarāṣṭrasya saṁyuge ,karṇena nirmitāṁ vīra guptāṁ vīrairmahārathaiḥ.
english: Behold, O Pārtha (Arjuna), this immense army of the son of Dhṛtarāṣṭra (Duryodhana) in battle, which was formed by Karṇa and is now protected by great chariot-warriors.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 7 Verse 23. Behold, O Pārtha (Arjuna), this immense army of the son of Dhṛtarāṣṭra (Duryodhana) in battle, which was formed by Karṇa and is now protected by great chariot-warriors.

sanskrit: हतवीरतमा ह्येषा धार्तराष्ट्री महाचमूः ।फल्गुशेषा महाबाहो तृणैस्तुल्या मता मम ॥२४॥
transliteration: hatavīratamā hyeṣā dhārtarāṣṭrī mahācamūḥ ,phalguśeṣā mahābāho tṛṇaistulyā matā mama.
english: Indeed, O mighty-armed one, this great army of Dhṛtarāṣṭra's sons, having lost its most valiant warriors, now has only a few remaining and is, in my opinion, comparable to mere grass.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 7 Verse 24. Indeed, O mighty-armed one, this great army of Dhṛtarāṣṭra's sons, having lost its most valiant warriors, now has only a few remaining and is, in my opinion, comparable to mere grass.

sanskrit: एको ह्यत्र महेष्वासः सूतपुत्रो व्यवस्थितः ।सदेवासुरगन्धर्वैः सकिंनरमहोरगैः ।चराचरैस्त्रिभिर्लोकैर्योऽजय्यो रथिनां वरः ॥२५॥
transliteration: eko hyatra maheṣvāsaḥ sūtaputro vyavasthitaḥ ,sadevāsuragandharvaiḥ sakiṁnaramahoragaiḥ ,carācaraistribhirlokairyo'jayyo rathināṁ varaḥ.
english: Here, indeed, stands the son of the charioteer (Sūtaputra Karna), a great archer, who is unconquerable by gods, asuras, gandharvas, kinnaras, great serpents, and by all moving and non-moving beings from the three worlds; he is the best among charioteers.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 7 Verse 25. Here, indeed, stands the son of the charioteer (Sūtaputra Karna), a great archer, who is unconquerable by gods, asuras, gandharvas, kinnaras, great serpents, and by all moving and non-moving beings from the three worlds; he is the best among charioteers.

sanskrit: तं हत्वाद्य महाबाहो विजयस्तव फल्गुन ।उद्धृतश्च भवेच्छल्यो मम द्वादशवार्षिकः ।एवं ज्ञात्वा महाबाहो व्यूहं व्यूह यथेच्छसि ॥२६॥
transliteration: taṁ hatvādya mahābāho vijayastava phalguna ,uddhṛtaśca bhavecchalyo mama dvādaśavārṣikaḥ ,evaṁ jñātvā mahābāho vyūhaṁ vyūha yathecchasi.
english: O mighty-armed Arjuna (Phalguna), by killing him today, victory will be yours. And my twelve-year-old thorn (śalya) of grief will be removed. Knowing this, O mighty-armed one, arrange the battle formation (vyūha) as you desire.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 7 Verse 26. O mighty-armed Arjuna (Phalguna), by killing him today, victory will be yours. And my twelve-year-old thorn (śalya) of grief will be removed. Knowing this, O mighty-armed one, arrange the battle formation (vyūha) as you desire.

sanskrit: भ्रातुस्तद्वचनं श्रुत्वा पाण्डवः श्वेतवाहनः ।अर्धचन्द्रेण व्यूहेन प्रत्यव्यूहत तां चमूम् ॥२७॥
transliteration: bhrātustadvacanaṁ śrutvā pāṇḍavaḥ śvetavāhanaḥ ,ardhacandreṇa vyūhena pratyavyūhata tāṁ camūm.
english: Having heard those words of his brother, Arjuna (Pāṇḍava), who is known as Śvetavāhana (the one with white steeds), arranged his army against them in a half-moon formation (vyūha).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 7 Verse 27. Having heard those words of his brother, Arjuna (Pāṇḍava), who is known as Śvetavāhana (the one with white steeds), arranged his army against them in a half-moon formation (vyūha).

sanskrit: वामपार्श्वेऽभवद्राजन्भीमसेनो व्यवस्थितः ।दक्षिणे च महेष्वासो धृष्टद्युम्नो महाबलः ॥२८॥
transliteration: vāmapārśve'bhavadrājanbhīmaseno vyavasthitaḥ ,dakṣiṇe ca maheṣvāso dhṛṣṭadyumno mahābalaḥ.
english: O King, Bhimasena was positioned on the left flank, and on the right, the mighty great archer Dhṛṣṭadyumna stood.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 7 Verse 28. O King, Bhimasena was positioned on the left flank, and on the right, the mighty great archer Dhṛṣṭadyumna stood.

sanskrit: मध्ये व्यूहस्य साक्षात्तु पाण्डवः कृष्णसारथिः ।नकुलः सहदेवश्च धर्मराजश्च पृष्ठतः ॥२९॥
transliteration: madhye vyūhasya sākṣāttu pāṇḍavaḥ kṛṣṇasārathiḥ ,nakulaḥ sahadevaśca dharmarājaśca pṛṣṭhataḥ.
english: The Pāṇḍava (Arjuna), whose charioteer was Kṛṣṇa, was directly in the middle of the formation. Nakula, Sahadeva, and Dharmarāja (Yudhiṣṭhira) were positioned behind him.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 7 Verse 29. The Pāṇḍava (Arjuna), whose charioteer was Kṛṣṇa, was directly in the middle of the formation. Nakula, Sahadeva, and Dharmarāja (Yudhiṣṭhira) were positioned behind him.

sanskrit: चक्ररक्षौ तु पाञ्चाल्यौ युधामन्यूत्तमौजसौ ।नार्जुनं जहतुर्युद्धे पाल्यमानौ किरीटिना ॥३०॥
transliteration: cakrarakṣau tu pāñcālyau yudhāmanyūttamaujasau ,nārjunaṁ jahaturyuddhe pālyamānau kirīṭinā.
english: The two Pāñcālas, Yudhāmanyu and Uttamaujas, who served as protectors of the chariot wheels, truly did not abandon Arjuna in battle, even though they were being protected by the diadem-wearer (Arjuna).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 7 Verse 30. The two Pāñcālas, Yudhāmanyu and Uttamaujas, who served as protectors of the chariot wheels, truly did not abandon Arjuna in battle, even though they were being protected by the diadem-wearer (Arjuna).

sanskrit: शेषा नृपतयो वीराः स्थिता व्यूहस्य दंशिताः ।यथाभावं यथोत्साहं यथासत्त्वं च भारत ॥३१॥
transliteration: śeṣā nṛpatayo vīrāḥ sthitā vyūhasya daṁśitāḥ ,yathābhāvaṁ yathotsāhaṁ yathāsattvaṁ ca bhārata.
english: O Bhārata, the remaining valiant kings, armored, were positioned in the battle array, each according to their nature, their enthusiasm, and their inherent strength.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 7 Verse 31. O Bhārata, the remaining valiant kings, armored, were positioned in the battle array, each according to their nature, their enthusiasm, and their inherent strength.

sanskrit: एवमेतन्महाव्यूहं व्यूह्य भारत पाण्डवाः ।तावकाश्च महेष्वासा युद्धायैव मनो दधुः ॥३२॥
transliteration: evametanmahāvyūhaṁ vyūhya bhārata pāṇḍavāḥ ,tāvakāśca maheṣvāsā yuddhāyaiva mano dadhuḥ.
english: O Bhārata, having thus arranged this magnificent battle formation, both the Pāṇḍavas and your great archer (Kaurava) warriors indeed set their minds solely on battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 7 Verse 32. O Bhārata, having thus arranged this magnificent battle formation, both the Pāṇḍavas and your great archer (Kaurava) warriors indeed set their minds solely on battle.

sanskrit: दृष्ट्वा व्यूढां तव चमूं सूतपुत्रेण संयुगे ।निहतान्पाण्डवान्मेने तव पुत्रः सहान्वयः ॥३३॥
transliteration: dṛṣṭvā vyūḍhāṁ tava camūṁ sūtaputreṇa saṁyuge ,nihatānpāṇḍavānmene tava putraḥ sahānvayaḥ.
english: Having seen your army, arrayed for battle by Karṇa (sūtaputra), your son [Duryodhana], along with his followers, believed the Pāṇḍavas were already slain.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 7 Verse 33. Having seen your army, arrayed for battle by Karṇa (sūtaputra), your son [Duryodhana], along with his followers, believed the Pāṇḍavas were already slain.

sanskrit: तथैव पाण्डवीं सेनां व्यूढां दृष्ट्वा युधिष्ठिरः ।धार्तराष्ट्रान्हतान्मेने सकर्णान्वै जनाधिप ॥३४॥
transliteration: tathaiva pāṇḍavīṁ senāṁ vyūḍhāṁ dṛṣṭvā yudhiṣṭhiraḥ ,dhārtarāṣṭrānhatānmene sakarṇānvai janādhipa.
english: Similarly, O lord of people (janādhipa), Yudhishthira, upon seeing the Pāṇḍava army arrayed for battle, indeed believed that the sons of Dhritarashtra (dhārtarāṣṭra), along with Karṇa, were already slain.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 7 Verse 34. Similarly, O lord of people (janādhipa), Yudhishthira, upon seeing the Pāṇḍava army arrayed for battle, indeed believed that the sons of Dhritarashtra (dhārtarāṣṭra), along with Karṇa, were already slain.

sanskrit: ततः शङ्खाश्च भेर्यश्च पणवानकगोमुखाः ।सहसैवाभ्यहन्यन्त सशब्दाश्च समन्ततः ॥३५॥
transliteration: tataḥ śaṅkhāśca bheryaśca paṇavānakagomukhāḥ ,sahasaivābhyahanyanta saśabdāśca samantataḥ.
english: Then, conches, kettle-drums, tabors, large drums (anakas), and cow-horn trumpets (gomukhas) were suddenly sounded from all directions with great noise.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 7 Verse 35. Then, conches, kettle-drums, tabors, large drums (anakas), and cow-horn trumpets (gomukhas) were suddenly sounded from all directions with great noise.

sanskrit: सेनयोरुभयो राजन्प्रावाद्यन्त महास्वनाः ।सिंहनादश्च संजज्ञे शूराणां जयगृद्धिनाम् ॥३६॥
transliteration: senayorubhayo rājanprāvādyanta mahāsvanāḥ ,siṁhanādaśca saṁjajñe śūrāṇāṁ jayagṛddhinām.
english: O King (rājan), great sounds were made in both armies, and a lion's roar (siṃhanāda) arose from the heroes who were eager for victory.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 7 Verse 36. O King (rājan), great sounds were made in both armies, and a lion's roar (siṃhanāda) arose from the heroes who were eager for victory.

sanskrit: हयहेषितशब्दाश्च वारणानां च बृंहितम् ।रथनेमिस्वनाश्चोग्राः संबभूवुर्जनाधिप ॥३७॥
transliteration: hayaheṣitaśabdāśca vāraṇānāṁ ca bṛṁhitam ,rathanemisvanāścogrāḥ saṁbabhūvurjanādhipa.
english: O lord of men, the sounds of horses neighing, the trumpeting of elephants, and the terrible sounds of chariot wheels arose.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 7 Verse 37. O lord of men, the sounds of horses neighing, the trumpeting of elephants, and the terrible sounds of chariot wheels arose.

sanskrit: न द्रोणव्यसनं कश्चिज्जानीते भरतर्षभ ।दृष्ट्वा कर्णं महेष्वासं मुखे व्यूहस्य दंशितम् ॥३८॥
transliteration: na droṇavyasanaṁ kaścijjānīte bharatarṣabha ,dṛṣṭvā karṇaṁ maheṣvāsaṁ mukhe vyūhasya daṁśitam.
english: O best among the Bharatas, no one knows Drona's distress, having seen Karna, the great archer, armored and positioned at the front of the battle formation.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 7 Verse 38. O best among the Bharatas, no one knows Drona's distress, having seen Karna, the great archer, armored and positioned at the front of the battle formation.

sanskrit: उभे सेने महासत्त्वे प्रहृष्टनरकुञ्जरे ।योद्धुकामे स्थिते राजन्हन्तुमन्योन्यमञ्जसा ॥३९॥
transliteration: ubhe sene mahāsattve prahṛṣṭanarakuñjare ,yoddhukāme sthite rājanhantumanyonyamañjasā.
english: O King, both mighty armies, with jubilant men and elephants, stood eager to fight, ready to quickly kill each other.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 7 Verse 39. O King, both mighty armies, with jubilant men and elephants, stood eager to fight, ready to quickly kill each other.

sanskrit: तत्र यत्तौ सुसंरब्धौ दृष्ट्वान्योन्यं व्यवस्थितौ ।अनीकमध्ये राजेन्द्र रेजतुः कर्णपाण्डवौ ॥४०॥
transliteration: tatra yattau susaṁrabdhau dṛṣṭvānyonyaṁ vyavasthitau ,anīkamadhye rājendra rejatuḥ karṇapāṇḍavau.
english: O King of kings, having seen each other arrayed in the midst of the army, Karna and the Pāṇḍava (Arjuna), both prepared and extremely furious, shone brightly.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 7 Verse 40. O King of kings, having seen each other arrayed in the midst of the army, Karna and the Pāṇḍava (Arjuna), both prepared and extremely furious, shone brightly.

sanskrit: नृत्यमाने तु ते सेने समेयातां परस्परम् ।तयोः पक्षैः प्रपक्षैश्च निर्जग्मुर्वै युयुत्सवः ॥४१॥
transliteration: nṛtyamāne tu te sene sameyātāṁ parasparam ,tayoḥ pakṣaiḥ prapakṣaiśca nirjagmurvai yuyutsavaḥ.
english: As those two moving armies met each other, warriors eager to fight certainly sallied forth from their flanks and extended wings.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 7 Verse 41. As those two moving armies met each other, warriors eager to fight certainly sallied forth from their flanks and extended wings.

sanskrit: ततः प्रववृते युद्धं नरवारणवाजिनाम् ।रथिनां च महाराज अन्योन्यं निघ्नतां दृढम् ॥४२॥
transliteration: tataḥ pravavṛte yuddhaṁ naravāraṇavājinām ,rathināṁ ca mahārāja anyonyaṁ nighnatāṁ dṛḍham.
english: Then, O great king, a fierce battle began among men, elephants, horses, and charioteers, as they strongly struck one another.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 7 Verse 42. Then, O great king, a fierce battle began among men, elephants, horses, and charioteers, as they strongly struck one another.

sanskrit: संजय उवाच ।ते सेनेऽन्योन्यमासाद्य प्रहृष्टाश्वनरद्विपे ।बृहत्यौ संप्रजह्राते देवासुरचमूपमे ॥१॥
transliteration: saṁjaya uvāca ,te sene'nyonyamāsādya prahṛṣṭāśvanaradvipe ,bṛhatyau saṁprajahrāte devāsuracamūpame.
english: Sanjaya said: Having engaged each other, those two vast armies, whose horses, men, and elephants were greatly exhilarated, clashed like the forces of gods and asuras.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 8 Verse 1. Sanjaya said: Having engaged each other, those two vast armies, whose horses, men, and elephants were greatly exhilarated, clashed like the forces of gods and asuras.

sanskrit: ततो गजा रथाश्चाश्वाः पत्तयश्च महाहवे ।संप्रहारं परं चक्रुर्देहपाप्मप्रणाशनम् ॥२॥
transliteration: tato gajā rathāścāśvāḥ pattayaśca mahāhave ,saṁprahāraṁ paraṁ cakrurdehapāpmapraṇāśanam.
english: Then, in that great battle, elephants, chariots, horses, and foot soldiers executed a supreme assault, which obliterated bodies and dissolved accumulated demerit (pāpma).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 8 Verse 2. Then, in that great battle, elephants, chariots, horses, and foot soldiers executed a supreme assault, which obliterated bodies and dissolved accumulated demerit (pāpma).

sanskrit: पूर्णचन्द्रार्कपद्मानां कान्तित्विड्गन्धतः समैः ।उत्तमाङ्गैर्नृसिंहानां नृसिंहास्तस्तरुर्महीम् ॥३॥
transliteration: pūrṇacandrārkapadmānāṁ kāntitviḍgandhataḥ samaiḥ ,uttamāṅgairnṛsiṁhānāṁ nṛsiṁhāstastarurmahīm.
english: The Narasimhas covered the earth with the severed heads of other man-lions, heads that rivaled the full moon, the sun, and lotuses in their radiance, luster, and fragrance.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 8 Verse 3. The Narasimhas covered the earth with the severed heads of other man-lions, heads that rivaled the full moon, the sun, and lotuses in their radiance, luster, and fragrance.

sanskrit: अर्धचन्द्रैस्तथा भल्लैः क्षुरप्रैरसिपट्टिशैः ।परश्वधैश्चाप्यकृन्तन्नुत्तमाङ्गानि युध्यताम् ॥४॥
transliteration: ardhacandraistathā bhallaiḥ kṣuraprairasipaṭṭiśaiḥ ,paraśvadhaiścāpyakṛntannuttamāṅgāni yudhyatām.
english: With half-moon arrows, barbed spears, razor-sharp arrows, swords, broadswords, and also battle-axes, they severed the heads of those engaged in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 8 Verse 4. With half-moon arrows, barbed spears, razor-sharp arrows, swords, broadswords, and also battle-axes, they severed the heads of those engaged in battle.

sanskrit: व्यायतायतबाहूनां व्यायतायतबाहुभिः ।व्यायता बाहवः पेतुश्छिन्नमुष्ट्यायुधाङ्गदाः ॥५॥
transliteration: vyāyatāyatabāhūnāṁ vyāyatāyatabāhubhiḥ ,vyāyatā bāhavaḥ petuśchinnamuṣṭyāyudhāṅgadāḥ.
english: The immensely long and outstretched arms of the warriors, themselves possessing immensely long and outstretched arms, fell to the ground, accompanied by other similarly long and outstretched arms, with their weapon-gripping fists and armlets severed.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 8 Verse 5. The immensely long and outstretched arms of the warriors, themselves possessing immensely long and outstretched arms, fell to the ground, accompanied by other similarly long and outstretched arms, with their weapon-gripping fists and armlets severed.

sanskrit: तैः स्फुरद्भिर्मही भाति रक्ताङ्गुलितलैस्तदा ।गरुडप्रहतैरुग्रैः पञ्चास्यैरिव पन्नगैः ॥६॥
transliteration: taiḥ sphuradbhirmahī bhāti raktāṅgulitalaistadā ,garuḍaprahatairugraiḥ pañcāsyairiva pannagaiḥ.
english: At that time, the earth appeared radiant with those quivering (arms) whose finger-palms were red, resembling fierce, five-headed serpents struck down by Garuda.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 8 Verse 6. At that time, the earth appeared radiant with those quivering (arms) whose finger-palms were red, resembling fierce, five-headed serpents struck down by Garuda.

sanskrit: हयस्यन्दननागेभ्यः पेतुर्वीरा द्विषद्धताः ।विमानेभ्यो यथा क्षीणे पुण्ये स्वर्गसदस्तथा ॥७॥
transliteration: hayasyandananāgebhyaḥ peturvīrā dviṣaddhatāḥ ,vimānebhyo yathā kṣīṇe puṇye svargasadastathā.
english: Warriors, struck down by their foes, fell from horses, chariots, and elephants, just as the dwellers of heaven fall from their celestial conveyances when their acquired merit (puṇya) is exhausted.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 8 Verse 7. Warriors, struck down by their foes, fell from horses, chariots, and elephants, just as the dwellers of heaven fall from their celestial conveyances when their acquired merit (puṇya) is exhausted.

sanskrit: गदाभिरन्यैर्गुर्वीभिः परिघैर्मुसलैरपि ।पोथिताः शतशः पेतुर्वीरा वीरतरै रणे ॥८॥
transliteration: gadābhiranyairgurvībhiḥ parighairmusalairapi ,pothitāḥ śataśaḥ peturvīrā vīratarai raṇe.
english: In battle, hundreds of warriors, struck down by maces, other heavy iron clubs, and pestles, fell, slain by their more valiant adversaries.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 8 Verse 8. In battle, hundreds of warriors, struck down by maces, other heavy iron clubs, and pestles, fell, slain by their more valiant adversaries.

sanskrit: रथा रथैर्विनिहता मत्ता मत्तैर्द्विपैर्द्विपाः ।सादिनः सादिभिश्चैव तस्मिन्परमसंकुले ॥९॥
transliteration: rathā rathairvinihatā mattā mattairdvipairdvipāḥ ,sādinaḥ sādibhiścaiva tasminparamasaṁkule.
english: In that extremely tumultuous battle, chariots were destroyed by chariots, maddened elephants by maddened elephants, and indeed, horsemen by horsemen.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 8 Verse 9. In that extremely tumultuous battle, chariots were destroyed by chariots, maddened elephants by maddened elephants, and indeed, horsemen by horsemen.
