sanskrit: रथा वररथैर्नागैरश्वारोहाश्च पत्तिभिः ।अश्वारोहैः पदाताश्च निहता युधि शेरते ॥१०॥
transliteration: rathā vararathairnāgairaśvārohāśca pattibhiḥ ,aśvārohaiḥ padātāśca nihatā yudhi śerate.
english: In battle, chariots were destroyed by superior chariots; horsemen by elephants and foot soldiers; and foot soldiers by horsemen. All these, struck down, now lie on the ground.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 8 Verse 10. In battle, chariots were destroyed by superior chariots; horsemen by elephants and foot soldiers; and foot soldiers by horsemen. All these, struck down, now lie on the ground.

sanskrit: रथाश्वपत्तयो नागै रथैर्नागाश्च पत्तयः ।रथपत्तिद्विपाश्चाश्वैर्नृभिश्चाश्वरथद्विपाः ॥११॥
transliteration: rathāśvapattayo nāgai rathairnāgāśca pattayaḥ ,rathapattidvipāścāśvairnṛbhiścāśvarathadvipāḥ.
english: Chariots, horses, and foot soldiers were attacked by elephants; similarly, elephants and foot soldiers were attacked by chariots. Chariots, foot soldiers, and elephants were attacked by horses, and in turn, horses, chariots, and elephants were attacked by men.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 8 Verse 11. Chariots, horses, and foot soldiers were attacked by elephants; similarly, elephants and foot soldiers were attacked by chariots. Chariots, foot soldiers, and elephants were attacked by horses, and in turn, horses, chariots, and elephants were attacked by men.

sanskrit: रथाश्वेभनराणां च नराश्वेभरथैः कृतम् ।पाणिपादैश्च शस्त्रैश्च रथैश्च कदनं महत् ॥१२॥
transliteration: rathāśvebhanarāṇāṁ ca narāśvebharathaiḥ kṛtam ,pāṇipādaiśca śastraiśca rathaiśca kadanaṁ mahat.
english: A great slaughter (kadana) of chariots, horses, elephants, and men was brought about by men, horses, elephants, and chariots. This immense destruction was also caused by (bare) hands and feet, by weapons, and by chariots.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 8 Verse 12. A great slaughter (kadana) of chariots, horses, elephants, and men was brought about by men, horses, elephants, and chariots. This immense destruction was also caused by (bare) hands and feet, by weapons, and by chariots.

sanskrit: तथा तस्मिन्बले शूरैर्वध्यमाने हतेऽपि च ।अस्मानभ्यागमन्पार्था वृकोदरपुरोगमाः ॥१३॥
transliteration: tathā tasminbale śūrairvadhyamāne hate'pi ca ,asmānabhyāgamanpārthā vṛkodarapurogamāḥ.
english: Thus, even as that army was being fiercely attacked and killed by the heroes, the Pāṇḍavas (Pārthāḥ), led by Vṛkodara, advanced upon us.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 8 Verse 13. Thus, even as that army was being fiercely attacked and killed by the heroes, the Pāṇḍavas (Pārthāḥ), led by Vṛkodara, advanced upon us.

sanskrit: धृष्टद्युम्नः शिखण्डी च द्रौपदेयाः प्रभद्रकाः ।सात्यकिश्चेकितानश्च द्रविडैः सैनिकैः सह ॥१४॥
transliteration: dhṛṣṭadyumnaḥ śikhaṇḍī ca draupadeyāḥ prabhadrakāḥ ,sātyakiścekitānaśca draviḍaiḥ sainikaiḥ saha.
english: Dhṛṣṭadyumna, Śikhaṇḍī, the sons of Draupadī, and the Prabhadrakas were present; also Sātyaki and Cekitāna, accompanied by Draviḍa soldiers.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 8 Verse 14. Dhṛṣṭadyumna, Śikhaṇḍī, the sons of Draupadī, and the Prabhadrakas were present; also Sātyaki and Cekitāna, accompanied by Draviḍa soldiers.

sanskrit: भृता वित्तेन महता पाण्ड्याश्चौड्राः सकेरलाः ।व्यूढोरस्का दीर्घभुजाः प्रांशवः प्रियदर्शनाः ॥१५॥
transliteration: bhṛtā vittena mahatā pāṇḍyāścauḍrāḥ sakeralāḥ ,vyūḍhoraskā dīrghabhujāḥ prāṁśavaḥ priyadarśanāḥ.
english: The Pāṇḍyas, along with the Choḷas and Keralas, were supported by great wealth. They were broad-chested, long-armed, tall, and pleasant to behold.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 8 Verse 15. The Pāṇḍyas, along with the Choḷas and Keralas, were supported by great wealth. They were broad-chested, long-armed, tall, and pleasant to behold.

sanskrit: आपीडिनो रक्तदन्ता मत्तमातङ्गविक्रमाः ।नानाविरागवसना गन्धचूर्णावचूर्णिताः ॥१६॥
transliteration: āpīḍino raktadantā mattamātaṅgavikramāḥ ,nānāvirāgavasanā gandhacūrṇāvacūrṇitāḥ.
english: They wore turbans and had reddish teeth, displaying the prowess of intoxicated elephants. They were adorned in multi-colored garments and dusted with fragrant powders.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 8 Verse 16. They wore turbans and had reddish teeth, displaying the prowess of intoxicated elephants. They were adorned in multi-colored garments and dusted with fragrant powders.

sanskrit: बद्धासयः पाशहस्ता वारणप्रतिवारणाः ।समानमृत्यवो राजन्ननीकस्थाः परस्परम् ॥१७॥
transliteration: baddhāsayaḥ pāśahastā vāraṇaprativāraṇāḥ ,samānamṛtyavo rājannanīkasthāḥ parasparam.
english: O King, they were positioned in the army ranks, with their swords fastened and ropes in hand, capable of warding off elephants, and mutually resolved to die together.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 8 Verse 17. O King, they were positioned in the army ranks, with their swords fastened and ropes in hand, capable of warding off elephants, and mutually resolved to die together.

sanskrit: कलापिनश्चापहस्ता दीर्घकेशाः प्रियाहवाः ।पत्तयः सात्यकेरन्ध्रा घोररूपपराक्रमाः ॥१८॥
transliteration: kalāpinaścāpahastā dīrghakeśāḥ priyāhavāḥ ,pattayaḥ sātyakerandhrā ghorarūpaparākramāḥ.
english: They were foot-soldiers, wearing quivers and holding bows, long-haired, fond of battle, as strong as Satyaki with no vulnerabilities, and formidable in appearance and valor.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 8 Verse 18. They were foot-soldiers, wearing quivers and holding bows, long-haired, fond of battle, as strong as Satyaki with no vulnerabilities, and formidable in appearance and valor.

sanskrit: अथापरे पुनः शूराश्चेदिपाञ्चालकेकयाः ।करूषाः कोसलाः काश्या मागधाश्चापि दुद्रुवुः ॥१९॥
transliteration: athāpare punaḥ śūrāścedipāñcālakekayāḥ ,karūṣāḥ kosalāḥ kāśyā māgadhāścāpi dudruvuḥ.
english: Then, again, other brave warriors - the Chedis, Panchalas, Kekayas, Karushas, Kosalas, Kasyas, and Magadhas - also fled.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 8 Verse 19. Then, again, other brave warriors - the Chedis, Panchalas, Kekayas, Karushas, Kosalas, Kasyas, and Magadhas - also fled.

sanskrit: तेषां रथाश्च नागाश्च प्रवराश्चापि पत्तयः ।नानाविधरवैर्हृष्टा नृत्यन्ति च हसन्ति च ॥२०॥
transliteration: teṣāṁ rathāśca nāgāśca pravarāścāpi pattayaḥ ,nānāvidharavairhṛṣṭā nṛtyanti ca hasanti ca.
english: Their chariots, elephants, and indeed their foremost foot-soldiers, joyous with various sounds, both danced and laughed.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 8 Verse 20. Their chariots, elephants, and indeed their foremost foot-soldiers, joyous with various sounds, both danced and laughed.

sanskrit: तस्य सैन्यस्य महतो महामात्रवरैर्वृतः ।मध्यं वृकोदरोऽभ्यागात्त्वदीयं नागधूर्गतः ॥२१॥
transliteration: tasya sainyasya mahato mahāmātravarairvṛtaḥ ,madhyaṁ vṛkodaro'bhyāgāttvadīyaṁ nāgadhūrgataḥ.
english: Bhima (Vrikodara), accompanied by excellent elephant-drivers, approached the middle of that great army of yours, riding on an elephant's back.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 8 Verse 21. Bhima (Vrikodara), accompanied by excellent elephant-drivers, approached the middle of that great army of yours, riding on an elephant's back.

sanskrit: स नागप्रवरोऽत्युग्रो विधिवत्कल्पितो बभौ ।उदयाद्र्यग्र्यभवनं यथाभ्युदितभास्करम् ॥२२॥
transliteration: sa nāgapravaro'tyugro vidhivatkalpito babhau ,udayādryagryabhavanaṁ yathābhyuditabhāskaram.
english: That excellent elephant, exceedingly fierce and properly prepared, shone forth like a lofty peak of the rising mountain (Udayadri) upon which the sun has just risen.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 8 Verse 22. That excellent elephant, exceedingly fierce and properly prepared, shone forth like a lofty peak of the rising mountain (Udayadri) upon which the sun has just risen.

sanskrit: तस्यायसं वर्मवरं वररत्नविभूषितम् ।तारोद्भासस्य नभसः शारदस्य समत्विषम् ॥२३॥
transliteration: tasyāyasaṁ varmavaraṁ vararatnavibhūṣitam ,tārodbhāsasya nabhasaḥ śāradasya samatviṣam.
english: His excellent iron armor, adorned with splendid jewels, had a luster equal to that of the star-studded autumnal sky.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 8 Verse 23. His excellent iron armor, adorned with splendid jewels, had a luster equal to that of the star-studded autumnal sky.

sanskrit: स तोमरप्रासकरश्चारुमौलिः स्वलंकृतः ।चरन्मध्यंदिनार्काभस्तेजसा व्यदहद्रिपून् ॥२४॥
transliteration: sa tomaraprāsakaraścārumauliḥ svalaṁkṛtaḥ ,caranmadhyaṁdinārkābhastejasā vyadahadripūn.
english: Holding a javelin and spear, with a beautiful crest and well-adorned, he charged, burning his enemies with his fiery energy, like the midday sun.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 8 Verse 24. Holding a javelin and spear, with a beautiful crest and well-adorned, he charged, burning his enemies with his fiery energy, like the midday sun.

sanskrit: तं दृष्ट्वा द्विरदं दूरात्क्षेमधूर्तिर्द्विपस्थितः ।आह्वयानोऽभिदुद्राव प्रमनाः प्रमनस्तरम् ॥२५॥
transliteration: taṁ dṛṣṭvā dviradaṁ dūrātkṣemadhūrtirdvipasthitaḥ ,āhvayāno'bhidudrāva pramanāḥ pramanastaram.
english: Kṣemadhūrti, seated on his elephant, saw that (other) elephant from afar. Greatly excited, he challenged it and charged towards it with even greater eagerness.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 8 Verse 25. Kṣemadhūrti, seated on his elephant, saw that (other) elephant from afar. Greatly excited, he challenged it and charged towards it with even greater eagerness.

sanskrit: तयोः समभवद्युद्धं द्विपयोरुग्ररूपयोः ।यदृच्छया द्रुमवतोर्महापर्वतयोरिव ॥२६॥
transliteration: tayoḥ samabhavadyuddhaṁ dvipayorugrarūpayoḥ ,yadṛcchayā drumavatormahāparvatayoriva.
english: A battle occurred between those two elephants of fierce form, like two great tree-covered mountains meeting by chance.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 8 Verse 26. A battle occurred between those two elephants of fierce form, like two great tree-covered mountains meeting by chance.

sanskrit: संसक्तनागौ तौ वीरौ तोमरैरितरेतरम् ।बलवत्सूर्यरश्म्याभैर्भित्त्वा भित्त्वा विनेदतुः ॥२७॥
transliteration: saṁsaktanāgau tau vīrau tomarairitaretaram ,balavatsūryaraśmyābhairbhittvā bhittvā vinedatuḥ.
english: Those two heroes, grappling intensely like (fighting) elephants, repeatedly pierced each other with javelins that shone like powerful sunbeams, and then they roared.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 8 Verse 27. Those two heroes, grappling intensely like (fighting) elephants, repeatedly pierced each other with javelins that shone like powerful sunbeams, and then they roared.

sanskrit: व्यपसृत्य तु नागाभ्यां मण्डलानि विचेरतुः ।प्रगृह्य चैव धनुषी जघ्नतुर्वै परस्परम् ॥२८॥
transliteration: vyapasṛtya tu nāgābhyāṁ maṇḍalāni viceratuḥ ,pragṛhya caiva dhanuṣī jaghnaturvai parasparam.
english: But having disengaged (from their grapple) like elephants, they moved about in circular formations. And indeed, taking up their bows, they struck each other.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 8 Verse 28. But having disengaged (from their grapple) like elephants, they moved about in circular formations. And indeed, taking up their bows, they struck each other.

sanskrit: क्ष्वेडितास्फोटितरवैर्बाणशब्दैश्च सर्वशः ।तौ जनान्हर्षयित्वा च सिंहनादान्प्रचक्रतुः ॥२९॥
transliteration: kṣveḍitāsphoṭitaravairbāṇaśabdaiśca sarvaśaḥ ,tau janānharṣayitvā ca siṁhanādānpracakratuḥ.
english: With sounds of battle cries, bowstring slaps, and arrow noises from all directions, those two, having thrilled the people, then let out lion-roars.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 8 Verse 29. With sounds of battle cries, bowstring slaps, and arrow noises from all directions, those two, having thrilled the people, then let out lion-roars.

sanskrit: समुद्यतकराभ्यां तौ द्विपाभ्यां कृतिनावुभौ ।वातोद्धूतपताकाभ्यां युयुधाते महाबलौ ॥३०॥
transliteration: samudyatakarābhyāṁ tau dvipābhyāṁ kṛtināvubhau ,vātoddhūtapatākābhyāṁ yuyudhāte mahābalau.
english: Both those two mighty and skilled warriors fought, resembling two elephants with raised trunks and banners fluttering in the wind.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 8 Verse 30. Both those two mighty and skilled warriors fought, resembling two elephants with raised trunks and banners fluttering in the wind.

sanskrit: तावन्योन्यस्य धनुषी छित्त्वान्योन्यं विनेदतुः ।शक्तितोमरवर्षेण प्रावृण्मेघाविवाम्बुभिः ॥३१॥
transliteration: tāvanyonyasya dhanuṣī chittvānyonyaṁ vinedatuḥ ,śaktitomaravarṣeṇa prāvṛṇmeghāvivāmbubhiḥ.
english: Having cut each other's bows, the two warriors roared at one another, showering each other with javelins and lances, just like monsoon clouds pour down water.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 8 Verse 31. Having cut each other's bows, the two warriors roared at one another, showering each other with javelins and lances, just like monsoon clouds pour down water.

sanskrit: क्षेमधूर्तिस्तदा भीमं तोमरेण स्तनान्तरे ।निर्बिभेद तु वेगेन षड्भिश्चाप्यपरैर्नदन् ॥३२॥
transliteration: kṣemadhūrtistadā bhīmaṁ tomareṇa stanāntare ,nirbibheda tu vegena ṣaḍbhiścāpyaparairnadan.
english: Then, Kshemadhūrti, roaring, swiftly pierced Bhīma in the chest with a lance, and indeed, also with six other lances.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 8 Verse 32. Then, Kshemadhūrti, roaring, swiftly pierced Bhīma in the chest with a lance, and indeed, also with six other lances.

sanskrit: स भीमसेनः शुशुभे तोमरैरङ्गमाश्रितैः ।क्रोधदीप्तवपुर्मेघैः सप्तसप्तिरिवांशुमान् ॥३३॥
transliteration: sa bhīmasenaḥ śuśubhe tomarairaṅgamāśritaiḥ ,krodhadīptavapurmeghaiḥ saptasaptirivāṁśumān.
english: That Bhīmasena, his body inflamed with anger, shone splendidly with the lances stuck in his limbs, just as the radiant sun (aṃśumān) with its seven horses (saptasapti) appears amidst clouds.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 8 Verse 33. That Bhīmasena, his body inflamed with anger, shone splendidly with the lances stuck in his limbs, just as the radiant sun (aṃśumān) with its seven horses (saptasapti) appears amidst clouds.

sanskrit: ततो भास्करवर्णाभमञ्जोगतिमयस्मयम् ।ससर्ज तोमरं भीमः प्रत्यमित्राय यत्नवान् ॥३४॥
transliteration: tato bhāskaravarṇābhamañjogatimayasmayam ,sasarja tomaraṁ bhīmaḥ pratyamitrāya yatnavān.
english: Then, diligent Bhīma, exerting himself, discharged a swift-moving iron lance, brilliant like the color of the sun, towards his enemy.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 8 Verse 34. Then, diligent Bhīma, exerting himself, discharged a swift-moving iron lance, brilliant like the color of the sun, towards his enemy.

sanskrit: ततः कुलूताधिपतिश्चापमायम्य सायकैः ।दशभिस्तोमरं छित्त्वा शक्त्या विव्याध पाण्डवम् ॥३५॥
transliteration: tataḥ kulūtādhipatiścāpamāyamya sāyakaiḥ ,daśabhistomaraṁ chittvā śaktyā vivyādha pāṇḍavam.
english: Then, the lord of Kulūta, drawing his bow, cut down the javelin with ten arrows and struck the Pāṇḍava with a javelin (śakti).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 8 Verse 35. Then, the lord of Kulūta, drawing his bow, cut down the javelin with ten arrows and struck the Pāṇḍava with a javelin (śakti).

sanskrit: अथ कार्मुकमादाय महाजलदनिस्वनम् ।रिपोरभ्यर्दयन्नागमुन्मदः पाण्डवः शरैः ॥३६॥
transliteration: atha kārmukamādāya mahājaladanisvanam ,riporabhyardayannāgamunmadaḥ pāṇḍavaḥ śaraiḥ.
english: Then, taking up his bow which resounded like a great thundercloud, the enraged Pāṇḍava attacked the enemy's elephant with arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 8 Verse 36. Then, taking up his bow which resounded like a great thundercloud, the enraged Pāṇḍava attacked the enemy's elephant with arrows.

sanskrit: स शरौघार्दितो नागो भीमसेनेन संयुगे ।निगृह्यमाणो नातिष्ठद्वातध्वस्त इवाम्बुदः ॥३७॥
transliteration: sa śaraughārdito nāgo bhīmasenena saṁyuge ,nigṛhyamāṇo nātiṣṭhadvātadhvasta ivāmbudaḥ.
english: That elephant, afflicted by a volley of arrows from Bhīmasena in battle, was being overpowered and could not stand firm, just like a cloud scattered by the wind.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 8 Verse 37. That elephant, afflicted by a volley of arrows from Bhīmasena in battle, was being overpowered and could not stand firm, just like a cloud scattered by the wind.

sanskrit: तामभ्यधावद्द्विरदं भीमसेनस्य नागराट् ।महावातेरितं मेघं वातोद्धूत इवाम्बुदः ॥३८॥
transliteration: tāmabhyadhāvaddviradaṁ bhīmasenasya nāgarāṭ ,mahāvāteritaṁ meghaṁ vātoddhūta ivāmbudaḥ.
english: Bhīmasena's king of elephants charged towards that elephant. That elephant was like a great cloud (megha) driven by a mighty wind, while the king of elephants himself was like a cloud (ambuda) scattered by the wind.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 8 Verse 38. Bhīmasena's king of elephants charged towards that elephant. That elephant was like a great cloud (megha) driven by a mighty wind, while the king of elephants himself was like a cloud (ambuda) scattered by the wind.

sanskrit: संनिवर्त्यात्मनो नागं क्षेमधूर्तिः प्रयत्नतः ।विव्याधाभिद्रुतं बाणैर्भीमसेनं सकुञ्जरम् ॥३९॥
transliteration: saṁnivartyātmano nāgaṁ kṣemadhūrtiḥ prayatnataḥ ,vivyādhābhidrutaṁ bāṇairbhīmasenaṁ sakuñjaram.
english: Kshemadhurti, having carefully turned back his own elephant, then pierced Bhimasena, who was charging along with his elephant, with arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 8 Verse 39. Kshemadhurti, having carefully turned back his own elephant, then pierced Bhimasena, who was charging along with his elephant, with arrows.

sanskrit: ततः साधुविसृष्टेन क्षुरेण पुरुषर्षभः ।छित्त्वा शरासनं शत्रोर्नागमामित्रमार्दयत् ॥४०॥
transliteration: tataḥ sādhuvisṛṣṭena kṣureṇa puruṣarṣabhaḥ ,chittvā śarāsanaṁ śatrornāgamāmitramārdayat.
english: Then, that bull among men (Bhimasena), with a well-released razor-edged arrow, having cut the enemy's bow, crushed the enemy's elephant.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 8 Verse 40. Then, that bull among men (Bhimasena), with a well-released razor-edged arrow, having cut the enemy's bow, crushed the enemy's elephant.

sanskrit: ततः खजाकया भीमं क्षेमधूर्तिः पराभिनत् ।जघान चास्य द्विरदं नाराचैः सर्वमर्मसु ॥४१॥
transliteration: tataḥ khajākayā bhīmaṁ kṣemadhūrtiḥ parābhinat ,jaghāna cāsya dviradaṁ nārācaiḥ sarvamarmasu.
english: Then Kshemadhurti struck Bhima with a club, and he killed his (Bhima's) elephant with iron arrows in all its vital parts.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 8 Verse 41. Then Kshemadhurti struck Bhima with a club, and he killed his (Bhima's) elephant with iron arrows in all its vital parts.

sanskrit: पुरा नागस्य पतनादवप्लुत्य स्थितो महीम् ।भीमसेनो रिपोर्नागं गदया समपोथयत् ॥४२॥
transliteration: purā nāgasya patanādavaplutya sthito mahīm ,bhīmaseno ripornāgaṁ gadayā samapothayat.
english: Before his (Bhima's) elephant fell, Bhimasena jumped down and stood on the ground, then crushed the enemy's elephant with his mace.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 8 Verse 42. Before his (Bhima's) elephant fell, Bhimasena jumped down and stood on the ground, then crushed the enemy's elephant with his mace.

sanskrit: तस्मात्प्रमथितान्नागात्क्षेमधूर्तिमवद्रुतम् ।उद्यतासिमुपायान्तं गदयाहन्वृकोदरः ॥४३॥
transliteration: tasmātpramathitānnāgātkṣemadhūrtimavadrutam ,udyatāsimupāyāntaṁ gadayāhanvṛkodaraḥ.
english: From that agitated elephant, Vṛkodara (Bhīma) struck Kṣemadhūrti with a mace, as Kṣemadhūrti, having dismounted (from it), was approaching (him) with an upraised sword.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 8 Verse 43. From that agitated elephant, Vṛkodara (Bhīma) struck Kṣemadhūrti with a mace, as Kṣemadhūrti, having dismounted (from it), was approaching (him) with an upraised sword.

sanskrit: स पपात हतः सासिर्व्यसुः स्वमभितो द्विपम् ।वज्रप्ररुग्णमचलं सिंहो वज्रहतो यथा ॥४४॥
transliteration: sa papāta hataḥ sāsirvyasuḥ svamabhito dvipam ,vajraprarugṇamacalaṁ siṁho vajrahato yathā.
english: He, struck down, fell lifeless with his sword near his elephant, just as a lion struck by a thunderbolt falls near a mountain shattered by a thunderbolt.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 8 Verse 44. He, struck down, fell lifeless with his sword near his elephant, just as a lion struck by a thunderbolt falls near a mountain shattered by a thunderbolt.

sanskrit: निहतं नृपतिं दृष्ट्वा कुलूतानां यशस्करम् ।प्राद्रवद्व्यथिता सेना त्वदीया भरतर्षभ ॥४५॥
transliteration: nihataṁ nṛpatiṁ dṛṣṭvā kulūtānāṁ yaśaskaram ,prādravadvyathitā senā tvadīyā bharatarṣabha.
english: O best of Bharatas (bharatarṣabha), having seen the king of the Kulūtas - who was a bringer of fame - killed, your army, distressed, fled.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 8 Verse 45. O best of Bharatas (bharatarṣabha), having seen the king of the Kulūtas - who was a bringer of fame - killed, your army, distressed, fled.

sanskrit: संजय उवाच ।ततः कर्णो महेष्वासः पाण्डवानामनीकिनीम् ।जघान समरे शूरः शरैः संनतपर्वभिः ॥१॥
transliteration: saṁjaya uvāca ,tataḥ karṇo maheṣvāsaḥ pāṇḍavānāmanīkinīm ,jaghāna samare śūraḥ śaraiḥ saṁnataparvabhiḥ.
english: Sañjaya said: Then Karṇa, that great archer and brave hero, struck the army of the Pāṇḍavas in battle with his well-jointed arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 9 Verse 1. Sañjaya said: Then Karṇa, that great archer and brave hero, struck the army of the Pāṇḍavas in battle with his well-jointed arrows.

sanskrit: तथैव पाण्डवा राजंस्तव पुत्रस्य वाहिनीम् ।कर्णस्य प्रमुखे क्रुद्धा विनिजघ्नुर्महारथाः ॥२॥
transliteration: tathaiva pāṇḍavā rājaṁstava putrasya vāhinīm ,karṇasya pramukhe kruddhā vinijaghnurmahārathāḥ.
english: O King, similarly, the enraged great charioteers among the Pāṇḍavas also struck down your son's army, with Karṇa leading them.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 9 Verse 2. O King, similarly, the enraged great charioteers among the Pāṇḍavas also struck down your son's army, with Karṇa leading them.

sanskrit: कर्णो राजन्महाबाहुर्न्यवधीत्पाण्डवीं चमूम् ।नाराचैरर्करश्म्याभैः कर्मारपरिमार्जितैः ॥३॥
transliteration: karṇo rājanmahābāhurnyavadhītpāṇḍavīṁ camūm ,nārācairarkaraśmyābhaiḥ karmāraparimārjitaiḥ.
english: O King, the mighty-armed Karṇa slew the Pāṇḍava army with arrows that were like sunbeams and had been sharpened by blacksmiths.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 9 Verse 3. O King, the mighty-armed Karṇa slew the Pāṇḍava army with arrows that were like sunbeams and had been sharpened by blacksmiths.

sanskrit: तत्र भारत कर्णेन नाराचैस्ताडिता गजाः ।नेदुः सेदुश्च मम्लुश्च बभ्रमुश्च दिशो दश ॥४॥
transliteration: tatra bhārata karṇena nārācaistāḍitā gajāḥ ,neduḥ seduśca mamluśca babhramuśca diśo daśa.
english: O Bhārata, there, the elephants, struck by Karṇa's arrows, roared, sank down, fainted, and scattered in all ten directions.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 9 Verse 4. O Bhārata, there, the elephants, struck by Karṇa's arrows, roared, sank down, fainted, and scattered in all ten directions.

sanskrit: वध्यमाने बले तस्मिन्सूतपुत्रेण मारिष ।नकुलोऽभ्यद्रवत्तूर्णं सूतपुत्रं महारणे ॥५॥
transliteration: vadhyamāne bale tasminsūtaputreṇa māriṣa ,nakulo'bhyadravattūrṇaṁ sūtaputraṁ mahāraṇe.
english: O venerable one, while that army was being slaughtered by Karṇa (sūtaputra), Nakula swiftly rushed towards Karṇa (sūtaputra) in the great battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 9 Verse 5. O venerable one, while that army was being slaughtered by Karṇa (sūtaputra), Nakula swiftly rushed towards Karṇa (sūtaputra) in the great battle.

sanskrit: भीमसेनस्तथा द्रौणिं कुर्वाणं कर्म दुष्करम् ।विन्दानुविन्दौ कैकेयौ सात्यकिः समवारयत् ॥६॥
transliteration: bhīmasenastathā drauṇiṁ kurvāṇaṁ karma duṣkaram ,vindānuvindau kaikeyau sātyakiḥ samavārayat.
english: Bhīmasena and Sātyaki restrained Drouṇi, who was performing a difficult action (karma), along with Vinda and Anuvinda, the two Kaikeya princes.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 9 Verse 6. Bhīmasena and Sātyaki restrained Drouṇi, who was performing a difficult action (karma), along with Vinda and Anuvinda, the two Kaikeya princes.

sanskrit: श्रुतकर्माणमायान्तं चित्रसेनो महीपतिः ।प्रतिविन्ध्यं तथा चित्रश्चित्रकेतनकार्मुकः ॥७॥
transliteration: śrutakarmāṇamāyāntaṁ citraseno mahīpatiḥ ,prativindhyaṁ tathā citraścitraketanakārmukaḥ.
english: King Citrasena (faced) Śrutakarman as he approached; and similarly, Citra, with his splendid banner and bow, (faced) Prativindhya.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 9 Verse 7. King Citrasena (faced) Śrutakarman as he approached; and similarly, Citra, with his splendid banner and bow, (faced) Prativindhya.

sanskrit: दुर्योधनस्तु राजानं धर्मपुत्रं युधिष्ठिरम् ।संशप्तकगणान्क्रुद्धो अभ्यधावद्धनंजयः ॥८॥
transliteration: duryodhanastu rājānaṁ dharmaputraṁ yudhiṣṭhiram ,saṁśaptakagaṇānkruddho abhyadhāvaddhanaṁjayaḥ.
english: Duryodhana, however, (advanced upon) King Yudhishthira, the son of Dharma (dharma). Enraged Dhananjaya (Arjuna) rushed towards the multitudes of the Saṃśaptakas.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 9 Verse 8. Duryodhana, however, (advanced upon) King Yudhishthira, the son of Dharma (dharma). Enraged Dhananjaya (Arjuna) rushed towards the multitudes of the Saṃśaptakas.

sanskrit: धृष्टद्युम्नः कृपं चाथ तस्मिन्वीरवरक्षये ।शिखण्डी कृतवर्माणं समासादयदच्युतम् ॥९॥
transliteration: dhṛṣṭadyumnaḥ kṛpaṁ cātha tasminvīravarakṣaye ,śikhaṇḍī kṛtavarmāṇaṁ samāsādayadacyutam.
english: Now, in that great destruction of excellent heroes, Dhṛṣṭadyumna (encountered) Kṛpa, and Śikhaṇḍī attacked the unswerving Kṛtavarman.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 9 Verse 9. Now, in that great destruction of excellent heroes, Dhṛṣṭadyumna (encountered) Kṛpa, and Śikhaṇḍī attacked the unswerving Kṛtavarman.

sanskrit: श्रुतकीर्तिस्तथा शल्यं माद्रीपुत्रः सुतं तव ।दुःशासनं महाराज सहदेवः प्रतापवान् ॥१०॥
transliteration: śrutakīrtistathā śalyaṁ mādrīputraḥ sutaṁ tava ,duḥśāsanaṁ mahārāja sahadevaḥ pratāpavān.
english: O great king, Śrutakīrti engaged Śalya; similarly, a son of Mādrī engaged your son; and the mighty Sahadeva engaged Duḥśāsana.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 9 Verse 10. O great king, Śrutakīrti engaged Śalya; similarly, a son of Mādrī engaged your son; and the mighty Sahadeva engaged Duḥśāsana.

sanskrit: केकयौ सात्यकिं युद्धे शरवर्षेण भास्वता ।सात्यकिः केकयौ चैव छादयामास भारत ॥११॥
transliteration: kekayau sātyakiṁ yuddhe śaravarṣeṇa bhāsvatā ,sātyakiḥ kekayau caiva chādayāmāsa bhārata.
english: O Bhārata, the two Kekaya warriors, with a brilliant shower of arrows, [attacked] Sātyaki in battle; and Sātyaki, in turn, indeed covered the two Kekayas (with his arrows).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 9 Verse 11. O Bhārata, the two Kekaya warriors, with a brilliant shower of arrows, [attacked] Sātyaki in battle; and Sātyaki, in turn, indeed covered the two Kekayas (with his arrows).

sanskrit: तावेनं भ्रातरौ वीरं जघ्नतुर्हृदये भृशम् ।विषाणाभ्यां यथा नागौ प्रतिनागं महाहवे ॥१२॥
transliteration: tāvenaṁ bhrātarau vīraṁ jaghnaturhṛdaye bhṛśam ,viṣāṇābhyāṁ yathā nāgau pratināgaṁ mahāhave.
english: Just as two elephants, with their tusks, fiercely strike an opposing elephant in a great battle, similarly, those two brothers (the Kekaya warriors) severely struck this hero (Sātyaki) in the heart.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 9 Verse 12. Just as two elephants, with their tusks, fiercely strike an opposing elephant in a great battle, similarly, those two brothers (the Kekaya warriors) severely struck this hero (Sātyaki) in the heart.

sanskrit: शरसंभिन्नवर्माणौ तावुभौ भ्रातरौ रणे ।सात्यकिं सत्यकर्माणं राजन्विव्यधतुः शरैः ॥१३॥
transliteration: śarasaṁbhinnavarmāṇau tāvubhau bhrātarau raṇe ,sātyakiṁ satyakarmāṇaṁ rājanvivyadhatuḥ śaraiḥ.
english: O king, those two brothers, both of whom had their armors pierced by arrows in battle, then pierced Sātyaki, who was true to his actions (karma), with [their own] arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 9 Verse 13. O king, those two brothers, both of whom had their armors pierced by arrows in battle, then pierced Sātyaki, who was true to his actions (karma), with [their own] arrows.

sanskrit: तौ सात्यकिर्महाराज प्रहसन्सर्वतोदिशम् ।छादयञ्शरवर्षेण वारयामास भारत ॥१४॥
transliteration: tau sātyakirmahārāja prahasansarvatodiśam ,chādayañśaravarṣeṇa vārayāmāsa bhārata.
english: O great king, O Bharata, Satyaki, laughing, stopped those two, overwhelming them from all directions with a rain of arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 9 Verse 14. O great king, O Bharata, Satyaki, laughing, stopped those two, overwhelming them from all directions with a rain of arrows.

sanskrit: वार्यमाणौ ततस्तौ तु शैनेयशरवृष्टिभिः ।शैनेयस्य रथं तूर्णं छादयामासतुः शरैः ॥१५॥
transliteration: vāryamāṇau tatastau tu śaineyaśaravṛṣṭibhiḥ ,śaineyasya rathaṁ tūrṇaṁ chādayāmāsatuḥ śaraiḥ.
english: Although those two were being restrained by showers of arrows from Shaineya (Satyaki), they quickly covered Shaineya's chariot with their own arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 9 Verse 15. Although those two were being restrained by showers of arrows from Shaineya (Satyaki), they quickly covered Shaineya's chariot with their own arrows.

sanskrit: तयोस्तु धनुषी चित्रे छित्त्वा शौरिर्महाहवे ।अथ तौ सायकैस्तीक्ष्णैश्छादयामास दुःसहैः ॥१६॥
transliteration: tayostu dhanuṣī citre chittvā śaurirmahāhave ,atha tau sāyakaistīkṣṇaiśchādayāmāsa duḥsahaiḥ.
english: But then, in that great battle, Shauri (Satyaki), having cut the two splendid bows of those two, overwhelmed them with sharp, unendurable arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 9 Verse 16. But then, in that great battle, Shauri (Satyaki), having cut the two splendid bows of those two, overwhelmed them with sharp, unendurable arrows.

sanskrit: अथान्ये धनुषी मृष्टे प्रगृह्य च महाशरान् ।सात्यकिं पूरयन्तौ तौ चेरतुर्लघु सुष्ठु च ॥१७॥
transliteration: athānye dhanuṣī mṛṣṭe pragṛhya ca mahāśarān ,sātyakiṁ pūrayantau tau ceraturlaghu suṣṭhu ca.
english: Then, those two, having taken up other splendid bows and great arrows, advanced towards Satyaki, overwhelming him, and fought very swiftly and skillfully.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 9 Verse 17. Then, those two, having taken up other splendid bows and great arrows, advanced towards Satyaki, overwhelming him, and fought very swiftly and skillfully.

sanskrit: ताभ्यां मुक्ता महाबाणाः कङ्कबर्हिणवाससः ।द्योतयन्तो दिशः सर्वाः संपेतुः स्वर्णभूषणाः ॥१८॥
transliteration: tābhyāṁ muktā mahābāṇāḥ kaṅkabarhiṇavāsasaḥ ,dyotayanto diśaḥ sarvāḥ saṁpetuḥ svarṇabhūṣaṇāḥ.
english: Great arrows, released by those two, flew forth, adorned with gold and feathered with heron and peacock plumes, illuminating all directions.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 9 Verse 18. Great arrows, released by those two, flew forth, adorned with gold and feathered with heron and peacock plumes, illuminating all directions.

sanskrit: बाणान्धकारमभवत्तयो राजन्महाहवे ।अन्योन्यस्य धनुश्चैव चिच्छिदुस्ते महारथाः ॥१९॥
transliteration: bāṇāndhakāramabhavattayo rājanmahāhave ,anyonyasya dhanuścaiva cicchiduste mahārathāḥ.
english: O King, in that great battle, a darkness of arrows arose between those two. Indeed, those mighty warriors (mahāratha) cut down each other's bows.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 9 Verse 19. O King, in that great battle, a darkness of arrows arose between those two. Indeed, those mighty warriors (mahāratha) cut down each other's bows.

sanskrit: ततः क्रुद्धो महाराज सात्वतो युद्धदुर्मदः ।धनुरन्यत्समादाय सज्यं कृत्वा च संयुगे ।क्षुरप्रेण सुतीक्ष्णेन अनुविन्दशिरोऽहरत् ॥२०॥
transliteration: tataḥ kruddho mahārāja sātvato yuddhadurmadaḥ ,dhanuranyatsamādāya sajyaṁ kṛtvā ca saṁyuge ,kṣurapreṇa sutīkṣṇena anuvindaśiro'harat.
english: Then, O great king, Sātvata, enraged and invincible in battle, took up another bow, strung it in the engagement, and with a very sharp razor-arrow, cut off Anuvinda's head.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 9 Verse 20. Then, O great king, Sātvata, enraged and invincible in battle, took up another bow, strung it in the engagement, and with a very sharp razor-arrow, cut off Anuvinda's head.

sanskrit: तच्छिरो न्यपतद्भूमौ कुण्डलोत्पीडितं महत् ।शम्बरस्य शिरो यद्वन्निहतस्य महारणे ।शोषयन्केकयान्सर्वाञ्जगामाशु वसुंधराम् ॥२१॥
transliteration: tacchiro nyapatadbhūmau kuṇḍalotpīḍitaṁ mahat ,śambarasya śiro yadvannihatasya mahāraṇe ,śoṣayankekayānsarvāñjagāmāśu vasuṁdharām.
english: That great head, adorned with earrings, fell upon the earth, just as the head of Śambara, when he was slain in a great battle, making all the Kekayas despondent, quickly reached the ground.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 9 Verse 21. That great head, adorned with earrings, fell upon the earth, just as the head of Śambara, when he was slain in a great battle, making all the Kekayas despondent, quickly reached the ground.

sanskrit: तं दृष्ट्वा निहतं शूरं भ्राता तस्य महारथः ।सज्यमन्यद्धनुः कृत्वा शैनेयं प्रत्यवारयत् ॥२२॥
transliteration: taṁ dṛṣṭvā nihataṁ śūraṁ bhrātā tasya mahārathaḥ ,sajyamanyaddhanuḥ kṛtvā śaineyaṁ pratyavārayat.
english: When his brother, a great charioteer, saw that hero slain, he strung another bow and repelled Saṁneya (Satyaki).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 9 Verse 22. When his brother, a great charioteer, saw that hero slain, he strung another bow and repelled Saṁneya (Satyaki).

sanskrit: स शक्त्या सात्यकिं विद्ध्वा स्वर्णपुङ्खैः शिलाशितैः ।ननाद बलवन्नादं तिष्ठ तिष्ठेति चाब्रवीत् ॥२३॥
transliteration: sa śaktyā sātyakiṁ viddhvā svarṇapuṅkhaiḥ śilāśitaiḥ ,nanāda balavannādaṁ tiṣṭha tiṣṭheti cābravīt.
english: Having pierced Satyaki with a spear (śakti) and with arrows that were gold-feathered and whetted on stone, he then let out a powerful roar and said, "Stop! Stop!"
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 9 Verse 23. Having pierced Satyaki with a spear (śakti) and with arrows that were gold-feathered and whetted on stone, he then let out a powerful roar and said, "Stop! Stop!"

sanskrit: स सात्यकिं पुनः क्रुद्धः केकयानां महारथः ।शरैरग्निशिखाकारैर्बाह्वोरुरसि चार्दयत् ॥२४॥
transliteration: sa sātyakiṁ punaḥ kruddhaḥ kekayānāṁ mahārathaḥ ,śarairagniśikhākārairbāhvorurasi cārdayat.
english: That great charioteer of the Kekayas, enraged, again afflicted Satyaki on his arms and chest with arrows shaped like flames of fire.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 9 Verse 24. That great charioteer of the Kekayas, enraged, again afflicted Satyaki on his arms and chest with arrows shaped like flames of fire.

sanskrit: स शरैः क्षतसर्वाङ्गः सात्वतः सत्त्वकोविदः ।रराज समरे राजन्सपत्र इव किंशुकः ॥२५॥
transliteration: sa śaraiḥ kṣatasarvāṅgaḥ sātvataḥ sattvakovidaḥ ,rarāja samare rājansapatra iva kiṁśukaḥ.
english: O King, Satyaki, whose entire body was wounded by arrows and who was an expert in courage, shone brightly in battle like a kiṃśuka tree covered with leaves.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 9 Verse 25. O King, Satyaki, whose entire body was wounded by arrows and who was an expert in courage, shone brightly in battle like a kiṃśuka tree covered with leaves.

sanskrit: सात्यकिः समरे विद्धः केकयेन महात्मना ।केकयं पञ्चविंशत्या विव्याध प्रहसन्निव ॥२६॥
transliteration: sātyakiḥ samare viddhaḥ kekayena mahātmanā ,kekayaṁ pañcaviṁśatyā vivyādha prahasanniva.
english: Saatyaki, wounded in battle by the great-souled Kekaya, then struck Kekaya with twenty-five (arrows) as if laughing.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 9 Verse 26. Saatyaki, wounded in battle by the great-souled Kekaya, then struck Kekaya with twenty-five (arrows) as if laughing.

sanskrit: शतचन्द्रचिते गृह्य चर्मणी सुभुजौ तु तौ ।व्यरोचेतां महारङ्गे निस्त्रिंशवरधारिणौ ।यथा देवासुरे युद्धे जम्भशक्रौ महाबलौ ॥२७॥
transliteration: śatacandracite gṛhya carmaṇī subhujau tu tau ,vyarocetāṁ mahāraṅge nistriṁśavaradhāriṇau ,yathā devāsure yuddhe jambhaśakrau mahābalau.
english: Those two, with their handsome arms, carrying shields adorned with a hundred moons and wielding excellent swords, shone brilliantly in the great arena, just like the mighty Jambha and Indra in the battle between gods and demons.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 9 Verse 27. Those two, with their handsome arms, carrying shields adorned with a hundred moons and wielding excellent swords, shone brilliantly in the great arena, just like the mighty Jambha and Indra in the battle between gods and demons.

sanskrit: मण्डलानि ततस्तौ च विचरन्तौ महारणे ।अन्योन्यमसिभिस्तूर्णं समाजघ्नतुराहवे ॥२८॥
transliteration: maṇḍalāni tatastau ca vicarantau mahāraṇe ,anyonyamasibhistūrṇaṁ samājaghnaturāhave.
english: And then, those two, moving in circles in the great battle, quickly struck each other with swords in the conflict.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 9 Verse 28. And then, those two, moving in circles in the great battle, quickly struck each other with swords in the conflict.

sanskrit: केकयस्य ततश्चर्म द्विधा चिच्छेद सात्वतः ।सात्यकेश्च तथैवासौ चर्म चिच्छेद पार्थिवः ॥२९॥
transliteration: kekayasya tataścarma dvidhā ciccheda sātvataḥ ,sātyakeśca tathaivāsau carma ciccheda pārthivaḥ.
english: Then Saatyaki cut Kekaya's shield in two, and similarly, that king (Kekaya) cut Saatyaki's shield.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 9 Verse 29. Then Saatyaki cut Kekaya's shield in two, and similarly, that king (Kekaya) cut Saatyaki's shield.

sanskrit: चर्म च्छित्त्वा तु कैकेयस्तारागणशतैर्वृतम् ।चचार मण्डलान्येव गतप्रत्यागतानि च ॥३०॥
transliteration: carma cchittvā tu kaikeyastārāgaṇaśatairvṛtam ,cacāra maṇḍalānyeva gatapratyāgatāni ca.
english: And the King of Kekaya, having cut through a shield adorned with hundreds of star-like embellishments, moved in circles, both advancing and retreating.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 9 Verse 30. And the King of Kekaya, having cut through a shield adorned with hundreds of star-like embellishments, moved in circles, both advancing and retreating.

sanskrit: तं चरन्तं महारङ्गे निस्त्रिंशवरधारिणम् ।अपहस्तेन चिच्छेद शैनेयस्त्वरयान्वितः ॥३१॥
transliteration: taṁ carantaṁ mahāraṅge nistriṁśavaradhāriṇam ,apahastena ciccheda śaineyastvarayānvitaḥ.
english: Swiftly, Śaineya, wielding an excellent sword, cut him down with a downward stroke as he moved about the great battlefield.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 9 Verse 31. Swiftly, Śaineya, wielding an excellent sword, cut him down with a downward stroke as he moved about the great battlefield.

sanskrit: सवर्मा केकयो राजन्द्विधा छिन्नो महाहवे ।निपपात महेष्वासो वज्रनुन्न इवाचलः ॥३२॥
transliteration: savarmā kekayo rājandvidhā chinno mahāhave ,nipapāta maheṣvāso vajranunna ivācalaḥ.
english: O King, the Kekaya warrior, armor and all, was cut in two in the great battle. That great archer fell down like a mountain struck by a thunderbolt.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 9 Verse 32. O King, the Kekaya warrior, armor and all, was cut in two in the great battle. That great archer fell down like a mountain struck by a thunderbolt.

sanskrit: तं निहत्य रणे शूरः शैनेयो रथसत्तमः ।युधामन्यो रथं तूर्णमारुरोह परंतपः ॥३३॥
transliteration: taṁ nihatya raṇe śūraḥ śaineyo rathasattamaḥ ,yudhāmanyo rathaṁ tūrṇamāruroha paraṁtapaḥ.
english: Having struck him down in battle, the heroic Śaineya, that best of charioteers and tormentor of foes, swiftly mounted Yudhamanyu's chariot.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 9 Verse 33. Having struck him down in battle, the heroic Śaineya, that best of charioteers and tormentor of foes, swiftly mounted Yudhamanyu's chariot.

sanskrit: ततोऽन्यं रथमास्थाय विधिवत्कल्पितं पुनः ।केकयानां महत्सैन्यं व्यधमत्सात्यकिः शरैः ॥३४॥
transliteration: tato'nyaṁ rathamāsthāya vidhivatkalpitaṁ punaḥ ,kekayānāṁ mahatsainyaṁ vyadhamatsātyakiḥ śaraiḥ.
english: Then, mounting another chariot that had been duly prepared again, Sātyaki shattered the large army of the Kekayas with his arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 9 Verse 34. Then, mounting another chariot that had been duly prepared again, Sātyaki shattered the large army of the Kekayas with his arrows.

sanskrit: सा वध्यमाना समरे केकयस्य महाचमूः ।तमुत्सृज्य रथं शत्रुं प्रदुद्राव दिशो दश ॥३५॥
transliteration: sā vadhyamānā samare kekayasya mahācamūḥ ,tamutsṛjya rathaṁ śatruṁ pradudrāva diśo daśa.
english: That vast army of the Kekaya king, being slain in battle, abandoned that enemy (Sātyaki) and his chariot, and fled in all ten directions.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 9 Verse 35. That vast army of the Kekaya king, being slain in battle, abandoned that enemy (Sātyaki) and his chariot, and fled in all ten directions.

sanskrit: संजय उवाच ।श्रुतकर्मा महाराज चित्रसेनं महीपतिम् ।आजघ्ने समरे क्रुद्धः पञ्चाशद्भिः शिलीमुखैः ॥१॥
transliteration: saṁjaya uvāca ,śrutakarmā mahārāja citrasenaṁ mahīpatim ,ājaghne samare kruddhaḥ pañcāśadbhiḥ śilīmukhaiḥ.
english: Sañjaya said: O great king, Śrutakarmā, enraged, struck King Citrasena in battle with fifty arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 10 Verse 1. Sañjaya said: O great king, Śrutakarmā, enraged, struck King Citrasena in battle with fifty arrows.

sanskrit: अभिसारस्तु तं राजा नवभिर्निशितैः शरैः ।श्रुतकर्माणमाहत्य सूतं विव्याध पञ्चभिः ॥२॥
transliteration: abhisārastu taṁ rājā navabhirniśitaiḥ śaraiḥ ,śrutakarmāṇamāhatya sūtaṁ vivyādha pañcabhiḥ.
english: But King Abhisāra, having struck that Śrutakarmā with nine sharp arrows, then pierced his charioteer with five (arrows).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 10 Verse 2. But King Abhisāra, having struck that Śrutakarmā with nine sharp arrows, then pierced his charioteer with five (arrows).

sanskrit: श्रुतकर्मा ततः क्रुद्धश्चित्रसेनं चमूमुखे ।नाराचेन सुतीक्ष्णेन मर्मदेशे समर्दयत् ॥३॥
transliteration: śrutakarmā tataḥ kruddhaścitrasenaṁ camūmukhe ,nārācena sutīkṣṇena marmadeśe samardayat.
english: Then, Śrutakarmā, enraged, severely wounded Citrasena in a vital spot in the vanguard of the army with a very sharp iron arrow.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 10 Verse 3. Then, Śrutakarmā, enraged, severely wounded Citrasena in a vital spot in the vanguard of the army with a very sharp iron arrow.

sanskrit: एतस्मिन्नन्तरे चैनं श्रुतकीर्तिर्महायशाः ।नवत्या जगतीपालं छादयामास पत्रिभिः ॥४॥
transliteration: etasminnantare cainaṁ śrutakīrtirmahāyaśāḥ ,navatyā jagatīpālaṁ chādayāmāsa patribhiḥ.
english: And at that very moment, the highly renowned Śrutakīrti covered that protector of the earth with ninety arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 10 Verse 4. And at that very moment, the highly renowned Śrutakīrti covered that protector of the earth with ninety arrows.

sanskrit: प्रतिलभ्य ततः संज्ञां चित्रसेनो महारथः ।धनुश्चिच्छेद भल्लेन तं च विव्याध सप्तभिः ॥५॥
transliteration: pratilabhya tataḥ saṁjñāṁ citraseno mahārathaḥ ,dhanuściccheda bhallena taṁ ca vivyādha saptabhiḥ.
english: Then, having regained consciousness, Citrasena, the great charioteer, cut his (Śrutakīrti's) bow with a bhalla arrow and pierced him (Śrutakīrti) with seven arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 10 Verse 5. Then, having regained consciousness, Citrasena, the great charioteer, cut his (Śrutakīrti's) bow with a bhalla arrow and pierced him (Śrutakīrti) with seven arrows.

sanskrit: सोऽन्यत्कार्मुकमादाय वेगघ्नं रुक्मभूषणम् ।चित्ररूपतरं चक्रे चित्रसेनं शरोर्मिभिः ॥६॥
transliteration: so'nyatkārmukamādāya vegaghnaṁ rukmabhūṣaṇam ,citrarūpataraṁ cakre citrasenaṁ śarormibhiḥ.
english: That one (Śrutakīrti), taking another golden-ornamented bow that destroys speed, made Citrasena appear even more wonderfully diverse (in appearance) with his waves of arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 10 Verse 6. That one (Śrutakīrti), taking another golden-ornamented bow that destroys speed, made Citrasena appear even more wonderfully diverse (in appearance) with his waves of arrows.

sanskrit: स शरैश्चित्रितो राजंश्चित्रमाल्यधरो युवा ।युवेव समशोभत्स गोष्ठीमध्ये स्वलंकृतः ॥७॥
transliteration: sa śaraiścitrito rājaṁścitramālyadharo yuvā ,yuveva samaśobhatsa goṣṭhīmadhye svalaṁkṛtaḥ.
english: O King, he, a young man adorned with arrows and wearing a variegated garland, shone splendidly in the midst of the assembly, just like a well-decorated youth.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 10 Verse 7. O King, he, a young man adorned with arrows and wearing a variegated garland, shone splendidly in the midst of the assembly, just like a well-decorated youth.

sanskrit: श्रुतकर्माणमथ वै नाराचेन स्तनान्तरे ।बिभेद समरे क्रुद्धस्तिष्ठ तिष्ठेति चाब्रवीत् ॥८॥
transliteration: śrutakarmāṇamatha vai nārācena stanāntare ,bibheda samare kruddhastiṣṭha tiṣṭheti cābravīt.
english: Then, enraged in battle, he pierced Śrutakarman in the chest with an iron arrow, and exclaimed, 'Stop! Stop!'
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 10 Verse 8. Then, enraged in battle, he pierced Śrutakarman in the chest with an iron arrow, and exclaimed, 'Stop! Stop!'

sanskrit: श्रुतकर्मापि समरे नाराचेन समर्दितः ।सुस्राव रुधिरं भूरि गैरिकाम्भ इवाचलः ॥९॥
transliteration: śrutakarmāpi samare nārācena samarditaḥ ,susrāva rudhiraṁ bhūri gairikāmbha ivācalaḥ.
english: Even Śrutakarman, struck by an iron arrow in battle, profusely shed blood, just like a mountain from which ochre-colored water flows.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 10 Verse 9. Even Śrutakarman, struck by an iron arrow in battle, profusely shed blood, just like a mountain from which ochre-colored water flows.

sanskrit: ततः स रुधिराक्ताङ्गो रुधिरेण कृतच्छविः ।रराज समरे राजन्सपुष्प इव किंशुकः ॥१०॥
transliteration: tataḥ sa rudhirāktāṅgo rudhireṇa kṛtacchaviḥ ,rarāja samare rājansapuṣpa iva kiṁśukaḥ.
english: Then, O King, his limbs smeared with blood, his appearance made glorious by it, he shone in battle like a kiṃśuka tree covered with flowers.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 10 Verse 10. Then, O King, his limbs smeared with blood, his appearance made glorious by it, he shone in battle like a kiṃśuka tree covered with flowers.

sanskrit: श्रुतकर्मा ततो राजञ्शत्रूणां समभिद्रुतः ।शत्रुसंवरणं कृत्वा द्विधा चिच्छेद कार्मुकम् ॥११॥
transliteration: śrutakarmā tato rājañśatrūṇāṁ samabhidrutaḥ ,śatrusaṁvaraṇaṁ kṛtvā dvidhā ciccheda kārmukam.
english: O king, then Śrutakarman, being assailed by his enemies, created a barrier (śatrusaṃvaraṇam) to protect himself and cut their bow into two parts.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 10 Verse 11. O king, then Śrutakarman, being assailed by his enemies, created a barrier (śatrusaṃvaraṇam) to protect himself and cut their bow into two parts.

sanskrit: अथैनं छिन्नधन्वानं नाराचानां त्रिभिः शतैः ।विव्याध भरतश्रेष्ठ श्रुतकर्मा महायशाः ॥१२॥
transliteration: athainaṁ chinnadhanvānaṁ nārācānāṁ tribhiḥ śataiḥ ,vivyādha bharataśreṣṭha śrutakarmā mahāyaśāḥ.
english: O best among the Bhāratas, then the greatly renowned Śrutakarman pierced him, whose bow was cut, with three hundred arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 10 Verse 12. O best among the Bhāratas, then the greatly renowned Śrutakarman pierced him, whose bow was cut, with three hundred arrows.

sanskrit: ततोऽपरेण भल्लेन भृशं तीक्ष्णेन सत्वरः ।जहार सशिरस्त्राणं शिरस्तस्य महात्मनः ॥१३॥
transliteration: tato'pareṇa bhallena bhṛśaṁ tīkṣṇena satvaraḥ ,jahāra saśirastrāṇaṁ śirastasya mahātmanaḥ.
english: Then, swiftly, with another very sharp broad-headed arrow (bhalla), he took away the head, along with its helmet, of that great-souled one (mahātman).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 10 Verse 13. Then, swiftly, with another very sharp broad-headed arrow (bhalla), he took away the head, along with its helmet, of that great-souled one (mahātman).

sanskrit: तच्छिरो न्यपतद्भूमौ सुमहच्चित्रवर्मणः ।यदृच्छया यथा चन्द्रश्च्युतः स्वर्गान्महीतले ॥१४॥
transliteration: tacchiro nyapatadbhūmau sumahaccitravarmaṇaḥ ,yadṛcchayā yathā candraścyutaḥ svargānmahītale.
english: That very great head of Chitravardhan fell to the ground, just as the moon falls to the earth by chance from heaven.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 10 Verse 14. That very great head of Chitravardhan fell to the ground, just as the moon falls to the earth by chance from heaven.

sanskrit: राजानं निहतं दृष्ट्वा अभिसारं च मारिष ।अभ्यद्रवन्त वेगेन चित्रसेनस्य सैनिकाः ॥१५॥
transliteration: rājānaṁ nihataṁ dṛṣṭvā abhisāraṁ ca māriṣa ,abhyadravanta vegena citrasenasya sainikāḥ.
english: O venerable one, having seen the king struck down and the [enemy's] assault, Citrasena's soldiers rushed forward swiftly.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 10 Verse 15. O venerable one, having seen the king struck down and the [enemy's] assault, Citrasena's soldiers rushed forward swiftly.

sanskrit: ततः क्रुद्धो महेष्वासस्तत्सैन्यं प्राद्रवच्छरैः ।अन्तकाले यथा क्रुद्धः सर्वभूतानि प्रेतराट् ।द्रावयन्निषुभिस्तूर्णं श्रुतकर्मा व्यरोचत ॥१६॥
transliteration: tataḥ kruddho maheṣvāsastatsainyaṁ prādravaccharaiḥ ,antakāle yathā kruddhaḥ sarvabhūtāni pretarāṭ ,drāvayanniṣubhistūrṇaṁ śrutakarmā vyarocata.
english: Then, enraged, the great archer (maheṣvāsa) attacked that army with arrows. Just as the enraged lord of spirits (pretarāṭ) [Yama] at the time of dissolution (antakāla) disperses all beings (sarvabhūtāni), so Śrutakarmā shone brilliantly, quickly scattering [the foes] with his arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 10 Verse 16. Then, enraged, the great archer (maheṣvāsa) attacked that army with arrows. Just as the enraged lord of spirits (pretarāṭ) [Yama] at the time of dissolution (antakāla) disperses all beings (sarvabhūtāni), so Śrutakarmā shone brilliantly, quickly scattering [the foes] with his arrows.

sanskrit: प्रतिविन्ध्यस्ततश्चित्रं भित्त्वा पञ्चभिराशुगैः ।सारथिं त्रिभिरानर्च्छद्ध्वजमेकेषुणा ततः ॥१७॥
transliteration: prativindhyastataścitraṁ bhittvā pañcabhirāśugaiḥ ,sārathiṁ tribhirānarcchaddhvajamekeṣuṇā tataḥ.
english: Then Prativindhya, having pierced Citra with five swift arrows, then struck [his] charioteer with three [arrows] and [his] banner with one arrow.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 10 Verse 17. Then Prativindhya, having pierced Citra with five swift arrows, then struck [his] charioteer with three [arrows] and [his] banner with one arrow.

sanskrit: तं चित्रो नवभिर्भल्लैर्बाह्वोरुरसि चार्दयत् ।स्वर्णपुङ्खैः शिलाधौतैः कङ्कबर्हिणवाजितैः ॥१८॥
transliteration: taṁ citro navabhirbhallairbāhvorurasi cārdayat ,svarṇapuṅkhaiḥ śilādhautaiḥ kaṅkabarhiṇavājitaiḥ.
english: Citra then wounded him [Prativindhya] on both arms and on the chest with nine broad-headed arrows, which were golden-shafted, sharpened on stone, and adorned with heron and peacock feathers.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 10 Verse 18. Citra then wounded him [Prativindhya] on both arms and on the chest with nine broad-headed arrows, which were golden-shafted, sharpened on stone, and adorned with heron and peacock feathers.

sanskrit: प्रतिविन्ध्यो धनुस्तस्य छित्त्वा भारत सायकैः ।पञ्चभिर्निशितैर्बाणैरथैनं संप्रजघ्निवान् ॥१९॥
transliteration: prativindhyo dhanustasya chittvā bhārata sāyakaiḥ ,pañcabhirniśitairbāṇairathainaṁ saṁprajaghnivān.
english: O descendant of Bharata, Prativindhya, using his arrows, first cut his (opponent's) bow, and then struck him thoroughly with five sharp arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 10 Verse 19. O descendant of Bharata, Prativindhya, using his arrows, first cut his (opponent's) bow, and then struck him thoroughly with five sharp arrows.

sanskrit: ततः शक्तिं महाराज हेमदण्डां दुरासदाम् ।प्राहिणोत्तव पुत्राय घोरामग्निशिखामिव ॥२०॥
transliteration: tataḥ śaktiṁ mahārāja hemadaṇḍāṁ durāsadām ,prāhiṇottava putrāya ghorāmagniśikhāmiva.
english: Then, O great king (Dhṛtarāṣṭra), he sent forth towards your son a formidable spear, which had a golden shaft and was dreadful like a flame of fire.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 10 Verse 20. Then, O great king (Dhṛtarāṣṭra), he sent forth towards your son a formidable spear, which had a golden shaft and was dreadful like a flame of fire.

sanskrit: तामापतन्तीं सहसा शक्तिमुल्कामिवाम्बरात् ।द्विधा चिच्छेद समरे प्रतिविन्ध्यो हसन्निव ॥२१॥
transliteration: tāmāpatantīṁ sahasā śaktimulkāmivāmbarāt ,dvidhā ciccheda samare prativindhyo hasanniva.
english: Prativindhya, as if laughing, swiftly cut that approaching spear, which was like a meteor falling from the sky, into two parts in the battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 10 Verse 21. Prativindhya, as if laughing, swiftly cut that approaching spear, which was like a meteor falling from the sky, into two parts in the battle.

sanskrit: सा पपात तदा छिन्ना प्रतिविन्ध्यशरैः शितैः ।युगान्ते सर्वभूतानि त्रासयन्ती यथाशनिः ॥२२॥
transliteration: sā papāta tadā chinnā prativindhyaśaraiḥ śitaiḥ ,yugānte sarvabhūtāni trāsayantī yathāśaniḥ.
english: That spear, thus cut into pieces by Prativindhya's sharp arrows, then fell, just like a thunderbolt (aśani) that terrifies all creatures at the end of a cosmic age (yuga).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 10 Verse 22. That spear, thus cut into pieces by Prativindhya's sharp arrows, then fell, just like a thunderbolt (aśani) that terrifies all creatures at the end of a cosmic age (yuga).

sanskrit: शक्तिं तां प्रहतां दृष्ट्वा चित्रो गृह्य महागदाम् ।प्रतिविन्ध्याय चिक्षेप रुक्मजालविभूषिताम् ॥२३॥
transliteration: śaktiṁ tāṁ prahatāṁ dṛṣṭvā citro gṛhya mahāgadām ,prativindhyāya cikṣepa rukmajālavibhūṣitām.
english: Seeing that hurled javelin, Citra, taking up a great mace adorned with golden netting, hurled it at Prativindhya.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 10 Verse 23. Seeing that hurled javelin, Citra, taking up a great mace adorned with golden netting, hurled it at Prativindhya.

sanskrit: सा जघान हयांस्तस्य सारथिं च महारणे ।रथं प्रमृद्य वेगेन धरणीमन्वपद्यत ॥२४॥
transliteration: sā jaghāna hayāṁstasya sārathiṁ ca mahāraṇe ,rathaṁ pramṛdya vegena dharaṇīmanvapadyata.
english: That (mace) struck down his horses and charioteer in the great battle. Having crushed the chariot with its speed, it then fell to the ground.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 10 Verse 24. That (mace) struck down his horses and charioteer in the great battle. Having crushed the chariot with its speed, it then fell to the ground.

sanskrit: एतस्मिन्नेव काले तु रथादाप्लुत्य भारत ।शक्तिं चिक्षेप चित्राय स्वर्णघण्टामलंकृताम् ॥२५॥
transliteration: etasminneva kāle tu rathādāplutya bhārata ,śaktiṁ cikṣepa citrāya svarṇaghaṇṭāmalaṁkṛtām.
english: At that very moment, O Bhārata, (Prativindhya) jumped down from his chariot and hurled a javelin, adorned with golden bells, at Citra.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 10 Verse 25. At that very moment, O Bhārata, (Prativindhya) jumped down from his chariot and hurled a javelin, adorned with golden bells, at Citra.

sanskrit: तामापतन्तीं जग्राह चित्रो राजन्महामनाः ।ततस्तामेव चिक्षेप प्रतिविन्ध्याय भारत ॥२६॥
transliteration: tāmāpatantīṁ jagrāha citro rājanmahāmanāḥ ,tatastāmeva cikṣepa prativindhyāya bhārata.
english: O King, the great-souled Citra caught that approaching (javelin). Then, O Bhārata, he hurled that very same (javelin) back at Prativindhya.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 10 Verse 26. O King, the great-souled Citra caught that approaching (javelin). Then, O Bhārata, he hurled that very same (javelin) back at Prativindhya.

sanskrit: समासाद्य रणे शूरं प्रतिविन्ध्यं महाप्रभा ।निर्भिद्य दक्षिणं बाहुं निपपात महीतले ।पतिताभासयच्चैव तं देशमशनिर्यथा ॥२७॥
transliteration: samāsādya raṇe śūraṁ prativindhyaṁ mahāprabhā ,nirbhidya dakṣiṇaṁ bāhuṁ nipapāta mahītale ,patitābhāsayaccaiva taṁ deśamaśaniryathā.
english: Having confronted the heroic Prativindhya in battle and having pierced his right arm, the greatly resplendent one (feminine) fell to the ground. And having fallen, she illuminated that spot just like a lightning bolt.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 10 Verse 27. Having confronted the heroic Prativindhya in battle and having pierced his right arm, the greatly resplendent one (feminine) fell to the ground. And having fallen, she illuminated that spot just like a lightning bolt.

sanskrit: प्रतिविन्ध्यस्ततो राजंस्तोमरं हेमभूषितम् ।प्रेषयामास संक्रुद्धश्चित्रस्य वधकाम्यया ॥२८॥
transliteration: prativindhyastato rājaṁstomaraṁ hemabhūṣitam ,preṣayāmāsa saṁkruddhaścitrasya vadhakāmyayā.
english: Then, O King, Prativindhya, enraged, hurled a gold-adorned spear (tomara), with the desire to kill Chitra.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 10 Verse 28. Then, O King, Prativindhya, enraged, hurled a gold-adorned spear (tomara), with the desire to kill Chitra.

sanskrit: स तस्य देवावरणं भित्त्वा हृदयमेव च ।जगाम धरणीं तूर्णं महोरग इवाशयम् ॥२९॥
transliteration: sa tasya devāvaraṇaṁ bhittvā hṛdayameva ca ,jagāma dharaṇīṁ tūrṇaṁ mahoraga ivāśayam.
english: That (spear), having pierced his divine armor and indeed his heart, quickly penetrated the earth, just as a great serpent enters its dwelling.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 10 Verse 29. That (spear), having pierced his divine armor and indeed his heart, quickly penetrated the earth, just as a great serpent enters its dwelling.

sanskrit: स पपात तदा राजंस्तोमरेण समाहतः ।प्रसार्य विपुलौ बाहू पीनौ परिघसंनिभौ ॥३०॥
transliteration: sa papāta tadā rājaṁstomareṇa samāhataḥ ,prasārya vipulau bāhū pīnau parighasaṁnibhau.
english: Then, O King, he fell, struck by that spear (tomara), spreading his two broad, stout arms that resembled iron clubs.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 10 Verse 30. Then, O King, he fell, struck by that spear (tomara), spreading his two broad, stout arms that resembled iron clubs.

sanskrit: चित्रं संप्रेक्ष्य निहतं तावका रणशोभिनः ।अभ्यद्रवन्त वेगेन प्रतिविन्ध्यं समन्ततः ॥३१॥
transliteration: citraṁ saṁprekṣya nihataṁ tāvakā raṇaśobhinaḥ ,abhyadravanta vegena prativindhyaṁ samantataḥ.
english: Astonished upon seeing [one of their own] struck down, your warriors, who glorified in battle, swiftly attacked Prativindhya from all sides.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 10 Verse 31. Astonished upon seeing [one of their own] struck down, your warriors, who glorified in battle, swiftly attacked Prativindhya from all sides.

sanskrit: सृजन्तो विविधान्बाणाञ्शतघ्नीश्च सकिङ्किणीः ।त एनं छादयामासुः सूर्यमभ्रगणा इव ॥३२॥
transliteration: sṛjanto vividhānbāṇāñśataghnīśca sakiṅkiṇīḥ ,ta enaṁ chādayāmāsuḥ sūryamabhragaṇā iva.
english: Emitting various arrows and cannons (śataghnī) with small bells, they covered him, just as masses of clouds conceal the sun.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 10 Verse 32. Emitting various arrows and cannons (śataghnī) with small bells, they covered him, just as masses of clouds conceal the sun.

sanskrit: तानपास्य महाबाहुः शरजालेन संयुगे ।व्यद्रावयत्तव चमूं वज्रहस्त इवासुरीम् ॥३३॥
transliteration: tānapāsya mahābāhuḥ śarajālena saṁyuge ,vyadrāvayattava camūṁ vajrahasta ivāsurīm.
english: Having repelled them with a volley of arrows in battle, the mighty-armed [Prativindhya] routed your army, just as the wielder of the thunderbolt (vajrahasta) [disperses] a demonic [horde].
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 10 Verse 33. Having repelled them with a volley of arrows in battle, the mighty-armed [Prativindhya] routed your army, just as the wielder of the thunderbolt (vajrahasta) [disperses] a demonic [horde].

sanskrit: ते वध्यमानाः समरे तावकाः पाण्डवैर्नृप ।विप्रकीर्यन्त सहसा वातनुन्ना घना इव ॥३४॥
transliteration: te vadhyamānāḥ samare tāvakāḥ pāṇḍavairnṛpa ,viprakīryanta sahasā vātanunnā ghanā iva.
english: O King, your warriors, being slain in battle by the Pāṇḍavas, were swiftly scattered like clouds driven by the wind.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 10 Verse 34. O King, your warriors, being slain in battle by the Pāṇḍavas, were swiftly scattered like clouds driven by the wind.

sanskrit: विप्रद्रुते बले तस्मिन्वध्यमाने समन्ततः ।द्रौणिरेकोऽभ्ययात्तूर्णं भीमसेनं महाबलम् ॥३५॥
transliteration: vipradrute bale tasminvadhyamāne samantataḥ ,drauṇireko'bhyayāttūrṇaṁ bhīmasenaṁ mahābalam.
english: When that army, having scattered, was being slaughtered on all sides, Droṇi alone swiftly advanced towards the mighty Bhīmasena.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 10 Verse 35. When that army, having scattered, was being slaughtered on all sides, Droṇi alone swiftly advanced towards the mighty Bhīmasena.

sanskrit: ततः समागमो घोरो बभूव सहसा तयोः ।यथा देवासुरे युद्धे वृत्रवासवयोरभूत् ॥३६॥
transliteration: tataḥ samāgamo ghoro babhūva sahasā tayoḥ ,yathā devāsure yuddhe vṛtravāsavayorabhūt.
english: Then, suddenly, a fierce encounter occurred between those two, just as it had between Vṛtra and Vāsava (Indra) in the war of the gods and asuras (devasura yuddha).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 10 Verse 36. Then, suddenly, a fierce encounter occurred between those two, just as it had between Vṛtra and Vāsava (Indra) in the war of the gods and asuras (devasura yuddha).

sanskrit: संजय उवाच ।भीमसेनं ततो द्रौणी राजन्विव्याध पत्रिणा ।त्वरया परया युक्तो दर्शयन्नस्त्रलाघवम् ॥१॥
transliteration: saṁjaya uvāca ,bhīmasenaṁ tato drauṇī rājanvivyādha patriṇā ,tvarayā parayā yukto darśayannastralāghavam.
english: Sañjaya said: "Then, O King, Droṇi, demonstrating his agility in using weapons and possessed of supreme speed, pierced Bhīmasena with an arrow."
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 11 Verse 1. Sañjaya said: "Then, O King, Droṇi, demonstrating his agility in using weapons and possessed of supreme speed, pierced Bhīmasena with an arrow."

sanskrit: अथैनं पुनराजघ्ने नवत्या निशितैः शरैः ।सर्वमर्माणि संप्रेक्ष्य मर्मज्ञो लघुहस्तवत् ॥२॥
transliteration: athainaṁ punarājaghne navatyā niśitaiḥ śaraiḥ ,sarvamarmāṇi saṁprekṣya marmajño laghuhastavat.
english: Then, the expert in vital spots (marmajña), who was like one with light hands (laghuhastavat), having carefully observed all his vital spots, struck him again with ninety sharpened arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 11 Verse 2. Then, the expert in vital spots (marmajña), who was like one with light hands (laghuhastavat), having carefully observed all his vital spots, struck him again with ninety sharpened arrows.

sanskrit: भीमसेनः समाकीर्णो द्रौणिना निशितैः शरैः ।रराज समरे राजन्रश्मिवानिव भास्करः ॥३॥
transliteration: bhīmasenaḥ samākīrṇo drauṇinā niśitaiḥ śaraiḥ ,rarāja samare rājanraśmivāniva bhāskaraḥ.
english: O king, Bhimasena, though completely covered by sharp arrows from Drona's son (Ashvatthama), shone brilliantly in battle, just like the radiant sun.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 11 Verse 3. O king, Bhimasena, though completely covered by sharp arrows from Drona's son (Ashvatthama), shone brilliantly in battle, just like the radiant sun.

sanskrit: ततः शरसहस्रेण सुप्रयुक्तेन पाण्डवः ।द्रोणपुत्रमवच्छाद्य सिंहनादममुञ्चत ॥४॥
transliteration: tataḥ śarasahasreṇa suprayuktena pāṇḍavaḥ ,droṇaputramavacchādya siṁhanādamamuñcata.
english: Then, the son of Pandu (Bhima), having completely covered Drona's son (Ashvatthama) with a thousand well-aimed arrows, let out a lion's roar.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 11 Verse 4. Then, the son of Pandu (Bhima), having completely covered Drona's son (Ashvatthama) with a thousand well-aimed arrows, let out a lion's roar.

sanskrit: शरैः शरांस्ततो द्रौणिः संवार्य युधि पाण्डवम् ।ललाटेऽभ्यहनद्राजन्नाराचेन स्मयन्निव ॥५॥
transliteration: śaraiḥ śarāṁstato drauṇiḥ saṁvārya yudhi pāṇḍavam ,lalāṭe'bhyahanadrājannārācena smayanniva.
english: O king, then Drona's son (Ashvatthama), having parried the Pandava's (Bhima's) arrows in battle, struck him on the forehead with an iron arrow, as if smiling.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 11 Verse 5. O king, then Drona's son (Ashvatthama), having parried the Pandava's (Bhima's) arrows in battle, struck him on the forehead with an iron arrow, as if smiling.

sanskrit: ललाटस्थं ततो बाणं धारयामास पाण्डवः ।यथा शृङ्गं वने दृप्तः खड्गो धारयते नृप ॥६॥
transliteration: lalāṭasthaṁ tato bāṇaṁ dhārayāmāsa pāṇḍavaḥ ,yathā śṛṅgaṁ vane dṛptaḥ khaḍgo dhārayate nṛpa.
english: O king, the Pandava (Bhima) then bore that arrow fixed on his forehead, just as a proud rhinoceros carries its horn in the forest.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 11 Verse 6. O king, the Pandava (Bhima) then bore that arrow fixed on his forehead, just as a proud rhinoceros carries its horn in the forest.

sanskrit: ततो द्रौणिं रणे भीमो यतमानं पराक्रमी ।त्रिभिर्विव्याध नाराचैर्ललाटे विस्मयन्निव ॥७॥
transliteration: tato drauṇiṁ raṇe bhīmo yatamānaṁ parākramī ,tribhirvivyādha nārācairlalāṭe vismayanniva.
english: Then, the valiant Bhima, as if in wonder, pierced Drauṇi, who was exerting himself in battle, on the forehead with three arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 11 Verse 7. Then, the valiant Bhima, as if in wonder, pierced Drauṇi, who was exerting himself in battle, on the forehead with three arrows.

sanskrit: ललाटस्थैस्ततो बाणैर्ब्राह्मणः स व्यरोचत ।प्रावृषीव यथा सिक्तस्त्रिशृङ्गः पर्वतोत्तमः ॥८॥
transliteration: lalāṭasthaistato bāṇairbrāhmaṇaḥ sa vyarocata ,prāvṛṣīva yathā siktastriśṛṅgaḥ parvatottamaḥ.
english: Then, with those arrows fixed on his forehead, that Brahmin (brāhmaṇa) shone like an excellent three-peaked mountain, sprinkled with rain in the monsoon season.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 11 Verse 8. Then, with those arrows fixed on his forehead, that Brahmin (brāhmaṇa) shone like an excellent three-peaked mountain, sprinkled with rain in the monsoon season.

sanskrit: ततः शरशतैर्द्रौणिमर्दयामास पाण्डवः ।न चैनं कम्पयामास मातरिश्वेव पर्वतम् ॥९॥
transliteration: tataḥ śaraśatairdrauṇimardayāmāsa pāṇḍavaḥ ,na cainaṁ kampayāmāsa mātariśveva parvatam.
english: Then, the Pāṇḍava (Pāṇḍava) afflicted Drauṇi with hundreds of arrows. But he did not cause him to tremble, just as the wind god (mātariśvan) does not cause a mountain to tremble.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 11 Verse 9. Then, the Pāṇḍava (Pāṇḍava) afflicted Drauṇi with hundreds of arrows. But he did not cause him to tremble, just as the wind god (mātariśvan) does not cause a mountain to tremble.

sanskrit: तथैव पाण्डवं युद्धे द्रौणिः शरशतैः शितैः ।नाकम्पयत संहृष्टो वार्योघ इव पर्वतम् ॥१०॥
transliteration: tathaiva pāṇḍavaṁ yuddhe drauṇiḥ śaraśataiḥ śitaiḥ ,nākampayata saṁhṛṣṭo vāryogha iva parvatam.
english: In the same way, Drauṇi, greatly rejoiced, did not cause the Pāṇḍava (Pāṇḍava) to tremble in battle with hundreds of sharp arrows, just as a torrent of water (vāryogha) does not cause a mountain to tremble.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 11 Verse 10. In the same way, Drauṇi, greatly rejoiced, did not cause the Pāṇḍava (Pāṇḍava) to tremble in battle with hundreds of sharp arrows, just as a torrent of water (vāryogha) does not cause a mountain to tremble.

sanskrit: तावन्योन्यं शरैर्घोरैश्छादयानौ महारथौ ।रथचर्यागतौ शूरौ शुशुभाते रणोत्कटौ ॥११॥
transliteration: tāvanyonyaṁ śarairghoraiśchādayānau mahārathau ,rathacaryāgatau śūrau śuśubhāte raṇotkaṭau.
english: Those two great charioteers, heroic and fierce in battle, covering each other with terrible arrows while engaged in chariot warfare, shone brilliantly.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 11 Verse 11. Those two great charioteers, heroic and fierce in battle, covering each other with terrible arrows while engaged in chariot warfare, shone brilliantly.

sanskrit: आदित्याविव संदीप्तौ लोकक्षयकरावुभौ ।स्वरश्मिभिरिवान्योन्यं तापयन्तौ शरोत्तमैः ॥१२॥
transliteration: ādityāviva saṁdīptau lokakṣayakarāvubhau ,svaraśmibhirivānyonyaṁ tāpayantau śarottamaiḥ.
english: Both of them, blazing intensely like two suns, bringing destruction to the worlds, tormented each other as if with their own rays, employing their excellent arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 11 Verse 12. Both of them, blazing intensely like two suns, bringing destruction to the worlds, tormented each other as if with their own rays, employing their excellent arrows.

sanskrit: कृतप्रतिकृते यत्नं कुर्वाणौ च महारणे ।कृतप्रतिकृते यत्नं चक्राते तावभीतवत् ॥१३॥
transliteration: kṛtapratikṛte yatnaṁ kurvāṇau ca mahāraṇe ,kṛtapratikṛte yatnaṁ cakrāte tāvabhītavat.
english: And in the great battle, making an effort for attack and counter-attack (kṛtapratikṛta), those two fearlessly exerted themselves in action and reaction.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 11 Verse 13. And in the great battle, making an effort for attack and counter-attack (kṛtapratikṛta), those two fearlessly exerted themselves in action and reaction.

sanskrit: व्याघ्राविव च संग्रामे चेरतुस्तौ महारथौ ।शरदंष्ट्रौ दुराधर्षौ चापव्यात्तौ भयानकौ ॥१४॥
transliteration: vyāghrāviva ca saṁgrāme ceratustau mahārathau ,śaradaṁṣṭrau durādharṣau cāpavyāttau bhayānakau.
english: And in the battle, those two great charioteers ranged like two tigers, having arrows for fangs and bows as wide-open mouths, unassailable and terrifying.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 11 Verse 14. And in the battle, those two great charioteers ranged like two tigers, having arrows for fangs and bows as wide-open mouths, unassailable and terrifying.

sanskrit: अभूतां तावदृश्यौ च शरजालैः समन्ततः ।मेघजालैरिव च्छन्नौ गगने चन्द्रभास्करौ ॥१५॥
transliteration: abhūtāṁ tāvadṛśyau ca śarajālaiḥ samantataḥ ,meghajālairiva cchannau gagane candrabhāskarau.
english: Meanwhile, both of them became invisible, completely covered by volleys of arrows, just as the moon and the sun are obscured by masses of clouds in the sky.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 11 Verse 15. Meanwhile, both of them became invisible, completely covered by volleys of arrows, just as the moon and the sun are obscured by masses of clouds in the sky.

sanskrit: प्रकाशौ च मुहूर्तेन तत्रैवास्तामरिंदमौ ।विमुक्तौ मेघजालेन शशिसूर्यौ यथा दिवि ॥१६॥
transliteration: prakāśau ca muhūrtena tatraivāstāmariṁdamau ,vimuktau meghajālena śaśisūryau yathā divi.
english: And instantly, the two subduers of enemies became visible right there, just as the moon and sun appear released from a mass of clouds in the sky.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 11 Verse 16. And instantly, the two subduers of enemies became visible right there, just as the moon and sun appear released from a mass of clouds in the sky.

sanskrit: अपसव्यं ततश्चक्रे द्रौणिस्तत्र वृकोदरम् ।किरञ्शरशतैरुग्रैर्धाराभिरिव पर्वतम् ॥१७॥
transliteration: apasavyaṁ tataścakre drauṇistatra vṛkodaram ,kirañśaraśatairugrairdhārābhiriva parvatam.
english: Then, Droṇi (Aśvatthāman) maneuvered to the left (apasavyam) against Vṛkodara (Bhīma) there, showering him with hundreds of fierce arrows, just as (rain) torrents deluge a mountain.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 11 Verse 17. Then, Droṇi (Aśvatthāman) maneuvered to the left (apasavyam) against Vṛkodara (Bhīma) there, showering him with hundreds of fierce arrows, just as (rain) torrents deluge a mountain.

sanskrit: न तु तन्ममृषे भीमः शत्रोर्विजयलक्षणम् ।प्रतिचक्रे च तं राजन्पाण्डवोऽप्यपसव्यतः ॥१८॥
transliteration: na tu tanmamṛṣe bhīmaḥ śatrorvijayalakṣaṇam ,praticakre ca taṁ rājanpāṇḍavo'pyapasavyataḥ.
english: But Bhīma certainly did not tolerate that display of the enemy's impending victory. And, O king, that Pāṇḍava (Bhīma) also counterattacked him by moving to his left (apasavyataḥ).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 11 Verse 18. But Bhīma certainly did not tolerate that display of the enemy's impending victory. And, O king, that Pāṇḍava (Bhīma) also counterattacked him by moving to his left (apasavyataḥ).

sanskrit: मण्डलानां विभागेषु गतप्रत्यागतेषु च ।बभूव तुमुलं युद्धं तयोस्तत्र महामृधे ॥१९॥
transliteration: maṇḍalānāṁ vibhāgeṣu gatapratyāgateṣu ca ,babhūva tumulaṁ yuddhaṁ tayostatra mahāmṛdhe.
english: In the sections of their battle formations, and amidst their advances and retreats, a tumultuous battle took place there between those two in that great conflict.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 11 Verse 19. In the sections of their battle formations, and amidst their advances and retreats, a tumultuous battle took place there between those two in that great conflict.

sanskrit: चरित्वा विविधान्मार्गान्मण्डलं स्थानमेव च ।शरैः पूर्णायतोत्सृष्टैरन्योन्यमभिजघ्नतुः ॥२०॥
transliteration: caritvā vividhānmārgānmaṇḍalaṁ sthānameva ca ,śaraiḥ pūrṇāyatotsṛṣṭairanyonyamabhijaghnatuḥ.
english: Moving through various paths, employing circular maneuvers, and holding their positions, they struck each other with arrows released from fully drawn bows.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 11 Verse 20. Moving through various paths, employing circular maneuvers, and holding their positions, they struck each other with arrows released from fully drawn bows.

sanskrit: अन्योन्यस्य वधे यत्नं चक्रतुस्तौ महारथौ ।ईषतुर्विरथं चैव कर्तुमन्योन्यमाहवे ॥२१॥
transliteration: anyonyasya vadhe yatnaṁ cakratustau mahārathau ,īṣaturvirathaṁ caiva kartumanyonyamāhave.
english: Those two great warriors made an effort to kill each other, and indeed, they both wished to render the other chariot-less in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 11 Verse 21. Those two great warriors made an effort to kill each other, and indeed, they both wished to render the other chariot-less in battle.

sanskrit: ततो द्रौणिर्महास्त्राणि प्रादुश्चक्रे महारथः ।तान्यस्त्रैरेव समरे प्रतिजघ्नेऽस्य पाण्डवः ॥२२॥
transliteration: tato drauṇirmahāstrāṇi prāduścakre mahārathaḥ ,tānyastraireva samare pratijaghne'sya pāṇḍavaḥ.
english: Then Droṇi (Aśvatthāman), the great warrior, manifested great weapons (astrāṇi). In battle, the Pāṇḍava (Arjuna) countered his (weapons) with his own weapons (astrāṇi).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 11 Verse 22. Then Droṇi (Aśvatthāman), the great warrior, manifested great weapons (astrāṇi). In battle, the Pāṇḍava (Arjuna) countered his (weapons) with his own weapons (astrāṇi).

sanskrit: ततो घोरं महाराज अस्त्रयुद्धमवर्तत ।ग्रहयुद्धं यथा घोरं प्रजासंहरणे अभूत् ॥२३॥
transliteration: tato ghoraṁ mahārāja astrayuddhamavartata ,grahayuddhaṁ yathā ghoraṁ prajāsaṁharaṇe abhūt.
english: O great king, a terrible battle using missiles then occurred, as dreadful as the conflict of planets at the time of universal dissolution.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 11 Verse 23. O great king, a terrible battle using missiles then occurred, as dreadful as the conflict of planets at the time of universal dissolution.

sanskrit: ते बाणाः समसज्जन्त क्षिप्तास्ताभ्यां तु भारत ।द्योतयन्तो दिशः सर्वास्तच्च सैन्यं समन्ततः ॥२४॥
transliteration: te bāṇāḥ samasajjanta kṣiptāstābhyāṁ tu bhārata ,dyotayanto diśaḥ sarvāstacca sainyaṁ samantataḥ.
english: O descendant of Bharata, those arrows, hurled by the two warriors, clashed together, illuminating all directions and the entire army on all sides.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 11 Verse 24. O descendant of Bharata, those arrows, hurled by the two warriors, clashed together, illuminating all directions and the entire army on all sides.

sanskrit: बाणसंघावृतं घोरमाकाशं समपद्यत ।उक्लापातकृतं यद्वत्प्रजानां संक्षये नृप ॥२५॥
transliteration: bāṇasaṁghāvṛtaṁ ghoramākāśaṁ samapadyata ,uklāpātakṛtaṁ yadvatprajānāṁ saṁkṣaye nṛpa.
english: O king, the dreadful sky became completely covered by masses of arrows, just as it appears at the destruction of beings due to a shower of meteors.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 11 Verse 25. O king, the dreadful sky became completely covered by masses of arrows, just as it appears at the destruction of beings due to a shower of meteors.

sanskrit: बाणाभिघातात्संजज्ञे तत्र भारत पावकः ।सविस्फुलिङ्गो दीप्तार्चिः सोऽदहद्वाहिनीद्वयम् ॥२६॥
transliteration: bāṇābhighātātsaṁjajñe tatra bhārata pāvakaḥ ,savisphuliṅgo dīptārciḥ so'dahadvāhinīdvayam.
english: O descendant of Bharata, from the impact of the arrows, a fire arose there, complete with sparks and blazing flames, and that fire consumed both armies.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 11 Verse 26. O descendant of Bharata, from the impact of the arrows, a fire arose there, complete with sparks and blazing flames, and that fire consumed both armies.

sanskrit: तत्र सिद्धा महाराज संपतन्तोऽब्रुवन्वचः ।अति युद्धानि सर्वाणि युद्धमेतत्ततोऽधिकम् ॥२७॥
transliteration: tatra siddhā mahārāja saṁpatanto'bruvanvacaḥ ,ati yuddhāni sarvāṇi yuddhametattato'dhikam.
english: O great king, the Siddhas, descending there, spoke these words: "This battle surpasses all previous battles; indeed, it is far greater than any of them."
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 11 Verse 27. O great king, the Siddhas, descending there, spoke these words: "This battle surpasses all previous battles; indeed, it is far greater than any of them."

sanskrit: सर्वयुद्धानि चैतस्य कलां नार्हन्ति षोडशीम् ।नैतादृशं पुनर्युद्धं न भूतं न भविष्यति ॥२८॥
transliteration: sarvayuddhāni caitasya kalāṁ nārhanti ṣoḍaśīm ,naitādṛśaṁ punaryuddhaṁ na bhūtaṁ na bhaviṣyati.
english: Indeed, all other battles do not deserve even a sixteenth part of this one. Such a battle has never occurred in the past, nor will it ever happen again in the future.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 11 Verse 28. Indeed, all other battles do not deserve even a sixteenth part of this one. Such a battle has never occurred in the past, nor will it ever happen again in the future.

sanskrit: अहो ज्ञानेन संयुक्तावुभौ चोग्रपराक्रमौ ।अहो भीमे बलं भीममेतयोश्च कृतास्त्रता ॥२९॥
transliteration: aho jñānena saṁyuktāvubhau cograparākramau ,aho bhīme balaṁ bhīmametayośca kṛtāstratā.
english: Oh, how remarkable that both are endowed with knowledge and possess fierce valor! And how astonishing is Bhima's tremendous strength, as well as the mastery of weapons demonstrated by these two!
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 11 Verse 29. Oh, how remarkable that both are endowed with knowledge and possess fierce valor! And how astonishing is Bhima's tremendous strength, as well as the mastery of weapons demonstrated by these two!

sanskrit: अहो वीर्यस्य सारत्वमहो सौष्ठवमेतयोः ।स्थितावेतौ हि समरे कालान्तकयमोपमौ ॥३०॥
transliteration: aho vīryasya sāratvamaho sauṣṭhavametayoḥ ,sthitāvetau hi samare kālāntakayamopamau.
english: Oh, what an essence of valor! Oh, what superb dexterity these two possess! Indeed, these two stand in battle resembling Kāla, Antaka, and Yama – the very embodiments of death.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 11 Verse 30. Oh, what an essence of valor! Oh, what superb dexterity these two possess! Indeed, these two stand in battle resembling Kāla, Antaka, and Yama – the very embodiments of death.

sanskrit: रुद्रौ द्वाविव संभूतौ यथा द्वाविव भास्करौ ।यमौ वा पुरुषव्याघ्रौ घोररूपाविमौ रणे ॥३१॥
transliteration: rudrau dvāviva saṁbhūtau yathā dvāviva bhāskarau ,yamau vā puruṣavyāghrau ghorarūpāvimau raṇe.
english: These two, formidable tigers among men (puruṣavyāghrau) in battle, appeared like two Rudras, or like two suns, or even like two Yamas, possessing terrifying forms.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 11 Verse 31. These two, formidable tigers among men (puruṣavyāghrau) in battle, appeared like two Rudras, or like two suns, or even like two Yamas, possessing terrifying forms.

sanskrit: श्रूयन्ते स्म तदा वाचः सिद्धानां वै मुहुर्मुहुः ।सिंहनादश्च संजज्ञे समेतानां दिवौकसाम् ।अद्भुतं चाप्यचिन्त्यं च दृष्ट्वा कर्म तयोर्मृधे ॥३२॥
transliteration: śrūyante sma tadā vācaḥ siddhānāṁ vai muhurmuhuḥ ,siṁhanādaśca saṁjajñe sametānāṁ divaukasām ,adbhutaṁ cāpyacintyaṁ ca dṛṣṭvā karma tayormṛdhe.
english: At that time, the words of the perfected beings (siddhas) were repeatedly heard. And a lion's roar arose from the assembled celestials, as they witnessed the wondrous and inconceivable deed of those two in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 11 Verse 32. At that time, the words of the perfected beings (siddhas) were repeatedly heard. And a lion's roar arose from the assembled celestials, as they witnessed the wondrous and inconceivable deed of those two in battle.
