sanskrit: तौ शूरौ समरे राजन्परस्परकृतागसौ ।परस्परमुदैक्षेतां क्रोधादुद्वृत्य चक्षुषी ॥३३॥
transliteration: tau śūrau samare rājanparasparakṛtāgasau ,parasparamudaikṣetāṁ krodhādudvṛtya cakṣuṣī.
english: O King, those two heroes, who had mutually wronged each other in battle, looked at each other, their eyes wide with anger.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 11 Verse 33. O King, those two heroes, who had mutually wronged each other in battle, looked at each other, their eyes wide with anger.

sanskrit: क्रोधरक्तेक्षणौ तौ तु क्रोधात्प्रस्फुरिताधरौ ।क्रोधात्संदष्टदशनौ संदष्टदशनच्छदौ ॥३४॥
transliteration: krodharaktekṣaṇau tau tu krodhātprasphuritādharau ,krodhātsaṁdaṣṭadaśanau saṁdaṣṭadaśanacchadau.
english: Indeed, those two had eyes red with anger, lips trembling from anger, teeth gnashed from anger, and lips bitten due to anger.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 11 Verse 34. Indeed, those two had eyes red with anger, lips trembling from anger, teeth gnashed from anger, and lips bitten due to anger.

sanskrit: अन्योन्यं छादयन्तौ स्म शरवृष्ट्या महारथौ ।शराम्बुधारौ समरे शस्त्रविद्युत्प्रकाशिनौ ॥३५॥
transliteration: anyonyaṁ chādayantau sma śaravṛṣṭyā mahārathau ,śarāmbudhārau samare śastravidyutprakāśinau.
english: The two great warriors, covering each other with a shower of arrows, shone in battle like lightning with their weapons, discharging streams of arrows like water.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 11 Verse 35. The two great warriors, covering each other with a shower of arrows, shone in battle like lightning with their weapons, discharging streams of arrows like water.

sanskrit: तावन्योन्यं ध्वजौ विद्ध्वा सारथी च महारथौ ।अन्योन्यस्य हयान्विद्ध्वा बिभिदाते परस्परम् ॥३६॥
transliteration: tāvanyonyaṁ dhvajau viddhvā sārathī ca mahārathau ,anyonyasya hayānviddhvā bibhidāte parasparam.
english: The two great warriors first pierced each other's banners and charioteers. Then, having pierced each other's horses, they proceeded to strike each other.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 11 Verse 36. The two great warriors first pierced each other's banners and charioteers. Then, having pierced each other's horses, they proceeded to strike each other.

sanskrit: ततः क्रुद्धौ महाराज बाणौ गृह्य महाहवे ।उभौ चिक्षिपतुस्तूर्णमन्योन्यस्य वधैषिणौ ॥३७॥
transliteration: tataḥ kruddhau mahārāja bāṇau gṛhya mahāhave ,ubhau cikṣipatustūrṇamanyonyasya vadhaiṣiṇau.
english: Then, O great king, both enraged warriors, intent on killing each other, quickly seized arrows in that great battle and hurled them.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 11 Verse 37. Then, O great king, both enraged warriors, intent on killing each other, quickly seized arrows in that great battle and hurled them.

sanskrit: तौ सायकौ महाराज द्योतमानौ चमूमुखे ।आजघ्नाते समासाद्य वज्रवेगौ दुरासदौ ॥३८॥
transliteration: tau sāyakau mahārāja dyotamānau camūmukhe ,ājaghnāte samāsādya vajravegau durāsadau.
english: O great king, those two brilliant and irresistible arrows, possessing the speed of a thunderbolt, met and struck each other at the vanguard of the army.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 11 Verse 38. O great king, those two brilliant and irresistible arrows, possessing the speed of a thunderbolt, met and struck each other at the vanguard of the army.

sanskrit: तौ परस्परवेगाच्च शराभ्यां च भृशाहतौ ।निपेततुर्महावीरौ स्वरथोपस्थयोस्तदा ॥३९॥
transliteration: tau parasparavegācca śarābhyāṁ ca bhṛśāhatau ,nipetaturmahāvīrau svarathopasthayostadā.
english: At that moment, those two great heroes, greatly wounded by the force of their encounter and by each other's arrows, fell down onto the floors of their respective chariots.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 11 Verse 39. At that moment, those two great heroes, greatly wounded by the force of their encounter and by each other's arrows, fell down onto the floors of their respective chariots.

sanskrit: ततस्तु सारथिर्ज्ञात्वा द्रोणपुत्रमचेतनम् ।अपोवाह रणाद्राजन्सर्वक्षत्रस्य पश्यतः ॥४०॥
transliteration: tatastu sārathirjñātvā droṇaputramacetanam ,apovāha raṇādrājansarvakṣatrasya paśyataḥ.
english: O King, then his charioteer, realizing that Drona's son (Aśvatthāman) was unconscious, drove him away from the battlefield as all the kṣatriyas (warriors) watched.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 11 Verse 40. O King, then his charioteer, realizing that Drona's son (Aśvatthāman) was unconscious, drove him away from the battlefield as all the kṣatriyas (warriors) watched.

sanskrit: तथैव पाण्डवं राजन्विह्वलन्तं मुहुर्मुहुः ।अपोवाह रथेनाजौ सारथिः शत्रुतापनम् ॥४१॥
transliteration: tathaiva pāṇḍavaṁ rājanvihvalantaṁ muhurmuhuḥ ,apovāha rathenājau sārathiḥ śatrutāpanam.
english: O King, similarly, his charioteer (Kṛṣṇa) drove the Pāṇḍava (Arjuna), the tormentor of enemies, who was repeatedly reeling and overcome, away from the battle in his chariot.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 11 Verse 41. O King, similarly, his charioteer (Kṛṣṇa) drove the Pāṇḍava (Arjuna), the tormentor of enemies, who was repeatedly reeling and overcome, away from the battle in his chariot.

sanskrit: धृतराष्ट्र उवाच ।यथा संशप्तकैः सार्धमर्जुनस्याभवद्रणः ।अन्येषां च मदीयानां पाण्डवैस्तद्ब्रवीहि मे ॥१॥
transliteration: dhṛtarāṣṭra uvāca ,yathā saṁśaptakaiḥ sārdhamarjunasyābhavadraṇaḥ ,anyeṣāṁ ca madīyānāṁ pāṇḍavaistadbravīhi me.
english: Dhṛtarāṣṭra said: "Please describe to me how Arjuna's battle with the Saṃśaptakas (warriors sworn to die or kill him) unfolded, and also how the combat of my other warriors against the Pāṇḍavas occurred."
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 12 Verse 1. Dhṛtarāṣṭra said: "Please describe to me how Arjuna's battle with the Saṃśaptakas (warriors sworn to die or kill him) unfolded, and also how the combat of my other warriors against the Pāṇḍavas occurred."

sanskrit: संजय उवाच ।शृणु राजन्यथावृत्तं संग्रामं ब्रुवतो मम ।वीराणां शत्रुभिः सार्धं देहपाप्मप्रणाशनम् ॥२॥
transliteration: saṁjaya uvāca ,śṛṇu rājanyathāvṛttaṁ saṁgrāmaṁ bruvato mama ,vīrāṇāṁ śatrubhiḥ sārdhaṁ dehapāpmapraṇāśanam.
english: Sañjaya said: "Listen, O King, as I recount the battle as it truly happened - a battle of heroes against their foes, which destroys the body's evils (pāpman) and sins."
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 12 Verse 2. Sañjaya said: "Listen, O King, as I recount the battle as it truly happened - a battle of heroes against their foes, which destroys the body's evils (pāpman) and sins."

sanskrit: पार्थः संशप्तकगणं प्रविश्यार्णवसंनिभम् ।व्यक्षोभयदमित्रघ्नो महावात इवार्णवम् ॥३॥
transliteration: pārthaḥ saṁśaptakagaṇaṁ praviśyārṇavasaṁnibham ,vyakṣobhayadamitraghno mahāvāta ivārṇavam.
english: Having entered the ocean-like host of the Saṃśaptakas, Pārtha, the slayer of enemies, agitated them just as a great wind stirs up an ocean.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 12 Verse 3. Having entered the ocean-like host of the Saṃśaptakas, Pārtha, the slayer of enemies, agitated them just as a great wind stirs up an ocean.

sanskrit: शिरांस्युन्मथ्य वीराणां शितैर्भल्लैर्धनंजयः ।पूर्णचन्द्राभवक्त्राणि स्वक्षिभ्रूदशनानि च ।संतस्तार क्षितिं क्षिप्रं विनालैर्नलिनैरिव ॥४॥
transliteration: śirāṁsyunmathya vīrāṇāṁ śitairbhallairdhanaṁjayaḥ ,pūrṇacandrābhavaktrāṇi svakṣibhrūdaśanāni ca ,saṁtastāra kṣitiṁ kṣipraṁ vinālairnalinairiva.
english: Dhanañjaya (Arjuna), with sharp spears, quickly strewed the earth with the severed heads of heroes - heads whose faces resembled the full moon and were complete with their eyes, brows, and teeth - just as one would strew lotuses without their stalks.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 12 Verse 4. Dhanañjaya (Arjuna), with sharp spears, quickly strewed the earth with the severed heads of heroes - heads whose faces resembled the full moon and were complete with their eyes, brows, and teeth - just as one would strew lotuses without their stalks.

sanskrit: सुवृत्तानायतान्पुष्टांश्चन्दनागुरुभूषितान् ।सायुधान्सतनुत्राणान्पञ्चास्योरगसंनिभान् ।बाहून्क्षुरैरमित्राणां विचकर्तार्जुनो रणे ॥५॥
transliteration: suvṛttānāyatānpuṣṭāṁścandanāgurubhūṣitān ,sāyudhānsatanutrāṇānpañcāsyoragasaṁnibhān ,bāhūnkṣurairamitrāṇāṁ vicakartārjuno raṇe.
english: In battle, Arjuna severed the enemies' arms with razor-edged arrows - arms that were well-rounded, long, and strong; adorned with sandalwood and agallochum; bearing weapons and armor; and resembling five-hooded serpents.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 12 Verse 5. In battle, Arjuna severed the enemies' arms with razor-edged arrows - arms that were well-rounded, long, and strong; adorned with sandalwood and agallochum; bearing weapons and armor; and resembling five-hooded serpents.

sanskrit: धुर्यान्धुर्यतरान्सूतान्ध्वजांश्चापानि सायकान् ।पाणीनरत्नीनसकृद्भल्लैश्चिच्छेद पाण्डवः ॥६॥
transliteration: dhuryāndhuryatarānsūtāndhvajāṁścāpāni sāyakān ,pāṇīnaratnīnasakṛdbhallaiściccheda pāṇḍavaḥ.
english: The Pāṇḍava, Arjuna, repeatedly severed with broad-headed arrows the lead horses, the best horses, the charioteers, banners, bows, arrows, hands, and forearms.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 12 Verse 6. The Pāṇḍava, Arjuna, repeatedly severed with broad-headed arrows the lead horses, the best horses, the charioteers, banners, bows, arrows, hands, and forearms.

sanskrit: द्विपान्हयान्रथांश्चैव सारोहानर्जुनो रणे ।शरैरनेकसाहस्रै राजन्निन्ये यमक्षयम् ॥७॥
transliteration: dvipānhayānrathāṁścaiva sārohānarjuno raṇe ,śarairanekasāhasrai rājanninye yamakṣayam.
english: O King, in battle, Arjuna sent elephants, horses, and chariots, along with their riders, to the abode of death (yamakṣaya) with countless thousands of arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 12 Verse 7. O King, in battle, Arjuna sent elephants, horses, and chariots, along with their riders, to the abode of death (yamakṣaya) with countless thousands of arrows.

sanskrit: तं प्रवीरं प्रतीयाता नर्दमाना इवर्षभाः ।वाशितार्थमभिक्रुद्धा हुंकृत्वा चाभिदुद्रुवुः ।निघ्नन्तमभिजघ्नुस्ते शरैः शृङ्गैरिवर्षभाः ॥८॥
transliteration: taṁ pravīraṁ pratīyātā nardamānā ivarṣabhāḥ ,vāśitārthamabhikruddhā huṁkṛtvā cābhidudruvuḥ ,nighnantamabhijaghnuste śaraiḥ śṛṅgairivarṣabhāḥ.
english: Like bulls roaring furiously, enraged for the sake of a female, they advanced towards that great hero (Arjuna). Having roared fiercely, they rushed towards him. Even as he struck them, those warriors, like bulls, struck him with arrows as if they were horns.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 12 Verse 8. Like bulls roaring furiously, enraged for the sake of a female, they advanced towards that great hero (Arjuna). Having roared fiercely, they rushed towards him. Even as he struck them, those warriors, like bulls, struck him with arrows as if they were horns.

sanskrit: तस्य तेषां च तद्युद्धमभवल्लोमहर्षणम् ।त्रैलोक्यविजये यादृग्दैत्यानां सह वज्रिणा ॥९॥
transliteration: tasya teṣāṁ ca tadyuddhamabhavallomaharṣaṇam ,trailokyavijaye yādṛgdaityānāṁ saha vajriṇā.
english: That battle between Arjuna and those warriors became hair-raising, similar to the one that took place during the conquest of the three worlds between the Daitas (demons) and Indra, the wielder of the thunderbolt.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 12 Verse 9. That battle between Arjuna and those warriors became hair-raising, similar to the one that took place during the conquest of the three worlds between the Daitas (demons) and Indra, the wielder of the thunderbolt.

sanskrit: अस्त्रैरस्त्राणि संवार्य द्विषतां सर्वतोऽर्जुनः ।इषुभिर्बहुभिस्तूर्णं विद्ध्वा प्राणान्ररास सः ॥१०॥
transliteration: astrairastrāṇi saṁvārya dviṣatāṁ sarvato'rjunaḥ ,iṣubhirbahubhistūrṇaṁ viddhvā prāṇānrarāsa saḥ.
english: He, Arjuna, having fended off the enemies' missiles from all sides with his own weapons, then swiftly pierced them with many arrows, thus taking their lives.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 12 Verse 10. He, Arjuna, having fended off the enemies' missiles from all sides with his own weapons, then swiftly pierced them with many arrows, thus taking their lives.

sanskrit: छिन्नत्रिवेणुचक्राक्षान्हतयोधाश्वसारथीन् ।विध्वस्तायुधतूणीरान्समुन्मथितकेतनान् ॥११॥
transliteration: chinnatriveṇucakrākṣānhatayodhāśvasārathīn ,vidhvastāyudhatūṇīrānsamunmathitaketanān.
english: (Arjuna shattered chariots which were) those whose three shafts, wheels, and axles were shattered; whose warriors, horses, and charioteers were slain; whose weapons and quivers were destroyed; and whose banners were torn down.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 12 Verse 11. (Arjuna shattered chariots which were) those whose three shafts, wheels, and axles were shattered; whose warriors, horses, and charioteers were slain; whose weapons and quivers were destroyed; and whose banners were torn down.

sanskrit: संछिन्नयोक्त्ररश्मीकान्वित्रिवेणून्विकूबरान् ।विध्वस्तबन्धुरयुगान्विशस्तायुधमण्डलान् ।रथान्विशकलीकुर्वन्महाभ्राणीव मारुतः ॥१२॥
transliteration: saṁchinnayoktraraśmīkānvitriveṇūnvikūbarān ,vidhvastabandhurayugānviśastāyudhamaṇḍalān ,rathānviśakalīkurvanmahābhrāṇīva mārutaḥ.
english: Arjuna, like the wind scattering great clouds, was shattering chariots: those whose yoke-ropes and reins were completely severed, whose three shafts were dislocated, whose main poles were broken, whose front frameworks and yokes were shattered, and whose arrays of weapons were completely broken.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 12 Verse 12. Arjuna, like the wind scattering great clouds, was shattering chariots: those whose yoke-ropes and reins were completely severed, whose three shafts were dislocated, whose main poles were broken, whose front frameworks and yokes were shattered, and whose arrays of weapons were completely broken.

sanskrit: विस्मापयन्प्रेक्षणीयं द्विषातां भयवर्धनम् ।महारथसहस्रस्य समं कर्मार्जुनोऽकरोत् ॥१३॥
transliteration: vismāpayanprekṣaṇīyaṁ dviṣātāṁ bhayavardhanam ,mahārathasahasrasya samaṁ karmārjuno'karot.
english: Arjuna performed a deed (karma) that was astonishing to behold and increased the fear of the enemies, an action equal to the combined efforts of a thousand great warriors (mahārathas).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 12 Verse 13. Arjuna performed a deed (karma) that was astonishing to behold and increased the fear of the enemies, an action equal to the combined efforts of a thousand great warriors (mahārathas).

sanskrit: सिद्धदेवर्षिसंघाश्च चारणाश्चैव तुष्टुवुः ।देवदुन्दुभयो नेदुः पुष्पवर्षाणि चापतन् ।केशवार्जुनयोर्मूर्ध्नि प्राह वाक्चाशरीरिणी ॥१४॥
transliteration: siddhadevarṣisaṁghāśca cāraṇāścaiva tuṣṭuvuḥ ,devadundubhayo neduḥ puṣpavarṣāṇi cāpatan ,keśavārjunayormūrdhni prāha vākcāśarīriṇī.
english: Groups of perfected beings (siddhas) and divine sages (devarṣis), as well as Caraṇas, offered praises. Divine drums resounded, and showers of flowers fell upon the heads of Keśava (Kṛṣṇa) and Arjuna. Then, an incorporeal voice spoke.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 12 Verse 14. Groups of perfected beings (siddhas) and divine sages (devarṣis), as well as Caraṇas, offered praises. Divine drums resounded, and showers of flowers fell upon the heads of Keśava (Kṛṣṇa) and Arjuna. Then, an incorporeal voice spoke.

sanskrit: चन्द्रार्कानिलवह्नीनां कान्तिदीप्तिबलद्युतीः ।यौ सदा बिभ्रतुर्वीरौ ताविमौ केशवार्जुनौ ॥१५॥
transliteration: candrārkānilavahnīnāṁ kāntidīptibaladyutīḥ ,yau sadā bibhraturvīrau tāvimau keśavārjunau.
english: These two heroes, Keśava (Kṛṣṇa) and Arjuna, who always possess the brilliance, luster, strength, and radiance of the moon, the sun, the wind, and fire.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 12 Verse 15. These two heroes, Keśava (Kṛṣṇa) and Arjuna, who always possess the brilliance, luster, strength, and radiance of the moon, the sun, the wind, and fire.

sanskrit: ब्रह्मेशानाविवाजय्यौ वीरावेकरथे स्थितौ ।सर्वभूतवरौ वीरौ नरनारायणावुभौ ॥१६॥
transliteration: brahmeśānāvivājayyau vīrāvekarathe sthitau ,sarvabhūtavarau vīrau naranārāyaṇāvubhau.
english: Both of these two heroes, Narā and Nārāyaṇa, are unconquerable like Brahmā and Īśāna (Śiva). They are the best among all beings, and are situated in a single chariot.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 12 Verse 16. Both of these two heroes, Narā and Nārāyaṇa, are unconquerable like Brahmā and Īśāna (Śiva). They are the best among all beings, and are situated in a single chariot.

sanskrit: इत्येतन्महदाश्चर्यं दृष्ट्वा श्रुत्वा च भारत ।अश्वत्थामा सुसंयत्तः कृष्णावभ्यद्रवद्रणे ॥१७॥
transliteration: ityetanmahadāścaryaṁ dṛṣṭvā śrutvā ca bhārata ,aśvatthāmā susaṁyattaḥ kṛṣṇāvabhyadravadraṇe.
english: O Bhārata, having seen and heard this great wonder, Aśvatthāmā, being well-composed, charged at the two Kṛṣṇas (Kṛṣṇa and Arjuna) in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 12 Verse 17. O Bhārata, having seen and heard this great wonder, Aśvatthāmā, being well-composed, charged at the two Kṛṣṇas (Kṛṣṇa and Arjuna) in battle.

sanskrit: अथ पाण्डवमस्यन्तं यमकालान्तकाञ्शरान् ।सेषुणा पाणिनाहूय हसन्द्रौणिरथाब्रवीत् ॥१८॥
transliteration: atha pāṇḍavamasyantaṁ yamakālāntakāñśarān ,seṣuṇā pāṇināhūya hasandrauṇirathābravīt.
english: Then Drauṇi, laughing, with his hand holding an arrow, summoned the Pāṇḍava (Arjuna), who was discharging arrows like Yama, Kāla, and Antaka (personifications of death). He then spoke.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 12 Verse 18. Then Drauṇi, laughing, with his hand holding an arrow, summoned the Pāṇḍava (Arjuna), who was discharging arrows like Yama, Kāla, and Antaka (personifications of death). He then spoke.

sanskrit: यदि मां मन्यसे वीर प्राप्तमर्हमिवातिथिम् ।ततः सर्वात्मनाद्य त्वं युद्धातिथ्यं प्रयच्छ मे ॥१९॥
transliteration: yadi māṁ manyase vīra prāptamarhamivātithim ,tataḥ sarvātmanādya tvaṁ yuddhātithyaṁ prayaccha me.
english: If, O hero, you consider me an arrived guest worthy of welcome, then today, with your whole being (ātman), you must offer me the hospitality of battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 12 Verse 19. If, O hero, you consider me an arrived guest worthy of welcome, then today, with your whole being (ātman), you must offer me the hospitality of battle.

sanskrit: एवमाचार्यपुत्रेण समाहूतो युयुत्सया ।बहु मेनेऽर्जुनोऽऽत्मानमिदं चाह जनार्दनम् ॥२०॥
transliteration: evamācāryaputreṇa samāhūto yuyutsayā ,bahu mene'rjuno''tmānamidaṁ cāha janārdanam.
english: Thus summoned for battle by the teacher's son (Drauṇi), Arjuna highly regarded himself (ātman) and then spoke these words to Janārdana (Kṛṣṇa).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 12 Verse 20. Thus summoned for battle by the teacher's son (Drauṇi), Arjuna highly regarded himself (ātman) and then spoke these words to Janārdana (Kṛṣṇa).

sanskrit: संशप्तकाश्च मे वध्या द्रौणिराह्वयते च माम् ।यदत्रानन्तरं प्राप्तं प्रशाधि त्वं महाभुज ॥२१॥
transliteration: saṁśaptakāśca me vadhyā drauṇirāhvayate ca mām ,yadatrānantaraṁ prāptaṁ praśādhi tvaṁ mahābhuja.
english: And the Saṃśaptakas are to be killed by me, and Drauṇi also challenges me. O mighty-armed (Kṛṣṇa), instruct me what is appropriate here to do next.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 12 Verse 21. And the Saṃśaptakas are to be killed by me, and Drauṇi also challenges me. O mighty-armed (Kṛṣṇa), instruct me what is appropriate here to do next.

sanskrit: एवमुक्तोऽवहत्पार्थं कृष्णो द्रोणात्मजान्तिकम् ।जैत्रेण विधिनाहूतं वायुरिन्द्रमिवाध्वरे ॥२२॥
transliteration: evamukto'vahatpārthaṁ kṛṣṇo droṇātmajāntikam ,jaitreṇa vidhināhūtaṁ vāyurindramivādhvare.
english: Thus addressed, Krishna carried Arjuna to the proximity of Droṇa's son (Aśvatthāmā), just as Vāyu (the wind god), invoked by a victorious ritual, transports Indra to a sacrifice.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 12 Verse 22. Thus addressed, Krishna carried Arjuna to the proximity of Droṇa's son (Aśvatthāmā), just as Vāyu (the wind god), invoked by a victorious ritual, transports Indra to a sacrifice.

sanskrit: तमामन्त्र्यैकमनसा केशवो द्रौणिमब्रवीत् ।अश्वत्थामन्स्थिरो भूत्वा प्रहराशु सहस्व च ॥२३॥
transliteration: tamāmantryaikamanasā keśavo drauṇimabravīt ,aśvatthāmansthiro bhūtvā praharāśu sahasva ca.
english: Having addressed him (Aśvatthāmā) with a focused mind, Keśava (Krishna) said to Droṇa's son: 'O Aśvatthāmā, remain resolute, strike swiftly, and endure!'
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 12 Verse 23. Having addressed him (Aśvatthāmā) with a focused mind, Keśava (Krishna) said to Droṇa's son: 'O Aśvatthāmā, remain resolute, strike swiftly, and endure!'

sanskrit: निर्वेष्टुं भर्तृपिण्डं हि कालोऽयमुपजीविनाम् ।सूक्ष्मो विवादो विप्राणां स्थूलौ क्षात्रौ जयाजयौ ॥२४॥
transliteration: nirveṣṭuṁ bhartṛpiṇḍaṁ hi kālo'yamupajīvinām ,sūkṣmo vivādo viprāṇāṁ sthūlau kṣātrau jayājayau.
english: Indeed, this is the time for those who depend on others to repay their master's sustenance. For Brahmins (viprāḥ), debates are subtle; for warriors, victory and defeat are stark (and consequential).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 12 Verse 24. Indeed, this is the time for those who depend on others to repay their master's sustenance. For Brahmins (viprāḥ), debates are subtle; for warriors, victory and defeat are stark (and consequential).

sanskrit: यां न संक्षमसे मोहाद्दिव्यां पार्थस्य सत्क्रियाम् ।तामाप्तुमिच्छन्युध्यस्व स्थिरो भूत्वाद्य पाण्डवम् ॥२५॥
transliteration: yāṁ na saṁkṣamase mohāddivyāṁ pārthasya satkriyām ,tāmāptumicchanyudhyasva sthiro bhūtvādya pāṇḍavam.
english: That divine honor (satkriyā) of Arjuna which you do not tolerate out of delusion, desiring to attain that yourself, remain resolute today and fight the Pāṇḍava (Arjuna).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 12 Verse 25. That divine honor (satkriyā) of Arjuna which you do not tolerate out of delusion, desiring to attain that yourself, remain resolute today and fight the Pāṇḍava (Arjuna).

sanskrit: इत्युक्तो वासुदेवेन तथेत्युक्त्वा द्विजोत्तमः ।विव्याध केशवं षष्ट्या नाराचैरर्जुनं त्रिभिः ॥२६॥
transliteration: ityukto vāsudevena tathetyuktvā dvijottamaḥ ,vivyādha keśavaṁ ṣaṣṭyā nārācairarjunaṁ tribhiḥ.
english: Having been addressed thus by Vāsudeva (Kṛṣṇa), the best among the twice-born (dvijottamaḥ) replied, "So be it," and then pierced Keśava (Kṛṣṇa) with sixty iron arrows and Arjuna with three.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 12 Verse 26. Having been addressed thus by Vāsudeva (Kṛṣṇa), the best among the twice-born (dvijottamaḥ) replied, "So be it," and then pierced Keśava (Kṛṣṇa) with sixty iron arrows and Arjuna with three.

sanskrit: तस्यार्जुनः सुसंक्रुद्धस्त्रिभिर्भल्लैः शरासनम् ।चिच्छेदाथान्यदादत्त द्रौणिर्घोरतरं धनुः ॥२७॥
transliteration: tasyārjunaḥ susaṁkruddhastribhirbhallaiḥ śarāsanam ,cicchedāthānyadādatta drauṇirghorataraṁ dhanuḥ.
english: Arjuna, exceedingly enraged at him, severed his bow (śarāsana) with three broad-headed arrows (bhalla). Thereupon, Drauṇi seized another, more formidable bow.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 12 Verse 27. Arjuna, exceedingly enraged at him, severed his bow (śarāsana) with three broad-headed arrows (bhalla). Thereupon, Drauṇi seized another, more formidable bow.

sanskrit: सज्यं कृत्वा निमेषात्तद्विव्याधार्जुनकेशवौ ।त्रिभिः शरैर्वासुदेवं सहस्रेण च पाण्डवम् ॥२८॥
transliteration: sajyaṁ kṛtvā nimeṣāttadvivyādhārjunakeśavau ,tribhiḥ śarairvāsudevaṁ sahasreṇa ca pāṇḍavam.
english: Stringing that (bow) in an instant, he pierced Arjuna and Keśava (Kṛṣṇa); specifically, Vāsudeva (Kṛṣṇa) with three arrows and the Pāṇḍava (Arjuna) with a thousand.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 12 Verse 28. Stringing that (bow) in an instant, he pierced Arjuna and Keśava (Kṛṣṇa); specifically, Vāsudeva (Kṛṣṇa) with three arrows and the Pāṇḍava (Arjuna) with a thousand.

sanskrit: ततः शरसहस्राणि प्रयुतान्यर्बुदानि च ।ससृजे द्रौणिरायस्तः संस्तभ्य च रणेऽर्जुनम् ॥२९॥
transliteration: tataḥ śarasahasrāṇi prayutānyarbudāni ca ,sasṛje drauṇirāyastaḥ saṁstabhya ca raṇe'rjunam.
english: Then, agitated, Drauṇi released thousands, millions, and tens of millions of arrows, having first checked Arjuna in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 12 Verse 29. Then, agitated, Drauṇi released thousands, millions, and tens of millions of arrows, having first checked Arjuna in battle.

sanskrit: इषुधेर्धनुषो ज्याया अङ्गुलीभ्यश्च मारिष ।बाह्वोः कराभ्यामुरसो वदनघ्राणनेत्रतः ॥३०॥
transliteration: iṣudherdhanuṣo jyāyā aṅgulībhyaśca māriṣa ,bāhvoḥ karābhyāmuraso vadanaghrāṇanetrataḥ.
english: O venerable one, from the quiver, from the bow, from the bowstring, and from the fingers; from the arms, from the hands, from the chest, and from the mouth, nose, and eyes.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 12 Verse 30. O venerable one, from the quiver, from the bow, from the bowstring, and from the fingers; from the arms, from the hands, from the chest, and from the mouth, nose, and eyes.

sanskrit: कर्णाभ्यां शिरसोऽङ्गेभ्यो लोमवर्त्मभ्य एव च ।रथध्वजेभ्यश्च शरा निष्पेतुर्ब्रह्मवादिनः ॥३१॥
transliteration: karṇābhyāṁ śiraso'ṅgebhyo lomavartmabhya eva ca ,rathadhvajebhyaśca śarā niṣpeturbrahmavādinaḥ.
english: And, O reciter of sacred texts (brahmavādin), arrows indeed flew out from the ears, from the head, from the limbs, from the hair-follicles, and from the chariot banners.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 12 Verse 31. And, O reciter of sacred texts (brahmavādin), arrows indeed flew out from the ears, from the head, from the limbs, from the hair-follicles, and from the chariot banners.

sanskrit: शरजालेन महता विद्ध्वा केशवपाण्डवौ ।ननाद मुदितो द्रौणिर्महामेघौघनिस्वनः ॥३२॥
transliteration: śarajālena mahatā viddhvā keśavapāṇḍavau ,nanāda mudito drauṇirmahāmeghaughanisvanaḥ.
english: Having wounded Keśava (Kṛṣṇa) and the Pāṇḍava (Arjuna) with a great shower of arrows, Drauṇi (Aśvatthāman), delighted, roared with a sound like a mass of mighty clouds.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 12 Verse 32. Having wounded Keśava (Kṛṣṇa) and the Pāṇḍava (Arjuna) with a great shower of arrows, Drauṇi (Aśvatthāman), delighted, roared with a sound like a mass of mighty clouds.

sanskrit: तस्य नानदतः श्रुत्वा पाण्डवोऽच्युतमब्रवीत् ।पश्य माधव दौरात्म्यं द्रोणपुत्रस्य मां प्रति ॥३३॥
transliteration: tasya nānadataḥ śrutvā pāṇḍavo'cyutamabravīt ,paśya mādhava daurātmyaṁ droṇaputrasya māṁ prati.
english: Having heard his (Drauṇi's) roaring, the Pāṇḍava (Arjuna) spoke to Acyuta (Kṛṣṇa): "Behold, O Mādhava (Kṛṣṇa), the wickedness of Droṇa's son (Aśvatthāman) directed towards me!"
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 12 Verse 33. Having heard his (Drauṇi's) roaring, the Pāṇḍava (Arjuna) spoke to Acyuta (Kṛṣṇa): "Behold, O Mādhava (Kṛṣṇa), the wickedness of Droṇa's son (Aśvatthāman) directed towards me!"

sanskrit: वधप्राप्तौ मन्यते नौ प्रवेश्य शरवेश्मनि ।एषोऽस्य हन्मि संकल्पं शिक्षया च बलेन च ॥३४॥
transliteration: vadhaprāptau manyate nau praveśya śaraveśmani ,eṣo'sya hanmi saṁkalpaṁ śikṣayā ca balena ca.
english: He believes that, having led us two into a deadly hail of arrows (literally, an arrow-chamber), we are now facing certain death. However, I will shatter his resolve (saṃkalpa) with both teaching and might.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 12 Verse 34. He believes that, having led us two into a deadly hail of arrows (literally, an arrow-chamber), we are now facing certain death. However, I will shatter his resolve (saṃkalpa) with both teaching and might.

sanskrit: अश्वत्थाम्नः शरानस्तांश्छित्त्वैकैकं त्रिधा त्रिधा ।व्यधमद्भरतश्रेष्ठो नीहारमिव मारुतः ॥३५॥
transliteration: aśvatthāmnaḥ śarānastāṁśchittvaikaikaṁ tridhā tridhā ,vyadhamadbharataśreṣṭho nīhāramiva mārutaḥ.
english: The best of the Bhāratas (Bharataśreṣṭha) cut each of Aśvatthāman's shot arrows into three pieces, just as the wind (māruta) scatters mist.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 12 Verse 35. The best of the Bhāratas (Bharataśreṣṭha) cut each of Aśvatthāman's shot arrows into three pieces, just as the wind (māruta) scatters mist.

sanskrit: ततः संशप्तकान्भूयः साश्वसूतरथद्विपान् ।ध्वजपत्तिगणानुग्रैर्बाणैर्विव्याध पाण्डवः ॥३६॥
transliteration: tataḥ saṁśaptakānbhūyaḥ sāśvasūtarathadvipān ,dhvajapattigaṇānugrairbāṇairvivyādha pāṇḍavaḥ.
english: Then, the Pāṇḍava (Arjuna) once more pierced the Saṃśaptakas - along with their horses, charioteers, chariots, elephants, banners, and infantry divisions - with fierce arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 12 Verse 36. Then, the Pāṇḍava (Arjuna) once more pierced the Saṃśaptakas - along with their horses, charioteers, chariots, elephants, banners, and infantry divisions - with fierce arrows.

sanskrit: ये ये ददृशिरे तत्र यद्यद्रूपं यथा यथा ।ते ते तत्तच्छरैर्व्याप्तं मेनिरेऽऽत्मानमेव च ॥३७॥
transliteration: ye ye dadṛśire tatra yadyadrūpaṁ yathā yathā ,te te tattaccharairvyāptaṁ menire''tmānameva ca.
english: Whoever saw any particular sight (rūpa) there, in whatever way, each of them perceived that very sight, and even their own self (ātman), to be completely pervaded by arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 12 Verse 37. Whoever saw any particular sight (rūpa) there, in whatever way, each of them perceived that very sight, and even their own self (ātman), to be completely pervaded by arrows.

sanskrit: ते गाण्डीवप्रणुदिता नानारूपाः पतत्रिणः ।क्रोशे साग्रे स्थितान्घ्नन्ति द्विपांश्च पुरुषान्रणे ॥३८॥
transliteration: te gāṇḍīvapraṇuditā nānārūpāḥ patatriṇaḥ ,krośe sāgre sthitānghnanti dvipāṁśca puruṣānraṇe.
english: Those arrows of various forms, propelled by the Gāṇḍīva bow, kill men and elephants standing at a distance of more than a krosa in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 12 Verse 38. Those arrows of various forms, propelled by the Gāṇḍīva bow, kill men and elephants standing at a distance of more than a krosa in battle.

sanskrit: भल्लैश्छिन्नाः कराः पेतुः करिणां मदकर्षिणाम् ।छिन्ना यथा परशुभिः प्रवृद्धाः शरदि द्रुमाः ॥३९॥
transliteration: bhallaiśchinnāḥ karāḥ petuḥ kariṇāṁ madakarṣiṇām ,chinnā yathā paraśubhiḥ pravṛddhāḥ śaradi drumāḥ.
english: The severed trunks of the powerful, ichor-emitting elephants fell, just as well-grown trees, cut down by axes, fall in autumn.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 12 Verse 39. The severed trunks of the powerful, ichor-emitting elephants fell, just as well-grown trees, cut down by axes, fall in autumn.

sanskrit: पश्चात्तु शैलवत्पेतुस्ते गजाः सह सादिभिः ।वज्रिवज्रप्रमथिता यथैवाद्रिचयास्तथा ॥४०॥
transliteration: paścāttu śailavatpetuste gajāḥ saha sādibhiḥ ,vajrivajrapramathitā yathaivādricayāstathā.
english: But afterwards, those elephants, along with their riders, fell like mountains, just as piles of mountains are similarly crushed by the thunderbolt of Indra.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 12 Verse 40. But afterwards, those elephants, along with their riders, fell like mountains, just as piles of mountains are similarly crushed by the thunderbolt of Indra.

sanskrit: गन्धर्वनगराकारान्विधिवत्कल्पितान्रथान् ।विनीतजवनान्युक्तानास्थितान्युद्धदुर्मदान् ॥४१॥
transliteration: gandharvanagarākārānvidhivatkalpitānrathān ,vinītajavanānyuktānāsthitānyuddhadurmadān.
english: Chariots appearing like cities of the Gandharvas, properly prepared, equipped with well-trained, swift horses, and mounted by warriors fiercely arrogant in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 12 Verse 41. Chariots appearing like cities of the Gandharvas, properly prepared, equipped with well-trained, swift horses, and mounted by warriors fiercely arrogant in battle.

sanskrit: शरैर्विशकलीकुर्वन्नमित्रानभ्यवीवृषत् ।अलंकृतानश्वसादीन्पत्तींश्चाहन्धनंजयः ॥४२॥
transliteration: śarairviśakalīkurvannamitrānabhyavīvṛṣat ,alaṁkṛtānaśvasādīnpattīṁścāhandhanaṁjayaḥ.
english: Dhananjaya, tearing his enemies to pieces with arrows, relentlessly showered them [with weapons] and struck down the adorned horse-riders and foot-soldiers.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 12 Verse 42. Dhananjaya, tearing his enemies to pieces with arrows, relentlessly showered them [with weapons] and struck down the adorned horse-riders and foot-soldiers.

sanskrit: धनंजययुगान्तार्कः संशप्तकमहार्णवम् ।व्यशोषयत दुःशोषं तीव्रैः शरगभस्तिभिः ॥४३॥
transliteration: dhanaṁjayayugāntārkaḥ saṁśaptakamahārṇavam ,vyaśoṣayata duḥśoṣaṁ tīvraiḥ śaragabhastibhiḥ.
english: Dhananjaya, like the sun at the end of an age, dried up the great ocean of the Saṃśaptakas, which was otherwise difficult to exhaust, with his fierce arrow-rays.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 12 Verse 43. Dhananjaya, like the sun at the end of an age, dried up the great ocean of the Saṃśaptakas, which was otherwise difficult to exhaust, with his fierce arrow-rays.

sanskrit: पुनर्द्रौणिमहाशैलं नाराचैः सूर्यसंनिभैः ।निर्बिभेद महावेगैस्त्वरन्वज्रीव पर्वतम् ॥४४॥
transliteration: punardrauṇimahāśailaṁ nārācaiḥ sūryasaṁnibhaiḥ ,nirbibheda mahāvegaistvaranvajrīva parvatam.
english: Again, he swiftly pierced Droṇa's son (Aśvatthāman), who was like a great mountain, with forceful, sun-like iron arrows, just as Indra (Vajrin) splits a mountain.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 12 Verse 44. Again, he swiftly pierced Droṇa's son (Aśvatthāman), who was like a great mountain, with forceful, sun-like iron arrows, just as Indra (Vajrin) splits a mountain.

sanskrit: तमाचार्यसुतः क्रुद्धः साश्वयन्तारमाशुगैः ।युयुत्सुर्नाशकद्योद्धुं पार्थस्तानन्तराच्छिनत् ॥४५॥
transliteration: tamācāryasutaḥ kruddhaḥ sāśvayantāramāśugaiḥ ,yuyutsurnāśakadyoddhuṁ pārthastānantarācchinat.
english: Enraged, the teacher's son (Aśvatthāman), wishing to fight him (Arjuna) with swift arrows, was unable to engage Arjuna, who was with his horse and charioteer; instead, Partha severed those [arrows] midway.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 12 Verse 45. Enraged, the teacher's son (Aśvatthāman), wishing to fight him (Arjuna) with swift arrows, was unable to engage Arjuna, who was with his horse and charioteer; instead, Partha severed those [arrows] midway.

sanskrit: ततः परमसंक्रुद्धः काण्डकोशानवासृजत् ।अश्वत्थामाभिरूपाय गृहानतिथये यथा ॥४६॥
transliteration: tataḥ paramasaṁkruddhaḥ kāṇḍakośānavāsṛjat ,aśvatthāmābhirūpāya gṛhānatithaye yathā.
english: Then, exceedingly enraged, Ashvatthama discharged arrows from his quivers without restraint, just as one would generously offer dwellings to a worthy guest.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 12 Verse 46. Then, exceedingly enraged, Ashvatthama discharged arrows from his quivers without restraint, just as one would generously offer dwellings to a worthy guest.

sanskrit: अथ संशप्तकांस्त्यक्त्वा पाण्डवो द्रौणिमभ्ययात् ।अपाङ्क्तेयमिव त्यक्त्वा दाता पाङ्क्तेयमर्थिनम् ॥४७॥
transliteration: atha saṁśaptakāṁstyaktvā pāṇḍavo drauṇimabhyayāt ,apāṅkteyamiva tyaktvā dātā pāṅkteyamarthinam.
english: Then, the son of Pandu (Arjuna), having abandoned the Samsaptakas, proceeded towards Droni (Ashvatthama), just as a donor might abandon a deserving petitioner, treating them as if they were unworthy.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 12 Verse 47. Then, the son of Pandu (Arjuna), having abandoned the Samsaptakas, proceeded towards Droni (Ashvatthama), just as a donor might abandon a deserving petitioner, treating them as if they were unworthy.

sanskrit: ततः समभवद्युद्धं शुक्राङ्गिरसवर्चसोः ।नक्षत्रमभितो व्योम्नि शुक्राङ्गिरसयोरिव ॥४८॥
transliteration: tataḥ samabhavadyuddhaṁ śukrāṅgirasavarcasoḥ ,nakṣatramabhito vyomni śukrāṅgirasayoriva.
english: Then, a battle commenced between those two (Arjuna and Ashvatthama), who shone with the brilliance of Shukra and Angiras, resembling the celestial clash of Shukra and Angiras themselves around a star in the sky.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 12 Verse 48. Then, a battle commenced between those two (Arjuna and Ashvatthama), who shone with the brilliance of Shukra and Angiras, resembling the celestial clash of Shukra and Angiras themselves around a star in the sky.

sanskrit: संतापयन्तावन्योन्यं दीप्तैः शरगभस्तिभिः ।लोकत्रासकरावास्तां विमार्गस्थौ ग्रहाविव ॥४९॥
transliteration: saṁtāpayantāvanyonyaṁ dīptaiḥ śaragabhastibhiḥ ,lokatrāsakarāvāstāṁ vimārgasthau grahāviva.
english: Harassing each other with their blazing, ray-like arrows, the two warriors became a source of terror to all beings, much like two planets that have strayed from their regular paths.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 12 Verse 49. Harassing each other with their blazing, ray-like arrows, the two warriors became a source of terror to all beings, much like two planets that have strayed from their regular paths.

sanskrit: ततोऽविध्यद्भ्रुवोर्मध्ये नाराचेनार्जुनो भृशम् ।स तेन विबभौ द्रौणिरूर्ध्वरश्मिर्यथा रविः ॥५०॥
transliteration: tato'vidhyadbhruvormadhye nārācenārjuno bhṛśam ,sa tena vibabhau drauṇirūrdhvaraśmiryathā raviḥ.
english: Then Arjuna fiercely pierced Ashvatthama between his eyebrows with an iron arrow. Struck by it, Drauṇi shone like the sun with its rays directed upwards.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 12 Verse 50. Then Arjuna fiercely pierced Ashvatthama between his eyebrows with an iron arrow. Struck by it, Drauṇi shone like the sun with its rays directed upwards.

sanskrit: अथ कृष्णौ शरशतैरश्वत्थाम्नार्दितौ भृशम् ।सरश्मिजालनिकरौ युगान्तार्काविवासतुः ॥५१॥
transliteration: atha kṛṣṇau śaraśatairaśvatthāmnārditau bhṛśam ,saraśmijālanikarau yugāntārkāvivāsatuḥ.
english: Then, the two Krishnas (Krishna and Arjuna), severely afflicted by Ashvatthama with hundreds of arrows, appeared like two suns at the end of a cosmic age (yuga), blazing with networks of rays.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 12 Verse 51. Then, the two Krishnas (Krishna and Arjuna), severely afflicted by Ashvatthama with hundreds of arrows, appeared like two suns at the end of a cosmic age (yuga), blazing with networks of rays.

sanskrit: ततोऽर्जुनः सर्वतोधारमस्त्रमवासृजद्वासुदेवाभिगुप्तः ।द्रौणायनिं चाभ्यहनत्पृषत्कैर्वज्राग्निवैवस्वतदण्डकल्पैः ॥५२॥
transliteration: tato'rjunaḥ sarvatodhāramastra;mavāsṛjadvāsudevābhiguptaḥ ,drauṇāyaniṁ cābhyahanatpṛṣatkai;rvajrāgnivaivasvatadaṇḍakalpaiḥ.
english: Then Arjuna, protected by Vasudeva (Krishna), released an all-penetrating missile. And he struck Ashvatthama (Drauṇāyani) with arrows that were like the thunderbolt, fire, and the mace of Yama (Vaivasvata).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 12 Verse 52. Then Arjuna, protected by Vasudeva (Krishna), released an all-penetrating missile. And he struck Ashvatthama (Drauṇāyani) with arrows that were like the thunderbolt, fire, and the mace of Yama (Vaivasvata).

sanskrit: स केशवं चार्जुनं चातितेजा विव्याध मर्मस्वतिरौद्रकर्मा ।बाणैः सुमुक्तैरतितीव्रवेगैर्यैराहतो मृत्युरपि व्यथेत ॥५३॥
transliteration: sa keśavaṁ cārjunaṁ cātitejā; vivyādha marmasvatiraudrakarmā ,bāṇaiḥ sumuktairatitīvravegai;ryairāhato mṛtyurapi vyatheta.
english: Ashvatthama (saḥ), of immense prowess (atitejaḥ) and exceedingly fierce actions (atiroudrakarmā), pierced Krishna (Keshava) and Arjuna in their vital points with arrows that were excellently discharged and of extremely sharp velocity – arrows by which even Death itself would be tormented.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 12 Verse 53. Ashvatthama (saḥ), of immense prowess (atitejaḥ) and exceedingly fierce actions (atiroudrakarmā), pierced Krishna (Keshava) and Arjuna in their vital points with arrows that were excellently discharged and of extremely sharp velocity – arrows by which even Death itself would be tormented.

sanskrit: द्रौणेरिषूनर्जुनः संनिवार्य व्यायच्छतस्तद्द्विगुणैः सुपुङ्खैः ।तं साश्वसूतध्वजमेकवीरमावृत्य संशप्तकसैन्यमार्छत् ॥५४॥
transliteration: drauṇeriṣūnarjunaḥ saṁnivārya; vyāyacchatastaddviguṇaiḥ supuṅkhaiḥ ,taṁ sāśvasūtadhvajamekavīra;māvṛtya saṁśaptakasainyamārchat.
english: Arjuna, having blocked the arrows of Droṇa's son, fought back with well-feathered arrows that were twice the number (of those he blocked). Then, the Saṃśaptaka army, having surrounded that lone hero – along with his horses, charioteer, and banner – attacked him.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 12 Verse 54. Arjuna, having blocked the arrows of Droṇa's son, fought back with well-feathered arrows that were twice the number (of those he blocked). Then, the Saṃśaptaka army, having surrounded that lone hero – along with his horses, charioteer, and banner – attacked him.

sanskrit: धनूंषि बाणानिषुधीर्धनुर्ज्याः पाणीन्भुजान्पाणिगतं च शस्त्रम् ।छत्राणि केतूंस्तुरगानथैषां वस्त्राणि माल्यान्यथ भूषणानि ॥५५॥
transliteration: dhanūṁṣi bāṇāniṣudhīrdhanurjyāḥ; pāṇīnbhujānpāṇigataṁ ca śastram ,chatrāṇi ketūṁsturagānathaiṣāṁ; vastrāṇi mālyānyatha bhūṣaṇāni.
english: He (Arjuna) cut down bows, arrows, quivers, bowstrings, hands, arms, and weapons held in hand. Also, their umbrellas, banners, horses, garments, garlands, and ornaments.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 12 Verse 55. He (Arjuna) cut down bows, arrows, quivers, bowstrings, hands, arms, and weapons held in hand. Also, their umbrellas, banners, horses, garments, garlands, and ornaments.

sanskrit: चर्माणि वर्माणि मनोरथांश्च प्रियाणि सर्वाणि शिरांसि चैव ।चिच्छेद पार्थो द्विषतां प्रमुक्तैर्बाणैः स्थितानामपराङ्मुखानाम् ॥५६॥
transliteration: carmāṇi varmāṇi manorathāṁśca; priyāṇi sarvāṇi śirāṁsi caiva ,ciccheda pārtho dviṣatāṁ pramuktai;rbāṇaiḥ sthitānāmaparāṅmukhānām.
english: Pārtha (Arjuna) severed shields, armors, and cherished desires, along with all their dear possessions and even their heads, with arrows released against those enemies who stood (their ground) and did not turn away.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 12 Verse 56. Pārtha (Arjuna) severed shields, armors, and cherished desires, along with all their dear possessions and even their heads, with arrows released against those enemies who stood (their ground) and did not turn away.

sanskrit: सुकल्पिताः स्यन्दनवाजिनागाः समास्थिताः कृतयत्नैर्नृवीरैः ।पार्थेरितैर्बाणगणैर्निरस्तास्तैरेव सार्धं नृवरैर्निपेतुः ॥५७॥
transliteration: sukalpitāḥ syandanavājināgāḥ; samāsthitāḥ kṛtayatnairnṛvīraiḥ ,pārtheritairbāṇagaṇairnirastā;staireva sārdhaṁ nṛvarairnipetuḥ.
english: The well-equipped chariots, horses, and elephants, mounted by valiant heroes who had made strenuous efforts, were struck down by the multitude of arrows shot by Pārtha (Arjuna), and fell along with those very best of men.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 12 Verse 57. The well-equipped chariots, horses, and elephants, mounted by valiant heroes who had made strenuous efforts, were struck down by the multitude of arrows shot by Pārtha (Arjuna), and fell along with those very best of men.

sanskrit: पद्मार्कपूर्णेन्दुसमाननानि किरीटमालामुकुटोत्कटानि ।भल्लार्धचन्द्रक्षुरहिंसितानि प्रपेतुरुर्व्यां नृशिरांस्यजस्रम् ॥५८॥
transliteration: padmārkapūrṇendusamānanāni; kirīṭamālāmukuṭotkaṭāni ,bhallārdhacandrakṣurahiṁsitāni; prapetururvyāṁ nṛśirāṁsyajasram.
english: Human heads, resembling lotuses, suns, and full moons, splendid with crowns, garlands, and diadems, and pierced by lances, crescent-shaped arrows, and razor-sharp arrows, continuously fell upon the earth.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 12 Verse 58. Human heads, resembling lotuses, suns, and full moons, splendid with crowns, garlands, and diadems, and pierced by lances, crescent-shaped arrows, and razor-sharp arrows, continuously fell upon the earth.

sanskrit: अथ द्विपैर्देवपतिद्विपाभैर्देवारिदर्पोल्बणमन्युदर्पैः ।कलिङ्गवङ्गाङ्गनिषादवीरा जिघांसवः पाण्डवमभ्यधावन् ॥५९॥
transliteration: atha dvipairdevapatidvipābhai;rdevāridarpolbaṇamanyudarpaiḥ ,kaliṅgavaṅgāṅganiṣādavīrā; jighāṁsavaḥ pāṇḍavamabhyadhāvan.
english: Then, the heroes of Kalinga, Vanga, Anga, and Niṣāda, desiring to kill the Pāṇḍava (Arjuna), rushed forward with their elephants, which resembled the elephants of the lord of the gods and whose arrogance and fury were magnified by the pride of the enemies of the gods.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 12 Verse 59. Then, the heroes of Kalinga, Vanga, Anga, and Niṣāda, desiring to kill the Pāṇḍava (Arjuna), rushed forward with their elephants, which resembled the elephants of the lord of the gods and whose arrogance and fury were magnified by the pride of the enemies of the gods.

sanskrit: तेषां द्विपानां विचकर्त पार्थो वर्माणि मर्माणि करान्नियन्तॄन् ।ध्वजाः पताकाश्च ततः प्रपेतुर्वज्राहतानीव गिरेः शिरांसि ॥६०॥
transliteration: teṣāṁ dvipānāṁ vicakarta pārtho; varmāṇi marmāṇi karānniyantṝn ,dhvajāḥ patākāśca tataḥ prapetu;rvajrāhatānīva gireḥ śirāṁsi.
english: Arjuna severed the armors, vital spots, trunks, and mahouts of those elephants. Then, their banners and flags fell down, just like mountain peaks struck by a thunderbolt.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 12 Verse 60. Arjuna severed the armors, vital spots, trunks, and mahouts of those elephants. Then, their banners and flags fell down, just like mountain peaks struck by a thunderbolt.

sanskrit: तेषु प्ररुग्णेषु गुरोस्तनूजं बाणैः किरीटी नवसूर्यवर्णैः ।प्रच्छादयामास महाभ्रजालैर्वायुः समुद्युक्तमिवांशुमन्तम् ॥६१॥
transliteration: teṣu prarugṇeṣu gurostanūjaṁ; bāṇaiḥ kirīṭī navasūryavarṇaiḥ ,pracchādayāmāsa mahābhrajālai;rvāyuḥ samudyuktamivāṁśumantam.
english: When those (warriors and elephants) were destroyed, the crowned one (Arjuna) covered the son of the preceptor (Aśvatthāmā) with arrows resembling the color of the newly risen sun, just as the wind covers the rising sun with great masses of clouds.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 12 Verse 61. When those (warriors and elephants) were destroyed, the crowned one (Arjuna) covered the son of the preceptor (Aśvatthāmā) with arrows resembling the color of the newly risen sun, just as the wind covers the rising sun with great masses of clouds.

sanskrit: ततोऽर्जुनेषूनिषुभिर्निरस्य द्रौणिः शरैरर्जुनवासुदेवौ ।प्रच्छादयित्व दिवि चन्द्रसूर्यौ ननाद सोऽम्भोद इवातपान्ते ॥६२॥
transliteration: tato'rjuneṣūniṣubhirnirasya; drauṇiḥ śarairarjunavāsudevau ,pracchādayitva divi candrasūryau; nanāda so'mbhoda ivātapānte.
english: Then, Ashvatthama (Drauṇi) first scattered Arjuna's arrows with his own arrows, and just like a cloud covering the sun and moon in the sky at the end of the hot season, he roared, covering both Arjuna and Krishna (Vāsudeva) with his shafts.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 12 Verse 62. Then, Ashvatthama (Drauṇi) first scattered Arjuna's arrows with his own arrows, and just like a cloud covering the sun and moon in the sky at the end of the hot season, he roared, covering both Arjuna and Krishna (Vāsudeva) with his shafts.

sanskrit: तमर्जुनस्तांश्च पुनस्त्वदीयानभ्यर्दितस्तैरविकृत्तशस्त्रैः ।बाणान्धकारं सहसैव कृत्वा विव्याध सर्वानिषुभिः सुपुङ्खैः ॥६३॥
transliteration: tamarjunastāṁśca punastvadīyā;nabhyarditastairavikṛttaśastraiḥ ,bāṇāndhakāraṁ sahasaiva kṛtvā; vivyādha sarvāniṣubhiḥ supuṅkhaiḥ.
english: Then, Arjuna, though assailed by those warriors of yours whose weapons were unceasing, suddenly created a darkness of arrows and pierced all of them, including Ashvatthama, with well-feathered shafts.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 12 Verse 63. Then, Arjuna, though assailed by those warriors of yours whose weapons were unceasing, suddenly created a darkness of arrows and pierced all of them, including Ashvatthama, with well-feathered shafts.

sanskrit: नाप्याददत्संदधन्नैव मुञ्चन्बाणान्रणेऽदृश्यत सव्यसाची ।हतांश्च नागांस्तुरगान्पदातीन्संस्यूतदेहान्ददृशू रथांश्च ॥६४॥
transliteration: nāpyādadatsaṁdadhannaiva muñca;nbāṇānraṇe'dṛśyata savyasācī ,hatāṁśca nāgāṁsturagānpadātī;nsaṁsyūtadehāndadṛśū rathāṁśca.
english: In battle, Savyasachin (Arjuna) was not seen taking, nor aiming, nor releasing arrows. Yet, they saw elephants, horses, and foot-soldiers, whose bodies were pierced through, lying dead, and chariots as well.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 12 Verse 64. In battle, Savyasachin (Arjuna) was not seen taking, nor aiming, nor releasing arrows. Yet, they saw elephants, horses, and foot-soldiers, whose bodies were pierced through, lying dead, and chariots as well.

sanskrit: संधाय नाराचवरान्दशाशु द्रौणिस्त्वरन्नेकमिवोत्ससर्ज ।तेषां च पञ्चार्जुनमभ्यविध्यन्पञ्चाच्युतं निर्बिभिदुः सुमुक्ताः ॥६५॥
transliteration: saṁdhāya nārācavarāndaśāśu; drauṇistvarannekamivotsasarja ,teṣāṁ ca pañcārjunamabhyavidhya;npañcācyutaṁ nirbibhiduḥ sumuktāḥ.
english: Ashvatthama (Drauṇi), hurrying, quickly fitted ten excellent iron arrows and released them as if they were one. Of these, five pierced Arjuna, and the other five, well-shot, penetrated Krishna (Acyuta).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 12 Verse 65. Ashvatthama (Drauṇi), hurrying, quickly fitted ten excellent iron arrows and released them as if they were one. Of these, five pierced Arjuna, and the other five, well-shot, penetrated Krishna (Acyuta).

sanskrit: तैराहतौ सर्वमनुष्यमुख्यावसृक्क्षरन्तौ धनदेन्द्रकल्पौ ।समाप्तविद्येन यथाभिभूतौ हतौ स्विदेतौ किमु मेनिरेऽन्ये ॥६६॥
transliteration: tairāhatau sarvamanuṣyamukhyā;vasṛkkṣarantau dhanadendrakalpau ,samāptavidyena yathābhibhūtau; hatau svidetau kimu menire'nye.
english: Struck by those (enemies), these two, the foremost among all men, resembling Kubera and Indra, and dripping blood, were indeed overcome as if by one with perfected knowledge (vidyā). Did others then think that they were killed?
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 12 Verse 66. Struck by those (enemies), these two, the foremost among all men, resembling Kubera and Indra, and dripping blood, were indeed overcome as if by one with perfected knowledge (vidyā). Did others then think that they were killed?

sanskrit: अथार्जुनं प्राह दशार्हनाथः प्रमाद्यसे किं जहि योधमेतम् ।कुर्याद्धि दोषं समुपेक्षितोऽसौ कष्टो भवेद्व्याधिरिवाक्रियावान् ॥६७॥
transliteration: athārjunaṁ prāha daśārhanāthaḥ; pramādyase kiṁ jahi yodhametam ,kuryāddhi doṣaṁ samupekṣito'sau; kaṣṭo bhavedvyādhirivākriyāvān.
english: Then, the Lord of the Daśārhas (Krishna) said to Arjuna, "Why are you negligent? Slay this warrior! For if he is neglected, he will surely cause harm, just as a disease left untreated becomes troublesome."
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 12 Verse 67. Then, the Lord of the Daśārhas (Krishna) said to Arjuna, "Why are you negligent? Slay this warrior! For if he is neglected, he will surely cause harm, just as a disease left untreated becomes troublesome."

sanskrit: तथेति चोक्त्वाच्युतमप्रमादी द्रौणिं प्रयत्नादिषुभिस्ततक्ष ।छित्त्वाश्वरश्मींस्तुरगानविध्यत्ते तं रणादूहुरतीव दूरम् ॥६८॥
transliteration: tatheti coktvācyutamapramādī; drauṇiṁ prayatnādiṣubhistatakṣa ,chittvāśvaraśmīṁsturagānavidhya;tte taṁ raṇādūhuratīva dūram.
english: And having said 'So be it' to Acyuta (Krishna), the vigilant (Arjuna) diligently pierced Droṇa's son (Drauṇi) with arrows. Having cut his horses' reins, he pierced the horses. They (the horses) then carried him far away from the battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 12 Verse 68. And having said 'So be it' to Acyuta (Krishna), the vigilant (Arjuna) diligently pierced Droṇa's son (Drauṇi) with arrows. Having cut his horses' reins, he pierced the horses. They (the horses) then carried him far away from the battle.

sanskrit: आवृत्य नेयेष पुनस्तु युद्धं पार्थेन सार्धं मतिमान्विमृश्य ।जानञ्जयं नियतं वृष्णिवीरे धनंजये चाङ्गिरसां वरिष्ठः ॥६९॥
transliteration: āvṛtya neyeṣa punastu yuddhaṁ; pārthena sārdhaṁ matimānvimṛśya ,jānañjayaṁ niyataṁ vṛṣṇivīre; dhanaṁjaye cāṅgirasāṁ variṣṭhaḥ.
english: But the intelligent and discerning (Aśvatthāman), the foremost among the Aṅgirasas, having turned back and deliberated, did not wish to fight again with Pārtha (Arjuna), knowing that victory (jaya) was certain for the Vṛṣṇi hero (Krishna) and for Dhanaṃjaya (Arjuna).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 12 Verse 69. But the intelligent and discerning (Aśvatthāman), the foremost among the Aṅgirasas, having turned back and deliberated, did not wish to fight again with Pārtha (Arjuna), knowing that victory (jaya) was certain for the Vṛṣṇi hero (Krishna) and for Dhanaṃjaya (Arjuna).

sanskrit: प्रतीपकाये तु रणादश्वत्थाम्नि हृते हयैः ।मन्त्रौषधिक्रियादानैर्व्याधौ देहादिवाहृते ॥७०॥
transliteration: pratīpakāye tu raṇādaśvatthāmni hṛte hayaiḥ ,mantrauṣadhikriyādānairvyādhau dehādivāhṛte.
english: But when Aśvatthāman was carried away from the battle by horses, it was as if a disease were removed from the body of an opponent by means of spells, medicines, rituals, and charitable gifts.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 12 Verse 70. But when Aśvatthāman was carried away from the battle by horses, it was as if a disease were removed from the body of an opponent by means of spells, medicines, rituals, and charitable gifts.

sanskrit: संशप्तकानभिमुखौ प्रयातौ केशवार्जुनौ ।वातोद्धूतपताकेन स्यन्दनेनौघनादिना ॥७१॥
transliteration: saṁśaptakānabhimukhau prayātau keśavārjunau ,vātoddhūtapatākena syandanenaughanādinā.
english: Keśava and Arjuna proceeded, facing the Saṃśaptakas, in a chariot whose banner was unfurled by the wind and which roared like a thunderous cloud.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 12 Verse 71. Keśava and Arjuna proceeded, facing the Saṃśaptakas, in a chariot whose banner was unfurled by the wind and which roared like a thunderous cloud.

sanskrit: संजय उवाच ।अथोत्तरेण पाण्डूनां सेनायां ध्वनिरुत्थितः ।रथनागाश्वपत्तीनां दण्डधारेण वध्यताम् ॥१॥
transliteration: saṁjaya uvāca ,athottareṇa pāṇḍūnāṁ senāyāṁ dhvanirutthitaḥ ,rathanāgāśvapattīnāṁ daṇḍadhāreṇa vadhyatām.
english: Saṃjaya said: Then, to the north, a tremendous sound arose in the army of the Pāṇḍavas, as chariots, elephants, horses, and foot soldiers were being killed by Yama, the wielder of the rod.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 13 Verse 1. Saṃjaya said: Then, to the north, a tremendous sound arose in the army of the Pāṇḍavas, as chariots, elephants, horses, and foot soldiers were being killed by Yama, the wielder of the rod.

sanskrit: निवर्तयित्वा तु रथं केशवोऽर्जुनमब्रवीत् ।वाहयन्नेव तुरगान्गरुडानिलरंहसः ॥२॥
transliteration: nivartayitvā tu rathaṁ keśavo'rjunamabravīt ,vāhayanneva turagāngaruḍānilaraṁhasaḥ.
english: But Keśava, having turned back the chariot, spoke to Arjuna, while still driving the horses whose swiftness was like that of Garuḍa and the wind.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 13 Verse 2. But Keśava, having turned back the chariot, spoke to Arjuna, while still driving the horses whose swiftness was like that of Garuḍa and the wind.

sanskrit: मागधोऽथाप्यतिक्रान्तो द्विरदेन प्रमाथिना ।भगदत्तादनवरः शिक्षया च बलेन च ॥३॥
transliteration: māgadho'thāpyatikrānto dviradena pramāthinā ,bhagadattādanavaraḥ śikṣayā ca balena ca.
english: The Magadha king, who was also excellent, with his formidable, crushing elephant, was not inferior to Bhagadatta in both training and strength.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 13 Verse 3. The Magadha king, who was also excellent, with his formidable, crushing elephant, was not inferior to Bhagadatta in both training and strength.

sanskrit: एनं हत्वा निहन्तासि पुनः संशप्तकानिति ।वाक्यान्ते प्रापयत्पार्थं दण्डधारान्तिकं प्रति ॥४॥
transliteration: enaṁ hatvā nihantāsi punaḥ saṁśaptakāniti ,vākyānte prāpayatpārthaṁ daṇḍadhārāntikaṁ prati.
english: He (the enemy) said, 'Having killed this one, you will again kill the Samśaptakas (saṃśaptaka).' After this statement, he dispatched Arjuna (pārtha) towards the realm of Yama (daṇḍadhāra).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 13 Verse 4. He (the enemy) said, 'Having killed this one, you will again kill the Samśaptakas (saṃśaptaka).' After this statement, he dispatched Arjuna (pārtha) towards the realm of Yama (daṇḍadhāra).

sanskrit: स मागधानां प्रवरोऽङ्कुशग्रहो ग्रहेष्वसह्यो विकचो यथा ग्रहः ।सपत्नसेनां प्रममाथ दारुणो महीं समग्रां विकचो यथा ग्रहः ॥५॥
transliteration: sa māgadhānāṁ pravaro'ṅkuśagraho; graheṣvasahyo vikaco yathā grahaḥ ,sapatnasenāṁ pramamātha dāruṇo; mahīṁ samagrāṁ vikaco yathā grahaḥ.
english: He, the chief among the Magadhas, a wielder of the elephant-goad, was irresistible among the adversaries, like a mighty, blazing planet. That formidable one crushed the entire enemy army, and indeed the entire earth, like a mighty, blazing planet.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 13 Verse 5. He, the chief among the Magadhas, a wielder of the elephant-goad, was irresistible among the adversaries, like a mighty, blazing planet. That formidable one crushed the entire enemy army, and indeed the entire earth, like a mighty, blazing planet.

sanskrit: सुकल्पितं दानवनागसंनिभं महाभ्रसंह्रादममित्रमर्दनम् ।रथाश्वमातङ्गगणान्सहस्रशः समास्थितो हन्ति शरैर्द्विपानपि ॥६॥
transliteration: sukalpitaṁ dānavanāgasaṁnibhaṁ; mahābhrasaṁhrādamamitramardanam ,rathāśvamātaṅgagaṇānsahasraśaḥ; samāsthito hanti śarairdvipānapi.
english: Having (an elephant that is) well-equipped, resembling a demon-elephant (dānava-nāga), with a great cloud-like roar, and crushing foes, he, being mounted, kills thousands of chariots, horses, and hosts of elephants, and even (other) elephants with his arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 13 Verse 6. Having (an elephant that is) well-equipped, resembling a demon-elephant (dānava-nāga), with a great cloud-like roar, and crushing foes, he, being mounted, kills thousands of chariots, horses, and hosts of elephants, and even (other) elephants with his arrows.

sanskrit: रथानधिष्ठाय सवाजिसारथीन्रथांश्च पद्भिस्त्वरितो व्यपोथयत् ।द्विपांश्च पद्भ्यां चरणैः करेण च द्विपास्थितो हन्ति स कालचक्रवत् ॥७॥
transliteration: rathānadhiṣṭhāya savājisārathī;nrathāṁśca padbhistvarito vyapothayat ,dvipāṁśca padbhyāṁ caraṇaiḥ kareṇa ca; dvipāsthito hanti sa kālacakravat.
english: Mounted on an elephant, he swiftly crushed chariots along with their horses and charioteers, having charged upon them with (his elephant's) feet. And that (same warrior), on an elephant, slays (other) elephants with (his elephant's) two feet, (its other) feet, and its trunk, like the destructive wheel of time.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 13 Verse 7. Mounted on an elephant, he swiftly crushed chariots along with their horses and charioteers, having charged upon them with (his elephant's) feet. And that (same warrior), on an elephant, slays (other) elephants with (his elephant's) two feet, (its other) feet, and its trunk, like the destructive wheel of time.

sanskrit: नरांश्च कार्ष्णायसवर्मभूषणान्निपात्य साश्वानपि पत्तिभिः सह ।व्यपोथयद्दन्तिवरेण शुष्मिणा सशब्दवत्स्थूलनडान्यथा तथा ॥८॥
transliteration: narāṁśca kārṣṇāyasavarmabhūṣaṇā;nnipātya sāśvānapi pattibhiḥ saha ,vyapothayaddantivareṇa śuṣmiṇā; saśabdavatsthūlanaḍānyathā tathā.
english: Having thrown down men adorned with iron armor, and also horses along with foot soldiers, he crushed them with his powerful, excellent elephant, loudly, just as thick reeds are (crushed).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 13 Verse 8. Having thrown down men adorned with iron armor, and also horses along with foot soldiers, he crushed them with his powerful, excellent elephant, loudly, just as thick reeds are (crushed).

sanskrit: अथार्जुनो ज्यातलनेमिनिस्वने मृदङ्गभेरीबहुशङ्खनादिते ।नराश्वमातङ्गसहस्रनादितै रथोत्तमेनाभ्यपतद्द्विपोत्तमम् ॥९॥
transliteration: athārjuno jyātalaneminisvane; mṛdaṅgabherībahuśaṅkhanādite ,narāśvamātaṅgasahasranāditai; rathottamenābhyapataddvipottamam.
english: Then Arjuna, amidst the clamor of bowstrings, hand-guards, and chariot wheels, and the resounding of drums, kettle-drums, and many conches, all made vibrant by the roars of thousands of men, horses, and elephants, charged with his excellent chariot towards the best elephant.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 13 Verse 9. Then Arjuna, amidst the clamor of bowstrings, hand-guards, and chariot wheels, and the resounding of drums, kettle-drums, and many conches, all made vibrant by the roars of thousands of men, horses, and elephants, charged with his excellent chariot towards the best elephant.

sanskrit: ततोऽर्जुनं द्वादशभिः शरोत्तमैर्जनार्दनं षोडशभिः समार्दयत् ।स दण्डधारस्तुरगांस्त्रिभिस्त्रिभिस्ततो ननाद प्रजहास चासकृत् ॥१०॥
transliteration: tato'rjunaṁ dvādaśabhiḥ śarottamai;rjanārdanaṁ ṣoḍaśabhiḥ samārdayat ,sa daṇḍadhārasturagāṁstribhistribhi;stato nanāda prajahāsa cāsakṛt.
english: Then, the wielder of the staff wounded Arjuna with twelve excellent arrows and Janardana (Krishna) with sixteen (arrows). He then struck the horses with three (arrows) each, and roared and laughed repeatedly.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 13 Verse 10. Then, the wielder of the staff wounded Arjuna with twelve excellent arrows and Janardana (Krishna) with sixteen (arrows). He then struck the horses with three (arrows) each, and roared and laughed repeatedly.

sanskrit: ततोऽस्य पार्थः सगुणेषुकार्मुकं चकर्त भल्लैर्ध्वजमप्यलंकृतम् ।पुनर्नियन्तॄन्सह पादगोप्तृभिस्ततस्तु चुक्रोध गिरिव्रजेश्वरः ॥११॥
transliteration: tato'sya pārthaḥ saguṇeṣukārmukaṁ; cakarta bhallairdhvajamapyalaṁkṛtam ,punarniyantṝnsaha pādagoptṛbhi;statastu cukrodha girivrajeśvaraḥ.
english: Then, Arjuna (Pārthaḥ), with broad-headed arrows (bhallaiḥ), cut down his opponent's strung bow and arrows (saguṇeṣukārmukam) and also his ornamented banner (dhvajam). Thereupon, the Lord of Girivraja (girivrajeśvaraḥ) became enraged, along with his charioteers and foot-guards.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 13 Verse 11. Then, Arjuna (Pārthaḥ), with broad-headed arrows (bhallaiḥ), cut down his opponent's strung bow and arrows (saguṇeṣukārmukam) and also his ornamented banner (dhvajam). Thereupon, the Lord of Girivraja (girivrajeśvaraḥ) became enraged, along with his charioteers and foot-guards.

sanskrit: ततोऽर्जुनं भिन्नकटेन दन्तिना घनाघनेनानिलतुल्यरंहसा ।अतीव चुक्षोभयिषुर्जनार्दनं धनंजयं चाभिजघान तोमरैः ॥१२॥
transliteration: tato'rjunaṁ bhinnakaṭena dantinā; ghanāghanenānilatulyaraṁhasā ,atīva cukṣobhayiṣurjanārdanaṁ; dhanaṁjayaṁ cābhijaghāna tomaraiḥ.
english: Then, greatly desiring to agitate Janardana (Kṛṣṇa) and Dhananjaya (Arjuna), [the Lord of Girivraja] attacked Dhananjaya (Arjuna) with javelins (tomaraiḥ), [while Arjuna was engaged with] an elephant (dantinā) whose temple was broken (bhinnakaṭena), which resembled a dense cloud (ghanāghanena), and possessed the speed of wind (anilatulyaraṃhasā).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 13 Verse 12. Then, greatly desiring to agitate Janardana (Kṛṣṇa) and Dhananjaya (Arjuna), [the Lord of Girivraja] attacked Dhananjaya (Arjuna) with javelins (tomaraiḥ), [while Arjuna was engaged with] an elephant (dantinā) whose temple was broken (bhinnakaṭena), which resembled a dense cloud (ghanāghanena), and possessed the speed of wind (anilatulyaraṃhasā).

sanskrit: अथास्य बाहू द्विपहस्तसंनिभौ शिरश्च पूर्णेन्दुनिभाननं त्रिभिः ।क्षुरैः प्रचिच्छेद सहैव पाण्डवस्ततो द्विपं बाणशतैः समार्दयत् ॥१३॥
transliteration: athāsya bāhū dvipahastasaṁnibhau; śiraśca pūrṇendunibhānanaṁ tribhiḥ ,kṣuraiḥ praciccheda sahaiva pāṇḍava;stato dvipaṁ bāṇaśataiḥ samārdayat.
english: Then, the Pāṇḍava (Pāṇḍavaḥ) Arjuna, at once, cut off his opponent's two arms (bāhū), which resembled elephant trunks (dvipahastasaṃnibhau), and his head (śiraḥ) with a face like the full moon (pūrṇendunibhānanaṃ), using three sharp arrows (kṣuraiḥ). Thereupon, he tormented the elephant (dvipaṃ) with hundreds of arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 13 Verse 13. Then, the Pāṇḍava (Pāṇḍavaḥ) Arjuna, at once, cut off his opponent's two arms (bāhū), which resembled elephant trunks (dvipahastasaṃnibhau), and his head (śiraḥ) with a face like the full moon (pūrṇendunibhānanaṃ), using three sharp arrows (kṣuraiḥ). Thereupon, he tormented the elephant (dvipaṃ) with hundreds of arrows.

sanskrit: स पार्थबाणैस्तपनीयभूषणैः समारुचत्काञ्चनवर्मभृद्द्विपः ।तथा चकाशे निशि पर्वतो यथा दवाग्निना प्रज्वलितौषधिद्रुमः ॥१४॥
transliteration: sa pārthabāṇaistapanīyabhūṣaṇaiḥ; samārucatkāñcanavarmabhṛddvipaḥ ,tathā cakāśe niśi parvato yathā; davāgninā prajvalitauṣadhidrumaḥ.
english: That elephant (dvipaḥ), adorned with golden armor (kāñcanavarmabhṛt), shone forth (samārucat) from Arjuna's arrows (pārthabāṇaiḥ), which were like golden ornaments (tapanīyabhūṣaṇaiḥ). It shone (cakāśe) like a mountain at night (niśi parvataḥ) whose medicinal plants and trees (auṣadhidrumaḥ) are ablaze (prajvalitaḥ) from a forest fire (davāgninā).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 13 Verse 14. That elephant (dvipaḥ), adorned with golden armor (kāñcanavarmabhṛt), shone forth (samārucat) from Arjuna's arrows (pārthabāṇaiḥ), which were like golden ornaments (tapanīyabhūṣaṇaiḥ). It shone (cakāśe) like a mountain at night (niśi parvataḥ) whose medicinal plants and trees (auṣadhidrumaḥ) are ablaze (prajvalitaḥ) from a forest fire (davāgninā).

sanskrit: स वेदनार्तोऽम्बुदनिस्वनो नदंश्चलन्भ्रमन्प्रस्खलितोऽऽतुरो द्रवन् ।पपात रुग्णः सनियन्तृकस्तथा यथा गिरिर्वज्रनिपातचूर्णितः ॥१५॥
transliteration: sa vedanārto'mbudanisvano nadaṁ;ścalanbhramanpraskhalito''turo dravan ,papāta rugṇaḥ saniyantṛkastathā; yathā girirvajranipātacūrṇitaḥ.
english: He, afflicted by pain, roaring like thunder, moving erratically, reeling, stumbling, distressed, and rushing, fell down, wounded, along with his charioteer, just like a mountain shattered by the strike of a thunderbolt.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 13 Verse 15. He, afflicted by pain, roaring like thunder, moving erratically, reeling, stumbling, distressed, and rushing, fell down, wounded, along with his charioteer, just like a mountain shattered by the strike of a thunderbolt.

sanskrit: हिमावदातेन सुवर्णमालिना हिमाद्रिकूटप्रतिमेन दन्तिना ।हते रणे भ्रातरि दण्ड आव्रजज्जिघांसुरिन्द्रावरजं धनंजयम् ॥१६॥
transliteration: himāvadātena suvarṇamālinā; himādrikūṭapratimena dantinā ,hate raṇe bhrātari daṇḍa āvraja;jjighāṁsurindrāvarajaṁ dhanaṁjayam.
english: When his brother was killed in battle by an elephant, one white as snow, adorned with a golden garland, and resembling a Himalayan peak, Daṇḍa advanced, intent on killing Dhananjaya, the younger brother of Indra.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 13 Verse 16. When his brother was killed in battle by an elephant, one white as snow, adorned with a golden garland, and resembling a Himalayan peak, Daṇḍa advanced, intent on killing Dhananjaya, the younger brother of Indra.

sanskrit: स तोमरैरर्ककरप्रभैस्त्रिभिर्जनार्दनं पञ्चभिरेव चार्जुनम् ।समर्पयित्वा विननाद चार्दयंस्ततोऽस्य बाहू विचकर्त पाण्डवः ॥१७॥
transliteration: sa tomarairarkakaraprabhaistribhi;rjanārdanaṁ pañcabhireva cārjunam ,samarpayitvā vinanāda cārdayaṁ;stato'sya bāhū vicakarta pāṇḍavaḥ.
english: Having struck Janardana (Krishna) with three javelins (tomaras) as brilliant as sunbeams, and Arjuna with five such javelins, he roared, afflicting them. Then, the Pāṇḍava (Arjuna) cut off his two arms.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 13 Verse 17. Having struck Janardana (Krishna) with three javelins (tomaras) as brilliant as sunbeams, and Arjuna with five such javelins, he roared, afflicting them. Then, the Pāṇḍava (Arjuna) cut off his two arms.

sanskrit: क्षुरप्रकृत्तौ सुभृशं सतोमरौ च्युताङ्गदौ चन्दनरूषितौ भुजौ ।गजात्पतन्तौ युगपद्विरेजतुर्यथाद्रिशृङ्गात्पतितौ महोरगौ ॥१८॥
transliteration: kṣuraprakṛttau subhṛśaṁ satomarau; cyutāṅgadau candanarūṣitau bhujau ,gajātpatantau yugapadvirejatu;ryathādriśṛṅgātpatitau mahoragau.
english: His two arms, exceedingly severed by razor-edged arrows (kṣurapra), still holding their javelins (tomaras), with their armlets fallen off, and smeared with sandalwood, fell from the elephant. They appeared simultaneously, just like two great serpents fallen from a mountain peak.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 13 Verse 18. His two arms, exceedingly severed by razor-edged arrows (kṣurapra), still holding their javelins (tomaras), with their armlets fallen off, and smeared with sandalwood, fell from the elephant. They appeared simultaneously, just like two great serpents fallen from a mountain peak.

sanskrit: अथार्धचन्द्रेण हृतं किरीटिना पपात दण्डस्य शिरः क्षितिं द्विपात् ।तच्छोणिताभं निपतद्विरेजे दिवाकरोऽस्तादिव पश्चिमां दिशम् ॥१९॥
transliteration: athārdhacandreṇa hṛtaṁ kirīṭinā; papāta daṇḍasya śiraḥ kṣitiṁ dvipāt ,tacchoṇitābhaṁ nipatadvireje; divākaro'stādiva paścimāṁ diśam.
english: Then, struck by the diadem-wearer (Arjuna) with a crescent-shaped arrow, the head of the elephant's trunk fell to the ground. As it fell, shining like blood, it resembled the sun descending into the western sky at sunset.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 13 Verse 19. Then, struck by the diadem-wearer (Arjuna) with a crescent-shaped arrow, the head of the elephant's trunk fell to the ground. As it fell, shining like blood, it resembled the sun descending into the western sky at sunset.

sanskrit: अथ द्विपं श्वेतनगाग्रसंनिभं दिवाकरांशुप्रतिमैः शरोत्तमैः ।बिभेद पार्थः स पपात नानदन्हिमाद्रिकूटः कुलिशाहतो यथा ॥२०॥
transliteration: atha dvipaṁ śvetanagāgrasaṁnibhaṁ; divākarāṁśupratimaiḥ śarottamaiḥ ,bibheda pārthaḥ sa papāta nānada;nhimādrikūṭaḥ kuliśāhato yathā.
english: Then Pārtha (Arjuna) pierced the elephant, which resembled a white mountain peak, with excellent arrows comparable to sunbeams. It fell, roaring, just like a peak of the Himālayan mountain struck by a thunderbolt.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 13 Verse 20. Then Pārtha (Arjuna) pierced the elephant, which resembled a white mountain peak, with excellent arrows comparable to sunbeams. It fell, roaring, just like a peak of the Himālayan mountain struck by a thunderbolt.

sanskrit: ततोऽपरे तत्प्रतिमा गजोत्तमा जिगीषवः संयति सव्यसाचिनम् ।तथा कृतास्तेन यथैव तौ द्विपौ ततः प्रभग्नं सुमहद्रिपोर्बलम् ॥२१॥
transliteration: tato'pare tatpratimā gajottamā; jigīṣavaḥ saṁyati savyasācinam ,tathā kṛtāstena yathaiva tau dvipau; tataḥ prabhagnaṁ sumahadriporbalam.
english: Then, other excellent elephants, resembling the previously mentioned one, eager for victory against Savyasācin (Arjuna) in battle, were similarly dealt with by him, just like those two elephants (previously mentioned). Consequently, the enemy's very great army was completely routed.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 13 Verse 21. Then, other excellent elephants, resembling the previously mentioned one, eager for victory against Savyasācin (Arjuna) in battle, were similarly dealt with by him, just like those two elephants (previously mentioned). Consequently, the enemy's very great army was completely routed.

sanskrit: गजा रथाश्वाः पुरुषाश्च संघशः परस्परघ्नाः परिपेतुराहवे ।परस्परप्रस्खलिताः समाहता भृशं च तत्तत्कुलभाषिणो हताः ॥२२॥
transliteration: gajā rathāśvāḥ puruṣāśca saṁghaśaḥ; parasparaghnāḥ paripeturāhave ,parasparapraskhalitāḥ samāhatā; bhṛśaṁ ca tattatkulabhāṣiṇo hatāḥ.
english: Elephants, chariots, horses, and men, in throngs, striking each other, scattered in battle. Stumbling over one another and thoroughly struck down, they were killed in great numbers, uttering their respective clan names.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 13 Verse 22. Elephants, chariots, horses, and men, in throngs, striking each other, scattered in battle. Stumbling over one another and thoroughly struck down, they were killed in great numbers, uttering their respective clan names.

sanskrit: अथार्जुनं स्वे परिवार्य सैनिकाः पुरंदरं देवगणा इवाब्रुवन् ।अभैष्म यस्मान्मरणादिव प्रजाः स वीर दिष्ट्या निहतस्त्वया रिपुः ॥२३॥
transliteration: athārjunaṁ sve parivārya sainikāḥ; puraṁdaraṁ devagaṇā ivābruvan ,abhaiṣma yasmānmaraṇādiva prajāḥ; sa vīra diṣṭyā nihatastvayā ripuḥ.
english: Then, his own soldiers, having surrounded Arjuna, spoke to him as the hosts of gods would speak to Purandara (Indra): "O hero, we feared him like people fear death, but fortunately, that enemy has been killed by you."
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 13 Verse 23. Then, his own soldiers, having surrounded Arjuna, spoke to him as the hosts of gods would speak to Purandara (Indra): "O hero, we feared him like people fear death, but fortunately, that enemy has been killed by you."

sanskrit: न चेत्परित्रास्य इमाञ्जनान्भयाद्द्विषद्भिरेवं बलिभिः प्रपीडितान् ।तथाभविष्यद्द्विषतां प्रमोदनं यथा हतेष्वेष्विह नोऽरिषु त्वया ॥२४॥
transliteration: na cetparitrāsya imāñjanānbhayā;ddviṣadbhirevaṁ balibhiḥ prapīḍitān ,tathābhaviṣyaddviṣatāṁ pramodanaṁ; yathā hateṣveṣviha no'riṣu tvayā.
english: "If I were not to protect these people, who are thus oppressed by powerful enemies, from fear, then it would be a source of delight for our enemies, just as it is for us when these enemies are killed here by you."
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 13 Verse 24. "If I were not to protect these people, who are thus oppressed by powerful enemies, from fear, then it would be a source of delight for our enemies, just as it is for us when these enemies are killed here by you."

sanskrit: इतीव भूयश्च सुहृद्भिरीरिता निशम्य वाचः सुमनास्ततोऽर्जुनः ।यथानुरूपं प्रतिपूज्य तं जनं जगाम संशप्तकसंघहा पुनः ॥२५॥
transliteration: itīva bhūyaśca suhṛdbhirīritā; niśamya vācaḥ sumanāstato'rjunaḥ ,yathānurūpaṁ pratipūjya taṁ janaṁ; jagāma saṁśaptakasaṁghahā punaḥ.
english: Arjuna, having heard such words spoken again by his friends, became cheerful. Then, suitably honoring those people, the slayer of the Saṃśaptaka host departed again.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 13 Verse 25. Arjuna, having heard such words spoken again by his friends, became cheerful. Then, suitably honoring those people, the slayer of the Saṃśaptaka host departed again.

sanskrit: संजय उवाच ।प्रत्यागत्य पुनर्जिष्णुरहन्संशप्तकान्बहून् ।वक्रानुवक्रगमनादङ्गारक इव ग्रहः ॥१॥
transliteration: saṁjaya uvāca ,pratyāgatya punarjiṣṇurahansaṁśaptakānbahūn ,vakrānuvakragamanādaṅgāraka iva grahaḥ.
english: Saṃjaya said: Jiṣṇu (Arjuna), having returned, again slew many Saṃśaptakas, just as the planet Aṅgāraka (Mars) moves with a winding and circuitous course.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 14 Verse 1. Saṃjaya said: Jiṣṇu (Arjuna), having returned, again slew many Saṃśaptakas, just as the planet Aṅgāraka (Mars) moves with a winding and circuitous course.

sanskrit: पार्थबाणहता राजन्नराश्वरथकुञ्जराः ।विचेलुर्बभ्रमुर्नेदुः पेतुर्मम्लुश्च मारिष ॥२॥
transliteration: pārthabāṇahatā rājannarāśvarathakuñjarāḥ ,vicelurbabhramurneduḥ peturmamluśca māriṣa.
english: O King (rājan), O respected sir (māriṣa), men, horses, chariots, and elephants, struck by Arjuna's (pārtha) arrows, trembled, staggered, cried out, fell, and fainted.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 14 Verse 2. O King (rājan), O respected sir (māriṣa), men, horses, chariots, and elephants, struck by Arjuna's (pārtha) arrows, trembled, staggered, cried out, fell, and fainted.

sanskrit: धुर्यं धुर्यतरान्सूतान्रथांश्च परिसंक्षिपन् ।पाणीन्पाणिगतं शस्त्रं बाहूनपि शिरांसि च ॥३॥
transliteration: dhuryaṁ dhuryatarānsūtānrathāṁśca parisaṁkṣipan ,pāṇīnpāṇigataṁ śastraṁ bāhūnapi śirāṁsi ca.
english: (Arjuna), completely cutting off the foremost (dhuryam), the even more excellent leaders (dhuryatarān), charioteers (sūtān) and chariots, also (api) [their] hands (pāṇīn), the weapons (śastram) held in their hands, [their] arms (bāhūn), and their heads (śirāṃsi).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 14 Verse 3. (Arjuna), completely cutting off the foremost (dhuryam), the even more excellent leaders (dhuryatarān), charioteers (sūtān) and chariots, also (api) [their] hands (pāṇīn), the weapons (śastram) held in their hands, [their] arms (bāhūn), and their heads (śirāṃsi).

sanskrit: भल्लैः क्षुरैरर्धचन्द्रैर्वत्सदन्तैश्च पाण्डवः ।चिच्छेदामित्रवीराणां समरे प्रतियुध्यताम् ॥४॥
transliteration: bhallaiḥ kṣurairardhacandrairvatsadantaiśca pāṇḍavaḥ ,cicchedāmitravīrāṇāṁ samare pratiyudhyatām.
english: The Pāṇḍava (pāṇḍava) Arjuna, in battle (samare), cut off [these parts] of the enemy heroes (amitra-vīrāṇām) who were fighting back, using lances (bhallaiḥ), razor-sharp arrows (kṣuraiḥ), half-moon shaped arrows (ardhacandraiḥ), and calf-tooth shaped arrows (vatsadantaiḥ).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 14 Verse 4. The Pāṇḍava (pāṇḍava) Arjuna, in battle (samare), cut off [these parts] of the enemy heroes (amitra-vīrāṇām) who were fighting back, using lances (bhallaiḥ), razor-sharp arrows (kṣuraiḥ), half-moon shaped arrows (ardhacandraiḥ), and calf-tooth shaped arrows (vatsadantaiḥ).

sanskrit: वाशितार्थे युयुत्सन्तो वृषभा वृषभं यथा ।आपतन्त्यर्जुनं शूराः शतशोऽथ सहस्रशः ॥५॥
transliteration: vāśitārthe yuyutsanto vṛṣabhā vṛṣabhaṁ yathā ,āpatantyarjunaṁ śūrāḥ śataśo'tha sahasraśaḥ.
english: Just as bulls (vṛṣabhāḥ) desiring to fight (yuyutsantaḥ) for a cow (vāśitārthe) charge at [another] bull (vṛṣabham), so too (atha) hundreds (śataśaḥ) and thousands (sahasraśaḥ) of heroes (śūrāḥ) rushed upon Arjuna (arjunam).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 14 Verse 5. Just as bulls (vṛṣabhāḥ) desiring to fight (yuyutsantaḥ) for a cow (vāśitārthe) charge at [another] bull (vṛṣabham), so too (atha) hundreds (śataśaḥ) and thousands (sahasraśaḥ) of heroes (śūrāḥ) rushed upon Arjuna (arjunam).

sanskrit: तेषां तस्य च तद्युद्धमभवल्लोमहर्षणम् ।त्रैलोक्यविजये यादृग्दैत्यानां सह वज्रिणा ॥६॥
transliteration: teṣāṁ tasya ca tadyuddhamabhavallomaharṣaṇam ,trailokyavijaye yādṛgdaityānāṁ saha vajriṇā.
english: Their battle with him was hair-raising, just like the one between the daityas and Indra (vajrin) that occurred during the conquest of the three worlds.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 14 Verse 6. Their battle with him was hair-raising, just like the one between the daityas and Indra (vajrin) that occurred during the conquest of the three worlds.

sanskrit: तमविध्यत्त्रिभिर्बाणैर्दन्दशूकैरिवाहिभिः ।उग्रायुधस्ततस्तस्य शिरः कायादपाहरत् ॥७॥
transliteration: tamavidhyattribhirbāṇairdandaśūkairivāhibhiḥ ,ugrāyudhastatastasya śiraḥ kāyādapāharat.
english: Ugrayudha pierced him with three arrows, which were like venomous, biting snakes. Then, he severed his head from his body.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 14 Verse 7. Ugrayudha pierced him with three arrows, which were like venomous, biting snakes. Then, he severed his head from his body.

sanskrit: तेऽर्जुनं सर्वतः क्रुद्धा नानाशस्त्रैरवीवृषन् ।मरुद्भिः प्रेषिता मेघा हिमवन्तमिवोष्णगे ॥८॥
transliteration: te'rjunaṁ sarvataḥ kruddhā nānāśastrairavīvṛṣan ,marudbhiḥ preṣitā meghā himavantamivoṣṇage.
english: Enraged, they showered Arjuna from all sides with various weapons, just as clouds sent by the Maruts (wind gods) shower upon the Himavat (Himalayas) during the hot season.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 14 Verse 8. Enraged, they showered Arjuna from all sides with various weapons, just as clouds sent by the Maruts (wind gods) shower upon the Himavat (Himalayas) during the hot season.

sanskrit: अस्त्रैरस्त्राणि संवार्य द्विषतां सर्वतोऽर्जुनः ।सम्यगस्तैः शरैः सर्वान्सहितानहनद्बहून् ॥९॥
transliteration: astrairastrāṇi saṁvārya dviṣatāṁ sarvato'rjunaḥ ,samyagastaiḥ śaraiḥ sarvānsahitānahanadbahūn.
english: Arjuna, having completely warded off the weapons of his enemies from all sides with his own weapons, then struck down many of them who were assembled there, with accurately aimed arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 14 Verse 9. Arjuna, having completely warded off the weapons of his enemies from all sides with his own weapons, then struck down many of them who were assembled there, with accurately aimed arrows.

sanskrit: छिन्नत्रिवेणुजङ्घेषान्निहतपार्ष्णिसारथीन् ।संछिन्नरश्मियोक्त्राक्षान्व्यनुकर्षयुगान्रथान् ।विध्वस्तसर्वसंनाहान्बाणैश्चक्रेऽर्जुनस्त्वरन् ॥१०॥
transliteration: chinnatriveṇujaṅgheṣānnihatapārṣṇisārathīn ,saṁchinnaraśmiyoktrākṣānvyanukarṣayugānrathān ,vidhvastasarvasaṁnāhānbāṇaiścakre'rjunastvaran.
english: Arjuna, acting swiftly with his arrows, rendered the chariots useless by breaking their three-rod seats, supports, and poles, killing their rear-guards and charioteers, completely cutting their reins, traces, and axles, making their yokes drag loosely, and destroying all their armor.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 14 Verse 10.
