Arjuna, acting swiftly with his arrows, rendered the chariots useless by breaking their three-rod seats, supports, and poles, killing their rear-guards and charioteers, completely cutting their reins, traces, and axles, making their yokes drag loosely, and destroying all their armor.

sanskrit: ते रथास्तत्र विध्वस्ताः परार्ध्या भान्त्यनेकशः ।धनिनामिव वेश्मानि हतान्यग्न्यनिलाम्बुभिः ॥११॥
transliteration: te rathāstatra vidhvastāḥ parārdhyā bhāntyanekaśaḥ ,dhanināmiva veśmāni hatānyagnyanilāmbubhiḥ.
english: Those extremely valuable chariots, destroyed there in many ways, appeared just like mansions of the wealthy that have been ruined by fire, wind, and water.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 14 Verse 11. Those extremely valuable chariots, destroyed there in many ways, appeared just like mansions of the wealthy that have been ruined by fire, wind, and water.

sanskrit: द्विपाः संभिन्नमर्माणो वज्राशनिसमैः शरैः ।पेतुर्गिर्यग्रवेश्मानि वज्रवाताग्निभिर्यथा ॥१२॥
transliteration: dvipāḥ saṁbhinnamarmāṇo vajrāśanisamaiḥ śaraiḥ ,peturgiryagraveśmāni vajravātāgnibhiryathā.
english: Elephants, whose vital parts were shattered by arrows resembling thunderbolts and lightning, fell just as palaces situated on mountain peaks are struck down by thunderbolts, wind, and fire.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 14 Verse 12. Elephants, whose vital parts were shattered by arrows resembling thunderbolts and lightning, fell just as palaces situated on mountain peaks are struck down by thunderbolts, wind, and fire.

sanskrit: सारोहास्तुरगाः पेतुर्बहवोऽर्जुनताडिताः ।निर्जिह्वान्त्राः क्षितौ क्षीणा रुधिरार्द्राः सुदुर्दृशः ॥१३॥
transliteration: sārohāsturagāḥ peturbahavo'rjunatāḍitāḥ ,nirjihvāntrāḥ kṣitau kṣīṇā rudhirārdrāḥ sudurdṛśaḥ.
english: Many horses, struck by Arjuna, fell along with their riders. They were mangled on the ground, their tongues and entrails torn out, drenched in blood, and were extremely ghastly to behold.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 14 Verse 13. Many horses, struck by Arjuna, fell along with their riders. They were mangled on the ground, their tongues and entrails torn out, drenched in blood, and were extremely ghastly to behold.

sanskrit: नराश्वनागा नाराचैः संस्यूताः सव्यसाचिना ।बभ्रमुश्चस्खलुः पेतुर्नेदुर्मम्लुश्च मारिष ॥१४॥
transliteration: narāśvanāgā nārācaiḥ saṁsyūtāḥ savyasācinā ,babhramuścaskhaluḥ peturnedurmamluśca māriṣa.
english: O respectable one, men, horses, and elephants, pierced through by Arjuna (Savyasācin) with iron arrows, reeled, stumbled, fell, cried out, and fainted.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 14 Verse 14. O respectable one, men, horses, and elephants, pierced through by Arjuna (Savyasācin) with iron arrows, reeled, stumbled, fell, cried out, and fainted.

sanskrit: अणकैश्च शिलाधौतैर्वज्राशनिविषोपमैः ।शरैर्निजघ्निवान्पार्थो महेन्द्र इव दानवान् ॥१५॥
transliteration: aṇakaiśca śilādhautairvajrāśaniviṣopamaiḥ ,śarairnijaghnivānpārtho mahendra iva dānavān.
english: Arjuna struck down [the enemies] with sharp arrows, sharpened on stone and resembling thunderbolts, lightning, and poison, just as the great Indra slew the Dānavas (demons).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 14 Verse 15. Arjuna struck down [the enemies] with sharp arrows, sharpened on stone and resembling thunderbolts, lightning, and poison, just as the great Indra slew the Dānavas (demons).

sanskrit: महार्हवर्माभरणा नानारूपाम्बरायुधाः ।सरथाः सध्वजा वीरा हताः पार्थेन शेरते ॥१६॥
transliteration: mahārhavarmābharaṇā nānārūpāmbarāyudhāḥ ,sarathāḥ sadhvajā vīrā hatāḥ pārthena śerate.
english: The heroes, adorned with very valuable armor and ornaments, dressed in various garments, and equipped with diverse weapons, along with their chariots and banners, lie slain by Arjuna.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 14 Verse 16. The heroes, adorned with very valuable armor and ornaments, dressed in various garments, and equipped with diverse weapons, along with their chariots and banners, lie slain by Arjuna.

sanskrit: विजिताः पुण्यकर्माणो विशिष्टाभिजनश्रुताः ।गताः शरीरैर्वसुधामूर्जितैः कर्मभिर्दिवम् ॥१७॥
transliteration: vijitāḥ puṇyakarmāṇo viśiṣṭābhijanaśrutāḥ ,gatāḥ śarīrairvasudhāmūrjitaiḥ karmabhirdivam.
english: They, who were vanquished (killed), whose actions (karma) were righteous, and who were renowned for their distinguished lineage and profound learning, have gone: their bodies to the earth, and through their mighty deeds (karma), their souls to heaven.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 14 Verse 17. They, who were vanquished (killed), whose actions (karma) were righteous, and who were renowned for their distinguished lineage and profound learning, have gone: their bodies to the earth, and through their mighty deeds (karma), their souls to heaven.

sanskrit: अथार्जुनरथं वीरास्त्वदीयाः समुपाद्रवन् ।नानाजनपदाध्यक्षाः सगणा जातमन्यवः ॥१८॥
transliteration: athārjunarathaṁ vīrāstvadīyāḥ samupādravan ,nānājanapadādhyakṣāḥ sagaṇā jātamanyavaḥ.
english: Then, your warriors - the enraged chiefs from various lands, accompanied by their contingents - rushed towards Arjuna's chariot.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 14 Verse 18. Then, your warriors - the enraged chiefs from various lands, accompanied by their contingents - rushed towards Arjuna's chariot.

sanskrit: उह्यमाना रथाश्वैस्ते पत्तयश्च जिघांसवः ।समभ्यधावन्नस्यन्तो विविधं क्षिप्रमायुधम् ॥१९॥
transliteration: uhyamānā rathāśvaiste pattayaśca jighāṁsavaḥ ,samabhyadhāvannasyanto vividhaṁ kṣipramāyudham.
english: Carried by chariot-horses, and also the foot-soldiers, those (warriors) eager to kill rushed forward, rapidly discharging various weapons.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 14 Verse 19. Carried by chariot-horses, and also the foot-soldiers, those (warriors) eager to kill rushed forward, rapidly discharging various weapons.

sanskrit: तदायुधमहावर्षं क्षिप्तं योधमहाम्बुदैः ।व्यधमन्निशितैर्बाणैः क्षिप्रमर्जुनमारुतः ॥२०॥
transliteration: tadāyudhamahāvarṣaṁ kṣiptaṁ yodhamahāmbudaiḥ ,vyadhamanniśitairbāṇaiḥ kṣipramarjunamārutaḥ.
english: That great downpour of weapons, hurled by the warrior-clouds, Arjuna, swift as a wind, quickly scattered with his sharp arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 14 Verse 20. That great downpour of weapons, hurled by the warrior-clouds, Arjuna, swift as a wind, quickly scattered with his sharp arrows.

sanskrit: साश्वपत्तिद्विपरथं महाशस्त्रौघमप्लवम् ।सहसा संतितीर्षन्तं पार्थं शस्त्रास्त्रसेतुना ॥२१॥
transliteration: sāśvapattidviparathaṁ mahāśastraughamaplavam ,sahasā saṁtitīrṣantaṁ pārthaṁ śastrāstrasetunā.
english: Arjuna, desiring to suddenly cross that great, boat-less flood of weapons - which was composed of horses, foot-soldiers, elephants, and chariots - by means of a bridge of weapons and missiles.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 14 Verse 21. Arjuna, desiring to suddenly cross that great, boat-less flood of weapons - which was composed of horses, foot-soldiers, elephants, and chariots - by means of a bridge of weapons and missiles.

sanskrit: अथाब्रवीद्वासुदेवः पार्थं किं क्रीडसेऽनघ ।संशप्तकान्प्रमथ्यैतांस्ततः कर्णवधे त्वर ॥२२॥
transliteration: athābravīdvāsudevaḥ pārthaṁ kiṁ krīḍase'nagha ,saṁśaptakānpramathyaitāṁstataḥ karṇavadhe tvara.
english: Then Vasudeva spoke to Partha (Arjuna), saying, 'Why do you delay, O blameless one? Having crushed these Saṃśaptakas (warriors who swore an oath), you must then hasten to slay Karṇa.'
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 14 Verse 22. Then Vasudeva spoke to Partha (Arjuna), saying, 'Why do you delay, O blameless one? Having crushed these Saṃśaptakas (warriors who swore an oath), you must then hasten to slay Karṇa.'

sanskrit: तथेत्युक्त्वार्जुनः क्षिप्रं शिष्टान्संशप्तकांस्तदा ।आक्षिप्य शस्त्रेण बलाद्दैत्यानिन्द्र इवावधीत् ॥२३॥
transliteration: tathetyuktvārjunaḥ kṣipraṁ śiṣṭānsaṁśaptakāṁstadā ,ākṣipya śastreṇa balāddaityānindra ivāvadhīt.
english: Arjuna, saying, 'So be it,' then swiftly attacked the remaining Saṃśaptakas with his weapon, and by sheer force, he slew them, just as Indra (the king of gods) slays the demons (Daityas).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 14 Verse 23. Arjuna, saying, 'So be it,' then swiftly attacked the remaining Saṃśaptakas with his weapon, and by sheer force, he slew them, just as Indra (the king of gods) slays the demons (Daityas).

sanskrit: आदधत्संदधन्नेषून्दृष्टः कैश्चिद्रणेऽर्जुनः ।विमुञ्चन्वा शराञ्शीघ्रं दृश्यते स्म हि कैरपि ॥२४॥
transliteration: ādadhatsaṁdadhanneṣūndṛṣṭaḥ kaiścidraṇe'rjunaḥ ,vimuñcanvā śarāñśīghraṁ dṛśyate sma hi kairapi.
english: In battle, Arjuna was seen by some either taking and fixing arrows [to his bow], or indeed, by others, he was seen swiftly releasing them.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 14 Verse 24. In battle, Arjuna was seen by some either taking and fixing arrows [to his bow], or indeed, by others, he was seen swiftly releasing them.

sanskrit: आश्चर्यमिति गोविन्दो ब्रुवन्नश्वानचोदयत् ।हंसांसगौरास्ते सेनां हंसाः सर इवाविशन् ॥२५॥
transliteration: āścaryamiti govindo bruvannaśvānacodayat ,haṁsāṁsagaurāste senāṁ haṁsāḥ sara ivāviśan.
english: Exclaiming, 'This is wondrous!', Govinda (Krishna) urged his horses forward. Those horses, white like swans on their shoulders, plunged into the enemy army just as swans enter a lake.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 14 Verse 25. Exclaiming, 'This is wondrous!', Govinda (Krishna) urged his horses forward. Those horses, white like swans on their shoulders, plunged into the enemy army just as swans enter a lake.

sanskrit: ततः संग्रामभूमिं तां वर्तमाने जनक्षये ।अवेक्षमाणो गोविन्दः सव्यसाचिनमब्रवीत् ॥२६॥
transliteration: tataḥ saṁgrāmabhūmiṁ tāṁ vartamāne janakṣaye ,avekṣamāṇo govindaḥ savyasācinamabravīt.
english: Then, Govinda, observing that battlefield where the destruction of people was taking place, spoke to Savyasācin (Arjuna).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 14 Verse 26. Then, Govinda, observing that battlefield where the destruction of people was taking place, spoke to Savyasācin (Arjuna).

sanskrit: एष पार्थ महारौद्रो वर्तते भरतक्षयः ।पृथिव्यां पार्थिवानां वै दुर्योधनकृते महान् ॥२७॥
transliteration: eṣa pārtha mahāraudro vartate bharatakṣayaḥ ,pṛthivyāṁ pārthivānāṁ vai duryodhanakṛte mahān.
english: O Pārtha (Arjuna), this exceedingly terrible and vast destruction of kings among the Bhāratas is occurring on earth, all due to Duryodhana.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 14 Verse 27. O Pārtha (Arjuna), this exceedingly terrible and vast destruction of kings among the Bhāratas is occurring on earth, all due to Duryodhana.

sanskrit: पश्य भारत चापानि रुक्मपृष्ठानि धन्विनाम् ।महतामपविद्धानि कलापानिषुधीस्तथा ॥२८॥
transliteration: paśya bhārata cāpāni rukmapṛṣṭhāni dhanvinām ,mahatāmapaviddhāni kalāpāniṣudhīstathā.
english: O Bhārata (Arjuna), behold the gold-backed bows of great archers, and also their abandoned quivers and arrow-holders.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 14 Verse 28. O Bhārata (Arjuna), behold the gold-backed bows of great archers, and also their abandoned quivers and arrow-holders.

sanskrit: जातरूपमयैः पुङ्खैः शरांश्च नतपर्वणः ।तैलधौतांश्च नाराचान्निर्मुक्तानिव पन्नगान् ॥२९॥
transliteration: jātarūpamayaiḥ puṅkhaiḥ śarāṁśca nataparvaṇaḥ ,tailadhautāṁśca nārācānnirmuktāniva pannagān.
english: (And see) the arrows with gold-fashioned shafts and flattened points, and the iron arrows, polished with oil, resembling cast-off snake skins.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 14 Verse 29. (And see) the arrows with gold-fashioned shafts and flattened points, and the iron arrows, polished with oil, resembling cast-off snake skins.

sanskrit: हस्तिदन्तत्सरून्खड्गाञ्जातरूपपरिष्कृतान् ।आकीर्णांस्तोमरांश्चापांश्चित्रान्हेमविभूषितान् ॥३०॥
transliteration: hastidantatsarūnkhaḍgāñjātarūpapariṣkṛtān ,ākīrṇāṁstomarāṁścāpāṁścitrānhemavibhūṣitān.
english: Swords with ivory handles, adorned with gold; scattered javelins, and variegated bows decorated with gold,
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 14 Verse 30. Swords with ivory handles, adorned with gold; scattered javelins, and variegated bows decorated with gold,

sanskrit: वर्माणि चापविद्धानि रुक्मपृष्ठानि भारत ।सुवर्णविकृतान्प्रासाञ्शक्तीः कनकभूषिताः ॥३१॥
transliteration: varmāṇi cāpaviddhāni rukmapṛṣṭhāni bhārata ,suvarṇavikṛtānprāsāñśaktīḥ kanakabhūṣitāḥ.
english: O descendant of Bharata (bhārata), there were armors pierced by arrows, with golden surfaces; spears fashioned from gold, and javelins (śakti) adorned with gold,
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 14 Verse 31. O descendant of Bharata (bhārata), there were armors pierced by arrows, with golden surfaces; spears fashioned from gold, and javelins (śakti) adorned with gold,

sanskrit: जाम्बूनदमयैः पट्टैर्बद्धाश्च विपुला गदाः ।जातरूपमयीश्चर्ष्टीः पट्टिशान्हेमभूषितान् ॥३२॥
transliteration: jāmbūnadamayaiḥ paṭṭairbaddhāśca vipulā gadāḥ ,jātarūpamayīścarṣṭīḥ paṭṭiśānhemabhūṣitān.
english: And massive maces fastened with Jāmbūnada gold bands; and spears (ṛṣṭi) made of gold, and broadswords adorned with gold,
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 14 Verse 32. And massive maces fastened with Jāmbūnada gold bands; and spears (ṛṣṭi) made of gold, and broadswords adorned with gold,

sanskrit: दण्डैः कनकचित्रैश्च विप्रविद्धान्परश्वधान् ।अयस्कुशान्तान्पतितान्मुसलानि गुरूणि च ॥३३॥
transliteration: daṇḍaiḥ kanakacitraiśca vipraviddhānparaśvadhān ,ayaskuśāntānpatitānmusalāni gurūṇi ca.
english: ...and battle-axes, scattered about, with golden-adorned handles and iron edges, fallen; and heavy pestles (musala).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 14 Verse 33. ...and battle-axes, scattered about, with golden-adorned handles and iron edges, fallen; and heavy pestles (musala).

sanskrit: शतघ्नीः पश्य चित्राश्च विपुलान्परिघांस्तथा ।चक्राणि चापविद्धानि मुद्गरांश्च बहून्रणे ॥३४॥
transliteration: śataghnīḥ paśya citrāśca vipulānparighāṁstathā ,cakrāṇi cāpaviddhāni mudgarāṁśca bahūnraṇe.
english: Observe the various 'hundred-killers' (śataghnī) and the massive iron clubs (parigha). Also, behold the discs (cakra) pierced by bows, and many hammers (mudgara) on the battlefield.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 14 Verse 34. Observe the various 'hundred-killers' (śataghnī) and the massive iron clubs (parigha). Also, behold the discs (cakra) pierced by bows, and many hammers (mudgara) on the battlefield.

sanskrit: नानाविधानि शस्त्राणि प्रगृह्य जयगृद्धिनः ।जीवन्त इव लक्ष्यन्ते गतसत्त्वास्तरस्विनः ॥३५॥
transliteration: nānāvidhāni śastrāṇi pragṛhya jayagṛddhinaḥ ,jīvanta iva lakṣyante gatasattvāstarasvinaḥ.
english: Though deprived of their life-force (sattva), those powerful warriors, who were eager for victory and held various weapons, appear as if they are still alive.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 14 Verse 35. Though deprived of their life-force (sattva), those powerful warriors, who were eager for victory and held various weapons, appear as if they are still alive.

sanskrit: गदाविमथितैर्गात्रैर्मुसलैर्भिन्नमस्तकान् ।गजवाजिरथक्षुण्णान्पश्य योधान्सहस्रशः ॥३६॥
transliteration: gadāvimathitairgātrairmusalairbhinnamastakān ,gajavājirathakṣuṇṇānpaśya yodhānsahasraśaḥ.
english: Observe thousands of warriors (yodha) whose heads are shattered by clubs, who are trampled by elephants, horses, and chariots, and whose limbs are crushed by maces.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 14 Verse 36. Observe thousands of warriors (yodha) whose heads are shattered by clubs, who are trampled by elephants, horses, and chariots, and whose limbs are crushed by maces.

sanskrit: मनुष्यगजवाजीनां शरशक्त्यृष्टितोमरैः ।निस्त्रिंशैः पट्टिशैः प्रासैर्नखरैर्लगुडैरपि ॥३७॥
transliteration: manuṣyagajavājīnāṁ śaraśaktyṛṣṭitomaraiḥ ,nistriṁśaiḥ paṭṭiśaiḥ prāsairnakharairlaguḍairapi.
english: Among men, elephants, and horses, [carnage is wrought] by arrows, spears (śakti), javelins (ṛṣṭi), and lances (tomara); by swords (nistriṁśa), broad-bladed spears (paṭṭiśa), and darts (prāsa); and also by claw-weapons (nakhara) and clubs (laguḍa).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 14 Verse 37. Among men, elephants, and horses, [carnage is wrought] by arrows, spears (śakti), javelins (ṛṣṭi), and lances (tomara); by swords (nistriṁśa), broad-bladed spears (paṭṭiśa), and darts (prāsa); and also by claw-weapons (nakhara) and clubs (laguḍa).

sanskrit: शरीरैर्बहुधा भिन्नैः शोणितौघपरिप्लुतैः ।गतासुभिरमित्रघ्न संवृता रणभूमयः ॥३८॥
transliteration: śarīrairbahudhā bhinnaiḥ śoṇitaughapariplutaiḥ ,gatāsubhiramitraghna saṁvṛtā raṇabhūmayaḥ.
english: O slayer of enemies (amitraghna), the battlefields (raṇabhūmayaḥ) are covered (saṃvṛtāḥ) by numerous bodies (śarīraiḥ) that are dismembered (bhinnaiḥ) in various ways (bahudhā), drenched in floods of blood (śoṇitaughapariputaiḥ), and lifeless (gatāsubhiḥ).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 14 Verse 38. O slayer of enemies (amitraghna), the battlefields (raṇabhūmayaḥ) are covered (saṃvṛtāḥ) by numerous bodies (śarīraiḥ) that are dismembered (bhinnaiḥ) in various ways (bahudhā), drenched in floods of blood (śoṇitaughapariputaiḥ), and lifeless (gatāsubhiḥ).

sanskrit: बाहुभिश्चन्दनादिग्धैः साङ्गदैः शुभभूषणैः ।सतलत्रैः सकेयूरैर्भाति भारत मेदिनी ॥३९॥
transliteration: bāhubhiścandanādigdhaiḥ sāṅgadaiḥ śubhabhūṣaṇaiḥ ,satalatraiḥ sakeyūrairbhāti bhārata medinī.
english: O descendant of Bharata (bhārata), the earth (medinī) shines (bhāti) with arms (bāhubhiḥ) that are smeared with sandalwood (candanādigdhaiḥ), adorned with armlets (sāṅgadaiḥ), beautiful ornaments (śubhbhūṣaṇaiḥ), arm-guards (satalatraiḥ), and upper-arm bracelets (sakeyūraiḥ).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 14 Verse 39. O descendant of Bharata (bhārata), the earth (medinī) shines (bhāti) with arms (bāhubhiḥ) that are smeared with sandalwood (candanādigdhaiḥ), adorned with armlets (sāṅgadaiḥ), beautiful ornaments (śubhbhūṣaṇaiḥ), arm-guards (satalatraiḥ), and upper-arm bracelets (sakeyūraiḥ).

sanskrit: साङ्गुलित्रैर्भुजाग्रैश्च विप्रविद्धैरलंकृतैः ।हस्तिहस्तोपमैश्छिन्नैरूरुभिश्च तरस्विनाम् ॥४०॥
transliteration: sāṅgulitrairbhujāgraiśca vipraviddhairalaṁkṛtaiḥ ,hastihastopamaiśchinnairūrubhiśca tarasvinām.
english: And with scattered, adorned forearms (bhujāgraiḥ) wearing finger-guards (sāṅgulitraiḥ); and with the severed thighs (ūrubhiḥ) of mighty (tarasvinām) warriors, resembling elephants' trunks (hastihastopamaiḥ).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 14 Verse 40. And with scattered, adorned forearms (bhujāgraiḥ) wearing finger-guards (sāṅgulitraiḥ); and with the severed thighs (ūrubhiḥ) of mighty (tarasvinām) warriors, resembling elephants' trunks (hastihastopamaiḥ).

sanskrit: बद्धचूडामणिवरैः शिरोभिश्च सकुण्डलैः ।निकृत्तैर्वृषभाक्षाणां विराजति वसुंधरा ॥४१॥
transliteration: baddhacūḍāmaṇivaraiḥ śirobhiśca sakuṇḍalaiḥ ,nikṛttairvṛṣabhākṣāṇāṁ virājati vasuṁdharā.
english: And the earth (vasundharā) shines brightly (virājati) with severed (nikṛttaiḥ) heads (śirobhiḥ) of the bull-eyed (vṛṣabhākṣāṇām) warriors, which are adorned with excellent crest-jewels (baddhacūḍāmaṇivaraiḥ) and earrings (sakuṇḍalaiḥ).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 14 Verse 41. And the earth (vasundharā) shines brightly (virājati) with severed (nikṛttaiḥ) heads (śirobhiḥ) of the bull-eyed (vṛṣabhākṣāṇām) warriors, which are adorned with excellent crest-jewels (baddhacūḍāmaṇivaraiḥ) and earrings (sakuṇḍalaiḥ).

sanskrit: कबन्धैः शोणितादिग्धैश्छिन्नगात्रशिरोधरैः ।भूर्भाति भरतश्रेष्ठ शान्तार्चिर्भिरिवाग्निभिः ॥४२॥
transliteration: kabandhaiḥ śoṇitādigdhaiśchinnagātraśirodharaiḥ ,bhūrbhāti bharataśreṣṭha śāntārcirbhirivāgnibhiḥ.
english: O best of Bharatas, the earth gleams with headless trunks, smeared with blood and with severed limbs and heads, resembling fires whose flames have been extinguished.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 14 Verse 42. O best of Bharatas, the earth gleams with headless trunks, smeared with blood and with severed limbs and heads, resembling fires whose flames have been extinguished.

sanskrit: रथान्बहुविधान्भग्नान्हेमकिङ्किणिनः शुभान् ।अश्वांश्च बहुधा पश्य शोणितेन परिप्लुतान् ॥४३॥
transliteration: rathānbahuvidhānbhagnānhemakiṅkiṇinaḥ śubhān ,aśvāṁśca bahudhā paśya śoṇitena pariplutān.
english: Behold the many kinds of chariots, broken, adorned with golden bells, and splendid, as well as the horses, thoroughly drenched in blood.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 14 Verse 43. Behold the many kinds of chariots, broken, adorned with golden bells, and splendid, as well as the horses, thoroughly drenched in blood.

sanskrit: योधानां च महाशङ्खान्पाण्डुरांश्च प्रकीर्णकान् ।निरस्तजिह्वान्मातङ्गाञ्शयानान्पर्वतोपमान् ॥४४॥
transliteration: yodhānāṁ ca mahāśaṅkhānpāṇḍurāṁśca prakīrṇakān ,nirastajihvānmātaṅgāñśayānānparvatopamān.
english: And behold the scattered great, white conch shells of the warriors, as well as the mountain-like elephants lying fallen with their tongues hanging out.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 14 Verse 44. And behold the scattered great, white conch shells of the warriors, as well as the mountain-like elephants lying fallen with their tongues hanging out.

sanskrit: वैजयन्तीविचित्रांश्च हतांश्च गजयोधिनः ।वारणानां परिस्तोमान्सुयुक्ताम्बरकम्बलान् ॥४५॥
transliteration: vaijayantīvicitrāṁśca hatāṁśca gajayodhinaḥ ,vāraṇānāṁ paristomānsuyuktāmbarakambalān.
english: And behold the slain elephant warriors, whose banners are diverse and colorful, and the caparisons of the elephants, which are adorned with well-fitted cloths and blankets.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 14 Verse 45. And behold the slain elephant warriors, whose banners are diverse and colorful, and the caparisons of the elephants, which are adorned with well-fitted cloths and blankets.

sanskrit: विपाटिता विचित्राश्च रूपचित्राः कुथास्तथा ।भिन्नाश्च बहुधा घण्टाः पतद्भिश्चूर्णिता गजैः ॥४६॥
transliteration: vipāṭitā vicitrāśca rūpacitrāḥ kuthāstathā ,bhinnāśca bahudhā ghaṇṭāḥ patadbhiścūrṇitā gajaiḥ.
english: Variegated and beautifully patterned elephant housings (kuthā) were torn apart, and bells, broken in many ways, were pulverized by the falling elephants.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 14 Verse 46. Variegated and beautifully patterned elephant housings (kuthā) were torn apart, and bells, broken in many ways, were pulverized by the falling elephants.

sanskrit: वैडूर्यमणिदण्डांश्च पतितानङ्कुशान्भुवि ।बद्धाः सादिध्वजाग्रेषु सुवर्णविकृताः कशाः ॥४७॥
transliteration: vaiḍūryamaṇidaṇḍāṁśca patitānaṅkuśānbhuvi ,baddhāḥ sādidhvajāgreṣu suvarṇavikṛtāḥ kaśāḥ.
english: Elephant goads (aṅkuśa) with handles adorned by sapphire (vaiḍūrya) gems lay fallen on the ground, while gold-decorated whips (kaśā) were found tied to the tips of the cavalry standards.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 14 Verse 47. Elephant goads (aṅkuśa) with handles adorned by sapphire (vaiḍūrya) gems lay fallen on the ground, while gold-decorated whips (kaśā) were found tied to the tips of the cavalry standards.

sanskrit: विचित्रान्मणिचित्रांश्च जातरूपपरिष्कृतान् ।अश्वास्तरपरिस्तोमान्राङ्कवान्पतितान्भुवि ॥४८॥
transliteration: vicitrānmaṇicitrāṁśca jātarūpapariṣkṛtān ,aśvāstaraparistomānrāṅkavānpatitānbhuvi.
english: Variegated, adorned with various gems, and embellished with gold, the horse coverings (aśvāstara) made of Rānku deer skin lay fallen on the ground.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 14 Verse 48. Variegated, adorned with various gems, and embellished with gold, the horse coverings (aśvāstara) made of Rānku deer skin lay fallen on the ground.

sanskrit: चूडामणीन्नरेन्द्राणां विचित्राः काञ्चनस्रजः ।छत्राणि चापविद्धानि चामरव्यजनानि च ॥४९॥
transliteration: cūḍāmaṇīnnarendrāṇāṁ vicitrāḥ kāñcanasrajaḥ ,chatrāṇi cāpaviddhāni cāmaravyajanāni ca.
english: The crest-jewels (cūḍāmaṇi) of kings, diverse golden garlands, umbrellas thrown aside, and chowries (cāmara) and fans (vyajana) were also found.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 14 Verse 49. The crest-jewels (cūḍāmaṇi) of kings, diverse golden garlands, umbrellas thrown aside, and chowries (cāmara) and fans (vyajana) were also found.

sanskrit: चन्द्रनक्षत्रभासैश्च वदनैश्चारुकुण्डलैः ।कॢप्तश्मश्रुभिरत्यर्थं वीराणां समलंकृतैः ।वदनैः पश्य संछन्नां महीं शोणितकर्दमाम् ॥५०॥
transliteration: candranakṣatrabhāsaiśca vadanaiścārukuṇḍalaiḥ ,kḷptaśmaśrubhiratyarthaṁ vīrāṇāṁ samalaṁkṛtaiḥ ,vadanaiḥ paśya saṁchannāṁ mahīṁ śoṇitakardamām.
english: Behold the earth, which is now muddy with blood, covered with the faces of exceedingly adorned heroes; these faces shine like the moon and stars, and are embellished with beautiful earrings and neatly trimmed beards.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 14 Verse 50. Behold the earth, which is now muddy with blood, covered with the faces of exceedingly adorned heroes; these faces shine like the moon and stars, and are embellished with beautiful earrings and neatly trimmed beards.

sanskrit: सजीवांश्च नरान्पश्य कूजमानान्समन्ततः ।उपास्यमानान्बहुभिर्न्यस्तशस्त्रैर्विशां पते ॥५१॥
transliteration: sajīvāṁśca narānpaśya kūjamānānsamantataḥ ,upāsyamānānbahubhirnyastaśastrairviśāṁ pate.
english: O lord of the people, behold living men crying out from all sides, men who are being attended to by many others who have laid down their weapons.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 14 Verse 51. O lord of the people, behold living men crying out from all sides, men who are being attended to by many others who have laid down their weapons.

sanskrit: ज्ञातिभिः सहितैस्तत्र रोदमानैर्मुहुर्मुहुः ।व्युत्क्रान्तानपरान्योधांश्छादयित्वा तरस्विनः ।पुनर्युद्धाय गच्छन्ति जयगृद्धाः प्रमन्यवः ॥५२॥
transliteration: jñātibhiḥ sahitaistatra rodamānairmuhurmuhuḥ ,vyutkrāntānaparānyodhāṁśchādayitvā tarasvinaḥ ,punaryuddhāya gacchanti jayagṛddhāḥ pramanyavaḥ.
english: There, the swift warriors, consumed by wrath and greedy for victory, having covered other fallen warriors (who are repeatedly lamented by their accompanying relatives), return to battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 14 Verse 52. There, the swift warriors, consumed by wrath and greedy for victory, having covered other fallen warriors (who are repeatedly lamented by their accompanying relatives), return to battle.

sanskrit: अपरे तत्र तत्रैव परिधावन्ति मानिनः ।ज्ञातिभिः पतितैः शूरैर्याच्यमानास्तथोदकम् ॥५३॥
transliteration: apare tatra tatraiva paridhāvanti māninaḥ ,jñātibhiḥ patitaiḥ śūrairyācyamānāstathodakam.
english: Still others, who are proud, rush about here and there, while being implored for water by their fallen, brave relatives.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 14 Verse 53. Still others, who are proud, rush about here and there, while being implored for water by their fallen, brave relatives.

sanskrit: जलार्थं च गताः केचिन्निष्प्राणा बहवोऽर्जुन ।संनिवृत्ताश्च ते शूरास्तान्दृष्ट्वैव विचेतसः ॥५४॥
transliteration: jalārthaṁ ca gatāḥ kecinniṣprāṇā bahavo'rjuna ,saṁnivṛttāśca te śūrāstāndṛṣṭvaiva vicetasaḥ.
english: O Arjuna, many who had gone to fetch water lay lifeless. Even brave warriors, upon seeing them, turned back, having lost their composure.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 14 Verse 54. O Arjuna, many who had gone to fetch water lay lifeless. Even brave warriors, upon seeing them, turned back, having lost their composure.

sanskrit: जलं दृष्ट्वा प्रधावन्ति क्रोशमानाः परस्परम् ।जलं पीत्वा मृतान्पश्य पिबतोऽन्यांश्च भारत ॥५५॥
transliteration: jalaṁ dṛṣṭvā pradhāvanti krośamānāḥ parasparam ,jalaṁ pītvā mṛtānpaśya pibato'nyāṁśca bhārata.
english: Upon seeing water, they run forward, crying out to one another. O Bhārata, observe those who died after drinking the water, and also others who are still drinking.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 14 Verse 55. Upon seeing water, they run forward, crying out to one another. O Bhārata, observe those who died after drinking the water, and also others who are still drinking.

sanskrit: परित्यज्य प्रियानन्ये बान्धवान्बान्धवप्रिय ।व्युत्क्रान्ताः समदृश्यन्त तत्र तत्र महारणे ॥५६॥
transliteration: parityajya priyānanye bāndhavānbāndhavapriya ,vyutkrāntāḥ samadṛśyanta tatra tatra mahāraṇe.
english: O beloved of kinsmen, others were seen having died here and there in that great battle, abandoning their dear ones and relatives.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 14 Verse 56. O beloved of kinsmen, others were seen having died here and there in that great battle, abandoning their dear ones and relatives.

sanskrit: पश्यापरान्नरश्रेष्ठ संदष्टौष्ठपुटान्पुनः ।भ्रुकुटीकुटिलैर्वक्त्रैः प्रेक्षमाणान्समन्ततः ॥५७॥
transliteration: paśyāparānnaraśreṣṭha saṁdaṣṭauṣṭhapuṭānpunaḥ ,bhrukuṭīkuṭilairvaktraiḥ prekṣamāṇānsamantataḥ.
english: O best among men, observe still others, who, having bitten their lips, look all around with faces contorted by furrowed brows.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 14 Verse 57. O best among men, observe still others, who, having bitten their lips, look all around with faces contorted by furrowed brows.

sanskrit: एतत्तवैवानुरूपं कर्मार्जुन महाहवे ।दिवि वा देवराजस्य त्वया यत्कृतमाहवे ॥५८॥
transliteration: etattavaivānurūpaṁ karmārjuna mahāhave ,divi vā devarājasya tvayā yatkṛtamāhave.
english: O Arjuna, this action (karma) of yours in this great battle is truly fitting, just like the deeds you performed in battle for the king of the gods in heaven.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 14 Verse 58. O Arjuna, this action (karma) of yours in this great battle is truly fitting, just like the deeds you performed in battle for the king of the gods in heaven.

sanskrit: एवं तां दर्शयन्कृष्णो युद्धभूमिं किरीटिने ।गच्छन्नेवाशृणोच्छब्दं दुर्योधनबले महत् ॥५९॥
transliteration: evaṁ tāṁ darśayankṛṣṇo yuddhabhūmiṁ kirīṭine ,gacchannevāśṛṇocchabdaṁ duryodhanabale mahat.
english: As Kṛṣṇa showed that battlefield to the crowned (Arjuna), he heard a loud sound from Duryodhana's army while they were still en route.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 14 Verse 59. As Kṛṣṇa showed that battlefield to the crowned (Arjuna), he heard a loud sound from Duryodhana's army while they were still en route.

sanskrit: शङ्खदुन्दुभिनिर्घोषान्भेरीपणवमिश्रितान् ।रथाश्वगजनादांश्च शस्त्रशब्दांश्च दारुणान् ॥६०॥
transliteration: śaṅkhadundubhinirghoṣānbherīpaṇavamiśritān ,rathāśvagajanādāṁśca śastraśabdāṁśca dāruṇān.
english: He heard the mingled sounds of conchs and large drums, blended with kettledrums and smaller drums, and also the roars of chariots, horses, and elephants, along with dreadful clashing of weapons.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 14 Verse 60. He heard the mingled sounds of conchs and large drums, blended with kettledrums and smaller drums, and also the roars of chariots, horses, and elephants, along with dreadful clashing of weapons.

sanskrit: प्रविश्य तद्बलं कृष्णस्तुरगैर्वातवेगिभिः ।पाण्ड्येनाभ्यर्दितां सेनां त्वदीयां वीक्ष्य धिष्ठितः ॥६१॥
transliteration: praviśya tadbalaṁ kṛṣṇasturagairvātavegibhiḥ ,pāṇḍyenābhyarditāṁ senāṁ tvadīyāṁ vīkṣya dhiṣṭhitaḥ.
english: Kṛṣṇa, entering that army with horses swift as the wind, and seeing your (Kaurava) army being harassed by the Pandya king, then took his stand.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 14 Verse 61. Kṛṣṇa, entering that army with horses swift as the wind, and seeing your (Kaurava) army being harassed by the Pandya king, then took his stand.

sanskrit: स हि नानाविधैर्बाणैरिष्वासप्रवरो युधि ।न्यहनद्द्विषतां व्रातान्गतासूनन्तको यथा ॥६२॥
transliteration: sa hi nānāvidhairbāṇairiṣvāsapravaro yudhi ,nyahanaddviṣatāṁ vrātāngatāsūnantako yathā.
english: Indeed, he, the foremost among archers, in battle, struck down multitudes of enemies, rendering them lifeless with various kinds of arrows, just as Antaka (the god of death) does.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 14 Verse 62. Indeed, he, the foremost among archers, in battle, struck down multitudes of enemies, rendering them lifeless with various kinds of arrows, just as Antaka (the god of death) does.

sanskrit: गजवाजिमनुष्याणां शरीराणि शितैः शरैः ।भित्त्वा प्रहरतां श्रेष्ठो विदेहासूंश्चकार सः ॥६३॥
transliteration: gajavājimanuṣyāṇāṁ śarīrāṇi śitaiḥ śaraiḥ ,bhittvā praharatāṁ śreṣṭho videhāsūṁścakāra saḥ.
english: He, the foremost among warriors, having pierced the bodies of elephants, horses, and men with sharp arrows, rendered them lifeless.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 14 Verse 63. He, the foremost among warriors, having pierced the bodies of elephants, horses, and men with sharp arrows, rendered them lifeless.

sanskrit: शत्रुप्रवीरैरस्तानि नानाशस्त्राणि सायकैः ।भित्त्वा तानहनत्पाण्ड्यः शत्रूञ्शक्र इवासुरान् ॥६४॥
transliteration: śatrupravīrairastāni nānāśastrāṇi sāyakaiḥ ,bhittvā tānahanatpāṇḍyaḥ śatrūñśakra ivāsurān.
english: Pāṇḍya, with his arrows, shattered the various weapons hurled by the leading enemy warriors, and then struck down those enemies, just as Śakra (Indra) strikes down the Asuras.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 14 Verse 64. Pāṇḍya, with his arrows, shattered the various weapons hurled by the leading enemy warriors, and then struck down those enemies, just as Śakra (Indra) strikes down the Asuras.

sanskrit: धृतराष्ट्र उवाच ।प्रोक्तस्त्वया पूर्वमेव प्रवीरो लोकविश्रुतः ।न त्वस्य कर्म संग्रामे त्वया संजय कीर्तितम् ॥१॥
transliteration: dhṛtarāṣṭra uvāca ,proktastvayā pūrvameva pravīro lokaviśrutaḥ ,na tvasya karma saṁgrāme tvayā saṁjaya kīrtitam.
english: Dhṛtarāṣṭra said: "You have indeed already mentioned the world-renowned hero. But, O Sañjaya, his actions (karma) in battle have not been recounted by you."
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 15 Verse 1. Dhṛtarāṣṭra said: "You have indeed already mentioned the world-renowned hero. But, O Sañjaya, his actions (karma) in battle have not been recounted by you."

sanskrit: तस्य विस्तरतो ब्रूहि प्रवीरस्याद्य विक्रमम् ।शिक्षां प्रभावं वीर्यं च प्रमाणं दर्पमेव च ॥२॥
transliteration: tasya vistarato brūhi pravīrasyādya vikramam ,śikṣāṁ prabhāvaṁ vīryaṁ ca pramāṇaṁ darpameva ca.
english: Tell me today, in detail, about the valor, training, influence, strength, authority, and pride of that great hero.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 15 Verse 2. Tell me today, in detail, about the valor, training, influence, strength, authority, and pride of that great hero.

sanskrit: संजय उवाच ।द्रोणभीष्मकृपद्रौणिकर्णार्जुनजनार्दनान् ।समाप्तविद्यान्धनुषि श्रेष्ठान्यान्मन्यसे युधि ॥३॥
transliteration: saṁjaya uvāca ,droṇabhīṣmakṛpadrauṇikarṇārjunajanārdanān ,samāptavidyāndhanuṣi śreṣṭhānyānmanyase yudhi.
english: Sañjaya said: (I shall speak of) those, like Droṇa, Bhīṣma, Kṛpa, Drauṇi, Karṇa, Arjuna, and Janārdana (Kṛṣṇa), whom you consider supreme in archery, accomplished in knowledge, and (mighty) in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 15 Verse 3. Sañjaya said: (I shall speak of) those, like Droṇa, Bhīṣma, Kṛpa, Drauṇi, Karṇa, Arjuna, and Janārdana (Kṛṣṇa), whom you consider supreme in archery, accomplished in knowledge, and (mighty) in battle.

sanskrit: तुल्यता कर्णभीष्माभ्यामात्मनो येन दृश्यते ।वासुदेवार्जुनाभ्यां च न्यूनतां नात्मनीच्छति ॥४॥
transliteration: tulyatā karṇabhīṣmābhyāmātmano yena dṛśyate ,vāsudevārjunābhyāṁ ca nyūnatāṁ nātmanīcchati.
english: He (the Pāṇḍya king) considers his own self (ātman) equal to Karṇa and Bhīṣma, and he does not wish for any inferiority for his own self (ātman) compared to Vāsudeva (Kṛṣṇa) and Arjuna.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 15 Verse 4. He (the Pāṇḍya king) considers his own self (ātman) equal to Karṇa and Bhīṣma, and he does not wish for any inferiority for his own self (ātman) compared to Vāsudeva (Kṛṣṇa) and Arjuna.

sanskrit: स पाण्ड्यो नृपतिश्रेष्ठः सर्वशस्त्रभृतां वरः ।कर्णस्यानीकमवधीत्परिभूत इवान्तकः ॥५॥
transliteration: sa pāṇḍyo nṛpatiśreṣṭhaḥ sarvaśastrabhṛtāṁ varaḥ ,karṇasyānīkamavadhītparibhūta ivāntakaḥ.
english: That Pāṇḍya king, the foremost of rulers and the best among all weapon-bearers, indeed destroyed Karṇa's army, like Antaka (the god of death), having been insulted.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 15 Verse 5. That Pāṇḍya king, the foremost of rulers and the best among all weapon-bearers, indeed destroyed Karṇa's army, like Antaka (the god of death), having been insulted.

sanskrit: तदुदीर्णरथाश्वं च पत्तिप्रवरकुञ्जरम् ।कुलालचक्रवद्भ्रान्तं पाण्ड्येनाधिष्ठितं बलम् ॥६॥
transliteration: tadudīrṇarathāśvaṁ ca pattipravarakuñjaram ,kulālacakravadbhrāntaṁ pāṇḍyenādhiṣṭhitaṁ balam.
english: The army, commanded by the Pāṇḍya, which was stirred with chariots, horses, and excellent infantry and elephants, whirled like a potter's wheel.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 15 Verse 6. The army, commanded by the Pāṇḍya, which was stirred with chariots, horses, and excellent infantry and elephants, whirled like a potter's wheel.

sanskrit: व्यश्वसूतध्वजरथान्विप्रविद्धायुधान्रिपून् ।सम्यगस्तैः शरैः पाण्ड्यो वायुर्मेघानिवाक्षिपत् ॥७॥
transliteration: vyaśvasūtadhvajarathānvipraviddhāyudhānripūn ,samyagastaiḥ śaraiḥ pāṇḍyo vāyurmeghānivākṣipat.
english: With accurately shot arrows, the Pāṇḍya king scattered his enemies - who had lost their horses, charioteers, banners, and chariots, and whose weapons lay discarded - just as the wind scatters clouds.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 15 Verse 7. With accurately shot arrows, the Pāṇḍya king scattered his enemies - who had lost their horses, charioteers, banners, and chariots, and whose weapons lay discarded - just as the wind scatters clouds.

sanskrit: द्विरदान्प्रहतप्रोथान्विपताकध्वजायुधान् ।सपादरक्षानवधीद्वज्रेणारीनिवारिहा ॥८॥
transliteration: dviradānprahataprothānvipatākadhvajāyudhān ,sapādarakṣānavadhīdvajreṇārīnivārihā.
english: He (the Pāṇḍya king) slew the elephants - whose coverings were struck down, whose banners, flags, and weapons were lost, along with their foot-guards - just as Indra, the slayer of enemies, kills his foes with a thunderbolt.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 15 Verse 8. He (the Pāṇḍya king) slew the elephants - whose coverings were struck down, whose banners, flags, and weapons were lost, along with their foot-guards - just as Indra, the slayer of enemies, kills his foes with a thunderbolt.

sanskrit: सशक्तिप्रासतूणीरानश्वारोहान्हयानपि ।पुलिन्दखशबाह्लीकान्निषादान्ध्रकतङ्गणान् ॥९॥
transliteration: saśaktiprāsatūṇīrānaśvārohānhayānapi ,pulindakhaśabāhlīkānniṣādāndhrakataṅgaṇān.
english: He also slew the horsemen - equipped with their spears, javelins, and quivers - and their horses. Similarly, he killed the Pulindas, Khaśas, Bāhlīkas, Niṣādas, Andhras, and Tangaṇas.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 15 Verse 9. He also slew the horsemen - equipped with their spears, javelins, and quivers - and their horses. Similarly, he killed the Pulindas, Khaśas, Bāhlīkas, Niṣādas, Andhras, and Tangaṇas.

sanskrit: दाक्षिणात्यांश्च भोजांश्च क्रूरान्संग्रामकर्कशान् ।विशस्त्रकवचान्बाणैः कृत्वा पाण्ड्योऽकरोद्व्यसून् ॥१०॥
transliteration: dākṣiṇātyāṁśca bhojāṁśca krūrānsaṁgrāmakarkaśān ,viśastrakavacānbāṇaiḥ kṛtvā pāṇḍyo'karodvyasūn.
english: The Pandyan king, using his arrows, rendered the Southerners and the Bhojas - who were cruel and unyielding in battle - weaponless and armorless, and then deprived them of life.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 15 Verse 10. The Pandyan king, using his arrows, rendered the Southerners and the Bhojas - who were cruel and unyielding in battle - weaponless and armorless, and then deprived them of life.

sanskrit: चतुरङ्गं बलं बाणैर्निघ्नन्तं पाण्ड्यमाहवे ।दृष्ट्वा द्रौणिरसंभ्रान्तमसंभ्रान्ततरोऽभ्ययात् ॥११॥
transliteration: caturaṅgaṁ balaṁ bāṇairnighnantaṁ pāṇḍyamāhave ,dṛṣṭvā drauṇirasaṁbhrāntamasaṁbhrāntataro'bhyayāt.
english: Having observed the Pandyan king, who was calm and undisturbed, striking down the four-limbed army with his arrows in battle, Droṇi's son (Aśvatthāman) approached him with even greater composure.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 15 Verse 11. Having observed the Pandyan king, who was calm and undisturbed, striking down the four-limbed army with his arrows in battle, Droṇi's son (Aśvatthāman) approached him with even greater composure.

sanskrit: आभाष्य चैनं मधुरमभि नृत्यन्नभीतवत् ।प्राह प्रहरतां श्रेष्ठः स्मितपूर्वं समाह्वयन् ॥१२॥
transliteration: ābhāṣya cainaṁ madhuramabhi nṛtyannabhītavat ,prāha praharatāṁ śreṣṭhaḥ smitapūrvaṁ samāhvayan.
english: And, having addressed him sweetly, dancing on the battlefield as if fearless, the best among warriors spoke with a smile, challenging him (to combat).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 15 Verse 12. And, having addressed him sweetly, dancing on the battlefield as if fearless, the best among warriors spoke with a smile, challenging him (to combat).

sanskrit: राजन्कमलपत्राक्ष प्रधानायुधवाहन ।वज्रसंहननप्रख्य प्रधानबलपौरुष ॥१३॥
transliteration: rājankamalapatrākṣa pradhānāyudhavāhana ,vajrasaṁhananaprakhya pradhānabalapauruṣa.
english: O King, whose eyes are like lotus petals! O wielder of chief weapons and master of mounts! O one whose physique resembles the firmness of a thunderbolt! O you of supreme strength and valor!
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 15 Verse 13. O King, whose eyes are like lotus petals! O wielder of chief weapons and master of mounts! O one whose physique resembles the firmness of a thunderbolt! O you of supreme strength and valor!

sanskrit: मुष्टिश्लिष्टायुधाभ्यां च व्यायताभ्यां महद्धनुः ।दोर्भ्यां विस्फारयन्भासि महाजलदवद्भृशम् ॥१४॥
transliteration: muṣṭiśliṣṭāyudhābhyāṁ ca vyāyatābhyāṁ mahaddhanuḥ ,dorbhyāṁ visphārayanbhāsi mahājaladavadbhṛśam.
english: With your two outstretched arms, firmly grasping your weapons in your fists, as you stretch your great bow, you shine intensely, like a vast rain cloud.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 15 Verse 14. With your two outstretched arms, firmly grasping your weapons in your fists, as you stretch your great bow, you shine intensely, like a vast rain cloud.

sanskrit: शरवर्षैर्महावेगैरमित्रानभिवर्षतः ।मदन्यं नानुपश्यामि प्रतिवीरं तवाहवे ॥१५॥
transliteration: śaravarṣairmahāvegairamitrānabhivarṣataḥ ,madanyaṁ nānupaśyāmi prativīraṁ tavāhave.
english: As you shower down arrows of great speed upon your enemies, I see no warrior who can match you in battle, other than myself.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 15 Verse 15. As you shower down arrows of great speed upon your enemies, I see no warrior who can match you in battle, other than myself.

sanskrit: रथद्विरदपत्त्यश्वानेकः प्रमथसे बहून् ।मृगसंघानिवारण्ये विभीर्भीमबलो हरिः ॥१६॥
transliteration: rathadviradapattyaśvānekaḥ pramathase bahūn ,mṛgasaṁghānivāraṇye vibhīrbhīmabalo hariḥ.
english: Alone, you crush many chariots, elephants, infantry, and horses, just as a fearless lion of tremendous strength destroys herds of deer in a forest.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 15 Verse 16. Alone, you crush many chariots, elephants, infantry, and horses, just as a fearless lion of tremendous strength destroys herds of deer in a forest.

sanskrit: महता रथघोषेण दिवं भूमिं च नादयन् ।वर्षान्ते सस्यहा पीथो भाभिरापूरयन्निव ॥१७॥
transliteration: mahatā rathaghoṣeṇa divaṁ bhūmiṁ ca nādayan ,varṣānte sasyahā pītho bhābhirāpūrayanniva.
english: Causing the sky and earth to resound with the mighty roar of your chariots, just as the sun at the end of the rainy season, which dries up crops, seems to fill everything with its rays.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 15 Verse 17. Causing the sky and earth to resound with the mighty roar of your chariots, just as the sun at the end of the rainy season, which dries up crops, seems to fill everything with its rays.

sanskrit: संस्पृशानः शरांस्तीक्ष्णांस्तूणादाशीविषोपमान् ।मयैवैकेन युध्यस्व त्र्यम्बकेणान्धको यथा ॥१८॥
transliteration: saṁspṛśānaḥ śarāṁstīkṣṇāṁstūṇādāśīviṣopamān ,mayaivaikena yudhyasva tryambakeṇāndhako yathā.
english: Taking sharp arrows from the quiver, arrows that resemble venomous snakes, fight only with me, just as Andhaka fought with Tryambaka (Lord Shiva).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 15 Verse 18. Taking sharp arrows from the quiver, arrows that resemble venomous snakes, fight only with me, just as Andhaka fought with Tryambaka (Lord Shiva).

sanskrit: एवमुक्तस्तथेत्युक्त्वा प्रहरेति च ताडितः ।कर्णिना द्रोणतनयं विव्याध मलयध्वजः ॥१९॥
transliteration: evamuktastathetyuktvā prahareti ca tāḍitaḥ ,karṇinā droṇatanayaṁ vivyādha malayadhvajaḥ.
english: Thus addressed, saying 'So be it' and 'Strike!', and though himself struck by arrows, Malayadhvaja pierced Drona's son (Aśvatthāmā) with his shafted arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 15 Verse 19. Thus addressed, saying 'So be it' and 'Strike!', and though himself struck by arrows, Malayadhvaja pierced Drona's son (Aśvatthāmā) with his shafted arrows.

sanskrit: मर्मभेदिभिरत्युग्रैर्बाणैरग्निशिखोपमैः ।स्मयन्नभ्यहनद्द्रौणिः पाण्ड्यमाचार्यसत्तमः ॥२०॥
transliteration: marmabhedibhiratyugrairbāṇairagniśikhopamaiḥ ,smayannabhyahanaddrauṇiḥ pāṇḍyamācāryasattamaḥ.
english: Smiling, Droṇi (Aśvatthāmā), the foremost among teachers (guru), struck the Pandya king (Malayadhvaja) with arrows that pierced vital spots, were exceedingly terrible, and resembled flames of fire.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 15 Verse 20. Smiling, Droṇi (Aśvatthāmā), the foremost among teachers (guru), struck the Pandya king (Malayadhvaja) with arrows that pierced vital spots, were exceedingly terrible, and resembled flames of fire.

sanskrit: ततो नवापरांस्तीक्ष्णान्नाराचान्कङ्कवाससः ।गत्या दशम्या संयुक्तानश्वत्थामा व्यवासृजत् ॥२१॥
transliteration: tato navāparāṁstīkṣṇānnārācānkaṅkavāsasaḥ ,gatyā daśamyā saṁyuktānaśvatthāmā vyavāsṛjat.
english: Then Aśvatthāmā released nine other sharp, iron arrows, fletched with heron feathers, which flew with extreme speed.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 15 Verse 21. Then Aśvatthāmā released nine other sharp, iron arrows, fletched with heron feathers, which flew with extreme speed.

sanskrit: तेषां पञ्चाच्छिनत्पाण्ड्यः पञ्चभिर्निशितैः शरैः ।चत्वारोऽभ्याहनन्वाहानाशु ते व्यसवोऽभवन् ॥२२॥
transliteration: teṣāṁ pañcācchinatpāṇḍyaḥ pañcabhirniśitaiḥ śaraiḥ ,catvāro'bhyāhananvāhānāśu te vyasavo'bhavan.
english: Pāṇḍya, with five sharp arrows, cut down five of his (opponent's) [arrows/weapons]. Four (of Pāṇḍya's arrows) struck the horses, and those (horses) immediately became lifeless.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 15 Verse 22. Pāṇḍya, with five sharp arrows, cut down five of his (opponent's) [arrows/weapons]. Four (of Pāṇḍya's arrows) struck the horses, and those (horses) immediately became lifeless.

sanskrit: अथ द्रोणसुतस्येषूंस्तांश्छित्त्वा निशितैः शरैः ।धनुर्ज्यां विततां पाण्ड्यश्चिच्छेदादित्यवर्चसः ॥२३॥
transliteration: atha droṇasutasyeṣūṁstāṁśchittvā niśitaiḥ śaraiḥ ,dhanurjyāṁ vitatāṁ pāṇḍyaścicchedādityavarcasaḥ.
english: Then, Pāṇḍya, brilliant as the sun, having severed those arrows of Droṇa's son (Ashvatthama) with sharp arrows, cut his (Ashvatthama's) strung bowstring.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 15 Verse 23. Then, Pāṇḍya, brilliant as the sun, having severed those arrows of Droṇa's son (Ashvatthama) with sharp arrows, cut his (Ashvatthama's) strung bowstring.

sanskrit: विज्यं धनुरथाधिज्यं कृत्वा द्रौणिरमित्रहा ।ततः शरसहस्राणि प्रेषयामास पाण्ड्यतः ।इषुसंबाधमाकाशमकरोद्दिश एव च ॥२४॥
transliteration: vijyaṁ dhanurathādhijyaṁ kṛtvā drauṇiramitrahā ,tataḥ śarasahasrāṇi preṣayāmāsa pāṇḍyataḥ ,iṣusaṁbādhamākāśamakaroddiśa eva ca.
english: Then, Droṇa's son (Ashvatthama), the slayer of enemies, having strung his bow that was without a string, thereafter dispatched thousands of arrows towards Pāṇḍya. He made the sky and all directions crowded with arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 15 Verse 24. Then, Droṇa's son (Ashvatthama), the slayer of enemies, having strung his bow that was without a string, thereafter dispatched thousands of arrows towards Pāṇḍya. He made the sky and all directions crowded with arrows.

sanskrit: ततस्तानस्यतः सर्वान्द्रौणेर्बाणान्महात्मनः ।जानानोऽप्यक्षयान्पाण्ड्योऽशातयत्पुरुषर्षभः ॥२५॥
transliteration: tatastānasyataḥ sarvāndrauṇerbāṇānmahātmanaḥ ,jānāno'pyakṣayānpāṇḍyo'śātayatpuruṣarṣabhaḥ.
english: Then, Pāṇḍya, the best among men (puruṣa), even though he knew those inexhaustible arrows being discharged by the great-souled Droṇa's son, struck them all down.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 15 Verse 25. Then, Pāṇḍya, the best among men (puruṣa), even though he knew those inexhaustible arrows being discharged by the great-souled Droṇa's son, struck them all down.

sanskrit: प्रहितांस्तान्प्रयत्नेन छित्त्वा द्रौणेरिषूनरिः ।चक्ररक्षौ ततस्तस्य प्राणुदन्निशितैः शरैः ॥२६॥
transliteration: prahitāṁstānprayatnena chittvā drauṇeriṣūnariḥ ,cakrarakṣau tatastasya prāṇudanniśitaiḥ śaraiḥ.
english: The enemy, having diligently cut down those arrows shot by Droṇa's son, then struck his two chariot-wheel protectors with sharp arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 15 Verse 26. The enemy, having diligently cut down those arrows shot by Droṇa's son, then struck his two chariot-wheel protectors with sharp arrows.

sanskrit: अथारेर्लाघवं दृष्ट्वा मण्डलीकृतकार्मुकः ।प्रास्यद्द्रोणसुतो बाणान्वृष्टिं पूषानुजो यथा ॥२७॥
transliteration: athārerlāghavaṁ dṛṣṭvā maṇḍalīkṛtakārmukaḥ ,prāsyaddroṇasuto bāṇānvṛṣṭiṁ pūṣānujo yathā.
english: Then, having witnessed the enemy's swiftness, Droṇa's son, with his bow drawn into a circle, shot a shower of arrows, just as the younger brother of Pūṣan (Indra) sends forth rain.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 15 Verse 27. Then, having witnessed the enemy's swiftness, Droṇa's son, with his bow drawn into a circle, shot a shower of arrows, just as the younger brother of Pūṣan (Indra) sends forth rain.

sanskrit: अष्टावष्टगवान्यूहुः शकटानि यदायुधम् ।अह्नस्तदष्टभागेन द्रौणिश्चिक्षेप मारिष ॥२८॥
transliteration: aṣṭāvaṣṭagavānyūhuḥ śakaṭāni yadāyudham ,ahnastadaṣṭabhāgena drauṇiścikṣepa māriṣa.
english: O venerable one, Droṇa's son (Drauṇi) fired off so many arrows that he emptied eight wagons, each drawn by eight oxen and laden with his weaponry, within just one-eighth part of that day.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 15 Verse 28. O venerable one, Droṇa's son (Drauṇi) fired off so many arrows that he emptied eight wagons, each drawn by eight oxen and laden with his weaponry, within just one-eighth part of that day.

sanskrit: तमन्तकमिव क्रुद्धमन्तकालान्तकोपमम् ।ये ये ददृशिरे तत्र विसंज्ञाः प्रायशोऽभवन् ॥२९॥
transliteration: tamantakamiva kruddhamantakālāntakopamam ,ye ye dadṛśire tatra visaṁjñāḥ prāyaśo'bhavan.
english: Those who saw him there, enraged like Yama, the Destroyer, and resembling the very destroyer of the Destroyer of Time (Shiva or Vishnu), mostly became senseless.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 15 Verse 29. Those who saw him there, enraged like Yama, the Destroyer, and resembling the very destroyer of the Destroyer of Time (Shiva or Vishnu), mostly became senseless.

sanskrit: पर्जन्य इव घर्मान्ते वृष्ट्या साद्रिद्रुमां महीम् ।आचार्यपुत्रस्तां सेनां बाणवृष्ट्याभ्यवीवृषत् ॥३०॥
transliteration: parjanya iva gharmānte vṛṣṭyā sādridrumāṁ mahīm ,ācāryaputrastāṁ senāṁ bāṇavṛṣṭyābhyavīvṛṣat.
english: Just as a rain-cloud at the end of summer showers the earth, along with its mountains and trees, with rain, similarly, the preceptor's son showered that army with a torrent of arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 15 Verse 30. Just as a rain-cloud at the end of summer showers the earth, along with its mountains and trees, with rain, similarly, the preceptor's son showered that army with a torrent of arrows.

sanskrit: द्रौणिपर्जन्यमुक्तां तां बाणवृष्टिं सुदुःसहाम् ।वायव्यास्त्रेण स क्षिप्रं रुद्ध्वा पाण्ड्यानिलोऽनदत् ॥३१॥
transliteration: drauṇiparjanyamuktāṁ tāṁ bāṇavṛṣṭiṁ suduḥsahām ,vāyavyāstreṇa sa kṣipraṁ ruddhvā pāṇḍyānilo'nadat.
english: That exceedingly difficult-to-endure rain of arrows, released by Drauni (who was like a rain-cloud), was quickly blocked by him. Then, the Pāṇḍya king, like a wind, roared aloud.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 15 Verse 31. That exceedingly difficult-to-endure rain of arrows, released by Drauni (who was like a rain-cloud), was quickly blocked by him. Then, the Pāṇḍya king, like a wind, roared aloud.

sanskrit: तस्य नानदतः केतुं चन्दनागुरुभूषितम् ।मलयप्रतिमं द्रौणिश्छित्त्वाश्वांश्चतुरोऽहनत् ॥३२॥
transliteration: tasya nānadataḥ ketuṁ candanāgurubhūṣitam ,malayapratimaṁ drauṇiśchittvāśvāṁścaturo'hanat.
english: While he (the Pāṇḍya) was roaring, Drauni cut down his banner – which was adorned with sandalwood and agallochum and resembled Mount Malaya – and then he killed his four horses.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 15 Verse 32. While he (the Pāṇḍya) was roaring, Drauni cut down his banner – which was adorned with sandalwood and agallochum and resembled Mount Malaya – and then he killed his four horses.

sanskrit: सूतमेकेषुणा हत्वा महाजलदनिस्वनम् ।धनुश्छित्त्वार्धचन्द्रेण व्यधमत्तिलशो रथम् ॥३३॥
transliteration: sūtamekeṣuṇā hatvā mahājaladanisvanam ,dhanuśchittvārdhacandreṇa vyadhamattilaśo ratham.
english: Having killed the charioteer (whose battle cry was like a great thundercloud) with a single arrow, and having cut his bow with a crescent-shaped arrow, he then completely shattered the chariot into tiny pieces.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 15 Verse 33. Having killed the charioteer (whose battle cry was like a great thundercloud) with a single arrow, and having cut his bow with a crescent-shaped arrow, he then completely shattered the chariot into tiny pieces.

sanskrit: अस्त्रैरस्त्राणि संवार्य छित्त्वा सर्वायुधानि च ।प्राप्तमप्यहितं द्रौणिर्न जघान रणेप्सया ॥३४॥
transliteration: astrairastrāṇi saṁvārya chittvā sarvāyudhāni ca ,prāptamapyahitaṁ drauṇirna jaghāna raṇepsayā.
english: Aśvatthāman, having parried weapons with his own and severed all of the enemy's armaments, did not kill the approaching foe, driven by his eagerness for battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 15 Verse 34. Aśvatthāman, having parried weapons with his own and severed all of the enemy's armaments, did not kill the approaching foe, driven by his eagerness for battle.

sanskrit: हतेश्वरो दन्तिवरः सुकल्पितस्त्वराभिसृष्टः प्रतिशर्मगो बली ।तमध्यतिष्ठन्मलयेश्वरो महान्यथाद्रिशृङ्गं हरिरुन्नदंस्तथा ॥३५॥
transliteration: hateśvaro dantivaraḥ sukalpita;stvarābhisṛṣṭaḥ pratiśarmago balī ,tamadhyatiṣṭhanmalayeśvaro mahā;nyathādriśṛṅgaṁ harirunnadaṁstathā.
english: The great and powerful lord of Malaya mounted that finest of elephants – an elephant whose master had been slain, which was well-trained, swiftly dispatched, and advancing against Śarma – just as a roaring lion ascends a mountain peak.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 15 Verse 35. The great and powerful lord of Malaya mounted that finest of elephants – an elephant whose master had been slain, which was well-trained, swiftly dispatched, and advancing against Śarma – just as a roaring lion ascends a mountain peak.

sanskrit: स तोमरं भास्कररश्मिसंनिभं बलास्त्रसर्गोत्तमयत्नमन्युभिः ।ससर्ज शीघ्रं प्रतिपीडयन्गजं गुरोः सुतायाद्रिपतीश्वरो नदन् ॥३६॥
transliteration: sa tomaraṁ bhāskararaśmisaṁnibhaṁ; balāstrasargottamayatnamanyubhiḥ ,sasarja śīghraṁ pratipīḍayangajaṁ; guroḥ sutāyādripatīśvaro nadan.
english: That great lord of the mountain (Malaya), roaring, swiftly launched a javelin, shining like the sun's rays, crushing the elephant, with his strength, supreme effort in weapon-release, and wrath, (targeting) the son of the preceptor (Aśvatthāman).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 15 Verse 36. That great lord of the mountain (Malaya), roaring, swiftly launched a javelin, shining like the sun's rays, crushing the elephant, with his strength, supreme effort in weapon-release, and wrath, (targeting) the son of the preceptor (Aśvatthāman).

sanskrit: मणिप्रतानोत्तमवज्रहाटकैरलंकृतं चांशुकमाल्यमौक्तिकैः ।हतोऽस्यसावित्यसकृन्मुदा नदन्पराभिनद्द्रौणिवराङ्गभूषणम् ॥३७॥
transliteration: maṇipratānottamavajrahāṭakai;ralaṁkṛtaṁ cāṁśukamālyamauktikaiḥ ,hato'syasāvityasakṛnmudā nada;nparābhinaddrauṇivarāṅgabhūṣaṇam.
english: And, repeatedly exclaiming with joy, 'His (elephant) is killed!', the roaring (lord of Malaya) shattered the splendid body ornament of Drauṇi (Aśvatthāman), an ornament adorned with excellent clusters of jewels, diamonds, gold, fine cloths, garlands, and pearls.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 15 Verse 37. And, repeatedly exclaiming with joy, 'His (elephant) is killed!', the roaring (lord of Malaya) shattered the splendid body ornament of Drauṇi (Aśvatthāman), an ornament adorned with excellent clusters of jewels, diamonds, gold, fine cloths, garlands, and pearls.

sanskrit: तदर्कचन्द्रग्रहपावकत्विषं भृशाभिघातात्पतितं विचूर्णितम् ।महेन्द्रवज्राभिहतं महावनं यथाद्रिशृङ्गं धरणीतले तथा ॥३८॥
transliteration: tadarkacandragrahapāvakatviṣaṁ; bhṛśābhighātātpatitaṁ vicūrṇitam ,mahendravajrābhihataṁ mahāvanaṁ; yathādriśṛṅgaṁ dharaṇītale tathā.
english: That being, brilliant with the splendor of the sun, moon, planets, and fire, fell from a mighty impact, completely crushed, just as a mountain peak struck by Indra's thunderbolt, or a great forest, lies shattered on the earth's surface.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 15 Verse 38. That being, brilliant with the splendor of the sun, moon, planets, and fire, fell from a mighty impact, completely crushed, just as a mountain peak struck by Indra's thunderbolt, or a great forest, lies shattered on the earth's surface.

sanskrit: ततः प्रजज्वाल परेण मन्युना पदाहतो नागपतिर्यथा तथा ।समादधे चान्तकदण्डसंनिभानिषूनमित्रान्तकरांश्चतुर्दश ॥३९॥
transliteration: tataḥ prajajvāla pareṇa manyunā; padāhato nāgapatiryathā tathā ,samādadhe cāntakadaṇḍasaṁnibhā;niṣūnamitrāntakarāṁścaturdaśa.
english: Then, just as a lord of elephants, when struck by a foot, flares up with extreme rage, so too did he blaze with intense anger. And he took up fourteen arrows, which resembled the staff of Death (Antaka) and were capable of ending his enemies.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 15 Verse 39. Then, just as a lord of elephants, when struck by a foot, flares up with extreme rage, so too did he blaze with intense anger. And he took up fourteen arrows, which resembled the staff of Death (Antaka) and were capable of ending his enemies.

sanskrit: द्विपस्य पादाग्रकरान्स पञ्चभिर्नृपस्य बाहू च शिरोऽथ च त्रिभिः ।जघान षड्भिः षडृतूत्तमत्विषः स पाण्ड्यराजानुचरान्महारथान् ॥४०॥
transliteration: dvipasya pādāgrakarānsa pañcabhi;rnṛpasya bāhū ca śiro'tha ca tribhiḥ ,jaghāna ṣaḍbhiḥ ṣaḍṛtūttamatviṣaḥ; sa pāṇḍyarājānucarānmahārathān.
english: He killed the elephant's four feet and trunk with five (arrows), and the king's two arms and head with three (arrows). With six (arrows), he struck down the Pāṇḍya king's great charioteer followers, who possessed a radiance like the finest of the six seasons.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 15 Verse 40. He killed the elephant's four feet and trunk with five (arrows), and the king's two arms and head with three (arrows). With six (arrows), he struck down the Pāṇḍya king's great charioteer followers, who possessed a radiance like the finest of the six seasons.

sanskrit: सुदीर्घवृत्तौ वरचन्दनोक्षितौ सुवर्णमुक्तामणिवज्रभूषितौ ।भुजौ धरायां पतितौ नृपस्य तौ विवेष्टतुस्तार्क्ष्यहताविवोरगौ ॥४१॥
transliteration: sudīrghavṛttau varacandanokṣitau; suvarṇamuktāmaṇivajrabhūṣitau ,bhujau dharāyāṁ patitau nṛpasya tau; viveṣṭatustārkṣyahatāvivoragau.
english: Those two arms of the king - very long, rounded, anointed with exquisite sandalwood paste, and adorned with gold, pearls, jewels, and diamonds - fell to the earth. There, they writhed like two snakes struck by Tārkṣya (Garuda).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 15 Verse 41. Those two arms of the king - very long, rounded, anointed with exquisite sandalwood paste, and adorned with gold, pearls, jewels, and diamonds - fell to the earth. There, they writhed like two snakes struck by Tārkṣya (Garuda).

sanskrit: शिरश्च तत्पूर्णशशिप्रभाननं सरोषताम्रायतनेत्रमुन्नसम् ।क्षितौ विबभ्राज पतत्सकुण्डलं विशाखयोर्मध्यगतः शशी यथा ॥४२॥
transliteration: śiraśca tatpūrṇaśaśiprabhānanaṁ; saroṣatāmrāyatanetramunnasam ,kṣitau vibabhrāja patatsakuṇḍalaṁ; viśākhayormadhyagataḥ śaśī yathā.
english: That head, with a face radiant like the full moon, angry, reddish, and wide eyes, and a prominent nose, lay fallen on the ground with its earrings, gleaming like the moon positioned between the two Viśākha stars.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 15 Verse 42. That head, with a face radiant like the full moon, angry, reddish, and wide eyes, and a prominent nose, lay fallen on the ground with its earrings, gleaming like the moon positioned between the two Viśākha stars.

sanskrit: समाप्तविद्यं तु गुरोः सुतं नृपः समाप्तकर्माणमुपेत्य ते सुतः ।सुहृद्वृतोऽत्यर्थमपूजयन्मुदा जिते बलौ विष्णुमिवामरेश्वरः ॥४३॥
transliteration: samāptavidyaṁ tu guroḥ sutaṁ nṛpaḥ; samāptakarmāṇamupetya te sutaḥ ,suhṛdvṛto'tyarthamapūjayanmudā; jite balau viṣṇumivāmareśvaraḥ.
english: Your son, O King, surrounded by friends, having approached the guru's son - who had completed his education (vidyā) and duties (karma) - greatly honored him with joy, just as the lord of the gods (Indra) honored Viṣṇu when the demon Bala was conquered.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 15 Verse 43. Your son, O King, surrounded by friends, having approached the guru's son - who had completed his education (vidyā) and duties (karma) - greatly honored him with joy, just as the lord of the gods (Indra) honored Viṣṇu when the demon Bala was conquered.

sanskrit: धृतराष्ट्र उवाच ।पाण्ड्ये हते किमकरोदर्जुनो युधि संजय ।एकवीरेण कर्णेन द्रावितेषु परेषु च ॥१॥
transliteration: dhṛtarāṣṭra uvāca ,pāṇḍye hate kimakarodarjuno yudhi saṁjaya ,ekavīreṇa karṇena drāviteṣu pareṣu ca.
english: Dhṛtarāṣṭra said: "O Sañjaya, what did Arjuna do in battle after the Pāṇḍya king was slain, and when the enemies had been put to flight by the singular hero Karṇa?"
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 16 Verse 1. Dhṛtarāṣṭra said: "O Sañjaya, what did Arjuna do in battle after the Pāṇḍya king was slain, and when the enemies had been put to flight by the singular hero Karṇa?"

sanskrit: समाप्तविद्यो बलवान्युक्तो वीरश्च पाण्डवः ।सर्वभूतेष्वनुज्ञातः शंकरेण महात्मना ॥२॥
transliteration: samāptavidyo balavānyukto vīraśca pāṇḍavaḥ ,sarvabhūteṣvanujñātaḥ śaṁkareṇa mahātmanā.
english: The Pāṇḍava, who had completed his education (vidyā), was mighty, disciplined (yoga), and heroic. He was acknowledged by the great-souled (mahātman) Śaṅkara among all beings.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 16 Verse 2. The Pāṇḍava, who had completed his education (vidyā), was mighty, disciplined (yoga), and heroic. He was acknowledged by the great-souled (mahātman) Śaṅkara among all beings.

sanskrit: तस्मान्महद्भयं तीव्रममित्रघ्नाद्धनंजयात् ।स यत्तत्राकरोत्पार्थस्तन्ममाचक्ष्व संजय ॥३॥
transliteration: tasmānmahadbhayaṁ tīvramamitraghnāddhanaṁjayāt ,sa yattatrākarotpārthastanmamācakṣva saṁjaya.
english: Therefore, tell me, Sañjaya, about the intense and great fear [that comes] from Dhanañjaya (Arjuna), the destroyer of enemies. Tell me what Pārtha (Arjuna) did in that situation.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 16 Verse 3. Therefore, tell me, Sañjaya, about the intense and great fear [that comes] from Dhanañjaya (Arjuna), the destroyer of enemies. Tell me what Pārtha (Arjuna) did in that situation.

sanskrit: संजय उवाच ।हते पाण्ड्येऽर्जुनं कृष्णस्त्वरन्नाह वचो हितम् ।पश्यातिमान्यं राजानमपयातांश्च पाण्डवान् ॥४॥
transliteration: saṁjaya uvāca ,hate pāṇḍye'rjunaṁ kṛṣṇastvarannāha vaco hitam ,paśyātimānyaṁ rājānamapayātāṁśca pāṇḍavān.
english: Sañjaya said: "When the Pāṇḍya king had been killed, Kṛṣṇa, in haste, spoke beneficial words to Arjuna: 'Behold the highly esteemed king and the Pāṇḍavas who have departed!'"
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 16 Verse 4. Sañjaya said: "When the Pāṇḍya king had been killed, Kṛṣṇa, in haste, spoke beneficial words to Arjuna: 'Behold the highly esteemed king and the Pāṇḍavas who have departed!'"

sanskrit: अश्वत्थाम्नश्च संकल्पाद्धताः कर्णेन सृञ्जयाः ।तथाश्वनरनागानां कृतं च कदनं महत् ।इत्याचष्ट सुदुर्धर्षो वासुदेवः किरीटिने ॥५॥
transliteration: aśvatthāmnaśca saṁkalpāddhatāḥ karṇena sṛñjayāḥ ,tathāśvanaranāgānāṁ kṛtaṁ ca kadanaṁ mahat ,ityācaṣṭa sudurdharṣo vāsudevaḥ kirīṭine.
english: And by the resolve of Aśvatthāman, the Sṛñjayas were slain by Karṇa. Likewise, a great slaughter of horses, men, and nāgas was carried out. Thus spoke the formidable Vāsudeva (Kṛṣṇa) to the crowned one (Arjuna).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 16 Verse 5. And by the resolve of Aśvatthāman, the Sṛñjayas were slain by Karṇa. Likewise, a great slaughter of horses, men, and nāgas was carried out. Thus spoke the formidable Vāsudeva (Kṛṣṇa) to the crowned one (Arjuna).

sanskrit: एतच्छ्रुत्वा च दृष्ट्वा च भ्रातुर्घोरं महद्भयम् ।वाहयाश्वान्हृषीकेश क्षिप्रमित्याह पाण्डवः ॥६॥
transliteration: etacchrutvā ca dṛṣṭvā ca bhrāturghoraṁ mahadbhayam ,vāhayāśvānhṛṣīkeśa kṣipramityāha pāṇḍavaḥ.
english: Having heard and seen this dreadful and great danger (bhayam) facing his brother, the Pāṇḍava (Arjuna) then said, 'O Hṛṣīkeśa (Kṛṣṇa), drive the horses quickly!'
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 16 Verse 6. Having heard and seen this dreadful and great danger (bhayam) facing his brother, the Pāṇḍava (Arjuna) then said, 'O Hṛṣīkeśa (Kṛṣṇa), drive the horses quickly!'

sanskrit: ततः प्रायाद्धृषीकेशो रथेनाप्रतियोधिना ।दारुणश्च पुनस्तत्र प्रादुरासीत्समागमः ॥७॥
transliteration: tataḥ prāyāddhṛṣīkeśo rathenāpratiyodhinā ,dāruṇaśca punastatra prādurāsītsamāgamaḥ.
english: Then Hṛṣīkeśa (Kṛṣṇa) proceeded in his invincible chariot. And again, a dreadful engagement took place there.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 16 Verse 7. Then Hṛṣīkeśa (Kṛṣṇa) proceeded in his invincible chariot. And again, a dreadful engagement took place there.

sanskrit: ततः प्रववृते भूयः संग्रामो राजसत्तम ।कर्णस्य पाण्डवानां च यमराष्ट्रविवर्धनः ॥८॥
transliteration: tataḥ pravavṛte bhūyaḥ saṁgrāmo rājasattama ,karṇasya pāṇḍavānāṁ ca yamarāṣṭravivardhanaḥ.
english: Then, O best of kings, the battle between Karṇa and the Pāṇḍavas began once more, a conflict that expanded the dominion of Yama, the god of death.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 16 Verse 8. Then, O best of kings, the battle between Karṇa and the Pāṇḍavas began once more, a conflict that expanded the dominion of Yama, the god of death.

sanskrit: धनूंषि बाणान्परिघानसितोमरपट्टिशान् ।मुसलानि भुशुण्डीश्च शक्तिऋष्टिपरश्वधान् ॥९॥
transliteration: dhanūṁṣi bāṇānparighānasitomarapaṭṭiśān ,musalāni bhuśuṇḍīśca śaktiṛṣṭiparaśvadhān.
english: They carried bows, arrows, iron clubs, black javelins and sharp-edged spears, maces, *bhuśuṇḍīs*, as well as spears, lances, and battle-axes.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 16 Verse 9. They carried bows, arrows, iron clubs, black javelins and sharp-edged spears, maces, *bhuśuṇḍīs*, as well as spears, lances, and battle-axes.

sanskrit: गदाः प्रासानसीन्कुन्तान्भिण्डिपालान्महाङ्कुशान् ।प्रगृह्य क्षिप्रमापेतुः परस्परजिगीषया ॥१०॥
transliteration: gadāḥ prāsānasīnkuntānbhiṇḍipālānmahāṅkuśān ,pragṛhya kṣipramāpetuḥ parasparajigīṣayā.
english: Seizing maces, darts, swords, lances, *bhiṇḍipālas*, and great hooks, they swiftly rushed (into battle) with the desire to conquer one another.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 16 Verse 10. Seizing maces, darts, swords, lances, *bhiṇḍipālas*, and great hooks, they swiftly rushed (into battle) with the desire to conquer one another.

sanskrit: बाणज्यातलशब्देन द्यां दिशः प्रदिशो वियत् ।पृथिवीं नेमिघोषेण नादयन्तोऽभ्ययुः परान् ॥११॥
transliteration: bāṇajyātalaśabdena dyāṁ diśaḥ pradiśo viyat ,pṛthivīṁ nemighoṣeṇa nādayanto'bhyayuḥ parān.
english: They advanced upon the enemies, making the sky, the main directions, the intermediate directions, the atmosphere, and the earth resound with the twang of their bowstrings and the roar of their chariot wheels.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 16 Verse 11. They advanced upon the enemies, making the sky, the main directions, the intermediate directions, the atmosphere, and the earth resound with the twang of their bowstrings and the roar of their chariot wheels.

sanskrit: तेन शब्देन महता संहृष्टाश्चक्रुराहवम् ।वीरा वीरैर्महाघोरं कलहान्तं तितीर्षवः ॥१२॥
transliteration: tena śabdena mahatā saṁhṛṣṭāścakrurāhavam ,vīrā vīrairmahāghoraṁ kalahāntaṁ titīrṣavaḥ.
english: Greatly emboldened by that mighty sound, the warriors, eager to conclude the terrible conflict, engaged in a dreadful battle with their opponents.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 16 Verse 12. Greatly emboldened by that mighty sound, the warriors, eager to conclude the terrible conflict, engaged in a dreadful battle with their opponents.

sanskrit: ज्यातलत्रधनुःशब्दाः कुञ्जराणां च बृंहितम् ।ताडितानां च पततां निनादः सुमहानभूत् ॥१३॥
transliteration: jyātalatradhanuḥśabdāḥ kuñjarāṇāṁ ca bṛṁhitam ,tāḍitānāṁ ca patatāṁ ninādaḥ sumahānabhūt.
english: There was a mighty roar comprising the twang of bowstrings, the sounds of arm-guards and bows, the trumpeting of elephants, and the cries of those struck and falling.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 16 Verse 13. There was a mighty roar comprising the twang of bowstrings, the sounds of arm-guards and bows, the trumpeting of elephants, and the cries of those struck and falling.

sanskrit: बाणशब्दांश्च विविधाञ्शूराणामभिगर्जताम् ।श्रुत्वा शब्दं भृशं त्रेसुर्जघ्नुर्मम्लुश्च भारत ॥१४॥
transliteration: bāṇaśabdāṁśca vividhāñśūrāṇāmabhigarjatām ,śrutvā śabdaṁ bhṛśaṁ tresurjaghnurmamluśca bhārata.
english: O descendant of Bharata, upon hearing the diverse sounds of arrows and the roaring of the warriors, they trembled greatly; some struck (at their foes), while others fainted.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 16 Verse 14. O descendant of Bharata, upon hearing the diverse sounds of arrows and the roaring of the warriors, they trembled greatly; some struck (at their foes), while others fainted.

sanskrit: तेषां नानद्यतां चैव शस्त्रवृष्टिं च मुञ्चताम् ।बहूनाधिरथिः कर्णः प्रममाथ रणेषुभिः ॥१५॥
transliteration: teṣāṁ nānadyatāṁ caiva śastravṛṣṭiṁ ca muñcatām ,bahūnādhirathiḥ karṇaḥ pramamātha raṇeṣubhiḥ.
english: Adhirathi Karṇa, with his battle-arrows, slaughtered many of those warriors in battle, even as they roared and unleashed a shower of weapons.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 16 Verse 15. Adhirathi Karṇa, with his battle-arrows, slaughtered many of those warriors in battle, even as they roared and unleashed a shower of weapons.

sanskrit: पञ्च पाञ्चालवीराणां रथान्दश च पञ्च च ।साश्वसूतध्वजान्कर्णः शरैर्निन्ये यमक्षयम् ॥१६॥
transliteration: pañca pāñcālavīrāṇāṁ rathāndaśa ca pañca ca ,sāśvasūtadhvajānkarṇaḥ śarairninye yamakṣayam.
english: Karṇa, using his arrows, dispatched fifteen chariots of the Pañchala heroes - complete with their horses, charioteers, and banners - to the abode of Yama (yamakṣayam), thus sending them to their death.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 16 Verse 16. Karṇa, using his arrows, dispatched fifteen chariots of the Pañchala heroes - complete with their horses, charioteers, and banners - to the abode of Yama (yamakṣayam), thus sending them to their death.

sanskrit: योधमुख्या महावीर्याः पाण्डूनां कर्णमाहवे ।शीघ्रास्त्रा दिवमावृत्य परिवव्रुः समन्ततः ॥१७॥
transliteration: yodhamukhyā mahāvīryāḥ pāṇḍūnāṁ karṇamāhave ,śīghrāstrā divamāvṛtya parivavruḥ samantataḥ.
english: The chief and greatly powerful warriors of the Pāṇḍavas, who were swift with their weapons, surrounded Karṇa in battle from all sides, covering the sky as they did so.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 16 Verse 17. The chief and greatly powerful warriors of the Pāṇḍavas, who were swift with their weapons, surrounded Karṇa in battle from all sides, covering the sky as they did so.

sanskrit: ततः कर्णो द्विषत्सेनां शरवर्षैर्विलोडयन् ।विजगाहेऽण्डजापूर्णां पद्मिनीमिव यूथपः ॥१८॥
transliteration: tataḥ karṇo dviṣatsenāṁ śaravarṣairviloḍayan ,vijagāhe'ṇḍajāpūrṇāṁ padminīmiva yūthapaḥ.
english: Then Karṇa, unsettling the enemy army with volleys of arrows, plunged into it just as a chief elephant would enter a lotus pond teeming with birds.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 16 Verse 18. Then Karṇa, unsettling the enemy army with volleys of arrows, plunged into it just as a chief elephant would enter a lotus pond teeming with birds.

sanskrit: द्विषन्मध्यमवस्कन्द्य राधेयो धनुरुत्तमम् ।विधुन्वानः शितैर्बाणैः शिरांस्युन्मथ्य पातयत् ॥१९॥
transliteration: dviṣanmadhyamavaskandya rādheyo dhanuruttamam ,vidhunvānaḥ śitairbāṇaiḥ śirāṁsyunmathya pātayat.
english: Karna, having assailed the ranks of his enemies, brandished his excellent bow and, with sharp arrows, crushed and made heads fall.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 16 Verse 19. Karna, having assailed the ranks of his enemies, brandished his excellent bow and, with sharp arrows, crushed and made heads fall.

sanskrit: चर्मवर्माणि संछिन्द्य निर्वापमिव देहिनाम् ।विषेहुर्नास्य संपर्कं द्वितीयस्य पतत्रिणः ॥२०॥
transliteration: carmavarmāṇi saṁchindya nirvāpamiva dehinām ,viṣehurnāsya saṁparkaṁ dvitīyasya patatriṇaḥ.
english: Having ripped through shields and armors, as though bringing about the cessation (nirvāpa) of embodied beings, they could not withstand his attack, which was like a second arrow.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 16 Verse 20. Having ripped through shields and armors, as though bringing about the cessation (nirvāpa) of embodied beings, they could not withstand his attack, which was like a second arrow.

sanskrit: वर्मदेहासुमथनैर्धनुषः प्रच्युतैः शरैः ।मौर्व्या तलत्रैर्न्यवधीत्कशया वाजिनो यथा ॥२१॥
transliteration: varmadehāsumathanairdhanuṣaḥ pracyutaiḥ śaraiḥ ,maurvyā talatrairnyavadhītkaśayā vājino yathā.
english: With arrows shot from his bow - arrows that crushed armor, bodies, and lives - he struck down [the enemies] by means of his bowstring and hand-guards, just as one controls horses with a whip.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 16 Verse 21. With arrows shot from his bow - arrows that crushed armor, bodies, and lives - he struck down [the enemies] by means of his bowstring and hand-guards, just as one controls horses with a whip.

sanskrit: पाण्डुसृञ्जयपाञ्चालाञ्शरगोचरमानयत् ।ममर्द कर्णस्तरसा सिंहो मृगगणानिव ॥२२॥
transliteration: pāṇḍusṛñjayapāñcālāñśaragocaramānayat ,mamarda karṇastarasā siṁho mṛgagaṇāniva.
english: Karna brought the Pandavas, Srinjayas, and Panchalas within the range of his arrows. He crushed them with great force, just as a lion overwhelms herds of deer.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 16 Verse 22. Karna brought the Pandavas, Srinjayas, and Panchalas within the range of his arrows. He crushed them with great force, just as a lion overwhelms herds of deer.

sanskrit: ततः पाञ्चालपुत्राश्च द्रौपदेयाश्च मारिष ।यमौ च युयुधानश्च सहिताः कर्णमभ्ययुः ॥२३॥
transliteration: tataḥ pāñcālaputrāśca draupadeyāśca māriṣa ,yamau ca yuyudhānaśca sahitāḥ karṇamabhyayuḥ.
english: Then, O revered one, the sons of Pañcāla, the sons of Draupadī, the twins (Nakula and Sahadeva), and Yuyudhāna, all together, advanced towards Karṇa.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 16 Verse 23. Then, O revered one, the sons of Pañcāla, the sons of Draupadī, the twins (Nakula and Sahadeva), and Yuyudhāna, all together, advanced towards Karṇa.

sanskrit: व्यायच्छमानाः सुभृशं कुरुपाण्डवसृञ्जयाः ।प्रियानसून्रणे त्यक्त्वा योधा जग्मुः परस्परम् ॥२४॥
transliteration: vyāyacchamānāḥ subhṛśaṁ kurupāṇḍavasṛñjayāḥ ,priyānasūnraṇe tyaktvā yodhā jagmuḥ parasparam.
english: The warriors among the Kurus, Pāṇḍavas, and Sṛñjayas, fighting very fiercely, abandoned their dear lives in battle and attacked each other.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 16 Verse 24. The warriors among the Kurus, Pāṇḍavas, and Sṛñjayas, fighting very fiercely, abandoned their dear lives in battle and attacked each other.

sanskrit: सुसंनद्धाः कवचिनः सशिरस्त्राणभूषणाः ।गदाभिर्मुसलैश्चान्ये परिघैश्च महारथाः ॥२५॥
transliteration: susaṁnaddhāḥ kavacinaḥ saśirastrāṇabhūṣaṇāḥ ,gadābhirmusalaiścānye parighaiśca mahārathāḥ.
english: Well-equipped, armored, and adorned with helmets, these great chariot-warriors [fought] with maces, and others with clubs and iron bars.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 16 Verse 25. Well-equipped, armored, and adorned with helmets, these great chariot-warriors [fought] with maces, and others with clubs and iron bars.

sanskrit: समभ्यधावन्त भृशं देवा दण्डैरिवोद्यतैः ।नदन्तश्चाह्वयन्तश्च प्रवल्गन्तश्च मारिष ॥२६॥
transliteration: samabhyadhāvanta bhṛśaṁ devā daṇḍairivodyataiḥ ,nadantaścāhvayantaśca pravalgantaśca māriṣa.
english: O revered one, they rushed forth fiercely, roaring, challenging, and exulting, like gods with upraised staffs.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 16 Verse 26. O revered one, they rushed forth fiercely, roaring, challenging, and exulting, like gods with upraised staffs.

sanskrit: ततो निजघ्नुरन्योन्यं पेतुश्चाहवताडिताः ।वमन्तो रुधिरं गात्रैर्विमस्तिष्केक्षणा युधि ॥२७॥
transliteration: tato nijaghnuranyonyaṁ petuścāhavatāḍitāḥ ,vamanto rudhiraṁ gātrairvimastiṣkekṣaṇā yudhi.
english: Then, struck by the battle, they violently struck each other, and fell. In the fight, they were vomiting blood from their bodies, their brains and eyes gushing out.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 16 Verse 27. Then, struck by the battle, they violently struck each other, and fell. In the fight, they were vomiting blood from their bodies, their brains and eyes gushing out.

sanskrit: दन्तपूर्णैः सरुधिरैर्वक्त्रैर्दाडिमसंनिभैः ।जीवन्त इव चाप्येते तस्थुः शस्त्रोपबृंहिताः ॥२८॥
transliteration: dantapūrṇaiḥ sarudhirairvaktrairdāḍimasaṁnibhaiḥ ,jīvanta iva cāpyete tasthuḥ śastropabṛṁhitāḥ.
english: With bloody mouths full of teeth, resembling pomegranates, they remained as if alive, supported by their weapons.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 16 Verse 28. With bloody mouths full of teeth, resembling pomegranates, they remained as if alive, supported by their weapons.

sanskrit: परस्परं चाप्यपरे पट्टिशैरसिभिस्तथा ।शक्तिभिर्भिण्डिपालैश्च नखरप्रासतोमरैः ॥२९॥
transliteration: parasparaṁ cāpyapare paṭṭiśairasibhistathā ,śaktibhirbhiṇḍipālaiśca nakharaprāsatomaraiḥ.
english: And still others attacked each other with paṭṭiśa spears, swords, śakti darts, bhiṇḍipāla javelins, and also with claws, prāsa spears, and tomara lances.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 16 Verse 29. And still others attacked each other with paṭṭiśa spears, swords, śakti darts, bhiṇḍipāla javelins, and also with claws, prāsa spears, and tomara lances.

sanskrit: ततक्षुश्चिच्छिदुश्चान्ये बिभिदुश्चिक्षिपुस्तथा ।संचकर्तुश्च जघ्नुश्च क्रुद्धा निर्बिभिदुश्च ह ॥३०॥
transliteration: tatakṣuścicchiduścānye bibhiduścikṣipustathā ,saṁcakartuśca jaghnuśca kruddhā nirbibhiduśca ha.
english: And other enraged warriors hewed, cut, pierced, and threw. They also chopped up, struck down, and indeed shattered others completely.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 16 Verse 30. And other enraged warriors hewed, cut, pierced, and threw. They also chopped up, struck down, and indeed shattered others completely.

sanskrit: पेतुरन्योन्यनिहता व्यसवो रुधिरोक्षिताः ।क्षरन्तः स्वरसं रक्तं प्रकृताश्चन्दना इव ॥३१॥
transliteration: peturanyonyanihatā vyasavo rudhirokṣitāḥ ,kṣarantaḥ svarasaṁ raktaṁ prakṛtāścandanā iva.
english: Lifeless warriors, struck down by each other and drenched in blood, fell, their blood flowing like the sap (svarasam) from cut sandalwood trees.
