search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 16 Verse 31. Lifeless warriors, struck down by each other and drenched in blood, fell, their blood flowing like the sap (svarasam) from cut sandalwood trees.

sanskrit: रथै रथा विनिहता हस्तिनश्चापि हस्तिभिः ।नरा नरवरैः पेतुरश्वाश्चाश्वैः सहस्रशः ॥३२॥
transliteration: rathai rathā vinihatā hastinaścāpi hastibhiḥ ,narā naravaraiḥ peturaśvāścāśvaiḥ sahasraśaḥ.
english: Chariots were struck down by chariots, and elephants by elephants. Men, by the best of men, and horses, by horses, fell by the thousands.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 16 Verse 32. Chariots were struck down by chariots, and elephants by elephants. Men, by the best of men, and horses, by horses, fell by the thousands.

sanskrit: ध्वजाः शिरांसि च्छत्राणि द्विपहस्ता नृणां भुजाः ।क्षुरैर्भल्लार्धचन्द्रैश्च छिन्नाः शस्त्राणि तत्यजुः ॥३३॥
transliteration: dhvajāḥ śirāṁsi cchatrāṇi dvipahastā nṛṇāṁ bhujāḥ ,kṣurairbhallārdhacandraiśca chinnāḥ śastrāṇi tatyajuḥ.
english: Banners, heads, parasols, elephant trunks, and the arms of men, severed by razor-edged arrows, lances, and half-moon arrows, fell, abandoning their weapons.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 16 Verse 33. Banners, heads, parasols, elephant trunks, and the arms of men, severed by razor-edged arrows, lances, and half-moon arrows, fell, abandoning their weapons.

sanskrit: नरांश्च नागांश्च रथान्हयान्ममृदुराहवे ।अश्वारोहैर्हताः शूराश्छिन्नहस्ताश्च दन्तिनः ॥३४॥
transliteration: narāṁśca nāgāṁśca rathānhayānmamṛdurāhave ,aśvārohairhatāḥ śūrāśchinnahastāśca dantinaḥ.
english: In battle, they crushed men, elephants, chariots, and horses. Heroes were killed by cavalry, and elephants had their trunks severed.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 16 Verse 34. In battle, they crushed men, elephants, chariots, and horses. Heroes were killed by cavalry, and elephants had their trunks severed.

sanskrit: सपताका ध्वजाः पेतुर्विशीर्णा इव पर्वताः ।पत्तिभिश्च समाप्लुत्य द्विरदाः स्यन्दनास्तथा ॥३५॥
transliteration: sapatākā dhvajāḥ peturviśīrṇā iva parvatāḥ ,pattibhiśca samāplutya dviradāḥ syandanāstathā.
english: Banners with their flags fell like shattered mountains. Similarly, elephants and chariots, having been overwhelmed by foot-soldiers, also collapsed.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 16 Verse 35. Banners with their flags fell like shattered mountains. Similarly, elephants and chariots, having been overwhelmed by foot-soldiers, also collapsed.

sanskrit: प्रहता हन्यमानाश्च पतिताश्चैव सर्वशः ।अश्वारोहाः समासाद्य त्वरिताः पत्तिभिर्हताः ।सादिभिः पत्तिसंघाश्च निहता युधि शेरते ॥३६॥
transliteration: prahatā hanyamānāśca patitāścaiva sarvaśaḥ ,aśvārohāḥ samāsādya tvaritāḥ pattibhirhatāḥ ,sādibhiḥ pattisaṁghāśca nihatā yudhi śerate.
english: Everywhere, some were struck down, others were being killed, and still others had already fallen. Swift horsemen, having been attacked, were slain by the infantry. And groups of foot-soldiers, struck down by the cavalry, lay dead on the battlefield.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 16 Verse 36. Everywhere, some were struck down, others were being killed, and still others had already fallen. Swift horsemen, having been attacked, were slain by the infantry. And groups of foot-soldiers, struck down by the cavalry, lay dead on the battlefield.

sanskrit: मृदितानीव पद्मानि प्रम्लाना इव च स्रजः ।हतानां वदनान्यासन्गात्राणि च महामते ॥३७॥
transliteration: mṛditānīva padmāni pramlānā iva ca srajaḥ ,hatānāṁ vadanānyāsangātrāṇi ca mahāmate.
english: O great-minded one, the faces and limbs of the slain were like trampled lotuses and withered garlands.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 16 Verse 37. O great-minded one, the faces and limbs of the slain were like trampled lotuses and withered garlands.

sanskrit: रूपाण्यत्यर्थकाम्यानि द्विरदाश्वनृणां नृप ।समुन्नानीव वस्त्राणि प्रापुर्दुर्दर्शतां परम् ॥३८॥
transliteration: rūpāṇyatyarthakāmyāni dviradāśvanṛṇāṁ nṛpa ,samunnānīva vastrāṇi prāpurdurdarśatāṁ param.
english: O King, the once exceedingly desirable forms of elephants, horses, and men attained an extreme unattractiveness, becoming like crumpled clothes.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 16 Verse 38. O King, the once exceedingly desirable forms of elephants, horses, and men attained an extreme unattractiveness, becoming like crumpled clothes.

sanskrit: संजय उवाच ।हस्तिभिस्तु महामात्रास्तव पुत्रेण चोदिताः ।धृष्टद्युम्नं जिघांसन्तः क्रुद्धाः पार्षतमभ्ययुः ॥१॥
transliteration: saṁjaya uvāca ,hastibhistu mahāmātrāstava putreṇa coditāḥ ,dhṛṣṭadyumnaṁ jighāṁsantaḥ kruddhāḥ pārṣatamabhyayuḥ.
english: Sanjaya said: But the great commanders, urged by your son, advanced with their elephants towards Dhṛṣṭadyumna, the Pārṣata, enraged and desiring to kill him.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 17 Verse 1. Sanjaya said: But the great commanders, urged by your son, advanced with their elephants towards Dhṛṣṭadyumna, the Pārṣata, enraged and desiring to kill him.

sanskrit: प्राच्याश्च दाक्षिणात्याश्च प्रवीरा गजयोधिनः ।अङ्गा वङ्गाश्च पुण्ड्राश्च मागधास्ताम्रलिप्तकाः ॥२॥
transliteration: prācyāśca dākṣiṇātyāśca pravīrā gajayodhinaḥ ,aṅgā vaṅgāśca puṇḍrāśca māgadhāstāmraliptakāḥ.
english: And from the eastern and southern regions came heroic elephant-warriors: the people of Aṅga, Vaṅga, Puṇḍra, Magadha, and Tāmralipta.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 17 Verse 2. And from the eastern and southern regions came heroic elephant-warriors: the people of Aṅga, Vaṅga, Puṇḍra, Magadha, and Tāmralipta.

sanskrit: मेकलाः कोशला मद्रा दशार्णा निषधास्तथा ।गजयुद्धेषु कुशलाः कलिङ्गैः सह भारत ॥३॥
transliteration: mekalāḥ kośalā madrā daśārṇā niṣadhāstathā ,gajayuddheṣu kuśalāḥ kaliṅgaiḥ saha bhārata.
english: O Bhārata, there were also the Mekalās, Kośalas, Madras, Daśārṇas, and Niṣadhas, who were skilled in elephant-battles, along with the Kaliṅgas.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 17 Verse 3. O Bhārata, there were also the Mekalās, Kośalas, Madras, Daśārṇas, and Niṣadhas, who were skilled in elephant-battles, along with the Kaliṅgas.

sanskrit: शरतोमरनाराचैर्वृष्टिमन्त इवाम्बुदाः ।सिषिचुस्ते ततः सर्वे पाञ्चालाचलमाहवे ॥४॥
transliteration: śaratomaranārācairvṛṣṭimanta ivāmbudāḥ ,siṣicuste tataḥ sarve pāñcālācalamāhave.
english: Then, all of them, like rain-laden clouds (ambudāḥ) with showers of arrows, spears, and iron missiles, drenched the Pāñcāla mountain (i.e., the Pāñcāla forces) in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 17 Verse 4. Then, all of them, like rain-laden clouds (ambudāḥ) with showers of arrows, spears, and iron missiles, drenched the Pāñcāla mountain (i.e., the Pāñcāla forces) in battle.

sanskrit: तान्संमिमर्दिषुर्नागान्पार्ष्ण्यङ्गुष्ठाङ्कुशैर्भृशम् ।पोथितान्पार्षतो बाणैर्नाराचैश्चाभ्यवीवृषत् ॥५॥
transliteration: tānsaṁmimardiṣurnāgānpārṣṇyaṅguṣṭhāṅkuśairbhṛśam ,pothitānpārṣato bāṇairnārācaiścābhyavīvṛṣat.
english: Dhṛṣṭadyumna (pārṣata) intensely rained arrows and iron arrows upon those elephants that, eager to crush (their enemies), were being fiercely driven with heels, toes, and goads, thereby crushing them.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 17 Verse 5. Dhṛṣṭadyumna (pārṣata) intensely rained arrows and iron arrows upon those elephants that, eager to crush (their enemies), were being fiercely driven with heels, toes, and goads, thereby crushing them.

sanskrit: एकैकं दशभिः षड्भिरष्टाभिरपि भारत ।द्विरदानभिविव्याध क्षिप्तैर्गिरिनिभाञ्शरैः ।प्रच्छाद्यमानो द्विरदैर्मेघैरिव दिवाकरः ॥६॥
transliteration: ekaikaṁ daśabhiḥ ṣaḍbhiraṣṭābhirapi bhārata ,dviradānabhivivyādha kṣiptairgirinibhāñśaraiḥ ,pracchādyamāno dviradairmeghairiva divākaraḥ.
english: O Bhārata, (he) pierced each mountain-like elephant with ten, six, and even eight swiftly shot arrows. He was being obscured by the elephants, just as the sun is obscured by clouds.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 17 Verse 6. O Bhārata, (he) pierced each mountain-like elephant with ten, six, and even eight swiftly shot arrows. He was being obscured by the elephants, just as the sun is obscured by clouds.

sanskrit: पर्यासुः पाण्डुपाञ्चाला नदन्तो निशितायुधाः ।तान्नागानभिवर्षन्तो ज्यातन्त्रीशरनादितैः ॥७॥
transliteration: paryāsuḥ pāṇḍupāñcālā nadanto niśitāyudhāḥ ,tānnāgānabhivarṣanto jyātantrīśaranāditaiḥ.
english: The Pāṇḍavas and Pāñcālas, holding sharpened weapons and roaring, surrounded those elephants, showering (arrows) upon them amidst the sounds of bowstrings and arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 17 Verse 7. The Pāṇḍavas and Pāñcālas, holding sharpened weapons and roaring, surrounded those elephants, showering (arrows) upon them amidst the sounds of bowstrings and arrows.

sanskrit: नकुलः सहदेवश्च द्रौपदेयाः प्रभद्रकाः ।सात्यकिश्च शिखण्डी च चेकितानश्च वीर्यवान् ॥८॥
transliteration: nakulaḥ sahadevaśca draupadeyāḥ prabhadrakāḥ ,sātyakiśca śikhaṇḍī ca cekitānaśca vīryavān.
english: Nakula, Sahadeva, the sons of Draupadī, the Prabhadrākas, Sātyaki, Śikhaṇḍī, and the valorous Cekitāna.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 17 Verse 8. Nakula, Sahadeva, the sons of Draupadī, the Prabhadrākas, Sātyaki, Śikhaṇḍī, and the valorous Cekitāna.

sanskrit: ते म्लेच्छैः प्रेषिता नागा नरानश्वान्रथानपि ।हस्तैराक्षिप्य ममृदुः पद्भिश्चाप्यतिमन्यवः ॥९॥
transliteration: te mlecchaiḥ preṣitā nāgā narānaśvānrathānapi ,hastairākṣipya mamṛduḥ padbhiścāpyatimanyavaḥ.
english: The extremely furious elephants, sent by the Mlecchas, seized men, horses, and chariots with their trunks and crushed them with their feet.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 17 Verse 9. The extremely furious elephants, sent by the Mlecchas, seized men, horses, and chariots with their trunks and crushed them with their feet.

sanskrit: बिभिदुश्च विषाणाग्रैः समाक्षिप्य च चिक्षिपुः ।विषाणलग्नैश्चाप्यन्ये परिपेतुर्विभीषणाः ॥१०॥
transliteration: bibhiduśca viṣāṇāgraiḥ samākṣipya ca cikṣipuḥ ,viṣāṇalagnaiścāpyanye paripeturvibhīṣaṇāḥ.
english: They [the elephants] pierced [the men, horses, and chariots] with the tips of their tusks, and seizing them, they threw them. And other frightful ones, caught on their tusks, fell down.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 17 Verse 10. They [the elephants] pierced [the men, horses, and chariots] with the tips of their tusks, and seizing them, they threw them. And other frightful ones, caught on their tusks, fell down.

sanskrit: प्रमुखे वर्तमानं तु द्विपं वङ्गस्य सात्यकिः ।नाराचेनोग्रवेगेन भित्त्वा मर्मण्यपातयत् ॥११॥
transliteration: pramukhe vartamānaṁ tu dvipaṁ vaṅgasya sātyakiḥ ,nārācenogravegena bhittvā marmaṇyapātayat.
english: But Satyaki, with an arrow of fierce speed, pierced the Vanga king's elephant, which was positioned at the front, in its vital spot, and brought it down.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 17 Verse 11. But Satyaki, with an arrow of fierce speed, pierced the Vanga king's elephant, which was positioned at the front, in its vital spot, and brought it down.

sanskrit: तस्यावर्जितनागस्य द्विरदादुत्पतिष्यतः ।नाराचेनाभिनद्वक्षः सोऽपतद्भुवि सात्यकेः ॥१२॥
transliteration: tasyāvarjitanāgasya dviradādutpatiṣyataḥ ,nārācenābhinadvakṣaḥ so'patadbhuvi sātyakeḥ.
english: As his elephant was turning away, and he was about to leap off it, Satyaki pierced his chest with an arrow, and he fell to the ground.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 17 Verse 12. As his elephant was turning away, and he was about to leap off it, Satyaki pierced his chest with an arrow, and he fell to the ground.

sanskrit: पुण्ड्रस्यापततो नागं चलन्तमिव पर्वतम् ।सहदेवः प्रयत्नात्तैर्नाराचैर्व्यहनत्त्रिभिः ॥१३॥
transliteration: puṇḍrasyāpatato nāgaṁ calantamiva parvatam ,sahadevaḥ prayatnāttairnārācairvyahanattribhiḥ.
english: Sahadeva, with great effort, struck down Pundra's elephant, which was falling like a moving mountain, using three iron arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 17 Verse 13. Sahadeva, with great effort, struck down Pundra's elephant, which was falling like a moving mountain, using three iron arrows.

sanskrit: विपताकं वियन्तारं विवर्मध्वजजीवितम् ।तं कृत्वा द्विरदं भूयः सहदेवोऽङ्गमभ्यगात् ॥१४॥
transliteration: vipatākaṁ viyantāraṁ vivarmadhvajajīvitam ,taṁ kṛtvā dviradaṁ bhūyaḥ sahadevo'ṅgamabhyagāt.
english: Having rendered that elephant devoid of its banner, its charioteer, its armor, its standard, and its life, Sahadeva then proceeded towards (the warrior) Anga.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 17 Verse 14. Having rendered that elephant devoid of its banner, its charioteer, its armor, its standard, and its life, Sahadeva then proceeded towards (the warrior) Anga.

sanskrit: सहदेवं तु नकुलो वारयित्वाङ्गमार्दयत् ।नाराचैर्यमदण्डाभैस्त्रिभिर्नागं शतेन च ॥१५॥
transliteration: sahadevaṁ tu nakulo vārayitvāṅgamārdayat ,nārācairyamadaṇḍābhaistribhirnāgaṁ śatena ca.
english: But Nakula, having stopped Sahadeva, assailed Anga and the elephant with three, and also a hundred, arrows that resembled the rod of Yama.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 17 Verse 15. But Nakula, having stopped Sahadeva, assailed Anga and the elephant with three, and also a hundred, arrows that resembled the rod of Yama.

sanskrit: दिवाकरकरप्रख्यानङ्गश्चिक्षेप तोमरान् ।नकुलाय शतान्यष्टौ त्रिधैकैकं तु सोऽच्छिनत् ॥१६॥
transliteration: divākarakaraprakhyānaṅgaścikṣepa tomarān ,nakulāya śatānyaṣṭau tridhaikaikaṁ tu so'cchinat.
english: Anga hurled eight hundred javelins, radiant like the sun's rays, at Nakula. But Nakula cut each and every one of them into three pieces.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 17 Verse 16. Anga hurled eight hundred javelins, radiant like the sun's rays, at Nakula. But Nakula cut each and every one of them into three pieces.

sanskrit: तथार्धचन्द्रेण शिरस्तस्य चिच्छेद पाण्डवः ।स पपात हतो म्लेच्छस्तेनैव सह दन्तिना ॥१७॥
transliteration: tathārdhacandreṇa śirastasya ciccheda pāṇḍavaḥ ,sa papāta hato mlecchastenaiva saha dantinā.
english: Thus, the son of Pāṇḍu cut off his head with a crescent-shaped arrow. That mleccha, having been killed, fell down along with that elephant.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 17 Verse 17. Thus, the son of Pāṇḍu cut off his head with a crescent-shaped arrow. That mleccha, having been killed, fell down along with that elephant.

sanskrit: आचार्यपुत्रे निहते हस्तिशिक्षाविशारदे ।अङ्गाः क्रुद्धा महामात्रा नागैर्नकुलमभ्ययुः ॥१८॥
transliteration: ācāryaputre nihate hastiśikṣāviśārade ,aṅgāḥ kruddhā mahāmātrā nāgairnakulamabhyayuḥ.
english: When the preceptor's son, skilled in elephant training, was killed, the enraged chief ministers of the Aṅgas advanced towards Nakula with their elephants.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 17 Verse 18. When the preceptor's son, skilled in elephant training, was killed, the enraged chief ministers of the Aṅgas advanced towards Nakula with their elephants.

sanskrit: चलत्पताकैः प्रमुखैर्हेमकक्ष्यातनुच्छदैः ।मिमर्दिषन्तस्त्वरिताः प्रदीप्तैरिव पर्वतैः ॥१९॥
transliteration: calatpatākaiḥ pramukhairhemakakṣyātanucchadaiḥ ,mimardiṣantastvaritāḥ pradīptairiva parvataiḥ.
english: (They, the elephants) with waving banners, leading the charge, and adorned with golden belts and coverings, swiftly advanced, eager to crush, like blazing mountains.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 17 Verse 19. (They, the elephants) with waving banners, leading the charge, and adorned with golden belts and coverings, swiftly advanced, eager to crush, like blazing mountains.

sanskrit: मेकलोत्कलकालिङ्गा निषादास्ताम्रलिप्तकाः ।शरतोमरवर्षाणि विमुञ्चन्तो जिघांसवः ॥२०॥
transliteration: mekalotkalakāliṅgā niṣādāstāmraliptakāḥ ,śaratomaravarṣāṇi vimuñcanto jighāṁsavaḥ.
english: The Mekalas, Utkalas, Kalingas, Niṣādas, and Tāmraliptakas, intent on killing, discharged showers of arrows and javelins.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 17 Verse 20. The Mekalas, Utkalas, Kalingas, Niṣādas, and Tāmraliptakas, intent on killing, discharged showers of arrows and javelins.

sanskrit: तैश्छाद्यमानं नकुलं दिवाकरमिवाम्बुदैः ।परि पेतुः सुसंरब्धाः पाण्डुपाञ्चालसोमकाः ॥२१॥
transliteration: taiśchādyamānaṁ nakulaṁ divākaramivāmbudaiḥ ,pari petuḥ susaṁrabdhāḥ pāṇḍupāñcālasomakāḥ.
english: The greatly enraged Pāṇḍavas, Pāñcālas, and Somakas surrounded Nakula, who was being covered by those enemies just as the sun is obscured by clouds.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 17 Verse 21. The greatly enraged Pāṇḍavas, Pāñcālas, and Somakas surrounded Nakula, who was being covered by those enemies just as the sun is obscured by clouds.

sanskrit: ततस्तदभवद्युद्धं रथिनां हस्तिभिः सह ।सृजतां शरवर्षाणि तोमरांश्च सहस्रशः ॥२२॥
transliteration: tatastadabhavadyuddhaṁ rathināṁ hastibhiḥ saha ,sṛjatāṁ śaravarṣāṇi tomarāṁśca sahasraśaḥ.
english: Then that battle ensued, involving charioteers fighting alongside elephants, as they released thousands of showers of arrows and spears.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 17 Verse 22. Then that battle ensued, involving charioteers fighting alongside elephants, as they released thousands of showers of arrows and spears.

sanskrit: नागानां प्रस्फुटुः कुम्भा मर्माणि विविधानि च ।दन्ताश्चैवातिविद्धानां नाराचैर्भूषणानि च ॥२३॥
transliteration: nāgānāṁ prasphuṭuḥ kumbhā marmāṇi vividhāni ca ,dantāścaivātividdhānāṁ nārācairbhūṣaṇāni ca.
english: The frontal protuberances and various vital spots of the elephants burst open. Also, the tusks and ornaments of those elephants that were heavily pierced by iron arrows were shattered.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 17 Verse 23. The frontal protuberances and various vital spots of the elephants burst open. Also, the tusks and ornaments of those elephants that were heavily pierced by iron arrows were shattered.

sanskrit: तेषामष्टौ महानागांश्चतुःषष्ट्या सुतेजनैः ।सहदेवो जघानाशु ते पेतुः सह सादिभिः ॥२४॥
transliteration: teṣāmaṣṭau mahānāgāṁścatuḥṣaṣṭyā sutejanaiḥ ,sahadevo jaghānāśu te petuḥ saha sādibhiḥ.
english: Sahadeva swiftly killed eight of those great elephants using sixty-four finely sharpened arrows. They (the elephants) fell down along with their riders.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 17 Verse 24. Sahadeva swiftly killed eight of those great elephants using sixty-four finely sharpened arrows. They (the elephants) fell down along with their riders.

sanskrit: अञ्जोगतिभिरायम्य प्रयत्नाद्धनुरुत्तमम् ।नाराचैरहनन्नागान्नकुलः कुरुनन्दन ॥२५॥
transliteration: añjogatibhirāyamya prayatnāddhanuruttamam ,nārācairahanannāgānnakulaḥ kurunandana.
english: O delight of the Kurus, Nakula, having carefully drawn his excellent bow with swift movements, struck the elephants with his arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 17 Verse 25. O delight of the Kurus, Nakula, having carefully drawn his excellent bow with swift movements, struck the elephants with his arrows.

sanskrit: ततः शैनेयपाञ्चाल्यौ द्रौपदेयाः प्रभद्रकाः ।शिखण्डी च महानागान्सिषिचुः शरवृष्टिभिः ॥२६॥
transliteration: tataḥ śaineyapāñcālyau draupadeyāḥ prabhadrakāḥ ,śikhaṇḍī ca mahānāgānsiṣicuḥ śaravṛṣṭibhiḥ.
english: Then, Śaineya and Pāñcālya, along with Draupadī's sons, the Prabhadrkas, and Śikhaṇḍī, showered the great elephants with volleys of arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 17 Verse 26. Then, Śaineya and Pāñcālya, along with Draupadī's sons, the Prabhadrkas, and Śikhaṇḍī, showered the great elephants with volleys of arrows.

sanskrit: ते पाण्डुयोधाम्बुधरैः शत्रुद्विरदपर्वताः ।बाणवर्षैर्हताः पेतुर्वज्रवर्षैरिवाचलाः ॥२७॥
transliteration: te pāṇḍuyodhāmbudharaiḥ śatrudviradaparvatāḥ ,bāṇavarṣairhatāḥ peturvajravarṣairivācalāḥ.
english: Those enemy elephants, massive as mountains, were struck down by the torrents of arrows from the Pāṇḍava warriors, who resembled rain clouds. They fell just as mountains are shattered by showers of thunderbolts.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 17 Verse 27. Those enemy elephants, massive as mountains, were struck down by the torrents of arrows from the Pāṇḍava warriors, who resembled rain clouds. They fell just as mountains are shattered by showers of thunderbolts.

sanskrit: एवं हत्वा तव गजांस्ते पाण्डुनरकुञ्जराः ।द्रुतं सेनामवैक्षन्त भिन्नकूलामिवापगाम् ॥२८॥
transliteration: evaṁ hatvā tava gajāṁste pāṇḍunarakuñjarāḥ ,drutaṁ senāmavaikṣanta bhinnakūlāmivāpagām.
english: Having thus killed your elephants, those Pāṇḍava warrior-chiefs quickly observed the (enemy) army, which was like a river with broken banks.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 17 Verse 28. Having thus killed your elephants, those Pāṇḍava warrior-chiefs quickly observed the (enemy) army, which was like a river with broken banks.

sanskrit: ते तां सेनामवालोक्य पाण्डुपुत्रस्य सैनिकाः ।विक्षोभयित्वा च पुनः कर्णमेवाभिदुद्रुवुः ॥२९॥
transliteration: te tāṁ senāmavālokya pāṇḍuputrasya sainikāḥ ,vikṣobhayitvā ca punaḥ karṇamevābhidudruvuḥ.
english: The (Kaurava) soldiers, having seen that army of the son of Pāṇḍu, after agitating it, again rushed specifically towards Karṇa.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 17 Verse 29. The (Kaurava) soldiers, having seen that army of the son of Pāṇḍu, after agitating it, again rushed specifically towards Karṇa.

sanskrit: सहदेवं ततः क्रुद्धं दहन्तं तव वाहिनीम् ।दुःशासनो महाराज भ्राता भ्रातरमभ्ययात् ॥३०॥
transliteration: sahadevaṁ tataḥ kruddhaṁ dahantaṁ tava vāhinīm ,duḥśāsano mahārāja bhrātā bhrātaramabhyayāt.
english: Then, O great king, Duḥśāsana, (your) brother, approached (his) brother Sahadeva, who was enraged and incinerating your army.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 17 Verse 30. Then, O great king, Duḥśāsana, (your) brother, approached (his) brother Sahadeva, who was enraged and incinerating your army.

sanskrit: तौ समेतौ महायुद्धे दृष्ट्वा तत्र नराधिपाः ।सिंहनादरवांश्चक्रुर्वासांस्यादुधुवुश्च ह ॥३१॥
transliteration: tau sametau mahāyuddhe dṛṣṭvā tatra narādhipāḥ ,siṁhanādaravāṁścakrurvāsāṁsyādudhuvuśca ha.
english: Having witnessed those two engaged in the great battle there, the kings (nara-adhipāḥ) let out lion-like roars and indeed waved their garments.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 17 Verse 31. Having witnessed those two engaged in the great battle there, the kings (nara-adhipāḥ) let out lion-like roars and indeed waved their garments.

sanskrit: ततो भारत क्रुद्धेन तव पुत्रेण धन्विना ।पाण्डुपुत्रस्त्रिभिर्बाणैर्वक्षस्यभिहतो बली ॥३२॥
transliteration: tato bhārata kruddhena tava putreṇa dhanvinā ,pāṇḍuputrastribhirbāṇairvakṣasyabhihato balī.
english: Then, O Bhārata (Dhṛtarāṣṭra), the mighty son of Pāṇḍu was struck on the chest with three arrows by your enraged son, the archer.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 17 Verse 32. Then, O Bhārata (Dhṛtarāṣṭra), the mighty son of Pāṇḍu was struck on the chest with three arrows by your enraged son, the archer.

sanskrit: सहदेवस्ततो राजन्नाराचेन तवात्मजम् ।विद्ध्वा विव्याध सप्तत्या सारथिं च त्रिभिस्त्रिभिः ॥३३॥
transliteration: sahadevastato rājannārācena tavātmajam ,viddhvā vivyādha saptatyā sārathiṁ ca tribhistribhiḥ.
english: O King, Sahadeva then, having pierced your son with an arrow, struck him again with seventy arrows, and also struck his charioteer with three arrows each.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 17 Verse 33. O King, Sahadeva then, having pierced your son with an arrow, struck him again with seventy arrows, and also struck his charioteer with three arrows each.

sanskrit: दुःशासनस्तदा राजंश्छित्त्वा चापं महाहवे ।सहदेवं त्रिसप्तत्या बाह्वोरुरसि चार्दयत् ॥३४॥
transliteration: duḥśāsanastadā rājaṁśchittvā cāpaṁ mahāhave ,sahadevaṁ trisaptatyā bāhvorurasi cārdayat.
english: O King, then Duḥśāsana, having cut Sahadeva's bow in the great battle, wounded him on both arms and on the chest with seventy-three arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 17 Verse 34. O King, then Duḥśāsana, having cut Sahadeva's bow in the great battle, wounded him on both arms and on the chest with seventy-three arrows.

sanskrit: सहदेवस्ततः क्रुद्धः खड्गं गृह्य महाहवे ।व्याविध्यत युधां श्रेष्ठः श्रीमांस्तव सुतं प्रति ॥३५॥
transliteration: sahadevastataḥ kruddhaḥ khaḍgaṁ gṛhya mahāhave ,vyāvidhyata yudhāṁ śreṣṭhaḥ śrīmāṁstava sutaṁ prati.
english: Then Sahadeva, glorious and foremost among warriors, became enraged. Taking up his sword in the great battle, he brandished it against your son.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 17 Verse 35. Then Sahadeva, glorious and foremost among warriors, became enraged. Taking up his sword in the great battle, he brandished it against your son.

sanskrit: समार्गणगणं चापं छित्त्वा तस्य महानसिः ।निपपात ततो भूमौ च्युतः सर्प इवाम्बरात् ॥३६॥
transliteration: samārgaṇagaṇaṁ cāpaṁ chittvā tasya mahānasiḥ ,nipapāta tato bhūmau cyutaḥ sarpa ivāmbarāt.
english: Having cut his bow along with its multitude of arrows, his great sword then fell to the ground, just like a snake fallen from the sky.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 17 Verse 36. Having cut his bow along with its multitude of arrows, his great sword then fell to the ground, just like a snake fallen from the sky.

sanskrit: अथान्यद्धनुरादाय सहदेवः प्रतापवान् ।दुःशासनाय चिक्षेप बाणमन्तकरं ततः ॥३७॥
transliteration: athānyaddhanurādāya sahadevaḥ pratāpavān ,duḥśāsanāya cikṣepa bāṇamantakaraṁ tataḥ.
english: Then, the mighty Sahadeva, having taken another bow, shot a deadly arrow at Duḥśāsana.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 17 Verse 37. Then, the mighty Sahadeva, having taken another bow, shot a deadly arrow at Duḥśāsana.

sanskrit: तमापतन्तं विशिखं यमदण्डोपमत्विषम् ।खड्गेन शितधारेण द्विधा चिच्छेद कौरवः ॥३८॥
transliteration: tamāpatantaṁ viśikhaṁ yamadaṇḍopamatviṣam ,khaḍgena śitadhāreṇa dvidhā ciccheda kauravaḥ.
english: The Kuru warrior (Duḥśāsana) cut that approaching arrow, which shone like the rod of Yama, into two with a sharp-edged sword.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 17 Verse 38. The Kuru warrior (Duḥśāsana) cut that approaching arrow, which shone like the rod of Yama, into two with a sharp-edged sword.

sanskrit: तमापतन्तं सहसा निस्त्रिंशं निशितैः शरैः ।पातयामास समरे सहदेवो हसन्निव ॥३९॥
transliteration: tamāpatantaṁ sahasā nistriṁśaṁ niśitaiḥ śaraiḥ ,pātayāmāsa samare sahadevo hasanniva.
english: Sahadeva, as if laughing, forcefully struck down that approaching sword in battle with his sharp arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 17 Verse 39. Sahadeva, as if laughing, forcefully struck down that approaching sword in battle with his sharp arrows.

sanskrit: ततो बाणांश्चतुःषष्टिं तव पुत्रो महारणे ।सहदेवरथे तूर्णं पातयामास भारत ॥४०॥
transliteration: tato bāṇāṁścatuḥṣaṣṭiṁ tava putro mahāraṇe ,sahadevarathe tūrṇaṁ pātayāmāsa bhārata.
english: Then, O Bhārata, your son (Duḥśāsana) quickly rained down sixty-four arrows upon Sahadeva's chariot in the great battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 17 Verse 40. Then, O Bhārata, your son (Duḥśāsana) quickly rained down sixty-four arrows upon Sahadeva's chariot in the great battle.

sanskrit: ताञ्शरान्समरे राजन्वेगेनापततो बहून् ।एकैकं पञ्चभिर्बाणैः सहदेवो न्यकृन्तत ॥४१॥
transliteration: tāñśarānsamare rājanvegenāpatato bahūn ,ekaikaṁ pañcabhirbāṇaiḥ sahadevo nyakṛntata.
english: O King, Sahadeva cut down those many arrows, swiftly falling in battle, each one with five of his own arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 17 Verse 41. O King, Sahadeva cut down those many arrows, swiftly falling in battle, each one with five of his own arrows.

sanskrit: स निवार्य महाबाणांस्तव पुत्रेण प्रेषितान् ।अथास्मै सुबहून्बाणान्माद्रीपुत्रः समाचिनोत् ॥४२॥
transliteration: sa nivārya mahābāṇāṁstava putreṇa preṣitān ,athāsmai subahūnbāṇānmādrīputraḥ samācinot.
english: Having warded off those great arrows dispatched by your son, the son of Madri (Sahadeva) then showered him with very many arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 17 Verse 42. Having warded off those great arrows dispatched by your son, the son of Madri (Sahadeva) then showered him with very many arrows.

sanskrit: ततः क्रुद्धो महाराज सहदेवः प्रतापवान् ।समाधत्त शरं घोरं मृत्युकालान्तकोपमम् ।विकृष्य बलवच्चापं तव पुत्राय सोऽसृजत् ॥४३॥
transliteration: tataḥ kruddho mahārāja sahadevaḥ pratāpavān ,samādhatta śaraṁ ghoraṁ mṛtyukālāntakopamam ,vikṛṣya balavaccāpaṁ tava putrāya so'sṛjat.
english: Then, O great king, the enraged and mighty Sahadeva, having powerfully drawn his bow, aimed a terrible arrow, which was like the god of death (Kāla) at the end of time, and shot it at your son.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 17 Verse 43. Then, O great king, the enraged and mighty Sahadeva, having powerfully drawn his bow, aimed a terrible arrow, which was like the god of death (Kāla) at the end of time, and shot it at your son.

sanskrit: स तं निर्भिद्य वेगेन भित्त्वा च कवचं महत् ।प्राविशद्धरणीं राजन्वल्मीकमिव पन्नगः ।ततः स मुमुहे राजंस्तव पुत्रो महारथः ॥४४॥
transliteration: sa taṁ nirbhidya vegena bhittvā ca kavacaṁ mahat ,prāviśaddharaṇīṁ rājanvalmīkamiva pannagaḥ ,tataḥ sa mumuhe rājaṁstava putro mahārathaḥ.
english: O King, that arrow, having swiftly pierced your son and shattered his great armor, entered the earth like a snake entering an anthill. Then, O King, your son, the great charioteer, became unconscious.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 17 Verse 44. O King, that arrow, having swiftly pierced your son and shattered his great armor, entered the earth like a snake entering an anthill. Then, O King, your son, the great charioteer, became unconscious.

sanskrit: मूढं चैनं समालक्ष्य सारथिस्त्वरितो रथम् ।अपोवाह भृशं त्रस्तो वध्यमानं शितैः शरैः ॥४५॥
transliteration: mūḍhaṁ cainaṁ samālakṣya sārathistvarito ratham ,apovāha bhṛśaṁ trasto vadhyamānaṁ śitaiḥ śaraiḥ.
english: And observing him to be stupefied, the charioteer, greatly terrified, swiftly drove the chariot away, as he was being wounded by sharp arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 17 Verse 45. And observing him to be stupefied, the charioteer, greatly terrified, swiftly drove the chariot away, as he was being wounded by sharp arrows.

sanskrit: पराजित्य रणे तं तु पाण्डवः पाण्डुपूर्वज ।दुर्योधनबलं हृष्टः प्रामथद्वै समन्ततः ॥४६॥
transliteration: parājitya raṇe taṁ tu pāṇḍavaḥ pāṇḍupūrvaja ,duryodhanabalaṁ hṛṣṭaḥ prāmathadvai samantataḥ.
english: O descendant of Pāṇḍu's ancestors, having indeed conquered him in battle, the Pāṇḍava, elated, crushed Duryodhana's army on all sides.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 17 Verse 46. O descendant of Pāṇḍu's ancestors, having indeed conquered him in battle, the Pāṇḍava, elated, crushed Duryodhana's army on all sides.

sanskrit: पिपीलिकापुटं राजन्यथामृद्नान्नरो रुषा ।तथा सा कौरवी सेना मृदिता तेन भारत ॥४७॥
transliteration: pipīlikāpuṭaṁ rājanyathāmṛdnānnaro ruṣā ,tathā sā kauravī senā mṛditā tena bhārata.
english: O King (rājan), just as a man angrily crushes an anthill, so too, O Bhārata, was that Kaurava army crushed by him.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 17 Verse 47. O King (rājan), just as a man angrily crushes an anthill, so too, O Bhārata, was that Kaurava army crushed by him.

sanskrit: नकुलं रभसं युद्धे दारयन्तं वरूथिनीम् ।कर्णो वैकर्तनो राजन्वारयामास वै तदा ॥४८॥
transliteration: nakulaṁ rabhasaṁ yuddhe dārayantaṁ varūthinīm ,karṇo vaikartano rājanvārayāmāsa vai tadā.
english: O King (rājan), then Karṇa, the son of Vīkartana (Vaikartana), indeed restrained Nakula, who was impetuous in battle and tearing apart the enemy host.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 17 Verse 48. O King (rājan), then Karṇa, the son of Vīkartana (Vaikartana), indeed restrained Nakula, who was impetuous in battle and tearing apart the enemy host.

sanskrit: नकुलश्च तदा कर्णं प्रहसन्निदमब्रवीत् ।चिरस्य बत दृष्टोऽहं दैवतैः सौम्यचक्षुषा ॥४९॥
transliteration: nakulaśca tadā karṇaṁ prahasannidamabravīt ,cirasya bata dṛṣṭo'haṁ daivataiḥ saumyacakṣuṣā.
english: Nakula then, laughing, said this to Karṇa: "Alas, after a long time, I am seen by the deities with a benevolent gaze."
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 17 Verse 49. Nakula then, laughing, said this to Karṇa: "Alas, after a long time, I am seen by the deities with a benevolent gaze."

sanskrit: यस्य मे त्वं रणे पाप चक्षुर्विषयमागतः ।त्वं हि मूलमनर्थानां वैरस्य कलहस्य च ॥५०॥
transliteration: yasya me tvaṁ raṇe pāpa cakṣurviṣayamāgataḥ ,tvaṁ hi mūlamanarthānāṁ vairasya kalahasya ca.
english: O wicked one, you have come into my range of sight in battle. Indeed, you are the very root of misfortunes, of enmity, and of conflict.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 17 Verse 50. O wicked one, you have come into my range of sight in battle. Indeed, you are the very root of misfortunes, of enmity, and of conflict.

sanskrit: त्वद्दोषात्कुरवः क्षीणाः समासाद्य परस्परम् ।त्वामद्य समरे हत्वा कृतकृत्योऽस्मि विज्वरः ॥५१॥
transliteration: tvaddoṣātkuravaḥ kṣīṇāḥ samāsādya parasparam ,tvāmadya samare hatvā kṛtakṛtyo'smi vijvaraḥ.
english: Due to your fault, the Kurus have been destroyed, having met each other in conflict. Having slain you today in battle, I am fulfilled (kṛtakṛtya) and free from distress (vijvaraḥ).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 17 Verse 51. Due to your fault, the Kurus have been destroyed, having met each other in conflict. Having slain you today in battle, I am fulfilled (kṛtakṛtya) and free from distress (vijvaraḥ).

sanskrit: एवमुक्तः प्रत्युवाच नकुलं सूतनन्दनः ।सदृशं राजपुत्रस्य धन्विनश्च विशेषतः ॥५२॥
transliteration: evamuktaḥ pratyuvāca nakulaṁ sūtanandanaḥ ,sadṛśaṁ rājaputrasya dhanvinaśca viśeṣataḥ.
english: Thus addressed, the son of Sūta (Karṇa) replied to Nakula, saying, "This is befitting for a prince, and especially for an archer."
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 17 Verse 52. Thus addressed, the son of Sūta (Karṇa) replied to Nakula, saying, "This is befitting for a prince, and especially for an archer."

sanskrit: प्रहरस्व रणे बाल पश्यामस्तव पौरुषम् ।कर्म कृत्वा रणे शूर ततः कत्थितुमर्हसि ॥५३॥
transliteration: praharasva raṇe bāla paśyāmastava pauruṣam ,karma kṛtvā raṇe śūra tataḥ katthitumarhasi.
english: O boy, strike in battle! Let us see your valor (pauruṣa). O hero, only after performing action (karma) in battle are you worthy to boast.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 17 Verse 53. O boy, strike in battle! Let us see your valor (pauruṣa). O hero, only after performing action (karma) in battle are you worthy to boast.

sanskrit: अनुक्त्वा समरे तात शूरा युध्यन्ति शक्तितः ।स युध्यस्व मया शक्त्या विनेष्ये दर्पमद्य ते ॥५४॥
transliteration: anuktvā samare tāta śūrā yudhyanti śaktitaḥ ,sa yudhyasva mayā śaktyā vineṣye darpamadya te.
english: O dear one, warriors (śūra) fight in battle without speaking, according to their strength (śakti). Therefore, fight with me with your might; today I will remove your pride.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 17 Verse 54. O dear one, warriors (śūra) fight in battle without speaking, according to their strength (śakti). Therefore, fight with me with your might; today I will remove your pride.

sanskrit: इत्युक्त्वा प्राहरत्तूर्णं पाण्डुपुत्राय सूतजः ।विव्याध चैनं समरे त्रिसप्तत्या शिलीमुखैः ॥५५॥
transliteration: ityuktvā prāharattūrṇaṁ pāṇḍuputrāya sūtajaḥ ,vivyādha cainaṁ samare trisaptatyā śilīmukhaiḥ.
english: Having spoken thus, the son of the charioteer (Sūtaputra) quickly struck Pāṇḍu's son. And in battle, he pierced him with seventy-three arrows (śilīmukha).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 17 Verse 55. Having spoken thus, the son of the charioteer (Sūtaputra) quickly struck Pāṇḍu's son. And in battle, he pierced him with seventy-three arrows (śilīmukha).

sanskrit: नकुलस्तु ततो विद्धः सूतपुत्रेण भारत ।अशीत्याशीविषप्रख्यैः सूतपुत्रमविध्यत ॥५६॥
transliteration: nakulastu tato viddhaḥ sūtaputreṇa bhārata ,aśītyāśīviṣaprakhyaiḥ sūtaputramavidhyata.
english: But then, O Bhārata, Nakula, who had been wounded by the son of the charioteer (Sūtaputra), pierced the son of the charioteer (Sūtaputra) with eighty arrows resembling venomous snakes.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 17 Verse 56. But then, O Bhārata, Nakula, who had been wounded by the son of the charioteer (Sūtaputra), pierced the son of the charioteer (Sūtaputra) with eighty arrows resembling venomous snakes.

sanskrit: तस्य कर्णो धनुश्छित्त्वा स्वर्णपुङ्खैः शिलाशितैः ।त्रिंशता परमेष्वासः शरैः पाण्डवमार्दयत् ॥५७॥
transliteration: tasya karṇo dhanuśchittvā svarṇapuṅkhaiḥ śilāśitaiḥ ,triṁśatā parameṣvāsaḥ śaraiḥ pāṇḍavamārdayat.
english: Having cut his [opponent's] bow, the great archer Karna tormented the Pāṇḍava (Nakula) with thirty arrows, which were golden-feathered and sharpened on stone.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 17 Verse 57. Having cut his [opponent's] bow, the great archer Karna tormented the Pāṇḍava (Nakula) with thirty arrows, which were golden-feathered and sharpened on stone.

sanskrit: ते तस्य कवचं भित्त्वा पपुः शोणितमाहवे ।आशीविषा यथा नागा भित्त्वा गां सलिलं पपुः ॥५८॥
transliteration: te tasya kavacaṁ bhittvā papuḥ śoṇitamāhave ,āśīviṣā yathā nāgā bhittvā gāṁ salilaṁ papuḥ.
english: Having pierced his armor, those [arrows] drank his blood in battle, just as venomous snakes, having pierced the earth, drink water.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 17 Verse 58. Having pierced his armor, those [arrows] drank his blood in battle, just as venomous snakes, having pierced the earth, drink water.

sanskrit: अथान्यद्धनुरादाय हेमपृष्ठं दुरासदम् ।कर्णं विव्याध विंशत्या सारथिं च त्रिभिः शरैः ॥५९॥
transliteration: athānyaddhanurādāya hemapṛṣṭhaṁ durāsadam ,karṇaṁ vivyādha viṁśatyā sārathiṁ ca tribhiḥ śaraiḥ.
english: Then, having taken up another formidable, gold-backed bow, he (Nakula) struck Karna with twenty arrows and his charioteer with three arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 17 Verse 59. Then, having taken up another formidable, gold-backed bow, he (Nakula) struck Karna with twenty arrows and his charioteer with three arrows.

sanskrit: ततः क्रुद्धो महाराज नकुलः परवीरहा ।क्षुरप्रेण सुतीक्ष्णेन कर्णस्य धनुरच्छिनत् ॥६०॥
transliteration: tataḥ kruddho mahārāja nakulaḥ paravīrahā ,kṣurapreṇa sutīkṣṇena karṇasya dhanuracchinat.
english: Then, O great king, the enraged Nakula, slayer of enemy heroes, cut off Karna's bow with a very sharp razor-tipped arrow.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 17 Verse 60. Then, O great king, the enraged Nakula, slayer of enemy heroes, cut off Karna's bow with a very sharp razor-tipped arrow.

sanskrit: अथैनं छिन्नधन्वानं सायकानां शतैस्त्रिभिः ।आजघ्ने प्रहसन्वीरः सर्वलोकमहारथम् ॥६१॥
transliteration: athainaṁ chinnadhanvānaṁ sāyakānāṁ śataistribhiḥ ,ājaghne prahasanvīraḥ sarvalokamahāratham.
english: Then, the hero, laughing, struck him - the great chariot-warrior (mahāratha) of all the worlds, whose bow had been cut - with three hundred arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 17 Verse 61. Then, the hero, laughing, struck him - the great chariot-warrior (mahāratha) of all the worlds, whose bow had been cut - with three hundred arrows.

sanskrit: कर्णमभ्यर्दितं दृष्ट्वा पाण्डुपुत्रेण मारिष ।विस्मयं परमं जग्मू रथिनः सह दैवतैः ॥६२॥
transliteration: karṇamabhyarditaṁ dṛṣṭvā pāṇḍuputreṇa māriṣa ,vismayaṁ paramaṁ jagmū rathinaḥ saha daivataiḥ.
english: O revered one, when the chariot-warriors, along with the gods, saw Karṇa afflicted by the son of Pāṇḍu, they were filled with supreme astonishment.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 17 Verse 62. O revered one, when the chariot-warriors, along with the gods, saw Karṇa afflicted by the son of Pāṇḍu, they were filled with supreme astonishment.

sanskrit: अथान्यद्धनुरादाय कर्णो वैकर्तनस्तदा ।नकुलं पञ्चभिर्बाणैर्जत्रुदेशे समार्दयत् ॥६३॥
transliteration: athānyaddhanurādāya karṇo vaikartanastadā ,nakulaṁ pañcabhirbāṇairjatrudeśe samārdayat.
english: Then, Karṇa (Vaikartana), taking up another bow, at that moment wounded Nakula with five arrows in the region of his collarbone.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 17 Verse 63. Then, Karṇa (Vaikartana), taking up another bow, at that moment wounded Nakula with five arrows in the region of his collarbone.

sanskrit: उरःस्थैरथ तैर्बाणैर्माद्रीपुत्रो व्यरोचत ।स्वरश्मिभिरिवादित्यो भुवने विसृजन्प्रभाम् ॥६४॥
transliteration: uraḥsthairatha tairbāṇairmādrīputro vyarocata ,svaraśmibhirivādityo bhuvane visṛjanprabhām.
english: Then, the son of Mādrī, adorned with those arrows lodged in his chest, shone like the sun emitting its own rays in the world.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 17 Verse 64. Then, the son of Mādrī, adorned with those arrows lodged in his chest, shone like the sun emitting its own rays in the world.

sanskrit: नकुलस्तु ततः कर्णं विद्ध्वा सप्तभिरायसैः ।अथास्य धनुषः कोटिं पुनश्चिच्छेद मारिष ॥६५॥
transliteration: nakulastu tataḥ karṇaṁ viddhvā saptabhirāyasaiḥ ,athāsya dhanuṣaḥ koṭiṁ punaściccheda māriṣa.
english: Then Nakula, O venerable one, after piercing Karṇa with seven iron arrows, again cut off the tip of his bow.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 17 Verse 65. Then Nakula, O venerable one, after piercing Karṇa with seven iron arrows, again cut off the tip of his bow.

sanskrit: सोऽन्यत्कार्मुकमादाय समरे वेगवत्तरम् ।नकुलस्य ततो बाणैः सर्वतोऽवारयद्दिशः ॥६६॥
transliteration: so'nyatkārmukamādāya samare vegavattaram ,nakulasya tato bāṇaiḥ sarvato'vārayaddiśaḥ.
english: Taking up another, swifter bow in battle, he then warded off Nakula's arrows from all directions.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 17 Verse 66. Taking up another, swifter bow in battle, he then warded off Nakula's arrows from all directions.

sanskrit: संछाद्यमानः सहसा कर्णचापच्युतैः शरैः ।चिच्छेद स शरांस्तूर्णं शरैरेव महारथः ॥६७॥
transliteration: saṁchādyamānaḥ sahasā karṇacāpacyutaiḥ śaraiḥ ,ciccheda sa śarāṁstūrṇaṁ śaraireva mahārathaḥ.
english: That great warrior, suddenly being covered by arrows discharged from Karṇa's bow, swiftly cut down those arrows with his own arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 17 Verse 67. That great warrior, suddenly being covered by arrows discharged from Karṇa's bow, swiftly cut down those arrows with his own arrows.

sanskrit: ततो बाणमयं जालं विततं व्योम्न्यदृश्यत ।खद्योतानां गणैरेव संपतद्भिर्यथा नभः ॥६८॥
transliteration: tato bāṇamayaṁ jālaṁ vitataṁ vyomnyadṛśyata ,khadyotānāṁ gaṇaireva saṁpatadbhiryathā nabhaḥ.
english: Then a widespread net of arrows was seen in the sky, just as the firmament appears with swarms of descending fireflies.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 17 Verse 68. Then a widespread net of arrows was seen in the sky, just as the firmament appears with swarms of descending fireflies.

sanskrit: तैर्विमुक्तैः शरशतैश्छादितं गगनं तदा ।शलभानां यथा व्रातैस्तद्वदासीत्समाकुलम् ॥६९॥
transliteration: tairvimuktaiḥ śaraśataiśchāditaṁ gaganaṁ tadā ,śalabhānāṁ yathā vrātaistadvadāsītsamākulam.
english: At that time, the sky was completely covered by the hundreds of arrows released by them, just as it would be crowded and obscured by swarms of locusts.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 17 Verse 69. At that time, the sky was completely covered by the hundreds of arrows released by them, just as it would be crowded and obscured by swarms of locusts.

sanskrit: ते शरा हेमविकृताः संपतन्तो मुहुर्मुहुः ।श्रेणीकृता अभासन्त हंसाः श्रेणीगता इव ॥७०॥
transliteration: te śarā hemavikṛtāḥ saṁpatanto muhurmuhuḥ ,śreṇīkṛtā abhāsanta haṁsāḥ śreṇīgatā iva.
english: Those golden-decorated arrows, falling repeatedly, shone in formations, appearing like swans arranged in rows.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 17 Verse 70. Those golden-decorated arrows, falling repeatedly, shone in formations, appearing like swans arranged in rows.

sanskrit: बाणजालावृते व्योम्नि छादिते च दिवाकरे ।समसर्पत्ततो भूतं किंचिदेव विशां पते ॥७१॥
transliteration: bāṇajālāvṛte vyomni chādite ca divākare ,samasarpattato bhūtaṁ kiṁcideva viśāṁ pate.
english: O lord of the people, as the sky became enveloped by a net of arrows and the sun was obscured, some kind of being then advanced.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 17 Verse 71. O lord of the people, as the sky became enveloped by a net of arrows and the sun was obscured, some kind of being then advanced.

sanskrit: निरुद्धे तत्र मार्गे तु शरसंघैः समन्ततः ।व्यरोचतां महाभागौ बालसूर्याविवोदितौ ॥७२॥
transliteration: niruddhe tatra mārge tu śarasaṁghaiḥ samantataḥ ,vyarocatāṁ mahābhāgau bālasūryāvivoditau.
english: But when that path was completely obstructed by masses of arrows, the two great and fortunate beings shone brightly, like two newly risen suns.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 17 Verse 72. But when that path was completely obstructed by masses of arrows, the two great and fortunate beings shone brightly, like two newly risen suns.

sanskrit: कर्णचापच्युतैर्बाणैर्वध्यमानास्तु सोमकाः ।अवालीयन्त राजेन्द्र वेदनार्ताः शरार्दिताः ॥७३॥
transliteration: karṇacāpacyutairbāṇairvadhyamānāstu somakāḥ ,avālīyanta rājendra vedanārtāḥ śarārditāḥ.
english: O King (rājendra), the Somakas, being struck by arrows released from Karna's bow and afflicted by arrows, retreated, tormented by pain.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 17 Verse 73. O King (rājendra), the Somakas, being struck by arrows released from Karna's bow and afflicted by arrows, retreated, tormented by pain.

sanskrit: नकुलस्य तथा बाणैर्वध्यमाना चमूस्तव ।व्यशीर्यत दिशो राजन्वातनुन्ना इवाम्बुदाः ॥७४॥
transliteration: nakulasya tathā bāṇairvadhyamānā camūstava ,vyaśīryata diśo rājanvātanunnā ivāmbudāḥ.
english: O King (rājan), similarly, your army, being struck down by Nakula's arrows, scattered in all directions like clouds driven by the wind.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 17 Verse 74. O King (rājan), similarly, your army, being struck down by Nakula's arrows, scattered in all directions like clouds driven by the wind.

sanskrit: ते सेने वध्यमाने तु ताभ्यां दिव्यैर्महाशरैः ।शरपातमपक्रम्य ततः प्रेक्षकवत्स्थिते ॥७५॥
transliteration: te sene vadhyamāne tu tābhyāṁ divyairmahāśaraiḥ ,śarapātamapakramya tataḥ prekṣakavatsthite.
english: But those two armies (sene), being struck down by their divine great arrows, retreated from the range of arrows and then remained as if they were spectators.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 17 Verse 75. But those two armies (sene), being struck down by their divine great arrows, retreated from the range of arrows and then remained as if they were spectators.

sanskrit: प्रोत्सारिते जने तस्मिन्कर्णपाण्डवयोः शरैः ।विव्याधाते महात्मानावन्योन्यं शरवृष्टिभिः ॥७६॥
transliteration: protsārite jane tasminkarṇapāṇḍavayoḥ śaraiḥ ,vivyādhāte mahātmānāvanyonyaṁ śaravṛṣṭibhiḥ.
english: After that populace had been driven away by the arrows of Karna and the Pāṇḍava, the two great souls (mahātman) (Karna and Nakula) then struck each other with showers of arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 17 Verse 76. After that populace had been driven away by the arrows of Karna and the Pāṇḍava, the two great souls (mahātman) (Karna and Nakula) then struck each other with showers of arrows.

sanskrit: निदर्शयन्तौ त्वस्त्राणि दिव्यानि रणमूर्धनि ।छादयन्तौ च सहसा परस्परवधैषिणौ ॥७७॥
transliteration: nidarśayantau tvastrāṇi divyāni raṇamūrdhani ,chādayantau ca sahasā parasparavadhaiṣiṇau.
english: Both, intent on killing each other, displayed divine weapons on the battlefield and swiftly overwhelmed one another.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 17 Verse 77. Both, intent on killing each other, displayed divine weapons on the battlefield and swiftly overwhelmed one another.

sanskrit: नकुलेन शरा मुक्ताः कङ्कबर्हिणवाससः ।ते तु कर्णमवच्छाद्य व्यतिष्ठन्त यथा परे ॥७८॥
transliteration: nakulena śarā muktāḥ kaṅkabarhiṇavāsasaḥ ,te tu karṇamavacchādya vyatiṣṭhanta yathā pare.
english: Arrows, decorated with vulture and peacock feathers, were released by Nakula. These, indeed, completely covered Karna and remained (stuck) there, just like other arrows (do).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 17 Verse 78. Arrows, decorated with vulture and peacock feathers, were released by Nakula. These, indeed, completely covered Karna and remained (stuck) there, just like other arrows (do).

sanskrit: शरवेश्मप्रविष्टौ तौ ददृशाते न कैश्चन ।चन्द्रसूर्यौ यथा राजंश्छाद्यमानौ जलागमे ॥७९॥
transliteration: śaraveśmapraviṣṭau tau dadṛśāte na kaiścana ,candrasūryau yathā rājaṁśchādyamānau jalāgame.
english: O King, those two, completely engulfed by a shower of arrows, were not visible to anyone, just as the moon and the sun are obscured during the arrival of clouds (rainy season).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 17 Verse 79. O King, those two, completely engulfed by a shower of arrows, were not visible to anyone, just as the moon and the sun are obscured during the arrival of clouds (rainy season).

sanskrit: ततः क्रुद्धो रणे कर्णः कृत्वा घोरतरं वपुः ।पाण्डवं छादयामास समन्ताच्छरवृष्टिभिः ॥८०॥
transliteration: tataḥ kruddho raṇe karṇaḥ kṛtvā ghorataraṁ vapuḥ ,pāṇḍavaṁ chādayāmāsa samantāccharavṛṣṭibhiḥ.
english: Then Karna, enraged in battle, assumed a very dreadful form and overwhelmed the Pandava (Nakula) from all sides with showers of arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 17 Verse 80. Then Karna, enraged in battle, assumed a very dreadful form and overwhelmed the Pandava (Nakula) from all sides with showers of arrows.

sanskrit: स च्छाद्यमानः समरे सूतपुत्रेण पाण्डवः ।न चकार व्यथां राजन्भास्करो जलदैर्यथा ॥८१॥
transliteration: sa cchādyamānaḥ samare sūtaputreṇa pāṇḍavaḥ ,na cakāra vyathāṁ rājanbhāskaro jaladairyathā.
english: O king, although that Pāṇḍava (Arjuna) was being covered in battle by Karṇa, the son of the charioteer, he felt no distress, just as the sun is not affected by clouds.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 17 Verse 81. O king, although that Pāṇḍava (Arjuna) was being covered in battle by Karṇa, the son of the charioteer, he felt no distress, just as the sun is not affected by clouds.

sanskrit: ततः प्रहस्याधिरथिः शरजालानि मारिष ।प्रेषयामास समरे शतशोऽथ सहस्रशः ॥८२॥
transliteration: tataḥ prahasyādhirathiḥ śarajālāni māriṣa ,preṣayāmāsa samare śataśo'tha sahasraśaḥ.
english: O revered king, then Karṇa, the great charioteer, laughed and discharged hundreds and thousands of arrow volleys in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 17 Verse 82. O revered king, then Karṇa, the great charioteer, laughed and discharged hundreds and thousands of arrow volleys in battle.

sanskrit: एकच्छायमभूत्सर्वं तस्य बाणैर्महात्मनः ।अभ्रच्छायेव संजज्ञे संपतद्भिः शरोत्तमैः ॥८३॥
transliteration: ekacchāyamabhūtsarvaṁ tasya bāṇairmahātmanaḥ ,abhracchāyeva saṁjajñe saṁpatadbhiḥ śarottamaiḥ.
english: The entire battlefield became a single shade from the arrows of that great-souled (mahātman) warrior (Karṇa). It was as if a cloud-shadow had been formed by his excellent, swiftly falling arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 17 Verse 83. The entire battlefield became a single shade from the arrows of that great-souled (mahātman) warrior (Karṇa). It was as if a cloud-shadow had been formed by his excellent, swiftly falling arrows.

sanskrit: ततः कर्णो महाराज धनुश्छित्त्वा महात्मनः ।सारथिं पातयामास रथनीडाद्धसन्निव ॥८४॥
transliteration: tataḥ karṇo mahārāja dhanuśchittvā mahātmanaḥ ,sārathiṁ pātayāmāsa rathanīḍāddhasanniva.
english: Then, O great king, Karṇa, after cutting the bow of the great-souled (mahātman) Arjuna, made his charioteer (Kṛṣṇa) fall from the chariot's seat, as if laughing.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 17 Verse 84. Then, O great king, Karṇa, after cutting the bow of the great-souled (mahātman) Arjuna, made his charioteer (Kṛṣṇa) fall from the chariot's seat, as if laughing.

sanskrit: तथाश्वांश्चतुरश्चास्य चतुर्भिर्निशितैः शरैः ।यमस्य सदनं तूर्णं प्रेषयामास भारत ॥८५॥
transliteration: tathāśvāṁścaturaścāsya caturbhirniśitaiḥ śaraiḥ ,yamasya sadanaṁ tūrṇaṁ preṣayāmāsa bhārata.
english: And similarly, O Bhārata, he swiftly dispatched his four horses to the abode of Yama with four sharp arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 17 Verse 85. And similarly, O Bhārata, he swiftly dispatched his four horses to the abode of Yama with four sharp arrows.

sanskrit: अथास्य तं रथं तूर्णं तिलशो व्यधमच्छरैः ।पताकां चक्ररक्षौ च ध्वजं खड्गं च मारिष ।शतचन्द्रं ततश्चर्म सर्वोपकरणानि च ॥८६॥
transliteration: athāsya taṁ rathaṁ tūrṇaṁ tilaśo vyadhamaccharaiḥ ,patākāṁ cakrarakṣau ca dhvajaṁ khaḍgaṁ ca māriṣa ,śatacandraṁ tataścarma sarvopakaraṇāni ca.
english: Then, O venerable one, he swiftly shattered his chariot into tiny pieces with arrows, and also its banner, the two wheel-guards, the standard, the sword, and then the 'hundred-moon' shield, along with all its equipment.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 17 Verse 86. Then, O venerable one, he swiftly shattered his chariot into tiny pieces with arrows, and also its banner, the two wheel-guards, the standard, the sword, and then the 'hundred-moon' shield, along with all its equipment.

sanskrit: हताश्वो विरथश्चैव विवर्मा च विशां पते ।अवतीर्य रथात्तूर्णं परिघं गृह्य विष्ठितः ॥८७॥
transliteration: hatāśvo virathaścaiva vivarmā ca viśāṁ pate ,avatīrya rathāttūrṇaṁ parighaṁ gṛhya viṣṭhitaḥ.
english: O lord of people, with his horses slain, chariotless, and without armor, he swiftly descended from his chariot and stood, grasping a mace.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 17 Verse 87. O lord of people, with his horses slain, chariotless, and without armor, he swiftly descended from his chariot and stood, grasping a mace.

sanskrit: तमुद्यतं महाघोरं परिघं तस्य सूतजः ।व्यहनत्सायकै राजञ्शतशोऽथ सहस्रशः ॥८८॥
transliteration: tamudyataṁ mahāghoraṁ parighaṁ tasya sūtajaḥ ,vyahanatsāyakai rājañśataśo'tha sahasraśaḥ.
english: Then, O king, the son of Sūta (Karṇa) struck that greatly dreadful mace, which had been raised by the opponent, with hundreds and thousands of arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 17 Verse 88. Then, O king, the son of Sūta (Karṇa) struck that greatly dreadful mace, which had been raised by the opponent, with hundreds and thousands of arrows.

sanskrit: व्यायुधं चैनमालक्ष्य शरैः संनतपर्वभिः ।आर्दयद्बहुशः कर्णो न चैनं समपीडयत् ॥८९॥
transliteration: vyāyudhaṁ cainamālakṣya śaraiḥ saṁnataparvabhiḥ ,ārdayadbahuśaḥ karṇo na cainaṁ samapīḍayat.
english: Seeing him disarmed, Karṇa repeatedly tormented him with swift, sharp-pointed arrows, yet he did not completely overwhelm him.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 17 Verse 89. Seeing him disarmed, Karṇa repeatedly tormented him with swift, sharp-pointed arrows, yet he did not completely overwhelm him.

sanskrit: स वध्यमानः समरे कृतास्त्रेण बलीयसा ।प्राद्रवत्सहसा राजन्नकुलो व्याकुलेन्द्रियः ॥९०॥
transliteration: sa vadhyamānaḥ samare kṛtāstreṇa balīyasā ,prādravatsahasā rājannakulo vyākulendriyaḥ.
english: O King, as Nakula was being struck down in battle by the mighty and weapon-skilled (Karṇa), he suddenly fled, his senses bewildered.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 17 Verse 90. O King, as Nakula was being struck down in battle by the mighty and weapon-skilled (Karṇa), he suddenly fled, his senses bewildered.

sanskrit: तमभिद्रुत्य राधेयः प्रहसन्वै पुनः पुनः ।सज्यमस्य धनुः कण्ठे सोऽवासृजत भारत ॥९१॥
transliteration: tamabhidrutya rādheyaḥ prahasanvai punaḥ punaḥ ,sajyamasya dhanuḥ kaṇṭhe so'vāsṛjata bhārata.
english: O Bhārata, the son of Rādhā (Karṇa), having chased after him and laughing repeatedly, then placed his (Nakula's) strung bow around his neck.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 17 Verse 91. O Bhārata, the son of Rādhā (Karṇa), having chased after him and laughing repeatedly, then placed his (Nakula's) strung bow around his neck.

sanskrit: ततः स शुशुभे राजन्कण्ठासक्तमहाधनुः ।परिवेषमनुप्राप्तो यथा स्याद्व्योम्नि चन्द्रमाः ।यथैव च सितो मेघः शक्रचापेन शोभितः ॥९२॥
transliteration: tataḥ sa śuśubhe rājankaṇṭhāsaktamahādhanuḥ ,pariveṣamanuprāpto yathā syādvyomni candramāḥ ,yathaiva ca sito meghaḥ śakracāpena śobhitaḥ.
english: O King, with that great bow hanging from his neck, he (Nakula) then shone brightly, just like the moon appears in the sky with a halo, and indeed, like a white cloud adorned by a rainbow (Indra's bow).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 17 Verse 92. O King, with that great bow hanging from his neck, he (Nakula) then shone brightly, just like the moon appears in the sky with a halo, and indeed, like a white cloud adorned by a rainbow (Indra's bow).

sanskrit: तमब्रवीत्तदा कर्णो व्यर्थं व्याहृतवानसि ।वदेदानीं पुनर्हृष्टो वध्यं मां त्वं पुनः पुनः ॥९३॥
transliteration: tamabravīttadā karṇo vyarthaṁ vyāhṛtavānasi ,vadedānīṁ punarhṛṣṭo vadhyaṁ māṁ tvaṁ punaḥ punaḥ.
english: Then Karṇa said to him, "You have spoken uselessly. Now, speak again, delighted, declaring me repeatedly as one who deserves to be killed."
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 17 Verse 93. Then Karṇa said to him, "You have spoken uselessly. Now, speak again, delighted, declaring me repeatedly as one who deserves to be killed."

sanskrit: मा योत्सीर्गुरुभिः सार्धं बलवद्भिश्च पाण्डव ।सदृशैस्तात युध्यस्व व्रीडां मा कुरु पाण्डव ।गृहं वा गच्छ माद्रेय यत्र वा कृष्णफल्गुनौ ॥९४॥
transliteration: mā yotsīrgurubhiḥ sārdhaṁ balavadbhiśca pāṇḍava ,sadṛśaistāta yudhyasva vrīḍāṁ mā kuru pāṇḍava ,gṛhaṁ vā gaccha mādreya yatra vā kṛṣṇaphalgunau.
english: O Pāṇḍava, do not fight with elders or with powerful individuals. Dear son, fight with those who are your equals. O Pāṇḍava, do not feel shame. Alternatively, O son of Mādrī, go home, or go to where Kṛṣṇa and Arjuna are.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 17 Verse 94. O Pāṇḍava, do not fight with elders or with powerful individuals. Dear son, fight with those who are your equals. O Pāṇḍava, do not feel shame. Alternatively, O son of Mādrī, go home, or go to where Kṛṣṇa and Arjuna are.

sanskrit: एवमुक्त्वा महाराज व्यसर्जयत तं ततः ।वधप्राप्तं तु तं राजन्नावधीत्सूतनन्दनः ।स्मृत्वा कुन्त्या वचो राजंस्तत एनं व्यसर्जयत् ॥९५॥
transliteration: evamuktvā mahārāja vyasarjayata taṁ tataḥ ,vadhaprāptaṁ tu taṁ rājannāvadhītsūtanandanaḥ ,smṛtvā kuntyā vaco rājaṁstata enaṁ vyasarjayat.
english: Having spoken thus, O great king, he then dismissed him. However, O king, the son of the charioteer (Karṇa) did not kill him, even though he was condemned to death. Remembering Kuntī's words, O king, he consequently released him.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 17 Verse 95. Having spoken thus, O great king, he then dismissed him. However, O king, the son of the charioteer (Karṇa) did not kill him, even though he was condemned to death. Remembering Kuntī's words, O king, he consequently released him.

sanskrit: विसृष्टः पाण्डवो राजन्सूतपुत्रेण धन्विना ।व्रीडन्निव जगामाथ युधिष्ठिररथं प्रति ॥९६॥
transliteration: visṛṣṭaḥ pāṇḍavo rājansūtaputreṇa dhanvinā ,vrīḍanniva jagāmātha yudhiṣṭhirarathaṁ prati.
english: O king, the Pāṇḍava, released by the son of the charioteer, the archer, then went, as if ashamed, towards Yudhiṣṭhira's chariot.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 17 Verse 96. O king, the Pāṇḍava, released by the son of the charioteer, the archer, then went, as if ashamed, towards Yudhiṣṭhira's chariot.

sanskrit: आरुरोह रथं चापि सूतपुत्रप्रतापितः ।निःश्वसन्दुःखसंतप्तः कुम्भे क्षिप्त इवोरगः ॥९७॥
transliteration: āruroha rathaṁ cāpi sūtaputrapratāpitaḥ ,niḥśvasanduḥkhasaṁtaptaḥ kumbhe kṣipta ivoragaḥ.
english: Deeply tormented by the son of Sūta (sūtaputra) and afflicted by sorrow, he ascended the chariot, sighing like a serpent thrown into a jar.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 17 Verse 97. Deeply tormented by the son of Sūta (sūtaputra) and afflicted by sorrow, he ascended the chariot, sighing like a serpent thrown into a jar.

sanskrit: तं विसृज्य रणे कर्णः पाञ्चालांस्त्वरितो ययौ ।रथेनातिपताकेन चन्द्रवर्णहयेन च ॥९८॥
transliteration: taṁ visṛjya raṇe karṇaḥ pāñcālāṁstvarito yayau ,rathenātipatākena candravarṇahayena ca.
english: Abandoning him on the battlefield, Karṇa swiftly proceeded towards the Pāñcālas with his chariot, which bore an exceedingly grand banner and was drawn by moon-colored horses.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 17 Verse 98. Abandoning him on the battlefield, Karṇa swiftly proceeded towards the Pāñcālas with his chariot, which bore an exceedingly grand banner and was drawn by moon-colored horses.

sanskrit: तत्राक्रन्दो महानासीत्पाण्डवानां विशां पते ।दृष्ट्वा सेनापतिं यान्तं पाञ्चालानां रथव्रजान् ॥९९॥
transliteration: tatrākrando mahānāsītpāṇḍavānāṁ viśāṁ pate ,dṛṣṭvā senāpatiṁ yāntaṁ pāñcālānāṁ rathavrajān.
english: O lord of the people, a great cry arose there among the Pāṇḍavas upon seeing the commander (Karṇa) advancing towards the chariot formations of the Pāñcālas.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 17 Verse 99. O lord of the people, a great cry arose there among the Pāṇḍavas upon seeing the commander (Karṇa) advancing towards the chariot formations of the Pāñcālas.

sanskrit: तत्राकरोन्महाराज कदनं सूतनन्दनः ।मध्यं गते दिनकरे चक्रवत्प्रचरन्प्रभुः ॥१००॥
transliteration: tatrākaronmahārāja kadanaṁ sūtanandanaḥ ,madhyaṁ gate dinakare cakravatpracaranprabhuḥ.
english: O great king (mahārāja), the son of Sūta (sūtanandana), a powerful lord, wrought destruction there when the sun (dinakara) was at its zenith, moving about like a whirling discus.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 17 Verse 100. O great king (mahārāja), the son of Sūta (sūtanandana), a powerful lord, wrought destruction there when the sun (dinakara) was at its zenith, moving about like a whirling discus.

sanskrit: भग्नचक्रै रथैः केचिच्छिन्नध्वजपताकिभिः ।ससूतैर्हतसूतैश्च भग्नाक्षैश्चैव मारिष ।ह्रियमाणानपश्याम पाञ्चालानां रथव्रजान् ॥१०१॥
transliteration: bhagnacakrai rathaiḥ kecicchinnadhvajapatākibhiḥ ,sasūtairhatasūtaiśca bhagnākṣaiścaiva māriṣa ,hriyamāṇānapaśyāma pāñcālānāṁ rathavrajān.
english: O venerable one, we saw some of the Pāñcāla chariot formations being carried away, their chariots having broken wheels, torn flags and banners, some with charioteers still present, others with their charioteers slain, and indeed with broken axles.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 17 Verse 101. O venerable one, we saw some of the Pāñcāla chariot formations being carried away, their chariots having broken wheels, torn flags and banners, some with charioteers still present, others with their charioteers slain, and indeed with broken axles.

sanskrit: तत्र तत्र च संभ्रान्ता विचेरुर्मत्तकुञ्जराः ।दवाग्निना परीताङ्गा यथैव स्युर्महावने ॥१०२॥
transliteration: tatra tatra ca saṁbhrāntā vicerurmattakuñjarāḥ ,davāgninā parītāṅgā yathaiva syurmahāvane.
english: And here and there, maddened elephants, their bodies as if surrounded by a raging forest fire in a great wilderness, wandered about in confusion.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 17 Verse 102. And here and there, maddened elephants, their bodies as if surrounded by a raging forest fire in a great wilderness, wandered about in confusion.

sanskrit: भिन्नकुम्भा विरुधिराश्छिन्नहस्ताश्च वारणाः ।भिन्नगात्रवराश्चैव च्छिन्नवालाश्च मारिष ।छिन्नाभ्राणीव संपेतुर्वध्यमाना महात्मना ॥१०३॥
transliteration: bhinnakumbhā virudhirāśchinnahastāśca vāraṇāḥ ,bhinnagātravarāścaiva cchinnavālāśca māriṣa ,chinnābhrāṇīva saṁpeturvadhyamānā mahātmanā.
english: O venerable one, elephants with shattered frontal globes, bleeding profusely, with severed trunks, and with their excellent bodies broken, and with their tails cut off, fell down like torn clouds, being slain by the great hero.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 17 Verse 103. O venerable one, elephants with shattered frontal globes, bleeding profusely, with severed trunks, and with their excellent bodies broken, and with their tails cut off, fell down like torn clouds, being slain by the great hero.

sanskrit: अपरे त्रासिता नागा नाराचशततोमरैः ।तमेवाभिमुखा यान्ति शलभा इव पावकम् ॥१०४॥
transliteration: apare trāsitā nāgā nārācaśatatomaraiḥ ,tamevābhimukhā yānti śalabhā iva pāvakam.
english: Other elephants, terrified by hundreds of arrows and javelins, rushed towards that (danger) as if moths were flying into a fire.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 17 Verse 104. Other elephants, terrified by hundreds of arrows and javelins, rushed towards that (danger) as if moths were flying into a fire.

sanskrit: अपरे निष्टनन्तः स्म व्यदृश्यन्त महाद्विपाः ।क्षरन्तः शोणितं गात्रैर्नगा इव जलप्लवम् ॥१०५॥
transliteration: apare niṣṭanantaḥ sma vyadṛśyanta mahādvipāḥ ,kṣarantaḥ śoṇitaṁ gātrairnagā iva jalaplavam.
english: Others, great elephants, were seen groaning, their bodies streaming blood like mountains gushing forth water.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 17 Verse 105. Others, great elephants, were seen groaning, their bodies streaming blood like mountains gushing forth water.

sanskrit: उरश्छदैर्विमुक्ताश्च वालबन्धैश्च वाजिनः ।राजतैश्च तथा कांस्यैः सौवर्णैश्चैव भूषणैः ॥१०६॥
transliteration: uraśchadairvimuktāśca vālabandhaiśca vājinaḥ ,rājataiśca tathā kāṁsyaiḥ sauvarṇaiścaiva bhūṣaṇaiḥ.
english: And horses were seen, devoid of their breast-plates and tail-coverings, as well as their silver, bronze, and golden ornaments.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 17 Verse 106. And horses were seen, devoid of their breast-plates and tail-coverings, as well as their silver, bronze, and golden ornaments.

sanskrit: हीना आस्तरणैश्चैव खलीनैश्च विवर्जिताः ।चामरैश्च कुथाभिश्च तूणीरैः पतितैरपि ॥१०७॥
transliteration: hīnā āstaraṇaiścaiva khalīnaiśca vivarjitāḥ ,cāmaraiśca kuthābhiśca tūṇīraiḥ patitairapi.
english: They were lacking their blankets and bits, and were also devoid of fly-whisks, saddle-cloths, and even fallen quivers.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 17 Verse 107. They were lacking their blankets and bits, and were also devoid of fly-whisks, saddle-cloths, and even fallen quivers.

sanskrit: निहतैः सादिभिश्चैव शूरैराहवशोभिभिः ।अपश्याम रणे तत्र भ्राम्यमाणान्हयोत्तमान् ॥१०८॥
transliteration: nihataiḥ sādibhiścaiva śūrairāhavaśobhibhiḥ ,apaśyāma raṇe tatra bhrāmyamāṇānhayottamān.
english: There on the battlefield, we saw the finest horses wandering, abandoned by the slain horsemen and by heroes who shone brightly in combat.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 17 Verse 108. There on the battlefield, we saw the finest horses wandering, abandoned by the slain horsemen and by heroes who shone brightly in combat.

sanskrit: प्रासैः खड्गैश्च संस्यूतानृष्टिभिश्च नराधिप ।हययोधानपश्याम कञ्चुकोष्णीषधारिणः ॥१०९॥
transliteration: prāsaiḥ khaḍgaiśca saṁsyūtānṛṣṭibhiśca narādhipa ,hayayodhānapaśyāma kañcukoṣṇīṣadhāriṇaḥ.
english: O king, we saw horse-warriors who, wearing armor and turbans, were pierced through by spears, swords, and lances.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 17 Verse 109. O king, we saw horse-warriors who, wearing armor and turbans, were pierced through by spears, swords, and lances.

sanskrit: रथान्हेमपरिष्कारान्सुयुक्ताञ्जवनैर्हयैः ।भ्रममाणानपश्याम हतेषु रथिषु द्रुतम् ॥११०॥
transliteration: rathānhemapariṣkārānsuyuktāñjavanairhayaiḥ ,bhramamāṇānapaśyāma hateṣu rathiṣu drutam.
english: We saw chariots, adorned with gold and well-yoked with swift horses, swiftly moving about when their charioteers had been slain.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 17 Verse 110. We saw chariots, adorned with gold and well-yoked with swift horses, swiftly moving about when their charioteers had been slain.

sanskrit: भग्नाक्षकूबरान्कांश्चिच्छिन्नचक्रांश्च मारिष ।विपताकाध्वजांश्चान्याञ्छिन्नेषायुगबन्धुरान् ॥१११॥
transliteration: bhagnākṣakūbarānkāṁścicchinnacakrāṁśca māriṣa ,vipatākādhvajāṁścānyāñchinneṣāyugabandhurān.
english: O respected one, we saw some chariots with broken axles and poles and with broken wheels, and others with lost flags and banners and with broken poles, yokes, and frames.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 17 Verse 111. O respected one, we saw some chariots with broken axles and poles and with broken wheels, and others with lost flags and banners and with broken poles, yokes, and frames.

sanskrit: विहीनान्रथिनस्तत्र धावमानान्समन्ततः ।सूर्यपुत्रशरैस्त्रस्तानपश्याम विशां पते ॥११२॥
transliteration: vihīnānrathinastatra dhāvamānānsamantataḥ ,sūryaputraśaraistrastānapaśyāma viśāṁ pate.
english: O lord of the people, we saw charioteers, deprived of their chariots, running in all directions, terrified by the arrows of Karṇa (sūryaputra).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 17 Verse 112. O lord of the people, we saw charioteers, deprived of their chariots, running in all directions, terrified by the arrows of Karṇa (sūryaputra).

sanskrit: विशस्त्रांश्च तथैवान्यान्सशस्त्रांश्च बहून्हतान् ।तावकाञ्जालसंछन्नानुरोघण्टाविभूषितान् ॥११३॥
transliteration: viśastrāṁśca tathaivānyānsaśastrāṁśca bahūnhatān ,tāvakāñjālasaṁchannānuroghaṇṭāvibhūṣitān.
english: We saw many of your forces (tāvakān), both disarmed and armed, who lay killed, covered with nets, and adorned with chest-bells.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 17 Verse 113. We saw many of your forces (tāvakān), both disarmed and armed, who lay killed, covered with nets, and adorned with chest-bells.

sanskrit: नानावर्णविचित्राभिः पताकाभिरलंकृतान् ।पदातीनन्वपश्याम धावमानान्समन्ततः ॥११४॥
transliteration: nānāvarṇavicitrābhiḥ patākābhiralaṁkṛtān ,padātīnanvapaśyāma dhāvamānānsamantataḥ.
english: We also observed infantry adorned with diverse, multi-colored flags, running everywhere.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 17 Verse 114. We also observed infantry adorned with diverse, multi-colored flags, running everywhere.

sanskrit: शिरांसि बाहूनूरूंश्च छिन्नानन्यांस्तथा युधि ।कर्णचापच्युतैर्बाणैरपश्याम विनाकृतान् ॥११५॥
transliteration: śirāṁsi bāhūnūrūṁśca chinnānanyāṁstathā yudhi ,karṇacāpacyutairbāṇairapaśyāma vinākṛtān.
english: We saw others in battle whose heads, arms, and thighs were severed and separated by arrows shot from Karṇa's bow.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 17 Verse 115. We saw others in battle whose heads, arms, and thighs were severed and separated by arrows shot from Karṇa's bow.

sanskrit: महान्व्यतिकरो रौद्रो योधानामन्वदृश्यत ।कर्णसायकनुन्नानां हतानां निशितैः शरैः ॥११६॥
transliteration: mahānvyatikaro raudro yodhānāmanvadṛśyata ,karṇasāyakanunnānāṁ hatānāṁ niśitaiḥ śaraiḥ.
english: A great and dreadful slaughter (vyatikaraḥ) of warriors was seen, of those killed by sharp arrows propelled by Karṇa's arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 17 Verse 116. A great and dreadful slaughter (vyatikaraḥ) of warriors was seen, of those killed by sharp arrows propelled by Karṇa's arrows.

sanskrit: ते वध्यमानाः समरे सूतपुत्रेण सृञ्जयाः ।तमेवाभिमुखा यान्ति पतंगा इव पावकम् ॥११७॥
transliteration: te vadhyamānāḥ samare sūtaputreṇa sṛñjayāḥ ,tamevābhimukhā yānti pataṁgā iva pāvakam.
english: The Srinjayas, though being slaughtered in battle by Karna, the son of a charioteer, advanced directly towards him, just as moths fly into a fire.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 17 Verse 117. The Srinjayas, though being slaughtered in battle by Karna, the son of a charioteer, advanced directly towards him, just as moths fly into a fire.

sanskrit: तं दहन्तमनीकानि तत्र तत्र महारथम् ।क्षत्रिया वर्जयामासुर्युगान्ताग्निमिवोल्बणम् ॥११८॥
transliteration: taṁ dahantamanīkāni tatra tatra mahāratham ,kṣatriyā varjayāmāsuryugāntāgnimivolbaṇam.
english: The Kshatriya warriors avoided that great chariot-fighter (mahāratha), who was incinerating their armies everywhere, just as one would avoid the fierce (ulbaṇa) fire (agni) at the end of a cosmic age (yugānta).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 17 Verse 118. The Kshatriya warriors avoided that great chariot-fighter (mahāratha), who was incinerating their armies everywhere, just as one would avoid the fierce (ulbaṇa) fire (agni) at the end of a cosmic age (yugānta).

sanskrit: हतशेषास्तु ये वीराः पाञ्चालानां महारथाः ।तान्प्रभग्नान्द्रुतान्कर्णः पृष्ठतो विकिरञ्शरैः ।अभ्यधावत तेजस्वी विशीर्णकवचध्वजान् ॥११९॥
transliteration: hataśeṣāstu ye vīrāḥ pāñcālānāṁ mahārathāḥ ,tānprabhagnāndrutānkarṇaḥ pṛṣṭhato vikirañśaraiḥ ,abhyadhāvata tejasvī viśīrṇakavacadhvajān.
english: But the brilliant (tejasvī) Karna, showering them with arrows from behind, pursued those surviving great chariot-warriors (mahārathas) of the Pancalas who were already routed, fleeing, and whose armors and banners were shattered.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 17 Verse 119. But the brilliant (tejasvī) Karna, showering them with arrows from behind, pursued those surviving great chariot-warriors (mahārathas) of the Pancalas who were already routed, fleeing, and whose armors and banners were shattered.

sanskrit: तापयामास तान्बाणैः सूतपुत्रो महारथः ।मध्यंदिनमनुप्राप्तो भूतानीव तमोनुदः ॥१२०॥
transliteration: tāpayāmāsa tānbāṇaiḥ sūtaputro mahārathaḥ ,madhyaṁdinamanuprāpto bhūtānīva tamonudaḥ.
english: Karna, the son of a charioteer (sūtaputra) and great chariot-warrior (mahāratha), tormented them with his arrows, just as the sun (tamonuda), having reached midday (madhyaṃdina), scorches all living beings.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 17 Verse 120. Karna, the son of a charioteer (sūtaputra) and great chariot-warrior (mahāratha), tormented them with his arrows, just as the sun (tamonuda), having reached midday (madhyaṃdina), scorches all living beings.

sanskrit: संजय उवाच ।युयुत्सुं तव पुत्रं तु प्राद्रवन्तं महद्बलम् ।उलूकोऽभ्यपतत्तूर्णं तिष्ठ तिष्ठेति चाब्रवीत् ॥१॥
transliteration: saṁjaya uvāca ,yuyutsuṁ tava putraṁ tu prādravantaṁ mahadbalam ,ulūko'bhyapatattūrṇaṁ tiṣṭha tiṣṭheti cābravīt.
english: Sañjaya said, "Ulūka quickly flew towards your son Yuyutsu, who was rushing forward with great force, and exclaimed, 'Stop! Stop!'"
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 18 Verse 1. Sañjaya said, "Ulūka quickly flew towards your son Yuyutsu, who was rushing forward with great force, and exclaimed, 'Stop! Stop!'"

sanskrit: युयुत्सुस्तु ततो राजञ्शितधारेण पत्रिणा ।उलूकं ताडयामास वज्रेणेन्द्र इवाचलम् ॥२॥
transliteration: yuyutsustu tato rājañśitadhāreṇa patriṇā ,ulūkaṁ tāḍayāmāsa vajreṇendra ivācalam.
english: O King, Yuyutsu then struck Ulūka with a sharp-edged arrow, just as Indra (Indra) strikes a mountain with his thunderbolt (vajra).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 18 Verse 2. O King, Yuyutsu then struck Ulūka with a sharp-edged arrow, just as Indra (Indra) strikes a mountain with his thunderbolt (vajra).

sanskrit: उलूकस्तु ततः क्रुद्धस्तव पुत्रस्य संयुगे ।क्षुरप्रेण धनुश्छित्त्वा ताडयामास कर्णिना ॥३॥
transliteration: ulūkastu tataḥ kruddhastava putrasya saṁyuge ,kṣurapreṇa dhanuśchittvā tāḍayāmāsa karṇinā.
english: But then, enraged, Ulūka, in that battle (saṃyuga), first cut your son's bow with a razor-sharp arrow (kṣurapra), and then struck him with a barbed arrow.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 18 Verse 3. But then, enraged, Ulūka, in that battle (saṃyuga), first cut your son's bow with a razor-sharp arrow (kṣurapra), and then struck him with a barbed arrow.

sanskrit: तदपास्य धनुश्छिन्नं युयुत्सुर्वेगवत्तरम् ।अन्यदादत्त सुमहच्चापं संरक्तलोचनः ॥४॥
transliteration: tadapāsya dhanuśchinnaṁ yuyutsurvegavattaram ,anyadādatta sumahaccāpaṁ saṁraktalocanaḥ.
english: Having cast aside that broken bow, Yuyutsu, with eyes reddened (saṃrakta), swiftly took up another very mighty bow.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 18 Verse 4. Having cast aside that broken bow, Yuyutsu, with eyes reddened (saṃrakta), swiftly took up another very mighty bow.

sanskrit: शाकुनिं च ततः षष्ट्या विव्याध भरतर्षभ ।सारथिं त्रिभिरानर्छत्तं च भूयो व्यविध्यत ॥५॥
transliteration: śākuniṁ ca tataḥ ṣaṣṭyā vivyādha bharatarṣabha ,sārathiṁ tribhirānarchattaṁ ca bhūyo vyavidhyata.
english: O bull among the Bharatas, then he pierced Shakuni with sixty [arrows]. And he struck his charioteer with three [arrows], and pierced him again.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 18 Verse 5. O bull among the Bharatas, then he pierced Shakuni with sixty [arrows]. And he struck his charioteer with three [arrows], and pierced him again.

sanskrit: उलूकस्तं तु विंशत्या विद्ध्वा हेमविभूषितैः ।अथास्य समरे क्रुद्धो ध्वजं चिच्छेद काञ्चनम् ॥६॥
transliteration: ulūkastaṁ tu viṁśatyā viddhvā hemavibhūṣitaiḥ ,athāsya samare kruddho dhvajaṁ ciccheda kāñcanam.
english: Enraged Uluka, having pierced him with twenty gold-adorned [arrows], then cut down his golden banner in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 18 Verse 6. Enraged Uluka, having pierced him with twenty gold-adorned [arrows], then cut down his golden banner in battle.

sanskrit: स च्छिन्नयष्टिः सुमहाञ्शीर्यमाणो महाध्वजः ।पपात प्रमुखे राजन्युयुत्सोः काञ्चनोज्ज्वलः ॥७॥
transliteration: sa cchinnayaṣṭiḥ sumahāñśīryamāṇo mahādhvajaḥ ,papāta pramukhe rājanyuyutsoḥ kāñcanojjvalaḥ.
english: O King, that very great banner, with its staff cut, crumbling, and gleaming with gold, fell directly in front of Yuyutsu.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 18 Verse 7. O King, that very great banner, with its staff cut, crumbling, and gleaming with gold, fell directly in front of Yuyutsu.

sanskrit: ध्वजमुन्मथितं दृष्ट्वा युयुत्सुः क्रोधमूर्छितः ।उलूकं पञ्चभिर्बाणैराजघान स्तनान्तरे ॥८॥
transliteration: dhvajamunmathitaṁ dṛṣṭvā yuyutsuḥ krodhamūrchitaḥ ,ulūkaṁ pañcabhirbāṇairājaghāna stanāntare.
english: Having seen the shattered banner, Yuyutsu, overcome with anger, struck Uluka in the chest with five arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 18 Verse 8. Having seen the shattered banner, Yuyutsu, overcome with anger, struck Uluka in the chest with five arrows.

sanskrit: उलूकस्तस्य भल्लेन तैलधौतेन मारिष ।शिरश्चिच्छेद सहसा यन्तुर्भरतसत्तम ॥९॥
transliteration: ulūkastasya bhallena tailadhautena māriṣa ,śiraściccheda sahasā yanturbharatasattama.
english: O respected one, O best of the Bharatas, Ulūka suddenly cut off the charioteer's head with his oil-polished spear.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 18 Verse 9. O respected one, O best of the Bharatas, Ulūka suddenly cut off the charioteer's head with his oil-polished spear.

sanskrit: जघान चतुरोऽश्वांश्च तं च विव्याध पञ्चभिः ।सोऽतिविद्धो बलवता प्रत्यपायाद्रथान्तरम् ॥१०॥
transliteration: jaghāna caturo'śvāṁśca taṁ ca vivyādha pañcabhiḥ ,so'tividdho balavatā pratyapāyādrathāntaram.
english: He (Ulūka) killed the four horses and also pierced him (the warrior) with five (arrows). That warrior, severely wounded by the powerful (Ulūka), retreated to another chariot.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 18 Verse 10. He (Ulūka) killed the four horses and also pierced him (the warrior) with five (arrows). That warrior, severely wounded by the powerful (Ulūka), retreated to another chariot.

sanskrit: तं निर्जित्य रणे राजन्नुलूकस्त्वरितो ययौ ।पाञ्चालान्सृञ्जयांश्चैव विनिघ्नन्निशितैः शरैः ॥११॥
transliteration: taṁ nirjitya raṇe rājannulūkastvarito yayau ,pāñcālānsṛñjayāṁścaiva vinighnanniśitaiḥ śaraiḥ.
english: O King, having defeated him in battle, Ulūka swiftly went, striking down the Pāñcālas and Sṛñjayas with sharp arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 18 Verse 11. O King, having defeated him in battle, Ulūka swiftly went, striking down the Pāñcālas and Sṛñjayas with sharp arrows.

sanskrit: शतानीकं महाराज श्रुतकर्मा सुतस्तव ।व्यश्वसूतरथं चक्रे निमेषार्धादसंभ्रमम् ॥१२॥
transliteration: śatānīkaṁ mahārāja śrutakarmā sutastava ,vyaśvasūtarathaṁ cakre nimeṣārdhādasaṁbhramam.
english: O great King, your son Shrutakarmā, without confusion, in half a moment, rendered Shatānīka's chariot horseless and charioteer-less.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 18 Verse 12. O great King, your son Shrutakarmā, without confusion, in half a moment, rendered Shatānīka's chariot horseless and charioteer-less.

sanskrit: हताश्वे तु रथे तिष्ठञ्शतानीको महाबलः ।गदां चिक्षेप संक्रुद्धस्तव पुत्रस्य मारिष ॥१३॥
transliteration: hatāśve tu rathe tiṣṭhañśatānīko mahābalaḥ ,gadāṁ cikṣepa saṁkruddhastava putrasya māriṣa.
