english: But Śatānīka, the mighty warrior, standing on his chariot after its horses had been killed, enraged, hurled his mace at your son, O venerable one.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 18 Verse 13. But Śatānīka, the mighty warrior, standing on his chariot after its horses had been killed, enraged, hurled his mace at your son, O venerable one.

sanskrit: सा कृत्वा स्यन्दनं भस्म हयांश्चैव ससारथीन् ।पपात धरणीं तूर्णं दारयन्तीव भारत ॥१४॥
transliteration: sā kṛtvā syandanaṁ bhasma hayāṁścaiva sasārathīn ,papāta dharaṇīṁ tūrṇaṁ dārayantīva bhārata.
english: Having reduced the chariot, and indeed the horses along with their charioteers, to ashes, she (the mace) quickly fell to the earth, as if splitting it, O Bhārata.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 18 Verse 14. Having reduced the chariot, and indeed the horses along with their charioteers, to ashes, she (the mace) quickly fell to the earth, as if splitting it, O Bhārata.

sanskrit: तावुभौ विरथौ वीरौ कुरूणां कीर्तिवर्धनौ ।अपाक्रमेतां युद्धार्तौ प्रेक्षमाणौ परस्परम् ॥१५॥
transliteration: tāvubhau virathau vīrau kurūṇāṁ kīrtivardhanau ,apākrametāṁ yuddhārtau prekṣamāṇau parasparam.
english: Those two heroes, both dismounted from their chariots, who were increasing the renown of the Kurus, retreated, weary from battle and looking at each other.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 18 Verse 15. Those two heroes, both dismounted from their chariots, who were increasing the renown of the Kurus, retreated, weary from battle and looking at each other.

sanskrit: पुत्रस्तु तव संभ्रान्तो विवित्सो रथमाविशत् ।शतानीकोऽपि त्वरितः प्रतिविन्ध्यरथं गतः ॥१६॥
transliteration: putrastu tava saṁbhrānto vivitso rathamāviśat ,śatānīko'pi tvaritaḥ prativindhyarathaṁ gataḥ.
english: But your son Vivimśati, bewildered, entered a chariot. And Śatānīka, too, swiftly went to Prativindhya's chariot.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 18 Verse 16. But your son Vivimśati, bewildered, entered a chariot. And Śatānīka, too, swiftly went to Prativindhya's chariot.

sanskrit: सुतसोमस्तु शकुनिं विव्याध निशितैः शरैः ।नाकम्पयत संरब्धो वार्योघ इव पर्वतम् ॥१७॥
transliteration: sutasomastu śakuniṁ vivyādha niśitaiḥ śaraiḥ ,nākampayata saṁrabdho vāryogha iva parvatam.
english: Sutasoma, however, pierced Śakuni with sharp arrows. Though enraged, he could not make him tremble, just as a flood of water cannot shake a mountain.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 18 Verse 17. Sutasoma, however, pierced Śakuni with sharp arrows. Though enraged, he could not make him tremble, just as a flood of water cannot shake a mountain.

sanskrit: सुतसोमस्तु तं दृष्ट्वा पितुरत्यन्तवैरिणम् ।शरैरनेकसाहस्रैश्छादयामास भारत ॥१८॥
transliteration: sutasomastu taṁ dṛṣṭvā pituratyantavairiṇam ,śarairanekasāhasraiśchādayāmāsa bhārata.
english: O Bhārata, Sutasoma, seeing him, the utter enemy of his father, covered him with many thousands of arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 18 Verse 18. O Bhārata, Sutasoma, seeing him, the utter enemy of his father, covered him with many thousands of arrows.

sanskrit: ताञ्शराञ्शकुनिस्तूर्णं चिच्छेदान्यैः पतत्रिभिः ।लघ्वस्त्रश्चित्रयोधी च जितकाशी च संयुगे ॥१९॥
transliteration: tāñśarāñśakunistūrṇaṁ cicchedānyaiḥ patatribhiḥ ,laghvastraścitrayodhī ca jitakāśī ca saṁyuge.
english: Śakuni, who was swift in wielding weapons, fought wondrously, and was victorious in battle, quickly cut down those arrows with other arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 18 Verse 19. Śakuni, who was swift in wielding weapons, fought wondrously, and was victorious in battle, quickly cut down those arrows with other arrows.

sanskrit: निवार्य समरे चापि शरांस्तान्निशितैः शरैः ।आजघान सुसंक्रुद्धः सुतसोमं त्रिभिः शरैः ॥२०॥
transliteration: nivārya samare cāpi śarāṁstānniśitaiḥ śaraiḥ ,ājaghāna susaṁkruddhaḥ sutasomaṁ tribhiḥ śaraiḥ.
english: And having repelled those arrows in battle with his own sharp arrows, he, greatly enraged, struck Sutasoma with three arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 18 Verse 20. And having repelled those arrows in battle with his own sharp arrows, he, greatly enraged, struck Sutasoma with three arrows.

sanskrit: तस्याश्वान्केतनं सूतं तिलशो व्यधमच्छरैः ।स्यालस्तव महावीर्यस्ततस्ते चुक्रुशुर्जनाः ॥२१॥
transliteration: tasyāśvānketanaṁ sūtaṁ tilaśo vyadhamaccharaiḥ ,syālastava mahāvīryastataste cukruśurjanāḥ.
english: Your exceedingly valorous brother-in-law shattered his opponent's horses, banner, and charioteer into tiny pieces with arrows. Then, your people cried out.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 18 Verse 21. Your exceedingly valorous brother-in-law shattered his opponent's horses, banner, and charioteer into tiny pieces with arrows. Then, your people cried out.

sanskrit: हताश्वो विरथश्चैव छिन्नधन्वा च मारिष ।धन्वी धनुर्वरं गृह्य रथाद्भूमावतिष्ठत ।व्यसृजत्सायकांश्चैव स्वर्णपुङ्खाञ्शिलाशितान् ॥२२॥
transliteration: hatāśvo virathaścaiva chinnadhanvā ca māriṣa ,dhanvī dhanurvaraṁ gṛhya rathādbhūmāvatiṣṭhata ,vyasṛjatsāyakāṁścaiva svarṇapuṅkhāñśilāśitān.
english: O respected one, with his horses killed, chariot destroyed, and bow broken, the archer (the opponent warrior) then took up an excellent bow and stood on the ground. He released arrows with golden shafts, sharpened on stone.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 18 Verse 22. O respected one, with his horses killed, chariot destroyed, and bow broken, the archer (the opponent warrior) then took up an excellent bow and stood on the ground. He released arrows with golden shafts, sharpened on stone.

sanskrit: छादयामासुरथ ते तव स्यालस्य तं रथम् ।पतंगानामिव व्राताः शरव्राता महारथम् ॥२३॥
transliteration: chādayāmāsuratha te tava syālasya taṁ ratham ,pataṁgānāmiva vrātāḥ śaravrātā mahāratham.
english: Then, those multitudes of arrows, like swarms of moths, covered the chariot of your great warrior brother-in-law, and the great warrior (himself).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 18 Verse 23. Then, those multitudes of arrows, like swarms of moths, covered the chariot of your great warrior brother-in-law, and the great warrior (himself).

sanskrit: रथोपस्थान्समीक्ष्यापि विव्यथे नैव सौबलः ।प्रमृद्नंश्च शरांस्तांस्ताञ्शरव्रातैर्महायशाः ॥२४॥
transliteration: rathopasthānsamīkṣyāpi vivyathe naiva saubalaḥ ,pramṛdnaṁśca śarāṁstāṁstāñśaravrātairmahāyaśāḥ.
english: Even upon seeing those (enemy warriors) on their chariots, Subala's son (Śakuni) was not at all disturbed. And that highly renowned warrior (Śakuni) crushed those very arrows with his own multitudes of arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 18 Verse 24. Even upon seeing those (enemy warriors) on their chariots, Subala's son (Śakuni) was not at all disturbed. And that highly renowned warrior (Śakuni) crushed those very arrows with his own multitudes of arrows.

sanskrit: तत्रातुष्यन्त योधाश्च सिद्धाश्चापि दिवि स्थिताः ।सुतसोमस्य तत्कर्म दृष्ट्वाश्रद्धेयमद्भुतम् ।रथस्थं नृपतिं तं तु पदातिः सन्नयोधयत् ॥२५॥
transliteration: tatrātuṣyanta yodhāśca siddhāścāpi divi sthitāḥ ,sutasomasya tatkarma dṛṣṭvāśraddheyamadbhutam ,rathasthaṁ nṛpatiṁ taṁ tu padātiḥ sannayodhayat.
english: The warriors and even the perfected beings (siddhas) residing in heaven were greatly pleased there, having witnessed that incredible and amazing feat of Sutasoma. For, though on foot, he fought that king who was mounted on a chariot.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 18 Verse 25. The warriors and even the perfected beings (siddhas) residing in heaven were greatly pleased there, having witnessed that incredible and amazing feat of Sutasoma. For, though on foot, he fought that king who was mounted on a chariot.

sanskrit: तस्य तीक्ष्णैर्महावेगैर्भल्लैः संनतपर्वभिः ।व्यहनत्कार्मुकं राजा तूणीरं चैव सर्वशः ॥२६॥
transliteration: tasya tīkṣṇairmahāvegairbhallaiḥ saṁnataparvabhiḥ ,vyahanatkārmukaṁ rājā tūṇīraṁ caiva sarvaśaḥ.
english: The king completely shattered his (Sutasoma's) bow and also his quiver with sharp, exceedingly swift lances (bhallas) that had bent shafts.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 18 Verse 26. The king completely shattered his (Sutasoma's) bow and also his quiver with sharp, exceedingly swift lances (bhallas) that had bent shafts.

sanskrit: स च्छिन्नधन्वा समरे खड्गमुद्यम्य नानदन् ।वैडूर्योत्पलवर्णाभं हस्तिदन्तमयत्सरुम् ॥२७॥
transliteration: sa cchinnadhanvā samare khaḍgamudyamya nānadan ,vaiḍūryotpalavarṇābhaṁ hastidantamayatsarum.
english: With his bow shattered in battle, he (Sutasoma) roared, having raised a sword that shone with the color of a cat's eye gem and a blue lotus, and which had a hilt made of elephant ivory.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 18 Verse 27. With his bow shattered in battle, he (Sutasoma) roared, having raised a sword that shone with the color of a cat's eye gem and a blue lotus, and which had a hilt made of elephant ivory.

sanskrit: भ्राम्यमाणं ततस्तं तु विमलाम्बरवर्चसम् ।कालोपमं ततो मेने सुतसोमस्य धीमतः ॥२८॥
transliteration: bhrāmyamāṇaṁ tatastaṁ tu vimalāmbaravarcasam ,kālopamaṁ tato mene sutasomasya dhīmataḥ.
english: Then, the king considered that whirling sword of the intelligent Sutasoma – a sword that possessed the radiance of a clear sky and was comparable to the destructive power of Time (Kāla).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 18 Verse 28. Then, the king considered that whirling sword of the intelligent Sutasoma – a sword that possessed the radiance of a clear sky and was comparable to the destructive power of Time (Kāla).

sanskrit: सोऽचरत्सहसा खड्गी मण्डलानि सहस्रशः ।चतुर्विंशन्महाराज शिक्षाबलसमन्वितः ॥२९॥
transliteration: so'caratsahasā khaḍgī maṇḍalāni sahasraśaḥ ,caturviṁśanmahārāja śikṣābalasamanvitaḥ.
english: O great king, that sword-wielder quickly performed thousands of maneuvers, possessing twenty-four (skills) and endowed with the strength of training.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 18 Verse 29. O great king, that sword-wielder quickly performed thousands of maneuvers, possessing twenty-four (skills) and endowed with the strength of training.

sanskrit: सौबलस्तु ततस्तस्य शरांश्चिक्षेप वीर्यवान् ।तानापतत एवाशु चिच्छेद परमासिना ॥३०॥
transliteration: saubalastu tatastasya śarāṁścikṣepa vīryavān ,tānāpatata evāśu ciccheda paramāsinā.
english: But then the valorous Saubala cast arrows at him. With his excellent sword, (the sword-wielder) immediately cut down those very arrows as they approached.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 18 Verse 30. But then the valorous Saubala cast arrows at him. With his excellent sword, (the sword-wielder) immediately cut down those very arrows as they approached.

sanskrit: ततः क्रुद्धो महाराज सौबलः परवीरहा ।प्राहिणोत्सुतसोमस्य शरानाशीविषोपमान् ॥३१॥
transliteration: tataḥ kruddho mahārāja saubalaḥ paravīrahā ,prāhiṇotsutasomasya śarānāśīviṣopamān.
english: O great king, then the enraged Saubala, the slayer of enemy heroes, dispatched arrows resembling venomous snakes towards Sutasoma.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 18 Verse 31. O great king, then the enraged Saubala, the slayer of enemy heroes, dispatched arrows resembling venomous snakes towards Sutasoma.

sanskrit: चिच्छेद तांश्च खड्गेन शिक्षया च बलेन च ।दर्शयँल्लाघवं युद्धे तार्क्ष्यवीर्यसमद्युतिः ॥३२॥
transliteration: ciccheda tāṁśca khaḍgena śikṣayā ca balena ca ,darśayaँllāghavaṁ yuddhe tārkṣyavīryasamadyutiḥ.
english: He cut down those (arrows) with his sword, and with both skill and strength, displaying agility in battle, shining with a radiance equal to the valor of Garuḍa.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 18 Verse 32. He cut down those (arrows) with his sword, and with both skill and strength, displaying agility in battle, shining with a radiance equal to the valor of Garuḍa.

sanskrit: तस्य संचरतो राजन्मण्डलावर्तने तदा ।क्षुरप्रेण सुतीक्ष्णेन खड्गं चिच्छेद सुप्रभम् ॥३३॥
transliteration: tasya saṁcarato rājanmaṇḍalāvartane tadā ,kṣurapreṇa sutīkṣṇena khaḍgaṁ ciccheda suprabham.
english: O king, at that moment, as he was moving in a circular manner, he cut his splendid sword with a very sharp crescent-shaped arrow.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 18 Verse 33. O king, at that moment, as he was moving in a circular manner, he cut his splendid sword with a very sharp crescent-shaped arrow.

sanskrit: स च्छिन्नः सहसा भूमौ निपपात महानसिः ।अवशस्य स्थितं हस्ते तं खड्गं सत्सरुं तदा ॥३४॥
transliteration: sa cchinnaḥ sahasā bhūmau nipapāta mahānasiḥ ,avaśasya sthitaṁ haste taṁ khaḍgaṁ satsaruṁ tadā.
english: That great sword (asi), having been severed, suddenly fell to the ground. Then, the (remaining) sword (khaḍga) with its hilt, which was held in the hand of the powerless one (avaśa),...
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 18 Verse 34. That great sword (asi), having been severed, suddenly fell to the ground. Then, the (remaining) sword (khaḍga) with its hilt, which was held in the hand of the powerless one (avaśa),...

sanskrit: छिन्नमाज्ञाय निस्त्रिंशमवप्लुत्य पदानि षट् ।प्राविध्यत ततः शेषं सुतसोमो महारथः ॥३५॥
transliteration: chinnamājñāya nistriṁśamavaplutya padāni ṣaṭ ,prāvidhyata tataḥ śeṣaṁ sutasomo mahārathaḥ.
english: Having realized that the sword (nistriṃśa) was severed, Sutasoma, the great warrior (mahāratha), then leaped back six steps and threw away the remaining portion.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 18 Verse 35. Having realized that the sword (nistriṃśa) was severed, Sutasoma, the great warrior (mahāratha), then leaped back six steps and threw away the remaining portion.

sanskrit: स च्छित्त्वा सगुणं चापं रणे तस्य महात्मनः ।पपात धरणीं तूर्णं स्वर्णवज्रविभूषितः ।सुतसोमस्ततोऽगच्छच्छ्रुतकीर्तेर्महारथम् ॥३६॥
transliteration: sa cchittvā saguṇaṁ cāpaṁ raṇe tasya mahātmanaḥ ,papāta dharaṇīṁ tūrṇaṁ svarṇavajravibhūṣitaḥ ,sutasomastato'gacchacchrutakīrtermahāratham.
english: He (Sutasoma), having cut the strung bow of that great-souled (mahātman) opponent in battle, (watched as the bow itself), adorned with gold and diamonds, quickly fell to the ground. Then Sutasoma went to Śrutakīrti, the great warrior (mahāratha).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 18 Verse 36. He (Sutasoma), having cut the strung bow of that great-souled (mahātman) opponent in battle, (watched as the bow itself), adorned with gold and diamonds, quickly fell to the ground. Then Sutasoma went to Śrutakīrti, the great warrior (mahāratha).

sanskrit: सौबलोऽपि धनुर्गृह्य घोरमन्यत्सुदुःसहम् ।अभ्ययात्पाण्डवानीकं निघ्नञ्शत्रुगणान्बहून् ॥३७॥
transliteration: saubalo'pi dhanurgṛhya ghoramanyatsuduḥsaham ,abhyayātpāṇḍavānīkaṁ nighnañśatrugaṇānbahūn.
english: Saubala (Śakuni) also, taking up another terrible and unbearable bow, attacked the Pāṇḍava army, striking down many hordes of enemies.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 18 Verse 37. Saubala (Śakuni) also, taking up another terrible and unbearable bow, attacked the Pāṇḍava army, striking down many hordes of enemies.

sanskrit: तत्र नादो महानासीत्पाण्डवानां विशां पते ।सौबलं समरे दृष्ट्वा विचरन्तमभीतवत् ॥३८॥
transliteration: tatra nādo mahānāsītpāṇḍavānāṁ viśāṁ pate ,saubalaṁ samare dṛṣṭvā vicarantamabhītavat.
english: O lord of the people, there was a great outcry from the Pāṇḍavas when they saw Saubala (Śakuni) moving about fearlessly in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 18 Verse 38. O lord of the people, there was a great outcry from the Pāṇḍavas when they saw Saubala (Śakuni) moving about fearlessly in battle.

sanskrit: तान्यनीकानि दृप्तानि शस्त्रवन्ति महान्ति च ।द्राव्यमाणान्यदृश्यन्त सौबलेन महात्मना ॥३९॥
transliteration: tānyanīkāni dṛptāni śastravanti mahānti ca ,drāvyamāṇānyadṛśyanta saubalena mahātmanā.
english: And those proud, well-armed, great armies were seen being routed by the magnanimous Saubala (Śakuni).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 18 Verse 39. And those proud, well-armed, great armies were seen being routed by the magnanimous Saubala (Śakuni).

sanskrit: यथा दैत्यचमूं राजन्देवराजो ममर्द ह ।तथैव पाण्डवीं सेनां सौबलेयो व्यनाशयत् ॥४०॥
transliteration: yathā daityacamūṁ rājandevarājo mamarda ha ,tathaiva pāṇḍavīṁ senāṁ saubaleyo vyanāśayat.
english: O king, just as the king of the gods (Indra) once crushed the army of the Daityas, so too did Saubaleya (Śakuni) destroy the Pāṇḍava army.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 18 Verse 40. O king, just as the king of the gods (Indra) once crushed the army of the Daityas, so too did Saubaleya (Śakuni) destroy the Pāṇḍava army.

sanskrit: धृष्टद्युम्नं कृपो राजन्वारयामास संयुगे ।यथा दृप्तं वने नागं शरभो वारयेद्युधि ॥४१॥
transliteration: dhṛṣṭadyumnaṁ kṛpo rājanvārayāmāsa saṁyuge ,yathā dṛptaṁ vane nāgaṁ śarabho vārayedyudhi.
english: O king, Kṛpa restrained Dhṛṣṭadyumna in battle, just as a śarabha (mythical eight-legged beast) would check an enraged elephant in a forest during a fight.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 18 Verse 41. O king, Kṛpa restrained Dhṛṣṭadyumna in battle, just as a śarabha (mythical eight-legged beast) would check an enraged elephant in a forest during a fight.

sanskrit: निरुद्धः पार्षतस्तेन गौतमेन बलीयसा ।पदात्पदं विचलितुं नाशक्नोत्तत्र भारत ॥४२॥
transliteration: niruddhaḥ pārṣatastena gautamena balīyasā ,padātpadaṁ vicalituṁ nāśaknottatra bhārata.
english: O Bhārata, Dhṛṣṭadyumna (pārṣata), restrained by that strong Gautama (Kṛpa), was unable to move even a single step there.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 18 Verse 42. O Bhārata, Dhṛṣṭadyumna (pārṣata), restrained by that strong Gautama (Kṛpa), was unable to move even a single step there.

sanskrit: गौतमस्य वपुर्दृष्ट्वा धृष्टद्युम्नरथं प्रति ।वित्रेसुः सर्वभूतानि क्षयं प्राप्तं च मेनिरे ॥४३॥
transliteration: gautamasya vapurdṛṣṭvā dhṛṣṭadyumnarathaṁ prati ,vitresuḥ sarvabhūtāni kṣayaṁ prāptaṁ ca menire.
english: Seeing the form of Gautama (Kṛpa) facing Dhṛṣṭadyumna's chariot, all beings trembled and considered that destruction had arrived.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 18 Verse 43. Seeing the form of Gautama (Kṛpa) facing Dhṛṣṭadyumna's chariot, all beings trembled and considered that destruction had arrived.

sanskrit: तत्रावोचन्विमनसो रथिनः सादिनस्तथा ।द्रोणस्य निधने नूनं संक्रुद्धो द्विपदां वरः ॥४४॥
transliteration: tatrāvocanvimanaso rathinaḥ sādinastathā ,droṇasya nidhane nūnaṁ saṁkruddho dvipadāṁ varaḥ.
english: There, the disheartened charioteers and cavalrymen also said: 'Surely, this best among men is greatly enraged by the death of Droṇa.'
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 18 Verse 44. There, the disheartened charioteers and cavalrymen also said: 'Surely, this best among men is greatly enraged by the death of Droṇa.'

sanskrit: शारद्वतो महातेजा दिव्यास्त्रविदुदारधीः ।अपि स्वस्ति भवेदद्य धृष्टद्युम्नस्य गौतमात् ॥४५॥
transliteration: śāradvato mahātejā divyāstravidudāradhīḥ ,api svasti bhavedadya dhṛṣṭadyumnasya gautamāt.
english: Will Dhṛṣṭadyumna be safe today from Gautama (Kṛpācārya), who is greatly brilliant, a knower of divine weapons, and noble-minded?
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 18 Verse 45. Will Dhṛṣṭadyumna be safe today from Gautama (Kṛpācārya), who is greatly brilliant, a knower of divine weapons, and noble-minded?

sanskrit: अपीयं वाहिनी कृत्स्ना मुच्येत महतो भयात् ।अप्ययं ब्राह्मणः सर्वान्न नो हन्यात्समागतान् ॥४६॥
transliteration: apīyaṁ vāhinī kṛtsnā mucyeta mahato bhayāt ,apyayaṁ brāhmaṇaḥ sarvānna no hanyātsamāgatān.
english: Will this entire army be freed from great danger? And will this Brahmin (Kṛpācārya) not kill all of us who have gathered?
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 18 Verse 46. Will this entire army be freed from great danger? And will this Brahmin (Kṛpācārya) not kill all of us who have gathered?

sanskrit: यादृशं दृश्यते रूपमन्तकप्रतिमं भृशम् ।गमिष्यत्यद्य पदवीं भारद्वाजस्य संयुगे ॥४७॥
transliteration: yādṛśaṁ dṛśyate rūpamantakapratimaṁ bhṛśam ,gamiṣyatyadya padavīṁ bhāradvājasya saṁyuge.
english: An appearance is seen today that is exceedingly like (the god of) death (Antaka). (Kṛpa) will attain the path (of heroic action) of Bhāradvāja (Droṇa) in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 18 Verse 47. An appearance is seen today that is exceedingly like (the god of) death (Antaka). (Kṛpa) will attain the path (of heroic action) of Bhāradvāja (Droṇa) in battle.

sanskrit: आचार्यः क्षिप्रहस्तश्च विजयी च सदा युधि ।अस्त्रवान्वीर्यसंपन्नः क्रोधेन च समन्वितः ॥४८॥
transliteration: ācāryaḥ kṣiprahastaśca vijayī ca sadā yudhi ,astravānvīryasaṁpannaḥ krodhena ca samanvitaḥ.
english: The preceptor is swift-handed and always victorious in battle. He is skilled in weapons, endowed with great prowess, and filled with wrath.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 18 Verse 48. The preceptor is swift-handed and always victorious in battle. He is skilled in weapons, endowed with great prowess, and filled with wrath.

sanskrit: पार्षतश्च भृशं युद्धे विमुखोऽद्यापि लक्ष्यते ।इत्येवं विविधा वाचस्तावकानां परैः सह ॥४९॥
transliteration: pārṣataśca bhṛśaṁ yuddhe vimukho'dyāpi lakṣyate ,ityevaṁ vividhā vācastāvakānāṁ paraiḥ saha.
english: Even today, Pārṣata (Dhṛṣṭadyumna) appears greatly disinclined in battle; thus, various discussions are exchanged between your men and the enemies.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 18 Verse 49. Even today, Pārṣata (Dhṛṣṭadyumna) appears greatly disinclined in battle; thus, various discussions are exchanged between your men and the enemies.

sanskrit: विनिःश्वस्य ततः क्रुद्धः कृपः शारद्वतो नृप ।पार्षतं छादयामास निश्चेष्टं सर्वमर्मसु ॥५०॥
transliteration: viniḥśvasya tataḥ kruddhaḥ kṛpaḥ śāradvato nṛpa ,pārṣataṁ chādayāmāsa niśceṣṭaṁ sarvamarmasu.
english: O king, then the enraged Kṛpa, son of Śaradvat, having sighed deeply, covered Pārṣata (Dhṛṣṭadyumna) in all his vital parts, rendering him motionless.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 18 Verse 50. O king, then the enraged Kṛpa, son of Śaradvat, having sighed deeply, covered Pārṣata (Dhṛṣṭadyumna) in all his vital parts, rendering him motionless.

sanskrit: स वध्यमानः समरे गौतमेन महात्मना ।कर्तव्यं न प्रजानाति मोहितः परमाहवे ॥५१॥
transliteration: sa vadhyamānaḥ samare gautamena mahātmanā ,kartavyaṁ na prajānāti mohitaḥ paramāhave.
english: Being assailed in battle by the great-souled Gautama (Kṛpa), he (Dhṛṣṭadyumna) did not know what to do, utterly bewildered in that supreme conflict.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 18 Verse 51. Being assailed in battle by the great-souled Gautama (Kṛpa), he (Dhṛṣṭadyumna) did not know what to do, utterly bewildered in that supreme conflict.

sanskrit: तमब्रवीत्ततो यन्ता कच्चित्क्षेमं नु पार्षत ।ईदृशं व्यसनं युद्धे न ते दृष्टं कदाचन ॥५२॥
transliteration: tamabravīttato yantā kaccitkṣemaṁ nu pārṣata ,īdṛśaṁ vyasanaṁ yuddhe na te dṛṣṭaṁ kadācana.
english: Then the charioteer said to him (Dhṛṣṭadyumna), 'O Pārṣata, are you well? Surely, such a misfortune in battle has never been witnessed by you before.'
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 18 Verse 52. Then the charioteer said to him (Dhṛṣṭadyumna), 'O Pārṣata, are you well? Surely, such a misfortune in battle has never been witnessed by you before.'

sanskrit: दैवयोगात्तु ते बाणा नातरन्मर्मभेदिनः ।प्रेषिता द्विजमुख्येन मर्माण्युद्दिश्य सर्वशः ॥५३॥
transliteration: daivayogāttu te bāṇā nātaranmarmabhedinaḥ ,preṣitā dvijamukhyena marmāṇyuddiśya sarvaśaḥ.
english: But those arrows, capable of piercing vital spots and dispatched by the chief of Brahmins, aiming at all the vital points, did not penetrate, due to the arrangement of destiny.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 18 Verse 53. But those arrows, capable of piercing vital spots and dispatched by the chief of Brahmins, aiming at all the vital points, did not penetrate, due to the arrangement of destiny.

sanskrit: व्यावर्तये तत्र रथं नदीवेगमिवार्णवात् ।अवध्यं ब्राह्मणं मन्ये येन ते विक्रमो हतः ॥५४॥
transliteration: vyāvartaye tatra rathaṁ nadīvegamivārṇavāt ,avadhyaṁ brāhmaṇaṁ manye yena te vikramo hataḥ.
english: In that situation, I turn back the chariot, just as the ocean repels the force of a river. I consider a Brahmin to be invulnerable, by whom your valor was indeed destroyed.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 18 Verse 54. In that situation, I turn back the chariot, just as the ocean repels the force of a river. I consider a Brahmin to be invulnerable, by whom your valor was indeed destroyed.

sanskrit: धृष्टद्युम्नस्ततो राजञ्शनकैरब्रवीद्वचः ।मुह्यते मे मनस्तात गात्रे स्वेदश्च जायते ॥५५॥
transliteration: dhṛṣṭadyumnastato rājañśanakairabravīdvacaḥ ,muhyate me manastāta gātre svedaśca jāyate.
english: Then, O King, Dhṛṣṭadyumna slowly spoke these words: "My mind is bewildered, dear father, and sweat appears on my limbs."
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 18 Verse 55. Then, O King, Dhṛṣṭadyumna slowly spoke these words: "My mind is bewildered, dear father, and sweat appears on my limbs."

sanskrit: वेपथुं च शरीरे मे रोमहर्षं च पश्य वै ।वर्जयन्ब्राह्मणं युद्धे शनैर्याहि यतोऽच्युतः ॥५६॥
transliteration: vepathuṁ ca śarīre me romaharṣaṁ ca paśya vai ,varjayanbrāhmaṇaṁ yuddhe śanairyāhi yato'cyutaḥ.
english: See, indeed, the trembling and hair standing on end on my body. Avoid the Brahmin in battle and proceed slowly to where Acyuta (Kṛṣṇa) is.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 18 Verse 56. See, indeed, the trembling and hair standing on end on my body. Avoid the Brahmin in battle and proceed slowly to where Acyuta (Kṛṣṇa) is.

sanskrit: अर्जुनं भीमसेनं वा समरे प्राप्य सारथे ।क्षेममद्य भवेद्यन्तरिति मे नैष्ठिकी मतिः ॥५७॥
transliteration: arjunaṁ bhīmasenaṁ vā samare prāpya sārathe ,kṣemamadya bhavedyantariti me naiṣṭhikī matiḥ.
english: O charioteer, should one encounter Arjuna or Bhimasena in battle, the driver will be safe today - such is my firm conviction.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 18 Verse 57. O charioteer, should one encounter Arjuna or Bhimasena in battle, the driver will be safe today - such is my firm conviction.

sanskrit: ततः प्रायान्महाराज सारथिस्त्वरयन्हयान् ।यतो भीमो महेष्वासो युयुधे तव सैनिकैः ॥५८॥
transliteration: tataḥ prāyānmahārāja sārathistvarayanhayān ,yato bhīmo maheṣvāso yuyudhe tava sainikaiḥ.
english: Then, O great king, the charioteer set forth, urging on the horses, to where Bhima, the great bowman, was fighting with your soldiers.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 18 Verse 58. Then, O great king, the charioteer set forth, urging on the horses, to where Bhima, the great bowman, was fighting with your soldiers.

sanskrit: प्रद्रुतं तु रथं दृष्ट्वा धृष्टद्युम्नस्य मारिष ।किरञ्शरशतान्येव गौतमोऽनुययौ तदा ॥५९॥
transliteration: pradrutaṁ tu rathaṁ dṛṣṭvā dhṛṣṭadyumnasya māriṣa ,kirañśaraśatānyeva gautamo'nuyayau tadā.
english: But, O respected one, having seen Dhrishtadyumna's chariot in flight, Gautama (Kripacharya) then pursued, showering hundreds of arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 18 Verse 59. But, O respected one, having seen Dhrishtadyumna's chariot in flight, Gautama (Kripacharya) then pursued, showering hundreds of arrows.

sanskrit: शङ्खं च पूरयामास मुहुर्मुहुररिंदमः ।पार्षतं प्राद्रवद्यन्तं महेन्द्र इव शम्बरम् ॥६०॥
transliteration: śaṅkhaṁ ca pūrayāmāsa muhurmuhurariṁdamaḥ ,pārṣataṁ prādravadyantaṁ mahendra iva śambaram.
english: And the subjugator of enemies repeatedly blew his conch, pursuing Parshata (Dhrishtadyumna) as he went, just as Mahendra (Indra) pursued Shambara.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 18 Verse 60. And the subjugator of enemies repeatedly blew his conch, pursuing Parshata (Dhrishtadyumna) as he went, just as Mahendra (Indra) pursued Shambara.

sanskrit: शिखण्डिनं तु समरे भीष्ममृत्युं दुरासदम् ।हार्दिक्यो वारयामास स्मयन्निव मुहुर्मुहुः ॥६१॥
transliteration: śikhaṇḍinaṁ tu samare bhīṣmamṛtyuṁ durāsadam ,hārdikyo vārayāmāsa smayanniva muhurmuhuḥ.
english: Hardikya repeatedly restrained Shikhandin in battle, smiling as he did so, even though Shikhandin was formidable and destined to be the cause of Bhishma's death.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 18 Verse 61. Hardikya repeatedly restrained Shikhandin in battle, smiling as he did so, even though Shikhandin was formidable and destined to be the cause of Bhishma's death.

sanskrit: शिखण्डी च समासाद्य हृदिकानां महारथम् ।पञ्चभिर्निशितैर्भल्लैर्जत्रुदेशे समार्दयत् ॥६२॥
transliteration: śikhaṇḍī ca samāsādya hṛdikānāṁ mahāratham ,pañcabhirniśitairbhallairjatrudeśe samārdayat.
english: And Shikhandin, having come upon the great chariot-warrior of the Hṛdikas (Kṛtavarmā), wounded him in the collarbone area with five sharp arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 18 Verse 62. And Shikhandin, having come upon the great chariot-warrior of the Hṛdikas (Kṛtavarmā), wounded him in the collarbone area with five sharp arrows.

sanskrit: कृतवर्मा तु संक्रुद्धो भित्त्वा षष्टिभिराशुगैः ।धनुरेकेन चिच्छेद हसन्राजन्महारथः ॥६३॥
transliteration: kṛtavarmā tu saṁkruddho bhittvā ṣaṣṭibhirāśugaiḥ ,dhanurekena ciccheda hasanrājanmahārathaḥ.
english: "But the great chariot-warrior Kritavarma, O king, became greatly enraged. Having pierced (Shikhandin) with sixty swift arrows, he then, laughing, broke his (Shikhandin's) bow with a single arrow."
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 18 Verse 63. "But the great chariot-warrior Kritavarma, O king, became greatly enraged. Having pierced (Shikhandin) with sixty swift arrows, he then, laughing, broke his (Shikhandin's) bow with a single arrow."

sanskrit: अथान्यद्धनुरादाय द्रुपदस्यात्मजो बली ।तिष्ठ तिष्ठेति संक्रुद्धो हार्दिक्यं प्रत्यभाषत ॥६४॥
transliteration: athānyaddhanurādāya drupadasyātmajo balī ,tiṣṭha tiṣṭheti saṁkruddho hārdikyaṁ pratyabhāṣata.
english: Then the mighty son of Drupada (Shikhandin), greatly enraged, took up another bow and, saying "Stop! Stop!", addressed Hardikya (Kṛtavarmā).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 18 Verse 64. Then the mighty son of Drupada (Shikhandin), greatly enraged, took up another bow and, saying "Stop! Stop!", addressed Hardikya (Kṛtavarmā).

sanskrit: ततोऽस्य नवतिं बाणान्रुक्मपुङ्खान्सुतेजनान् ।प्रेषयामास राजेन्द्र तेऽस्याभ्रश्यन्त वर्मणः ॥६५॥
transliteration: tato'sya navatiṁ bāṇānrukmapuṅkhānsutejanān ,preṣayāmāsa rājendra te'syābhraśyanta varmaṇaḥ.
english: Then he dispatched ninety well-sharpened, gold-feathered arrows at him. O King, those arrows slipped off his armor.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 18 Verse 65. Then he dispatched ninety well-sharpened, gold-feathered arrows at him. O King, those arrows slipped off his armor.

sanskrit: वितथांस्तान्समालक्ष्य पतितांश्च महीतले ।क्षुरप्रेण सुतीक्ष्णेन कार्मुकं चिच्छिदे बली ॥६६॥
transliteration: vitathāṁstānsamālakṣya patitāṁśca mahītale ,kṣurapreṇa sutīkṣṇena kārmukaṁ cicchide balī.
english: Seeing those arrows fall futilely to the ground, the powerful warrior cut his (opponent's) bow with a very sharp, razor-headed arrow.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 18 Verse 66. Seeing those arrows fall futilely to the ground, the powerful warrior cut his (opponent's) bow with a very sharp, razor-headed arrow.

sanskrit: अथैनं छिन्नधन्वानं भग्नशृङ्गमिवर्षभम् ।अशीत्या मार्गणैः क्रुद्धो बाह्वोरुरसि चार्दयत् ॥६७॥
transliteration: athainaṁ chinnadhanvānaṁ bhagnaśṛṅgamivarṣabham ,aśītyā mārgaṇaiḥ kruddho bāhvorurasi cārdayat.
english: Then, enraged, he tormented him - whose bow was cut, like a bull with broken horns - wounding him with eighty arrows on his arms and chest.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 18 Verse 67. Then, enraged, he tormented him - whose bow was cut, like a bull with broken horns - wounding him with eighty arrows on his arms and chest.

sanskrit: कृतवर्मा तु संक्रुद्धो मार्गणैः कृतविक्षतः ।धनुरन्यत्समादाय समार्गणगणं प्रभो ।शिखण्डिनं बाणवरैः स्कन्धदेशेऽभ्यताडयत् ॥६८॥
transliteration: kṛtavarmā tu saṁkruddho mārgaṇaiḥ kṛtavikṣataḥ ,dhanuranyatsamādāya samārgaṇagaṇaṁ prabho ,śikhaṇḍinaṁ bāṇavaraiḥ skandhadeśe'bhyatāḍayat.
english: But Kritavarman, deeply enraged and wounded by arrows, O Lord, took up another bow with a fresh quiver of arrows and struck Shikhandin on the shoulder region with excellent arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 18 Verse 68. But Kritavarman, deeply enraged and wounded by arrows, O Lord, took up another bow with a fresh quiver of arrows and struck Shikhandin on the shoulder region with excellent arrows.

sanskrit: स्कन्धदेशे स्थितैर्बाणैः शिखण्डी च रराज ह ।शाखाप्रतानैर्विमलैः सुमहान्स यथा द्रुमः ॥६९॥
transliteration: skandhadeśe sthitairbāṇaiḥ śikhaṇḍī ca rarāja ha ,śākhāpratānairvimalaiḥ sumahānsa yathā drumaḥ.
english: Śikhaṇḍī, with arrows situated on his shoulder region, shone splendidly, just like a mighty tree with pure, widespread branches.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 18 Verse 69. Śikhaṇḍī, with arrows situated on his shoulder region, shone splendidly, just like a mighty tree with pure, widespread branches.

sanskrit: तावन्योन्यं भृशं विद्ध्वा रुधिरेण समुक्षितौ ।अन्योन्यशृङ्गाभिहतौ रेजतुर्वृषभाविव ॥७०॥
transliteration: tāvanyonyaṁ bhṛśaṁ viddhvā rudhireṇa samukṣitau ,anyonyaśṛṅgābhihatau rejaturvṛṣabhāviva.
english: Those two, having deeply pierced each other and become drenched with blood, shone like two bulls that have gored each other with their horns.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 18 Verse 70. Those two, having deeply pierced each other and become drenched with blood, shone like two bulls that have gored each other with their horns.

sanskrit: अन्योन्यस्य वधे यत्नं कुर्वाणौ तौ महारथौ ।रथाभ्यां चेरतुस्तत्र मण्डलानि सहस्रशः ॥७१॥
transliteration: anyonyasya vadhe yatnaṁ kurvāṇau tau mahārathau ,rathābhyāṁ ceratustatra maṇḍalāni sahasraśaḥ.
english: Those two great chariot warriors, striving to kill each other, moved their chariots in thousands of circular maneuvers there.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 18 Verse 71. Those two great chariot warriors, striving to kill each other, moved their chariots in thousands of circular maneuvers there.

sanskrit: कृतवर्मा महाराज पार्षतं निशितैः शरैः ।रणे विव्याध सप्तत्या स्वर्णपुङ्खैः शिलाशितैः ॥७२॥
transliteration: kṛtavarmā mahārāja pārṣataṁ niśitaiḥ śaraiḥ ,raṇe vivyādha saptatyā svarṇapuṅkhaiḥ śilāśitaiḥ.
english: O great king, Kṛtavarmā pierced Pārṣata in battle with seventy sharp arrows, which had golden shafts and were honed on stone.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 18 Verse 72. O great king, Kṛtavarmā pierced Pārṣata in battle with seventy sharp arrows, which had golden shafts and were honed on stone.

sanskrit: ततोऽस्य समरे बाणं भोजः प्रहरतां वरः ।जीवितान्तकरं घोरं व्यसृजत्त्वरयान्वितः ॥७३॥
transliteration: tato'sya samare bāṇaṁ bhojaḥ praharatāṁ varaḥ ,jīvitāntakaraṁ ghoraṁ vyasṛjattvarayānvitaḥ.
english: Then, Bhoja, that excellent warrior, swiftly released a dreadful, life-ending arrow at him in the battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 18 Verse 73. Then, Bhoja, that excellent warrior, swiftly released a dreadful, life-ending arrow at him in the battle.

sanskrit: स तेनाभिहतो राजन्मूर्छामाशु समाविशत् ।ध्वजयष्टिं च सहसा शिश्रिये कश्मलावृतः ॥७४॥
transliteration: sa tenābhihato rājanmūrchāmāśu samāviśat ,dhvajayaṣṭiṁ ca sahasā śiśriye kaśmalāvṛtaḥ.
english: O king, struck by that (arrow), he quickly fell unconscious. Overwhelmed by faintness, he suddenly leaned upon his flagpole.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 18 Verse 74. O king, struck by that (arrow), he quickly fell unconscious. Overwhelmed by faintness, he suddenly leaned upon his flagpole.

sanskrit: अपोवाह रणात्तं तु सारथी रथिनां वरम् ।हार्दिक्यशरसंतप्तं निःश्वसन्तं पुनः पुनः ॥७५॥
transliteration: apovāha raṇāttaṁ tu sārathī rathināṁ varam ,hārdikyaśarasaṁtaptaṁ niḥśvasantaṁ punaḥ punaḥ.
english: However, his charioteer carried him, that best of charioteers, away from the battle, as he was tormented by Hārdikya's arrows and repeatedly sighing.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 18 Verse 75. However, his charioteer carried him, that best of charioteers, away from the battle, as he was tormented by Hārdikya's arrows and repeatedly sighing.

sanskrit: पराजिते ततः शूरे द्रुपदस्य सुते प्रभो ।प्राद्रवत्पाण्डवी सेना वध्यमाना समन्ततः ॥७६॥
transliteration: parājite tataḥ śūre drupadasya sute prabho ,prādravatpāṇḍavī senā vadhyamānā samantataḥ.
english: O lord, after the brave son of Drupada (Dhṛṣṭadyumna) was defeated, the Pāṇḍava army then fled, being slaughtered from all sides.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 18 Verse 76. O lord, after the brave son of Drupada (Dhṛṣṭadyumna) was defeated, the Pāṇḍava army then fled, being slaughtered from all sides.

sanskrit: संजय उवाच ।श्वेताश्वोऽपि महाराज व्यधमत्तावकं बलम् ।यथा वायुः समासाद्य तूलराशिं समन्ततः ॥१॥
transliteration: saṁjaya uvāca ,śvetāśvo'pi mahārāja vyadhamattāvakaṁ balam ,yathā vāyuḥ samāsādya tūlarāśiṁ samantataḥ.
english: Saṃjaya said: "O great king, the white-horsed Arjuna also scattered your army, just as the wind, having encountered a heap of cotton, disperses it in all directions."
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 19 Verse 1. Saṃjaya said: "O great king, the white-horsed Arjuna also scattered your army, just as the wind, having encountered a heap of cotton, disperses it in all directions."

sanskrit: प्रत्युद्ययुस्त्रिगर्तास्तं शिबयः कौरवैः सह ।शाल्वाः संशप्तकाश्चैव नारायणबलं च यत् ॥२॥
transliteration: pratyudyayustrigartāstaṁ śibayaḥ kauravaiḥ saha ,śālvāḥ saṁśaptakāścaiva nārāyaṇabalaṁ ca yat.
english: The Trigartas, Sibis along with the Kauravas, and also the Salvas and Saṃśaptakas, as well as the Narayana army which was there, advanced against him.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 19 Verse 2. The Trigartas, Sibis along with the Kauravas, and also the Salvas and Saṃśaptakas, as well as the Narayana army which was there, advanced against him.

sanskrit: सत्यसेनः सत्यकीर्तिर्मित्रदेवः श्रुतंजयः ।सौश्रुतिश्चित्रसेनश्च मित्रवर्मा च भारत ॥३॥
transliteration: satyasenaḥ satyakīrtirmitradevaḥ śrutaṁjayaḥ ,sauśrutiścitrasenaśca mitravarmā ca bhārata.
english: Satyasena, Satyakīrti, Mitradeva, Śrutanjaya, Sauśruti, Citrasena, and Mitravarmā – these, O Bhārata.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 19 Verse 3. Satyasena, Satyakīrti, Mitradeva, Śrutanjaya, Sauśruti, Citrasena, and Mitravarmā – these, O Bhārata.

sanskrit: त्रिगर्तराजः समरे भ्रातृभिः परिवारितः ।पुत्रैश्चैव महेष्वासैर्नानाशस्त्रधरैर्युधि ॥४॥
transliteration: trigartarājaḥ samare bhrātṛbhiḥ parivāritaḥ ,putraiścaiva maheṣvāsairnānāśastradharairyudhi.
english: The king of the Trigartas, surrounded in battle by his brothers and by his sons who were great archers and wielded various weapons, [also advanced].
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 19 Verse 4. The king of the Trigartas, surrounded in battle by his brothers and by his sons who were great archers and wielded various weapons, [also advanced].

sanskrit: ते सृजन्तः शरव्रातान्किरन्तोऽर्जुनमाहवे ।अभ्यद्रवन्त समरे वार्योघा इव सागरम् ॥५॥
transliteration: te sṛjantaḥ śaravrātānkiranto'rjunamāhave ,abhyadravanta samare vāryoghā iva sāgaram.
english: In battle, those warriors, releasing hosts of arrows and showering them upon Arjuna, rushed forward like torrents of water pouring into the ocean.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 19 Verse 5. In battle, those warriors, releasing hosts of arrows and showering them upon Arjuna, rushed forward like torrents of water pouring into the ocean.

sanskrit: ते त्वर्जुनं समासाद्य योधाः शतसहस्रशः ।अगच्छन्विलयं सर्वे तार्क्ष्यं दृष्ट्वेव पन्नगाः ॥६॥
transliteration: te tvarjunaṁ samāsādya yodhāḥ śatasahasraśaḥ ,agacchanvilayaṁ sarve tārkṣyaṁ dṛṣṭveva pannagāḥ.
english: But those warriors, hundreds of thousands of them, after approaching Arjuna, all met their demise, just as serpents vanish upon seeing Garuḍa.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 19 Verse 6. But those warriors, hundreds of thousands of them, after approaching Arjuna, all met their demise, just as serpents vanish upon seeing Garuḍa.

sanskrit: ते वध्यमानाः समरे नाजहुः पाण्डवं तदा ।दह्यमाना यथा राजञ्शलभा इव पावकम् ॥७॥
transliteration: te vadhyamānāḥ samare nājahuḥ pāṇḍavaṁ tadā ,dahyamānā yathā rājañśalabhā iva pāvakam.
english: O King, though they were being killed in battle, they did not abandon the son of Pāṇḍu then, just as moths, even as they are being consumed, do not abandon the fire.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 19 Verse 7. O King, though they were being killed in battle, they did not abandon the son of Pāṇḍu then, just as moths, even as they are being consumed, do not abandon the fire.

sanskrit: सत्यसेनस्त्रिभिर्बाणैर्विव्याध युधि पाण्डवम् ।मित्रदेवस्त्रिषष्ट्या च चन्द्रदेवश्च सप्तभिः ॥८॥
transliteration: satyasenastribhirbāṇairvivyādha yudhi pāṇḍavam ,mitradevastriṣaṣṭyā ca candradevaśca saptabhiḥ.
english: Satyaseṇa pierced the son of Pāṇḍu in battle with three arrows. Mitradeva also [pierced him] with sixty-three, and Chandradeva with seven.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 19 Verse 8. Satyaseṇa pierced the son of Pāṇḍu in battle with three arrows. Mitradeva also [pierced him] with sixty-three, and Chandradeva with seven.

sanskrit: मित्रवर्मा त्रिसप्तत्या सौश्रुतिश्चापि पञ्चभिः ।शत्रुंजयश्च विंशत्या सुशर्मा नवभिः शरैः ॥९॥
transliteration: mitravarmā trisaptatyā sauśrutiścāpi pañcabhiḥ ,śatruṁjayaśca viṁśatyā suśarmā navabhiḥ śaraiḥ.
english: Mitravarmā (struck) with seventy-three arrows, Sauśruti with five, Śatruñjaya with twenty, and Suśarmā with nine arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 19 Verse 9. Mitravarmā (struck) with seventy-three arrows, Sauśruti with five, Śatruñjaya with twenty, and Suśarmā with nine arrows.

sanskrit: शत्रुंजयं च राजानं हत्वा तत्र शिलाशितैः ।सौश्रुतेः सशिरस्त्राणं शिरः कायादपाहरत् ।त्वरितश्चन्द्रदेवं च शरैर्निन्ये यमक्षयम् ॥१०॥
transliteration: śatruṁjayaṁ ca rājānaṁ hatvā tatra śilāśitaiḥ ,sauśruteḥ saśirastrāṇaṁ śiraḥ kāyādapāharat ,tvaritaścandradevaṁ ca śarairninye yamakṣayam.
english: Having killed King Śatruñjaya there with stone-sharpened arrows, he severed Sauśruti's head, complete with its helmet, from his body. Quickly, with arrows, he also sent Candradeva to the abode of Yama.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 19 Verse 10. Having killed King Śatruñjaya there with stone-sharpened arrows, he severed Sauśruti's head, complete with its helmet, from his body. Quickly, with arrows, he also sent Candradeva to the abode of Yama.

sanskrit: अथेतरान्महाराज यतमानान्महारथान् ।पञ्चभिः पञ्चभिर्बाणैरेकैकं प्रत्यवारयत् ॥११॥
transliteration: athetarānmahārāja yatamānānmahārathān ,pañcabhiḥ pañcabhirbāṇairekaikaṁ pratyavārayat.
english: Then, O great king, he warded off those other great warriors (mahāratha) who were striving, striking each one with five arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 19 Verse 11. Then, O great king, he warded off those other great warriors (mahāratha) who were striving, striking each one with five arrows.

sanskrit: सत्यसेनस्तु संक्रुद्धस्तोमरं व्यसृजन्महत् ।समुद्दिश्य रणे कृष्णं सिंहनादं ननाद च ॥१२॥
transliteration: satyasenastu saṁkruddhastomaraṁ vyasṛjanmahat ,samuddiśya raṇe kṛṣṇaṁ siṁhanādaṁ nanāda ca.
english: But Satyaseṇa, greatly enraged, hurled a mighty javelin, aiming it at Krishna in battle, and roared a lion's roar.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 19 Verse 12. But Satyaseṇa, greatly enraged, hurled a mighty javelin, aiming it at Krishna in battle, and roared a lion's roar.

sanskrit: स निर्भिद्य भुजं सव्यं माधवस्य महात्मनः ।अयस्मयो महाचण्डो जगाम धरणीं तदा ॥१३॥
transliteration: sa nirbhidya bhujaṁ savyaṁ mādhavasya mahātmanaḥ ,ayasmayo mahācaṇḍo jagāma dharaṇīṁ tadā.
english: Then, that very fierce, iron weapon, having pierced the left arm of the great-souled Mādhava (Kṛṣṇa), fell to the earth.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 19 Verse 13. Then, that very fierce, iron weapon, having pierced the left arm of the great-souled Mādhava (Kṛṣṇa), fell to the earth.

sanskrit: माधवस्य तु विद्धस्य तोमरेण महारणे ।प्रतोदः प्रापतद्धस्ताद्रश्मयश्च विशां पते ॥१४॥
transliteration: mādhavasya tu viddhasya tomareṇa mahāraṇe ,pratodaḥ prāpataddhastādraśmayaśca viśāṁ pate.
english: But for Mādhava (Kṛṣṇa), who was wounded by the spear in the great battle, the goad and the reins fell from his hand, O lord of the people.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 19 Verse 14. But for Mādhava (Kṛṣṇa), who was wounded by the spear in the great battle, the goad and the reins fell from his hand, O lord of the people.

sanskrit: स प्रतोदं पुनर्गृह्य रश्मींश्चैव महायशाः ।वाहयामास तानश्वान्सत्यसेनरथं प्रति ॥१५॥
transliteration: sa pratodaṁ punargṛhya raśmīṁścaiva mahāyaśāḥ ,vāhayāmāsa tānaśvānsatyasenarathaṁ prati.
english: The greatly renowned one, having again taken the goad and the reins, drove those horses toward Satyaseana's chariot.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 19 Verse 15. The greatly renowned one, having again taken the goad and the reins, drove those horses toward Satyaseana's chariot.

sanskrit: विष्वक्सेनं तु निर्भिन्नं प्रेक्ष्य पार्थो धनंजयः ।सत्यसेनं शरैस्तीक्ष्णैर्दारयित्वा महाबलः ॥१६॥
transliteration: viṣvaksenaṁ tu nirbhinnaṁ prekṣya pārtho dhanaṁjayaḥ ,satyasenaṁ śaraistīkṣṇairdārayitvā mahābalaḥ.
english: But the mighty Dhanañjaya (Arjuna), having seen Viṣvaksena pierced, and having shattered Satyaseana with sharp arrows,
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 19 Verse 16. But the mighty Dhanañjaya (Arjuna), having seen Viṣvaksena pierced, and having shattered Satyaseana with sharp arrows,

sanskrit: ततः सुनिशितैर्बाणै राज्ञस्तस्य महच्छिरः ।कुण्डलोपचितं कायाच्चकर्त पृतनान्तरे ॥१७॥
transliteration: tataḥ suniśitairbāṇai rājñastasya mahacchiraḥ ,kuṇḍalopacitaṁ kāyāccakarta pṛtanāntare.
english: Then, with very sharp arrows, he severed the great head of that king, adorned with earrings, from his body in the midst of the army.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 19 Verse 17. Then, with very sharp arrows, he severed the great head of that king, adorned with earrings, from his body in the midst of the army.

sanskrit: तं निहत्य शितैर्बाणैर्मित्रवर्माणमाक्षिपत् ।वत्सदन्तेन तीक्ष्णेन सारथिं चास्य मारिष ॥१८॥
transliteration: taṁ nihatya śitairbāṇairmitravarmāṇamākṣipat ,vatsadantena tīkṣṇena sārathiṁ cāsya māriṣa.
english: O respected one (māriṣa), having killed him with sharp arrows, he struck down Mitravarman; and he also killed his charioteer with a sharp arrow called Vatsadanta.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 19 Verse 18. O respected one (māriṣa), having killed him with sharp arrows, he struck down Mitravarman; and he also killed his charioteer with a sharp arrow called Vatsadanta.

sanskrit: ततः शरशतैर्भूयः संशप्तकगणान्वशी ।पातयामास संक्रुद्धः शतशोऽथ सहस्रशः ॥१९॥
transliteration: tataḥ śaraśatairbhūyaḥ saṁśaptakagaṇānvaśī ,pātayāmāsa saṁkruddhaḥ śataśo'tha sahasraśaḥ.
english: Then, the greatly enraged master (vaśī) once more caused the legions of Saṃśaptakas to fall by hundreds and thousands with hundreds of arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 19 Verse 19. Then, the greatly enraged master (vaśī) once more caused the legions of Saṃśaptakas to fall by hundreds and thousands with hundreds of arrows.

sanskrit: ततो रजतपुङ्खेन राज्ञः शीर्षं महात्मनः ।मित्रदेवस्य चिच्छेद क्षुरप्रेण महायशाः ।सुशर्माणं च संक्रुद्धो जत्रुदेशे समार्दयत् ॥२०॥
transliteration: tato rajatapuṅkhena rājñaḥ śīrṣaṁ mahātmanaḥ ,mitradevasya ciccheda kṣurapreṇa mahāyaśāḥ ,suśarmāṇaṁ ca saṁkruddho jatrudeśe samārdayat.
english: Then, the greatly glorious one (mahāyaśāḥ), greatly enraged, severed the head of the great-souled King Mitradeva with a razor-sharp arrow (kṣurapreṇa) that had a silver shaft (rajata-puṅkhena); and he also crushed Suśarman in the region of the collarbone.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 19 Verse 20. Then, the greatly glorious one (mahāyaśāḥ), greatly enraged, severed the head of the great-souled King Mitradeva with a razor-sharp arrow (kṣurapreṇa) that had a silver shaft (rajata-puṅkhena); and he also crushed Suśarman in the region of the collarbone.

sanskrit: ततः संशप्तकाः सर्वे परिवार्य धनंजयम् ।शस्त्रौघैर्ममृदुः क्रुद्धा नादयन्तो दिशो दश ॥२१॥
transliteration: tataḥ saṁśaptakāḥ sarve parivārya dhanaṁjayam ,śastraughairmamṛduḥ kruddhā nādayanto diśo daśa.
english: Then, all the enraged Saṃśaptakas, surrounding Dhananjaya (Arjuna), assailed him with volleys of weapons, causing the ten directions to resound.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 19 Verse 21. Then, all the enraged Saṃśaptakas, surrounding Dhananjaya (Arjuna), assailed him with volleys of weapons, causing the ten directions to resound.

sanskrit: अभ्यर्दितस्तु तैर्जिष्णुः शक्रतुल्यपराक्रमः ।ऐन्द्रमस्त्रममेयात्मा प्रादुश्चक्रे महारथः ।ततः शरसहस्राणि प्रादुरासन्विशां पते ॥२२॥
transliteration: abhyarditastu tairjiṣṇuḥ śakratulyaparākramaḥ ,aindramastramameyātmā prāduścakre mahārathaḥ ,tataḥ śarasahasrāṇi prādurāsanviśāṁ pate.
english: But Jishnu (Arjuna), a great warrior (mahāratha) of immeasurable spirit (ātman) and valor equal to Indra, though assailed by them, manifested the weapon (astra) of Indra. Then, O lord of the people, thousands of arrows appeared.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 19 Verse 22. But Jishnu (Arjuna), a great warrior (mahāratha) of immeasurable spirit (ātman) and valor equal to Indra, though assailed by them, manifested the weapon (astra) of Indra. Then, O lord of the people, thousands of arrows appeared.

sanskrit: ध्वजानां छिद्यमानानां कार्मुकाणां च संयुगे ।रथानां सपताकानां तूणीराणां शरैः सह ॥२३॥
transliteration: dhvajānāṁ chidyamānānāṁ kārmukāṇāṁ ca saṁyuge ,rathānāṁ sapatākānāṁ tūṇīrāṇāṁ śaraiḥ saha.
english: In battle, [there was the destruction] of banners being severed, and of bows, of chariots with their flags, and of quivers along with their arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 19 Verse 23. In battle, [there was the destruction] of banners being severed, and of bows, of chariots with their flags, and of quivers along with their arrows.

sanskrit: अक्षाणामथ योक्त्राणां चक्राणां रश्मिभिः सह ।कूबराणां वरूथानां पृषत्कानां च संयुगे ॥२४॥
transliteration: akṣāṇāmatha yoktrāṇāṁ cakrāṇāṁ raśmibhiḥ saha ,kūbarāṇāṁ varūthānāṁ pṛṣatkānāṁ ca saṁyuge.
english: Moreover, in battle, [there was the destruction] of axles, and of harnesses, of wheels along with reins, of chariot poles, of protective coverings (varūtha), and of arrows (pṛṣatka).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 19 Verse 24. Moreover, in battle, [there was the destruction] of axles, and of harnesses, of wheels along with reins, of chariot poles, of protective coverings (varūtha), and of arrows (pṛṣatka).

sanskrit: अश्मनां पततां चैव प्रासानामृष्टिभिः सह ।गदानां परिघाणां च शक्तीनां तोमरैः सह ॥२५॥
transliteration: aśmanāṁ patatāṁ caiva prāsānāmṛṣṭibhiḥ saha ,gadānāṁ parighāṇāṁ ca śaktīnāṁ tomaraiḥ saha.
english: Of falling stones, and of spears along with javelins; of maces, iron clubs, and lances along with darts.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 19 Verse 25. Of falling stones, and of spears along with javelins; of maces, iron clubs, and lances along with darts.

sanskrit: शतघ्नीनां सचक्राणां भुजानामूरुभिः सह ।कण्ठसूत्राङ्गदानां च केयूराणां च मारिष ॥२६॥
transliteration: śataghnīnāṁ sacakrāṇāṁ bhujānāmūrubhiḥ saha ,kaṇṭhasūtrāṅgadānāṁ ca keyūrāṇāṁ ca māriṣa.
english: Of "śataghnīs" (a weapon) with their wheels; of arms along with thighs; and of necklaces, armlets, and bracelets, O respected one.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 19 Verse 26. Of "śataghnīs" (a weapon) with their wheels; of arms along with thighs; and of necklaces, armlets, and bracelets, O respected one.

sanskrit: हाराणामथ निष्काणां तनुत्राणां च भारत ।छत्राणां व्यजनानां च शिरसां मुकुटैः सह ।अश्रूयत महाञ्शब्दस्तत्र तत्र विशां पते ॥२७॥
transliteration: hārāṇāmatha niṣkāṇāṁ tanutrāṇāṁ ca bhārata ,chatrāṇāṁ vyajanānāṁ ca śirasāṁ mukuṭaiḥ saha ,aśrūyata mahāñśabdastatra tatra viśāṁ pate.
english: Of necklaces, gold ornaments, and armors, O descendant of Bharata; of parasols and fans, and of heads along with crowns. A great sound was heard here and there, O lord of the people.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 19 Verse 27. Of necklaces, gold ornaments, and armors, O descendant of Bharata; of parasols and fans, and of heads along with crowns. A great sound was heard here and there, O lord of the people.

sanskrit: सकुण्डलानि स्वक्षीणि पूर्णचन्द्रनिभानि च ।शिरांस्युर्व्यामदृश्यन्त तारागण इवाम्बरे ॥२८॥
transliteration: sakuṇḍalāni svakṣīṇi pūrṇacandranibhāni ca ,śirāṁsyurvyāmadṛśyanta tārāgaṇa ivāmbare.
english: And heads, adorned with earrings, possessing beautiful eyes, and resembling full moons, were seen upon the earth, like a cluster of stars in the sky.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 19 Verse 28. And heads, adorned with earrings, possessing beautiful eyes, and resembling full moons, were seen upon the earth, like a cluster of stars in the sky.

sanskrit: सुस्रग्वीणि सुवासांसि चन्दनेनोक्षितानि च ।शरीराणि व्यदृश्यन्त हतानां च महीतले ।गन्धर्वनगराकारं घोरमायोधनं तदा ॥२९॥
transliteration: susragvīṇi suvāsāṁsi candanenokṣitāni ca ,śarīrāṇi vyadṛśyanta hatānāṁ ca mahītale ,gandharvanagarākāraṁ ghoramāyodhanaṁ tadā.
english: The bodies of the slain, adorned with beautiful garlands and fine garments, and sprinkled with sandalwood paste, were seen on the ground. At that time, the dreadful battlefield resembled a city of Gandharvas.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 19 Verse 29. The bodies of the slain, adorned with beautiful garlands and fine garments, and sprinkled with sandalwood paste, were seen on the ground. At that time, the dreadful battlefield resembled a city of Gandharvas.

sanskrit: निहतै राजपुत्रैश्च क्षत्रियैश्च महाबलैः ।हस्तिभिः पतितैश्चैव तुरगैश्चाभवन्मही ।अगम्यमार्गा समरे विशीर्णैरिव पर्वतैः ॥३०॥
transliteration: nihatai rājaputraiśca kṣatriyaiśca mahābalaiḥ ,hastibhiḥ patitaiścaiva turagaiścābhavanmahī ,agamyamārgā samare viśīrṇairiva parvataiḥ.
english: The ground became impassable in battle, due to the slain princes and mighty kṣatriyas, and also due to fallen elephants and horses, resembling scattered mountains.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 19 Verse 30. The ground became impassable in battle, due to the slain princes and mighty kṣatriyas, and also due to fallen elephants and horses, resembling scattered mountains.

sanskrit: नासीच्चक्रपथश्चैव पाण्डवस्य महात्मनः ।निघ्नतः शात्रवान्भल्लैर्हस्त्यश्वं चामितं महत् ॥३१॥
transliteration: nāsīccakrapathaścaiva pāṇḍavasya mahātmanaḥ ,nighnataḥ śātravānbhallairhastyaśvaṁ cāmitaṁ mahat.
english: For the great-souled (mahātman) Pāṇḍava, who was slaying enemies with arrows, there was neither a path for his chariot wheels, nor (was there a path through) the immense, great mass of elephants and horses.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 19 Verse 31. For the great-souled (mahātman) Pāṇḍava, who was slaying enemies with arrows, there was neither a path for his chariot wheels, nor (was there a path through) the immense, great mass of elephants and horses.

sanskrit: आ तुम्बादवसीदन्ति रथचक्राणि मारिष ।रणे विचरतस्तस्य तस्मिँल्लोहितकर्दमे ॥३२॥
transliteration: ā tumbādavasīdanti rathacakrāṇi māriṣa ,raṇe vicaratastasya tasmiँllohitakardame.
english: O respected one (māriṣa), the chariot wheels of him (Pāṇḍava) who was moving about in that blood-mud (lohita-kardame) in battle sank up to their hubs (tumbāt).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 19 Verse 32. O respected one (māriṣa), the chariot wheels of him (Pāṇḍava) who was moving about in that blood-mud (lohita-kardame) in battle sank up to their hubs (tumbāt).

sanskrit: सीदमानानि चक्राणि समूहुस्तुरगा भृशम् ।श्रमेण महता युक्ता मनोमारुतरंहसः ॥३३॥
transliteration: sīdamānāni cakrāṇi samūhusturagā bhṛśam ,śrameṇa mahatā yuktā manomārutaraṁhasaḥ.
english: Though afflicted by great fatigue, the horses, possessing the swiftness of mind and wind, pulled exceedingly hard at the sinking wheels.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 19 Verse 33. Though afflicted by great fatigue, the horses, possessing the swiftness of mind and wind, pulled exceedingly hard at the sinking wheels.

sanskrit: वध्यमानं तु तत्सैन्यं पाण्डुपुत्रेण धन्विना ।प्रायशो विमुखं सर्वं नावतिष्ठत संयुगे ॥३४॥
transliteration: vadhyamānaṁ tu tatsainyaṁ pāṇḍuputreṇa dhanvinā ,prāyaśo vimukhaṁ sarvaṁ nāvatiṣṭhata saṁyuge.
english: Indeed, that entire army, being struck down by Arjuna, the son of Pāṇḍu and a skilled archer, mostly turned away and did not stand firm in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 19 Verse 34. Indeed, that entire army, being struck down by Arjuna, the son of Pāṇḍu and a skilled archer, mostly turned away and did not stand firm in battle.

sanskrit: ताञ्जित्वा समरे जिष्णुः संशप्तकगणान्बहून् ।रराज स महाराज विधूमोऽग्निरिव ज्वलन् ॥३५॥
transliteration: tāñjitvā samare jiṣṇuḥ saṁśaptakagaṇānbahūn ,rarāja sa mahārāja vidhūmo'gniriva jvalan.
english: O great king, having conquered those many groups of Saṃśaptakas in battle, the Victorious (Jiṣṇuḥ) Arjuna shone like a blazing, smokeless fire.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 19 Verse 35. O great king, having conquered those many groups of Saṃśaptakas in battle, the Victorious (Jiṣṇuḥ) Arjuna shone like a blazing, smokeless fire.

sanskrit: युधिष्ठिरं महाराज विसृजन्तं शरान्बहून् ।स्वयं दुर्योधनो राजा प्रत्यगृह्णादभीतवत् ॥३६॥
transliteration: yudhiṣṭhiraṁ mahārāja visṛjantaṁ śarānbahūn ,svayaṁ duryodhano rājā pratyagṛhṇādabhītavat.
english: O great king, King Duryodhana himself fearlessly countered Yudhiṣṭhira, as he was releasing many arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 19 Verse 36. O great king, King Duryodhana himself fearlessly countered Yudhiṣṭhira, as he was releasing many arrows.

sanskrit: तमापतन्तं सहसा तव पुत्रं महाबलम् ।धर्मराजो द्रुतं विद्ध्वा तिष्ठ तिष्ठेति चाब्रवीत् ॥३७॥
transliteration: tamāpatantaṁ sahasā tava putraṁ mahābalam ,dharmarājo drutaṁ viddhvā tiṣṭha tiṣṭheti cābravīt.
english: King Yudhiṣṭhira (Dharmarāja), having quickly struck your mighty son who was swiftly rushing towards him, exclaimed, "Stop! Stop!"
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 19 Verse 37. King Yudhiṣṭhira (Dharmarāja), having quickly struck your mighty son who was swiftly rushing towards him, exclaimed, "Stop! Stop!"

sanskrit: स च तं प्रतिविव्याध नवभिर्निशितैः शरैः ।सारथिं चास्य भल्लेन भृशं क्रुद्धोऽभ्यताडयत् ॥३८॥
transliteration: sa ca taṁ prativivyādha navabhirniśitaiḥ śaraiḥ ,sārathiṁ cāsya bhallena bhṛśaṁ kruddho'bhyatāḍayat.
english: And he (Duryodhana) struck him (Yudhiṣṭhira) back with nine sharp arrows. Greatly enraged, he also forcefully struck Yudhiṣṭhira's charioteer with a broad-headed arrow.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 19 Verse 38. And he (Duryodhana) struck him (Yudhiṣṭhira) back with nine sharp arrows. Greatly enraged, he also forcefully struck Yudhiṣṭhira's charioteer with a broad-headed arrow.

sanskrit: ततो युधिष्ठिरो राजा हेमपुङ्खाञ्शिलीमुखान् ।दुर्योधनाय चिक्षेप त्रयोदश शिलाशितान् ॥३९॥
transliteration: tato yudhiṣṭhiro rājā hemapuṅkhāñśilīmukhān ,duryodhanāya cikṣepa trayodaśa śilāśitān.
english: Then King Yudhiṣṭhira shot thirteen arrows, with golden shafts and sharpened on stone, at Duryodhana.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 19 Verse 39. Then King Yudhiṣṭhira shot thirteen arrows, with golden shafts and sharpened on stone, at Duryodhana.

sanskrit: चतुर्भिश्चतुरो वाहांस्तस्य हत्वा महारथः ।पञ्चमेन शिरः कायात्सारथेस्तु समाक्षिपत् ॥४०॥
transliteration: caturbhiścaturo vāhāṁstasya hatvā mahārathaḥ ,pañcamena śiraḥ kāyātsārathestu samākṣipat.
english: The great charioteer (Yudhiṣṭhira), having killed Duryodhana's four horses with four (arrows), then struck off the head of his (Duryodhana's) charioteer from his body with the fifth (arrow).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 19 Verse 40. The great charioteer (Yudhiṣṭhira), having killed Duryodhana's four horses with four (arrows), then struck off the head of his (Duryodhana's) charioteer from his body with the fifth (arrow).

sanskrit: षष्ठेन च ध्वजं राज्ञः सप्तमेन च कार्मुकम् ।अष्टमेन तथा खड्गं पातयामास भूतले ।पञ्चभिर्नृपतिं चापि धर्मराजोऽर्दयद्भृशम् ॥४१॥
transliteration: ṣaṣṭhena ca dhvajaṁ rājñaḥ saptamena ca kārmukam ,aṣṭamena tathā khaḍgaṁ pātayāmāsa bhūtale ,pañcabhirnṛpatiṁ cāpi dharmarājo'rdayadbhṛśam.
english: With the sixth (arrow), Yudhiṣṭhira (dharma-rāja) struck down the king's banner, and with the seventh, his bow. Similarly, with the eighth (arrow), he caused his sword to fall to the ground. Yudhiṣṭhira also severely wounded the king with five (arrows).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 19 Verse 41. With the sixth (arrow), Yudhiṣṭhira (dharma-rāja) struck down the king's banner, and with the seventh, his bow. Similarly, with the eighth (arrow), he caused his sword to fall to the ground. Yudhiṣṭhira also severely wounded the king with five (arrows).

sanskrit: हताश्वात्तु रथात्तस्मादवप्लुत्य सुतस्तव ।उत्तमं व्यसनं प्राप्तो भूमावेव व्यतिष्ठत ॥४२॥
transliteration: hatāśvāttu rathāttasmādavaplutya sutastava ,uttamaṁ vyasanaṁ prāpto bhūmāveva vyatiṣṭhata.
english: But your son, having jumped down from that chariot whose horses had been killed, having met with extreme misfortune, remained standing right on the ground.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 19 Verse 42. But your son, having jumped down from that chariot whose horses had been killed, having met with extreme misfortune, remained standing right on the ground.

sanskrit: तं तु कृच्छ्रगतं दृष्ट्वा कर्णद्रौणिकृपादयः ।अभ्यवर्तन्त सहिताः परीप्सन्तो नराधिपम् ॥४३॥
transliteration: taṁ tu kṛcchragataṁ dṛṣṭvā karṇadrauṇikṛpādayaḥ ,abhyavartanta sahitāḥ parīpsanto narādhipam.
english: But seeing him fallen into great difficulty, Karṇa, Droṇa's son (Aśvatthāmā), Kṛpa, and others advanced together, desiring to protect the king.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 19 Verse 43. But seeing him fallen into great difficulty, Karṇa, Droṇa's son (Aśvatthāmā), Kṛpa, and others advanced together, desiring to protect the king.

sanskrit: अथ पाण्डुसुताः सर्वे परिवार्य युधिष्ठिरम् ।अभ्ययुः समरे राजंस्ततो युद्धमवर्तत ॥४४॥
transliteration: atha pāṇḍusutāḥ sarve parivārya yudhiṣṭhiram ,abhyayuḥ samare rājaṁstato yuddhamavartata.
english: Then, O King, all the sons of Pāṇḍu, having surrounded Yudhiṣṭhira, advanced into battle. Consequently, a combat (yuddha) ensued.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 19 Verse 44. Then, O King, all the sons of Pāṇḍu, having surrounded Yudhiṣṭhira, advanced into battle. Consequently, a combat (yuddha) ensued.

sanskrit: अथ तूर्यसहस्राणि प्रावाद्यन्त महामृधे ।क्ष्वेडाः किलकिलाशब्दाः प्रादुरासन्महीपते ।यदभ्यगच्छन्समरे पाञ्चालाः कौरवैः सह ॥४५॥
transliteration: atha tūryasahasrāṇi prāvādyanta mahāmṛdhe ,kṣveḍāḥ kilakilāśabdāḥ prādurāsanmahīpate ,yadabhyagacchansamare pāñcālāḥ kauravaiḥ saha.
english: O King, then thousands of musical instruments were loudly played in the great battle. Roars and tumultuous cries arose when the Pāñcālas engaged the Kauravas in combat.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 19 Verse 45. O King, then thousands of musical instruments were loudly played in the great battle. Roars and tumultuous cries arose when the Pāñcālas engaged the Kauravas in combat.

sanskrit: नरा नरैः समाजग्मुर्वारणा वरवारणैः ।रथाश्च रथिभिः सार्धं हयाश्च हयसादिभिः ॥४६॥
transliteration: narā naraiḥ samājagmurvāraṇā varavāraṇaiḥ ,rathāśca rathibhiḥ sārdhaṁ hayāśca hayasādibhiḥ.
english: Men fought with men, elephants with excellent elephants; and chariots with charioteers, and horses with horsemen.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 19 Verse 46. Men fought with men, elephants with excellent elephants; and chariots with charioteers, and horses with horsemen.

sanskrit: द्वंद्वान्यासन्महाराज प्रेक्षणीयानि संयुगे ।विस्मापनान्यचिन्त्यानि शस्त्रवन्त्युत्तमानि च ॥४७॥
transliteration: dvaṁdvānyāsanmahārāja prekṣaṇīyāni saṁyuge ,vismāpanānyacintyāni śastravantyuttamāni ca.
english: O great King, the duels that took place in that battle were spectacular, astonishing, unimaginable, and supreme, with combatants wielding weapons.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 19 Verse 47. O great King, the duels that took place in that battle were spectacular, astonishing, unimaginable, and supreme, with combatants wielding weapons.

sanskrit: अयुध्यन्त महावेगाः परस्परवधैषिणः ।अन्योन्यं समरे जघ्नुर्योधव्रतमनुष्ठिताः ।न हि ते समरं चक्रुः पृष्ठतो वै कथंचन ॥४८॥
transliteration: ayudhyanta mahāvegāḥ parasparavadhaiṣiṇaḥ ,anyonyaṁ samare jaghnuryodhavratamanuṣṭhitāḥ ,na hi te samaraṁ cakruḥ pṛṣṭhato vai kathaṁcana.
english: Those of great impetuosity, desiring each other's death, fought. They killed one another in battle, observing the warrior's code. Indeed, they never fought from behind by any means.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 19 Verse 48. Those of great impetuosity, desiring each other's death, fought. They killed one another in battle, observing the warrior's code. Indeed, they never fought from behind by any means.

sanskrit: मुहूर्तमेव तद्युद्धमासीन्मधुरदर्शनम् ।तत उन्मत्तवद्राजन्निर्मर्यादमवर्तत ॥४९॥
transliteration: muhūrtameva tadyuddhamāsīnmadhuradarśanam ,tata unmattavadrājannirmaryādamavartata.
english: For just a moment, that battle was a beautiful sight. Then, O King, it became unrestrained, raging wildly.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 19 Verse 49. For just a moment, that battle was a beautiful sight. Then, O King, it became unrestrained, raging wildly.

sanskrit: रथी नागं समासाद्य विचरन्रणमूर्धनि ।प्रेषयामास कालाय शरैः संनतपर्वभिः ॥५०॥
transliteration: rathī nāgaṁ samāsādya vicaranraṇamūrdhani ,preṣayāmāsa kālāya śaraiḥ saṁnataparvabhiḥ.
english: Moving on the forefront of the battle, the chariot warrior, upon encountering an elephant, dispatched it to Yama with his well-fitted arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 19 Verse 50. Moving on the forefront of the battle, the chariot warrior, upon encountering an elephant, dispatched it to Yama with his well-fitted arrows.

sanskrit: नागा हयान्समासाद्य विक्षिपन्तो बहूनथ ।द्रावयामासुरत्युग्रास्तत्र तत्र तदा तदा ॥५१॥
transliteration: nāgā hayānsamāsādya vikṣipanto bahūnatha ,drāvayāmāsuratyugrāstatra tatra tadā tadā.
english: Then, the exceedingly fierce elephants, having attacked many horses and scattered them, repeatedly put them to flight everywhere.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 19 Verse 51. Then, the exceedingly fierce elephants, having attacked many horses and scattered them, repeatedly put them to flight everywhere.

sanskrit: विद्राव्य च बहूनश्वान्नागा राजन्बलोत्कटाः ।विषाणैश्चापरे जघ्नुर्ममृदुश्चापरे भृशम् ॥५२॥
transliteration: vidrāvya ca bahūnaśvānnāgā rājanbalotkaṭāḥ ,viṣāṇaiścāpare jaghnurmamṛduścāpare bhṛśam.
english: And, O King, the mighty elephants, after scattering many horses, also attacked others with their tusks, and still others they crushed severely.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 19 Verse 52. And, O King, the mighty elephants, after scattering many horses, also attacked others with their tusks, and still others they crushed severely.

sanskrit: साश्वारोहांश्च तुरगान्विषाणैर्बिभिदू रणे ।अपरांश्चिक्षिपुर्वेगात्प्रगृह्यातिबलास्तथा ॥५३॥
transliteration: sāśvārohāṁśca turagānviṣāṇairbibhidū raṇe ,aparāṁścikṣipurvegātpragṛhyātibalāstathā.
english: The exceedingly powerful ones (atibalāḥ), using their tusks, pierced the horses along with their riders in battle. And, seizing others, they violently threw them.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 19 Verse 53. The exceedingly powerful ones (atibalāḥ), using their tusks, pierced the horses along with their riders in battle. And, seizing others, they violently threw them.

sanskrit: पादातैराहता नागा विवरेषु समन्ततः ।चक्रुरार्तस्वरं घोरं व्यद्रवन्त दिशो दश ॥५४॥
transliteration: pādātairāhatā nāgā vivareṣu samantataḥ ,cakrurārtasvaraṁ ghoraṁ vyadravanta diśo daśa.
english: The elephants, struck on all sides by the foot-soldiers in their pits, let out a terrible cry of distress and fled in all ten directions.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 19 Verse 54. The elephants, struck on all sides by the foot-soldiers in their pits, let out a terrible cry of distress and fled in all ten directions.

sanskrit: पदातीनां तु सहसा प्रद्रुतानां महामृधे ।उत्सृज्याभरणं तूर्णमवप्लुत्य रणाजिरे ॥५५॥
transliteration: padātīnāṁ tu sahasā pradrutānāṁ mahāmṛdhe ,utsṛjyābharaṇaṁ tūrṇamavaplutya raṇājire.
english: But suddenly, of those foot-soldiers who had fled in the great battle, having abandoned their ornaments, and quickly leapt down onto the battlefield...
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 19 Verse 55. But suddenly, of those foot-soldiers who had fled in the great battle, having abandoned their ornaments, and quickly leapt down onto the battlefield...

sanskrit: निमित्तं मन्यमानास्तु परिणम्य महागजाः ।जगृहुर्बिभिदुश्चैव चित्राण्याभरणानि च ॥५६॥
transliteration: nimittaṁ manyamānāstu pariṇamya mahāgajāḥ ,jagṛhurbibhiduścaiva citrāṇyābharaṇāni ca.
english: But the great elephants, considering it an omen, having transformed, both seized and pierced the various ornaments as well.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 19 Verse 56. But the great elephants, considering it an omen, having transformed, both seized and pierced the various ornaments as well.

sanskrit: प्रतिमानेषु कुम्भेषु दन्तवेष्टेषु चापरे ।निगृहीता भृशं नागाः प्रासतोमरशक्तिभिः ॥५७॥
transliteration: pratimāneṣu kumbheṣu dantaveṣṭeṣu cāpare ,nigṛhītā bhṛśaṁ nāgāḥ prāsatomaraśaktibhiḥ.
english: And other elephants, struck on their frontal globes, face coverings, and tusk sheaths, were fiercely restrained by javelins, lances, and spears.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 19 Verse 57. And other elephants, struck on their frontal globes, face coverings, and tusk sheaths, were fiercely restrained by javelins, lances, and spears.

sanskrit: निगृह्य च गदाः केचित्पार्श्वस्थैर्भृशदारुणैः ।रथाश्वसादिभिस्तत्र संभिन्ना न्यपतन्भुवि ॥५८॥
transliteration: nigṛhya ca gadāḥ kecitpārśvasthairbhṛśadāruṇaiḥ ,rathāśvasādibhistatra saṁbhinnā nyapatanbhuvi.
english: And some (warriors), having grasped their maces, were struck down there on the ground by the extremely fierce chariot-riders and horsemen standing by their sides.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 19 Verse 58. And some (warriors), having grasped their maces, were struck down there on the ground by the extremely fierce chariot-riders and horsemen standing by their sides.

sanskrit: सरथं सादिनं तत्र अपरे तु महागजाः ।भूमावमृद्नन्वेगेन सवर्माणं पताकिनम् ॥५९॥
transliteration: sarathaṁ sādinaṁ tatra apare tu mahāgajāḥ ,bhūmāvamṛdnanvegena savarmāṇaṁ patākinam.
english: But there, other great elephants swiftly crushed a horseman along with his chariot, and an armored standard-bearer, onto the ground.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 19 Verse 59. But there, other great elephants swiftly crushed a horseman along with his chariot, and an armored standard-bearer, onto the ground.

sanskrit: रथं नागाः समासाद्य धुरि गृह्य च मारिष ।व्याक्षिपन्सहसा तत्र घोररूपे महामृधे ॥६०॥
transliteration: rathaṁ nāgāḥ samāsādya dhuri gṛhya ca māriṣa ,vyākṣipansahasā tatra ghorarūpe mahāmṛdhe.
english: O respected one, the elephants, having attacked a chariot and seized it by its yoke, suddenly hurled it there in that ghastly, great battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 19 Verse 60. O respected one, the elephants, having attacked a chariot and seized it by its yoke, suddenly hurled it there in that ghastly, great battle.

sanskrit: नाराचैर्निहतश्चापि निपपात महागजः ।पर्वतस्येव शिखरं वज्रभग्नं महीतले ॥६१॥
transliteration: nārācairnihataścāpi nipapāta mahāgajaḥ ,parvatasyeva śikharaṁ vajrabhagnaṁ mahītale.
english: Even though struck by iron arrows, the great elephant fell to the ground, like a mountain peak shattered by a thunderbolt.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 19 Verse 61. Even though struck by iron arrows, the great elephant fell to the ground, like a mountain peak shattered by a thunderbolt.

sanskrit: योधा योधान्समासाद्य मुष्टिभिर्व्यहनन्युधि ।केशेष्वन्योन्यमाक्षिप्य चिच्छिदुर्बिभिदुः सह ॥६२॥
transliteration: yodhā yodhānsamāsādya muṣṭibhirvyahananyudhi ,keśeṣvanyonyamākṣipya cicchidurbibhiduḥ saha.
english: In battle, warriors, encountering other warriors, struck them with their fists. Grabbing each other by the hair, they jointly cut and pierced.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 19 Verse 62. In battle, warriors, encountering other warriors, struck them with their fists. Grabbing each other by the hair, they jointly cut and pierced.

sanskrit: उद्यम्य च भुजावन्यो निक्षिप्य च महीतले ।पदा चोरः समाक्रम्य स्फुरतो व्यहनच्छिरः ॥६३॥
transliteration: udyamya ca bhujāvanyo nikṣipya ca mahītale ,padā coraḥ samākramya sphurato vyahanacchiraḥ.
english: Another (warrior), raising his arms and throwing (the opponent) to the ground, then stepped on his chest with his foot and struck off the head of the struggling (foe).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 19 Verse 63. Another (warrior), raising his arms and throwing (the opponent) to the ground, then stepped on his chest with his foot and struck off the head of the struggling (foe).

sanskrit: मृतमन्यो महाराज पद्भ्यां ताडितवांस्तदा ।जीवतश्च तथैवान्यः शस्त्रं काये न्यमज्जयत् ॥६४॥
transliteration: mṛtamanyo mahārāja padbhyāṁ tāḍitavāṁstadā ,jīvataśca tathaivānyaḥ śastraṁ kāye nyamajjayat.
english: O great king, at that time, one (warrior) kicked a dead (foe) with his feet. And similarly, another (warrior) plunged a weapon into the body of a living one.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 19 Verse 64. O great king, at that time, one (warrior) kicked a dead (foe) with his feet. And similarly, another (warrior) plunged a weapon into the body of a living one.

sanskrit: मुष्टियुद्धं महच्चासीद्योधानां तत्र भारत ।तथा केशग्रहश्चोग्रो बाहुयुद्धं च केवलम् ॥६५॥
transliteration: muṣṭiyuddhaṁ mahaccāsīdyodhānāṁ tatra bhārata ,tathā keśagrahaścogro bāhuyuddhaṁ ca kevalam.
english: O Bhārata, among the warriors there, there was a great fist-fight, and also fierce hair-pulling and only arm-to-arm combat.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 19 Verse 65. O Bhārata, among the warriors there, there was a great fist-fight, and also fierce hair-pulling and only arm-to-arm combat.

sanskrit: समासक्तस्य चान्येन अविज्ञातस्तथापरः ।जहार समरे प्राणान्नानाशस्त्रैरनेकधा ॥६६॥
transliteration: samāsaktasya cānyena avijñātastathāparaḥ ,jahāra samare prāṇānnānāśastrairanekadhā.
english: While one (warrior) was engaged with another, yet another, unperceived, took away (his) life in battle in various ways with diverse weapons.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 19 Verse 66. While one (warrior) was engaged with another, yet another, unperceived, took away (his) life in battle in various ways with diverse weapons.

sanskrit: संसक्तेषु च योधेषु वर्तमाने च संकुले ।कबन्धान्युत्थितानि स्म शतशोऽथ सहस्रशः ॥६७॥
transliteration: saṁsakteṣu ca yodheṣu vartamāne ca saṁkule ,kabandhānyutthitāni sma śataśo'tha sahasraśaḥ.
english: As the warriors were engaged and the tumult (saṃkula) was ongoing, headless trunks (kabandha) rose up by hundreds and thousands.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 19 Verse 67. As the warriors were engaged and the tumult (saṃkula) was ongoing, headless trunks (kabandha) rose up by hundreds and thousands.

sanskrit: लोहितैः सिच्यमानानि शस्त्राणि कवचानि च ।महारङ्गानुरक्तानि वस्त्राणीव चकाशिरे ॥६८॥
transliteration: lohitaiḥ sicyamānāni śastrāṇi kavacāni ca ,mahāraṅgānuraktāni vastrāṇīva cakāśire.
english: The weapons and armors, drenched with blood, shone like garments dyed with deep red color.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 19 Verse 68. The weapons and armors, drenched with blood, shone like garments dyed with deep red color.

sanskrit: एवमेतन्महायुद्धं दारुणं भृशसंकुलम् ।उन्मत्तरङ्गप्रतिमं शब्देनापूरयज्जगत् ॥६९॥
transliteration: evametanmahāyuddhaṁ dāruṇaṁ bhṛśasaṁkulam ,unmattaraṅgapratimaṁ śabdenāpūrayajjagat.
english: This great battle, terrible and extremely chaotic, resembling frenzied waves, thus filled the world with its sound.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 19 Verse 69. This great battle, terrible and extremely chaotic, resembling frenzied waves, thus filled the world with its sound.

sanskrit: नैव स्वे न परे राजन्विज्ञायन्ते शरातुराः ।योद्धव्यमिति युध्यन्ते राजानो जयगृद्धिनः ॥७०॥
transliteration: naiva sve na pare rājanvijñāyante śarāturāḥ ,yoddhavyamiti yudhyante rājāno jayagṛddhinaḥ.
english: O King, neither one's own people nor the enemies, afflicted by arrows, are recognized. The kings, greedy for victory, fight with the thought, 'One must fight!'
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 19 Verse 70. O King, neither one's own people nor the enemies, afflicted by arrows, are recognized. The kings, greedy for victory, fight with the thought, 'One must fight!'

sanskrit: स्वान्स्वे जघ्नुर्महाराज परांश्चैव समागतान् ।उभयोः सेनयोर्वीरैर्व्याकुलं समपद्यत ॥७१॥
transliteration: svānsve jaghnurmahārāja parāṁścaiva samāgatān ,ubhayoḥ senayorvīrairvyākulaṁ samapadyata.
english: O great king, their own men killed their own men, and likewise those enemies who had come together. The scene became chaotic due to the warriors of both armies.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 19 Verse 71. O great king, their own men killed their own men, and likewise those enemies who had come together. The scene became chaotic due to the warriors of both armies.

sanskrit: रथैर्भग्नैर्महाराज वारणैश्च निपातितैः ।हयैश्च पतितैस्तत्र नरैश्च विनिपातितैः ॥७२॥
transliteration: rathairbhagnairmahārāja vāraṇaiśca nipātitaiḥ ,hayaiśca patitaistatra naraiśca vinipātitaiḥ.
english: O great king, the battlefield was filled with broken chariots, felled elephants, fallen horses, and slaughtered men.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 19 Verse 72. O great king, the battlefield was filled with broken chariots, felled elephants, fallen horses, and slaughtered men.

sanskrit: अगम्यरूपा पृथिवी मांसशोणितकर्दमा ।क्षणेनासीन्महाराज क्षतजौघप्रवर्तिनी ॥७३॥
transliteration: agamyarūpā pṛthivī māṁsaśoṇitakardamā ,kṣaṇenāsīnmahārāja kṣatajaughapravartinī.
english: O great king, the earth, having an unapproachable form, instantly became a mire of flesh and blood, flowing with torrents of gore.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 19 Verse 73. O great king, the earth, having an unapproachable form, instantly became a mire of flesh and blood, flowing with torrents of gore.

sanskrit: पाञ्चालानवधीत्कर्णस्त्रिगर्तांश्च धनंजयः ।भीमसेनः कुरून्राजन्हस्त्यनीकं च सर्वशः ॥७४॥
transliteration: pāñcālānavadhītkarṇastrigartāṁśca dhanaṁjayaḥ ,bhīmasenaḥ kurūnrājanhastyanīkaṁ ca sarvaśaḥ.
english: O king, Karṇa killed the Pāñcālas, and Arjuna (Dhananjaya) killed the Trigartas. Bhīmasena, on his part, completely destroyed the Kurus and their elephant army.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 19 Verse 74. O king, Karṇa killed the Pāñcālas, and Arjuna (Dhananjaya) killed the Trigartas. Bhīmasena, on his part, completely destroyed the Kurus and their elephant army.

sanskrit: एवमेष क्षयो वृत्तः कुरुपाण्डवसेनयोः ।अपराह्णे महाराज काङ्क्षन्त्योर्विपुलं जयम् ॥७५॥
transliteration: evameṣa kṣayo vṛttaḥ kurupāṇḍavasenayoḥ ,aparāhṇe mahārāja kāṅkṣantyorvipulaṁ jayam.
english: O great king, this destruction of the armies of the Kurus and Pāṇḍavas occurred thus in the afternoon, as both sides were longing for a great victory.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 19 Verse 75. O great king, this destruction of the armies of the Kurus and Pāṇḍavas occurred thus in the afternoon, as both sides were longing for a great victory.

sanskrit: धृतराष्ट्र उवाच ।अतितीव्राणि दुःखानि दुःसहानि बहूनि च ।तवाहं संजयाश्रौषं पुत्राणां मम संक्षयम् ॥१॥
transliteration: dhṛtarāṣṭra uvāca ,atitīvrāṇi duḥkhāni duḥsahāni bahūni ca ,tavāhaṁ saṁjayāśrauṣaṁ putrāṇāṁ mama saṁkṣayam.
