english: Dhṛtarāṣṭra said: "O Saṃjaya, I have heard from you about my sons' utter destruction, along with many other extremely intense and unbearable sorrows."
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 20 Verse 1. Dhṛtarāṣṭra said: "O Saṃjaya, I have heard from you about my sons' utter destruction, along with many other extremely intense and unbearable sorrows."

sanskrit: तथा तु मे कथयसे यथा युद्धं तु वर्तते ।न सन्ति सूत कौरव्या इति मे नैष्ठिकी मतिः ॥२॥
transliteration: tathā tu me kathayase yathā yuddhaṁ tu vartate ,na santi sūta kauravyā iti me naiṣṭhikī matiḥ.
english: You narrate to me how the battle is indeed progressing. My firm conviction is that the Kauravas no longer exist, O charioteer.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 20 Verse 2. You narrate to me how the battle is indeed progressing. My firm conviction is that the Kauravas no longer exist, O charioteer.

sanskrit: दुर्योधनस्तु विरथः कृतस्तत्र महारणे ।धर्मपुत्रः कथं चक्रे तस्मिन्वा नृपतिः कथम् ॥३॥
transliteration: duryodhanastu virathaḥ kṛtastatra mahāraṇe ,dharmaputraḥ kathaṁ cakre tasminvā nṛpatiḥ katham.
english: But Duryodhana was rendered without a chariot in that great battle. How did Dharmaputra (Yudhiṣṭhira) then act, or how did the king (Yudhiṣṭhira) behave in that situation?
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 20 Verse 3. But Duryodhana was rendered without a chariot in that great battle. How did Dharmaputra (Yudhiṣṭhira) then act, or how did the king (Yudhiṣṭhira) behave in that situation?

sanskrit: अपराह्णे कथं युद्धमभवल्लोमहर्षणम् ।तन्ममाचक्ष्व तत्त्वेन कुशलो ह्यसि संजय ॥४॥
transliteration: aparāhṇe kathaṁ yuddhamabhavallomaharṣaṇam ,tanmamācakṣva tattvena kuśalo hyasi saṁjaya.
english: How did the hair-raising battle occur in the afternoon? O Saṃjaya, describe that to me truthfully, for you are indeed an expert.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 20 Verse 4. How did the hair-raising battle occur in the afternoon? O Saṃjaya, describe that to me truthfully, for you are indeed an expert.

sanskrit: संजय उवाच ।संसक्तेषु च सैन्येषु युध्यमानेषु भागशः ।रथमन्यं समास्थाय पुत्रस्तव विशां पते ॥५॥
transliteration: saṁjaya uvāca ,saṁsakteṣu ca sainyeṣu yudhyamāneṣu bhāgaśaḥ ,rathamanyaṁ samāsthāya putrastava viśāṁ pate.
english: Saṃjaya said: O lord of men, while the armies were engaged and fighting in various divisions, your son, having mounted another chariot...
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 20 Verse 5. Saṃjaya said: O lord of men, while the armies were engaged and fighting in various divisions, your son, having mounted another chariot...

sanskrit: क्रोधेन महताविष्टः सविषो भुजगो यथा ।दुर्योधनस्तु दृष्ट्वा वै धर्मराजं युधिष्ठिरम् ।उवाच सूत त्वरितं याहि याहीति भारत ॥६॥
transliteration: krodhena mahatāviṣṭaḥ saviṣo bhujago yathā ,duryodhanastu dṛṣṭvā vai dharmarājaṁ yudhiṣṭhiram ,uvāca sūta tvaritaṁ yāhi yāhīti bhārata.
english: Overwhelmed by great anger, like a poisonous snake, Duryodhana, having seen King Yudhiṣṭhira, the upholder of natural law (dharma), said to his charioteer, "Quickly go, go, O Bhārata!"
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 20 Verse 6. Overwhelmed by great anger, like a poisonous snake, Duryodhana, having seen King Yudhiṣṭhira, the upholder of natural law (dharma), said to his charioteer, "Quickly go, go, O Bhārata!"

sanskrit: अत्र मां प्रापय क्षिप्रं सारथे यत्र पाण्डवः ।ध्रियमाणेन छत्रेण राजा राजति दंशितः ॥७॥
transliteration: atra māṁ prāpaya kṣipraṁ sārathe yatra pāṇḍavaḥ ,dhriyamāṇena chatreṇa rājā rājati daṁśitaḥ.
english: "O charioteer, quickly bring me to where that Pāṇḍava is. The king, wearing armor, shines with an umbrella being held over him."
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 20 Verse 7. "O charioteer, quickly bring me to where that Pāṇḍava is. The king, wearing armor, shines with an umbrella being held over him."

sanskrit: स सूतश्चोदितो राज्ञा राज्ञः स्यन्दनमुत्तमम् ।युधिष्ठिरस्याभिमुखं प्रेषयामास संयुगे ॥८॥
transliteration: sa sūtaścodito rājñā rājñaḥ syandanamuttamam ,yudhiṣṭhirasyābhimukhaṁ preṣayāmāsa saṁyuge.
english: That charioteer, urged by the king, sent the king's excellent chariot forward, facing Yudhiṣṭhira, in the battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 20 Verse 8. That charioteer, urged by the king, sent the king's excellent chariot forward, facing Yudhiṣṭhira, in the battle.

sanskrit: ततो युधिष्ठिरः क्रुद्धः प्रमत्त इव सद्गवः ।सारथिं चोदयामास याहि यत्र सुयोधनः ॥९॥
transliteration: tato yudhiṣṭhiraḥ kruddhaḥ pramatta iva sadgavaḥ ,sārathiṁ codayāmāsa yāhi yatra suyodhanaḥ.
english: Then Yudhiṣṭhira, enraged like a powerful bull in rut, urged his charioteer, "Go to where Suyodhana is!"
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 20 Verse 9. Then Yudhiṣṭhira, enraged like a powerful bull in rut, urged his charioteer, "Go to where Suyodhana is!"

sanskrit: तौ समाजग्मतुर्वीरौ भ्रातरौ रथसत्तमौ ।समेत्य च महावीर्यौ संनद्धौ युद्धदुर्मदौ ।ततक्षतुर्महेष्वासौ शरैरन्योन्यमाहवे ॥१०॥
transliteration: tau samājagmaturvīrau bhrātarau rathasattamau ,sametya ca mahāvīryau saṁnaddhau yuddhadurmadau ,tatakṣaturmaheṣvāsau śarairanyonyamāhave.
english: Those two heroic brothers, the best of charioteers, met. Having met, those two exceedingly powerful, armored, and battle-furious great archers began to strike each other with arrows in the combat.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 20 Verse 10. Those two heroic brothers, the best of charioteers, met. Having met, those two exceedingly powerful, armored, and battle-furious great archers began to strike each other with arrows in the combat.

sanskrit: ततो दुर्योधनो राजा धर्मशीलस्य मारिष ।शिलाशितेन भल्लेन धनुश्चिच्छेद संयुगे ।तं नामृष्यत संक्रुद्धो व्यवसायं युधिष्ठिरः ॥११॥
transliteration: tato duryodhano rājā dharmaśīlasya māriṣa ,śilāśitena bhallena dhanuściccheda saṁyuge ,taṁ nāmṛṣyata saṁkruddho vyavasāyaṁ yudhiṣṭhiraḥ.
english: Then, O esteemed one, King Duryodhana, with a *bhalla* arrow sharpened on a stone, cut the bow of Yudhishthira, whose intrinsic nature (dharma) was righteousness, in battle. Greatly enraged, Yudhishthira did not tolerate this act.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 20 Verse 11. Then, O esteemed one, King Duryodhana, with a *bhalla* arrow sharpened on a stone, cut the bow of Yudhishthira, whose intrinsic nature (dharma) was righteousness, in battle. Greatly enraged, Yudhishthira did not tolerate this act.

sanskrit: अपविध्य धनुश्छिन्नं क्रोधसंरक्तलोचनः ।अन्यत्कार्मुकमादाय धर्मपुत्रश्चमूमुखे ॥१२॥
transliteration: apavidhya dhanuśchinnaṁ krodhasaṁraktalocanaḥ ,anyatkārmukamādāya dharmaputraścamūmukhe.
english: Having cast aside the broken bow, the son of Dharma (Yudhishthira), whose eyes were reddened with rage, took up another bow at the vanguard of the army.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 20 Verse 12. Having cast aside the broken bow, the son of Dharma (Yudhishthira), whose eyes were reddened with rage, took up another bow at the vanguard of the army.

sanskrit: दुर्योधनस्य चिच्छेद ध्वजं कार्मुकमेव च ।अथान्यद्धनुरादाय प्रत्यविध्यत पाण्डवम् ॥१३॥
transliteration: duryodhanasya ciccheda dhvajaṁ kārmukameva ca ,athānyaddhanurādāya pratyavidhyata pāṇḍavam.
english: Then (Yudhishthira) cut down Duryodhana's banner and his bow. Thereupon, (Duryodhana) taking another bow, struck the Pandava (Yudhishthira) in retaliation.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 20 Verse 13. Then (Yudhishthira) cut down Duryodhana's banner and his bow. Thereupon, (Duryodhana) taking another bow, struck the Pandava (Yudhishthira) in retaliation.

sanskrit: तावन्योन्यं सुसंरब्धौ शरवर्षाण्यमुञ्चताम् ।सिंहाविव सुसंक्रुद्धौ परस्परजिगीषया ॥१४॥
transliteration: tāvanyonyaṁ susaṁrabdhau śaravarṣāṇyamuñcatām ,siṁhāviva susaṁkruddhau parasparajigīṣayā.
english: Those two, extremely enraged and highly furious with each other, released showers of arrows. They were like two very angry lions, driven by the desire to conquer (jigīṣā) one another.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 20 Verse 14. Those two, extremely enraged and highly furious with each other, released showers of arrows. They were like two very angry lions, driven by the desire to conquer (jigīṣā) one another.

sanskrit: अन्योन्यं जघ्नतुश्चैव नर्दमानौ वृषाविव ।अन्योन्यं प्रेक्षमाणौ च चेरतुस्तौ महारथौ ॥१५॥
transliteration: anyonyaṁ jaghnatuścaiva nardamānau vṛṣāviva ,anyonyaṁ prekṣamāṇau ca ceratustau mahārathau.
english: And indeed, roaring like two bulls, they struck each other. And those two great warriors (mahārathau), eyeing each other, fought on.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 20 Verse 15. And indeed, roaring like two bulls, they struck each other. And those two great warriors (mahārathau), eyeing each other, fought on.

sanskrit: ततः पूर्णायतोत्सृष्टैरन्योन्यं सुकृतव्रणौ ।विरेजतुर्महाराज पुष्पिताविव किंशुकौ ॥१६॥
transliteration: tataḥ pūrṇāyatotsṛṣṭairanyonyaṁ sukṛtavraṇau ,virejaturmahārāja puṣpitāviva kiṁśukau.
english: Then, O great king, those two, having inflicted severe wounds upon each other with arrows released from fully drawn bows, shone splendidly, like two palāśa trees (kiṃśuka) in bloom.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 20 Verse 16. Then, O great king, those two, having inflicted severe wounds upon each other with arrows released from fully drawn bows, shone splendidly, like two palāśa trees (kiṃśuka) in bloom.

sanskrit: ततो राजन्प्रतिभयान्सिंहनादान्मुहुर्मुहुः ।तलयोश्च तथा शब्दान्धनुषोश्च महाहवे ॥१७॥
transliteration: tato rājanpratibhayānsiṁhanādānmuhurmuhuḥ ,talayośca tathā śabdāndhanuṣośca mahāhave.
english: Then, O King, in that great battle (mahāhave), there were terrifying lion-like roars again and again, as well as the sounds of their palms and bows.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 20 Verse 17. Then, O King, in that great battle (mahāhave), there were terrifying lion-like roars again and again, as well as the sounds of their palms and bows.

sanskrit: शङ्खशब्दरवांश्चैव चक्रतुस्तौ रथोत्तमौ ।अन्योन्यं च महाराज पीडयां चक्रतुर्भृशम् ॥१८॥
transliteration: śaṅkhaśabdaravāṁścaiva cakratustau rathottamau ,anyonyaṁ ca mahārāja pīḍayāṁ cakraturbhṛśam.
english: O great king, those two excellent charioteers (or the occupants of the excellent chariots) both blew their conch shells loudly, and they severely afflicted each other.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 20 Verse 18. O great king, those two excellent charioteers (or the occupants of the excellent chariots) both blew their conch shells loudly, and they severely afflicted each other.

sanskrit: ततो युधिष्ठिरो राजा तव पुत्रं त्रिभिः शरैः ।आजघानोरसि क्रुद्धो वज्रवेगो दुरासदः ॥१९॥
transliteration: tato yudhiṣṭhiro rājā tava putraṁ tribhiḥ śaraiḥ ,ājaghānorasi kruddho vajravego durāsadaḥ.
english: Then King Yudhiṣṭhira, enraged and having the speed of a thunderbolt, difficult to approach (or irresistible), struck your son on the chest with three arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 20 Verse 19. Then King Yudhiṣṭhira, enraged and having the speed of a thunderbolt, difficult to approach (or irresistible), struck your son on the chest with three arrows.

sanskrit: प्रतिविव्याध तं तूर्णं तव पुत्रो महीपतिम् ।पञ्चभिर्निशितैर्बाणैर्हेमपुङ्खैः शिलाशितैः ॥२०॥
transliteration: prativivyādha taṁ tūrṇaṁ tava putro mahīpatim ,pañcabhirniśitairbāṇairhemapuṅkhaiḥ śilāśitaiḥ.
english: Your son quickly retaliated, piercing that king (Yudhiṣṭhira) with five sharp arrows, which had golden shafts and were honed on stone.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 20 Verse 20. Your son quickly retaliated, piercing that king (Yudhiṣṭhira) with five sharp arrows, which had golden shafts and were honed on stone.

sanskrit: ततो दुर्योधनो राजा शक्तिं चिक्षेप भारत ।सर्वपारशवीं तीक्ष्णां महोल्काप्रतिमां तदा ॥२१॥
transliteration: tato duryodhano rājā śaktiṁ cikṣepa bhārata ,sarvapāraśavīṁ tīkṣṇāṁ maholkāpratimāṁ tadā.
english: Then, O Bhārata, King Duryodhana hurled a sharp, entirely iron spear (śakti) that resembled a great meteor, at that time.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 20 Verse 21. Then, O Bhārata, King Duryodhana hurled a sharp, entirely iron spear (śakti) that resembled a great meteor, at that time.

sanskrit: तामापतन्तीं सहसा धर्मराजः शिलाशितैः ।त्रिभिश्चिच्छेद सहसा तं च विव्याध सप्तभिः ॥२२॥
transliteration: tāmāpatantīṁ sahasā dharmarājaḥ śilāśitaiḥ ,tribhiściccheda sahasā taṁ ca vivyādha saptabhiḥ.
english: Dharmarāja (Dharmarāja) quickly cut down that rapidly approaching (weapon) with three arrows sharpened on stone, and then swiftly pierced him with seven (arrows).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 20 Verse 22. Dharmarāja (Dharmarāja) quickly cut down that rapidly approaching (weapon) with three arrows sharpened on stone, and then swiftly pierced him with seven (arrows).

sanskrit: निपपात ततः साथ हेमदण्डा महाघना ।निपतन्ती महोल्केव व्यराजच्छिखिसंनिभा ॥२३॥
transliteration: nipapāta tataḥ sātha hemadaṇḍā mahāghanā ,nipatantī maholkeva vyarājacchikhisaṁnibhā.
english: Then, that golden-shafted and exceedingly heavy (weapon) fell down. As it fell, it blazed like a great meteor (maholkā), resembling fire.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 20 Verse 23. Then, that golden-shafted and exceedingly heavy (weapon) fell down. As it fell, it blazed like a great meteor (maholkā), resembling fire.

sanskrit: शक्तिं विनिहतां दृष्ट्वा पुत्रस्तव विशां पते ।नवभिर्निशितैर्भल्लैर्निजघान युधिष्ठिरम् ॥२४॥
transliteration: śaktiṁ vinihatāṁ dṛṣṭvā putrastava viśāṁ pate ,navabhirniśitairbhallairnijaghāna yudhiṣṭhiram.
english: O lord of people (viśām pati), your son, having seen the spear (śakti) destroyed, struck Yudhiṣṭhira with nine sharpened arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 20 Verse 24. O lord of people (viśām pati), your son, having seen the spear (śakti) destroyed, struck Yudhiṣṭhira with nine sharpened arrows.

sanskrit: सोऽतिविद्धो बलवतामग्रणीः शत्रुतापनः ।दुर्योधनं समुद्दिश्य बाणं जग्राह सत्वरः ॥२५॥
transliteration: so'tividdho balavatāmagraṇīḥ śatrutāpanaḥ ,duryodhanaṁ samuddiśya bāṇaṁ jagrāha satvaraḥ.
english: Though severely wounded, he, the foremost among the strong and the tormentor of enemies, quickly took an arrow, aiming it at Duryodhana.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 20 Verse 25. Though severely wounded, he, the foremost among the strong and the tormentor of enemies, quickly took an arrow, aiming it at Duryodhana.

sanskrit: समाधत्त च तं बाणं धनुष्युग्रं महाबलः ।चिक्षेप च ततो राजा राज्ञः क्रुद्धः पराक्रमी ॥२६॥
transliteration: samādhatta ca taṁ bāṇaṁ dhanuṣyugraṁ mahābalaḥ ,cikṣepa ca tato rājā rājñaḥ kruddhaḥ parākramī.
english: The greatly powerful king fixed that fierce arrow on his bow. Then, enraged and valorous, he shot it at the (other) king.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 20 Verse 26. The greatly powerful king fixed that fierce arrow on his bow. Then, enraged and valorous, he shot it at the (other) king.

sanskrit: स तु बाणः समासाद्य तव पुत्रं महारथम् ।व्यमोहयत राजानं धरणीं च जगाम ह ॥२७॥
transliteration: sa tu bāṇaḥ samāsādya tava putraṁ mahāratham ,vyamohayata rājānaṁ dharaṇīṁ ca jagāma ha.
english: That arrow, indeed, having struck your son, the great warrior, rendered the king unconscious and then penetrated the earth.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 20 Verse 27. That arrow, indeed, having struck your son, the great warrior, rendered the king unconscious and then penetrated the earth.

sanskrit: ततो दुर्योधनः क्रुद्धो गदामुद्यम्य वेगितः ।विधित्सुः कलहस्यान्तमभिदुद्राव पाण्डवम् ॥२८॥
transliteration: tato duryodhanaḥ kruddho gadāmudyamya vegitaḥ ,vidhitsuḥ kalahasyāntamabhidudrāva pāṇḍavam.
english: Then Duryodhana, enraged and swift, having lifted his mace, and intending to bring an end to the quarrel, ran towards the Pāṇḍava.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 20 Verse 28. Then Duryodhana, enraged and swift, having lifted his mace, and intending to bring an end to the quarrel, ran towards the Pāṇḍava.

sanskrit: तमालक्ष्योद्यतगदं दण्डहस्तमिवान्तकम् ।धर्मराजो महाशक्तिं प्राहिणोत्तव सूनवे ।दीप्यमानां महावेगां महोल्कां ज्वलितामिव ॥२९॥
transliteration: tamālakṣyodyatagadaṁ daṇḍahastamivāntakam ,dharmarājo mahāśaktiṁ prāhiṇottava sūnave ,dīpyamānāṁ mahāvegāṁ maholkāṁ jvalitāmiva.
english: Having perceived him (Duryodhana) with his mace upraised, resembling Death (Antaka) holding his staff, the King of natural law (dharma), Yudhiṣṭhira, dispatched a great spear (śakti) towards your son, blazing brightly and tremendously swift, like a flaming meteor.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 20 Verse 29. Having perceived him (Duryodhana) with his mace upraised, resembling Death (Antaka) holding his staff, the King of natural law (dharma), Yudhiṣṭhira, dispatched a great spear (śakti) towards your son, blazing brightly and tremendously swift, like a flaming meteor.

sanskrit: रथस्थः स तया विद्धो वर्म भित्त्वा महाहवे ।भृशं संविग्नहृदयः पपात च मुमोह च ॥३०॥
transliteration: rathasthaḥ sa tayā viddho varma bhittvā mahāhave ,bhṛśaṁ saṁvignahṛdayaḥ papāta ca mumoha ca.
english: In the great battle, standing on his chariot, he was struck by that (arrow) which had pierced his armor. With a greatly agitated heart, he fell and fainted.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 20 Verse 30. In the great battle, standing on his chariot, he was struck by that (arrow) which had pierced his armor. With a greatly agitated heart, he fell and fainted.

sanskrit: ततस्त्वरितमागत्य कृतवर्मा तवात्मजम् ।प्रत्यपद्यत राजानं मग्नं वै व्यसनार्णवे ॥३१॥
transliteration: tatastvaritamāgatya kṛtavarmā tavātmajam ,pratyapadyata rājānaṁ magnaṁ vai vyasanārṇave.
english: Then, Kṛtavarmā, having quickly arrived, came to the aid of your son, the king, who was truly submerged in an ocean of distress.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 20 Verse 31. Then, Kṛtavarmā, having quickly arrived, came to the aid of your son, the king, who was truly submerged in an ocean of distress.

sanskrit: भीमोऽपि महतीं गृह्य गदां हेमपरिष्कृताम् ।अभिदुद्राव वेगेन कृतवर्माणमाहवे ।एवं तदभवद्युद्धं त्वदीयानां परैः सह ॥३२॥
transliteration: bhīmo'pi mahatīṁ gṛhya gadāṁ hemapariṣkṛtām ,abhidudrāva vegena kṛtavarmāṇamāhave ,evaṁ tadabhavadyuddhaṁ tvadīyānāṁ paraiḥ saha.
english: Bhīma also, seizing his great mace adorned with gold, swiftly rushed towards Kṛtavarmā in the battle. Thus, the combat unfolded between your warriors and the enemies.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 20 Verse 32. Bhīma also, seizing his great mace adorned with gold, swiftly rushed towards Kṛtavarmā in the battle. Thus, the combat unfolded between your warriors and the enemies.

sanskrit: संजय उवाच ।ततः कर्णं पुरस्कृत्य त्वदीया युद्धदुर्मदाः ।पुनरावृत्य संग्रामं चक्रुर्देवासुरोपमम् ॥१॥
transliteration: saṁjaya uvāca ,tataḥ karṇaṁ puraskṛtya tvadīyā yuddhadurmadāḥ ,punarāvṛtya saṁgrāmaṁ cakrurdevāsuropamam.
english: Sañjaya said: 'Then your warriors, fierce in battle, returned with Karṇa at the forefront and engaged in a battle resembling that between gods and demons.'
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 21 Verse 1. Sañjaya said: 'Then your warriors, fierce in battle, returned with Karṇa at the forefront and engaged in a battle resembling that between gods and demons.'

sanskrit: द्विरदरथनराश्वशङ्खशब्दैः परिहृषिता विविधैश्च शस्त्रपातैः ।द्विरदरथपदातिसार्थवाहाः परिपतिताभिमुखाः प्रजह्रिरे ते ॥२॥
transliteration: dviradarathanarāśvaśaṅkhaśabdaiḥ; parihṛṣitā vividhaiśca śastrapātaiḥ ,dviradarathapadātisārthavāhāḥ; paripatitābhimukhāḥ prajahrire te.
english: The elephant-riders, chariot-warriors, foot-soldiers, and their commanders, greatly exhilarated by the sounds of elephants, chariots, men, horses, and conches, and by various impacts of weapons, rushed forward fiercely and attacked.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 21 Verse 2. The elephant-riders, chariot-warriors, foot-soldiers, and their commanders, greatly exhilarated by the sounds of elephants, chariots, men, horses, and conches, and by various impacts of weapons, rushed forward fiercely and attacked.

sanskrit: शरपरशुवरासिपट्टिशैरिषुभिरनेकविधैश्च सादिताः ।द्विरदरथहया महाहवे वरपुरुषैः पुरुषाश्च वाहनैः ॥३॥
transliteration: śaraparaśuvarāsipaṭṭiśai;riṣubhiranekavidhaiśca sāditāḥ ,dviradarathahayā mahāhave; varapuruṣaiḥ puruṣāśca vāhanaiḥ.
english: In that great battle, elephants, chariots, and horses were struck down by excellent warriors, and men (puruṣa) were also struck down by mounts, all by means of arrows, axes, excellent swords, pattiśas, and various other kinds of arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 21 Verse 3. In that great battle, elephants, chariots, and horses were struck down by excellent warriors, and men (puruṣa) were also struck down by mounts, all by means of arrows, axes, excellent swords, pattiśas, and various other kinds of arrows.

sanskrit: कमलदिनकरेन्दुसंनिभैः सितदशनैः सुमुखाक्षिनासिकैः ।रुचिरमुकुटकुण्डलैर्मही पुरुषशिरोभिरवस्तृता बभौ ॥४॥
transliteration: kamaladinakarendusaṁnibhaiḥ; sitadaśanaiḥ sumukhākṣināsikaiḥ ,ruciramukuṭakuṇḍalairmahī; puruṣaśirobhiravastṛtā babhau.
english: The earth shone, strewn with the heads of men (puruṣa) that resembled lotuses, the sun, and the moon, possessing white teeth, beautiful faces, eyes, and noses, and adorned with splendid crowns and earrings.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 21 Verse 4. The earth shone, strewn with the heads of men (puruṣa) that resembled lotuses, the sun, and the moon, possessing white teeth, beautiful faces, eyes, and noses, and adorned with splendid crowns and earrings.

sanskrit: परिघमुसलशक्तितोमरैर्नखरभुशुण्डिगदाशतैर्द्रुताः ।द्विरदनरहयाः सहस्रशो रुधिरनदीप्रवहास्तदाभवन् ॥५॥
transliteration: parighamusalaśaktitomarai;rnakharabhuśuṇḍigadāśatairdrutāḥ ,dviradanarahayāḥ sahasraśo; rudhiranadīpravahāstadābhavan.
english: Then, elephants, men (puruṣa), and horses, shattered by thousands by maces, clubs, spears, javelins, daggers, slings, and hundreds of other maces, transformed into streams of flowing blood.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 21 Verse 5. Then, elephants, men (puruṣa), and horses, shattered by thousands by maces, clubs, spears, javelins, daggers, slings, and hundreds of other maces, transformed into streams of flowing blood.

sanskrit: प्रहतनररथाश्वकुञ्जरं प्रतिभयदर्शनमुल्बणं तदा ।तदहितनिहतं बभौ बलं पितृपतिराष्ट्रमिव प्रजाक्षये ॥६॥
transliteration: prahatanararathāśvakuñjaraṁ; pratibhayadarśanamulbaṇaṁ tadā ,tadahitanihataṁ babhau balaṁ; pitṛpatirāṣṭramiva prajākṣaye.
english: Then, that formidable army, with its men, chariots, horses, and elephants struck down, and appearing terrifying, having been destroyed by its enemies, looked like the realm of the Lord of Death (pitṛpati) at the time of the annihilation of creation (prajākṣaye).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 21 Verse 6. Then, that formidable army, with its men, chariots, horses, and elephants struck down, and appearing terrifying, having been destroyed by its enemies, looked like the realm of the Lord of Death (pitṛpati) at the time of the annihilation of creation (prajākṣaye).

sanskrit: अथ तव नरदेव सैनिकास्तव च सुताः सुरसूनुसंनिभाः ।अमितबलपुरःसरा रणे कुरुवृषभाः शिनिपुत्रमभ्ययुः ॥७॥
transliteration: atha tava naradeva sainikā;stava ca sutāḥ surasūnusaṁnibhāḥ ,amitabalapuraḥsarā raṇe; kuruvṛṣabhāḥ śiniputramabhyayuḥ.
english: Then, O king (naradeva), your soldiers and your sons, who were like the sons of the gods and were the best among the Kurus (kuruvṛṣabhāḥ), having immeasurable strength as their vanguard, attacked the son of Shini (śiniputra) in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 21 Verse 7. Then, O king (naradeva), your soldiers and your sons, who were like the sons of the gods and were the best among the Kurus (kuruvṛṣabhāḥ), having immeasurable strength as their vanguard, attacked the son of Shini (śiniputra) in battle.

sanskrit: तदतिरुचिरभीममाबभौ पुरुषवराश्वरथद्विपाकुलम् ।लवणजलसमुद्धतस्वनं बलममरासुरसैन्यसंनिभम् ॥८॥
transliteration: tadatirucirabhīmamābabhau; puruṣavarāśvarathadvipākulam ,lavaṇajalasamuddhatasvanaṁ; balamamarāsurasainyasaṁnibham.
english: That army (balam), filled with excellent men, horses, chariots, and elephants, and whose roar was like the tumultuous ocean, appeared both exceedingly splendid and terrifying, resembling the hosts of gods and asuras.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 21 Verse 8. That army (balam), filled with excellent men, horses, chariots, and elephants, and whose roar was like the tumultuous ocean, appeared both exceedingly splendid and terrifying, resembling the hosts of gods and asuras.

sanskrit: सुरपतिसमविक्रमस्ततस्त्रिदशवरावरजोपमं युधि ।दिनकरकिरणप्रभैः पृषत्कै रवितनयोऽभ्यहनच्छिनिप्रवीरम् ॥९॥
transliteration: surapatisamavikramastata;stridaśavarāvarajopamaṁ yudhi ,dinakarakiraṇaprabhaiḥ pṛṣatkai; ravitanayo'bhyahanacchinipravīram.
english: Then, Karṇa (ravitanaya), whose valor was equal to that of Indra (surapati), attacked the brave hero of Shini's lineage (śinipravīram), who was like the younger brother of the best of the gods, in battle, with arrows gleaming like the rays of the sun.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 21 Verse 9. Then, Karṇa (ravitanaya), whose valor was equal to that of Indra (surapati), attacked the brave hero of Shini's lineage (śinipravīram), who was like the younger brother of the best of the gods, in battle, with arrows gleaming like the rays of the sun.

sanskrit: तमपि सरथवाजिसारथिं शिनिवृषभो विविधैः शरैस्त्वरन् ।भुजगविषसमप्रभै रणे पुरुषवरं समवास्तृणोत्तदा ॥१०॥
transliteration: tamapi sarathavājisārathiṁ; śinivṛṣabho vividhaiḥ śaraistvaran ,bhujagaviṣasamaprabhai raṇe; puruṣavaraṁ samavāstṛṇottadā.
english: Satyaki, the foremost of the Śini clan, then swiftly covered that best among men (puruṣa) (Karṇa) – along with his chariot, horses, and charioteer – with various arrows shining like snake venom in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 21 Verse 10. Satyaki, the foremost of the Śini clan, then swiftly covered that best among men (puruṣa) (Karṇa) – along with his chariot, horses, and charioteer – with various arrows shining like snake venom in battle.

sanskrit: शिनिवृषभशरप्रपीडितं तव सुहृदो वसुषेणमभ्ययुः ।त्वरितमतिरथा रथर्षभं द्विरदरथाश्वपदातिभिः सह ॥११॥
transliteration: śinivṛṣabhaśaraprapīḍitaṁ; tava suhṛdo vasuṣeṇamabhyayuḥ ,tvaritamatirathā ratharṣabhaṁ; dviradarathāśvapadātibhiḥ saha.
english: Your friends, accompanied by elephants, chariots, horses, and foot soldiers, swiftly approached Vasuṣeṇa (Karṇa), that foremost of charioteers, who was severely tormented by the arrows of the foremost of the Śini clan.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 21 Verse 11. Your friends, accompanied by elephants, chariots, horses, and foot soldiers, swiftly approached Vasuṣeṇa (Karṇa), that foremost of charioteers, who was severely tormented by the arrows of the foremost of the Śini clan.

sanskrit: तमुदधिनिभमाद्रवद्बली त्वरिततरैः समभिद्रुतं परैः ।द्रुपदसुतसखस्तदाकरोत्पुरुषरथाश्वगजक्षयं महत् ॥१२॥
transliteration: tamudadhinibhamādravadbalī; tvaritataraiḥ samabhidrutaṁ paraiḥ ,drupadasutasakhastadākaro;tpuruṣarathāśvagajakṣayaṁ mahat.
english: Then, the mighty one (Arjuna), the friend of Drupada's son, swiftly advanced against Karṇa, who was surrounded by swift enemies and as vast as an ocean (of troops). He then wrought immense destruction upon men, chariots, horses, and elephants.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 21 Verse 12. Then, the mighty one (Arjuna), the friend of Drupada's son, swiftly advanced against Karṇa, who was surrounded by swift enemies and as vast as an ocean (of troops). He then wrought immense destruction upon men, chariots, horses, and elephants.

sanskrit: अथ पुरुषवरौ कृताह्निकौ भवमभिपूज्य यथाविधि प्रभुम् ।अरिवधकृतनिश्चयौ द्रुतं तव बलमर्जुनकेशवौ सृतौ ॥१३॥
transliteration: atha puruṣavarau kṛtāhnikau; bhavamabhipūjya yathāvidhi prabhum ,arivadhakṛtaniścayau drutaṁ; tava balamarjunakeśavau sṛtau.
english: Then, the two foremost among men (puruṣa), Arjuna and Keśava, having performed their daily rites, and having duly worshipped the all-powerful Lord (Śiva), and with a firm resolve to slay the enemies, swiftly proceeded towards your army.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 21 Verse 13. Then, the two foremost among men (puruṣa), Arjuna and Keśava, having performed their daily rites, and having duly worshipped the all-powerful Lord (Śiva), and with a firm resolve to slay the enemies, swiftly proceeded towards your army.

sanskrit: जलदनिनदनिस्वनं रथं पवनविधूतपताककेतनम् ।सितहयमुपयान्तमन्तिकं हृतमनसो ददृशुस्तदारयः ॥१४॥
transliteration: jaladaninadanisvanaṁ rathaṁ; pavanavidhūtapatākaketanam ,sitahayamupayāntamantikaṁ; hṛtamanaso dadṛśustadārayaḥ.
english: Then, the enemies, their minds captivated, saw the chariot approaching close by. Its sound was like a thunderclap, its flags and banners waved by the wind, and it was drawn by white horses.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 21 Verse 14. Then, the enemies, their minds captivated, saw the chariot approaching close by. Its sound was like a thunderclap, its flags and banners waved by the wind, and it was drawn by white horses.

sanskrit: अथ विस्फार्य गाण्डीवं रणे नृत्यन्निवार्जुनः ।शरसंबाधमकरोत्खं दिशः प्रदिशस्तथा ॥१५॥
transliteration: atha visphārya gāṇḍīvaṁ raṇe nṛtyannivārjunaḥ ,śarasaṁbādhamakarotkhaṁ diśaḥ pradiśastathā.
english: Then Arjuna, as if dancing in battle, drew his Gaṇḍīva (bow) and filled the sky, all cardinal directions, and intermediate directions with a dense shower of arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 21 Verse 15. Then Arjuna, as if dancing in battle, drew his Gaṇḍīva (bow) and filled the sky, all cardinal directions, and intermediate directions with a dense shower of arrows.

sanskrit: रथान्विमानप्रतिमान्सज्जयन्त्रायुधध्वजान् ।ससारथींस्तदा बाणैरभ्राणीवानिलोऽवधीत् ॥१६॥
transliteration: rathānvimānapratimānsajjayantrāyudhadhvajān ,sasārathīṁstadā bāṇairabhrāṇīvānilo'vadhīt.
english: Then, with his arrows, Arjuna struck down the chariots - which resembled celestial vehicles, fully equipped with their devices, weapons, and banners, and including their charioteers - just as the wind (anila) scatters clouds.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 21 Verse 16. Then, with his arrows, Arjuna struck down the chariots - which resembled celestial vehicles, fully equipped with their devices, weapons, and banners, and including their charioteers - just as the wind (anila) scatters clouds.

sanskrit: गजान्गजप्रयन्तॄंश्च वैजयन्त्यायुधध्वजान् ।सादिनोऽश्वांश्च पत्तींश्च शरैर्निन्ये यमक्षयम् ॥१७॥
transliteration: gajāngajaprayantṝṁśca vaijayantyāyudhadhvajān ,sādino'śvāṁśca pattīṁśca śarairninye yamakṣayam.
english: With his arrows, he dispatched to Yama's abode (yamakṣayam) the elephants, their drivers, those bearing victory banners, weapons, and flags, as well as the horsemen, their horses, and the foot soldiers.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 21 Verse 17. With his arrows, he dispatched to Yama's abode (yamakṣayam) the elephants, their drivers, those bearing victory banners, weapons, and flags, as well as the horsemen, their horses, and the foot soldiers.

sanskrit: तमन्तकमिव क्रुद्धमनिवार्यं महारथम् ।दुर्योधनोऽभ्ययादेको निघ्नन्बाणैः पृथग्विधैः ॥१८॥
transliteration: tamantakamiva kruddhamanivāryaṁ mahāratham ,duryodhano'bhyayādeko nighnanbāṇaiḥ pṛthagvidhaiḥ.
english: Duryodhana alone attacked him, who was like the enraged, irresistible god of death (Antaka) and a great chariot-warrior (mahāratha), striking him with various kinds of arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 21 Verse 18. Duryodhana alone attacked him, who was like the enraged, irresistible god of death (Antaka) and a great chariot-warrior (mahāratha), striking him with various kinds of arrows.

sanskrit: तस्यार्जुनो धनुः सूतं केतुमश्वांश्च सायकैः ।हत्वा सप्तभिरेकैकं छत्रं चिच्छेद पत्रिणा ॥१९॥
transliteration: tasyārjuno dhanuḥ sūtaṁ ketumaśvāṁśca sāyakaiḥ ,hatvā saptabhirekaikaṁ chatraṁ ciccheda patriṇā.
english: Arjuna, having struck down his bow, charioteer, banner, and horses, each with seven arrows, then cut his royal parasol (chatra) with another feathered arrow.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 21 Verse 19. Arjuna, having struck down his bow, charioteer, banner, and horses, each with seven arrows, then cut his royal parasol (chatra) with another feathered arrow.

sanskrit: नवमं च समासाद्य व्यसृजत्प्रतिघातिनम् ।दुर्योधनायेषुवरं तं द्रौणिः सप्तधाच्छिनत् ॥२०॥
transliteration: navamaṁ ca samāsādya vyasṛjatpratighātinam ,duryodhanāyeṣuvaraṁ taṁ drauṇiḥ saptadhācchinat.
english: And having taken up a ninth excellent counter-attacking arrow (iṣuvara), he discharged it towards Duryodhana, but Droṇa's son (Drauṇi) cut that very arrow into seven pieces.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 21 Verse 20. And having taken up a ninth excellent counter-attacking arrow (iṣuvara), he discharged it towards Duryodhana, but Droṇa's son (Drauṇi) cut that very arrow into seven pieces.

sanskrit: ततो द्रौणेर्धनुश्छित्त्वा हत्वा चाश्ववराञ्शरैः ।कृपस्यापि तथात्युग्रं धनुश्चिच्छेद पाण्डवः ॥२१॥
transliteration: tato drauṇerdhanuśchittvā hatvā cāśvavarāñśaraiḥ ,kṛpasyāpi tathātyugraṁ dhanuściccheda pāṇḍavaḥ.
english: Then the Pāṇḍava (Arjuna), having cut Droṇa's son's (Drauṇi's) bow and killed his excellent horses (aśvavara) with arrows, similarly cut Kṛpa's very fierce bow as well.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 21 Verse 21. Then the Pāṇḍava (Arjuna), having cut Droṇa's son's (Drauṇi's) bow and killed his excellent horses (aśvavara) with arrows, similarly cut Kṛpa's very fierce bow as well.

sanskrit: हार्दिक्यस्य धनुश्छित्त्वा ध्वजं चाश्वं तथावधीत् ।दुःशासनस्येषुवरं छित्त्वा राधेयमभ्ययात् ॥२२॥
transliteration: hārdikyasya dhanuśchittvā dhvajaṁ cāśvaṁ tathāvadhīt ,duḥśāsanasyeṣuvaraṁ chittvā rādheyamabhyayāt.
english: Having cut Hārdikya's bow and killed his banner and horses, he then, after cutting Duḥśāsana's excellent arrow, advanced towards the son of Rādhā (Karṇa).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 21 Verse 22. Having cut Hārdikya's bow and killed his banner and horses, he then, after cutting Duḥśāsana's excellent arrow, advanced towards the son of Rādhā (Karṇa).

sanskrit: अथ सात्यकिमुत्सृज्य त्वरन्कर्णोऽर्जुनं त्रिभिः ।विद्ध्वा विव्याध विंशत्या कृष्णं पार्थं पुनस्त्रिभिः ॥२३॥
transliteration: atha sātyakimutsṛjya tvarankarṇo'rjunaṁ tribhiḥ ,viddhvā vivyādha viṁśatyā kṛṣṇaṁ pārthaṁ punastribhiḥ.
english: Then Karṇa, hastily leaving Sātyaki, pierced Arjuna with three arrows. He then struck Kṛṣṇa with twenty (arrows) and Pārtha (Arjuna) again with three (arrows).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 21 Verse 23. Then Karṇa, hastily leaving Sātyaki, pierced Arjuna with three arrows. He then struck Kṛṣṇa with twenty (arrows) and Pārtha (Arjuna) again with three (arrows).

sanskrit: अथ सात्यकिरागत्य कर्णं विद्ध्वा शितैः शरैः ।नवत्या नवभिश्चोग्रैः शतेन पुनरार्दयत् ॥२४॥
transliteration: atha sātyakirāgatya karṇaṁ viddhvā śitaiḥ śaraiḥ ,navatyā navabhiścograiḥ śatena punarārdayat.
english: Then Sātyaki, having arrived, pierced Karṇa with sharp arrows, tormenting him again with ninety-nine fierce ones, and (then) with another hundred.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 21 Verse 24. Then Sātyaki, having arrived, pierced Karṇa with sharp arrows, tormenting him again with ninety-nine fierce ones, and (then) with another hundred.

sanskrit: ततः प्रवीराः पाण्डूनां सर्वे कर्णमपीडयन् ।युधामन्युः शिखण्डी च द्रौपदेयाः प्रभद्रकाः ॥२५॥
transliteration: tataḥ pravīrāḥ pāṇḍūnāṁ sarve karṇamapīḍayan ,yudhāmanyuḥ śikhaṇḍī ca draupadeyāḥ prabhadrakāḥ.
english: Then all the excellent heroes of the Pāṇḍavas - Yudhāmanyu, Śikhaṇḍī, the sons of Draupadī, and the Prabhadrakas - afflicted Karṇa.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 21 Verse 25. Then all the excellent heroes of the Pāṇḍavas - Yudhāmanyu, Śikhaṇḍī, the sons of Draupadī, and the Prabhadrakas - afflicted Karṇa.

sanskrit: उत्तमौजा युयुत्सुश्च यमौ पार्षत एव च ।चेदिकारूषमत्स्यानां केकयानां च यद्बलम् ।चेकितानश्च बलवान्धर्मराजश्च सुव्रतः ॥२६॥
transliteration: uttamaujā yuyutsuśca yamau pārṣata eva ca ,cedikārūṣamatsyānāṁ kekayānāṁ ca yadbalam ,cekitānaśca balavāndharmarājaśca suvrataḥ.
english: Uttamaujas, Yuyutsu, the two twins (Nakula and Sahadeva), and Dhṛṣṭadyumna; additionally, the strength belonging to the Cedis, Kārūṣas, Matsyas, and Kekayas; and the mighty Chekitāna, as well as the virtuous King Yudhiṣṭhira (dharmarāja).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 21 Verse 26. Uttamaujas, Yuyutsu, the two twins (Nakula and Sahadeva), and Dhṛṣṭadyumna; additionally, the strength belonging to the Cedis, Kārūṣas, Matsyas, and Kekayas; and the mighty Chekitāna, as well as the virtuous King Yudhiṣṭhira (dharmarāja).

sanskrit: एते रथाश्वद्विरदैः पत्तिभिश्चोग्रविक्रमैः ।परिवार्य रणे कर्णं नानाशस्त्रैरवाकिरन् ।भाषन्तो वाग्भिरुग्राभिः सर्वे कर्णवधे वृताः ॥२७॥
transliteration: ete rathāśvadviradaiḥ pattibhiścogravikramaiḥ ,parivārya raṇe karṇaṁ nānāśastrairavākiran ,bhāṣanto vāgbhirugrābhiḥ sarve karṇavadhe vṛtāḥ.
english: All these warriors and forces, along with their chariots, horses, elephants, and fiercely valorous foot soldiers, surrounded Karṇa in battle. They showered him with various weapons, and all of them, resolved on Karṇa's death, also spoke harsh words.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 21 Verse 27. All these warriors and forces, along with their chariots, horses, elephants, and fiercely valorous foot soldiers, surrounded Karṇa in battle. They showered him with various weapons, and all of them, resolved on Karṇa's death, also spoke harsh words.

sanskrit: तां शस्त्रवृष्टिं बहुधा छित्त्वा कर्णः शितैः शरैः ।अपोवाह स्म तान्सर्वान्द्रुमान्भङ्क्त्वेव मारुतः ॥२८॥
transliteration: tāṁ śastravṛṣṭiṁ bahudhā chittvā karṇaḥ śitaiḥ śaraiḥ ,apovāha sma tānsarvāndrumānbhaṅktveva mārutaḥ.
english: Karṇa, having repelled that shower of weapons (śastravṛṣṭi) in various ways with his sharp arrows, drove away all those (warriors), just as the wind (māruta) breaks and carries away trees.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 21 Verse 28. Karṇa, having repelled that shower of weapons (śastravṛṣṭi) in various ways with his sharp arrows, drove away all those (warriors), just as the wind (māruta) breaks and carries away trees.

sanskrit: रथिनः समहामात्रान्गजानश्वान्ससादिनः ।शरव्रातांश्च संक्रुद्धो निघ्नन्कर्णो व्यदृश्यत ॥२९॥
transliteration: rathinaḥ samahāmātrāngajānaśvānsasādinaḥ ,śaravrātāṁśca saṁkruddho nighnankarṇo vyadṛśyata.
english: Karṇa, intensely enraged, was seen striking down charioteers along with their chief officers, elephants, horses with their riders, and also hosts of archers.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 21 Verse 29. Karṇa, intensely enraged, was seen striking down charioteers along with their chief officers, elephants, horses with their riders, and also hosts of archers.

sanskrit: तद्वध्यमानं पाण्डूनां बलं कर्णास्त्रतेजसा ।विशस्त्रक्षतदेहं च प्राय आसीत्पराङ्मुखम् ॥३०॥
transliteration: tadvadhyamānaṁ pāṇḍūnāṁ balaṁ karṇāstratejasā ,viśastrakṣatadehaṁ ca prāya āsītparāṅmukham.
english: That Pāṇḍava army, being slaughtered by the power of Karṇa's missile (astra) and having its bodies wounded while disarmed, was mostly turning away in retreat.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 21 Verse 30. That Pāṇḍava army, being slaughtered by the power of Karṇa's missile (astra) and having its bodies wounded while disarmed, was mostly turning away in retreat.

sanskrit: अथ कर्णास्त्रमस्त्रेण प्रतिहत्यार्जुनः स्वयम् ।दिशः खं चैव भूमिं च प्रावृणोच्छरवृष्टिभिः ॥३१॥
transliteration: atha karṇāstramastreṇa pratihatyārjunaḥ svayam ,diśaḥ khaṁ caiva bhūmiṁ ca prāvṛṇoccharavṛṣṭibhiḥ.
english: Then Arjuna himself, having countered Karṇa's missile (astra) with his own missile (astra), covered the directions, the sky, and the earth with volleys of arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 21 Verse 31. Then Arjuna himself, having countered Karṇa's missile (astra) with his own missile (astra), covered the directions, the sky, and the earth with volleys of arrows.

sanskrit: मुसलानीव निष्पेतुः परिघा इव चेषवः ।शतघ्न्य इव चाप्यन्ये वज्राण्युग्राणि वापरे ॥३२॥
transliteration: musalānīva niṣpetuḥ parighā iva ceṣavaḥ ,śataghnya iva cāpyanye vajrāṇyugrāṇi vāpare.
english: The arrows flew forth like maces, and some were like iron clubs. Others, again, were like *śataghnīs* (a type of weapon), and still others like terrible thunderbolts.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 21 Verse 32. The arrows flew forth like maces, and some were like iron clubs. Others, again, were like *śataghnīs* (a type of weapon), and still others like terrible thunderbolts.

sanskrit: तैर्वध्यमानं तत्सैन्यं सपत्त्यश्वरथद्विपम् ।निमीलिताक्षमत्यर्थमुदभ्राम्यत्समन्ततः ॥३३॥
transliteration: tairvadhyamānaṁ tatsainyaṁ sapattyaśvarathadvipam ,nimīlitākṣamatyarthamudabhrāmyatsamantataḥ.
english: That army, including its infantry, horses, chariots, and elephants, being devastated by those (arrows), reeled around everywhere with eyes closed and in utter confusion.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 21 Verse 33. That army, including its infantry, horses, chariots, and elephants, being devastated by those (arrows), reeled around everywhere with eyes closed and in utter confusion.

sanskrit: निष्कैवल्यं तदा युद्धं प्रापुरश्वनरद्विपाः ।वध्यमानाः शरैरन्ये तदा भीताः प्रदुद्रुवुः ॥३४॥
transliteration: niṣkaivalyaṁ tadā yuddhaṁ prāpuraśvanaradvipāḥ ,vadhyamānāḥ śarairanye tadā bhītāḥ pradudruvuḥ.
english: Then, horses, men, and elephants engaged in an inextricable battle. Others, being struck down by arrows, then became frightened and fled.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 21 Verse 34. Then, horses, men, and elephants engaged in an inextricable battle. Others, being struck down by arrows, then became frightened and fled.

sanskrit: एवं तेषां तदा युद्धे संसक्तानां जयैषिणाम् ।गिरिमस्तं समासाद्य प्रत्यपद्यत भानुमान् ॥३५॥
transliteration: evaṁ teṣāṁ tadā yuddhe saṁsaktānāṁ jayaiṣiṇām ,girimastaṁ samāsādya pratyapadyata bhānumān.
english: Thus, while they were engaged in that battle, striving for victory, the sun (bhānumān) then reached the western mountain (girimastam), setting.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 21 Verse 35. Thus, while they were engaged in that battle, striving for victory, the sun (bhānumān) then reached the western mountain (girimastam), setting.

sanskrit: तमसा च महाराज रजसा च विशेषतः ।न किंचित्प्रत्यपश्याम शुभं वा यदि वाशुभम् ॥३६॥
transliteration: tamasā ca mahārāja rajasā ca viśeṣataḥ ,na kiṁcitpratyapaśyāma śubhaṁ vā yadi vāśubham.
english: O great king, due to the darkness and especially due to the dust, we could not perceive anything, whether auspicious or inauspicious.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 21 Verse 36. O great king, due to the darkness and especially due to the dust, we could not perceive anything, whether auspicious or inauspicious.

sanskrit: ते त्रसन्तो महेष्वासा रात्रियुद्धस्य भारत ।अपयानं ततश्चक्रुः सहिताः सर्ववाजिभिः ॥३७॥
transliteration: te trasanto maheṣvāsā rātriyuddhasya bhārata ,apayānaṁ tataścakruḥ sahitāḥ sarvavājibhiḥ.
english: O Bhārata, those great archers, fearing the night battle, then made a retreat, accompanied by all their horses.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 21 Verse 37. O Bhārata, those great archers, fearing the night battle, then made a retreat, accompanied by all their horses.

sanskrit: कौरवेषु च यातेषु तदा राजन्दिनक्षये ।जयं सुमनसः प्राप्य पार्थाः स्वशिबिरं ययुः ॥३८॥
transliteration: kauraveṣu ca yāteṣu tadā rājandinakṣaye ,jayaṁ sumanasaḥ prāpya pārthāḥ svaśibiraṁ yayuḥ.
english: Then, O King, at the end of the day, when the Kauravas had departed, the joyful Pārthas, having obtained victory, went to their own camp.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 21 Verse 38. Then, O King, at the end of the day, when the Kauravas had departed, the joyful Pārthas, having obtained victory, went to their own camp.

sanskrit: वादित्रशब्दैर्विविधैः सिंहनादैश्च नर्तितैः ।परानवहसन्तश्च स्तुवन्तश्चाच्युतार्जुनौ ॥३९॥
transliteration: vāditraśabdairvividhaiḥ siṁhanādaiśca nartitaiḥ ,parānavahasantaśca stuvantaścācyutārjunau.
english: Accompanied by various sounds of musical instruments and lion-like roars, with dancing, laughing at their enemies, and praising Acyuta (Kṛṣṇa) and Arjuna.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 21 Verse 39. Accompanied by various sounds of musical instruments and lion-like roars, with dancing, laughing at their enemies, and praising Acyuta (Kṛṣṇa) and Arjuna.

sanskrit: कृतेऽवहारे तैर्वीरैः सैनिकाः सर्व एव ते ।आशिषः पाण्डवेयेषु प्रायुज्यन्त नरेश्वराः ॥४०॥
transliteration: kṛte'vahāre tairvīraiḥ sainikāḥ sarva eva te ,āśiṣaḥ pāṇḍaveyeṣu prāyujyanta nareśvarāḥ.
english: O rulers of men, when the withdrawal was effected by those heroes, all those soldiers offered blessings upon the Pandavas.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 21 Verse 40. O rulers of men, when the withdrawal was effected by those heroes, all those soldiers offered blessings upon the Pandavas.

sanskrit: ततः कृतेऽवहारे च प्रहृष्टाः कुरुपाण्डवाः ।निशायां शिबिरं गत्वा न्यविशन्त नरेश्वराः ॥४१॥
transliteration: tataḥ kṛte'vahāre ca prahṛṣṭāḥ kurupāṇḍavāḥ ,niśāyāṁ śibiraṁ gatvā nyaviśanta nareśvarāḥ.
english: And then, O rulers of men, once the withdrawal was made, the greatly delighted Pandavas, having gone to their camp in the night, settled down.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 21 Verse 41. And then, O rulers of men, once the withdrawal was made, the greatly delighted Pandavas, having gone to their camp in the night, settled down.

sanskrit: यक्षरक्षःपिशाचाश्च श्वापदानि च संघशः ।जग्मुरायोधनं घोरं रुद्रस्यानर्तनोपमम् ॥४२॥
transliteration: yakṣarakṣaḥpiśācāśca śvāpadāni ca saṁghaśaḥ ,jagmurāyodhanaṁ ghoraṁ rudrasyānartanopamam.
english: Yaksha demons, Rākṣasa demons, Pishāca spirits, and predatory beasts, all in large groups, went to the dreadful battlefield, which resembled the terrifying dance of Rudra.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 21 Verse 42. Yaksha demons, Rākṣasa demons, Pishāca spirits, and predatory beasts, all in large groups, went to the dreadful battlefield, which resembled the terrifying dance of Rudra.

sanskrit: धृतराष्ट्र उवाच ।स्वेन च्छन्देन नः सर्वान्नावधीद्व्यक्तमर्जुनः ।न ह्यस्य समरे मुच्येतान्तकोऽप्याततायिनः ॥१॥
transliteration: dhṛtarāṣṭra uvāca ,svena cchandena naḥ sarvānnāvadhīdvyaktamarjunaḥ ,na hyasya samare mucyetāntako'pyātatāyinaḥ.
english: Dhṛtarāṣṭra said: "Surely, Arjuna did not kill all of us by his own free will. For in battle, even Death (antaka) himself, if he were an aggressor, would not escape Arjuna."
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 22 Verse 1. Dhṛtarāṣṭra said: "Surely, Arjuna did not kill all of us by his own free will. For in battle, even Death (antaka) himself, if he were an aggressor, would not escape Arjuna."

sanskrit: पार्थो ह्येकोऽहरद्भद्रामेकश्चाग्निमतर्पयत् ।एकश्चेमां महीं जित्वा चक्रे बलिभृतो नृपान् ॥२॥
transliteration: pārtho hyeko'haradbhadrāmekaścāgnimatarpayat ,ekaścemāṁ mahīṁ jitvā cakre balibhṛto nṛpān.
english: For Pārtha (Arjuna) alone brought prosperity, and he alone satisfied Agni (the fire-god); and alone, having conquered this earth, he made kings into tribute-bearers.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 22 Verse 2. For Pārtha (Arjuna) alone brought prosperity, and he alone satisfied Agni (the fire-god); and alone, having conquered this earth, he made kings into tribute-bearers.

sanskrit: एको निवातकवचानवधीद्दिव्यकार्मुकः ।एकः किरातरूपेण स्थितं शर्वमयोधयत् ॥३॥
transliteration: eko nivātakavacānavadhīddivyakārmukaḥ ,ekaḥ kirātarūpeṇa sthitaṁ śarvamayodhayat.
english: Alone, possessing a divine bow, he killed the Nivātakavacas (a race of powerful demons); and alone, he fought with Śarva (Śiva), who was present in the guise of a Kirāta (a mountain tribesman).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 22 Verse 3. Alone, possessing a divine bow, he killed the Nivātakavacas (a race of powerful demons); and alone, he fought with Śarva (Śiva), who was present in the guise of a Kirāta (a mountain tribesman).

sanskrit: एकोऽभ्यरक्षद्भरतानेको भवमतोषयत् ।तेनैकेन जिताः सर्वे मदीया उग्रतेजसः ।ते न निन्द्याः प्रशस्याश्च यत्ते चक्रुर्ब्रवीहि तत् ॥४॥
transliteration: eko'bhyarakṣadbharatāneko bhavamatoṣayat ,tenaikena jitāḥ sarve madīyā ugratejasaḥ ,te na nindyāḥ praśasyāśca yatte cakrurbravīhi tat.
english: One individual protected the Bhāratas, and one individual pleased Bhava (Śiva). By that one individual, all my fierce-spirited warriors were conquered. Those warriors of mine are not to be blamed, but rather praised. Please tell me what action they performed.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 22 Verse 4. One individual protected the Bhāratas, and one individual pleased Bhava (Śiva). By that one individual, all my fierce-spirited warriors were conquered. Those warriors of mine are not to be blamed, but rather praised. Please tell me what action they performed.

sanskrit: संजय उवाच ।हतप्रहतविध्वस्ता विवर्मायुधवाहनाः ।दीनस्वरा दूयमाना मानिनः शत्रुभिर्जिताः ॥५॥
transliteration: saṁjaya uvāca ,hataprahatavidhvastā vivarmāyudhavāhanāḥ ,dīnasvarā dūyamānā māninaḥ śatrubhirjitāḥ.
english: Sañjaya said: [They were] struck, wounded, and shattered, stripped of their armor, weapons, and vehicles. Though proud, they were conquered by their enemies, their voices sorrowful and their hearts grieving.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 22 Verse 5. Sañjaya said: [They were] struck, wounded, and shattered, stripped of their armor, weapons, and vehicles. Though proud, they were conquered by their enemies, their voices sorrowful and their hearts grieving.

sanskrit: शिबिरस्थाः पुनर्मन्त्रं मन्त्रयन्ति स्म कौरवाः ।भग्नदंष्ट्रा हतविषाः पदाक्रान्ता इवोरगाः ॥६॥
transliteration: śibirasthāḥ punarmantraṁ mantrayanti sma kauravāḥ ,bhagnadaṁṣṭrā hataviṣāḥ padākrāntā ivoragāḥ.
english: The Kauravas, situated in their camp, deliberated a plan again. They were like snakes with broken fangs and destroyed venom, trampled underfoot.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 22 Verse 6. The Kauravas, situated in their camp, deliberated a plan again. They were like snakes with broken fangs and destroyed venom, trampled underfoot.

sanskrit: तानब्रवीत्ततः कर्णः क्रुद्धः सर्प इव श्वसन् ।करं करेणाभिपीड्य प्रेक्षमाणस्तवात्मजम् ॥७॥
transliteration: tānabravīttataḥ karṇaḥ kruddhaḥ sarpa iva śvasan ,karaṁ kareṇābhipīḍya prekṣamāṇastavātmajam.
english: Then, Karṇa, enraged and hissing like a snake, pressed his hand with [the other] hand, and said to them, while looking at your son (Duryodhana).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 22 Verse 7. Then, Karṇa, enraged and hissing like a snake, pressed his hand with [the other] hand, and said to them, while looking at your son (Duryodhana).

sanskrit: यत्तो दृढश्च दक्षश्च धृतिमानर्जुनः सदा ।स बोधयति चाप्येनं प्राप्तकालमधोक्षजः ॥८॥
transliteration: yatto dṛḍhaśca dakṣaśca dhṛtimānarjunaḥ sadā ,sa bodhayati cāpyenaṁ prāptakālamadhokṣajaḥ.
english: Arjuna is always diligent, firm, skillful, and resolute. And Adhokṣaja (Krishna) enlightens him at the appropriate time.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 22 Verse 8. Arjuna is always diligent, firm, skillful, and resolute. And Adhokṣaja (Krishna) enlightens him at the appropriate time.

sanskrit: सहसास्त्रविसर्गेण वयं तेनाद्य वञ्चिताः ।श्वस्त्वहं तस्य संकल्पं सर्वं हन्ता महीपते ॥९॥
transliteration: sahasāstravisargeṇa vayaṁ tenādya vañcitāḥ ,śvastvahaṁ tasya saṁkalpaṁ sarvaṁ hantā mahīpate.
english: By that sudden discharge of weapons, we were deceived today. But tomorrow, O king, I will destroy all his resolve.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 22 Verse 9. By that sudden discharge of weapons, we were deceived today. But tomorrow, O king, I will destroy all his resolve.

sanskrit: एवमुक्तस्तथेत्युक्त्वा सोऽनुजज्ञे नृपोत्तमान् ।सुखोषितास्ते रजनीं हृष्टा युद्धाय निर्ययुः ॥१०॥
transliteration: evamuktastathetyuktvā so'nujajñe nṛpottamān ,sukhoṣitāste rajanīṁ hṛṣṭā yuddhāya niryayuḥ.
english: Having been addressed thus, he said 'So be it' and then permitted the best of kings. Having comfortably spent the night, they joyfully set out for battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 22 Verse 10. Having been addressed thus, he said 'So be it' and then permitted the best of kings. Having comfortably spent the night, they joyfully set out for battle.

sanskrit: तेऽपश्यन्विहितं व्यूहं धर्मराजेन दुर्जयम् ।प्रयत्नात्कुरुमुख्येन बृहस्पत्युशनोमतात् ॥११॥
transliteration: te'paśyanvihitaṁ vyūhaṁ dharmarājena durjayam ,prayatnātkurumukhyena bṛhaspatyuśanomatāt.
english: They saw the battle array, impossible to conquer, which was carefully arranged by King Yudhiṣṭhira (dharma-rāja), the chief among the Kurus, whose strategy was like that of Bṛhaspati and Uśanas (Śukra).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 22 Verse 11. They saw the battle array, impossible to conquer, which was carefully arranged by King Yudhiṣṭhira (dharma-rāja), the chief among the Kurus, whose strategy was like that of Bṛhaspati and Uśanas (Śukra).

sanskrit: अथ प्रतीपकर्तारं सततं विजितात्मनाम् ।सस्मार वृषभस्कन्धं कर्णं दुर्योधनस्तदा ॥१२॥
transliteration: atha pratīpakartāraṁ satataṁ vijitātmanām ,sasmāra vṛṣabhaskandhaṁ karṇaṁ duryodhanastadā.
english: Then Duryodhana remembered Karna, whose shoulders were like those of a bull and who was constantly able to counteract even those who had mastered their inner self (ātman).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 22 Verse 12. Then Duryodhana remembered Karna, whose shoulders were like those of a bull and who was constantly able to counteract even those who had mastered their inner self (ātman).

sanskrit: पुरंदरसमं युद्धे मरुद्गणसमं बले ।कार्तवीर्यसमं वीर्ये कर्णं राज्ञोऽगमन्मनः ।सूतपुत्रं महेष्वासं बन्धुमात्ययिकेष्विव ॥१३॥
transliteration: puraṁdarasamaṁ yuddhe marudgaṇasamaṁ bale ,kārtavīryasamaṁ vīrye karṇaṁ rājño'gamanmanaḥ ,sūtaputraṁ maheṣvāsaṁ bandhumātyayikeṣviva.
english: The king's mind turned to Karna, the great archer, son of a charioteer, who was like Purandara (Indra) in battle, like the host of Maruts in strength, and like Kārtavīrya in prowess, just as one's mind turns to a kinsman in emergencies.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 22 Verse 13. The king's mind turned to Karna, the great archer, son of a charioteer, who was like Purandara (Indra) in battle, like the host of Maruts in strength, and like Kārtavīrya in prowess, just as one's mind turns to a kinsman in emergencies.

sanskrit: धृतराष्ट्र उवाच ।यद्वोऽगमन्मनो मन्दाः कर्णं वैकर्तनं तदा ।अप्यद्राक्षत तं यूयं शीतार्ता इव भास्करम् ॥१४॥
transliteration: dhṛtarāṣṭra uvāca ,yadvo'gamanmano mandāḥ karṇaṁ vaikartanaṁ tadā ,apyadrākṣata taṁ yūyaṁ śītārtā iva bhāskaram.
english: Dhritarashtra said: "O foolish ones, since your minds then turned to Karna, the son of Vikartana, did you actually see him, just as those suffering from cold see the sun?"
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 22 Verse 14. Dhritarashtra said: "O foolish ones, since your minds then turned to Karna, the son of Vikartana, did you actually see him, just as those suffering from cold see the sun?"

sanskrit: कृतेऽवहारे सैन्यानां प्रवृत्ते च रणे पुनः ।कथं वैकर्तनः कर्णस्तत्रायुध्यत संजय ।कथं च पाण्डवाः सर्वे युयुधुस्तत्र सूतजम् ॥१५॥
transliteration: kṛte'vahāre sainyānāṁ pravṛtte ca raṇe punaḥ ,kathaṁ vaikartanaḥ karṇastatrāyudhyata saṁjaya ,kathaṁ ca pāṇḍavāḥ sarve yuyudhustatra sūtajam.
english: Sanjaya, when the armies had withdrawn and the battle had begun again, how did Karna, the son of Vikartana, fight there? And how did all the Pandavas fight against the charioteer's son (Karna) there?
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 22 Verse 15. Sanjaya, when the armies had withdrawn and the battle had begun again, how did Karna, the son of Vikartana, fight there? And how did all the Pandavas fight against the charioteer's son (Karna) there?

sanskrit: कर्णो ह्येको महाबाहुर्हन्यात्पार्थान्ससोमकान् ।कर्णस्य भुजयोर्वीर्यं शक्रविष्णुसमं मतम् ।तथास्त्राणि सुघोराणि विक्रमश्च महात्मनः ॥१६॥
transliteration: karṇo hyeko mahābāhurhanyātpārthānsasomakān ,karṇasya bhujayorvīryaṁ śakraviṣṇusamaṁ matam ,tathāstrāṇi sughorāṇi vikramaśca mahātmanaḥ.
english: Indeed, the mighty-armed Karna alone could slay the sons of Pṛthā (Pandavas) along with the Somakas. The power of Karna's arms is considered equal to that of Indra and Vishnu. Furthermore, his exceedingly dreadful weapons and the prowess of that great soul (mahātman) are also formidable.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 22 Verse 16. Indeed, the mighty-armed Karna alone could slay the sons of Pṛthā (Pandavas) along with the Somakas. The power of Karna's arms is considered equal to that of Indra and Vishnu. Furthermore, his exceedingly dreadful weapons and the prowess of that great soul (mahātman) are also formidable.

sanskrit: दुर्योधनं तदा दृष्ट्वा पाण्डवेन भृशार्दितम् ।पराक्रान्तान्पाण्डुसुतान्दृष्ट्वा चापि महाहवे ॥१७॥
transliteration: duryodhanaṁ tadā dṛṣṭvā pāṇḍavena bhṛśārditam ,parākrāntānpāṇḍusutāndṛṣṭvā cāpi mahāhave.
english: Having then seen Duryodhana greatly distressed by the Pandavas, and having also witnessed the valiant sons of Pandu (pāṇḍusutān) in the great battle (mahāhave)...
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 22 Verse 17. Having then seen Duryodhana greatly distressed by the Pandavas, and having also witnessed the valiant sons of Pandu (pāṇḍusutān) in the great battle (mahāhave)...

sanskrit: कर्णमाश्रित्य संग्रामे दर्पो दुर्योधने पुनः ।जेतुमुत्सहते पार्थान्सपुत्रान्सहकेशवान् ॥१८॥
transliteration: karṇamāśritya saṁgrāme darpo duryodhane punaḥ ,jetumutsahate pārthānsaputrānsahakeśavān.
english: Relying on Karna in battle, Duryodhana's pride (darpa) again endeavors to conquer the Pandavas, along with their sons and Krishna (Keśava).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 22 Verse 18. Relying on Karna in battle, Duryodhana's pride (darpa) again endeavors to conquer the Pandavas, along with their sons and Krishna (Keśava).

sanskrit: अहो बत महद्दुःखं यत्र पाण्डुसुतान्रणे ।नातरद्रभसः कर्णो दैवं नूनं परायणम् ।अहो द्यूतस्य निष्ठेयं घोरा संप्रति वर्तते ॥१९॥
transliteration: aho bata mahadduḥkhaṁ yatra pāṇḍusutānraṇe ,nātaradrabhasaḥ karṇo daivaṁ nūnaṁ parāyaṇam ,aho dyūtasya niṣṭheyaṁ ghorā saṁprati vartate.
english: Alas, what great sorrow! For the impetuous Karna could not vanquish the sons of Pandu (pāṇḍusutān) in battle. Surely, fate (daiva) is the ultimate recourse. Alas, this dreadful consequence of gambling (dyūta) now prevails.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 22 Verse 19. Alas, what great sorrow! For the impetuous Karna could not vanquish the sons of Pandu (pāṇḍusutān) in battle. Surely, fate (daiva) is the ultimate recourse. Alas, this dreadful consequence of gambling (dyūta) now prevails.

sanskrit: अहो दुःखानि तीव्राणि दुर्योधनकृतान्यहम् ।सहिष्यामि सुघोराणि शल्यभूतानि संजय ॥२०॥
transliteration: aho duḥkhāni tīvrāṇi duryodhanakṛtānyaham ,sahiṣyāmi sughorāṇi śalyabhūtāni saṁjaya.
english: Oh, Sañjaya, I shall endure these terrible, excruciating sorrows, which are like piercing darts, all caused by Duryodhana.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 22 Verse 20. Oh, Sañjaya, I shall endure these terrible, excruciating sorrows, which are like piercing darts, all caused by Duryodhana.

sanskrit: सौबलं च तथा तातनीतिमानिति मन्यते ॥२१॥
transliteration: saubalaṁ ca tathā tātanītimāniti manyate.
english: And similarly, dear father, he considers the son of Subala (Śakuni) to be a man of policy.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 22 Verse 21. And similarly, dear father, he considers the son of Subala (Śakuni) to be a man of policy.

sanskrit: युद्धेषु नाम दिव्येषु वर्तमानेषु संजय ।अश्रौषं निहतान्पुत्रान्नित्यमेव च निर्जितान् ॥२२॥
transliteration: yuddheṣu nāma divyeṣu vartamāneṣu saṁjaya ,aśrauṣaṁ nihatānputrānnityameva ca nirjitān.
english: Sañjaya, in the ongoing, indeed divine, battles, I heard that my sons were slain and constantly defeated.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 22 Verse 22. Sañjaya, in the ongoing, indeed divine, battles, I heard that my sons were slain and constantly defeated.

sanskrit: न पाण्डवानां समरे कश्चिदस्ति निवारकः ।स्त्रीमध्यमिव गाहन्ति दैवं हि बलवत्तरम् ॥२३॥
transliteration: na pāṇḍavānāṁ samare kaścidasti nivārakaḥ ,strīmadhyamiva gāhanti daivaṁ hi balavattaram.
english: There is no one who can stop the Pāṇḍavas in battle. They advance as easily as entering among women, for fate is indeed more powerful.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 22 Verse 23. There is no one who can stop the Pāṇḍavas in battle. They advance as easily as entering among women, for fate is indeed more powerful.

sanskrit: संजय उवाच ।अतिक्रान्तं हि यत्कार्यं पश्चाच्चिन्तयतीति च ।तच्चास्य न भवेत्कार्यं चिन्तया च विनश्यति ॥२४॥
transliteration: saṁjaya uvāca ,atikrāntaṁ hi yatkāryaṁ paścāccintayatīti ca ,taccāsya na bhavetkāryaṁ cintayā ca vinaśyati.
english: Sañjaya said: Indeed, any action that has already transpired, and which one subsequently ponders over, will not bear fruit for that person; moreover, it is destroyed by such contemplation.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 22 Verse 24. Sañjaya said: Indeed, any action that has already transpired, and which one subsequently ponders over, will not bear fruit for that person; moreover, it is destroyed by such contemplation.

sanskrit: तदिदं तव कार्यं तु दूरप्राप्तं विजानता ।न कृतं यत्त्वया पूर्वं प्राप्ताप्राप्तविचारणे ॥२५॥
transliteration: tadidaṁ tava kāryaṁ tu dūraprāptaṁ vijānatā ,na kṛtaṁ yattvayā pūrvaṁ prāptāprāptavicāraṇe.
english: This task of yours, which has now reached its culmination, was not properly executed by you previously, even though you were aware of it and had to consider what was achievable and what was not.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 22 Verse 25. This task of yours, which has now reached its culmination, was not properly executed by you previously, even though you were aware of it and had to consider what was achievable and what was not.

sanskrit: उक्तोऽसि बहुधा राजन्मा युध्यस्वेति पाण्डवैः ।गृह्णीषे न च तन्मोहात्पाण्डवेषु विशां पते ॥२६॥
transliteration: ukto'si bahudhā rājanmā yudhyasveti pāṇḍavaiḥ ,gṛhṇīṣe na ca tanmohātpāṇḍaveṣu viśāṁ pate.
english: O King, you have been repeatedly advised by the Pāṇḍavas, "Do not wage war!" Yet, O lord of the people, you do not heed this advice regarding the Pāṇḍavas, due to delusion (moha).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 22 Verse 26. O King, you have been repeatedly advised by the Pāṇḍavas, "Do not wage war!" Yet, O lord of the people, you do not heed this advice regarding the Pāṇḍavas, due to delusion (moha).

sanskrit: त्वया पापानि घोराणि समाचीर्णानि पाण्डुषु ।त्वत्कृते वर्तते घोरः पार्थिवानां जनक्षयः ॥२७॥
transliteration: tvayā pāpāni ghorāṇi samācīrṇāni pāṇḍuṣu ,tvatkṛte vartate ghoraḥ pārthivānāṁ janakṣayaḥ.
english: You have committed terrible sins against the Pāṇḍavas. It is because of you that a dreadful destruction of kings (pārthivānām) is occurring.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 22 Verse 27. You have committed terrible sins against the Pāṇḍavas. It is because of you that a dreadful destruction of kings (pārthivānām) is occurring.

sanskrit: तत्त्विदानीमतिक्रम्य मा शुचो भरतर्षभ ।शृणु सर्वं यथावृत्तं घोरं वैशसमच्युत ॥२८॥
transliteration: tattvidānīmatikramya mā śuco bharatarṣabha ,śṛṇu sarvaṁ yathāvṛttaṁ ghoraṁ vaiśasamacyuta.
english: Now, putting that aside, do not grieve, O best of the Bharatas. Listen, O Acyuta, to everything that has transpired, this terrible slaughter.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 22 Verse 28. Now, putting that aside, do not grieve, O best of the Bharatas. Listen, O Acyuta, to everything that has transpired, this terrible slaughter.

sanskrit: प्रभातायां रजन्यां तु कर्णो राजानमभ्ययात् ।समेत्य च महाबाहुर्दुर्योधनमभाषत ॥२९॥
transliteration: prabhātāyāṁ rajanyāṁ tu karṇo rājānamabhyayāt ,sametya ca mahābāhurduryodhanamabhāṣata.
english: But when the night dawned, Karṇa approached the king. And having met [him], the mighty-armed one (Karṇa) spoke to Duryodhana.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 22 Verse 29. But when the night dawned, Karṇa approached the king. And having met [him], the mighty-armed one (Karṇa) spoke to Duryodhana.

sanskrit: अद्य राजन्समेष्यामि पाण्डवेन यशस्विना ।हनिष्यामि च तं वीरं स वा मां निहनिष्यति ॥३०॥
transliteration: adya rājansameṣyāmi pāṇḍavena yaśasvinā ,haniṣyāmi ca taṁ vīraṁ sa vā māṁ nihaniṣyati.
english: Today, O king, I shall confront the glorious Pāṇḍava. I shall either slay that hero, or he will slay me.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 22 Verse 30. Today, O king, I shall confront the glorious Pāṇḍava. I shall either slay that hero, or he will slay me.

sanskrit: बहुत्वान्मम कार्याणां तथा पार्थस्य पार्थिव ।नाभूत्समागमो राजन्मम चैवार्जुनस्य च ॥३१॥
transliteration: bahutvānmama kāryāṇāṁ tathā pārthasya pārthiva ,nābhūtsamāgamo rājanmama caivārjunasya ca.
english: O ruler of the earth, O king, due to the multitude of my duties, and similarly Pārtha's (Arjuna's), there has been no encounter between me and Arjuna.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 22 Verse 31. O ruler of the earth, O king, due to the multitude of my duties, and similarly Pārtha's (Arjuna's), there has been no encounter between me and Arjuna.

sanskrit: इदं तु मे यथाप्रज्ञं शृणु वाक्यं विशां पते ।अनिहत्य रणे पार्थं नाहमेष्यामि भारत ॥३२॥
transliteration: idaṁ tu me yathāprajñaṁ śṛṇu vākyaṁ viśāṁ pate ,anihatya raṇe pārthaṁ nāhameṣyāmi bhārata.
english: O lord of the people (viśām pate), O Bhārata, hear this statement of mine, made to the best of my understanding. I shall not return without having killed Pārtha in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 22 Verse 32. O lord of the people (viśām pate), O Bhārata, hear this statement of mine, made to the best of my understanding. I shall not return without having killed Pārtha in battle.

sanskrit: हतप्रवीरे सैन्येऽस्मिन्मयि चैव स्थिते युधि ।अभियास्यति मां पार्थः शक्रशक्त्या विनाकृतम् ॥३३॥
transliteration: hatapravīre sainye'sminmayi caiva sthite yudhi ,abhiyāsyati māṁ pārthaḥ śakraśaktyā vinākṛtam.
english: While this army (sainya), whose chief warriors have been slain, and I myself am stationed in battle (yudhi), Pārtha will attack me, who am deprived of Śakra's power (śakti).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 22 Verse 33. While this army (sainya), whose chief warriors have been slain, and I myself am stationed in battle (yudhi), Pārtha will attack me, who am deprived of Śakra's power (śakti).

sanskrit: ततः श्रेयस्करं यत्ते तन्निबोध जनेश्वर ।आयुधानां च यद्वीर्यं द्रव्याणामर्जुनस्य च ॥३४॥
transliteration: tataḥ śreyaskaraṁ yatte tannibodha janeśvara ,āyudhānāṁ ca yadvīryaṁ dravyāṇāmarjunasya ca.
english: Therefore, O lord of people (janeśvara), understand what is beneficial for you, and also the prowess (vīrya) of the weapons, of the substances, and of Arjuna.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 22 Verse 34. Therefore, O lord of people (janeśvara), understand what is beneficial for you, and also the prowess (vīrya) of the weapons, of the substances, and of Arjuna.

sanskrit: कायस्य महतो भेदे लाघवे दूरपातने ।सौष्ठवे चास्त्रयोगे च सव्यसाची न मत्समः ॥३५॥
transliteration: kāyasya mahato bhede lāghave dūrapātane ,sauṣṭhave cāstrayoge ca savyasācī na matsamaḥ.
english: In piercing a mighty body, in agility, in long-range shooting, and in the skillful application of weapons (astra), Savyasācī (Arjuna) is not my equal.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 22 Verse 35. In piercing a mighty body, in agility, in long-range shooting, and in the skillful application of weapons (astra), Savyasācī (Arjuna) is not my equal.

sanskrit: सर्वायुधमहामात्रं विजयं नाम तद्धनुः ।इन्द्रार्थमभिकामेन निर्मितं विश्वकर्मणा ॥३६॥
transliteration: sarvāyudhamahāmātraṁ vijayaṁ nāma taddhanuḥ ,indrārthamabhikāmena nirmitaṁ viśvakarmaṇā.
english: That bow, named Vijaya, which is a master among all weapons, was fashioned by Viśvakarmā for Indra's sake, as he ardently desired it.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 22 Verse 36. That bow, named Vijaya, which is a master among all weapons, was fashioned by Viśvakarmā for Indra's sake, as he ardently desired it.

sanskrit: येन दैत्यगणान्राजञ्जितवान्वै शतक्रतुः ।यस्य घोषेण दैत्यानां विमुह्यन्ति दिशो दश ।तद्भार्गवाय प्रायच्छच्छक्रः परमसंमतम् ॥३७॥
transliteration: yena daityagaṇānrājañjitavānvai śatakratuḥ ,yasya ghoṣeṇa daityānāṁ vimuhyanti diśo daśa ,tadbhārgavāya prāyacchacchakraḥ paramasaṁmatam.
english: O king, by which bow Indra (śatakratuḥ) indeed conquered the hosts of demons; and by whose roar the ten directions become utterly bewildered for the demons. That exceedingly revered (bow), Indra (śakraḥ) gave to Bhārgava.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 22 Verse 37. O king, by which bow Indra (śatakratuḥ) indeed conquered the hosts of demons; and by whose roar the ten directions become utterly bewildered for the demons. That exceedingly revered (bow), Indra (śakraḥ) gave to Bhārgava.

sanskrit: तद्दिव्यं भार्गवो मह्यमददाद्धनुरुत्तमम् ।येन योत्स्ये महाबाहुमर्जुनं जयतां वरम् ।यथेन्द्रः समरे सर्वान्दैतेयान्वै समागतान् ॥३८॥
transliteration: taddivyaṁ bhārgavo mahyamadadāddhanuruttamam ,yena yotsye mahābāhumarjunaṁ jayatāṁ varam ,yathendraḥ samare sarvāndaiteyānvai samāgatān.
english: Bhārgava gave me that divine, excellent bow. With it, I will fight the mighty-armed Arjuna, the best among conquerors, just as Indra in battle indeed fought all the assembled demons (daiteya).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 22 Verse 38. Bhārgava gave me that divine, excellent bow. With it, I will fight the mighty-armed Arjuna, the best among conquerors, just as Indra in battle indeed fought all the assembled demons (daiteya).

sanskrit: धनुर्घोरं रामदत्तं गाण्डीवात्तद्विशिष्यते ।त्रिःसप्तकृत्वः पृथिवी धनुषा तेन निर्जिता ॥३९॥
transliteration: dhanurghoraṁ rāmadattaṁ gāṇḍīvāttadviśiṣyate ,triḥsaptakṛtvaḥ pṛthivī dhanuṣā tena nirjitā.
english: This formidable bow, given by Rāma, is superior to Gāṇḍīva. By that bow, the earth was conquered twenty-one times.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 22 Verse 39. This formidable bow, given by Rāma, is superior to Gāṇḍīva. By that bow, the earth was conquered twenty-one times.

sanskrit: धनुषो यस्य कर्माणि दिव्यानि प्राह भार्गवः ।तद्रामो ह्यददान्मह्यं येन योत्स्यामि पाण्डवम् ॥४०॥
transliteration: dhanuṣo yasya karmāṇi divyāni prāha bhārgavaḥ ,tadrāmo hyadadānmahyaṁ yena yotsyāmi pāṇḍavam.
english: Bhargava indeed spoke of the divine deeds (karma) of this bow. Rama then gave that very bow to me, with which I will fight the Pandava (Arjuna).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 22 Verse 40. Bhargava indeed spoke of the divine deeds (karma) of this bow. Rama then gave that very bow to me, with which I will fight the Pandava (Arjuna).

sanskrit: अद्य दुर्योधनाहं त्वां नन्दयिष्ये सबान्धवम् ।निहत्य समरे वीरमर्जुनं जयतां वरम् ॥४१॥
transliteration: adya duryodhanāhaṁ tvāṁ nandayiṣye sabāndhavam ,nihatya samare vīramarjunaṁ jayatāṁ varam.
english: Today, O Duryodhana, I will delight you and your relatives by slaying in battle the heroic Arjuna, who is the best among conquerors.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 22 Verse 41. Today, O Duryodhana, I will delight you and your relatives by slaying in battle the heroic Arjuna, who is the best among conquerors.

sanskrit: सपर्वतवनद्वीपा हतद्विड्भूः ससागरा ।पुत्रपौत्रप्रतिष्ठा ते भविष्यत्यद्य पार्थिव ॥४२॥
transliteration: saparvatavanadvīpā hatadviḍbhūḥ sasāgarā ,putrapautrapratiṣṭhā te bhaviṣyatyadya pārthiva.
english: O king, today, the earth – with its mountains, forests, islands, and oceans – will be firmly established for your sons and grandsons, with all enemies slain.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 22 Verse 42. O king, today, the earth – with its mountains, forests, islands, and oceans – will be firmly established for your sons and grandsons, with all enemies slain.

sanskrit: नासाध्यं विद्यते मेऽद्य त्वत्प्रियार्थं विशेषतः ।सम्यग्धर्मानुरक्तस्य सिद्धिरात्मवतो यथा ॥४३॥
transliteration: nāsādhyaṁ vidyate me'dya tvatpriyārthaṁ viśeṣataḥ ,samyagdharmānuraktasya siddhirātmavato yathā.
english: Today, nothing is impossible for me, especially for your sake. It will be just like the attainment of success for one who is properly devoted to natural law (dharma) and possesses a spiritual self (ātman).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 22 Verse 43. Today, nothing is impossible for me, especially for your sake. It will be just like the attainment of success for one who is properly devoted to natural law (dharma) and possesses a spiritual self (ātman).

sanskrit: न हि मां समरे सोढुं स शक्तोऽग्निं तरुर्यथा ।अवश्यं तु मया वाच्यं येन हीनोऽस्मि फल्गुनात् ॥४४॥
transliteration: na hi māṁ samare soḍhuṁ sa śakto'gniṁ taruryathā ,avaśyaṁ tu mayā vācyaṁ yena hīno'smi phalgunāt.
english: Indeed, he (Arjuna) is not capable of enduring me in battle, just as a tree cannot endure fire. However, I must certainly speak of that by which I am inferior to Arjuna (Phalguna).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 22 Verse 44. Indeed, he (Arjuna) is not capable of enduring me in battle, just as a tree cannot endure fire. However, I must certainly speak of that by which I am inferior to Arjuna (Phalguna).

sanskrit: ज्या तस्य धनुषो दिव्या तथाक्षय्यौ महेषुधी ।तस्य दिव्यं धनुः श्रेष्ठं गाण्डीवमजरं युधि ॥४५॥
transliteration: jyā tasya dhanuṣo divyā tathākṣayyau maheṣudhī ,tasya divyaṁ dhanuḥ śreṣṭhaṁ gāṇḍīvamajaraṁ yudhi.
english: The string of his bow is divine, and his two great quivers are inexhaustible. His supreme, divine bow, the Gāṇḍīva, is undecaying in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 22 Verse 45. The string of his bow is divine, and his two great quivers are inexhaustible. His supreme, divine bow, the Gāṇḍīva, is undecaying in battle.

sanskrit: विजयं च महद्दिव्यं ममापि धनुरुत्तमम् ।तत्राहमधिकः पार्थाद्धनुषा तेन पार्थिव ॥४६॥
transliteration: vijayaṁ ca mahaddivyaṁ mamāpi dhanuruttamam ,tatrāhamadhikaḥ pārthāddhanuṣā tena pārthiva.
english: And my great, divine, excellent bow is also victorious. In that regard, O King (pārthiva), I am superior to Arjuna (Pārtha) with that bow.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 22 Verse 46. And my great, divine, excellent bow is also victorious. In that regard, O King (pārthiva), I am superior to Arjuna (Pārtha) with that bow.

sanskrit: मया चाभ्यधिको वीरः पाण्डवस्तन्निबोध मे ।रश्मिग्राहश्च दाशार्हः सर्वलोकनमस्कृतः ॥४७॥
transliteration: mayā cābhyadhiko vīraḥ pāṇḍavastannibodha me ,raśmigrāhaśca dāśārhaḥ sarvalokanamaskṛtaḥ.
english: And a Pāṇḍava hero is indeed vastly superior to me; understand that from me. And there is the Dāśārha (Kṛṣṇa), the charioteer (raśmi-grāha), worshipped by all the worlds.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 22 Verse 47. And a Pāṇḍava hero is indeed vastly superior to me; understand that from me. And there is the Dāśārha (Kṛṣṇa), the charioteer (raśmi-grāha), worshipped by all the worlds.

sanskrit: अग्निदत्तश्च वै दिव्यो रथः काञ्चनभूषणः ।अच्छेद्यः सर्वतो वीर वाजिनश्च मनोजवाः ।ध्वजश्च दिव्यो द्युतिमान्वानरो विस्मयंकरः ॥४८॥
transliteration: agnidattaśca vai divyo rathaḥ kāñcanabhūṣaṇaḥ ,acchedyaḥ sarvato vīra vājinaśca manojavāḥ ,dhvajaśca divyo dyutimānvānaro vismayaṁkaraḥ.
english: And a divine chariot, given by Agni, adorned with gold, impenetrable on all sides, O hero. Its horses are swift as thought, and its divine, radiant banner, bearing a monkey, causes astonishment.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 22 Verse 48. And a divine chariot, given by Agni, adorned with gold, impenetrable on all sides, O hero. Its horses are swift as thought, and its divine, radiant banner, bearing a monkey, causes astonishment.

sanskrit: कृष्णश्च स्रष्टा जगतो रथं तमभिरक्षति ।एभिर्द्रव्यैरहं हीनो योद्धुमिच्छामि पाण्डवम् ॥४९॥
transliteration: kṛṣṇaśca sraṣṭā jagato rathaṁ tamabhirakṣati ,ebhirdravyairahaṁ hīno yoddhumicchāmi pāṇḍavam.
english: And Kṛṣṇa, the creator of the world, protects that chariot. Even though I am devoid of these resources, I still wish to fight the Pāṇḍava (Arjuna).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 22 Verse 49. And Kṛṣṇa, the creator of the world, protects that chariot. Even though I am devoid of these resources, I still wish to fight the Pāṇḍava (Arjuna).

sanskrit: अयं तु सदृशो वीरः शल्यः समितिशोभनः ।सारथ्यं यदि मे कुर्याद्ध्रुवस्ते विजयो भवेत् ॥५०॥
transliteration: ayaṁ tu sadṛśo vīraḥ śalyaḥ samitiśobhanaḥ ,sārathyaṁ yadi me kuryāddhruvaste vijayo bhavet.
english: However, this hero Śalya, who is distinguished in battle, is a suitable match. If he were to act as my charioteer, then your victory would certainly be assured.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 22 Verse 50. However, this hero Śalya, who is distinguished in battle, is a suitable match. If he were to act as my charioteer, then your victory would certainly be assured.

sanskrit: तस्य मे सारथिः शल्यो भवत्वसुकरः परैः ।नाराचान्गार्ध्रपत्रांश्च शकटानि वहन्तु मे ॥५१॥
transliteration: tasya me sārathiḥ śalyo bhavatvasukaraḥ paraiḥ ,nārācāngārdhrapatrāṁśca śakaṭāni vahantu me.
english: Let that Śalya be my charioteer, who is difficult for enemies to overcome. And let wagons carry my iron arrows (nārāca) feathered with vulture plumes.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 22 Verse 51. Let that Śalya be my charioteer, who is difficult for enemies to overcome. And let wagons carry my iron arrows (nārāca) feathered with vulture plumes.

sanskrit: रथाश्च मुख्या राजेन्द्र युक्ता वाजिभिरुत्तमैः ।आयान्तु पश्चात्सततं मामेव भरतर्षभ ॥५२॥
transliteration: rathāśca mukhyā rājendra yuktā vājibhiruttamaiḥ ,āyāntu paścātsatataṁ māmeva bharatarṣabha.
english: O king of kings, O best of Bharatas, let the principal chariots, yoked with the best horses, always come behind me.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 22 Verse 52. O king of kings, O best of Bharatas, let the principal chariots, yoked with the best horses, always come behind me.

sanskrit: एवमभ्यधिकः पार्थाद्भविष्यामि गुणैरहम् ।शल्यो ह्यभ्यधिकः कृष्णादर्जुनादधिको ह्यहम् ॥५३॥
transliteration: evamabhyadhikaḥ pārthādbhaviṣyāmi guṇairaham ,śalyo hyabhyadhikaḥ kṛṣṇādarjunādadhiko hyaham.
english: Thus, I shall be superior to Pārtha (Arjuna) in qualities. Indeed, Śalya is superior to Kṛṣṇa, and I am certainly superior to Arjuna.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 22 Verse 53. Thus, I shall be superior to Pārtha (Arjuna) in qualities. Indeed, Śalya is superior to Kṛṣṇa, and I am certainly superior to Arjuna.

sanskrit: यथाश्वहृदयं वेद दाशार्हः परवीरहा ।तथा शल्योऽपि जानीते हयानां वै महारथः ॥५४॥
transliteration: yathāśvahṛdayaṁ veda dāśārhaḥ paravīrahā ,tathā śalyo'pi jānīte hayānāṁ vai mahārathaḥ.
english: Just as Kṛṣṇa (dāśārha), the destroyer of enemy heroes, knows the science of horses, so also Śalya, the great chariot warrior, indeed understands horses.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 22 Verse 54. Just as Kṛṣṇa (dāśārha), the destroyer of enemy heroes, knows the science of horses, so also Śalya, the great chariot warrior, indeed understands horses.

sanskrit: बाहुवीर्ये समो नास्ति मद्रराजस्य कश्चन ।तथास्त्रैर्मत्समो नास्ति कश्चिदेव धनुर्धरः ॥५५॥
transliteration: bāhuvīrye samo nāsti madrarājasya kaścana ,tathāstrairmatsamo nāsti kaścideva dhanurdharaḥ.
english: No one is equal to the king of Madras (Śalya) in arm-strength. Similarly, among archers, no one is indeed equal to me in wielding weapons.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 22 Verse 55. No one is equal to the king of Madras (Śalya) in arm-strength. Similarly, among archers, no one is indeed equal to me in wielding weapons.
