sanskrit: तथा शल्यसमो नास्ति हययाने ह कश्चन ।सोऽयमभ्यधिकः पार्थाद्भविष्यति रथो मम ॥५६॥
transliteration: tathā śalyasamo nāsti hayayāne ha kaścana ,so'yamabhyadhikaḥ pārthādbhaviṣyati ratho mama.
english: Indeed, there is no one equal to Śalya in driving horses. This very man will be my charioteer, superior even to Pārtha (Arjuna).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 22 Verse 56. Indeed, there is no one equal to Śalya in driving horses. This very man will be my charioteer, superior even to Pārtha (Arjuna).

sanskrit: एतत्कृतं महाराज त्वयेच्छामि परंतप ।एवं कृते कृतं मह्यं सर्वकामैर्भविष्यति ॥५७॥
transliteration: etatkṛtaṁ mahārāja tvayecchāmi paraṁtapa ,evaṁ kṛte kṛtaṁ mahyaṁ sarvakāmairbhaviṣyati.
english: O great king, tormentor of foes, I wish that this be done by you. Once this is accomplished, all my desires will be fulfilled.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 22 Verse 57. O great king, tormentor of foes, I wish that this be done by you. Once this is accomplished, all my desires will be fulfilled.

sanskrit: ततो द्रष्टासि समरे यत्करिष्यामि भारत ।सर्वथा पाण्डवान्सर्वाञ्जेष्याम्यद्य समागतान् ॥५८॥
transliteration: tato draṣṭāsi samare yatkariṣyāmi bhārata ,sarvathā pāṇḍavānsarvāñjeṣyāmyadya samāgatān.
english: Then, O Bhārata, you will witness in battle what I shall accomplish. I shall certainly conquer all the assembled Pāṇḍavas today.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 22 Verse 58. Then, O Bhārata, you will witness in battle what I shall accomplish. I shall certainly conquer all the assembled Pāṇḍavas today.

sanskrit: दुर्योधन उवाच ।सर्वमेतत्करिष्यामि यथा त्वं कर्ण मन्यसे ।सोपासङ्गा रथाः साश्वा अनुयास्यन्ति सूतज ॥५९॥
transliteration: duryodhana uvāca ,sarvametatkariṣyāmi yathā tvaṁ karṇa manyase ,sopāsaṅgā rathāḥ sāśvā anuyāsyanti sūtaja.
english: Duryodhana said: 'I shall arrange all this just as you wish, O Karṇa. The chariots, complete with quivers and horses, will accompany you, O son of a charioteer (sūta-ja).'
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 22 Verse 59. Duryodhana said: 'I shall arrange all this just as you wish, O Karṇa. The chariots, complete with quivers and horses, will accompany you, O son of a charioteer (sūta-ja).'

sanskrit: नाराचान्गार्ध्रपक्षांश्च शकटानि वहन्तु ते ।अनुयास्याम कर्ण त्वां वयं सर्वे च पार्थिवाः ॥६०॥
transliteration: nārācāngārdhrapakṣāṁśca śakaṭāni vahantu te ,anuyāsyāma karṇa tvāṁ vayaṁ sarve ca pārthivāḥ.
english: Let them carry the iron arrows, the eagle-feathered shafts, and the carts. O Karna, all of us kings will follow you.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 22 Verse 60. Let them carry the iron arrows, the eagle-feathered shafts, and the carts. O Karna, all of us kings will follow you.

sanskrit: संजय उवाच ।एवमुक्त्वा महाराज तव पुत्राः प्रतापवान् ।अभिगम्याब्रवीद्राजा मद्रराजमिदं वचः ॥६१॥
transliteration: saṁjaya uvāca ,evamuktvā mahārāja tava putrāḥ pratāpavān ,abhigamyābravīdrājā madrarājamidaṁ vacaḥ.
english: Sañjaya said: O great king, having spoken thus, your mighty son, the king, approached the king of Madras and spoke these words.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 22 Verse 61. Sañjaya said: O great king, having spoken thus, your mighty son, the king, approached the king of Madras and spoke these words.

sanskrit: संजय उवाच ।पुत्रस्तव महाराज मद्रराजमिदं वचः ।विनयेनोपसंगम्य प्रणयाद्वाक्यमब्रवीत् ॥१॥
transliteration: saṁjaya uvāca ,putrastava mahārāja madrarājamidaṁ vacaḥ ,vinayenopasaṁgamya praṇayādvākyamabravīt.
english: Sañjaya said: O great king, your son, having approached the king of Madras with humility and out of affection, spoke these words.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 23 Verse 1. Sañjaya said: O great king, your son, having approached the king of Madras with humility and out of affection, spoke these words.

sanskrit: सत्यव्रत महाभाग द्विषतामघवर्धन ।मद्रेश्वर रणे शूर परसैन्यभयंकर ॥२॥
transliteration: satyavrata mahābhāga dviṣatāmaghavardhana ,madreśvara raṇe śūra parasainyabhayaṁkara.
english: O keeper of true vows, O greatly glorious one, O you who increase the misfortune of enemies! O Lord of Madras, O hero in battle, O terror to enemy armies!
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 23 Verse 2. O keeper of true vows, O greatly glorious one, O you who increase the misfortune of enemies! O Lord of Madras, O hero in battle, O terror to enemy armies!

sanskrit: श्रुतवानसि कर्णस्य ब्रुवतो वदतां वर ।यथा नृपतिसिंहानां मध्ये त्वां वरयत्ययम् ॥३॥
transliteration: śrutavānasi karṇasya bruvato vadatāṁ vara ,yathā nṛpatisiṁhānāṁ madhye tvāṁ varayatyayam.
english: O best among speakers, you have heard Karna speaking about how this one (Karna) selects you from among the foremost kings.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 23 Verse 3. O best among speakers, you have heard Karna speaking about how this one (Karna) selects you from among the foremost kings.

sanskrit: तस्मात्पार्थविनाशार्थं हितार्थं मम चैव हि ।सारथ्यं रथिनां श्रेष्ठ सुमनाः कर्तुमर्हसि ॥४॥
transliteration: tasmātpārthavināśārthaṁ hitārthaṁ mama caiva hi ,sārathyaṁ rathināṁ śreṣṭha sumanāḥ kartumarhasi.
english: Therefore, O best among warriors on chariots, you should gladly undertake the role of a charioteer for the destruction of Arjuna (Pārtha) and indeed for my benefit.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 23 Verse 4. Therefore, O best among warriors on chariots, you should gladly undertake the role of a charioteer for the destruction of Arjuna (Pārtha) and indeed for my benefit.

sanskrit: अस्याभीशुग्रहो लोके नान्योऽस्ति भवता समः ।स पातु सर्वतः कर्णं भवान्ब्रह्मेव शंकरम् ॥५॥
transliteration: asyābhīśugraho loke nānyo'sti bhavatā samaḥ ,sa pātu sarvataḥ karṇaṁ bhavānbrahmeva śaṁkaram.
english: In this world, there is no other charioteer for him (Karna) who is your equal. May your honor protect Karna from all sides, just as Brahmā (brahmā) protects Śaṃkara.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 23 Verse 5. In this world, there is no other charioteer for him (Karna) who is your equal. May your honor protect Karna from all sides, just as Brahmā (brahmā) protects Śaṃkara.

sanskrit: पार्थस्य सचिवः कृष्णो यथाभीशुग्रहो वरः ।तथा त्वमपि राधेयं सर्वतः परिपालय ॥६॥
transliteration: pārthasya sacivaḥ kṛṣṇo yathābhīśugraho varaḥ ,tathā tvamapi rādheyaṁ sarvataḥ paripālaya.
english: Just as Kṛṣṇa, the excellent charioteer and counselor, is for Arjuna (Pārtha), so you also protect Karna (Rādheya) from all sides.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 23 Verse 6. Just as Kṛṣṇa, the excellent charioteer and counselor, is for Arjuna (Pārtha), so you also protect Karna (Rādheya) from all sides.

sanskrit: भीष्मो द्रोणः कृपः कर्णो भवान्भोजश्च वीर्यवान् ।शकुनिः सौबलो द्रौणिरहमेव च नो बलम् ।एषामेव कृतो भागो नवधा पृतनापते ॥७॥
transliteration: bhīṣmo droṇaḥ kṛpaḥ karṇo bhavānbhojaśca vīryavān ,śakuniḥ saubalo drauṇirahameva ca no balam ,eṣāmeva kṛto bhāgo navadhā pṛtanāpate.
english: Bhīṣma, Droṇa, Kṛpa, Karṇa, you (Bhūriśravas), and the mighty Bhoja (Kṛtavarman); Śakuni, the son of Subala, Droṇi (Aśvatthāman), and I myself - this indeed is our strength. O commander of the army, the division of these (warriors) into nine parts has truly been made.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 23 Verse 7. Bhīṣma, Droṇa, Kṛpa, Karṇa, you (Bhūriśravas), and the mighty Bhoja (Kṛtavarman); Śakuni, the son of Subala, Droṇi (Aśvatthāman), and I myself - this indeed is our strength. O commander of the army, the division of these (warriors) into nine parts has truly been made.

sanskrit: नैव भागोऽत्र भीष्मस्य द्रोणस्य च महात्मनः ।ताभ्यामतीत्य तौ भागौ निहता मम शत्रवः ॥८॥
transliteration: naiva bhāgo'tra bhīṣmasya droṇasya ca mahātmanaḥ ,tābhyāmatītya tau bhāgau nihatā mama śatravaḥ.
english: Here, indeed, there is no share of ultimate victory for Bhīṣma and the great-souled (mahātman) Droṇa. My enemies were slain by them, (but it was only) after they (Bhīṣma and Droṇa) had neglected or been unable to maintain those two divisions (of the army).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 23 Verse 8. Here, indeed, there is no share of ultimate victory for Bhīṣma and the great-souled (mahātman) Droṇa. My enemies were slain by them, (but it was only) after they (Bhīṣma and Droṇa) had neglected or been unable to maintain those two divisions (of the army).

sanskrit: वृद्धौ हि तौ नरव्याघ्रौ छलेन निहतौ च तौ ।कृत्वा नसुकरं कर्म गतौ स्वर्गमितोऽनघ ॥९॥
transliteration: vṛddhau hi tau naravyāghrau chalena nihatau ca tau ,kṛtvā nasukaraṁ karma gatau svargamito'nagha.
english: Indeed, those two old tiger-like men (Bhīṣma and Droṇa) were slain by deceit. Having accomplished a difficult deed, those two went to heaven from this world, O sinless one.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 23 Verse 9. Indeed, those two old tiger-like men (Bhīṣma and Droṇa) were slain by deceit. Having accomplished a difficult deed, those two went to heaven from this world, O sinless one.

sanskrit: तथान्ये पुरुषव्याघ्राः परैर्विनिहता युधि ।अस्मदीयाश्च बहवः स्वर्गायोपगता रणे ।त्यक्त्वा प्राणान्यथाशक्ति चेष्टाः कृत्वा च पुष्कलाः ॥१०॥
transliteration: tathānye puruṣavyāghrāḥ parairvinihatā yudhi ,asmadīyāśca bahavaḥ svargāyopagatā raṇe ,tyaktvā prāṇānyathāśakti ceṣṭāḥ kṛtvā ca puṣkalāḥ.
english: Similarly, other tiger-like men (excellent warriors) were slain in battle by the enemies. And many of our own warriors also went to heaven in the battle, having given up their lives according to their capacity (śakti) and having performed numerous valiant efforts.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 23 Verse 10. Similarly, other tiger-like men (excellent warriors) were slain in battle by the enemies. And many of our own warriors also went to heaven in the battle, having given up their lives according to their capacity (śakti) and having performed numerous valiant efforts.

sanskrit: कर्णो ह्येको महाबाहुरस्मत्प्रियहिते रतः ।भवांश्च पुरुषव्याघ्र सर्वलोकमहारथः ।तस्मिञ्जयाशा विपुला मम मद्रजनाधिप ॥११॥
transliteration: karṇo hyeko mahābāhurasmatpriyahite rataḥ ,bhavāṁśca puruṣavyāghra sarvalokamahārathaḥ ,tasmiñjayāśā vipulā mama madrajanādhipa.
english: O lord of the Madra people, indeed, Karna alone, the mighty-armed hero, is devoted to our welfare and benefit. And you, O tiger among men, are a great chariot-warrior renowned throughout all worlds. In him (Karna), my hope for victory is immense.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 23 Verse 11. O lord of the Madra people, indeed, Karna alone, the mighty-armed hero, is devoted to our welfare and benefit. And you, O tiger among men, are a great chariot-warrior renowned throughout all worlds. In him (Karna), my hope for victory is immense.

sanskrit: पार्थस्य समरे कृष्णो यथाभीशुवरग्रहः ।तेन युक्तो रणे पार्थो रक्ष्यमाणश्च पार्थिव ।यानि कर्माणि कुरुते प्रत्यक्षाणि तथैव ते ॥१२॥
transliteration: pārthasya samare kṛṣṇo yathābhīśuvaragrahaḥ ,tena yukto raṇe pārtho rakṣyamāṇaśca pārthiva ,yāni karmāṇi kurute pratyakṣāṇi tathaiva te.
english: O king (pārthiva), just as Krishna is the master of the finest reins for Arjuna (Pārtha) in battle, so too is Arjuna (Pārtha) protected by him in battle. All the actions (karma) he performs are directly evident to you.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 23 Verse 12. O king (pārthiva), just as Krishna is the master of the finest reins for Arjuna (Pārtha) in battle, so too is Arjuna (Pārtha) protected by him in battle. All the actions (karma) he performs are directly evident to you.

sanskrit: पूर्वं न समरे ह्येवमवधीदर्जुनो रिपून् ।अहन्यहनि मद्रेश द्रावयन्दृश्यते युधि ॥१३॥
transliteration: pūrvaṁ na samare hyevamavadhīdarjuno ripūn ,ahanyahani madreśa drāvayandṛśyate yudhi.
english: O lord of Madras (madreśa), previously Arjuna never killed his enemies in battle in this manner. But now, day by day, he is seen routing them in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 23 Verse 13. O lord of Madras (madreśa), previously Arjuna never killed his enemies in battle in this manner. But now, day by day, he is seen routing them in battle.

sanskrit: भागोऽवशिष्टः कर्णस्य तव चैव महाद्युते ।तं भागं सह कर्णेन युगपन्नाशयाहवे ॥१४॥
transliteration: bhāgo'vaśiṣṭaḥ karṇasya tava caiva mahādyute ,taṁ bhāgaṁ saha karṇena yugapannāśayāhave.
english: O greatly lustrous one (mahādyute), the remaining portion [of the enemy] is for Karna and for you. Therefore, destroy that portion simultaneously with Karna in battle!
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 23 Verse 14. O greatly lustrous one (mahādyute), the remaining portion [of the enemy] is for Karna and for you. Therefore, destroy that portion simultaneously with Karna in battle!

sanskrit: सूर्यारुणौ यथा दृष्ट्वा तमो नश्यति मारिष ।तथा नश्यन्तु कौन्तेयाः सपाञ्चालाः ससृञ्जयाः ॥१५॥
transliteration: sūryāruṇau yathā dṛṣṭvā tamo naśyati māriṣa ,tathā naśyantu kaunteyāḥ sapāñcālāḥ sasṛñjayāḥ.
english: O respected one, just as darkness perishes upon seeing the sun and its charioteer (Aruna), so too may the sons of Kunti, along with the Panchalas and the Srinjayas, be destroyed.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 23 Verse 15. O respected one, just as darkness perishes upon seeing the sun and its charioteer (Aruna), so too may the sons of Kunti, along with the Panchalas and the Srinjayas, be destroyed.

sanskrit: रथानां प्रवरः कर्णो यन्तॄणां प्रवरो भवान् ।संनिपातः समो लोके भवतोर्नास्ति कश्चन ॥१६॥
transliteration: rathānāṁ pravaraḥ karṇo yantṝṇāṁ pravaro bhavān ,saṁnipātaḥ samo loke bhavatornāsti kaścana.
english: Karna is the foremost among chariot-warriors, and you are the foremost among charioteers. There is no equal combination of you two in the world.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 23 Verse 16. Karna is the foremost among chariot-warriors, and you are the foremost among charioteers. There is no equal combination of you two in the world.

sanskrit: यथा सर्वास्ववस्थासु वार्ष्णेयः पाति पाण्डवम् ।तथा भवान्परित्रातु कर्णं वैकर्तनं रणे ॥१७॥
transliteration: yathā sarvāsvavasthāsu vārṣṇeyaḥ pāti pāṇḍavam ,tathā bhavānparitrātu karṇaṁ vaikartanaṁ raṇe.
english: Just as Krishna (Vārṣṇeya) protects the son of Pandu (Arjuna) in all circumstances, so may you protect Karna, the son of Vikartana (Surya), in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 23 Verse 17. Just as Krishna (Vārṣṇeya) protects the son of Pandu (Arjuna) in all circumstances, so may you protect Karna, the son of Vikartana (Surya), in battle.

sanskrit: त्वया सारथिना ह्येष अप्रधृष्यो भविष्यति ।देवतानामपि रणे सशक्राणां महीपते ।किं पुनः पाण्डवेयानां मातिशङ्कीर्वचो मम ॥१८॥
transliteration: tvayā sārathinā hyeṣa apradhṛṣyo bhaviṣyati ,devatānāmapi raṇe saśakrāṇāṁ mahīpate ,kiṁ punaḥ pāṇḍaveyānāṁ mātiśaṅkīrvaco mama.
english: O king (mahīpati), indeed, with you as his charioteer, he (Karna) will be unconquerable even by the gods, including Indra, in battle. How much more so for the sons of Pandu? Do not doubt my word.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 23 Verse 18. O king (mahīpati), indeed, with you as his charioteer, he (Karna) will be unconquerable even by the gods, including Indra, in battle. How much more so for the sons of Pandu? Do not doubt my word.

sanskrit: दुर्योधनवचः श्रुत्वा शल्यः क्रोधसमन्वितः ।त्रिशिखां भ्रुकुटीं कृत्वा धुन्वन्हस्तौ पुनः पुनः ॥१९॥
transliteration: duryodhanavacaḥ śrutvā śalyaḥ krodhasamanvitaḥ ,triśikhāṁ bhrukuṭīṁ kṛtvā dhunvanhastau punaḥ punaḥ.
english: Shalya, filled with anger after hearing Duryodhana's words, frowned with three furrows and repeatedly shook his hands.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 23 Verse 19. Shalya, filled with anger after hearing Duryodhana's words, frowned with three furrows and repeatedly shook his hands.

sanskrit: क्रोधरक्ते महानेत्रे परिवर्त्य महाभुजः ।कुलैश्वर्यश्रुतिबलैर्दृप्तः शल्योऽब्रवीदिदम् ॥२०॥
transliteration: krodharakte mahānetre parivartya mahābhujaḥ ,kulaiśvaryaśrutibalairdṛptaḥ śalyo'bravīdidam.
english: The great-armed Shalya, having rolled his large eyes, which were reddened with anger, and being arrogant due to his noble lineage, wealth, learning, and strength, spoke these words.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 23 Verse 20. The great-armed Shalya, having rolled his large eyes, which were reddened with anger, and being arrogant due to his noble lineage, wealth, learning, and strength, spoke these words.

sanskrit: अवमन्यसे मां गान्धारे ध्रुवं मां परिशङ्कसे ।यन्मां ब्रवीषि विस्रब्धं सारथ्यं क्रियतामिति ॥२१॥
transliteration: avamanyase māṁ gāndhāre dhruvaṁ māṁ pariśaṅkase ,yanmāṁ bravīṣi visrabdhaṁ sārathyaṁ kriyatāmiti.
english: O son of Gandhari (Gāndhāre), you disrespect me; indeed, you surely doubt me, because you confidently tell me, "Let charioteering be done!"
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 23 Verse 21. O son of Gandhari (Gāndhāre), you disrespect me; indeed, you surely doubt me, because you confidently tell me, "Let charioteering be done!"

sanskrit: अस्मत्तोऽभ्यधिकं कर्णं मन्यमानः प्रशंससि ।न चाहं युधि राधेयं गणये तुल्यमात्मना ॥२२॥
transliteration: asmatto'bhyadhikaṁ karṇaṁ manyamānaḥ praśaṁsasi ,na cāhaṁ yudhi rādheyaṁ gaṇaye tulyamātmanā.
english: You praise Karṇa, considering him superior to me, yet I do not deem Radheya (Karṇa) to be my equal to myself (ātmanā) in battle (yudhi).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 23 Verse 22. You praise Karṇa, considering him superior to me, yet I do not deem Radheya (Karṇa) to be my equal to myself (ātmanā) in battle (yudhi).

sanskrit: आदिश्यतामभ्यधिको ममांशः पृथिवीपते ।तमहं समरे हत्वा गमिष्यामि यथागतम् ॥२३॥
transliteration: ādiśyatāmabhyadhiko mamāṁśaḥ pṛthivīpate ,tamahaṁ samare hatvā gamiṣyāmi yathāgatam.
english: O ruler of the earth, assign a greater share to me. I will slay him in battle and return just as I came.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 23 Verse 23. O ruler of the earth, assign a greater share to me. I will slay him in battle and return just as I came.

sanskrit: अथ वाप्येक एवाहं योत्स्यामि कुरुनन्दन ।पश्य वीर्यं ममाद्य त्वं संग्रामे दहतो रिपून् ॥२४॥
transliteration: atha vāpyeka evāhaṁ yotsyāmi kurunandana ,paśya vīryaṁ mamādya tvaṁ saṁgrāme dahato ripūn.
english: Or else, O delight of the Kurus, I alone will fight. Today, witness my prowess as I incinerate the enemies in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 23 Verse 24. Or else, O delight of the Kurus, I alone will fight. Today, witness my prowess as I incinerate the enemies in battle.

sanskrit: न चाभिकामान्कौरव्य विधाय हृदये पुमान् ।अस्मद्विधः प्रवर्तेत मा मा त्वमतिशङ्किथाः ॥२५॥
transliteration: na cābhikāmānkauravya vidhāya hṛdaye pumān ,asmadvidhaḥ pravarteta mā mā tvamatiśaṅkithāḥ.
english: O Kuru, a man like us would not act having harbored desires in his heart. Do not, I implore you, doubt me excessively!
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 23 Verse 25. O Kuru, a man like us would not act having harbored desires in his heart. Do not, I implore you, doubt me excessively!

sanskrit: युधि चाप्यवमानो मे न कर्तव्यः कथंचन ।पश्य हीमौ मम भुजौ वज्रसंहननोपमौ ॥२६॥
transliteration: yudhi cāpyavamāno me na kartavyaḥ kathaṁcana ,paśya hīmau mama bhujau vajrasaṁhananopamau.
english: And by no means should any disrespect be shown to me in battle. For indeed, behold these two arms of mine, which are like structures as firm as a thunderbolt.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 23 Verse 26. And by no means should any disrespect be shown to me in battle. For indeed, behold these two arms of mine, which are like structures as firm as a thunderbolt.

sanskrit: धनुः पश्य च मे चित्रं शरांश्चाशीविषोपमान् ।रथं पश्य च मे कॢप्तं सदश्वैर्वातवेगितैः ।गदां च पश्य गान्धारे हेमपट्टविभूषिताम् ॥२७॥
transliteration: dhanuḥ paśya ca me citraṁ śarāṁścāśīviṣopamān ,rathaṁ paśya ca me kḷptaṁ sadaśvairvātavegitaiḥ ,gadāṁ ca paśya gāndhāre hemapaṭṭavibhūṣitām.
english: O son of Gāndhārī, behold my extraordinary bow and my arrows, which are like venomous snakes. Also, observe my chariot, expertly prepared and drawn by excellent horses swift as the wind, and my mace, adorned with golden plates.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 23 Verse 27. O son of Gāndhārī, behold my extraordinary bow and my arrows, which are like venomous snakes. Also, observe my chariot, expertly prepared and drawn by excellent horses swift as the wind, and my mace, adorned with golden plates.

sanskrit: दारयेयं महीं क्रुद्धो विकिरेयं च पर्वतान् ।शोषयेयं समुद्रांश्च तेजसा स्वेन पार्थिव ॥२८॥
transliteration: dārayeyaṁ mahīṁ kruddho vikireyaṁ ca parvatān ,śoṣayeyaṁ samudrāṁśca tejasā svena pārthiva.
english: O king, in my wrath, I could cleave the earth, scatter the mountains, and dry up the oceans with my own power.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 23 Verse 28. O king, in my wrath, I could cleave the earth, scatter the mountains, and dry up the oceans with my own power.

sanskrit: तन्मामेवंविधं जानन्समर्थमरिनिग्रहे ।कस्माद्युनक्षि सारथ्ये न्यूनस्याधिरथेर्नृप ॥२९॥
transliteration: tanmāmevaṁvidhaṁ jānansamarthamarinigrahe ,kasmādyunakṣi sārathye nyūnasyādhirathernṛpa.
english: O king, knowing me to be of such a nature and so capable in restraining enemies, why do you appoint me to charioteering for Adhiratha's son, who is so inferior?
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 23 Verse 29. O king, knowing me to be of such a nature and so capable in restraining enemies, why do you appoint me to charioteering for Adhiratha's son, who is so inferior?

sanskrit: न नाम धुरि राजेन्द्र प्रयोक्तुं त्वमिहार्हसि ।न हि पापीयसः श्रेयान्भूत्वा प्रेष्यत्वमुत्सहे ॥३०॥
transliteration: na nāma dhuri rājendra prayoktuṁ tvamihārhasi ,na hi pāpīyasaḥ śreyānbhūtvā preṣyatvamutsahe.
english: O king of kings, you should certainly not appoint me here to such a position of inferiority. Indeed, I, being superior, would not tolerate the servitude of one who is so much more inferior.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 23 Verse 30. O king of kings, you should certainly not appoint me here to such a position of inferiority. Indeed, I, being superior, would not tolerate the servitude of one who is so much more inferior.

sanskrit: यो ह्यभ्युपगतं प्रीत्या गरीयांसं वशे स्थितम् ।वशे पापीयसो धत्ते तत्पापमधरोत्तरम् ॥३१॥
transliteration: yo hyabhyupagataṁ prītyā garīyāṁsaṁ vaśe sthitam ,vaśe pāpīyaso dhatte tatpāpamadharottaram.
english: Indeed, whoever places a more venerable person, who has come to him with affection and remains under his control, under the control of a baser, wicked person, that sin is utterly perverse.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 23 Verse 31. Indeed, whoever places a more venerable person, who has come to him with affection and remains under his control, under the control of a baser, wicked person, that sin is utterly perverse.

sanskrit: ब्राह्मणा ब्रह्मणा सृष्टा मुखात्क्षत्रमथोरसः ।ऊरुभ्यामसृजद्वैश्याञ्शूद्रान्पद्भ्यामिति श्रुतिः ।तेभ्यो वर्णविशेषाश्च प्रतिलोमानुलोमजाः ॥३२॥
transliteration: brāhmaṇā brahmaṇā sṛṣṭā mukhātkṣatramathorasaḥ ,ūrubhyāmasṛjadvaiśyāñśūdrānpadbhyāmiti śrutiḥ ,tebhyo varṇaviśeṣāśca pratilomānulomajāḥ.
english: Brahmins were created from the mouth by (brahman) (Brahmā), then Kṣatriyas from the chest. He created Vaiśyas from the thighs and Śūdras from the feet; thus says the sacred tradition (śruti). And from them (these four varnas) arose specific mixed castes, born from both "pratiloma" and "anuloma" unions.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 23 Verse 32. Brahmins were created from the mouth by (brahman) (Brahmā), then Kṣatriyas from the chest. He created Vaiśyas from the thighs and Śūdras from the feet; thus says the sacred tradition (śruti). And from them (these four varnas) arose specific mixed castes, born from both "pratiloma" and "anuloma" unions.

sanskrit: अथान्योन्यस्य संयोगाच्चातुर्वर्ण्यस्य भारत ।गोप्तारः संग्रहीतारो दातारः क्षत्रियाः स्मृताः ॥३३॥
transliteration: athānyonyasya saṁyogāccāturvarṇyasya bhārata ,goptāraḥ saṁgrahītāro dātāraḥ kṣatriyāḥ smṛtāḥ.
english: Moreover, O Bhārata, in the context of the mutual interaction of the four social classes, Kṣatriyas are considered protectors, upholders, and givers.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 23 Verse 33. Moreover, O Bhārata, in the context of the mutual interaction of the four social classes, Kṣatriyas are considered protectors, upholders, and givers.

sanskrit: याजनाध्यापनैर्विप्रा विशुद्धैश्च प्रतिग्रहैः ।लोकस्यानुग्रहार्थाय स्थापिता ब्रह्मणा भुवि ॥३४॥
transliteration: yājanādhyāpanairviprā viśuddhaiśca pratigrahaiḥ ,lokasyānugrahārthāya sthāpitā brahmaṇā bhuvi.
english: Brahmins (viprāḥ) were established on earth by (brahman) (Brahmā) for the sake of benefiting the world, through performing sacrifices for others, teaching, and accepting pure gifts.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 23 Verse 34. Brahmins (viprāḥ) were established on earth by (brahman) (Brahmā) for the sake of benefiting the world, through performing sacrifices for others, teaching, and accepting pure gifts.

sanskrit: कृषिश्च पाशुपाल्यं च विशां दानं च सर्वशः ।ब्रह्मक्षत्रविशां शूद्रा विहिताः परिचारकाः ॥३५॥
transliteration: kṛṣiśca pāśupālyaṁ ca viśāṁ dānaṁ ca sarvaśaḥ ,brahmakṣatraviśāṁ śūdrā vihitāḥ paricārakāḥ.
english: Agriculture, animal husbandry, and charity are universally prescribed for the Vaiśyas. The Śūdras are ordained as servants for the Brāhmaṇas, Kṣatriyas, and Vaiśyas.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 23 Verse 35. Agriculture, animal husbandry, and charity are universally prescribed for the Vaiśyas. The Śūdras are ordained as servants for the Brāhmaṇas, Kṣatriyas, and Vaiśyas.

sanskrit: ब्रह्मक्षत्रस्य विहिताः सूता वै परिचारकाः ।न विट्शूद्रस्य तत्रैव शृणु वाक्यं ममानघ ॥३६॥
transliteration: brahmakṣatrasya vihitāḥ sūtā vai paricārakāḥ ,na viṭśūdrasya tatraiva śṛṇu vākyaṁ mamānagha.
english: Indeed, Sūtas are ordained as servants for Brāhmaṇas and Kṣatriyas, but not for Vaiśyas and Śūdras in that very context. O sinless one, listen to my statement.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 23 Verse 36. Indeed, Sūtas are ordained as servants for Brāhmaṇas and Kṣatriyas, but not for Vaiśyas and Śūdras in that very context. O sinless one, listen to my statement.

sanskrit: सोऽहं मूर्धावसिक्तः सन्राजर्षिकुलसंभवः ।महारथः समाख्यातः सेव्यः स्तव्यश्च बन्दिनाम् ॥३७॥
transliteration: so'haṁ mūrdhāvasiktaḥ sanrājarṣikulasaṁbhavaḥ ,mahārathaḥ samākhyātaḥ sevyaḥ stavyaśca bandinām.
english: Being consecrated by sprinkling on the head and born in the lineage of royal sages (rājṛṣi), I am renowned as a great warrior (mahāratha), worthy of being served and praised by panegyrists.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 23 Verse 37. Being consecrated by sprinkling on the head and born in the lineage of royal sages (rājṛṣi), I am renowned as a great warrior (mahāratha), worthy of being served and praised by panegyrists.

sanskrit: सोऽहमेतादृशो भूत्वा नेहारिकुलमर्दन ।सूतपुत्रस्य संग्रामे सारथ्यं कर्तुमुत्सहे ॥३८॥
transliteration: so'hametādṛśo bhūtvā nehārikulamardana ,sūtaputrasya saṁgrāme sārathyaṁ kartumutsahe.
english: Having become such (a person)  -  O destroyer of the Nehāra family  -  I am eager to perform the charioteering for the Sūta's son (Karṇa) in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 23 Verse 38. Having become such (a person)  -  O destroyer of the Nehāra family  -  I am eager to perform the charioteering for the Sūta's son (Karṇa) in battle.

sanskrit: अवमानमहं प्राप्य न योत्स्यामि कथंचन ।आपृच्छ्य त्वाद्य गान्धारे गमिष्यामि यथागतम् ॥३९॥
transliteration: avamānamahaṁ prāpya na yotsyāmi kathaṁcana ,āpṛcchya tvādya gāndhāre gamiṣyāmi yathāgatam.
english: Having received this insult, I will not fight under any circumstances. Having bid you farewell today, O son of Gandhari, I will depart just as I arrived.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 23 Verse 39. Having received this insult, I will not fight under any circumstances. Having bid you farewell today, O son of Gandhari, I will depart just as I arrived.

sanskrit: एवमुक्त्वा नरव्याघ्रः शल्यः समितिशोभनः ।उत्थाय प्रययौ तूर्णं राजमध्यादमर्षितः ॥४०॥
transliteration: evamuktvā naravyāghraḥ śalyaḥ samitiśobhanaḥ ,utthāya prayayau tūrṇaṁ rājamadhyādamarṣitaḥ.
english: Having spoken thus, Shalya, that tiger among men, brilliant in assemblies, indignantly rose and swiftly departed from the midst of the kings.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 23 Verse 40. Having spoken thus, Shalya, that tiger among men, brilliant in assemblies, indignantly rose and swiftly departed from the midst of the kings.

sanskrit: प्रणयाद्बहुमानाच्च तं निगृह्य सुतस्तव ।अब्रवीन्मधुरं वाक्यं साम सर्वार्थसाधकम् ॥४१॥
transliteration: praṇayādbahumānācca taṁ nigṛhya sutastava ,abravīnmadhuraṁ vākyaṁ sāma sarvārthasādhakam.
english: Your son, restraining him (Shalya) out of affection and great respect, spoke sweet, conciliatory words that were effective for all purposes.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 23 Verse 41. Your son, restraining him (Shalya) out of affection and great respect, spoke sweet, conciliatory words that were effective for all purposes.

sanskrit: यथा शल्य त्वमात्थेदमेवमेतदसंशयम् ।अभिप्रायस्तु मे कश्चित्तं निबोध जनेश्वर ॥४२॥
transliteration: yathā śalya tvamātthedamevametadasaṁśayam ,abhiprāyastu me kaścittaṁ nibodha janeśvara.
english: O Shalya, what you have just said is undoubtedly true. But I have a certain intention; understand that, O lord of men.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 23 Verse 42. O Shalya, what you have just said is undoubtedly true. But I have a certain intention; understand that, O lord of men.

sanskrit: न कर्णोऽभ्यधिकस्त्वत्तः शङ्के नैव कथंचन ।न हि मद्रेश्वरो राजा कुर्याद्यदनृतं भवेत् ॥४३॥
transliteration: na karṇo'bhyadhikastvattaḥ śaṅke naiva kathaṁcana ,na hi madreśvaro rājā kuryādyadanṛtaṁ bhavet.
english: I certainly do not believe that Karṇa is superior to you in any way. For, indeed, the king, the lord of Madra, would not do anything that would be false.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 23 Verse 43. I certainly do not believe that Karṇa is superior to you in any way. For, indeed, the king, the lord of Madra, would not do anything that would be false.

sanskrit: ऋतमेव हि पूर्वास्ते वहन्ति पुरुषोत्तमाः ।तस्मादार्तायनिः प्रोक्तो भवानिति मतिर्मम ॥४४॥
transliteration: ṛtameva hi pūrvāste vahanti puruṣottamāḥ ,tasmādārtāyaniḥ prokto bhavāniti matirmama.
english: For indeed, your excellent ancestors always upheld the truth (ṛtam). Therefore, it is my opinion that you are called Ārtāyani.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 23 Verse 44. For indeed, your excellent ancestors always upheld the truth (ṛtam). Therefore, it is my opinion that you are called Ārtāyani.

sanskrit: शल्यभूतश्च शत्रूणां यस्मात्त्वं भुवि मानद ।तस्माच्छल्येति ते नाम कथ्यते पृथिवीपते ॥४५॥
transliteration: śalyabhūtaśca śatrūṇāṁ yasmāttvaṁ bhuvi mānada ,tasmācchalyeti te nāma kathyate pṛthivīpate.
english: O giver of honor (mānada)! And because you are like a thorn (śalya) to enemies on earth, therefore, your name, O lord of the earth (pṛthivīpati), is called Śalya.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 23 Verse 45. O giver of honor (mānada)! And because you are like a thorn (śalya) to enemies on earth, therefore, your name, O lord of the earth (pṛthivīpati), is called Śalya.

sanskrit: यदेव व्याहृतं पूर्वं भवता भूरिदक्षिण ।तदेव कुरु धर्मज्ञ मदर्थं यद्यदुच्यसे ॥४६॥
transliteration: yadeva vyāhṛtaṁ pūrvaṁ bhavatā bhūridakṣiṇa ,tadeva kuru dharmajña madarthaṁ yadyaducyase.
english: O giver of ample gifts (bhūridakṣiṇa)! Whatever was indeed spoken previously by you, that very thing, O knower of (dharma), do for my sake, whatever you are commanded.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 23 Verse 46. O giver of ample gifts (bhūridakṣiṇa)! Whatever was indeed spoken previously by you, that very thing, O knower of (dharma), do for my sake, whatever you are commanded.

sanskrit: न च त्वत्तो हि राधेयो न चाहमपि वीर्यवान् ।वृणीमस्त्वां हयाग्र्याणां यन्तारमिति संयुगे ॥४७॥
transliteration: na ca tvatto hi rādheyo na cāhamapi vīryavān ,vṛṇīmastvāṁ hayāgryāṇāṁ yantāramiti saṁyuge.
english: Indeed, neither Rādeya (Karṇa) is more valorous than you, nor am I. Therefore, we choose you as the charioteer for the excellent horses in this battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 23 Verse 47. Indeed, neither Rādeya (Karṇa) is more valorous than you, nor am I. Therefore, we choose you as the charioteer for the excellent horses in this battle.

sanskrit: यथा ह्यभ्यधिकं कर्णं गुणैस्तात धनंजयात् ।वासुदेवादपि त्वां च लोकोऽयमिति मन्यते ॥४८॥
transliteration: yathā hyabhyadhikaṁ karṇaṁ guṇaistāta dhanaṁjayāt ,vāsudevādapi tvāṁ ca loko'yamiti manyate.
english: O dear one, indeed, just as this world considers Karṇa superior to Dhanañjaya (Arjuna) in qualities, so also it considers you even superior to Vāsudeva (Kṛṣṇa).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 23 Verse 48. O dear one, indeed, just as this world considers Karṇa superior to Dhanañjaya (Arjuna) in qualities, so also it considers you even superior to Vāsudeva (Kṛṣṇa).

sanskrit: कर्णो ह्यभ्यधिकः पार्थादस्त्रैरेव नरर्षभ ।भवानप्यधिकः कृष्णादश्वयाने बले तथा ॥४९॥
transliteration: karṇo hyabhyadhikaḥ pārthādastraireva nararṣabha ,bhavānapyadhikaḥ kṛṣṇādaśvayāne bale tathā.
english: O best of men, Karṇa is certainly superior to Pārtha (Arjuna) only in weapons. You, however, are also superior to Kṛṣṇa in horsemanship and likewise in strength.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 23 Verse 49. O best of men, Karṇa is certainly superior to Pārtha (Arjuna) only in weapons. You, however, are also superior to Kṛṣṇa in horsemanship and likewise in strength.

sanskrit: यथाश्वहृदयं वेद वासुदेवो महामनाः ।द्विगुणं त्वं तथा वेत्थ मद्रराज न संशयः ॥५०॥
transliteration: yathāśvahṛdayaṁ veda vāsudevo mahāmanāḥ ,dviguṇaṁ tvaṁ tathā vettha madrarāja na saṁśayaḥ.
english: O King of Madras, just as the great-minded Vāsudeva (Kṛṣṇa) knows the science of horses, so you know it twice as well. There is no doubt about this.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 23 Verse 50. O King of Madras, just as the great-minded Vāsudeva (Kṛṣṇa) knows the science of horses, so you know it twice as well. There is no doubt about this.

sanskrit: शल्य उवाच ।यन्मा ब्रवीषि गान्धारे मध्ये सैन्यस्य कौरव ।विशिष्टं देवकीपुत्रात्प्रीतिमानस्म्यहं त्वयि ॥५१॥
transliteration: śalya uvāca ,yanmā bravīṣi gāndhāre madhye sainyasya kaurava ,viśiṣṭaṁ devakīputrātprītimānasmyahaṁ tvayi.
english: Śalya said: "O Kuru, son of Gāndhārī, because you speak of me as superior to Devakī's son (Kṛṣṇa) amidst the army, I am delighted with you."
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 23 Verse 51. Śalya said: "O Kuru, son of Gāndhārī, because you speak of me as superior to Devakī's son (Kṛṣṇa) amidst the army, I am delighted with you."

sanskrit: एष सारथ्यमातिष्ठे राधेयस्य यशस्विनः ।युध्यतः पाण्डवाग्र्येण यथा त्वं वीर मन्यसे ॥५२॥
transliteration: eṣa sārathyamātiṣṭhe rādheyasya yaśasvinaḥ ,yudhyataḥ pāṇḍavāgryeṇa yathā tvaṁ vīra manyase.
english: O hero, I will undertake the charioteering of the glorious son of Rādhā (Karṇa) as he fights the foremost of the Pāṇḍavas (Arjuna), just as you deem fit.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 23 Verse 52. O hero, I will undertake the charioteering of the glorious son of Rādhā (Karṇa) as he fights the foremost of the Pāṇḍavas (Arjuna), just as you deem fit.

sanskrit: समयश्च हि मे वीर कश्चिद्वैकर्तनं प्रति ।उत्सृजेयं यथाश्रद्धमहं वाचोऽस्य संनिधौ ॥५३॥
transliteration: samayaśca hi me vīra kaścidvaikartanaṁ prati ,utsṛjeyaṁ yathāśraddhamahaṁ vāco'sya saṁnidhau.
english: And indeed, O hero, I have made a certain pact concerning Vaikartana (Karṇa), that I may utter words as I wish in his presence.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 23 Verse 53. And indeed, O hero, I have made a certain pact concerning Vaikartana (Karṇa), that I may utter words as I wish in his presence.

sanskrit: संजय उवाच ।तथेति राजन्पुत्रस्ते सह कर्णेन भारत ।अब्रवीन्मद्रराजस्य सुतं भरतसत्तम ॥५४॥
transliteration: saṁjaya uvāca ,tatheti rājanputraste saha karṇena bhārata ,abravīnmadrarājasya sutaṁ bharatasattama.
english: Saṃjaya said: "O King (Dhṛtarāṣṭra), O best of Bhāratas, your son (Duryodhana), along with Karṇa, then said 'So be it!' to the son of the King of Madras (Śalya)."
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 23 Verse 54. Saṃjaya said: "O King (Dhṛtarāṣṭra), O best of Bhāratas, your son (Duryodhana), along with Karṇa, then said 'So be it!' to the son of the King of Madras (Śalya)."

sanskrit: दुर्योधन उवाच ।भूय एव तु मद्रेश यत्ते वक्ष्यामि तच्छृणु ।यथा पुरा वृत्तमिदं युद्धे देवासुरे विभो ॥१॥
transliteration: duryodhana uvāca ,bhūya eva tu madreśa yatte vakṣyāmi tacchṛṇu ,yathā purā vṛttamidaṁ yuddhe devāsure vibho.
english: Duryodhana said: "O Lord of Madras, O mighty one, listen again to what I am about to tell you: how this event previously occurred in the war between the gods and asuras."
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 24 Verse 1. Duryodhana said: "O Lord of Madras, O mighty one, listen again to what I am about to tell you: how this event previously occurred in the war between the gods and asuras."

sanskrit: यदुक्तवान्पितुर्मह्यं मार्कण्डेयो महानृषिः ।तदशेषेण ब्रुवतो मम राजर्षिसत्तम ।त्वं निबोध न चाप्यत्र कर्तव्या ते विचारणा ॥२॥
transliteration: yaduktavānpiturmahyaṁ mārkaṇḍeyo mahānṛṣiḥ ,tadaśeṣeṇa bruvato mama rājarṣisattama ,tvaṁ nibodha na cāpyatra kartavyā te vicāraṇā.
english: O best of royal sages, understand this fully from me as I narrate what the great sage Mārkaṇḍeya once told my father. There is no need for you to deliberate further on this matter.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 24 Verse 2. O best of royal sages, understand this fully from me as I narrate what the great sage Mārkaṇḍeya once told my father. There is no need for you to deliberate further on this matter.

sanskrit: देवानामसुराणां च महानासीत्समागमः ।बभूव प्रथमो राजन्संग्रामस्तारकामयः ।निर्जिताश्च तदा दैत्या दैवतैरिति नः श्रुतम् ॥३॥
transliteration: devānāmasurāṇāṁ ca mahānāsītsamāgamaḥ ,babhūva prathamo rājansaṁgrāmastārakāmayaḥ ,nirjitāśca tadā daityā daivatairiti naḥ śrutam.
english: O King, there was a great assembly of the gods and asuras, and the first major battle (saṃgrāma), known as Tārakāmaya, occurred. We have heard that the Daityas were then conquered by the gods.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 24 Verse 3. O King, there was a great assembly of the gods and asuras, and the first major battle (saṃgrāma), known as Tārakāmaya, occurred. We have heard that the Daityas were then conquered by the gods.

sanskrit: निर्जितेषु च दैत्येषु तारकस्य सुतास्त्रयः ।ताराक्षः कमलाक्षश्च विद्युन्माली च पार्थिव ॥४॥
transliteration: nirjiteṣu ca daityeṣu tārakasya sutāstrayaḥ ,tārākṣaḥ kamalākṣaśca vidyunmālī ca pārthiva.
english: O King, when the Daityas were defeated, Tāraka's three sons were Tārākṣa, Kamalākṣa, and Vidyunmālī.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 24 Verse 4. O King, when the Daityas were defeated, Tāraka's three sons were Tārākṣa, Kamalākṣa, and Vidyunmālī.

sanskrit: तप उग्रं समास्थाय नियमे परमे स्थिताः ।तपसा कर्शयामासुर्देहान्स्वाञ्शत्रुतापन ॥५॥
transliteration: tapa ugraṁ samāsthāya niyame parame sthitāḥ ,tapasā karśayāmāsurdehānsvāñśatrutāpana.
english: O tormentor of enemies, having undertaken severe asceticism (tapas) and established themselves in supreme self-restraint (niyama), they emaciated their own bodies through that asceticism (tapas).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 24 Verse 5. O tormentor of enemies, having undertaken severe asceticism (tapas) and established themselves in supreme self-restraint (niyama), they emaciated their own bodies through that asceticism (tapas).

sanskrit: दमेन तपसा चैव नियमेन च पार्थिव ।तेषां पितामहः प्रीतो वरदः प्रददौ वरान् ॥६॥
transliteration: damena tapasā caiva niyamena ca pārthiva ,teṣāṁ pitāmahaḥ prīto varadaḥ pradadau varān.
english: O King (pārthiva), their grandfather (Pitamaha), being pleased and a giver of boons, granted them boons through their self-control (dama), asceticism (tapas), and self-restraint (niyama).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 24 Verse 6. O King (pārthiva), their grandfather (Pitamaha), being pleased and a giver of boons, granted them boons through their self-control (dama), asceticism (tapas), and self-restraint (niyama).

sanskrit: अवध्यत्वं च ते राजन्सर्वभूतेषु सर्वदा ।सहिता वरयामासुः सर्वलोकपितामहम् ॥७॥
transliteration: avadhyatvaṁ ca te rājansarvabhūteṣu sarvadā ,sahitā varayāmāsuḥ sarvalokapitāmaham.
english: O King, united, they always sought the state of invulnerability from all beings from the grandfather (Pitamaha) of all worlds.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 24 Verse 7. O King, united, they always sought the state of invulnerability from all beings from the grandfather (Pitamaha) of all worlds.

sanskrit: तानब्रवीत्तदा देवो लोकानां प्रभुरीश्वरः ।नास्ति सर्वामरत्वं हि निवर्तध्वमतोऽसुराः ।वरमन्यं वृणीध्वं वै यादृशं संप्ररोचते ॥८॥
transliteration: tānabravīttadā devo lokānāṁ prabhurīśvaraḥ ,nāsti sarvāmaratvaṁ hi nivartadhvamato'surāḥ ,varamanyaṁ vṛṇīdhvaṁ vai yādṛśaṁ saṁprarocate.
english: Then the divine lord (īśvara), the master of the worlds, spoke to them: "Indeed, complete immortality is not possible. Therefore, O Asuras, withdraw your request! Choose another boon, whatever is agreeable to you."
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 24 Verse 8. Then the divine lord (īśvara), the master of the worlds, spoke to them: "Indeed, complete immortality is not possible. Therefore, O Asuras, withdraw your request! Choose another boon, whatever is agreeable to you."

sanskrit: ततस्ते सहिता राजन्संप्रधार्यासकृद्बहु ।सर्वलोकेश्वरं वाक्यं प्रणम्यैनमथाब्रुवन् ॥९॥
transliteration: tataste sahitā rājansaṁpradhāryāsakṛdbahu ,sarvalokeśvaraṁ vākyaṁ praṇamyainamathābruvan.
english: O king, then, having repeatedly and extensively deliberated, they, united, bowed to the Lord of all worlds and then spoke this statement to him.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 24 Verse 9. O king, then, having repeatedly and extensively deliberated, they, united, bowed to the Lord of all worlds and then spoke this statement to him.

sanskrit: अस्माकं त्वं वरं देव प्रयच्छेमं पितामह ।वयं पुराणि त्रीण्येव समास्थाय महीमिमाम् ।विचरिष्याम लोकेऽस्मिंस्त्वत्प्रसादपुरस्कृताः ॥१०॥
transliteration: asmākaṁ tvaṁ varaṁ deva prayacchemaṁ pitāmaha ,vayaṁ purāṇi trīṇyeva samāsthāya mahīmimām ,vicariṣyāma loke'smiṁstvatprasādapuraskṛtāḥ.
english: O divine grandfather (pitāmaha), grant us this boon! We, having established ourselves in these three cities, shall wander this earth, empowered by your grace.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 24 Verse 10. O divine grandfather (pitāmaha), grant us this boon! We, having established ourselves in these three cities, shall wander this earth, empowered by your grace.

sanskrit: ततो वर्षसहस्रे तु समेष्यामः परस्परम् ।एकीभावं गमिष्यन्ति पुराण्येतानि चानघ ॥११॥
transliteration: tato varṣasahasre tu sameṣyāmaḥ parasparam ,ekībhāvaṁ gamiṣyanti purāṇyetāni cānagha.
english: Then, after a thousand years, we shall unite with each other. And these cities, O sinless one (anagha), will achieve a state of oneness.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 24 Verse 11. Then, after a thousand years, we shall unite with each other. And these cities, O sinless one (anagha), will achieve a state of oneness.

sanskrit: समागतानि चैतानि यो हन्याद्भगवंस्तदा ।एकेषुणा देववरः स नो मृत्युर्भविष्यति ।एवमस्त्विति तान्देवः प्रत्युक्त्वा प्राविशद्दिवम् ॥१२॥
transliteration: samāgatāni caitāni yo hanyādbhagavaṁstadā ,ekeṣuṇā devavaraḥ sa no mṛtyurbhaviṣyati ,evamastviti tāndevaḥ pratyuktvā prāviśaddivam.
english: O Lord (bhagavan), whoever destroys these cities when they are united, that best of gods, with a single arrow, will be the cause of our death. Having said "So be it" to them, the god entered heaven.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 24 Verse 12. O Lord (bhagavan), whoever destroys these cities when they are united, that best of gods, with a single arrow, will be the cause of our death. Having said "So be it" to them, the god entered heaven.

sanskrit: ते तु लब्धवराः प्रीताः संप्रधार्य परस्परम् ।पुरत्रयविसृष्ट्यर्थं मयं वव्रुर्महासुरम् ।विश्वकर्माणमजरं दैत्यदानवपूजितम् ॥१३॥
transliteration: te tu labdhavarāḥ prītāḥ saṁpradhārya parasparam ,puratrayavisṛṣṭyarthaṁ mayaṁ vavrurmahāsuram ,viśvakarmāṇamajaraṁ daityadānavapūjitam.
english: But they, having obtained boons, joyfully deliberated among themselves. For the creation of the three cities, they chose Maya, the great Asura, the imperishable architect, revered by the Daityas and Dānavas.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 24 Verse 13. But they, having obtained boons, joyfully deliberated among themselves. For the creation of the three cities, they chose Maya, the great Asura, the imperishable architect, revered by the Daityas and Dānavas.

sanskrit: ततो मयः स्वतपसा चक्रे धीमान्पुराणि ह ।त्रीणि काञ्चनमेकं तु रौप्यं कार्ष्णायसं तथा ॥१४॥
transliteration: tato mayaḥ svatapasā cakre dhīmānpurāṇi ha ,trīṇi kāñcanamekaṁ tu raupyaṁ kārṣṇāyasaṁ tathā.
english: Then, the wise Maya, through his own spiritual discipline (tapas), indeed created cities: one golden, one silver, and another made of black iron.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 24 Verse 14. Then, the wise Maya, through his own spiritual discipline (tapas), indeed created cities: one golden, one silver, and another made of black iron.

sanskrit: काञ्चनं दिवि तत्रासीदन्तरिक्षे च राजतम् ।आयसं चाभवद्भूमौ चक्रस्थं पृथिवीपते ॥१५॥
transliteration: kāñcanaṁ divi tatrāsīdantarikṣe ca rājatam ,āyasaṁ cābhavadbhūmau cakrasthaṁ pṛthivīpate.
english: The golden city was in the sky there, and the silver one in the atmosphere. And, O lord of the earth, the iron city was on the ground, situated on a wheel.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 24 Verse 15. The golden city was in the sky there, and the silver one in the atmosphere. And, O lord of the earth, the iron city was on the ground, situated on a wheel.

sanskrit: एकैकं योजनशतं विस्तारायामसंमितम् ।गृहाट्टाट्टालकयुतं बृहत्प्राकारतोरणम् ॥१६॥
transliteration: ekaikaṁ yojanaśataṁ vistārāyāmasaṁmitam ,gṛhāṭṭāṭṭālakayutaṁ bṛhatprākāratoraṇam.
english: Each city measured one hundred yojanas in both breadth and length, furnished with dwellings and watchtowers, and possessing great ramparts and gateways.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 24 Verse 16. Each city measured one hundred yojanas in both breadth and length, furnished with dwellings and watchtowers, and possessing great ramparts and gateways.

sanskrit: गुणप्रसवसंबाधमसंबाधमनामयम् ।प्रासादैर्विविधैश्चैव द्वारैश्चाप्युपशोभितम् ॥१७॥
transliteration: guṇaprasavasaṁbādhamasaṁbādhamanāmayam ,prāsādairvividhaiścaiva dvāraiścāpyupaśobhitam.
english: It was dense with the manifestation of qualities (guṇas), yet unobstructed and free from affliction. It was also splendidly adorned with various palaces and gates.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 24 Verse 17. It was dense with the manifestation of qualities (guṇas), yet unobstructed and free from affliction. It was also splendidly adorned with various palaces and gates.

sanskrit: पुरेषु चाभवन्राजन्राजानो वै पृथक्पृथक् ।काञ्चनं तारकाक्षस्य चित्रमासीन्महात्मनः ।राजतं कमलाक्षस्य विद्युन्मालिन आयसम् ॥१८॥
transliteration: pureṣu cābhavanrājanrājāno vai pṛthakpṛthak ,kāñcanaṁ tārakākṣasya citramāsīnmahātmanaḥ ,rājataṁ kamalākṣasya vidyunmālina āyasam.
english: And in those cities, O King, there were separate kings, each ruling his own. Tārakākṣa, that great being, possessed a splendid golden city. Kamalākṣa had a silver city, and Vidyunmālin possessed one made of iron.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 24 Verse 18. And in those cities, O King, there were separate kings, each ruling his own. Tārakākṣa, that great being, possessed a splendid golden city. Kamalākṣa had a silver city, and Vidyunmālin possessed one made of iron.

sanskrit: त्रयस्ते दैत्यराजानस्त्रीँल्लोकानाशु तेजसा ।आक्रम्य तस्थुर्वर्षाणां पूगान्नाम प्रजापतिः ॥१९॥
transliteration: trayaste daityarājānastrīँllokānāśu tejasā ,ākramya tasthurvarṣāṇāṁ pūgānnāma prajāpatiḥ.
english: Those three demon kings, by their splendor (tejas), swiftly conquered the three worlds and remained established there for multitudes of years, as ordained by Prajāpati.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 24 Verse 19. Those three demon kings, by their splendor (tejas), swiftly conquered the three worlds and remained established there for multitudes of years, as ordained by Prajāpati.

sanskrit: तेषां दानवमुख्यानां प्रयुतान्यर्बुदानि च ।कोट्यश्चाप्रतिवीराणां समाजग्मुस्ततस्ततः ।महदैश्वर्यमिच्छन्तस्त्रिपुरं दुर्गमाश्रिताः ॥२०॥
transliteration: teṣāṁ dānavamukhyānāṁ prayutānyarbudāni ca ,koṭyaścāprativīrāṇāṁ samājagmustatastataḥ ,mahadaiśvaryamicchantastripuraṁ durgamāśritāḥ.
english: Myriads, billions, and crores of those chief Dānavas, unrivaled warriors, came from various places. Desiring great dominion, they took refuge in the Triple City, that formidable fortress.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 24 Verse 20. Myriads, billions, and crores of those chief Dānavas, unrivaled warriors, came from various places. Desiring great dominion, they took refuge in the Triple City, that formidable fortress.

sanskrit: सर्वेषां च पुनस्तेषां सर्वयोगवहो मयः ।तमाश्रित्य हि ते सर्वे अवर्तन्ताकुतोभयाः ॥२१॥
transliteration: sarveṣāṁ ca punasteṣāṁ sarvayogavaho mayaḥ ,tamāśritya hi te sarve avartantākutobhayāḥ.
english: And again, among all of them, Maya was the one who managed all their undertakings. Indeed, all of them, relying on him, lived fearlessly.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 24 Verse 21. And again, among all of them, Maya was the one who managed all their undertakings. Indeed, all of them, relying on him, lived fearlessly.

sanskrit: यो हि यं मनसा कामं दध्यौ त्रिपुरसंश्रयः ।तस्मै कामं मयस्तं तं विदधे मायया तदा ॥२२॥
transliteration: yo hi yaṁ manasā kāmaṁ dadhyau tripurasaṁśrayaḥ ,tasmai kāmaṁ mayastaṁ taṁ vidadhe māyayā tadā.
english: Indeed, whichever desire one residing in Tripura conceived in his mind, Maya then granted him that very desire through his magical power (māyā).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 24 Verse 22. Indeed, whichever desire one residing in Tripura conceived in his mind, Maya then granted him that very desire through his magical power (māyā).

sanskrit: तारकाक्षसुतश्चासीद्धरिर्नाम महाबलः ।तपस्तेपे परमकं येनातुष्यत्पितामहः ॥२३॥
transliteration: tārakākṣasutaścāsīddharirnāma mahābalaḥ ,tapastepe paramakaṁ yenātuṣyatpitāmahaḥ.
english: And there was a very mighty son of Tārakākṣa, named Hari. He performed supreme austerity (tapas), by which the grandfather (Brahmā) was pleased.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 24 Verse 23. And there was a very mighty son of Tārakākṣa, named Hari. He performed supreme austerity (tapas), by which the grandfather (Brahmā) was pleased.

sanskrit: स तुष्टमवृणोद्देवं वापी भवतु नः पुरे ।शस्त्रैर्विनिहता यत्र क्षिप्ताः स्युर्बलवत्तराः ॥२४॥
transliteration: sa tuṣṭamavṛṇoddevaṁ vāpī bhavatu naḥ pure ,śastrairvinihatā yatra kṣiptāḥ syurbalavattarāḥ.
english: He then asked the pleased god, 'Let there be a pond in our city, where those slain by weapons, when thrown in, would become even mightier.'
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 24 Verse 24. He then asked the pleased god, 'Let there be a pond in our city, where those slain by weapons, when thrown in, would become even mightier.'

sanskrit: स तु लब्ध्वा वरं वीरस्तारकाक्षसुतो हरिः ।ससृजे तत्र वापीं तां मृतानां जीवनीं प्रभो ॥२५॥
transliteration: sa tu labdhvā varaṁ vīrastārakākṣasuto hariḥ ,sasṛje tatra vāpīṁ tāṁ mṛtānāṁ jīvanīṁ prabho.
english: But that hero, Hari, the son of Tārakākṣa, having obtained a boon, created there, O lord, that pond which was capable of bringing the dead back to life.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 24 Verse 25. But that hero, Hari, the son of Tārakākṣa, having obtained a boon, created there, O lord, that pond which was capable of bringing the dead back to life.

sanskrit: येन रूपेण दैत्यस्तु येन वेषेण चैव ह ।मृतस्तस्यां परिक्षिप्तस्तादृशेनैव जज्ञिवान् ॥२६॥
transliteration: yena rūpeṇa daityastu yena veṣeṇa caiva ha ,mṛtastasyāṁ parikṣiptastādṛśenaiva jajñivān.
english: Whatever form or guise a demon had when he died and was cast into that pond, he was reborn in precisely that same form.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 24 Verse 26. Whatever form or guise a demon had when he died and was cast into that pond, he was reborn in precisely that same form.

sanskrit: तां प्राप्य त्रैपुरस्थास्तु सर्वाँल्लोकान्बबाधिरे ।महता तपसा सिद्धाः सुराणां भयवर्धनाः ।न तेषामभवद्राजन्क्षयो युद्धे कथंचन ॥२७॥
transliteration: tāṁ prāpya traipurasthāstu sarvāँllokānbabādhire ,mahatā tapasā siddhāḥ surāṇāṁ bhayavardhanāḥ ,na teṣāmabhavadrājankṣayo yuddhe kathaṁcana.
english: Having acquired that (pond), those residing in Tripura, who had achieved great power (siddhi) through severe asceticism (tapas) and were a cause of increasing fear for the gods, began to oppress all worlds. O king, there was never any destruction for them in battle by any means.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 24 Verse 27. Having acquired that (pond), those residing in Tripura, who had achieved great power (siddhi) through severe asceticism (tapas) and were a cause of increasing fear for the gods, began to oppress all worlds. O king, there was never any destruction for them in battle by any means.

sanskrit: ततस्ते लोभमोहाभ्यामभिभूता विचेतसः ।निर्ह्रीकाः संस्थितिं सर्वे स्थापितां समलूलुपन् ॥२८॥
transliteration: tataste lobhamohābhyāmabhibhūtā vicetasaḥ ,nirhrīkāḥ saṁsthitiṁ sarve sthāpitāṁ samalūlupan.
english: Then all of them, overcome by greed and delusion, became senseless and shameless, and they violated the established order.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 24 Verse 28. Then all of them, overcome by greed and delusion, became senseless and shameless, and they violated the established order.

sanskrit: विद्राव्य सगणान्देवांस्तत्र तत्र तदा तदा ।विचेरुः स्वेन कामेन वरदानेन दर्पिताः ॥२९॥
transliteration: vidrāvya sagaṇāndevāṁstatra tatra tadā tadā ,viceruḥ svena kāmena varadānena darpitāḥ.
english: Having scattered the gods along with their hosts repeatedly in various places, they roamed as they pleased, arrogant due to the boon.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 24 Verse 29. Having scattered the gods along with their hosts repeatedly in various places, they roamed as they pleased, arrogant due to the boon.

sanskrit: देवारण्यानि सर्वाणि प्रियाणि च दिवौकसाम् ।ऋषीणामाश्रमान्पुण्यान्यूपाञ्जनपदांस्तथा ।व्यनाशयन्त मर्यादा दानवा दुष्टचारिणः ॥३०॥
transliteration: devāraṇyāni sarvāṇi priyāṇi ca divaukasām ,ṛṣīṇāmāśramānpuṇyānyūpāñjanapadāṁstathā ,vyanāśayanta maryādā dānavā duṣṭacāriṇaḥ.
english: And the wicked demons (dānava), destructive in their ways, utterly destroyed all the divine forests dear to the gods, the sacred hermitages (āśrama) of the sages, the sacrificial posts (yūpa), the populated regions, and also the established norms.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 24 Verse 30. And the wicked demons (dānava), destructive in their ways, utterly destroyed all the divine forests dear to the gods, the sacred hermitages (āśrama) of the sages, the sacrificial posts (yūpa), the populated regions, and also the established norms.

sanskrit: ते देवाः सहिताः सर्वे पितामहमरिंदम ।अभिजग्मुस्तदाख्यातुं विप्रकारं सुरेतरैः ॥३१॥
transliteration: te devāḥ sahitāḥ sarve pitāmahamariṁdama ,abhijagmustadākhyātuṁ viprakāraṁ suretaraiḥ.
english: Then, all those assembled gods approached the grandfather (Brahma), O vanquisher of foes, in order to relate the harm inflicted upon them by the demons (suretara).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 24 Verse 31. Then, all those assembled gods approached the grandfather (Brahma), O vanquisher of foes, in order to relate the harm inflicted upon them by the demons (suretara).

sanskrit: ते तत्त्वं सर्वमाख्याय शिरसाभिप्रणम्य च ।वधोपायमपृच्छन्त भगवन्तं पितामहम् ॥३२॥
transliteration: te tattvaṁ sarvamākhyāya śirasābhipraṇamya ca ,vadhopāyamapṛcchanta bhagavantaṁ pitāmaham.
english: Having narrated the entire situation (tattva) and having bowed down with their heads, they (the gods) then asked the venerable grandfather (Brahma) for the means of destruction.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 24 Verse 32. Having narrated the entire situation (tattva) and having bowed down with their heads, they (the gods) then asked the venerable grandfather (Brahma) for the means of destruction.

sanskrit: श्रुत्वा तद्भगवान्देवो देवानिदमुवाच ह ।असुराश्च दुरात्मानस्ते चापि विबुधद्विषः ।अपराध्यन्ति सततं ये युष्मान्पीडयन्त्युत ॥३३॥
transliteration: śrutvā tadbhagavāndevo devānidamuvāca ha ,asurāśca durātmānaste cāpi vibudhadviṣaḥ ,aparādhyanti satataṁ ye yuṣmānpīḍayantyuta.
english: Having heard that, the glorious god indeed spoke this to the gods: "Those Asuras, who are evil-minded and hostile to the gods, constantly commit offenses and torment you."
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 24 Verse 33. Having heard that, the glorious god indeed spoke this to the gods: "Those Asuras, who are evil-minded and hostile to the gods, constantly commit offenses and torment you."

sanskrit: अहं हि तुल्यः सर्वेषां भूतानां नात्र संशयः ।अधार्मिकास्तु हन्तव्या इत्यहं प्रब्रवीमि वः ॥३४॥
transliteration: ahaṁ hi tulyaḥ sarveṣāṁ bhūtānāṁ nātra saṁśayaḥ ,adhārmikāstu hantavyā ityahaṁ prabravīmi vaḥ.
english: Indeed, I am impartial to all beings; there is no doubt about that. However, those who violate natural law (adharma) must be slain – this is what I declare to you.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 24 Verse 34. Indeed, I am impartial to all beings; there is no doubt about that. However, those who violate natural law (adharma) must be slain – this is what I declare to you.

sanskrit: ते यूयं स्थाणुमीशानं जिष्णुमक्लिष्टकारिणम् ।योद्धारं वृणुतादित्याः स तान्हन्ता सुरेतरान् ॥३५॥
transliteration: te yūyaṁ sthāṇumīśānaṁ jiṣṇumakliṣṭakāriṇam ,yoddhāraṁ vṛṇutādityāḥ sa tānhantā suretarān.
english: Therefore, O Adityas, you must choose Sthanu, Ishana, Jishnu, the one whose actions are effortless, as your warrior. He will slay those who are not gods (the demons).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 24 Verse 35. Therefore, O Adityas, you must choose Sthanu, Ishana, Jishnu, the one whose actions are effortless, as your warrior. He will slay those who are not gods (the demons).

sanskrit: इति तस्य वचः श्रुत्वा देवाः शक्रपुरोगमाः ।ब्रह्माणमग्रतः कृत्वा वृषाङ्कं शरणं ययुः ॥३६॥
transliteration: iti tasya vacaḥ śrutvā devāḥ śakrapurogamāḥ ,brahmāṇamagrataḥ kṛtvā vṛṣāṅkaṁ śaraṇaṁ yayuḥ.
english: Thus, having heard his words, the gods, led by Indra, placed Brahma (brahman) at their forefront and went to Vṛṣāṅka (Shiva) for refuge.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 24 Verse 36. Thus, having heard his words, the gods, led by Indra, placed Brahma (brahman) at their forefront and went to Vṛṣāṅka (Shiva) for refuge.

sanskrit: तपः परं समातस्थुर्गृणन्तो ब्रह्म शाश्वतम् ।ऋषिभिः सह धर्मज्ञा भवं सर्वात्मना गताः ॥३७॥
transliteration: tapaḥ paraṁ samātasthurgṛṇanto brahma śāśvatam ,ṛṣibhiḥ saha dharmajñā bhavaṁ sarvātmanā gatāḥ.
english: Those who understood the natural law (dharma), accompanied by the sages, practiced supreme austerity (tapas) while chanting the eternal reality (brahman), and they attained Bhava (Śiva) with their whole being (ātman).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 24 Verse 37. Those who understood the natural law (dharma), accompanied by the sages, practiced supreme austerity (tapas) while chanting the eternal reality (brahman), and they attained Bhava (Śiva) with their whole being (ātman).

sanskrit: तुष्टुवुर्वाग्भिरर्थ्याभिर्भयेष्वभयकृत्तमम् ।सर्वात्मानं महात्मानं येनाप्तं सर्वमात्मना ॥३८॥
transliteration: tuṣṭuvurvāgbhirarthyābhirbhayeṣvabhayakṛttamam ,sarvātmānaṁ mahātmānaṁ yenāptaṁ sarvamātmanā.
english: They praised with meaningful words Him who is the supreme giver of fearlessness in times of fear, the self (ātman) of all, the great self (ātman), by whom everything was obtained through his own being (ātman).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 24 Verse 38. They praised with meaningful words Him who is the supreme giver of fearlessness in times of fear, the self (ātman) of all, the great self (ātman), by whom everything was obtained through his own being (ātman).

sanskrit: तपोविशेषैर्बहुभिर्योगं यो वेद चात्मनः ।यः सांख्यमात्मनो वेद यस्य चात्मा वशे सदा ॥३९॥
transliteration: tapoviśeṣairbahubhiryogaṁ yo veda cātmanaḥ ,yaḥ sāṁkhyamātmano veda yasya cātmā vaśe sadā.
english: He who knows the practice (yoga) of his own being (ātman) through many special austerities (tapas), and he who knows the Sāṅkhya philosophy of his own being (ātman), and whose self (ātman) is always under his control.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 24 Verse 39. He who knows the practice (yoga) of his own being (ātman) through many special austerities (tapas), and he who knows the Sāṅkhya philosophy of his own being (ātman), and whose self (ātman) is always under his control.

sanskrit: ते तं ददृशुरीशानं तेजोराशिमुमापतिम् ।अनन्यसदृशं लोके व्रतवन्तमकल्मषम् ॥४०॥
transliteration: te taṁ dadṛśurīśānaṁ tejorāśimumāpatim ,ananyasadṛśaṁ loke vratavantamakalmaṣam.
english: They saw Him, the Lord (Īśāna), a mass of brilliance, the husband of Umā, who is unequalled in the world, steadfast in his vows, and free from blemish.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 24 Verse 40. They saw Him, the Lord (Īśāna), a mass of brilliance, the husband of Umā, who is unequalled in the world, steadfast in his vows, and free from blemish.

sanskrit: एकं च भगवन्तं ते नानारूपमकल्पयन् ।आत्मनः प्रतिरूपाणि रूपाण्यथ महात्मनि ।परस्परस्य चापश्यन्सर्वे परमविस्मिताः ॥४१॥
transliteration: ekaṁ ca bhagavantaṁ te nānārūpamakalpayan ,ātmanaḥ pratirūpāṇi rūpāṇyatha mahātmani ,parasparasya cāpaśyansarve paramavismitāḥ.
english: And they imagined the one revered Lord to have many forms. Then, within that great Supreme Being (ātman), they saw forms that were reflections of their own selves (ātman). All of them looked upon each other, utterly astonished.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 24 Verse 41. And they imagined the one revered Lord to have many forms. Then, within that great Supreme Being (ātman), they saw forms that were reflections of their own selves (ātman). All of them looked upon each other, utterly astonished.

sanskrit: सर्वभूतमयं चेशं तमजं जगतः पतिम् ।देवा ब्रह्मर्षयश्चैव शिरोभिर्धरणीं गताः ॥४२॥
transliteration: sarvabhūtamayaṁ ceśaṁ tamajaṁ jagataḥ patim ,devā brahmarṣayaścaiva śirobhirdharaṇīṁ gatāḥ.
english: And the gods and the Brahmarṣis (brahmarṣi) indeed bowed to the ground with their heads before that unborn Lord, the master of the world, who embodies all beings.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 24 Verse 42. And the gods and the Brahmarṣis (brahmarṣi) indeed bowed to the ground with their heads before that unborn Lord, the master of the world, who embodies all beings.

sanskrit: तान्स्वस्तिवाक्येनाभ्यर्च्य समुत्थाप्य च शंकरः ।ब्रूत ब्रूतेति भगवान्स्मयमानोऽभ्यभाषत ॥४३॥
transliteration: tānsvastivākyenābhyarcya samutthāpya ca śaṁkaraḥ ,brūta brūteti bhagavānsmayamāno'bhyabhāṣata.
english: And having honored them with words of blessing and having raised them up, the revered Lord Śaṅkara (śaṅkara) spoke to them smiling, saying, "Speak, speak!"
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 24 Verse 43. And having honored them with words of blessing and having raised them up, the revered Lord Śaṅkara (śaṅkara) spoke to them smiling, saying, "Speak, speak!"

sanskrit: त्र्यम्बकेणाभ्यनुज्ञातास्ततस्तेऽस्वस्थचेतसः ।नमो नमस्तेऽस्तु विभो तत इत्यब्रुवन्भवम् ॥४४॥
transliteration: tryambakeṇābhyanujñātāstataste'svasthacetasaḥ ,namo namaste'stu vibho tata ityabruvanbhavam.
english: Then, having received permission from Tryambaka (tryambaka), they, with restless minds, said to Bhava (bhava), "Salutations, salutations to you, O all-pervading Lord, may it be so!"
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 24 Verse 44. Then, having received permission from Tryambaka (tryambaka), they, with restless minds, said to Bhava (bhava), "Salutations, salutations to you, O all-pervading Lord, may it be so!"

sanskrit: नमो देवातिदेवाय धन्विने चातिमन्यवे ।प्रजापतिमखघ्नाय प्रजापतिभिरीड्यसे ॥४५॥
transliteration: namo devātidevāya dhanvine cātimanyave ,prajāpatimakhaghnāya prajāpatibhirīḍyase.
english: Salutations to the God of gods, to the wielder of the bow, and to the exceedingly wrathful one. You are praised by the lords of creation, you who destroyed Prajāpati's sacrifice.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 24 Verse 45. Salutations to the God of gods, to the wielder of the bow, and to the exceedingly wrathful one. You are praised by the lords of creation, you who destroyed Prajāpati's sacrifice.

sanskrit: नमः स्तुताय स्तुत्याय स्तूयमानाय मृत्यवे ।विलोहिताय रुद्राय नीलग्रीवाय शूलिने ॥४६॥
transliteration: namaḥ stutāya stutyāya stūyamānāya mṛtyave ,vilohitāya rudrāya nīlagrīvāya śūline.
english: Salutations to the one who is praised, to the one who is worthy of praise, and to the one who is being praised; salutations to Death (mṛtyu). Salutations to the intensely red one, to Rudra, to the blue-necked one, and to the wielder of the trident.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 24 Verse 46. Salutations to the one who is praised, to the one who is worthy of praise, and to the one who is being praised; salutations to Death (mṛtyu). Salutations to the intensely red one, to Rudra, to the blue-necked one, and to the wielder of the trident.

sanskrit: अमोघाय मृगाक्षाय प्रवरायुधयोधिने ।दुर्वारणाय शुक्राय ब्रह्मणे ब्रह्मचारिणे ॥४७॥
transliteration: amoghāya mṛgākṣāya pravarāyudhayodhine ,durvāraṇāya śukrāya brahmaṇe brahmacāriṇe.
english: Salutations to the unfailing one, to the deer-eyed one, and to the warrior who fights with excellent weapons. Salutations to the one difficult to repel, to the brilliant one, to Brahmā, and to the one who observes a chaste (brahmacarya) life.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 24 Verse 47. Salutations to the unfailing one, to the deer-eyed one, and to the warrior who fights with excellent weapons. Salutations to the one difficult to repel, to the brilliant one, to Brahmā, and to the one who observes a chaste (brahmacarya) life.

sanskrit: ईशानायाप्रमेयाय नियन्त्रे चर्मवाससे ।तपोनित्याय पिङ्गाय व्रतिने कृत्तिवाससे ॥४८॥
transliteration: īśānāyāprameyāya niyantre carmavāsase ,taponityāya piṅgāya vratine kṛttivāsase.
english: Salutations to the Lord (Īśāna), to the immeasurable one, to the controller, and to the one clothed in animal skin. Salutations to the one whose constant practice is asceticism (tapas), to the tawny-colored one, to the observer of vows, and to the one clad in a hide.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 24 Verse 48. Salutations to the Lord (Īśāna), to the immeasurable one, to the controller, and to the one clothed in animal skin. Salutations to the one whose constant practice is asceticism (tapas), to the tawny-colored one, to the observer of vows, and to the one clad in a hide.

sanskrit: कुमारपित्रे त्र्यक्षाय प्रवरायुधधारिणे ।प्रपन्नार्तिविनाशाय ब्रह्मद्विट्संघघातिने ॥४९॥
transliteration: kumārapitre tryakṣāya pravarāyudhadhāriṇe ,prapannārtivināśāya brahmadviṭsaṁghaghātine.
english: Salutations to the father of Kumāra, to the three-eyed one, to the wielder of excellent weapons, to the destroyer of the distress of those who have sought refuge, and to the slayer of the hordes who hate Brahmins (brahmadvit).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 24 Verse 49. Salutations to the father of Kumāra, to the three-eyed one, to the wielder of excellent weapons, to the destroyer of the distress of those who have sought refuge, and to the slayer of the hordes who hate Brahmins (brahmadvit).

sanskrit: वनस्पतीनां पतये नराणां पतये नमः ।गवां च पतये नित्यं यज्ञानां पतये नमः ॥५०॥
transliteration: vanaspatīnāṁ pataye narāṇāṁ pataye namaḥ ,gavāṁ ca pataye nityaṁ yajñānāṁ pataye namaḥ.
english: Salutations to the lord of trees, to the lord of humans. And always, salutations to the lord of cows and the lord of Vedic rituals (yajña).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 24 Verse 50. Salutations to the lord of trees, to the lord of humans. And always, salutations to the lord of cows and the lord of Vedic rituals (yajña).

sanskrit: नमोऽस्तु ते ससैन्याय त्र्यम्बकायोग्रतेजसे ।मनोवाक्कर्मभिर्देव त्वां प्रपन्नान्भजस्व नः ॥५१॥
transliteration: namo'stu te sasainyāya tryambakāyogratejase ,manovākkarmabhirdeva tvāṁ prapannānbhajasva naḥ.
english: Salutations to you, O three-eyed one (Tryambaka), who are accompanied by your army and possess fierce splendor. O divine one, please cherish us who have sought refuge in you through our thoughts, words, and actions (karma).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 24 Verse 51. Salutations to you, O three-eyed one (Tryambaka), who are accompanied by your army and possess fierce splendor. O divine one, please cherish us who have sought refuge in you through our thoughts, words, and actions (karma).

sanskrit: ततः प्रसन्नो भगवान्स्वागतेनाभिनन्द्य तान् ।प्रोवाच व्येतु वस्त्रासो ब्रूत किं करवाणि वः ॥५२॥
transliteration: tataḥ prasanno bhagavānsvāgatenābhinandya tān ,provāca vyetu vastrāso brūta kiṁ karavāṇi vaḥ.
english: Then, the pleased Lord, after welcoming them, spoke: "Let your fear depart. Tell me, what may I do for you?"
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 24 Verse 52. Then, the pleased Lord, after welcoming them, spoke: "Let your fear depart. Tell me, what may I do for you?"

sanskrit: पितृदेवर्षिसंघेभ्यो वरे दत्ते महात्मना ।सत्कृत्य शंकरं प्राह ब्रह्मा लोकहितं वचः ॥५३॥
transliteration: pitṛdevarṣisaṁghebhyo vare datte mahātmanā ,satkṛtya śaṁkaraṁ prāha brahmā lokahitaṁ vacaḥ.
english: After the great soul (mahātman) had granted the boon to the assemblies of ancestors, gods, and sages, Brahmā respectfully addressed Śaṅkara with words beneficial to the world.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 24 Verse 53. After the great soul (mahātman) had granted the boon to the assemblies of ancestors, gods, and sages, Brahmā respectfully addressed Śaṅkara with words beneficial to the world.

sanskrit: तवातिसर्गाद्देवेश प्राजापत्यमिदं पदम् ।मयाधितिष्ठता दत्तो दानवेभ्यो महान्वरः ॥५४॥
transliteration: tavātisargāddeveśa prājāpatyamidaṁ padam ,mayādhitiṣṭhatā datto dānavebhyo mahānvaraḥ.
english: O Lord of gods (deveśa), due to your permission [or, creation], while I was occupying this position of Prajāpati, a great boon was granted by me to the Dānavas.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 24 Verse 54. O Lord of gods (deveśa), due to your permission [or, creation], while I was occupying this position of Prajāpati, a great boon was granted by me to the Dānavas.

sanskrit: तानतिक्रान्तमर्यादान्नान्यः संहर्तुमर्हति ।त्वामृते भूतभव्येश त्वं ह्येषां प्रत्यरिर्वधे ॥५५॥
transliteration: tānatikrāntamaryādānnānyaḥ saṁhartumarhati ,tvāmṛte bhūtabhavyeśa tvaṁ hyeṣāṁ pratyarirvadhe.
english: No one else is capable of destroying those Dānavas who have transgressed all boundaries, except for you, O Lord of past and future (bhūtabhavyeśa); for you are indeed their direct adversary in their destruction.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 24 Verse 55. No one else is capable of destroying those Dānavas who have transgressed all boundaries, except for you, O Lord of past and future (bhūtabhavyeśa); for you are indeed their direct adversary in their destruction.

sanskrit: स त्वं देव प्रपन्नानां याचतां च दिवौकसाम् ।कुरु प्रसादं देवेश दानवाञ्जहि शूलभृत् ॥५६॥
transliteration: sa tvaṁ deva prapannānāṁ yācatāṁ ca divaukasām ,kuru prasādaṁ deveśa dānavāñjahi śūlabhṛt.
english: Therefore, O God (deva), O Lord of gods (deveśa), O wielder of the spear (śūlabhṛt), bestow your grace upon the gods (divaukas) who have taken refuge in you and are supplicating; destroy the Dānavas!
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 24 Verse 56. Therefore, O God (deva), O Lord of gods (deveśa), O wielder of the spear (śūlabhṛt), bestow your grace upon the gods (divaukas) who have taken refuge in you and are supplicating; destroy the Dānavas!

sanskrit: श्रीभगवानुवाच ।हन्तव्याः शत्रवः सर्वे युष्माकमिति मे मतिः ।न त्वेकोऽहं वधे तेषां समर्थो वै सुरद्विषाम् ॥५७॥
transliteration: śrībhagavānuvāca ,hantavyāḥ śatravaḥ sarve yuṣmākamiti me matiḥ ,na tveko'haṁ vadhe teṣāṁ samartho vai suradviṣām.
english: The Lord said: "It is my opinion that all your enemies should be killed. However, I alone am certainly not capable of slaying those enemies of the gods."
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 24 Verse 57. The Lord said: "It is my opinion that all your enemies should be killed. However, I alone am certainly not capable of slaying those enemies of the gods."

sanskrit: ते यूयं सहिताः सर्वे मदीयेनास्त्रतेजसा ।जयध्वं युधि ताञ्शत्रून्संघातो हि महाबलः ॥५८॥
transliteration: te yūyaṁ sahitāḥ sarve madīyenāstratejasā ,jayadhvaṁ yudhi tāñśatrūnsaṁghāto hi mahābalaḥ.
english: You all, united with the power of my weapon, conquer those enemies in battle! For indeed, unity is immensely powerful.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 24 Verse 58. You all, united with the power of my weapon, conquer those enemies in battle! For indeed, unity is immensely powerful.

sanskrit: देवा ऊचुः ।अस्मत्तेजोबलं यावत्तावद्द्विगुणमेव च ।तेषामिति ह मन्यामो दृष्टतेजोबला हि ते ॥५९॥
transliteration: devā ūcuḥ ,asmattejobalaṁ yāvattāvaddviguṇameva ca ,teṣāmiti ha manyāmo dṛṣṭatejobalā hi te.
english: The gods said: "We believe that their power and might are twice as great as ours, for their strength and power have already been witnessed."
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 24 Verse 59. The gods said: "We believe that their power and might are twice as great as ours, for their strength and power have already been witnessed."

sanskrit: भगवानुवाच ।वध्यास्ते सर्वतः पापा ये युष्मास्वपराधिनः ।मम तेजोबलार्धेन सर्वांस्तान्घ्नत शात्रवान् ॥६०॥
transliteration: bhagavānuvāca ,vadhyāste sarvataḥ pāpā ye yuṣmāsvaparādhinaḥ ,mama tejobalārdhena sarvāṁstānghnata śātravān.
english: The Lord said: "Those sinful ones who are offenders against you are to be utterly destroyed. Slay all those enemies using half of my power and might!"
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 24 Verse 60. The Lord said: "Those sinful ones who are offenders against you are to be utterly destroyed. Slay all those enemies using half of my power and might!"

sanskrit: देवा ऊचुः ।बिभर्तुं तेजसोऽर्धं ते न शक्ष्यामो महेश्वर ।सर्वेषां नो बलार्धेन त्वमेव जहि शात्रवान् ॥६१॥
transliteration: devā ūcuḥ ,bibhartuṁ tejaso'rdhaṁ te na śakṣyāmo maheśvara ,sarveṣāṁ no balārdhena tvameva jahi śātravān.
english: The gods said: "O great lord, we cannot bear even half of your splendor. Therefore, you alone, empowered by half the strength of all of us, must destroy the enemies."
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 24 Verse 61. The gods said: "O great lord, we cannot bear even half of your splendor. Therefore, you alone, empowered by half the strength of all of us, must destroy the enemies."

sanskrit: दुर्योधन उवाच ।ततस्तथेति देवेशस्तैरुक्तो राजसत्तम ।अर्धमादाय सर्वेभ्यस्तेजसाभ्यधिकोऽभवत् ॥६२॥
transliteration: duryodhana uvāca ,tatastatheti deveśastairukto rājasattama ,ardhamādāya sarvebhyastejasābhyadhiko'bhavat.
english: Duryodhana said: "O best of kings, when the lord of the gods was told 'So be it' by them, he took half of the energy from all of them, and thereby became exceedingly superior in splendor."
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 24 Verse 62. Duryodhana said: "O best of kings, when the lord of the gods was told 'So be it' by them, he took half of the energy from all of them, and thereby became exceedingly superior in splendor."

sanskrit: स तु देवो बलेनासीत्सर्वेभ्यो बलवत्तरः ।महादेव इति ख्यातस्तदाप्रभृति शंकरः ॥६३॥
transliteration: sa tu devo balenāsītsarvebhyo balavattaraḥ ,mahādeva iti khyātastadāprabhṛti śaṁkaraḥ.
english: That god, Śaṅkara, truly became stronger than all (the other gods) in might. From that time onwards, he was renowned as the great god.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 24 Verse 63. That god, Śaṅkara, truly became stronger than all (the other gods) in might. From that time onwards, he was renowned as the great god.

sanskrit: ततोऽब्रवीन्महादेवो धनुर्बाणधरस्त्वहम् ।हनिष्यामि रथेनाजौ तान्रिपून्वै दिवौकसः ॥६४॥
transliteration: tato'bravīnmahādevo dhanurbāṇadharastvaham ,haniṣyāmi rathenājau tānripūnvai divaukasaḥ.
english: Then the great god, holding his bow and arrow, declared: "Indeed, I will destroy those enemies - the celestial dwellers - in battle with my chariot."
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 24 Verse 64. Then the great god, holding his bow and arrow, declared: "Indeed, I will destroy those enemies - the celestial dwellers - in battle with my chariot."

sanskrit: ते यूयं मे रथं चैव धनुर्बाणं तथैव च ।पश्यध्वं यावदद्यैतान्पातयामि महीतले ॥६५॥
transliteration: te yūyaṁ me rathaṁ caiva dhanurbāṇaṁ tathaiva ca ,paśyadhvaṁ yāvadadyaitānpātayāmi mahītale.
english: You all should now behold my chariot, bow, and arrows, as I cast these (enemies) down to the earth today.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 24 Verse 65. You all should now behold my chariot, bow, and arrows, as I cast these (enemies) down to the earth today.

sanskrit: देवा ऊचुः ।मूर्तिसर्वस्वमादाय त्रैलोक्यस्य ततस्ततः ।रथं ते कल्पयिष्याम देवेश्वर महौजसम् ॥६६॥
transliteration: devā ūcuḥ ,mūrtisarvasvamādāya trailokyasya tatastataḥ ,rathaṁ te kalpayiṣyāma deveśvara mahaujasam.
english: The gods said: "O Lord of gods, we will fashion for you a magnificent chariot of great splendor, having gathered the essence of all forms from various places throughout the three worlds."
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 24 Verse 66. The gods said: "O Lord of gods, we will fashion for you a magnificent chariot of great splendor, having gathered the essence of all forms from various places throughout the three worlds."

sanskrit: तथैव बुद्ध्या विहितं विश्वकर्मकृतं शुभम् ।ततो विबुधशार्दूलास्तं रथं समकल्पयन् ॥६७॥
transliteration: tathaiva buddhyā vihitaṁ viśvakarmakṛtaṁ śubham ,tato vibudhaśārdūlāstaṁ rathaṁ samakalpayan.
english: Similarly, that auspicious chariot, conceived with divine intelligence and crafted by Viśvakarman, was then assembled by the foremost among the gods.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 24 Verse 67. Similarly, that auspicious chariot, conceived with divine intelligence and crafted by Viśvakarman, was then assembled by the foremost among the gods.

sanskrit: वन्धुरं पृथिवीं देवीं विशालपुरमालिनीम् ।सपर्वतवनद्वीपां चक्रुर्भूतधरां तदा ॥६८॥
transliteration: vandhuraṁ pṛthivīṁ devīṁ viśālapuramālinīm ,saparvatavanadvīpāṁ cakrurbhūtadharāṁ tadā.
english: Then they transformed the goddess Earth, with her vast cities, mountains, forests, and islands, and her capacity to bear all beings, into the very body of the chariot.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 24 Verse 68. Then they transformed the goddess Earth, with her vast cities, mountains, forests, and islands, and her capacity to bear all beings, into the very body of the chariot.

sanskrit: मन्दरं पर्वतं चाक्षं जङ्घास्तस्य महानदीः ।दिशश्च प्रदिशश्चैव परिवारं रथस्य हि ॥६९॥
transliteration: mandaraṁ parvataṁ cākṣaṁ jaṅghāstasya mahānadīḥ ,diśaśca pradiśaścaiva parivāraṁ rathasya hi.
english: Mandara mountain and other mountains were indeed made its axle. The great rivers became its shanks, and the directions and intermediate directions were truly the chariot's retinue.
