search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 24 Verse 69. Mandara mountain and other mountains were indeed made its axle. The great rivers became its shanks, and the directions and intermediate directions were truly the chariot's retinue.

sanskrit: अनुकर्षान्ग्रहान्दीप्तान्वरूथं चापि तारकाः ।धर्मार्थकामसंयुक्तं त्रिवेणुं चापि बन्धुरम् ।ओषधीर्विविधास्तत्र नानापुष्पफलोद्गमाः ॥७०॥
transliteration: anukarṣāngrahāndīptānvarūthaṁ cāpi tārakāḥ ,dharmārthakāmasaṁyuktaṁ triveṇuṁ cāpi bandhuram ,oṣadhīrvividhāstatra nānāpuṣpaphalodgamāḥ.
english: The shining planets were the draft-poles, and the stars also formed its body or fence. Furthermore, the beautiful three-shafted pole was endowed with (natural law) dharma, wealth, and desire. There were various herbs there, from which diverse flowers and fruits emerged.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 24 Verse 70. The shining planets were the draft-poles, and the stars also formed its body or fence. Furthermore, the beautiful three-shafted pole was endowed with (natural law) dharma, wealth, and desire. There were various herbs there, from which diverse flowers and fruits emerged.

sanskrit: सूर्याचन्द्रमसौ कृत्वा चक्रे रथवरोत्तमे ।पक्षौ पूर्वापरौ तत्र कृते रात्र्यहनी शुभे ॥७१॥
transliteration: sūryācandramasau kṛtvā cakre rathavarottame ,pakṣau pūrvāparau tatra kṛte rātryahanī śubhe.
english: Having made the Sun and Moon into the two most excellent chariot-wheels, there, the auspicious night and day were made the two eastern and western sides.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 24 Verse 71. Having made the Sun and Moon into the two most excellent chariot-wheels, there, the auspicious night and day were made the two eastern and western sides.

sanskrit: दश नागपतीनीषां धृतराष्ट्रमुखान्दृढाम् ।द्यां युगं युगचर्माणि संवर्तकबलाहकान् ॥७२॥
transliteration: daśa nāgapatīnīṣāṁ dhṛtarāṣṭramukhāndṛḍhām ,dyāṁ yugaṁ yugacarmāṇi saṁvartakabalāhakān.
english: The ten lords of serpents, led by Dhṛtarāṣṭra, formed the strong chariot-pole. The sky became the yoke, and the Saṃvartaka and Balāhaka clouds became the yoke-straps.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 24 Verse 72. The ten lords of serpents, led by Dhṛtarāṣṭra, formed the strong chariot-pole. The sky became the yoke, and the Saṃvartaka and Balāhaka clouds became the yoke-straps.

sanskrit: शम्यां धृतिं च मेधां च स्थितिं संनतिमेव च ।ग्रहनक्षत्रताराभिश्चर्म चित्रं नभस्तलम् ॥७३॥
transliteration: śamyāṁ dhṛtiṁ ca medhāṁ ca sthitiṁ saṁnatimeva ca ,grahanakṣatratārābhiścarma citraṁ nabhastalam.
english: The linchpin, firmness (dhṛti), intelligence (medhā), stability, and humility were fashioned. The variegated surface of the sky (nabhastalam), adorned by planets (graha), constellations (nakṣatra), and stars, became its shield (carma).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 24 Verse 73. The linchpin, firmness (dhṛti), intelligence (medhā), stability, and humility were fashioned. The variegated surface of the sky (nabhastalam), adorned by planets (graha), constellations (nakṣatra), and stars, became its shield (carma).

sanskrit: सुराम्बुप्रेतवित्तानां पतीँल्लोकेश्वरान्हयान् ।सिनीवालीमनुमतिं कुहूं राकां च सुव्रताम् ।योक्त्राणि चक्रुर्वाहानां रोहकांश्चापि कण्ठकम् ॥७४॥
transliteration: surāmbupretavittānāṁ patīँllokeśvarānhayān ,sinīvālīmanumatiṁ kuhūṁ rākāṁ ca suvratām ,yoktrāṇi cakrurvāhānāṁ rohakāṁścāpi kaṇṭhakam.
english: They fashioned the lords of the gods, waters, spirits (preta), and wealth - who are the rulers of the worlds - and the horses. They also fashioned the virtuous (suvratā) goddesses Sinīvālī, Anumati, Kuhū, and Rākā. For the draught-animals (vāha), they made the harnesses, along with the reins (rohaka) and a neck-rope (kaṇṭhaka).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 24 Verse 74. They fashioned the lords of the gods, waters, spirits (preta), and wealth - who are the rulers of the worlds - and the horses. They also fashioned the virtuous (suvratā) goddesses Sinīvālī, Anumati, Kuhū, and Rākā. For the draught-animals (vāha), they made the harnesses, along with the reins (rohaka) and a neck-rope (kaṇṭhaka).

sanskrit: कर्म सत्यं तपोऽर्थश्च विहितास्तत्र रश्मयः ।अधिष्ठानं मनस्त्वासीत्परिरथ्यं सरस्वती ॥७५॥
transliteration: karma satyaṁ tapo'rthaśca vihitāstatra raśmayaḥ ,adhiṣṭhānaṁ manastvāsītparirathyaṁ sarasvatī.
english: There, action (karma), truth (satya), austerity (tapas), and purpose (artha) were fashioned as the reins. The mind (manas), indeed, was the seat, and Sarasvatī the surrounding parts of the chariot.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 24 Verse 75. There, action (karma), truth (satya), austerity (tapas), and purpose (artha) were fashioned as the reins. The mind (manas), indeed, was the seat, and Sarasvatī the surrounding parts of the chariot.

sanskrit: नानावर्णाश्च चित्राश्च पताकाः पवनेरिताः ।विद्युदिन्द्रधनुर्नद्धं रथं दीप्तं व्यदीपयत् ॥७६॥
transliteration: nānāvarṇāśca citrāśca patākāḥ pavaneritāḥ ,vidyudindradhanurnaddhaṁ rathaṁ dīptaṁ vyadīpayat.
english: Multicolored and variegated banners, swayed by the wind, were present. Lightning (vidyut) illuminated the brilliant chariot, which was equipped with a rainbow (indradhanu).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 24 Verse 76. Multicolored and variegated banners, swayed by the wind, were present. Lightning (vidyut) illuminated the brilliant chariot, which was equipped with a rainbow (indradhanu).

sanskrit: एवं तस्मिन्महाराज कल्पिते रथसत्तमे ।देवैर्मनुजशार्दूल द्विषतामभिमर्दने ॥७७॥
transliteration: evaṁ tasminmahārāja kalpite rathasattame ,devairmanujaśārdūla dviṣatāmabhimardane.
english: Thus, O great king, O tiger among men, when that best of chariots, fashioned by the gods for crushing the enemies, was prepared,
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 24 Verse 77. Thus, O great king, O tiger among men, when that best of chariots, fashioned by the gods for crushing the enemies, was prepared,

sanskrit: स्वान्यायुधानि मुख्यानि न्यदधाच्छंकरो रथे ।रथयष्टिं वियत्कृष्टां स्थापयामास गोवृषम् ॥७८॥
transliteration: svānyāyudhāni mukhyāni nyadadhācchaṁkaro rathe ,rathayaṣṭiṁ viyatkṛṣṭāṁ sthāpayāmāsa govṛṣam.
english: Shankara placed his own principal weapons in the chariot. He set up the sky-reaching chariot-pole, which was a bull (or bore a bull emblem).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 24 Verse 78. Shankara placed his own principal weapons in the chariot. He set up the sky-reaching chariot-pole, which was a bull (or bore a bull emblem).

sanskrit: ब्रह्मदण्डः कालदण्डो रुद्रदण्डस्तथा ज्वरः ।परिस्कन्दा रथस्यास्य सर्वतोदिशमुद्यताः ॥७९॥
transliteration: brahmadaṇḍaḥ kāladaṇḍo rudradaṇḍastathā jvaraḥ ,pariskandā rathasyāsya sarvatodiśamudyatāḥ.
english: Brahma's rod, Kala's rod, Rudra's rod, and also Fever (Jvara) were the attendants of this chariot, standing ready in all directions.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 24 Verse 79. Brahma's rod, Kala's rod, Rudra's rod, and also Fever (Jvara) were the attendants of this chariot, standing ready in all directions.

sanskrit: अथर्वाङ्गिरसावास्तां चक्ररक्षौ महात्मनः ।ऋग्वेदः सामवेदश्च पुराणं च पुरःसराः ॥८०॥
transliteration: atharvāṅgirasāvāstāṁ cakrarakṣau mahātmanaḥ ,ṛgvedaḥ sāmavedaśca purāṇaṁ ca puraḥsarāḥ.
english: Atharvan and Angiras were the two protectors of the wheels of the great one (Shiva). The Rigveda, the Samaveda, and the Purana were the vanguard.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 24 Verse 80. Atharvan and Angiras were the two protectors of the wheels of the great one (Shiva). The Rigveda, the Samaveda, and the Purana were the vanguard.

sanskrit: इतिहासयजुर्वेदौ पृष्ठरक्षौ बभूवतुः ।दिव्या वाचश्च विद्याश्च परिपार्श्वचराः कृताः ॥८१॥
transliteration: itihāsayajurvedau pṛṣṭharakṣau babhūvatuḥ ,divyā vācaśca vidyāśca paripārśvacarāḥ kṛtāḥ.
english: History (itihāsa) and the Yajurveda became the protectors of his rear. Divine speeches and various branches of knowledge (vidyās) were made his attendants.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 24 Verse 81. History (itihāsa) and the Yajurveda became the protectors of his rear. Divine speeches and various branches of knowledge (vidyās) were made his attendants.

sanskrit: तोत्त्रादयश्च राजेन्द्र वषट्कारस्तथैव च ।ओंकारश्च मुखे राजन्नतिशोभाकरोऽभवत् ॥८२॥
transliteration: tottrādayaśca rājendra vaṣaṭkārastathaiva ca ,oṁkāraśca mukhe rājannatiśobhākaro'bhavat.
english: O king (rājendra), goads and similar instruments, as well as the ritual exclamation (vaṣaṭkāra), and the sacred syllable Oṃ (oṃkāra) in his mouth, became exceedingly splendid.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 24 Verse 82. O king (rājendra), goads and similar instruments, as well as the ritual exclamation (vaṣaṭkāra), and the sacred syllable Oṃ (oṃkāra) in his mouth, became exceedingly splendid.

sanskrit: विचित्रमृतुभिः षड्भिः कृत्वा संवत्सरं धनुः ।तस्मान्नॄणां कालरात्रिर्ज्या कृता धनुषोऽजरा ॥८३॥
transliteration: vicitramṛtubhiḥ ṣaḍbhiḥ kṛtvā saṁvatsaraṁ dhanuḥ ,tasmānnṝṇāṁ kālarātrirjyā kṛtā dhanuṣo'jarā.
english: Having made the year (saṃvatsara) into a bow with its six varied seasons, the undecaying Night of Time (kālarātri) was therefore made the bowstring of that bow for human beings.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 24 Verse 83. Having made the year (saṃvatsara) into a bow with its six varied seasons, the undecaying Night of Time (kālarātri) was therefore made the bowstring of that bow for human beings.

sanskrit: इषुश्चाप्यभवद्विष्णुर्ज्वलनः सोम एव च ।अग्नीषोमौ जगत्कृत्स्नं वैष्णवं चोच्यते जगत् ॥८४॥
transliteration: iṣuścāpyabhavadviṣṇurjvalanaḥ soma eva ca ,agnīṣomau jagatkṛtsnaṁ vaiṣṇavaṁ cocyate jagat.
english: And the arrow (iṣu) also became Viṣṇu, fire (jvalana), and indeed Soma. Agni and Soma together constitute the entire universe (jagat), and the universe (jagat) is also declared to be Vaishnava (vaiṣṇavam), belonging to Viṣṇu.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 24 Verse 84. And the arrow (iṣu) also became Viṣṇu, fire (jvalana), and indeed Soma. Agni and Soma together constitute the entire universe (jagat), and the universe (jagat) is also declared to be Vaishnava (vaiṣṇavam), belonging to Viṣṇu.

sanskrit: विष्णुश्चात्मा भगवतो भवस्यामिततेजसः ।तस्माद्धनुर्ज्यासंस्पर्शं न विषेहुर्हरस्य ते ॥८५॥
transliteration: viṣṇuścātmā bhagavato bhavasyāmitatejasaḥ ,tasmāddhanurjyāsaṁsparśaṁ na viṣehurharasya te.
english: And Vishnu is the very essence (ātman) of the glorious Bhava (Shiva), who possesses immeasurable splendor. For this reason, those opposing him could not withstand the touch of Hara's (Shiva's) bowstring.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 24 Verse 85. And Vishnu is the very essence (ātman) of the glorious Bhava (Shiva), who possesses immeasurable splendor. For this reason, those opposing him could not withstand the touch of Hara's (Shiva's) bowstring.

sanskrit: तस्मिञ्शरे तिग्ममन्युर्मुमोचाविषहं प्रभुः ।भृग्वङ्गिरोमन्युभवं क्रोधाग्निमतिदुःसहम् ॥८६॥
transliteration: tasmiñśare tigmamanyurmumocāviṣahaṁ prabhuḥ ,bhṛgvaṅgiromanyubhavaṁ krodhāgnimatiduḥsaham.
english: The Lord (Shiva), filled with fierce wrath, discharged into that arrow an irresistible, exceedingly formidable fire of wrath, which was born from the indignation of Bhrigu and Angiras.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 24 Verse 86. The Lord (Shiva), filled with fierce wrath, discharged into that arrow an irresistible, exceedingly formidable fire of wrath, which was born from the indignation of Bhrigu and Angiras.

sanskrit: स नीललोहितो धूम्रः कृत्तिवासा भयंकरः ।आदित्यायुतसंकाशस्तेजोज्वालावृतो ज्वलन् ॥८७॥
transliteration: sa nīlalohito dhūmraḥ kṛttivāsā bhayaṁkaraḥ ,ādityāyutasaṁkāśastejojvālāvṛto jvalan.
english: He (Shiva), blue-red, dusky, clad in animal hides, terrifying, resembling ten thousand suns, enveloped in blazing flames of splendor, shone intensely.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 24 Verse 87. He (Shiva), blue-red, dusky, clad in animal hides, terrifying, resembling ten thousand suns, enveloped in blazing flames of splendor, shone intensely.

sanskrit: दुश्च्यावश्च्यावनो जेता हन्ता ब्रह्मद्विषां हरः ।नित्यं त्राता च हन्ता च धर्माधर्माश्रिताञ्जनान् ॥८८॥
transliteration: duścyāvaścyāvano jetā hantā brahmadviṣāṁ haraḥ ,nityaṁ trātā ca hantā ca dharmādharmāśritāñjanān.
english: Hara (Shiva) is the unshakeable yet the shaker, the conqueror, and the slayer of those hostile to Brahman. He is perpetually both the protector and the destroyer of beings who are entangled with both natural law (dharma) and its opposite (adharma).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 24 Verse 88. Hara (Shiva) is the unshakeable yet the shaker, the conqueror, and the slayer of those hostile to Brahman. He is perpetually both the protector and the destroyer of beings who are entangled with both natural law (dharma) and its opposite (adharma).

sanskrit: प्रमाथिभिर्घोररूपैर्भीमोदग्रैर्गणैर्वृतः ।विभाति भगवान्स्थाणुस्तैरेवात्मगुणैर्वृतः ॥८९॥
transliteration: pramāthibhirghorarūpairbhīmodagrairgaṇairvṛtaḥ ,vibhāti bhagavānsthāṇustairevātmaguṇairvṛtaḥ.
english: The divine Lord Sthāṇu (Shiva) shines, surrounded by hosts (gaṇaiḥ) that are tormenting, of dreadful appearance, and fierce and formidable; and truly, he is endowed with these very same intrinsic qualities (ātmaguṇaiḥ).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 24 Verse 89. The divine Lord Sthāṇu (Shiva) shines, surrounded by hosts (gaṇaiḥ) that are tormenting, of dreadful appearance, and fierce and formidable; and truly, he is endowed with these very same intrinsic qualities (ātmaguṇaiḥ).

sanskrit: तस्याङ्गानि समाश्रित्य स्थितं विश्वमिदं जगत् ।जङ्गमाजङ्गमं राजञ्शुशुभेऽद्भुतदर्शनम् ॥९०॥
transliteration: tasyāṅgāni samāśritya sthitaṁ viśvamidaṁ jagat ,jaṅgamājaṅgamaṁ rājañśuśubhe'dbhutadarśanam.
english: O King, this entire universe (viśvam jagat), both moving and non-moving, having taken shelter in his (Shiva's) limbs, shone forth with a wondrous appearance.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 24 Verse 90. O King, this entire universe (viśvam jagat), both moving and non-moving, having taken shelter in his (Shiva's) limbs, shone forth with a wondrous appearance.

sanskrit: दृष्ट्वा तु तं रथं दिव्यं कवची स शरासनी ।बाणमादत्त तं दिव्यं सोमविष्ण्वग्निसंभवम् ॥९१॥
transliteration: dṛṣṭvā tu taṁ rathaṁ divyaṁ kavacī sa śarāsanī ,bāṇamādatta taṁ divyaṁ somaviṣṇvagnisaṁbhavam.
english: But having seen that divine chariot, he, who was armored and bore a bow, took up that divine arrow, which had originated from Soma, Viṣṇu, and Agni.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 24 Verse 91. But having seen that divine chariot, he, who was armored and bore a bow, took up that divine arrow, which had originated from Soma, Viṣṇu, and Agni.

sanskrit: तस्य वाजांस्ततो देवाः कल्पयां चक्रिरे विभोः ।पुण्यगन्धवहं राजञ्श्वसनं राजसत्तम ॥९२॥
transliteration: tasya vājāṁstato devāḥ kalpayāṁ cakrire vibhoḥ ,puṇyagandhavahaṁ rājañśvasanaṁ rājasattama.
english: Then, O King, O best of kings, the gods created mighty powers (vājān) for that Lord (vibhoḥ), and they made the wind (śvasanam) a carrier of sacred fragrance.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 24 Verse 92. Then, O King, O best of kings, the gods created mighty powers (vājān) for that Lord (vibhoḥ), and they made the wind (śvasanam) a carrier of sacred fragrance.

sanskrit: तमास्थाय महादेवस्त्रासयन्दैवतान्यपि ।आरुरोह तदा यत्तः कम्पयन्निव रोदसी ॥९३॥
transliteration: tamāsthāya mahādevastrāsayandaivatānyapi ,āruroha tadā yattaḥ kampayanniva rodasī.
english: Lord Shiva, having taken up that (chariot), then mounted it, prepared, frightening even the deities and seemingly making both heaven and earth tremble.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 24 Verse 93. Lord Shiva, having taken up that (chariot), then mounted it, prepared, frightening even the deities and seemingly making both heaven and earth tremble.

sanskrit: स शोभमानो वरदः खड्गी बाणी शरासनी ।हसन्निवाब्रवीद्देवो सारथिः को भविष्यति ॥९४॥
transliteration: sa śobhamāno varadaḥ khaḍgī bāṇī śarāsanī ,hasannivābravīddevo sārathiḥ ko bhaviṣyati.
english: That god, shining, bestowing boons, and equipped with a sword, arrows, and a bow, spoke as if smiling, "Who will be my charioteer?"
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 24 Verse 94. That god, shining, bestowing boons, and equipped with a sword, arrows, and a bow, spoke as if smiling, "Who will be my charioteer?"

sanskrit: तमब्रुवन्देवगणा यं भवान्संनियोक्ष्यते ।स भविष्यति देवेश सारथिस्ते न संशयः ॥९५॥
transliteration: tamabruvandevagaṇā yaṁ bhavānsaṁniyokṣyate ,sa bhaviṣyati deveśa sārathiste na saṁśayaḥ.
english: The hosts of gods said to him, "Whomever you (bhavān) appoint, O Lord of gods, he will certainly be your charioteer; there is no doubt about it."
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 24 Verse 95. The hosts of gods said to him, "Whomever you (bhavān) appoint, O Lord of gods, he will certainly be your charioteer; there is no doubt about it."

sanskrit: तानब्रवीत्पुनर्देवो मत्तः श्रेष्ठतरो हि यः ।तं सारथिं कुरुध्वं मे स्वयं संचिन्त्य माचिरम् ॥९६॥
transliteration: tānabravītpunardevo mattaḥ śreṣṭhataro hi yaḥ ,taṁ sārathiṁ kurudhvaṁ me svayaṁ saṁcintya māciram.
english: The god then said to them again, "Indeed, whoever is superior to me, choose him as my charioteer yourselves, and do not delay."
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 24 Verse 96. The god then said to them again, "Indeed, whoever is superior to me, choose him as my charioteer yourselves, and do not delay."

sanskrit: एतच्छ्रुत्वा ततो देवा वाक्यमुक्तं महात्मना ।गत्वा पितामहं देवं प्रसाद्यैवं वचोऽब्रुवन् ॥९७॥
transliteration: etacchrutvā tato devā vākyamuktaṁ mahātmanā ,gatvā pitāmahaṁ devaṁ prasādyaivaṁ vaco'bruvan.
english: Having heard these words spoken by the great soul, the gods then went to the divine grandfather (Brahma), and after appeasing him, they spoke these words.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 24 Verse 97. Having heard these words spoken by the great soul, the gods then went to the divine grandfather (Brahma), and after appeasing him, they spoke these words.

sanskrit: देव त्वयेदं कथितं त्रिदशारिनिबर्हणम् ।तथा च कृतमस्माभिः प्रसन्नो वृषभध्वजः ॥९८॥
transliteration: deva tvayedaṁ kathitaṁ tridaśārinibarhaṇam ,tathā ca kṛtamasmābhiḥ prasanno vṛṣabhadhvajaḥ.
english: O God, you had spoken of this destruction of the enemies of the gods. And indeed, it has been accomplished by us, and Lord Shiva (Vṛṣabhadhvaja) is pleased.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 24 Verse 98. O God, you had spoken of this destruction of the enemies of the gods. And indeed, it has been accomplished by us, and Lord Shiva (Vṛṣabhadhvaja) is pleased.

sanskrit: रथश्च विहितोऽस्माभिर्विचित्रायुधसंवृतः ।सारथिं तु न जानीमः कः स्यात्तस्मिन्रथोत्तमे ॥९९॥
transliteration: rathaśca vihito'smābhirvicitrāyudhasaṁvṛtaḥ ,sārathiṁ tu na jānīmaḥ kaḥ syāttasminrathottame.
english: And a chariot, equipped with various weapons, has been prepared by us. But we do not know who might be the charioteer for that supreme chariot.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 24 Verse 99. And a chariot, equipped with various weapons, has been prepared by us. But we do not know who might be the charioteer for that supreme chariot.

sanskrit: तस्माद्विधीयतां कश्चित्सारथिर्देवसत्तम ।सफलां तां गिरं देव कर्तुमर्हसि नो विभो ॥१००॥
transliteration: tasmādvidhīyatāṁ kaścitsārathirdevasattama ,saphalāṁ tāṁ giraṁ deva kartumarhasi no vibho.
english: Therefore, O best among gods, please appoint someone as a charioteer. O divine Lord, you ought to make our word successful.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 24 Verse 100. Therefore, O best among gods, please appoint someone as a charioteer. O divine Lord, you ought to make our word successful.

sanskrit: एवमस्मासु हि पुरा भगवन्नुक्तवानसि ।हितं कर्तास्मि भवतामिति तत्कर्तुमर्हसि ॥१०१॥
transliteration: evamasmāsu hi purā bhagavannuktavānasi ,hitaṁ kartāsmi bhavatāmiti tatkartumarhasi.
english: O revered one (bhagavan), you indeed told us previously, 'I will act for your welfare.' Therefore, you should now do that.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 24 Verse 101. O revered one (bhagavan), you indeed told us previously, 'I will act for your welfare.' Therefore, you should now do that.

sanskrit: स देव युक्तो रथसत्तमो नो दुरावरो द्रावणः शात्रवाणाम् ।पिनाकपाणिर्विहितोऽत्र योद्धा विभीषयन्दानवानुद्यतोऽसौ ॥१०२॥
transliteration: sa deva yukto rathasattamo no; durāvaro drāvaṇaḥ śātravāṇām ,pinākapāṇirvihito'tra yoddhā; vibhīṣayandānavānudyato'sau.
english: O divine one, that best of chariots of ours, hard to restrain and capable of routing enemies, has been appointed here, with Pinaka in hand, as a warrior. He stands ready, terrifying the Dānavas.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 24 Verse 102. O divine one, that best of chariots of ours, hard to restrain and capable of routing enemies, has been appointed here, with Pinaka in hand, as a warrior. He stands ready, terrifying the Dānavas.

sanskrit: तथैव वेदाश्चतुरो हयाग्र्या धरा सशैला च रथो महात्मन् ।नक्षत्रवंशोऽनुगतो वरूथे यस्मिन्योद्धा सारथिनाभिरक्ष्यः ॥१०३॥
transliteration: tathaiva vedāścaturo hayāgryā; dharā saśailā ca ratho mahātman ,nakṣatravaṁśo'nugato varūthe; yasminyoddhā sārathinābhirakṣyaḥ.
english: O great soul (mahātman), similarly, the four excellent Vedas are the horses, and the earth with its mountains is the chariot. The host of constellations follows within the chariot's protective body (varūtha), in which the warrior is to be guarded by the charioteer.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 24 Verse 103. O great soul (mahātman), similarly, the four excellent Vedas are the horses, and the earth with its mountains is the chariot. The host of constellations follows within the chariot's protective body (varūtha), in which the warrior is to be guarded by the charioteer.

sanskrit: तत्र सारथिरेष्टव्यः सर्वैरेतैर्विशेषवान् ।तत्प्रतिष्ठो रथो देव हया योद्धा तथैव च ।कवचानि च शस्त्राणि कार्मुकं च पितामह ॥१०४॥
transliteration: tatra sārathireṣṭavyaḥ sarvairetairviśeṣavān ,tatpratiṣṭho ratho deva hayā yoddhā tathaiva ca ,kavacāni ca śastrāṇi kārmukaṁ ca pitāmaha.
english: O divine one, O Grandfather (pitāmaha), in that matter, an excellent charioteer, endowed with special qualities, should be chosen by all these. The chariot, the horses, the warrior, and likewise the armors, weapons, and the bow, all depend on that (charioteer).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 24 Verse 104. O divine one, O Grandfather (pitāmaha), in that matter, an excellent charioteer, endowed with special qualities, should be chosen by all these. The chariot, the horses, the warrior, and likewise the armors, weapons, and the bow, all depend on that (charioteer).

sanskrit: त्वामृते सारथिं तत्र नान्यं पश्यामहे वयम् ।त्वं हि सर्वैर्गुणैर्युक्तो देवताभ्योऽधिकः प्रभो ।सारथ्ये तूर्णमारोह संयच्छ परमान्हयान् ॥१०५॥
transliteration: tvāmṛte sārathiṁ tatra nānyaṁ paśyāmahe vayam ,tvaṁ hi sarvairguṇairyukto devatābhyo'dhikaḥ prabho ,sārathye tūrṇamāroha saṁyaccha paramānhayān.
english: O Lord, we see no other charioteer there apart from you. Indeed, you are endowed with all excellent qualities and are superior even to the gods. Quickly ascend to the charioteer's seat and control the supreme horses.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 24 Verse 105. O Lord, we see no other charioteer there apart from you. Indeed, you are endowed with all excellent qualities and are superior even to the gods. Quickly ascend to the charioteer's seat and control the supreme horses.

sanskrit: इति ते शिरसा नत्वा त्रिलोकेशं पितामहम् ।देवाः प्रसादयामासुः सारथ्यायेति नः श्रुतम् ॥१०६॥
transliteration: iti te śirasā natvā trilokeśaṁ pitāmaham ,devāḥ prasādayāmāsuḥ sārathyāyeti naḥ śrutam.
english: Thus, bowing their heads to that Grandfather (Brahmā), the Lord of the three worlds, the gods propitiated him for the role of charioteer; so we have heard.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 24 Verse 106. Thus, bowing their heads to that Grandfather (Brahmā), the Lord of the three worlds, the gods propitiated him for the role of charioteer; so we have heard.

sanskrit: ब्रह्मोवाच ।नात्र किंचिन्मृषा वाक्यं यदुक्तं वो दिवौकसः ।संयच्छामि हयानेष युध्यतो वै कपर्दिनः ॥१०७॥
transliteration: brahmovāca ,nātra kiṁcinmṛṣā vākyaṁ yaduktaṁ vo divaukasaḥ ,saṁyacchāmi hayāneṣa yudhyato vai kapardinaḥ.
english: Brahmā said: 'O dwellers of heaven, there is nothing false in what you have said. Indeed, I shall control the horses for this Kapardin (Śiva) as he fights.'
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 24 Verse 107. Brahmā said: 'O dwellers of heaven, there is nothing false in what you have said. Indeed, I shall control the horses for this Kapardin (Śiva) as he fights.'

sanskrit: ततः स भगवान्देवो लोकस्रष्टा पितामहः ।सारथ्ये कल्पितो देवैरीशानस्य महात्मनः ॥१०८॥
transliteration: tataḥ sa bhagavāndevo lokasraṣṭā pitāmahaḥ ,sārathye kalpito devairīśānasya mahātmanaḥ.
english: Then, that divine and revered Grandfather (Brahmā), the creator of the worlds, was appointed by the gods for the charioteer's role of the great-souled Īśāna (Śiva).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 24 Verse 108. Then, that divine and revered Grandfather (Brahmā), the creator of the worlds, was appointed by the gods for the charioteer's role of the great-souled Īśāna (Śiva).

sanskrit: तस्मिन्नारोहति क्षिप्रं स्यन्दनं लोकपूजिते ।शिरोभिरगमंस्तूर्णं ते हया वातरंहसः ॥१०९॥
transliteration: tasminnārohati kṣipraṁ syandanaṁ lokapūjite ,śirobhiragamaṁstūrṇaṁ te hayā vātaraṁhasaḥ.
english: When that deity, revered by all worlds, quickly mounted the chariot, those horses, swift as the wind, swiftly moved forward, leading with their heads.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 24 Verse 109. When that deity, revered by all worlds, quickly mounted the chariot, those horses, swift as the wind, swiftly moved forward, leading with their heads.

sanskrit: महेश्वरे त्वारुहतिजानुभ्यामगमन्महीम् ॥११०॥
transliteration: maheśvare tvāruhatijānubhyāmagamanmahīm.
english: But when Maheśvara mounted, they (the horses) touched the earth with their knees (i.e., knelt down).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 24 Verse 110. But when Maheśvara mounted, they (the horses) touched the earth with their knees (i.e., knelt down).

sanskrit: अभीशून्हि त्रिलोकेशः संगृह्य प्रपितामहः ।तानश्वांश्चोदयामास मनोमारुतरंहसः ॥१११॥
transliteration: abhīśūnhi trilokeśaḥ saṁgṛhya prapitāmahaḥ ,tānaśvāṁścodayāmāsa manomārutaraṁhasaḥ.
english: Indeed, the Lord of the three worlds, the great grandfather (Prapitāmaha), having grasped the reins, drove those horses, whose speed was like that of the mind and the wind.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 24 Verse 111. Indeed, the Lord of the three worlds, the great grandfather (Prapitāmaha), having grasped the reins, drove those horses, whose speed was like that of the mind and the wind.

sanskrit: ततोऽधिरूढे वरदे प्रयाते चासुरान्प्रति ।साधु साध्विति विश्वेशः स्मयमानोऽभ्यभाषत ॥११२॥
transliteration: tato'dhirūḍhe varade prayāte cāsurānprati ,sādhu sādhviti viśveśaḥ smayamāno'bhyabhāṣata.
english: Then, when the boon-giver (Maheśvara) had mounted and set off towards the Asuras, the Lord of the universe, smiling, said, "Excellent! Excellent!"
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 24 Verse 112. Then, when the boon-giver (Maheśvara) had mounted and set off towards the Asuras, the Lord of the universe, smiling, said, "Excellent! Excellent!"

sanskrit: याहि देव यतो दैत्याश्चोदयाश्वानतन्द्रितः ।पश्य बाह्वोर्बलं मेऽद्य निघ्नतः शात्रवान्रणे ॥११३॥
transliteration: yāhi deva yato daityāścodayāśvānatandritaḥ ,paśya bāhvorbalaṁ me'dya nighnataḥ śātravānraṇe.
english: Go, O god, to where the demons are; tirelessly urge the horses on. Today, witness the strength of my arms as I strike down the enemies in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 24 Verse 113. Go, O god, to where the demons are; tirelessly urge the horses on. Today, witness the strength of my arms as I strike down the enemies in battle.

sanskrit: ततस्तांश्चोदयामास वायुवेगसमाञ्जवे ।येन तत्त्रिपुरं राजन्दैत्यदानवरक्षितम् ॥११४॥
transliteration: tatastāṁścodayāmāsa vāyuvegasamāñjave ,yena tattripuraṁ rājandaityadānavarakṣitam.
english: Then he urged those horses, swift as the wind, O King, towards that triple city (Tripura) which was guarded by the Daityas and Dānavas.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 24 Verse 114. Then he urged those horses, swift as the wind, O King, towards that triple city (Tripura) which was guarded by the Daityas and Dānavas.

sanskrit: अथाधिज्यं धनुः कृत्वा शर्वः संधाय तं शरम् ।युक्त्वा पाशुपतास्त्रेण त्रिपुरं समचिन्तयत् ॥११५॥
transliteration: athādhijyaṁ dhanuḥ kṛtvā śarvaḥ saṁdhāya taṁ śaram ,yuktvā pāśupatāstreṇa tripuraṁ samacintayat.
english: Then Shiva, having strung his bow, placed that arrow and empowered it with the Pāśupata missile, and thus contemplated the triple city.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 24 Verse 115. Then Shiva, having strung his bow, placed that arrow and empowered it with the Pāśupata missile, and thus contemplated the triple city.

sanskrit: तस्मिन्स्थिते तदा राजन्क्रुद्धे विधृतकार्मुके ।पुराणि तानि कालेन जग्मुरेकत्वतां तदा ॥११६॥
transliteration: tasminsthite tadā rājankruddhe vidhṛtakārmuke ,purāṇi tāni kālena jagmurekatvatāṁ tadā.
english: At that very moment, O King, as he stood there enraged with his bow drawn, those cities, by the opportune moment, merged into a single entity.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 24 Verse 116. At that very moment, O King, as he stood there enraged with his bow drawn, those cities, by the opportune moment, merged into a single entity.

sanskrit: एकीभावं गते चैव त्रिपुरे समुपागते ।बभूव तुमुलो हर्षो दैवतानां महात्मनाम् ॥११७॥
transliteration: ekībhāvaṁ gate caiva tripure samupāgate ,babhūva tumulo harṣo daivatānāṁ mahātmanām.
english: And indeed, when the three cities (Tripura) converged into a single state, there was tremendous joy among the great-souled deities.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 24 Verse 117. And indeed, when the three cities (Tripura) converged into a single state, there was tremendous joy among the great-souled deities.

sanskrit: ततो देवगणाः सर्वे सिद्धाश्च परमर्षयः ।जयेति वाचो मुमुचुः संस्तुवन्तो मुदान्विताः ॥११८॥
transliteration: tato devagaṇāḥ sarve siddhāśca paramarṣayaḥ ,jayeti vāco mumucuḥ saṁstuvanto mudānvitāḥ.
english: Then all the hosts of gods, the perfected beings (Siddhas), and the great sages (paramarṣayas), filled with joy, uttered words like 'Victory!' as they offered praises.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 24 Verse 118. Then all the hosts of gods, the perfected beings (Siddhas), and the great sages (paramarṣayas), filled with joy, uttered words like 'Victory!' as they offered praises.

sanskrit: ततोऽग्रतः प्रादुरभूत्त्रिपुरं जघ्नुषोऽसुरान् ।अनिर्देश्योग्रवपुषो देवस्यासह्यतेजसः ॥११९॥
transliteration: tato'grataḥ prādurabhūttripuraṁ jaghnuṣo'surān ,anirdeśyogravapuṣo devasyāsahyatejasaḥ.
english: Then, in front, manifested the god who was the slayer of the Asuras of Tripura, a deity possessing an indescribably fierce form and unbearable brilliance (tejas).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 24 Verse 119. Then, in front, manifested the god who was the slayer of the Asuras of Tripura, a deity possessing an indescribably fierce form and unbearable brilliance (tejas).

sanskrit: स तद्विकृष्य भगवान्दिव्यं लोकेश्वरो धनुः ।त्रैलोक्यसारं तमिषुं मुमोच त्रिपुरं प्रति ।तत्सासुरगणं दग्ध्वा प्राक्षिपत्पश्चिमार्णवे ॥१२०॥
transliteration: sa tadvikṛṣya bhagavāndivyaṁ lokeśvaro dhanuḥ ,trailokyasāraṁ tamiṣuṁ mumoca tripuraṁ prati ,tatsāsuragaṇaṁ dagdhvā prākṣipatpaścimārṇave.
english: That divine lord (bhagavān), the lord of the worlds (lokeśvara), having drawn his divine bow, released that arrow - the very essence of the three worlds - towards Tripura. Having incinerated that (city) along with its hosts of Asuras, he cast it into the western ocean.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 24 Verse 120. That divine lord (bhagavān), the lord of the worlds (lokeśvara), having drawn his divine bow, released that arrow - the very essence of the three worlds - towards Tripura. Having incinerated that (city) along with its hosts of Asuras, he cast it into the western ocean.

sanskrit: एवं तत्त्रिपुरं दग्धं दानवाश्चाप्यशेषतः ।महेश्वरेण क्रुद्धेन त्रैलोक्यस्य हितैषिणा ॥१२१॥
transliteration: evaṁ tattripuraṁ dagdhaṁ dānavāścāpyaśeṣataḥ ,maheśvareṇa kruddhena trailokyasya hitaiṣiṇā.
english: In this way, that Tripura (the three cities) was completely burnt, along with all the demons, by the enraged Maheśvara, who was a well-wisher of the three worlds.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 24 Verse 121. In this way, that Tripura (the three cities) was completely burnt, along with all the demons, by the enraged Maheśvara, who was a well-wisher of the three worlds.

sanskrit: स चात्मक्रोधजो वह्निर्हाहेत्युक्त्वा निवारितः ।मा कार्षीर्भस्मसाल्लोकानिति त्र्यक्षोऽब्रवीच्च तम् ॥१२२॥
transliteration: sa cātmakrodhajo vahnirhāhetyuktvā nivāritaḥ ,mā kārṣīrbhasmasāllokāniti tryakṣo'bravīcca tam.
english: And that fire, which was born from his own anger (krodha), was restrained after it cried out, "Alas! Alas!" The three-eyed Shiva then said to it, "Do not turn the worlds into ashes!"
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 24 Verse 122. And that fire, which was born from his own anger (krodha), was restrained after it cried out, "Alas! Alas!" The three-eyed Shiva then said to it, "Do not turn the worlds into ashes!"

sanskrit: ततः प्रकृतिमापन्ना देवा लोकास्तथर्षयः ।तुष्टुवुर्वाग्भिरर्थ्याभिः स्थाणुमप्रतिमौजसम् ॥१२३॥
transliteration: tataḥ prakṛtimāpannā devā lokāstatharṣayaḥ ,tuṣṭuvurvāgbhirarthyābhiḥ sthāṇumapratimaujasam.
english: Then, the gods, the worlds, and also the sages, having returned to their normal state (prakṛti), praised Sthāṇu (Shiva), who possessed incomparable might, with appropriate words.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 24 Verse 123. Then, the gods, the worlds, and also the sages, having returned to their normal state (prakṛti), praised Sthāṇu (Shiva), who possessed incomparable might, with appropriate words.

sanskrit: तेऽनुज्ञाता भगवता जग्मुः सर्वे यथागतम् ।कृतकामाः प्रसन्नेन प्रजापतिमुखाः सुराः ॥१२४॥
transliteration: te'nujñātā bhagavatā jagmuḥ sarve yathāgatam ,kṛtakāmāḥ prasannena prajāpatimukhāḥ surāḥ.
english: All of them, the gods headed by Prajāpati (Brahma), having been granted permission by the revered Lord and with their desires fulfilled by the gracious one, returned to where they had come from.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 24 Verse 124. All of them, the gods headed by Prajāpati (Brahma), having been granted permission by the revered Lord and with their desires fulfilled by the gracious one, returned to where they had come from.

sanskrit: यथैव भगवान्ब्रह्मा लोकधाता पितामहः ।संयच्छ त्वं हयानस्य राधेयस्य महात्मनः ॥१२५॥
transliteration: yathaiva bhagavānbrahmā lokadhātā pitāmahaḥ ,saṁyaccha tvaṁ hayānasya rādheyasya mahātmanaḥ.
english: Just as the venerable Lord Brahmā himself is the creator of worlds and the great grandfather, so should you (Shalya) control the horses of this great-souled son of Rādhā (Rādheya).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 24 Verse 125. Just as the venerable Lord Brahmā himself is the creator of worlds and the great grandfather, so should you (Shalya) control the horses of this great-souled son of Rādhā (Rādheya).

sanskrit: त्वं हि कृष्णाच्च कर्णाच्च फल्गुनाच्च विशेषतः ।विशिष्टो राजशार्दूल नास्ति तत्र विचारणा ॥१२६॥
transliteration: tvaṁ hi kṛṣṇācca karṇācca phalgunācca viśeṣataḥ ,viśiṣṭo rājaśārdūla nāsti tatra vicāraṇā.
english: Indeed, you, O tiger among kings (rājaśārdūla), are superior to Kṛṣṇa, Karna, and especially to Arjuna (Phālguna). There is no doubt in that matter.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 24 Verse 126. Indeed, you, O tiger among kings (rājaśārdūla), are superior to Kṛṣṇa, Karna, and especially to Arjuna (Phālguna). There is no doubt in that matter.

sanskrit: युद्धे ह्ययं रुद्रकल्पस्त्वं च ब्रह्मसमोऽनघ ।तस्माच्छक्तौ युवां जेतुं मच्छत्रूंस्ताविवासुरान् ॥१२७॥
transliteration: yuddhe hyayaṁ rudrakalpastvaṁ ca brahmasamo'nagha ,tasmācchaktau yuvāṁ jetuṁ macchatrūṁstāvivāsurān.
english: Indeed, in battle, this one (Karna) is like Rudra, and you, O faultless one, are equal to Brahmā. Therefore, you two are capable of conquering my enemies, who are like the Asuras.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 24 Verse 127. Indeed, in battle, this one (Karna) is like Rudra, and you, O faultless one, are equal to Brahmā. Therefore, you two are capable of conquering my enemies, who are like the Asuras.

sanskrit: यथा शल्याद्य कर्णोऽयं श्वेताश्वं कृष्णसारथिम् ।प्रमथ्य हन्यात्कौन्तेयं तथा शीघ्रं विधीयताम् ।त्वयि कर्णश्च राज्यं च वयं चैव प्रतिष्ठिताः ॥१२८॥
transliteration: yathā śalyādya karṇo'yaṁ śvetāśvaṁ kṛṣṇasārathim ,pramathya hanyātkaunteyaṁ tathā śīghraṁ vidhīyatām ,tvayi karṇaśca rājyaṁ ca vayaṁ caiva pratiṣṭhitāḥ.
english: O Shalya, arrange it quickly so that this Karna may crush and kill Arjuna (Kaunteya), who has white horses and Kṛṣṇa as his charioteer. Karna, the kingdom, and indeed we ourselves are dependent on you.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 24 Verse 128. O Shalya, arrange it quickly so that this Karna may crush and kill Arjuna (Kaunteya), who has white horses and Kṛṣṇa as his charioteer. Karna, the kingdom, and indeed we ourselves are dependent on you.

sanskrit: इमं चाप्यपरं भूय इतिहासं निबोध मे ।पितुर्मम सकाशे यं ब्राह्मणः प्राह धर्मवित् ॥१२९॥
transliteration: imaṁ cāpyaparaṁ bhūya itihāsaṁ nibodha me ,piturmama sakāśe yaṁ brāhmaṇaḥ prāha dharmavit.
english: And also learn from me this other narrative, which a Brahmin, a knower of righteousness (dharma), recounted in my father's presence.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 24 Verse 129. And also learn from me this other narrative, which a Brahmin, a knower of righteousness (dharma), recounted in my father's presence.

sanskrit: श्रुत्वा चैतद्वचश्चित्रं हेतुकार्यार्थसंहितम् ।कुरु शल्य विनिश्चित्य मा भूदत्र विचारणा ॥१३०॥
transliteration: śrutvā caitadvacaścitraṁ hetukāryārthasaṁhitam ,kuru śalya viniścitya mā bhūdatra vicāraṇā.
english: O Śalya, having heard this remarkable statement, which clearly explains cause and effect, decide firmly and act immediately; let there be no further deliberation on this matter.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 24 Verse 130. O Śalya, having heard this remarkable statement, which clearly explains cause and effect, decide firmly and act immediately; let there be no further deliberation on this matter.

sanskrit: भार्गवाणां कुले जातो जमदग्निर्महातपाः ।तस्य रामेति विख्यातः पुत्रस्तेजोगुणान्वितः ॥१३१॥
transliteration: bhārgavāṇāṁ kule jāto jamadagnirmahātapāḥ ,tasya rāmeti vikhyātaḥ putrastejoguṇānvitaḥ.
english: Jamadagni, a great ascetic (tapas), was born into the family of the Bhārgavas. His son, renowned as Rāma, was endowed with splendid qualities.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 24 Verse 131. Jamadagni, a great ascetic (tapas), was born into the family of the Bhārgavas. His son, renowned as Rāma, was endowed with splendid qualities.

sanskrit: स तीव्रं तप आस्थाय प्रसादयितवान्भवम् ।अस्त्रहेतोः प्रसन्नात्मा नियतः संयतेन्द्रियः ॥१३२॥
transliteration: sa tīvraṁ tapa āsthāya prasādayitavānbhavam ,astrahetoḥ prasannātmā niyataḥ saṁyatendriyaḥ.
english: Having undertaken severe asceticism (tapas), he pleased Bhava (Lord Śiva) for the sake of obtaining divine weapons. He was resolute in his purpose, with a serene mind (ātman) and controlled senses.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 24 Verse 132. Having undertaken severe asceticism (tapas), he pleased Bhava (Lord Śiva) for the sake of obtaining divine weapons. He was resolute in his purpose, with a serene mind (ātman) and controlled senses.

sanskrit: तस्य तुष्टो महादेवो भक्त्या च प्रशमेन च ।हृद्गतं चास्य विज्ञाय दर्शयामास शंकरः ॥१३३॥
transliteration: tasya tuṣṭo mahādevo bhaktyā ca praśamena ca ,hṛdgataṁ cāsya vijñāya darśayāmāsa śaṁkaraḥ.
english: Mahadeva Shankara, pleased with his devotion (bhakti) and self-control, and knowing his heart's desire, manifested himself to him.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 24 Verse 133. Mahadeva Shankara, pleased with his devotion (bhakti) and self-control, and knowing his heart's desire, manifested himself to him.

sanskrit: ईश्वर उवाच ।राम तुष्टोऽस्मि भद्रं ते विदितं मे तवेप्सितम् ।कुरुष्व पूतमात्मानं सर्वमेतदवाप्स्यसि ॥१३४॥
transliteration: īśvara uvāca ,rāma tuṣṭo'smi bhadraṁ te viditaṁ me tavepsitam ,kuruṣva pūtamātmānaṁ sarvametadavāpsyasi.
english: The Lord said: "O Rama, I am pleased. May good fortune be with you. Your desire is known to me. Purify your self (ātman), and you shall obtain all this."
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 24 Verse 134. The Lord said: "O Rama, I am pleased. May good fortune be with you. Your desire is known to me. Purify your self (ātman), and you shall obtain all this."

sanskrit: दास्यामि ते तदास्त्राणि यदा पूतो भविष्यसि ।अपात्रमसमर्थं च दहन्त्यस्त्राणि भार्गव ॥१३५॥
transliteration: dāsyāmi te tadāstrāṇi yadā pūto bhaviṣyasi ,apātramasamarthaṁ ca dahantyastrāṇi bhārgava.
english: I will give you the divine weapons then, when you become purified. For these weapons burn those who are unworthy and incapable, O Bhargava.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 24 Verse 135. I will give you the divine weapons then, when you become purified. For these weapons burn those who are unworthy and incapable, O Bhargava.

sanskrit: इत्युक्तो जामदग्न्यस्तु देवदेवेन शूलिना ।प्रत्युवाच महात्मानं शिरसावनतः प्रभुम् ॥१३६॥
transliteration: ityukto jāmadagnyastu devadevena śūlinā ,pratyuvāca mahātmānaṁ śirasāvanataḥ prabhum.
english: Thus addressed by the God of gods, the trident-wielder, Parashurama, humbly bowing his head, replied to the great-souled Lord.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 24 Verse 136. Thus addressed by the God of gods, the trident-wielder, Parashurama, humbly bowing his head, replied to the great-souled Lord.

sanskrit: यदा जानासि देवेश पात्रं मामस्त्रधारणे ।तदा शुश्रूषतेऽस्त्राणि भवान्मे दातुमर्हति ॥१३७॥
transliteration: yadā jānāsi deveśa pātraṁ māmastradhāraṇe ,tadā śuśrūṣate'strāṇi bhavānme dātumarhati.
english: When you, O lord of gods, recognize me as worthy of bearing arms, then you should kindly grant me those weapons, attending to my request.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 24 Verse 137. When you, O lord of gods, recognize me as worthy of bearing arms, then you should kindly grant me those weapons, attending to my request.

sanskrit: दुर्योधन उवाच ।ततः स तपसा चैव दमेन नियमेन च ।पूजोपहारबलिभिर्होममन्त्रपुरस्कृतैः ॥१३८॥
transliteration: duryodhana uvāca ,tataḥ sa tapasā caiva damena niyamena ca ,pūjopahārabalibhirhomamantrapuraskṛtaiḥ.
english: Duryodhana said: Thereafter, he (Parashurama), through asceticism (tapas) and indeed by self-restraint (dama) and discipline (niyama), and with offerings, gifts, and sacrifices accompanied by fire rituals (homa) and mantras...
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 24 Verse 138. Duryodhana said: Thereafter, he (Parashurama), through asceticism (tapas) and indeed by self-restraint (dama) and discipline (niyama), and with offerings, gifts, and sacrifices accompanied by fire rituals (homa) and mantras...

sanskrit: आराधयितवाञ्शर्वं बहून्वर्षगणांस्तदा ।प्रसन्नश्च महादेवो भार्गवस्य महात्मनः ॥१३९॥
transliteration: ārādhayitavāñśarvaṁ bahūnvarṣagaṇāṁstadā ,prasannaśca mahādevo bhārgavasya mahātmanaḥ.
english: He (Parashurama) worshipped Śarva for many groups of years at that time. And the great god (Mahādeva) became pleased with the great-souled Bhārgava (Parashurama).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 24 Verse 139. He (Parashurama) worshipped Śarva for many groups of years at that time. And the great god (Mahādeva) became pleased with the great-souled Bhārgava (Parashurama).

sanskrit: अब्रवीत्तस्य बहुशो गुणान्देव्याः समीपतः ।भक्तिमानेष सततं मयि रामो दृढव्रतः ॥१४०॥
transliteration: abravīttasya bahuśo guṇāndevyāḥ samīpataḥ ,bhaktimāneṣa satataṁ mayi rāmo dṛḍhavrataḥ.
english: He (Mahadeva) repeatedly spoke to him (Bhārgava, Parashurama) about the qualities of the goddess, in her presence: 'This Rāma is always devout (bhakti-mān) towards me and firm in his vows.'
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 24 Verse 140. He (Mahadeva) repeatedly spoke to him (Bhārgava, Parashurama) about the qualities of the goddess, in her presence: 'This Rāma is always devout (bhakti-mān) towards me and firm in his vows.'

sanskrit: एवं तस्य गुणान्प्रीतो बहुशोऽकथयत्प्रभुः ।देवतानां पितॄणां च समक्षमरिसूदनः ॥१४१॥
transliteration: evaṁ tasya guṇānprīto bahuśo'kathayatprabhuḥ ,devatānāṁ pitṝṇāṁ ca samakṣamarisūdanaḥ.
english: The lord, delighted and the slayer of enemies, thus repeatedly described his virtues in the presence of both the gods and the ancestors.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 24 Verse 141. The lord, delighted and the slayer of enemies, thus repeatedly described his virtues in the presence of both the gods and the ancestors.

sanskrit: एतस्मिन्नेव काले तु दैत्या आसन्महाबलाः ।तैस्तदा दर्पमोहान्धैरबाध्यन्त दिवौकसः ॥१४२॥
transliteration: etasminneva kāle tu daityā āsanmahābalāḥ ,taistadā darpamohāndhairabādhyanta divaukasaḥ.
english: At that very time, there were extremely powerful demons. Blinded by arrogance and delusion, they then tormented the inhabitants of heaven (divaukasaḥ).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 24 Verse 142. At that very time, there were extremely powerful demons. Blinded by arrogance and delusion, they then tormented the inhabitants of heaven (divaukasaḥ).

sanskrit: ततः संभूय विबुधास्तान्हन्तुं कृतनिश्चयाः ।चक्रुः शत्रुवधे यत्नं न शेकुर्जेतुमेव ते ॥१४३॥
transliteration: tataḥ saṁbhūya vibudhāstānhantuṁ kṛtaniścayāḥ ,cakruḥ śatruvadhe yatnaṁ na śekurjetumeva te.
english: Thereupon, the gods, having assembled and firmly resolved to kill those demons, made an effort to slay their enemies. Yet, they were truly unable to conquer them.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 24 Verse 143. Thereupon, the gods, having assembled and firmly resolved to kill those demons, made an effort to slay their enemies. Yet, they were truly unable to conquer them.

sanskrit: अभिगम्य ततो देवा महेश्वरमथाब्रुवन् ।प्रसादयन्तस्तं भक्त्या जहि शत्रुगणानिति ॥१४४॥
transliteration: abhigamya tato devā maheśvaramathābruvan ,prasādayantastaṁ bhaktyā jahi śatrugaṇāniti.
english: Having approached Maheśvara, the gods then spoke, propitiating him with devotion (bhakti), saying, "Destroy these multitudes of enemies!"
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 24 Verse 144. Having approached Maheśvara, the gods then spoke, propitiating him with devotion (bhakti), saying, "Destroy these multitudes of enemies!"

sanskrit: प्रतिज्ञाय ततो देवो देवतानां रिपुक्षयम् ।रामं भार्गवमाहूय सोऽभ्यभाषत शंकरः ॥१४५॥
transliteration: pratijñāya tato devo devatānāṁ ripukṣayam ,rāmaṁ bhārgavamāhūya so'bhyabhāṣata śaṁkaraḥ.
english: Then, having pledged the destruction of the enemies of the gods, the deity Śaṅkara, after summoning Rāma Bhārgava, addressed him.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 24 Verse 145. Then, having pledged the destruction of the enemies of the gods, the deity Śaṅkara, after summoning Rāma Bhārgava, addressed him.

sanskrit: रिपून्भार्गव देवानां जहि सर्वान्समागतान् ।लोकानां हितकामार्थं मत्प्रीत्यर्थं तथैव च ॥१४६॥
transliteration: ripūnbhārgava devānāṁ jahi sarvānsamāgatān ,lokānāṁ hitakāmārthaṁ matprītyarthaṁ tathaiva ca.
english: O Bhārgava, destroy all the assembled enemies of the gods for the sake of the welfare of the worlds, and likewise for my satisfaction.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 24 Verse 146. O Bhārgava, destroy all the assembled enemies of the gods for the sake of the welfare of the worlds, and likewise for my satisfaction.

sanskrit: राम उवाच ।अकृतास्त्रस्य देवेश का शक्तिर्मे महेश्वर ।निहन्तुं दानवान्सर्वान्कृतास्त्रान्युद्धदुर्मदान् ॥१४७॥
transliteration: rāma uvāca ,akṛtāstrasya deveśa kā śaktirme maheśvara ,nihantuṁ dānavānsarvānkṛtāstrānyuddhadurmadān.
english: Rāma said: "O Lord of the gods, O Great Lord, what (śakti) power do I possess, being unskilled in weapons, to kill all the dānavas who are skilled in weapons and maddened by battle?"
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 24 Verse 147. Rāma said: "O Lord of the gods, O Great Lord, what (śakti) power do I possess, being unskilled in weapons, to kill all the dānavas who are skilled in weapons and maddened by battle?"

sanskrit: ईश्वर उवाच ।गच्छ त्वं मदनुध्यानान्निहनिष्यसि दानवान् ।विजित्य च रिपून्सर्वान्गुणान्प्राप्स्यसि पुष्कलान् ॥१४८॥
transliteration: īśvara uvāca ,gaccha tvaṁ madanudhyānānnihaniṣyasi dānavān ,vijitya ca ripūnsarvānguṇānprāpsyasi puṣkalān.
english: Īśvara said: "Go forth! Through my contemplation (dhyāna), you will surely slay the dānavas. And having conquered all enemies, you will acquire abundant excellent qualities."
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 24 Verse 148. Īśvara said: "Go forth! Through my contemplation (dhyāna), you will surely slay the dānavas. And having conquered all enemies, you will acquire abundant excellent qualities."

sanskrit: दुर्योधन उवाच ।एतच्छ्रुत्वा च वचनं प्रतिगृह्य च सर्वशः ।रामः कृतस्वस्त्ययनः प्रययौ दानवान्प्रति ॥१४९॥
transliteration: duryodhana uvāca ,etacchrutvā ca vacanaṁ pratigṛhya ca sarvaśaḥ ,rāmaḥ kṛtasvastyayanaḥ prayayau dānavānprati.
english: Duryodhana said: Having heard this statement and accepted it completely, Rama, after having auspicious rites performed, set out towards the Dānavas.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 24 Verse 149. Duryodhana said: Having heard this statement and accepted it completely, Rama, after having auspicious rites performed, set out towards the Dānavas.

sanskrit: अवधीद्देवशत्रूंस्तान्मददर्पबलान्वितान् ।वज्राशनिसमस्पर्शैः प्रहारैरेव भार्गवः ॥१५०॥
transliteration: avadhīddevaśatrūṁstānmadadarpabalānvitān ,vajrāśanisamasparśaiḥ prahāraireva bhārgavaḥ.
english: Bhārgava (Paraśurāma) killed those enemies of the gods, who were filled with pride, arrogance, and power, with blows whose impact was like that of Indra's thunderbolt.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 24 Verse 150. Bhārgava (Paraśurāma) killed those enemies of the gods, who were filled with pride, arrogance, and power, with blows whose impact was like that of Indra's thunderbolt.

sanskrit: स दानवैः क्षततनुर्जामदग्न्यो द्विजोत्तमः ।संस्पृष्टः स्थाणुना सद्यो निर्व्रणः समजायत ॥१५१॥
transliteration: sa dānavaiḥ kṣatatanurjāmadagnyo dvijottamaḥ ,saṁspṛṣṭaḥ sthāṇunā sadyo nirvraṇaḥ samajāyata.
english: That Jāmadagnya (Paraśurāma), the foremost among the twice-born (dvija), whose body had been wounded by the Dānavas, immediately became free of wounds after being touched by Sthāṇu (Śiva).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 24 Verse 151. That Jāmadagnya (Paraśurāma), the foremost among the twice-born (dvija), whose body had been wounded by the Dānavas, immediately became free of wounds after being touched by Sthāṇu (Śiva).

sanskrit: प्रीतश्च भगवान्देवः कर्मणा तेन तस्य वै ।वरान्प्रादाद्ब्रह्मविदे भार्गवाय महात्मने ॥१५२॥
transliteration: prītaśca bhagavāndevaḥ karmaṇā tena tasya vai ,varānprādādbrahmavide bhārgavāya mahātmane.
english: And the divine lord (deva), pleased by that deed (karma) of his (Paraśurāma), indeed gave boons to Bhārgava, the great-souled knower of Brahman (brahman).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 24 Verse 152. And the divine lord (deva), pleased by that deed (karma) of his (Paraśurāma), indeed gave boons to Bhārgava, the great-souled knower of Brahman (brahman).

sanskrit: उक्तश्च देवदेवेन प्रीतियुक्तेन शूलिना ।निपातात्तव शस्त्राणां शरीरे याभवद्रुजा ॥१५३॥
transliteration: uktaśca devadevena prītiyuktena śūlinā ,nipātāttava śastrāṇāṁ śarīre yābhavadrujā.
english: And it was said by the God of gods, Shiva (śūlinā), who was filled with affection: 'The pain which occurred in your body from the impact of your weapons...'
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 24 Verse 153. And it was said by the God of gods, Shiva (śūlinā), who was filled with affection: 'The pain which occurred in your body from the impact of your weapons...'

sanskrit: तया ते मानुषं कर्म व्यपोढं भृगुनन्दन ।गृहाणास्त्राणि दिव्यानि मत्सकाशाद्यथेप्सितम् ॥१५४॥
transliteration: tayā te mānuṣaṁ karma vyapoḍhaṁ bhṛgunandana ,gṛhāṇāstrāṇi divyāni matsakāśādyathepsitam.
english: ...by that pain, O son of Bhrigu (bhṛgunandana), your human actions (karma) have been dispelled. Now, take these divine weapons from me as you wish.'
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 24 Verse 154. ...by that pain, O son of Bhrigu (bhṛgunandana), your human actions (karma) have been dispelled. Now, take these divine weapons from me as you wish.'

sanskrit: ततोऽस्त्राणि समस्तानि वरांश्च मनसेप्सितान् ।लब्ध्वा बहुविधान्रामः प्रणम्य शिरसा शिवम् ॥१५५॥
transliteration: tato'strāṇi samastāni varāṁśca manasepsitān ,labdhvā bahuvidhānrāmaḥ praṇamya śirasā śivam.
english: Thereupon, having obtained all the weapons and many kinds of boons that he desired in his mind, Rama (rāmaḥ) bowed his head to Shiva (śivam).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 24 Verse 155. Thereupon, having obtained all the weapons and many kinds of boons that he desired in his mind, Rama (rāmaḥ) bowed his head to Shiva (śivam).

sanskrit: अनुज्ञां प्राप्य देवेशाज्जगाम स महातपाः ।एवमेतत्पुरावृत्तं तदा कथितवानृषिः ॥१५६॥
transliteration: anujñāṁ prāpya deveśājjagāma sa mahātapāḥ ,evametatpurāvṛttaṁ tadā kathitavānṛṣiḥ.
english: Having obtained permission from the Lord of gods (deveśāt), that great ascetic (mahātapāḥ) departed. Thus, the sage (ṛṣiḥ) narrated this ancient event (purāvṛttam) at that time.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 24 Verse 156. Having obtained permission from the Lord of gods (deveśāt), that great ascetic (mahātapāḥ) departed. Thus, the sage (ṛṣiḥ) narrated this ancient event (purāvṛttam) at that time.

sanskrit: भार्गवोऽप्यददात्सर्वं धनुर्वेदं महात्मने ।कर्णाय पुरुषव्याघ्र सुप्रीतेनान्तरात्मना ॥१५७॥
transliteration: bhārgavo'pyadadātsarvaṁ dhanurvedaṁ mahātmane ,karṇāya puruṣavyāghra suprītenāntarātmanā.
english: O tiger among men (puruṣavyāghra), Bhargava (Paraśurāma), with his inner self (antarātman) greatly pleased, bestowed the entire science of archery upon the great-souled (mahātman) Karna.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 24 Verse 157. O tiger among men (puruṣavyāghra), Bhargava (Paraśurāma), with his inner self (antarātman) greatly pleased, bestowed the entire science of archery upon the great-souled (mahātman) Karna.

sanskrit: वृजिनं हि भवेत्किंचिद्यदि कर्णस्य पार्थिव ।नास्मै ह्यस्त्राणि दिव्यानि प्रादास्यद्भृगुनन्दनः ॥१५८॥
transliteration: vṛjinaṁ hi bhavetkiṁcidyadi karṇasya pārthiva ,nāsmai hyastrāṇi divyāni prādāsyadbhṛgunandanaḥ.
english: O King, if there had indeed been any fault in Karna, surely the son of Bhṛgu (Paraśurāma) would not have bestowed divine weapons upon him.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 24 Verse 158. O King, if there had indeed been any fault in Karna, surely the son of Bhṛgu (Paraśurāma) would not have bestowed divine weapons upon him.

sanskrit: नापि सूतकुले जातं कर्णं मन्ये कथंचन ।देवपुत्रमहं मन्ये क्षत्रियाणां कुलोद्भवम् ॥१५९॥
transliteration: nāpi sūtakule jātaṁ karṇaṁ manye kathaṁcana ,devaputramahaṁ manye kṣatriyāṇāṁ kulodbhavam.
english: I do not consider Karna to have been born into the Sūta (charioteer) family by any means. I consider him a son of a god (devaputra), born of a Kṣatriya family.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 24 Verse 159. I do not consider Karna to have been born into the Sūta (charioteer) family by any means. I consider him a son of a god (devaputra), born of a Kṣatriya family.

sanskrit: सकुण्डलं सकवचं दीर्घबाहुं महारथम् ।कथमादित्यसदृशं मृगी व्याघ्रं जनिष्यति ॥१६०॥
transliteration: sakuṇḍalaṁ sakavacaṁ dīrghabāhuṁ mahāratham ,kathamādityasadṛśaṁ mṛgī vyāghraṁ janiṣyati.
english: How could a doe give birth to a tiger - one who possesses earrings, armor, long arms, is a great charioteer, and resembles the sun?
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 24 Verse 160. How could a doe give birth to a tiger - one who possesses earrings, armor, long arms, is a great charioteer, and resembles the sun?

sanskrit: पश्य ह्यस्य भुजौ पीनौ नागराजकरोपमौ ।वक्षः पश्य विशालं च सर्वशत्रुनिबर्हणम् ॥१६१॥
transliteration: paśya hyasya bhujau pīnau nāgarājakaropamau ,vakṣaḥ paśya viśālaṁ ca sarvaśatrunibarhaṇam.
english: Just look at his two massive arms, resembling the trunks of great elephants! And behold his broad chest, which annihilates all enemies.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 24 Verse 161. Just look at his two massive arms, resembling the trunks of great elephants! And behold his broad chest, which annihilates all enemies.

sanskrit: दुर्योधन उवाच ।एवं स भगवान्देवः सर्वलोकपितामहः ।सारथ्यमकरोत्तत्र यत्र रुद्रोऽभवद्रथी ॥१॥
transliteration: duryodhana uvāca ,evaṁ sa bhagavāndevaḥ sarvalokapitāmahaḥ ,sārathyamakarottatra yatra rudro'bhavadrathī.
english: Duryodhana said: "Thus, that divine, illustrious god, the grandfather of all worlds (Brahma), performed charioteering there where Rudra (Shiva) himself was the warrior in the chariot."
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 25 Verse 1. Duryodhana said: "Thus, that divine, illustrious god, the grandfather of all worlds (Brahma), performed charioteering there where Rudra (Shiva) himself was the warrior in the chariot."

sanskrit: रथिनाभ्यधिको वीरः कर्तव्यो रथसारथिः ।तस्मात्त्वं पुरुषव्याघ्र नियच्छ तुरगान्युधि ॥२॥
transliteration: rathinābhyadhiko vīraḥ kartavyo rathasārathiḥ ,tasmāttvaṁ puruṣavyāghra niyaccha turagānyudhi.
english: A hero who is superior to (even other) charioteers should be appointed as the charioteer. Therefore, O tiger among men (puruṣavyāghra), you must control the horses in battle!
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 25 Verse 2. A hero who is superior to (even other) charioteers should be appointed as the charioteer. Therefore, O tiger among men (puruṣavyāghra), you must control the horses in battle!

sanskrit: संजय उवाच ।ततः शल्यः परिष्वज्य सुतं ते वाक्यमब्रवीत् ।दुर्योधनममित्रघ्नः प्रीतो मद्राधिपस्तदा ॥३॥
transliteration: saṁjaya uvāca ,tataḥ śalyaḥ pariṣvajya sutaṁ te vākyamabravīt ,duryodhanamamitraghnaḥ prīto madrādhipastadā.
english: Sanjaya said: "Then Shalya, the pleased king of Madras and slayer of enemies, having embraced your son Duryodhana, spoke these words."
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 25 Verse 3. Sanjaya said: "Then Shalya, the pleased king of Madras and slayer of enemies, having embraced your son Duryodhana, spoke these words."

sanskrit: एवं चेन्मन्यसे राजन्गान्धारे प्रियदर्शन ।तस्मात्ते यत्प्रियं किंचित्तत्सर्वं करवाण्यहम् ॥४॥
transliteration: evaṁ cenmanyase rājangāndhāre priyadarśana ,tasmātte yatpriyaṁ kiṁcittatsarvaṁ karavāṇyaham.
english: If you think this way, O King, beloved son of Gandhari, then whatever little is dear to you, all that I shall certainly do.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 25 Verse 4. If you think this way, O King, beloved son of Gandhari, then whatever little is dear to you, all that I shall certainly do.

sanskrit: यत्रास्मि भरतश्रेष्ठ योग्यः कर्मणि कर्हिचित् ।तत्र सर्वात्मना युक्तो वक्ष्ये कार्यधुरं तव ॥५॥
transliteration: yatrāsmi bharataśreṣṭha yogyaḥ karmaṇi karhicit ,tatra sarvātmanā yukto vakṣye kāryadhuraṁ tava.
english: O best among the Bharatas, wherever I am capable in any task, there I shall, with all my being (ātman), diligently bear your burden of duty.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 25 Verse 5. O best among the Bharatas, wherever I am capable in any task, there I shall, with all my being (ātman), diligently bear your burden of duty.

sanskrit: यत्तु कर्णमहं ब्रूयां हितकामः प्रियाप्रियम् ।मम तत्क्षमतां सर्वं भवान्कर्णश्च सर्वशः ॥६॥
transliteration: yattu karṇamahaṁ brūyāṁ hitakāmaḥ priyāpriyam ,mama tatkṣamatāṁ sarvaṁ bhavānkarṇaśca sarvaśaḥ.
english: But whatever I, desiring your welfare, might say to Karna, whether it be pleasant or unpleasant, may you and Karna completely forgive all that (speech) of mine.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 25 Verse 6. But whatever I, desiring your welfare, might say to Karna, whether it be pleasant or unpleasant, may you and Karna completely forgive all that (speech) of mine.

sanskrit: कर्ण उवाच ।ईशानस्य यथा ब्रह्मा यथा पार्थस्य केशवः ।तथा नित्यं हिते युक्तो मद्रराज भजस्व नः ॥७॥
transliteration: karṇa uvāca ,īśānasya yathā brahmā yathā pārthasya keśavaḥ ,tathā nityaṁ hite yukto madrarāja bhajasva naḥ.
english: Karna said: 'Just as Brahma serves Īśāna, and just as Keśava (Krishna) serves Pārtha (Arjuna), so too, O King of Madras, you should always be engaged in our welfare and serve us.'
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 25 Verse 7. Karna said: 'Just as Brahma serves Īśāna, and just as Keśava (Krishna) serves Pārtha (Arjuna), so too, O King of Madras, you should always be engaged in our welfare and serve us.'

sanskrit: शल्य उवाच ।आत्मनिन्दात्मपूजा च परनिन्दा परस्तवः ।अनाचरितमार्याणां वृत्तमेतच्चतुर्विधम् ॥८॥
transliteration: śalya uvāca ,ātmanindātmapūjā ca paranindā parastavaḥ ,anācaritamāryāṇāṁ vṛttametaccaturvidham.
english: Śalya said: Self-criticism and self-praise, as well as the criticism of others and flattery of others - this four-fold behavior (vṛttam) is not practiced by noble people.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 25 Verse 8. Śalya said: Self-criticism and self-praise, as well as the criticism of others and flattery of others - this four-fold behavior (vṛttam) is not practiced by noble people.

sanskrit: यत्तु विद्वन्प्रवक्ष्यामि प्रत्ययार्थमहं तव ।आत्मनः स्तवसंयुक्तं तन्निबोध यथातथम् ॥९॥
transliteration: yattu vidvanpravakṣyāmi pratyayārthamahaṁ tava ,ātmanaḥ stavasaṁyuktaṁ tannibodha yathātatham.
english: But O learned one, I will declare to you, for the sake of your conviction, that which consists of self-praise; understand that truly, as it actually is.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 25 Verse 9. But O learned one, I will declare to you, for the sake of your conviction, that which consists of self-praise; understand that truly, as it actually is.

sanskrit: अहं शक्रस्य सारथ्ये योग्यो मातलिवत्प्रभो ।अप्रमादप्रयोगाच्च ज्ञानविद्याचिकित्सितैः ॥१०॥
transliteration: ahaṁ śakrasya sārathye yogyo mātalivatprabho ,apramādaprayogācca jñānavidyācikitsitaiḥ.
english: O Lord, I am capable of charioteering for Indra, just like Mātali, due to my diligent application and my knowledge, skill, and (use of) remedies.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 25 Verse 10. O Lord, I am capable of charioteering for Indra, just like Mātali, due to my diligent application and my knowledge, skill, and (use of) remedies.

sanskrit: ततः पार्थेन संग्रामे युध्यमानस्य तेऽनघ ।वाहयिष्यामि तुरगान्विज्वरो भव सूतज ॥११॥
transliteration: tataḥ pārthena saṁgrāme yudhyamānasya te'nagha ,vāhayiṣyāmi turagānvijvaro bhava sūtaja.
english: Therefore, O sinless one, I will drive your horses while you are fighting in battle with Arjuna. Be free from anxiety, O son of a charioteer (sūtaja).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 25 Verse 11. Therefore, O sinless one, I will drive your horses while you are fighting in battle with Arjuna. Be free from anxiety, O son of a charioteer (sūtaja).

sanskrit: दुर्योधन उवाच ।अयं ते कर्ण सारथ्यं मद्रराजः करिष्यति ।कृष्णादभ्यधिको यन्ता देवेन्द्रस्येव मातलिः ॥१॥
transliteration: duryodhana uvāca ,ayaṁ te karṇa sārathyaṁ madrarājaḥ kariṣyati ,kṛṣṇādabhyadhiko yantā devendrasyeva mātaliḥ.
english: Duryodhana said: "O Karna, this King of Madra will perform the charioteering for you. He is a charioteer superior to Krishna, just as Matali is for the king of the gods (Indra)."
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 26 Verse 1. Duryodhana said: "O Karna, this King of Madra will perform the charioteering for you. He is a charioteer superior to Krishna, just as Matali is for the king of the gods (Indra)."

sanskrit: यथा हरिहयैर्युक्तं संगृह्णाति स मातलिः ।शल्यस्तव तथाद्यायं संयन्ता रथवाजिनाम् ॥२॥
transliteration: yathā harihayairyuktaṁ saṁgṛhṇāti sa mātaliḥ ,śalyastava tathādyāyaṁ saṁyantā rathavājinām.
english: Just as that Matali controls the horses yoked to Indra's chariot, so too this Shalya will be the charioteer of your chariot-horses today.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 26 Verse 2. Just as that Matali controls the horses yoked to Indra's chariot, so too this Shalya will be the charioteer of your chariot-horses today.

sanskrit: योधे त्वयि रथस्थे च मद्रराजे च सारथौ ।रथश्रेष्ठो ध्रुवं संख्ये पार्थो नाभिभविष्यति ॥३॥
transliteration: yodhe tvayi rathasthe ca madrarāje ca sārathau ,rathaśreṣṭho dhruvaṁ saṁkhye pārtho nābhibhaviṣyati.
english: With you as the warrior mounted on the chariot, and the King of Madra as the charioteer, Arjuna, the foremost of chariot-warriors, will certainly not be able to defeat (you) in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 26 Verse 3. With you as the warrior mounted on the chariot, and the King of Madra as the charioteer, Arjuna, the foremost of chariot-warriors, will certainly not be able to defeat (you) in battle.

sanskrit: संजय उवाच ।ततो दुर्योधनो भूयो मद्रराजं तरस्विनम् ।उवाच राजन्संग्रामे संयच्छन्तं हयोत्तमान् ॥४॥
transliteration: saṁjaya uvāca ,tato duryodhano bhūyo madrarājaṁ tarasvinam ,uvāca rājansaṁgrāme saṁyacchantaṁ hayottamān.
english: Sanjaya said: "Then Duryodhana, O King (Dhritarashtra), spoke again to the powerful King of Madra, who was controlling the best horses in battle."
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 26 Verse 4. Sanjaya said: "Then Duryodhana, O King (Dhritarashtra), spoke again to the powerful King of Madra, who was controlling the best horses in battle."

sanskrit: त्वयाभिगुप्तो राधेयो विजेष्यति धनंजयम् ।इत्युक्तो रथमास्थाय तथेति प्राह भारत ॥५॥
transliteration: tvayābhigupto rādheyo vijeṣyati dhanaṁjayam ,ityukto rathamāsthāya tatheti prāha bhārata.
english: “Radheya (Karna), protected by you, will conquer Dhanañjaya (Arjuna).” Having been told this, O descendant of Bharata, (Shalya) mounted the chariot and said, “So be it.”
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 26 Verse 5. “Radheya (Karna), protected by you, will conquer Dhanañjaya (Arjuna).” Having been told this, O descendant of Bharata, (Shalya) mounted the chariot and said, “So be it.”

sanskrit: शल्येऽभ्युपगते कर्णः सारथिं सुमनोऽब्रवीत् ।स्वं सूत स्यन्दनं मह्यं कल्पयेत्यसकृत्त्वरन् ॥६॥
transliteration: śalye'bhyupagate karṇaḥ sārathiṁ sumano'bravīt ,svaṁ sūta syandanaṁ mahyaṁ kalpayetyasakṛttvaran.
english: When Shalya had assented, Karna, with a cheerful mind, repeatedly urged the charioteer, saying, “O charioteer, prepare my chariot for me!”
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 26 Verse 6. When Shalya had assented, Karna, with a cheerful mind, repeatedly urged the charioteer, saying, “O charioteer, prepare my chariot for me!”

sanskrit: ततो जैत्रं रथवरं गन्धर्वनगरोपमम् ।विधिवत्कल्पितं भर्त्रे जयेत्युक्त्वा न्यवेदयत् ॥७॥
transliteration: tato jaitraṁ rathavaraṁ gandharvanagaropamam ,vidhivatkalpitaṁ bhartre jayetyuktvā nyavedayat.
english: Then, having declared “Victory!”, (Shalya) presented the excellent, victorious chariot, which resembled a city of the Gandharvas and had been ritually prepared for his master (Karna).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 26 Verse 7. Then, having declared “Victory!”, (Shalya) presented the excellent, victorious chariot, which resembled a city of the Gandharvas and had been ritually prepared for his master (Karna).

sanskrit: तं रथं रथिनां श्रेष्ठः कर्णोऽभ्यर्च्य यथाविधि ।संपादितं ब्रह्मविदा पूर्वमेव पुरोधसा ॥८॥
transliteration: taṁ rathaṁ rathināṁ śreṣṭhaḥ karṇo'bhyarcya yathāvidhi ,saṁpāditaṁ brahmavidā pūrvameva purodhasā.
english: The best of charioteers, Karna, having worshipped that chariot according to ritual – that chariot having been prepared beforehand by the chief priest, a knower of brahman (brahman) – ...
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 26 Verse 8. The best of charioteers, Karna, having worshipped that chariot according to ritual – that chariot having been prepared beforehand by the chief priest, a knower of brahman (brahman) – ...

sanskrit: कृत्वा प्रदक्षिणं यत्नादुपस्थाय च भास्करम् ।समीपस्थं मद्रराजं समारोपयदग्रतः ॥९॥
transliteration: kṛtvā pradakṣiṇaṁ yatnādupasthāya ca bhāskaram ,samīpasthaṁ madrarājaṁ samāropayadagrataḥ.
english: Having carefully performed a circumambulation and reverently approached the Sun god (bhāskara), he then seated the king of Madra (madrarāja), who was standing nearby, in the front (of the chariot).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 26 Verse 9. Having carefully performed a circumambulation and reverently approached the Sun god (bhāskara), he then seated the king of Madra (madrarāja), who was standing nearby, in the front (of the chariot).

sanskrit: ततः कर्णस्य दुर्धर्षं स्यन्दनप्रवरं महत् ।आरुरोह महातेजाः शल्यः सिंह इवाचलम् ॥१०॥
transliteration: tataḥ karṇasya durdharṣaṁ syandanapravaraṁ mahat ,āruroha mahātejāḥ śalyaḥ siṁha ivācalam.
english: Then, the highly glorious (mahātejāḥ) Śalya ascended Karṇa's great, invincible (durdharṣam), and excellent chariot, just as a lion ascends a mountain.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 26 Verse 10. Then, the highly glorious (mahātejāḥ) Śalya ascended Karṇa's great, invincible (durdharṣam), and excellent chariot, just as a lion ascends a mountain.

sanskrit: ततः शल्यास्थितं राजन्कर्णः स्वरथमुत्तमम् ।अध्यतिष्ठद्यथाम्भोदं विद्युत्वन्तं दिवाकरः ॥११॥
transliteration: tataḥ śalyāsthitaṁ rājankarṇaḥ svarathamuttamam ,adhyatiṣṭhadyathāmbhodaṁ vidyutvantaṁ divākaraḥ.
english: Then, O king (rājan), Karṇa mounted his own excellent chariot, with Śalya already seated in it, just as the sun (divākaraḥ) ascends a lightning-bearing cloud.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 26 Verse 11. Then, O king (rājan), Karṇa mounted his own excellent chariot, with Śalya already seated in it, just as the sun (divākaraḥ) ascends a lightning-bearing cloud.

sanskrit: तावेकरथमारूढावादित्याग्निसमत्विषौ ।व्यभ्राजेतां यथा मेघं सूर्याग्नी सहितौ दिवि ॥१२॥
transliteration: tāvekarathamārūḍhāvādityāgnisamatviṣau ,vyabhrājetāṁ yathā meghaṁ sūryāgnī sahitau divi.
english: Those two, having ascended a single chariot, and whose splendor was equal to that of the sun and fire, blazed forth in the sky, just as the sun and fire together shine brightly against a cloud.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 26 Verse 12. Those two, having ascended a single chariot, and whose splendor was equal to that of the sun and fire, blazed forth in the sky, just as the sun and fire together shine brightly against a cloud.

sanskrit: संस्तूयमानौ तौ वीरौ तदास्तां द्युतिमत्तरौ ।ऋत्विक्सदस्यैरिन्द्राग्नी हूयमानाविवाध्वरे ॥१३॥
transliteration: saṁstūyamānau tau vīrau tadāstāṁ dyutimattarau ,ṛtviksadasyairindrāgnī hūyamānāvivādhvare.
english: Then, those two highly praised heroes appeared most radiant, just like Indra and Agni being invoked by the officiating priests and assembly members during a ritual sacrifice.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 26 Verse 13. Then, those two highly praised heroes appeared most radiant, just like Indra and Agni being invoked by the officiating priests and assembly members during a ritual sacrifice.

sanskrit: स शल्यसंगृहीताश्वे रथे कर्णः स्थितोऽभवत् ।धनुर्विस्फारयन्घोरं परिवेषीव भास्करः ॥१४॥
transliteration: sa śalyasaṁgṛhītāśve rathe karṇaḥ sthito'bhavat ,dhanurvisphārayanghoraṁ pariveṣīva bhāskaraḥ.
english: Karṇa stood on his chariot, whose horses were guided by Śalya, stretching his formidable bow. He appeared like the sun (bhāskara) encircled by a halo.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 26 Verse 14. Karṇa stood on his chariot, whose horses were guided by Śalya, stretching his formidable bow. He appeared like the sun (bhāskara) encircled by a halo.

sanskrit: आस्थितः स रथश्रेष्ठं कर्णः शरगभस्तिमान् ।प्रबभौ पुरुषव्याघ्रो मन्दरस्थ इवांशुमान् ॥१५॥
transliteration: āsthitaḥ sa rathaśreṣṭhaṁ karṇaḥ śaragabhastimān ,prababhau puruṣavyāghro mandarastha ivāṁśumān.
english: Seated upon his excellent chariot, Karṇa, that tiger among men (puruṣavyāghra) and wielder of arrow-rays, shone brilliantly, like the sun (aṃśumān) positioned atop Mount Mandara.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 26 Verse 15. Seated upon his excellent chariot, Karṇa, that tiger among men (puruṣavyāghra) and wielder of arrow-rays, shone brilliantly, like the sun (aṃśumān) positioned atop Mount Mandara.

sanskrit: तं रथस्थं महावीरं यान्तं चामिततेजसम् ।दुर्योधनः स्म राधेयमिदं वचनमब्रवीत् ॥१६॥
transliteration: taṁ rathasthaṁ mahāvīraṁ yāntaṁ cāmitatejasam ,duryodhanaḥ sma rādheyamidaṁ vacanamabravīt.
english: Duryodhana then spoke these words to him, Karṇa (rādheya), the great hero (mahāvīra) who was situated in his chariot and possessed immeasurable splendor (amitatejas).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 26 Verse 16. Duryodhana then spoke these words to him, Karṇa (rādheya), the great hero (mahāvīra) who was situated in his chariot and possessed immeasurable splendor (amitatejas).

sanskrit: अकृतं द्रोणभीष्माभ्यां दुष्करं कर्म संयुगे ।कुरुष्वाधिरथे वीर मिषतां सर्वधन्विनाम् ॥१७॥
transliteration: akṛtaṁ droṇabhīṣmābhyāṁ duṣkaraṁ karma saṁyuge ,kuruṣvādhirathe vīra miṣatāṁ sarvadhanvinām.
english: O hero, son of Adhiratha (Karna), accomplish in battle that formidable deed (karma) which Drona and Bhishma have not done, while all archers are watching.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 26 Verse 17. O hero, son of Adhiratha (Karna), accomplish in battle that formidable deed (karma) which Drona and Bhishma have not done, while all archers are watching.

sanskrit: मनोगतं मम ह्यासीद्भीष्मद्रोणौ महारथौ ।अर्जुनं भीमसेनं च निहन्ताराविति ध्रुवम् ॥१८॥
transliteration: manogataṁ mama hyāsīdbhīṣmadroṇau mahārathau ,arjunaṁ bhīmasenaṁ ca nihantārāviti dhruvam.
english: Indeed, it was certainly my firm belief (manogatam) that Bhishma and Drona, the great charioteers, would kill Arjuna and Bhimasena.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 26 Verse 18. Indeed, it was certainly my firm belief (manogatam) that Bhishma and Drona, the great charioteers, would kill Arjuna and Bhimasena.

sanskrit: ताभ्यां यदकृतं वीर वीरकर्म महामृधे ।तत्कर्म कुरु राधेय वज्रपाणिरिवापरः ॥१९॥
transliteration: tābhyāṁ yadakṛtaṁ vīra vīrakarma mahāmṛdhe ,tatkarma kuru rādheya vajrapāṇirivāparaḥ.
english: O hero, O son of Radha (Karna), perform that heroic deed in the great battle which was not accomplished by them (Drona and Bhishma), like another Vajrapāṇi (Indra).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 26 Verse 19. O hero, O son of Radha (Karna), perform that heroic deed in the great battle which was not accomplished by them (Drona and Bhishma), like another Vajrapāṇi (Indra).

sanskrit: गृहाण धर्मराजं वा जहि वा त्वं धनंजयम् ।भीमसेनं च राधेय माद्रीपुत्रौ यमावपि ॥२०॥
transliteration: gṛhāṇa dharmarājaṁ vā jahi vā tvaṁ dhanaṁjayam ,bhīmasenaṁ ca rādheya mādrīputrau yamāvapi.
english: O son of Radha (Karna), either capture Dharmaraja (Yudhishthira) or you kill Dhananjaya (Arjuna) and Bhimasena, and also the two sons of Madri (Nakula and Sahadeva), who are like the twin Yamas.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 26 Verse 20. O son of Radha (Karna), either capture Dharmaraja (Yudhishthira) or you kill Dhananjaya (Arjuna) and Bhimasena, and also the two sons of Madri (Nakula and Sahadeva), who are like the twin Yamas.

sanskrit: जयश्च तेऽस्तु भद्रं च प्रयाहि पुरुषर्षभ ।पाण्डुपुत्रस्य सैन्यानि कुरु सर्वाणि भस्मसात् ॥२१॥
transliteration: jayaśca te'stu bhadraṁ ca prayāhi puruṣarṣabha ,pāṇḍuputrasya sainyāni kuru sarvāṇi bhasmasāt.
english: May victory and well-being be yours! Go forth, O best among men (puruṣarṣabha)! Turn all the armies of Pandu's son into ashes.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 26 Verse 21. May victory and well-being be yours! Go forth, O best among men (puruṣarṣabha)! Turn all the armies of Pandu's son into ashes.

sanskrit: ततस्तूर्यसहस्राणि भेरीणामयुतानि च ।वाद्यमानान्यरोचन्त मेघशब्दा यथा दिवि ॥२२॥
transliteration: tatastūryasahasrāṇi bherīṇāmayutāni ca ,vādyamānānyarocanta meghaśabdā yathā divi.
english: Then, thousands of trumpets and tens of thousands of drums, being played, resounded splendidly, just like thunderclaps in the sky (divi).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 26 Verse 22. Then, thousands of trumpets and tens of thousands of drums, being played, resounded splendidly, just like thunderclaps in the sky (divi).

sanskrit: प्रतिगृह्य तु तद्वाक्यं रथस्थो रथसत्तमः ।अभ्यभाषत राधेयः शल्यं युद्धविशारदम् ॥२३॥
transliteration: pratigṛhya tu tadvākyaṁ rathastho rathasattamaḥ ,abhyabhāṣata rādheyaḥ śalyaṁ yuddhaviśāradam.
english: But having received that statement, Radheya (Karna), who was stationed on his chariot and was the best among charioteers (rathasattama), spoke to Salya, who was expert in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 26 Verse 23. But having received that statement, Radheya (Karna), who was stationed on his chariot and was the best among charioteers (rathasattama), spoke to Salya, who was expert in battle.

sanskrit: चोदयाश्वान्महाबाहो यावद्धन्मि धनंजयम् ।भीमसेनं यमौ चोभौ राजानं च युधिष्ठिरम् ॥२४॥
transliteration: codayāśvānmahābāho yāvaddhanmi dhanaṁjayam ,bhīmasenaṁ yamau cobhau rājānaṁ ca yudhiṣṭhiram.
english: O mighty-armed (mahābāho) one, drive the horses until I kill Dhananjaya, Bhimasena, both the two Yamas (Nakula and Sahadeva), and King Yudhishthira!
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 26 Verse 24. O mighty-armed (mahābāho) one, drive the horses until I kill Dhananjaya, Bhimasena, both the two Yamas (Nakula and Sahadeva), and King Yudhishthira!

sanskrit: अद्य पश्यतु मे शल्य बाहुवीर्यं धनंजयः ।अस्यतः कङ्कपत्राणां सहस्राणि शतानि च ॥२५॥
transliteration: adya paśyatu me śalya bāhuvīryaṁ dhanaṁjayaḥ ,asyataḥ kaṅkapatrāṇāṁ sahasrāṇi śatāni ca.
english: Today, O Shalya, let Dhananjaya witness the might of my arm as I shoot hundreds and thousands of heron-feathered arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 26 Verse 25. Today, O Shalya, let Dhananjaya witness the might of my arm as I shoot hundreds and thousands of heron-feathered arrows.

sanskrit: अद्य क्षेप्स्याम्यहं शल्य शरान्परमतेजनान् ।पाण्डवानां विनाशाय दुर्योधनजयाय च ॥२६॥
transliteration: adya kṣepsyāmyahaṁ śalya śarānparamatejanān ,pāṇḍavānāṁ vināśāya duryodhanajayāya ca.
english: Today, O Shalya, I shall shoot exceedingly sharp arrows for the destruction of the Pāṇḍavas and for Duryodhana's victory.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 26 Verse 26. Today, O Shalya, I shall shoot exceedingly sharp arrows for the destruction of the Pāṇḍavas and for Duryodhana's victory.

sanskrit: शल्य उवाच ।सूतपुत्र कथं नु त्वं पाण्डवानवमन्यसे ।सर्वास्त्रज्ञान्महेष्वासान्सर्वानेव महारथान् ॥२७॥
transliteration: śalya uvāca ,sūtaputra kathaṁ nu tvaṁ pāṇḍavānavamanyase ,sarvāstrajñānmaheṣvāsānsarvāneva mahārathān.
english: Shalya said, "O son of Sūta, how can you possibly disrespect the Pāṇḍavas, who are all skilled in every weapon, great archers, and indeed, mighty charioteers?"
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 26 Verse 27. Shalya said, "O son of Sūta, how can you possibly disrespect the Pāṇḍavas, who are all skilled in every weapon, great archers, and indeed, mighty charioteers?"

sanskrit: अनिवर्तिनो महाभागानजेयान्सत्यविक्रमान् ।अपि संजनयेयुर्ये भयं साक्षाच्छतक्रतोः ॥२८॥
transliteration: anivartino mahābhāgānajeyānsatyavikramān ,api saṁjanayeyurye bhayaṁ sākṣācchatakratoḥ.
