english: who are unretreating, greatly endowed, invincible, and truly mighty in valor; indeed, they could inspire fear even in Śatakratu (Indra) himself.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 26 Verse 28. who are unretreating, greatly endowed, invincible, and truly mighty in valor; indeed, they could inspire fear even in Śatakratu (Indra) himself.

sanskrit: यदा श्रोष्यसि निर्घोषं विस्फूर्जितमिवाशनेः ।राधेय गाण्डिवस्याजौ तदा नैवं वदिष्यसि ॥२९॥
transliteration: yadā śroṣyasi nirghoṣaṁ visphūrjitamivāśaneḥ ,rādheya gāṇḍivasyājau tadā naivaṁ vadiṣyasi.
english: O son of Rādhā, when you hear the roar of the Gāṇḍīva bow in battle, like the crackling sound of a thunderbolt, then you will not speak in this manner.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 26 Verse 29. O son of Rādhā, when you hear the roar of the Gāṇḍīva bow in battle, like the crackling sound of a thunderbolt, then you will not speak in this manner.

sanskrit: संजय उवाच ।अनादृत्य तु तद्वाक्यं मद्रराजेन भाषितम् ।द्रक्ष्यस्यद्येत्यवोचद्वै शल्यं कर्णो नरेश्वर ॥३०॥
transliteration: saṁjaya uvāca ,anādṛtya tu tadvākyaṁ madrarājena bhāṣitam ,drakṣyasyadyetyavocadvai śalyaṁ karṇo nareśvara.
english: Sañjaya said: O lord of men, Karṇa, having indeed disregarded those words spoken by the king of Madras, Śalya, then said, 'You will see today!'
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 26 Verse 30. Sañjaya said: O lord of men, Karṇa, having indeed disregarded those words spoken by the king of Madras, Śalya, then said, 'You will see today!'

sanskrit: दृष्ट्वा कर्णं महेष्वासं युयुत्सुं समवस्थितम् ।चुक्रुशुः कुरवः सर्वे हृष्टरूपाः परंतप ॥३१॥
transliteration: dṛṣṭvā karṇaṁ maheṣvāsaṁ yuyutsuṁ samavasthitam ,cukruśuḥ kuravaḥ sarve hṛṣṭarūpāḥ paraṁtapa.
english: O tormentor of foes, having seen Karṇa, the great archer, standing firm and eager for battle, all the Kurus, with joyful expressions, roared.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 26 Verse 31. O tormentor of foes, having seen Karṇa, the great archer, standing firm and eager for battle, all the Kurus, with joyful expressions, roared.

sanskrit: ततो दुन्दुभिघोषेण भेरीणां निनदेन च ।बाणशब्दैश्च विविधैर्गर्जितैश्च तरस्विनाम् ।निर्ययुस्तावका युद्धे मृत्युं कृत्वा निवर्तनम् ॥३२॥
transliteration: tato dundubhighoṣeṇa bherīṇāṁ ninadena ca ,bāṇaśabdaiśca vividhairgarjitaiśca tarasvinām ,niryayustāvakā yuddhe mṛtyuṁ kṛtvā nivartanam.
english: Then, your men sallied forth into battle, with the sound of war drums and the roar of kettle-drums, and with various sounds of arrows and the roars of the mighty ones, having made death their only retreat.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 26 Verse 32. Then, your men sallied forth into battle, with the sound of war drums and the roar of kettle-drums, and with various sounds of arrows and the roars of the mighty ones, having made death their only retreat.

sanskrit: प्रयाते तु ततः कर्णे योधेषु मुदितेषु च ।चचाल पृथिवी राजन्ररास च सुविस्वरम् ॥३३॥
transliteration: prayāte tu tataḥ karṇe yodheṣu muditeṣu ca ,cacāla pṛthivī rājanrarāsa ca suvisvaram.
english: O King, when Karna had then departed, and the warriors were delighted, the earth trembled and roared with a terrible sound.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 26 Verse 33. O King, when Karna had then departed, and the warriors were delighted, the earth trembled and roared with a terrible sound.

sanskrit: निश्चरन्तो व्यदृश्यन्त सूर्यात्सप्त महाग्रहाः ।उल्कापातश्च संजज्ञे दिशां दाहस्तथैव च ।तथाशन्यश्च संपेतुर्ववुर्वाताश्च दारुणाः ॥३४॥
transliteration: niścaranto vyadṛśyanta sūryātsapta mahāgrahāḥ ,ulkāpātaśca saṁjajñe diśāṁ dāhastathaiva ca ,tathāśanyaśca saṁpeturvavurvātāśca dāruṇāḥ.
english: Seven great celestial bodies were seen emerging from the sun. A shower of meteors occurred, and similarly, the quarters of the sky appeared to be burning. Also, thunderbolts fell, and dreadful winds blew.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 26 Verse 34. Seven great celestial bodies were seen emerging from the sun. A shower of meteors occurred, and similarly, the quarters of the sky appeared to be burning. Also, thunderbolts fell, and dreadful winds blew.

sanskrit: मृगपक्षिगणाश्चैव बहुशः पृतनां तव ।अपसव्यं तदा चक्रुर्वेदयन्तो महद्भयम् ॥३५॥
transliteration: mṛgapakṣigaṇāścaiva bahuśaḥ pṛtanāṁ tava ,apasavyaṁ tadā cakrurvedayanto mahadbhayam.
english: Then, numerous flocks of deer and birds went around your army in an inauspicious, counter-clockwise direction, indeed indicating a great danger.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 26 Verse 35. Then, numerous flocks of deer and birds went around your army in an inauspicious, counter-clockwise direction, indeed indicating a great danger.

sanskrit: प्रस्थितस्य च कर्णस्य निपेतुस्तुरगा भुवि ।अस्थिवर्षं च पतितमन्तरिक्षाद्भयानकम् ॥३६॥
transliteration: prasthitasya ca karṇasya nipetusturagā bhuvi ,asthivarṣaṁ ca patitamantarikṣādbhayānakam.
english: And as Karna was departing, his horses fell to the ground. A terrifying shower of bones also fell from the sky.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 26 Verse 36. And as Karna was departing, his horses fell to the ground. A terrifying shower of bones also fell from the sky.

sanskrit: जज्वलुश्चैव शस्त्राणि ध्वजाश्चैव चकम्पिरे ।अश्रूणि च व्यमुञ्चन्त वाहनानि विशां पते ॥३७॥
transliteration: jajvaluścaiva śastrāṇi dhvajāścaiva cakampire ,aśrūṇi ca vyamuñcanta vāhanāni viśāṁ pate.
english: O lord of the people, weapons blazed, flags quivered, and the animals (mounts) shed tears.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 26 Verse 37. O lord of the people, weapons blazed, flags quivered, and the animals (mounts) shed tears.

sanskrit: एते चान्ये च बहव उत्पातास्तत्र मारिष ।समुत्पेतुर्विनाशाय कौरवाणां सुदारुणाः ॥३८॥
transliteration: ete cānye ca bahava utpātāstatra māriṣa ,samutpeturvināśāya kauravāṇāṁ sudāruṇāḥ.
english: O respected one, these and many other extremely dreadful omens appeared there, destined for the destruction of the Kauravas.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 26 Verse 38. O respected one, these and many other extremely dreadful omens appeared there, destined for the destruction of the Kauravas.

sanskrit: न च तान्गणयामासुः सर्वे ते दैवमोहिताः ।प्रस्थितं सूतपुत्रं च जयेत्यूचुर्नरा भुवि ।निर्जितान्पाण्डवांश्चैव मेनिरे तव कौरवाः ॥३९॥
transliteration: na ca tāngaṇayāmāsuḥ sarve te daivamohitāḥ ,prasthitaṁ sūtaputraṁ ca jayetyūcurnarā bhuvi ,nirjitānpāṇḍavāṁścaiva menire tava kauravāḥ.
english: However, all of them, deluded by fate, disregarded those (omens). And the people on the battlefield hailed the son of the charioteer (Karṇa) who had set out, crying 'Victory!' Your Kauravas indeed believed the Pandavas to be already conquered.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 26 Verse 39. However, all of them, deluded by fate, disregarded those (omens). And the people on the battlefield hailed the son of the charioteer (Karṇa) who had set out, crying 'Victory!' Your Kauravas indeed believed the Pandavas to be already conquered.

sanskrit: ततो रथस्थः परवीरहन्ता भीष्मद्रोणावात्तवीर्यौ निरीक्ष्य ।समज्वलद्भारत पावकाभो वैकर्तनोऽसौ रथकुञ्जरो वृषः ॥४०॥
transliteration: tato rathasthaḥ paravīrahantā; bhīṣmadroṇāvāttavīryau nirīkṣya ,samajvaladbhārata pāvakābho; vaikartano'sau rathakuñjaro vṛṣaḥ.
english: Then, O descendant of Bharata, that great charioteer (Karṇa), the son of Vikartana, mighty as a bull and a slayer of enemy heroes, observed Bhishma and Drona, whose strength seemed diminished, and he blazed forth like fire from his chariot.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 26 Verse 40. Then, O descendant of Bharata, that great charioteer (Karṇa), the son of Vikartana, mighty as a bull and a slayer of enemy heroes, observed Bhishma and Drona, whose strength seemed diminished, and he blazed forth like fire from his chariot.

sanskrit: स शल्यमाभाष्य जगाद वाक्यं पार्थस्य कर्माप्रतिमं च दृष्ट्वा ।मानेन दर्पेण च दह्यमानः क्रोधेन दीप्यन्निव निःश्वसित्वा ॥४१॥
transliteration: sa śalyamābhāṣya jagāda vākyaṁ; pārthasya karmāpratimaṁ ca dṛṣṭvā ,mānena darpeṇa ca dahyamānaḥ; krodhena dīpyanniva niḥśvasitvā.
english: Tormented by pride and arrogance, and breathing heavily as if aflame with anger, he addressed Shalya and spoke, having witnessed Partha's unparalleled deed (karma).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 26 Verse 41. Tormented by pride and arrogance, and breathing heavily as if aflame with anger, he addressed Shalya and spoke, having witnessed Partha's unparalleled deed (karma).

sanskrit: नाहं महेन्द्रादपि वज्रपाणेः क्रुद्धाद्बिभेम्यात्तधनू रथस्थः ।दृष्ट्वा तु भीष्मप्रमुखाञ्शयानान्न त्वेव मां स्थिरता संजहाति ॥४२॥
transliteration: nāhaṁ mahendrādapi vajrapāṇeḥ; kruddhādbibhemyāttadhanū rathasthaḥ ,dṛṣṭvā tu bhīṣmapramukhāñśayānā;nna tveva māṁ sthiratā saṁjahāti.
english: Standing on my chariot with my bow drawn, I do not fear even the wrathful great Indra, the wielder of the thunderbolt. However, even after seeing Bhishma and other prominent warriors lying (dead), my steadfastness does not abandon me.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 26 Verse 42. Standing on my chariot with my bow drawn, I do not fear even the wrathful great Indra, the wielder of the thunderbolt. However, even after seeing Bhishma and other prominent warriors lying (dead), my steadfastness does not abandon me.

sanskrit: महेन्द्रविष्णुप्रतिमावनिन्दितौ रथाश्वनागप्रवरप्रमाथिनौ ।अवध्यकल्पौ निहतौ यदा परैस्ततो ममाद्यापि रणेऽस्ति साध्वसम् ॥४३॥
transliteration: mahendraviṣṇupratimāvaninditau; rathāśvanāgapravarapramāthinau ,avadhyakalpau nihatau yadā parai;stato mamādyāpi raṇe'sti sādhvasam.
english: My fear (sādhvasam) still lingers on the battlefield, even today, because those two, who were like the great Indra and Vishnu, blameless, destroyers of the finest chariots, horses, and elephants, and virtually invincible, were killed by the enemies.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 26 Verse 43. My fear (sādhvasam) still lingers on the battlefield, even today, because those two, who were like the great Indra and Vishnu, blameless, destroyers of the finest chariots, horses, and elephants, and virtually invincible, were killed by the enemies.

sanskrit: समीक्ष्य संख्येऽतिबलान्नराधिपैर्नराश्वमातङ्गरथाञ्शरैर्हतान् ।कथं न सर्वानहितान्रणेऽवधीन्महास्त्रविद्ब्राह्मणपुंगवो गुरुः ॥४४॥
transliteration: samīkṣya saṁkhye'tibalānnarādhipai;rnarāśvamātaṅgarathāñśarairhatān ,kathaṁ na sarvānahitānraṇe'vadhī;nmahāstravidbrāhmaṇapuṁgavo guruḥ.
english: Having observed in battle mighty (forces of) kings, men, horses, elephants, and chariots slain by arrows, how did the teacher (guru), the foremost among Brahmins and a master of great weapons, not kill all the enemies on the battlefield?
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 26 Verse 44. Having observed in battle mighty (forces of) kings, men, horses, elephants, and chariots slain by arrows, how did the teacher (guru), the foremost among Brahmins and a master of great weapons, not kill all the enemies on the battlefield?

sanskrit: स संस्मरन्द्रोणहवं महाहवे ब्रवीमि सत्यं कुरवो निबोधत ।न वो मदन्यः प्रसहेद्रणेऽर्जुनं क्रमागतं मृत्युमिवोग्ररूपिणम् ॥४५॥
transliteration: sa saṁsmarandroṇahavaṁ mahāhave; bravīmi satyaṁ kuravo nibodhata ,na vo madanyaḥ prasahedraṇe'rjunaṁ; kramāgataṁ mṛtyumivograrūpiṇam.
english: Remembering Drona's call to battle (drona-hava) in the great conflict (maha-hava), I speak the truth, O Kurus, understand this: No one among you, apart from myself, can withstand Arjuna in battle, who is like formidable death (mṛtyu) that arrives in its destined time.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 26 Verse 45. Remembering Drona's call to battle (drona-hava) in the great conflict (maha-hava), I speak the truth, O Kurus, understand this: No one among you, apart from myself, can withstand Arjuna in battle, who is like formidable death (mṛtyu) that arrives in its destined time.

sanskrit: शिक्षा प्रसादश्च बलं धृतिश्च द्रोणे महास्त्राणि च संनतिश्च ।स चेदगान्मृत्युवशं महात्मा सर्वानन्यानातुरानद्य मन्ये ॥४६॥
transliteration: śikṣā prasādaśca balaṁ dhṛtiśca; droṇe mahāstrāṇi ca saṁnatiśca ,sa cedagānmṛtyuvaśaṁ mahātmā; sarvānanyānāturānadya manye.
english: Discipline, grace, strength, and steadfastness (dhṛti), along with mighty weapons and humility (sannati), were all present in Drona. If that great soul (mahātman) has succumbed to the power of death (mṛtyu), then I consider all others now to be helpless and suffering.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 26 Verse 46. Discipline, grace, strength, and steadfastness (dhṛti), along with mighty weapons and humility (sannati), were all present in Drona. If that great soul (mahātman) has succumbed to the power of death (mṛtyu), then I consider all others now to be helpless and suffering.

sanskrit: नेह ध्रुवं किंचिदपि प्रचिन्त्यं विदुर्लोके कर्मणोऽनित्ययोगात् ।सूर्योदये को हि विमुक्तसंशयो गर्वं कुर्वीताद्य गुरौ निपातिते ॥४७॥
transliteration: neha dhruvaṁ kiṁcidapi pracintyaṁ; vidurloke karmaṇo'nityayogāt ,sūryodaye ko hi vimuktasaṁśayo; garvaṁ kurvītādya gurau nipātite.
english: In this world, nothing at all can be considered truly permanent (dhruva), for people know that everything is impermanent due to its connection with action (karma). With the guru now fallen, who indeed, free from all doubt, could feel pride (garva) at the rising of the sun today?
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 26 Verse 47. In this world, nothing at all can be considered truly permanent (dhruva), for people know that everything is impermanent due to its connection with action (karma). With the guru now fallen, who indeed, free from all doubt, could feel pride (garva) at the rising of the sun today?

sanskrit: न नूनमस्त्राणि बलं पराक्रमः क्रिया सुनीतं परमायुधानि वा ।अलं मनुष्यस्य सुखाय वर्तितुं तथा हि युद्धे निहतः परैर्गुरुः ॥४८॥
transliteration: na nūnamastrāṇi balaṁ parākramaḥ; kriyā sunītaṁ paramāyudhāni vā ,alaṁ manuṣyasya sukhāya vartituṁ; tathā hi yuddhe nihataḥ parairguruḥ.
english: Surely, neither weapons, nor strength, nor valor, nor [ritual] actions, nor excellent policy, nor even ultimate weapons, are sufficient for a person to live happily (sukha); for indeed, the guru has thus been slain by enemies in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 26 Verse 48. Surely, neither weapons, nor strength, nor valor, nor [ritual] actions, nor excellent policy, nor even ultimate weapons, are sufficient for a person to live happily (sukha); for indeed, the guru has thus been slain by enemies in battle.

sanskrit: हुताशनादित्यसमानतेजसं पराक्रमे विष्णुपुरंदरोपमम् ।नये बृहस्पत्युशनःसमं सदा न चैनमस्त्रं तदपात्सुदुःसहम् ॥४९॥
transliteration: hutāśanādityasamānatejasaṁ; parākrame viṣṇupuraṁdaropamam ,naye bṛhaspatyuśanaḥsamaṁ sadā; na cainamastraṁ tadapātsuduḥsaham.
english: Whose brilliance was like fire and the sun, whose prowess was like Viṣṇu and Indra, and whose statesmanship was always like Bṛhaspati and Uśanas - even that exceedingly formidable weapon could not harm him.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 26 Verse 49. Whose brilliance was like fire and the sun, whose prowess was like Viṣṇu and Indra, and whose statesmanship was always like Bṛhaspati and Uśanas - even that exceedingly formidable weapon could not harm him.

sanskrit: संप्रक्रुष्टे रुदितस्त्रीकुमारे पराभूते पौरुषे धार्तराष्ट्रे ।मया कृत्यमिति जानामि शल्य प्रयाहि तस्माद्द्विषतामनीकम् ॥५०॥
transliteration: saṁprakruṣṭe ruditastrīkumāre; parābhūte pauruṣe dhārtarāṣṭre ,mayā kṛtyamiti jānāmi śalya; prayāhi tasmāddviṣatāmanīkam.
english: O Śalya, when the valor of Dhṛtarāṣṭra's son (Duryodhana) is defeated, and weeping women and children cry out loudly, I know that it is my duty. Therefore, go forth to the army of our enemies!
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 26 Verse 50. O Śalya, when the valor of Dhṛtarāṣṭra's son (Duryodhana) is defeated, and weeping women and children cry out loudly, I know that it is my duty. Therefore, go forth to the army of our enemies!

sanskrit: यत्र राजा पाण्डवः सत्यसंधो व्यवस्थितो भीमसेनार्जुनौ च ।वासुदेवः सृञ्जयाः सात्यकिश्च यमौ च कस्तौ विषहेन्मदन्यः ॥५१॥
transliteration: yatra rājā pāṇḍavaḥ satyasaṁdho; vyavasthito bhīmasenārjunau ca ,vāsudevaḥ sṛñjayāḥ sātyakiśca; yamau ca kastau viṣahenmadanyaḥ.
english: Where the Pāṇḍava king (Yudhiṣṭhira), true to his pledge (dharma), stands firm, along with Bhīmasena and Arjuna, Vāsudeva (Kṛṣṇa), the Sṛñjayas, Sātyaki, and the two twins (Nakula and Sahadeva) - who among others besides me could withstand them?
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 26 Verse 51. Where the Pāṇḍava king (Yudhiṣṭhira), true to his pledge (dharma), stands firm, along with Bhīmasena and Arjuna, Vāsudeva (Kṛṣṇa), the Sṛñjayas, Sātyaki, and the two twins (Nakula and Sahadeva) - who among others besides me could withstand them?

sanskrit: तस्मात्क्षिप्रं मद्रपते प्रयाहि रणे पाञ्चालान्पाण्डवान्सृञ्जयांश्च ।तान्वा हनिष्यामि समेत्य संख्ये यास्यामि वा द्रोणमुखाय मन्ये ॥५२॥
transliteration: tasmātkṣipraṁ madrapate prayāhi; raṇe pāñcālānpāṇḍavānsṛñjayāṁśca ,tānvā haniṣyāmi sametya saṁkhye; yāsyāmi vā droṇamukhāya manye.
english: Therefore, O King of Madra (Śalya), go quickly into battle! I will either slay those Pāñcālas, Pāṇḍavas, and Sṛñjayas when I encounter them in combat, or I will go to the realm where Droṇa has gone (i.e., die heroically), I believe.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 26 Verse 52. Therefore, O King of Madra (Śalya), go quickly into battle! I will either slay those Pāñcālas, Pāṇḍavas, and Sṛñjayas when I encounter them in combat, or I will go to the realm where Droṇa has gone (i.e., die heroically), I believe.

sanskrit: न त्वेवाहं न गमिष्यामि मध्यं तेषां शूराणामिति मा शल्य विद्धि ।मित्रद्रोहो मर्षणीयो न मेऽयं त्यक्त्वा प्राणाननुयास्यामि द्रोणम् ॥५३॥
transliteration: na tvevāhaṁ na gamiṣyāmi madhyaṁ; teṣāṁ śūrāṇāmiti mā śalya viddhi ,mitradroho marṣaṇīyo na me'yaṁ; tyaktvā prāṇānanuyāsyāmi droṇam.
english: O Śalya, understand this about me: I will certainly not go among those heroes (of the enemy). This betrayal of a friend is not forgivable by me. I will follow Droṇa, abandoning my very life.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 26 Verse 53. O Śalya, understand this about me: I will certainly not go among those heroes (of the enemy). This betrayal of a friend is not forgivable by me. I will follow Droṇa, abandoning my very life.

sanskrit: प्राज्ञस्य मूढस्य च जीवितान्ते प्राणप्रमोक्षोऽन्तकवक्त्रगस्य ।अतो विद्वन्नभियास्यामि पार्थं दिष्टं न शक्यं व्यतिवर्तितुं वै ॥५४॥
transliteration: prājñasya mūḍhasya ca jīvitānte; prāṇapramokṣo'ntakavaktragasya ,ato vidvannabhiyāsyāmi pārthaṁ; diṣṭaṁ na śakyaṁ vyativartituṁ vai.
english: At the end of life, the release of vital breaths (prāṇa) into the mouth of Death (Antaka) occurs for both the wise and the foolish. Therefore, O wise one, I will advance against Pārtha (Arjuna). Indeed, destiny cannot be averted.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 26 Verse 54. At the end of life, the release of vital breaths (prāṇa) into the mouth of Death (Antaka) occurs for both the wise and the foolish. Therefore, O wise one, I will advance against Pārtha (Arjuna). Indeed, destiny cannot be averted.

sanskrit: कल्याणवृत्तः सततं हि राजन्वैचित्रवीर्यस्य सुतो ममासीत् ।तस्यार्थसिद्ध्यर्थमहं त्यजामि प्रियान्भोगान्दुस्त्यजं जीवितं च ॥५५॥
transliteration: kalyāṇavṛttaḥ satataṁ hi rāja;nvaicitravīryasya suto mamāsīt ,tasyārthasiddhyarthamahaṁ tyajāmi; priyānbhogāndustyajaṁ jīvitaṁ ca.
english: Indeed, O King, the son of Vicitravīrya, who always conducted himself virtuously, was (like) my own. Therefore, for the attainment of his goal, I abandon dear pleasures and even my life, which is difficult to give up.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 26 Verse 55. Indeed, O King, the son of Vicitravīrya, who always conducted himself virtuously, was (like) my own. Therefore, for the attainment of his goal, I abandon dear pleasures and even my life, which is difficult to give up.

sanskrit: वैयाघ्रचर्माणमकूजनाक्षं हैमत्रिकोशं रजतत्रिवेणुम् ।रथप्रबर्हं तुरगप्रबर्हैर्युक्तं प्रादान्मह्यमिदं हि रामः ॥५६॥
transliteration: vaiyāghracarmāṇamakūjanākṣaṁ; haimatrikośaṁ rajatatriveṇum ,rathaprabarhaṁ turagaprabarhai;ryuktaṁ prādānmahyamidaṁ hi rāmaḥ.
english: Indeed, Rāma gave me this excellent chariot, adorned with a tiger-skin cover, possessing noiseless axles, a triple golden casing, and three silver bamboo-shafts, and yoked with excellent horses.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 26 Verse 56. Indeed, Rāma gave me this excellent chariot, adorned with a tiger-skin cover, possessing noiseless axles, a triple golden casing, and three silver bamboo-shafts, and yoked with excellent horses.

sanskrit: धनूंषि चित्राणि निरीक्ष्य शल्य ध्वजं गदां सायकांश्चोग्ररूपान् ।असिं च दीप्तं परमायुधं च शङ्खं च शुभ्रं स्वनवन्तमुग्रम् ॥५७॥
transliteration: dhanūṁṣi citrāṇi nirīkṣya śalya; dhvajaṁ gadāṁ sāyakāṁścograrūpān ,asiṁ ca dīptaṁ paramāyudhaṁ ca; śaṅkhaṁ ca śubhraṁ svanavantamugram.
english: O Shalya, having observed his diverse bows, his banner, his mace, his fearsome arrows, his blazing sword, his supreme weapon, and his bright, resounding, formidable conch shell -
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 26 Verse 57. O Shalya, having observed his diverse bows, his banner, his mace, his fearsome arrows, his blazing sword, his supreme weapon, and his bright, resounding, formidable conch shell -

sanskrit: पताकिनं वज्रनिपातनिस्वनं सिताश्वयुक्तं शुभतूणशोभितम् ।इमं समास्थाय रथं रथर्षभं रणे हनिष्याम्यहमर्जुनं बलात् ॥५८॥
transliteration: patākinaṁ vajranipātanisvanaṁ; sitāśvayuktaṁ śubhatūṇaśobhitam ,imaṁ samāsthāya rathaṁ ratharṣabhaṁ; raṇe haniṣyāmyahamarjunaṁ balāt.
english: and having mounted this supreme chariot - which bears a banner, thunders like a falling thunderbolt, is yoked with white horses, and is adorned with beautiful quivers - I will forcibly kill Arjuna in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 26 Verse 58. and having mounted this supreme chariot - which bears a banner, thunders like a falling thunderbolt, is yoked with white horses, and is adorned with beautiful quivers - I will forcibly kill Arjuna in battle.

sanskrit: तं चेन्मृत्युः सर्वहरोऽभिरक्षते सदाप्रमत्तः समरे पाण्डुपुत्रम् ।तं वा हनिष्यामि समेत्य युद्धे यास्यामि वा भीष्ममुखो यमाय ॥५९॥
transliteration: taṁ cenmṛtyuḥ sarvaharo'bhirakṣate; sadāpramattaḥ samare pāṇḍuputram ,taṁ vā haniṣyāmi sametya yuddhe; yāsyāmi vā bhīṣmamukho yamāya.
english: If even all-destroying Death, ever vigilant in battle, protects that son of Pāṇḍu (Arjuna), then I will either kill him after confronting him in battle, or I myself, led by Bhīṣma, will go to Yama (the god of death).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 26 Verse 59. If even all-destroying Death, ever vigilant in battle, protects that son of Pāṇḍu (Arjuna), then I will either kill him after confronting him in battle, or I myself, led by Bhīṣma, will go to Yama (the god of death).

sanskrit: यमवरुणकुबेरवासवा वा यदि युगपत्सगणा महाहवे ।जुगुपिषव इहैत्य पाण्डवं किमु बहुना सह तैर्जयामि तम् ॥६०॥
transliteration: yamavaruṇakuberavāsavā vā; yadi yugapatsagaṇā mahāhave ,jugupiṣava ihaitya pāṇḍavaṁ; kimu bahunā saha tairjayāmi tam.
english: Even if Yama, Varuṇa, Kubera, and Indra, along with their retinues, were to come here simultaneously to the great battle, desiring to protect Arjuna, what need is there for much talk? I will conquer him, along with all of them!
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 26 Verse 60. Even if Yama, Varuṇa, Kubera, and Indra, along with their retinues, were to come here simultaneously to the great battle, desiring to protect Arjuna, what need is there for much talk? I will conquer him, along with all of them!

sanskrit: इति रणरभसस्य कत्थतस्तदुपनिशम्य वचः स मद्रराट् ।अवहसदवमन्य वीर्यवान्प्रतिषिषिधे च जगाद चोत्तरम् ॥६१॥
transliteration: iti raṇarabhasasya katthata;stadupaniśamya vacaḥ sa madrarāṭ ,avahasadavamanya vīryavā;npratiṣiṣidhe ca jagāda cottaram.
english: Hearing these words from him, who was boasting with battle-fury, that mighty king of Madras (Shalya) laughed contemptuously, restrained him, and gave a reply.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 26 Verse 61. Hearing these words from him, who was boasting with battle-fury, that mighty king of Madras (Shalya) laughed contemptuously, restrained him, and gave a reply.

sanskrit: विरम विरम कर्ण कत्थनादतिरभसोऽस्यति चाप्ययुक्तवाक् ।क्व च हि नरवरो धनंजयः क्व पुनरिह त्वमुपारमाबुध ॥६२॥
transliteration: virama virama karṇa katthanā;datirabhaso'syati cāpyayuktavāk ,kva ca hi naravaro dhanaṁjayaḥ; kva punariha tvamupāramābudha.
english: Stop, stop, Karna, desist from this boasting! You are exceedingly impetuous and your words are inappropriate. Where, indeed, is Dhananjaya (Arjuna), the foremost among men, and where, again, are you here, O ignorant one?
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 26 Verse 62. Stop, stop, Karna, desist from this boasting! You are exceedingly impetuous and your words are inappropriate. Where, indeed, is Dhananjaya (Arjuna), the foremost among men, and where, again, are you here, O ignorant one?

sanskrit: यदुसदनमुपेन्द्रपालितं त्रिदिवमिवामरराजरक्षितम् ।प्रसभमिह विलोक्य को हरेत्पुरुषवरावरजामृतेऽर्जुनात् ॥६३॥
transliteration: yadusadanamupendrapālitaṁ; tridivamivāmararājarakṣitam ,prasabhamiha vilokya ko hare;tpuruṣavarāvarajāmṛte'rjunāt.
english: Upon seeing the Yadava capital, which is protected by Upendra (Krishna) just as heaven is guarded by the king of gods (Indra), who here would forcibly seize it, except for Arjuna, the foremost among men and the younger-born?
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 26 Verse 63. Upon seeing the Yadava capital, which is protected by Upendra (Krishna) just as heaven is guarded by the king of gods (Indra), who here would forcibly seize it, except for Arjuna, the foremost among men and the younger-born?

sanskrit: त्रिभुवनसृजमीश्वरेश्वरं क इह पुमान्भवमाह्वयेद्युधि ।मृगवधकलहे ऋतेऽर्जुनात्सुरपतिवीर्यसमप्रभावतः ॥६४॥
transliteration: tribhuvanasṛjamīśvareśvaraṁ; ka iha pumānbhavamāhvayedyudhi ,mṛgavadhakalahe ṛte'rjunā;tsurapativīryasamaprabhāvataḥ.
english: Who among men here would challenge Bhava (Shiva), the creator of the three worlds and the lord of lords, in battle, specifically during the dispute over the slain deer, except for Arjuna, whose prowess equals that of the king of gods (Indra)?
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 26 Verse 64. Who among men here would challenge Bhava (Shiva), the creator of the three worlds and the lord of lords, in battle, specifically during the dispute over the slain deer, except for Arjuna, whose prowess equals that of the king of gods (Indra)?

sanskrit: असुरसुरमहोरगान्नरान्गरुडपिशाचसयक्षराक्षसान् ।इषुभिरजयदग्निगौरवात्स्वभिलषितं च हविर्ददौ जयः ॥६५॥
transliteration: asurasuramahoragānnarā;ngaruḍapiśācasayakṣarākṣasān ,iṣubhirajayadagnigauravā;tsvabhilaṣitaṁ ca havirdadau jayaḥ.
english: Out of reverence for Agni, Jaya conquered with his arrows asuras, suras, great serpents, humans, garudas, piśācas, yakṣas, and rākṣasas, and he offered the desired oblation.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 26 Verse 65. Out of reverence for Agni, Jaya conquered with his arrows asuras, suras, great serpents, humans, garudas, piśācas, yakṣas, and rākṣasas, and he offered the desired oblation.

sanskrit: स्मरसि ननु यदा परैर्हृतः स च धृतराष्ट्रसुतो विमोक्षितः ।दिनकरज नरोत्तमैर्यदा मरुषु बहून्विनिहत्य तानरीन् ॥६६॥
transliteration: smarasi nanu yadā parairhṛtaḥ; sa ca dhṛtarāṣṭrasuto vimokṣitaḥ ,dinakaraja narottamairyadā; maruṣu bahūnvinihatya tānarīn.
english: Surely you remember, O son of the sun (Karṇa), when that son of Dhṛtarāṣṭra was carried off by enemies and then liberated? (Do you not recall) when the best of men, having thoroughly defeated many of those foes in the desert, (secured his release)?
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 26 Verse 66. Surely you remember, O son of the sun (Karṇa), when that son of Dhṛtarāṣṭra was carried off by enemies and then liberated? (Do you not recall) when the best of men, having thoroughly defeated many of those foes in the desert, (secured his release)?

sanskrit: प्रथममपि पलायिते त्वयि प्रियकलहा धृतराष्ट्रसूनवः ।स्मरसि ननु यदा प्रमोचिताः खचरगणानवजित्य पाण्डवैः ॥६७॥
transliteration: prathamamapi palāyite tvayi; priyakalahā dhṛtarāṣṭrasūnavaḥ ,smarasi nanu yadā pramocitāḥ; khacaragaṇānavajitya pāṇḍavaiḥ.
english: Surely you remember, when the sons of Dhṛtarāṣṭra, who were fond of quarrel, were rescued by the Pāṇḍavas after they had defeated the hosts of sky-dwellers (Gandharvas), even though you had fled first?
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 26 Verse 67. Surely you remember, when the sons of Dhṛtarāṣṭra, who were fond of quarrel, were rescued by the Pāṇḍavas after they had defeated the hosts of sky-dwellers (Gandharvas), even though you had fled first?

sanskrit: समुदितबलवाहनाः पुनः पुरुषवरेण जिताः स्थ गोग्रहे ।सगुरुगुरुसुताः सभीष्मकाः किमु न जितः स तदा त्वयार्जुनः ॥६८॥
transliteration: samuditabalavāhanāḥ punaḥ; puruṣavareṇa jitāḥ stha gograhe ,sagurugurusutāḥ sabhīṣmakāḥ; kimu na jitaḥ sa tadā tvayārjunaḥ.
english: Furthermore, when you, with your assembled forces and vehicles, including your preceptors and their sons, and Bhīṣma, were defeated in the cattle raid by the best of men, why then was that Arjuna not conquered by you?
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 26 Verse 68. Furthermore, when you, with your assembled forces and vehicles, including your preceptors and their sons, and Bhīṣma, were defeated in the cattle raid by the best of men, why then was that Arjuna not conquered by you?

sanskrit: इदमपरमुपस्थितं पुनस्तव निधनाय सुयुद्धमद्य वै ।यदि न रिपुभयात्पलायसे समरगतोऽद्य हतोऽसि सूतज ॥६९॥
transliteration: idamaparamupasthitaṁ puna;stava nidhanāya suyuddhamadya vai ,yadi na ripubhayātpalāyase; samaragato'dya hato'si sūtaja.
english: Indeed, another great battle has come upon you again today, for your destruction. O son of a charioteer, if you do not flee out of fear of the enemy, then, having entered this battle today, you will surely be killed.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 26 Verse 69. Indeed, another great battle has come upon you again today, for your destruction. O son of a charioteer, if you do not flee out of fear of the enemy, then, having entered this battle today, you will surely be killed.

sanskrit: संजय उवाच ।इति बहुपरुषं प्रभाषति प्रमनसि मद्रपतौ रिपुस्तवम् ।भृशमतिरुषितः परं वृषः कुरुपृतनापतिराह मद्रपम् ॥७०॥
transliteration: saṁjaya uvāca ,iti bahuparuṣaṁ prabhāṣati; pramanasi madrapatau ripustavam ,bhṛśamatiruṣitaḥ paraṁ vṛṣaḥ; kurupṛtanāpatirāha madrapam.
english: Sañjaya said: As the King of Madra, his mind agitated, was thus speaking many harsh words and praising the enemy, Karna (Vṛṣa), the commander of the Kuru army, became exceedingly enraged and then spoke to the King of Madra.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 26 Verse 70. Sañjaya said: As the King of Madra, his mind agitated, was thus speaking many harsh words and praising the enemy, Karna (Vṛṣa), the commander of the Kuru army, became exceedingly enraged and then spoke to the King of Madra.

sanskrit: भवतु भवतु किं विकत्थसे ननु मम तस्य च युद्धमुद्यतम् ।यदि स जयति मां महाहवे तत इदमस्तु सुकत्थितं तव ॥७१॥
transliteration: bhavatu bhavatu kiṁ vikatthase; nanu mama tasya ca yuddhamudyatam ,yadi sa jayati māṁ mahāhave; tata idamastu sukatthitaṁ tava.
english: Enough, enough! Why do you keep boasting? Indeed, the battle between him and me is at hand. If he (Arjuna) defeats me in the great battle, then your boasting will truly be considered well-said.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 26 Verse 71. Enough, enough! Why do you keep boasting? Indeed, the battle between him and me is at hand. If he (Arjuna) defeats me in the great battle, then your boasting will truly be considered well-said.

sanskrit: एवमस्त्विति मद्रेश उक्त्वा नोत्तरमुक्तवान् ।याहि मद्रेश चाप्येनं कर्णः प्राह युयुत्सया ॥७२॥
transliteration: evamastviti madreśa uktvā nottaramuktavān ,yāhi madreśa cāpyenaṁ karṇaḥ prāha yuyutsayā.
english: Having said, 'So be it,' the King of Madra gave no further reply. Then Karna, with a strong desire to fight, said to him, 'Proceed, O King of Madra!'
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 26 Verse 72. Having said, 'So be it,' the King of Madra gave no further reply. Then Karna, with a strong desire to fight, said to him, 'Proceed, O King of Madra!'

sanskrit: स रथः प्रययौ शत्रूञ्श्वेताश्वः शल्यसारथिः ।निघ्नन्नमित्रान्समरे तमो घ्नन्सविता यथा ॥७३॥
transliteration: sa rathaḥ prayayau śatrūñśvetāśvaḥ śalyasārathiḥ ,nighnannamitrānsamare tamo ghnansavitā yathā.
english: That chariot, with its white horses and Shalya as charioteer, proceeded against the enemies in battle, striking down foes, just as the sun destroys darkness.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 26 Verse 73. That chariot, with its white horses and Shalya as charioteer, proceeded against the enemies in battle, striking down foes, just as the sun destroys darkness.

sanskrit: ततः प्रायात्प्रीतिमान्वै रथेन वैयाघ्रेण श्वेतयुजाथ कर्णः ।स चालोक्य ध्वजिनीं पाण्डवानां धनंजयं त्वरया पर्यपृच्छत् ॥७४॥
transliteration: tataḥ prāyātprītimānvai rathena; vaiyāghreṇa śvetayujātha karṇaḥ ,sa cālokya dhvajinīṁ pāṇḍavānāṁ; dhanaṁjayaṁ tvarayā paryapṛcchat.
english: Then, indeed, Karna, joyful, proceeded by his tiger-emblazoned chariot, yoked with white horses. And observing the army of the Pandavas, he quickly questioned Dhananjaya (Arjuna).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 26 Verse 74. Then, indeed, Karna, joyful, proceeded by his tiger-emblazoned chariot, yoked with white horses. And observing the army of the Pandavas, he quickly questioned Dhananjaya (Arjuna).

sanskrit: संजय उवाच ।प्रयानेव तदा कर्णो हर्षयन्वाहिनीं तव ।एकैकं समरे दृष्ट्वा पाण्डवं पर्यपृच्छत ॥१॥
transliteration: saṁjaya uvāca ,prayāneva tadā karṇo harṣayanvāhinīṁ tava ,ekaikaṁ samare dṛṣṭvā pāṇḍavaṁ paryapṛcchata.
english: Sanjaya said: Karna, while proceeding then, indeed gladdening your army, questioned each Pandava one by one after seeing them in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 27 Verse 1. Sanjaya said: Karna, while proceeding then, indeed gladdening your army, questioned each Pandava one by one after seeing them in battle.

sanskrit: यो ममाद्य महात्मानं दर्शयेच्छ्वेतवाहनम् ।तस्मै दद्यामभिप्रेतं वरं यं मनसेच्छति ॥२॥
transliteration: yo mamādya mahātmānaṁ darśayecchvetavāhanam ,tasmai dadyāmabhipretaṁ varaṁ yaṁ manasecchati.
english: Whoever shows me today the great-souled one, Arjuna (śvetavāhana), to him I would give the desired boon which he wishes for in his mind.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 27 Verse 2. Whoever shows me today the great-souled one, Arjuna (śvetavāhana), to him I would give the desired boon which he wishes for in his mind.

sanskrit: स चेत्तदभिमन्येत तस्मै दद्यामहं पुनः ।शकटं रत्नसंपूर्णं यो मे ब्रूयाद्धनंजयम् ॥३॥
transliteration: sa cettadabhimanyeta tasmai dadyāmahaṁ punaḥ ,śakaṭaṁ ratnasaṁpūrṇaṁ yo me brūyāddhanaṁjayam.
english: If he were to agree to that, I would give him again a cart full of jewels, whoever would tell me about Dhananjaya (Arjuna).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 27 Verse 3. If he were to agree to that, I would give him again a cart full of jewels, whoever would tell me about Dhananjaya (Arjuna).

sanskrit: स चेत्तदभिमन्येत पुरुषोऽर्जुनदर्शिवान् ।अन्यं तस्मै पुनर्दद्यां सौवर्णं हस्तिषड्गवम् ॥४॥
transliteration: sa cettadabhimanyeta puruṣo'rjunadarśivān ,anyaṁ tasmai punardadyāṁ sauvarṇaṁ hastiṣaḍgavam.
english: If that man, who has seen Arjuna, were to agree to that, I would again give him another gift: a golden elephant and six golden cows.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 27 Verse 4. If that man, who has seen Arjuna, were to agree to that, I would again give him another gift: a golden elephant and six golden cows.

sanskrit: तथा तस्मै पुनर्दद्यां स्त्रीणां शतमलंकृतम् ।श्यामानां निष्ककण्ठीनां गीतवाद्यविपश्चिताम् ॥५॥
transliteration: tathā tasmai punardadyāṁ strīṇāṁ śatamalaṁkṛtam ,śyāmānāṁ niṣkakaṇṭhīnāṁ gītavādyavipaścitām.
english: Similarly, I would again give him a hundred adorned women, dark-complexioned, adorned with gold coin necklaces, and skilled in singing and playing musical instruments.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 27 Verse 5. Similarly, I would again give him a hundred adorned women, dark-complexioned, adorned with gold coin necklaces, and skilled in singing and playing musical instruments.

sanskrit: स चेत्तदभिमन्येत पुरुषोऽर्जुनदर्शिवान् ।अन्यं तस्मै वरं दद्यां श्वेतान्पञ्चशतान्हयान् ॥६॥
transliteration: sa cettadabhimanyeta puruṣo'rjunadarśivān ,anyaṁ tasmai varaṁ dadyāṁ śvetānpañcaśatānhayān.
english: If that man, who has seen Arjuna, were to agree to that, I would give him another excellent gift: five hundred white horses.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 27 Verse 6. If that man, who has seen Arjuna, were to agree to that, I would give him another excellent gift: five hundred white horses.

sanskrit: हेमभाण्डपरिच्छन्नान्सुमृष्टमणिकुण्डलान् ।सुदान्तानपि चैवाहं दद्यामष्टशतान्परान् ॥७॥
transliteration: hemabhāṇḍaparicchannānsumṛṣṭamaṇikuṇḍalān ,sudāntānapi caivāhaṁ dadyāmaṣṭaśatānparān.
english: And I would indeed give eight hundred other excellent (horses), adorned with golden trappings and brilliant jewel earrings, and also very well-trained.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 27 Verse 7. And I would indeed give eight hundred other excellent (horses), adorned with golden trappings and brilliant jewel earrings, and also very well-trained.

sanskrit: रथं च शुभ्रं सौवर्णं दद्यां तस्मै स्वलंकृतम् ।युक्तं परमकाम्बोजैर्यो मे ब्रूयाद्धनंजयम् ॥८॥
transliteration: rathaṁ ca śubhraṁ sauvarṇaṁ dadyāṁ tasmai svalaṁkṛtam ,yuktaṁ paramakāmbojairyo me brūyāddhanaṁjayam.
english: And I would give to him a splendid, golden chariot, well-decorated and yoked with excellent Kamboja horses, whoever would tell me the whereabouts of Dhanañjaya (Arjuna).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 27 Verse 8. And I would give to him a splendid, golden chariot, well-decorated and yoked with excellent Kamboja horses, whoever would tell me the whereabouts of Dhanañjaya (Arjuna).

sanskrit: अन्यं तस्मै वरं दद्यां कुञ्जराणां शतानि षट् ।काञ्चनैर्विविधैर्भाण्डैराच्छन्नान्हेममालिनः ।उत्पन्नानपरान्तेषु विनीतान्हस्तिशिक्षकैः ॥९॥
transliteration: anyaṁ tasmai varaṁ dadyāṁ kuñjarāṇāṁ śatāni ṣaṭ ,kāñcanairvividhairbhāṇḍairācchannānhemamālinaḥ ,utpannānaparānteṣu vinītānhastiśikṣakaiḥ.
english: I would give him another excellent gift (vara): six hundred elephants, adorned with various golden trappings and wearing golden garlands, born in the western regions, and well-trained by elephant trainers.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 27 Verse 9. I would give him another excellent gift (vara): six hundred elephants, adorned with various golden trappings and wearing golden garlands, born in the western regions, and well-trained by elephant trainers.

sanskrit: स चेत्तदभिमन्येत पुरुषोऽर्जुनदर्शिवान् ।अन्यं तस्मै वरं दद्यां यमसौ कामयेत्स्वयम् ॥१०॥
transliteration: sa cettadabhimanyeta puruṣo'rjunadarśivān ,anyaṁ tasmai varaṁ dadyāṁ yamasau kāmayetsvayam.
english: If that individual (puruṣa) who has seen Arjuna would approve of these offers, I would give him any other boon that he himself might desire.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 27 Verse 10. If that individual (puruṣa) who has seen Arjuna would approve of these offers, I would give him any other boon that he himself might desire.

sanskrit: पुत्रदारान्विहारांश्च यदन्यद्वित्तमस्ति मे ।तच्च तस्मै पुनर्दद्यां यद्यत्स मनसेच्छति ॥११॥
transliteration: putradārānvihārāṁśca yadanyadvittamasti me ,tacca tasmai punardadyāṁ yadyatsa manasecchati.
english: Whatever other wealth I possess, including my sons, wives, and places of enjoyment, I would give all that to him again, whatever he desires in his mind.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 27 Verse 11. Whatever other wealth I possess, including my sons, wives, and places of enjoyment, I would give all that to him again, whatever he desires in his mind.

sanskrit: हत्वा च सहितौ कृष्णौ तयोर्वित्तानि सर्वशः ।तस्मै दद्यामहं यो मे प्रब्रूयात्केशवार्जुनौ ॥१२॥
transliteration: hatvā ca sahitau kṛṣṇau tayorvittāni sarvaśaḥ ,tasmai dadyāmahaṁ yo me prabrūyātkeśavārjunau.
english: And after killing both Kṛṣṇas (Keśava and Arjuna), I would give all their wealth entirely to him who would tell me their whereabouts.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 27 Verse 12. And after killing both Kṛṣṇas (Keśava and Arjuna), I would give all their wealth entirely to him who would tell me their whereabouts.

sanskrit: एता वाचः सुबहुशः कर्ण उच्चारयन्युधि ।दध्मौ सागरसंभूतं सुस्वनं शङ्खमुत्तमम् ॥१३॥
transliteration: etā vācaḥ subahuśaḥ karṇa uccārayanyudhi ,dadhmau sāgarasaṁbhūtaṁ susvanaṁ śaṅkhamuttamam.
english: Repeatedly uttering these words in battle, Karna blew his excellent, loud-sounding conch, which was born from the ocean.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 27 Verse 13. Repeatedly uttering these words in battle, Karna blew his excellent, loud-sounding conch, which was born from the ocean.

sanskrit: ता वाचः सूतपुत्रस्य तथा युक्ता निशम्य तु ।दुर्योधनो महाराज प्रहृष्टः सानुगोऽभवत् ॥१४॥
transliteration: tā vācaḥ sūtaputrasya tathā yuktā niśamya tu ,duryodhano mahārāja prahṛṣṭaḥ sānugo'bhavat.
english: O great king, having heard those appropriate words of Karna, the son of Sūta, Duryodhana became greatly delighted along with his followers.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 27 Verse 14. O great king, having heard those appropriate words of Karna, the son of Sūta, Duryodhana became greatly delighted along with his followers.

sanskrit: ततो दुन्दुभिनिर्घोषो मृदङ्गानां च सर्वशः ।सिंहनादः सवादित्रः कुञ्जराणां च निस्वनः ॥१५॥
transliteration: tato dundubhinirghoṣo mṛdaṅgānāṁ ca sarvaśaḥ ,siṁhanādaḥ savāditraḥ kuñjarāṇāṁ ca nisvanaḥ.
english: Then, from all sides, there arose the loud sound of large drums and mṛdaṅgas, a roaring battle cry accompanied by musical instruments, and the trumpeting of elephants.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 27 Verse 15. Then, from all sides, there arose the loud sound of large drums and mṛdaṅgas, a roaring battle cry accompanied by musical instruments, and the trumpeting of elephants.

sanskrit: प्रादुरासीत्तदा राजंस्त्वत्सैन्ये भरतर्षभ ।योधानां संप्रहृष्टानां तथा समभवत्स्वनः ॥१६॥
transliteration: prādurāsīttadā rājaṁstvatsainye bharatarṣabha ,yodhānāṁ saṁprahṛṣṭānāṁ tathā samabhavatsvanaḥ.
english: O King, best of the Bharatas, at that moment, in your army, such a roar arose from the exceedingly delighted warriors.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 27 Verse 16. O King, best of the Bharatas, at that moment, in your army, such a roar arose from the exceedingly delighted warriors.

sanskrit: तथा प्रहृष्टे सैन्ये तु प्लवमानं महारथम् ।विकत्थमानं समरे राधेयमरिकर्शनम् ।मद्रराजः प्रहस्येदं वचनं प्रत्यभाषत ॥१७॥
transliteration: tathā prahṛṣṭe sainye tu plavamānaṁ mahāratham ,vikatthamānaṁ samare rādheyamarikarśanam ,madrarājaḥ prahasyedaṁ vacanaṁ pratyabhāṣata.
english: But while the army was thus greatly delighted, the King of Madras (Śalya) laughed loudly and spoke these words in reply to Karna (the son of Rādhā), the tormentor of enemies, who was boasting and leaping about in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 27 Verse 17. But while the army was thus greatly delighted, the King of Madras (Śalya) laughed loudly and spoke these words in reply to Karna (the son of Rādhā), the tormentor of enemies, who was boasting and leaping about in battle.

sanskrit: मा सूतपुत्र मानेन सौवर्णं हस्तिषड्गवम् ।प्रयच्छ पुरुषायाद्य द्रक्ष्यसि त्वं धनंजयम् ॥१८॥
transliteration: mā sūtaputra mānena sauvarṇaṁ hastiṣaḍgavam ,prayaccha puruṣāyādya drakṣyasi tvaṁ dhanaṁjayam.
english: O son of a charioteer, do not, out of pride, offer (this) golden elephant-six-cow (weight of gold) to any man today, for you will (soon) see Dhananjaya (Arjuna).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 27 Verse 18. O son of a charioteer, do not, out of pride, offer (this) golden elephant-six-cow (weight of gold) to any man today, for you will (soon) see Dhananjaya (Arjuna).

sanskrit: बाल्यादिव त्वं त्यजसि वसु वैश्रवणो यथा ।अयत्नेनैव राधेय द्रष्टास्यद्य धनंजयम् ॥१९॥
transliteration: bālyādiva tvaṁ tyajasi vasu vaiśravaṇo yathā ,ayatnenaiva rādheya draṣṭāsyadya dhanaṁjayam.
english: You renounce wealth from childhood, just like Kubera (Vaiśravaṇa). O son of Rādhā (Rādheya), today you will effortlessly see Arjuna (Dhanañjaya).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 27 Verse 19. You renounce wealth from childhood, just like Kubera (Vaiśravaṇa). O son of Rādhā (Rādheya), today you will effortlessly see Arjuna (Dhanañjaya).

sanskrit: परासृजसि मिथ्या किं किं च त्वं बहु मूढवत् ।अपात्रदाने ये दोषास्तान्मोहान्नावबुध्यसे ॥२०॥
transliteration: parāsṛjasi mithyā kiṁ kiṁ ca tvaṁ bahu mūḍhavat ,apātradāne ye doṣāstānmohānnāvabudhyase.
english: Why do you, like a fool, create so many falsehoods? Due to delusion (moha), you do not understand the faults (doṣa) that arise from giving (dāna) to an unworthy person.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 27 Verse 20. Why do you, like a fool, create so many falsehoods? Due to delusion (moha), you do not understand the faults (doṣa) that arise from giving (dāna) to an unworthy person.

sanskrit: यत्प्रवेदयसे वित्तं बहुत्वेन खलु त्वया ।शक्यं बहुविधैर्यज्ञैर्यष्टुं सूत यजस्व तैः ॥२१॥
transliteration: yatpravedayase vittaṁ bahutvena khalu tvayā ,śakyaṁ bahuvidhairyajñairyaṣṭuṁ sūta yajasva taiḥ.
english: O Sūta (Karna), the great wealth you announce can indeed be used by you to perform many kinds of sacrificial rituals (yajña); therefore, perform them (sacrifices) with that (wealth)!
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 27 Verse 21. O Sūta (Karna), the great wealth you announce can indeed be used by you to perform many kinds of sacrificial rituals (yajña); therefore, perform them (sacrifices) with that (wealth)!

sanskrit: यच्च प्रार्थयसे हन्तुं कृष्णौ मोहान्मृषैव तत् ।न हि शुश्रुम संमर्दे क्रोष्ट्रा सिंहौ निपातितौ ॥२२॥
transliteration: yacca prārthayase hantuṁ kṛṣṇau mohānmṛṣaiva tat ,na hi śuśruma saṁmarde kroṣṭrā siṁhau nipātitau.
english: And that which you desire, out of delusion (moha), to kill the two Krishnas (Kṛṣṇau) - that is certainly false. Indeed, we have never heard of two lions (siṃha) being felled by a jackal (kroṣṭṛ) in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 27 Verse 22. And that which you desire, out of delusion (moha), to kill the two Krishnas (Kṛṣṇau) - that is certainly false. Indeed, we have never heard of two lions (siṃha) being felled by a jackal (kroṣṭṛ) in battle.

sanskrit: अप्रार्थितं प्रार्थयसे सुहृदो न हि सन्ति ते ।ये त्वां न वारयन्त्याशु प्रपतन्तं हुताशने ॥२३॥
transliteration: aprārthitaṁ prārthayase suhṛdo na hi santi te ,ye tvāṁ na vārayantyāśu prapatantaṁ hutāśane.
english: You desire something undesirable. Indeed, those are not your friends who do not quickly restrain you as you are about to fall into a fire.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 27 Verse 23. You desire something undesirable. Indeed, those are not your friends who do not quickly restrain you as you are about to fall into a fire.

sanskrit: कालकार्यं न जानीषे कालपक्वोऽस्यसंशयम् ।बह्वबद्धमकर्णीयं को हि ब्रूयाज्जिजीविषुः ॥२४॥
transliteration: kālakāryaṁ na jānīṣe kālapakvo'syasaṁśayam ,bahvabaddhamakarṇīyaṁ ko hi brūyājjijīviṣuḥ.
english: You do not understand the appropriate time for action. Without a doubt, your time is ripe (for calamity). Indeed, who, desiring to live, would utter such a multitude of foolish and unlistenable words?
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 27 Verse 24. You do not understand the appropriate time for action. Without a doubt, your time is ripe (for calamity). Indeed, who, desiring to live, would utter such a multitude of foolish and unlistenable words?

sanskrit: समुद्रतरणं दोर्भ्यां कण्ठे बद्ध्वा यथा शिलाम् ।गिर्यग्राद्वा निपतनं तादृक्तव चिकीर्षितम् ॥२५॥
transliteration: samudrataraṇaṁ dorbhyāṁ kaṇṭhe baddhvā yathā śilām ,giryagrādvā nipatanaṁ tādṛktava cikīrṣitam.
english: Your intention (cīkīrṣita) is like trying to cross the ocean by merely using your arms while a stone is tied to your neck, or like falling from a mountain peak.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 27 Verse 25. Your intention (cīkīrṣita) is like trying to cross the ocean by merely using your arms while a stone is tied to your neck, or like falling from a mountain peak.

sanskrit: सहितः सर्वयोधैस्त्वं व्यूढानीकैः सुरक्षितः ।धनंजयेन युध्यस्व श्रेयश्चेत्प्राप्तुमिच्छसि ॥२६॥
transliteration: sahitaḥ sarvayodhaistvaṁ vyūḍhānīkaiḥ surakṣitaḥ ,dhanaṁjayena yudhyasva śreyaścetprāptumicchasi.
english: If you wish to obtain what is auspicious (śreyas) or good fortune, you should fight with Dhanañjaya (Arjuna), accompanied by all your warriors and protected by your well-arrayed armies.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 27 Verse 26. If you wish to obtain what is auspicious (śreyas) or good fortune, you should fight with Dhanañjaya (Arjuna), accompanied by all your warriors and protected by your well-arrayed armies.

sanskrit: हितार्थं धार्तराष्ट्रस्य ब्रवीमि त्वा न हिंसया ।श्रद्धत्स्वैतन्मया प्रोक्तं यदि तेऽस्ति जिजीविषा ॥२७॥
transliteration: hitārthaṁ dhārtarāṣṭrasya bravīmi tvā na hiṁsayā ,śraddhatsvaitanmayā proktaṁ yadi te'sti jijīviṣā.
english: I speak to you for the welfare of Dhṛtarāṣṭra's son, not with any intention of harming you. If you have a desire to live, then believe this which has been said by me.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 27 Verse 27. I speak to you for the welfare of Dhṛtarāṣṭra's son, not with any intention of harming you. If you have a desire to live, then believe this which has been said by me.

sanskrit: कर्ण उवाच ।स्ववीर्येऽहं पराश्वस्य प्रार्थयाम्यर्जुनं रणे ।त्वं तु मित्रमुखः शत्रुर्मां भीषयितुमिच्छसि ॥२८॥
transliteration: karṇa uvāca ,svavīrye'haṁ parāśvasya prārthayāmyarjunaṁ raṇe ,tvaṁ tu mitramukhaḥ śatrurmāṁ bhīṣayitumicchasi.
english: Karṇa said: 'Relying on my own valor, I challenge Arjuna in battle. But you, an enemy with a friendly face, wish to frighten me.'
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 27 Verse 28. Karṇa said: 'Relying on my own valor, I challenge Arjuna in battle. But you, an enemy with a friendly face, wish to frighten me.'

sanskrit: न मामस्मादभिप्रायात्कश्चिदद्य निवर्तयेत् ।अपीन्द्रो वज्रमुद्यम्य किं नु मर्त्यः करिष्यति ॥२९॥
transliteration: na māmasmādabhiprāyātkaścidadya nivartayet ,apīndro vajramudyamya kiṁ nu martyaḥ kariṣyati.
english: No one can turn me away from this resolve today. Even if Indra raises his thunderbolt, what can a mere mortal do?
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 27 Verse 29. No one can turn me away from this resolve today. Even if Indra raises his thunderbolt, what can a mere mortal do?

sanskrit: संजय उवाच ।इति कर्णस्य वाक्यान्ते शल्यः प्राहोत्तरं वचः ।चुकोपयिषुरत्यर्थं कर्णं मद्रेश्वरः पुनः ॥३०॥
transliteration: saṁjaya uvāca ,iti karṇasya vākyānte śalyaḥ prāhottaraṁ vacaḥ ,cukopayiṣuratyarthaṁ karṇaṁ madreśvaraḥ punaḥ.
english: Saṃjaya said: 'At the end of Karṇa's speech, Śalya, the lord of the Madras, spoke another word (or "replied"), intending to provoke Karṇa exceedingly, once again.'
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 27 Verse 30. Saṃjaya said: 'At the end of Karṇa's speech, Śalya, the lord of the Madras, spoke another word (or "replied"), intending to provoke Karṇa exceedingly, once again.'

sanskrit: यदा वै त्वां फल्गुनवेगनुन्ना ज्याचोदिता हस्तवता विसृष्टाः ।अन्वेतारः कङ्कपत्राः शिताग्रास्तदा तप्स्यस्यर्जुनस्याभियोगात् ॥३१॥
transliteration: yadā vai tvāṁ phalgunaveganunnā; jyācoditā hastavatā visṛṣṭāḥ ,anvetāraḥ kaṅkapatrāḥ śitāgrā;stadā tapsyasyarjunasyābhiyogāt.
english: Indeed, when those sharp-pointed arrows, feathered with heron plumes, released by the skillful archer, impelled by the bowstring and driven by the force of Arjuna (phalguna), pursue you, then you will surely repent due to Arjuna's assault.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 27 Verse 31. Indeed, when those sharp-pointed arrows, feathered with heron plumes, released by the skillful archer, impelled by the bowstring and driven by the force of Arjuna (phalguna), pursue you, then you will surely repent due to Arjuna's assault.

sanskrit: यदा दिव्यं धनुरादाय पार्थः प्रभासयन्पृतनां सव्यसाची ।त्वामर्दयेत निशितैः पृषत्कैस्तदा पश्चात्तप्स्यसे सूतपुत्र ॥३२॥
transliteration: yadā divyaṁ dhanurādāya pārthaḥ; prabhāsayanpṛtanāṁ savyasācī ,tvāmardayeta niśitaiḥ pṛṣatkai;stadā paścāttapsyase sūtaputra.
english: O son of a charioteer (sūtaputra), when Pārtha (Arjuna), the ambidextrous (savyasācī), takes up his divine bow and, illuminating the army, afflicts you with his sharp arrows, then you will surely repent later.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 27 Verse 32. O son of a charioteer (sūtaputra), when Pārtha (Arjuna), the ambidextrous (savyasācī), takes up his divine bow and, illuminating the army, afflicts you with his sharp arrows, then you will surely repent later.

sanskrit: बालश्चन्द्रं मातुरङ्के शयानो यथा कश्चित्प्रार्थयतेऽपहर्तुम् ।तद्वन्मोहाद्यतमानो रथस्थस्त्वं प्रार्थयस्यर्जुनमद्य जेतुम् ॥३३॥
transliteration: bālaścandraṁ māturaṅke śayāno; yathā kaścitprārthayate'pahartum ,tadvanmohādyatamāno rathastha;stvaṁ prārthayasyarjunamadya jetum.
english: Just as a child lying in his mother's lap wishes to seize the moon, so too you, standing in your chariot and striving out of delusion (moha), wish to conquer Arjuna today.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 27 Verse 33. Just as a child lying in his mother's lap wishes to seize the moon, so too you, standing in your chariot and striving out of delusion (moha), wish to conquer Arjuna today.

sanskrit: त्रिशूलमाश्लिष्य सुतीक्ष्णधारं सर्वाणि गात्राणि निघर्षसि त्वम् ।सुतीक्ष्णधारोपमकर्मणा त्वं युयुत्ससे योऽर्जुनेनाद्य कर्ण ॥३४॥
transliteration: triśūlamāśliṣya sutīkṣṇadhāraṁ; sarvāṇi gātrāṇi nigharṣasi tvam ,sutīkṣṇadhāropamakarmaṇā tvaṁ; yuyutsase yo'rjunenādya karṇa.
english: O Karna, you who wish to fight Arjuna today, it is as if you are embracing a very sharp-edged trident and rubbing all your limbs against it. By engaging in such an act, which is comparable to a very sharp edge, you seek to battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 27 Verse 34. O Karna, you who wish to fight Arjuna today, it is as if you are embracing a very sharp-edged trident and rubbing all your limbs against it. By engaging in such an act, which is comparable to a very sharp edge, you seek to battle.

sanskrit: सिद्धं सिंहं केसरिणं बृहन्तं बालो मूढः क्षुद्रमृगस्तरस्वी ।समाह्वयेत्तद्वदेतत्तवाद्य समाह्वानं सूतपुत्रार्जुनस्य ॥३५॥
transliteration: siddhaṁ siṁhaṁ kesariṇaṁ bṛhantaṁ; bālo mūḍhaḥ kṣudramṛgastarasvī ,samāhvayettadvadetattavādya; samāhvānaṁ sūtaputrārjunasya.
english: Just as a foolish, impetuous, insignificant creature (or child) would challenge a powerful, great, maned lion, so too is this challenge of yours today, O son of Sūta, truly Arjuna's.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 27 Verse 35. Just as a foolish, impetuous, insignificant creature (or child) would challenge a powerful, great, maned lion, so too is this challenge of yours today, O son of Sūta, truly Arjuna's.

sanskrit: मा सूतपुत्राह्वय राजपुत्रं महावीर्यं केसरिणं यथैव ।वने सृगालः पिशितस्य तृप्तो मा पार्थमासाद्य विनङ्क्ष्यसि त्वम् ॥३६॥
transliteration: mā sūtaputrāhvaya rājaputraṁ; mahāvīryaṁ kesariṇaṁ yathaiva ,vane sṛgālaḥ piśitasya tṛpto; mā pārthamāsādya vinaṅkṣyasi tvam.
english: O son of Sūta, do not challenge the prince (Arjuna), who is like a mighty, maned lion. Just as a jackal in the forest, sated with flesh, would be destroyed upon approaching Arjuna (Pārtha), so too will you be destroyed!
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 27 Verse 36. O son of Sūta, do not challenge the prince (Arjuna), who is like a mighty, maned lion. Just as a jackal in the forest, sated with flesh, would be destroyed upon approaching Arjuna (Pārtha), so too will you be destroyed!

sanskrit: ईषादन्तं महानागं प्रभिन्नकरटामुखम् ।शशकाह्वयसे युद्धे कर्ण पार्थं धनंजयम् ॥३७॥
transliteration: īṣādantaṁ mahānāgaṁ prabhinnakaraṭāmukham ,śaśakāhvayase yuddhe karṇa pārthaṁ dhanaṁjayam.
english: O Karṇa, like a hare, you challenge Pārtha (Arjuna) Dhanaṃjaya in battle, who is like a mighty elephant whose temples and mouth stream with ichor and whose tusks are like plowshares.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 27 Verse 37. O Karṇa, like a hare, you challenge Pārtha (Arjuna) Dhanaṃjaya in battle, who is like a mighty elephant whose temples and mouth stream with ichor and whose tusks are like plowshares.

sanskrit: बिलस्थं कृष्णसर्पं त्वं बाल्यात्काष्ठेन विध्यसि ।महाविषं पूर्णकोशं यत्पार्थं योद्धुमिच्छसि ॥३८॥
transliteration: bilasthaṁ kṛṣṇasarpaṁ tvaṁ bālyātkāṣṭhena vidhyasi ,mahāviṣaṁ pūrṇakośaṁ yatpārthaṁ yoddhumicchasi.
english: Out of foolishness (bālyāt), you (tvam) strike with a stick (kāṣṭhena) a black serpent (kṛṣṇasarpam) that resides in a hole (bilastham), a serpent that is exceedingly venomous (mahāviṣam) and full of poison (pūrṇakośam). This is why you wish to fight Pārtha (Arjuna).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 27 Verse 38. Out of foolishness (bālyāt), you (tvam) strike with a stick (kāṣṭhena) a black serpent (kṛṣṇasarpam) that resides in a hole (bilastham), a serpent that is exceedingly venomous (mahāviṣam) and full of poison (pūrṇakośam). This is why you wish to fight Pārtha (Arjuna).

sanskrit: सिंहं केसरिणं क्रुद्धमतिक्रम्याभिनर्दसि ।सृगाल इव मूढत्वान्नृसिंहं कर्ण पाण्डवम् ॥३९॥
transliteration: siṁhaṁ kesariṇaṁ kruddhamatikramyābhinardasi ,sṛgāla iva mūḍhatvānnṛsiṁhaṁ karṇa pāṇḍavam.
english: O Karṇa, out of foolishness, you roar against the man-lion Pāṇḍava (Arjuna), just like a jackal, even while you surpass (in daring) a furious, maned lion.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 27 Verse 39. O Karṇa, out of foolishness, you roar against the man-lion Pāṇḍava (Arjuna), just like a jackal, even while you surpass (in daring) a furious, maned lion.

sanskrit: सुपर्णं पतगश्रेष्ठं वैनतेयं तरस्विनम् ।लट्वेवाह्वयसे पाते कर्ण पार्थं धनंजयम् ॥४०॥
transliteration: suparṇaṁ patagaśreṣṭhaṁ vainateyaṁ tarasvinam ,laṭvevāhvayase pāte karṇa pārthaṁ dhanaṁjayam.
english: O Karṇa, you challenge Pārtha Dhanaṃjaya, who is like Suparṇa, the best of birds, the mighty Vainateya (Garuda), just as a quail challenges (such a bird) at the moment of its own fall.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 27 Verse 40. O Karṇa, you challenge Pārtha Dhanaṃjaya, who is like Suparṇa, the best of birds, the mighty Vainateya (Garuda), just as a quail challenges (such a bird) at the moment of its own fall.

sanskrit: सर्वाम्भोनिलयं भीममूर्मिमन्तं झषायुतम् ।चन्द्रोदये विवर्तन्तमप्लवः संतितीर्षसि ॥४१॥
transliteration: sarvāmbhonilayaṁ bhīmamūrmimantaṁ jhaṣāyutam ,candrodaye vivartantamaplavaḥ saṁtitīrṣasi.
english: You, being without a boat, wish to cross the terrible ocean, which is the reservoir of all waters, full of waves, teeming with fish, and agitated by the moon's rise.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 27 Verse 41. You, being without a boat, wish to cross the terrible ocean, which is the reservoir of all waters, full of waves, teeming with fish, and agitated by the moon's rise.

sanskrit: ऋषभं दुन्दुभिग्रीवं तीक्ष्णशृङ्गं प्रहारिणम् ।वत्स आह्वयसे युद्धे कर्ण पार्थं धनंजयम् ॥४२॥
transliteration: ṛṣabhaṁ dundubhigrīvaṁ tīkṣṇaśṛṅgaṁ prahāriṇam ,vatsa āhvayase yuddhe karṇa pārthaṁ dhanaṁjayam.
english: O Karṇa, you, like a calf (vatsa), challenge Pārtha Dhanaṃjaya in battle, who is like a bull (ṛṣabha) with a powerful drum-like neck, sharp horns, and a fierce striking ability.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 27 Verse 42. O Karṇa, you, like a calf (vatsa), challenge Pārtha Dhanaṃjaya in battle, who is like a bull (ṛṣabha) with a powerful drum-like neck, sharp horns, and a fierce striking ability.

sanskrit: महाघोषं महामेघं दर्दुरः प्रतिनर्दसि ।कामतोयप्रदं लोके नरपर्जन्यमर्जुनम् ॥४३॥
transliteration: mahāghoṣaṁ mahāmeghaṁ darduraḥ pratinardasi ,kāmatoyapradaṁ loke naraparjanyamarjunam.
english: Just as a frog croaks in imitation of a great, thundering cloud, which provides desired water to the world, so do you croak in defiance of Arjuna, who is a man like a rain-cloud.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 27 Verse 43. Just as a frog croaks in imitation of a great, thundering cloud, which provides desired water to the world, so do you croak in defiance of Arjuna, who is a man like a rain-cloud.

sanskrit: यथा च स्वगृहस्थः श्वा व्याघ्रं वनगतं भषेत् ।तथा त्वं भषसे कर्ण नरव्याघ्रं धनंजयम् ॥४४॥
transliteration: yathā ca svagṛhasthaḥ śvā vyāghraṁ vanagataṁ bhaṣet ,tathā tvaṁ bhaṣase karṇa naravyāghraṁ dhanaṁjayam.
english: Just as a dog, confined to its own house, might bark at a tiger located in the forest, so too, O Karna, do you bark at Dhananjaya (Arjuna), who is a tiger among men.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 27 Verse 44. Just as a dog, confined to its own house, might bark at a tiger located in the forest, so too, O Karna, do you bark at Dhananjaya (Arjuna), who is a tiger among men.

sanskrit: सृगालोऽपि वने कर्ण शशैः परिवृतो वसन् ।मन्यते सिंहमात्मानं यावत्सिंहं न पश्यति ॥४५॥
transliteration: sṛgālo'pi vane karṇa śaśaiḥ parivṛto vasan ,manyate siṁhamātmānaṁ yāvatsiṁhaṁ na paśyati.
english: O Karna, even a jackal living in the forest, surrounded by rabbits, considers its own self (ātman) to be a lion, as long as it does not see a (real) lion.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 27 Verse 45. O Karna, even a jackal living in the forest, surrounded by rabbits, considers its own self (ātman) to be a lion, as long as it does not see a (real) lion.

sanskrit: तथा त्वमपि राधेय सिंहमात्मानमिच्छसि ।अपश्यञ्शत्रुदमनं नरव्याघ्रं धनंजयम् ॥४६॥
transliteration: tathā tvamapi rādheya siṁhamātmānamicchasi ,apaśyañśatrudamanaṁ naravyāghraṁ dhanaṁjayam.
english: Similarly, O son of Radha (Karna), you also desire your own self (ātman) to be a lion, without seeing Dhananjaya (Arjuna), the subduer of enemies, who is a tiger among men.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 27 Verse 46. Similarly, O son of Radha (Karna), you also desire your own self (ātman) to be a lion, without seeing Dhananjaya (Arjuna), the subduer of enemies, who is a tiger among men.

sanskrit: व्याघ्रं त्वं मन्यसेऽऽत्मानं यावत्कृष्णौ न पश्यसि ।समास्थितावेकरथे सूर्याचन्द्रमसाविव ॥४७॥
transliteration: vyāghraṁ tvaṁ manyase''tmānaṁ yāvatkṛṣṇau na paśyasi ,samāsthitāvekarathe sūryācandramasāviva.
english: As long as you do not see the two Krishnas (Arjuna and Vāsudeva), seated together in a single chariot like the sun and the moon, you consider yourself (ātman) a tiger.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 27 Verse 47. As long as you do not see the two Krishnas (Arjuna and Vāsudeva), seated together in a single chariot like the sun and the moon, you consider yourself (ātman) a tiger.

sanskrit: यावद्गाण्डीवनिर्घोषं न शृणोषि महाहवे ।तावदेव त्वया कर्ण शक्यं वक्तुं यथेच्छसि ॥४८॥
transliteration: yāvadgāṇḍīvanirghoṣaṁ na śṛṇoṣi mahāhave ,tāvadeva tvayā karṇa śakyaṁ vaktuṁ yathecchasi.
english: Karna, as long as you do not hear the roar of the Gāṇḍīva bow in the great battle, only then can you speak whatever you wish.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 27 Verse 48. Karna, as long as you do not hear the roar of the Gāṇḍīva bow in the great battle, only then can you speak whatever you wish.

sanskrit: रथशब्दधनुःशब्दैर्नादयन्तं दिशो दश ।नर्दन्तमिव शार्दूलं दृष्ट्वा क्रोष्टा भविष्यसि ॥४९॥
transliteration: rathaśabdadhanuḥśabdairnādayantaṁ diśo daśa ,nardantamiva śārdūlaṁ dṛṣṭvā kroṣṭā bhaviṣyasi.
english: When you see him making the ten directions resound with the sounds of chariots and bows, roaring like a tiger, you will become a mere jackal.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 27 Verse 49. When you see him making the ten directions resound with the sounds of chariots and bows, roaring like a tiger, you will become a mere jackal.

sanskrit: नित्यमेव सृगालस्त्वं नित्यं सिंहो धनंजयः ।वीरप्रद्वेषणान्मूढ नित्यं क्रोष्टेव लक्ष्यसे ॥५०॥
transliteration: nityameva sṛgālastvaṁ nityaṁ siṁho dhanaṁjayaḥ ,vīrapradveṣaṇānmūḍha nityaṁ kroṣṭeva lakṣyase.
english: You are always a jackal, and Dhananjaya (Arjuna) is always a lion. O foolish one, due to your hatred of heroes, you always appear like a jackal.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 27 Verse 50. You are always a jackal, and Dhananjaya (Arjuna) is always a lion. O foolish one, due to your hatred of heroes, you always appear like a jackal.

sanskrit: यथाखुः स्याद्बिडालश्च श्वा व्याघ्रश्च बलाबले ।यथा सृगालः सिंहश्च यथा च शशकुञ्जरौ ॥५१॥
transliteration: yathākhuḥ syādbiḍālaśca śvā vyāghraśca balābale ,yathā sṛgālaḥ siṁhaśca yathā ca śaśakuñjarau.
english: Just as a mouse is to a cat, a dog to a tiger in terms of relative strength, and just as a jackal is to a lion, and a rabbit to an elephant.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 27 Verse 51. Just as a mouse is to a cat, a dog to a tiger in terms of relative strength, and just as a jackal is to a lion, and a rabbit to an elephant.

sanskrit: यथानृतं च सत्यं च यथा चापि विषामृते ।तथा त्वमपि पार्थश्च प्रख्यातावात्मकर्मभिः ॥५२॥
transliteration: yathānṛtaṁ ca satyaṁ ca yathā cāpi viṣāmṛte ,tathā tvamapi pārthaśca prakhyātāvātmakarmabhiḥ.
english: Just as falsehood and truth, and just as poison and nectar are compared, so too are you, Karna, and you, Partha (Arjuna), renowned for your own individual actions (karma).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 27 Verse 52. Just as falsehood and truth, and just as poison and nectar are compared, so too are you, Karna, and you, Partha (Arjuna), renowned for your own individual actions (karma).

sanskrit: संजय उवाच ।अधिक्षिप्तस्तु राधेयः शल्येनामिततेजसा ।शल्यमाह सुसंक्रुद्धो वाक्शल्यमवधारयन् ॥५३॥
transliteration: saṁjaya uvāca ,adhikṣiptastu rādheyaḥ śalyenāmitatejasā ,śalyamāha susaṁkruddho vākśalyamavadhārayan.
english: Sañjaya said: But Karna (Rādheya), having been insulted by Śalya, whose prowess was immeasurable, became extremely enraged. He spoke to Śalya, taking his words as piercing darts (śalya).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 27 Verse 53. Sañjaya said: But Karna (Rādheya), having been insulted by Śalya, whose prowess was immeasurable, became extremely enraged. He spoke to Śalya, taking his words as piercing darts (śalya).

sanskrit: गुणान्गुणवतः शल्य गुणवान्वेत्ति नागुणः ।त्वं तु नित्यं गुणैर्हीनः किं ज्ञास्यस्यगुणो गुणान् ॥५४॥
transliteration: guṇānguṇavataḥ śalya guṇavānvetti nāguṇaḥ ,tvaṁ tu nityaṁ guṇairhīnaḥ kiṁ jñāsyasyaguṇo guṇān.
english: O Śalya, a person possessing merits recognizes the merits of another meritorious person, not one who lacks them. But you are always devoid of good qualities; how will you, being without qualities, ever comprehend qualities?
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 27 Verse 54. O Śalya, a person possessing merits recognizes the merits of another meritorious person, not one who lacks them. But you are always devoid of good qualities; how will you, being without qualities, ever comprehend qualities?

sanskrit: अर्जुनस्य महास्त्राणि क्रोधं वीर्यं धनुः शरान् ।अहं शल्याभिजानामि न त्वं जानासि तत्तथा ॥५५॥
transliteration: arjunasya mahāstrāṇi krodhaṁ vīryaṁ dhanuḥ śarān ,ahaṁ śalyābhijānāmi na tvaṁ jānāsi tattathā.
english: O Śalya, I know very well Arjuna's great weapons, his wrath, his valor, his bow, and his arrows. You, however, do not know them in that manner.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 27 Verse 55. O Śalya, I know very well Arjuna's great weapons, his wrath, his valor, his bow, and his arrows. You, however, do not know them in that manner.

sanskrit: एवमेवात्मनो वीर्यमहं वीर्यं च पाण्डवे ।जानन्नेवाह्वये युद्धे शल्य नाग्निं पतंगवत् ॥५६॥
transliteration: evamevātmano vīryamahaṁ vīryaṁ ca pāṇḍave ,jānannevāhvaye yuddhe śalya nāgniṁ pataṁgavat.
english: O Śalya, knowing full well my own prowess (vīrya) and the prowess (vīrya) of the son of Pāṇḍu (Arjuna), I challenge him in battle, not like a moth to a fire.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 27 Verse 56. O Śalya, knowing full well my own prowess (vīrya) and the prowess (vīrya) of the son of Pāṇḍu (Arjuna), I challenge him in battle, not like a moth to a fire.

sanskrit: अस्ति चायमिषुः शल्य सुपुङ्खो रक्तभोजनः ।एकतूणीशयः पत्री सुधौतः समलंकृतः ॥५७॥
transliteration: asti cāyamiṣuḥ śalya supuṅkho raktabhojanaḥ ,ekatūṇīśayaḥ patrī sudhautaḥ samalaṁkṛtaḥ.
english: And O Śalya, this particular arrow exists: it is well-feathered, blood-devouring, housed in a single quiver, winged, thoroughly polished, and perfectly adorned.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 27 Verse 57. And O Śalya, this particular arrow exists: it is well-feathered, blood-devouring, housed in a single quiver, winged, thoroughly polished, and perfectly adorned.

sanskrit: शेते चन्दनपूर्णेन पूजितो बहुलाः समाः ।आहेयो विषवानुग्रो नराश्वद्विपसंघहा ॥५८॥
transliteration: śete candanapūrṇena pūjito bahulāḥ samāḥ ,āheyo viṣavānugro narāśvadvipasaṁghahā.
english: It lies, honored for many years with sandalwood paste. It is a formidable, venomous serpent-like (āheya) weapon, capable of destroying armies of men, horses, and elephants.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 27 Verse 58. It lies, honored for many years with sandalwood paste. It is a formidable, venomous serpent-like (āheya) weapon, capable of destroying armies of men, horses, and elephants.

sanskrit: एकवीरो महारौद्रस्तनुत्रास्थिविदारणः ।निर्भिन्द्यां येन रुष्टोऽहमपि मेरुं महागिरिम् ॥५९॥
transliteration: ekavīro mahāraudrastanutrāsthividāraṇaḥ ,nirbhindyāṁ yena ruṣṭo'hamapi meruṁ mahāgirim.
english: I am a singular hero, exceedingly dreadful, one who tears apart armor and bones. When enraged, I could shatter even the great Mount Meru with *that* (weapon).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 27 Verse 59. I am a singular hero, exceedingly dreadful, one who tears apart armor and bones. When enraged, I could shatter even the great Mount Meru with *that* (weapon).

sanskrit: तमहं जातु नास्येयमन्यस्मिन्फल्गुनादृते ।कृष्णाद्वा देवकीपुत्रात्सत्यं चात्र शृणुष्व मे ॥६०॥
transliteration: tamahaṁ jātu nāsyeyamanyasminphalgunādṛte ,kṛṣṇādvā devakīputrātsatyaṁ cātra śṛṇuṣva me.
english: I would never ever release *it* (the arrow) at anyone else, except for Phalguna (Arjuna) or Krishna, the son of Devaki. Listen to my truth regarding this.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 27 Verse 60. I would never ever release *it* (the arrow) at anyone else, except for Phalguna (Arjuna) or Krishna, the son of Devaki. Listen to my truth regarding this.

sanskrit: तेनाहमिषुणा शल्य वासुदेवधनंजयौ ।योत्स्ये परमसंक्रुद्धस्तत्कर्म सदृशं मम ॥६१॥
transliteration: tenāhamiṣuṇā śalya vāsudevadhanaṁjayau ,yotsye paramasaṁkruddhastatkarma sadṛśaṁ mama.
english: O Śalya, I, utterly enraged, shall fight Vāsudeva (Krishna) and Dhanañjaya (Arjuna) with that very arrow. That action will be truly befitting of me.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 27 Verse 61. O Śalya, I, utterly enraged, shall fight Vāsudeva (Krishna) and Dhanañjaya (Arjuna) with that very arrow. That action will be truly befitting of me.

sanskrit: सर्वेषां वासुदेवानां कृष्णे लक्ष्मीः प्रतिष्ठिता ।सर्वेषां पाण्डुपुत्राणां जयः पार्थे प्रतिष्ठितः ।उभयं तत्समासाद्य कोऽतिवर्तितुमर्हति ॥६२॥
transliteration: sarveṣāṁ vāsudevānāṁ kṛṣṇe lakṣmīḥ pratiṣṭhitā ,sarveṣāṁ pāṇḍuputrāṇāṁ jayaḥ pārthe pratiṣṭhitaḥ ,ubhayaṁ tatsamāsādya ko'tivartitumarhati.
english: Prosperity (lakṣmī) for all Vāsudevas is established in Krishna. Victory for all the sons of Pāṇḍu is established in Pārtha (Arjuna). Who, having attained both of these, can possibly overcome them?
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 27 Verse 62. Prosperity (lakṣmī) for all Vāsudevas is established in Krishna. Victory for all the sons of Pāṇḍu is established in Pārtha (Arjuna). Who, having attained both of these, can possibly overcome them?

sanskrit: तावेतौ पुरुषव्याघ्रौ समेतौ स्यन्दने स्थितौ ।मामेकमभिसंयातौ सुजातं शल्य पश्य मे ॥६३॥
transliteration: tāvetau puruṣavyāghrau sametau syandane sthitau ,māmekamabhisaṁyātau sujātaṁ śalya paśya me.
english: O Śalya, behold my prowess! These two, foremost among men, united and standing in their chariot, are advancing towards me alone.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 27 Verse 63. O Śalya, behold my prowess! These two, foremost among men, united and standing in their chariot, are advancing towards me alone.

sanskrit: पितृष्वसामातुलजौ भ्रातरावपराजितौ ।मणी सूत्र इव प्रोतौ द्रष्टासि निहतौ मया ॥६४॥
transliteration: pitṛṣvasāmātulajau bhrātarāvaparājitau ,maṇī sūtra iva protau draṣṭāsi nihatau mayā.
english: You will see these two, the unconquered sons of a paternal aunt and maternal uncle (Arjuna and Krishna), like two jewels strung on a thread, struck down by me.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 27 Verse 64. You will see these two, the unconquered sons of a paternal aunt and maternal uncle (Arjuna and Krishna), like two jewels strung on a thread, struck down by me.

sanskrit: अर्जुने गाण्डिवं कृष्णे चक्रं तार्क्ष्यकपिध्वजौ ।भीरूणां त्रासजननौ शल्य हर्षकरौ मम ॥६५॥
transliteration: arjune gāṇḍivaṁ kṛṣṇe cakraṁ tārkṣyakapidhvajau ,bhīrūṇāṁ trāsajananau śalya harṣakarau mama.
english: Arjuna has the Gaṇḍīva (bow) and Krishna has the discus (cakra). Those two, with the Garuda and monkey banners, are terrifying to the fearful, O Śalya, but a cause of joy for me.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 27 Verse 65. Arjuna has the Gaṇḍīva (bow) and Krishna has the discus (cakra). Those two, with the Garuda and monkey banners, are terrifying to the fearful, O Śalya, but a cause of joy for me.

sanskrit: त्वं तु दुष्प्रकृतिर्मूढो महायुद्धेष्वकोविदः ।भयावतीर्णः संत्रासादबद्धं बहु भाषसे ॥६६॥
transliteration: tvaṁ tu duṣprakṛtirmūḍho mahāyuddheṣvakovidaḥ ,bhayāvatīrṇaḥ saṁtrāsādabaddhaṁ bahu bhāṣase.
english: But you, O Śalya, are of a wicked intrinsic nature (prakṛti), foolish, and inexperienced in great battles. Overcome by terror, you speak much incoherent nonsense out of sheer fear.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 27 Verse 66. But you, O Śalya, are of a wicked intrinsic nature (prakṛti), foolish, and inexperienced in great battles. Overcome by terror, you speak much incoherent nonsense out of sheer fear.

sanskrit: संस्तौषि त्वं तु केनापि हेतुना तौ कुदेशज ।तौ हत्वा समरे हन्ता त्वामद्धा सहबान्धवम् ॥६७॥
transliteration: saṁstauṣi tvaṁ tu kenāpi hetunā tau kudeśaja ,tau hatvā samare hantā tvāmaddhā sahabāndhavam.
english: O you, born in a bad land, you indeed praise those two for some reason. Having killed those two in battle, I shall certainly kill you along with your relatives.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 27 Verse 67. O you, born in a bad land, you indeed praise those two for some reason. Having killed those two in battle, I shall certainly kill you along with your relatives.

sanskrit: पापदेशज दुर्बुद्धे क्षुद्र क्षत्रियपांसन ।सुहृद्भूत्वा रिपुः किं मां कृष्णाभ्यां भीषयन्नसि ॥६८॥
transliteration: pāpadeśaja durbuddhe kṣudra kṣatriyapāṁsana ,suhṛdbhūtvā ripuḥ kiṁ māṁ kṛṣṇābhyāṁ bhīṣayannasi.
english: O you, born in a sinful land, O evil-minded one, O despicable one, O disgrace among kṣatriyas! Why are you, having become an enemy (after being a friend), trying to frighten me with the two Kṛṣṇas?
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 27 Verse 68. O you, born in a sinful land, O evil-minded one, O despicable one, O disgrace among kṣatriyas! Why are you, having become an enemy (after being a friend), trying to frighten me with the two Kṛṣṇas?

sanskrit: तौ वा ममाद्य हन्तारौ हन्तास्मि समरे स्थितौ ।नाहं बिभेमि कृष्णाभ्यां विजानन्नात्मनो बलम् ॥६९॥
transliteration: tau vā mamādya hantārau hantāsmi samare sthitau ,nāhaṁ bibhemi kṛṣṇābhyāṁ vijānannātmano balam.
english: Either those two will be my killers today, or I shall kill them as they stand in battle. I do not fear the two Kṛṣṇas, for I know the strength of my own self (ātman).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 27 Verse 69. Either those two will be my killers today, or I shall kill them as they stand in battle. I do not fear the two Kṛṣṇas, for I know the strength of my own self (ātman).

sanskrit: वासुदेवसहस्रं वा फल्गुनानां शतानि च ।अहमेको हनिष्यामि जोषमास्स्व कुदेशज ॥७०॥
transliteration: vāsudevasahasraṁ vā phalgunānāṁ śatāni ca ,ahameko haniṣyāmi joṣamāssva kudeśaja.
english: I alone will kill even a thousand Vāsudevas or hundreds of Phalgunas (Arjunas). O you, born in a bad land, you just stay silent!
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 27 Verse 70. I alone will kill even a thousand Vāsudevas or hundreds of Phalgunas (Arjunas). O you, born in a bad land, you just stay silent!

sanskrit: स्त्रियो बालाश्च वृद्धाश्च प्रायः क्रीडागता जनाः ।या गाथाः संप्रगायन्ति कुर्वन्तोऽध्ययनं यथा ।ता गाथाः शृणु मे शल्य मद्रकेषु दुरात्मसु ॥७१॥
transliteration: striyo bālāśca vṛddhāśca prāyaḥ krīḍāgatā janāḥ ,yā gāthāḥ saṁpragāyanti kurvanto'dhyayanaṁ yathā ,tā gāthāḥ śṛṇu me śalya madrakeṣu durātmasu.
english: Women, children, and old people, who are generally engaged in play, sing these verses (gāthāḥ) as if they were studying. O Śalya, listen to those verses from me concerning the wicked-minded Madras.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 27 Verse 71. Women, children, and old people, who are generally engaged in play, sing these verses (gāthāḥ) as if they were studying. O Śalya, listen to those verses from me concerning the wicked-minded Madras.

sanskrit: ब्राह्मणैः कथिताः पूर्वं यथावद्राजसंनिधौ ।श्रुत्वा चैकमना मूढ क्षम वा ब्रूहि वोत्तरम् ॥७२॥
transliteration: brāhmaṇaiḥ kathitāḥ pūrvaṁ yathāvadrājasaṁnidhau ,śrutvā caikamanā mūḍha kṣama vā brūhi vottaram.
english: These were formerly narrated properly by Brahmins in the presence of kings. O fool, after hearing (them), either be attentive and patiently bear (them), or speak your reply.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 27 Verse 72. These were formerly narrated properly by Brahmins in the presence of kings. O fool, after hearing (them), either be attentive and patiently bear (them), or speak your reply.

sanskrit: मित्रध्रुङ्मद्रको नित्यं यो नो द्वेष्टि स मद्रकः ।मद्रके संगतं नास्ति क्षुद्रवाक्ये नराधमे ॥७३॥
transliteration: mitradhruṅmadrako nityaṁ yo no dveṣṭi sa madrakaḥ ,madrake saṁgataṁ nāsti kṣudravākye narādhame.
english: A Madra is always a betrayer of friends. He who hates us is a Madra. There is no good association with a Madra, who is characterized by petty speech and is the lowest of men.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 27 Verse 73. A Madra is always a betrayer of friends. He who hates us is a Madra. There is no good association with a Madra, who is characterized by petty speech and is the lowest of men.

sanskrit: दुरात्मा मद्रको नित्यं नित्यं चानृतिकोऽनृजुः ।यावदन्तं हि दौरात्म्यं मद्रकेष्विति नः श्रुतम् ॥७४॥
transliteration: durātmā madrako nityaṁ nityaṁ cānṛtiko'nṛjuḥ ,yāvadantaṁ hi daurātmyaṁ madrakeṣviti naḥ śrutam.
english: A Madra is always evil-minded (durātman), and always a liar, dishonest. Indeed, it is heard by us that this wickedness (daurātmya) among the Madras persists indefinitely, to the very end.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 27 Verse 74. A Madra is always evil-minded (durātman), and always a liar, dishonest. Indeed, it is heard by us that this wickedness (daurātmya) among the Madras persists indefinitely, to the very end.

sanskrit: पिता माता च पुत्रश्च श्वश्रूश्वशुरमातुलाः ।जामाता दुहिता भ्राता नप्ता ते ते च बान्धवाः ॥७५॥
