transliteration: pitā mātā ca putraśca śvaśrūśvaśuramātulāḥ ,jāmātā duhitā bhrātā naptā te te ca bāndhavāḥ.
english: Father, mother, and son; mother-in-law, father-in-law, and maternal uncles; son-in-law, daughter, brother, and grandson - these are those relatives.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 27 Verse 75. Father, mother, and son; mother-in-law, father-in-law, and maternal uncles; son-in-law, daughter, brother, and grandson - these are those relatives.

sanskrit: वयस्याभ्यागताश्चान्ये दासीदासं च संगतम् ।पुंभिर्विमिश्रा नार्यश्च ज्ञाताज्ञाताः स्वयेच्छया ॥७६॥
transliteration: vayasyābhyāgatāścānye dāsīdāsaṁ ca saṁgatam ,puṁbhirvimiśrā nāryaśca jñātājñātāḥ svayecchayā.
english: Companions, guests, and others; male and female servants gathered together; and women, both known and unknown, mingled with men, all according to their own desire.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 27 Verse 76. Companions, guests, and others; male and female servants gathered together; and women, both known and unknown, mingled with men, all according to their own desire.

sanskrit: येषां गृहेषु शिष्टानां सक्तुमन्थाशिनां सदा ।पीत्वा सीधुं सगोमांसं नर्दन्ति च हसन्ति च ॥७७॥
transliteration: yeṣāṁ gṛheṣu śiṣṭānāṁ saktumanthāśināṁ sadā ,pītvā sīdhuṁ sagomāṁsaṁ nardanti ca hasanti ca.
english: In the homes of those cultured people who always (normally) consume flour-gruel, they (now) drink liquor with beef, and then they roar and laugh.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 27 Verse 77. In the homes of those cultured people who always (normally) consume flour-gruel, they (now) drink liquor with beef, and then they roar and laugh.

sanskrit: यानि चैवाप्यबद्धानि प्रवर्तन्ते च कामतः ।कामप्रलापिनोऽन्योन्यं तेषु धर्मः कथं भवेत् ॥७८॥
transliteration: yāni caivāpyabaddhāni pravartante ca kāmataḥ ,kāmapralāpino'nyonyaṁ teṣu dharmaḥ kathaṁ bhavet.
english: And moreover, those who are unrestrained, acting according to their desires, senselessly babbling with each other - how can there be natural law (dharma) among them?
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 27 Verse 78. And moreover, those who are unrestrained, acting according to their desires, senselessly babbling with each other - how can there be natural law (dharma) among them?

sanskrit: मद्रकेषु विलुप्तेषु प्रख्याताशुभकर्मसु ।नापि वैरं न सौहार्दं मद्रकेषु समाचरेत् ॥७९॥
transliteration: madrakeṣu vilupteṣu prakhyātāśubhakarmasu ,nāpi vairaṁ na sauhārdaṁ madrakeṣu samācaret.
english: One should neither show enmity nor friendship towards the Madrakas who are known for their inauspicious deeds, even if they are ruined.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 27 Verse 79. One should neither show enmity nor friendship towards the Madrakas who are known for their inauspicious deeds, even if they are ruined.

sanskrit: मद्रके संगतं नास्ति मद्रको हि सचापलः ।मद्रकेषु च दुःस्पर्शं शौचं गान्धारकेषु च ॥८०॥
transliteration: madrake saṁgataṁ nāsti madrako hi sacāpalaḥ ,madrakeṣu ca duḥsparśaṁ śaucaṁ gāndhārakeṣu ca.
english: One should not associate with a Madraka, for a Madraka is indeed fickle. And among the Madrakas, as well as among the Gandharakas, purity (śaucam) is difficult to find.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 27 Verse 80. One should not associate with a Madraka, for a Madraka is indeed fickle. And among the Madrakas, as well as among the Gandharakas, purity (śaucam) is difficult to find.

sanskrit: राजयाजकयाज्येन नष्टंदत्तं हविर्भवेत् ॥८१॥
transliteration: rājayājakayājyena naṣṭaṁdattaṁ havirbhavet.
english: An oblation (havis) offered becomes wasted if it is presented by a priest who serves as a royal sacrificer and is also a client (yājya) for other priests.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 27 Verse 81. An oblation (havis) offered becomes wasted if it is presented by a priest who serves as a royal sacrificer and is also a client (yājya) for other priests.

sanskrit: शूद्रसंस्कारको विप्रो यथा याति पराभवम् ।तथा ब्रह्मद्विषो नित्यं गच्छन्तीह पराभवम् ॥८२॥
transliteration: śūdrasaṁskārako vipro yathā yāti parābhavam ,tathā brahmadviṣo nityaṁ gacchantīha parābhavam.
english: Just as a Brahmin (vipra) who performs purificatory rites (saṃskāra) for Śūdras certainly meets with ruin, similarly, those who are hostile to Brahmins always meet with ruin (parābhava) in this world.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 27 Verse 82. Just as a Brahmin (vipra) who performs purificatory rites (saṃskāra) for Śūdras certainly meets with ruin, similarly, those who are hostile to Brahmins always meet with ruin (parābhava) in this world.

sanskrit: मद्रके संगतं नास्ति हतं वृश्चिकतो विषम् ।आथर्वणेन मन्त्रेण सर्वा शान्तिः कृता भवेत् ॥८३॥
transliteration: madrake saṁgataṁ nāsti hataṁ vṛścikato viṣam ,ātharvaṇena mantreṇa sarvā śāntiḥ kṛtā bhavet.
english: Madraka's involvement is irrelevant when scorpion poison is neutralized. Complete pacification (śānti) should be achieved through an Atharvaṇa sacred chant (mantra).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 27 Verse 83. Madraka's involvement is irrelevant when scorpion poison is neutralized. Complete pacification (śānti) should be achieved through an Atharvaṇa sacred chant (mantra).

sanskrit: इति वृश्चिकदष्टस्य नानाविषहतस्य च ।कुर्वन्ति भेषजं प्राज्ञाः सत्यं तच्चापि दृश्यते ।एवं विद्वञ्जोषमास्स्व शृणु चात्रोत्तरं वचः ॥८४॥
transliteration: iti vṛścikadaṣṭasya nānāviṣahatasya ca ,kurvanti bheṣajaṁ prājñāḥ satyaṁ taccāpi dṛśyate ,evaṁ vidvañjoṣamāssva śṛṇu cātrottaraṁ vacaḥ.
english: Thus, for someone bitten by a scorpion and for someone afflicted by various poisons, the wise ones prepare remedies. And truly, that [efficacy] is also observed. Therefore, O learned one, remain quiet and listen to these further words on this matter.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 27 Verse 84. Thus, for someone bitten by a scorpion and for someone afflicted by various poisons, the wise ones prepare remedies. And truly, that [efficacy] is also observed. Therefore, O learned one, remain quiet and listen to these further words on this matter.

sanskrit: वासांस्युत्सृज्य नृत्यन्ति स्त्रियो या मद्यमोहिताः ।मिथुनेऽसंयताश्चापि यथाकामचराश्च ताः ।तासां पुत्रः कथं धर्मं मद्रको वक्तुमर्हति ॥८५॥
transliteration: vāsāṁsyutsṛjya nṛtyanti striyo yā madyamohitāḥ ,mithune'saṁyatāścāpi yathākāmacarāśca tāḥ ,tāsāṁ putraḥ kathaṁ dharmaṁ madrako vaktumarhati.
english: How can Madraka, the son of those women who, intoxicated by wine, dance after casting off their clothes, and who are unrestrained in sexual union and act as they please - how can he be fit to proclaim the natural law (dharma)?
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 27 Verse 85. How can Madraka, the son of those women who, intoxicated by wine, dance after casting off their clothes, and who are unrestrained in sexual union and act as they please - how can he be fit to proclaim the natural law (dharma)?

sanskrit: यास्तिष्ठन्त्यः प्रमेहन्ति यथैवोष्ट्रीदशेरके ।तासां विभ्रष्टलज्जानां निर्लज्जानां ततस्ततः ।त्वं पुत्रस्तादृशीनां हि धर्मं वक्तुमिहेच्छसि ॥८६॥
transliteration: yāstiṣṭhantyaḥ pramehanti yathaivoṣṭrīdaśerake ,tāsāṁ vibhraṣṭalajjānāṁ nirlajjānāṁ tatastataḥ ,tvaṁ putrastādṛśīnāṁ hi dharmaṁ vaktumihecchasi.
english: You, the son of those women - who stand and urinate everywhere just like she-camels in the desert, and who are completely devoid of shame and utterly shameless - you indeed wish to declare the natural law (dharma) here?
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 27 Verse 86. You, the son of those women - who stand and urinate everywhere just like she-camels in the desert, and who are completely devoid of shame and utterly shameless - you indeed wish to declare the natural law (dharma) here?

sanskrit: सुवीरकं याच्यमाना मद्रका कषति स्फिजौ ।अदातुकामा वचनमिदं वदति दारुणम् ॥८७॥
transliteration: suvīrakaṁ yācyamānā madrakā kaṣati sphijau ,adātukāmā vacanamidaṁ vadati dāruṇam.
english: When implored for the fragrant powder (suvīraka), the Madra woman rubbed her buttocks. Unwilling to part with it, she spoke these harsh words.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 27 Verse 87. When implored for the fragrant powder (suvīraka), the Madra woman rubbed her buttocks. Unwilling to part with it, she spoke these harsh words.

sanskrit: मा मा सुवीरकं कश्चिद्याचतां दयितो मम ।पुत्रं दद्यां प्रतिपदं न तु दद्यां सुवीरकम् ॥८८॥
transliteration: mā mā suvīrakaṁ kaścidyācatāṁ dayito mama ,putraṁ dadyāṁ pratipadaṁ na tu dadyāṁ suvīrakam.
english: Oh, may no one, especially not my beloved, ever ask me for the fragrant powder (suvīraka)! I would rather give away a son than part with my suvīraka.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 27 Verse 88. Oh, may no one, especially not my beloved, ever ask me for the fragrant powder (suvīraka)! I would rather give away a son than part with my suvīraka.

sanskrit: नार्यो बृहत्यो निर्ह्रीका मद्रकाः कम्बलावृताः ।घस्मरा नष्टशौचाश्च प्राय इत्यनुशुश्रुम ॥८९॥
transliteration: nāryo bṛhatyo nirhrīkā madrakāḥ kambalāvṛtāḥ ,ghasmarā naṣṭaśaucāśca prāya ityanuśuśruma.
english: It has been said that Madra women are generally tall, immodest, wrapped in blankets, gluttonous, and impure.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 27 Verse 89. It has been said that Madra women are generally tall, immodest, wrapped in blankets, gluttonous, and impure.

sanskrit: एवमादि मयान्यैर्वा शक्यं वक्तुं भवेद्बहु ।आ केशाग्रान्नखाग्राच्च वक्तव्येषु कुवर्त्मसु ॥९०॥
transliteration: evamādi mayānyairvā śakyaṁ vaktuṁ bhavedbahu ,ā keśāgrānnakhāgrācca vaktavyeṣu kuvartmasu.
english: Much more of this kind could be said by me or by others, about these evil ways (kuvartman) that ought to be exposed, from the tips of their hair to the tips of their nails.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 27 Verse 90. Much more of this kind could be said by me or by others, about these evil ways (kuvartman) that ought to be exposed, from the tips of their hair to the tips of their nails.

sanskrit: मद्रकाः सिन्धुसौवीरा धर्मं विद्युः कथं त्विह ।पापदेशोद्भवा म्लेच्छा धर्माणामविचक्षणाः ॥९१॥
transliteration: madrakāḥ sindhusauvīrā dharmaṁ vidyuḥ kathaṁ tviha ,pāpadeśodbhavā mlecchā dharmāṇāmavicakṣaṇāḥ.
english: How can the Madras and Sindhu-Sauviras possibly understand natural law (dharma) in this situation? They are barbarians (mlecchāḥ), originating from a sinful land, and utterly lacking discernment regarding the intrinsic nature (dharma) of things.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 27 Verse 91. How can the Madras and Sindhu-Sauviras possibly understand natural law (dharma) in this situation? They are barbarians (mlecchāḥ), originating from a sinful land, and utterly lacking discernment regarding the intrinsic nature (dharma) of things.

sanskrit: एष मुख्यतमो धर्मः क्षत्रियस्येति नः श्रुतम् ।यदाजौ निहतः शेते सद्भिः समभिपूजितः ॥९२॥
transliteration: eṣa mukhyatamo dharmaḥ kṣatriyasyeti naḥ śrutam ,yadājau nihataḥ śete sadbhiḥ samabhipūjitaḥ.
english: We have heard that this is the foremost intrinsic nature (dharma) for a kshatriya (kṣatriya): when he lies slain in battle, revered and honored by the virtuous.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 27 Verse 92. We have heard that this is the foremost intrinsic nature (dharma) for a kshatriya (kṣatriya): when he lies slain in battle, revered and honored by the virtuous.

sanskrit: आयुधानां संपराये यन्मुच्येयमहं ततः ।न मे स प्रथमः कल्पो निधने स्वर्गमिच्छतः ॥९३॥
transliteration: āyudhānāṁ saṁparāye yanmucyeyamahaṁ tataḥ ,na me sa prathamaḥ kalpo nidhane svargamicchataḥ.
english: If I were to escape from that clash of weapons, that would not be my primary choice, for I am one who desires heaven (svarga) upon my death.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 27 Verse 93. If I were to escape from that clash of weapons, that would not be my primary choice, for I am one who desires heaven (svarga) upon my death.

sanskrit: सोऽहं प्रियः सखा चास्मि धार्तराष्ट्रस्य धीमतः ।तदर्थे हि मम प्राणा यच्च मे विद्यते वसु ॥९४॥
transliteration: so'haṁ priyaḥ sakhā cāsmi dhārtarāṣṭrasya dhīmataḥ ,tadarthe hi mama prāṇā yacca me vidyate vasu.
english: I am indeed that dear friend of the intelligent son of Dhritarashtra (Dhṛtarāṣṭra). My very life (prāṇāḥ) is for his sake, as is whatever wealth I possess.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 27 Verse 94. I am indeed that dear friend of the intelligent son of Dhritarashtra (Dhṛtarāṣṭra). My very life (prāṇāḥ) is for his sake, as is whatever wealth I possess.

sanskrit: व्यक्तं त्वमप्युपहितः पाण्डवैः पापदेशज ।यथा ह्यमित्रवत्सर्वं त्वमस्मासु प्रवर्तसे ॥९५॥
transliteration: vyaktaṁ tvamapyupahitaḥ pāṇḍavaiḥ pāpadeśaja ,yathā hyamitravatsarvaṁ tvamasmāsu pravartase.
english: Clearly, you too are instigated by the Pāṇḍavas, O one born in a sinful land! For indeed, you behave entirely like an enemy towards us.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 27 Verse 95. Clearly, you too are instigated by the Pāṇḍavas, O one born in a sinful land! For indeed, you behave entirely like an enemy towards us.

sanskrit: कामं न खलु शक्योऽहं त्वद्विधानां शतैरपि ।संग्रामाद्विमुखः कर्तुं धर्मज्ञ इव नास्तिकैः ॥९६॥
transliteration: kāmaṁ na khalu śakyo'haṁ tvadvidhānāṁ śatairapi ,saṁgrāmādvimukhaḥ kartuṁ dharmajña iva nāstikaiḥ.
english: Indeed, even by hundreds of those like you, I certainly cannot be made to turn away from battle, just as a knower of natural law (dharma) cannot be swayed by atheists.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 27 Verse 96. Indeed, even by hundreds of those like you, I certainly cannot be made to turn away from battle, just as a knower of natural law (dharma) cannot be swayed by atheists.

sanskrit: सारङ्ग इव घर्मार्तः कामं विलप शुष्य च ।नाहं भीषयितुं शक्यः क्षत्रवृत्ते व्यवस्थितः ॥९७॥
transliteration: sāraṅga iva gharmārtaḥ kāmaṁ vilapa śuṣya ca ,nāhaṁ bhīṣayituṁ śakyaḥ kṣatravṛtte vyavasthitaḥ.
english: Wail and dry up, indeed, like a deer afflicted by heat. I cannot be frightened, being established in the warrior's code of conduct (kṣatravṛtta).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 27 Verse 97. Wail and dry up, indeed, like a deer afflicted by heat. I cannot be frightened, being established in the warrior's code of conduct (kṣatravṛtta).

sanskrit: तनुत्यजां नृसिंहानामाहवेष्वनिवर्तिनाम् ।या गतिर्गुरुणा प्राङ्मे प्रोक्ता रामेण तां स्मर ॥९८॥
transliteration: tanutyajāṁ nṛsiṁhānāmāhaveṣvanivartinām ,yā gatirguruṇā prāṅme proktā rāmeṇa tāṁ smara.
english: Remember that destiny which was previously declared to me by the preceptor (guru) Rāma regarding those lion-like men who sacrifice their bodies and do not retreat in battles.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 27 Verse 98. Remember that destiny which was previously declared to me by the preceptor (guru) Rāma regarding those lion-like men who sacrifice their bodies and do not retreat in battles.

sanskrit: स्वेषां त्राणार्थमुद्युक्तं वधाय द्विषतामपि ।विद्धि मामास्थितं वृत्तं पौरूरवसमुत्तमम् ॥९९॥
transliteration: sveṣāṁ trāṇārthamudyuktaṁ vadhāya dviṣatāmapi ,viddhi māmāsthitaṁ vṛttaṁ paurūravasamuttamam.
english: Understand that I have adopted the excellent conduct (vṛtta) of Purūravas, being ready for the protection of my own kin and also for the annihilation of my foes.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 27 Verse 99. Understand that I have adopted the excellent conduct (vṛtta) of Purūravas, being ready for the protection of my own kin and also for the annihilation of my foes.

sanskrit: न तद्भूतं प्रपश्यामि त्रिषु लोकेषु मद्रक ।यो मामस्मादभिप्रायाद्वारयेदिति मे मतिः ॥१००॥
transliteration: na tadbhūtaṁ prapaśyāmi triṣu lokeṣu madraka ,yo māmasmādabhiprāyādvārayediti me matiḥ.
english: O Madraka, I do not see any being in the three worlds who could prevent me from this resolve; such is my conviction.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 27 Verse 100. O Madraka, I do not see any being in the three worlds who could prevent me from this resolve; such is my conviction.

sanskrit: एवं विद्वञ्जोषमास्स्व त्रासात्किं बहु भाषसे ।मा त्वा हत्वा प्रदास्यामि क्रव्याद्भ्यो मद्रकाधम ॥१०१॥
transliteration: evaṁ vidvañjoṣamāssva trāsātkiṁ bahu bhāṣase ,mā tvā hatvā pradāsyāmi kravyādbhyo madrakādhama.
english: Therefore, O wise one, remain silent! Why do you speak so much out of fear? Stop speaking, lest I kill you and give you to the carnivores, O vilest of Madrakas!
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 27 Verse 101. Therefore, O wise one, remain silent! Why do you speak so much out of fear? Stop speaking, lest I kill you and give you to the carnivores, O vilest of Madrakas!

sanskrit: मित्रप्रतीक्षया शल्य धार्तराष्ट्रस्य चोभयोः ।अपवादतितिक्षाभिस्त्रिभिरेतैर्हि जीवसि ॥१०२॥
transliteration: mitrapratīkṣayā śalya dhārtarāṣṭrasya cobhayoḥ ,apavādatitikṣābhistribhiretairhi jīvasi.
english: O Shalya, you certainly live by these three means: by waiting for friends, by relying on both the Dhārtarāṣṭras (i.e., Duryodhana and his closest allies), and by tolerating insults.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 27 Verse 102. O Shalya, you certainly live by these three means: by waiting for friends, by relying on both the Dhārtarāṣṭras (i.e., Duryodhana and his closest allies), and by tolerating insults.

sanskrit: पुनश्चेदीदृशं वाक्यं मद्रराज वदिष्यसि ।शिरस्ते पातयिष्यामि गदया वज्रकल्पया ॥१०३॥
transliteration: punaścedīdṛśaṁ vākyaṁ madrarāja vadiṣyasi ,śiraste pātayiṣyāmi gadayā vajrakalpayā.
english: O king of Madras, if you speak such a statement again, I will strike down your head with a mace resembling a thunderbolt.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 27 Verse 103. O king of Madras, if you speak such a statement again, I will strike down your head with a mace resembling a thunderbolt.

sanskrit: श्रोतारस्त्विदमद्येह द्रष्टारो वा कुदेशज ।कर्णं वा जघ्नतुः कृष्णौ कर्णो वापि जघान तौ ॥१०४॥
transliteration: śrotārastvidamadyeha draṣṭāro vā kudeśaja ,karṇaṁ vā jaghnatuḥ kṛṣṇau karṇo vāpi jaghāna tau.
english: O you born in a bad land, who among the listeners or those who witness this today, here, will truly say whether the two Krishnas killed Karna, or Karna killed them both?
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 27 Verse 104. O you born in a bad land, who among the listeners or those who witness this today, here, will truly say whether the two Krishnas killed Karna, or Karna killed them both?

sanskrit: एवमुक्त्वा तु राधेयः पुनरेव विशां पते ।अब्रवीन्मद्रराजानं याहि याहीत्यसंभ्रमम् ॥१०५॥
transliteration: evamuktvā tu rādheyaḥ punareva viśāṁ pate ,abravīnmadrarājānaṁ yāhi yāhītyasaṁbhramam.
english: Having spoken thus, Karna (rādheya) then, O lord of the people, said again to the king of Madras, 'Go! Go!' calmly.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 27 Verse 105. Having spoken thus, Karna (rādheya) then, O lord of the people, said again to the king of Madras, 'Go! Go!' calmly.

sanskrit: संजय उवाच ।मारिषाधिरथेः श्रुत्वा वचो युद्धाभिनन्दिनः ।शल्योऽब्रवीत्पुनः कर्णं निदर्शनमुदाहरन् ॥१॥
transliteration: saṁjaya uvāca ,māriṣādhiratheḥ śrutvā vaco yuddhābhinandinaḥ ,śalyo'bravītpunaḥ karṇaṁ nidarśanamudāharan.
english: Sañjaya said: O respected one, having heard the words of Karna (adhiratheḥ), who was delighting in battle, Shalya spoke to Karna again, illustrating his point with an example.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 28 Verse 1. Sañjaya said: O respected one, having heard the words of Karna (adhiratheḥ), who was delighting in battle, Shalya spoke to Karna again, illustrating his point with an example.

sanskrit: यथैव मत्तो मद्येन त्वं तथा न च वा तथा ।तथाहं त्वां प्रमाद्यन्तं चिकित्सामि सुहृत्तया ॥२॥
transliteration: yathaiva matto madyena tvaṁ tathā na ca vā tathā ,tathāhaṁ tvāṁ pramādyantaṁ cikitsāmi suhṛttayā.
english: Just as you are intoxicated by liquor, or perhaps not quite in that manner, so I, out of friendship, treat you who are acting heedlessly.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 28 Verse 2. Just as you are intoxicated by liquor, or perhaps not quite in that manner, so I, out of friendship, treat you who are acting heedlessly.

sanskrit: इमां काकोपमां कर्ण प्रोच्यमानां निबोध मे ।श्रुत्वा यथेष्टं कुर्यास्त्वं विहीन कुलपांसन ॥३॥
transliteration: imāṁ kākopamāṁ karṇa procyamānāṁ nibodha me ,śrutvā yatheṣṭaṁ kuryāstvaṁ vihīna kulapāṁsana.
english: O Karṇa, listen to this analogy of the crow that I am about to relate. Having heard it, you, O wretched one, O disgracer of your family, may then do as you please.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 28 Verse 3. O Karṇa, listen to this analogy of the crow that I am about to relate. Having heard it, you, O wretched one, O disgracer of your family, may then do as you please.

sanskrit: नाहमात्मनि किंचिद्वै किल्बिषं कर्ण संस्मरे ।येन त्वं मां महाबाहो हन्तुमिच्छस्यनागसम् ॥४॥
transliteration: nāhamātmani kiṁcidvai kilbiṣaṁ karṇa saṁsmare ,yena tvaṁ māṁ mahābāho hantumicchasyanāgasam.
english: O Karṇa, I truly do not remember any offense (kilbiṣam) within myself (ātman) by which you, O mighty-armed one, desire to kill me, an innocent one.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 28 Verse 4. O Karṇa, I truly do not remember any offense (kilbiṣam) within myself (ātman) by which you, O mighty-armed one, desire to kill me, an innocent one.

sanskrit: अवश्यं तु मया वाच्यं बुध्यतां यदि ते हितम् ।विशेषतो रथस्थेन राज्ञश्चैव हितैषिणा ॥५॥
transliteration: avaśyaṁ tu mayā vācyaṁ budhyatāṁ yadi te hitam ,viśeṣato rathasthena rājñaścaiva hitaiṣiṇā.
english: However, what is beneficial for you must certainly be spoken by me, if it is to be understood. Especially so, as I am stationed on a chariot and am a well-wisher of the king.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 28 Verse 5. However, what is beneficial for you must certainly be spoken by me, if it is to be understood. Especially so, as I am stationed on a chariot and am a well-wisher of the king.

sanskrit: समं च विषमं चैव रथिनश्च बलाबलम् ।श्रमः खेदश्च सततं हयानां रथिना सह ॥६॥
transliteration: samaṁ ca viṣamaṁ caiva rathinaśca balābalam ,śramaḥ khedaśca satataṁ hayānāṁ rathinā saha.
english: Both even and uneven terrain, as well as the strengths and weaknesses of the charioteers, and the constant fatigue and weariness experienced by the horses and their charioteers.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 28 Verse 6. Both even and uneven terrain, as well as the strengths and weaknesses of the charioteers, and the constant fatigue and weariness experienced by the horses and their charioteers.

sanskrit: आयुधस्य परिज्ञानं रुतं च मृगपक्षिणाम् ।भारश्चाप्यतिभारश्च शल्यानां च प्रतिक्रिया ॥७॥
transliteration: āyudhasya parijñānaṁ rutaṁ ca mṛgapakṣiṇām ,bhāraścāpyatibhāraśca śalyānāṁ ca pratikriyā.
english: Knowledge of weapons, the cries of wild animals and birds, both normal and excessive burdens, and the remedies for projectiles.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 28 Verse 7. Knowledge of weapons, the cries of wild animals and birds, both normal and excessive burdens, and the remedies for projectiles.

sanskrit: अस्त्रयोगश्च युद्धं च निमित्तानि तथैव च ।सर्वमेतन्मया ज्ञेयं रथस्यास्य कुटुम्बिना ।अतस्त्वां कथये कर्ण निदर्शनमिदं पुनः ॥८॥
transliteration: astrayogaśca yuddhaṁ ca nimittāni tathaiva ca ,sarvametanmayā jñeyaṁ rathasyāsya kuṭumbinā ,atastvāṁ kathaye karṇa nidarśanamidaṁ punaḥ.
english: The deployment of weapons (yoga), warfare, omens, and similar matters - all of this must be known by me, the master of this chariot. Therefore, Karna, I will recount this example to you again.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 28 Verse 8. The deployment of weapons (yoga), warfare, omens, and similar matters - all of this must be known by me, the master of this chariot. Therefore, Karna, I will recount this example to you again.

sanskrit: वैश्यः किल समुद्रान्ते प्रभूतधनधान्यवान् ।यज्वा दानपतिः क्षान्तः स्वकर्मस्थोऽभवच्छुचिः ॥९॥
transliteration: vaiśyaḥ kila samudrānte prabhūtadhanadhānyavān ,yajvā dānapatiḥ kṣāntaḥ svakarmastho'bhavacchuciḥ.
english: Indeed, a Vaiśya, possessing abundant wealth and grain, lived by the seashore. He was a performer of Vedic rituals (yajña), a lord of charity (dāna), patient, steadfast in his own duty (sva-karma), and became pure.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 28 Verse 9. Indeed, a Vaiśya, possessing abundant wealth and grain, lived by the seashore. He was a performer of Vedic rituals (yajña), a lord of charity (dāna), patient, steadfast in his own duty (sva-karma), and became pure.

sanskrit: बहुपुत्रः प्रियापत्यः सर्वभूतानुकम्पकः ।राज्ञो धर्मप्रधानस्य राष्ट्रे वसति निर्भयः ॥१०॥
transliteration: bahuputraḥ priyāpatyaḥ sarvabhūtānukampakaḥ ,rājño dharmapradhānasya rāṣṭre vasati nirbhayaḥ.
english: A person who has many children and dear offspring, and is compassionate towards all beings, lives fearlessly in the kingdom of a king whose primary principle is righteousness (dharma).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 28 Verse 10. A person who has many children and dear offspring, and is compassionate towards all beings, lives fearlessly in the kingdom of a king whose primary principle is righteousness (dharma).

sanskrit: पुत्राणां तस्य बालानां कुमाराणां यशस्विनाम् ।काको बहूनामभवदुच्छिष्टकृतभोजनः ॥११॥
transliteration: putrāṇāṁ tasya bālānāṁ kumārāṇāṁ yaśasvinām ,kāko bahūnāmabhavaducchiṣṭakṛtabhojanaḥ.
english: Among his many young and illustrious sons, a crow became accustomed to eating their leftover food.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 28 Verse 11. Among his many young and illustrious sons, a crow became accustomed to eating their leftover food.

sanskrit: तस्मै सदा प्रयच्छन्ति वैश्यपुत्राः कुमारकाः ।मांसोदनं दधि क्षीरं पायसं मधुसर्पिषी ॥१२॥
transliteration: tasmai sadā prayacchanti vaiśyaputrāḥ kumārakāḥ ,māṁsodanaṁ dadhi kṣīraṁ pāyasaṁ madhusarpiṣī.
english: The young sons of the merchant class (Vaiśyas) always offer him (the crow) meat-rice, curds, milk, sweet rice pudding, and honey and ghee.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 28 Verse 12. The young sons of the merchant class (Vaiśyas) always offer him (the crow) meat-rice, curds, milk, sweet rice pudding, and honey and ghee.

sanskrit: स चोच्छिष्टभृतः काको वैश्यपुत्रैः कुमारकैः ।सदृशान्पक्षिणो दृप्तः श्रेयसश्चावमन्यते ॥१३॥
transliteration: sa cocchiṣṭabhṛtaḥ kāko vaiśyaputraiḥ kumārakaiḥ ,sadṛśānpakṣiṇo dṛptaḥ śreyasaścāvamanyate.
english: And that crow, nourished by the leftovers from the young Vaiśya sons, grew proud and disdained both his peers and even superior birds.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 28 Verse 13. And that crow, nourished by the leftovers from the young Vaiśya sons, grew proud and disdained both his peers and even superior birds.

sanskrit: अथ हंसाः समुद्रान्ते कदाचिदभिपातिनः ।गरुडस्य गतौ तुल्याश्चक्राङ्गा हृष्टचेतसः ॥१४॥
transliteration: atha haṁsāḥ samudrānte kadācidabhipātinaḥ ,garuḍasya gatau tulyāścakrāṅgā hṛṣṭacetasaḥ.
english: Then, once, joyful-minded swans, graceful in appearance, flying down to the ocean's edge, were equal to Garuda in their swift movement.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 28 Verse 14. Then, once, joyful-minded swans, graceful in appearance, flying down to the ocean's edge, were equal to Garuda in their swift movement.

sanskrit: कुमारकास्ततो हंसान्दृष्ट्वा काकमथाब्रुवन् ।भवानेव विशिष्टो हि पतत्रिभ्यो विहंगम ॥१५॥
transliteration: kumārakāstato haṁsāndṛṣṭvā kākamathābruvan ,bhavāneva viśiṣṭo hi patatribhyo vihaṁgama.
english: Then, the young boys, having seen the swans, said to a crow, 'Indeed, O bird, you are truly superior among all flying creatures!'
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 28 Verse 15. Then, the young boys, having seen the swans, said to a crow, 'Indeed, O bird, you are truly superior among all flying creatures!'

sanskrit: प्रतार्यमाणस्तु स तैरल्पबुद्धिभिरण्डजः ।तद्वचः सत्यमित्येव मौर्ख्याद्दर्पाच्च मन्यते ॥१६॥
transliteration: pratāryamāṇastu sa tairalpabuddhibhiraṇḍajaḥ ,tadvacaḥ satyamityeva maurkhyāddarpācca manyate.
english: But he, the crow (aṇḍaja), being deceived by those foolish boys, believes their words to be true, solely out of foolishness and pride.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 28 Verse 16. But he, the crow (aṇḍaja), being deceived by those foolish boys, believes their words to be true, solely out of foolishness and pride.

sanskrit: तान्सोऽभिपत्य जिज्ञासुः क एषां श्रेष्ठभागिति ।उच्छिष्टदर्पितः काको बहूनां दूरपातिनाम् ॥१७॥
transliteration: tānso'bhipatya jijñāsuḥ ka eṣāṁ śreṣṭhabhāgiti ,ucchiṣṭadarpitaḥ kāko bahūnāṁ dūrapātinām.
english: That crow, made conceited by the flattering words, approached those many far-flying (swans), curious to know, 'Who among these is the foremost?'
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 28 Verse 17. That crow, made conceited by the flattering words, approached those many far-flying (swans), curious to know, 'Who among these is the foremost?'

sanskrit: तेषां यं प्रवरं मेने हंसानां दूरपातिनाम् ।तमाह्वयत दुर्बुद्धिः पताम इति पक्षिणम् ॥१८॥
transliteration: teṣāṁ yaṁ pravaraṁ mene haṁsānāṁ dūrapātinām ,tamāhvayata durbuddhiḥ patāma iti pakṣiṇam.
english: Among those far-flying swans (haṃsa), the foolish bird (durbuddhi) considered one to be the best and challenged him, saying, "Let us fly (patāma)!"
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 28 Verse 18. Among those far-flying swans (haṃsa), the foolish bird (durbuddhi) considered one to be the best and challenged him, saying, "Let us fly (patāma)!"

sanskrit: तच्छ्रुत्वा प्राहसन्हंसा ये तत्रासन्समागताः ।भाषतो बहु काकस्य बलिनः पततां वराः ।इदमूचुश्च चक्राङ्गा वचः काकं विहंगमाः ॥१९॥
transliteration: tacchrutvā prāhasanhaṁsā ye tatrāsansamāgatāḥ ,bhāṣato bahu kākasya balinaḥ patatāṁ varāḥ ,idamūcuśca cakrāṅgā vacaḥ kākaṁ vihaṁgamāḥ.
english: Having heard that, the swans (haṃsa), who were gathered there and were the foremost among flyers, laughed as the mighty crow (kāka) spoke boastfully. And these birds (cakrāṅgāḥ, vihaṅgamāḥ) then spoke these words to the crow (kāka).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 28 Verse 19. Having heard that, the swans (haṃsa), who were gathered there and were the foremost among flyers, laughed as the mighty crow (kāka) spoke boastfully. And these birds (cakrāṅgāḥ, vihaṅgamāḥ) then spoke these words to the crow (kāka).

sanskrit: वयं हंसाश्चरामेमां पृथिवीं मानसौकसः ।पक्षिणां च वयं नित्यं दूरपातेन पूजिताः ॥२०॥
transliteration: vayaṁ haṁsāścarāmemāṁ pṛthivīṁ mānasaukasaḥ ,pakṣiṇāṁ ca vayaṁ nityaṁ dūrapātena pūjitāḥ.
english: We are swans (haṃsa), dwellers of Lake Manasa, and we roam this earth. Among all birds, we are always revered for our ability to fly great distances.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 28 Verse 20. We are swans (haṃsa), dwellers of Lake Manasa, and we roam this earth. Among all birds, we are always revered for our ability to fly great distances.

sanskrit: कथं नु हंसं बलिनं वज्राङ्गं दूरपातिनम् ।काको भूत्वा निपतने समाह्वयसि दुर्मते ।कथं त्वं पतनं काक सहास्माभिर्ब्रवीषि तत् ॥२१॥
transliteration: kathaṁ nu haṁsaṁ balinaṁ vajrāṅgaṁ dūrapātinam ,kāko bhūtvā nipatane samāhvayasi durmate ,kathaṁ tvaṁ patanaṁ kāka sahāsmābhirbravīṣi tat.
english: O foolish one (durmate), how indeed, being a crow (kāka), do you challenge a powerful, thunderbolt-bodied, far-flying swan (haṃsa) to a flying contest? O crow (kāka), how can you even propose flying with us?
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 28 Verse 21. O foolish one (durmate), how indeed, being a crow (kāka), do you challenge a powerful, thunderbolt-bodied, far-flying swan (haṃsa) to a flying contest? O crow (kāka), how can you even propose flying with us?

sanskrit: अथ हंसवचो मूढः कुत्सयित्वा पुनः पुनः ।प्रजगादोत्तरं काकः कत्थनो जातिलाघवात् ॥२२॥
transliteration: atha haṁsavaco mūḍhaḥ kutsayitvā punaḥ punaḥ ,prajagādottaraṁ kākaḥ katthano jātilāghavāt.
english: Then, the foolish crow, repeatedly scorning the swan's words, gave his reply, boasting due to the inherent inferiority of his kind (jāti).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 28 Verse 22. Then, the foolish crow, repeatedly scorning the swan's words, gave his reply, boasting due to the inherent inferiority of his kind (jāti).

sanskrit: शतमेकं च पातानां पतितास्मि न संशयः ।शतयोजनमेकैकं विचित्रं विविधं तथा ॥२३॥
transliteration: śatamekaṁ ca pātānāṁ patitāsmi na saṁśayaḥ ,śatayojanamekaikaṁ vicitraṁ vividhaṁ tathā.
english: I have undoubtedly performed a hundred flights (pāta), each one hundred yojanas in length, and each one diverse and manifold.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 28 Verse 23. I have undoubtedly performed a hundred flights (pāta), each one hundred yojanas in length, and each one diverse and manifold.

sanskrit: उड्डीनमवडीनं च प्रडीनं डीनमेव च ।निडीनमथ संडीनं तिर्यक्चातिगतानि च ॥२४॥
transliteration: uḍḍīnamavaḍīnaṁ ca praḍīnaṁ ḍīnameva ca ,niḍīnamatha saṁḍīnaṁ tiryakcātigatāni ca.
english: Soaring flight, downward flight, forward flight, and just general flight; then, inward flight, collective flight, and also sideways and overpassing flights.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 28 Verse 24. Soaring flight, downward flight, forward flight, and just general flight; then, inward flight, collective flight, and also sideways and overpassing flights.

sanskrit: विडीनं परिडीनं च पराडीनं सुडीनकम् ।अतिडीनं महाडीनं निडीनं परिडीनकम् ॥२५॥
transliteration: viḍīnaṁ pariḍīnaṁ ca parāḍīnaṁ suḍīnakam ,atiḍīnaṁ mahāḍīnaṁ niḍīnaṁ pariḍīnakam.
english: Spread-out flight, encircling flight, and retreating flight, as well as excellent flight; surpassing flight, great flight, settling flight, and a further encircling flight.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 28 Verse 25. Spread-out flight, encircling flight, and retreating flight, as well as excellent flight; surpassing flight, great flight, settling flight, and a further encircling flight.

sanskrit: गतागतप्रतिगता बह्वीश्च निकुडीनिकाः ।कर्तास्मि मिषतां वोऽद्य ततो द्रक्ष्यथ मे बलम् ॥२६॥
transliteration: gatāgatapratigatā bahvīśca nikuḍīnikāḥ ,kartāsmi miṣatāṁ vo'dya tato drakṣyatha me balam.
english: Today, while you watch, I will perform many maneuvers of going, coming, and returning, along with numerous evasive dives. Then you will witness my power.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 28 Verse 26. Today, while you watch, I will perform many maneuvers of going, coming, and returning, along with numerous evasive dives. Then you will witness my power.

sanskrit: एवमुक्ते तु काकेन प्रहस्यैको विहंगमः ।उवाच हंसस्तं काकं वचनं तन्निबोध मे ॥२७॥
transliteration: evamukte tu kākena prahasyaiko vihaṁgamaḥ ,uvāca haṁsastaṁ kākaṁ vacanaṁ tannibodha me.
english: When the crow had spoken thus, one bird, a swan, laughed and said to that crow, 'Now listen to my words.'
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 28 Verse 27. When the crow had spoken thus, one bird, a swan, laughed and said to that crow, 'Now listen to my words.'

sanskrit: शतमेकं च पातानां त्वं काक पतिता ध्रुवम् ।एकमेव तु ये पातं विदुः सर्वे विहंगमाः ॥२८॥
transliteration: śatamekaṁ ca pātānāṁ tvaṁ kāka patitā dhruvam ,ekameva tu ye pātaṁ viduḥ sarve vihaṁgamāḥ.
english: O crow, you certainly perform a hundred types of dives. However, all other birds know only one true dive.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 28 Verse 28. O crow, you certainly perform a hundred types of dives. However, all other birds know only one true dive.

sanskrit: तमहं पतिता काक नान्यं जानामि कंचन ।पत त्वमपि रक्ताक्ष येन वा तेन मन्यसे ॥२९॥
transliteration: tamahaṁ patitā kāka nānyaṁ jānāmi kaṁcana ,pata tvamapi raktākṣa yena vā tena manyase.
english: O crow, that one (dive) is what I know as the true dive; I know no other whatsoever. You too, O red-eyed one, may fly however you deem appropriate.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 28 Verse 29. O crow, that one (dive) is what I know as the true dive; I know no other whatsoever. You too, O red-eyed one, may fly however you deem appropriate.

sanskrit: अथ काकाः प्रजहसुर्ये तत्रासन्समागताः ।कथमेकेन पातेन हंसः पातशतं जयेत् ॥३०॥
transliteration: atha kākāḥ prajahasurye tatrāsansamāgatāḥ ,kathamekena pātena haṁsaḥ pātaśataṁ jayet.
english: Then the crows who had gathered there laughed loudly, (saying) "How can a swan conquer a hundred swoops with just one swoop?"
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 28 Verse 30. Then the crows who had gathered there laughed loudly, (saying) "How can a swan conquer a hundred swoops with just one swoop?"

sanskrit: एकेनैव शतस्यैकं पातेनाभिभविष्यति ।हंसस्य पतितं काको बलवानाशुविक्रमः ॥३१॥
transliteration: ekenaiva śatasyaikaṁ pātenābhibhaviṣyati ,haṁsasya patitaṁ kāko balavānāśuvikramaḥ.
english: The powerful crow, swift in valor, will indeed overpower a single (swoop) out of a hundred of the swan's swoops with just one (of its own) swoop.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 28 Verse 31. The powerful crow, swift in valor, will indeed overpower a single (swoop) out of a hundred of the swan's swoops with just one (of its own) swoop.

sanskrit: प्रपेततुः स्पर्धयाथ ततस्तौ हंसवायसौ ।एकपाती च चक्राङ्गः काकः पातशतेन च ॥३२॥
transliteration: prapetatuḥ spardhayātha tatastau haṁsavāyasau ,ekapātī ca cakrāṅgaḥ kākaḥ pātaśatena ca.
english: Then, in rivalry, the two, the swan and the crow, flew down. Chakrāṅga (the swan), who performs a single swoop, and the crow, with a hundred swoops.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 28 Verse 32. Then, in rivalry, the two, the swan and the crow, flew down. Chakrāṅga (the swan), who performs a single swoop, and the crow, with a hundred swoops.

sanskrit: पेतिवानथ चक्राङ्गः पेतिवानथ वायसः ।विसिस्मापयिषुः पातैराचक्षाणोऽऽत्मनः क्रियाम् ॥३३॥
transliteration: petivānatha cakrāṅgaḥ petivānatha vāyasaḥ ,visismāpayiṣuḥ pātairācakṣāṇo''tmanaḥ kriyām.
english: Then Chakrāṅga (the swan) swooped down, and then the crow swooped down, eager to astonish with its swoops, demonstrating its own action.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 28 Verse 33. Then Chakrāṅga (the swan) swooped down, and then the crow swooped down, eager to astonish with its swoops, demonstrating its own action.

sanskrit: अथ काकस्य चित्राणि पतितानीतराणि च ।दृष्ट्वा प्रमुदिताः काका विनेदुरथ तैः स्वरैः ॥३४॥
transliteration: atha kākasya citrāṇi patitānītarāṇi ca ,dṛṣṭvā pramuditāḥ kākā vineduratha taiḥ svaraiḥ.
english: Then, upon seeing the crow's strange things, both those that had fallen and others, the crows, greatly delighted, cried out with those very sounds.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 28 Verse 34. Then, upon seeing the crow's strange things, both those that had fallen and others, the crows, greatly delighted, cried out with those very sounds.

sanskrit: हंसांश्चावहसन्ति स्म प्रावदन्नप्रियाणि च ।उत्पत्योत्पत्य च प्राहुर्मुहूर्तमिति चेति च ॥३५॥
transliteration: haṁsāṁścāvahasanti sma prāvadannapriyāṇi ca ,utpatyotpatya ca prāhurmuhūrtamiti ceti ca.
english: They laughed at the geese and also uttered harsh words. Leaping up again and again, they said, "For just a moment!" and "For just a moment!" (as if taunting them about the brevity of their success).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 28 Verse 35. They laughed at the geese and also uttered harsh words. Leaping up again and again, they said, "For just a moment!" and "For just a moment!" (as if taunting them about the brevity of their success).

sanskrit: वृक्षाग्रेभ्यः स्थलेभ्यश्च निपतन्त्युत्पतन्ति च ।कुर्वाणा विविधान्रावानाशंसन्तस्तदा जयम् ॥३६॥
transliteration: vṛkṣāgrebhyaḥ sthalebhyaśca nipatantyutpatanti ca ,kurvāṇā vividhānrāvānāśaṁsantastadā jayam.
english: Falling down from the treetops and from the ground, and flying up, they were making various cries and at that time anticipating victory.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 28 Verse 36. Falling down from the treetops and from the ground, and flying up, they were making various cries and at that time anticipating victory.

sanskrit: हंसस्तु मृदुकेनैव विक्रान्तुमुपचक्रमे ।प्रत्यहीयत काकाच्च मुहूर्तमिव मारिष ॥३७॥
transliteration: haṁsastu mṛdukenaiva vikrāntumupacakrame ,pratyahīyata kākācca muhūrtamiva māriṣa.
english: But the goose began to display prowess only gently; yet he was repelled by the crow, as if only for a moment, O venerable one.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 28 Verse 37. But the goose began to display prowess only gently; yet he was repelled by the crow, as if only for a moment, O venerable one.

sanskrit: अवमन्य रयं हंसानिदं वचनमब्रवीत् ।योऽसावुत्पतितो हंसः सोऽसावेव प्रहीयते ॥३८॥
transliteration: avamanya rayaṁ haṁsānidaṁ vacanamabravīt ,yo'sāvutpatito haṁsaḥ so'sāveva prahīyate.
english: Disdaining (the crow's) speed, (the crow) spoke this word to the swans: "That swan who has flown up, that very one will certainly fall."
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 28 Verse 38. Disdaining (the crow's) speed, (the crow) spoke this word to the swans: "That swan who has flown up, that very one will certainly fall."

sanskrit: अथ हंसः स तच्छ्रुत्वा प्रापतत्पश्चिमां दिशम् ।उपर्युपरि वेगेन सागरं वरुणालयम् ॥३९॥
transliteration: atha haṁsaḥ sa tacchrutvā prāpatatpaścimāṁ diśam ,uparyupari vegena sāgaraṁ varuṇālayam.
english: Then that swan, having heard that (challenge), flew westward with great speed, high above the ocean, the abode of Varuṇa.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 28 Verse 39. Then that swan, having heard that (challenge), flew westward with great speed, high above the ocean, the abode of Varuṇa.

sanskrit: ततो भीः प्राविशत्काकं तदा तत्र विचेतसम् ।द्वीपद्रुमानपश्यन्तं निपतन्तं श्रमान्वितम् ।निपतेयं क्व नु श्रान्त इति तस्मिञ्जलार्णवे ॥४०॥
transliteration: tato bhīḥ prāviśatkākaṁ tadā tatra vicetasam ,dvīpadrumānapaśyantaṁ nipatantaṁ śramānvitam ,nipateyaṁ kva nu śrānta iti tasmiñjalārṇave.
english: Then fear entered the crow, which was at that moment bewildered. Weary, falling, and unable to see any island trees, (it thought): "Where can I fall, exhausted, in this watery ocean?"
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 28 Verse 40. Then fear entered the crow, which was at that moment bewildered. Weary, falling, and unable to see any island trees, (it thought): "Where can I fall, exhausted, in this watery ocean?"

sanskrit: अविषह्यः समुद्रो हि बहुसत्त्वगणालयः ।महाभूतशतोद्भासी नभसोऽपि विशिष्यते ॥४१॥
transliteration: aviṣahyaḥ samudro hi bahusattvagaṇālayaḥ ,mahābhūtaśatodbhāsī nabhaso'pi viśiṣyate.
english: The ocean, indeed, is insurmountable, an abode for countless groups of beings, and it shines with hundreds of great elements; it surpasses even the sky.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 28 Verse 41. The ocean, indeed, is insurmountable, an abode for countless groups of beings, and it shines with hundreds of great elements; it surpasses even the sky.

sanskrit: गाम्भीर्याद्धि समुद्रस्य न विशेषः कुलाधम ।दिगम्बराम्भसां कर्ण समुद्रस्था हि दुर्जयाः ।विदूरपातात्तोयस्य किं पुनः कर्ण वायसः ॥४२॥
transliteration: gāmbhīryāddhi samudrasya na viśeṣaḥ kulādhama ,digambarāmbhasāṁ karṇa samudrasthā hi durjayāḥ ,vidūrapātāttoyasya kiṁ punaḥ karṇa vāyasaḥ.
english: Indeed, O lowest of your lineage (kulādhamā), there is nothing particularly special about the ocean's depth. O Karṇa, the waters (ambhas) that gather in the ocean from all directions are truly unconquerable. What significance, then, O Karṇa, does a crow hold regarding water falling from a great height?
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 28 Verse 42. Indeed, O lowest of your lineage (kulādhamā), there is nothing particularly special about the ocean's depth. O Karṇa, the waters (ambhas) that gather in the ocean from all directions are truly unconquerable. What significance, then, O Karṇa, does a crow hold regarding water falling from a great height?

sanskrit: अथ हंसोऽभ्यतिक्रम्य मुहूर्तमिति चेति च ।अवेक्षमाणस्तं काकं नाशक्नोद्व्यपसर्पितुम् ।अतिक्रम्य च चक्राङ्गः काकं तं समुदैक्षत ॥४३॥
transliteration: atha haṁso'bhyatikramya muhūrtamiti ceti ca ,avekṣamāṇastaṁ kākaṁ nāśaknodvyapasarpitum ,atikramya ca cakrāṅgaḥ kākaṁ taṁ samudaikṣata.
english: Then the swan, after having moved past for a moment, here and there, and observing that crow, was unable to move away. And the cakrāṅga (swan), having passed beyond (the crow), looked intently at it.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 28 Verse 43. Then the swan, after having moved past for a moment, here and there, and observing that crow, was unable to move away. And the cakrāṅga (swan), having passed beyond (the crow), looked intently at it.

sanskrit: तं तथा हीयमानं च हंसो दृष्ट्वाब्रवीदिदम् ।उज्जिहीर्षुर्निमज्जन्तं स्मरन्सत्पुरुषव्रतम् ॥४४॥
transliteration: taṁ tathā hīyamānaṁ ca haṁso dṛṣṭvābravīdidam ,ujjihīrṣurnimajjantaṁ smaransatpuruṣavratam.
english: The swan, seeing him thus diminishing, spoke these words, desiring to uplift him who was sinking, remembering the sacred duty (vrata) of noble individuals.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 28 Verse 44. The swan, seeing him thus diminishing, spoke these words, desiring to uplift him who was sinking, remembering the sacred duty (vrata) of noble individuals.

sanskrit: बहूनि पतनानि त्वमाचक्षाणो मुहुर्मुहुः ।पतस्यव्याहरंश्चेदं न नो गुह्यं प्रभाषसे ॥४५॥
transliteration: bahūni patanāni tvamācakṣāṇo muhurmuhuḥ ,patasyavyāharaṁścedaṁ na no guhyaṁ prabhāṣase.
english: You, repeatedly speaking of many downfalls, yourself fall while remaining silent about this (your situation). You do not disclose your secret (guhya) to us.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 28 Verse 45. You, repeatedly speaking of many downfalls, yourself fall while remaining silent about this (your situation). You do not disclose your secret (guhya) to us.

sanskrit: किं नाम पतनं काक यत्त्वं पतसि सांप्रतम् ।जलं स्पृशसि पक्षाभ्यां तुण्डेन च पुनः पुनः ॥४६॥
transliteration: kiṁ nāma patanaṁ kāka yattvaṁ patasi sāṁpratam ,jalaṁ spṛśasi pakṣābhyāṁ tuṇḍena ca punaḥ punaḥ.
english: O crow, what kind of falling is this, that you are performing right now? You repeatedly touch the water with your wings and with your beak.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 28 Verse 46. O crow, what kind of falling is this, that you are performing right now? You repeatedly touch the water with your wings and with your beak.

sanskrit: स पक्षाभ्यां स्पृशन्नार्तस्तुण्डेन जलमर्णवे ।काको दृढं परिश्रान्तः सहसा निपपात ह ॥४७॥
transliteration: sa pakṣābhyāṁ spṛśannārtastuṇḍena jalamarṇave ,kāko dṛḍhaṁ pariśrāntaḥ sahasā nipapāta ha.
english: Afflicted, that crow, touching the water in the ocean with his wings and beak, became greatly exhausted and suddenly fell down.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 28 Verse 47. Afflicted, that crow, touching the water in the ocean with his wings and beak, became greatly exhausted and suddenly fell down.

sanskrit: हंस उवाच ।शतमेकं च पातानां यत्प्रभाषसि वायस ।नानाविधानीह पुरा तच्चानृतमिहाद्य ते ॥४८॥
transliteration: haṁsa uvāca ,śatamekaṁ ca pātānāṁ yatprabhāṣasi vāyasa ,nānāvidhānīha purā taccānṛtamihādya te.
english: The swan said: 'O crow, what you declare as one hundred and one falls, previously of various kinds, that is now untrue for you here today.'
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 28 Verse 48. The swan said: 'O crow, what you declare as one hundred and one falls, previously of various kinds, that is now untrue for you here today.'

sanskrit: काक उवाच ।उच्छिष्टदर्पितो हंस मन्येऽऽत्मानं सुपर्णवत् ।अवमन्य बहूंश्चाहं काकानन्यांश्च पक्षिणः ।प्राणैर्हंस प्रपद्ये त्वां द्वीपान्तं प्रापयस्व माम् ॥४९॥
transliteration: kāka uvāca ,ucchiṣṭadarpito haṁsa manye''tmānaṁ suparṇavat ,avamanya bahūṁścāhaṁ kākānanyāṁśca pakṣiṇaḥ ,prāṇairhaṁsa prapadye tvāṁ dvīpāntaṁ prāpayasva mām.
english: The crow said: 'O swan, made proud by scraps, I consider my self (ātman) to be like Suparṇa, having disregarded many other crows and birds. O swan, I take refuge (prapadya) in you with my life; please lead me to another island.'
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 28 Verse 49. The crow said: 'O swan, made proud by scraps, I consider my self (ātman) to be like Suparṇa, having disregarded many other crows and birds. O swan, I take refuge (prapadya) in you with my life; please lead me to another island.'

sanskrit: यद्यहं स्वस्तिमान्हंस स्वदेशं प्राप्नुयां पुनः ।न कंचिदवमन्येयमापदो मां समुद्धर ॥५०॥
transliteration: yadyahaṁ svastimānhaṁsa svadeśaṁ prāpnuyāṁ punaḥ ,na kaṁcidavamanyeyamāpado māṁ samuddhara.
english: If, O swan, I am safe and sound and can reach my own country again, I will not disrespect anyone. Please deliver me from these dangers.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 28 Verse 50. If, O swan, I am safe and sound and can reach my own country again, I will not disrespect anyone. Please deliver me from these dangers.

sanskrit: तमेवंवादिनं दीनं विलपन्तमचेतनम् ।काक काकेति वाशन्तं निमज्जन्तं महार्णवे ॥५१॥
transliteration: tamevaṁvādinaṁ dīnaṁ vilapantamacetanam ,kāka kāketi vāśantaṁ nimajjantaṁ mahārṇave.
english: That one, speaking in such a way, miserable, lamenting, unconscious, crying out 'Crow! Crow!' as he was sinking in the great ocean.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 28 Verse 51. That one, speaking in such a way, miserable, lamenting, unconscious, crying out 'Crow! Crow!' as he was sinking in the great ocean.

sanskrit: तथैत्य वायसं हंसो जलक्लिन्नं सुदुर्दशम् ।पद्भ्यामुत्क्षिप्य वेपन्तं पृष्ठमारोपयच्छनैः ॥५२॥
transliteration: tathaitya vāyasaṁ haṁso jalaklinnaṁ sudurdaśam ,padbhyāmutkṣipya vepantaṁ pṛṣṭhamāropayacchanaiḥ.
english: Thus, the swan approached the crow, who was drenched with water, barely visible, and trembling. Lifting him with his two feet, he slowly placed him upon his own back.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 28 Verse 52. Thus, the swan approached the crow, who was drenched with water, barely visible, and trembling. Lifting him with his two feet, he slowly placed him upon his own back.

sanskrit: आरोप्य पृष्ठं काकं तं हंसः कर्ण विचेतसम् ।आजगाम पुनर्द्वीपं स्पर्धया पेततुर्यतः ॥५३॥
transliteration: āropya pṛṣṭhaṁ kākaṁ taṁ haṁsaḥ karṇa vicetasam ,ājagāma punardvīpaṁ spardhayā petaturyataḥ.
english: Having placed that crow, Karṇa, who was utterly unconscious, on his back, the swan returned to the island from where they both had fallen due to their rivalry.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 28 Verse 53. Having placed that crow, Karṇa, who was utterly unconscious, on his back, the swan returned to the island from where they both had fallen due to their rivalry.

sanskrit: संस्थाप्य तं चापि पुनः समाश्वास्य च खेचरम् ।गतो यथेप्सितं देशं हंसो मन इवाशुगः ॥५४॥
transliteration: saṁsthāpya taṁ cāpi punaḥ samāśvāsya ca khecaram ,gato yathepsitaṁ deśaṁ haṁso mana ivāśugaḥ.
english: After setting him down and comforting the bird again, the swan departed to his desired destination, swift like the mind.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 28 Verse 54. After setting him down and comforting the bird again, the swan departed to his desired destination, swift like the mind.

sanskrit: उच्छिष्टभोजनात्काको यथा वैश्यकुले तु सः ।एवं त्वमुच्छिष्टभृतो धार्तराष्ट्रैर्न संशयः ।सदृशाञ्श्रेयसश्चापि सर्वान्कर्णातिमन्यसे ॥५५॥
transliteration: ucchiṣṭabhojanātkāko yathā vaiśyakule tu saḥ ,evaṁ tvamucchiṣṭabhṛto dhārtarāṣṭrairna saṁśayaḥ ,sadṛśāñśreyasaścāpi sarvānkarṇātimanyase.
english: Just as a crow lives on leftovers in a merchant's household, so too, you are undoubtedly sustained by the sons of Dhṛtarāṣṭra (Kauravas). Yet, you disregard all those who are equal to or superior to Karṇa.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 28 Verse 55. Just as a crow lives on leftovers in a merchant's household, so too, you are undoubtedly sustained by the sons of Dhṛtarāṣṭra (Kauravas). Yet, you disregard all those who are equal to or superior to Karṇa.

sanskrit: द्रोणद्रौणिकृपैर्गुप्तो भीष्मेणान्यैश्च कौरवैः ।विराटनगरे पार्थमेकं किं नावधीस्तदा ॥५६॥
transliteration: droṇadrauṇikṛpairgupto bhīṣmeṇānyaiśca kauravaiḥ ,virāṭanagare pārthamekaṁ kiṁ nāvadhīstadā.
english: When you were protected by Droṇa, Drauṇi (Aśvatthāmā), Kṛpa, Bhīṣma, and other Kauravas, why did you not kill Arjuna, who was all alone, in the city of Virāṭa then?
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 28 Verse 56. When you were protected by Droṇa, Drauṇi (Aśvatthāmā), Kṛpa, Bhīṣma, and other Kauravas, why did you not kill Arjuna, who was all alone, in the city of Virāṭa then?

sanskrit: यत्र व्यस्ताः समस्ताश्च निर्जिताः स्थ किरीटिना ।सृगाला इव सिंहेन क्व ते वीर्यमभूत्तदा ॥५७॥
transliteration: yatra vyastāḥ samastāśca nirjitāḥ stha kirīṭinā ,sṛgālā iva siṁhena kva te vīryamabhūttadā.
english: At that time, when you all, both scattered and assembled, were defeated by Arjuna (Kirīṭin) just as jackals are by a lion, where was your prowess then?
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 28 Verse 57. At that time, when you all, both scattered and assembled, were defeated by Arjuna (Kirīṭin) just as jackals are by a lion, where was your prowess then?

sanskrit: भ्रातरं च हतं दृष्ट्वा निर्जितः सव्यसाचिना ।पश्यतां कुरुवीराणां प्रथमं त्वं पलायथाः ॥५८॥
transliteration: bhrātaraṁ ca hataṁ dṛṣṭvā nirjitaḥ savyasācinā ,paśyatāṁ kuruvīrāṇāṁ prathamaṁ tvaṁ palāyathāḥ.
english: And having seen your brother killed and yourself defeated by Arjuna (savyasācin), you were the first to flee while the Kuru heroes watched.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 28 Verse 58. And having seen your brother killed and yourself defeated by Arjuna (savyasācin), you were the first to flee while the Kuru heroes watched.

sanskrit: तथा द्वैतवने कर्ण गन्धर्वैः समभिद्रुतः ।कुरून्समग्रानुत्सृज्य प्रथमं त्वं पलायथाः ॥५९॥
transliteration: tathā dvaitavane karṇa gandharvaiḥ samabhidrutaḥ ,kurūnsamagrānutsṛjya prathamaṁ tvaṁ palāyathāḥ.
english: Similarly, O Karna, when attacked by the Gandharvas in Dwaitavana, you were the first to flee, abandoning all the Kurus.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 28 Verse 59. Similarly, O Karna, when attacked by the Gandharvas in Dwaitavana, you were the first to flee, abandoning all the Kurus.

sanskrit: हत्वा जित्वा च गन्धर्वांश्चित्रसेनमुखान्रणे ।कर्ण दुर्योधनं पार्थः सभार्यं सममोचयत् ॥६०॥
transliteration: hatvā jitvā ca gandharvāṁścitrasenamukhānraṇe ,karṇa duryodhanaṁ pārthaḥ sabhāryaṁ samamocayat.
english: O Karna, Arjuna, having killed and conquered the Gandharvas led by Chitrasena in battle, freed Duryodhana along with his wife.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 28 Verse 60. O Karna, Arjuna, having killed and conquered the Gandharvas led by Chitrasena in battle, freed Duryodhana along with his wife.

sanskrit: पुनः प्रभावः पार्थस्य पुराणः केशवस्य च ।कथितः कर्ण रामेण सभायां राजसंसदि ॥६१॥
transliteration: punaḥ prabhāvaḥ pārthasya purāṇaḥ keśavasya ca ,kathitaḥ karṇa rāmeṇa sabhāyāṁ rājasaṁsadi.
english: Again, O Karna, the ancient glory of Arjuna and Krishna (keśava) was recounted by Rama (Paraśurāma) in the assembly before the gathering of kings.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 28 Verse 61. Again, O Karna, the ancient glory of Arjuna and Krishna (keśava) was recounted by Rama (Paraśurāma) in the assembly before the gathering of kings.

sanskrit: सततं च तदश्रौषीर्वचनं द्रोणभीष्मयोः ।अवध्यौ वदतोः कृष्णौ संनिधौ वै महीक्षिताम् ॥६२॥
transliteration: satataṁ ca tadaśrauṣīrvacanaṁ droṇabhīṣmayoḥ ,avadhyau vadatoḥ kṛṣṇau saṁnidhau vai mahīkṣitām.
english: And you constantly heard that statement of Drona and Bhishma, who, in the presence of kings, indeed declared Krishna and Arjuna to be inviolable.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 28 Verse 62. And you constantly heard that statement of Drona and Bhishma, who, in the presence of kings, indeed declared Krishna and Arjuna to be inviolable.

sanskrit: कियन्तं तत्र वक्ष्यामि येन येन धनंजयः ।त्वत्तोऽतिरिक्तः सर्वेभ्यो भूतेभ्यो ब्राह्मणो यथा ॥६३॥
transliteration: kiyantaṁ tatra vakṣyāmi yena yena dhanaṁjayaḥ ,tvatto'tiriktaḥ sarvebhyo bhūtebhyo brāhmaṇo yathā.
english: How much shall I tell you further about that, by which Dhananjaya (Arjuna) is, in every way, superior to you, just as a brahmin is superior to all beings.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 28 Verse 63. How much shall I tell you further about that, by which Dhananjaya (Arjuna) is, in every way, superior to you, just as a brahmin is superior to all beings.

sanskrit: इदानीमेव द्रष्टासि प्रधने स्यन्दने स्थितौ ।पुत्रं च वसुदेवस्य पाण्डवं च धनंजयम् ॥६४॥
transliteration: idānīmeva draṣṭāsi pradhane syandane sthitau ,putraṁ ca vasudevasya pāṇḍavaṁ ca dhanaṁjayam.
english: Indeed, right now you will see the son of Vasudeva (Kṛṣṇa) and the Pāṇḍava Dhananjaya (Arjuna) standing in their chariot in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 28 Verse 64. Indeed, right now you will see the son of Vasudeva (Kṛṣṇa) and the Pāṇḍava Dhananjaya (Arjuna) standing in their chariot in battle.

sanskrit: देवासुरमनुष्येषु प्रख्यातौ यौ नरर्षभौ ।प्रकाशेनाभिविख्यातौ त्वं तु खद्योतवन्नृषु ॥६५॥
transliteration: devāsuramanuṣyeṣu prakhyātau yau nararṣabhau ,prakāśenābhivikhyātau tvaṁ tu khadyotavannṛṣu.
english: Those two, who are renowned as the foremost of men (nararṣabhau) among gods, asuras, and humans, are exceedingly famous for their brilliance. But you, among men, are merely like a firefly.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 28 Verse 65. Those two, who are renowned as the foremost of men (nararṣabhau) among gods, asuras, and humans, are exceedingly famous for their brilliance. But you, among men, are merely like a firefly.

sanskrit: एवं विद्वान्मावमंस्थाः सूतपुत्राच्युतार्जुनौ ।नृसिंहौ तौ नरश्वा त्वं जोषमास्स्व विकत्थन ॥६६॥
transliteration: evaṁ vidvānmāvamaṁsthāḥ sūtaputrācyutārjunau ,nṛsiṁhau tau naraśvā tvaṁ joṣamāssva vikatthana.
english: O son of Suta (sūtaputra), having understood this, do not disparage Acyuta (Kṛṣṇa) and Arjuna! Those two are like lions among men (nṛsiṃhau), while you are merely a man. O boastful one (vikatthana), remain silent!
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 28 Verse 66. O son of Suta (sūtaputra), having understood this, do not disparage Acyuta (Kṛṣṇa) and Arjuna! Those two are like lions among men (nṛsiṃhau), while you are merely a man. O boastful one (vikatthana), remain silent!

sanskrit: संजय उवाच ।मद्राधिपस्याधिरथिस्तदैवं वचो निशम्याप्रियमप्रतीतः ।उवाच शल्यं विदितं ममैतद्यथाविधावर्जुनवासुदेवौ ॥१॥
transliteration: saṁjaya uvāca ,madrādhipasyādhirathistadaivaṁ; vaco niśamyāpriyamapratītaḥ ,uvāca śalyaṁ viditaṁ mamaita;dyathāvidhāvarjunavāsudevau.
english: Sanjaya said: Having heard such unpleasant words from the King of Madra, Adhirathi (Karna), though displeased, then spoke to Shalya: "This is known to me, the true nature of Arjuna and Vāsudeva (Kṛṣṇa)."
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 29 Verse 1. Sanjaya said: Having heard such unpleasant words from the King of Madra, Adhirathi (Karna), though displeased, then spoke to Shalya: "This is known to me, the true nature of Arjuna and Vāsudeva (Kṛṣṇa)."

sanskrit: शौरे रथं वाहयतोऽर्जुनस्य बलं महास्त्राणि च पाण्डवस्य ।अहं विजानामि यथावदद्य परोक्षभूतं तव तत्तु शल्य ॥२॥
transliteration: śaure rathaṁ vāhayato'rjunasya; balaṁ mahāstrāṇi ca pāṇḍavasya ,ahaṁ vijānāmi yathāvadadya; parokṣabhūtaṁ tava tattu śalya.
english: O Shalya, I, Karna, truly know the strength and great weapons of Arjuna, the Pāṇḍava, as he drives the chariot of Śauri (Kṛṣṇa). But that knowledge is indeed hidden from you today.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 29 Verse 2. O Shalya, I, Karna, truly know the strength and great weapons of Arjuna, the Pāṇḍava, as he drives the chariot of Śauri (Kṛṣṇa). But that knowledge is indeed hidden from you today.

sanskrit: तौ चाप्रधृष्यौ शस्त्रभृतां वरिष्ठौ व्यपेतभीर्योधयिष्यामि कृष्णौ ।संतापयत्यभ्यधिकं तु रामाच्छापोऽद्य मां ब्राह्मणसत्तमाच्च ॥३॥
transliteration: tau cāpradhṛṣyau śastrabhṛtāṁ variṣṭhau; vyapetabhīryodhayiṣyāmi kṛṣṇau ,saṁtāpayatyabhyadhikaṁ tu rāmā;cchāpo'dya māṁ brāhmaṇasattamācca.
english: Those two Kṛṣṇas (Kṛṣṇa and Arjuna) are indeed unconquerable, the foremost among weapon-bearers. Still, having cast off all fear, I will fight them. However, today the curse from Paraśurāma (Rāma) and from that excellent Brahmin (brāhmaṇasattamāt) torments me exceedingly.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 29 Verse 3. Those two Kṛṣṇas (Kṛṣṇa and Arjuna) are indeed unconquerable, the foremost among weapon-bearers. Still, having cast off all fear, I will fight them. However, today the curse from Paraśurāma (Rāma) and from that excellent Brahmin (brāhmaṇasattamāt) torments me exceedingly.

sanskrit: अवात्सं वै ब्राह्मणच्छद्मनाहं रामे पुरा दिव्यमस्त्रं चिकीर्षुः ।तत्रापि मे देवराजेन विघ्नो हितार्थिना फल्गुनस्यैव शल्य ॥४॥
transliteration: avātsaṁ vai brāhmaṇacchadmanāhaṁ; rāme purā divyamastraṁ cikīrṣuḥ ,tatrāpi me devarājena vighno; hitārthinā phalgunasyaiva śalya.
english: Indeed, I once resided with Rāma in the disguise of a Brahmin, wishing to acquire a divine weapon. Even there, O Śalya, an obstacle was created for me by the king of the gods, who sought Arjuna (Phalguna)'s welfare.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 29 Verse 4. Indeed, I once resided with Rāma in the disguise of a Brahmin, wishing to acquire a divine weapon. Even there, O Śalya, an obstacle was created for me by the king of the gods, who sought Arjuna (Phalguna)'s welfare.

sanskrit: कृतोऽवभेदेन ममोरुमेत्य प्रविश्य कीटस्य तनुं विरूपाम् ।गुरोर्भयाच्चापि न चेलिवानहं तच्चावबुद्धो ददृशे स विप्रः ॥५॥
transliteration: kṛto'vabhedena mamorumetya; praviśya kīṭasya tanuṁ virūpām ,gurorbhayāccāpi na celivānahaṁ; taccāvabuddho dadṛśe sa vipraḥ.
english: The obstacle was created for me when (Indra, in the form of an insect) entered an ugly insect's body, and having split my thigh. And I, out of fear for my guru (teacher), did not move. That Brahmin, having understood this, then beheld (the situation).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 29 Verse 5. The obstacle was created for me when (Indra, in the form of an insect) entered an ugly insect's body, and having split my thigh. And I, out of fear for my guru (teacher), did not move. That Brahmin, having understood this, then beheld (the situation).

sanskrit: पृष्टश्चाहं तमवोचं महर्षिं सूतोऽहमस्मीति स मां शशाप ।सूतोपधावाप्तमिदं त्वयास्त्रं न कर्मकाले प्रतिभास्यति त्वाम् ॥६॥
transliteration: pṛṣṭaścāhaṁ tamavocaṁ maharṣiṁ; sūto'hamasmīti sa māṁ śaśāpa ,sūtopadhāvāptamidaṁ tvayāstraṁ; na karmakāle pratibhāsyati tvām.
english: And when questioned, I told that great sage, 'I am a sūta.' Thereupon, he cursed me: 'This weapon, obtained by you through sūta-deception, will not manifest to you at the crucial time of action (karma).'
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 29 Verse 6. And when questioned, I told that great sage, 'I am a sūta.' Thereupon, he cursed me: 'This weapon, obtained by you through sūta-deception, will not manifest to you at the crucial time of action (karma).'

sanskrit: अन्यत्र यस्मात्तव मृत्युकालादब्राह्मणे ब्रह्म न हि ध्रुवं स्यात् ।तदद्य पर्याप्तमतीव शस्त्रमस्मिन्संग्रामे तुमुले तात भीमे ॥७॥
transliteration: anyatra yasmāttava mṛtyukālā;dabrāhmaṇe brahma na hi dhruvaṁ syāt ,tadadya paryāptamatīva śastra;masminsaṁgrāme tumule tāta bhīme.
english: For indeed, divine knowledge (brahman) cannot be firm within a non-Brahmin, except at the time of your death. Therefore, O dear one, this weapon is now exceedingly potent in this terrible and tumultuous battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 29 Verse 7. For indeed, divine knowledge (brahman) cannot be firm within a non-Brahmin, except at the time of your death. Therefore, O dear one, this weapon is now exceedingly potent in this terrible and tumultuous battle.

sanskrit: अपां पतिर्वेगवानप्रमेयो निमज्जयिष्यन्निवहान्प्रजानाम् ।महानगं यः कुरुते समुद्रं वेलैव तं वारयत्यप्रमेयम् ॥८॥
transliteration: apāṁ patirvegavānaprameyo; nimajjayiṣyannivahānprajānām ,mahānagaṁ yaḥ kurute samudraṁ; velaiva taṁ vārayatyaprameyam.
english: Just as the immeasurable (aprameya) lord of waters (apāṃ patiḥ) – who is swift, boundless, about to submerge multitudes of beings, and who can turn a great mountain into an ocean – is restrained by the shore (velā), so too will I restrain that immeasurable one.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 29 Verse 8. Just as the immeasurable (aprameya) lord of waters (apāṃ patiḥ) – who is swift, boundless, about to submerge multitudes of beings, and who can turn a great mountain into an ocean – is restrained by the shore (velā), so too will I restrain that immeasurable one.

sanskrit: प्रमुञ्चन्तं बाणसंघानमोघान्मर्मच्छिदो वीरहणः सपत्रान् ।कुन्तीपुत्रं प्रतियोत्स्यामि युद्धे ज्याकर्षिणामुत्तममद्य लोके ॥९॥
transliteration: pramuñcantaṁ bāṇasaṁghānamoghā;nmarmacchido vīrahaṇaḥ sapatrān ,kuntīputraṁ pratiyotsyāmi yuddhe; jyākarṣiṇāmuttamamadya loke.
english: Today in battle, I will fight against Kuntīputra (Arjuna) – the best among bowmen in the world – who releases unerring, vital-spot-piercing, hero-slaying, feathered volleys of arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 29 Verse 9. Today in battle, I will fight against Kuntīputra (Arjuna) – the best among bowmen in the world – who releases unerring, vital-spot-piercing, hero-slaying, feathered volleys of arrows.

sanskrit: एवं बलेनातिबलं महास्त्रं समुद्रकल्पं सुदुरापमुग्रम् ।शरौघिणं पार्थिवान्मज्जयन्तं वेलेव पार्थमिषुभिः संसहिष्ये ॥१०॥
transliteration: evaṁ balenātibalaṁ mahāstraṁ; samudrakalpaṁ sudurāpamugram ,śaraughiṇaṁ pārthivānmajjayantaṁ; veleva pārthamiṣubhiḥ saṁsahiṣye.
english: Thus, with my strength, I will withstand Pārtha (Arjuna) like a shore (velā) withstands the ocean, even though he is exceedingly powerful, wields great weapons, is ocean-like, very difficult to overcome, formidable, showers arrows, and submerges kings with his arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 29 Verse 10. Thus, with my strength, I will withstand Pārtha (Arjuna) like a shore (velā) withstands the ocean, even though he is exceedingly powerful, wields great weapons, is ocean-like, very difficult to overcome, formidable, showers arrows, and submerges kings with his arrows.

sanskrit: अद्याहवे यस्य न तुल्यमन्यं मन्ये मनुष्यं धनुराददानम् ।सुरासुरान्वै युधि यो जयेत तेनाद्य मे पश्य युद्धं सुघोरम् ॥११॥
transliteration: adyāhave yasya na tulyamanyaṁ; manye manuṣyaṁ dhanurādadānam ,surāsurānvai yudhi yo jayeta; tenādya me paśya yuddhaṁ sughoram.
english: Today in battle, I consider no other man his equal – a man who, taking up a bow, could indeed conquer gods and demons in conflict. With him, behold my exceedingly dreadful battle today!
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 29 Verse 11. Today in battle, I consider no other man his equal – a man who, taking up a bow, could indeed conquer gods and demons in conflict. With him, behold my exceedingly dreadful battle today!

sanskrit: अतिमानी पाण्डवो युद्धकामो अमानुषैरेष्यति मे महास्त्रैः ।तस्यास्त्रमस्त्रैरभिहत्य संख्ये शरोत्तमैः पातयिष्यामि पार्थम् ॥१२॥
transliteration: atimānī pāṇḍavo yuddhakāmo; amānuṣaireṣyati me mahāstraiḥ ,tasyāstramastrairabhihatya saṁkhye; śarottamaiḥ pātayiṣyāmi pārtham.
english: That exceedingly proud Pāṇḍava, eager for battle, will confront me with his divine great weapons. Having countered his weapon with my own in battle, I will strike down Pārtha with my excellent arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 29 Verse 12. That exceedingly proud Pāṇḍava, eager for battle, will confront me with his divine great weapons. Having countered his weapon with my own in battle, I will strike down Pārtha with my excellent arrows.

sanskrit: दिवाकरेणापि समं तपन्तं समाप्तरश्मिं यशसा ज्वलन्तम् ।तमोनुदं मेघ इवातिमात्रो धनंजयं छादयिष्यामि बाणैः ॥१३॥
transliteration: divākareṇāpi samaṁ tapantaṁ; samāptaraśmiṁ yaśasā jvalantam ,tamonudaṁ megha ivātimātro; dhanaṁjayaṁ chādayiṣyāmi bāṇaiḥ.
english: I will envelop Dhanañjaya with arrows, just as an immense cloud covers the sun, even though he shines like the sun, blazing with full radiance and glory, dispelling all darkness.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 29 Verse 13. I will envelop Dhanañjaya with arrows, just as an immense cloud covers the sun, even though he shines like the sun, blazing with full radiance and glory, dispelling all darkness.

sanskrit: वैश्वानरं धूमशिखं ज्वलन्तं तेजस्विनं लोकमिमं दहन्तम् ।मेघो भूत्वा शरवर्षैर्यथाग्निं तथा पार्थं शमयिष्यामि युद्धे ॥१४॥
transliteration: vaiśvānaraṁ dhūmaśikhaṁ jvalantaṁ; tejasvinaṁ lokamimaṁ dahantam ,megho bhūtvā śaravarṣairyathāgniṁ; tathā pārthaṁ śamayiṣyāmi yuddhe.
english: Just as a cloud, by forming and showering rain, extinguishes fire, similarly, in battle, I will pacify Pārtha with showers of arrows – Pārtha, who is like the blazing universal fire (vaiśvānara), radiating smoky flames, brilliant, and incinerating this world.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 29 Verse 14. Just as a cloud, by forming and showering rain, extinguishes fire, similarly, in battle, I will pacify Pārtha with showers of arrows – Pārtha, who is like the blazing universal fire (vaiśvānara), radiating smoky flames, brilliant, and incinerating this world.

sanskrit: प्रमाथिनं बलवन्तं प्रहारिणं प्रभञ्जनं मातरिश्वानमुग्रम् ।युद्धे सहिष्ये हिमवानिवाचलो धनंजयं क्रुद्धममृष्यमाणम् ॥१५॥
transliteration: pramāthinaṁ balavantaṁ prahāriṇaṁ; prabhañjanaṁ mātariśvānamugram ,yuddhe sahiṣye himavānivācalo; dhanaṁjayaṁ kruddhamamṛṣyamāṇam.
english: In battle, I will endure Dhanañjaya, who is destructive, powerful, a fierce assailant, a breaker of foes, and as terrible as the wind (mātariśvan), all while he is enraged and unyielding. I will stand firm and unmoving like the Himālaya mountain.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 29 Verse 15. In battle, I will endure Dhanañjaya, who is destructive, powerful, a fierce assailant, a breaker of foes, and as terrible as the wind (mātariśvan), all while he is enraged and unyielding. I will stand firm and unmoving like the Himālaya mountain.

sanskrit: विशारदं रथमार्गेष्वसक्तं धुर्यं नित्यं समरेषु प्रवीरम् ।लोके वरं सर्वधनुर्धराणां धनंजयं संयुगे संसहिष्ये ॥१६॥
transliteration: viśāradaṁ rathamārgeṣvasaktaṁ; dhuryaṁ nityaṁ samareṣu pravīram ,loke varaṁ sarvadhanurdharāṇāṁ; dhanaṁjayaṁ saṁyuge saṁsahiṣye.
english: I shall face Dhananjaya (Arjuna) in battle, who is skilled in chariot warfare, unhindered, always a great hero in battles, a leader, and the best among all archers in the world.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 29 Verse 16. I shall face Dhananjaya (Arjuna) in battle, who is skilled in chariot warfare, unhindered, always a great hero in battles, a leader, and the best among all archers in the world.

sanskrit: अद्याहवे यस्य न तुल्यमन्यं मध्येमनुष्यं धनुराददानम् ।सर्वामिमां यः पृथिवीं सहेत तथा विद्वान्योत्स्यमानोऽस्मि तेन ॥१७॥
transliteration: adyāhave yasya na tulyamanyaṁ; madhyemanuṣyaṁ dhanurādadānam ,sarvāmimāṁ yaḥ pṛthivīṁ saheta; tathā vidvānyotsyamāno'smi tena.
english: Today in battle, there is no other man among people equal to him who takes up the bow, and who could conquer this entire earth; therefore, understanding this, I am about to fight with him.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 29 Verse 17. Today in battle, there is no other man among people equal to him who takes up the bow, and who could conquer this entire earth; therefore, understanding this, I am about to fight with him.

sanskrit: यः सर्वभूतानि सदेवकानि प्रस्थेऽजयत्खाण्डवे सव्यसाची ।को जीवितं रक्षमाणो हि तेन युयुत्सते मामृते मानुषोऽन्यः ॥१८॥
transliteration: yaḥ sarvabhūtāni sadevakāni; prasthe'jayatkhāṇḍave savyasācī ,ko jīvitaṁ rakṣamāṇo hi tena; yuyutsate māmṛte mānuṣo'nyaḥ.
english: Savyasachin (Arjuna) conquered all beings, including the gods, in the Khandava forest region. Indeed, what other man, seeking to preserve his life, would fight with him, except for me?
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 29 Verse 18. Savyasachin (Arjuna) conquered all beings, including the gods, in the Khandava forest region. Indeed, what other man, seeking to preserve his life, would fight with him, except for me?

sanskrit: अहं तस्य पौरुषं पाण्डवस्य ब्रूयां हृष्टः समितौ क्षत्रियाणाम् ।किं त्वं मूर्खः प्रभषन्मूढचेता मामवोचः पौरुषमर्जुनस्य ॥१९॥
transliteration: ahaṁ tasya pauruṣaṁ pāṇḍavasya; brūyāṁ hṛṣṭaḥ samitau kṣatriyāṇām ,kiṁ tvaṁ mūrkhaḥ prabhaṣanmūḍhacetā; māmavocaḥ pauruṣamarjunasya.
english: I would gladly speak of the valor and prowess of that Pandava (Arjuna) in an assembly of warriors. Why did you, a foolish and deluded person, uttering such words, speak to me about Arjuna's valor?
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 29 Verse 19. I would gladly speak of the valor and prowess of that Pandava (Arjuna) in an assembly of warriors. Why did you, a foolish and deluded person, uttering such words, speak to me about Arjuna's valor?

sanskrit: अप्रियो यः परुषो निष्ठुरो हि क्षुद्रः क्षेप्ता क्षमिणश्चाक्षमावान् ।हन्यामहं तादृशानां शतानि क्षमामि त्वां क्षमया कालयोगात् ॥२०॥
transliteration: apriyo yaḥ paruṣo niṣṭhuro hi; kṣudraḥ kṣeptā kṣamiṇaścākṣamāvān ,hanyāmahaṁ tādṛśānāṁ śatāni; kṣamāmi tvāṁ kṣamayā kālayogāt.
english: Indeed, one who is disagreeable, harsh, cruel, petty, an insultor, and unforgiving towards patient individuals – I would kill hundreds of such people. Yet, I forgive you now, owing to the exigencies of time.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 29 Verse 20. Indeed, one who is disagreeable, harsh, cruel, petty, an insultor, and unforgiving towards patient individuals – I would kill hundreds of such people. Yet, I forgive you now, owing to the exigencies of time.

sanskrit: अवोचस्त्वं पाण्डवार्थेऽप्रियाणि प्रधर्षयन्मां मूढवत्पापकर्मन् ।मय्यार्जवे जिह्मगतिर्हतस्त्वं मित्रद्रोही सप्तपदं हि मित्रम् ॥२१॥
transliteration: avocastvaṁ pāṇḍavārthe'priyāṇi; pradharṣayanmāṁ mūḍhavatpāpakarman ,mayyārjave jihmagatirhatastvaṁ; mitradrohī saptapadaṁ hi mitram.
english: You spoke disagreeable words for the sake of the Pāṇḍavas, O evil-doer, assailing me like a fool. You, with your deceitful conduct, are ruined in my presence, who am honest. You are a betrayer of a friend, for indeed, one becomes a friend after (walking) seven steps.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 29 Verse 21. You spoke disagreeable words for the sake of the Pāṇḍavas, O evil-doer, assailing me like a fool. You, with your deceitful conduct, are ruined in my presence, who am honest. You are a betrayer of a friend, for indeed, one becomes a friend after (walking) seven steps.

sanskrit: कालस्त्वयं मृत्युमयोऽतिदारुणो दुर्योधनो युद्धमुपागमद्यत् ।तस्यार्थसिद्धिमभिकाङ्क्षमाणस्तमभ्येष्ये यत्र नैकान्त्यमस्ति ॥२२॥
transliteration: kālastvayaṁ mṛtyumayo'tidāruṇo; duryodhano yuddhamupāgamadyat ,tasyārthasiddhimabhikāṅkṣamāṇa;stamabhyeṣye yatra naikāntyamasti.
english: Indeed, this time is full of death and exceedingly dreadful, for Duryodhana has approached battle. Desiring his success, I shall follow him to a place where there is no exclusive outcome (no certainty of victory).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 29 Verse 22. Indeed, this time is full of death and exceedingly dreadful, for Duryodhana has approached battle. Desiring his success, I shall follow him to a place where there is no exclusive outcome (no certainty of victory).

sanskrit: मित्रं मिदेर्नन्दतेः प्रीयतेर्वा संत्रायतेर्मानद मोदतेर्वा ।ब्रवीति तच्चामुत विप्रपूर्वात्तच्चापि सर्वं मम दुर्योधनेऽस्ति ॥२३॥
transliteration: mitraṁ midernandateḥ prīyatervā; saṁtrāyatermānada modatervā ,bravīti taccāmuta viprapūrvā;ttaccāpi sarvaṁ mama duryodhane'sti.
english: O giver of honor, the word 'friend' (mitra) is derived from "mid" (to love), or "nand" (to rejoice), or "prī" (to please/love), or "saṃtrā" (to protect completely), or "mod" (to be glad). And this (meaning) is also declared by ancient learned tradition (viprapūrva). And all those qualities (of a friend) are present in my Duryodhana.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 29 Verse 23. O giver of honor, the word 'friend' (mitra) is derived from "mid" (to love), or "nand" (to rejoice), or "prī" (to please/love), or "saṃtrā" (to protect completely), or "mod" (to be glad). And this (meaning) is also declared by ancient learned tradition (viprapūrva). And all those qualities (of a friend) are present in my Duryodhana.

sanskrit: शत्रुः शदेः शासतेः शायतेर्वा शृणातेर्वा श्वयतेर्वापि सर्गे ।उपसर्गाद्बहुधा सूदतेश्च प्रायेण सर्वं त्वयि तच्च मह्यम् ॥२४॥
transliteration: śatruḥ śadeḥ śāsateḥ śāyatervā; śṛṇātervā śvayatervāpi sarge ,upasargādbahudhā sūdateśca; prāyeṇa sarvaṁ tvayi tacca mahyam.
english: The word 'enemy' (śatru) is derived from roots like 'śad' (to cause to fall), 'śās' (to punish), 'śāy' (to cause to lie low or to waste), 'śṛṇā' (to injure), or even 'śvi' (to swell, implying one who causes trouble by growing strong), in its origin. Its meaning is formed in many ways from various prefixes (upasargas) and from the root 'sūd' (to destroy). For the most part, all of these characteristics (of an enemy) are found in you, and that (hostility) is directed towards me.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 29 Verse 24. The word 'enemy' (śatru) is derived from roots like 'śad' (to cause to fall), 'śās' (to punish), 'śāy' (to cause to lie low or to waste), 'śṛṇā' (to injure), or even 'śvi' (to swell, implying one who causes trouble by growing strong), in its origin. Its meaning is formed in many ways from various prefixes (upasargas) and from the root 'sūd' (to destroy). For the most part, all of these characteristics (of an enemy) are found in you, and that (hostility) is directed towards me.

sanskrit: दुर्योधनार्थं तव चाप्रियार्थं यशोर्थमात्मार्थमपीश्वरार्थम् ।तस्मादहं पाण्डववासुदेवौ योत्स्ये यत्नात्कर्म तत्पश्य मेऽद्य ॥२५॥
transliteration: duryodhanārthaṁ tava cāpriyārthaṁ; yaśorthamātmārthamapīśvarārtham ,tasmādahaṁ pāṇḍavavāsudevau; yotsye yatnātkarma tatpaśya me'dya.
english: I will fight diligently against the Pāṇḍava and Vāsudeva (Kṛṣṇa) for Duryodhana's sake, and to cause your displeasure, for fame, and also for my own self (ātman), and for my lord (īśvara). Therefore, behold my action (karma) today.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 29 Verse 25. I will fight diligently against the Pāṇḍava and Vāsudeva (Kṛṣṇa) for Duryodhana's sake, and to cause your displeasure, for fame, and also for my own self (ātman), and for my lord (īśvara). Therefore, behold my action (karma) today.

sanskrit: अस्त्राणि पश्याद्य ममोत्तमानि ब्राह्माणि दिव्यान्यथ मानुषाणि ।आसादयिष्याम्यहमुग्रवीर्यं द्विपोत्तमं मत्तमिवाभिमत्तः ॥२६॥
transliteration: astrāṇi paśyādya mamottamāni; brāhmāṇi divyānyatha mānuṣāṇi ,āsādayiṣyāmyahamugravīryaṁ; dvipottamaṁ mattamivābhimattaḥ.
english: Today, behold my excellent weapons (astras) – those of Brahmā, the celestial (divya), and also the human ones! Like a maddened person, I, who am greatly enraged, will confront and overpower the best of elephants (dvipottama), even if it is fiercely powerful.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 29 Verse 26. Today, behold my excellent weapons (astras) – those of Brahmā, the celestial (divya), and also the human ones! Like a maddened person, I, who am greatly enraged, will confront and overpower the best of elephants (dvipottama), even if it is fiercely powerful.

sanskrit: अस्त्रं ब्राह्मं मनसा तद्ध्यजय्यं क्षेप्स्ये पार्थायाप्रतिमं जयाय ।तेनापि मे नैव मुच्येत युद्धे न चेत्पतेद्विषमे मेऽद्य चक्रम् ॥२७॥
transliteration: astraṁ brāhmaṁ manasā taddhyajayyaṁ; kṣepsye pārthāyāpratimaṁ jayāya ,tenāpi me naiva mucyeta yuddhe; na cetpatedviṣame me'dya cakram.
english: I will mentally launch that unconquerable, unrivaled divine weapon (astra) of Brahmā against Pārtha (Arjuna) for victory. Even with that (astra), Pārtha would certainly not be freed in battle *if* my chariot wheel (cakra) does *not* get stuck in a difficult spot today.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 29 Verse 27. I will mentally launch that unconquerable, unrivaled divine weapon (astra) of Brahmā against Pārtha (Arjuna) for victory. Even with that (astra), Pārtha would certainly not be freed in battle *if* my chariot wheel (cakra) does *not* get stuck in a difficult spot today.
