sanskrit: वैवस्वताद्दण्डहस्ताद्वरुणाद्वापि पाशिनः ।सगदाद्वा धनपतेः सवज्राद्वापि वासवात् ॥२८॥
transliteration: vaivasvatāddaṇḍahastādvaruṇādvāpi pāśinaḥ ,sagadādvā dhanapateḥ savajrādvāpi vāsavāt.
english: From Yama, who holds a staff, or even from Varuṇa, who holds a noose; or from Kubera, the lord of wealth, who holds a mace, or even from Indra (Vāsava), who holds a thunderbolt.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 29 Verse 28. From Yama, who holds a staff, or even from Varuṇa, who holds a noose; or from Kubera, the lord of wealth, who holds a mace, or even from Indra (Vāsava), who holds a thunderbolt.

sanskrit: नान्यस्मादपि कस्माच्चिद्बिभिमो ह्याततायिनः ।इति शल्य विजानीहि यथा नाहं बिभेम्यभीः ॥२९॥
transliteration: nānyasmādapi kasmāccidbibhimo hyātatāyinaḥ ,iti śalya vijānīhi yathā nāhaṁ bibhemyabhīḥ.
english: Indeed, we do not fear any aggressor (ātātāyin), nor anyone else. Therefore, O Śalya, understand that I am fearless and do not fear.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 29 Verse 29. Indeed, we do not fear any aggressor (ātātāyin), nor anyone else. Therefore, O Śalya, understand that I am fearless and do not fear.

sanskrit: तस्माद्भयं न मे पार्थान्नापि चैव जनार्दनात् ।अद्य युद्धं हि ताभ्यां मे संपराये भविष्यति ॥३०॥
transliteration: tasmādbhayaṁ na me pārthānnāpi caiva janārdanāt ,adya yuddhaṁ hi tābhyāṁ me saṁparāye bhaviṣyati.
english: Therefore, I have no fear from Arjuna (Pārtha), nor indeed from Kṛṣṇa (Janārdana). For today, a battle will indeed take place for me against those two in this engagement (saṃparāya).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 29 Verse 30. Therefore, I have no fear from Arjuna (Pārtha), nor indeed from Kṛṣṇa (Janārdana). For today, a battle will indeed take place for me against those two in this engagement (saṃparāya).

sanskrit: श्वभ्रे ते पततां चक्रमिति मे ब्राह्मणोऽवदत् ।युध्यमानस्य संग्रामे प्राप्तस्यैकायने भयम् ॥३१॥
transliteration: śvabhre te patatāṁ cakramiti me brāhmaṇo'vadat ,yudhyamānasya saṁgrāme prāptasyaikāyane bhayam.
english: A Brahmin (brāhmaṇa) told me, 'Let your chariot wheel fall into a pit!' For one fighting in battle, who has reached a desperate situation (ekāyana), fear arises.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 29 Verse 31. A Brahmin (brāhmaṇa) told me, 'Let your chariot wheel fall into a pit!' For one fighting in battle, who has reached a desperate situation (ekāyana), fear arises.

sanskrit: तस्माद्बिभेमि बलवद्ब्राह्मणव्याहृतादहम् ।एते हि सोमराजान ईश्वराः सुखदुःखयोः ॥३२॥
transliteration: tasmādbibhemi balavadbrāhmaṇavyāhṛtādaham ,ete hi somarājāna īśvarāḥ sukhaduḥkhayoḥ.
english: I strongly fear the pronouncements of Brahmins, because these officiants of Soma sacrifices are indeed the masters of both happiness and sorrow.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 29 Verse 32. I strongly fear the pronouncements of Brahmins, because these officiants of Soma sacrifices are indeed the masters of both happiness and sorrow.

sanskrit: होमधेन्वा वत्समस्य प्रमत्त इषुणाहनम् ।चरन्तमजने शल्य ब्राह्मणात्तपसो निधेः ॥३३॥
transliteration: homadhenvā vatsamasya pramatta iṣuṇāhanam ,carantamajane śalya brāhmaṇāttapaso nidheḥ.
english: O Śalya, I, being careless, killed with an arrow the calf of a sacrificial cow, which was wandering in a solitary place. This calf belonged to a Brahmin, a veritable treasure of spiritual austerities (tapas).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 29 Verse 33. O Śalya, I, being careless, killed with an arrow the calf of a sacrificial cow, which was wandering in a solitary place. This calf belonged to a Brahmin, a veritable treasure of spiritual austerities (tapas).

sanskrit: ईषादन्तान्सप्तशतान्दासीदासशतानि च ।ददतो द्विजमुख्याय प्रसादं न चकार मे ॥३४॥
transliteration: īṣādantānsaptaśatāndāsīdāsaśatāni ca ,dadato dvijamukhyāya prasādaṁ na cakāra me.
english: Even after I offered seven hundred bullocks (īṣādantān) with plowshare-like tusks, and hundreds of male and female servants, he (the chief of the twice-born (dvija)) did not grant me his grace (prasāda).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 29 Verse 34. Even after I offered seven hundred bullocks (īṣādantān) with plowshare-like tusks, and hundreds of male and female servants, he (the chief of the twice-born (dvija)) did not grant me his grace (prasāda).

sanskrit: कृष्णानां श्वेतवत्सानां सहस्राणि चतुर्दश ।आहरन्न लभे तस्मात्प्रसादं द्विजसत्तमात् ॥३५॥
transliteration: kṛṣṇānāṁ śvetavatsānāṁ sahasrāṇi caturdaśa ,āharanna labhe tasmātprasādaṁ dvijasattamāt.
english: Even after offering fourteen thousand black cows with white calves, I could not obtain grace (prasāda) from that best of the twice-born (dvija).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 29 Verse 35. Even after offering fourteen thousand black cows with white calves, I could not obtain grace (prasāda) from that best of the twice-born (dvija).

sanskrit: ऋद्धं गेहं सर्वकामैर्यच्च मे वसु किंचन ।तत्सर्वमस्मै सत्कृत्य प्रयच्छामि न चेच्छति ॥३६॥
transliteration: ṛddhaṁ gehaṁ sarvakāmairyacca me vasu kiṁcana ,tatsarvamasmai satkṛtya prayacchāmi na cecchati.
english: My prosperous house, complete with all comforts, and whatever little wealth I possess, all of that I respectfully offer to him, if he does not desire it.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 29 Verse 36. My prosperous house, complete with all comforts, and whatever little wealth I possess, all of that I respectfully offer to him, if he does not desire it.

sanskrit: ततोऽब्रवीन्मां याचन्तमपराद्धं प्रयत्नतः ।व्याहृतं यन्मया सूत तत्तथा न तदन्यथा ॥३७॥
transliteration: tato'bravīnmāṁ yācantamaparāddhaṁ prayatnataḥ ,vyāhṛtaṁ yanmayā sūta tattathā na tadanyathā.
english: Then he said to me, as I was begging, 'You have committed a transgression through your persistence. Whatever was spoken by me, O charioteer (sūta), that is truly so; it cannot be otherwise.'
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 29 Verse 37. Then he said to me, as I was begging, 'You have committed a transgression through your persistence. Whatever was spoken by me, O charioteer (sūta), that is truly so; it cannot be otherwise.'

sanskrit: अनृतोक्तं प्रजा हन्यात्ततः पापमवाप्नुयात् ।तस्माद्धर्माभिरक्षार्थं नानृतं वक्तुमुत्सहे ॥३८॥
transliteration: anṛtoktaṁ prajā hanyāttataḥ pāpamavāpnuyāt ,tasmāddharmābhirakṣārthaṁ nānṛtaṁ vaktumutsahe.
english: Speaking an untruth (anṛta) would destroy people, and from that, one would incur sin. Therefore, for the sake of upholding the natural law (dharma), I am not able to speak a falsehood.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 29 Verse 38. Speaking an untruth (anṛta) would destroy people, and from that, one would incur sin. Therefore, for the sake of upholding the natural law (dharma), I am not able to speak a falsehood.

sanskrit: मा त्वं ब्रह्मगतिं हिंस्याः प्रायश्चित्तं कृतं त्वया ।मद्वाक्यं नानृतं लोके कश्चित्कुर्यात्समाप्नुहि ॥३९॥
transliteration: mā tvaṁ brahmagatiṁ hiṁsyāḥ prāyaścittaṁ kṛtaṁ tvayā ,madvākyaṁ nānṛtaṁ loke kaścitkuryātsamāpnuhi.
english: Do not hinder the spiritual path (brahmagati) of a Brahmin. Atonement (prāyaścitta) has already been performed by you. Let no one in the world render my words false. Therefore, fulfill your purpose!
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 29 Verse 39. Do not hinder the spiritual path (brahmagati) of a Brahmin. Atonement (prāyaścitta) has already been performed by you. Let no one in the world render my words false. Therefore, fulfill your purpose!

sanskrit: इत्येतत्ते मया प्रोक्तं क्षिप्तेनापि सुहृत्तया ।जानामि त्वाधिक्षिपन्तं जोषमास्स्वोत्तरं शृणु ॥४०॥
transliteration: ityetatte mayā proktaṁ kṣiptenāpi suhṛttayā ,jānāmi tvādhikṣipantaṁ joṣamāssvottaraṁ śṛṇu.
english: I have said this to you out of friendship, even though you have insulted me. I understand that you are reviling me; now be silent and listen to what comes next.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 29 Verse 40. I have said this to you out of friendship, even though you have insulted me. I understand that you are reviling me; now be silent and listen to what comes next.

sanskrit: संजय उवाच ।ततः पुनर्महाराज मद्रराजमरिंदमम् ।अभ्यभाषत राधेयः संनिवार्योत्तरं वचः ॥१॥
transliteration: saṁjaya uvāca ,tataḥ punarmahārāja madrarājamariṁdamam ,abhyabhāṣata rādheyaḥ saṁnivāryottaraṁ vacaḥ.
english: Sanjaya said: O great king (Dhritarashtra), then again, Radheya (Karna), having restrained the Madra king (Shalya), that subduer of enemies, spoke these following words.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 30 Verse 1. Sanjaya said: O great king (Dhritarashtra), then again, Radheya (Karna), having restrained the Madra king (Shalya), that subduer of enemies, spoke these following words.

sanskrit: यत्त्वं निदर्शनार्थं मां शल्य जल्पितवानसि ।नाहं शक्यस्त्वया वाचा विभीषयितुमाहवे ॥२॥
transliteration: yattvaṁ nidarśanārthaṁ māṁ śalya jalpitavānasi ,nāhaṁ śakyastvayā vācā vibhīṣayitumāhave.
english: O Shalya, as for what you have spoken to me by way of example, I cannot be intimidated by your words in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 30 Verse 2. O Shalya, as for what you have spoken to me by way of example, I cannot be intimidated by your words in battle.

sanskrit: यदि मां देवताः सर्वा योधयेयुः सवासवाः ।तथापि मे भयं न स्यात्किमु पार्थात्सकेशवात् ॥३॥
transliteration: yadi māṁ devatāḥ sarvā yodhayeyuḥ savāsavāḥ ,tathāpi me bhayaṁ na syātkimu pārthātsakeśavāt.
english: Even if all the gods, including Indra, were to fight against me, I would still have no fear. How much less, then, should I fear Partha (Arjuna) who is accompanied by Keshava (Krishna)?
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 30 Verse 3. Even if all the gods, including Indra, were to fight against me, I would still have no fear. How much less, then, should I fear Partha (Arjuna) who is accompanied by Keshava (Krishna)?

sanskrit: नाहं भीषयितुं शक्यो वाङ्मात्रेण कथंचन ।अन्यं जानीहि यः शक्यस्त्वया भीषयितुं रणे ॥४॥
transliteration: nāhaṁ bhīṣayituṁ śakyo vāṅmātreṇa kathaṁcana ,anyaṁ jānīhi yaḥ śakyastvayā bhīṣayituṁ raṇe.
english: I am certainly not capable of being intimidated by mere words in any way. Know that someone else can be intimidated by you in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 30 Verse 4. I am certainly not capable of being intimidated by mere words in any way. Know that someone else can be intimidated by you in battle.

sanskrit: नीचस्य बलमेतावत्पारुष्यं यत्त्वमात्थ माम् ।अशक्तोऽस्मद्गुणान्प्राप्तुं वल्गसे बहु दुर्मते ॥५॥
transliteration: nīcasya balametāvatpāruṣyaṁ yattvamāttha mām ,aśakto'smadguṇānprāptuṁ valgase bahu durmate.
english: This harshness with which you speak to me is the extent of a base person's strength. O evil-minded one, you are unable to achieve my virtues, yet you boast excessively.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 30 Verse 5. This harshness with which you speak to me is the extent of a base person's strength. O evil-minded one, you are unable to achieve my virtues, yet you boast excessively.

sanskrit: न हि कर्णः समुद्भूतो भयार्थमिह मारिष ।विक्रमार्थमहं जातो यशोर्थं च तथैव च ॥६॥
transliteration: na hi karṇaḥ samudbhūto bhayārthamiha māriṣa ,vikramārthamahaṁ jāto yaśorthaṁ ca tathaiva ca.
english: Indeed, Karṇa was not born here, O respectable one, for the purpose of fear. I was born for the purpose of valor and likewise for the purpose of fame.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 30 Verse 6. Indeed, Karṇa was not born here, O respectable one, for the purpose of fear. I was born for the purpose of valor and likewise for the purpose of fame.

sanskrit: इदं तु मे त्वमेकाग्रः शृणु मद्रजनाधिप ।संनिधौ धृतराष्ट्रस्य प्रोच्यमानं मया श्रुतम् ॥७॥
transliteration: idaṁ tu me tvamekāgraḥ śṛṇu madrajanādhipa ,saṁnidhau dhṛtarāṣṭrasya procyamānaṁ mayā śrutam.
english: But you, O king of the Madras, listen to these words from me with concentrated attention. I heard this being spoken in the presence of Dhṛtarāṣṭra.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 30 Verse 7. But you, O king of the Madras, listen to these words from me with concentrated attention. I heard this being spoken in the presence of Dhṛtarāṣṭra.

sanskrit: देशांश्च विविधांश्चित्रान्पूर्ववृत्तांश्च पार्थिवान् ।ब्राह्मणाः कथयन्तः स्म धृतराष्ट्रमुपासते ॥८॥
transliteration: deśāṁśca vividhāṁścitrānpūrvavṛttāṁśca pārthivān ,brāhmaṇāḥ kathayantaḥ sma dhṛtarāṣṭramupāsate.
english: The Brahmins serve (upāsate) Dhṛtarāṣṭra, continuously narrating (kathayantaḥ sma) stories about various, wonderful lands and ancient kings.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 30 Verse 8. The Brahmins serve (upāsate) Dhṛtarāṣṭra, continuously narrating (kathayantaḥ sma) stories about various, wonderful lands and ancient kings.

sanskrit: तत्र वृद्धः पुरावृत्ताः कथाः काश्चिद्द्विजोत्तमः ।बाह्लीकदेशं मद्रांश्च कुत्सयन्वाक्यमब्रवीत् ॥९॥
transliteration: tatra vṛddhaḥ purāvṛttāḥ kathāḥ kāściddvijottamaḥ ,bāhlīkadeśaṁ madrāṁśca kutsayanvākyamabravīt.
english: There, an old, excellent Brahmin (dvijottama) spoke, disparaging (kutsayan) the Bāhlīka country and the Madras, while narrating some ancient stories.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 30 Verse 9. There, an old, excellent Brahmin (dvijottama) spoke, disparaging (kutsayan) the Bāhlīka country and the Madras, while narrating some ancient stories.

sanskrit: बहिष्कृता हिमवता गङ्गया च तिरस्कृताः ।सरस्वत्या यमुनया कुरुक्षेत्रेण चापि ये ॥१०॥
transliteration: bahiṣkṛtā himavatā gaṅgayā ca tiraskṛtāḥ ,sarasvatyā yamunayā kurukṣetreṇa cāpi ye.
english: Those who are excluded (bahiṣkṛtāḥ) by the Himālaya and rejected (tiraskṛtāḥ) by the Gaṅgā, and also by the Sarasvatī, Yamunā, and Kurukṣetra (are despised).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 30 Verse 10. Those who are excluded (bahiṣkṛtāḥ) by the Himālaya and rejected (tiraskṛtāḥ) by the Gaṅgā, and also by the Sarasvatī, Yamunā, and Kurukṣetra (are despised).

sanskrit: पञ्चानां सिन्धुषष्ठानां नदीनां येऽन्तराश्रिताः ।तान्धर्मबाह्यानशुचीन्बाह्लीकान्परिवर्जयेत् ॥११॥
transliteration: pañcānāṁ sindhuṣaṣṭhānāṁ nadīnāṁ ye'ntarāśritāḥ ,tāndharmabāhyānaśucīnbāhlīkānparivarjayet.
english: One should completely avoid (parivarjayet) those Bāhlīkas who reside between the five rivers, with the Sindhu as the sixth, as they are outside (dharma) (dharmabāhyān) and impure.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 30 Verse 11. One should completely avoid (parivarjayet) those Bāhlīkas who reside between the five rivers, with the Sindhu as the sixth, as they are outside (dharma) (dharmabāhyān) and impure.

sanskrit: गोवर्धनो नाम वटः सुभाण्डं नाम चत्वरम् ।एतद्राजकुलद्वारमाकुमारः स्मराम्यहम् ॥१२॥
transliteration: govardhano nāma vaṭaḥ subhāṇḍaṁ nāma catvaram ,etadrājakuladvāramākumāraḥ smarāmyaham.
english: I remember, since my childhood, a banyan tree named Govardhana, a square named Subhāṇḍa, and this gate of the royal palace.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 30 Verse 12. I remember, since my childhood, a banyan tree named Govardhana, a square named Subhāṇḍa, and this gate of the royal palace.

sanskrit: कार्येणात्यर्थगाढेन बाह्लीकेषूषितं मया ।तत एषां समाचारः संवासाद्विदितो मम ॥१३॥
transliteration: kāryeṇātyarthagāḍhena bāhlīkeṣūṣitaṁ mayā ,tata eṣāṁ samācāraḥ saṁvāsādvidito mama.
english: I resided among the Bāhlīkas because of a very urgent matter. Consequently, I learned their customs and conduct through my close association (saṃvāsa) with them.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 30 Verse 13. I resided among the Bāhlīkas because of a very urgent matter. Consequently, I learned their customs and conduct through my close association (saṃvāsa) with them.

sanskrit: शाकलं नाम नगरमापगा नाम निम्नगा ।जर्तिका नाम बाह्लीकास्तेषां वृत्तं सुनिन्दितम् ॥१४॥
transliteration: śākalaṁ nāma nagaramāpagā nāma nimnagā ,jartikā nāma bāhlīkāsteṣāṁ vṛttaṁ suninditam.
english: There is a city called Śākala, and a river known as Āpagā. These Bāhlīkas, who are known as Jartikā, have conduct that is extremely reprehensible.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 30 Verse 14. There is a city called Śākala, and a river known as Āpagā. These Bāhlīkas, who are known as Jartikā, have conduct that is extremely reprehensible.

sanskrit: धानागौडासवे पीत्वा गोमांसं लशुनैः सह ।अपूपमांसवाट्यानामाशिनः शीलवर्जिताः ॥१५॥
transliteration: dhānāgauḍāsave pītvā gomāṁsaṁ laśunaiḥ saha ,apūpamāṁsavāṭyānāmāśinaḥ śīlavarjitāḥ.
english: Having consumed liquor made from parched barley and molasses, and eaten beef with garlic, along with cakes, meat, and vāṭya (a kind of cake), they are utterly devoid of moral conduct (śīla).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 30 Verse 15. Having consumed liquor made from parched barley and molasses, and eaten beef with garlic, along with cakes, meat, and vāṭya (a kind of cake), they are utterly devoid of moral conduct (śīla).

sanskrit: हसन्ति गान्ति नृत्यन्ति स्त्रीभिर्मत्ता विवाससः ।नगरागारवप्रेषु बहिर्माल्यानुलेपनाः ॥१६॥
transliteration: hasanti gānti nṛtyanti strībhirmattā vivāsasaḥ ,nagarāgāravapreṣu bahirmālyānulepanāḥ.
english: Intoxicated and naked, with external garlands and anointments, they laugh, sing, and dance with women on the ramparts of cities and houses.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 30 Verse 16. Intoxicated and naked, with external garlands and anointments, they laugh, sing, and dance with women on the ramparts of cities and houses.

sanskrit: मत्तावगीतैर्विविधैः खरोष्ट्रनिनदोपमैः ।आहुरन्योन्यमुक्तानि प्रब्रुवाणा मदोत्कटाः ॥१७॥
transliteration: mattāvagītairvividhaiḥ kharoṣṭraninadopamaiḥ ,āhuranyonyamuktāni prabruvāṇā madotkaṭāḥ.
english: Overcome by intense intoxication, they speak to one another, uttering various maddened and abusive words that resemble the braying of donkeys and camels.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 30 Verse 17. Overcome by intense intoxication, they speak to one another, uttering various maddened and abusive words that resemble the braying of donkeys and camels.

sanskrit: हा हते हा हतेत्येव स्वामिभर्तृहतेति च ।आक्रोशन्त्यः प्रनृत्यन्ति मन्दाः पर्वस्वसंयताः ॥१८॥
transliteration: hā hate hā hatetyeva svāmibhartṛhateti ca ,ākrośantyaḥ pranṛtyanti mandāḥ parvasvasaṁyatāḥ.
english: The foolish and unrestrained (women), crying out 'Alas, he is slain! Alas, he is slain!' and 'Alas, my husband and master are slain!', dance excessively during festivals.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 30 Verse 18. The foolish and unrestrained (women), crying out 'Alas, he is slain! Alas, he is slain!' and 'Alas, my husband and master are slain!', dance excessively during festivals.

sanskrit: तेषां किलावलिप्तानां निवसन्कुरुजाङ्गले ।कश्चिद्बाह्लीकमुख्यानां नातिहृष्टमना जगौ ॥१९॥
transliteration: teṣāṁ kilāvaliptānāṁ nivasankurujāṅgale ,kaścidbāhlīkamukhyānāṁ nātihṛṣṭamanā jagau.
english: It is said that a certain person, dwelling in Kurujāṅgala among those arrogant chiefs of the Bāhlīkas, sang with a mind not exceedingly joyful.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 30 Verse 19. It is said that a certain person, dwelling in Kurujāṅgala among those arrogant chiefs of the Bāhlīkas, sang with a mind not exceedingly joyful.

sanskrit: सा नूनं बृहती गौरी सूक्ष्मकम्बलवासिनी ।मामनुस्मरती शेते बाह्लीकं कुरुवासिनम् ॥२०॥
transliteration: sā nūnaṁ bṛhatī gaurī sūkṣmakambalavāsinī ,māmanusmaratī śete bāhlīkaṁ kuruvāsinam.
english: That tall, fair-complexioned woman, wrapped in a fine woolen blanket, surely lies remembering me, the Bāhlīka man who dwells in the Kuru country.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 30 Verse 20. That tall, fair-complexioned woman, wrapped in a fine woolen blanket, surely lies remembering me, the Bāhlīka man who dwells in the Kuru country.

sanskrit: शतद्रुकनदीं तीर्त्वा तां च रम्यामिरावतीम् ।गत्वा स्वदेशं द्रक्ष्यामि स्थूलशङ्खाः शुभाः स्त्रियः ॥२१॥
transliteration: śatadrukanadīṁ tīrtvā tāṁ ca ramyāmirāvatīm ,gatvā svadeśaṁ drakṣyāmi sthūlaśaṅkhāḥ śubhāḥ striyaḥ.
english: After crossing the Satadru river and that beautiful Iravati, and having returned to my own land, I will see the auspicious women, whose arms are adorned with thick conch-shell bracelets.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 30 Verse 21. After crossing the Satadru river and that beautiful Iravati, and having returned to my own land, I will see the auspicious women, whose arms are adorned with thick conch-shell bracelets.

sanskrit: मनःशिलोज्ज्वलापाङ्गा गौर्यस्त्रिककुदाञ्जनाः ।केवलाजिनसंवीताः कूर्दन्त्यः प्रियदर्शनाः ॥२२॥
transliteration: manaḥśilojjvalāpāṅgā gauryastrikakudāñjanāḥ ,kevalājinasaṁvītāḥ kūrdantyaḥ priyadarśanāḥ.
english: The fair-complexioned women, with eye-corners bright as realgar, their eyes darkened with a triple-peaked application of collyrium, clad only in deerskins, are leaping about, lovely to behold.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 30 Verse 22. The fair-complexioned women, with eye-corners bright as realgar, their eyes darkened with a triple-peaked application of collyrium, clad only in deerskins, are leaping about, lovely to behold.

sanskrit: मृदङ्गानकशङ्खानां मर्दलानां च निस्वनैः ।खरोष्ट्राश्वतरैश्चैव मत्ता यास्यामहे सुखम् ॥२३॥
transliteration: mṛdaṅgānakaśaṅkhānāṁ mardalānāṁ ca nisvanaiḥ ,kharoṣṭrāśvataraiścaiva mattā yāsyāmahe sukham.
english: To the sounds of mṛdaṅgas, ānakas, conch shells, and mardalas, and indeed, carried by donkeys, camels, and mules, we will go joyfully and comfortably.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 30 Verse 23. To the sounds of mṛdaṅgas, ānakas, conch shells, and mardalas, and indeed, carried by donkeys, camels, and mules, we will go joyfully and comfortably.

sanskrit: शमीपीलुकरीराणां वनेषु सुखवर्त्मसु ।अपूपान्सक्तुपिण्डीश्च खादन्तो मथितान्विताः ॥२४॥
transliteration: śamīpīlukarīrāṇāṁ vaneṣu sukhavartmasu ,apūpānsaktupiṇḍīśca khādanto mathitānvitāḥ.
english: They eat cakes and balls of ground flour, accompanied by buttermilk, in the forests where śamī, pīlu, and karīra trees grow, and where the paths are pleasant.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 30 Verse 24. They eat cakes and balls of ground flour, accompanied by buttermilk, in the forests where śamī, pīlu, and karīra trees grow, and where the paths are pleasant.

sanskrit: पथिषु प्रबला भूत्वा कदासमृदितेऽध्वनि ।खलोपहारं कुर्वाणास्ताडयिष्याम भूयसः ॥२५॥
transliteration: pathiṣu prabalā bhūtvā kadāsamṛdite'dhvani ,khalopahāraṁ kurvāṇāstāḍayiṣyāma bhūyasaḥ.
english: Having become powerful on the paths, and taking tribute from villains on rarely frequented roads, we shall strike the many (of them).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 30 Verse 25. Having become powerful on the paths, and taking tribute from villains on rarely frequented roads, we shall strike the many (of them).

sanskrit: एवं हीनेषु व्रात्येषु बाह्लीकेषु दुरात्मसु ।कश्चेतयानो निवसेन्मुहूर्तमपि मानवः ॥२६॥
transliteration: evaṁ hīneṣu vrātyeṣu bāhlīkeṣu durātmasu ,kaścetayāno nivasenmuhūrtamapi mānavaḥ.
english: Among such degraded, outcast, and evil-minded Bāhlīkas, what discerning human being would dwell even for a moment?
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 30 Verse 26. Among such degraded, outcast, and evil-minded Bāhlīkas, what discerning human being would dwell even for a moment?

sanskrit: ईदृशा ब्राह्मणेनोक्ता बाह्लीका मोघचारिणः ।येषां षड्भागहर्ता त्वमुभयोः शुभपापयोः ॥२७॥
transliteration: īdṛśā brāhmaṇenoktā bāhlīkā moghacāriṇaḥ ,yeṣāṁ ṣaḍbhāgahartā tvamubhayoḥ śubhapāpayoḥ.
english: Such Bāhlīkas, whose actions are futile, have been described by Brahmins. You (Shalya) are the one who collects a sixth share of both their merit and their sin.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 30 Verse 27. Such Bāhlīkas, whose actions are futile, have been described by Brahmins. You (Shalya) are the one who collects a sixth share of both their merit and their sin.

sanskrit: इत्युक्त्वा ब्राह्मणः साधुरुत्तरं पुनरुक्तवान् ।बाह्लीकेष्वविनीतेषु प्रोच्यमानं निबोधत ॥२८॥
transliteration: ityuktvā brāhmaṇaḥ sādhuruttaraṁ punaruktavān ,bāhlīkeṣvavinīteṣu procyamānaṁ nibodhata.
english: Having spoken thus, the virtuous Brahmin again gave a reply: 'Understand what is being said among the ill-behaved Bahlīkas.'
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 30 Verse 28. Having spoken thus, the virtuous Brahmin again gave a reply: 'Understand what is being said among the ill-behaved Bahlīkas.'

sanskrit: तत्र स्म राक्षसी गाति सदा कृष्णचतुर्दशीम् ।नगरे शाकले स्फीते आहत्य निशि दुन्दुभिम् ॥२९॥
transliteration: tatra sma rākṣasī gāti sadā kṛṣṇacaturdaśīm ,nagare śākale sphīte āhatya niśi dundubhim.
english: There, a female demon (rākṣasī) used to sing constantly on the Kṛṣṇa Caturdaśī, having beaten a drum at night in the prosperous city of Śākala.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 30 Verse 29. There, a female demon (rākṣasī) used to sing constantly on the Kṛṣṇa Caturdaśī, having beaten a drum at night in the prosperous city of Śākala.

sanskrit: कदा वा घोषिका गाथाः पुनर्गास्यन्ति शाकले ।गव्यस्य तृप्ता मांसस्य पीत्वा गौडं महासवम् ॥३०॥
transliteration: kadā vā ghoṣikā gāthāḥ punargāsyanti śākale ,gavyasya tṛptā māṁsasya pītvā gauḍaṁ mahāsavam.
english: When will the dairy women (ghoṣikāḥ) in Śākala again sing songs, after having eaten their fill of beef and drunk the strong jaggery liquor (mahāsava)?
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 30 Verse 30. When will the dairy women (ghoṣikāḥ) in Śākala again sing songs, after having eaten their fill of beef and drunk the strong jaggery liquor (mahāsava)?

sanskrit: गौरीभिः सह नारीभिर्बृहतीभिः स्वलंकृताः ।पलाण्डुगण्डूषयुतान्खादन्ते चैडकान्बहून् ॥३१॥
transliteration: gaurībhiḥ saha nārībhirbṛhatībhiḥ svalaṁkṛtāḥ ,palāṇḍugaṇḍūṣayutānkhādante caiḍakānbahūn.
english: And, well-adorned, they eat many lambs (eḍaka) that are served with mouthfuls of onion, together with fair-complexioned and stout women.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 30 Verse 31. And, well-adorned, they eat many lambs (eḍaka) that are served with mouthfuls of onion, together with fair-complexioned and stout women.

sanskrit: वाराहं कौक्कुटं मांसं गव्यं गार्दभमौष्ट्रकम् ।ऐडं च ये न खादन्ति तेषां जन्म निरर्थकम् ॥३२॥
transliteration: vārāhaṁ kaukkuṭaṁ māṁsaṁ gavyaṁ gārdabhamauṣṭrakam ,aiḍaṁ ca ye na khādanti teṣāṁ janma nirarthakam.
english: Those who do not consume pork, chicken meat, beef, donkey meat, camel meat, and goat or lamb meat - their birth (janma) is considered meaningless.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 30 Verse 32. Those who do not consume pork, chicken meat, beef, donkey meat, camel meat, and goat or lamb meat - their birth (janma) is considered meaningless.

sanskrit: इति गायन्ति ये मत्ताः शीधुना शाकलावतः ।सबालवृद्धाः कूर्दन्तस्तेषु वृत्तं कथं भवेत् ॥३३॥
transliteration: iti gāyanti ye mattāḥ śīdhunā śākalāvataḥ ,sabālavṛddhāḥ kūrdantasteṣu vṛttaṁ kathaṁ bhavet.
english: How can proper conduct exist among those who, intoxicated by the liquor of Śākala, sing, while young and old alike leap about?
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 30 Verse 33. How can proper conduct exist among those who, intoxicated by the liquor of Śākala, sing, while young and old alike leap about?

sanskrit: इति शल्य विजानीहि हन्त भूयो ब्रवीमि ते ।यदन्योऽप्युक्तवानस्मान्ब्राह्मणः कुरुसंसदि ॥३४॥
transliteration: iti śalya vijānīhi hanta bhūyo bravīmi te ,yadanyo'pyuktavānasmānbrāhmaṇaḥ kurusaṁsadi.
english: Therefore, O Śalya, understand this! Indeed, I will tell you further what another Brahmin also spoke to us in the Kuru assembly.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 30 Verse 34. Therefore, O Śalya, understand this! Indeed, I will tell you further what another Brahmin also spoke to us in the Kuru assembly.

sanskrit: पञ्च नद्यो वहन्त्येता यत्र पीलुवनान्यपि ।शतद्रुश्च विपाशा च तृतीयेरावती तथा ।चन्द्रभागा वितस्ता च सिन्धुषष्ठा बहिर्गताः ॥३५॥
transliteration: pañca nadyo vahantyetā yatra pīluvanānyapi ,śatadruśca vipāśā ca tṛtīyerāvatī tathā ,candrabhāgā vitastā ca sindhuṣaṣṭhā bahirgatāḥ.
english: Where Pīlu forests also exist, these five rivers flow: the Śatadru (Sutlej), the Vipāśā (Beas), and the third, the Irāvatī (Ravi), as well as the Candrabhāgā (Chenab) and the Vitastā (Jhelum). Along with the Sindhu (Indus) as the sixth, they all flow outwards.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 30 Verse 35. Where Pīlu forests also exist, these five rivers flow: the Śatadru (Sutlej), the Vipāśā (Beas), and the third, the Irāvatī (Ravi), as well as the Candrabhāgā (Chenab) and the Vitastā (Jhelum). Along with the Sindhu (Indus) as the sixth, they all flow outwards.

sanskrit: आरट्टा नाम ते देशा नष्टधर्मान्न तान्व्रजेत् ।व्रात्यानां दासमीयानां विदेहानामयज्वनाम् ॥३६॥
transliteration: āraṭṭā nāma te deśā naṣṭadharmānna tānvrajet ,vrātyānāṁ dāsamīyānāṁ videhānāmayajvanām.
english: Those regions, named Āraṭṭa, are devoid of natural law (dharma); therefore, one should not visit them. They belong to "vrātyas" (those who have deviated from orthodox practices), "dāsamīyas" (a specific group or those associated with servants), the Videhas, and those who do not perform Vedic rituals (yajña).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 30 Verse 36. Those regions, named Āraṭṭa, are devoid of natural law (dharma); therefore, one should not visit them. They belong to "vrātyas" (those who have deviated from orthodox practices), "dāsamīyas" (a specific group or those associated with servants), the Videhas, and those who do not perform Vedic rituals (yajña).

sanskrit: न देवाः प्रतिगृह्णन्ति पितरो ब्राह्मणास्तथा ।तेषां प्रनष्टधर्माणां बाह्लीकानामिति श्रुतिः ॥३७॥
transliteration: na devāḥ pratigṛhṇanti pitaro brāhmaṇāstathā ,teṣāṁ pranaṣṭadharmāṇāṁ bāhlīkānāmiti śrutiḥ.
english: The gods do not accept (offerings), nor do the ancestors or Brahmins (brāhmaṇa), from those Bāhlīkas whose intrinsic nature (dharma) has been utterly corrupted. Such is the sacred tradition (śruti).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 30 Verse 37. The gods do not accept (offerings), nor do the ancestors or Brahmins (brāhmaṇa), from those Bāhlīkas whose intrinsic nature (dharma) has been utterly corrupted. Such is the sacred tradition (śruti).

sanskrit: ब्राह्मणेन तथा प्रोक्तं विदुषा साधुसंसदि ।काष्ठकुण्डेषु बाह्लीका मृण्मयेषु च भुञ्जते ।सक्तुवाट्यावलिप्तेषु श्वादिलीढेषु निर्घृणाः ॥३८॥
transliteration: brāhmaṇena tathā proktaṁ viduṣā sādhusaṁsadi ,kāṣṭhakuṇḍeṣu bāhlīkā mṛṇmayeṣu ca bhuñjate ,saktuvāṭyāvalipteṣu śvādilīḍheṣu nirghṛṇāḥ.
english: A wise Brahmin (brāhmaṇa) declared this in an assembly of virtuous people: "The Bāhlīkas, being ruthless, eat from wooden and earthen bowls that are smeared with barley-gruel and licked by dogs and other animals."
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 30 Verse 38. A wise Brahmin (brāhmaṇa) declared this in an assembly of virtuous people: "The Bāhlīkas, being ruthless, eat from wooden and earthen bowls that are smeared with barley-gruel and licked by dogs and other animals."

sanskrit: आविकं चौष्ट्रिकं चैव क्षीरं गार्दभमेव च ।तद्विकारांश्च बाह्लीकाः खादन्ति च पिबन्ति च ॥३९॥
transliteration: āvikaṁ cauṣṭrikaṁ caiva kṣīraṁ gārdabhameva ca ,tadvikārāṁśca bāhlīkāḥ khādanti ca pibanti ca.
english: The Bāhlīkas eat and drink sheep's milk, camel's milk, and donkey's milk, as well as products derived from them.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 30 Verse 39. The Bāhlīkas eat and drink sheep's milk, camel's milk, and donkey's milk, as well as products derived from them.

sanskrit: पुत्रसंकरिणो जाल्माः सर्वान्नक्षीरभोजनाः ।आरट्टा नाम बाह्लीका वर्जनीया विपश्चिता ॥४०॥
transliteration: putrasaṁkariṇo jālmāḥ sarvānnakṣīrabhojanāḥ ,āraṭṭā nāma bāhlīkā varjanīyā vipaścitā.
english: The cruel and despicable Bāhlīka people called Āraṭṭas, who allow the intermixing of offspring (putrasaṃkariṇaḥ) and consume all kinds of food and milk indiscriminately, should be avoided by a wise person.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 30 Verse 40. The cruel and despicable Bāhlīka people called Āraṭṭas, who allow the intermixing of offspring (putrasaṃkariṇaḥ) and consume all kinds of food and milk indiscriminately, should be avoided by a wise person.

sanskrit: उत शल्य विजानीहि हन्त भूयो ब्रवीमि ते ।यदन्योऽप्युक्तवान्सभ्यो ब्राह्मणः कुरुसंसदि ॥४१॥
transliteration: uta śalya vijānīhi hanta bhūyo bravīmi te ,yadanyo'pyuktavānsabhyo brāhmaṇaḥ kurusaṁsadi.
english: O Śalya, pay heed! Moreover, I shall tell you further what another respectable brāhmin also spoke in the assembly (saṃsad) of the Kurus.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 30 Verse 41. O Śalya, pay heed! Moreover, I shall tell you further what another respectable brāhmin also spoke in the assembly (saṃsad) of the Kurus.

sanskrit: युगंधरे पयः पीत्वा प्रोष्य चाप्यच्युतस्थले ।तद्वद्भूतिलये स्नात्वा कथं स्वर्गं गमिष्यति ॥४२॥
transliteration: yugaṁdhare payaḥ pītvā proṣya cāpyacyutasthale ,tadvadbhūtilaye snātvā kathaṁ svargaṁ gamiṣyati.
english: Having drunk milk in Yugaṃdhara, and also having dwelt in Acyutasthala, and similarly having bathed in Bhūtilaya, how will he attain heaven (svarga)?
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 30 Verse 42. Having drunk milk in Yugaṃdhara, and also having dwelt in Acyutasthala, and similarly having bathed in Bhūtilaya, how will he attain heaven (svarga)?

sanskrit: पञ्च नद्यो वहन्त्येता यत्र निःसृत्य पर्वतात् ।आरट्टा नाम बाह्लीका न तेष्वार्यो द्व्यहं वसेत् ॥४३॥
transliteration: pañca nadyo vahantyetā yatra niḥsṛtya parvatāt ,āraṭṭā nāma bāhlīkā na teṣvāryo dvyahaṁ vaset.
english: Where these five rivers flow, having emerged from a mountain, among those Āraṭṭas (people) of Bāhlīka, a noble person (ārya) should not dwell for even two days.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 30 Verse 43. Where these five rivers flow, having emerged from a mountain, among those Āraṭṭas (people) of Bāhlīka, a noble person (ārya) should not dwell for even two days.

sanskrit: बहिश्च नाम ह्लीकश्च विपाशायां पिशाचकौ ।तयोरपत्यं बाह्लीका नैषा सृष्टिः प्रजापतेः ॥४४॥
transliteration: bahiśca nāma hlīkaśca vipāśāyāṁ piśācakau ,tayorapatyaṁ bāhlīkā naiṣā sṛṣṭiḥ prajāpateḥ.
english: There were two fiends (piśācas) named Bahiṣ and Hlīka living in the region of the Vipāśā river. Their descendants are the Bāhlīkas. This particular creation (sṛṣṭi) is not attributed to Prajāpati.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 30 Verse 44. There were two fiends (piśācas) named Bahiṣ and Hlīka living in the region of the Vipāśā river. Their descendants are the Bāhlīkas. This particular creation (sṛṣṭi) is not attributed to Prajāpati.

sanskrit: कारस्करान्महिषकान्कलिङ्गान्कीकटाटवीन् ।कर्कोटकान्वीरकांश्च दुर्धर्मांश्च विवर्जयेत् ॥४५॥
transliteration: kāraskarānmahiṣakānkaliṅgānkīkaṭāṭavīn ,karkoṭakānvīrakāṁśca durdharmāṁśca vivarjayet.
english: One should avoid the Kāraskaras, Mahiṣakas, Kaliṅgas, and the forest dwellers of Kīkaṭa, as well as the Karkoṭakas, Vīrakas, and those who adhere to corrupted (dharma) practices.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 30 Verse 45. One should avoid the Kāraskaras, Mahiṣakas, Kaliṅgas, and the forest dwellers of Kīkaṭa, as well as the Karkoṭakas, Vīrakas, and those who adhere to corrupted (dharma) practices.

sanskrit: इति तीर्थानुसर्तारं राक्षसी काचिदब्रवीत् ।एकरात्रा शमीगेहे महोलूखलमेखला ॥४६॥
transliteration: iti tīrthānusartāraṁ rākṣasī kācidabravīt ,ekarātrā śamīgehe maholūkhalamekhalā.
english: Thus, a certain demoness (rākṣasī) spoke to a pilgrim (tīrthānusartāram)  -  a demoness who had spent one night in a Śamī tree house, and whose girdle was a mighty pestle.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 30 Verse 46. Thus, a certain demoness (rākṣasī) spoke to a pilgrim (tīrthānusartāram)  -  a demoness who had spent one night in a Śamī tree house, and whose girdle was a mighty pestle.

sanskrit: आरट्टा नाम ते देशा बाह्लीका नाम ते जनाः ।वसातिसिन्धुसौवीरा इति प्रायो विकुत्सिताः ॥४७॥
transliteration: āraṭṭā nāma te deśā bāhlīkā nāma te janāḥ ,vasātisindhusauvīrā iti prāyo vikutsitāḥ.
english: Those regions are called Āraṭṭa by name, and those people are called Bāhlīka by name. Similarly, the Vasātis, Sindhus, and Sauvīras are generally regarded as contemptible.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 30 Verse 47. Those regions are called Āraṭṭa by name, and those people are called Bāhlīka by name. Similarly, the Vasātis, Sindhus, and Sauvīras are generally regarded as contemptible.

sanskrit: उत शल्य विजानीहि हन्त भूयो ब्रवीमि ते ।उच्यमानं मया सम्यक्तदेकाग्रमनाः शृणु ॥४८॥
transliteration: uta śalya vijānīhi hanta bhūyo bravīmi te ,ucyamānaṁ mayā samyaktadekāgramanāḥ śṛṇu.
english: O Śalya, understand this! Indeed, I will tell you more. Listen with a concentrated mind to that which is being properly explained by me.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 30 Verse 48. O Śalya, understand this! Indeed, I will tell you more. Listen with a concentrated mind to that which is being properly explained by me.

sanskrit: ब्राह्मणः शिल्पिनो गेहमभ्यगच्छत्पुरातिथिः ।आचारं तत्र संप्रेक्ष्य प्रीतः शिल्पिनमब्रवीत् ॥४९॥
transliteration: brāhmaṇaḥ śilpino gehamabhyagacchatpurātithiḥ ,ācāraṁ tatra saṁprekṣya prītaḥ śilpinamabravīt.
english: Once, a brahmin went as a guest to the house of an artisan. Having observed the conduct (ācāra) there, he became pleased and spoke to the artisan.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 30 Verse 49. Once, a brahmin went as a guest to the house of an artisan. Having observed the conduct (ācāra) there, he became pleased and spoke to the artisan.

sanskrit: मया हिमवतः शृङ्गमेकेनाध्युषितं चिरम् ।दृष्टाश्च बहवो देशा नानाधर्मसमाकुलाः ॥५०॥
transliteration: mayā himavataḥ śṛṅgamekenādhyuṣitaṁ ciram ,dṛṣṭāśca bahavo deśā nānādharmasamākulāḥ.
english: I have dwelt alone on a peak of the Himalayas for a long time. I have also seen many countries, each filled with its own diverse natural laws (dharma).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 30 Verse 50. I have dwelt alone on a peak of the Himalayas for a long time. I have also seen many countries, each filled with its own diverse natural laws (dharma).

sanskrit: न च केन च धर्मेण विरुध्यन्ते प्रजा इमाः ।सर्वे हि तेऽब्रुवन्धर्मं यथोक्तं वेदपारगैः ॥५१॥
transliteration: na ca kena ca dharmeṇa virudhyante prajā imāḥ ,sarve hi te'bruvandharmaṁ yathoktaṁ vedapāragaiḥ.
english: And these beings do not conflict with any natural law (dharma). For indeed, all of them declared the natural law (dharma) just as it was stated by those who have mastered the Vedas.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 30 Verse 51. And these beings do not conflict with any natural law (dharma). For indeed, all of them declared the natural law (dharma) just as it was stated by those who have mastered the Vedas.

sanskrit: अटता तु सदा देशान्नानाधर्मसमाकुलान् ।आगच्छता महाराज बाह्लीकेषु निशामितम् ॥५२॥
transliteration: aṭatā tu sadā deśānnānādharmasamākulān ,āgacchatā mahārāja bāhlīkeṣu niśāmitam.
english: O great king, while I was constantly wandering through various lands, which are full of diverse social customs (dharma), and upon arriving here, I observed the following among the Bāhlīkas:
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 30 Verse 52. O great king, while I was constantly wandering through various lands, which are full of diverse social customs (dharma), and upon arriving here, I observed the following among the Bāhlīkas:

sanskrit: तत्रैव ब्राह्मणो भूत्वा ततो भवति क्षत्रियः ।वैश्यः शूद्रश्च बाह्लीकस्ततो भवति नापितः ॥५३॥
transliteration: tatraiva brāhmaṇo bhūtvā tato bhavati kṣatriyaḥ ,vaiśyaḥ śūdraśca bāhlīkastato bhavati nāpitaḥ.
english: In that very place [among the Bāhlīkas], a person, having become a Brahmin, then becomes a Kṣatriya. Subsequently, a Bāhlīka also becomes a Vaiśya, a Śūdra, and then a barber.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 30 Verse 53. In that very place [among the Bāhlīkas], a person, having become a Brahmin, then becomes a Kṣatriya. Subsequently, a Bāhlīka also becomes a Vaiśya, a Śūdra, and then a barber.

sanskrit: नापितश्च ततो भूत्वा पुनर्भवति ब्राह्मणः ।द्विजो भूत्वा च तत्रैव पुनर्दासोऽपि जायते ॥५४॥
transliteration: nāpitaśca tato bhūtvā punarbhavati brāhmaṇaḥ ,dvijo bhūtvā ca tatraiva punardāso'pi jāyate.
english: And having then become a barber, one again becomes a Brahmin. And, in that very place, even after having been a twice-born (dvija), one is born again as a servant (dāsa).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 30 Verse 54. And having then become a barber, one again becomes a Brahmin. And, in that very place, even after having been a twice-born (dvija), one is born again as a servant (dāsa).

sanskrit: भवत्येकः कुले विप्रः शिष्टान्ये कामचारिणः ।गान्धारा मद्रकाश्चैव बाह्लीकाः केऽप्यचेतसः ॥५५॥
transliteration: bhavatyekaḥ kule vipraḥ śiṣṭānye kāmacāriṇaḥ ,gāndhārā madrakāścaiva bāhlīkāḥ ke'pyacetasaḥ.
english: One person in a family becomes a Brahmin, while the others who remain act as they please, having no fixed social custom (dharma) or status. The Gāndhāras, Madrakas, and Bāhlīkas are indeed some who are mindless.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 30 Verse 55. One person in a family becomes a Brahmin, while the others who remain act as they please, having no fixed social custom (dharma) or status. The Gāndhāras, Madrakas, and Bāhlīkas are indeed some who are mindless.

sanskrit: एतन्मया श्रुतं तत्र धर्मसंकरकारकम् ।कृत्स्नामटित्वा पृथिवीं बाह्लीकेषु विपर्ययः ॥५६॥
transliteration: etanmayā śrutaṁ tatra dharmasaṁkarakārakam ,kṛtsnāmaṭitvā pṛthivīṁ bāhlīkeṣu viparyayaḥ.
english: This I heard there: having traveled throughout the entire earth, I found among the Bāhlīkas a situation of perversion that causes the confusion of the natural law (dharma).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 30 Verse 56. This I heard there: having traveled throughout the entire earth, I found among the Bāhlīkas a situation of perversion that causes the confusion of the natural law (dharma).

sanskrit: उत शल्य विजानीहि हन्त भूयो ब्रवीमि ते ।यदप्यन्योऽब्रवीद्वाक्यं बाह्लीकानां विकुत्सितम् ॥५७॥
transliteration: uta śalya vijānīhi hanta bhūyo bravīmi te ,yadapyanyo'bravīdvākyaṁ bāhlīkānāṁ vikutsitam.
english: O Śalya, listen! Indeed, I will tell you more, including what another person said - a statement greatly condemning the Bāhlīkas.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 30 Verse 57. O Śalya, listen! Indeed, I will tell you more, including what another person said - a statement greatly condemning the Bāhlīkas.

sanskrit: सती पुरा हृता काचिदारट्टा किल दस्युभिः ।अधर्मतश्चोपयाता सा तानभ्यशपत्ततः ॥५८॥
transliteration: satī purā hṛtā kācidāraṭṭā kila dasyubhiḥ ,adharmataścopayātā sā tānabhyaśapattataḥ.
english: Long ago, a chaste Āraṭṭa woman, it is said, was abducted by robbers. Because she was violated unlawfully (adharma), she then cursed them.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 30 Verse 58. Long ago, a chaste Āraṭṭa woman, it is said, was abducted by robbers. Because she was violated unlawfully (adharma), she then cursed them.

sanskrit: बालां बन्धुमतीं यन्मामधर्मेणोपगच्छथ ।तस्मान्नार्यो भविष्यन्ति बन्धक्यो वै कुलेषु वः ।न चैवास्मात्प्रमोक्ष्यध्वं घोरात्पापान्नराधमाः ॥५९॥
transliteration: bālāṁ bandhumatīṁ yanmāmadharmeṇopagacchatha ,tasmānnāryo bhaviṣyanti bandhakyo vai kuleṣu vaḥ ,na caivāsmātpramokṣyadhvaṁ ghorātpāpānnarādhamāḥ.
english: Because you violate me, a young woman with kin, by means of unrighteousness (adharma), therefore, women in your families will certainly become unchaste. And you, O vilest of men, will not be released from this terrible sin.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 30 Verse 59. Because you violate me, a young woman with kin, by means of unrighteousness (adharma), therefore, women in your families will certainly become unchaste. And you, O vilest of men, will not be released from this terrible sin.

sanskrit: कुरवः सहपाञ्चालाः शाल्वा मत्स्याः सनैमिषाः ।कोसलाः काशयोऽङ्गाश्च कलिङ्गा मगधास्तथा ॥६०॥
transliteration: kuravaḥ sahapāñcālāḥ śālvā matsyāḥ sanaimiṣāḥ ,kosalāḥ kāśayo'ṅgāśca kaliṅgā magadhāstathā.
english: The Kurus with the Pāñcālas, the Śālvas, the Matsyas with the Naimiṣas, the Kosalas, the Kāśis, the Aṅgas, the Kaliṅgas, and the Magadhas (are all mentioned).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 30 Verse 60. The Kurus with the Pāñcālas, the Śālvas, the Matsyas with the Naimiṣas, the Kosalas, the Kāśis, the Aṅgas, the Kaliṅgas, and the Magadhas (are all mentioned).

sanskrit: चेदयश्च महाभागा धर्मं जानन्ति शाश्वतम् ।नानादेशेषु सन्तश्च प्रायो बाह्या लयादृते ॥६१॥
transliteration: cedayaśca mahābhāgā dharmaṁ jānanti śāśvatam ,nānādeśeṣu santaśca prāyo bāhyā layādṛte.
english: And the highly esteemed Cedis, as well as good people (santaḥ) in various lands, generally know the eternal natural law (dharma). Indeed, most outsiders (bāhyāḥ) also know it, with the exception of those absorbed in material dissolution (laya).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 30 Verse 61. And the highly esteemed Cedis, as well as good people (santaḥ) in various lands, generally know the eternal natural law (dharma). Indeed, most outsiders (bāhyāḥ) also know it, with the exception of those absorbed in material dissolution (laya).

sanskrit: आ मत्स्येभ्यः कुरुपाञ्चालदेश्या आ नैमिषाच्चेदयो ये विशिष्टाः ।धर्मं पुराणमुपजीवन्ति सन्तो मद्रानृते पञ्चनदांश्च जिह्मान् ॥६२॥
transliteration: ā matsyebhyaḥ kurupāñcāladeśyā; ā naimiṣāccedayo ye viśiṣṭāḥ ,dharmaṁ purāṇamupajīvanti santo; madrānṛte pañcanadāṁśca jihmān.
english: The distinguished (viśiṣṭāḥ) people from the Matsyas, those from the Kuru-Pañcāla regions, and the Cedis from Naimiṣa – these good people (santaḥ) follow the ancient natural law (dharma), with the exception of the Madras and the deceitful (jihmān) Pañcanadas.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 30 Verse 62. The distinguished (viśiṣṭāḥ) people from the Matsyas, those from the Kuru-Pañcāla regions, and the Cedis from Naimiṣa – these good people (santaḥ) follow the ancient natural law (dharma), with the exception of the Madras and the deceitful (jihmān) Pañcanadas.

sanskrit: एवं विद्वन्धर्मकथांश्च राजंस्तूष्णींभूतो जडवच्छल्य भूयाः ।त्वं तस्य गोप्ता च जनस्य राजा षड्भागहर्ता शुभदुष्कृतस्य ॥६३॥
transliteration: evaṁ vidvandharmakathāṁśca rājaṁ;stūṣṇīṁbhūto jaḍavacchalya bhūyāḥ ,tvaṁ tasya goptā ca janasya rājā; ṣaḍbhāgahartā śubhaduṣkṛtasya.
english: Therefore, O wise one, O King, O Śalya, having heard these discourses on natural law (dharma), you should remain silent like an inert person. You are the protector and king of that people, and you receive one-sixth of their merits and demerits.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 30 Verse 63. Therefore, O wise one, O King, O Śalya, having heard these discourses on natural law (dharma), you should remain silent like an inert person. You are the protector and king of that people, and you receive one-sixth of their merits and demerits.

sanskrit: अथ वा दुष्कृतस्य त्वं हर्ता तेषामरक्षिता ।रक्षिता पुण्यभाग्राजा प्रजानां त्वं त्वपुण्यभाक् ॥६४॥
transliteration: atha vā duṣkṛtasya tvaṁ hartā teṣāmarakṣitā ,rakṣitā puṇyabhāgrājā prajānāṁ tvaṁ tvapuṇyabhāk.
english: Alternatively, you are the remover of misdeeds, and you are not the protector of those who commit them. You, the virtuous king, are the protector of subjects, but you do not partake in demerit.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 30 Verse 64. Alternatively, you are the remover of misdeeds, and you are not the protector of those who commit them. You, the virtuous king, are the protector of subjects, but you do not partake in demerit.

sanskrit: पूज्यमाने पुरा धर्मे सर्वदेशेषु शाश्वते ।धर्मं पाञ्चनदं दृष्ट्वा धिगित्याह पितामहः ॥६५॥
transliteration: pūjyamāne purā dharme sarvadeśeṣu śāśvate ,dharmaṁ pāñcanadaṁ dṛṣṭvā dhigityāha pitāmahaḥ.
english: Formerly, when the eternal natural law (dharma) was revered in all lands, the Grandfather (pitāmaha), upon seeing the law (dharma) of Pañcanada, exclaimed, 'Fie!'
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 30 Verse 65. Formerly, when the eternal natural law (dharma) was revered in all lands, the Grandfather (pitāmaha), upon seeing the law (dharma) of Pañcanada, exclaimed, 'Fie!'

sanskrit: व्रात्यानां दाशमीयानां कृतेऽप्यशुभकर्मणाम् ।इति पाञ्चनदं धर्ममवमेने पितामहः ।स्वधर्मस्थेषु वर्णेषु सोऽप्येतं नाभिपूजयेत् ॥६६॥
transliteration: vrātyānāṁ dāśamīyānāṁ kṛte'pyaśubhakarmaṇām ,iti pāñcanadaṁ dharmamavamene pitāmahaḥ ,svadharmastheṣu varṇeṣu so'pyetaṁ nābhipūjayet.
english: The Grandfather (pitāmaha) thus scorned the natural law (dharma) of Pañcanada, deeming it suitable even for Vrātyas, Dāśamīyas, and those of impure actions. He (pitāmaha) would not honor this (dharma) even among social classes (varṇa) who uphold their own intrinsic nature (dharma).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 30 Verse 66. The Grandfather (pitāmaha) thus scorned the natural law (dharma) of Pañcanada, deeming it suitable even for Vrātyas, Dāśamīyas, and those of impure actions. He (pitāmaha) would not honor this (dharma) even among social classes (varṇa) who uphold their own intrinsic nature (dharma).

sanskrit: उत शल्य विजानीहि हन्त भूयो ब्रवीमि ते ।कल्माषपादः सरसि निमज्जन्राक्षसोऽब्रवीत् ॥६७॥
transliteration: uta śalya vijānīhi hanta bhūyo bravīmi te ,kalmāṣapādaḥ sarasi nimajjanrākṣaso'bravīt.
english: O Śalya, pay heed! Indeed, I shall tell you more. Kalmāṣapāda, the demon (rākṣasa), said this while submerging in the lake.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 30 Verse 67. O Śalya, pay heed! Indeed, I shall tell you more. Kalmāṣapāda, the demon (rākṣasa), said this while submerging in the lake.

sanskrit: क्षत्रियस्य मलं भैक्षं ब्राह्मणस्यानृतं मलम् ।मलं पृथिव्या बाह्लीकाः स्त्रीणां मद्रस्त्रियो मलम् ॥६८॥
transliteration: kṣatriyasya malaṁ bhaikṣaṁ brāhmaṇasyānṛtaṁ malam ,malaṁ pṛthivyā bāhlīkāḥ strīṇāṁ madrastriyo malam.
english: For a kṣatriya, begging is an impurity; for a brāhmaṇa, falsehood is an impurity. The Bāhlīkas are considered the impurity of the earth, and Madra women are the impurity among women.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 30 Verse 68. For a kṣatriya, begging is an impurity; for a brāhmaṇa, falsehood is an impurity. The Bāhlīkas are considered the impurity of the earth, and Madra women are the impurity among women.

sanskrit: निमज्जमानमुद्धृत्य कश्चिद्राजा निशाचरम् ।अपृच्छत्तेन चाख्यातं प्रोक्तवान्यन्निबोध तत् ॥६९॥
transliteration: nimajjamānamuddhṛtya kaścidrājā niśācaram ,apṛcchattena cākhyātaṁ proktavānyannibodha tat.
english: A certain king, having rescued a drowning night-wanderer (nिशाचर), questioned him. Learn what was related by the night-wanderer, and what (the king) then declared.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 30 Verse 69. A certain king, having rescued a drowning night-wanderer (nिशाचर), questioned him. Learn what was related by the night-wanderer, and what (the king) then declared.

sanskrit: मानुषाणां मलं म्लेच्छा म्लेच्छानां मौष्टिका मलम् ।मौष्टिकानां मलं शण्डाः शण्डानां राजयाजकाः ॥७०॥
transliteration: mānuṣāṇāṁ malaṁ mlecchā mlecchānāṁ mauṣṭikā malam ,mauṣṭikānāṁ malaṁ śaṇḍāḥ śaṇḍānāṁ rājayājakāḥ.
english: Mlecchas are the impurity of human beings; Mauṣṭikas are the impurity of Mlecchas. Śaṇḍas are the impurity of Mauṣṭikas, and royal priests are the impurity of Śaṇḍas.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 30 Verse 70. Mlecchas are the impurity of human beings; Mauṣṭikas are the impurity of Mlecchas. Śaṇḍas are the impurity of Mauṣṭikas, and royal priests are the impurity of Śaṇḍas.

sanskrit: राजयाजकयाज्यानां मद्रकाणां च यन्मलम् ।तद्भवेद्वै तव मलं यद्यस्मान्न विमुञ्चसि ॥७१॥
transliteration: rājayājakayājyānāṁ madrakāṇāṁ ca yanmalam ,tadbhavedvai tava malaṁ yadyasmānna vimuñcasi.
english: And that impurity which applies to the patrons of royal priests and to the Madrakas – that very impurity will indeed become yours if you do not release us.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 30 Verse 71. And that impurity which applies to the patrons of royal priests and to the Madrakas – that very impurity will indeed become yours if you do not release us.

sanskrit: इति रक्षोपसृष्टेषु विषवीर्यहतेषु च ।राक्षसं भेषजं प्रोक्तं संसिद्धं वचनोत्तरम् ॥७२॥
transliteration: iti rakṣopasṛṣṭeṣu viṣavīryahateṣu ca ,rākṣasaṁ bheṣajaṁ proktaṁ saṁsiddhaṁ vacanottaram.
english: Thus, for those afflicted by evil spirits (rakṣas) and for those destroyed by the potency of poison, a powerful remedy (rākṣasa), perfected by potent incantations, is declared.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 30 Verse 72. Thus, for those afflicted by evil spirits (rakṣas) and for those destroyed by the potency of poison, a powerful remedy (rākṣasa), perfected by potent incantations, is declared.

sanskrit: ब्राह्मं पाञ्चालाः कौरवेयाः स्वधर्मः सत्यं मत्स्याः शूरसेनाश्च यज्ञः ।प्राच्या दासा वृषला दाक्षिणात्याः स्तेना बाह्लीकाः संकरा वै सुराष्ट्राः ॥७३॥
transliteration: brāhmaṁ pāñcālāḥ kauraveyāḥ svadharmaḥ; satyaṁ matsyāḥ śūrasenāśca yajñaḥ ,prācyā dāsā vṛṣalā dākṣiṇātyāḥ; stenā bāhlīkāḥ saṁkarā vai surāṣṭrāḥ.
english: The Pañcālas and Kauraveyas embody "brāhmam" (Brāhmic knowledge/qualities) as their intrinsic nature (svadharma), while the Matsyas and Śūrasenas uphold truth (satyam) and Vedic ritual (yajña). The Easterners (Prācyas) are "dāsas" (servants) and "vṛṣalas" (śūdras); the Southerners (Dākṣiṇātyas) are thieves; the Bāhlīkas are of mixed origin (saṅkarāḥ), and the Surāṣṭras are indeed also of mixed origin.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 30 Verse 73. The Pañcālas and Kauraveyas embody "brāhmam" (Brāhmic knowledge/qualities) as their intrinsic nature (svadharma), while the Matsyas and Śūrasenas uphold truth (satyam) and Vedic ritual (yajña). The Easterners (Prācyas) are "dāsas" (servants) and "vṛṣalas" (śūdras); the Southerners (Dākṣiṇātyas) are thieves; the Bāhlīkas are of mixed origin (saṅkarāḥ), and the Surāṣṭras are indeed also of mixed origin.

sanskrit: कृतघ्नता परवित्तापहारः सुरापानं गुरुदारावमर्शः ।येषां धर्मस्तान्प्रति नास्त्यधर्म आरट्टकान्पाञ्चनदान्धिगस्तु ॥७४॥
transliteration: kṛtaghnatā paravittāpahāraḥ; surāpānaṁ gurudārāvamarśaḥ ,yeṣāṁ dharmastānprati nāstyadharma; āraṭṭakānpāñcanadāndhigastu.
english: Ingratitude, taking away others' wealth, drinking liquor, and violating the guru's wife - these are the customs (dharma) of those people. For them, there is no unrighteousness (adharma). Shame upon the Āraṭṭakas and the Pañcanadas!
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 30 Verse 74. Ingratitude, taking away others' wealth, drinking liquor, and violating the guru's wife - these are the customs (dharma) of those people. For them, there is no unrighteousness (adharma). Shame upon the Āraṭṭakas and the Pañcanadas!

sanskrit: आ पाञ्चालेभ्यः कुरवो नैमिषाश्च मत्स्याश्चैवाप्यथ जानन्ति धर्मम् ।कलिङ्गकाश्चाङ्गका मागधाश्च शिष्टान्धर्मानुपजीवन्ति वृद्धाः ॥७५॥
transliteration: ā pāñcālebhyaḥ kuravo naimiṣāśca; matsyāścaivāpyatha jānanti dharmam ,kaliṅgakāścāṅgakā māgadhāśca; śiṣṭāndharmānupajīvanti vṛddhāḥ.
english: From the Pañcālas onwards, the Kurus, the Naimiṣas, and even the Matsyas - all of them indeed know the natural law (dharma). And the elders among the Kaliṅgas, the Aṅgas, and the Magadhas live according to the refined natural laws (dharma).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 30 Verse 75. From the Pañcālas onwards, the Kurus, the Naimiṣas, and even the Matsyas - all of them indeed know the natural law (dharma). And the elders among the Kaliṅgas, the Aṅgas, and the Magadhas live according to the refined natural laws (dharma).

sanskrit: प्राचीं दिशं श्रिता देवा जातवेदःपुरोगमाः ।दक्षिणां पितरो गुप्तां यमेन शुभकर्मणा ॥७६॥
transliteration: prācīṁ diśaṁ śritā devā jātavedaḥpurogamāḥ ,dakṣiṇāṁ pitaro guptāṁ yamena śubhakarmaṇā.
english: The gods, led by Agni (Jātavedas), reside in the eastern direction. The ancestors (pitaraḥ), protected by Yama, who performs auspicious actions, reside in the southern direction.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 30 Verse 76. The gods, led by Agni (Jātavedas), reside in the eastern direction. The ancestors (pitaraḥ), protected by Yama, who performs auspicious actions, reside in the southern direction.

sanskrit: प्रतीचीं वरुणः पाति पालयन्नसुरान्बली ।उदीचीं भगवान्सोमो ब्रह्मण्यो ब्राह्मणैः सह ॥७७॥
transliteration: pratīcīṁ varuṇaḥ pāti pālayannasurānbalī ,udīcīṁ bhagavānsomo brahmaṇyo brāhmaṇaiḥ saha.
english: Varuṇa, the powerful one, protects the western direction, guarding the asuras. The venerable Soma, devoted to brahmans, together with the brahmans, protects the northern direction.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 30 Verse 77. Varuṇa, the powerful one, protects the western direction, guarding the asuras. The venerable Soma, devoted to brahmans, together with the brahmans, protects the northern direction.

sanskrit: रक्षःपिशाचान्हिमवान्गुह्यकान्गन्धमादनः ।ध्रुवः सर्वाणि भूतानि विष्णुर्लोकाञ्जनार्दनः ॥७८॥
transliteration: rakṣaḥpiśācānhimavānguhyakāngandhamādanaḥ ,dhruvaḥ sarvāṇi bhūtāni viṣṇurlokāñjanārdanaḥ.
english: Himavan (the Himalayas) controls the rākṣasas and piśācas. Gandhamādana controls the guhyakas. Dhruva (the Pole Star) controls all beings. Viṣṇu, the dispeller of evil (janārdana), controls the worlds.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 30 Verse 78. Himavan (the Himalayas) controls the rākṣasas and piśācas. Gandhamādana controls the guhyakas. Dhruva (the Pole Star) controls all beings. Viṣṇu, the dispeller of evil (janārdana), controls the worlds.

sanskrit: इङ्गितज्ञाश्च मगधाः प्रेक्षितज्ञाश्च कोसलाः ।अर्धोक्ताः कुरुपाञ्चालाः शाल्वाः कृत्स्नानुशासनाः ।पार्वतीयाश्च विषमा यथैव गिरयस्तथा ॥७९॥
transliteration: iṅgitajñāśca magadhāḥ prekṣitajñāśca kosalāḥ ,ardhoktāḥ kurupāñcālāḥ śālvāḥ kṛtsnānuśāsanāḥ ,pārvatīyāśca viṣamā yathaiva girayastathā.
english: The people of Magadha are skilled at understanding gestures, and those of Kosala understand glances. The Kurupāñcālas comprehend half-spoken words. The Śālvas follow complete instructions. And the mountaineers are as rugged and difficult as the mountains themselves.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 30 Verse 79. The people of Magadha are skilled at understanding gestures, and those of Kosala understand glances. The Kurupāñcālas comprehend half-spoken words. The Śālvas follow complete instructions. And the mountaineers are as rugged and difficult as the mountains themselves.

sanskrit: सर्वज्ञा यवना राजञ्शूराश्चैव विशेषतः ।म्लेच्छाः स्वसंज्ञानियता नानुक्त इतरो जनः ॥८०॥
transliteration: sarvajñā yavanā rājañśūrāścaiva viśeṣataḥ ,mlecchāḥ svasaṁjñāniyatā nānukta itaro janaḥ.
english: O King, the Yavanas are omniscient and especially brave. Though they are foreigners (mlecchāḥ), they are disciplined by their own customs (dharma). It is not as if other people are undescribed.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 30 Verse 80. O King, the Yavanas are omniscient and especially brave. Though they are foreigners (mlecchāḥ), they are disciplined by their own customs (dharma). It is not as if other people are undescribed.

sanskrit: प्रतिरब्धास्तु बाह्लीका न च केचन मद्रकाः ।स त्वमेतादृशः शल्य नोत्तरं वक्तुमर्हसि ॥८१॥
transliteration: pratirabdhāstu bāhlīkā na ca kecana madrakāḥ ,sa tvametādṛśaḥ śalya nottaraṁ vaktumarhasi.
english: However, the Bahlikas have undertaken (action/effort), and not a single Madraka is (like that). Therefore, O Salya, you, being such a person, ought not to speak a reply.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 30 Verse 81. However, the Bahlikas have undertaken (action/effort), and not a single Madraka is (like that). Therefore, O Salya, you, being such a person, ought not to speak a reply.

sanskrit: एतज्ज्ञात्वा जोषमास्स्व प्रतीपं मा स्म वै कृथाः ।स त्वां पूर्वमहं हत्वा हनिष्ये केशवार्जुनौ ॥८२॥
transliteration: etajjñātvā joṣamāssva pratīpaṁ mā sma vai kṛthāḥ ,sa tvāṁ pūrvamahaṁ hatvā haniṣye keśavārjunau.
english: Understanding this, remain silent. Do not, indeed, act hostilely. First, I shall kill you, and then Kesava (Kṛṣṇa) and Arjuna.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 30 Verse 82. Understanding this, remain silent. Do not, indeed, act hostilely. First, I shall kill you, and then Kesava (Kṛṣṇa) and Arjuna.

sanskrit: शल्य उवाच ।आतुराणां परित्यागः स्वदारसुतविक्रयः ।अङ्गेषु वर्तते कर्ण येषामधिपतिर्भवान् ॥८३॥
transliteration: śalya uvāca ,āturāṇāṁ parityāgaḥ svadārasutavikrayaḥ ,aṅgeṣu vartate karṇa yeṣāmadhipatirbhavān.
english: Salya said: "O Karna, the abandonment of the distressed and the selling of one's own wives and children occurs among the Angas, of whom you are the lord."
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 30 Verse 83. Salya said: "O Karna, the abandonment of the distressed and the selling of one's own wives and children occurs among the Angas, of whom you are the lord."

sanskrit: रथातिरथसंख्यायां यत्त्वा भीष्मस्तदाब्रवीत् ।तान्विदित्वात्मनो दोषान्निर्मन्युर्भव मा क्रुधः ॥८४॥
transliteration: rathātirathasaṁkhyāyāṁ yattvā bhīṣmastadābravīt ,tānviditvātmano doṣānnirmanyurbhava mā krudhaḥ.
english: Regarding what Bhishma then said to you concerning the enumeration of great warriors (rathātiratha), understand your own faults, become free from anger, and do not be enraged.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 30 Verse 84. Regarding what Bhishma then said to you concerning the enumeration of great warriors (rathātiratha), understand your own faults, become free from anger, and do not be enraged.

sanskrit: सर्वत्र ब्राह्मणाः सन्ति सन्ति सर्वत्र क्षत्रियाः ।वैश्याः शूद्रास्तथा कर्ण स्त्रियः साध्व्यश्च सुव्रताः ॥८५॥
transliteration: sarvatra brāhmaṇāḥ santi santi sarvatra kṣatriyāḥ ,vaiśyāḥ śūdrāstathā karṇa striyaḥ sādhvyaśca suvratāḥ.
english: O Karṇa, Brahmins are found everywhere, and Kshatriyas are found everywhere. Also, there are Vaishyas, Shudras, and virtuous women who uphold good vows.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 30 Verse 85. O Karṇa, Brahmins are found everywhere, and Kshatriyas are found everywhere. Also, there are Vaishyas, Shudras, and virtuous women who uphold good vows.

sanskrit: रमन्ते चोपहासेन पुरुषाः पुरुषैः सह ।अन्योन्यमवतक्षन्तो देशे देशे समैथुनाः ॥८६॥
transliteration: ramante copahāsena puruṣāḥ puruṣaiḥ saha ,anyonyamavatakṣanto deśe deśe samaithunāḥ.
english: And men (puruṣa) take pleasure in jesting with other men (puruṣa), mocking each other in every region, engaged in sexual activity together.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 30 Verse 86. And men (puruṣa) take pleasure in jesting with other men (puruṣa), mocking each other in every region, engaged in sexual activity together.

sanskrit: परवाच्येषु निपुणः सर्वो भवति सर्वदा ।आत्मवाच्यं न जानीते जानन्नपि विमुह्यति ॥८७॥
transliteration: paravācyeṣu nipuṇaḥ sarvo bhavati sarvadā ,ātmavācyaṁ na jānīte jānannapi vimuhyati.
english: Everyone is always skilled in finding the faults of others, but they do not know their own faults, and even when knowing them, they become deluded.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 30 Verse 87. Everyone is always skilled in finding the faults of others, but they do not know their own faults, and even when knowing them, they become deluded.

sanskrit: संजय उवाच ।कर्णोऽपि नोत्तरं प्राह शल्योऽप्यभिमुखः परान् ।पुनः प्रहस्य राधेयः पुनर्याहीत्यचोदयत् ॥८८॥
transliteration: saṁjaya uvāca ,karṇo'pi nottaraṁ prāha śalyo'pyabhimukhaḥ parān ,punaḥ prahasya rādheyaḥ punaryāhītyacodayat.
english: Sañjaya said: Karna gave no reply, and Salya, too, faced the enemies. Smiling again, Radheya (Karna) then urged (him) once more, "Go!"
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 30 Verse 88. Sañjaya said: Karna gave no reply, and Salya, too, faced the enemies. Smiling again, Radheya (Karna) then urged (him) once more, "Go!"

sanskrit: संजय उवाच ।ततः परानीकभिदं व्यूहमप्रतिमं परैः ।समीक्ष्य कर्णः पार्थानां धृष्टद्युम्नाभिरक्षितम् ॥१॥
transliteration: saṁjaya uvāca ,tataḥ parānīkabhidaṁ vyūhamapratimaṁ paraiḥ ,samīkṣya karṇaḥ pārthānāṁ dhṛṣṭadyumnābhirakṣitam.
english: Sañjaya said: Then Karna, observing that battle formation of the Pārthas (sons of Pṛthā), which was protected by Dhṛṣṭadyumna and was unparalleled by enemies, being a breaker of enemy armies...
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 31 Verse 1. Sañjaya said: Then Karna, observing that battle formation of the Pārthas (sons of Pṛthā), which was protected by Dhṛṣṭadyumna and was unparalleled by enemies, being a breaker of enemy armies...

sanskrit: प्रययौ रथघोषेण सिंहनादरवेण च ।वादित्राणां च निनदैः कम्पयन्निव मेदिनीम् ॥२॥
transliteration: prayayau rathaghoṣeṇa siṁhanādaraveṇa ca ,vāditrāṇāṁ ca ninadaiḥ kampayanniva medinīm.
english: He advanced with the rumble of chariots, the roar of lions, and the din of musical instruments, as if making the earth tremble.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 31 Verse 2. He advanced with the rumble of chariots, the roar of lions, and the din of musical instruments, as if making the earth tremble.

sanskrit: वेपमान इव क्रोधाद्युद्धशौण्डः परंतपः ।प्रतिव्यूह्य महातेजा यथावद्भरतर्षभ ॥३॥
transliteration: vepamāna iva krodhādyuddhaśauṇḍaḥ paraṁtapaḥ ,prativyūhya mahātejā yathāvadbharatarṣabha.
english: ...the greatly refulgent tormentor of enemies, eager for battle, as if trembling with rage, having properly arrayed his own forces in counter-formation, O best of the Bharatas.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 31 Verse 3. ...the greatly refulgent tormentor of enemies, eager for battle, as if trembling with rage, having properly arrayed his own forces in counter-formation, O best of the Bharatas.

sanskrit: व्यधमत्पाण्डवीं सेनामासुरीं मघवानिव ।युधिष्ठिरं चाभिभवन्नपसव्यं चकार ह ॥४॥
transliteration: vyadhamatpāṇḍavīṁ senāmāsurīṁ maghavāniva ,yudhiṣṭhiraṁ cābhibhavannapasavyaṁ cakāra ha.
english: He scattered the Pandava army as if it were a demonic host, just like Indra (Maghavan) does, and indeed, overpowering Yudhishthira, he forced him to retreat.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 31 Verse 4. He scattered the Pandava army as if it were a demonic host, just like Indra (Maghavan) does, and indeed, overpowering Yudhishthira, he forced him to retreat.

sanskrit: धृतराष्ट्र उवाच ।कथं संजय राधेयः प्रत्यव्यूहत पाण्डवान् ।धृष्टद्युम्नमुखान्वीरान्भीमसेनाभिरक्षितान् ॥५॥
transliteration: dhṛtarāṣṭra uvāca ,kathaṁ saṁjaya rādheyaḥ pratyavyūhata pāṇḍavān ,dhṛṣṭadyumnamukhānvīrānbhīmasenābhirakṣitān.
english: Dhritarashtra said, "O Sanjaya, how did Radheya (Karna) array his forces against the Pandavas, those heroes who were led by Dhrishtadyumna and protected by Bhimasena?"
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 31 Verse 5. Dhritarashtra said, "O Sanjaya, how did Radheya (Karna) array his forces against the Pandavas, those heroes who were led by Dhrishtadyumna and protected by Bhimasena?"

sanskrit: के च प्रपक्षौ पक्षौ वा मम सैन्यस्य संजय ।प्रविभज्य यथान्यायं कथं वा समवस्थिताः ॥६॥
transliteration: ke ca prapakṣau pakṣau vā mama sainyasya saṁjaya ,pravibhajya yathānyāyaṁ kathaṁ vā samavasthitāḥ.
english: And who formed the main flanks, or indeed the wings, of my army, O Sanjaya? And how were they positioned after being appropriately deployed?
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 31 Verse 6. And who formed the main flanks, or indeed the wings, of my army, O Sanjaya? And how were they positioned after being appropriately deployed?

sanskrit: कथं पाण्डुसुताश्चापि प्रत्यव्यूहन्त मामकान् ।कथं चैतन्महायुद्धं प्रावर्तत सुदारुणम् ॥७॥
transliteration: kathaṁ pāṇḍusutāścāpi pratyavyūhanta māmakān ,kathaṁ caitanmahāyuddhaṁ prāvartata sudāruṇam.
english: And how, furthermore, did the sons of Pandu (Pandu-sutas) array themselves against my warriors? And how did this exceedingly dreadful great battle commence?
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 31 Verse 7. And how, furthermore, did the sons of Pandu (Pandu-sutas) array themselves against my warriors? And how did this exceedingly dreadful great battle commence?

sanskrit: क्व च बीभत्सुरभवद्यत्कर्णोऽयाद्युधिष्ठिरम् ।को ह्यर्जुनस्य सांनिध्ये शक्तोऽभ्येतुं युधिष्ठिरम् ॥८॥
transliteration: kva ca bībhatsurabhavadyatkarṇo'yādyudhiṣṭhiram ,ko hyarjunasya sāṁnidhye śakto'bhyetuṁ yudhiṣṭhiram.
english: Where was Bībhatsu (Arjuna) when Karṇa attacked Yudhīṣṭhira? Indeed, who would be capable of attacking Yudhīṣṭhira in Arjuna's presence?
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 31 Verse 8. Where was Bībhatsu (Arjuna) when Karṇa attacked Yudhīṣṭhira? Indeed, who would be capable of attacking Yudhīṣṭhira in Arjuna's presence?

sanskrit: सर्वभूतानि यो ह्येकः खाण्डवे जितवान्पुरा ।कस्तमन्यत्र राधेयात्प्रतियुध्येज्जिजीविषुः ॥९॥
transliteration: sarvabhūtāni yo hyekaḥ khāṇḍave jitavānpurā ,kastamanyatra rādheyātpratiyudhyejjijīviṣuḥ.
english: Who, desiring to live, would dare to fight against him (Arjuna) – the very one who, alone, conquered all beings in Khāṇḍava long ago – except for the son of Rādhā (Karṇa)?
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 31 Verse 9. Who, desiring to live, would dare to fight against him (Arjuna) – the very one who, alone, conquered all beings in Khāṇḍava long ago – except for the son of Rādhā (Karṇa)?

sanskrit: संजय उवाच ।शृणु व्यूहस्य रचनामर्जुनश्च यथा गतः ।परिदाय नृपं तेभ्यः संग्रामश्चाभवद्यथा ॥१०॥
transliteration: saṁjaya uvāca ,śṛṇu vyūhasya racanāmarjunaśca yathā gataḥ ,paridāya nṛpaṁ tebhyaḥ saṁgrāmaścābhavadyathā.
english: Saṃjaya said: Listen to the formation of the battle array, and how Arjuna went, leaving the king to them, and how the battle ensued.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 31 Verse 10. Saṃjaya said: Listen to the formation of the battle array, and how Arjuna went, leaving the king to them, and how the battle ensued.

sanskrit: कृपः शारद्वतो राजन्मागधश्च तरस्विनः ।सात्वतः कृतवर्मा च दक्षिणं पक्षमाश्रिताः ॥११॥
transliteration: kṛpaḥ śāradvato rājanmāgadhaśca tarasvinaḥ ,sātvataḥ kṛtavarmā ca dakṣiṇaṁ pakṣamāśritāḥ.
english: O King, Kṛpa, the son of Śaradvan, and the powerful Māgadha, along with Sātvata (Kṛtavarman), took their positions on the southern flank.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 31 Verse 11. O King, Kṛpa, the son of Śaradvan, and the powerful Māgadha, along with Sātvata (Kṛtavarman), took their positions on the southern flank.

sanskrit: तेषां प्रपक्षे शकुनिरुलूकश्च महारथः ।सादिभिर्विमलप्रासैस्तवानीकमरक्षताम् ॥१२॥
transliteration: teṣāṁ prapakṣe śakunirulūkaśca mahārathaḥ ,sādibhirvimalaprāsaistavānīkamarakṣatām.
english: Shakuni and Uluka, both great charioteers, protected your army on their right flank with horsemen bearing shining spears.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 31 Verse 12. Shakuni and Uluka, both great charioteers, protected your army on their right flank with horsemen bearing shining spears.

sanskrit: गान्धारिभिरसंभ्रान्तैः पार्वतीयैश्च दुर्जयैः ।शलभानामिव व्रातैः पिशाचैरिव दुर्दृशैः ॥१३॥
transliteration: gāndhāribhirasaṁbhrāntaiḥ pārvatīyaiśca durjayaiḥ ,śalabhānāmiva vrātaiḥ piśācairiva durdṛśaiḥ.
english: By the unperturbed Gandharas and the unconquerable mountaineers, who were like swarms of locusts and like hideous Pisacas (demons).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 31 Verse 13. By the unperturbed Gandharas and the unconquerable mountaineers, who were like swarms of locusts and like hideous Pisacas (demons).

sanskrit: चतुस्त्रिंशत्सहस्राणि रथानामनिवर्तिनाम् ।संशप्तका युद्धशौण्डा वामं पार्श्वमपालयन् ॥१४॥
transliteration: catustriṁśatsahasrāṇi rathānāmanivartinām ,saṁśaptakā yuddhaśauṇḍā vāmaṁ pārśvamapālayan.
english: Thirty-four thousand Samshaptakas, fierce in battle and never retreating, protected the left flank with their chariots.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 31 Verse 14. Thirty-four thousand Samshaptakas, fierce in battle and never retreating, protected the left flank with their chariots.

sanskrit: समुच्चितास्तव सुतैः कृष्णार्जुनजिघांसवः ।तेषां प्रपक्षः काम्बोजाः शकाश्च यवनैः सह ॥१५॥
transliteration: samuccitāstava sutaiḥ kṛṣṇārjunajighāṁsavaḥ ,teṣāṁ prapakṣaḥ kāmbojāḥ śakāśca yavanaiḥ saha.
english: Assembled by your sons, these warriors, desiring to kill Krishna and Arjuna - the Kambojas, Shakas, and Yavanas - formed their (the Samshaptakas') right flank (or vanguard), together with other Yavanas.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 31 Verse 15. Assembled by your sons, these warriors, desiring to kill Krishna and Arjuna - the Kambojas, Shakas, and Yavanas - formed their (the Samshaptakas') right flank (or vanguard), together with other Yavanas.

sanskrit: निदेशात्सूतपुत्रस्य सरथाः साश्वपत्तयः ।आह्वयन्तोऽर्जुनं तस्थुः केशवं च महाबलम् ॥१६॥
transliteration: nideśātsūtaputrasya sarathāḥ sāśvapattayaḥ ,āhvayanto'rjunaṁ tasthuḥ keśavaṁ ca mahābalam.
english: At the command of Karṇa, the son of Sūta, those with chariots, horses, and foot soldiers took their positions, challenging Arjuna and the mighty Keśava.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 31 Verse 16. At the command of Karṇa, the son of Sūta, those with chariots, horses, and foot soldiers took their positions, challenging Arjuna and the mighty Keśava.

sanskrit: मध्येसेनामुखं कर्णो व्यवातिष्ठत दंशितः ।चित्रवर्माङ्गदः स्रग्वी पालयन्ध्वजिनीमुखम् ॥१७॥
transliteration: madhyesenāmukhaṁ karṇo vyavātiṣṭhata daṁśitaḥ ,citravarmāṅgadaḥ sragvī pālayandhvajinīmukham.
english: Karṇa, armored and adorned with splendid armor and armlets, wearing a garland, was stationed in the middle of the army's front, protecting the vanguard.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 31 Verse 17. Karṇa, armored and adorned with splendid armor and armlets, wearing a garland, was stationed in the middle of the army's front, protecting the vanguard.

sanskrit: रक्ष्यमाणः सुसंरब्धैः पुत्रैः शस्त्रभृतां वरः ।वाहिनीप्रमुखं वीरः संप्रकर्षन्नशोभत ॥१८॥
transliteration: rakṣyamāṇaḥ susaṁrabdhaiḥ putraiḥ śastrabhṛtāṁ varaḥ ,vāhinīpramukhaṁ vīraḥ saṁprakarṣannaśobhata.
english: The hero Karṇa, the best among weapon-bearers, was protected by his very enraged sons, and he shone brightly as he led the army's vanguard.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 31 Verse 18. The hero Karṇa, the best among weapon-bearers, was protected by his very enraged sons, and he shone brightly as he led the army's vanguard.

sanskrit: अयोरत्निर्महाबाहुः सूर्यवैश्वानरद्युतिः ।महाद्विपस्कन्धगतः पिङ्गलः प्रियदर्शनः ।दुःशासनो वृतः सैन्यैः स्थितो व्यूहस्य पृष्ठतः ॥१९॥
transliteration: ayoratnirmahābāhuḥ sūryavaiśvānaradyutiḥ ,mahādvipaskandhagataḥ piṅgalaḥ priyadarśanaḥ ,duḥśāsano vṛtaḥ sainyaiḥ sthito vyūhasya pṛṣṭhataḥ.
english: Duḥśāsana, who had iron armlets and mighty arms, whose radiance was like the sun and fire, who rode on the shoulder of a great elephant, tawny-colored, and of pleasing appearance, stood surrounded by his troops at the rear of the battle formation.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 31 Verse 19. Duḥśāsana, who had iron armlets and mighty arms, whose radiance was like the sun and fire, who rode on the shoulder of a great elephant, tawny-colored, and of pleasing appearance, stood surrounded by his troops at the rear of the battle formation.

sanskrit: तमन्वयान्महाराज स्वयं दुर्योधनो नृपः ।चित्राश्वैश्चित्रसंनाहैः सोदर्यैरभिरक्षितः ॥२०॥
transliteration: tamanvayānmahārāja svayaṁ duryodhano nṛpaḥ ,citrāśvaiścitrasaṁnāhaiḥ sodaryairabhirakṣitaḥ.
english: O great king, King Duryodhana himself followed that warrior, well-protected by his own brothers who were mounted on splendid, diversely colored horses and adorned in magnificent armor.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 31 Verse 20. O great king, King Duryodhana himself followed that warrior, well-protected by his own brothers who were mounted on splendid, diversely colored horses and adorned in magnificent armor.

sanskrit: रक्ष्यमाणो महावीर्यैः सहितैर्मद्रकेकयैः ।अशोभत महाराज देवैरिव शतक्रतुः ॥२१॥
transliteration: rakṣyamāṇo mahāvīryaiḥ sahitairmadrakekayaiḥ ,aśobhata mahārāja devairiva śatakratuḥ.
english: O great king, protected by powerful warriors, accompanied by the Madras and Kekayas, he shone radiantly, just like Indra among the gods.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 31 Verse 21. O great king, protected by powerful warriors, accompanied by the Madras and Kekayas, he shone radiantly, just like Indra among the gods.

sanskrit: अश्वत्थामा कुरूणां च ये प्रवीरा महारथाः ।नित्यमत्ताश्च मातङ्गाः शूरैर्म्लेच्छैरधिष्ठिताः ।अन्वयुस्तद्रथानीकं क्षरन्त इव तोयदाः ॥२२॥
transliteration: aśvatthāmā kurūṇāṁ ca ye pravīrā mahārathāḥ ,nityamattāśca mātaṅgāḥ śūrairmlecchairadhiṣṭhitāḥ ,anvayustadrathānīkaṁ kṣaranta iva toyadāḥ.
english: Aśvatthāmā and those other supreme Kuru warriors, great chariot fighters, along with the perpetually rutting elephants (mātaṅga) guided by brave Mleccha warriors, followed that chariot division, appearing like clouds (toyada) pouring down rain.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 31 Verse 22. Aśvatthāmā and those other supreme Kuru warriors, great chariot fighters, along with the perpetually rutting elephants (mātaṅga) guided by brave Mleccha warriors, followed that chariot division, appearing like clouds (toyada) pouring down rain.

sanskrit: ते ध्वजैर्वैजयन्तीभिर्ज्वलद्भिः परमायुधैः ।सादिभिश्चास्थिता रेजुर्द्रुमवन्त इवाचलाः ॥२३॥
transliteration: te dhvajairvaijayantībhirjvaladbhiḥ paramāyudhaiḥ ,sādibhiścāsthitā rejurdrumavanta ivācalāḥ.
english: They, arrayed with their banners, victory flags, blazing supreme weapons, and attended by their mounted warriors, shone brilliantly, like mountains covered with trees.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 31 Verse 23. They, arrayed with their banners, victory flags, blazing supreme weapons, and attended by their mounted warriors, shone brilliantly, like mountains covered with trees.

sanskrit: तेषां पदातिनागानां पादरक्षाः सहस्रशः ।पट्टिशासिधराः शूरा बभूवुरनिवर्तिनः ॥२४॥
transliteration: teṣāṁ padātināgānāṁ pādarakṣāḥ sahasraśaḥ ,paṭṭiśāsidharāḥ śūrā babhūvuranivartinaḥ.
english: For those foot-soldiers and elephants, there were thousands of brave and unwavering protectors, armed with battle-axes and swords.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 31 Verse 24. For those foot-soldiers and elephants, there were thousands of brave and unwavering protectors, armed with battle-axes and swords.

sanskrit: सादिभिः स्यन्दनैर्नागैरधिकं समलंकृतैः ।स व्यूहराजो विबभौ देवासुरचमूपमः ॥२५॥
transliteration: sādibhiḥ syandanairnāgairadhikaṁ samalaṁkṛtaiḥ ,sa vyūharājo vibabhau devāsuracamūpamaḥ.
english: That king of arrays (vyūharāja), highly adorned with cavalry, chariots, and elephants, shone brilliantly like an army of gods and asuras.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 31 Verse 25. That king of arrays (vyūharāja), highly adorned with cavalry, chariots, and elephants, shone brilliantly like an army of gods and asuras.

sanskrit: बार्हस्पत्यः सुविहितो नायकेन विपश्चिता ।नृत्यतीव महाव्यूहः परेषामादधद्भयम् ॥२६॥
transliteration: bārhaspatyaḥ suvihito nāyakena vipaścitā ,nṛtyatīva mahāvyūhaḥ pareṣāmādadhadbhayam.
english: This great array (mahāvyūha), a Bārhaspatya formation, expertly arranged by a wise (vipaścita) leader, appears to dance as it inspires fear in its enemies.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 31 Verse 26. This great array (mahāvyūha), a Bārhaspatya formation, expertly arranged by a wise (vipaścita) leader, appears to dance as it inspires fear in its enemies.

sanskrit: तस्य पक्षप्रपक्षेभ्यो निष्पतन्ति युयुत्सवः ।पत्त्यश्वरथमातङ्गाः प्रावृषीव बलाहकाः ॥२७॥
transliteration: tasya pakṣaprapakṣebhyo niṣpatanti yuyutsavaḥ ,pattyaśvarathamātaṅgāḥ prāvṛṣīva balāhakāḥ.
english: From its main wings and secondary wings, warriors emerge - foot-soldiers, cavalry, chariots, and elephants - just like clouds issue forth in the rainy season.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 31 Verse 27. From its main wings and secondary wings, warriors emerge - foot-soldiers, cavalry, chariots, and elephants - just like clouds issue forth in the rainy season.

sanskrit: ततः सेनामुखे कर्णं दृष्ट्वा राजा युधिष्ठिरः ।धनंजयममित्रघ्नमेकवीरमुवाच ह ॥२८॥
transliteration: tataḥ senāmukhe karṇaṁ dṛṣṭvā rājā yudhiṣṭhiraḥ ,dhanaṁjayamamitraghnamekavīramuvāca ha.
