english: Then, seeing Karṇa at the forefront of the army, King Yudhiṣṭhira spoke to Dhanañjaya (Arjuna), the destroyer of foes and unparalleled hero.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 31 Verse 28. Then, seeing Karṇa at the forefront of the army, King Yudhiṣṭhira spoke to Dhanañjaya (Arjuna), the destroyer of foes and unparalleled hero.

sanskrit: पश्यार्जुन महाव्यूहं कर्णेन विहितं रणे ।युक्तं पक्षैः प्रपक्षैश्च सेनानीकं प्रकाशते ॥२९॥
transliteration: paśyārjuna mahāvyūhaṁ karṇena vihitaṁ raṇe ,yuktaṁ pakṣaiḥ prapakṣaiśca senānīkaṁ prakāśate.
english: Look, Arjuna, at this formidable battle formation (mahāvyūha) arranged by Karṇa in the battle! The army division shines, equipped with its wings and subdivisions.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 31 Verse 29. Look, Arjuna, at this formidable battle formation (mahāvyūha) arranged by Karṇa in the battle! The army division shines, equipped with its wings and subdivisions.

sanskrit: तदेतद्वै समालोक्य प्रत्यमित्रं महद्बलम् ।यथा नाभिभवत्यस्मांस्तथा नीतिर्विधीयताम् ॥३०॥
transliteration: tadetadvai samālokya pratyamitraṁ mahadbalam ,yathā nābhibhavatyasmāṁstathā nītirvidhīyatām.
english: Having thus indeed observed this great enemy force, let a proper policy (nīti) be formulated so that it does not overpower us.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 31 Verse 30. Having thus indeed observed this great enemy force, let a proper policy (nīti) be formulated so that it does not overpower us.

sanskrit: एवमुक्तोऽर्जुनो राज्ञा प्राञ्जलिर्नृपमब्रवीत् ।यथा भवानाह तथा तत्सर्वं न तदन्यथा ॥३१॥
transliteration: evamukto'rjuno rājñā prāñjalirnṛpamabravīt ,yathā bhavānāha tathā tatsarvaṁ na tadanyathā.
english: Thus addressed by the king, Arjuna, with folded hands, said to the monarch: 'Whatever Your Honor commands, all of that will be exactly so, and not otherwise.'
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 31 Verse 31. Thus addressed by the king, Arjuna, with folded hands, said to the monarch: 'Whatever Your Honor commands, all of that will be exactly so, and not otherwise.'

sanskrit: यस्त्वस्य विहितो घातस्तं करिष्यामि भारत ।प्रधानवध एवास्य विनाशस्तं करोम्यहम् ॥३२॥
transliteration: yastvasya vihito ghātastaṁ kariṣyāmi bhārata ,pradhānavadha evāsya vināśastaṁ karomyaham.
english: O descendant of Bharata, I will indeed carry out the killing that is prescribed for him. The slaying of the chief among them is certainly his destruction, and that I shall accomplish.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 31 Verse 32. O descendant of Bharata, I will indeed carry out the killing that is prescribed for him. The slaying of the chief among them is certainly his destruction, and that I shall accomplish.

sanskrit: युधिष्ठिर उवाच ।तस्मात्त्वमेव राधेयं भीमसेनः सुयोधनम् ।वृषसेनं च नकुलः सहदेवोऽपि सौबलम् ॥३३॥
transliteration: yudhiṣṭhira uvāca ,tasmāttvameva rādheyaṁ bhīmasenaḥ suyodhanam ,vṛṣasenaṁ ca nakulaḥ sahadevo'pi saubalam.
english: Yudhishthira said: "Therefore, you yourself (Arjuna) shall deal with Radheya (Karna); Bhimasena with Suyodhana (Duryodhana); Nakula with Vrishasena; and Sahadeva also with Saubala (Shakuni)."
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 31 Verse 33. Yudhishthira said: "Therefore, you yourself (Arjuna) shall deal with Radheya (Karna); Bhimasena with Suyodhana (Duryodhana); Nakula with Vrishasena; and Sahadeva also with Saubala (Shakuni)."

sanskrit: दुःशासनं शतानीको हार्दिक्यं शिनिपुंगवः ।धृष्टद्युम्नस्तथा द्रौणिं स्वयं यास्याम्यहं कृपम् ॥३४॥
transliteration: duḥśāsanaṁ śatānīko hārdikyaṁ śinipuṁgavaḥ ,dhṛṣṭadyumnastathā drauṇiṁ svayaṁ yāsyāmyahaṁ kṛpam.
english: Shatanika shall deal with Duhshasana; the best among the Shinis (Satyaki) with Hardikya (Kritavarman); and Dhrishtadyumna with Drauni (Ashvatthama). I myself will engage Kripa.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 31 Verse 34. Shatanika shall deal with Duhshasana; the best among the Shinis (Satyaki) with Hardikya (Kritavarman); and Dhrishtadyumna with Drauni (Ashvatthama). I myself will engage Kripa.

sanskrit: द्रौपदेया धार्तराष्ट्राञ्शिष्टान्सह शिखण्डिना ।ते ते च तांस्तानहितानस्माकं घ्नन्तु मामकाः ॥३५॥
transliteration: draupadeyā dhārtarāṣṭrāñśiṣṭānsaha śikhaṇḍinā ,te te ca tāṁstānahitānasmākaṁ ghnantu māmakāḥ.
english: The sons of Draupadi, along with Shikhandi, shall slay the remaining Dhartarashtras (Kauravas). And let those (other) people of ours kill those particular enemies of ours.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 31 Verse 35. The sons of Draupadi, along with Shikhandi, shall slay the remaining Dhartarashtras (Kauravas). And let those (other) people of ours kill those particular enemies of ours.

sanskrit: संजय उवाच ।इत्युक्तो धर्मराजेन तथेत्युक्त्वा धनंजयः ।व्यादिदेश स्वसैन्यानि स्वयं चागाच्चमूमुखम् ॥३६॥
transliteration: saṁjaya uvāca ,ityukto dharmarājena tathetyuktvā dhanaṁjayaḥ ,vyādideśa svasainyāni svayaṁ cāgāccamūmukham.
english: Sanjaya said: Thus addressed by Dharmarāja, Dhanaṃjaya (Arjuna), saying "So be it," commanded his own armies and himself proceeded to the vanguard of the army.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 31 Verse 36. Sanjaya said: Thus addressed by Dharmarāja, Dhanaṃjaya (Arjuna), saying "So be it," commanded his own armies and himself proceeded to the vanguard of the army.

sanskrit: अथ तं रथमायान्तं दृष्ट्वात्यद्भुतदर्शनम् ।उवाचाधिरथिं शल्यः पुनस्तं युद्धदुर्मदम् ॥३७॥
transliteration: atha taṁ rathamāyāntaṁ dṛṣṭvātyadbhutadarśanam ,uvācādhirathiṁ śalyaḥ punastaṁ yuddhadurmadam.
english: Then, having seen that approaching chariot, which had an exceedingly wondrous appearance, Shalya again spoke to Adhirathi (Karṇa), who was fierce in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 31 Verse 37. Then, having seen that approaching chariot, which had an exceedingly wondrous appearance, Shalya again spoke to Adhirathi (Karṇa), who was fierce in battle.

sanskrit: अयं स रथ आयाति श्वेताश्वः कृष्णसारथिः ।निघ्नन्नमित्रान्कौन्तेयो यं यं त्वं परिपृच्छसि ॥३८॥
transliteration: ayaṁ sa ratha āyāti śvetāśvaḥ kṛṣṇasārathiḥ ,nighnannamitrānkaunteyo yaṁ yaṁ tvaṁ paripṛcchasi.
english: That is the chariot approaching, drawn by white horses and with Kṛṣṇa as its charioteer. This is Kaunteya (Arjuna), slaying his enemies, whom you keep inquiring about.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 31 Verse 38. That is the chariot approaching, drawn by white horses and with Kṛṣṇa as its charioteer. This is Kaunteya (Arjuna), slaying his enemies, whom you keep inquiring about.

sanskrit: श्रूयते तुमुलः शब्दो रथनेमिस्वनो महान् ।एष रेणुः समुद्भूतो दिवमावृत्य तिष्ठति ॥३९॥
transliteration: śrūyate tumulaḥ śabdo rathanemisvano mahān ,eṣa reṇuḥ samudbhūto divamāvṛtya tiṣṭhati.
english: A great and tumultuous sound of chariot wheels is heard. This dust, which has risen, stands covering the sky.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 31 Verse 39. A great and tumultuous sound of chariot wheels is heard. This dust, which has risen, stands covering the sky.

sanskrit: चक्रनेमिप्रणुन्ना च कम्पते कर्ण मेदिनी ।प्रवात्येष महावायुरभितस्तव वाहिनीम् ।क्रव्यादा व्याहरन्त्येते मृगाः कुर्वन्ति भैरवम् ॥४०॥
transliteration: cakranemipraṇunnā ca kampate karṇa medinī ,pravātyeṣa mahāvāyurabhitastava vāhinīm ,kravyādā vyāharantyete mṛgāḥ kurvanti bhairavam.
english: O Karna, the earth itself trembles, propelled by the rims of chariot wheels. A mighty wind blows all around your army. These carnivorous animals cry out, and other beasts make dreadful sounds.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 31 Verse 40. O Karna, the earth itself trembles, propelled by the rims of chariot wheels. A mighty wind blows all around your army. These carnivorous animals cry out, and other beasts make dreadful sounds.

sanskrit: पश्य कर्ण महाघोरं भयदं लोमहर्षणम् ।कबन्धं मेघसंकाशं भानुमावृत्य संस्थितम् ॥४१॥
transliteration: paśya karṇa mahāghoraṁ bhayadaṁ lomaharṣaṇam ,kabandhaṁ meghasaṁkāśaṁ bhānumāvṛtya saṁsthitam.
english: Behold, O Karna, that exceedingly dreadful, terrifying, and hair-raising headless trunk, which, appearing like a cloud, stands there, having enveloped the sun.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 31 Verse 41. Behold, O Karna, that exceedingly dreadful, terrifying, and hair-raising headless trunk, which, appearing like a cloud, stands there, having enveloped the sun.

sanskrit: पश्य यूथैर्बहुविधैर्मृगाणां सर्वतोदिशम् ।बलिभिर्दृप्तशार्दूलैरादित्योऽभिनिरीक्ष्यते ॥४२॥
transliteration: paśya yūthairbahuvidhairmṛgāṇāṁ sarvatodiśam ,balibhirdṛptaśārdūlairādityo'bhinirīkṣyate.
english: Behold, in all directions, the sun is intently gazed upon by various kinds of animal herds, and by strong, proud tigers.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 31 Verse 42. Behold, in all directions, the sun is intently gazed upon by various kinds of animal herds, and by strong, proud tigers.

sanskrit: पश्य कङ्कांश्च गृध्रांश्च समवेतान्सहस्रशः ।स्थितानभिमुखान्घोरानन्योन्यमभिभाषतः ॥४३॥
transliteration: paśya kaṅkāṁśca gṛdhrāṁśca samavetānsahasraśaḥ ,sthitānabhimukhānghorānanyonyamabhibhāṣataḥ.
english: Behold, thousands of dreadful cranes and vultures, gathered and standing face-to-face, speaking to one another.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 31 Verse 43. Behold, thousands of dreadful cranes and vultures, gathered and standing face-to-face, speaking to one another.

sanskrit: सिताश्चाश्वाः समायुक्तास्तव कर्ण महारथे ।प्रदराः प्रज्वलन्त्येते ध्वजश्चैव प्रकम्पते ॥४४॥
transliteration: sitāścāśvāḥ samāyuktāstava karṇa mahārathe ,pradarāḥ prajvalantyete dhvajaścaiva prakampate.
english: O Karna, your white horses are yoked to your great chariot. These darts are blazing brightly, and your banner is indeed trembling.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 31 Verse 44. O Karna, your white horses are yoked to your great chariot. These darts are blazing brightly, and your banner is indeed trembling.

sanskrit: उदीर्यतो हयान्पश्य महाकायान्महाजवान् ।प्लवमानान्दर्शनीयानाकाशे गरुडानिव ॥४५॥
transliteration: udīryato hayānpaśya mahākāyānmahājavān ,plavamānāndarśanīyānākāśe garuḍāniva.
english: Behold the horses – immense-bodied, exceptionally swift, and beautiful – soaring and hovering in the sky as if they were Garuḍas (divine eagles).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 31 Verse 45. Behold the horses – immense-bodied, exceptionally swift, and beautiful – soaring and hovering in the sky as if they were Garuḍas (divine eagles).

sanskrit: ध्रुवमेषु निमित्तेषु भूमिमावृत्य पार्थिवाः ।स्वप्स्यन्ति निहताः कर्ण शतशोऽथ सहस्रशः ॥४६॥
transliteration: dhruvameṣu nimitteṣu bhūmimāvṛtya pārthivāḥ ,svapsyanti nihatāḥ karṇa śataśo'tha sahasraśaḥ.
english: O Karna, these omens surely indicate that kings, filling the earth, will lie dead, slain by hundreds and thousands.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 31 Verse 46. O Karna, these omens surely indicate that kings, filling the earth, will lie dead, slain by hundreds and thousands.

sanskrit: शङ्खानां तुमुलः शब्दः श्रूयते लोमहर्षणः ।आनकानां च राधेय मृदङ्गानां च सर्वशः ॥४७॥
transliteration: śaṅkhānāṁ tumulaḥ śabdaḥ śrūyate lomaharṣaṇaḥ ,ānakānāṁ ca rādheya mṛdaṅgānāṁ ca sarvaśaḥ.
english: O son of Rādhā (Karna), the tumultuous and hair-raising sound of conch shells is heard everywhere, as is the sound of ānaka drums and mṛdaṅgas.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 31 Verse 47. O son of Rādhā (Karna), the tumultuous and hair-raising sound of conch shells is heard everywhere, as is the sound of ānaka drums and mṛdaṅgas.

sanskrit: बाणशब्दान्बहुविधान्नराश्वरथनिस्वनान् ।ज्यातलत्रेषुशब्दांश्च शृणु कर्ण महात्मनाम् ॥४८॥
transliteration: bāṇaśabdānbahuvidhānnarāśvarathanisvanān ,jyātalatreṣuśabdāṁśca śṛṇu karṇa mahātmanām.
english: O Karna, listen to the various sounds of arrows, the noises of men, horses, and chariots, and the sounds of bowstrings, finger-guards, and arrows, all coming from the great warriors (mahātman).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 31 Verse 48. O Karna, listen to the various sounds of arrows, the noises of men, horses, and chariots, and the sounds of bowstrings, finger-guards, and arrows, all coming from the great warriors (mahātman).

sanskrit: हेमरूप्यप्रमृष्टानां वाससां शिल्पिनिर्मिताः ।नानावर्णा रथे भान्ति श्वसनेन प्रकम्पिताः ॥४९॥
transliteration: hemarūpyapramṛṣṭānāṁ vāsasāṁ śilpinirmitāḥ ,nānāvarṇā rathe bhānti śvasanena prakampitāḥ.
english: On the chariot, banners of various colors, skillfully crafted and adorned with gold and silver, flutter and shine, trembling in the wind.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 31 Verse 49. On the chariot, banners of various colors, skillfully crafted and adorned with gold and silver, flutter and shine, trembling in the wind.

sanskrit: सहेमचन्द्रतारार्काः पताकाः किङ्किणीयुताः ।पश्य कर्णार्जुनस्यैताः सौदामिन्य इवाम्बुदे ॥५०॥
transliteration: sahemacandratārārkāḥ patākāḥ kiṅkiṇīyutāḥ ,paśya karṇārjunasyaitāḥ saudāminya ivāmbude.
english: O Karna, behold these banners of Arjuna, adorned with golden moons, stars, and suns, and furnished with tinkling bells, shining like streaks of lightning in a cloud.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 31 Verse 50. O Karna, behold these banners of Arjuna, adorned with golden moons, stars, and suns, and furnished with tinkling bells, shining like streaks of lightning in a cloud.

sanskrit: ध्वजाः कणकणायन्ते वातेनाभिसमीरिताः ।सपताका रथाश्चापि पाञ्चालानां महात्मनाम् ॥५१॥
transliteration: dhvajāḥ kaṇakaṇāyante vātenābhisamīritāḥ ,sapatākā rathāścāpi pāñcālānāṁ mahātmanām.
english: The banners of the great Pancala warriors, stirred by the wind, make a rattling sound, and so too do their chariots adorned with flags.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 31 Verse 51. The banners of the great Pancala warriors, stirred by the wind, make a rattling sound, and so too do their chariots adorned with flags.

sanskrit: नागाश्वरथपत्त्यौघांस्तावकान्समभिघ्नतः ।ध्वजाग्रं दृश्यते त्वस्य ज्याशब्दश्चापि श्रूयते ॥५२॥
transliteration: nāgāśvarathapattyaughāṁstāvakānsamabhighnataḥ ,dhvajāgraṁ dṛśyate tvasya jyāśabdaścāpi śrūyate.
english: As your multitudes of elephants, horses, chariots, and infantry are being decimated, his banner-top (dhvajāgra) becomes visible, and the sound of his bowstring (jyāśabda) is also heard.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 31 Verse 52. As your multitudes of elephants, horses, chariots, and infantry are being decimated, his banner-top (dhvajāgra) becomes visible, and the sound of his bowstring (jyāśabda) is also heard.

sanskrit: अद्य द्रष्टासि तं वीरं श्वेताश्वं कृष्णसारथिम् ।निघ्नन्तं शात्रवान्संख्ये यं कर्ण परिपृच्छसि ॥५३॥
transliteration: adya draṣṭāsi taṁ vīraṁ śvetāśvaṁ kṛṣṇasārathim ,nighnantaṁ śātravānsaṁkhye yaṁ karṇa paripṛcchasi.
english: Today, Karna, you will see that hero (vīra) with white horses and Kṛṣṇa as his charioteer, slaying enemies (śātravān) in battle (saṅkhye) – the very one about whom you inquire.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 31 Verse 53. Today, Karna, you will see that hero (vīra) with white horses and Kṛṣṇa as his charioteer, slaying enemies (śātravān) in battle (saṅkhye) – the very one about whom you inquire.

sanskrit: अद्य तौ पुरुषव्याघ्रौ लोहिताक्षौ परंतपौ ।वासुदेवार्जुनौ कर्ण द्रष्टास्येकरथस्थितौ ॥५४॥
transliteration: adya tau puruṣavyāghrau lohitākṣau paraṁtapau ,vāsudevārjunau karṇa draṣṭāsyekarathasthitau.
english: Today, Karna, you will see those two supreme men (puruṣavyāghrau), Vāsudeva and Arjuna, both having reddish eyes and being tormentors of foes (paraṃtapau), standing together on a single chariot (ekarathasthitau).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 31 Verse 54. Today, Karna, you will see those two supreme men (puruṣavyāghrau), Vāsudeva and Arjuna, both having reddish eyes and being tormentors of foes (paraṃtapau), standing together on a single chariot (ekarathasthitau).

sanskrit: सारथिर्यस्य वार्ष्णेयो गाण्डीवं यस्य कार्मुकम् ।तं चेद्धन्तासि राधेय त्वं नो राजा भविष्यसि ॥५५॥
transliteration: sārathiryasya vārṣṇeyo gāṇḍīvaṁ yasya kārmukam ,taṁ ceddhantāsi rādheya tvaṁ no rājā bhaviṣyasi.
english: O son of Rādhā (Rādheya), if you manage to kill that one whose charioteer is Vārṣṇeya (Kṛṣṇa) and whose bow is the Gāṇḍīva, then you shall become our king.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 31 Verse 55. O son of Rādhā (Rādheya), if you manage to kill that one whose charioteer is Vārṣṇeya (Kṛṣṇa) and whose bow is the Gāṇḍīva, then you shall become our king.

sanskrit: एष संशप्तकाहूतस्तानेवाभिमुखो गतः ।करोति कदनं चैषां संग्रामे द्विषतां बली ।इति ब्रुवाणं मद्रेशं कर्णः प्राहातिमन्युमान् ॥५६॥
transliteration: eṣa saṁśaptakāhūtastānevābhimukho gataḥ ,karoti kadanaṁ caiṣāṁ saṁgrāme dviṣatāṁ balī ,iti bruvāṇaṁ madreśaṁ karṇaḥ prāhātimanyumān.
english: Śalya said: "This powerful (Arjuna), having been challenged by the Saṃśaptakas, has gone to face them and is indeed causing great destruction to these enemies in battle." To the king of Madra (Śalya), who was speaking thus, Karṇa, filled with excessive anger, replied.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 31 Verse 56. Śalya said: "This powerful (Arjuna), having been challenged by the Saṃśaptakas, has gone to face them and is indeed causing great destruction to these enemies in battle." To the king of Madra (Śalya), who was speaking thus, Karṇa, filled with excessive anger, replied.

sanskrit: पश्य संशप्तकैः क्रुद्धैः सर्वतः समभिद्रुतः ।एष सूर्य इवाम्भोदैश्छन्नः पार्थो न दृश्यते ।एतदन्तोऽर्जुनः शल्य निमग्नः शोकसागरे ॥५७॥
transliteration: paśya saṁśaptakaiḥ kruddhaiḥ sarvataḥ samabhidrutaḥ ,eṣa sūrya ivāmbhodaiśchannaḥ pārtho na dṛśyate ,etadanto'rjunaḥ śalya nimagnaḥ śokasāgare.
english: Behold! This Arjuna, surrounded on all sides by the enraged Saṃśaptakas, is not visible, just like the sun is covered by clouds. O Śalya, Arjuna's end is near; he is submerged in an ocean of sorrow.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 31 Verse 57. Behold! This Arjuna, surrounded on all sides by the enraged Saṃśaptakas, is not visible, just like the sun is covered by clouds. O Śalya, Arjuna's end is near; he is submerged in an ocean of sorrow.

sanskrit: शल्य उवाच ।वरुणं कोऽम्भसा हन्यादिन्धनेन च पावकम् ।को वानिलं निगृह्णीयात्पिबेद्वा को महार्णवम् ॥५८॥
transliteration: śalya uvāca ,varuṇaṁ ko'mbhasā hanyādindhanena ca pāvakam ,ko vānilaṁ nigṛhṇīyātpibedvā ko mahārṇavam.
english: Śalya said: "Who could kill Varuṇa with water, or fire with fuel? Or who could restrain the wind, or drink up the great ocean?"
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 31 Verse 58. Śalya said: "Who could kill Varuṇa with water, or fire with fuel? Or who could restrain the wind, or drink up the great ocean?"

sanskrit: ईदृग्रूपमहं मन्ये पार्थस्य युधि निग्रहम् ।न हि शक्योऽर्जुनो जेतुं सेन्द्रैः सर्वैः सुरासुरैः ॥५९॥
transliteration: īdṛgrūpamahaṁ manye pārthasya yudhi nigraham ,na hi śakyo'rjuno jetuṁ sendraiḥ sarvaiḥ surāsuraiḥ.
english: I believe that defeating Arjuna in battle is of such an impossible nature. Indeed, Arjuna cannot be conquered even by all the gods and asuras (demons) combined, including Indra.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 31 Verse 59. I believe that defeating Arjuna in battle is of such an impossible nature. Indeed, Arjuna cannot be conquered even by all the gods and asuras (demons) combined, including Indra.

sanskrit: अथैवं परितोषस्ते वाचोक्त्वा सुमना भव ।न स शक्यो युधा जेतुमन्यं कुरु मनोरथम् ॥६०॥
transliteration: athaivaṁ paritoṣaste vācoktvā sumanā bhava ,na sa śakyo yudhā jetumanyaṁ kuru manoratham.
english: Therefore, having expressed your satisfaction in this manner, calm your mind. He cannot be defeated in battle; make another plan.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 31 Verse 60. Therefore, having expressed your satisfaction in this manner, calm your mind. He cannot be defeated in battle; make another plan.

sanskrit: बाहुभ्यामुद्धरेद्भूमिं दहेत्क्रुद्ध इमाः प्रजाः ।पातयेत्त्रिदिवाद्देवान्योऽर्जुनं समरे जयेत् ॥६१॥
transliteration: bāhubhyāmuddharedbhūmiṁ dahetkruddha imāḥ prajāḥ ,pātayettridivāddevānyo'rjunaṁ samare jayet.
english: Whoever could conquer Arjuna in battle, that person would be capable of lifting the earth with their arms, burning these creatures in a rage, and casting down the gods from heaven.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 31 Verse 61. Whoever could conquer Arjuna in battle, that person would be capable of lifting the earth with their arms, burning these creatures in a rage, and casting down the gods from heaven.

sanskrit: पश्य कुन्तीसुतं वीरं भीममक्लिष्टकारिणम् ।प्रभासन्तं महाबाहुं स्थितं मेरुमिवाचलम् ॥६२॥
transliteration: paśya kuntīsutaṁ vīraṁ bhīmamakliṣṭakāriṇam ,prabhāsantaṁ mahābāhuṁ sthitaṁ merumivācalam.
english: Behold the heroic son of Kunti, Bhima, who accomplishes deeds with ease, shining brightly, mighty-armed, standing firm like an immovable Meru mountain.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 31 Verse 62. Behold the heroic son of Kunti, Bhima, who accomplishes deeds with ease, shining brightly, mighty-armed, standing firm like an immovable Meru mountain.

sanskrit: अमर्षी नित्यसंरब्धश्चिरं वैरमनुस्मरन् ।एष भीमो जयप्रेप्सुर्युधि तिष्ठति वीर्यवान् ॥६३॥
transliteration: amarṣī nityasaṁrabdhaściraṁ vairamanusmaran ,eṣa bhīmo jayaprepsuryudhi tiṣṭhati vīryavān.
english: This Bhima, unforgiving and perpetually enraged, remembering the long-standing hostility, stands valorous in battle, eager for victory.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 31 Verse 63. This Bhima, unforgiving and perpetually enraged, remembering the long-standing hostility, stands valorous in battle, eager for victory.

sanskrit: एष धर्मभृतां श्रेष्ठो धर्मराजो युधिष्ठिरः ।तिष्ठत्यसुकरः संख्ये परैः परपुरंजयः ॥६४॥
transliteration: eṣa dharmabhṛtāṁ śreṣṭho dharmarājo yudhiṣṭhiraḥ ,tiṣṭhatyasukaraḥ saṁkhye paraiḥ parapuraṁjayaḥ.
english: This is Yudhishthira, the best among the upholders of natural law (dharma), the king of natural law (dharma). He stands in battle, difficult for enemies to overcome, the conqueror of enemy cities.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 31 Verse 64. This is Yudhishthira, the best among the upholders of natural law (dharma), the king of natural law (dharma). He stands in battle, difficult for enemies to overcome, the conqueror of enemy cities.

sanskrit: एतौ च पुरुषव्याघ्रावश्विनाविव सोदरौ ।नकुलः सहदेवश्च तिष्ठतो युधि दुर्जयौ ॥६५॥
transliteration: etau ca puruṣavyāghrāvaśvināviva sodarau ,nakulaḥ sahadevaśca tiṣṭhato yudhi durjayau.
english: And these two tiger-like men, Nakula and Sahadeva, stand in battle like the Ashvins, unconquerable; they are uterine brothers.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 31 Verse 65. And these two tiger-like men, Nakula and Sahadeva, stand in battle like the Ashvins, unconquerable; they are uterine brothers.

sanskrit: दृश्यन्त एते कार्ष्णेयाः पञ्च पञ्चाचला इव ।व्यवस्थिता योत्स्यमानाः सर्वेऽर्जुनसमा युधि ॥६६॥
transliteration: dṛśyanta ete kārṣṇeyāḥ pañca pañcācalā iva ,vyavasthitā yotsyamānāḥ sarve'rjunasamā yudhi.
english: These five sons of Draupadī (Kārṣṇeyas) are seen, arrayed like five mountains, all equal to Arjuna in battle and about to fight.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 31 Verse 66. These five sons of Draupadī (Kārṣṇeyas) are seen, arrayed like five mountains, all equal to Arjuna in battle and about to fight.

sanskrit: एते द्रुपदपुत्राश्च धृष्टद्युम्नपुरोगमाः ।हीनाः सत्यजिता वीरास्तिष्ठन्ति परमौजसः ॥६७॥
transliteration: ete drupadaputrāśca dhṛṣṭadyumnapurogamāḥ ,hīnāḥ satyajitā vīrāstiṣṭhanti paramaujasaḥ.
english: And these sons of Drupada, led by Dhṛṣṭadyumna, stand as heroes of supreme might (paramaujasa), though they are without Satyajit.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 31 Verse 67. And these sons of Drupada, led by Dhṛṣṭadyumna, stand as heroes of supreme might (paramaujasa), though they are without Satyajit.

sanskrit: इति संवदतोरेव तयोः पुरुषसिंहयोः ।ते सेने समसज्जेतां गङ्गायमुनवद्भृशम् ॥६८॥
transliteration: iti saṁvadatoreva tayoḥ puruṣasiṁhayoḥ ,te sene samasajjetāṁ gaṅgāyamunavadbhṛśam.
english: Thus, even as those two lion-like men (puruṣasiṃha) were speaking, those two armies swiftly arrayed themselves like the confluence of the Gaṅgā and Yamunā rivers.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 31 Verse 68. Thus, even as those two lion-like men (puruṣasiṃha) were speaking, those two armies swiftly arrayed themselves like the confluence of the Gaṅgā and Yamunā rivers.

sanskrit: धृतराष्ट्र उवाच ।तथा व्यूढेष्वनीकेषु संसक्तेषु च संजय ।संशप्तकान्कथं पार्थो गतः कर्णश्च पाण्डवान् ॥१॥
transliteration: dhṛtarāṣṭra uvāca ,tathā vyūḍheṣvanīkeṣu saṁsakteṣu ca saṁjaya ,saṁśaptakānkathaṁ pārtho gataḥ karṇaśca pāṇḍavān.
english: Dhṛtarāṣṭra said: "O Saṃjaya, when the armies were thus arrayed and engaged, how did Pārtha (Arjuna) go to the Saṃśaptakas, and Karṇa to the Pāṇḍavas?"
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 32 Verse 1. Dhṛtarāṣṭra said: "O Saṃjaya, when the armies were thus arrayed and engaged, how did Pārtha (Arjuna) go to the Saṃśaptakas, and Karṇa to the Pāṇḍavas?"

sanskrit: एतद्विस्तरतो युद्धं प्रब्रूहि कुशलो ह्यसि ।न हि तृप्यामि वीराणां शृण्वानो विक्रमान्रणे ॥२॥
transliteration: etadvistarato yuddhaṁ prabrūhi kuśalo hyasi ,na hi tṛpyāmi vīrāṇāṁ śṛṇvāno vikramānraṇe.
english: Narrate this battle in detail, for you are indeed an expert. I am never satisfied hearing about the valor of the heroes in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 32 Verse 2. Narrate this battle in detail, for you are indeed an expert. I am never satisfied hearing about the valor of the heroes in battle.

sanskrit: संजय उवाच ।तत्स्थाने समवस्थाप्य प्रत्यमित्रं महाबलम् ।अव्यूहतार्जुनो व्यूहं पुत्रस्य तव दुर्नये ॥३॥
transliteration: saṁjaya uvāca ,tatsthāne samavasthāpya pratyamitraṁ mahābalam ,avyūhatārjuno vyūhaṁ putrasya tava durnaye.
english: Saṃjaya said: "Having positioned the mighty opposing force in that place, Arjuna then formed a battle formation (vyūha), all due to your son's wicked policy (durnaya)."
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 32 Verse 3. Saṃjaya said: "Having positioned the mighty opposing force in that place, Arjuna then formed a battle formation (vyūha), all due to your son's wicked policy (durnaya)."

sanskrit: तत्सादिनागकलिलं पदातिरथसंकुलम् ।धृष्टद्युम्नमुखैर्व्यूढमशोभत महद्बलम् ॥४॥
transliteration: tatsādināgakalilaṁ padātirathasaṁkulam ,dhṛṣṭadyumnamukhairvyūḍhamaśobhata mahadbalam.
english: That great army, arrayed by leaders like Dhṛṣṭadyumna, looked splendid as it was crowded with cavalry and elephants, and densely packed with infantry and chariots.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 32 Verse 4. That great army, arrayed by leaders like Dhṛṣṭadyumna, looked splendid as it was crowded with cavalry and elephants, and densely packed with infantry and chariots.

sanskrit: पारावतसवर्णाश्वश्चन्द्रादित्यसमद्युतिः ।पार्षतः प्रबभौ धन्वी कालो विग्रहवानिव ॥५॥
transliteration: pārāvatasavarṇāśvaścandrādityasamadyutiḥ ,pārṣataḥ prababhau dhanvī kālo vigrahavāniva.
english: The archer Dhṛṣṭadyumna, the son of Pṛṣata, shone forth with horses the color of doves and a radiance equal to the moon and the sun, appearing as if Time (Kāla) himself had taken a physical form.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 32 Verse 5. The archer Dhṛṣṭadyumna, the son of Pṛṣata, shone forth with horses the color of doves and a radiance equal to the moon and the sun, appearing as if Time (Kāla) himself had taken a physical form.

sanskrit: पार्षतं त्वभि संतस्थुर्द्रौपदेया युयुत्सवः ।सानुगा भीमवपुषश्चन्द्रं तारागणा इव ॥६॥
transliteration: pārṣataṁ tvabhi saṁtasthurdraupadeyā yuyutsavaḥ ,sānugā bhīmavapuṣaścandraṁ tārāgaṇā iva.
english: But the sons of Draupadī, eager for battle (yuyutsavaḥ), with their followers and mighty bodies, gathered around Dhṛṣṭadyumna, just as hosts of stars surround the moon.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 32 Verse 6. But the sons of Draupadī, eager for battle (yuyutsavaḥ), with their followers and mighty bodies, gathered around Dhṛṣṭadyumna, just as hosts of stars surround the moon.

sanskrit: अथ व्यूढेष्वनीकेषु प्रेक्ष्य संशप्तकान्रणे ।क्रुद्धोऽर्जुनोऽभिदुद्राव व्याक्षिपन्गाण्डिवं धनुः ॥७॥
transliteration: atha vyūḍheṣvanīkeṣu prekṣya saṁśaptakānraṇe ,kruddho'rjuno'bhidudrāva vyākṣipangāṇḍivaṁ dhanuḥ.
english: Then, having seen the Saṃśaptakas arrayed in battle among the armies, an enraged Arjuna rushed forward, brandishing his Gāṇḍīva bow.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 32 Verse 7. Then, having seen the Saṃśaptakas arrayed in battle among the armies, an enraged Arjuna rushed forward, brandishing his Gāṇḍīva bow.

sanskrit: अथ संशप्तकाः पार्थमभ्यधावन्वधैषिणः ।विजये कृतसंकल्पा मृत्युं कृत्वा निवर्तनम् ॥८॥
transliteration: atha saṁśaptakāḥ pārthamabhyadhāvanvadhaiṣiṇaḥ ,vijaye kṛtasaṁkalpā mṛtyuṁ kṛtvā nivartanam.
english: Then, the Saṃśaptakas, desiring to kill, rushed towards Pārtha (Arjuna). They had made a firm resolve for victory, considering death to be their only way back from the battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 32 Verse 8. Then, the Saṃśaptakas, desiring to kill, rushed towards Pārtha (Arjuna). They had made a firm resolve for victory, considering death to be their only way back from the battle.

sanskrit: तदश्वसंघबहुलं मत्तनागरथाकुलम् ।पत्तिमच्छूरवीरौघैर्द्रुतमर्जुनमाद्रवत् ॥९॥
transliteration: tadaśvasaṁghabahulaṁ mattanāgarathākulam ,pattimacchūravīraughairdrutamarjunamādravat.
english: That army, abundant with horse troops and swarming with intoxicated elephants and chariots, swiftly rushed towards Arjuna, accompanied by masses of foot soldiers and valiant heroes.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 32 Verse 9. That army, abundant with horse troops and swarming with intoxicated elephants and chariots, swiftly rushed towards Arjuna, accompanied by masses of foot soldiers and valiant heroes.

sanskrit: स संप्रहारस्तुमुलस्तेषामासीत्किरीटिना ।तस्यैव नः श्रुतो यादृङ्निवातकवचैः सह ॥१०॥
transliteration: sa saṁprahārastumulasteṣāmāsītkirīṭinā ,tasyaiva naḥ śruto yādṛṅnivātakavacaiḥ saha.
english: That tumultuous clash they had with the crowned one (Arjuna) was, we have heard, like the one he previously had with the Nivātakavacas.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 32 Verse 10. That tumultuous clash they had with the crowned one (Arjuna) was, we have heard, like the one he previously had with the Nivātakavacas.

sanskrit: रथानश्वान्ध्वजान्नागान्पत्तीन्रथपतीनपि ।इषून्धनूंषि खड्गांश्च चक्राणि च परश्वधान् ॥११॥
transliteration: rathānaśvāndhvajānnāgānpattīnrathapatīnapi ,iṣūndhanūṁṣi khaḍgāṁśca cakrāṇi ca paraśvadhān.
english: Chariots, horses, banners, elephants, foot soldiers, and even chariot commanders; arrows, bows, swords, along with wheels (or discuses) and battle-axes.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 32 Verse 11. Chariots, horses, banners, elephants, foot soldiers, and even chariot commanders; arrows, bows, swords, along with wheels (or discuses) and battle-axes.

sanskrit: सायुधानुद्यतान्बाहूनुद्यतान्यायुधानि च ।चिच्छेद द्विषतां पार्थः शिरांसि च सहस्रशः ॥१२॥
transliteration: sāyudhānudyatānbāhūnudyatānyāyudhāni ca ,ciccheda dviṣatāṁ pārthaḥ śirāṁsi ca sahasraśaḥ.
english: Arjuna (Pārthaḥ) severed thousands of the enemies' uplifted arms along with their weapons, as well as their upraised weapons and their heads.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 32 Verse 12. Arjuna (Pārthaḥ) severed thousands of the enemies' uplifted arms along with their weapons, as well as their upraised weapons and their heads.

sanskrit: तस्मिन्सैन्ये महावर्ते पातालावर्तसंनिभे ।निमग्नं तं रथं मत्वा नेदुः संशप्तका मुदा ॥१३॥
transliteration: tasminsainye mahāvarte pātālāvartasaṁnibhe ,nimagnaṁ taṁ rathaṁ matvā neduḥ saṁśaptakā mudā.
english: The Saṃśaptakas, believing that chariot to be submerged in that army - a great whirlpool resembling the eddies of the netherworld (Pātāla) - roared with delight.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 32 Verse 13. The Saṃśaptakas, believing that chariot to be submerged in that army - a great whirlpool resembling the eddies of the netherworld (Pātāla) - roared with delight.

sanskrit: स पुरस्तादरीन्हत्वा पश्चार्धेनोत्तरेण च ।दक्षिणेन च बीभत्सुः क्रुद्धो रुद्रः पशूनिव ॥१४॥
transliteration: sa purastādarīnhatvā paścārdhenottareṇa ca ,dakṣiṇena ca bībhatsuḥ kruddho rudraḥ paśūniva.
english: Having slain enemies from the front, from the western half, from the north, and from the south, Bibhatsu (Arjuna) acted like an enraged Rudra destroying sacrificial animals.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 32 Verse 14. Having slain enemies from the front, from the western half, from the north, and from the south, Bibhatsu (Arjuna) acted like an enraged Rudra destroying sacrificial animals.

sanskrit: अथ पाञ्चालचेदीनां सृञ्जयानां च मारिष ।त्वदीयैः सह संग्राम आसीत्परमदारुणः ॥१५॥
transliteration: atha pāñcālacedīnāṁ sṛñjayānāṁ ca māriṣa ,tvadīyaiḥ saha saṁgrāma āsītparamadāruṇaḥ.
english: Then, O respected one (māriṣa), an exceedingly dreadful battle (saṃgrāma) ensued between your warriors and those of the Pañcālas, Cedis, and Sṛñjayas.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 32 Verse 15. Then, O respected one (māriṣa), an exceedingly dreadful battle (saṃgrāma) ensued between your warriors and those of the Pañcālas, Cedis, and Sṛñjayas.

sanskrit: कृपश्च कृतवर्मा च शकुनिश्चापि सौबलः ।हृष्टसेनाः सुसंरब्धा रथानीकैः प्रहारिणः ॥१६॥
transliteration: kṛpaśca kṛtavarmā ca śakuniścāpi saubalaḥ ,hṛṣṭasenāḥ susaṁrabdhā rathānīkaiḥ prahāriṇaḥ.
english: Kripa, Kritavarma, and Shakuni, the son of Subala, were greatly enraged. With their eager armies, they attacked with their chariot divisions.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 32 Verse 16. Kripa, Kritavarma, and Shakuni, the son of Subala, were greatly enraged. With their eager armies, they attacked with their chariot divisions.

sanskrit: कोसलैः काशिमत्स्यैश्च कारूषैः केकयैरपि ।शूरसेनैः शूरवीरैर्युयुधुर्युद्धदुर्मदाः ॥१७॥
transliteration: kosalaiḥ kāśimatsyaiśca kārūṣaiḥ kekayairapi ,śūrasenaiḥ śūravīrairyuyudhuryuddhadurmadāḥ.
english: They, intoxicated with battle, fought alongside the Kosalas, the Kaśis and Matsyas, the Karūṣas, the Kekayas, and also the Śūrasenas, who were all brave heroes.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 32 Verse 17. They, intoxicated with battle, fought alongside the Kosalas, the Kaśis and Matsyas, the Karūṣas, the Kekayas, and also the Śūrasenas, who were all brave heroes.

sanskrit: तेषामन्तकरं युद्धं देहपाप्मप्रणाशनम् ।शूद्रविट्क्षत्रवीराणां धर्म्यं स्वर्ग्यं यशस्करम् ॥१८॥
transliteration: teṣāmantakaraṁ yuddhaṁ dehapāpmapraṇāśanam ,śūdraviṭkṣatravīrāṇāṁ dharmyaṁ svargyaṁ yaśaskaram.
english: For them (referring to the brave warriors of the Śūdras, Vaiśyas, and Kṣatriyas), this battle brings an end to their lives and destroys their bodily sins. It is righteous (dharmya), leads to heaven, and brings fame.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 32 Verse 18. For them (referring to the brave warriors of the Śūdras, Vaiśyas, and Kṣatriyas), this battle brings an end to their lives and destroys their bodily sins. It is righteous (dharmya), leads to heaven, and brings fame.

sanskrit: दुर्योधनोऽपि सहितो भ्रातृभिर्भरतर्षभ ।गुप्तः कुरुप्रवीरैश्च मद्राणां च महारथैः ॥१९॥
transliteration: duryodhano'pi sahito bhrātṛbhirbharatarṣabha ,guptaḥ kurupravīraiśca madrāṇāṁ ca mahārathaiḥ.
english: O best of the Bharatas, Duryodhana, accompanied by his brothers, was protected by the chief Kuru warriors and also by the great charioteers of the Madras.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 32 Verse 19. O best of the Bharatas, Duryodhana, accompanied by his brothers, was protected by the chief Kuru warriors and also by the great charioteers of the Madras.

sanskrit: पाण्डवैः सहपाञ्चालैश्चेदिभिः सात्यकेन च ।युध्यमानं रणे कर्णं कुरुवीरोऽभ्यपालयत् ॥२०॥
transliteration: pāṇḍavaiḥ sahapāñcālaiścedibhiḥ sātyakena ca ,yudhyamānaṁ raṇe karṇaṁ kuruvīro'bhyapālayat.
english: A Kuru hero protected Karṇa on the battlefield, as Karṇa fought against the Pāṇḍavas, along with the Pāñcālas, the Cedis, and Sātyaki.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 32 Verse 20. A Kuru hero protected Karṇa on the battlefield, as Karṇa fought against the Pāṇḍavas, along with the Pāñcālas, the Cedis, and Sātyaki.

sanskrit: कर्णोऽपि निशितैर्बाणैर्विनिहत्य महाचमूम् ।प्रमृद्य च रथश्रेष्ठान्युधिष्ठिरमपीडयत् ॥२१॥
transliteration: karṇo'pi niśitairbāṇairvinihatya mahācamūm ,pramṛdya ca rathaśreṣṭhānyudhiṣṭhiramapīḍayat.
english: Karṇa, too, having struck down the great army with his sharp arrows and having crushed the foremost charioteers, afflicted Yudhiṣṭhira.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 32 Verse 21. Karṇa, too, having struck down the great army with his sharp arrows and having crushed the foremost charioteers, afflicted Yudhiṣṭhira.

sanskrit: विपत्रायुधदेहासून्कृत्वा शत्रून्सहस्रशः ।युक्त्वा स्वर्गयशोभ्यां च स्वेभ्यो मुदमुदावहत् ॥२२॥
transliteration: vipatrāyudhadehāsūnkṛtvā śatrūnsahasraśaḥ ,yuktvā svargayaśobhyāṁ ca svebhyo mudamudāvahat.
english: Having rendered thousands of enemies without weapons and without life, he attained both heaven and renown, and brought joy to his own people.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 32 Verse 22. Having rendered thousands of enemies without weapons and without life, he attained both heaven and renown, and brought joy to his own people.

sanskrit: धृतराष्ट्र उवाच ।यत्तत्प्रविश्य पार्थानां सेनां कुर्वञ्जनक्षयम् ।कर्णो राजानमभ्यर्च्छत्तन्ममाचक्ष्व संजय ॥२३॥
transliteration: dhṛtarāṣṭra uvāca ,yattatpraviśya pārthānāṁ senāṁ kurvañjanakṣayam ,karṇo rājānamabhyarcchattanmamācakṣva saṁjaya.
english: Dhṛtarāṣṭra said: "O Sañjaya, tell me how Karṇa, having penetrated the army of the Pārthas and inflicting great slaughter, then approached King Yudhiṣṭhira."
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 32 Verse 23. Dhṛtarāṣṭra said: "O Sañjaya, tell me how Karṇa, having penetrated the army of the Pārthas and inflicting great slaughter, then approached King Yudhiṣṭhira."

sanskrit: के च प्रवीराः पार्थानां युधि कर्णमवारयन् ।कांश्च प्रमथ्याधिरथिर्युधिष्ठिरमपीडयत् ॥२४॥
transliteration: ke ca pravīrāḥ pārthānāṁ yudhi karṇamavārayan ,kāṁśca pramathyādhirathiryudhiṣṭhiramapīḍayat.
english: And which great heroes among the Pārthas (Pāṇḍavas) obstructed Karṇa in battle? And having crushed whom, did Karṇa, the son of Adhiratha, torment Yudhiṣṭhira?
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 32 Verse 24. And which great heroes among the Pārthas (Pāṇḍavas) obstructed Karṇa in battle? And having crushed whom, did Karṇa, the son of Adhiratha, torment Yudhiṣṭhira?

sanskrit: संजय उवाच ।धृष्टद्युम्नमुखान्पार्थान्दृष्ट्वा कर्णो व्यवस्थितान् ।समभ्यधावत्त्वरितः पाञ्चालाञ्शत्रुकर्शनः ॥२५॥
transliteration: saṁjaya uvāca ,dhṛṣṭadyumnamukhānpārthāndṛṣṭvā karṇo vyavasthitān ,samabhyadhāvattvaritaḥ pāñcālāñśatrukarśanaḥ.
english: Sañjaya said: Seeing the Pārthas (Pāṇḍavas), with Dhṛṣṭadyumna as their leader, arrayed (for battle), Karṇa, the tormentor of enemies, swiftly rushed towards the Pañcālas.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 32 Verse 25. Sañjaya said: Seeing the Pārthas (Pāṇḍavas), with Dhṛṣṭadyumna as their leader, arrayed (for battle), Karṇa, the tormentor of enemies, swiftly rushed towards the Pañcālas.

sanskrit: तं तूर्णमभिधावन्तं पाञ्चाला जितकाशिनः ।प्रत्युद्ययुर्महाराज हंसा इव महार्णवम् ॥२६॥
transliteration: taṁ tūrṇamabhidhāvantaṁ pāñcālā jitakāśinaḥ ,pratyudyayurmahārāja haṁsā iva mahārṇavam.
english: O great king, the victorious Pañcālas went to meet him as he rushed swiftly, just like swans (advance towards) a great ocean.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 32 Verse 26. O great king, the victorious Pañcālas went to meet him as he rushed swiftly, just like swans (advance towards) a great ocean.

sanskrit: ततः शङ्खसहस्राणां निस्वनो हृदयंगमः ।प्रादुरासीदुभयतो भेरीशब्दश्च दारुणः ॥२७॥
transliteration: tataḥ śaṅkhasahasrāṇāṁ nisvano hṛdayaṁgamaḥ ,prādurāsīdubhayato bherīśabdaśca dāruṇaḥ.
english: Then, a captivating sound of thousands of conches arose, and from both sides, a terrible sound of drums also appeared.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 32 Verse 27. Then, a captivating sound of thousands of conches arose, and from both sides, a terrible sound of drums also appeared.

sanskrit: नानावादित्रनादश्च द्विपाश्वरथनिस्वनः ।सिंहनादश्च वीराणामभवद्दारुणस्तदा ॥२८॥
transliteration: nānāvāditranādaśca dvipāśvarathanisvanaḥ ,siṁhanādaśca vīrāṇāmabhavaddāruṇastadā.
english: At that time, a terrible din arose, consisting of the sounds of various musical instruments, the roars of elephants, horses, and chariots, and the battle cries of the warriors.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 32 Verse 28. At that time, a terrible din arose, consisting of the sounds of various musical instruments, the roars of elephants, horses, and chariots, and the battle cries of the warriors.

sanskrit: साद्रिद्रुमार्णवा भूमिः सवाताम्बुदमम्बरम् ।सार्केन्दुग्रहनक्षत्रा द्यौश्च व्यक्तं व्यघूर्णत ॥२९॥
transliteration: sādridrumārṇavā bhūmiḥ savātāmbudamambaram ,sārkendugrahanakṣatrā dyauśca vyaktaṁ vyaghūrṇata.
english: The earth, along with its mountains, trees, and oceans; the sky, with its winds and clouds; and heaven, with its sun, moon, planets, and stars, clearly began to reel.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 32 Verse 29. The earth, along with its mountains, trees, and oceans; the sky, with its winds and clouds; and heaven, with its sun, moon, planets, and stars, clearly began to reel.

sanskrit: अति भूतानि तं शब्दं मेनिरेऽति च विव्यथुः ।यानि चाप्लवसत्त्वानि प्रायस्तानि मृतानि च ॥३०॥
transliteration: ati bhūtāni taṁ śabdaṁ menire'ti ca vivyathuḥ ,yāni cāplavasattvāni prāyastāni mṛtāni ca.
english: All creatures considered that sound to be overwhelming and trembled excessively. And those aquatic beings, in particular, were mostly killed.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 32 Verse 30. All creatures considered that sound to be overwhelming and trembled excessively. And those aquatic beings, in particular, were mostly killed.

sanskrit: अथ कर्णो भृशं क्रुद्धः शीघ्रमस्त्रमुदीरयन् ।जघान पाण्डवीं सेनामासुरीं मघवानिव ॥३१॥
transliteration: atha karṇo bhṛśaṁ kruddhaḥ śīghramastramudīrayan ,jaghāna pāṇḍavīṁ senāmāsurīṁ maghavāniva.
english: Then Karna, intensely enraged and swiftly discharging his missile, struck the Pāṇḍava army just as Indra (Maghavan) would strike a demonic host.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 32 Verse 31. Then Karna, intensely enraged and swiftly discharging his missile, struck the Pāṇḍava army just as Indra (Maghavan) would strike a demonic host.

sanskrit: स पाण्डवरथांस्तूर्णं प्रविश्य विसृजञ्शरान् ।प्रभद्रकाणां प्रवरानहनत्सप्तसप्ततिम् ॥३२॥
transliteration: sa pāṇḍavarathāṁstūrṇaṁ praviśya visṛjañśarān ,prabhadrakāṇāṁ pravarānahanatsaptasaptatim.
english: Approaching the Pandava chariots swiftly and discharging arrows, he killed seventy-seven of the foremost Prabhadrakas.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 32 Verse 32. Approaching the Pandava chariots swiftly and discharging arrows, he killed seventy-seven of the foremost Prabhadrakas.

sanskrit: ततः सुपुङ्खैर्निशितै रथश्रेष्ठो रथेषुभिः ।अवधीत्पञ्चविंशत्या पाञ्चालान्पञ्चविंशतिम् ॥३३॥
transliteration: tataḥ supuṅkhairniśitai rathaśreṣṭho ratheṣubhiḥ ,avadhītpañcaviṁśatyā pāñcālānpañcaviṁśatim.
english: Then, that best of charioteers killed twenty-five Pañcālas with twenty-five sharp, well-feathered chariot-arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 32 Verse 33. Then, that best of charioteers killed twenty-five Pañcālas with twenty-five sharp, well-feathered chariot-arrows.

sanskrit: सुवर्णपुङ्खैर्नाराचैः परकायविदारणैः ।चेदिकानवधीद्वीरः शतशोऽथ सहस्रशः ॥३४॥
transliteration: suvarṇapuṅkhairnārācaiḥ parakāyavidāraṇaiḥ ,cedikānavadhīdvīraḥ śataśo'tha sahasraśaḥ.
english: The hero killed the Chedis by hundreds and then by thousands, using gold-feathered iron arrows that tore apart the bodies of his enemies.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 32 Verse 34. The hero killed the Chedis by hundreds and then by thousands, using gold-feathered iron arrows that tore apart the bodies of his enemies.

sanskrit: तं तथा समरे कर्म कुर्वाणमतिमानुषम् ।परिवव्रुर्महाराज पाञ्चालानां रथव्रजाः ॥३५॥
transliteration: taṁ tathā samare karma kurvāṇamatimānuṣam ,parivavrurmahārāja pāñcālānāṁ rathavrajāḥ.
english: O great king, the divisions of chariots of the Pañcālas surrounded him, who was performing such a superhuman feat (karma) in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 32 Verse 35. O great king, the divisions of chariots of the Pañcālas surrounded him, who was performing such a superhuman feat (karma) in battle.

sanskrit: ततः संधाय विशिखान्पञ्च भारत दुःसहान् ।पाञ्चालानवधीत्पञ्च कर्णो वैकर्तनो वृषः ॥३६॥
transliteration: tataḥ saṁdhāya viśikhānpañca bhārata duḥsahān ,pāñcālānavadhītpañca karṇo vaikartano vṛṣaḥ.
english: Then, O Bhārata, Karṇa, the mighty son of Vikartana, having aimed five unbearable arrows, killed five Pāñcālas.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 32 Verse 36. Then, O Bhārata, Karṇa, the mighty son of Vikartana, having aimed five unbearable arrows, killed five Pāñcālas.

sanskrit: भानुदेवं चित्रसेनं सेनाबिन्दुं च भारत ।तपनं शूरसेनं च पाञ्चालानवधीद्रणे ॥३७॥
transliteration: bhānudevaṁ citrasenaṁ senābinduṁ ca bhārata ,tapanaṁ śūrasenaṁ ca pāñcālānavadhīdraṇe.
english: And, O Bhārata, he killed Bhānudeva, Citrasena, Senābindu, Tapana, and Śūrasena – these Pāñcālas – in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 32 Verse 37. And, O Bhārata, he killed Bhānudeva, Citrasena, Senābindu, Tapana, and Śūrasena – these Pāñcālas – in battle.

sanskrit: पाञ्चालेषु च शूरेषु वध्यमानेषु सायकैः ।हाहाकारो महानासीत्पाञ्चालानां महाहवे ॥३८॥
transliteration: pāñcāleṣu ca śūreṣu vadhyamāneṣu sāyakaiḥ ,hāhākāro mahānāsītpāñcālānāṁ mahāhave.
english: And as the brave Pāñcālas were being killed by arrows in the great battle, a loud cry of distress arose among the Pāñcālas.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 32 Verse 38. And as the brave Pāñcālas were being killed by arrows in the great battle, a loud cry of distress arose among the Pāñcālas.

sanskrit: तेषां संकीर्यमाणानां हाहाकारकृता दिशः ।पुनरेव च तान्कर्णो जघानाशु पतत्रिभिः ॥३९॥
transliteration: teṣāṁ saṁkīryamāṇānāṁ hāhākārakṛtā diśaḥ ,punareva ca tānkarṇo jaghānāśu patatribhiḥ.
english: While they were being scattered and the directions were filled with cries of distress, Karṇa quickly struck them again with his arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 32 Verse 39. While they were being scattered and the directions were filled with cries of distress, Karṇa quickly struck them again with his arrows.

sanskrit: चक्ररक्षौ तु कर्णस्य पुत्रौ मारिष दुर्जयौ ।सुषेणः सत्यसेनश्च त्यक्त्वा प्राणानयुध्यताम् ॥४०॥
transliteration: cakrarakṣau tu karṇasya putrau māriṣa durjayau ,suṣeṇaḥ satyasenaśca tyaktvā prāṇānayudhyatām.
english: O respected one, Karṇa's two unconquerable sons, Susheṇa and Satyaseṇa, who served as his chariot-wheel protectors, fought having abandoned their lives.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 32 Verse 40. O respected one, Karṇa's two unconquerable sons, Susheṇa and Satyaseṇa, who served as his chariot-wheel protectors, fought having abandoned their lives.

sanskrit: पृष्ठगोपस्तु कर्णस्य ज्येष्ठः पुत्रो महारथः ।वृषसेनः स्वयं कर्णं पृष्ठतः पर्यपालयत् ॥४१॥
transliteration: pṛṣṭhagopastu karṇasya jyeṣṭhaḥ putro mahārathaḥ ,vṛṣasenaḥ svayaṁ karṇaṁ pṛṣṭhataḥ paryapālayat.
english: But Karṇa's eldest son, the great charioteer (mahāratha) Vṛṣasena, himself protected Karṇa from behind, serving as his rear protector.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 32 Verse 41. But Karṇa's eldest son, the great charioteer (mahāratha) Vṛṣasena, himself protected Karṇa from behind, serving as his rear protector.

sanskrit: धृष्टद्युम्नः सात्यकिश्च द्रौपदेया वृकोदरः ।जनमेजयः शिखण्डी च प्रवीराश्च प्रभद्रकाः ॥४२॥
transliteration: dhṛṣṭadyumnaḥ sātyakiśca draupadeyā vṛkodaraḥ ,janamejayaḥ śikhaṇḍī ca pravīrāśca prabhadrakāḥ.
english: Dhṛṣṭadyumna, Satyaki, the sons of Draupadi, Vṛkodara (Bhīma), Janamejaya, Śikhaṇḍī, and the great heroes (pravīrāḥ) and the Prabhadrakas (a specific contingent of warriors) were present.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 32 Verse 42. Dhṛṣṭadyumna, Satyaki, the sons of Draupadi, Vṛkodara (Bhīma), Janamejaya, Śikhaṇḍī, and the great heroes (pravīrāḥ) and the Prabhadrakas (a specific contingent of warriors) were present.

sanskrit: चेदिकेकयपाञ्चाला यमौ मत्स्याश्च दंशिताः ।समभ्यधावन्राधेयं जिघांसन्तः प्रहारिणः ॥४३॥
transliteration: cedikekayapāñcālā yamau matsyāśca daṁśitāḥ ,samabhyadhāvanrādheyaṁ jighāṁsantaḥ prahāriṇaḥ.
english: The Chedis, Kekayas, Panchalas, the two twins (Nakula and Sahadeva), and the Matsyas, all armored and acting as assailants, rushed towards Rādheya (Karṇa) desiring to kill him.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 32 Verse 43. The Chedis, Kekayas, Panchalas, the two twins (Nakula and Sahadeva), and the Matsyas, all armored and acting as assailants, rushed towards Rādheya (Karṇa) desiring to kill him.

sanskrit: त एनं विविधैः शस्त्रैः शरधाराभिरेव च ।अभ्यवर्षन्विमृद्नन्तः प्रावृषीवाम्बुदा गिरिम् ॥४४॥
transliteration: ta enaṁ vividhaiḥ śastraiḥ śaradhārābhireva ca ,abhyavarṣanvimṛdnantaḥ prāvṛṣīvāmbudā girim.
english: They showered him with various weapons and torrents of arrows, crushing him like clouds in the rainy season deluge a mountain.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 32 Verse 44. They showered him with various weapons and torrents of arrows, crushing him like clouds in the rainy season deluge a mountain.

sanskrit: पितरं तु परीप्सन्तः कर्णपुत्राः प्रहारिणः ।त्वदीयाश्चापरे राजन्वीरा वीरानवारयन् ॥४५॥
transliteration: pitaraṁ tu parīpsantaḥ karṇaputrāḥ prahāriṇaḥ ,tvadīyāścāpare rājanvīrā vīrānavārayan.
english: However, Karna's sons, striking forcefully and eager to protect their father, as well as your other warriors, O King, held back (the enemy) warriors.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 32 Verse 45. However, Karna's sons, striking forcefully and eager to protect their father, as well as your other warriors, O King, held back (the enemy) warriors.

sanskrit: सुषेणो भीमसेनस्य छित्त्वा भल्लेन कार्मुकम् ।नाराचैः सप्तभिर्विद्ध्वा हृदि भीमं ननाद ह ॥४६॥
transliteration: suṣeṇo bhīmasenasya chittvā bhallena kārmukam ,nārācaiḥ saptabhirviddhvā hṛdi bhīmaṁ nanāda ha.
english: Suṣeṇa, having cut Bhīmasena's bow with a broad-headed arrow (bhalla), then pierced Bhīma in the heart with seven iron arrows (nārāca) and roared.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 32 Verse 46. Suṣeṇa, having cut Bhīmasena's bow with a broad-headed arrow (bhalla), then pierced Bhīma in the heart with seven iron arrows (nārāca) and roared.

sanskrit: अथान्यद्धनुरादाय सुदृढं भीमविक्रमः ।सज्यं वृकोदरः कृत्वा सुषेणस्याच्छिनद्धनुः ॥४७॥
transliteration: athānyaddhanurādāya sudṛḍhaṁ bhīmavikramaḥ ,sajyaṁ vṛkodaraḥ kṛtvā suṣeṇasyācchinaddhanuḥ.
english: Then, Bhīma, the one of terrible might, taking up another very strong bow and stringing it, cut Suṣeṇa's bow.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 32 Verse 47. Then, Bhīma, the one of terrible might, taking up another very strong bow and stringing it, cut Suṣeṇa's bow.

sanskrit: विव्याध चैनं नवभिः क्रुद्धो नृत्यन्निवेषुभिः ।कर्णं च तूर्णं विव्याध त्रिसप्तत्या शितैः शरैः ॥४८॥
transliteration: vivyādha cainaṁ navabhiḥ kruddho nṛtyanniveṣubhiḥ ,karṇaṁ ca tūrṇaṁ vivyādha trisaptatyā śitaiḥ śaraiḥ.
english: Enraged, and as if dancing, he struck him with nine arrows, and then quickly pierced Karṇa with seventy-three sharp arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 32 Verse 48. Enraged, and as if dancing, he struck him with nine arrows, and then quickly pierced Karṇa with seventy-three sharp arrows.

sanskrit: सत्यसेनं च दशभिः साश्वसूतध्वजायुधम् ।पश्यतां सुहृदां मध्ये कर्णपुत्रमपातयत् ॥४९॥
transliteration: satyasenaṁ ca daśabhiḥ sāśvasūtadhvajāyudham ,paśyatāṁ suhṛdāṁ madhye karṇaputramapātayat.
english: And, with ten (arrows), he struck down Karṇa's son, Satyaseṇa, along with his horses, charioteer, banner, and weapons, in the midst of his watching friends.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 32 Verse 49. And, with ten (arrows), he struck down Karṇa's son, Satyaseṇa, along with his horses, charioteer, banner, and weapons, in the midst of his watching friends.

sanskrit: क्षुरप्रणुन्नं तत्तस्य शिरश्चन्द्रनिभाननम् ।शुभदर्शनमेवासीन्नालभ्रष्टमिवाम्बुजम् ॥५०॥
transliteration: kṣurapraṇunnaṁ tattasya śiraścandranibhānanam ,śubhadarśanamevāsīnnālabhraṣṭamivāmbujam.
english: That head of his, with a moon-like face and pierced by a razor-arrow, was indeed beautiful to behold; it was not merely like a lotus fallen from its stalk.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 32 Verse 50. That head of his, with a moon-like face and pierced by a razor-arrow, was indeed beautiful to behold; it was not merely like a lotus fallen from its stalk.

sanskrit: हत्वा कर्णसुतं भीमस्तावकान्पुनरार्दयत् ।कृपहार्दिक्ययोश्छित्त्वा चापे तावप्यथार्दयत् ॥५१॥
transliteration: hatvā karṇasutaṁ bhīmastāvakānpunarārdayat ,kṛpahārdikyayośchittvā cāpe tāvapyathārdayat.
english: Having slain Karṇa's son, Bhīma again harassed your men. Then, having severed the bows of Kṛpa and Hārdikya, he tormented those two as well.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 32 Verse 51. Having slain Karṇa's son, Bhīma again harassed your men. Then, having severed the bows of Kṛpa and Hārdikya, he tormented those two as well.

sanskrit: दुःशासनं त्रिभिर्विद्ध्वा शकुनिं षड्भिरायसैः ।उलूकं च पतत्रिं च चकार विरथावुभौ ॥५२॥
transliteration: duḥśāsanaṁ tribhirviddhvā śakuniṁ ṣaḍbhirāyasaiḥ ,ulūkaṁ ca patatriṁ ca cakāra virathāvubhau.
english: Having struck Duḥśāsana with three (arrows) and Śakuni with six iron (arrows), (Bhīma) rendered both Ulūka and Patatri chariot-less.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 32 Verse 52. Having struck Duḥśāsana with three (arrows) and Śakuni with six iron (arrows), (Bhīma) rendered both Ulūka and Patatri chariot-less.

sanskrit: हे सुषेण हतोऽसीति ब्रुवन्नादत्त सायकम् ।तमस्य कर्णश्चिच्छेद त्रिभिश्चैनमताडयत् ॥५३॥
transliteration: he suṣeṇa hato'sīti bruvannādatta sāyakam ,tamasya karṇaściccheda tribhiścainamatāḍayat.
english: Saying, "O Suṣeṇa, you are slain!", (Bhīma) took an arrow. Karṇa, however, cut off that arrow of his and struck him (Bhīma) with three (arrows).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 32 Verse 53. Saying, "O Suṣeṇa, you are slain!", (Bhīma) took an arrow. Karṇa, however, cut off that arrow of his and struck him (Bhīma) with three (arrows).

sanskrit: अथान्यमपि जग्राह सुपर्वाणं सुतेजनम् ।सुषेणायासृजद्भीमस्तमप्यस्याच्छिनद्वृषः ॥५४॥
transliteration: athānyamapi jagrāha suparvāṇaṁ sutejanam ,suṣeṇāyāsṛjadbhīmastamapyasyācchinadvṛṣaḥ.
english: Then (Bhīma) took yet another well-jointed, very sharp (arrow). Bhīma discharged it towards Suṣeṇa, but the Bull (Karṇa) also cut off that (arrow) of his.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 32 Verse 54. Then (Bhīma) took yet another well-jointed, very sharp (arrow). Bhīma discharged it towards Suṣeṇa, but the Bull (Karṇa) also cut off that (arrow) of his.

sanskrit: पुनः कर्णस्त्रिसप्तत्या भीमसेनं रथेषुभिः ।पुत्रं परीप्सन्विव्याध क्रूरं क्रूरैर्जिघांसया ॥५५॥
transliteration: punaḥ karṇastrisaptatyā bhīmasenaṁ ratheṣubhiḥ ,putraṁ parīpsanvivyādha krūraṁ krūrairjighāṁsayā.
english: Again, Karṇa, desiring to protect his son, struck the formidable Bhīmasena with seventy-three chariot-arrows, piercing the cruel one with cruel (arrows) with the intention to kill.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 32 Verse 55. Again, Karṇa, desiring to protect his son, struck the formidable Bhīmasena with seventy-three chariot-arrows, piercing the cruel one with cruel (arrows) with the intention to kill.

sanskrit: सुषेणस्तु धनुर्गृह्य भारसाधनमुत्तमम् ।नकुलं पञ्चभिर्बाणैर्बाह्वोरुरसि चार्दयत् ॥५६॥
transliteration: suṣeṇastu dhanurgṛhya bhārasādhanamuttamam ,nakulaṁ pañcabhirbāṇairbāhvorurasi cārdayat.
english: Suṣeṇa, however, taking up his excellent bow, which was capable of bearing heavy strain, tormented Nakula with five arrows in both his arms and his chest.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 32 Verse 56. Suṣeṇa, however, taking up his excellent bow, which was capable of bearing heavy strain, tormented Nakula with five arrows in both his arms and his chest.

sanskrit: नकुलस्तं तु विंशत्या विद्ध्वा भारसहैर्दृढैः ।ननाद बलवन्नादं कर्णस्य भयमादधत् ॥५७॥
transliteration: nakulastaṁ tu viṁśatyā viddhvā bhārasahairdṛḍhaiḥ ,nanāda balavannādaṁ karṇasya bhayamādadhat.
english: But Nakula, having pierced him (Suṣeṇa) with twenty strong arrows capable of bearing heavy strain, let out a mighty roar, causing fear to Karṇa.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 32 Verse 57. But Nakula, having pierced him (Suṣeṇa) with twenty strong arrows capable of bearing heavy strain, let out a mighty roar, causing fear to Karṇa.

sanskrit: तं सुषेणो महाराज विद्ध्वा दशभिराशुगैः ।चिच्छेद च धनुः शीघ्रं क्षुरप्रेण महारथः ॥५८॥
transliteration: taṁ suṣeṇo mahārāja viddhvā daśabhirāśugaiḥ ,ciccheda ca dhanuḥ śīghraṁ kṣurapreṇa mahārathaḥ.
english: O great king, Suṣeṇa, that great warrior (mahāratha), having struck him (Nakula) with ten swift arrows, swiftly cut off his (Nakula's) bow with a razor-sharp arrow.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 32 Verse 58. O great king, Suṣeṇa, that great warrior (mahāratha), having struck him (Nakula) with ten swift arrows, swiftly cut off his (Nakula's) bow with a razor-sharp arrow.

sanskrit: अथान्यद्धनुरादाय नकुलः क्रोधमूर्च्छितः ।सुषेणं बहुभिर्बाणैर्वारयामास संयुगे ॥५९॥
transliteration: athānyaddhanurādāya nakulaḥ krodhamūrcchitaḥ ,suṣeṇaṁ bahubhirbāṇairvārayāmāsa saṁyuge.
english: Then Nakula, overcome with anger, took up another bow and restrained Suṣeṇa with many arrows in the battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 32 Verse 59. Then Nakula, overcome with anger, took up another bow and restrained Suṣeṇa with many arrows in the battle.

sanskrit: स तु बाणैर्दिशो राजन्नाच्छाद्य परवीरहा ।आजघ्ने सारथिं चास्य सुषेणं च ततस्त्रिभिः ।चिच्छेद चास्य सुदृढं धनुर्भल्लैस्त्रिभिस्त्रिधा ॥६०॥
transliteration: sa tu bāṇairdiśo rājannācchādya paravīrahā ,ājaghne sārathiṁ cāsya suṣeṇaṁ ca tatastribhiḥ ,ciccheda cāsya sudṛḍhaṁ dhanurbhallaistribhistridhā.
english: O king, that slayer of enemy heroes, having covered all directions with arrows, then struck his charioteer and Suṣeṇa with three (arrows). And with three sharp arrows, he cut his very strong bow into three pieces.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 32 Verse 60. O king, that slayer of enemy heroes, having covered all directions with arrows, then struck his charioteer and Suṣeṇa with three (arrows). And with three sharp arrows, he cut his very strong bow into three pieces.

sanskrit: अथान्यद्धनुरादाय सुषेणः क्रोधमूर्छितः ।अविध्यन्नकुलं षष्ट्या सहदेवं च सप्तभिः ॥६१॥
transliteration: athānyaddhanurādāya suṣeṇaḥ krodhamūrchitaḥ ,avidhyannakulaṁ ṣaṣṭyā sahadevaṁ ca saptabhiḥ.
english: Then, Suṣeṇa, overcome with rage, taking another bow, pierced Nakula with sixty (arrows) and Sahadeva with seven (arrows).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 32 Verse 61. Then, Suṣeṇa, overcome with rage, taking another bow, pierced Nakula with sixty (arrows) and Sahadeva with seven (arrows).

sanskrit: तद्युद्धं सुमहद्घोरमासीद्देवासुरोपमम् ।निघ्नतां सायकैस्तूर्णमन्योन्यस्य वधं प्रति ॥६२॥
transliteration: tadyuddhaṁ sumahadghoramāsīddevāsuropamam ,nighnatāṁ sāyakaistūrṇamanyonyasya vadhaṁ prati.
english: That battle was exceedingly terrible and resembled a conflict between gods and demons, as they rapidly struck each other to kill.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 32 Verse 62. That battle was exceedingly terrible and resembled a conflict between gods and demons, as they rapidly struck each other to kill.

sanskrit: सात्यकिर्वृषसेनस्य हत्वा सूतं त्रिभिः शरैः ।धनुश्चिच्छेद भल्लेन जघानाश्वांश्च सप्तभिः ।ध्वजमेकेषुणोन्मथ्य त्रिभिस्तं हृद्यताडयत् ॥६३॥
transliteration: sātyakirvṛṣasenasya hatvā sūtaṁ tribhiḥ śaraiḥ ,dhanuściccheda bhallena jaghānāśvāṁśca saptabhiḥ ,dhvajamekeṣuṇonmathya tribhistaṁ hṛdyatāḍayat.
english: Sātyaki, having killed Vṛṣasena's charioteer with three arrows, cut his bow with a sharp arrow, and killed his horses with seven (arrows). Having overthrown his banner with one arrow, he then struck him in the heart with three (arrows).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 32 Verse 63. Sātyaki, having killed Vṛṣasena's charioteer with three arrows, cut his bow with a sharp arrow, and killed his horses with seven (arrows). Having overthrown his banner with one arrow, he then struck him in the heart with three (arrows).

sanskrit: अथावसन्नः स्वरथे मुहूर्तात्पुनरुत्थितः ।अथो जिघांसुः शैनेयं खड्गचर्मभृदभ्ययात् ॥६४॥
transliteration: athāvasannaḥ svarathe muhūrtātpunarutthitaḥ ,atho jighāṁsuḥ śaineyaṁ khaḍgacarmabhṛdabhyayāt.
english: Then, having fallen unconscious in his own chariot, he rose again after a moment. And then, desiring to kill Saityaki (Śaineya), he advanced, bearing a sword and shield.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 32 Verse 64. Then, having fallen unconscious in his own chariot, he rose again after a moment. And then, desiring to kill Saityaki (Śaineya), he advanced, bearing a sword and shield.

sanskrit: तस्य चाप्लवतः शीघ्रं वृषसेनस्य सात्यकिः ।वराहकर्णैर्दशभिरविध्यदसिचर्मणी ॥६५॥
transliteration: tasya cāplavataḥ śīghraṁ vṛṣasenasya sātyakiḥ ,varāhakarṇairdaśabhiravidhyadasicarmaṇī.
english: And Saityaki (Śaineya) quickly pierced the sword and shield of Vṛṣasena, who was leaping forward, with ten boar-eared arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 32 Verse 65. And Saityaki (Śaineya) quickly pierced the sword and shield of Vṛṣasena, who was leaping forward, with ten boar-eared arrows.

sanskrit: दुःशासनस्तु तं दृष्ट्वा विरथं व्यायुधं कृतम् ।आरोप्य स्वरथे तूर्णमपोवाह रथान्तरम् ॥६६॥
transliteration: duḥśāsanastu taṁ dṛṣṭvā virathaṁ vyāyudhaṁ kṛtam ,āropya svarathe tūrṇamapovāha rathāntaram.
english: But Duśśāsana, seeing him (Vṛṣasena) rendered charioless and weaponless, quickly took him up into his own chariot and carried him away to a different position.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 32 Verse 66. But Duśśāsana, seeing him (Vṛṣasena) rendered charioless and weaponless, quickly took him up into his own chariot and carried him away to a different position.

sanskrit: अथान्यं रथमास्थाय वृषसेनो महारथः ।कर्णस्य युधि दुर्धर्षः पुनः पृष्ठमपालयत् ॥६७॥
transliteration: athānyaṁ rathamāsthāya vṛṣaseno mahārathaḥ ,karṇasya yudhi durdharṣaḥ punaḥ pṛṣṭhamapālayat.
english: Then, Vṛṣasena, the great warrior (mahāratha) and irresistible in battle, having mounted another chariot, again guarded Karṇa's rear.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 32 Verse 67. Then, Vṛṣasena, the great warrior (mahāratha) and irresistible in battle, having mounted another chariot, again guarded Karṇa's rear.

sanskrit: दुःशासनं तु शैनेयो नवैर्नवभिराशुगैः ।विसूताश्वरथं कृत्वा ललाटे त्रिभिरार्पयत् ॥६८॥
transliteration: duḥśāsanaṁ tu śaineyo navairnavabhirāśugaiḥ ,visūtāśvarathaṁ kṛtvā lalāṭe tribhirārpayat.
english: Śaineya, however, using eighty-one swift arrows, first deprived Duḥśāsana of his charioteer, horses, and chariot. Then, he struck him on the forehead with three (more arrows).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 32 Verse 68. Śaineya, however, using eighty-one swift arrows, first deprived Duḥśāsana of his charioteer, horses, and chariot. Then, he struck him on the forehead with three (more arrows).

sanskrit: स त्वन्यं रथमास्थाय विधिवत्कल्पितं पुनः ।युयुधे पाण्डुभिः सार्धं कर्णस्याप्याययन्बलम् ॥६९॥
transliteration: sa tvanyaṁ rathamāsthāya vidhivatkalpitaṁ punaḥ ,yuyudhe pāṇḍubhiḥ sārdhaṁ karṇasyāpyāyayanbalam.
english: He (Duḥśāsana), however, having mounted another chariot that was properly prepared, fought again with the Pāṇḍavas, thereby increasing Karṇa's strength.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 32 Verse 69. He (Duḥśāsana), however, having mounted another chariot that was properly prepared, fought again with the Pāṇḍavas, thereby increasing Karṇa's strength.

sanskrit: धृष्टद्युम्नस्ततः कर्णमविध्यद्दशभिः शरैः ।द्रौपदेयास्त्रिसप्तत्या युयुधानस्तु सप्तभिः ॥७०॥
transliteration: dhṛṣṭadyumnastataḥ karṇamavidhyaddaśabhiḥ śaraiḥ ,draupadeyāstrisaptatyā yuyudhānastu saptabhiḥ.
english: Then Dhṛṣṭadyumna struck Karṇa with ten arrows. The sons of Draupadī (struck him) with seventy-three, and Yuyudhāna with seven.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 32 Verse 70. Then Dhṛṣṭadyumna struck Karṇa with ten arrows. The sons of Draupadī (struck him) with seventy-three, and Yuyudhāna with seven.

sanskrit: भीमसेनश्चतुःषष्ट्या सहदेवश्च पञ्चभिः ।नकुलस्त्रिंशता बाणैः शतानीकश्च सप्तभिः ।शिखण्डी दशभिर्वीरो धर्मराजः शतेन तु ॥७१॥
transliteration: bhīmasenaścatuḥṣaṣṭyā sahadevaśca pañcabhiḥ ,nakulastriṁśatā bāṇaiḥ śatānīkaśca saptabhiḥ ,śikhaṇḍī daśabhirvīro dharmarājaḥ śatena tu.
english: Bhīmasena (struck him) with sixty-four (arrows); Sahadeva with five; Nakula with thirty arrows; and Śatānīka with seven. The hero Śikhaṇḍī (struck him) with ten, and Dharmarāja (Yudhiṣṭhira) with a hundred.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 32 Verse 71. Bhīmasena (struck him) with sixty-four (arrows); Sahadeva with five; Nakula with thirty arrows; and Śatānīka with seven. The hero Śikhaṇḍī (struck him) with ten, and Dharmarāja (Yudhiṣṭhira) with a hundred.

sanskrit: एते चान्ये च राजेन्द्र प्रवीरा जयगृद्धिनः ।अभ्यर्दयन्महेष्वासं सूतपुत्रं महामृधे ॥७२॥
transliteration: ete cānye ca rājendra pravīrā jayagṛddhinaḥ ,abhyardayanmaheṣvāsaṁ sūtaputraṁ mahāmṛdhe.
english: O King (rājendra), these and other great heroes, eager for victory, attacked the great archer, Karna (sūtaputra), in the great battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 32 Verse 72. O King (rājendra), these and other great heroes, eager for victory, attacked the great archer, Karna (sūtaputra), in the great battle.

sanskrit: तान्सूतपुत्रो विशिखैर्दशभिर्दशभिः शितैः ।रथे चारु चरन्वीरः प्रत्यविध्यदरिंदमः ॥७३॥
transliteration: tānsūtaputro viśikhairdaśabhirdaśabhiḥ śitaiḥ ,rathe cāru caranvīraḥ pratyavidhyadariṁdamaḥ.
english: The heroic Karna (sūtaputra), the subduer of enemies (arindama), skillfully moving on his chariot, struck them back with ten sharp arrows each.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 32 Verse 73. The heroic Karna (sūtaputra), the subduer of enemies (arindama), skillfully moving on his chariot, struck them back with ten sharp arrows each.

sanskrit: तत्रास्त्रवीर्यं कर्णस्य लाघवं च महात्मनः ।अपश्याम महाराज तदद्भुतमिवाभवत् ॥७४॥
transliteration: tatrāstravīryaṁ karṇasya lāghavaṁ ca mahātmanaḥ ,apaśyāma mahārāja tadadbhutamivābhavat.
english: O great king (mahārāja), then we witnessed the prowess with weapons (astravīrya) and the agility of the great-souled Karna (mahātman); that was truly wondrous.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 32 Verse 74. O great king (mahārāja), then we witnessed the prowess with weapons (astravīrya) and the agility of the great-souled Karna (mahātman); that was truly wondrous.

sanskrit: न ह्याददानं ददृशुः संदधानं च सायकान् ।विमुञ्चन्तं च संरम्भाद्ददृशुस्ते महारथम् ॥७५॥
transliteration: na hyādadānaṁ dadṛśuḥ saṁdadhānaṁ ca sāyakān ,vimuñcantaṁ ca saṁrambhāddadṛśuste mahāratham.
english: Indeed, they did not perceive him taking up arrows, nor stringing them, nor releasing them. They merely saw that great warrior (mahāratha) in the heat of his furious action (saṃrambha).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 32 Verse 75. Indeed, they did not perceive him taking up arrows, nor stringing them, nor releasing them. They merely saw that great warrior (mahāratha) in the heat of his furious action (saṃrambha).

sanskrit: द्यौर्वियद्भूर्दिशश्चाशु प्रणुन्ना निशितैः शरैः ।अरुणाभ्रावृताकारं तस्मिन्देशे बभौ वियत् ॥७६॥
transliteration: dyaurviyadbhūrdiśaścāśu praṇunnā niśitaiḥ śaraiḥ ,aruṇābhrāvṛtākāraṁ tasmindeśe babhau viyat.
english: The heavens, the atmosphere, the earth, and the directions were quickly pierced by sharp arrows. In that region, the sky shone, appearing covered with reddish clouds.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 32 Verse 76. The heavens, the atmosphere, the earth, and the directions were quickly pierced by sharp arrows. In that region, the sky shone, appearing covered with reddish clouds.

sanskrit: नृत्यन्निव हि राधेयश्चापहस्तः प्रतापवान् ।यैर्विद्धः प्रत्यविध्यत्तानेकैकं त्रिगुणैः शरैः ॥७७॥
transliteration: nṛtyanniva hi rādheyaścāpahastaḥ pratāpavān ,yairviddhaḥ pratyavidhyattānekaikaṁ triguṇaiḥ śaraiḥ.
english: Indeed, the mighty Karṇa, son of Rādhā, with his bow in hand, was as if dancing. Struck by their arrows, he retaliated, piercing each of them with three arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 32 Verse 77. Indeed, the mighty Karṇa, son of Rādhā, with his bow in hand, was as if dancing. Struck by their arrows, he retaliated, piercing each of them with three arrows.

sanskrit: दशभिर्दशभिश्चैनान्पुनर्विद्ध्वा ननाद ह ।साश्वसूतध्वजच्छत्रास्ततस्ते विवरं ददुः ॥७८॥
transliteration: daśabhirdaśabhiścainānpunarviddhvā nanāda ha ,sāśvasūtadhvajacchatrāstataste vivaraṁ daduḥ.
english: Having pierced them again, each with ten arrows, he roared. Then, they - with their horses, charioteers, banners, and umbrellas - gave way.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 32 Verse 78. Having pierced them again, each with ten arrows, he roared. Then, they - with their horses, charioteers, banners, and umbrellas - gave way.

sanskrit: तान्प्रमृद्नन्महेष्वासान्राधेयः शरवृष्टिभिः ।राजानीकमसंबाधं प्राविशच्छत्रुकर्शनः ॥७९॥
transliteration: tānpramṛdnanmaheṣvāsānrādheyaḥ śaravṛṣṭibhiḥ ,rājānīkamasaṁbādhaṁ prāviśacchatrukarśanaḥ.
english: Crushing those mighty archers with volleys of arrows, Karṇa, the annihilator of foes, entered the royal army unopposed.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 32 Verse 79. Crushing those mighty archers with volleys of arrows, Karṇa, the annihilator of foes, entered the royal army unopposed.

sanskrit: स रथांस्त्रिशतान्हत्वा चेदीनामनिवर्तिनाम् ।राधेयो निशितैर्बाणैस्ततोऽभ्यार्च्छद्युधिष्ठिरम् ॥८०॥
transliteration: sa rathāṁstriśatānhatvā cedīnāmanivartinām ,rādheyo niśitairbāṇaistato'bhyārcchadyudhiṣṭhiram.
english: After destroying three hundred chariots of the unyielding Chedis, Karna (Rādheya) then attacked Yudhishthira with sharp arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 32 Verse 80. After destroying three hundred chariots of the unyielding Chedis, Karna (Rādheya) then attacked Yudhishthira with sharp arrows.

sanskrit: ततस्ते पाण्डवा राजञ्शिखण्डी च ससात्यकिः ।राधेयात्परिरक्षन्तो राजानं पर्यवारयन् ॥८१॥
transliteration: tataste pāṇḍavā rājañśikhaṇḍī ca sasātyakiḥ ,rādheyātparirakṣanto rājānaṁ paryavārayan.
english: Then, O King, those Pāṇḍavas, along with Śikhaṇḍī and Satyaki, surrounded the king (Yudhiṣṭhira), protecting him from Karna (Rādheya).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 32 Verse 81. Then, O King, those Pāṇḍavas, along with Śikhaṇḍī and Satyaki, surrounded the king (Yudhiṣṭhira), protecting him from Karna (Rādheya).

sanskrit: तथैव तावकाः सर्वे कर्णं दुर्वारणं रणे ।यत्ताः सेनामहेष्वासाः पर्यरक्षन्त सर्वशः ॥८२॥
transliteration: tathaiva tāvakāḥ sarve karṇaṁ durvāraṇaṁ raṇe ,yattāḥ senāmaheṣvāsāḥ paryarakṣanta sarvaśaḥ.
english: Similarly, all your warriors, who were great archers of the army and ready, completely protected Karna, who was irresistible in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 32 Verse 82. Similarly, all your warriors, who were great archers of the army and ready, completely protected Karna, who was irresistible in battle.

sanskrit: नानावादित्रघोषाश्च प्रादुरासन्विशां पते ।सिंहनादश्च संजज्ञे शूराणामनिवर्तिनाम् ॥८३॥
transliteration: nānāvāditraghoṣāśca prādurāsanviśāṁ pate ,siṁhanādaśca saṁjajñe śūrāṇāmanivartinām.
english: And, O lord of the people, the sounds of various musical instruments arose, and the lion's roar of the unyielding heroes also resounded.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 32 Verse 83. And, O lord of the people, the sounds of various musical instruments arose, and the lion's roar of the unyielding heroes also resounded.

sanskrit: ततः पुनः समाजग्मुरभीताः कुरुपाण्डवाः ।युधिष्ठिरमुखाः पार्थाः सूतपुत्रमुखा वयम् ॥८४॥
transliteration: tataḥ punaḥ samājagmurabhītāḥ kurupāṇḍavāḥ ,yudhiṣṭhiramukhāḥ pārthāḥ sūtaputramukhā vayam.
english: Then again, the fearless warriors of both the Kuru and Pāṇḍava sides gathered. The Pārthas (Pandavas) were led by Yudhishthira, and we (the Kaurava forces) were led by the son of Sūta (Karṇa).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 32 Verse 84. Then again, the fearless warriors of both the Kuru and Pāṇḍava sides gathered. The Pārthas (Pandavas) were led by Yudhishthira, and we (the Kaurava forces) were led by the son of Sūta (Karṇa).

sanskrit: संजय उवाच ।विदार्य कर्णस्तां सेनां धर्मराजमुपाद्रवत् ।रथहस्त्यश्वपत्तीनां सहस्रैः परिवारितः ॥१॥
transliteration: saṁjaya uvāca ,vidārya karṇastāṁ senāṁ dharmarājamupādravat ,rathahastyaśvapattīnāṁ sahasraiḥ parivāritaḥ.
english: Sañjaya said: Karṇa, having torn apart that army, rushed towards Dharmarāja (Yudhishthira), surrounded by thousands of chariots, elephants, horses, and foot-soldiers.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 33 Verse 1. Sañjaya said: Karṇa, having torn apart that army, rushed towards Dharmarāja (Yudhishthira), surrounded by thousands of chariots, elephants, horses, and foot-soldiers.

sanskrit: नानायुधसहस्राणि प्रेषितान्यरिभिर्वृषः ।छित्त्वा बाणशतैरुग्रैस्तानविध्यदसंभ्रमः ॥२॥
transliteration: nānāyudhasahasrāṇi preṣitānyaribhirvṛṣaḥ ,chittvā bāṇaśatairugraistānavidhyadasaṁbhramaḥ.
english: Undismayed, Karṇa (Vṛṣa) cut down thousands of various weapons that were launched by the enemies, and then pierced those enemies with hundreds of fierce arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 33 Verse 2. Undismayed, Karṇa (Vṛṣa) cut down thousands of various weapons that were launched by the enemies, and then pierced those enemies with hundreds of fierce arrows.

sanskrit: निचकर्त शिरांस्येषां बाहूनूरूंश्च सर्वशः ।ते हता वसुधां पेतुर्भग्नाश्चान्ये विदुद्रुवुः ॥३॥
transliteration: nicakarta śirāṁsyeṣāṁ bāhūnūrūṁśca sarvaśaḥ ,te hatā vasudhāṁ peturbhagnāścānye vidudruvuḥ.
english: He completely severed their heads, arms, and thighs. Struck down, they fell to the earth, while others, defeated, fled.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 33 Verse 3. He completely severed their heads, arms, and thighs. Struck down, they fell to the earth, while others, defeated, fled.

sanskrit: द्रविडान्ध्रनिषादास्तु पुनः सात्यकिचोदिताः ।अभ्यर्दयञ्जिघांसन्तः पत्तयः कर्णमाहवे ॥४॥
transliteration: draviḍāndhraniṣādāstu punaḥ sātyakicoditāḥ ,abhyardayañjighāṁsantaḥ pattayaḥ karṇamāhave.
english: Again, the Dravida, Andhra, and Niṣāda foot soldiers, urged by Sātyaki, harassed Karna in battle, intent on killing him.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 33 Verse 4. Again, the Dravida, Andhra, and Niṣāda foot soldiers, urged by Sātyaki, harassed Karna in battle, intent on killing him.

sanskrit: ते विबाहुशिरस्त्राणाः प्रहताः कर्णसायकैः ।पेतुः पृथिव्यां युगपच्छिन्नं शालवनं यथा ॥५॥
transliteration: te vibāhuśirastrāṇāḥ prahatāḥ karṇasāyakaiḥ ,petuḥ pṛthivyāṁ yugapacchinnaṁ śālavanaṁ yathā.
english: Struck by Karna's arrows, their arms and helmets shattered, they simultaneously fell to the earth, just like a śāla forest cut down.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 33 Verse 5. Struck by Karna's arrows, their arms and helmets shattered, they simultaneously fell to the earth, just like a śāla forest cut down.

sanskrit: एवं योधशतान्याजौ सहस्राण्ययुतानि च ।हतानीयुर्महीं देहैर्यशसापूरयन्दिशः ॥६॥
transliteration: evaṁ yodhaśatānyājau sahasrāṇyayutāni ca ,hatānīyurmahīṁ dehairyaśasāpūrayandiśaḥ.
english: Thus, hundreds, thousands, and tens of thousands of warriors, killed in battle, went to the earth with their bodies, their fame filling the directions.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 33 Verse 6. Thus, hundreds, thousands, and tens of thousands of warriors, killed in battle, went to the earth with their bodies, their fame filling the directions.

sanskrit: अथ वैकर्तनं कर्णं रणे क्रुद्धमिवान्तकम् ।रुरुधुः पाण्डुपाञ्चाला व्याधिं मन्त्रौषधैरिव ॥७॥
transliteration: atha vaikartanaṁ karṇaṁ raṇe kruddhamivāntakam ,rurudhuḥ pāṇḍupāñcālā vyādhiṁ mantrauṣadhairiva.
english: Then, in battle, the Pandavas and Panchalas checked Karna, the son of Vikartana, who was enraged like the god of death, just as one checks a disease with incantations (mantra) and medicines.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 33 Verse 7. Then, in battle, the Pandavas and Panchalas checked Karna, the son of Vikartana, who was enraged like the god of death, just as one checks a disease with incantations (mantra) and medicines.

sanskrit: स तान्प्रमृद्याभ्यपतत्पुनरेव युधिष्ठिरम् ।मन्त्रौषधिक्रियातीतो व्याधिरत्युल्बणो यथा ॥८॥
transliteration: sa tānpramṛdyābhyapatatpunareva yudhiṣṭhiram ,mantrauṣadhikriyātīto vyādhiratyulbaṇo yathā.
english: Having crushed them, he rushed again upon Yudhiṣṭhira, just like an exceedingly virulent disease that is beyond the reach of incantations, medicines, or rituals.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 33 Verse 8. Having crushed them, he rushed again upon Yudhiṣṭhira, just like an exceedingly virulent disease that is beyond the reach of incantations, medicines, or rituals.

sanskrit: स राजगृद्धिभी रुद्धः पाण्डुपाञ्चालकेकयैः ।नाशकत्तानतिक्रान्तुं मृत्युर्ब्रह्मविदो यथा ॥९॥
transliteration: sa rājagṛddhibhī ruddhaḥ pāṇḍupāñcālakekayaiḥ ,nāśakattānatikrāntuṁ mṛtyurbrahmavido yathā.
english: He, obstructed by the Pāṇḍavas, Pāñcālas, and Kekayas, who were desirous of kingship, was unable to overcome them, just as death (mṛtyu) cannot overcome one who knows the ultimate reality (brahman).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 33 Verse 9. He, obstructed by the Pāṇḍavas, Pāñcālas, and Kekayas, who were desirous of kingship, was unable to overcome them, just as death (mṛtyu) cannot overcome one who knows the ultimate reality (brahman).

sanskrit: ततो युधिष्ठिरः कर्णमदूरस्थं निवारितम् ।अब्रवीत्परवीरघ्नः क्रोधसंरक्तलोचनः ॥१०॥
transliteration: tato yudhiṣṭhiraḥ karṇamadūrasthaṁ nivāritam ,abravītparavīraghnaḥ krodhasaṁraktalocanaḥ.
english: Then Yudhiṣṭhira, the slayer of enemy heroes, whose eyes were red with anger, spoke to Karṇa, who was nearby and had been restrained.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 33 Verse 10. Then Yudhiṣṭhira, the slayer of enemy heroes, whose eyes were red with anger, spoke to Karṇa, who was nearby and had been restrained.

sanskrit: कर्ण कर्ण वृथादृष्टे सूतपुत्र वचः शृणु ।सदा स्पर्धसि संग्रामे फल्गुनेन यशस्विना ।तथास्मान्बाधसे नित्यं धार्तराष्ट्रमते स्थितः ॥११॥
transliteration: karṇa karṇa vṛthādṛṣṭe sūtaputra vacaḥ śṛṇu ,sadā spardhasi saṁgrāme phalgunena yaśasvinā ,tathāsmānbādhase nityaṁ dhārtarāṣṭramate sthitaḥ.
english: Karṇa, Karṇa, O you of misguided vision, son of a charioteer, listen to my words! You always compete with the glorious Arjuna in battle. And thus, standing firm in the opinion of Dhṛtarāṣṭra's son, you constantly torment us.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 33 Verse 11. Karṇa, Karṇa, O you of misguided vision, son of a charioteer, listen to my words! You always compete with the glorious Arjuna in battle. And thus, standing firm in the opinion of Dhṛtarāṣṭra's son, you constantly torment us.

sanskrit: यद्बलं यच्च ते वीर्यं प्रद्वेषो यश्च पाण्डुषु ।तत्सर्वं दर्शयस्वाद्य पौरुषं महदास्थितः ।युद्धश्रद्धां स तेऽद्याहं विनेष्यामि महाहवे ॥१२॥
transliteration: yadbalaṁ yacca te vīryaṁ pradveṣo yaśca pāṇḍuṣu ,tatsarvaṁ darśayasvādya pauruṣaṁ mahadāsthitaḥ ,yuddhaśraddhāṁ sa te'dyāhaṁ vineṣyāmi mahāhave.
