english: Whatever strength and valor you possess, and whatever hatred you harbor for the Pāṇḍavas, display all that great prowess (pauruṣa) today, having taken a firm stand. For I will surely extinguish your desire for battle (yuddhaśraddhā) today in this great war.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 33 Verse 12. Whatever strength and valor you possess, and whatever hatred you harbor for the Pāṇḍavas, display all that great prowess (pauruṣa) today, having taken a firm stand. For I will surely extinguish your desire for battle (yuddhaśraddhā) today in this great war.

sanskrit: एवमुक्त्वा महाराज कर्णं पाण्डुसुतस्तदा ।सुवर्णपुङ्खैर्दशभिर्विव्याधायस्मयैः शितैः ॥१३॥
transliteration: evamuktvā mahārāja karṇaṁ pāṇḍusutastadā ,suvarṇapuṅkhairdaśabhirvivyādhāyasmayaiḥ śitaiḥ.
english: Having spoken thus, O great king (Mahārāja), the son of Pāṇḍu (Arjuna) then pierced Karṇa with ten sharp, iron-made arrows, feathered with gold.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 33 Verse 13. Having spoken thus, O great king (Mahārāja), the son of Pāṇḍu (Arjuna) then pierced Karṇa with ten sharp, iron-made arrows, feathered with gold.

sanskrit: तं सूतपुत्रो नवभिः प्रत्यविध्यदरिंदमः ।वत्सदन्तैर्महेष्वासः प्रहसन्निव भारत ॥१४॥
transliteration: taṁ sūtaputro navabhiḥ pratyavidhyadariṁdamaḥ ,vatsadantairmaheṣvāsaḥ prahasanniva bhārata.
english: O Bhārata, that son of Sūta (Karṇa), the subduer of enemies (arindama), who was a great archer (maheṣvāsa), pierced him (Arjuna) back with nine arrows shaped like calf's teeth (vatsadanta), as if smiling.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 33 Verse 14. O Bhārata, that son of Sūta (Karṇa), the subduer of enemies (arindama), who was a great archer (maheṣvāsa), pierced him (Arjuna) back with nine arrows shaped like calf's teeth (vatsadanta), as if smiling.

sanskrit: ततः क्षुराभ्यां पाञ्चाल्यौ चक्ररक्षौ महात्मनः ।जघान समरे शूरः शरैः संनतपर्वभिः ॥१५॥
transliteration: tataḥ kṣurābhyāṁ pāñcālyau cakrarakṣau mahātmanaḥ ,jaghāna samare śūraḥ śaraiḥ saṁnataparvabhiḥ.
english: Then, the heroic Karṇa killed the two Pāñcāla warriors, who were the wheel-guards of the great-souled Arjuna, in battle with two crescent-shaped arrows (kṣura) and other arrows having bent joints (saṃnataparva).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 33 Verse 15. Then, the heroic Karṇa killed the two Pāñcāla warriors, who were the wheel-guards of the great-souled Arjuna, in battle with two crescent-shaped arrows (kṣura) and other arrows having bent joints (saṃnataparva).

sanskrit: तावुभौ धर्मराजस्य प्रवीरौ परिपार्श्वतः ।रथाभ्याशे चकाशेते चन्द्रस्येव पुनर्वसू ॥१६॥
transliteration: tāvubhau dharmarājasya pravīrau paripārśvataḥ ,rathābhyāśe cakāśete candrasyeva punarvasū.
english: Those two excellent heroes, standing on both sides of the king of righteousness (dharma) near his chariot, shone brightly like the Punarvasu stars shine alongside the Moon.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 33 Verse 16. Those two excellent heroes, standing on both sides of the king of righteousness (dharma) near his chariot, shone brightly like the Punarvasu stars shine alongside the Moon.

sanskrit: युधिष्ठिरः पुनः कर्णमविध्यत्त्रिंशता शरैः ।सुषेणं सत्यसेनं च त्रिभिस्त्रिभिरताडयत् ॥१७॥
transliteration: yudhiṣṭhiraḥ punaḥ karṇamavidhyattriṁśatā śaraiḥ ,suṣeṇaṁ satyasenaṁ ca tribhistribhiratāḍayat.
english: Yudhiṣṭhira, in turn, pierced Karṇa with thirty arrows, and he struck Suṣeṇa and Satyasena with three arrows each.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 33 Verse 17. Yudhiṣṭhira, in turn, pierced Karṇa with thirty arrows, and he struck Suṣeṇa and Satyasena with three arrows each.

sanskrit: शल्यं नवत्या विव्याध त्रिसप्तत्या च सूतजम् ।तांश्चास्य गोप्तॄन्विव्याध त्रिभिस्त्रिभिरजिह्मगैः ॥१८॥
transliteration: śalyaṁ navatyā vivyādha trisaptatyā ca sūtajam ,tāṁścāsya goptṝnvivyādha tribhistribhirajihmagaiḥ.
english: He pierced Śalya with ninety (arrows), and the son of the charioteer (Karṇa) with seventy-three. And he pierced Karṇa's protectors, each with three straight-flying arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 33 Verse 18. He pierced Śalya with ninety (arrows), and the son of the charioteer (Karṇa) with seventy-three. And he pierced Karṇa's protectors, each with three straight-flying arrows.

sanskrit: ततः प्रहस्याधिरथिर्विधुन्वानः स कार्मुकम् ।भित्त्वा भल्लेन राजानं विद्ध्वा षष्ट्यानदन्मुदा ॥१९॥
transliteration: tataḥ prahasyādhirathirvidhunvānaḥ sa kārmukam ,bhittvā bhallena rājānaṁ viddhvā ṣaṣṭyānadanmudā.
english: Then Adhirathi (Karṇa), laughing aloud and brandishing his bow, split (Yudhiṣṭhira's defenses) with a bhalla-tipped arrow, then pierced the king with sixty (arrows) and roared with delight.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 33 Verse 19. Then Adhirathi (Karṇa), laughing aloud and brandishing his bow, split (Yudhiṣṭhira's defenses) with a bhalla-tipped arrow, then pierced the king with sixty (arrows) and roared with delight.

sanskrit: ततः प्रवीराः पाण्डूनामभ्यधावन्युधिष्ठिरम् ।सूतपुत्रात्परीप्सन्तः कर्णमभ्यर्दयञ्शरैः ॥२०॥
transliteration: tataḥ pravīrāḥ pāṇḍūnāmabhyadhāvanyudhiṣṭhiram ,sūtaputrātparīpsantaḥ karṇamabhyardayañśaraiḥ.
english: Then, the great warriors of the Pāṇḍavas, desiring to protect Yudhiṣṭhira from the son of Sūta (Karṇa), rushed forward and attacked Karṇa with arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 33 Verse 20. Then, the great warriors of the Pāṇḍavas, desiring to protect Yudhiṣṭhira from the son of Sūta (Karṇa), rushed forward and attacked Karṇa with arrows.

sanskrit: सात्यकिश्चेकितानश्च युयुत्सुः पाण्ड्य एव च ।धृष्टद्युम्नः शिखण्डी च द्रौपदेयाः प्रभद्रकाः ॥२१॥
transliteration: sātyakiścekitānaśca yuyutsuḥ pāṇḍya eva ca ,dhṛṣṭadyumnaḥ śikhaṇḍī ca draupadeyāḥ prabhadrakāḥ.
english: Sātyaki, Cekitāna, Yuyutsu, and also Pāṇḍya; Dhṛṣṭadyumna, Śikhaṇḍī, the sons of Draupadī (Draupadeyāḥ), and the Prabhadrkas (were there).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 33 Verse 21. Sātyaki, Cekitāna, Yuyutsu, and also Pāṇḍya; Dhṛṣṭadyumna, Śikhaṇḍī, the sons of Draupadī (Draupadeyāḥ), and the Prabhadrkas (were there).

sanskrit: यमौ च भीमसेनश्च शिशुपालस्य चात्मजः ।कारूषा मत्स्यशेषाश्च केकयाः काशिकोसलाः ।एते च त्वरिता वीरा वसुषेणमवारयन् ॥२२॥
transliteration: yamau ca bhīmasenaśca śiśupālasya cātmajaḥ ,kārūṣā matsyaśeṣāśca kekayāḥ kāśikosalāḥ ,ete ca tvaritā vīrā vasuṣeṇamavārayan.
english: And the two twins, Bhīmasena, and the son of Śiśupāla; the Kārūṣas, the remaining Matsyas, the Kekayas, the Kāśis, and the Kosalas – these swift warriors obstructed Vasuṣeṇa (Karṇa).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 33 Verse 22. And the two twins, Bhīmasena, and the son of Śiśupāla; the Kārūṣas, the remaining Matsyas, the Kekayas, the Kāśis, and the Kosalas – these swift warriors obstructed Vasuṣeṇa (Karṇa).

sanskrit: जनमेजयश्च पाञ्चाल्यः कर्णं विव्याध सायकैः ।वराहकर्णैर्नाराचैर्नालीकैर्निशितैः शरैः ।वत्सदन्तैर्विपाठैश्च क्षुरप्रैश्चटकामुखैः ॥२३॥
transliteration: janamejayaśca pāñcālyaḥ karṇaṁ vivyādha sāyakaiḥ ,varāhakarṇairnārācairnālīkairniśitaiḥ śaraiḥ ,vatsadantairvipāṭhaiśca kṣurapraiścaṭakāmukhaiḥ.
english: Janamejaya, the Pāñcāla, pierced Karṇa with various types of arrows: boar-eared (varāhakarṇaiḥ), iron (nārācaiḥ), tube-shaped (nālīkaiḥ), sharp (niśitaiḥ), calf-tooth-shaped (vatsadantaiḥ), broad-headed (vipāṭhaiḥ), razor-edged (kṣurapraiḥ), and sparrow-mouthed (caṭakāmukhaiḥ).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 33 Verse 23. Janamejaya, the Pāñcāla, pierced Karṇa with various types of arrows: boar-eared (varāhakarṇaiḥ), iron (nārācaiḥ), tube-shaped (nālīkaiḥ), sharp (niśitaiḥ), calf-tooth-shaped (vatsadantaiḥ), broad-headed (vipāṭhaiḥ), razor-edged (kṣurapraiḥ), and sparrow-mouthed (caṭakāmukhaiḥ).

sanskrit: नानाप्रहरणैश्चोग्रै रथहस्त्यश्वसादिनः ।सर्वतोऽभ्याद्रवन्कर्णं परिवार्य जिघांसया ॥२४॥
transliteration: nānāpraharaṇaiścograi rathahastyaśvasādinaḥ ,sarvato'bhyādravankarṇaṁ parivārya jighāṁsayā.
english: Chariot-warriors, elephant-riders, horse-riders, and foot-soldiers, armed with various fierce weapons, rushed towards Karṇa from all sides, surrounding him with the intention to kill.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 33 Verse 24. Chariot-warriors, elephant-riders, horse-riders, and foot-soldiers, armed with various fierce weapons, rushed towards Karṇa from all sides, surrounding him with the intention to kill.

sanskrit: स पाण्डवानां प्रवरैः सर्वतः समभिद्रुतः ।उदैरयद्ब्राह्ममस्त्रं शरैः संपूरयन्दिशः ॥२५॥
transliteration: sa pāṇḍavānāṁ pravaraiḥ sarvataḥ samabhidrutaḥ ,udairayadbrāhmamastraṁ śaraiḥ saṁpūrayandiśaḥ.
english: Though he was assailed from all sides by the foremost of the Pāṇḍavas, he released the divine weapon (brahman-astra), filling all directions with his arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 33 Verse 25. Though he was assailed from all sides by the foremost of the Pāṇḍavas, he released the divine weapon (brahman-astra), filling all directions with his arrows.

sanskrit: ततः शरमहाज्वालो वीर्योष्मा कर्णपावकः ।निर्दहन्पाण्डववनं चारु पर्यचरद्रणे ॥२६॥
transliteration: tataḥ śaramahājvālo vīryoṣmā karṇapāvakaḥ ,nirdahanpāṇḍavavanaṁ cāru paryacaradraṇe.
english: Then, Karṇa, who was like a fire with great flames of arrows and the heat of his valor, wonderfully moved about in battle, incinerating the Pāṇḍava army (lit. 'forest of Pāṇḍavas').
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 33 Verse 26. Then, Karṇa, who was like a fire with great flames of arrows and the heat of his valor, wonderfully moved about in battle, incinerating the Pāṇḍava army (lit. 'forest of Pāṇḍavas').

sanskrit: स संवार्य महास्त्राणि महेष्वासो महात्मनाम् ।प्रहस्य पुरुषेन्द्रस्य शरैश्चिच्छेद कार्मुकम् ॥२७॥
transliteration: sa saṁvārya mahāstrāṇi maheṣvāso mahātmanām ,prahasya puruṣendrasya śaraiściccheda kārmukam.
english: He, the great archer, having parried the great weapons of the noble (Pāṇḍavas), then laughed and, with his arrows, cut the bow of the best among men (Arjuna).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 33 Verse 27. He, the great archer, having parried the great weapons of the noble (Pāṇḍavas), then laughed and, with his arrows, cut the bow of the best among men (Arjuna).

sanskrit: ततः संधाय नवतिं निमेषान्नतपर्वणाम् ।बिभेद कवचं राज्ञो रणे कर्णः शितैः शरैः ॥२८॥
transliteration: tataḥ saṁdhāya navatiṁ nimeṣānnataparvaṇām ,bibheda kavacaṁ rājño raṇe karṇaḥ śitaiḥ śaraiḥ.
english: Then, Karna, in an instant, having fixed ninety powerful arrows, pierced the king's armor in battle with his sharp arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 33 Verse 28. Then, Karna, in an instant, having fixed ninety powerful arrows, pierced the king's armor in battle with his sharp arrows.

sanskrit: तद्वर्म हेमविकृतं रराज निपतत्तदा ।सविद्युदभ्रं सवितुः शिष्टं वातहतं यथा ॥२९॥
transliteration: tadvarma hemavikṛtaṁ rarāja nipatattadā ,savidyudabhraṁ savituḥ śiṣṭaṁ vātahataṁ yathā.
english: That armor, adorned with gold, shone as it fell then, like a lightning-streaked cloud scattered by the wind and left behind by the sun.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 33 Verse 29. That armor, adorned with gold, shone as it fell then, like a lightning-streaked cloud scattered by the wind and left behind by the sun.

sanskrit: तदङ्गं पुरुषेन्द्रस्य भ्रष्टवर्म व्यरोचत ।रत्नैरलंकृतं दिव्यैर्व्यभ्रं निशि यथा नभः ॥३०॥
transliteration: tadaṅgaṁ puruṣendrasya bhraṣṭavarma vyarocata ,ratnairalaṁkṛtaṁ divyairvyabhraṁ niśi yathā nabhaḥ.
english: That body of the chief of men (puruṣendra), with its armor fallen, shone like a cloudless night sky adorned with divine jewels.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 33 Verse 30. That body of the chief of men (puruṣendra), with its armor fallen, shone like a cloudless night sky adorned with divine jewels.

sanskrit: स विवर्मा शरैः पार्थो रुधिरेण समुक्षितः ।क्रुद्धः सर्वायसीं शक्तिं चिक्षेपाधिरथिं प्रति ॥३१॥
transliteration: sa vivarmā śaraiḥ pārtho rudhireṇa samukṣitaḥ ,kruddhaḥ sarvāyasīṁ śaktiṁ cikṣepādhirathiṁ prati.
english: He, Pārtha, without armor, drenched with blood from the arrows, enraged, hurled the entirely iron spear (śakti) against Adhirathi.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 33 Verse 31. He, Pārtha, without armor, drenched with blood from the arrows, enraged, hurled the entirely iron spear (śakti) against Adhirathi.

sanskrit: तां ज्वलन्तीमिवाकाशे शरैश्चिच्छेद सप्तभिः ।सा छिन्ना भूमिमपतन्महेष्वासस्य सायकैः ॥३२॥
transliteration: tāṁ jvalantīmivākāśe śaraiściccheda saptabhiḥ ,sā chinnā bhūmimapatanmaheṣvāsasya sāyakaiḥ.
english: He pierced that (celestial weapon), blazing as if in the sky, with seven arrows. Thus severed, she fell to the ground, struck by the shafts of the great archer.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 33 Verse 32. He pierced that (celestial weapon), blazing as if in the sky, with seven arrows. Thus severed, she fell to the ground, struck by the shafts of the great archer.

sanskrit: ततो बाह्वोर्ललाटे च हृदि चैव युधिष्ठिरः ।चतुर्भिस्तोमरैः कर्णं ताडयित्वा मुदानदत् ॥३३॥
transliteration: tato bāhvorlalāṭe ca hṛdi caiva yudhiṣṭhiraḥ ,caturbhistomaraiḥ karṇaṁ tāḍayitvā mudānadat.
english: Then Yudhishthira, after striking Karna with four javelins on both arms, on the forehead, and on the chest, roared with joy.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 33 Verse 33. Then Yudhishthira, after striking Karna with four javelins on both arms, on the forehead, and on the chest, roared with joy.

sanskrit: उद्भिन्नरुधिरः कर्णः क्रुद्धः सर्प इव श्वसन् ।ध्वजं चिच्छेद भल्लेन त्रिभिर्विव्याध पाण्डवम् ।इषुधी चास्य चिच्छेद रथं च तिलशोऽच्छिनत् ॥३४॥
transliteration: udbhinnarudhiraḥ karṇaḥ kruddhaḥ sarpa iva śvasan ,dhvajaṁ ciccheda bhallena tribhirvivyādha pāṇḍavam ,iṣudhī cāsya ciccheda rathaṁ ca tilaśo'cchinat.
english: Karna, with blood gushing forth and hissing like an enraged serpent, cut down the banner with an arrow. He then pierced the Pandava (Yudhishthira) with three (arrows), severed his quivers, and broke his chariot into tiny pieces.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 33 Verse 34. Karna, with blood gushing forth and hissing like an enraged serpent, cut down the banner with an arrow. He then pierced the Pandava (Yudhishthira) with three (arrows), severed his quivers, and broke his chariot into tiny pieces.

sanskrit: एवं पार्थो व्यपायात्स निहतप्रार्ष्टिसारथिः ।अशक्नुवन्प्रमुखतः स्थातुं कर्णस्य दुर्मनाः ॥३५॥
transliteration: evaṁ pārtho vyapāyātsa nihataprārṣṭisārathiḥ ,aśaknuvanpramukhataḥ sthātuṁ karṇasya durmanāḥ.
english: In this way, Yudhishthira, the son of Pritha, retreated, as his spearman and charioteer had been killed. Being disheartened, he was unable to stand firm in front of Karna.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 33 Verse 35. In this way, Yudhishthira, the son of Pritha, retreated, as his spearman and charioteer had been killed. Being disheartened, he was unable to stand firm in front of Karna.

sanskrit: तमभिद्रुत्य राधेयः स्कन्धं संस्पृश्य पाणिना ।अब्रवीत्प्रहसन्राजन्कुत्सयन्निव पाण्डवम् ॥३६॥
transliteration: tamabhidrutya rādheyaḥ skandhaṁ saṁspṛśya pāṇinā ,abravītprahasanrājankutsayanniva pāṇḍavam.
english: Rādheya (Karṇa) rushed to him, touched his shoulder with his hand, and, laughing, O King, spoke to the Pāṇḍava (Arjuna) as if reviling him.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 33 Verse 36. Rādheya (Karṇa) rushed to him, touched his shoulder with his hand, and, laughing, O King, spoke to the Pāṇḍava (Arjuna) as if reviling him.

sanskrit: कथं नाम कुले जातः क्षत्रधर्मे व्यवस्थितः ।प्रजह्यात्समरे शत्रून्प्राणान्रक्षन्महाहवे ॥३७॥
transliteration: kathaṁ nāma kule jātaḥ kṣatradharme vyavasthitaḥ ,prajahyātsamare śatrūnprāṇānrakṣanmahāhave.
english: How, indeed, could someone born in a noble lineage and established in the natural law (dharma) of a warrior abandon his enemies in battle while protecting his own life in a great war?
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 33 Verse 37. How, indeed, could someone born in a noble lineage and established in the natural law (dharma) of a warrior abandon his enemies in battle while protecting his own life in a great war?

sanskrit: न भवान्क्षत्रधर्मेषु कुशलोऽसीति मे मतिः ।ब्राह्मे बले भवान्युक्तः स्वाध्याये यज्ञकर्मणि ॥३८॥
transliteration: na bhavānkṣatradharmeṣu kuśalo'sīti me matiḥ ,brāhme bale bhavānyuktaḥ svādhyāye yajñakarmaṇi.
english: My opinion is that you are not skilled in the intrinsic nature (dharma) of a warrior. You are engaged in spiritual strength, self-study, and the performance of Vedic rituals (yajñakarma).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 33 Verse 38. My opinion is that you are not skilled in the intrinsic nature (dharma) of a warrior. You are engaged in spiritual strength, self-study, and the performance of Vedic rituals (yajñakarma).

sanskrit: मा स्म युध्यस्व कौन्तेय मा च वीरान्समासदः ।मा चैनानप्रियं ब्रूहि मा च व्रज महारणम् ॥३९॥
transliteration: mā sma yudhyasva kaunteya mā ca vīrānsamāsadaḥ ,mā cainānapriyaṁ brūhi mā ca vraja mahāraṇam.
english: "O son of Kuntī (Arjuna), do not fight! And do not face these heroes! Do not speak unkindly to them! And do not go to a great battle!"
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 33 Verse 39. "O son of Kuntī (Arjuna), do not fight! And do not face these heroes! Do not speak unkindly to them! And do not go to a great battle!"

sanskrit: एवमुक्त्वा ततः पार्थं विसृज्य च महाबलः ।न्यहनत्पाण्डवीं सेनां वज्रहस्त इवासुरीम् ।ततः प्रायाद्द्रुतं राजन्व्रीडन्निव जनेश्वरः ॥४०॥
transliteration: evamuktvā tataḥ pārthaṁ visṛjya ca mahābalaḥ ,nyahanatpāṇḍavīṁ senāṁ vajrahasta ivāsurīm ,tataḥ prāyāddrutaṁ rājanvrīḍanniva janeśvaraḥ.
english: Having spoken thus, the exceedingly powerful (Karna) then dismissed Arjuna (Pārtha) and struck down the Pāṇḍava army, just as Indra, who wields the thunderbolt, struck down the asuras. Then, O King, the lord of men (Karna) quickly departed, as if feeling shame.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 33 Verse 40. Having spoken thus, the exceedingly powerful (Karna) then dismissed Arjuna (Pārtha) and struck down the Pāṇḍava army, just as Indra, who wields the thunderbolt, struck down the asuras. Then, O King, the lord of men (Karna) quickly departed, as if feeling shame.

sanskrit: अथ प्रयान्तं राजानमन्वयुस्ते तदाच्युतम् ।चेदिपाण्डवपाञ्चालाः सात्यकिश्च महारथः ।द्रौपदेयास्तथा शूरा माद्रीपुत्रौ च पाण्डवौ ॥४१॥
transliteration: atha prayāntaṁ rājānamanvayuste tadācyutam ,cedipāṇḍavapāñcālāḥ sātyakiśca mahārathaḥ ,draupadeyāstathā śūrā mādrīputrau ca pāṇḍavau.
english: Then, at that time, as the unwavering (acyuta) king (Karna) was departing, the Cedis, Pāṇḍavas, and Pāñcālas followed him, along with Sātyaki, the great chariot-warrior (mahāratha), and similarly, the brave sons of Draupadī, and the two Pāṇḍava sons of Mādrī.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 33 Verse 41. Then, at that time, as the unwavering (acyuta) king (Karna) was departing, the Cedis, Pāṇḍavas, and Pāñcālas followed him, along with Sātyaki, the great chariot-warrior (mahāratha), and similarly, the brave sons of Draupadī, and the two Pāṇḍava sons of Mādrī.

sanskrit: ततो युधिष्ठिरानीकं दृष्ट्वा कर्णः पराङ्मुखम् ।कुरुभिः सहितो वीरैः पृष्ठगैः पृष्ठमन्वयात् ॥४२॥
transliteration: tato yudhiṣṭhirānīkaṁ dṛṣṭvā karṇaḥ parāṅmukham ,kurubhiḥ sahito vīraiḥ pṛṣṭhagaiḥ pṛṣṭhamanvayāt.
english: Then Karna, having seen Yudhiṣṭhira's army turned away, pursued its rear, accompanied by brave Kuru warriors who were following behind him.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 33 Verse 42. Then Karna, having seen Yudhiṣṭhira's army turned away, pursued its rear, accompanied by brave Kuru warriors who were following behind him.

sanskrit: शङ्खभेरीनिनादैश्च कार्मुकाणां च निस्वनैः ।बभूव धार्तराष्ट्राणां सिंहनादरवस्तदा ॥४३॥
transliteration: śaṅkhabherīninādaiśca kārmukāṇāṁ ca nisvanaiḥ ,babhūva dhārtarāṣṭrāṇāṁ siṁhanādaravastadā.
english: And then, with the sounds of conches and drums, and the twangs of bows, there arose a lion-like roar (siṃhanāda-rava) from the Dhārtarāṣṭras.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 33 Verse 43. And then, with the sounds of conches and drums, and the twangs of bows, there arose a lion-like roar (siṃhanāda-rava) from the Dhārtarāṣṭras.

sanskrit: युधिष्ठिरस्तु कौरव्य रथमारुह्य सत्वरः ।श्रुतकीर्तेर्महाराज दृष्टवान्कर्णविक्रमम् ॥४४॥
transliteration: yudhiṣṭhirastu kauravya rathamāruhya satvaraḥ ,śrutakīrtermahārāja dṛṣṭavānkarṇavikramam.
english: O descendant of Kuru, O great king whose fame is heard, Yudhiṣṭhira, however, having swiftly mounted his chariot, observed the valor of Karna.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 33 Verse 44. O descendant of Kuru, O great king whose fame is heard, Yudhiṣṭhira, however, having swiftly mounted his chariot, observed the valor of Karna.

sanskrit: काल्यमानं बलं दृष्ट्वा धर्मराजो युधिष्ठिरः ।तान्योधानब्रवीत्क्रुद्धो हतैनं वै सहस्रशः ॥४५॥
transliteration: kālyamānaṁ balaṁ dṛṣṭvā dharmarājo yudhiṣṭhiraḥ ,tānyodhānabravītkruddho hatainaṁ vai sahasraśaḥ.
english: Having seen his army being driven back, King Yudhiṣṭhira, the upholder of natural law (dharma), angrily said to those warriors, "Indeed, kill him with thousands (of attacks)!"
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 33 Verse 45. Having seen his army being driven back, King Yudhiṣṭhira, the upholder of natural law (dharma), angrily said to those warriors, "Indeed, kill him with thousands (of attacks)!"

sanskrit: ततो राज्ञाभ्यनुज्ञाताः पाण्डवानां महारथाः ।भीमसेनमुखाः सर्वे पुत्रांस्ते प्रत्युपाद्रवन् ॥४६॥
transliteration: tato rājñābhyanujñātāḥ pāṇḍavānāṁ mahārathāḥ ,bhīmasenamukhāḥ sarve putrāṁste pratyupādravan.
english: Then, all the great charioteers (mahāratha) of the Pāṇḍavas, led by Bhīmasena and permitted by the king, attacked your sons.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 33 Verse 46. Then, all the great charioteers (mahāratha) of the Pāṇḍavas, led by Bhīmasena and permitted by the king, attacked your sons.

sanskrit: अभवत्तुमुलः शब्दो योधानां तत्र भारत ।हस्त्यश्वरथपत्तीनां शस्त्राणां च ततस्ततः ॥४७॥
transliteration: abhavattumulaḥ śabdo yodhānāṁ tatra bhārata ,hastyaśvarathapattīnāṁ śastrāṇāṁ ca tatastataḥ.
english: O Bhārata, a tumultuous sound arose there from the warriors, and from the elephants, horses, chariots, and foot-soldiers, as well as from weapons, coming from all directions.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 33 Verse 47. O Bhārata, a tumultuous sound arose there from the warriors, and from the elephants, horses, chariots, and foot-soldiers, as well as from weapons, coming from all directions.

sanskrit: उत्तिष्ठत प्रहरत प्रैताभिपततेति च ।इति ब्रुवाणा अन्योन्यं जघ्नुर्योधा रणाजिरे ॥४८॥
transliteration: uttiṣṭhata praharata praitābhipatateti ca ,iti bruvāṇā anyonyaṁ jaghnuryodhā raṇājire.
english: “Stand up! Strike! Go and attack!” Saying such words to each other, the warriors fought and struck one another on the battlefield.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 33 Verse 48. “Stand up! Strike! Go and attack!” Saying such words to each other, the warriors fought and struck one another on the battlefield.

sanskrit: अभ्रच्छायेव तत्रासीच्छरवृष्टिभिरम्बरे ।समावृत्तैर्नरवरैर्निघ्नद्भिरितरेतरम् ॥४९॥
transliteration: abhracchāyeva tatrāsīccharavṛṣṭibhirambare ,samāvṛttairnaravarairnighnadbhiritaretaram.
english: There was a shower of arrows in the sky, like the shadow of a cloud, as excellent warriors (naravara) struck each other, fully engaged in the conflict.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 33 Verse 49. There was a shower of arrows in the sky, like the shadow of a cloud, as excellent warriors (naravara) struck each other, fully engaged in the conflict.

sanskrit: विपताकाध्वजच्छत्रा व्यश्वसूतायुधा रणे ।व्यङ्गाङ्गावयवाः पेतुः क्षितौ क्षीणा हतेश्वराः ॥५०॥
transliteration: vipatākādhvajacchatrā vyaśvasūtāyudhā raṇe ,vyaṅgāṅgāvayavāḥ petuḥ kṣitau kṣīṇā hateśvarāḥ.
english: In the battle, those who had lost their banners, flags, and royal umbrellas, whose horses, charioteers, and weapons were gone, fell to the earth with their limbs mutilated, exhausted, and their commanders slain.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 33 Verse 50. In the battle, those who had lost their banners, flags, and royal umbrellas, whose horses, charioteers, and weapons were gone, fell to the earth with their limbs mutilated, exhausted, and their commanders slain.

sanskrit: प्रवराणीव शैलानां शिखराणि द्विपोत्तमाः ।सारोहा निहताः पेतुर्वज्रभिन्ना इवाद्रयः ॥५१॥
transliteration: pravarāṇīva śailānāṁ śikharāṇi dvipottamāḥ ,sārohā nihatāḥ peturvajrabhinnā ivādrayaḥ.
english: The finest elephants (dvipa), with their riders, struck down, fell like the magnificent peaks of mountains, or like mountains rent by a thunderbolt (vajra).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 33 Verse 51. The finest elephants (dvipa), with their riders, struck down, fell like the magnificent peaks of mountains, or like mountains rent by a thunderbolt (vajra).

sanskrit: छिन्नभिन्नविपर्यस्तैर्वर्मालंकारविग्रहैः ।सारोहास्तुरगाः पेतुर्हतवीराः सहस्रशः ॥५२॥
transliteration: chinnabhinnaviparyastairvarmālaṁkāravigrahaiḥ ,sārohāsturagāḥ peturhatavīrāḥ sahasraśaḥ.
english: Thousands of horses, with their riders still mounted, fell. Their warriors had been slain, and their armor, ornaments, and bodies were cut apart, shattered, and strewn in disarray.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 33 Verse 52. Thousands of horses, with their riders still mounted, fell. Their warriors had been slain, and their armor, ornaments, and bodies were cut apart, shattered, and strewn in disarray.

sanskrit: विप्रविद्धायुधाङ्गाश्च द्विरदाश्वरथैर्हताः ।प्रतिवीरैश्च संमर्दे पत्तिसंघाः सहस्रशः ॥५३॥
transliteration: vipraviddhāyudhāṅgāśca dviradāśvarathairhatāḥ ,prativīraiśca saṁmarde pattisaṁghāḥ sahasraśaḥ.
english: And in the midst of the great conflict, thousands of foot soldier groups, whose weapons and limbs were strewn about, were slain by elephants, horses, and chariots, and by opposing warriors.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 33 Verse 53. And in the midst of the great conflict, thousands of foot soldier groups, whose weapons and limbs were strewn about, were slain by elephants, horses, and chariots, and by opposing warriors.

sanskrit: विशालायतताम्राक्षैः पद्मेन्दुसदृशाननैः ।शिरोभिर्युद्धशौण्डानां सर्वतः संस्तृता मही ॥५४॥
transliteration: viśālāyatatāmrākṣaiḥ padmendusadṛśānanaiḥ ,śirobhiryuddhaśauṇḍānāṁ sarvataḥ saṁstṛtā mahī.
english: The earth was completely covered everywhere by the heads of battle-fierce warriors, heads which possessed large, expansive, copper-red eyes and faces resembling lotuses and moons.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 33 Verse 54. The earth was completely covered everywhere by the heads of battle-fierce warriors, heads which possessed large, expansive, copper-red eyes and faces resembling lotuses and moons.

sanskrit: तथा तु वितते व्योम्नि निस्वनं शुश्रुवुर्जनाः ।विमानैरप्सरःसंघैर्गीतवादित्रनिस्वनैः ॥५५॥
transliteration: tathā tu vitate vyomni nisvanaṁ śuśruvurjanāḥ ,vimānairapsaraḥsaṁghairgītavāditranisvanaiḥ.
english: Thus, in the expansive sky, people indeed heard a sound produced by celestial chariots, by throngs of Apsaras, and by the sounds of songs and musical instruments.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 33 Verse 55. Thus, in the expansive sky, people indeed heard a sound produced by celestial chariots, by throngs of Apsaras, and by the sounds of songs and musical instruments.

sanskrit: हतान्कृत्तानभिमुखान्वीरान्वीरैः सहस्रशः ।आरोप्यारोप्य गच्छन्ति विमानेष्वप्सरोगणाः ॥५६॥
transliteration: hatānkṛttānabhimukhānvīrānvīraiḥ sahasraśaḥ ,āropyāropya gacchanti vimāneṣvapsarogaṇāḥ.
english: Groups of Apsaras (celestial nymphs) repeatedly lift thousands of slain, wounded, and valorous heroes who had faced the enemy, and carry them away in celestial cars (vimānas).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 33 Verse 56. Groups of Apsaras (celestial nymphs) repeatedly lift thousands of slain, wounded, and valorous heroes who had faced the enemy, and carry them away in celestial cars (vimānas).

sanskrit: तद्दृष्ट्वा महदाश्चर्यं प्रत्यक्षं स्वर्गलिप्सया ।प्रहृष्टमनसः शूराः क्षिप्रं जग्मुः परस्परम् ॥५७॥
transliteration: taddṛṣṭvā mahadāścaryaṁ pratyakṣaṁ svargalipsayā ,prahṛṣṭamanasaḥ śūrāḥ kṣipraṁ jagmuḥ parasparam.
english: Witnessing that great and manifest wonder firsthand, with a fervent desire to attain heaven (svarga), the brave warriors, their minds filled with joy, swiftly engaged in battle with one another.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 33 Verse 57. Witnessing that great and manifest wonder firsthand, with a fervent desire to attain heaven (svarga), the brave warriors, their minds filled with joy, swiftly engaged in battle with one another.

sanskrit: रथिनो रथिभिः सार्धं चित्रं युयुधुराहवे ।पत्तयः पत्तिभिर्नागा नागैः सह हयैर्हयाः ॥५८॥
transliteration: rathino rathibhiḥ sārdhaṁ citraṁ yuyudhurāhave ,pattayaḥ pattibhirnāgā nāgaiḥ saha hayairhayāḥ.
english: In that fierce battle, charioteers fought vividly with other charioteers, foot soldiers with foot soldiers, elephants with elephants, and horses with horses.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 33 Verse 58. In that fierce battle, charioteers fought vividly with other charioteers, foot soldiers with foot soldiers, elephants with elephants, and horses with horses.

sanskrit: एवं प्रवृत्ते संग्रामे गजवाजिजनक्षये ।सैन्ये च रजसा व्याप्ते स्वे स्वाञ्जघ्नुः परे परान् ॥५९॥
transliteration: evaṁ pravṛtte saṁgrāme gajavājijanakṣaye ,sainye ca rajasā vyāpte sve svāñjaghnuḥ pare parān.
english: Thus, as the battle raged, with elephants, horses, and men being annihilated, and the army entirely enveloped in dust, soldiers of the same side killed their own comrades, while the enemies killed their respective adversaries.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 33 Verse 59. Thus, as the battle raged, with elephants, horses, and men being annihilated, and the army entirely enveloped in dust, soldiers of the same side killed their own comrades, while the enemies killed their respective adversaries.

sanskrit: कचाकचि बभौ युद्धं दन्तादन्ति नखानखि ।मुष्टियुद्धं नियुद्धं च देहपाप्मविनाशनम् ॥६०॥
transliteration: kacākaci babhau yuddhaṁ dantādanti nakhānakhi ,muṣṭiyuddhaṁ niyuddhaṁ ca dehapāpmavināśanam.
english: The battle, characterized by hair-pulling, tooth-and-tooth combat, nail-and-nail fighting, fist-fighting, and wrestling, appeared splendid and was a destroyer of bodily impurities.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 33 Verse 60. The battle, characterized by hair-pulling, tooth-and-tooth combat, nail-and-nail fighting, fist-fighting, and wrestling, appeared splendid and was a destroyer of bodily impurities.

sanskrit: तथा वर्तति संग्रामे गजवाजिजनक्षये ।नराश्वगजदेहेभ्यः प्रसृता लोहितापगा ।नराश्वगजदेहान्सा व्युवाह पतितान्बहून् ॥६१॥
transliteration: tathā vartati saṁgrāme gajavājijanakṣaye ,narāśvagajadehebhyaḥ prasṛtā lohitāpagā ,narāśvagajadehānsā vyuvāha patitānbahūn.
english: As the battle raged on, amidst the annihilation of elephants, horses, and men, a river of blood flowed forth from the bodies of men, horses, and elephants. That river carried away many fallen bodies of men, horses, and elephants.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 33 Verse 61. As the battle raged on, amidst the annihilation of elephants, horses, and men, a river of blood flowed forth from the bodies of men, horses, and elephants. That river carried away many fallen bodies of men, horses, and elephants.

sanskrit: नराश्वगजसंबाधे नराश्वगजसादिनाम् ।लोहितोदा महाघोरा नदी लोहितकर्दमा ।नराश्वगजदेहान्सा वहन्ती भीरुभीषणी ॥६२॥
transliteration: narāśvagajasaṁbādhe narāśvagajasādinām ,lohitodā mahāghorā nadī lohitakardamā ,narāśvagajadehānsā vahantī bhīrubhīṣaṇī.
english: Amidst the dense mass of men, horses, and elephants, and of the warriors (men, horsemen, and elephant-riders), there was a very dreadful river, whose water was blood and whose mud was blood, carrying the bodies of men, horses, and elephants, and terrifying the fearful.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 33 Verse 62. Amidst the dense mass of men, horses, and elephants, and of the warriors (men, horsemen, and elephant-riders), there was a very dreadful river, whose water was blood and whose mud was blood, carrying the bodies of men, horses, and elephants, and terrifying the fearful.

sanskrit: तस्याः परमपारं च व्रजन्ति विजयैषिणः ।गाधेन च प्लवन्तश्च निमज्ज्योन्मज्ज्य चापरे ॥६३॥
transliteration: tasyāḥ paramapāraṁ ca vrajanti vijayaiṣiṇaḥ ,gādhena ca plavantaśca nimajjyonmajjya cāpare.
english: Those desiring victory cross to the far bank of that river. Some go by wading through its shallow parts, while others swim, and still others cross by diving and resurfacing.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 33 Verse 63. Those desiring victory cross to the far bank of that river. Some go by wading through its shallow parts, while others swim, and still others cross by diving and resurfacing.

sanskrit: ते तु लोहितदिग्धाङ्गा रक्तवर्मायुधाम्बराः ।सस्नुस्तस्यां पपुश्चासृङ्मम्लुश्च भरतर्षभ ॥६४॥
transliteration: te tu lohitadigdhāṅgā raktavarmāyudhāmbarāḥ ,sasnustasyāṁ papuścāsṛṅmamluśca bharatarṣabha.
english: O best of Bharatas, those (warriors), whose limbs were smeared with blood and whose armor and garments were stained with blood, bathed in it, drank the blood, and became faint.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 33 Verse 64. O best of Bharatas, those (warriors), whose limbs were smeared with blood and whose armor and garments were stained with blood, bathed in it, drank the blood, and became faint.

sanskrit: रथानश्वान्नरान्नागानायुधाभरणानि च ।वसनान्यथ वर्माणि हन्यमानान्हतानपि ।भूमिं खं द्यां दिशश्चैव प्रायः पश्याम लोहितम् ॥६५॥
transliteration: rathānaśvānnarānnāgānāyudhābharaṇāni ca ,vasanānyatha varmāṇi hanyamānānhatānapi ,bhūmiṁ khaṁ dyāṁ diśaścaiva prāyaḥ paśyāma lohitam.
english: We mostly perceive everything as blood (red): chariots, horses, men, elephants, and also weapons, ornaments, garments, armors, those being slaughtered and those already slain; the earth, the sky, heaven, and the cardinal directions.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 33 Verse 65. We mostly perceive everything as blood (red): chariots, horses, men, elephants, and also weapons, ornaments, garments, armors, those being slaughtered and those already slain; the earth, the sky, heaven, and the cardinal directions.

sanskrit: लोहितस्य तु गन्धेन स्पर्शेन च रसेन च ।रूपेण चातिरिक्तेन शब्देन च विसर्पता ।विषादः सुमहानासीत्प्रायः सैन्यस्य भारत ॥६६॥
transliteration: lohitasya tu gandhena sparśena ca rasena ca ,rūpeṇa cātiriktena śabdena ca visarpatā ,viṣādaḥ sumahānāsītprāyaḥ sainyasya bhārata.
english: O Bharata, a very great dismay arose primarily within the army, caused by the smell, touch, taste, overwhelming appearance, and pervading sound (e.g., of flowing blood or cries) of blood.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 33 Verse 66. O Bharata, a very great dismay arose primarily within the army, caused by the smell, touch, taste, overwhelming appearance, and pervading sound (e.g., of flowing blood or cries) of blood.

sanskrit: तत्तु विप्रहतं सैन्यं भीमसेनमुखैस्तव ।भूयः समाद्रवन्वीराः सात्यकिप्रमुखा रथाः ॥६७॥
transliteration: tattu viprahataṁ sainyaṁ bhīmasenamukhaistava ,bhūyaḥ samādravanvīrāḥ sātyakipramukhā rathāḥ.
english: But that army of yours, though heavily defeated by Bhīmasena and his leading warriors, again charged forward, particularly the heroic charioteers led by Sātyaki.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 33 Verse 67. But that army of yours, though heavily defeated by Bhīmasena and his leading warriors, again charged forward, particularly the heroic charioteers led by Sātyaki.

sanskrit: तेषामापततां वेगमविषह्य महात्मनाम् ।पुत्राणां ते महत्सैन्यमासीद्राजन्पराङ्मुखम् ॥६८॥
transliteration: teṣāmāpatatāṁ vegamaviṣahya mahātmanām ,putrāṇāṁ te mahatsainyamāsīdrājanparāṅmukham.
english: O King, your sons' great army turned its back, unable to bear the irresistible charge of those great-souled ones (mahātmanām) who were attacking.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 33 Verse 68. O King, your sons' great army turned its back, unable to bear the irresistible charge of those great-souled ones (mahātmanām) who were attacking.

sanskrit: तत्प्रकीर्णरथाश्वेभं नरवाजिसमाकुलम् ।विध्वस्तचर्मकवचं प्रविद्धायुधकार्मुकम् ॥६९॥
transliteration: tatprakīrṇarathāśvebhaṁ naravājisamākulam ,vidhvastacarmakavacaṁ praviddhāyudhakārmukam.
english: That [army was characterized by] scattered chariots, horses, and elephants; it was chaotic with men and horses; its shields and armors were shattered; and its weapons and bows were cast aside.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 33 Verse 69. That [army was characterized by] scattered chariots, horses, and elephants; it was chaotic with men and horses; its shields and armors were shattered; and its weapons and bows were cast aside.

sanskrit: व्यद्रवत्तावकं सैन्यं लोड्यमानं समन्ततः ।सिंहार्दितं महारण्ये यथा गजकुलं तथा ॥७०॥
transliteration: vyadravattāvakaṁ sainyaṁ loḍyamānaṁ samantataḥ ,siṁhārditaṁ mahāraṇye yathā gajakulaṁ tathā.
english: Your army fled, being tossed about in every direction, just as a herd of elephants, harassed by a lion, scatters in a great forest.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 33 Verse 70. Your army fled, being tossed about in every direction, just as a herd of elephants, harassed by a lion, scatters in a great forest.

sanskrit: संजय उवाच ।तानभिद्रवतो दृष्ट्वा पाण्डवांस्तावकं बलम् ।क्रोशतस्तव पुत्रस्य न स्म राजन्न्यवर्तत ॥१॥
transliteration: saṁjaya uvāca ,tānabhidravato dṛṣṭvā pāṇḍavāṁstāvakaṁ balam ,krośatastava putrasya na sma rājannyavartata.
english: Sañjaya said: "O King, having seen those Pāṇḍavas attacking, your army did not retreat, despite your son's cries."
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 34 Verse 1. Sañjaya said: "O King, having seen those Pāṇḍavas attacking, your army did not retreat, despite your son's cries."

sanskrit: ततः पक्षात्प्रपक्षाच्च प्रपक्षैश्चापि दक्षिणात् ।उदस्तशस्त्राः कुरवो भीममभ्यद्रवन्रणे ॥२॥
transliteration: tataḥ pakṣātprapakṣācca prapakṣaiścāpi dakṣiṇāt ,udastaśastrāḥ kuravo bhīmamabhyadravanraṇe.
english: Then, from the main flank, from the sub-flanks, and also from the right (south) side, the Kurus, having raised their weapons, rushed towards Bhima in the battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 34 Verse 2. Then, from the main flank, from the sub-flanks, and also from the right (south) side, the Kurus, having raised their weapons, rushed towards Bhima in the battle.

sanskrit: कर्णोऽपि दृष्ट्वा द्रवतो धार्तराष्ट्रान्पराङ्मुखान् ।हंसवर्णान्हयाग्र्यांस्तान्प्रैषीद्यत्र वृकोदरम् ॥३॥
transliteration: karṇo'pi dṛṣṭvā dravato dhārtarāṣṭrānparāṅmukhān ,haṁsavarṇānhayāgryāṁstānpraiṣīdyatra vṛkodaram.
english: Karna, upon seeing the sons of Dhritarashtra (Dhārtarāṣṭra) fleeing with their backs turned, also sent those excellent swan-colored horses to where Bhima (Vṛkodara) was.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 34 Verse 3. Karna, upon seeing the sons of Dhritarashtra (Dhārtarāṣṭra) fleeing with their backs turned, also sent those excellent swan-colored horses to where Bhima (Vṛkodara) was.

sanskrit: ते प्रेषिता महाराज शल्येनाहवशोभिना ।भीमसेनरथं प्राप्य समसज्जन्त वाजिनः ॥४॥
transliteration: te preṣitā mahārāja śalyenāhavaśobhinā ,bhīmasenarathaṁ prāpya samasajjanta vājinaḥ.
english: O great king, those horses, dispatched by Salya (Śalya), who shone brightly in battle, upon reaching Bhimasena's chariot, became engaged.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 34 Verse 4. O great king, those horses, dispatched by Salya (Śalya), who shone brightly in battle, upon reaching Bhimasena's chariot, became engaged.

sanskrit: दृष्ट्वा कर्णं समायान्तं भीमः क्रोधसमन्वितः ।मतिं दध्रे विनाशाय कर्णस्य भरतर्षभ ॥५॥
transliteration: dṛṣṭvā karṇaṁ samāyāntaṁ bhīmaḥ krodhasamanvitaḥ ,matiṁ dadhre vināśāya karṇasya bharatarṣabha.
english: O bull among the Bharatas, Bhima, filled with wrath, upon seeing Karna approaching, resolved upon Karna's destruction.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 34 Verse 5. O bull among the Bharatas, Bhima, filled with wrath, upon seeing Karna approaching, resolved upon Karna's destruction.

sanskrit: सोऽब्रवीत्सात्यकिं वीरं धृष्टद्युम्नं च पार्षतम् ।एनं रक्षत राजानं धर्मात्मानं युधिष्ठिरम् ।संशयान्महतो मुक्तं कथंचित्प्रेक्षतो मम ॥६॥
transliteration: so'bravītsātyakiṁ vīraṁ dhṛṣṭadyumnaṁ ca pārṣatam ,enaṁ rakṣata rājānaṁ dharmātmānaṁ yudhiṣṭhiram ,saṁśayānmahato muktaṁ kathaṁcitprekṣato mama.
english: He said to the heroic Sātyaki and to Dhṛṣṭadyumna, the son of Pṛṣata: "Protect this King Yudhiṣṭhira, whose intrinsic nature (dharma) is righteousness, whom I somehow watched being freed from great danger, even as I looked on."
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 34 Verse 6. He said to the heroic Sātyaki and to Dhṛṣṭadyumna, the son of Pṛṣata: "Protect this King Yudhiṣṭhira, whose intrinsic nature (dharma) is righteousness, whom I somehow watched being freed from great danger, even as I looked on."

sanskrit: अग्रतो मे कृतो राजा छिन्नसर्वपरिच्छदः ।दुर्योधनस्य प्रीत्यर्थं राधेयेन दुरात्मना ॥७॥
transliteration: agrato me kṛto rājā chinnasarvaparicchadaḥ ,duryodhanasya prītyarthaṁ rādheyena durātmanā.
english: The King was placed before me, stripped of all his possessions, by the evil-minded son of Rādhā (Karṇa) for Duryodhana's pleasure.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 34 Verse 7. The King was placed before me, stripped of all his possessions, by the evil-minded son of Rādhā (Karṇa) for Duryodhana's pleasure.

sanskrit: अन्तमद्य करिष्यामि तस्य दुःखस्य पार्षत ।हन्ता वास्मि रणे कर्णं स वा मां निहनिष्यति ।संग्रामेण सुघोरेण सत्यमेतद्ब्रवीमि वः ॥८॥
transliteration: antamadya kariṣyāmi tasya duḥkhasya pārṣata ,hantā vāsmi raṇe karṇaṁ sa vā māṁ nihaniṣyati ,saṁgrāmeṇa sughoreṇa satyametadbravīmi vaḥ.
english: Today I will put an end to that sorrow, O son of Pṛṣata. Either I will kill Karṇa in a most terrible battle, or he will kill me. I speak this truth to you.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 34 Verse 8. Today I will put an end to that sorrow, O son of Pṛṣata. Either I will kill Karṇa in a most terrible battle, or he will kill me. I speak this truth to you.

sanskrit: राजानमद्य भवतां न्यासभूतं ददामि वै ।अस्य संरक्षणे सर्वे यतध्वं विगतज्वराः ॥९॥
transliteration: rājānamadya bhavatāṁ nyāsabhūtaṁ dadāmi vai ,asya saṁrakṣaṇe sarve yatadhvaṁ vigatajvarāḥ.
english: Today I entrust this King to you as a sacred deposit. All of you, strive for his protection, free from anxiety.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 34 Verse 9. Today I entrust this King to you as a sacred deposit. All of you, strive for his protection, free from anxiety.

sanskrit: एवमुक्त्वा महाबाहुः प्रायादाधिरथिं प्रति ।सिंहनादेन महता सर्वाः संनादयन्दिशः ॥१०॥
transliteration: evamuktvā mahābāhuḥ prāyādādhirathiṁ prati ,siṁhanādena mahatā sarvāḥ saṁnādayandiśaḥ.
english: Having spoken thus, the mighty-armed (Bhīma) proceeded towards the son of Adhiratha (Karṇa), causing all directions to reverberate with a mighty lion's roar.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 34 Verse 10. Having spoken thus, the mighty-armed (Bhīma) proceeded towards the son of Adhiratha (Karṇa), causing all directions to reverberate with a mighty lion's roar.

sanskrit: दृष्ट्वा त्वरितमायान्तं भीमं युद्धाभिनन्दिनम् ।सूतपुत्रमथोवाच मद्राणामीश्वरो विभुः ॥११॥
transliteration: dṛṣṭvā tvaritamāyāntaṁ bhīmaṁ yuddhābhinandinam ,sūtaputramathovāca madrāṇāmīśvaro vibhuḥ.
english: Having seen Bhīma, who was delighting in battle, swiftly approaching, the powerful lord of the Madras then spoke to the son of the charioteer (Karṇa).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 34 Verse 11. Having seen Bhīma, who was delighting in battle, swiftly approaching, the powerful lord of the Madras then spoke to the son of the charioteer (Karṇa).

sanskrit: पश्य कर्ण महाबाहुं क्रुद्धं पाण्डवनन्दनम् ।दीर्घकालार्जितं क्रोधं मोक्तुकामं त्वयि ध्रुवम् ॥१२॥
transliteration: paśya karṇa mahābāhuṁ kruddhaṁ pāṇḍavanandanam ,dīrghakālārjitaṁ krodhaṁ moktukāmaṁ tvayi dhruvam.
english: Behold, Karṇa, the mighty-armed son of Pāṇḍu, enraged and certainly desirous of unleashing his long-accumulated anger upon you.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 34 Verse 12. Behold, Karṇa, the mighty-armed son of Pāṇḍu, enraged and certainly desirous of unleashing his long-accumulated anger upon you.

sanskrit: ईदृशं नास्य रूपं मे दृष्टपूर्वं कदाचन ।अभिमन्यौ हते कर्ण राक्षसे वा घटोत्कचे ॥१३॥
transliteration: īdṛśaṁ nāsya rūpaṁ me dṛṣṭapūrvaṁ kadācana ,abhimanyau hate karṇa rākṣase vā ghaṭotkace.
english: Karṇa, I have never before seen such an appearance of his, not even when Abhimanyu was slain, or when the Rākṣasa Ghaṭotkaca (was killed).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 34 Verse 13. Karṇa, I have never before seen such an appearance of his, not even when Abhimanyu was slain, or when the Rākṣasa Ghaṭotkaca (was killed).

sanskrit: त्रैलोक्यस्य समस्तस्य शक्तः क्रुद्धो निवारणे ।बिभर्ति यादृशं रूपं कालाग्निसदृशं शुभम् ॥१४॥
transliteration: trailokyasya samastasya śaktaḥ kruddho nivāraṇe ,bibharti yādṛśaṁ rūpaṁ kālāgnisadṛśaṁ śubham.
english: When enraged, he assumes a splendid form, resembling the fire of cosmic dissolution, capable of repelling all three worlds.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 34 Verse 14. When enraged, he assumes a splendid form, resembling the fire of cosmic dissolution, capable of repelling all three worlds.

sanskrit: इति ब्रुवति राधेयं मद्राणामीश्वरे नृप ।अभ्यवर्तत वै कर्णं क्रोधदीप्तो वृकोदरः ॥१५॥
transliteration: iti bruvati rādheyaṁ madrāṇāmīśvare nṛpa ,abhyavartata vai karṇaṁ krodhadīpto vṛkodaraḥ.
english: O King, as the lord of the Madras was speaking thus to Radheya (Karṇa), Vṛkodara (Bhīma), blazing with anger, indeed advanced towards Karṇa.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 34 Verse 15. O King, as the lord of the Madras was speaking thus to Radheya (Karṇa), Vṛkodara (Bhīma), blazing with anger, indeed advanced towards Karṇa.

sanskrit: तथागतं तु संप्रेक्ष्य भीमं युद्धाभिनन्दिनम् ।अब्रवीद्वचनं शल्यं राधेयः प्रहसन्निव ॥१६॥
transliteration: tathāgataṁ tu saṁprekṣya bhīmaṁ yuddhābhinandinam ,abravīdvacanaṁ śalyaṁ rādheyaḥ prahasanniva.
english: But having seen Bhīma, who had thus arrived eager for battle, Radheya (Karṇa), as if laughing, spoke these words to Śalya.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 34 Verse 16. But having seen Bhīma, who had thus arrived eager for battle, Radheya (Karṇa), as if laughing, spoke these words to Śalya.

sanskrit: यदुक्तं वचनं मेऽद्य त्वया मद्रजनेश्वर ।भीमसेनं प्रति विभो तत्सत्यं नात्र संशयः ॥१७॥
transliteration: yaduktaṁ vacanaṁ me'dya tvayā madrajaneśvara ,bhīmasenaṁ prati vibho tatsatyaṁ nātra saṁśayaḥ.
english: O King of the Madras (madrajaneśvara), O powerful one (vibho), what you said to me today concerning Bhīmasena, that is indeed true; there is no doubt about it.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 34 Verse 17. O King of the Madras (madrajaneśvara), O powerful one (vibho), what you said to me today concerning Bhīmasena, that is indeed true; there is no doubt about it.

sanskrit: एष शूरश्च वीरश्च क्रोधनश्च वृकोदरः ।निरपेक्षः शरीरे च प्राणतश्च बलाधिकः ॥१८॥
transliteration: eṣa śūraśca vīraśca krodhanaśca vṛkodaraḥ ,nirapekṣaḥ śarīre ca prāṇataśca balādhikaḥ.
english: This Vṛkodara (Bhīma) is heroic, brave, and wrathful. He is indifferent to his body and life, and possesses superior strength.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 34 Verse 18. This Vṛkodara (Bhīma) is heroic, brave, and wrathful. He is indifferent to his body and life, and possesses superior strength.

sanskrit: अज्ञातवासं वसता विराटनगरे तदा ।द्रौपद्याः प्रियकामेन केवलं बाहुसंश्रयात् ।गूढभावं समाश्रित्य कीचकः सगणो हतः ॥१९॥
transliteration: ajñātavāsaṁ vasatā virāṭanagare tadā ,draupadyāḥ priyakāmena kevalaṁ bāhusaṁśrayāt ,gūḍhabhāvaṁ samāśritya kīcakaḥ sagaṇo hataḥ.
english: At that time, while (Bhīma was) dwelling in anonymous exile (ajñātavāsa) in the city of Virāṭa, Kīcaka, along with his followers (sagaṇa), was killed by Bhīma, who desired Draupadī's well-being and, solely relying on the strength of his arms, had adopted a disguised persona.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 34 Verse 19. At that time, while (Bhīma was) dwelling in anonymous exile (ajñātavāsa) in the city of Virāṭa, Kīcaka, along with his followers (sagaṇa), was killed by Bhīma, who desired Draupadī's well-being and, solely relying on the strength of his arms, had adopted a disguised persona.

sanskrit: सोऽद्य संग्रामशिरसि संनद्धः क्रोधमूर्च्छितः ।किंकरोद्यतदण्डेन मृत्युनापि व्रजेद्रणम् ॥२०॥
transliteration: so'dya saṁgrāmaśirasi saṁnaddhaḥ krodhamūrcchitaḥ ,kiṁkarodyatadaṇḍena mṛtyunāpi vrajedraṇam.
english: He (Bhīma), arrayed today at the forefront of battle and overcome with rage, would enter the fight even if Death himself, with his servant holding an upraised staff, were present.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 34 Verse 20. He (Bhīma), arrayed today at the forefront of battle and overcome with rage, would enter the fight even if Death himself, with his servant holding an upraised staff, were present.

sanskrit: चिरकालाभिलषितो ममायं तु मनोरथः ।अर्जुनं समरे हन्यां मां वा हन्याद्धनंजयः ।स मे कदाचिदद्यैव भवेद्भीमसमागमात् ॥२१॥
transliteration: cirakālābhilaṣito mamāyaṁ tu manorathaḥ ,arjunaṁ samare hanyāṁ māṁ vā hanyāddhanaṁjayaḥ ,sa me kadācidadyaiva bhavedbhīmasamāgamāt.
english: This long-cherished desire of mine is that I either slay Arjuna in battle, or Dhanaṃjaya (Arjuna) slays me. May that wish of mine be fulfilled perhaps even today, owing to this encounter (which Bhīma's presence has brought about).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 34 Verse 21. This long-cherished desire of mine is that I either slay Arjuna in battle, or Dhanaṃjaya (Arjuna) slays me. May that wish of mine be fulfilled perhaps even today, owing to this encounter (which Bhīma's presence has brought about).

sanskrit: निहते भीमसेने तु यदि वा विरथीकृते ।अभियास्यति मां पार्थस्तन्मे साधु भविष्यति ।अत्र यन्मन्यसे प्राप्तं तच्छीघ्रं संप्रधारय ॥२२॥
transliteration: nihate bhīmasene tu yadi vā virathīkṛte ,abhiyāsyati māṁ pārthastanme sādhu bhaviṣyati ,atra yanmanyase prāptaṁ tacchīghraṁ saṁpradhāraya.
english: If Bhimasena is killed or made chariot-less, then Arjuna (pārtha) will surely attack me. That will be beneficial for me. Whatever you consider suitable in this situation, decide quickly.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 34 Verse 22. If Bhimasena is killed or made chariot-less, then Arjuna (pārtha) will surely attack me. That will be beneficial for me. Whatever you consider suitable in this situation, decide quickly.

sanskrit: एतच्छ्रुत्वा तु वचनं राधेयस्य महात्मनः ।उवाच वचनं शल्यः सूतपुत्रं तथागतम् ॥२३॥
transliteration: etacchrutvā tu vacanaṁ rādheyasya mahātmanaḥ ,uvāca vacanaṁ śalyaḥ sūtaputraṁ tathāgatam.
english: Having heard these words of the great-souled (mahātman) son of Radha (Rādheya), Shalya then spoke words to the charioteer's son (sūtaputra) who had spoken in such a manner.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 34 Verse 23. Having heard these words of the great-souled (mahātman) son of Radha (Rādheya), Shalya then spoke words to the charioteer's son (sūtaputra) who had spoken in such a manner.

sanskrit: अभियासि महाबाहो भीमसेनं महाबलम् ।निरस्य भीमसेनं तु ततः प्राप्स्यसि फल्गुनम् ॥२४॥
transliteration: abhiyāsi mahābāho bhīmasenaṁ mahābalam ,nirasya bhīmasenaṁ tu tataḥ prāpsyasi phalgunam.
english: O mighty-armed one, you must attack the immensely powerful Bhimasena. Indeed, after overcoming Bhimasena, you will then reach Arjuna (phalguna).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 34 Verse 24. O mighty-armed one, you must attack the immensely powerful Bhimasena. Indeed, after overcoming Bhimasena, you will then reach Arjuna (phalguna).

sanskrit: यस्ते कामोऽभिलषितश्चिरात्प्रभृति हृद्गतः ।स वै संपत्स्यते कर्ण सत्यमेतद्ब्रवीमि ते ॥२५॥
transliteration: yaste kāmo'bhilaṣitaścirātprabhṛti hṛdgataḥ ,sa vai saṁpatsyate karṇa satyametadbravīmi te.
english: O Karna, that long-cherished desire (kāma) of yours, which has resided in your heart for a long time, will certainly be fulfilled. I tell you this truthfully.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 34 Verse 25. O Karna, that long-cherished desire (kāma) of yours, which has resided in your heart for a long time, will certainly be fulfilled. I tell you this truthfully.

sanskrit: एवमुक्ते ततः कर्णः शल्यं पुनरभाषत ।हन्ताहमर्जुनं संख्ये मां वा हन्ता धनंजयः ।युद्धे मनः समाधाय याहि याहीत्यचोदयत् ॥२६॥
transliteration: evamukte tataḥ karṇaḥ śalyaṁ punarabhāṣata ,hantāhamarjunaṁ saṁkhye māṁ vā hantā dhanaṁjayaḥ ,yuddhe manaḥ samādhāya yāhi yāhītyacodayat.
english: Upon hearing this, Karna then spoke to Shalya again: "Either I will kill Arjuna in battle, or Dhananjaya will kill me. Fix your mind on the fight and proceed, proceed!" Thus he urged him.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 34 Verse 26. Upon hearing this, Karna then spoke to Shalya again: "Either I will kill Arjuna in battle, or Dhananjaya will kill me. Fix your mind on the fight and proceed, proceed!" Thus he urged him.

sanskrit: ततः प्रायाद्रथेनाशु शल्यस्तत्र विशां पते ।यत्र भीमो महेष्वासो व्यद्रावयत वाहिनीम् ॥२७॥
transliteration: tataḥ prāyādrathenāśu śalyastatra viśāṁ pate ,yatra bhīmo maheṣvāso vyadrāvayata vāhinīm.
english: Then, O King, Shalya quickly set off by chariot to the place where Bhima, the great archer, was putting the army to flight.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 34 Verse 27. Then, O King, Shalya quickly set off by chariot to the place where Bhima, the great archer, was putting the army to flight.

sanskrit: ततस्तूर्यनिनादश्च भेरीणां च महास्वनः ।उदतिष्ठत राजेन्द्र कर्णभीमसमागमे ॥२८॥
transliteration: tatastūryaninādaśca bherīṇāṁ ca mahāsvanaḥ ,udatiṣṭhata rājendra karṇabhīmasamāgame.
english: Then, O King, a loud sound of trumpets and a great roar of war drums arose at the meeting of Karna and Bhima.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 34 Verse 28. Then, O King, a loud sound of trumpets and a great roar of war drums arose at the meeting of Karna and Bhima.

sanskrit: भीमसेनोऽथ संक्रुद्धस्तव सैन्यं दुरासदम् ।नाराचैर्विमलैस्तीक्ष्णैर्दिशः प्राद्रावयद्बली ॥२९॥
transliteration: bhīmaseno'tha saṁkruddhastava sainyaṁ durāsadam ,nārācairvimalaistīkṣṇairdiśaḥ prādrāvayadbalī.
english: Then the mighty Bhimasena, greatly enraged, put your impregnable army to flight in all directions with his shining, sharp arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 34 Verse 29. Then the mighty Bhimasena, greatly enraged, put your impregnable army to flight in all directions with his shining, sharp arrows.

sanskrit: स संनिपातस्तुमुलो भीमरूपो विशां पते ।आसीद्रौद्रो महाराज कर्णपाण्डवयोर्मृधे ।ततो मुहूर्ताद्राजेन्द्र पाण्डवः कर्णमाद्रवत् ॥३०॥
transliteration: sa saṁnipātastumulo bhīmarūpo viśāṁ pate ,āsīdraudro mahārāja karṇapāṇḍavayormṛdhe ,tato muhūrtādrājendra pāṇḍavaḥ karṇamādravat.
english: O lord of men, O great king, that terrible and dreadful clash became fierce in the battle between Karṇa and the Pāṇḍava. Then, O king of kings, after a moment, the Pāṇḍava attacked Karṇa.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 34 Verse 30. O lord of men, O great king, that terrible and dreadful clash became fierce in the battle between Karṇa and the Pāṇḍava. Then, O king of kings, after a moment, the Pāṇḍava attacked Karṇa.

sanskrit: तमापतन्तं संप्रेक्ष्य कर्णो वैकर्तनो वृषः ।आजघानोरसि क्रुद्धो नाराचेन स्तनान्तरे ।पुनश्चैनममेयात्मा शरवर्षैरवाकिरत् ॥३१॥
transliteration: tamāpatantaṁ saṁprekṣya karṇo vaikartano vṛṣaḥ ,ājaghānorasi kruddho nārācena stanāntare ,punaścainamameyātmā śaravarṣairavākirat.
english: Having seen him approaching, Karṇa, the son of Vīkartana, a bull among men, enraged, struck him on the chest between the breasts with an iron arrow. And again, that one of immeasurable spirit (ātman) covered him with showers of arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 34 Verse 31. Having seen him approaching, Karṇa, the son of Vīkartana, a bull among men, enraged, struck him on the chest between the breasts with an iron arrow. And again, that one of immeasurable spirit (ātman) covered him with showers of arrows.

sanskrit: स विद्धः सूतपुत्रेण छादयामास पत्रिभिः ।विव्याध निशितैः कर्ण नवभिर्नतपर्वभिः ॥३२॥
transliteration: sa viddhaḥ sūtaputreṇa chādayāmāsa patribhiḥ ,vivyādha niśitaiḥ karṇa navabhirnataparvabhiḥ.
english: Pierced by the son of the charioteer (Karṇa), he (Arjuna) covered him (Karṇa) with arrows. He then pierced Karṇa with nine sharp arrows that had depressed nodes.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 34 Verse 32. Pierced by the son of the charioteer (Karṇa), he (Arjuna) covered him (Karṇa) with arrows. He then pierced Karṇa with nine sharp arrows that had depressed nodes.

sanskrit: तस्य कर्णो धनुर्मध्ये द्विधा चिच्छेद पत्रिणा ।अथ तं छिन्नधन्वानमभ्यविध्यत्स्तनान्तरे ।नाराचेन सुतीक्ष्णेन सर्वावरणभेदिना ॥३३॥
transliteration: tasya karṇo dhanurmadhye dvidhā ciccheda patriṇā ,atha taṁ chinnadhanvānamabhyavidhyatstanāntare ,nārācena sutīkṣṇena sarvāvaraṇabhedinā.
english: Karṇa, with an arrow, cut his (Arjuna's) bow in the middle into two pieces. Then, he (Karṇa) struck that bow-less warrior between the breasts with a very sharp iron arrow, capable of piercing all armor.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 34 Verse 33. Karṇa, with an arrow, cut his (Arjuna's) bow in the middle into two pieces. Then, he (Karṇa) struck that bow-less warrior between the breasts with a very sharp iron arrow, capable of piercing all armor.

sanskrit: सोऽन्यत्कार्मुकमादाय सूतपुत्रं वृकोदरः ।राजन्मर्मसु मर्मज्ञो विद्ध्वा सुनिशितैः शरैः ।ननाद बलवन्नादं कम्पयन्निव रोदसी ॥३४॥
transliteration: so'nyatkārmukamādāya sūtaputraṁ vṛkodaraḥ ,rājanmarmasu marmajño viddhvā suniśitaiḥ śaraiḥ ,nanāda balavannādaṁ kampayanniva rodasī.
english: O King, Bhīma (Vṛkodara), skilled in striking vital spots, took another bow. Having pierced Karṇa, the son of Sūta, in his vital spots with very sharp arrows, he let out a powerful roar, as if making the heavens and earth tremble.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 34 Verse 34. O King, Bhīma (Vṛkodara), skilled in striking vital spots, took another bow. Having pierced Karṇa, the son of Sūta, in his vital spots with very sharp arrows, he let out a powerful roar, as if making the heavens and earth tremble.

sanskrit: तं कर्णः पञ्चविंशत्या नाराचानां समार्दयत् ।मदोत्कटं वने दृप्तमुल्काभिरिव कुञ्जरम् ॥३५॥
transliteration: taṁ karṇaḥ pañcaviṁśatyā nārācānāṁ samārdayat ,madotkaṭaṁ vane dṛptamulkābhiriva kuñjaram.
english: Karṇa tormented him (Bhīma) with twenty-five iron arrows (nārāca), just as one would torment a proud, rutting elephant in a forest with flaming brands.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 34 Verse 35. Karṇa tormented him (Bhīma) with twenty-five iron arrows (nārāca), just as one would torment a proud, rutting elephant in a forest with flaming brands.

sanskrit: ततः सायकभिन्नाङ्गः पाण्डवः क्रोधमूर्च्छितः ।संरम्भामर्षताम्राक्षः सूतपुत्रवधेच्छया ॥३६॥
transliteration: tataḥ sāyakabhinnāṅgaḥ pāṇḍavaḥ krodhamūrcchitaḥ ,saṁrambhāmarṣatāmrākṣaḥ sūtaputravadhecchayā.
english: Then the Pāṇḍava (Bhīma), his limbs pierced by arrows and overcome with rage, had eyes red with fury and indignation, desiring the slaying of Karṇa, the son of Sūta.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 34 Verse 36. Then the Pāṇḍava (Bhīma), his limbs pierced by arrows and overcome with rage, had eyes red with fury and indignation, desiring the slaying of Karṇa, the son of Sūta.

sanskrit: स कार्मुके महावेगं भारसाधनमुत्तमम् ।गिरीणामपि भेत्तारं सायकं समयोजयत् ॥३७॥
transliteration: sa kārmuke mahāvegaṁ bhārasādhanamuttamam ,girīṇāmapi bhettāraṁ sāyakaṁ samayojayat.
english: He (Bhīma) fixed onto his bow an excellent (uttamam) and exceedingly swift (mahāvegam) arrow (sāyakam), which was capable of achieving great feats (bhārasādhanam) and even piercing mountains (girīṇām api bhettāram).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 34 Verse 37. He (Bhīma) fixed onto his bow an excellent (uttamam) and exceedingly swift (mahāvegam) arrow (sāyakam), which was capable of achieving great feats (bhārasādhanam) and even piercing mountains (girīṇām api bhettāram).

sanskrit: विकृष्य बलवच्चापमा कर्णादतिमारुतिः ।तं मुमोच महेष्वासः क्रुद्धः कर्णजिघांसया ॥३८॥
transliteration: vikṛṣya balavaccāpamā karṇādatimārutiḥ ,taṁ mumoca maheṣvāsaḥ kruddhaḥ karṇajighāṁsayā.
english: The great son of Marut (Bhima), a mighty archer, angrily drew his bow forcefully to his ear and released it at him (Karna) with the intent to kill Karna.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 34 Verse 38. The great son of Marut (Bhima), a mighty archer, angrily drew his bow forcefully to his ear and released it at him (Karna) with the intent to kill Karna.

sanskrit: स विसृष्टो बलवता बाणो वज्राशनिस्वनः ।अदारयद्रणे कर्णं वज्रवेग इवाचलम् ॥३९॥
transliteration: sa visṛṣṭo balavatā bāṇo vajrāśanisvanaḥ ,adārayadraṇe karṇaṁ vajravega ivācalam.
english: That arrow, released by the mighty one (Bhima) and sounding like a thunderbolt, pierced Karna in battle, just as a mountain is rent by the force of a thunderbolt.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 34 Verse 39. That arrow, released by the mighty one (Bhima) and sounding like a thunderbolt, pierced Karna in battle, just as a mountain is rent by the force of a thunderbolt.

sanskrit: स भीमसेनाभिहतो सूतपुत्रः कुरूद्वह ।निषसाद रथोपस्थे विसंज्ञः पृतनापतिः ॥४०॥
transliteration: sa bhīmasenābhihato sūtaputraḥ kurūdvaha ,niṣasāda rathopasthe visaṁjñaḥ pṛtanāpatiḥ.
english: O upholder of the Kurus (Dhritarashtra), that son of the charioteer (Karna), struck by Bhima, became unconscious and slumped onto the seat of the chariot, he, the commander of the army.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 34 Verse 40. O upholder of the Kurus (Dhritarashtra), that son of the charioteer (Karna), struck by Bhima, became unconscious and slumped onto the seat of the chariot, he, the commander of the army.

sanskrit: ततो मद्राधिपो दृष्ट्वा विसंज्ञं सूतनन्दनम् ।अपोवाह रथेनाजौ कर्णमाहवशोभिनम् ॥४१॥
transliteration: tato madrādhipo dṛṣṭvā visaṁjñaṁ sūtanandanam ,apovāha rathenājau karṇamāhavaśobhinam.
english: Then, the king of Madra, seeing the unconscious son of the charioteer (Karna), carried away Karna, who was resplendent in battle, with his chariot during the engagement.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 34 Verse 41. Then, the king of Madra, seeing the unconscious son of the charioteer (Karna), carried away Karna, who was resplendent in battle, with his chariot during the engagement.

sanskrit: ततः पराजिते कर्णे धार्तराष्ट्रीं महाचमूम् ।व्यद्रावयद्भीमसेनो यथेन्द्रो दानवीं चमूम् ॥४२॥
transliteration: tataḥ parājite karṇe dhārtarāṣṭrīṁ mahācamūm ,vyadrāvayadbhīmaseno yathendro dānavīṁ camūm.
english: Then, after Karna was defeated, Bhīmasena routed the great army of Dhṛtarāṣṭra's sons, just as Indra routed the army of the Danavas.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 34 Verse 42. Then, after Karna was defeated, Bhīmasena routed the great army of Dhṛtarāṣṭra's sons, just as Indra routed the army of the Danavas.

sanskrit: धृतराष्ट्र उवाच ।सुदुष्करमिदं कर्म कृतं भीमेन संजय ।येन कर्णो महाबाहू रथोपस्थे निपातितः ॥१॥
transliteration: dhṛtarāṣṭra uvāca ,suduṣkaramidaṁ karma kṛtaṁ bhīmena saṁjaya ,yena karṇo mahābāhū rathopasthe nipātitaḥ.
english: Dhṛtarāṣṭra said: "O Sanjaya, this extremely difficult feat (karma) was accomplished by Bhīma, by whom the mighty-armed Karna was struck down from his chariot seat."
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 35 Verse 1. Dhṛtarāṣṭra said: "O Sanjaya, this extremely difficult feat (karma) was accomplished by Bhīma, by whom the mighty-armed Karna was struck down from his chariot seat."

sanskrit: कर्णो ह्येको रणे हन्ता सृञ्जयान्पाण्डवैः सह ।इति दुर्योधनः सूत प्राब्रवीन्मां मुहुर्मुहुः ॥२॥
transliteration: karṇo hyeko raṇe hantā sṛñjayānpāṇḍavaiḥ saha ,iti duryodhanaḥ sūta prābravīnmāṁ muhurmuhuḥ.
english: "Karna alone is surely capable of slaying the Sṛñjayas and the Pandavas together in battle!"  -  thus Duryodhana, O charioteer (sūta), repeatedly said to me.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 35 Verse 2. "Karna alone is surely capable of slaying the Sṛñjayas and the Pandavas together in battle!"  -  thus Duryodhana, O charioteer (sūta), repeatedly said to me.

sanskrit: पराजितं तु राधेयं दृष्ट्वा भीमेन संयुगे ।ततः परं किमकरोत्पुत्रो दुर्योधनो मम ॥३॥
transliteration: parājitaṁ tu rādheyaṁ dṛṣṭvā bhīmena saṁyuge ,tataḥ paraṁ kimakarotputro duryodhano mama.
english: But after seeing Radheya (Karna) defeated by Bhīma in battle, what did my son Duryodhana do next?
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 35 Verse 3. But after seeing Radheya (Karna) defeated by Bhīma in battle, what did my son Duryodhana do next?

sanskrit: संजय उवाच ।विभ्रान्तं प्रेक्ष्य राधेयं सूतपुत्रं महाहवे ।महत्या सेनया राजन्सोदर्यान्समभाषत ॥४॥
transliteration: saṁjaya uvāca ,vibhrāntaṁ prekṣya rādheyaṁ sūtaputraṁ mahāhave ,mahatyā senayā rājansodaryānsamabhāṣata.
english: Sañjaya said: "O King, observing Rādhā's son, the son of the charioteer (Karna), bewildered in the great battle, (Duryodhana) addressed his uterine brothers, accompanied by a vast army."
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 35 Verse 4. Sañjaya said: "O King, observing Rādhā's son, the son of the charioteer (Karna), bewildered in the great battle, (Duryodhana) addressed his uterine brothers, accompanied by a vast army."

sanskrit: शीघ्रं गच्छत भद्रं वो राधेयं परिरक्षत ।भीमसेनभयागाधे मज्जन्तं व्यसनार्णवे ॥५॥
transliteration: śīghraṁ gacchata bhadraṁ vo rādheyaṁ parirakṣata ,bhīmasenabhayāgādhe majjantaṁ vyasanārṇave.
english: (Duryodhana said:) "Go quickly! May good fortune be yours! Protect Rādhā's son (Karna), who is sinking into an ocean of distress, deeply immersed in the fear of Bhīmasena."
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 35 Verse 5. (Duryodhana said:) "Go quickly! May good fortune be yours! Protect Rādhā's son (Karna), who is sinking into an ocean of distress, deeply immersed in the fear of Bhīmasena."

sanskrit: ते तु राज्ञा समादिष्टा भीमसेनजिघांसवः ।अभ्यवर्तन्त संक्रुद्धाः पतंगा इव पावकम् ॥६॥
transliteration: te tu rājñā samādiṣṭā bhīmasenajighāṁsavaḥ ,abhyavartanta saṁkruddhāḥ pataṁgā iva pāvakam.
english: But they, commanded by the King, desiring to kill Bhīmasena, angrily rushed forth like moths into a fire.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 35 Verse 6. But they, commanded by the King, desiring to kill Bhīmasena, angrily rushed forth like moths into a fire.

sanskrit: श्रुतायुर्दुर्धरः क्राथो विवित्सुर्विकटः समः ।निषङ्गी कवची पाशी तथा नन्दोपनन्दकौ ॥७॥
transliteration: śrutāyurdurdharaḥ krātho vivitsurvikaṭaḥ samaḥ ,niṣaṅgī kavacī pāśī tathā nandopanandakau.
english: They were Śrutāyu, Durdhara, Krātha, Vivitsu, Vikaṭa, Sama, Niṣaṅgī (who carried a quiver), Kavacī (who wore armor), Pāśī (who wielded a noose), and also Nanda and Upanandaka.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 35 Verse 7. They were Śrutāyu, Durdhara, Krātha, Vivitsu, Vikaṭa, Sama, Niṣaṅgī (who carried a quiver), Kavacī (who wore armor), Pāśī (who wielded a noose), and also Nanda and Upanandaka.

sanskrit: दुष्प्रधर्षः सुबाहुश्च वातवेगसुवर्चसौ ।धनुर्ग्राहो दुर्मदश्च तथा सत्त्वसमः सहः ॥८॥
transliteration: duṣpradharṣaḥ subāhuśca vātavegasuvarcasau ,dhanurgrāho durmadaśca tathā sattvasamaḥ sahaḥ.
english: Duṣpradharṣa, Subāhu, Vātavega and Suvarcasa, Dhanurgraha, Durmada, and also Sattvasama and Saha.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 35 Verse 8. Duṣpradharṣa, Subāhu, Vātavega and Suvarcasa, Dhanurgraha, Durmada, and also Sattvasama and Saha.

sanskrit: एते रथैः परिवृता वीर्यवन्तो महाबलाः ।भीमसेनं समासाद्य समन्तात्पर्यवारयन् ।ते व्यमुञ्चञ्शरव्रातान्नानालिङ्गान्समन्ततः ॥९॥
transliteration: ete rathaiḥ parivṛtā vīryavanto mahābalāḥ ,bhīmasenaṁ samāsādya samantātparyavārayan ,te vyamuñcañśaravrātānnānāliṅgānsamantataḥ.
english: Surrounded by their chariots, these valorous and mighty warriors, having approached Bhīmasena, encircled him from all sides. They then released showers of arrows of various types from every direction.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 35 Verse 9. Surrounded by their chariots, these valorous and mighty warriors, having approached Bhīmasena, encircled him from all sides. They then released showers of arrows of various types from every direction.

sanskrit: स तैरभ्यर्द्यमानस्तु भीमसेनो महाबलः ।तेषामापततां क्षिप्रं सुतानां ते नराधिप ।रथैः पञ्चाशता सार्धं पञ्चाशन्न्यहनद्रथान् ॥१०॥
transliteration: sa tairabhyardyamānastu bhīmaseno mahābalaḥ ,teṣāmāpatatāṁ kṣipraṁ sutānāṁ te narādhipa ,rathaiḥ pañcāśatā sārdhaṁ pañcāśannyahanadrathān.
english: O king, that mighty Bhīmasena, though being assailed by your attacking sons, swiftly destroyed fifty chariots along with their fifty warriors.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 35 Verse 10. O king, that mighty Bhīmasena, though being assailed by your attacking sons, swiftly destroyed fifty chariots along with their fifty warriors.

sanskrit: विवित्सोस्तु ततः क्रुद्धो भल्लेनापाहरच्छिरः ।सकुण्डलशिरस्त्राणं पूर्णचन्द्रोपमं तदा ।भीमेन च महाराज स पपात हतो भुवि ॥११॥
transliteration: vivitsostu tataḥ kruddho bhallenāpāharacchiraḥ ,sakuṇḍalaśirastrāṇaṁ pūrṇacandropamaṁ tadā ,bhīmena ca mahārāja sa papāta hato bhuvi.
english: Then, Bhīmasena, enraged, with a spear severed the head of Vivitsu. That head, adorned with earrings and a helmet, resembled the full moon. And, O great king, Vivitsu, struck down by Bhīmasena, fell to the ground.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 35 Verse 11. Then, Bhīmasena, enraged, with a spear severed the head of Vivitsu. That head, adorned with earrings and a helmet, resembled the full moon. And, O great king, Vivitsu, struck down by Bhīmasena, fell to the ground.

sanskrit: तं दृष्ट्वा निहतं शूरं भ्रातरः सर्वतः प्रभो ।अभ्यद्रवन्त समरे भीमं भीमपराक्रमम् ॥१२॥
transliteration: taṁ dṛṣṭvā nihataṁ śūraṁ bhrātaraḥ sarvataḥ prabho ,abhyadravanta samare bhīmaṁ bhīmaparākramam.
english: O lord, having seen that hero killed, his brothers from all sides rushed into battle towards Bhima, who possessed terrible prowess.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 35 Verse 12. O lord, having seen that hero killed, his brothers from all sides rushed into battle towards Bhima, who possessed terrible prowess.

sanskrit: ततोऽपराभ्यां भल्लाभ्यां पुत्रयोस्ते महाहवे ।जहार समरे प्राणान्भीमो भीमपराक्रमः ॥१३॥
transliteration: tato'parābhyāṁ bhallābhyāṁ putrayoste mahāhave ,jahāra samare prāṇānbhīmo bhīmaparākramaḥ.
english: Then, in that great battle, Bhima, of terrible prowess, took the lives of your two sons with two other broad-headed arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 35 Verse 13. Then, in that great battle, Bhima, of terrible prowess, took the lives of your two sons with two other broad-headed arrows.

sanskrit: तौ धरामन्वपद्येतां वातरुग्णाविव द्रुमौ ।विकटश्च समश्चोभौ देवगर्भसमौ नृप ॥१४॥
transliteration: tau dharāmanvapadyetāṁ vātarugṇāviva drumau ,vikaṭaśca samaścobhau devagarbhasamau nṛpa.
english: O king, those two (sons), Vikata and Sama, both resembling sons of gods, fell to the ground like two trees broken by the wind.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 35 Verse 14. O king, those two (sons), Vikata and Sama, both resembling sons of gods, fell to the ground like two trees broken by the wind.

sanskrit: ततस्तु त्वरितो भीमः क्राथं निन्ये यमक्षयम् ।नाराचेन सुतीक्ष्णेन स हतो न्यपतद्भुवि ॥१५॥
transliteration: tatastu tvarito bhīmaḥ krāthaṁ ninye yamakṣayam ,nārācena sutīkṣṇena sa hato nyapatadbhuvi.
english: Then, swift Bhima sent Kratha to the abode of Yama. Struck by a very sharp arrow, he fell to the ground.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 35 Verse 15. Then, swift Bhima sent Kratha to the abode of Yama. Struck by a very sharp arrow, he fell to the ground.

sanskrit: हाहाकारस्ततस्तीव्रः संबभूव जनेश्वर ।वध्यमानेषु ते राजंस्तदा पुत्रेषु धन्विषु ॥१६॥
transliteration: hāhākārastatastīvraḥ saṁbabhūva janeśvara ,vadhyamāneṣu te rājaṁstadā putreṣu dhanviṣu.
english: O King, a terrible cry of lamentation then arose when your archer sons were being slain at that time.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 35 Verse 16. O King, a terrible cry of lamentation then arose when your archer sons were being slain at that time.

sanskrit: तेषां संलुलिते सैन्ये भीमसेनो महाबलः ।नन्दोपनन्दौ समरे प्रापयद्यमसादनम् ॥१७॥
transliteration: teṣāṁ saṁlulite sainye bhīmaseno mahābalaḥ ,nandopanandau samare prāpayadyamasādanam.
english: When their army was thrown into confusion, Bhimasena, of great strength, sent Nanda and Upananda to the abode of Yama (yamasādanam) in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 35 Verse 17. When their army was thrown into confusion, Bhimasena, of great strength, sent Nanda and Upananda to the abode of Yama (yamasādanam) in battle.

sanskrit: ततस्ते प्राद्रवन्भीताः पुत्रास्ते विह्वलीकृताः ।भीमसेनं रणे दृष्ट्वा कालान्तकयमोपमम् ॥१८॥
transliteration: tataste prādravanbhītāḥ putrāste vihvalīkṛtāḥ ,bhīmasenaṁ raṇe dṛṣṭvā kālāntakayamopamam.
english: Then they, your sons, bewildered and fearful, fled after seeing Bhimasena in battle, who was like Yama, the destroyer of time.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 35 Verse 18. Then they, your sons, bewildered and fearful, fled after seeing Bhimasena in battle, who was like Yama, the destroyer of time.

sanskrit: पुत्रांस्ते निहतान्दृष्ट्वा सूतपुत्रो महामनाः ।हंसवर्णान्हयान्भूयः प्राहिणोद्यत्र पाण्डवः ॥१९॥
transliteration: putrāṁste nihatāndṛṣṭvā sūtaputro mahāmanāḥ ,haṁsavarṇānhayānbhūyaḥ prāhiṇodyatra pāṇḍavaḥ.
english: Seeing your sons slain, the great-minded son of Sūta (Karṇa) again sent his swan-colored horses to where the Pāṇḍava was.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 35 Verse 19. Seeing your sons slain, the great-minded son of Sūta (Karṇa) again sent his swan-colored horses to where the Pāṇḍava was.

sanskrit: ते प्रेषिता महाराज मद्रराजेन वाजिनः ।भीमसेनरथं प्राप्य समसज्जन्त वेगिताः ॥२०॥
transliteration: te preṣitā mahārāja madrarājena vājinaḥ ,bhīmasenarathaṁ prāpya samasajjanta vegitāḥ.
english: O great king, those swift horses, sent by the king of Madras, reached Bhīmasena's chariot and became yoked.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 35 Verse 20. O great king, those swift horses, sent by the king of Madras, reached Bhīmasena's chariot and became yoked.

sanskrit: स संनिपातस्तुमुलो घोररूपो विशां पते ।आसीद्रौद्रो महाराज कर्णपाण्डवयोर्मृधे ॥२१॥
transliteration: sa saṁnipātastumulo ghorarūpo viśāṁ pate ,āsīdraudro mahārāja karṇapāṇḍavayormṛdhe.
english: O lord of the people, O great king, that tumultuous, dreadful, and fierce encounter took place in the battle between Karṇa and the Pāṇḍava.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 35 Verse 21. O lord of the people, O great king, that tumultuous, dreadful, and fierce encounter took place in the battle between Karṇa and the Pāṇḍava.

sanskrit: दृष्ट्वा मम महाराज तौ समेतौ महारथौ ।आसीद्बुद्धिः कथं नूनमेतदद्य भविष्यति ॥२२॥
transliteration: dṛṣṭvā mama mahārāja tau sametau mahārathau ,āsīdbuddhiḥ kathaṁ nūnametadadya bhaviṣyati.
english: O great king, seeing those two great chariot warriors joined together, a thought arose in me: 'How, indeed, will this turn out today?'
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 35 Verse 22. O great king, seeing those two great chariot warriors joined together, a thought arose in me: 'How, indeed, will this turn out today?'

sanskrit: ततो मुहूर्ताद्राजेन्द्र नातिकृच्छ्राद्धसन्निव ।विरथं भीमकर्माणं भीमं कर्णश्चकार ह ॥२३॥
transliteration: tato muhūrtādrājendra nātikṛcchrāddhasanniva ,virathaṁ bhīmakarmāṇaṁ bhīmaṁ karṇaścakāra ha.
english: Then, O chief of kings, after a short while, Karṇa, as if laughing effortlessly, rendered Bhīma, who performed terrible deeds, chariot-less.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 35 Verse 23. Then, O chief of kings, after a short while, Karṇa, as if laughing effortlessly, rendered Bhīma, who performed terrible deeds, chariot-less.

sanskrit: विरथो भरतश्रेष्ठः प्रहसन्ननिलोपमः ।गदाहस्तो महाबाहुरपतत्स्यन्दनोत्तमात् ॥२४॥
transliteration: viratho bharataśreṣṭhaḥ prahasannanilopamaḥ ,gadāhasto mahābāhurapatatsyandanottamāt.
english: The best among the Bhāratas, the mighty-armed hero, swift as the wind, fell from his excellent chariot, mace in hand, even as he was smiling, having been rendered charioless.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 35 Verse 24. The best among the Bhāratas, the mighty-armed hero, swift as the wind, fell from his excellent chariot, mace in hand, even as he was smiling, having been rendered charioless.

sanskrit: नागान्सप्तशतान्राजन्नीषादन्तान्प्रहारिणः ।व्यधमत्सहसा भीमः क्रुद्धरूपः परंतपः ॥२५॥
transliteration: nāgānsaptaśatānrājannīṣādantānprahāriṇaḥ ,vyadhamatsahasā bhīmaḥ kruddharūpaḥ paraṁtapaḥ.
english: O King, Bhīma, the tormentor of foes, appearing in an enraged form, suddenly scattered seven hundred attacking elephants, complete with their mahouts and tusks.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 35 Verse 25. O King, Bhīma, the tormentor of foes, appearing in an enraged form, suddenly scattered seven hundred attacking elephants, complete with their mahouts and tusks.

sanskrit: दन्तवेष्टेषु नेत्रेषु कम्भेषु स कटेषु च ।मर्मस्वपि च मर्मज्ञो निनदन्व्यधमद्भृशम् ॥२६॥
transliteration: dantaveṣṭeṣu netreṣu kambheṣu sa kaṭeṣu ca ,marmasvapi ca marmajño ninadanvyadhamadbhṛśam.
english: And he, the knower of vital spots, roaring, fiercely destroyed them in the areas around their tusks, in their eyes, temples, and flanks, and even in their vital spots.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 35 Verse 26. And he, the knower of vital spots, roaring, fiercely destroyed them in the areas around their tusks, in their eyes, temples, and flanks, and even in their vital spots.

sanskrit: ततस्ते प्राद्रवन्भीताः प्रतीपं प्रहिताः पुनः ।महामात्रैस्तमावव्रुर्मेघा इव दिवाकरम् ॥२७॥
transliteration: tataste prādravanbhītāḥ pratīpaṁ prahitāḥ punaḥ ,mahāmātraistamāvavrurmeghā iva divākaram.
english: Then those terrified elephants fled. But the mahouts, having dispatched them again to face him, surrounded him (Bhīma), just as clouds envelop the sun.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 35 Verse 27. Then those terrified elephants fled. But the mahouts, having dispatched them again to face him, surrounded him (Bhīma), just as clouds envelop the sun.

sanskrit: तान्स सप्तशतान्नागान्सारोहायुधकेतनान् ।भूमिष्ठो गदया जघ्ने शरन्मेघानिवानिलः ॥२८॥
transliteration: tānsa saptaśatānnāgānsārohāyudhaketanān ,bhūmiṣṭho gadayā jaghne śaranmeghānivānilaḥ.
english: Standing on the ground, he struck down those seven hundred elephants, along with their riders, weapons, and banners, with his mace, just as the wind scatters autumnal clouds.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 35 Verse 28. Standing on the ground, he struck down those seven hundred elephants, along with their riders, weapons, and banners, with his mace, just as the wind scatters autumnal clouds.

sanskrit: ततः सुबलपुत्रस्य नागानतिबलान्पुनः ।पोथयामास कौन्तेयो द्वापञ्चाशतमाहवे ॥२९॥
transliteration: tataḥ subalaputrasya nāgānatibalānpunaḥ ,pothayāmāsa kaunteyo dvāpañcāśatamāhave.
english: Then, the son of Kunti (Bhīma) again destroyed fifty-two extremely powerful elephants belonging to the son of Subala (Śakuni) in that battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 35 Verse 29. Then, the son of Kunti (Bhīma) again destroyed fifty-two extremely powerful elephants belonging to the son of Subala (Śakuni) in that battle.

sanskrit: तथा रथशतं साग्रं पत्तींश्च शतशोऽपरान् ।न्यहनत्पाण्डवो युद्धे तापयंस्तव वाहिनीम् ॥३०॥
