transliteration: tathā rathaśataṁ sāgraṁ pattīṁśca śataśo'parān ,nyahanatpāṇḍavo yuddhe tāpayaṁstava vāhinīm.
english: In the same way, that Pāṇḍava (Bhīma) killed over a hundred chariots and hundreds of other foot soldiers in battle, causing great distress to your army.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 35 Verse 30. In the same way, that Pāṇḍava (Bhīma) killed over a hundred chariots and hundreds of other foot soldiers in battle, causing great distress to your army.

sanskrit: प्रताप्यमानं सूर्येण भीमेन च महात्मना ।तव सैन्यं संचुकोच चर्म वह्निगतं यथा ॥३१॥
transliteration: pratāpyamānaṁ sūryeṇa bhīmena ca mahātmanā ,tava sainyaṁ saṁcukoca carma vahnigataṁ yathā.
english: Your army, being tormented by the sun and by the great-souled Bhīma, shriveled up like leather thrown into fire.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 35 Verse 31. Your army, being tormented by the sun and by the great-souled Bhīma, shriveled up like leather thrown into fire.

sanskrit: ते भीमभयसंत्रस्तास्तावका भरतर्षभ ।विहाय समरे भीमं दुद्रुवुर्वै दिशो दश ॥३२॥
transliteration: te bhīmabhayasaṁtrastāstāvakā bharatarṣabha ,vihāya samare bhīmaṁ dudruvurvai diśo daśa.
english: O best of the Bhāratas (Bharatārṣabha), your warriors, greatly terrified by Bhīma, indeed abandoned the battlefield where Bhīma was and fled in all ten directions.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 35 Verse 32. O best of the Bhāratas (Bharatārṣabha), your warriors, greatly terrified by Bhīma, indeed abandoned the battlefield where Bhīma was and fled in all ten directions.

sanskrit: रथाः पञ्चशताश्चान्ये ह्रादिनश्चर्मवर्मिणः ।भीममभ्यद्रवंस्तूर्णं शरपूगैः समन्ततः ॥३३॥
transliteration: rathāḥ pañcaśatāścānye hrādinaścarmavarmiṇaḥ ,bhīmamabhyadravaṁstūrṇaṁ śarapūgaiḥ samantataḥ.
english: And five hundred other chariots, with their warriors roaring and clad in leather armor, swiftly rushed Bhīma from all sides with showers of arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 35 Verse 33. And five hundred other chariots, with their warriors roaring and clad in leather armor, swiftly rushed Bhīma from all sides with showers of arrows.

sanskrit: तान्ससूतरथान्सर्वान्सपताकाध्वजायुधान् ।पोथयामास गदया भीमो विष्णुरिवासुरान् ॥३४॥
transliteration: tānsasūtarathānsarvānsapatākādhvajāyudhān ,pothayāmāsa gadayā bhīmo viṣṇurivāsurān.
english: Bhīma crushed all of them - along with their charioteers and chariots, and with their banners, flags, and weapons - with his mace, just as Viṣṇu crushes the asuras.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 35 Verse 34. Bhīma crushed all of them - along with their charioteers and chariots, and with their banners, flags, and weapons - with his mace, just as Viṣṇu crushes the asuras.

sanskrit: ततः शकुनिनिर्दिष्टाः सादिनः शूरसंमताः ।त्रिसाहस्रा ययुर्भीमं शक्त्यृष्टिप्रासपाणयः ॥३५॥
transliteration: tataḥ śakuninirdiṣṭāḥ sādinaḥ śūrasaṁmatāḥ ,trisāhasrā yayurbhīmaṁ śaktyṛṣṭiprāsapāṇayaḥ.
english: Then, three thousand cavalrymen, specifically instructed by Śakuni and esteemed as valiant, advanced towards Bhīma, holding "śakti"s, "ṛṣṭi"s, and "prāsa"s in their hands.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 35 Verse 35. Then, three thousand cavalrymen, specifically instructed by Śakuni and esteemed as valiant, advanced towards Bhīma, holding "śakti"s, "ṛṣṭi"s, and "prāsa"s in their hands.

sanskrit: तान्प्रत्युद्गम्य यवनानश्वारोहान्वरारिहा ।विचरन्विविधान्मार्गान्घातयामास पोथयन् ॥३६॥
transliteration: tānpratyudgamya yavanānaśvārohānvarārihā ,vicaranvividhānmārgānghātayāmāsa pothayan.
english: The slayer of formidable enemies went forth to meet those Yavana horsemen, and moving about on various paths, he crushed and slew them.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 35 Verse 36. The slayer of formidable enemies went forth to meet those Yavana horsemen, and moving about on various paths, he crushed and slew them.

sanskrit: तेषामासीन्महाञ्शब्दस्ताडितानां च सार्वशः ।असिभिश्छिद्यमानानां नडानामिव भारत ॥३७॥
transliteration: teṣāmāsīnmahāñśabdastāḍitānāṁ ca sārvaśaḥ ,asibhiśchidyamānānāṁ naḍānāmiva bhārata.
english: O Bhārata, a great clamor arose from them, those (Yavanas) who were being struck and cut everywhere by swords, like the sound of reeds being severed.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 35 Verse 37. O Bhārata, a great clamor arose from them, those (Yavanas) who were being struck and cut everywhere by swords, like the sound of reeds being severed.

sanskrit: एवं सुबलपुत्रस्य त्रिसाहस्रान्हयोत्तमान् ।हत्वान्यं रथमास्थाय क्रुद्धो राधेयमभ्ययात् ॥३८॥
transliteration: evaṁ subalaputrasya trisāhasrānhayottamān ,hatvānyaṁ rathamāsthāya kruddho rādheyamabhyayāt.
english: Thus, having slain three thousand excellent horses belonging to Subala's son (Śakuni), the enraged (hero) then mounted another chariot and advanced against the son of Rādhā (Karṇa).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 35 Verse 38. Thus, having slain three thousand excellent horses belonging to Subala's son (Śakuni), the enraged (hero) then mounted another chariot and advanced against the son of Rādhā (Karṇa).

sanskrit: कर्णोऽपि समरे राजन्धर्मपुत्रमरिंदमम् ।शरैः प्रच्छादयामास सारथिं चाप्यपातयत् ॥३९॥
transliteration: karṇo'pi samare rājandharmaputramariṁdamam ,śaraiḥ pracchādayāmāsa sārathiṁ cāpyapātayat.
english: O King, Karṇa too, in that battle, completely covered the son of natural law (dharma) (Yudhiṣṭhira), the subduer of enemies, with arrows, and also struck down his charioteer.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 35 Verse 39. O King, Karṇa too, in that battle, completely covered the son of natural law (dharma) (Yudhiṣṭhira), the subduer of enemies, with arrows, and also struck down his charioteer.

sanskrit: ततः संप्रद्रुतं संख्ये रथं दृष्ट्वा महारथः ।अन्वधावत्किरन्बाणैः कङ्कपत्रैरजिह्मगैः ॥४०॥
transliteration: tataḥ saṁpradrutaṁ saṁkhye rathaṁ dṛṣṭvā mahārathaḥ ,anvadhāvatkiranbāṇaiḥ kaṅkapatrairajihmagaiḥ.
english: Then, seeing the chariot flee from the battlefield, the great warrior pursued it, showering it with straight-flying arrows feathered with heron plumes.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 35 Verse 40. Then, seeing the chariot flee from the battlefield, the great warrior pursued it, showering it with straight-flying arrows feathered with heron plumes.

sanskrit: राजानमभि धावन्तं शरैरावृत्य रोदसी ।क्रुद्धः प्रच्छादयामास शरजालेन मारुतिः ॥४१॥
transliteration: rājānamabhi dhāvantaṁ śarairāvṛtya rodasī ,kruddhaḥ pracchādayāmāsa śarajālena mārutiḥ.
english: Enraged, Bhima (Mārutiḥ), covering the heavens and earth with arrows, completely enveloped the king (Duryodhana), who was rushing towards him, with a net of arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 35 Verse 41. Enraged, Bhima (Mārutiḥ), covering the heavens and earth with arrows, completely enveloped the king (Duryodhana), who was rushing towards him, with a net of arrows.

sanskrit: संनिवृत्तस्ततस्तूर्णं राधेयः शत्रुकर्शनः ।भीमं प्रच्छादयामास समन्तान्निशितैः शरैः ॥४२॥
transliteration: saṁnivṛttastatastūrṇaṁ rādheyaḥ śatrukarśanaḥ ,bhīmaṁ pracchādayāmāsa samantānniśitaiḥ śaraiḥ.
english: Then, Karna (Rādheyaḥ), the tormentor of enemies, quickly turned back and completely covered Bhima from all sides with sharp arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 35 Verse 42. Then, Karna (Rādheyaḥ), the tormentor of enemies, quickly turned back and completely covered Bhima from all sides with sharp arrows.

sanskrit: भीमसेनरथव्यग्रं कर्णं भारत सात्यकिः ।अभ्यर्दयदमेयात्मा पार्ष्णिग्रहणकारणात् ।अभ्यवर्तत कर्णस्तमर्दितोऽपि शरैर्भृशम् ॥४३॥
transliteration: bhīmasenarathavyagraṁ karṇaṁ bhārata sātyakiḥ ,abhyardayadameyātmā pārṣṇigrahaṇakāraṇāt ,abhyavartata karṇastamardito'pi śarairbhṛśam.
english: O Bhārata, Satyaki, whose spirit (ātman) was immeasurable, attacked Karna, who was busy with Bhimasena's chariot, from the rear. Even though greatly afflicted by arrows, Karna fiercely turned back towards him.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 35 Verse 43. O Bhārata, Satyaki, whose spirit (ātman) was immeasurable, attacked Karna, who was busy with Bhimasena's chariot, from the rear. Even though greatly afflicted by arrows, Karna fiercely turned back towards him.

sanskrit: तावन्योन्यं समासाद्य वृषभौ सर्वधन्विनाम् ।विसृजन्तौ शरांश्चित्रान्विभ्राजेतां मनस्विनौ ॥४४॥
transliteration: tāvanyonyaṁ samāsādya vṛṣabhau sarvadhanvinām ,visṛjantau śarāṁścitrānvibhrājetāṁ manasvinau.
english: Having met each other, those two high-minded ones, who were like bulls among all archers, shone brightly as they released diverse arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 35 Verse 44. Having met each other, those two high-minded ones, who were like bulls among all archers, shone brightly as they released diverse arrows.

sanskrit: ताभ्यां वियति राजेन्द्र विततं भीमदर्शनम् ।क्रौञ्चपृष्ठारुणं रौद्रं बाणजालं व्यदृश्यत ॥४५॥
transliteration: tābhyāṁ viyati rājendra vitataṁ bhīmadarśanam ,krauñcapṛṣṭhāruṇaṁ raudraṁ bāṇajālaṁ vyadṛśyata.
english: O King, by those two (warriors), a vast, terrifying, and fierce multitude of arrows became visible in the sky, appearing reddish like the back of a curlew.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 35 Verse 45. O King, by those two (warriors), a vast, terrifying, and fierce multitude of arrows became visible in the sky, appearing reddish like the back of a curlew.

sanskrit: नैव सूर्यप्रभां खं वा न दिशः प्रदिशः कुतः ।प्राज्ञासिष्म वयं ताभ्यां शरैर्मुक्तैः सहस्रशः ॥४६॥
transliteration: naiva sūryaprabhāṁ khaṁ vā na diśaḥ pradiśaḥ kutaḥ ,prājñāsiṣma vayaṁ tābhyāṁ śarairmuktaiḥ sahasraśaḥ.
english: Due to the thousands of arrows released by those two (warriors), we could not discern even the sun's brilliance or the sky, nor the cardinal directions, much less the intermediate directions.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 35 Verse 46. Due to the thousands of arrows released by those two (warriors), we could not discern even the sun's brilliance or the sky, nor the cardinal directions, much less the intermediate directions.

sanskrit: मध्याह्ने तपतो राजन्भास्करस्य महाप्रभाः ।हृताः सर्वाः शरौघैस्तैः कर्णमाधवयोस्तदा ॥४७॥
transliteration: madhyāhne tapato rājanbhāskarasya mahāprabhāḥ ,hṛtāḥ sarvāḥ śaraughaistaiḥ karṇamādhavayostadā.
english: O King, at that time, all the great splendors of the scorching midday sun were obscured by those multitudes of arrows from Karna and Madhava.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 35 Verse 47. O King, at that time, all the great splendors of the scorching midday sun were obscured by those multitudes of arrows from Karna and Madhava.

sanskrit: सौबलं कृतवर्माणं द्रौणिमाधिरथिं कृपम् ।संसक्तान्पाण्डवैर्दृष्ट्वा निवृत्ताः कुरवः पुनः ॥४८॥
transliteration: saubalaṁ kṛtavarmāṇaṁ drauṇimādhirathiṁ kṛpam ,saṁsaktānpāṇḍavairdṛṣṭvā nivṛttāḥ kuravaḥ punaḥ.
english: Upon seeing Saubala (Śakuni), Kṛtavarman, Drauṇi (Aśvatthāman), Adhirathi (Vṛṣasena), and Kṛpa engaged in battle with the Pāṇḍavas, the Kurus again turned back.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 35 Verse 48. Upon seeing Saubala (Śakuni), Kṛtavarman, Drauṇi (Aśvatthāman), Adhirathi (Vṛṣasena), and Kṛpa engaged in battle with the Pāṇḍavas, the Kurus again turned back.

sanskrit: तेषामापततां शब्दस्तीव्र आसीद्विशां पते ।उद्धूतानां यथा वृष्ट्या सागराणां भयावहः ॥४९॥
transliteration: teṣāmāpatatāṁ śabdastīvra āsīdviśāṁ pate ,uddhūtānāṁ yathā vṛṣṭyā sāgarāṇāṁ bhayāvahaḥ.
english: O lord of the people, the intense roar of those (soldiers) attacking was terrifying, just like the roar of oceans agitated by heavy rain.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 35 Verse 49. O lord of the people, the intense roar of those (soldiers) attacking was terrifying, just like the roar of oceans agitated by heavy rain.

sanskrit: ते सेने भृशसंविग्ने दृष्ट्वान्योन्यं महारणे ।हर्षेण महता युक्ते परिगृह्य परस्परम् ॥५०॥
transliteration: te sene bhṛśasaṁvigne dṛṣṭvānyonyaṁ mahāraṇe ,harṣeṇa mahatā yukte parigṛhya parasparam.
english: Seeing each other in the great battle, those two armies, though greatly agitated, were filled with great joy and engaged with one another.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 35 Verse 50. Seeing each other in the great battle, those two armies, though greatly agitated, were filled with great joy and engaged with one another.

sanskrit: ततः प्रववृते युद्धं मध्यं प्राप्ते दिवाकरे ।यादृशं न कदाचिद्धि दृष्टपूर्वं न च श्रुतम् ॥५१॥
transliteration: tataḥ pravavṛte yuddhaṁ madhyaṁ prāpte divākare ,yādṛśaṁ na kadāciddhi dṛṣṭapūrvaṁ na ca śrutam.
english: Then the battle commenced when the sun reached its midpoint (midday), a battle of such a kind that it had never before been seen or heard of.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 35 Verse 51. Then the battle commenced when the sun reached its midpoint (midday), a battle of such a kind that it had never before been seen or heard of.

sanskrit: बलौघस्तु समासाद्य बलौघं सहसा रणे ।उपासर्पत वेगेन जलौघ इव सागरम् ॥५२॥
transliteration: balaughastu samāsādya balaughaṁ sahasā raṇe ,upāsarpata vegena jalaugha iva sāgaram.
english: A mass of troops, having suddenly met another mass of troops in battle, advanced swiftly, just as a torrent of water rushes towards the ocean.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 35 Verse 52. A mass of troops, having suddenly met another mass of troops in battle, advanced swiftly, just as a torrent of water rushes towards the ocean.

sanskrit: आसीन्निनादः सुमहान्बलौघानां परस्परम् ।गर्जतां सागरौघाणां यथा स्यान्निस्वनो महान् ॥५३॥
transliteration: āsīnninādaḥ sumahānbalaughānāṁ parasparam ,garjatāṁ sāgaraughāṇāṁ yathā syānnisvano mahān.
english: A very great roar arose among the masses of troops, similar to the mighty sound of roaring ocean waves.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 35 Verse 53. A very great roar arose among the masses of troops, similar to the mighty sound of roaring ocean waves.

sanskrit: ते तु सेने समासाद्य वेगवत्यौ परस्परम् ।एकीभावमनुप्राप्ते नद्याविव समागमे ॥५४॥
transliteration: te tu sene samāsādya vegavatyau parasparam ,ekībhāvamanuprāpte nadyāviva samāgame.
english: And those two swift armies, having met each other, became united, just like two rivers merge at their confluence.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 35 Verse 54. And those two swift armies, having met each other, became united, just like two rivers merge at their confluence.

sanskrit: ततः प्रववृते युद्धं घोररूपं विशां पते ।कुरूणां पाण्डवानां च लिप्सतां सुमहद्यशः ॥५५॥
transliteration: tataḥ pravavṛte yuddhaṁ ghorarūpaṁ viśāṁ pate ,kurūṇāṁ pāṇḍavānāṁ ca lipsatāṁ sumahadyaśaḥ.
english: Then, O protector of the people, a dreadful battle commenced between the Kurus and the Pandavas, both of whom were eager to attain great glory.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 35 Verse 55. Then, O protector of the people, a dreadful battle commenced between the Kurus and the Pandavas, both of whom were eager to attain great glory.

sanskrit: कुरूणां गर्जतां तत्र अविच्छेदकृता गिरः ।श्रूयन्ते विविधा राजन्नामान्युद्दिश्य भारत ॥५६॥
transliteration: kurūṇāṁ garjatāṁ tatra avicchedakṛtā giraḥ ,śrūyante vividhā rājannāmānyuddiśya bhārata.
english: O King (rājan), O descendant of Bharata (bhārata), the various and incessant shouts of the roaring Kurus were heard there, specifically calling out names.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 35 Verse 56. O King (rājan), O descendant of Bharata (bhārata), the various and incessant shouts of the roaring Kurus were heard there, specifically calling out names.

sanskrit: यस्य यद्धि रणे न्यङ्गं पितृतो मातृतोऽपि वा ।कर्मतः शीलतो वापि स तच्छ्रावयते युधि ॥५७॥
transliteration: yasya yaddhi raṇe nyaṅgaṁ pitṛto mātṛto'pi vā ,karmataḥ śīlato vāpi sa tacchrāvayate yudhi.
english: Whatever blemish or deficiency a warrior might have in battle, whether inherited from their father's or mother's side, or stemming from their past actions (karma) or character, that is what they indeed proclaim loudly in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 35 Verse 57. Whatever blemish or deficiency a warrior might have in battle, whether inherited from their father's or mother's side, or stemming from their past actions (karma) or character, that is what they indeed proclaim loudly in battle.

sanskrit: तान्दृष्ट्वा समरे शूरांस्तर्जयानान्परस्परम् ।अभवन्मे मती राजन्नैषामस्तीति जीवितम् ॥५८॥
transliteration: tāndṛṣṭvā samare śūrāṁstarjayānānparasparam ,abhavanme matī rājannaiṣāmastīti jīvitam.
english: O King (rājan), when I saw those heroes menacing each other in battle, the thought (mati) arose in me: 'These warriors will not survive' (literally, 'there is no life for them').
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 35 Verse 58. O King (rājan), when I saw those heroes menacing each other in battle, the thought (mati) arose in me: 'These warriors will not survive' (literally, 'there is no life for them').

sanskrit: तेषां दृष्ट्वा तु क्रुद्धानां वपूंष्यमिततेजसाम् ।अभवन्मे भयं तीव्रं कथमेतद्भविष्यति ॥५९॥
transliteration: teṣāṁ dṛṣṭvā tu kruddhānāṁ vapūṁṣyamitatejasām ,abhavanme bhayaṁ tīvraṁ kathametadbhaviṣyati.
english: But (tu) when I saw the bodies of those angry warriors, who possessed immeasurable might and splendor, an intense fear (bhaya) arose in me, thinking, 'How will this (battle) unfold?'
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 35 Verse 59. But (tu) when I saw the bodies of those angry warriors, who possessed immeasurable might and splendor, an intense fear (bhaya) arose in me, thinking, 'How will this (battle) unfold?'

sanskrit: ततस्ते पाण्डवा राजन्कौरवाश्च महारथाः ।ततक्षुः सायकैस्तीक्ष्णैर्निघ्नन्तो हि परस्परम् ॥६०॥
transliteration: tataste pāṇḍavā rājankauravāśca mahārathāḥ ,tatakṣuḥ sāyakaistīkṣṇairnighnanto hi parasparam.
english: Then, O King, those Pāṇḍavas and the great chariot-warriors (mahāratha) among the Kauravas, indeed killing each other, assailed one another with sharp arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 35 Verse 60. Then, O King, those Pāṇḍavas and the great chariot-warriors (mahāratha) among the Kauravas, indeed killing each other, assailed one another with sharp arrows.

sanskrit: संजय उवाच ।क्षत्रियास्ते महाराज परस्परवधैषिणः ।अन्योन्यं समरे जघ्नुः कृतवैराः परस्परम् ॥१॥
transliteration: saṁjaya uvāca ,kṣatriyāste mahārāja parasparavadhaiṣiṇaḥ ,anyonyaṁ samare jaghnuḥ kṛtavairāḥ parasparam.
english: Sañjaya said: O great king, those kṣatriyas, desiring to kill one another, having fostered mutual animosity, slew each other in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 36 Verse 1. Sañjaya said: O great king, those kṣatriyas, desiring to kill one another, having fostered mutual animosity, slew each other in battle.

sanskrit: रथौघाश्च हयौघाश्च नरौघाश्च समन्ततः ।गजौघाश्च महाराज संसक्ताः स्म परस्परम् ॥२॥
transliteration: rathaughāśca hayaughāśca naraughāśca samantataḥ ,gajaughāśca mahārāja saṁsaktāḥ sma parasparam.
english: O great king, from all sides, masses of chariots, masses of horses, masses of men, and masses of elephants became completely intertwined with one another.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 36 Verse 2. O great king, from all sides, masses of chariots, masses of horses, masses of men, and masses of elephants became completely intertwined with one another.

sanskrit: गदानां परिघाणां च कणपानां च सर्पताम् ।प्रासानां भिण्डिपालानां भुशुण्डीनां च सर्वशः ॥३॥
transliteration: gadānāṁ parighāṇāṁ ca kaṇapānāṁ ca sarpatām ,prāsānāṁ bhiṇḍipālānāṁ bhuśuṇḍīnāṁ ca sarvaśaḥ.
english: From all sides, there were maces, iron clubs (parigha), and spears (kaṇapa) flying about; also lances (prāsa), short spears (bhiṇḍipāla), and bhuśuṇḍīs.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 36 Verse 3. From all sides, there were maces, iron clubs (parigha), and spears (kaṇapa) flying about; also lances (prāsa), short spears (bhiṇḍipāla), and bhuśuṇḍīs.

sanskrit: संपातं चान्वपश्याम संग्रामे भृशदारुणे ।शलभा इव संपेतुः समन्ताच्छरवृष्टयः ॥४॥
transliteration: saṁpātaṁ cānvapaśyāma saṁgrāme bhṛśadāruṇe ,śalabhā iva saṁpetuḥ samantāccharavṛṣṭayaḥ.
english: We observed the conflict in that exceedingly dreadful battle, where showers of arrows fell from all sides like locusts.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 36 Verse 4. We observed the conflict in that exceedingly dreadful battle, where showers of arrows fell from all sides like locusts.

sanskrit: नागा नागान्समासाद्य व्यधमन्त परस्परम् ।हया हयांश्च समरे रथिनो रथिनस्तथा ।पत्तयः पत्तिसंघैश्च हयसंघैर्हयास्तथा ॥५॥
transliteration: nāgā nāgānsamāsādya vyadhamanta parasparam ,hayā hayāṁśca samare rathino rathinastathā ,pattayaḥ pattisaṁghaiśca hayasaṁghairhayāstathā.
english: Having encountered other elephants, the elephants assailed one another. Similarly, in battle, horses attacked horses and charioteers fought charioteers. Foot soldiers battled against formations of other foot soldiers, and horses against formations of other horses.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 36 Verse 5. Having encountered other elephants, the elephants assailed one another. Similarly, in battle, horses attacked horses and charioteers fought charioteers. Foot soldiers battled against formations of other foot soldiers, and horses against formations of other horses.

sanskrit: पत्तयो रथमातङ्गान्रथा हस्त्यश्वमेव च ।नागाश्च समरे त्र्यङ्गं ममृदुः शीघ्रगा नृप ॥६॥
transliteration: pattayo rathamātaṅgānrathā hastyaśvameva ca ,nāgāśca samare tryaṅgaṁ mamṛduḥ śīghragā nṛpa.
english: O King, foot soldiers crushed chariots and elephants, and chariots, in turn, crushed elephants and horses. Moreover, in battle, the swift-moving elephants trampled the three divisions [of the enemy forces].
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 36 Verse 6. O King, foot soldiers crushed chariots and elephants, and chariots, in turn, crushed elephants and horses. Moreover, in battle, the swift-moving elephants trampled the three divisions [of the enemy forces].

sanskrit: पततां तत्र शूराणां क्रोशतां च परस्परम् ।घोरमायोधनं जज्ञे पशूनां वैशसं यथा ॥७॥
transliteration: patatāṁ tatra śūrāṇāṁ krośatāṁ ca parasparam ,ghoramāyodhanaṁ jajñe paśūnāṁ vaiśasaṁ yathā.
english: There, as heroes fell and cried out to one another, a terrible battle ensued, resembling the slaughter of animals.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 36 Verse 7. There, as heroes fell and cried out to one another, a terrible battle ensued, resembling the slaughter of animals.

sanskrit: रुधिरेण समास्तीर्णा भाति भारत मेदिनी ।शक्रगोपगणाकीर्णा प्रावृषीव यथा धरा ॥८॥
transliteration: rudhireṇa samāstīrṇā bhāti bhārata medinī ,śakragopagaṇākīrṇā prāvṛṣīva yathā dharā.
english: O Bhārata, the earth appears covered with blood, just like the ground in the rainy season is teeming with swarms of indragopa insects.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 36 Verse 8. O Bhārata, the earth appears covered with blood, just like the ground in the rainy season is teeming with swarms of indragopa insects.

sanskrit: यथा वा वाससी शुक्ले महारजनरञ्जिते ।बिभृयाद्युवतिः श्यामा तद्वदासीद्वसुंधरा ।मांसशोणितचित्रेव शातकौम्भमयीव च ॥९॥
transliteration: yathā vā vāsasī śukle mahārajanarañjite ,bibhṛyādyuvatiḥ śyāmā tadvadāsīdvasuṁdharā ,māṁsaśoṇitacitreva śātakaumbhamayīva ca.
english: Just as a dark-skinned young woman might wear two white garments dyed with a deep red pigment, so too was the earth. It was as if adorned with paintings of flesh and blood, and as if made of pure gold.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 36 Verse 9. Just as a dark-skinned young woman might wear two white garments dyed with a deep red pigment, so too was the earth. It was as if adorned with paintings of flesh and blood, and as if made of pure gold.

sanskrit: छिन्नानां चोत्तमाङ्गानां बाहूनां चोरुभिः सह ।कुण्डलानां प्रविद्धानां भूषणानां च भारत ॥१०॥
transliteration: chinnānāṁ cottamāṅgānāṁ bāhūnāṁ corubhiḥ saha ,kuṇḍalānāṁ praviddhānāṁ bhūṣaṇānāṁ ca bhārata.
english: O Bhārata, (the field was strewn) with severed heads, and with arms along with thighs, and with pierced earrings, and with ornaments.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 36 Verse 10. O Bhārata, (the field was strewn) with severed heads, and with arms along with thighs, and with pierced earrings, and with ornaments.

sanskrit: निष्काणामधिसूत्राणां शरीराणां च धन्विनाम् ।वर्मणां सपताकानां संघास्तत्रापतन्भुवि ॥११॥
transliteration: niṣkāṇāmadhisūtrāṇāṁ śarīrāṇāṁ ca dhanvinām ,varmaṇāṁ sapatākānāṁ saṁghāstatrāpatanbhuvi.
english: Masses of golden necklaces, sacred threads (sūtra), bodies of archers, and armors with their banners fell there on the ground.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 36 Verse 11. Masses of golden necklaces, sacred threads (sūtra), bodies of archers, and armors with their banners fell there on the ground.

sanskrit: शरैः कनकपुङ्खैस्तु चिता रेजुर्गजोत्तमाः ।उल्काभिः संप्रदीप्ताग्राः पर्वता इव मारिष ॥१६॥
transliteration: śaraiḥ kanakapuṅkhaistu citā rejurgajottamāḥ ,ulkābhiḥ saṁpradīptāgrāḥ parvatā iva māriṣa.
english: O venerable one, the magnificent elephants, covered with golden-shafted arrows, shone like mountains with their peaks brightly lit by blazing meteors.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 36 Verse 16. O venerable one, the magnificent elephants, covered with golden-shafted arrows, shone like mountains with their peaks brightly lit by blazing meteors.

sanskrit: केचिदभ्याहता नागा नागैर्नगनिभा भुवि ।निपेतुः समरे तस्मिन्पक्षवन्त इवाद्रयः ॥१७॥
transliteration: kecidabhyāhatā nāgā nāgairnaganibhā bhuvi ,nipetuḥ samare tasminpakṣavanta ivādrayaḥ.
english: In that battle, some mountain-like elephants, struck by (other) elephants, fell to the ground like winged mountains.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 36 Verse 17. In that battle, some mountain-like elephants, struck by (other) elephants, fell to the ground like winged mountains.

sanskrit: अपरे प्राद्रवन्नागाः शल्यार्ता व्रणपीडिताः ।प्रतिमानैश्च कुम्भैश्च पेतुरुर्व्यां महाहवे ॥१८॥
transliteration: apare prādravannāgāḥ śalyārtā vraṇapīḍitāḥ ,pratimānaiśca kumbhaiśca petururvyāṁ mahāhave.
english: Others, distressed by spears and afflicted by wounds, fled. And (some elephants), with their foreheads and frontal globes (temples) shattered, fell upon the earth in the great battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 36 Verse 18. Others, distressed by spears and afflicted by wounds, fled. And (some elephants), with their foreheads and frontal globes (temples) shattered, fell upon the earth in the great battle.

sanskrit: निषेदुः सिंहवच्चान्ये नदन्तो भैरवान्रवान् ।मम्लुश्च बहवो राजंश्चुकूजुश्चापरे तथा ॥१९॥
transliteration: niṣeduḥ siṁhavaccānye nadanto bhairavānravān ,mamluśca bahavo rājaṁścukūjuścāpare tathā.
english: O King, others collapsed like (wounded) lions, uttering terrifying roars. And many became faint, while still others shrieked out similarly.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 36 Verse 19. O King, others collapsed like (wounded) lions, uttering terrifying roars. And many became faint, while still others shrieked out similarly.

sanskrit: हयाश्च निहता बाणैः स्वर्णभाण्डपरिच्छदाः ।निषेदुश्चैव मम्लुश्च बभ्रमुश्च दिशो दश ॥२०॥
transliteration: hayāśca nihatā bāṇaiḥ svarṇabhāṇḍaparicchadāḥ ,niṣeduścaiva mamluśca babhramuśca diśo daśa.
english: The horses, adorned with golden ornaments, were struck down by arrows. Some sat down, some became faint, and others reeled in the ten directions.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 36 Verse 20. The horses, adorned with golden ornaments, were struck down by arrows. Some sat down, some became faint, and others reeled in the ten directions.

sanskrit: अपरे कृष्यमाणाश्च विवेष्टन्तो महीतले ।भावान्बहुविधांश्चक्रुस्ताडिताः शरतोमरैः ॥२१॥
transliteration: apare kṛṣyamāṇāśca viveṣṭanto mahītale ,bhāvānbahuvidhāṁścakrustāḍitāḥ śaratomaraiḥ.
english: Others, being dragged and writhing on the ground, showed various conditions, having been struck by arrows and javelins.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 36 Verse 21. Others, being dragged and writhing on the ground, showed various conditions, having been struck by arrows and javelins.

sanskrit: नरास्तु निहता भूमौ कूजन्तस्तत्र मारिष ।दृष्ट्वा च बान्धवानन्ये पितॄनन्ये पितामहान् ॥२२॥
transliteration: narāstu nihatā bhūmau kūjantastatra māriṣa ,dṛṣṭvā ca bāndhavānanye pitṝnanye pitāmahān.
english: O venerable one, the men struck down on the ground were crying out there. And some, having seen their relatives, others their fathers, and still others their grandfathers...
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 36 Verse 22. O venerable one, the men struck down on the ground were crying out there. And some, having seen their relatives, others their fathers, and still others their grandfathers...

sanskrit: धावमानान्परांश्चैव दृष्ट्वान्ये तत्र भारत ।गोत्रनामानि ख्यातानि शशंसुरितरेतरम् ॥२३॥
transliteration: dhāvamānānparāṁścaiva dṛṣṭvānye tatra bhārata ,gotranāmāni khyātāni śaśaṁsuritaretaram.
english: O Bhārata, others there, having seen their fleeing enemies, cried out their famous family names to one another.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 36 Verse 23. O Bhārata, others there, having seen their fleeing enemies, cried out their famous family names to one another.

sanskrit: तेषां छिन्ना महाराज भुजाः कनकभूषणाः ।उद्वेष्टन्ते विवेष्टन्ते पतन्ते चोत्पतन्ति च ॥२४॥
transliteration: teṣāṁ chinnā mahārāja bhujāḥ kanakabhūṣaṇāḥ ,udveṣṭante viveṣṭante patante cotpatanti ca.
english: O great king, their severed arms, adorned with gold ornaments, were writhing, twisting, falling, and even leaping up.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 36 Verse 24. O great king, their severed arms, adorned with gold ornaments, were writhing, twisting, falling, and even leaping up.

sanskrit: निपतन्ति तथा भूमौ स्फुरन्ति च सहस्रशः ।वेगांश्चान्ये रणे चक्रुः स्फुरन्त इव पन्नगाः ॥२५॥
transliteration: nipatanti tathā bhūmau sphuranti ca sahasraśaḥ ,vegāṁścānye raṇe cakruḥ sphuranta iva pannagāḥ.
english: Similarly, thousands of them fall to the ground and twitch. Others in battle made swift movements, like writhing snakes.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 36 Verse 25. Similarly, thousands of them fall to the ground and twitch. Others in battle made swift movements, like writhing snakes.

sanskrit: ते भुजा भोगिभोगाभाश्चन्दनाक्ता विशां पते ।लोहितार्द्रा भृशं रेजुस्तपनीयध्वजा इव ॥२६॥
transliteration: te bhujā bhogibhogābhāścandanāktā viśāṁ pate ,lohitārdrā bhṛśaṁ rejustapanīyadhvajā iva.
english: O lord of the people, those arms, resembling the bodies of serpents, smeared with sandalwood paste, and drenched with blood, shone brilliantly like banners of refined gold.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 36 Verse 26. O lord of the people, those arms, resembling the bodies of serpents, smeared with sandalwood paste, and drenched with blood, shone brilliantly like banners of refined gold.

sanskrit: वर्तमाने तथा घोरे संकुले सर्वतोदिशम् ।अविज्ञाताः स्म युध्यन्ते विनिघ्नन्तः परस्परम् ॥२७॥
transliteration: vartamāne tathā ghore saṁkule sarvatodiśam ,avijñātāḥ sma yudhyante vinighnantaḥ parasparam.
english: While such a terrible and chaotic battle raged in all directions, they fought, unrecognized, killing each other.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 36 Verse 27. While such a terrible and chaotic battle raged in all directions, they fought, unrecognized, killing each other.

sanskrit: ता नद्यो घोररूपाश्च नयन्त्यो यमसादनम् ।अवगाढा मज्जयन्त्यः क्षत्रस्याजनयन्भयम् ॥३२॥
transliteration: tā nadyo ghorarūpāśca nayantyo yamasādanam ,avagāḍhā majjayantyaḥ kṣatrasyājanayanbhayam.
english: Those terrifying rivers, leading to the abode of Yama, submerging all who entered them, generated great fear among the warriors.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 36 Verse 32. Those terrifying rivers, leading to the abode of Yama, submerging all who entered them, generated great fear among the warriors.

sanskrit: क्रव्यादानां नरव्याघ्र नर्दतां तत्र तत्र ह ।घोरमायोधनं जज्ञे प्रेतराजपुरोपमम् ॥३३॥
transliteration: kravyādānāṁ naravyāghra nardatāṁ tatra tatra ha ,ghoramāyodhanaṁ jajñe pretarājapuropamam.
english: O tiger among men, there, amidst the roaring flesh-eaters, a terrible battle arose, resembling the city of the lord of spirits.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 36 Verse 33. O tiger among men, there, amidst the roaring flesh-eaters, a terrible battle arose, resembling the city of the lord of spirits.

sanskrit: उत्थितान्यगणेयानि कबन्धानि समन्ततः ।नृत्यन्ति वै भूतगणाः संतृप्ता मांसशोणितैः ॥३४॥
transliteration: utthitānyagaṇeyāni kabandhāni samantataḥ ,nṛtyanti vai bhūtagaṇāḥ saṁtṛptā māṁsaśoṇitaiḥ.
english: Innumerable headless trunks arose all around, and indeed, hosts of spirits danced, fully sated with flesh and blood.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 36 Verse 34. Innumerable headless trunks arose all around, and indeed, hosts of spirits danced, fully sated with flesh and blood.

sanskrit: पीत्वा च शोणितं तत्र वसां पीत्वा च भारत ।मेदोमज्जावसातृप्तास्तृप्ता मांसस्य चैव हि ।धावमानाश्च दृश्यन्ते काकगृध्रबलास्तथा ॥३५॥
transliteration: pītvā ca śoṇitaṁ tatra vasāṁ pītvā ca bhārata ,medomajjāvasātṛptāstṛptā māṁsasya caiva hi ,dhāvamānāśca dṛśyante kākagṛdhrabalāstathā.
english: O Bhārata, having drunk blood there and also having drunk fat, they were sated with marrow, fat, and grease, and indeed thoroughly satisfied with flesh; hosts of crows and vultures were also seen running about.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 36 Verse 35. O Bhārata, having drunk blood there and also having drunk fat, they were sated with marrow, fat, and grease, and indeed thoroughly satisfied with flesh; hosts of crows and vultures were also seen running about.

sanskrit: शूरास्तु समरे राजन्भयं त्यक्त्वा सुदुस्त्यजम् ।योधव्रतसमाख्याताश्चक्रुः कर्माण्यभीतवत् ॥३६॥
transliteration: śūrāstu samare rājanbhayaṁ tyaktvā sudustyajam ,yodhavratasamākhyātāścakruḥ karmāṇyabhītavat.
english: O King, the brave warriors, having abandoned the fear that is so very difficult to overcome, and known for their adherence to the warrior's code, performed their deeds (karma) fearlessly.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 36 Verse 36. O King, the brave warriors, having abandoned the fear that is so very difficult to overcome, and known for their adherence to the warrior's code, performed their deeds (karma) fearlessly.

sanskrit: शरशक्तिसमाकीर्णे क्रव्यादगणसंकुले ।व्यचरन्त गणैः शूराः ख्यापयन्तः स्वपौरुषम् ॥३७॥
transliteration: śaraśaktisamākīrṇe kravyādagaṇasaṁkule ,vyacaranta gaṇaiḥ śūrāḥ khyāpayantaḥ svapauruṣam.
english: The brave warriors, amidst a battlefield strewn with arrows and spears and teeming with hosts of flesh-eaters, moved about with their contingents, making known their own valor.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 36 Verse 37. The brave warriors, amidst a battlefield strewn with arrows and spears and teeming with hosts of flesh-eaters, moved about with their contingents, making known their own valor.

sanskrit: अन्योन्यं श्रावयन्ति स्म नामगोत्राणि भारत ।पितृनामानि च रणे गोत्रनामानि चाभितः ॥३८॥
transliteration: anyonyaṁ śrāvayanti sma nāmagotrāṇi bhārata ,pitṛnāmāni ca raṇe gotranāmāni cābhitaḥ.
english: O Bhārata, in battle, they called out to each other their names and lineages, proclaiming their fathers' names and clan names all around.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 36 Verse 38. O Bhārata, in battle, they called out to each other their names and lineages, proclaiming their fathers' names and clan names all around.

sanskrit: श्रावयन्तो हि बहवस्तत्र योधा विशां पते ।अन्योन्यमवमृद्नन्तः शक्तितोमरपट्टिशैः ॥३९॥
transliteration: śrāvayanto hi bahavastatra yodhā viśāṁ pate ,anyonyamavamṛdnantaḥ śaktitomarapaṭṭiśaiḥ.
english: Indeed, O lord of the people, many warriors there, proclaiming their names and lineages, crushed each other with spears, javelins, and broadswords.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 36 Verse 39. Indeed, O lord of the people, many warriors there, proclaiming their names and lineages, crushed each other with spears, javelins, and broadswords.

sanskrit: वर्तमाने तदा युद्धे घोररूपे सुदारुणे ।व्यषीदत्कौरवी सेना भिन्ना नौरिव सागरे ॥४०॥
transliteration: vartamāne tadā yuddhe ghorarūpe sudāruṇe ,vyaṣīdatkauravī senā bhinnā nauriva sāgare.
english: At that time, while the extremely dreadful and terrible battle was ongoing, the Kaurava army became dispirited, much like a broken boat sinking in the ocean.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 36 Verse 40. At that time, while the extremely dreadful and terrible battle was ongoing, the Kaurava army became dispirited, much like a broken boat sinking in the ocean.

sanskrit: संजय उवाच ।वर्तमाने तदा युद्धे क्षत्रियाणां निमज्जने ।गाण्डीवस्य महान्घोषः शुश्रुवे युधि मारिष ॥१॥
transliteration: saṁjaya uvāca ,vartamāne tadā yuddhe kṣatriyāṇāṁ nimajjane ,gāṇḍīvasya mahānghoṣaḥ śuśruve yudhi māriṣa.
english: Sañjaya said: O revered one (māriṣa), at that time, as the battle proceeded and the kṣatriyas were being overwhelmed, the great roar of the Gāṇḍīva bow was heard in the conflict.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 37 Verse 1. Sañjaya said: O revered one (māriṣa), at that time, as the battle proceeded and the kṣatriyas were being overwhelmed, the great roar of the Gāṇḍīva bow was heard in the conflict.

sanskrit: संशप्तकानां कदनमकरोद्यत्र पाण्डवः ।कोसलानां तथा राजन्नारायणबलस्य च ॥२॥
transliteration: saṁśaptakānāṁ kadanamakarodyatra pāṇḍavaḥ ,kosalānāṁ tathā rājannārāyaṇabalasya ca.
english: O king (rājan), it was there that the Pāṇḍava (Arjuna) wreaked havoc upon the Saṃśaptakas, as well as the Kosalas and Nārāyaṇa's army.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 37 Verse 2. O king (rājan), it was there that the Pāṇḍava (Arjuna) wreaked havoc upon the Saṃśaptakas, as well as the Kosalas and Nārāyaṇa's army.

sanskrit: संशप्तकास्तु समरे शरवृष्टिं समन्ततः ।अपातयन्पार्थमूर्ध्नि जयगृद्धाः प्रमन्यवः ॥३॥
transliteration: saṁśaptakāstu samare śaravṛṣṭiṁ samantataḥ ,apātayanpārthamūrdhni jayagṛddhāḥ pramanyavaḥ.
english: However, the Saṃśaptakas, being eager for victory and filled with great anger, showered a rain of arrows from all sides upon Arjuna's head in the battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 37 Verse 3. However, the Saṃśaptakas, being eager for victory and filled with great anger, showered a rain of arrows from all sides upon Arjuna's head in the battle.

sanskrit: तां वृष्टिं सहसा राजंस्तरसा धारयन्प्रभुः ।व्यगाहत रणे पार्थो विनिघ्नन्रथिनां वरः ॥४॥
transliteration: tāṁ vṛṣṭiṁ sahasā rājaṁstarasā dhārayanprabhuḥ ,vyagāhata raṇe pārtho vinighnanrathināṁ varaḥ.
english: O King, the mighty Pārtha, the foremost of charioteers, swiftly and forcefully withstood that rain (of arrows) and plunged into the battle, striking down his adversaries.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 37 Verse 4. O King, the mighty Pārtha, the foremost of charioteers, swiftly and forcefully withstood that rain (of arrows) and plunged into the battle, striking down his adversaries.

sanskrit: निगृह्य तु रथानीकं कङ्कपत्रैः शिलाशितैः ।आससाद रणे पार्थः सुशर्माणं महारथम् ॥५॥
transliteration: nigṛhya tu rathānīkaṁ kaṅkapatraiḥ śilāśitaiḥ ,āsasāda raṇe pārthaḥ suśarmāṇaṁ mahāratham.
english: Indeed, in battle, Pārtha, having restrained the chariot army with arrows sharpened on stone and fitted with vulture feathers, advanced upon Suśarman, the great charioteer.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 37 Verse 5. Indeed, in battle, Pārtha, having restrained the chariot army with arrows sharpened on stone and fitted with vulture feathers, advanced upon Suśarman, the great charioteer.

sanskrit: स तस्य शरवर्षाणि ववर्ष रथिनां वरः ।तथा संशप्तकाश्चैव पार्थस्य समरे स्थिताः ॥६॥
transliteration: sa tasya śaravarṣāṇi vavarṣa rathināṁ varaḥ ,tathā saṁśaptakāścaiva pārthasya samare sthitāḥ.
english: He, the foremost of charioteers (Suśarman), rained showers of arrows upon him (Arjuna). And similarly, the Saṃśaptakas also stood arrayed in battle against Pārtha.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 37 Verse 6. He, the foremost of charioteers (Suśarman), rained showers of arrows upon him (Arjuna). And similarly, the Saṃśaptakas also stood arrayed in battle against Pārtha.

sanskrit: सुशर्मा तु ततः पार्थं विद्ध्वा नवभिराशुगैः ।जनार्दनं त्रिभिर्बाणैरभ्यहन्दक्षिणे भुजे ।ततोऽपरेण भल्लेन केतुं विव्याध मारिष ॥७॥
transliteration: suśarmā tu tataḥ pārthaṁ viddhvā navabhirāśugaiḥ ,janārdanaṁ tribhirbāṇairabhyahandakṣiṇe bhuje ,tato'pareṇa bhallena ketuṁ vivyādha māriṣa.
english: But then Suśarman, having pierced Pārtha (Arjuna) with nine swift arrows, struck Janārdana (Kṛṣṇa) with three arrows on his right arm. Then, O respected one, he pierced the banner with another broad-headed arrow.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 37 Verse 7. But then Suśarman, having pierced Pārtha (Arjuna) with nine swift arrows, struck Janārdana (Kṛṣṇa) with three arrows on his right arm. Then, O respected one, he pierced the banner with another broad-headed arrow.

sanskrit: स वानरवरो राजन्विश्वकर्मकृतो महान् ।ननाद सुमहन्नादं भीषयन्वै ननर्द च ॥८॥
transliteration: sa vānaravaro rājanviśvakarmakṛto mahān ,nanāda sumahannādaṁ bhīṣayanvai nanarda ca.
english: O King, that great chief of monkeys, crafted by Viśvakarmā, roared a very mighty roar and indeed thundered, terrifying (everyone).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 37 Verse 8. O King, that great chief of monkeys, crafted by Viśvakarmā, roared a very mighty roar and indeed thundered, terrifying (everyone).

sanskrit: कपेस्तु निनदं श्रुत्वा संत्रस्ता तव वाहिनी ।भयं विपुलमादाय निश्चेष्टा समपद्यत ॥९॥
transliteration: kapestu ninadaṁ śrutvā saṁtrastā tava vāhinī ,bhayaṁ vipulamādāya niśceṣṭā samapadyata.
english: But hearing the monkey's roar, your army became terrified and, seized by immense fear, became completely motionless.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 37 Verse 9. But hearing the monkey's roar, your army became terrified and, seized by immense fear, became completely motionless.

sanskrit: ततः सा शुशुभे सेना निश्चेष्टावस्थिता नृप ।नानापुष्पसमाकीर्णं यथा चैत्ररथं वनम् ॥१०॥
transliteration: tataḥ sā śuśubhe senā niśceṣṭāvasthitā nṛpa ,nānāpuṣpasamākīrṇaṁ yathā caitrarathaṁ vanam.
english: Then, O King, that army, standing motionless, appeared beautiful, like the Caitraratha forest strewn with various flowers.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 37 Verse 10. Then, O King, that army, standing motionless, appeared beautiful, like the Caitraratha forest strewn with various flowers.

sanskrit: प्रतिलभ्य ततः संज्ञां योधास्ते कुरुसत्तम ।अर्जुनं सिषिचुर्बाणैः पर्वतं जलदा इव ।परिवव्रुस्तदा सर्वे पाण्डवस्य महारथम् ॥११॥
transliteration: pratilabhya tataḥ saṁjñāṁ yodhāste kurusattama ,arjunaṁ siṣicurbāṇaiḥ parvataṁ jaladā iva ,parivavrustadā sarve pāṇḍavasya mahāratham.
english: Then, O best of Kurus, those warriors, having regained consciousness, showered Arjuna with arrows, just as clouds shower a mountain. At that time, all of them surrounded the Pāṇḍava's great chariot.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 37 Verse 11. Then, O best of Kurus, those warriors, having regained consciousness, showered Arjuna with arrows, just as clouds shower a mountain. At that time, all of them surrounded the Pāṇḍava's great chariot.

sanskrit: ते हयान्रथचक्रे च रथेषाश्चापि भारत ।निगृह्य बलवत्तूर्णं सिंहनादमथानदन् ॥१२॥
transliteration: te hayānrathacakre ca ratheṣāścāpi bhārata ,nigṛhya balavattūrṇaṁ siṁhanādamathānadan.
english: O descendant of Bharata, they, having powerfully and quickly seized the horses, the chariot wheels, and the chariot shafts, then let out a lion's roar.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 37 Verse 12. O descendant of Bharata, they, having powerfully and quickly seized the horses, the chariot wheels, and the chariot shafts, then let out a lion's roar.

sanskrit: अपरे जगृहुश्चैव केशवस्य महाभुजौ ।पार्थमन्ये महाराज रथस्थं जगृहुर्मुदा ॥१३॥
transliteration: apare jagṛhuścaiva keśavasya mahābhujau ,pārthamanye mahārāja rathasthaṁ jagṛhurmudā.
english: O great king, some others seized Keśava's mighty arms, and still others happily seized Pārtha, who was standing in the chariot.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 37 Verse 13. O great king, some others seized Keśava's mighty arms, and still others happily seized Pārtha, who was standing in the chariot.

sanskrit: केशवस्तु तदा बाहू विधुन्वन्रणमूर्धनि ।पातयामास तान्सर्वान्दुष्टहस्तीव हस्तिनः ॥१४॥
transliteration: keśavastu tadā bāhū vidhunvanraṇamūrdhani ,pātayāmāsa tānsarvānduṣṭahastīva hastinaḥ.
english: But then Keśava, shaking his arms amidst the battle, threw all of them down, just as a rogue elephant throws down other elephants.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 37 Verse 14. But then Keśava, shaking his arms amidst the battle, threw all of them down, just as a rogue elephant throws down other elephants.

sanskrit: ततः क्रुद्धो रणे पार्थः संवृतस्तैर्महारथैः ।निगृहीतं रथं दृष्ट्वा केशवं चाप्यभिद्रुतम् ।रथारूढांश्च सुबहून्पदातींश्चाप्यपातयत् ॥१५॥
transliteration: tataḥ kruddho raṇe pārthaḥ saṁvṛtastairmahārathaiḥ ,nigṛhītaṁ rathaṁ dṛṣṭvā keśavaṁ cāpyabhidrutam ,rathārūḍhāṁśca subahūnpadātīṁścāpyapātayat.
english: Then Pārtha, angered in the battle and surrounded by those great chariot-warriors, seeing his chariot seized and Keśava also attacked, threw down very many chariot-riders and also foot soldiers.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 37 Verse 15. Then Pārtha, angered in the battle and surrounded by those great chariot-warriors, seeing his chariot seized and Keśava also attacked, threw down very many chariot-riders and also foot soldiers.

sanskrit: आसन्नांश्च ततो योधाञ्शरैरासन्नयोधिभिः ।च्यावयामास समरे केशवं चेदमब्रवीत् ॥१६॥
transliteration: āsannāṁśca tato yodhāñśarairāsannayodhibhiḥ ,cyāvayāmāsa samare keśavaṁ cedamabravīt.
english: Then, with arrows effective for close-quarters fighting, he made the nearby warriors fall in battle and said this to Keśava (Krishna).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 37 Verse 16. Then, with arrows effective for close-quarters fighting, he made the nearby warriors fall in battle and said this to Keśava (Krishna).

sanskrit: पश्य कृष्ण महाबाहो संशप्तकगणान्मया ।कुर्वाणान्दारुणं कर्म वध्यमानान्सहस्रशः ॥१७॥
transliteration: paśya kṛṣṇa mahābāho saṁśaptakagaṇānmayā ,kurvāṇāndāruṇaṁ karma vadhyamānānsahasraśaḥ.
english: Behold, O mighty-armed Krishna, these hosts of Saṃśaptaka warriors, performing dreadful deeds, whom I am slaying by thousands.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 37 Verse 17. Behold, O mighty-armed Krishna, these hosts of Saṃśaptaka warriors, performing dreadful deeds, whom I am slaying by thousands.

sanskrit: रथबन्धमिमं घोरं पृथिव्यां नास्ति कश्चन ।यः सहेत पुमाँल्लोके मदन्यो यदुपुंगव ॥१८॥
transliteration: rathabandhamimaṁ ghoraṁ pṛthivyāṁ nāsti kaścana ,yaḥ saheta pumāँlloke madanyo yadupuṁgava.
english: O best of the Yadus, there is no one on earth, no man in the world apart from me, who could endure this dreadful entanglement of the chariot.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 37 Verse 18. O best of the Yadus, there is no one on earth, no man in the world apart from me, who could endure this dreadful entanglement of the chariot.

sanskrit: इत्येवमुक्त्वा बीभत्सुर्देवदत्तमथाधमत् ।पाञ्चजन्यं च कृष्णोऽपि पूरयन्निव रोदसी ॥१९॥
transliteration: ityevamuktvā bībhatsurdevadattamathādhamat ,pāñcajanyaṁ ca kṛṣṇo'pi pūrayanniva rodasī.
english: Having spoken thus, Bībhatsu (Arjuna) then blew his Devadatta conch, and Krishna, too, blew his Pāñcajanya conch, as if filling both heaven and earth.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 37 Verse 19. Having spoken thus, Bībhatsu (Arjuna) then blew his Devadatta conch, and Krishna, too, blew his Pāñcajanya conch, as if filling both heaven and earth.

sanskrit: तं तु शङ्खस्वनं श्रुत्वा संशप्तकवरूथिनी ।संचचाल महाराज वित्रस्ता चाभवद्भृशम् ॥२०॥
transliteration: taṁ tu śaṅkhasvanaṁ śrutvā saṁśaptakavarūthinī ,saṁcacāla mahārāja vitrastā cābhavadbhṛśam.
english: O great king, hearing that conch sound, the army of the Saṃśaptakas began to move and became exceedingly terrified.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 37 Verse 20. O great king, hearing that conch sound, the army of the Saṃśaptakas began to move and became exceedingly terrified.

sanskrit: पदबन्धं ततश्चक्रे पाण्डवः परवीरहा ।नागमस्त्रं महाराज संप्रोदीर्य मुहुर्मुहुः ॥२१॥
transliteration: padabandhaṁ tataścakre pāṇḍavaḥ paravīrahā ,nāgamastraṁ mahārāja saṁprodīrya muhurmuhuḥ.
english: O great king, then the son of Pāṇḍu, the slayer of enemy heroes, repeatedly invoked and employed the Nāgāstra (nāga weapon), which binds the feet.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 37 Verse 21. O great king, then the son of Pāṇḍu, the slayer of enemy heroes, repeatedly invoked and employed the Nāgāstra (nāga weapon), which binds the feet.

sanskrit: यानुद्दिश्य रणे पार्थः पदबन्धं चकार ह ।ते बद्धाः पदबन्धेन पाण्डवेन महात्मना ।निश्चेष्टा अभवन्राजन्नश्मसारमया इव ॥२२॥
transliteration: yānuddiśya raṇe pārthaḥ padabandhaṁ cakāra ha ,te baddhāḥ padabandhena pāṇḍavena mahātmanā ,niśceṣṭā abhavanrājannaśmasāramayā iva.
english: O king, those whom Pārtha targeted in battle and against whom he indeed employed the foot-binding, became bound by the foot-binding of the great-souled one (mahātman), the son of Pāṇḍu. They became motionless, as if they were made of iron.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 37 Verse 22. O king, those whom Pārtha targeted in battle and against whom he indeed employed the foot-binding, became bound by the foot-binding of the great-souled one (mahātman), the son of Pāṇḍu. They became motionless, as if they were made of iron.

sanskrit: निश्चेष्टांस्तु ततो योधानवधीत्पाण्डुनन्दनः ।यथेन्द्रः समरे दैत्यांस्तारकस्य वधे पुरा ॥२३॥
transliteration: niśceṣṭāṁstu tato yodhānavadhītpāṇḍunandanaḥ ,yathendraḥ samare daityāṁstārakasya vadhe purā.
english: Then, the son of Pāṇḍu slew those motionless warriors, just as Indra, in ancient times, killed the demons (daitya) in battle during the slaying of Tāraka.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 37 Verse 23. Then, the son of Pāṇḍu slew those motionless warriors, just as Indra, in ancient times, killed the demons (daitya) in battle during the slaying of Tāraka.

sanskrit: ते वध्यमानाः समरे मुमुचुस्तं रथोत्तमम् ।आयुधानि च सर्वाणि विस्रष्टुमुपचक्रमुः ॥२४॥
transliteration: te vadhyamānāḥ samare mumucustaṁ rathottamam ,āyudhāni ca sarvāṇi visraṣṭumupacakramuḥ.
english: As they were being struck in battle, they abandoned that excellent chariot and began to drop all their weapons.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 37 Verse 24. As they were being struck in battle, they abandoned that excellent chariot and began to drop all their weapons.

sanskrit: ततः सुशर्मा राजेन्द्र गृहीतां वीक्ष्य वाहिनीम् ।सौपर्णमस्त्रं त्वरितः प्रादुश्चक्रे महारथः ॥२५॥
transliteration: tataḥ suśarmā rājendra gṛhītāṁ vīkṣya vāhinīm ,sauparṇamastraṁ tvaritaḥ prāduścakre mahārathaḥ.
english: Then, O king, Suśarman, the great warrior, quickly observing his army seized, deployed the Suparṇa (Garuda) missile.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 37 Verse 25. Then, O king, Suśarman, the great warrior, quickly observing his army seized, deployed the Suparṇa (Garuda) missile.

sanskrit: ततः सुपर्णाः संपेतुर्भक्षयन्तो भुजंगमान् ।ते वै विदुद्रुवुर्नागा दृष्ट्वा तान्खचरान्नृप ॥२६॥
transliteration: tataḥ suparṇāḥ saṁpeturbhakṣayanto bhujaṁgamān ,te vai vidudruvurnāgā dṛṣṭvā tānkhacarānnṛpa.
english: Then the Suparṇas (Garudas) rushed forth, devouring the serpents. O king, indeed, having seen those sky-dwellers, the serpents (nāgāḥ) fled away.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 37 Verse 26. Then the Suparṇas (Garudas) rushed forth, devouring the serpents. O king, indeed, having seen those sky-dwellers, the serpents (nāgāḥ) fled away.

sanskrit: बभौ बलं तद्विमुक्तं पदबन्धाद्विशां पते ।मेघवृन्दाद्यथा मुक्तो भास्करस्तापयन्प्रजाः ॥२७॥
transliteration: babhau balaṁ tadvimuktaṁ padabandhādviśāṁ pate ,meghavṛndādyathā mukto bhāskarastāpayanprajāḥ.
english: O lord of the people, that army, liberated from its entanglement, shone just as the sun (bhāskaraḥ), released from a cluster of clouds, heats up the people (prajāḥ).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 37 Verse 27. O lord of the people, that army, liberated from its entanglement, shone just as the sun (bhāskaraḥ), released from a cluster of clouds, heats up the people (prajāḥ).

sanskrit: विप्रमुक्तास्तु ते योधाः फल्गुनस्य रथं प्रति ।ससृजुर्बाणसंघांश्च शस्त्रसंघांश्च मारिष ॥२८॥
transliteration: vipramuktāstu te yodhāḥ phalgunasya rathaṁ prati ,sasṛjurbāṇasaṁghāṁśca śastrasaṁghāṁśca māriṣa.
english: O venerable one, those warriors, having been released (from the effect of Arjuna's weapon), discharged volleys of arrows and showers of weapons towards Arjuna's chariot.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 37 Verse 28. O venerable one, those warriors, having been released (from the effect of Arjuna's weapon), discharged volleys of arrows and showers of weapons towards Arjuna's chariot.

sanskrit: तां महास्त्रमयीं वृष्टिं संछिद्य शरवृष्टिभिः ।व्यवातिष्ठत्ततो योधान्वासविः परवीरहा ॥२९॥
transliteration: tāṁ mahāstramayīṁ vṛṣṭiṁ saṁchidya śaravṛṣṭibhiḥ ,vyavātiṣṭhattato yodhānvāsaviḥ paravīrahā.
english: Having cut down that shower of powerful (mahāstra) weapons with volleys of his own arrows, Vāsavi, the slayer of enemy heroes, then resisted those warriors.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 37 Verse 29. Having cut down that shower of powerful (mahāstra) weapons with volleys of his own arrows, Vāsavi, the slayer of enemy heroes, then resisted those warriors.

sanskrit: सुशर्मा तु ततो राजन्बाणेनानतपर्वणा ।अर्जुनं हृदये विद्ध्वा विव्याधान्यैस्त्रिभिः शरैः ।स गाढविद्धो व्यथितो रथोपस्थ उपाविशत् ॥३०॥
transliteration: suśarmā tu tato rājanbāṇenānataparvaṇā ,arjunaṁ hṛdaye viddhvā vivyādhānyaistribhiḥ śaraiḥ ,sa gāḍhaviddho vyathito rathopastha upāviśat.
english: But then, O King, Suśarmā, having pierced Arjuna in the heart with an arrow having a curved shaft, also pierced him with three other arrows. Deeply wounded and distressed, he sat down in the front part of his chariot.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 37 Verse 30. But then, O King, Suśarmā, having pierced Arjuna in the heart with an arrow having a curved shaft, also pierced him with three other arrows. Deeply wounded and distressed, he sat down in the front part of his chariot.

sanskrit: प्रतिलभ्य ततः संज्ञां श्वेताश्वः कृष्णसारथिः ।ऐन्द्रमस्त्रममेयात्मा प्रादुश्चक्रे त्वरान्वितः ।ततो बाणसहस्राणि समुत्पन्नानि मारिष ॥३१॥
transliteration: pratilabhya tataḥ saṁjñāṁ śvetāśvaḥ kṛṣṇasārathiḥ ,aindramastramameyātmā prāduścakre tvarānvitaḥ ,tato bāṇasahasrāṇi samutpannāni māriṣa.
english: O venerable one, having then regained his consciousness, Arjuna (Śvetāśva), whose charioteer was Krishna, being of immeasurable spirit (ātman), swiftly manifested Indra's divine weapon (astra). Thereupon, thousands of arrows arose.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 37 Verse 31. O venerable one, having then regained his consciousness, Arjuna (Śvetāśva), whose charioteer was Krishna, being of immeasurable spirit (ātman), swiftly manifested Indra's divine weapon (astra). Thereupon, thousands of arrows arose.

sanskrit: सर्वदिक्षु व्यदृश्यन्त सूदयन्तो नृप द्विपान् ।हयान्रथांश्च समरे शस्त्रैः शतसहस्रशः ॥३२॥
transliteration: sarvadikṣu vyadṛśyanta sūdayanto nṛpa dvipān ,hayānrathāṁśca samare śastraiḥ śatasahasraśaḥ.
english: O King, in every direction, they were seen slaughtering hundreds of thousands of elephants, horses, and chariots in battle with their weapons.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 37 Verse 32. O King, in every direction, they were seen slaughtering hundreds of thousands of elephants, horses, and chariots in battle with their weapons.

sanskrit: वध्यमाने ततः सैन्ये विपुला भीः समाविशत् ।संशप्तकगणानां च गोपालानां च भारत ।न हि कश्चित्पुमांस्तत्र योऽर्जुनं प्रत्ययुध्यत ॥३३॥
transliteration: vadhyamāne tataḥ sainye vipulā bhīḥ samāviśat ,saṁśaptakagaṇānāṁ ca gopālānāṁ ca bhārata ,na hi kaścitpumāṁstatra yo'rjunaṁ pratyayudhyata.
english: O Bhārata, as that army was being slaughtered, an immense fear gripped the divisions of the Saṃśaptakas and the Gopālas. Indeed, there was no man among them who would fight back against Arjuna.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 37 Verse 33. O Bhārata, as that army was being slaughtered, an immense fear gripped the divisions of the Saṃśaptakas and the Gopālas. Indeed, there was no man among them who would fight back against Arjuna.

sanskrit: पश्यतां तत्र वीराणामहन्यत महद्बलम् ।हन्यमानमपश्यंश्च निश्चेष्टाः स्म पराक्रमे ॥३४॥
transliteration: paśyatāṁ tatra vīrāṇāmahanyata mahadbalam ,hanyamānamapaśyaṁśca niśceṣṭāḥ sma parākrame.
english: There, as the heroes watched, a great force was being slaughtered. And seeing it being cut down, they remained motionless in their effort.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 37 Verse 34. There, as the heroes watched, a great force was being slaughtered. And seeing it being cut down, they remained motionless in their effort.

sanskrit: अयुतं तत्र योधानां हत्वा पाण्डुसुतो रणे ।व्यभ्राजत रणे राजन्विधूमोऽग्निरिव ज्वलन् ॥३५॥
transliteration: ayutaṁ tatra yodhānāṁ hatvā pāṇḍusuto raṇe ,vyabhrājata raṇe rājanvidhūmo'gniriva jvalan.
english: O King, having slain ten thousand warriors in that battle, the son of Pāṇḍu (Arjuna) shone brightly in the fray, blazing like a smokeless fire.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 37 Verse 35. O King, having slain ten thousand warriors in that battle, the son of Pāṇḍu (Arjuna) shone brightly in the fray, blazing like a smokeless fire.

sanskrit: चतुर्दश सहस्राणि यानि शिष्टानि भारत ।रथानामयुतं चैव त्रिसाहस्राश्च दन्तिनः ॥३६॥
transliteration: caturdaśa sahasrāṇi yāni śiṣṭāni bhārata ,rathānāmayutaṁ caiva trisāhasrāśca dantinaḥ.
english: O Bhārata, fourteen thousand (warriors) remained, and indeed ten thousand chariots and three thousand elephants.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 37 Verse 36. O Bhārata, fourteen thousand (warriors) remained, and indeed ten thousand chariots and three thousand elephants.

sanskrit: ततः संशप्तका भूयः परिवव्रुर्धनंजयम् ।मर्तव्यमिति निश्चित्य जयं वापि निवर्तनम् ॥३७॥
transliteration: tataḥ saṁśaptakā bhūyaḥ parivavrurdhanaṁjayam ,martavyamiti niścitya jayaṁ vāpi nivartanam.
english: Then the Saṃśaptakas again surrounded Dhanañjaya, having resolved, "Either victory or death (no turning back)."
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 37 Verse 37. Then the Saṃśaptakas again surrounded Dhanañjaya, having resolved, "Either victory or death (no turning back)."

sanskrit: तत्र युद्धं महद्ध्यासीत्तावकानां विशां पते ।शूरेण बलिना सार्धं पाण्डवेन किरीटिना ॥३८॥
transliteration: tatra yuddhaṁ mahaddhyāsīttāvakānāṁ viśāṁ pate ,śūreṇa balinā sārdhaṁ pāṇḍavena kirīṭinā.
english: O lord of the people, there indeed was a great battle for your warriors, together with the heroic and mighty Pāṇḍava (Arjuna), the crowned one.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 37 Verse 38. O lord of the people, there indeed was a great battle for your warriors, together with the heroic and mighty Pāṇḍava (Arjuna), the crowned one.

sanskrit: संजय उवाच ।कृतवर्मा कृपो द्रौणिः सूतपुत्रश्च मारिष ।उलूकः सौबलश्चैव राजा च सह सोदरैः ॥१॥
transliteration: saṁjaya uvāca ,kṛtavarmā kṛpo drauṇiḥ sūtaputraśca māriṣa ,ulūkaḥ saubalaścaiva rājā ca saha sodaraiḥ.
english: Saṃjaya said: "O venerable one, Kṛtavarmā, Kṛpa, Drauṇi, and Sūtaputra (Karṇa); Ulūka, and indeed Saubala (Śakuni); and the King (Duryodhana) along with his brothers (were among them)."
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 38 Verse 1. Saṃjaya said: "O venerable one, Kṛtavarmā, Kṛpa, Drauṇi, and Sūtaputra (Karṇa); Ulūka, and indeed Saubala (Śakuni); and the King (Duryodhana) along with his brothers (were among them)."

sanskrit: सीदमानां चमूं दृष्ट्वा पाण्डुपुत्रभयार्दिताम् ।समुज्जिहीर्षुर्वेगेन भिन्नां नावमिवार्णवे ॥२॥
transliteration: sīdamānāṁ camūṁ dṛṣṭvā pāṇḍuputrabhayārditām ,samujjihīrṣurvegena bhinnāṁ nāvamivārṇave.
english: Upon seeing the army, which was sinking and distressed by fear of the sons of Pāṇḍu, (he desired) to swiftly rescue it, just as one would a shattered boat in the ocean.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 38 Verse 2. Upon seeing the army, which was sinking and distressed by fear of the sons of Pāṇḍu, (he desired) to swiftly rescue it, just as one would a shattered boat in the ocean.

sanskrit: ततो युद्धमतीवासीन्मुहूर्तमिव भारत ।भीरूणां त्रासजननं शूराणां हर्षवर्धनम् ॥३॥
transliteration: tato yuddhamatīvāsīnmuhūrtamiva bhārata ,bhīrūṇāṁ trāsajananaṁ śūrāṇāṁ harṣavardhanam.
english: Then, O Bhārata, the battle became exceedingly intense for a brief moment, inspiring fear in the timid and increasing joy in the brave.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 38 Verse 3. Then, O Bhārata, the battle became exceedingly intense for a brief moment, inspiring fear in the timid and increasing joy in the brave.

sanskrit: कृपेण शरवर्षाणि विप्रमुक्तानि संयुगे ।सृञ्जयाः शातयामासुः शलभानां व्रजा इव ॥४॥
transliteration: kṛpeṇa śaravarṣāṇi vipramuktāni saṁyuge ,sṛñjayāḥ śātayāmāsuḥ śalabhānāṁ vrajā iva.
english: The Sṛñjayas destroyed the torrents of arrows unleashed by Kṛpa in battle, just as (one disperses) swarms of locusts.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 38 Verse 4. The Sṛñjayas destroyed the torrents of arrows unleashed by Kṛpa in battle, just as (one disperses) swarms of locusts.

sanskrit: शिखण्डी तु ततः क्रुद्धो गौतमं त्वरितो ययौ ।ववर्ष शरवर्षाणि समन्तादेव ब्राह्मणे ॥५॥
transliteration: śikhaṇḍī tu tataḥ kruddho gautamaṁ tvarito yayau ,vavarṣa śaravarṣāṇi samantādeva brāhmaṇe.
english: But then, enraged, Śikhaṇḍī swiftly advanced towards Gautama (Kṛpa) and rained showers of arrows upon that Brahmin from all sides.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 38 Verse 5. But then, enraged, Śikhaṇḍī swiftly advanced towards Gautama (Kṛpa) and rained showers of arrows upon that Brahmin from all sides.

sanskrit: कृपस्तु शरवर्षं तद्विनिहत्य महास्त्रवित् ।शिखण्डिनं रणे क्रुद्धो विव्याध दशभिः शरैः ॥६॥
transliteration: kṛpastu śaravarṣaṁ tadvinihatya mahāstravit ,śikhaṇḍinaṁ raṇe kruddho vivyādha daśabhiḥ śaraiḥ.
english: Kripa, an expert in great weapons, enraged, completely countered that shower of arrows, and then, in battle, pierced Shikhaṇḍin with ten arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 38 Verse 6. Kripa, an expert in great weapons, enraged, completely countered that shower of arrows, and then, in battle, pierced Shikhaṇḍin with ten arrows.

sanskrit: ततः शिखण्डी कुपीतः शरैः सप्तभिराहवे ।कृपं विव्याध सुभृशं कङ्कपत्रैरजिह्मगैः ॥७॥
transliteration: tataḥ śikhaṇḍī kupītaḥ śaraiḥ saptabhirāhave ,kṛpaṁ vivyādha subhṛśaṁ kaṅkapatrairajihmagaiḥ.
english: Then Shikhaṇḍin, greatly enraged, in that battle, pierced Kripa exceedingly with seven straight-flying arrows feathered with vulture plumes.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 38 Verse 7. Then Shikhaṇḍin, greatly enraged, in that battle, pierced Kripa exceedingly with seven straight-flying arrows feathered with vulture plumes.

sanskrit: ततः कृपः शरैस्तीक्ष्णैः सोऽतिविद्धो महारथः ।व्यश्वसूतरथं चक्रे पार्षतं तु द्विजोत्तमः ॥८॥
transliteration: tataḥ kṛpaḥ śaraistīkṣṇaiḥ so'tividdho mahārathaḥ ,vyaśvasūtarathaṁ cakre pārṣataṁ tu dvijottamaḥ.
english: Then Kripa, the foremost of brahmins (dvijottama), that great warrior (mahāratha), though severely wounded by sharp arrows, indeed rendered Pārṣata (Shikhaṇḍin) bereft of his horses, charioteer, and chariot.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 38 Verse 8. Then Kripa, the foremost of brahmins (dvijottama), that great warrior (mahāratha), though severely wounded by sharp arrows, indeed rendered Pārṣata (Shikhaṇḍin) bereft of his horses, charioteer, and chariot.

sanskrit: हताश्वात्तु ततो यानादवप्लुत्य महारथः ।चर्मखड्गे च संगृह्य सत्वरं ब्राह्मणं ययौ ॥९॥
transliteration: hatāśvāttu tato yānādavaplutya mahārathaḥ ,carmakhaḍge ca saṁgṛhya satvaraṁ brāhmaṇaṁ yayau.
english: But then, that great warrior (Shikhaṇḍin), having jumped down from his chariot whose horses had been killed, quickly took up his shield and sword and went towards the brahmin (Kripa).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 38 Verse 9. But then, that great warrior (Shikhaṇḍin), having jumped down from his chariot whose horses had been killed, quickly took up his shield and sword and went towards the brahmin (Kripa).

sanskrit: तमापतन्तं सहसा शरैः संनतपर्वभिः ।छादयामास समरे तदद्भुतमिवाभवत् ॥१०॥
transliteration: tamāpatantaṁ sahasā śaraiḥ saṁnataparvabhiḥ ,chādayāmāsa samare tadadbhutamivābhavat.
english: He swiftly covered that approaching person with well-jointed arrows in battle. That was truly astounding.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 38 Verse 10. He swiftly covered that approaching person with well-jointed arrows in battle. That was truly astounding.

sanskrit: तत्राद्भुतमपश्याम शिलानां प्लवनं यथा ।निश्चेष्टो यद्रणे राजञ्शिखण्डी समतिष्ठत ॥११॥
transliteration: tatrādbhutamapaśyāma śilānāṁ plavanaṁ yathā ,niśceṣṭo yadraṇe rājañśikhaṇḍī samatiṣṭhata.
english: O King, it was a wonder we saw there, as if stones were floating, that Shikhandi stood motionless in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 38 Verse 11. O King, it was a wonder we saw there, as if stones were floating, that Shikhandi stood motionless in battle.

sanskrit: कृपेण छादितं दृष्ट्वा नृपोत्तम शिखण्डिनम् ।प्रत्युद्ययौ कृपं तूर्णं धृष्टद्युम्नो महारथः ॥१२॥
transliteration: kṛpeṇa chāditaṁ dṛṣṭvā nṛpottama śikhaṇḍinam ,pratyudyayau kṛpaṁ tūrṇaṁ dhṛṣṭadyumno mahārathaḥ.
english: O best of kings, seeing Shikhandi covered by Kripa, the great charioteer Dhṛṣṭadyumna swiftly advanced against Kripa.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 38 Verse 12. O best of kings, seeing Shikhandi covered by Kripa, the great charioteer Dhṛṣṭadyumna swiftly advanced against Kripa.

sanskrit: धृष्टद्युम्नं ततो यान्तं शारद्वतरथं प्रति ।प्रतिजग्राह वेगेन कृतवर्मा महारथः ॥१३॥
transliteration: dhṛṣṭadyumnaṁ tato yāntaṁ śāradvatarathaṁ prati ,pratijagrāha vegena kṛtavarmā mahārathaḥ.
english: Then, the great charioteer Kṛtavarma swiftly intercepted Dhṛṣṭadyumna as he advanced towards the chariot of Śāradvata (Kripa).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 38 Verse 13. Then, the great charioteer Kṛtavarma swiftly intercepted Dhṛṣṭadyumna as he advanced towards the chariot of Śāradvata (Kripa).

sanskrit: युधिष्ठिरमथायान्तं शारद्वतरथं प्रति ।सपुत्रं सहसेनं च द्रोणपुत्रो न्यवारयत् ॥१४॥
transliteration: yudhiṣṭhiramathāyāntaṁ śāradvatarathaṁ prati ,saputraṁ sahasenaṁ ca droṇaputro nyavārayat.
english: Then Droṇa's son (Aśvatthāman) obstructed Yudhiṣṭhira, who was approaching Kṛpa's chariot along with his son and army.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 38 Verse 14. Then Droṇa's son (Aśvatthāman) obstructed Yudhiṣṭhira, who was approaching Kṛpa's chariot along with his son and army.

sanskrit: नकुलं सहदेवं च त्वरमाणौ महारथौ ।प्रतिजग्राह ते पुत्रः शरवर्षेण वारयन् ॥१५॥
transliteration: nakulaṁ sahadevaṁ ca tvaramāṇau mahārathau ,pratijagrāha te putraḥ śaravarṣeṇa vārayan.
english: Your son intercepted Nakula and Sahadeva, both great charioteers who were hastening forward, obstructing them with a shower of arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 38 Verse 15. Your son intercepted Nakula and Sahadeva, both great charioteers who were hastening forward, obstructing them with a shower of arrows.

sanskrit: भीमसेनं करूषांश्च केकयान्सहसृञ्जयान् ।कर्णो वैकर्तनो युद्धे वारयामास भारत ॥१६॥
transliteration: bhīmasenaṁ karūṣāṁśca kekayānsahasṛñjayān ,karṇo vaikartano yuddhe vārayāmāsa bhārata.
english: O Bhārata, Karṇa, the son of the Sun god (Vaikartana), stopped Bhīmasena, the Karūṣas, the Kekayas, and the Sṛñjayas in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 38 Verse 16. O Bhārata, Karṇa, the son of the Sun god (Vaikartana), stopped Bhīmasena, the Karūṣas, the Kekayas, and the Sṛñjayas in battle.

sanskrit: शिखण्डिनस्ततो बाणान्कृपः शारद्वतो युधि ।प्राहिणोत्त्वरया युक्तो दिधक्षुरिव मारिष ॥१७॥
transliteration: śikhaṇḍinastato bāṇānkṛpaḥ śāradvato yudhi ,prāhiṇottvarayā yukto didhakṣuriva māriṣa.
english: O venerable one, then Kṛpa, the son of Śaradvan, quickly dispatched arrows at Śikhaṇḍin in battle, as if desiring to burn him.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 38 Verse 17. O venerable one, then Kṛpa, the son of Śaradvan, quickly dispatched arrows at Śikhaṇḍin in battle, as if desiring to burn him.

sanskrit: ताञ्शरान्प्रेषितांस्तेन समन्ताद्धेमभूषणान् ।चिच्छेद खड्गमाविध्य भ्रामयंश्च पुनः पुनः ॥१८॥
transliteration: tāñśarānpreṣitāṁstena samantāddhemabhūṣaṇān ,ciccheda khaḍgamāvidhya bhrāmayaṁśca punaḥ punaḥ.
english: Brandishing his sword and whirling it repeatedly, he cut off all those gold-adorned arrows that had been sent by him.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 38 Verse 18. Brandishing his sword and whirling it repeatedly, he cut off all those gold-adorned arrows that had been sent by him.

sanskrit: शतचन्द्रं ततश्चर्म गौतमः पार्षतस्य ह ।व्यधमत्सायकैस्तूर्णं तत उच्चुक्रुशुर्जनाः ॥१९॥
transliteration: śatacandraṁ tataścarma gautamaḥ pārṣatasya ha ,vyadhamatsāyakaistūrṇaṁ tata uccukruśurjanāḥ.
english: Then Gautama (Kṛpa) swiftly struck down Pārṣata's (Dṛṣṭadyumna's) shield, which was marked with a hundred moons, using arrows. At that, the people cried out.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 38 Verse 19. Then Gautama (Kṛpa) swiftly struck down Pārṣata's (Dṛṣṭadyumna's) shield, which was marked with a hundred moons, using arrows. At that, the people cried out.

sanskrit: स विचर्मा महाराज खड्गपाणिरुपाद्रवत् ।कृपस्य वशमापन्नो मृत्योरास्यमिवातुरः ॥२०॥
transliteration: sa vicarmā mahārāja khaḍgapāṇirupādravat ,kṛpasya vaśamāpanno mṛtyorāsyamivāturaḥ.
english: O great king, he, now without a shield but sword in hand, rushed forward. Having fallen into Kṛpa's power, he was like a distressed man entering the mouth of death.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 38 Verse 20. O great king, he, now without a shield but sword in hand, rushed forward. Having fallen into Kṛpa's power, he was like a distressed man entering the mouth of death.

sanskrit: शारद्वतशरैर्ग्रस्तं क्लिश्यमानं महाबलम् ।चित्रकेतुसुतो राजन्सुकेतुस्त्वरितो ययौ ॥२१॥
transliteration: śāradvataśarairgrastaṁ kliśyamānaṁ mahābalam ,citraketusuto rājansuketustvarito yayau.
english: O king, Citraketu's son Suketu swiftly went to the mighty warrior who was suffering and had been afflicted by Śāradvata's (Kṛpa's) arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 38 Verse 21. O king, Citraketu's son Suketu swiftly went to the mighty warrior who was suffering and had been afflicted by Śāradvata's (Kṛpa's) arrows.

sanskrit: विकिरन्ब्राह्मणं युद्धे बहुभिर्निशितैः शरैः ।अभ्यापतदमेयात्मा गौतमस्य रथं प्रति ॥२२॥
transliteration: vikiranbrāhmaṇaṁ yuddhe bahubhirniśitaiḥ śaraiḥ ,abhyāpatadameyātmā gautamasya rathaṁ prati.
english: The noble-minded one (Dhrishtadyumna), showering the brahmin (Droṇa) in battle with many sharp arrows, then rushed towards the chariot of Gautama (Kṛpa).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 38 Verse 22. The noble-minded one (Dhrishtadyumna), showering the brahmin (Droṇa) in battle with many sharp arrows, then rushed towards the chariot of Gautama (Kṛpa).

sanskrit: दृष्ट्वाविषह्यं तं युद्धे ब्राह्मणं चरितव्रतम् ।अपयातस्ततस्तूर्णं शिखण्डी राजसत्तम ॥२३॥
transliteration: dṛṣṭvāviṣahyaṁ taṁ yuddhe brāhmaṇaṁ caritavratam ,apayātastatastūrṇaṁ śikhaṇḍī rājasattama.
english: Having seen that irresistible brahmin (Droṇa) of steadfast vows in battle, Shikhaṇḍī quickly retreated from there, O best of kings.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 38 Verse 23. Having seen that irresistible brahmin (Droṇa) of steadfast vows in battle, Shikhaṇḍī quickly retreated from there, O best of kings.

sanskrit: सुकेतुस्तु ततो राजन्गौतमं नवभिः शरैः ।विद्ध्वा विव्याध सप्तत्या पुनश्चैनं त्रिभिः शरैः ॥२४॥
transliteration: suketustu tato rājangautamaṁ navabhiḥ śaraiḥ ,viddhvā vivyādha saptatyā punaścainaṁ tribhiḥ śaraiḥ.
english: But then, O king, Suketu, having first pierced Gautama (Kṛpa) with nine arrows, also struck him with seventy (arrows), and again with three more arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 38 Verse 24. But then, O king, Suketu, having first pierced Gautama (Kṛpa) with nine arrows, also struck him with seventy (arrows), and again with three more arrows.

sanskrit: अथास्य सशरं चापं पुनश्चिच्छेद मारिष ।सारथिं च शरेणास्य भृशं मर्मण्यताडयत् ॥२५॥
transliteration: athāsya saśaraṁ cāpaṁ punaściccheda māriṣa ,sārathiṁ ca śareṇāsya bhṛśaṁ marmaṇyatāḍayat.
english: Then, O revered one, he (Suketu) again cut his (Gautama's) arrow-bearing bow. And with an arrow, he fiercely struck his (Gautama's) charioteer in a vital spot.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 38 Verse 25. Then, O revered one, he (Suketu) again cut his (Gautama's) arrow-bearing bow. And with an arrow, he fiercely struck his (Gautama's) charioteer in a vital spot.

sanskrit: गौतमस्तु ततः क्रुद्धो धनुर्गृह्य नवं दृढम् ।सुकेतुं त्रिंशता बाणैः सर्वमर्मस्वताडयत् ॥२६॥
transliteration: gautamastu tataḥ kruddho dhanurgṛhya navaṁ dṛḍham ,suketuṁ triṁśatā bāṇaiḥ sarvamarmasvatāḍayat.
english: Then, enraged, Gautama took a new, strong bow and struck Suketu in all his vital spots with thirty arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 38 Verse 26. Then, enraged, Gautama took a new, strong bow and struck Suketu in all his vital spots with thirty arrows.

sanskrit: स विह्वलितसर्वाङ्गः प्रचचाल रथोत्तमे ।भूमिचाले यथा वृक्षश्चलत्याकम्पितो भृशम् ॥२७॥
transliteration: sa vihvalitasarvāṅgaḥ pracacāla rathottame ,bhūmicāle yathā vṛkṣaścalatyākampito bhṛśam.
english: With all his limbs trembling, he swayed in the excellent chariot, just as a tree, greatly shaken, sways violently during an earthquake.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 38 Verse 27. With all his limbs trembling, he swayed in the excellent chariot, just as a tree, greatly shaken, sways violently during an earthquake.

sanskrit: चलतस्तस्य कायात्तु शिरो ज्वलितकुण्डलम् ।सोष्णीषं सशिरस्त्राणं क्षुरप्रेणान्वपातयत् ॥२८॥
transliteration: calatastasya kāyāttu śiro jvalitakuṇḍalam ,soṣṇīṣaṁ saśirastrāṇaṁ kṣurapreṇānvapātayat.
english: But from his swaying body, (Gautama) struck down his head – adorned with shining earrings, a turban, and a helmet – with a razor-sharp arrow (kṣurapra).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 38 Verse 28. But from his swaying body, (Gautama) struck down his head – adorned with shining earrings, a turban, and a helmet – with a razor-sharp arrow (kṣurapra).

sanskrit: तच्छिरः प्रापतद्भूमौ श्येनाहृतमिवामिषम् ।ततोऽस्य कायो वसुधां पश्चात्प्राप तदा च्युतः ॥२९॥
transliteration: tacchiraḥ prāpatadbhūmau śyenāhṛtamivāmiṣam ,tato'sya kāyo vasudhāṁ paścātprāpa tadā cyutaḥ.
english: That head fell to the ground like a piece of meat snatched by a hawk. Then, his (Suketu's) body, detached (from the head), subsequently fell to the earth.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 38 Verse 29. That head fell to the ground like a piece of meat snatched by a hawk. Then, his (Suketu's) body, detached (from the head), subsequently fell to the earth.

sanskrit: तस्मिन्हते महाराज त्रस्तास्तस्य पदानुगाः ।गौतमं समरे त्यक्त्वा दुद्रुवुस्ते दिशो दश ॥३०॥
transliteration: tasminhate mahārāja trastāstasya padānugāḥ ,gautamaṁ samare tyaktvā dudruvuste diśo daśa.
english: O great king, when he was slain, his terrified followers abandoned Gautama (Kṛpācārya) on the battlefield and fled in all ten directions.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 38 Verse 30. O great king, when he was slain, his terrified followers abandoned Gautama (Kṛpācārya) on the battlefield and fled in all ten directions.

sanskrit: धृष्टद्युम्नं तु समरे संनिवार्य महाबलः ।कृतवर्माब्रवीद्धृष्टस्तिष्ठ तिष्ठेति पार्षतम् ॥३१॥
transliteration: dhṛṣṭadyumnaṁ tu samare saṁnivārya mahābalaḥ ,kṛtavarmābravīddhṛṣṭastiṣṭha tiṣṭheti pārṣatam.
english: But the mighty Kṛtavarmā, having checked Dhṛṣṭadyumna in battle, fiercely said to the Pārṣata (Dhṛṣṭadyumna), 'Stop! Stop!'
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 38 Verse 31. But the mighty Kṛtavarmā, having checked Dhṛṣṭadyumna in battle, fiercely said to the Pārṣata (Dhṛṣṭadyumna), 'Stop! Stop!'

sanskrit: तदभूत्तुमुलं युद्धं वृष्णिपार्षतयो रणे ।आमिषार्थे यथा युद्धं श्येनयोर्गृद्धयोर्नृप ॥३२॥
transliteration: tadabhūttumulaṁ yuddhaṁ vṛṣṇipārṣatayo raṇe ,āmiṣārthe yathā yuddhaṁ śyenayorgṛddhayornṛpa.
english: Then, O king, a tumultuous battle ensued on the battlefield between the Vṛṣṇi (Kṛtavarmā) and the Pārṣata (Dhṛṣṭadyumna), just like a battle between two hawks or two vultures fighting over a piece of meat.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 38 Verse 32. Then, O king, a tumultuous battle ensued on the battlefield between the Vṛṣṇi (Kṛtavarmā) and the Pārṣata (Dhṛṣṭadyumna), just like a battle between two hawks or two vultures fighting over a piece of meat.

sanskrit: धृष्टद्युम्नस्तु समरे हार्दिक्यं नवभिः शरैः ।आजघानोरसि क्रुद्धः पीडयन्हृदिकात्मजम् ॥३३॥
transliteration: dhṛṣṭadyumnastu samare hārdikyaṁ navabhiḥ śaraiḥ ,ājaghānorasi kruddhaḥ pīḍayanhṛdikātmajam.
english: But Dhṛṣṭadyumna, enraged on the battlefield, struck Hārdikya (Kṛtavarmā) with nine arrows in the chest, afflicting the son of Hṛdika.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 38 Verse 33. But Dhṛṣṭadyumna, enraged on the battlefield, struck Hārdikya (Kṛtavarmā) with nine arrows in the chest, afflicting the son of Hṛdika.

sanskrit: कृतवर्मा तु समरे पार्षतेन दृढाहतः ।पार्षतं सरथं साश्वं छादयामास सायकैः ॥३४॥
transliteration: kṛtavarmā tu samare pārṣatena dṛḍhāhataḥ ,pārṣataṁ sarathaṁ sāśvaṁ chādayāmāsa sāyakaiḥ.
english: Although Kṛtavarmā was severely struck by Dhṛṣṭadyumna in battle, he completely covered Dhṛṣṭadyumna, along with his chariot and horses, with arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 38 Verse 34. Although Kṛtavarmā was severely struck by Dhṛṣṭadyumna in battle, he completely covered Dhṛṣṭadyumna, along with his chariot and horses, with arrows.

sanskrit: सरथश्छादितो राजन्धृष्टद्युम्नो न दृश्यते ।मेघैरिव परिच्छन्नो भास्करो जलदागमे ॥३५॥
transliteration: sarathaśchādito rājandhṛṣṭadyumno na dṛśyate ,meghairiva paricchanno bhāskaro jaladāgame.
english: O king, Dhṛṣṭadyumna, along with his chariot and covered by arrows, is no longer visible, just as the sun is obscured by clouds during the monsoon season.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 38 Verse 35. O king, Dhṛṣṭadyumna, along with his chariot and covered by arrows, is no longer visible, just as the sun is obscured by clouds during the monsoon season.

sanskrit: विधूय तं बाणगणं शरैः कनकभूषणैः ।व्यरोचत रणे राजन्धृष्टद्युम्नः कृतव्रणः ॥३६॥
transliteration: vidhūya taṁ bāṇagaṇaṁ śaraiḥ kanakabhūṣaṇaiḥ ,vyarocata raṇe rājandhṛṣṭadyumnaḥ kṛtavraṇaḥ.
english: O king, having dispelled that shower of arrows with his own gold-ornamented arrows, Dhṛṣṭadyumna, though wounded, shone brilliantly on the battlefield.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 38 Verse 36. O king, having dispelled that shower of arrows with his own gold-ornamented arrows, Dhṛṣṭadyumna, though wounded, shone brilliantly on the battlefield.

sanskrit: ततस्तु पार्षतः क्रुद्धः शस्त्रवृष्टिं सुदारुणाम् ।कृतवर्माणमासाद्य व्यसृजत्पृतनापतिः ॥३७॥
transliteration: tatastu pārṣataḥ kruddhaḥ śastravṛṣṭiṁ sudāruṇām ,kṛtavarmāṇamāsādya vyasṛjatpṛtanāpatiḥ.
english: Then, the enraged Dhṛṣṭadyumna, commander of the army, having approached Kṛtavarmā, unleashed a very terrible shower of weapons.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 38 Verse 37. Then, the enraged Dhṛṣṭadyumna, commander of the army, having approached Kṛtavarmā, unleashed a very terrible shower of weapons.

sanskrit: तामापतन्तीं सहसा शस्त्रवृष्टिं निरन्तराम् ।शरैरनेकसाहस्रैर्हार्दिक्यो व्यधमद्युधि ॥३८॥
