transliteration: tāmāpatantīṁ sahasā śastravṛṣṭiṁ nirantarām ,śarairanekasāhasrairhārdikyo vyadhamadyudhi.
english: Hārdikya, in battle, swiftly dispersed that incessant shower of weapons descending [upon him] with thousands of arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 38 Verse 38. Hārdikya, in battle, swiftly dispersed that incessant shower of weapons descending [upon him] with thousands of arrows.

sanskrit: दृष्ट्वा तु दारितां युद्धे शस्त्रवृष्टिं दुरुत्तराम् ।कृतवर्माणमभ्येत्य वारयामास पार्षतः ॥३९॥
transliteration: dṛṣṭvā tu dāritāṁ yuddhe śastravṛṣṭiṁ duruttarām ,kṛtavarmāṇamabhyetya vārayāmāsa pārṣataḥ.
english: Seeing that the formidable shower of weapons was scattered in battle, Dhṛṣṭadyumna (Pārṣata) approached Kṛtavarmā and restrained him.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 38 Verse 39. Seeing that the formidable shower of weapons was scattered in battle, Dhṛṣṭadyumna (Pārṣata) approached Kṛtavarmā and restrained him.

sanskrit: सारथिं चास्य तरसा प्राहिणोद्यमसादनम् ।भल्लेन शितधारेण स हतः प्रापतद्रथात् ॥४०॥
transliteration: sārathiṁ cāsya tarasā prāhiṇodyamasādanam ,bhallena śitadhāreṇa sa hataḥ prāpatadrathāt.
english: And swiftly, with a sharp-edged bhalla arrow, he (Dhṛṣṭadyumna) sent Kṛtavarmā's charioteer to Yama's abode (to death). Struck by it, that charioteer fell from the chariot.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 38 Verse 40. And swiftly, with a sharp-edged bhalla arrow, he (Dhṛṣṭadyumna) sent Kṛtavarmā's charioteer to Yama's abode (to death). Struck by it, that charioteer fell from the chariot.

sanskrit: धृष्टद्युम्नस्तु बलवाञ्जित्वा शत्रुं महारथम् ।कौरवान्समरे तूर्णं वारयामास सायकैः ॥४१॥
transliteration: dhṛṣṭadyumnastu balavāñjitvā śatruṁ mahāratham ,kauravānsamare tūrṇaṁ vārayāmāsa sāyakaiḥ.
english: The mighty Dhṛṣṭadyumna, having defeated the great warrior who was his enemy, swiftly restrained the Kauravas in battle with his arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 38 Verse 41. The mighty Dhṛṣṭadyumna, having defeated the great warrior who was his enemy, swiftly restrained the Kauravas in battle with his arrows.

sanskrit: ततस्ते तावका योधा धृष्टद्युम्नमुपाद्रवन् ।सिंहनादरवं कृत्वा ततो युद्धमवर्तत ॥४२॥
transliteration: tataste tāvakā yodhā dhṛṣṭadyumnamupādravan ,siṁhanādaravaṁ kṛtvā tato yuddhamavartata.
english: Then your warriors, after letting out a lion's roar, attacked Dhṛṣṭadyumna. Subsequently, the battle commenced.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 38 Verse 42. Then your warriors, after letting out a lion's roar, attacked Dhṛṣṭadyumna. Subsequently, the battle commenced.

sanskrit: संजय उवाच ।द्रौणिर्युधिष्ठिरं दृष्ट्वा शैनेयेनाभिरक्षितम् ।द्रौपदेयैस्तथा शूरैरभ्यवर्तत हृष्टवत् ॥१॥
transliteration: saṁjaya uvāca ,drauṇiryudhiṣṭhiraṁ dṛṣṭvā śaineyenābhirakṣitam ,draupadeyaistathā śūrairabhyavartata hṛṣṭavat.
english: Sañjaya said: Upon seeing Yudhiṣṭhira well-guarded by Sātyaki (Śaineya) and also by the brave sons of Draupadī (Draupadeyas), Droṇa's son (Drauṇi) advanced with joy.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 39 Verse 1. Sañjaya said: Upon seeing Yudhiṣṭhira well-guarded by Sātyaki (Śaineya) and also by the brave sons of Draupadī (Draupadeyas), Droṇa's son (Drauṇi) advanced with joy.

sanskrit: किरन्निषुगणान्घोरान्स्वर्णपुङ्खाञ्शिलाशितान् ।दर्शयन्विविधान्मार्गाञ्शिक्षार्थं लघुहस्तवत् ॥२॥
transliteration: kiranniṣugaṇānghorānsvarṇapuṅkhāñśilāśitān ,darśayanvividhānmārgāñśikṣārthaṁ laghuhastavat.
english: Scattering terrible, gold-shafted arrows sharpened on stone, and demonstrating various techniques as if for the purpose of instruction, with the swiftness of hand (laghuhastavat).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 39 Verse 2. Scattering terrible, gold-shafted arrows sharpened on stone, and demonstrating various techniques as if for the purpose of instruction, with the swiftness of hand (laghuhastavat).

sanskrit: ततः खं पूरयामास शरैर्दिव्यास्त्रमन्त्रितैः ।युधिष्ठिरं च समरे पर्यवारयदस्त्रवित् ॥३॥
transliteration: tataḥ khaṁ pūrayāmāsa śarairdivyāstramantritaiḥ ,yudhiṣṭhiraṁ ca samare paryavārayadastravit.
english: Then, the expert in weapons (astravit) filled the sky with arrows consecrated by divine weapon (divyāstra) incantations (mantra), and he surrounded Yudhiṣṭhira in the battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 39 Verse 3. Then, the expert in weapons (astravit) filled the sky with arrows consecrated by divine weapon (divyāstra) incantations (mantra), and he surrounded Yudhiṣṭhira in the battle.

sanskrit: द्रौणायनिशरच्छन्नं न प्राज्ञायत किंचन ।बाणभूतमभूत्सर्वमायोधनशिरो हि तत् ॥४॥
transliteration: drauṇāyaniśaracchannaṁ na prājñāyata kiṁcana ,bāṇabhūtamabhūtsarvamāyodhanaśiro hi tat.
english: Indeed, the entire battlefield (āyodhanaśiras), completely covered by Aśvatthāman's (Drauṇāyani) arrows, transformed into nothing but arrows, and nothing at all could be discerned.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 39 Verse 4. Indeed, the entire battlefield (āyodhanaśiras), completely covered by Aśvatthāman's (Drauṇāyani) arrows, transformed into nothing but arrows, and nothing at all could be discerned.

sanskrit: बाणजालं दिविष्ठं तत्स्वर्णजालविभूषितम् ।शुशुभे भरतश्रेष्ठ वितानमिव विष्ठितम् ॥५॥
transliteration: bāṇajālaṁ diviṣṭhaṁ tatsvarṇajālavibhūṣitam ,śuśubhe bharataśreṣṭha vitānamiva viṣṭhitam.
english: O best of Bharatas, that network of arrows, situated in the sky and adorned with golden nets, shone beautifully like a canopy spread out.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 39 Verse 5. O best of Bharatas, that network of arrows, situated in the sky and adorned with golden nets, shone beautifully like a canopy spread out.

sanskrit: तेन छन्ने रणे राजन्बाणजालेन भास्वता ।अभ्रच्छायेव संजज्ञे बाणरुद्धे नभस्तले ॥६॥
transliteration: tena channe raṇe rājanbāṇajālena bhāsvatā ,abhracchāyeva saṁjajñe bāṇaruddhe nabhastale.
english: O King, as that brilliant network of arrows covered the battlefield, it created the appearance of a cloud's shadow in the sky, which was completely obstructed by the arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 39 Verse 6. O King, as that brilliant network of arrows covered the battlefield, it created the appearance of a cloud's shadow in the sky, which was completely obstructed by the arrows.

sanskrit: तत्राश्चर्यमपश्याम बाणभूते तथाविधे ।न स्म संपतते भूमौ दृष्ट्वा द्रौणेः पराक्रमम् ॥७॥
transliteration: tatrāścaryamapaśyāma bāṇabhūte tathāvidhe ,na sma saṁpatate bhūmau dṛṣṭvā drauṇeḥ parākramam.
english: There we witnessed a wonder: even though it had become a field of arrows of that nature (so dense), and upon seeing Aśvatthāman's (Drauṇi) valor (parākrama), not a single arrow fell to the ground.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 39 Verse 7. There we witnessed a wonder: even though it had become a field of arrows of that nature (so dense), and upon seeing Aśvatthāman's (Drauṇi) valor (parākrama), not a single arrow fell to the ground.

sanskrit: लाघवं द्रोणपुत्रस्य दृष्ट्वा तत्र महारथाः ।व्यस्मयन्त महाराज न चैनं प्रतिवीक्षितुम् ।शेकुस्ते सर्वराजानस्तपन्तमिव भास्करम् ॥८॥
transliteration: lāghavaṁ droṇaputrasya dṛṣṭvā tatra mahārathāḥ ,vyasmayanta mahārāja na cainaṁ prativīkṣitum ,śekuste sarvarājānastapantamiva bhāskaram.
english: O great king, having witnessed the swiftness of Drona's son, the great charioteers there were astonished. Indeed, none of those kings were able to confront him, who was like the blazing sun.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 39 Verse 8. O great king, having witnessed the swiftness of Drona's son, the great charioteers there were astonished. Indeed, none of those kings were able to confront him, who was like the blazing sun.

sanskrit: सात्यकिर्यतमानस्तु धर्मराजश्च पाण्डवः ।तथेतराणि सैन्यानि न स्म चक्रुः पराक्रमम् ॥९॥
transliteration: sātyakiryatamānastu dharmarājaśca pāṇḍavaḥ ,tathetarāṇi sainyāni na sma cakruḥ parākramam.
english: But even though Satyaki and the Pandava King Yudhishthira (dharma-rāja) were striving, the other armies likewise failed to demonstrate their prowess.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 39 Verse 9. But even though Satyaki and the Pandava King Yudhishthira (dharma-rāja) were striving, the other armies likewise failed to demonstrate their prowess.

sanskrit: वध्यमाने ततः सैन्ये द्रौपदेया महारथाः ।सात्यकिर्धर्मराजश्च पाञ्चालाश्चापि संगताः ।त्यक्त्वा मृत्युभयं घोरं द्रौणायनिमुपाद्रवन् ॥१०॥
transliteration: vadhyamāne tataḥ sainye draupadeyā mahārathāḥ ,sātyakirdharmarājaśca pāñcālāścāpi saṁgatāḥ ,tyaktvā mṛtyubhayaṁ ghoraṁ drauṇāyanimupādravan.
english: Then, as the army was being slaughtered, the great charioteers - Draupadi's sons, Satyaki, King Yudhishthira (dharma-rāja), and also the assembled Panchalas - abandoned their dreadful fear of death and rushed to attack Drona's son.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 39 Verse 10. Then, as the army was being slaughtered, the great charioteers - Draupadi's sons, Satyaki, King Yudhishthira (dharma-rāja), and also the assembled Panchalas - abandoned their dreadful fear of death and rushed to attack Drona's son.

sanskrit: सात्यकिः पञ्चविंशत्या द्रौणिं विद्ध्वा शिलामुखैः ।पुनर्विव्याध नाराचैः सप्तभिः स्वर्णभूषितैः ॥११॥
transliteration: sātyakiḥ pañcaviṁśatyā drauṇiṁ viddhvā śilāmukhaiḥ ,punarvivyādha nārācaiḥ saptabhiḥ svarṇabhūṣitaiḥ.
english: Satyaki first wounded Drona's son with twenty-five stone-tipped arrows, and then again pierced him with seven gold-embellished iron arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 39 Verse 11. Satyaki first wounded Drona's son with twenty-five stone-tipped arrows, and then again pierced him with seven gold-embellished iron arrows.

sanskrit: युधिष्ठिरस्त्रिसप्तत्या प्रतिविन्ध्यश्च सप्तभिः ।श्रुतकर्मा त्रिभिर्बाणैः श्रुतकीर्तिस्तु सप्तभिः ॥१२॥
transliteration: yudhiṣṭhirastrisaptatyā prativindhyaśca saptabhiḥ ,śrutakarmā tribhirbāṇaiḥ śrutakīrtistu saptabhiḥ.
english: Yudhiṣṭhira (the son of Dharma) struck with seventy-three arrows, Prativindhya with seven, Śrutakarmā with three arrows, and Śrutakīrti with seven.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 39 Verse 12. Yudhiṣṭhira (the son of Dharma) struck with seventy-three arrows, Prativindhya with seven, Śrutakarmā with three arrows, and Śrutakīrti with seven.

sanskrit: सुतसोमश्च नवभिः शतानीकश्च सप्तभिः ।अन्ये च बहवः शूरा विव्यधुस्तं समन्ततः ॥१३॥
transliteration: sutasomaśca navabhiḥ śatānīkaśca saptabhiḥ ,anye ca bahavaḥ śūrā vivyadhustaṁ samantataḥ.
english: Sutasoma (son of Bhīma) struck with nine arrows, and Śatānīka (son of Nakula) with seven. And many other brave warriors pierced him from all sides.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 39 Verse 13. Sutasoma (son of Bhīma) struck with nine arrows, and Śatānīka (son of Nakula) with seven. And many other brave warriors pierced him from all sides.

sanskrit: सोऽतिक्रुद्धस्ततो राजन्नाशीविष इव श्वसन् ।सात्यकिं पञ्चविंशत्या प्राविध्यत शिलाशितैः ॥१४॥
transliteration: so'tikruddhastato rājannāśīviṣa iva śvasan ,sātyakiṁ pañcaviṁśatyā prāvidhyata śilāśitaiḥ.
english: Then, O King, he, extremely enraged, hissing like a venomous snake, pierced Sātyaki with twenty-five stone-sharpened arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 39 Verse 14. Then, O King, he, extremely enraged, hissing like a venomous snake, pierced Sātyaki with twenty-five stone-sharpened arrows.

sanskrit: श्रुतकीर्तिं च नवभिः सुतसोमं च पञ्चभिः ।अष्टभिः श्रुतकर्माणं प्रतिविन्ध्यं त्रिभिः शरैः ।शतानीकं च नवभिर्धर्मपुत्रं च सप्तभिः ॥१५॥
transliteration: śrutakīrtiṁ ca navabhiḥ sutasomaṁ ca pañcabhiḥ ,aṣṭabhiḥ śrutakarmāṇaṁ prativindhyaṁ tribhiḥ śaraiḥ ,śatānīkaṁ ca navabhirdharmaputraṁ ca saptabhiḥ.
english: (He pierced) Śrutakīrti with nine (arrows), Sutasoma with five, Śrutakarmā with eight, Prativindhya with three arrows, Śatānīka with nine, and the son of Dharma (Yudhiṣṭhira) with seven.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 39 Verse 15. (He pierced) Śrutakīrti with nine (arrows), Sutasoma with five, Śrutakarmā with eight, Prativindhya with three arrows, Śatānīka with nine, and the son of Dharma (Yudhiṣṭhira) with seven.

sanskrit: अथेतरांस्ततः शूरान्द्वाभ्यां द्वाभ्यामताडयत् ।श्रुतकीर्तेस्तथा चापं चिच्छेद निशितैः शरैः ॥१६॥
transliteration: athetarāṁstataḥ śūrāndvābhyāṁ dvābhyāmatāḍayat ,śrutakīrtestathā cāpaṁ ciccheda niśitaiḥ śaraiḥ.
english: Then he struck the other brave warriors, two by two. He also cut Śrutakīrti's bow with sharp arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 39 Verse 16. Then he struck the other brave warriors, two by two. He also cut Śrutakīrti's bow with sharp arrows.

sanskrit: अथान्यद्धनुरादाय श्रुतकीर्तिर्महारथः ।द्रौणायनिं त्रिभिर्विद्ध्वा विव्याधान्यैः शितैः शरैः ॥१७॥
transliteration: athānyaddhanurādāya śrutakīrtirmahārathaḥ ,drauṇāyaniṁ tribhirviddhvā vivyādhānyaiḥ śitaiḥ śaraiḥ.
english: Then Śrutakīrti, the great chariot warrior, took another bow. Having pierced Droṇāyāni (Aśvatthāman) with three arrows, he then struck him again with other sharp arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 39 Verse 17. Then Śrutakīrti, the great chariot warrior, took another bow. Having pierced Droṇāyāni (Aśvatthāman) with three arrows, he then struck him again with other sharp arrows.

sanskrit: ततो द्रौणिर्महाराज शरवर्षेण भारत ।छादयामास तत्सैन्यं समन्ताच्च शरैर्नृपान् ॥१८॥
transliteration: tato drauṇirmahārāja śaravarṣeṇa bhārata ,chādayāmāsa tatsainyaṁ samantācca śarairnṛpān.
english: Then, O great king, O Bhārata, Droṇi (Aśvatthāman) covered that army with a shower of arrows, and the kings all around with (more) arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 39 Verse 18. Then, O great king, O Bhārata, Droṇi (Aśvatthāman) covered that army with a shower of arrows, and the kings all around with (more) arrows.

sanskrit: ततः पुनरमेयात्मा धर्मराजस्य कार्मुकम् ।द्रौणिश्चिच्छेद विहसन्विव्याध च शरैस्त्रिभिः ॥१९॥
transliteration: tataḥ punarameyātmā dharmarājasya kārmukam ,drauṇiściccheda vihasanvivyādha ca śaraistribhiḥ.
english: Then again, Droṇi (Aśvatthāman), whose spirit (ātman) was immeasurable, laughing, cut Dharmarāja's bow and pierced him with three arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 39 Verse 19. Then again, Droṇi (Aśvatthāman), whose spirit (ātman) was immeasurable, laughing, cut Dharmarāja's bow and pierced him with three arrows.

sanskrit: ततो धर्मसुतो राजन्प्रगृह्यान्यन्महद्धनुः ।द्रौणिं विव्याध सप्तत्या बाह्वोरुरसि चार्दयत् ॥२०॥
transliteration: tato dharmasuto rājanpragṛhyānyanmahaddhanuḥ ,drauṇiṁ vivyādha saptatyā bāhvorurasi cārdayat.
english: O King, then Yudhiṣṭhira, the son of Dharma, taking up another great bow, pierced Droṇa's son (Aśvatthāman) with seventy arrows and afflicted him in both arms and the chest.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 39 Verse 20. O King, then Yudhiṣṭhira, the son of Dharma, taking up another great bow, pierced Droṇa's son (Aśvatthāman) with seventy arrows and afflicted him in both arms and the chest.

sanskrit: सात्यकिस्तु ततः क्रुद्धो द्रौणेः प्रहरतो रणे ।अर्धचन्द्रेण तीक्ष्णेन धनुश्छित्त्वानदद्भृशम् ॥२१॥
transliteration: sātyakistu tataḥ kruddho drauṇeḥ praharato raṇe ,ardhacandreṇa tīkṣṇena dhanuśchittvānadadbhṛśam.
english: But then, enraged, Sātyaki, while Droṇa's son (Aśvatthāman) was attacking in battle, cut his bow with a sharp, crescent-shaped arrow and roared loudly.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 39 Verse 21. But then, enraged, Sātyaki, while Droṇa's son (Aśvatthāman) was attacking in battle, cut his bow with a sharp, crescent-shaped arrow and roared loudly.

sanskrit: छिन्नधन्वा ततो द्रौणिः शक्त्या शक्तिमतां वरः ।सारथिं पातयामास शैनेयस्य रथाद्द्रुतम् ॥२२॥
transliteration: chinnadhanvā tato drauṇiḥ śaktyā śaktimatāṁ varaḥ ,sārathiṁ pātayāmāsa śaineyasya rathāddrutam.
english: Then Droṇa's son (Aśvatthāman), whose bow had been cut, (though he was) the best among the powerful, quickly struck down Śaineya's (Sātyaki's) charioteer from the chariot with a spear.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 39 Verse 22. Then Droṇa's son (Aśvatthāman), whose bow had been cut, (though he was) the best among the powerful, quickly struck down Śaineya's (Sātyaki's) charioteer from the chariot with a spear.

sanskrit: अथान्यद्धनुरादाय द्रोणपुत्रः प्रतापवान् ।शैनेयं शरवर्षेण छादयामास भारत ॥२३॥
transliteration: athānyaddhanurādāya droṇaputraḥ pratāpavān ,śaineyaṁ śaravarṣeṇa chādayāmāsa bhārata.
english: O Bhārata, then Droṇa's powerful son (Aśvatthāman), taking another bow, enveloped Śaineya (Sātyaki) with a shower of arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 39 Verse 23. O Bhārata, then Droṇa's powerful son (Aśvatthāman), taking another bow, enveloped Śaineya (Sātyaki) with a shower of arrows.

sanskrit: तस्याश्वाः प्रद्रुताः संख्ये पतिते रथसारथौ ।तत्र तत्रैव धावन्तः समदृश्यन्त भारत ॥२४॥
transliteration: tasyāśvāḥ pradrutāḥ saṁkhye patite rathasārathau ,tatra tatraiva dhāvantaḥ samadṛśyanta bhārata.
english: O Bhārata, after her chariot driver had fallen in battle, her horses were seen fleeing, running erratically here and there.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 39 Verse 24. O Bhārata, after her chariot driver had fallen in battle, her horses were seen fleeing, running erratically here and there.

sanskrit: युधिष्ठिरपुरोगास्ते द्रौणिं शस्त्रभृतां वरम् ।अभ्यवर्षन्त वेगेन विसृजन्तः शिताञ्शरान् ॥२५॥
transliteration: yudhiṣṭhirapurogāste drauṇiṁ śastrabhṛtāṁ varam ,abhyavarṣanta vegena visṛjantaḥ śitāñśarān.
english: Led by Yudhiṣṭhira, they swiftly showered Droṇi (Aśvatthāman), who was the foremost among weapon-bearers, by releasing sharp arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 39 Verse 25. Led by Yudhiṣṭhira, they swiftly showered Droṇi (Aśvatthāman), who was the foremost among weapon-bearers, by releasing sharp arrows.

sanskrit: आगच्छमानांस्तान्दृष्ट्वा रौद्ररूपान्परंतपः ।प्रहसन्प्रतिजग्राह द्रोणपुत्रो महारणे ॥२६॥
transliteration: āgacchamānāṁstāndṛṣṭvā raudrarūpānparaṁtapaḥ ,prahasanpratijagrāha droṇaputro mahāraṇe.
english: Having seen those approaching warriors, who were terrible in form, Droṇa's son (Aśvatthāman), the tormentor of foes, smilingly received them in the great battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 39 Verse 26. Having seen those approaching warriors, who were terrible in form, Droṇa's son (Aśvatthāman), the tormentor of foes, smilingly received them in the great battle.

sanskrit: ततः शरशतज्वालः सेनाकक्षं महारथः ।द्रौणिर्ददाह समरे कक्षमग्निर्यथा वने ॥२७॥
transliteration: tataḥ śaraśatajvālaḥ senākakṣaṁ mahārathaḥ ,drauṇirdadāha samare kakṣamagniryathā vane.
english: Then, the great warrior Droṇi (Aśvatthāman), whose arrows were like a hundred flames, burned the enemy's ranks in battle, just as a fire consumes dry undergrowth in a forest.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 39 Verse 27. Then, the great warrior Droṇi (Aśvatthāman), whose arrows were like a hundred flames, burned the enemy's ranks in battle, just as a fire consumes dry undergrowth in a forest.

sanskrit: तद्बलं पाण्डुपुत्रस्य द्रोणपुत्रप्रतापितम् ।चुक्षुभे भरतश्रेष्ठ तिमिनेव नदीमुखम् ॥२८॥
transliteration: tadbalaṁ pāṇḍuputrasya droṇaputrapratāpitam ,cukṣubhe bharataśreṣṭha timineva nadīmukham.
english: O best among the Bharatas, that army of Yudhishthira, tormented by Drona's son, Ashvatthama, was agitated just like the mouth of a river is churned by a large sea monster.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 39 Verse 28. O best among the Bharatas, that army of Yudhishthira, tormented by Drona's son, Ashvatthama, was agitated just like the mouth of a river is churned by a large sea monster.

sanskrit: दृष्ट्वा ते च महाराज द्रोणपुत्रपराक्रमम् ।निहतान्मेनिरे सर्वान्पाण्डून्द्रोणसुतेन वै ॥२९॥
transliteration: dṛṣṭvā te ca mahārāja droṇaputraparākramam ,nihatānmenire sarvānpāṇḍūndroṇasutena vai.
english: And they, O great king, having witnessed the valor of Drona's son, Ashvatthama, truly believed that all the Pandavas had been slain by Drona's son.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 39 Verse 29. And they, O great king, having witnessed the valor of Drona's son, Ashvatthama, truly believed that all the Pandavas had been slain by Drona's son.

sanskrit: युधिष्ठिरस्तु त्वरितो द्रौणिं श्लिष्य महारथम् ।अब्रवीद्द्रोणपुत्रं तु रोषामर्षसमन्वितः ॥३०॥
transliteration: yudhiṣṭhirastu tvarito drauṇiṁ śliṣya mahāratham ,abravīddroṇaputraṁ tu roṣāmarṣasamanvitaḥ.
english: But Yudhishthira, having quickly embraced Droni, the great chariot-warrior, then spoke to Drona's son, filled with anger and indignation.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 39 Verse 30. But Yudhishthira, having quickly embraced Droni, the great chariot-warrior, then spoke to Drona's son, filled with anger and indignation.

sanskrit: नैव नाम तव प्रीतिर्नैव नाम कृतज्ञता ।यतस्त्वं पुरुषव्याघ्र मामेवाद्य जिघांससि ॥३१॥
transliteration: naiva nāma tava prītirnaiva nāma kṛtajñatā ,yatastvaṁ puruṣavyāghra māmevādya jighāṁsasi.
english: There is certainly no affection (prīti) for you, nor indeed any gratitude (kṛtajñatā), for you, O tiger among men (puruṣa), desire to kill me alone today.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 39 Verse 31. There is certainly no affection (prīti) for you, nor indeed any gratitude (kṛtajñatā), for you, O tiger among men (puruṣa), desire to kill me alone today.

sanskrit: ब्राह्मणेन तपः कार्यं दानमध्ययनं तथा ।क्षत्रियेण धनुर्नाम्यं स भवान्ब्राह्मणब्रुवः ॥३२॥
transliteration: brāhmaṇena tapaḥ kāryaṁ dānamadhyayanaṁ tathā ,kṣatriyeṇa dhanurnāmyaṁ sa bhavānbrāhmaṇabruvaḥ.
english: A Brahmin should perform austerity (tapas), give gifts (dāna), and engage in study. A Kṣatriya should wield the bow. But you, sir, are merely one who claims to be a Brahmin.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 39 Verse 32. A Brahmin should perform austerity (tapas), give gifts (dāna), and engage in study. A Kṣatriya should wield the bow. But you, sir, are merely one who claims to be a Brahmin.

sanskrit: मिषतस्ते महाबाहो जेष्यामि युधि कौरवान् ।कुरुष्व समरे कर्म ब्रह्मबन्धुरसि ध्रुवम् ॥३३॥
transliteration: miṣataste mahābāho jeṣyāmi yudhi kauravān ,kuruṣva samare karma brahmabandhurasi dhruvam.
english: O mighty-armed one, while you watch, I will conquer the Kauravas in battle. Perform your duty (karma) in this conflict, for you are certainly just a kinsman of Brahmins.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 39 Verse 33. O mighty-armed one, while you watch, I will conquer the Kauravas in battle. Perform your duty (karma) in this conflict, for you are certainly just a kinsman of Brahmins.

sanskrit: एवमुक्तो महाराज द्रोणपुत्रः स्मयन्निव ।युक्तत्वं तच्च संचिन्त्य नोत्तरं किंचिदब्रवीत् ॥३४॥
transliteration: evamukto mahārāja droṇaputraḥ smayanniva ,yuktatvaṁ tacca saṁcintya nottaraṁ kiṁcidabravīt.
english: O great king, Drona's son, thus addressed, as if smiling, carefully considered the appropriateness of that statement. He then gave no reply.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 39 Verse 34. O great king, Drona's son, thus addressed, as if smiling, carefully considered the appropriateness of that statement. He then gave no reply.

sanskrit: अनुक्त्वा च ततः किंचिच्छरवर्षेण पाण्डवम् ।छादयामास समरे क्रुद्धोऽन्तक इव प्रजाः ॥३५॥
transliteration: anuktvā ca tataḥ kiṁciccharavarṣeṇa pāṇḍavam ,chādayāmāsa samare kruddho'ntaka iva prajāḥ.
english: And then, without saying anything further, enraged, he covered the Pāṇḍava (Arjuna) in battle with a shower of arrows, just as Antaka (the god of death) overwhelms living beings.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 39 Verse 35. And then, without saying anything further, enraged, he covered the Pāṇḍava (Arjuna) in battle with a shower of arrows, just as Antaka (the god of death) overwhelms living beings.

sanskrit: संछाद्यमानस्तु तदा द्रोणपुत्रेण मारिष ।पार्थोऽपयातः शीघ्रं वै विहाय महतीं चमूम् ॥३६॥
transliteration: saṁchādyamānastu tadā droṇaputreṇa māriṣa ,pārtho'payātaḥ śīghraṁ vai vihāya mahatīṁ camūm.
english: O revered one, at that time Arjuna (Pārtha), being overpowered by Droṇa's son (Aśvatthāmā), quickly retreated, abandoning his large army.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 39 Verse 36. O revered one, at that time Arjuna (Pārtha), being overpowered by Droṇa's son (Aśvatthāmā), quickly retreated, abandoning his large army.

sanskrit: अपयाते ततस्तस्मिन्धर्मपुत्रे युधिष्ठिरे ।द्रोणपुत्रः स्थितो राजन्प्रत्यादेशान्महात्मनः ॥३७॥
transliteration: apayāte tatastasmindharmaputre yudhiṣṭhire ,droṇaputraḥ sthito rājanpratyādeśānmahātmanaḥ.
english: O King, when Yudhiṣṭhira, the son of Dharma (dharma), had thus departed, Droṇa's son (Aśvatthāmā) remained, following the command of the great-souled one.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 39 Verse 37. O King, when Yudhiṣṭhira, the son of Dharma (dharma), had thus departed, Droṇa's son (Aśvatthāmā) remained, following the command of the great-souled one.

sanskrit: ततो युधिष्ठिरो राजा त्यक्त्वा द्रौणिं महाहवे ।प्रययौ तावकं सैन्यं युक्तः क्रूराय कर्मणे ॥३८॥
transliteration: tato yudhiṣṭhiro rājā tyaktvā drauṇiṁ mahāhave ,prayayau tāvakaṁ sainyaṁ yuktaḥ krūrāya karmaṇe.
english: Then King Yudhiṣṭhira, leaving Droṇa's son (Aśvatthāmā) in the great battle, advanced upon your army, ready for a cruel action (karma).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 39 Verse 38. Then King Yudhiṣṭhira, leaving Droṇa's son (Aśvatthāmā) in the great battle, advanced upon your army, ready for a cruel action (karma).

sanskrit: संजय उवाच ।भीमसेनं सपाञ्चाल्यं चेदिकेकयसंवृतम् ।वैकर्तनः स्वयं रुद्ध्वा वारयामास सायकैः ॥१॥
transliteration: saṁjaya uvāca ,bhīmasenaṁ sapāñcālyaṁ cedikekayasaṁvṛtam ,vaikartanaḥ svayaṁ ruddhvā vārayāmāsa sāyakaiḥ.
english: Sañjaya said: Karṇa (Vaikartana) himself confronted and restrained Bhīmasena, who was accompanied by the Pāñcālas and surrounded by the Cedis and Kekayas, holding him back with his arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 40 Verse 1. Sañjaya said: Karṇa (Vaikartana) himself confronted and restrained Bhīmasena, who was accompanied by the Pāñcālas and surrounded by the Cedis and Kekayas, holding him back with his arrows.

sanskrit: ततस्तु चेदिकारूषान्सृञ्जयांश्च महारथान् ।कर्णो जघान संक्रुद्धो भीमसेनस्य पश्यतः ॥२॥
transliteration: tatastu cedikārūṣānsṛñjayāṁśca mahārathān ,karṇo jaghāna saṁkruddho bhīmasenasya paśyataḥ.
english: Then, enraged, Karṇa killed the great charioteers, namely the Cedis, Kārūṣas, and Sṛñjayas, even as Bhīmasena watched.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 40 Verse 2. Then, enraged, Karṇa killed the great charioteers, namely the Cedis, Kārūṣas, and Sṛñjayas, even as Bhīmasena watched.

sanskrit: भीमसेनस्ततः कर्णं विहाय रथसत्तमम् ।प्रययौ कौरवं सैन्यं कक्षमग्निरिव ज्वलन् ॥३॥
transliteration: bhīmasenastataḥ karṇaṁ vihāya rathasattamam ,prayayau kauravaṁ sainyaṁ kakṣamagniriva jvalan.
english: Then Bhīmasena, abandoning Karṇa, that best of charioteers, proceeded towards the Kuru army, blazing like a fire consuming dry grass.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 40 Verse 3. Then Bhīmasena, abandoning Karṇa, that best of charioteers, proceeded towards the Kuru army, blazing like a fire consuming dry grass.

sanskrit: सूतपुत्रोऽपि समरे पाञ्चालान्केकयांस्तथा ।सृञ्जयांश्च महेष्वासान्निजघान सहस्रशः ॥४॥
transliteration: sūtaputro'pi samare pāñcālānkekayāṁstathā ,sṛñjayāṁśca maheṣvāsānnijaghāna sahasraśaḥ.
english: And Sūtaputra (Karṇa) in battle slew thousands of the Pañcālas, Kekayas, and Sṛñjayas, who were mighty archers.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 40 Verse 4. And Sūtaputra (Karṇa) in battle slew thousands of the Pañcālas, Kekayas, and Sṛñjayas, who were mighty archers.

sanskrit: संशप्तकेषु पार्थश्च कौरवेषु वृकोदरः ।पाञ्चालेषु तथा कर्णः क्षयं चक्रूर्महारथाः ॥५॥
transliteration: saṁśaptakeṣu pārthaśca kauraveṣu vṛkodaraḥ ,pāñcāleṣu tathā karṇaḥ kṣayaṁ cakrūrmahārathāḥ.
english: And Pārtha (Arjuna) among the Saṃśaptakas, Vṛkodara (Bhīma) among the Kauravas, and Karṇa among the Pañcālas - these great charioteers wreaked havoc.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 40 Verse 5. And Pārtha (Arjuna) among the Saṃśaptakas, Vṛkodara (Bhīma) among the Kauravas, and Karṇa among the Pañcālas - these great charioteers wreaked havoc.

sanskrit: ते क्षत्रिया दह्यमानास्त्रिभिस्तैः पावकोपमैः ।जग्मुर्विनाशं समरे राजन्दुर्मन्त्रिते तव ॥६॥
transliteration: te kṣatriyā dahyamānāstribhistaiḥ pāvakopamaiḥ ,jagmurvināśaṁ samare rājandurmantrite tava.
english: O King, those Kṣatriya warriors, consumed by those three who were like fire, met their demise in battle because of your poor counsel.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 40 Verse 6. O King, those Kṣatriya warriors, consumed by those three who were like fire, met their demise in battle because of your poor counsel.

sanskrit: ततो दुर्योधनः क्रुद्धो नकुलं नवभिः शरैः ।विव्याध भरतश्रेष्ठ चतुरश्चास्य वाजिनः ॥७॥
transliteration: tato duryodhanaḥ kruddho nakulaṁ navabhiḥ śaraiḥ ,vivyādha bharataśreṣṭha caturaścāsya vājinaḥ.
english: Then, O best of the Bharatas, enraged Duryodhana pierced Nakula with nine arrows, and also (pierced) four of his horses.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 40 Verse 7. Then, O best of the Bharatas, enraged Duryodhana pierced Nakula with nine arrows, and also (pierced) four of his horses.

sanskrit: ततः पुनरमेयात्मा तव पुत्रो जनाधिपः ।क्षुरेण सहदेवस्य ध्वजं चिच्छेद काञ्चनम् ॥८॥
transliteration: tataḥ punarameyātmā tava putro janādhipaḥ ,kṣureṇa sahadevasya dhvajaṁ ciccheda kāñcanam.
english: Then again, O lord of people, your son, the one of immeasurable spirit (ātman), cut Sahadeva's golden banner with a razor-sharp (arrow).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 40 Verse 8. Then again, O lord of people, your son, the one of immeasurable spirit (ātman), cut Sahadeva's golden banner with a razor-sharp (arrow).

sanskrit: नकुलस्तु ततः क्रुद्धस्तव पुत्रं त्रिसप्तभिः ।जघान समरे राजन्सहदेवश्च पञ्चभिः ॥९॥
transliteration: nakulastu tataḥ kruddhastava putraṁ trisaptabhiḥ ,jaghāna samare rājansahadevaśca pañcabhiḥ.
english: But then, O King, enraged Nakula struck your son in battle with twenty-one (arrows), and Sahadeva (struck him) with five.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 40 Verse 9. But then, O King, enraged Nakula struck your son in battle with twenty-one (arrows), and Sahadeva (struck him) with five.

sanskrit: तावुभौ भरतश्रेष्ठौ श्रेष्ठौ सर्वधनुष्मताम् ।विव्याधोरसि संक्रुद्धः पञ्चभिः पञ्चभिः शरैः ॥१०॥
transliteration: tāvubhau bharataśreṣṭhau śreṣṭhau sarvadhanuṣmatām ,vivyādhorasi saṁkruddhaḥ pañcabhiḥ pañcabhiḥ śaraiḥ.
english: That enraged warrior pierced both of them – the best among the Bharatas and the best of all archers – in the chest with five arrows each.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 40 Verse 10. That enraged warrior pierced both of them – the best among the Bharatas and the best of all archers – in the chest with five arrows each.

sanskrit: ततोऽपराभ्यां भल्लाभ्यां धनुषी समकृन्तत ।यमयोः प्रहसन्राजन्विव्याधैव च सप्तभिः ॥११॥
transliteration: tato'parābhyāṁ bhallābhyāṁ dhanuṣī samakṛntata ,yamayoḥ prahasanrājanvivyādhaiva ca saptabhiḥ.
english: Then, O King, with two other broad-headed arrows, he cut the two bows of the twins. Laughing, he then pierced them with seven more arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 40 Verse 11. Then, O King, with two other broad-headed arrows, he cut the two bows of the twins. Laughing, he then pierced them with seven more arrows.

sanskrit: तावन्ये धनुषी श्रेष्ठे शक्रचापनिभे शुभे ।प्रगृह्य रेजतुः शूरौ देवपुत्रसमौ युधि ॥१२॥
transliteration: tāvanye dhanuṣī śreṣṭhe śakracāpanibhe śubhe ,pragṛhya rejatuḥ śūrau devaputrasamau yudhi.
english: Taking up two other excellent and beautiful bows, which resembled the rainbow, those two brave warriors, looking like sons of gods, shone brightly in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 40 Verse 12. Taking up two other excellent and beautiful bows, which resembled the rainbow, those two brave warriors, looking like sons of gods, shone brightly in battle.

sanskrit: ततस्तौ रभसौ युद्धे भ्रातरौ भ्रातरं नृप ।शरैर्ववर्षतुर्घोरैर्महामेघौ यथाचलम् ॥१३॥
transliteration: tatastau rabhasau yuddhe bhrātarau bhrātaraṁ nṛpa ,śarairvavarṣaturghorairmahāmeghau yathācalam.
english: Then, O King, those two impetuous brothers, in the midst of battle, rained dreadful arrows upon their brother, just as two great clouds pour rain upon a mountain.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 40 Verse 13. Then, O King, those two impetuous brothers, in the midst of battle, rained dreadful arrows upon their brother, just as two great clouds pour rain upon a mountain.

sanskrit: ततः क्रुद्धो महाराज तव पुत्रो महारथः ।पाण्डुपुत्रौ महेष्वासौ वारयामास पत्रिभिः ॥१४॥
transliteration: tataḥ kruddho mahārāja tava putro mahārathaḥ ,pāṇḍuputrau maheṣvāsau vārayāmāsa patribhiḥ.
english: O great king, then your enraged son, the great charioteer, warded off the two mighty archer sons of Pāṇḍu with his arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 40 Verse 14. O great king, then your enraged son, the great charioteer, warded off the two mighty archer sons of Pāṇḍu with his arrows.

sanskrit: धनुर्मण्डलमेवास्य दृश्यते युधि भारत ।सायकाश्चैव दृश्यन्ते निश्चरन्तः समन्ततः ॥१५॥
transliteration: dhanurmaṇḍalamevāsya dṛśyate yudhi bhārata ,sāyakāścaiva dṛśyante niścarantaḥ samantataḥ.
english: O Bhārata, in battle, only the circle of his bow is visible, and indeed, arrows are seen issuing forth from all directions.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 40 Verse 15. O Bhārata, in battle, only the circle of his bow is visible, and indeed, arrows are seen issuing forth from all directions.

sanskrit: तस्य सायकसंछन्नौ चकाशेतां च पाण्डवौ ।मेघच्छन्नौ यथा व्योम्नि चन्द्रसूर्यौ हतप्रभौ ॥१६॥
transliteration: tasya sāyakasaṁchannau cakāśetāṁ ca pāṇḍavau ,meghacchannau yathā vyomni candrasūryau hataprabhau.
english: And those two Pāṇḍavas, completely covered by his arrows, shone like the moon and sun in the sky when they are hidden by clouds and their radiance is diminished.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 40 Verse 16. And those two Pāṇḍavas, completely covered by his arrows, shone like the moon and sun in the sky when they are hidden by clouds and their radiance is diminished.

sanskrit: ते तु बाणा महाराज हेमपुङ्खाः शिलाशिताः ।आच्छादयन्दिशः सर्वाः सूर्यस्येवांशवस्तदा ॥१७॥
transliteration: te tu bāṇā mahārāja hemapuṅkhāḥ śilāśitāḥ ,ācchādayandiśaḥ sarvāḥ sūryasyevāṁśavastadā.
english: But, O great king, those arrows, golden-shafted and sharpened on stone, then covered all directions, just like the rays of the sun.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 40 Verse 17. But, O great king, those arrows, golden-shafted and sharpened on stone, then covered all directions, just like the rays of the sun.

sanskrit: बाणभूते ततस्तस्मिन्संछन्ने च नभस्तले ।यमाभ्यां ददृशे रूपं कालान्तकयमोपमम् ॥१८॥
transliteration: bāṇabhūte tatastasminsaṁchanne ca nabhastale ,yamābhyāṁ dadṛśe rūpaṁ kālāntakayamopamam.
english: Then, when that sky became covered and transformed into (filled with) arrows, the two sons of Madri (Nakula and Sahadeva) saw a form resembling Yama, the destroyer of time (Kālāntaka).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 40 Verse 18. Then, when that sky became covered and transformed into (filled with) arrows, the two sons of Madri (Nakula and Sahadeva) saw a form resembling Yama, the destroyer of time (Kālāntaka).

sanskrit: पराक्रमं तु तं दृष्ट्वा तव सूनोर्महारथाः ।मृत्योरुपान्तिकं प्राप्तौ माद्रीपुत्रौ स्म मेनिरे ॥१९॥
transliteration: parākramaṁ tu taṁ dṛṣṭvā tava sūnormahārathāḥ ,mṛtyorupāntikaṁ prāptau mādrīputrau sma menire.
english: But having seen that prowess of your son (Abhimanyu), the great charioteers believed the two sons of Madri (Mādrīputrau) had come to the very doorstep of death.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 40 Verse 19. But having seen that prowess of your son (Abhimanyu), the great charioteers believed the two sons of Madri (Mādrīputrau) had come to the very doorstep of death.

sanskrit: ततः सेनापती राजन्पाण्डवस्य महात्मनः ।पार्षतः प्रययौ तत्र यत्र राजा सुयोधनः ॥२०॥
transliteration: tataḥ senāpatī rājanpāṇḍavasya mahātmanaḥ ,pārṣataḥ prayayau tatra yatra rājā suyodhanaḥ.
english: Then, O king, Dhrishtadyumna (Pārṣata), the commander of the great-souled Pandava (Pāṇḍava) army, proceeded to the place where King Duryodhana (Suyodhana) was.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 40 Verse 20. Then, O king, Dhrishtadyumna (Pārṣata), the commander of the great-souled Pandava (Pāṇḍava) army, proceeded to the place where King Duryodhana (Suyodhana) was.

sanskrit: माद्रीपुत्रौ ततः शूरौ व्यतिक्रम्य महारथौ ।धृष्टद्युम्नस्तव सुतं ताडयामास सायकैः ॥२१॥
transliteration: mādrīputrau tataḥ śūrau vyatikramya mahārathau ,dhṛṣṭadyumnastava sutaṁ tāḍayāmāsa sāyakaiḥ.
english: Then, having bypassed the two heroic great charioteers, the sons of Madri (Mādrīputrau), Dhrishtadyumna struck your son (Duryodhana) with arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 40 Verse 21. Then, having bypassed the two heroic great charioteers, the sons of Madri (Mādrīputrau), Dhrishtadyumna struck your son (Duryodhana) with arrows.

sanskrit: तमविध्यदमेयात्मा तव पुत्रोऽत्यमर्षणः ।पाञ्चाल्यं पञ्चविंशत्या प्रहस्य पुरुषर्षभ ॥२२॥
transliteration: tamavidhyadameyātmā tava putro'tyamarṣaṇaḥ ,pāñcālyaṁ pañcaviṁśatyā prahasya puruṣarṣabha.
english: O best among men, your exceedingly furious son, whose spirit (ātman) is immeasurable, laughed loudly and pierced the Pañcāla prince (Pāñcālya) with twenty-five arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 40 Verse 22. O best among men, your exceedingly furious son, whose spirit (ātman) is immeasurable, laughed loudly and pierced the Pañcāla prince (Pāñcālya) with twenty-five arrows.

sanskrit: ततः पुनरमेयात्मा पुत्रस्ते पृथिवीपते ।विद्ध्वा ननाद पाञ्चाल्यं षष्ट्या पञ्चभिरेव च ॥२३॥
transliteration: tataḥ punarameyātmā putraste pṛthivīpate ,viddhvā nanāda pāñcālyaṁ ṣaṣṭyā pañcabhireva ca.
english: Then again, O lord of the earth, your son, whose spirit (ātman) is immeasurable, having pierced the Pañcāla prince (Pāñcālya) with sixty-five arrows, roared.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 40 Verse 23. Then again, O lord of the earth, your son, whose spirit (ātman) is immeasurable, having pierced the Pañcāla prince (Pāñcālya) with sixty-five arrows, roared.

sanskrit: अथास्य सशरं चापं हस्तावापं च मारिष ।क्षुरप्रेण सुतीक्ष्णेन राजा चिच्छेद संयुगे ॥२४॥
transliteration: athāsya saśaraṁ cāpaṁ hastāvāpaṁ ca māriṣa ,kṣurapreṇa sutīkṣṇena rājā ciccheda saṁyuge.
english: Then, O venerable one, the king (Duryodhana), with a very sharp, razor-sharp arrow, cut his bow with its arrows and his arm-guard in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 40 Verse 24. Then, O venerable one, the king (Duryodhana), with a very sharp, razor-sharp arrow, cut his bow with its arrows and his arm-guard in battle.

sanskrit: तदपास्य धनुश्छिन्नं पाञ्चाल्यः शत्रुकर्शनः ।अन्यदादत्त वेगेन धनुर्भारसहं नवम् ॥२५॥
transliteration: tadapāsya dhanuśchinnaṁ pāñcālyaḥ śatrukarśanaḥ ,anyadādatta vegena dhanurbhārasahaṁ navam.
english: Having cast aside that broken bow, the Pañcāla prince (Pāñcālya), the tormentor of enemies, swiftly took up another new bow capable of bearing great strain.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 40 Verse 25. Having cast aside that broken bow, the Pañcāla prince (Pāñcālya), the tormentor of enemies, swiftly took up another new bow capable of bearing great strain.

sanskrit: प्रज्वलन्निव वेगेन संरम्भाद्रुधिरेक्षणः ।अशोभत महेष्वासो धृष्टद्युम्नः कृतव्रणः ॥२६॥
transliteration: prajvalanniva vegena saṁrambhādrudhirekṣaṇaḥ ,aśobhata maheṣvāso dhṛṣṭadyumnaḥ kṛtavraṇaḥ.
english: Dhṛṣṭadyumna, the great archer, appeared splendid, as if blazing fiercely with speed and fury, his eyes bloodshot, now bearing wounds.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 40 Verse 26. Dhṛṣṭadyumna, the great archer, appeared splendid, as if blazing fiercely with speed and fury, his eyes bloodshot, now bearing wounds.

sanskrit: स पञ्चदश नाराचाञ्श्वसतः पन्नगानिव ।जिघांसुर्भरतश्रेष्ठं धृष्टद्युम्नो व्यवासृजत् ॥२७॥
transliteration: sa pañcadaśa nārācāñśvasataḥ pannagāniva ,jighāṁsurbharataśreṣṭhaṁ dhṛṣṭadyumno vyavāsṛjat.
english: Dhṛṣṭadyumna, intent on killing the best of the Bharatas (Bhīṣma), unleashed fifteen arrows that resembled hissing snakes.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 40 Verse 27. Dhṛṣṭadyumna, intent on killing the best of the Bharatas (Bhīṣma), unleashed fifteen arrows that resembled hissing snakes.

sanskrit: ते वर्म हेमविकृतं भित्त्वा राज्ञः शिलाशिताः ।विविशुर्वसुधां वेगात्कङ्कबर्हिणवाससः ॥२८॥
transliteration: te varma hemavikṛtaṁ bhittvā rājñaḥ śilāśitāḥ ,viviśurvasudhāṁ vegātkaṅkabarhiṇavāsasaḥ.
english: Those arrows, sharpened on stone and feathered with the plumes of herons and peacocks, having pierced the king's (Bhīṣma's) armor embellished with gold, swiftly entered the earth.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 40 Verse 28. Those arrows, sharpened on stone and feathered with the plumes of herons and peacocks, having pierced the king's (Bhīṣma's) armor embellished with gold, swiftly entered the earth.

sanskrit: सोऽतिविद्धो महाराज पुत्रस्तेऽतिव्यराजत ।वसन्ते पुष्पशबलः सपुष्प इव किंशुकः ॥२९॥
transliteration: so'tividdho mahārāja putraste'tivyarājata ,vasante puṣpaśabalaḥ sapuṣpa iva kiṁśukaḥ.
english: O great king, your son, though gravely wounded, shone brilliantly, like a kimśuka tree (palāśa) vibrant with flowers in springtime.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 40 Verse 29. O great king, your son, though gravely wounded, shone brilliantly, like a kimśuka tree (palāśa) vibrant with flowers in springtime.

sanskrit: स छिन्नवर्मा नाराचैः प्रहारैर्जर्जरच्छविः ।धृष्टद्युम्नस्य भल्लेन क्रुद्धश्चिच्छेद कार्मुकम् ॥३०॥
transliteration: sa chinnavarmā nārācaiḥ prahārairjarjaracchaviḥ ,dhṛṣṭadyumnasya bhallena kruddhaściccheda kārmukam.
english: With his armor shattered by arrows and his skin mangled by blows, he, enraged, cut Dhrishtadyumna's bow with a broad-headed arrow.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 40 Verse 30. With his armor shattered by arrows and his skin mangled by blows, he, enraged, cut Dhrishtadyumna's bow with a broad-headed arrow.

sanskrit: अथैनं छिन्नधन्वानं त्वरमाणो महीपतिः ।सायकैर्दशभी राजन्भ्रुवोर्मध्ये समार्दयत् ॥३१॥
transliteration: athainaṁ chinnadhanvānaṁ tvaramāṇo mahīpatiḥ ,sāyakairdaśabhī rājanbhruvormadhye samārdayat.
english: Then, O King, the hurrying ruler (mahīpati) struck him, who had lost his bow, with ten arrows in the middle of his eyebrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 40 Verse 31. Then, O King, the hurrying ruler (mahīpati) struck him, who had lost his bow, with ten arrows in the middle of his eyebrows.

sanskrit: तस्य तेऽशोभयन्वक्त्रं कर्मारपरिमार्जिताः ।प्रफुल्लं चम्पकं यद्वद्भ्रमरा मधुलिप्सवः ॥३२॥
transliteration: tasya te'śobhayanvaktraṁ karmāraparimārjitāḥ ,praphullaṁ campakaṁ yadvadbhramarā madhulipsavaḥ.
english: Those arrows, polished by blacksmiths, adorned his face just as honey-seeking bees beautify a blooming champak flower.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 40 Verse 32. Those arrows, polished by blacksmiths, adorned his face just as honey-seeking bees beautify a blooming champak flower.

sanskrit: तदपास्य धनुश्छिन्नं धृष्टद्युम्नो महामनाः ।अन्यदादत्त वेगेन धनुर्भल्लांश्च षोडश ॥३३॥
transliteration: tadapāsya dhanuśchinnaṁ dhṛṣṭadyumno mahāmanāḥ ,anyadādatta vegena dhanurbhallāṁśca ṣoḍaśa.
english: Discarding that broken bow, the great-souled Dhrishtadyumna swiftly took up another bow and sixteen broad-headed arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 40 Verse 33. Discarding that broken bow, the great-souled Dhrishtadyumna swiftly took up another bow and sixteen broad-headed arrows.

sanskrit: ततो दुर्योधनस्याश्वान्हत्वा सूतं च पञ्चभिः ।धनुश्चिच्छेद भल्लेन जातरूपपरिष्कृतम् ॥३४॥
transliteration: tato duryodhanasyāśvānhatvā sūtaṁ ca pañcabhiḥ ,dhanuściccheda bhallena jātarūpapariṣkṛtam.
english: Then, with five (arrows), he killed Duryodhana's horses and charioteer, and with a bhalla (arrow), he cut his gold-adorned bow.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 40 Verse 34. Then, with five (arrows), he killed Duryodhana's horses and charioteer, and with a bhalla (arrow), he cut his gold-adorned bow.

sanskrit: रथं सोपस्करं छत्रं शक्तिं खड्गं गदां ध्वजम् ।भल्लैश्चिच्छेद नवभिः पुत्रस्य तव पार्षतः ॥३५॥
transliteration: rathaṁ sopaskaraṁ chatraṁ śaktiṁ khaḍgaṁ gadāṁ dhvajam ,bhallaiściccheda navabhiḥ putrasya tava pārṣataḥ.
english: Your son's (Duryodhana's) chariot with its equipment, his umbrella, spear (śakti), sword, mace, and banner were cut down by Pārṣata (Dhṛṣṭadyumna) with nine bhalla (arrows).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 40 Verse 35. Your son's (Duryodhana's) chariot with its equipment, his umbrella, spear (śakti), sword, mace, and banner were cut down by Pārṣata (Dhṛṣṭadyumna) with nine bhalla (arrows).

sanskrit: तपनीयाङ्गदं चित्रं नागं मणिमयं शुभम् ।ध्वजं कुरुपतेश्छिन्नं ददृशुः सर्वपार्थिवाः ॥३६॥
transliteration: tapanīyāṅgadaṁ citraṁ nāgaṁ maṇimayaṁ śubham ,dhvajaṁ kurupateśchinnaṁ dadṛśuḥ sarvapārthivāḥ.
english: All the kings saw the banner of the lord of Kurus (Duryodhana), cut down, which was a wondrous, auspicious, jewel-studded serpent (emblem) adorned with golden armlets.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 40 Verse 36. All the kings saw the banner of the lord of Kurus (Duryodhana), cut down, which was a wondrous, auspicious, jewel-studded serpent (emblem) adorned with golden armlets.

sanskrit: दुर्योधनं तु विरथं छिन्नसर्वायुधं रणे ।भ्रातरः पर्यरक्षन्त सोदर्या भरतर्षभ ॥३७॥
transliteration: duryodhanaṁ tu virathaṁ chinnasarvāyudhaṁ raṇe ,bhrātaraḥ paryarakṣanta sodaryā bharatarṣabha.
english: O best of the Bharatas (Dhṛtarāṣṭra), however, his own full brothers protected Duryodhana in battle, who was left without a chariot and with all his weapons destroyed.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 40 Verse 37. O best of the Bharatas (Dhṛtarāṣṭra), however, his own full brothers protected Duryodhana in battle, who was left without a chariot and with all his weapons destroyed.

sanskrit: तमारोप्य रथे राजन्दण्डधारो जनाधिपम् ।अपोवाह च संभ्रान्तो धृष्टद्युम्नस्य पश्यतः ॥३८॥
transliteration: tamāropya rathe rājandaṇḍadhāro janādhipam ,apovāha ca saṁbhrānto dhṛṣṭadyumnasya paśyataḥ.
english: O King, having seated that lord of men on the chariot, the agitated charioteer carried him away, while Dhṛṣṭadyumna watched.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 40 Verse 38. O King, having seated that lord of men on the chariot, the agitated charioteer carried him away, while Dhṛṣṭadyumna watched.

sanskrit: कर्णस्तु सात्यकिं जित्वा राजगृद्धी महाबलः ।द्रोणहन्तारमुग्रेषुं ससाराभिमुखं रणे ॥३९॥
transliteration: karṇastu sātyakiṁ jitvā rājagṛddhī mahābalaḥ ,droṇahantāramugreṣuṁ sasārābhimukhaṁ raṇe.
english: But Karṇa, mighty and ambitious for kingship, having defeated Sātyaki, advanced towards the slayer of Droṇa, the fierce archer, confronting him in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 40 Verse 39. But Karṇa, mighty and ambitious for kingship, having defeated Sātyaki, advanced towards the slayer of Droṇa, the fierce archer, confronting him in battle.

sanskrit: तं पृष्ठतोऽभ्ययात्तूर्णं शैनेयो वितुदञ्शरैः ।वारणं जघनोपान्ते विषाणाभ्यामिव द्विपः ॥४०॥
transliteration: taṁ pṛṣṭhato'bhyayāttūrṇaṁ śaineyo vitudañśaraiḥ ,vāraṇaṁ jaghanopānte viṣāṇābhyāmiva dvipaḥ.
english: Śaineya quickly pursued him from behind, piercing with arrows, just as an elephant (dvipaḥ) would gore another elephant (vāraṇam) in its hindquarters with its two tusks.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 40 Verse 40. Śaineya quickly pursued him from behind, piercing with arrows, just as an elephant (dvipaḥ) would gore another elephant (vāraṇam) in its hindquarters with its two tusks.

sanskrit: स भारत महानासीद्योधानां सुमहात्मनाम् ।कर्णपार्षतयोर्मध्ये त्वदीयानां महारणः ॥४१॥
transliteration: sa bhārata mahānāsīdyodhānāṁ sumahātmanām ,karṇapārṣatayormadhye tvadīyānāṁ mahāraṇaḥ.
english: O Bhārata, that was a great, mighty battle among the supremely valiant warriors, fought between Karṇa and Pārṣata (Dhṛṣṭadyumna), with those of your side also involved.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 40 Verse 41. O Bhārata, that was a great, mighty battle among the supremely valiant warriors, fought between Karṇa and Pārṣata (Dhṛṣṭadyumna), with those of your side also involved.

sanskrit: न पाण्डवानां नास्माकं योधः कश्चित्पराङ्मुखः ।प्रत्यदृश्यत यत्कर्णः पाञ्चालांस्त्वरितो ययौ ॥४२॥
transliteration: na pāṇḍavānāṁ nāsmākaṁ yodhaḥ kaścitparāṅmukhaḥ ,pratyadṛśyata yatkarṇaḥ pāñcālāṁstvarito yayau.
english: No warrior, neither among the Pāṇḍavas nor among our own forces, was seen to turn away (from battle), even as Karṇa swiftly advanced towards the Pañcālas.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 40 Verse 42. No warrior, neither among the Pāṇḍavas nor among our own forces, was seen to turn away (from battle), even as Karṇa swiftly advanced towards the Pañcālas.

sanskrit: तस्मिन्क्षणे नरश्रेष्ठ गजवाजिनरक्षयः ।प्रादुरासीदुभयतो राजन्मध्यंगतेऽहनि ॥४३॥
transliteration: tasminkṣaṇe naraśreṣṭha gajavājinarakṣayaḥ ,prādurāsīdubhayato rājanmadhyaṁgate'hani.
english: O best of men, O King, at that moment, when the day was at its midpoint, widespread destruction of elephants, horses, and men occurred on both sides (of the army).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 40 Verse 43. O best of men, O King, at that moment, when the day was at its midpoint, widespread destruction of elephants, horses, and men occurred on both sides (of the army).

sanskrit: पाञ्चालास्तु महाराज त्वरिता विजिगीषवः ।सर्वतोऽभ्यद्रवन्कर्णं पतत्रिण इव द्रुमम् ॥४४॥
transliteration: pāñcālāstu mahārāja tvaritā vijigīṣavaḥ ,sarvato'bhyadravankarṇaṁ patatriṇa iva drumam.
english: But the Pañcālas, O great King, swift and desirous of victory, rushed from all sides towards Karṇa, just as birds (rush) towards a tree.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 40 Verse 44. But the Pañcālas, O great King, swift and desirous of victory, rushed from all sides towards Karṇa, just as birds (rush) towards a tree.

sanskrit: तेषामाधिरथिः क्रुद्धो यतमानान्मनस्विनः ।विचिन्वन्नेव बाणाग्रैः समासादयदग्रतः ॥४५॥
transliteration: teṣāmādhirathiḥ kruddho yatamānānmanasvinaḥ ,vicinvanneva bāṇāgraiḥ samāsādayadagrataḥ.
english: Enraged, Karṇa (the son of Adhiratha) encountered those resolute and striving (Pañcāla warriors) in front, continuously targeting them with his arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 40 Verse 45. Enraged, Karṇa (the son of Adhiratha) encountered those resolute and striving (Pañcāla warriors) in front, continuously targeting them with his arrows.

sanskrit: व्याघ्रकेतुं सुशर्माणं शङ्कुं चोग्रं धनंजयम् ।शुक्लं च रोचमानं च सिंहसेनं च दुर्जयम् ॥४६॥
transliteration: vyāghraketuṁ suśarmāṇaṁ śaṅkuṁ cograṁ dhanaṁjayam ,śuklaṁ ca rocamānaṁ ca siṁhasenaṁ ca durjayam.
english: They (struck down) Vyāghraketu, Suśarman, Śaṅku, Ugra, Dhanañjaya, Śukla, Rocamāna, Siṃhasena, and Durjaya.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 40 Verse 46. They (struck down) Vyāghraketu, Suśarman, Śaṅku, Ugra, Dhanañjaya, Śukla, Rocamāna, Siṃhasena, and Durjaya.

sanskrit: ते वीरा रथवेगेन परिवव्रुर्नरोत्तमम् ।सृजन्तं सायकान्क्रुद्धं कर्णमाहवशोभिनम् ॥४७॥
transliteration: te vīrā rathavegena parivavrurnarottamam ,sṛjantaṁ sāyakānkruddhaṁ karṇamāhavaśobhinam.
english: Those heroes swiftly surrounded Karṇa, the best of men, who was enraged, showering arrows, and brilliantly shining in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 40 Verse 47. Those heroes swiftly surrounded Karṇa, the best of men, who was enraged, showering arrows, and brilliantly shining in battle.

sanskrit: युध्यमानांस्तु ताञ्शूरान्मनुजेन्द्रः प्रतापवान् ।अष्टाभिरष्टौ राधेयो न्यहनन्निशितैः शरैः ॥४८॥
transliteration: yudhyamānāṁstu tāñśūrānmanujendraḥ pratāpavān ,aṣṭābhiraṣṭau rādheyo nyahananniśitaiḥ śaraiḥ.
english: But the mighty lord of men, Karṇa (rādheya), with eight sharp arrows, struck down those eight brave warriors who were fighting.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 40 Verse 48. But the mighty lord of men, Karṇa (rādheya), with eight sharp arrows, struck down those eight brave warriors who were fighting.

sanskrit: अथापरान्महाराज सूतपुत्रः प्रतापवान् ।जघान बहुसाहस्रान्योधान्युद्धविशारदः ॥४९॥
transliteration: athāparānmahārāja sūtaputraḥ pratāpavān ,jaghāna bahusāhasrānyodhānyuddhaviśāradaḥ.
english: Then, O great king, the mighty son of a charioteer (Karṇa), who was skilled in battle, slew thousands of other warriors.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 40 Verse 49. Then, O great king, the mighty son of a charioteer (Karṇa), who was skilled in battle, slew thousands of other warriors.

sanskrit: विष्णुं च विष्णुकर्माणं देवापिं भद्रमेव च ।दण्डं च समरे राजंश्चित्रं चित्रायुधं हरिम् ॥५०॥
transliteration: viṣṇuṁ ca viṣṇukarmāṇaṁ devāpiṁ bhadrameva ca ,daṇḍaṁ ca samare rājaṁścitraṁ citrāyudhaṁ harim.
english: O King, in that battle, he (killed) Vishnu, Vishnukarman, Devapi, and Bhadra; and also Daṇḍa, Citra, Citrayudha, and Hari.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 40 Verse 50. O King, in that battle, he (killed) Vishnu, Vishnukarman, Devapi, and Bhadra; and also Daṇḍa, Citra, Citrayudha, and Hari.

sanskrit: सिंहकेतुं रोचमानं शलभं च महारथम् ।निजघान सुसंक्रुद्धश्चेदीनां च महारथान् ॥५१॥
transliteration: siṁhaketuṁ rocamānaṁ śalabhaṁ ca mahāratham ,nijaghāna susaṁkruddhaścedīnāṁ ca mahārathān.
english: Greatly enraged, he killed Simhaketu, Rocamana, and Salabha, who was a great charioteer; and also the great charioteers of the Cedis.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 40 Verse 51. Greatly enraged, he killed Simhaketu, Rocamana, and Salabha, who was a great charioteer; and also the great charioteers of the Cedis.

sanskrit: तेषामाददतः प्राणानासीदाधिरथेर्वपुः ।शोणिताभ्युक्षिताङ्गस्य रुद्रस्येवोर्जितं महत् ॥५२॥
transliteration: teṣāmādadataḥ prāṇānāsīdādhirathervapuḥ ,śoṇitābhyukṣitāṅgasya rudrasyevorjitaṁ mahat.
english: As he took their lives, the body of Adhirathi's son (Karṇa) became mighty and great, with limbs drenched in blood, like that of Rudra.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 40 Verse 52. As he took their lives, the body of Adhirathi's son (Karṇa) became mighty and great, with limbs drenched in blood, like that of Rudra.

sanskrit: तत्र भारत कर्णेन मातङ्गास्ताडिताः शरैः ।सर्वतोऽभ्यद्रवन्भीताः कुर्वन्तो महदाकुलम् ॥५३॥
transliteration: tatra bhārata karṇena mātaṅgāstāḍitāḥ śaraiḥ ,sarvato'bhyadravanbhītāḥ kurvanto mahadākulam.
english: There, O Bhārata, the elephants, struck by Karṇa with arrows, fled in fear from all directions, creating great confusion.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 40 Verse 53. There, O Bhārata, the elephants, struck by Karṇa with arrows, fled in fear from all directions, creating great confusion.

sanskrit: निपेतुरुर्व्यां समरे कर्णसायकपीडिताः ।कुर्वन्तो विविधान्नादान्वज्रनुन्ना इवाचलाः ॥५४॥
transliteration: nipetururvyāṁ samare karṇasāyakapīḍitāḥ ,kurvanto vividhānnādānvajranunnā ivācalāḥ.
english: Afflicted by Karna's arrows in battle, they fell to the earth, uttering various cries, like mountains struck down by a thunderbolt.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 40 Verse 54. Afflicted by Karna's arrows in battle, they fell to the earth, uttering various cries, like mountains struck down by a thunderbolt.

sanskrit: गजवाजिमनुष्यैश्च निपतद्भिः समन्ततः ।रथैश्चावगतैर्मार्गे पर्यस्तीर्यत मेदिनी ॥५५॥
transliteration: gajavājimanuṣyaiśca nipatadbhiḥ samantataḥ ,rathaiścāvagatairmārge paryastīryata medinī.
english: The ground (medinī) was strewn all over with falling elephants, horses, and men, and with chariots that had fallen along the path.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 40 Verse 55. The ground (medinī) was strewn all over with falling elephants, horses, and men, and with chariots that had fallen along the path.

sanskrit: नैव भीष्मो न च द्रोणो नाप्यन्ये युधि तावकाः ।चक्रुः स्म तादृशं कर्म यादृशं वै कृतं रणे ॥५६॥
transliteration: naiva bhīṣmo na ca droṇo nāpyanye yudhi tāvakāḥ ,cakruḥ sma tādṛśaṁ karma yādṛśaṁ vai kṛtaṁ raṇe.
english: Neither Bhishma nor Drona, nor indeed any other of your warriors (tāvaka) in battle, performed such a deed (karma) as was accomplished in this fight.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 40 Verse 56. Neither Bhishma nor Drona, nor indeed any other of your warriors (tāvaka) in battle, performed such a deed (karma) as was accomplished in this fight.

sanskrit: सूतपुत्रेण नागेषु रथेषु च हयेषु च ।नरेषु च नरव्याघ्र कृतं स्म कदनं महत् ॥५७॥
transliteration: sūtaputreṇa nāgeṣu ratheṣu ca hayeṣu ca ,nareṣu ca naravyāghra kṛtaṁ sma kadanaṁ mahat.
english: By the charioteer's son (Karna), O tiger among men, truly great destruction was inflicted upon elephants, chariots, horses, and men.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 40 Verse 57. By the charioteer's son (Karna), O tiger among men, truly great destruction was inflicted upon elephants, chariots, horses, and men.

sanskrit: मृगमध्ये यथा सिंहो दृश्यते निर्भयश्चरन् ।पाञ्चालानां तथा मध्ये कर्णोऽचरदभीतवत् ॥५८॥
transliteration: mṛgamadhye yathā siṁho dṛśyate nirbhayaścaran ,pāñcālānāṁ tathā madhye karṇo'caradabhītavat.
english: Just as a lion is seen roaming fearlessly among deer, similarly, Karna roamed fearlessly among the Panchalas.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 40 Verse 58. Just as a lion is seen roaming fearlessly among deer, similarly, Karna roamed fearlessly among the Panchalas.

sanskrit: यथा मृगगणांस्त्रस्तान्सिंहो द्रावयते दिशः ।पाञ्चालानां रथव्रातान्कर्णो द्रावयते तथा ॥५९॥
transliteration: yathā mṛgagaṇāṁstrastānsiṁho drāvayate diśaḥ ,pāñcālānāṁ rathavrātānkarṇo drāvayate tathā.
english: Just as a lion scatters terrified herds of deer in all directions, similarly, Karna scatters the hosts of chariots of the Panchalas.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 40 Verse 59. Just as a lion scatters terrified herds of deer in all directions, similarly, Karna scatters the hosts of chariots of the Panchalas.

sanskrit: सिंहास्यं च यथा प्राप्य न जीवन्ति मृगाः क्वचित् ।तथा कर्णमनुप्राप्य न जीवन्ति महारथाः ॥६०॥
transliteration: siṁhāsyaṁ ca yathā prāpya na jīvanti mṛgāḥ kvacit ,tathā karṇamanuprāpya na jīvanti mahārathāḥ.
english: And just as deer, having entered a lion's mouth, never survive anywhere, similarly, great charioteers, having encountered Karna, do not survive.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 40 Verse 60. And just as deer, having entered a lion's mouth, never survive anywhere, similarly, great charioteers, having encountered Karna, do not survive.

sanskrit: वैश्वानरं यथा दीप्तं दह्यन्ते प्राप्य वै जनाः ।कर्णाग्निना रणे तद्वद्दग्धा भारत सृञ्जयाः ॥६१॥
transliteration: vaiśvānaraṁ yathā dīptaṁ dahyante prāpya vai janāḥ ,karṇāgninā raṇe tadvaddagdhā bhārata sṛñjayāḥ.
english: O Bharata, just as people are indeed consumed by a blazing fire (vaiśvānara) upon approaching it, similarly, the Srinjayas were consumed by the fire of Karna (karṇāgni) in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 40 Verse 61. O Bharata, just as people are indeed consumed by a blazing fire (vaiśvānara) upon approaching it, similarly, the Srinjayas were consumed by the fire of Karna (karṇāgni) in battle.

sanskrit: कर्णेन चेदिष्वेकेन पाञ्चालेषु च भारत ।विश्राव्य नाम निहता बहवः शूरसंमताः ॥६२॥
transliteration: karṇena cediṣvekena pāñcāleṣu ca bhārata ,viśrāvya nāma nihatā bahavaḥ śūrasaṁmatāḥ.
english: O descendant of Bharata, Karna alone, having made his name known, slew many who were considered heroes among the Chedis and the Pāñcālas.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 40 Verse 62. O descendant of Bharata, Karna alone, having made his name known, slew many who were considered heroes among the Chedis and the Pāñcālas.

sanskrit: मम चासीन्मनुष्येन्द्र दृष्ट्वा कर्णस्य विक्रमम् ।नैकोऽप्याधिरथेर्जीवन्पाञ्चाल्यो मोक्ष्यते युधि ॥६३॥
transliteration: mama cāsīnmanuṣyendra dṛṣṭvā karṇasya vikramam ,naiko'pyādhiratherjīvanpāñcālyo mokṣyate yudhi.
english: And O king of men, upon seeing Karna's valor (vikrama), I believed that not even one Pāñcāla would escape alive from Adhiratha's son in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 40 Verse 63. And O king of men, upon seeing Karna's valor (vikrama), I believed that not even one Pāñcāla would escape alive from Adhiratha's son in battle.

sanskrit: पाञ्चालान्विधमन्संख्ये सूतपुत्रः प्रतापवान् ।अभ्यधावत संक्रुद्धो धर्मपुत्रं युधिष्ठिरम् ॥६४॥
transliteration: pāñcālānvidhamansaṁkhye sūtaputraḥ pratāpavān ,abhyadhāvata saṁkruddho dharmaputraṁ yudhiṣṭhiram.
english: The mighty son of Sūta (Karna), scattering the Pāñcālas in battle, became enraged and rushed towards Dharmaputra (son of dharma) Yudhiṣṭhira.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 40 Verse 64. The mighty son of Sūta (Karna), scattering the Pāñcālas in battle, became enraged and rushed towards Dharmaputra (son of dharma) Yudhiṣṭhira.

sanskrit: धृष्टद्युम्नश्च राजानं द्रौपदेयाश्च मारिष ।परिवव्रुरमित्रघ्नं शतशश्चापरे जनाः ॥६५॥
transliteration: dhṛṣṭadyumnaśca rājānaṁ draupadeyāśca māriṣa ,parivavruramitraghnaṁ śataśaścāpare janāḥ.
english: And O venerable one, Dhṛṣṭadyumna, the sons of Draupadī, and hundreds of other people surrounded the king, the slayer of enemies.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 40 Verse 65. And O venerable one, Dhṛṣṭadyumna, the sons of Draupadī, and hundreds of other people surrounded the king, the slayer of enemies.

sanskrit: शिखण्डी सहदेवश्च नकुलो नाकुलिस्तथा ।जनमेजयः शिनेर्नप्ता बहवश्च प्रभद्रकाः ॥६६॥
transliteration: śikhaṇḍī sahadevaśca nakulo nākulistathā ,janamejayaḥ śinernaptā bahavaśca prabhadrakāḥ.
english: Shikhaṇḍī, Sahadeva, Nakula, Nākuli, Janamejaya (the grandson of Śini), and many Prabhadrakas were present.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 40 Verse 66. Shikhaṇḍī, Sahadeva, Nakula, Nākuli, Janamejaya (the grandson of Śini), and many Prabhadrakas were present.

sanskrit: एते पुरोगमा भूत्वा धृष्टद्युम्नस्य संयुगे ।कर्णमस्यन्तमिष्वस्त्रैर्विचेरुरमितौजसः ॥६७॥
transliteration: ete purogamā bhūtvā dhṛṣṭadyumnasya saṁyuge ,karṇamasyantamiṣvastrairviceruramitaujasaḥ.
english: These warriors, acting as Dhṛṣṭadyumna's vanguard in battle, moved about with immense power, assailing Karṇa with their arrow-weapons.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 40 Verse 67. These warriors, acting as Dhṛṣṭadyumna's vanguard in battle, moved about with immense power, assailing Karṇa with their arrow-weapons.

sanskrit: तांस्तत्राधिरथिः संख्ये चेदिपाञ्चालपाण्डवान् ।एको बहूनभ्यपतद्गरुत्मन्पन्नगानिव ॥६८॥
transliteration: tāṁstatrādhirathiḥ saṁkhye cedipāñcālapāṇḍavān ,eko bahūnabhyapatadgarutmanpannagāniva.
english: There, Adhirathi (Karṇa), alone in battle, attacked those many Chedi, Pāñcāla, and Pāṇḍava warriors, just as Garuḍa swoops down upon serpents.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 40 Verse 68. There, Adhirathi (Karṇa), alone in battle, attacked those many Chedi, Pāñcāla, and Pāṇḍava warriors, just as Garuḍa swoops down upon serpents.

sanskrit: भीमसेनस्तु संक्रुद्धः कुरून्मद्रान्सकेकयान् ।एकः संख्ये महेष्वासो योधयन्बह्वशोभत ॥६९॥
transliteration: bhīmasenastu saṁkruddhaḥ kurūnmadrānsakekayān ,ekaḥ saṁkhye maheṣvāso yodhayanbahvaśobhata.
english: But Bhīmasena, greatly enraged, himself a mighty archer, shone splendidly in battle while fighting the Kurus, Madras, and Kekayas.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 40 Verse 69. But Bhīmasena, greatly enraged, himself a mighty archer, shone splendidly in battle while fighting the Kurus, Madras, and Kekayas.

sanskrit: तत्र मर्मसु भीमेन नाराचैस्ताडिता गजाः ।प्रपतन्तो हतारोहाः कम्पयन्ति स्म मेदिनीम् ॥७०॥
transliteration: tatra marmasu bhīmena nārācaistāḍitā gajāḥ ,prapatanto hatārohāḥ kampayanti sma medinīm.
english: There, elephants, struck in their vital parts by (Bhīma) Bhīmasena's arrows, fell with their riders killed, causing the earth to tremble.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 40 Verse 70. There, elephants, struck in their vital parts by (Bhīma) Bhīmasena's arrows, fell with their riders killed, causing the earth to tremble.

sanskrit: वाजिनश्च हतारोहाः पत्तयश्च गतासवः ।शेरते युधि निर्भिन्ना वमन्तो रुधिरं बहु ॥७१॥
transliteration: vājinaśca hatārohāḥ pattayaśca gatāsavaḥ ,śerate yudhi nirbhinnā vamanto rudhiraṁ bahu.
english: And horses whose riders were killed, along with foot soldiers who had lost their lives, lay pierced on the battlefield, profusely vomiting blood.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 40 Verse 71. And horses whose riders were killed, along with foot soldiers who had lost their lives, lay pierced on the battlefield, profusely vomiting blood.

sanskrit: सहस्रशश्च रथिनः पतिताः पतितायुधाः ।अक्षताः समदृश्यन्त भीमाद्भीता गतासवः ॥७२॥
transliteration: sahasraśaśca rathinaḥ patitāḥ patitāyudhāḥ ,akṣatāḥ samadṛśyanta bhīmādbhītā gatāsavaḥ.
english: And thousands of charioteers, though seemingly unharmed and with their weapons fallen, were seen lying dead, terrified by (Bhīma) Bhīmasena.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 40 Verse 72. And thousands of charioteers, though seemingly unharmed and with their weapons fallen, were seen lying dead, terrified by (Bhīma) Bhīmasena.

sanskrit: रथिभिर्वाजिभिः सूतैः पत्तिभिश्च तथा गजैः ।भीमसेनशरच्छिन्नैरास्तीर्णा वसुधाभवत् ॥७३॥
transliteration: rathibhirvājibhiḥ sūtaiḥ pattibhiśca tathā gajaiḥ ,bhīmasenaśaracchinnairāstīrṇā vasudhābhavat.
english: The earth became covered with charioteers, horses, chariot drivers, foot soldiers, and elephants, all of whom had been torn apart by (Bhīma) Bhīmasena's arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 40 Verse 73. The earth became covered with charioteers, horses, chariot drivers, foot soldiers, and elephants, all of whom had been torn apart by (Bhīma) Bhīmasena's arrows.

sanskrit: तत्स्तम्भितमिवातिष्ठद्भीमसेनबलार्दितम् ।दुर्योधनबलं राजन्निरुत्साहं कृतव्रणम् ॥७४॥
transliteration: tatstambhitamivātiṣṭhadbhīmasenabalārditam ,duryodhanabalaṁ rājannirutsāhaṁ kṛtavraṇam.
english: O King, Duryodhana's army, afflicted by Bhimasena's might, stood as if paralyzed, dispirited and covered with wounds.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 40 Verse 74. O King, Duryodhana's army, afflicted by Bhimasena's might, stood as if paralyzed, dispirited and covered with wounds.

sanskrit: निश्चेष्टं तुमुले दीनं बभौ तस्मिन्महारणे ।प्रसन्नसलिलः काले यथा स्यात्सागरो नृप ॥७५॥
transliteration: niśceṣṭaṁ tumule dīnaṁ babhau tasminmahāraṇe ,prasannasalilaḥ kāle yathā syātsāgaro nṛpa.
english: O King, in that great battle, it (Duryodhana's army) appeared helpless, motionless, and miserable, just as an ocean in the dry season becomes calm with clear waters.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 40 Verse 75. O King, in that great battle, it (Duryodhana's army) appeared helpless, motionless, and miserable, just as an ocean in the dry season becomes calm with clear waters.

sanskrit: मन्युवीर्यबलोपेतं बलात्पर्यवरोपितम् ।अभवत्तव पुत्रस्य तत्सैन्यमिषुभिस्तदा ।रुधिरौघपरिक्लिन्नं रुधिरार्द्रं बभूव ह ॥७६॥
transliteration: manyuvīryabalopetaṁ balātparyavaropitam ,abhavattava putrasya tatsainyamiṣubhistadā ,rudhiraughapariklinnaṁ rudhirārdraṁ babhūva ha.
english: Then, your son's army, despite being endowed with fury, valor, and strength, was forcibly subdued by arrows. Indeed, that army became thoroughly drenched and soaked with streams of blood.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 40 Verse 76. Then, your son's army, despite being endowed with fury, valor, and strength, was forcibly subdued by arrows. Indeed, that army became thoroughly drenched and soaked with streams of blood.

sanskrit: सूतपुत्रो रणे क्रुद्धः पाण्डवानामनीकिनीम् ।भीमसेनः कुरूंश्चापि द्रावयन्बह्वशोभत ॥७७॥
transliteration: sūtaputro raṇe kruddhaḥ pāṇḍavānāmanīkinīm ,bhīmasenaḥ kurūṁścāpi drāvayanbahvaśobhata.
english: The son of Suta (Karṇa), enraged in battle, appeared splendid as he routed the army of the Pandavas; and Bhimasena likewise shone greatly as he put the Kurus to flight.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 40 Verse 77. The son of Suta (Karṇa), enraged in battle, appeared splendid as he routed the army of the Pandavas; and Bhimasena likewise shone greatly as he put the Kurus to flight.

sanskrit: वर्तमाने तथा रौद्रे संग्रामेऽद्भुतदर्शने ।निहत्य पृतनामध्ये संशप्तकगणान्बहून् ॥७८॥
transliteration: vartamāne tathā raudre saṁgrāme'dbhutadarśane ,nihatya pṛtanāmadhye saṁśaptakagaṇānbahūn.
english: In the midst of the terrible and wondrous battle currently ongoing, having slain many hosts of Saṃśaptakas within the army,
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 40 Verse 78. In the midst of the terrible and wondrous battle currently ongoing, having slain many hosts of Saṃśaptakas within the army,

sanskrit: अर्जुनो जयतां श्रेष्ठो वासुदेवमथाब्रवीत् ।प्रभग्नं बलमेतद्धि योत्स्यमानं जनार्दन ॥७९॥
transliteration: arjuno jayatāṁ śreṣṭho vāsudevamathābravīt ,prabhagnaṁ balametaddhi yotsyamānaṁ janārdana.
english: Arjuna, the best among conquerors, then said to Vāsudeva: "O Janārdana, this army, which was about to fight, is indeed routed!"
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 40 Verse 79. Arjuna, the best among conquerors, then said to Vāsudeva: "O Janārdana, this army, which was about to fight, is indeed routed!"

sanskrit: एते धावन्ति सगणाः संशप्तकमहारथाः ।अपारयन्तो मद्बाणान्सिंहशब्दान्मृगा इव ॥८०॥
transliteration: ete dhāvanti sagaṇāḥ saṁśaptakamahārathāḥ ,apārayanto madbāṇānsiṁhaśabdānmṛgā iva.
english: These great chariot warriors of the Saṃśaptakas, along with their hosts, are fleeing, unable to withstand my arrows, just like deer (flee) from the roar of a lion.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 40 Verse 80. These great chariot warriors of the Saṃśaptakas, along with their hosts, are fleeing, unable to withstand my arrows, just like deer (flee) from the roar of a lion.

sanskrit: दीर्यते च महत्सैन्यं सृञ्जयानां महारणे ।हस्तिकक्ष्यो ह्यसौ कृष्ण केतुः कर्णस्य धीमतः ।दृश्यते राजसैन्यस्य मध्ये विचरतो मुहुः ॥८१॥
transliteration: dīryate ca mahatsainyaṁ sṛñjayānāṁ mahāraṇe ,hastikakṣyo hyasau kṛṣṇa ketuḥ karṇasya dhīmataḥ ,dṛśyate rājasainyasya madhye vicarato muhuḥ.
english: And the great army of the Sṛñjayas is being torn apart in the great battle. O Kṛṣṇa, that standard bearing an elephant's girth - the banner of the intelligent Karṇa - is repeatedly seen moving about in the midst of the royal army.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 40 Verse 81. And the great army of the Sṛñjayas is being torn apart in the great battle. O Kṛṣṇa, that standard bearing an elephant's girth - the banner of the intelligent Karṇa - is repeatedly seen moving about in the midst of the royal army.

sanskrit: न च कर्णं रणे शक्ता जेतुमन्ये महारथाः ।जानीते हि भवान्कर्णं वीर्यवन्तं पराक्रमे ॥८२॥
transliteration: na ca karṇaṁ raṇe śaktā jetumanye mahārathāḥ ,jānīte hi bhavānkarṇaṁ vīryavantaṁ parākrame.
english: Furthermore, other great charioteers (mahārathas) are unable to conquer Karna in battle. Indeed, you yourself know Karna to be valorous in might.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 40 Verse 82. Furthermore, other great charioteers (mahārathas) are unable to conquer Karna in battle. Indeed, you yourself know Karna to be valorous in might.

sanskrit: तत्र याहि यतः कर्णोद्रावयत्येष नो बलम् ॥८३॥
transliteration: tatra yāhi yataḥ karṇodrāvayatyeṣa no balam.
english: Go there, to that place from which Karna is routing our forces.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 40 Verse 83. Go there, to that place from which Karna is routing our forces.

sanskrit: वर्जयित्वा रणे याहि सूतपुत्रं महारथम् ।श्रमो मा बाधते कृष्ण यथा वा तव रोचते ॥८४॥
transliteration: varjayitvā raṇe yāhi sūtaputraṁ mahāratham ,śramo mā bādhate kṛṣṇa yathā vā tava rocate.
english: Proceed in battle, avoiding the great charioteer (mahāratha), the son of Suta. May weariness (śrama) not afflict me, Krishna, or do as it pleases you.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 40 Verse 84. Proceed in battle, avoiding the great charioteer (mahāratha), the son of Suta. May weariness (śrama) not afflict me, Krishna, or do as it pleases you.

sanskrit: एतच्छ्रुत्वा महाराज गोविन्दः प्रहसन्निव ।अब्रवीदर्जुनं तूर्णं कौरवाञ्जहि पाण्डव ॥८५॥
transliteration: etacchrutvā mahārāja govindaḥ prahasanniva ,abravīdarjunaṁ tūrṇaṁ kauravāñjahi pāṇḍava.
english: Having heard this, O great king (mahārāja), Govinda, as if smiling, immediately (tūrṇaṃ) said to Arjuna: 'O son of Pandu (Pāṇḍava), conquer the Kauravas!'
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 40 Verse 85. Having heard this, O great king (mahārāja), Govinda, as if smiling, immediately (tūrṇaṃ) said to Arjuna: 'O son of Pandu (Pāṇḍava), conquer the Kauravas!'

sanskrit: ततस्तव महत्सैन्यं गोविन्दप्रेरिता हयाः ।हंसवर्णाः प्रविविशुर्वहन्तः कृष्णपाण्डवौ ॥८६॥
transliteration: tatastava mahatsainyaṁ govindapreritā hayāḥ ,haṁsavarṇāḥ praviviśurvahantaḥ kṛṣṇapāṇḍavau.
english: Then, your great army was entered by the swan-colored horses, impelled by Govinda (Krishna), as they carried Krishna and Arjuna.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 40 Verse 86. Then, your great army was entered by the swan-colored horses, impelled by Govinda (Krishna), as they carried Krishna and Arjuna.

sanskrit: केशवप्रहितैरश्वैः श्वेतैः काञ्चनभूषणैः ।प्रविशद्भिस्तव बलं चतुर्दिशमभिद्यत ॥८७॥
transliteration: keśavaprahitairaśvaiḥ śvetaiḥ kāñcanabhūṣaṇaiḥ ,praviśadbhistava balaṁ caturdiśamabhidyata.
english: Your army was broken apart in all four directions by the white horses, adorned with gold ornaments, sent by Keśava (Krishna), as they entered.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 40 Verse 87. Your army was broken apart in all four directions by the white horses, adorned with gold ornaments, sent by Keśava (Krishna), as they entered.

sanskrit: तौ विदार्य महासेनां प्रविष्टौ केशवार्जुनौ ।क्रुद्धौ संरम्भरक्ताक्षौ व्यभ्राजेतां महाद्युती ॥८८॥
transliteration: tau vidārya mahāsenāṁ praviṣṭau keśavārjunau ,kruddhau saṁrambharaktākṣau vyabhrājetāṁ mahādyutī.
english: Those two, Keśava (Krishna) and Arjuna, having torn apart and entered the great army, appeared enraged, with eyes reddened by fury, and shone with great radiance.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 40 Verse 88. Those two, Keśava (Krishna) and Arjuna, having torn apart and entered the great army, appeared enraged, with eyes reddened by fury, and shone with great radiance.

sanskrit: युद्धशौण्डौ समाहूतावरिभिस्तौ रणाध्वरम् ।यज्वभिर्विधिनाहूतौ मखे देवाविवाश्विनौ ॥८९॥
transliteration: yuddhaśauṇḍau samāhūtāvaribhistau raṇādhvaram ,yajvabhirvidhināhūtau makhe devāvivāśvinau.
english: Called by their enemies to the battlefield which is a Vedic ritual (raṇādhvara), those two brave warriors, skilled in battle, were like the two Aśvin gods, duly invited by sacrificers to a Vedic ritual (makha).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 40 Verse 89. Called by their enemies to the battlefield which is a Vedic ritual (raṇādhvara), those two brave warriors, skilled in battle, were like the two Aśvin gods, duly invited by sacrificers to a Vedic ritual (makha).

sanskrit: क्रुद्धौ तौ तु नरव्याघ्रौ वेगवन्तौ बभूवतुः ।तलशब्देन रुषितौ यथा नागौ महाहवे ॥९०॥
transliteration: kruddhau tau tu naravyāghrau vegavantau babhūvatuḥ ,talaśabdena ruṣitau yathā nāgau mahāhave.
english: Those two, who were like tigers among men, became enraged and swift. They were provoked by the sound of palms, just like two elephants become furious in a great battle (mahāhava).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 40 Verse 90. Those two, who were like tigers among men, became enraged and swift. They were provoked by the sound of palms, just like two elephants become furious in a great battle (mahāhava).

sanskrit: विगाहन्स रथानीकमश्वसंघांश्च फल्गुनः ।व्यचरत्पृतनामध्ये पाशहस्त इवान्तकः ॥९१॥
transliteration: vigāhansa rathānīkamaśvasaṁghāṁśca phalgunaḥ ,vyacaratpṛtanāmadhye pāśahasta ivāntakaḥ.
english: Arjuna, penetrating the formations of chariots and the throngs of horses, moved through the midst of the army like Yama, the god of death, holding a noose in his hand.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 40 Verse 91. Arjuna, penetrating the formations of chariots and the throngs of horses, moved through the midst of the army like Yama, the god of death, holding a noose in his hand.

sanskrit: तं दृष्ट्वा युधि विक्रान्तं सेनायां तव भारत ।संशप्तकगणान्भूयः पुत्रस्ते समचोदयत् ॥९२॥
transliteration: taṁ dṛṣṭvā yudhi vikrāntaṁ senāyāṁ tava bhārata ,saṁśaptakagaṇānbhūyaḥ putraste samacodayat.
english: O Bhārata, having seen him displaying valor in your army during battle, your son (Duryodhana) once again incited the battalions of the Saṃśaptakas.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 40 Verse 92. O Bhārata, having seen him displaying valor in your army during battle, your son (Duryodhana) once again incited the battalions of the Saṃśaptakas.

sanskrit: ततो रथसहस्रेण द्विरदानां त्रिभिः शतैः ।चतुर्दशसहस्रैश्च तुरगाणां महाहवे ॥९३॥
transliteration: tato rathasahasreṇa dviradānāṁ tribhiḥ śataiḥ ,caturdaśasahasraiśca turagāṇāṁ mahāhave.
english: Then, in that great battle (mahāhava), with a thousand chariots, three hundred elephants, and fourteen thousand horses, (he advanced/attacked, referring to the mobilized troops under Duryodhana's command).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 40 Verse 93. Then, in that great battle (mahāhava), with a thousand chariots, three hundred elephants, and fourteen thousand horses, (he advanced/attacked, referring to the mobilized troops under Duryodhana's command).

sanskrit: द्वाभ्यां शतसहस्राभ्यां पदातीनां च धन्विनाम् ।शूराणां नामलब्धानां विदितानां समन्ततः ।अभ्यवर्तन्त तौ वीरौ छादयन्तो महारथाः ॥९४॥
transliteration: dvābhyāṁ śatasahasrābhyāṁ padātīnāṁ ca dhanvinām ,śūrāṇāṁ nāmalabdhānāṁ viditānāṁ samantataḥ ,abhyavartanta tau vīrau chādayanto mahārathāḥ.
english: Those two heroes, the great charioteers, advanced, covering [the battlefield], accompanied by a combined force of two hundred thousand foot-soldiers and archers, who were valiant and renowned, known from all directions.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 40 Verse 94. Those two heroes, the great charioteers, advanced, covering [the battlefield], accompanied by a combined force of two hundred thousand foot-soldiers and archers, who were valiant and renowned, known from all directions.

sanskrit: स छाद्यमानः समरे शरैः परबलार्दनः ।दर्शयन्रौद्रमात्मानं पाशहस्त इवान्तकः ।निघ्नन्संशप्तकान्पार्थः प्रेक्षणीयतरोऽभवत् ॥९५॥
transliteration: sa chādyamānaḥ samare śaraiḥ parabalārdanaḥ ,darśayanraudramātmānaṁ pāśahasta ivāntakaḥ ,nighnansaṁśaptakānpārthaḥ prekṣaṇīyataro'bhavat.
english: Covered by arrows in battle, that tormentor of enemy forces, Partha, became even more spectacular to behold. He displayed his fierce true nature (ātman) like Antaka (the god of death) holding a noose, as he struck down the Saṃśaptaka warriors.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 40 Verse 95. Covered by arrows in battle, that tormentor of enemy forces, Partha, became even more spectacular to behold. He displayed his fierce true nature (ātman) like Antaka (the god of death) holding a noose, as he struck down the Saṃśaptaka warriors.

sanskrit: ततो विद्युत्प्रभैर्बाणैः कार्तस्वरविभूषितैः ।निरन्तरमिवाकाशमासीन्नुन्नैः किरीटिना ॥९६॥
transliteration: tato vidyutprabhairbāṇaiḥ kārtasvaravibhūṣitaiḥ ,nirantaramivākāśamāsīnnunnaiḥ kirīṭinā.
english: Then, the sky became as if entirely filled, without any gaps, due to the arrows, which shone like lightning, were adorned with gold, and were shot by Kiriti (Arjuna).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 40 Verse 96. Then, the sky became as if entirely filled, without any gaps, due to the arrows, which shone like lightning, were adorned with gold, and were shot by Kiriti (Arjuna).
