sanskrit: किरीटिभुजनिर्मुक्तैः संपतद्भिर्महाशरैः ।समाच्छन्नं बभौ सर्वं काद्रवेयैरिव प्रभो ॥९७॥
transliteration: kirīṭibhujanirmuktaiḥ saṁpatadbhirmahāśaraiḥ ,samācchannaṁ babhau sarvaṁ kādraveyairiva prabho.
english: O lord, with great arrows swiftly flying and released from Kiriti's (Arjuna's) arm, everything became completely covered and shone brightly, as if by the Kadruveyas (serpents).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 40 Verse 97. O lord, with great arrows swiftly flying and released from Kiriti's (Arjuna's) arm, everything became completely covered and shone brightly, as if by the Kadruveyas (serpents).

sanskrit: रुक्मपुङ्खान्प्रसन्नाग्राञ्शरान्संनतपर्वणः ।अदर्शयदमेयात्मा दिक्षु सर्वासु पाण्डवः ॥९८॥
transliteration: rukmapuṅkhānprasannāgrāñśarānsaṁnataparvaṇaḥ ,adarśayadameyātmā dikṣu sarvāsu pāṇḍavaḥ.
english: The Pandava, whose soul (ātman) was immeasurable, displayed arrows with golden shafts, sharp tips, and bent nodes in all directions.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 40 Verse 98. The Pandava, whose soul (ātman) was immeasurable, displayed arrows with golden shafts, sharp tips, and bent nodes in all directions.

sanskrit: हत्वा दश सहस्राणि पार्थिवानां महारथः ।संशप्तकानां कौन्तेयः प्रपक्षं त्वरितोऽभ्ययात् ॥९९॥
transliteration: hatvā daśa sahasrāṇi pārthivānāṁ mahārathaḥ ,saṁśaptakānāṁ kaunteyaḥ prapakṣaṁ tvarito'bhyayāt.
english: Having slain ten thousand kings (pārthiva) from the Saṃśaptaka division, the great chariot-warrior (mahāratha), Kaunteya, swiftly advanced towards the flank (of the army).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 40 Verse 99. Having slain ten thousand kings (pārthiva) from the Saṃśaptaka division, the great chariot-warrior (mahāratha), Kaunteya, swiftly advanced towards the flank (of the army).

sanskrit: प्रपक्षं स समासाद्य पार्थः काम्बोजरक्षितम् ।प्रममाथ बलाद्बाणैर्दानवानिव वासवः ॥१००॥
transliteration: prapakṣaṁ sa samāsādya pārthaḥ kāmbojarakṣitam ,pramamātha balādbāṇairdānavāniva vāsavaḥ.
english: Having reached that flank, which was protected by the Kambojas, Partha (Arjuna) forcefully crushed it with arrows, just as Vasava (Indra) crushed the Danavas.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 40 Verse 100. Having reached that flank, which was protected by the Kambojas, Partha (Arjuna) forcefully crushed it with arrows, just as Vasava (Indra) crushed the Danavas.

sanskrit: प्रचिच्छेदाशु भल्लैश्च द्विषतामाततायिनाम् ।शस्त्रपाणींस्तथा बाहूंस्तथापि च शिरांस्युत ॥१०१॥
transliteration: pracicchedāśu bhallaiśca dviṣatāmātatāyinām ,śastrapāṇīṁstathā bāhūṁstathāpi ca śirāṁsyuta.
english: He swiftly cut off, with lances, the weapon-wielding arms of his aggressive enemies, and also their heads.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 40 Verse 101. He swiftly cut off, with lances, the weapon-wielding arms of his aggressive enemies, and also their heads.

sanskrit: अङ्गाङ्गावयवैश्छिन्नैर्व्यायुधास्तेऽपतन्क्षितौ ।विष्वग्वाताभिसंभग्ना बहुशाखा इव द्रुमाः ॥१०२॥
transliteration: aṅgāṅgāvayavaiśchinnairvyāyudhāste'patankṣitau ,viṣvagvātābhisaṁbhagnā bahuśākhā iva drumāḥ.
english: They fell to the ground with their limbs severed and weapons scattered, like multi-branched trees shattered in all directions by a powerful wind.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 40 Verse 102. They fell to the ground with their limbs severed and weapons scattered, like multi-branched trees shattered in all directions by a powerful wind.

sanskrit: हस्त्यश्वरथपत्तीनां व्रातान्निघ्नन्तमर्जुनम् ।सुदक्षिणादवरजः शरवृष्ट्याभ्यवीवृषत् ॥१०३॥
transliteration: hastyaśvarathapattīnāṁ vrātānnighnantamarjunam ,sudakṣiṇādavarajaḥ śaravṛṣṭyābhyavīvṛṣat.
english: Sudakṣiṇa's younger brother showered Arjuna, who was slaughtering hosts of elephants, horses, chariots, and foot-soldiers, with a barrage of arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 40 Verse 103. Sudakṣiṇa's younger brother showered Arjuna, who was slaughtering hosts of elephants, horses, chariots, and foot-soldiers, with a barrage of arrows.

sanskrit: अस्यास्यतोऽर्धचन्द्राभ्यां स बाहू परिघोपमौ ।पूर्णचन्द्राभवक्त्रं च क्षुरेणाभ्यहनच्छिरः ॥१०४॥
transliteration: asyāsyato'rdhacandrābhyāṁ sa bāhū parighopamau ,pūrṇacandrābhavaktraṁ ca kṣureṇābhyahanacchiraḥ.
english: With two crescent-shaped arrows, he (Arjuna) struck the two mace-like arms of his (opponent) who was shooting. And with a razor-sharp arrow (kṣura), he struck his (opponent's) head, whose face shone like a full moon.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 40 Verse 104. With two crescent-shaped arrows, he (Arjuna) struck the two mace-like arms of his (opponent) who was shooting. And with a razor-sharp arrow (kṣura), he struck his (opponent's) head, whose face shone like a full moon.

sanskrit: स पपात ततो वाहात्स्वलोहितपरिस्रवः ।मनःशिलागिरेः शृङ्गं वज्रेणेवावदारितम् ॥१०५॥
transliteration: sa papāta tato vāhātsvalohitaparisravaḥ ,manaḥśilāgireḥ śṛṅgaṁ vajreṇevāvadāritam.
english: He then fell from his chariot, with his own blood flowing profusely, like a peak of a realgar mountain shattered by a thunderbolt.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 40 Verse 105. He then fell from his chariot, with his own blood flowing profusely, like a peak of a realgar mountain shattered by a thunderbolt.

sanskrit: सुदक्षिणादवरजं काम्बोजं ददृशुर्हतम् ।प्रांशुं कमलपत्राक्षमत्यर्थं प्रियदर्शनम् ।काञ्चनस्तम्भसंकाशं भिन्नं हेमगिरिं यथा ॥१०६॥
transliteration: sudakṣiṇādavarajaṁ kāmbojaṁ dadṛśurhatam ,prāṁśuṁ kamalapatrākṣamatyarthaṁ priyadarśanam ,kāñcanastambhasaṁkāśaṁ bhinnaṁ hemagiriṁ yathā.
english: They saw Kāmboja, Sudakṣiṇā's younger brother, who was slain. He was tall, with eyes like lotus petals, exceedingly pleasant to behold, and resembled a golden pillar, like a shattered golden mountain.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 40 Verse 106. They saw Kāmboja, Sudakṣiṇā's younger brother, who was slain. He was tall, with eyes like lotus petals, exceedingly pleasant to behold, and resembled a golden pillar, like a shattered golden mountain.

sanskrit: ततोऽभवत्पुनर्युद्धं घोरमद्भुतदर्शनम् ।नानावस्थाश्च योधानां बभूवुस्तत्र युध्यताम् ॥१०७॥
transliteration: tato'bhavatpunaryuddhaṁ ghoramadbhutadarśanam ,nānāvasthāśca yodhānāṁ babhūvustatra yudhyatām.
english: Then, a terrible and wondrous battle took place again. And the warriors fighting there found themselves in various conditions.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 40 Verse 107. Then, a terrible and wondrous battle took place again. And the warriors fighting there found themselves in various conditions.

sanskrit: एतेष्वावर्जितैरश्वैः काम्बोजैर्यवनैः शकैः ।शोणिताक्तैस्तदा रक्तं सर्वमासीद्विशां पते ॥१०८॥
transliteration: eteṣvāvarjitairaśvaiḥ kāmbojairyavanaiḥ śakaiḥ ,śoṇitāktaistadā raktaṁ sarvamāsīdviśāṁ pate.
english: Then, O lord of the people, among these combatants, the Kambojas, Yavanas, and Shakas, with their horses that were forced to turn back, were all drenched in blood.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 40 Verse 108. Then, O lord of the people, among these combatants, the Kambojas, Yavanas, and Shakas, with their horses that were forced to turn back, were all drenched in blood.

sanskrit: रथै रथाश्वसूतैश्च हतारोहैश्च वाजिभिः ।द्विरदैश्च हतारोहैर्महामात्रैर्हतद्विपैः ।अन्योन्येन महाराज कृतो घोरो जनक्षयः ॥१०९॥
transliteration: rathai rathāśvasūtaiśca hatārohaiśca vājibhiḥ ,dviradaiśca hatārohairmahāmātrairhatadvipaiḥ ,anyonyena mahārāja kṛto ghoro janakṣayaḥ.
english: O great king, a terrible destruction of people was caused by mutual combat, by chariots, by charioteers and their chariot-horses, by horses whose riders were slain, by elephants whose riders were killed, and by chief mahouts whose elephants had been destroyed.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 40 Verse 109. O great king, a terrible destruction of people was caused by mutual combat, by chariots, by charioteers and their chariot-horses, by horses whose riders were slain, by elephants whose riders were killed, and by chief mahouts whose elephants had been destroyed.

sanskrit: तस्मिन्प्रपक्षे पक्षे च वध्यमाने महात्मना ।अर्जुनं जयतां श्रेष्ठं त्वरितो द्रौणिराययौ ॥११०॥
transliteration: tasminprapakṣe pakṣe ca vadhyamāne mahātmanā ,arjunaṁ jayatāṁ śreṣṭhaṁ tvarito drauṇirāyayau.
english: As that vanguard (prapakṣa) and flank (pakṣa) were being slaughtered by the great-souled (mahātman) Arjuna, Droṇi swiftly advanced towards Arjuna, the foremost among victors.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 40 Verse 110. As that vanguard (prapakṣa) and flank (pakṣa) were being slaughtered by the great-souled (mahātman) Arjuna, Droṇi swiftly advanced towards Arjuna, the foremost among victors.

sanskrit: विधुन्वानो महच्चापं कार्तस्वरविभूषितम् ।आददानः शरान्घोरान्स्वरश्मीनिव भास्करः ॥१११॥
transliteration: vidhunvāno mahaccāpaṁ kārtasvaravibhūṣitam ,ādadānaḥ śarānghorānsvaraśmīniva bhāskaraḥ.
english: Wielding his great bow, which was adorned with gold, and taking up terrible arrows, he (Droṇi) appeared like the sun gathering its own rays.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 40 Verse 111. Wielding his great bow, which was adorned with gold, and taking up terrible arrows, he (Droṇi) appeared like the sun gathering its own rays.

sanskrit: तैः पतद्भिर्महाराज द्रौणिमुक्तैः समन्ततः ।संछादितौ रथस्थौ तावुभौ कृष्णधनंजयौ ॥११२॥
transliteration: taiḥ patadbhirmahārāja drauṇimuktaiḥ samantataḥ ,saṁchāditau rathasthau tāvubhau kṛṣṇadhanaṁjayau.
english: O great king (mahārāja), those falling arrows, released by Droṇi from all directions, completely covered both Kṛṣṇa and Arjuna, who were seated in their chariot.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 40 Verse 112. O great king (mahārāja), those falling arrows, released by Droṇi from all directions, completely covered both Kṛṣṇa and Arjuna, who were seated in their chariot.

sanskrit: ततः शरशतैस्तीक्ष्णैर्भारद्वाजः प्रतापवान् ।निश्चेष्टौ तावुभौ चक्रे युद्धे माधवपाण्डवौ ॥११३॥
transliteration: tataḥ śaraśataistīkṣṇairbhāradvājaḥ pratāpavān ,niśceṣṭau tāvubhau cakre yuddhe mādhavapāṇḍavau.
english: Then, the powerful Bhāradvāja (Droṇi), with hundreds of sharp arrows, rendered both Mādhava (Kṛṣṇa) and the Pāṇḍava (Arjuna) motionless in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 40 Verse 113. Then, the powerful Bhāradvāja (Droṇi), with hundreds of sharp arrows, rendered both Mādhava (Kṛṣṇa) and the Pāṇḍava (Arjuna) motionless in battle.

sanskrit: हाहाकृतमभूत्सर्वं जङ्गमं स्थावरं तथा ।चराचरस्य गोप्तारौ दृष्ट्वा संछादितौ शरैः ॥११४॥
transliteration: hāhākṛtamabhūtsarvaṁ jaṅgamaṁ sthāvaraṁ tathā ,carācarasya goptārau dṛṣṭvā saṁchāditau śaraiḥ.
english: All moving and stationary beings cried out in dismay upon seeing the two protectors of the entire universe enveloped by arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 40 Verse 114. All moving and stationary beings cried out in dismay upon seeing the two protectors of the entire universe enveloped by arrows.

sanskrit: सिद्धचारणसंघाश्च संपेतुर्वै समन्ततः ।चिन्तयन्तो भवेदद्य लोकानां स्वस्त्यपीत्यह ॥११५॥
transliteration: siddhacāraṇasaṁghāśca saṁpeturvai samantataḥ ,cintayanto bhavedadya lokānāṁ svastyapītyaha.
english: And indeed, hosts of Siddhas and Cāraṇas rushed from all sides, wondering, "Will there truly be well-being for the worlds today?"
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 40 Verse 115. And indeed, hosts of Siddhas and Cāraṇas rushed from all sides, wondering, "Will there truly be well-being for the worlds today?"

sanskrit: न मया तादृशो राजन्दृष्टपूर्वः पराक्रमः ।संजज्ञे यादृशो द्रौणेः कृष्णौ संछादयिष्यतः ॥११६॥
transliteration: na mayā tādṛśo rājandṛṣṭapūrvaḥ parākramaḥ ,saṁjajñe yādṛśo drauṇeḥ kṛṣṇau saṁchādayiṣyataḥ.
english: O King, I have never before seen such valor as that manifested by Droṇa's son (Aśvatthāmā), who was about to overwhelm both Kṛṣṇa and Arjuna.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 40 Verse 116. O King, I have never before seen such valor as that manifested by Droṇa's son (Aśvatthāmā), who was about to overwhelm both Kṛṣṇa and Arjuna.

sanskrit: द्रौणेस्तु धनुषः शब्दमहितत्रासनं रणे ।अश्रौषं बहुशो राजन्सिंहस्य नदतो यथा ॥११७॥
transliteration: drauṇestu dhanuṣaḥ śabdamahitatrāsanaṁ raṇe ,aśrauṣaṁ bahuśo rājansiṁhasya nadato yathā.
english: O King, I repeatedly heard the sound of Droṇa's son's bow in battle, a sound terrifying to his enemies, just like the roar of a lion.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 40 Verse 117. O King, I repeatedly heard the sound of Droṇa's son's bow in battle, a sound terrifying to his enemies, just like the roar of a lion.

sanskrit: ज्या चास्य चरतो युद्धे सव्यदक्षिणमस्यतः ।विद्युदम्बुदमध्यस्था भ्राजमानेव साभवत् ॥११८॥
transliteration: jyā cāsya carato yuddhe savyadakṣiṇamasyataḥ ,vidyudambudamadhyasthā bhrājamāneva sābhavat.
english: And as he moved in battle, shooting (arrows) left and right, his bowstring became like a flash of lightning gleaming amidst the clouds.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 40 Verse 118. And as he moved in battle, shooting (arrows) left and right, his bowstring became like a flash of lightning gleaming amidst the clouds.

sanskrit: स तथा क्षिप्रकारी च दृढहस्तश्च पाण्डवः ।संमोहं परमं गत्वा प्रैक्षत द्रोणजं ततः ॥११९॥
transliteration: sa tathā kṣiprakārī ca dṛḍhahastaśca pāṇḍavaḥ ,saṁmohaṁ paramaṁ gatvā praikṣata droṇajaṁ tataḥ.
english: Though that Pāṇḍava (Arjuna) was so swift in action and firm-handed, he became utterly bewildered (saṃmoha) and then looked at Drona's son (Aśvatthāman).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 40 Verse 119. Though that Pāṇḍava (Arjuna) was so swift in action and firm-handed, he became utterly bewildered (saṃmoha) and then looked at Drona's son (Aśvatthāman).

sanskrit: स विक्रमं हृतं मेने आत्मनः सुमहात्मना ।तथास्य समरे राजन्वपुरासीत्सुदुर्दृशम् ॥१२०॥
transliteration: sa vikramaṁ hṛtaṁ mene ātmanaḥ sumahātmanā ,tathāsya samare rājanvapurāsītsudurdṛśam.
english: He (Arjuna) considered his own valor (vikrama) to have been diminished by that supremely great soul (Aśvatthāman). O King, his form (vapus) in battle was then very difficult to behold.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 40 Verse 120. He (Arjuna) considered his own valor (vikrama) to have been diminished by that supremely great soul (Aśvatthāman). O King, his form (vapus) in battle was then very difficult to behold.

sanskrit: द्रौणिपाण्डवयोरेवं वर्तमाने महारणे ।वर्धमाने च राजेन्द्र द्रोणपुत्रे महाबले ।हीयमाने च कौन्तेये कृष्णं रोषः समभ्ययात् ॥१२१॥
transliteration: drauṇipāṇḍavayorevaṁ vartamāne mahāraṇe ,vardhamāne ca rājendra droṇaputre mahābale ,hīyamāne ca kaunteye kṛṣṇaṁ roṣaḥ samabhyayāt.
english: O King of kings, as this great battle (mahāraṇa) raged between Droṇa's son (Aśvatthāman) and Pāṇḍu's son (Arjuna), with the greatly powerful son of Droṇa gaining the upper hand and Kuntī's son (Arjuna) losing ground, anger suddenly overwhelmed Kṛṣṇa.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 40 Verse 121. O King of kings, as this great battle (mahāraṇa) raged between Droṇa's son (Aśvatthāman) and Pāṇḍu's son (Arjuna), with the greatly powerful son of Droṇa gaining the upper hand and Kuntī's son (Arjuna) losing ground, anger suddenly overwhelmed Kṛṣṇa.

sanskrit: स रोषान्निःश्वसन्राजन्निर्दहन्निव चक्षुषा ।द्रौणिं ह्यपश्यत्संग्रामे फल्गुनं च मुहुर्मुहुः ॥१२२॥
transliteration: sa roṣānniḥśvasanrājannirdahanniva cakṣuṣā ,drauṇiṁ hyapaśyatsaṁgrāme phalgunaṁ ca muhurmuhuḥ.
english: O King, he (Duryodhana), sighing with rage, as if burning with his eyes, repeatedly saw Drauni and Arjuna (Phalguna) in the battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 40 Verse 122. O King, he (Duryodhana), sighing with rage, as if burning with his eyes, repeatedly saw Drauni and Arjuna (Phalguna) in the battle.

sanskrit: ततः क्रुद्धोऽब्रवीत्कृष्णः पार्थं सप्रणयं तदा ।अत्यद्भुतमिदं पार्थ तव पश्यामि संयुगे ।अतिशेते हि यत्र त्वा द्रोणपुत्रोऽद्य भारत ॥१२३॥
transliteration: tataḥ kruddho'bravītkṛṣṇaḥ pārthaṁ sapraṇayaṁ tadā ,atyadbhutamidaṁ pārtha tava paśyāmi saṁyuge ,atiśete hi yatra tvā droṇaputro'dya bhārata.
english: Then, Krishna, angered, spoke affectionately to Arjuna (Pārtha) at that time: "O Pārtha, this is very astonishing that I see in your combat today, O Bhārata, where Droṇa's son indeed surpasses you."
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 40 Verse 123. Then, Krishna, angered, spoke affectionately to Arjuna (Pārtha) at that time: "O Pārtha, this is very astonishing that I see in your combat today, O Bhārata, where Droṇa's son indeed surpasses you."

sanskrit: कच्चित्ते गाण्डिवं हस्ते रथे तिष्ठसि चार्जुन ।कच्चित्कुशलिनौ बाहू कच्चिद्वीर्यं तदेव ते ॥१२४॥
transliteration: kaccitte gāṇḍivaṁ haste rathe tiṣṭhasi cārjuna ,kaccitkuśalinau bāhū kaccidvīryaṁ tadeva te.
english: O Arjuna, is your Gaṇḍīva bow in your hand, and are you indeed standing on your chariot? Are your two arms sound and capable? Is that same valor (vīrya) still yours?
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 40 Verse 124. O Arjuna, is your Gaṇḍīva bow in your hand, and are you indeed standing on your chariot? Are your two arms sound and capable? Is that same valor (vīrya) still yours?

sanskrit: एवमुक्तस्तु कृष्णेन क्षिप्त्वा भल्लांश्चतुर्दश ।त्वरमाणस्त्वराकाले द्रौणेर्धनुरथाच्छिनत् ।ध्वजं छत्रं पताकां च रथं शक्तिं गदां तथा ॥१२५॥
transliteration: evamuktastu kṛṣṇena kṣiptvā bhallāṁścaturdaśa ,tvaramāṇastvarākāle drauṇerdhanurathācchinat ,dhvajaṁ chatraṁ patākāṁ ca rathaṁ śaktiṁ gadāṁ tathā.
english: Having been addressed thus by Krishna, and then, hurrying in that moment of urgency, he (Arjuna) shot fourteen special arrows (bhallāḥ), and cut off Drauni's bow, banner, umbrella, flag, as well as his chariot, spear (śakti), and mace (gadā).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 40 Verse 125. Having been addressed thus by Krishna, and then, hurrying in that moment of urgency, he (Arjuna) shot fourteen special arrows (bhallāḥ), and cut off Drauni's bow, banner, umbrella, flag, as well as his chariot, spear (śakti), and mace (gadā).

sanskrit: जत्रुदेशे च सुभृशं वत्सदन्तैरताडयत् ।स मूर्च्छां परमां गत्वा ध्वजयष्टिं समाश्रितः ॥१२६॥
transliteration: jatrudeśe ca subhṛśaṁ vatsadantairatāḍayat ,sa mūrcchāṁ paramāṁ gatvā dhvajayaṣṭiṁ samāśritaḥ.
english: And he struck him very severely in the collarbone region with "calf-teeth" (vatsadanta). Having fallen into a deep faint, he then leaned upon the flagstaff.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 40 Verse 126. And he struck him very severely in the collarbone region with "calf-teeth" (vatsadanta). Having fallen into a deep faint, he then leaned upon the flagstaff.

sanskrit: तं विसंज्ञं महाराज किरीटिभयपीडितम् ।अपोवाह रणात्सूतो रक्षमाणो धनंजयात् ॥१२७॥
transliteration: taṁ visaṁjñaṁ mahārāja kirīṭibhayapīḍitam ,apovāha raṇātsūto rakṣamāṇo dhanaṁjayāt.
english: O great king, the charioteer carried him, unconscious and tormented by fear of Arjuna (Kirīṭin), away from the battle, protecting him from Arjuna (Dhanañjaya).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 40 Verse 127. O great king, the charioteer carried him, unconscious and tormented by fear of Arjuna (Kirīṭin), away from the battle, protecting him from Arjuna (Dhanañjaya).

sanskrit: एतस्मिन्नेव काले तु विजयः शत्रुतापनः ।न्यवधीत्तावकं सैन्यं शतशोऽथ सहस्रशः ।पश्यतस्तव पुत्रस्य तस्य वीरस्य भारत ॥१२८॥
transliteration: etasminneva kāle tu vijayaḥ śatrutāpanaḥ ,nyavadhīttāvakaṁ sainyaṁ śataśo'tha sahasraśaḥ ,paśyatastava putrasya tasya vīrasya bhārata.
english: O Bhārata, at that very same time, Vijaya (Arjuna), the tormentor of enemies, annihilated your army by hundreds and thousands, even as your heroic son watched.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 40 Verse 128. O Bhārata, at that very same time, Vijaya (Arjuna), the tormentor of enemies, annihilated your army by hundreds and thousands, even as your heroic son watched.

sanskrit: एवमेष क्षयो वृत्तस्तावकानां परैः सह ।क्रूरो विशसनो घोरो राजन्दुर्मन्त्रिते तव ॥१२९॥
transliteration: evameṣa kṣayo vṛttastāvakānāṁ paraiḥ saha ,krūro viśasano ghoro rājandurmantrite tava.
english: O king, this cruel, devastating, and dreadful destruction of your side by the enemies has thus occurred due to your ill-advised counsel.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 40 Verse 129. O king, this cruel, devastating, and dreadful destruction of your side by the enemies has thus occurred due to your ill-advised counsel.

sanskrit: संशप्तकांश्च कौन्तेयः कुरूंश्चापि वृकोदरः ।वसुषेणं च पाञ्चालः कृत्स्नेन व्यधमद्रणे ॥१३०॥
transliteration: saṁśaptakāṁśca kaunteyaḥ kurūṁścāpi vṛkodaraḥ ,vasuṣeṇaṁ ca pāñcālaḥ kṛtsnena vyadhamadraṇe.
english: In battle, Kaunteya (Arjuna) annihilated the Saṃśaptakas, and Vṛkodara (Bhīma) also [annihilated] the Kurus; and the Pāñcāla prince [Dhṛṣṭadyumna] completely destroyed Vasuṣeṇa (Karṇa).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 40 Verse 130. In battle, Kaunteya (Arjuna) annihilated the Saṃśaptakas, and Vṛkodara (Bhīma) also [annihilated] the Kurus; and the Pāñcāla prince [Dhṛṣṭadyumna] completely destroyed Vasuṣeṇa (Karṇa).

sanskrit: संजय उवाच ।त्वरमाणः पुनः कृष्णः पार्थमभ्यवदच्छनैः ।पश्य कौरव्य राजानमपयातांश्च पाण्डवान् ॥१॥
transliteration: saṁjaya uvāca ,tvaramāṇaḥ punaḥ kṛṣṇaḥ pārthamabhyavadacchanaiḥ ,paśya kauravya rājānamapayātāṁśca pāṇḍavān.
english: Sañjaya said: Then Kṛṣṇa, in haste, spoke gently to Pārtha (Arjuna). (Sañjaya continued, addressing Dhṛtarāṣṭra): 'O scion of Kuru, behold King [Duryodhana] and the Pāṇḍavas, who have retreated!'
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 41 Verse 1. Sañjaya said: Then Kṛṣṇa, in haste, spoke gently to Pārtha (Arjuna). (Sañjaya continued, addressing Dhṛtarāṣṭra): 'O scion of Kuru, behold King [Duryodhana] and the Pāṇḍavas, who have retreated!'

sanskrit: कर्णं पश्य महारङ्गे ज्वलन्तमिव पावकम् ।असौ भीमो महेष्वासः संनिवृत्तो रणं प्रति ॥२॥
transliteration: karṇaṁ paśya mahāraṅge jvalantamiva pāvakam ,asau bhīmo maheṣvāsaḥ saṁnivṛtto raṇaṁ prati.
english: Behold Karṇa in the great battlefield, blazing like fire! That Bhīma, the great archer, has completely returned to the battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 41 Verse 2. Behold Karṇa in the great battlefield, blazing like fire! That Bhīma, the great archer, has completely returned to the battle.

sanskrit: तमेतेऽनु निवर्तन्ते धृष्टद्युम्नपुरोगमाः ।पाञ्चालानां सृञ्जयानां पाण्डवानां च यन्मुखम् ।निवृत्तैश्च तथा पार्थैर्भग्नं शत्रुबलं महत् ॥३॥
transliteration: tamete'nu nivartante dhṛṣṭadyumnapurogamāḥ ,pāñcālānāṁ sṛñjayānāṁ pāṇḍavānāṁ ca yanmukham ,nivṛttaiśca tathā pārthairbhagnaṁ śatrubalaṁ mahat.
english: These [warriors], led by Dhṛṣṭadyumna, follow him (Bhīma) and return, forming the vanguard of the Pāñcālas, Sṛñjayas, and Pāṇḍavas. And similarly, by these returning Pārthas, the great enemy force was shattered.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 41 Verse 3. These [warriors], led by Dhṛṣṭadyumna, follow him (Bhīma) and return, forming the vanguard of the Pāñcālas, Sṛñjayas, and Pāṇḍavas. And similarly, by these returning Pārthas, the great enemy force was shattered.

sanskrit: कौरवान्द्रवतो ह्येष कर्णो धारयतेऽर्जुन ।अन्तकप्रतिमो वेगे शक्रतुल्यपराक्रमः ॥४॥
transliteration: kauravāndravato hyeṣa karṇo dhārayate'rjuna ,antakapratimo vege śakratulyaparākramaḥ.
english: Indeed, O Arjuna, this Karṇa, who is like Yama in speed and equal to Indra in valor, is holding back the Kauravas who are fleeing.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 41 Verse 4. Indeed, O Arjuna, this Karṇa, who is like Yama in speed and equal to Indra in valor, is holding back the Kauravas who are fleeing.

sanskrit: असौ गच्छति कौरव्य द्रौणिरस्त्रभृतां वरः ।तमेष प्रद्रुतः संख्ये धृष्टद्युम्नो महारथः ॥५॥
transliteration: asau gacchati kauravya drauṇirastrabhṛtāṁ varaḥ ,tameṣa pradrutaḥ saṁkhye dhṛṣṭadyumno mahārathaḥ.
english: O descendant of Kuru, there goes Droṇi (Aśvatthāman), the best of weapon-bearers. Towards him, that great charioteer Dhṛṣṭadyumna swiftly advances in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 41 Verse 5. O descendant of Kuru, there goes Droṇi (Aśvatthāman), the best of weapon-bearers. Towards him, that great charioteer Dhṛṣṭadyumna swiftly advances in battle.

sanskrit: सर्वं व्याचष्ट दुर्धर्षो वासुदेवः किरीटिने ।ततो राजन्प्रादुरासीन्महाघोरो महारणः ॥६॥
transliteration: sarvaṁ vyācaṣṭa durdharṣo vāsudevaḥ kirīṭine ,tato rājanprādurāsīnmahāghoro mahāraṇaḥ.
english: The irresistible Vāsudeva (Kṛṣṇa) explained everything to the diademed one (Arjuna). Then, O King, a very terrible great battle ensued.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 41 Verse 6. The irresistible Vāsudeva (Kṛṣṇa) explained everything to the diademed one (Arjuna). Then, O King, a very terrible great battle ensued.

sanskrit: सिंहनादरवाश्चात्र प्रादुरासन्समागमे ।उभयोः सेनयो राजन्मृत्युं कृत्वा निवर्तनम् ॥७॥
transliteration: siṁhanādaravāścātra prādurāsansamāgame ,ubhayoḥ senayo rājanmṛtyuṁ kṛtvā nivartanam.
english: And there, O King, in that engagement, roars like that of lions arose from both armies, making death a turning point.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 41 Verse 7. And there, O King, in that engagement, roars like that of lions arose from both armies, making death a turning point.

sanskrit: संजय उवाच ।ततः पुनः समाजग्मुरभीताः कुरुसृञ्जयाः ।युधिष्ठिरमुखाः पार्था वैकर्तनमुखा वयम् ॥१॥
transliteration: saṁjaya uvāca ,tataḥ punaḥ samājagmurabhītāḥ kurusṛñjayāḥ ,yudhiṣṭhiramukhāḥ pārthā vaikartanamukhā vayam.
english: Sañjaya said: Then, fearless, the Kurus and the Sṛñjayas  -  specifically, the Pārthas led by Yudhiṣṭhira and we (Kauravas) led by Vaikartana (Karṇa)  -  again assembled.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 42 Verse 1. Sañjaya said: Then, fearless, the Kurus and the Sṛñjayas  -  specifically, the Pārthas led by Yudhiṣṭhira and we (Kauravas) led by Vaikartana (Karṇa)  -  again assembled.

sanskrit: ततः प्रववृते भीमः संग्रामो लोमहर्षणः ।कर्णस्य पाण्डवानां च यमराष्ट्रविवर्धनः ॥२॥
transliteration: tataḥ pravavṛte bhīmaḥ saṁgrāmo lomaharṣaṇaḥ ,karṇasya pāṇḍavānāṁ ca yamarāṣṭravivardhanaḥ.
english: Then a terrible (bhīma), hair-raising (lomaharṣaṇa) battle (saṅgrāma) began, one that expanded the realm of Yama (yamarāṣṭravivardhana), between Karṇa and the Pāṇḍavas.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 42 Verse 2. Then a terrible (bhīma), hair-raising (lomaharṣaṇa) battle (saṅgrāma) began, one that expanded the realm of Yama (yamarāṣṭravivardhana), between Karṇa and the Pāṇḍavas.

sanskrit: तस्मिन्प्रवृत्ते संग्रामे तुमुले शोणितोदके ।संशप्तकेषु शूरेषु किंचिच्छिष्टेषु भारत ॥३॥
transliteration: tasminpravṛtte saṁgrāme tumule śoṇitodake ,saṁśaptakeṣu śūreṣu kiṁcicchiṣṭeṣu bhārata.
english: O Bhārata (Dhṛtarāṣṭra)! When that tumultuous (tumula) battle (saṅgrāma) was ongoing, whose water was blood (śoṇitodaka), and when only a few (kiñcit śiṣṭa) of the valiant Saṃśaptakas remained.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 42 Verse 3. O Bhārata (Dhṛtarāṣṭra)! When that tumultuous (tumula) battle (saṅgrāma) was ongoing, whose water was blood (śoṇitodaka), and when only a few (kiñcit śiṣṭa) of the valiant Saṃśaptakas remained.

sanskrit: धृष्टद्युम्नो महाराज सहितः सर्वराजभिः ।कर्णमेवाभिदुद्राव पाण्डवाश्च महारथाः ॥४॥
transliteration: dhṛṣṭadyumno mahārāja sahitaḥ sarvarājabhiḥ ,karṇamevābhidudrāva pāṇḍavāśca mahārathāḥ.
english: O great king (mahārāja)! Dhṛṣṭadyumna, accompanied by all the kings (sarvarājabhiḥ), and the great charioteers (mahārathāḥ) Pāṇḍavas, rushed directly towards Karṇa.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 42 Verse 4. O great king (mahārāja)! Dhṛṣṭadyumna, accompanied by all the kings (sarvarājabhiḥ), and the great charioteers (mahārathāḥ) Pāṇḍavas, rushed directly towards Karṇa.

sanskrit: आगच्छमानांस्तान्संख्ये प्रहृष्टान्विजयैषिणः ।दधारैको रणे कर्णो जलौघानिव पर्वतः ॥५॥
transliteration: āgacchamānāṁstānsaṁkhye prahṛṣṭānvijayaiṣiṇaḥ ,dadhāraiko raṇe karṇo jalaughāniva parvataḥ.
english: In battle, Karna alone withstood those approaching, who were greatly exhilarated and desirous of victory, just as a mountain withstands torrents of water.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 42 Verse 5. In battle, Karna alone withstood those approaching, who were greatly exhilarated and desirous of victory, just as a mountain withstands torrents of water.

sanskrit: तमासाद्य तु ते कर्णं व्यशीर्यन्त महारथाः ।यथाचलं समासाद्य जलौघाः सर्वतोदिशम् ।तयोरासीन्महाराज संग्रामो लोमहर्षणः ॥६॥
transliteration: tamāsādya tu te karṇaṁ vyaśīryanta mahārathāḥ ,yathācalaṁ samāsādya jalaughāḥ sarvatodiśam ,tayorāsīnmahārāja saṁgrāmo lomaharṣaṇaḥ.
english: But upon approaching Karna, those great charioteers were shattered, just as torrents of water from all directions are scattered after reaching a mountain. O great king, the battle between those two was hair-raising.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 42 Verse 6. But upon approaching Karna, those great charioteers were shattered, just as torrents of water from all directions are scattered after reaching a mountain. O great king, the battle between those two was hair-raising.

sanskrit: धृष्टद्युम्नस्तु राधेयं शरेण नतपर्वणा ।ताडयामास संक्रुद्धस्तिष्ठ तिष्ठेति चाब्रवीत् ॥७॥
transliteration: dhṛṣṭadyumnastu rādheyaṁ śareṇa nataparvaṇā ,tāḍayāmāsa saṁkruddhastiṣṭha tiṣṭheti cābravīt.
english: But Dhṛṣṭadyumna, greatly enraged, struck Radheya (Karna) with a well-jointed arrow, and he said, 'Stay! Stay!'
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 42 Verse 7. But Dhṛṣṭadyumna, greatly enraged, struck Radheya (Karna) with a well-jointed arrow, and he said, 'Stay! Stay!'

sanskrit: विजयं तु धनुःश्रेष्ठं विधुन्वानो महारथः ।पार्षतस्य धनुश्छित्त्वा शरानाशीविषोपमान् ।ताडयामास संक्रुद्धः पार्षतं नवभिः शरैः ॥८॥
transliteration: vijayaṁ tu dhanuḥśreṣṭhaṁ vidhunvāno mahārathaḥ ,pārṣatasya dhanuśchittvā śarānāśīviṣopamān ,tāḍayāmāsa saṁkruddhaḥ pārṣataṁ navabhiḥ śaraiḥ.
english: But that great charioteer (Karna), wielding his excellent bow named Vijaya, and greatly enraged, first broke the bow of Pṛṣata's son (Dhṛṣṭadyumna), and then struck Pṛṣata's son with nine arrows that were like venomous snakes.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 42 Verse 8. But that great charioteer (Karna), wielding his excellent bow named Vijaya, and greatly enraged, first broke the bow of Pṛṣata's son (Dhṛṣṭadyumna), and then struck Pṛṣata's son with nine arrows that were like venomous snakes.

sanskrit: ते वर्म हेमविकृतं भित्त्वा तस्य महात्मनः ।शोणिताक्ता व्यराजन्त शक्रगोपा इवानघ ॥९॥
transliteration: te varma hemavikṛtaṁ bhittvā tasya mahātmanaḥ ,śoṇitāktā vyarājanta śakragopā ivānagha.
english: O sinless one, those [arrows], having pierced the gold-adorned armor of that great-souled [Karna] (mahātman), shone forth, covered in blood, like red cochineal insects.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 42 Verse 9. O sinless one, those [arrows], having pierced the gold-adorned armor of that great-souled [Karna] (mahātman), shone forth, covered in blood, like red cochineal insects.

sanskrit: तदपास्य धनुश्छिन्नं धृष्टद्युम्नो महारथः ।अन्यद्धनुरुपादाय शरांश्चाशीविषोपमान् ।कर्णं विव्याध सप्तत्या शरैः संनतपर्वभिः ॥१०॥
transliteration: tadapāsya dhanuśchinnaṁ dhṛṣṭadyumno mahārathaḥ ,anyaddhanurupādāya śarāṁścāśīviṣopamān ,karṇaṁ vivyādha saptatyā śaraiḥ saṁnataparvabhiḥ.
english: The great warrior Dhṛṣṭadyumna, having cast aside that broken bow, then took up another bow and arrows resembling venomous snakes, and pierced Karna with seventy arrows having well-bent shafts.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 42 Verse 10. The great warrior Dhṛṣṭadyumna, having cast aside that broken bow, then took up another bow and arrows resembling venomous snakes, and pierced Karna with seventy arrows having well-bent shafts.

sanskrit: तथैव राजन्कर्णोऽपि पार्षतं शत्रुतापनम् ।द्रोणशत्रुं महेष्वासो विव्याध निशितैः शरैः ॥११॥
transliteration: tathaiva rājankarṇo'pi pārṣataṁ śatrutāpanam ,droṇaśatruṁ maheṣvāso vivyādha niśitaiḥ śaraiḥ.
english: Likewise, O King, Karna too, the great archer, pierced the son of Pṛṣata (Dhṛṣṭadyumna), who was the tormentor of enemies and the foe of Droṇa, with sharpened arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 42 Verse 11. Likewise, O King, Karna too, the great archer, pierced the son of Pṛṣata (Dhṛṣṭadyumna), who was the tormentor of enemies and the foe of Droṇa, with sharpened arrows.

sanskrit: तस्य कर्णो महाराज शरं कनकभूषणम् ।प्रेषयामास संक्रुद्धो मृत्युदण्डमिवापरम् ॥१२॥
transliteration: tasya karṇo mahārāja śaraṁ kanakabhūṣaṇam ,preṣayāmāsa saṁkruddho mṛtyudaṇḍamivāparam.
english: O Great King, Karna, intensely enraged, dispatched a gold-adorned arrow at him, which was like a second staff of Death.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 42 Verse 12. O Great King, Karna, intensely enraged, dispatched a gold-adorned arrow at him, which was like a second staff of Death.

sanskrit: तमापतन्तं सहसा घोररूपं विशां पते ।चिच्छेद सप्तधा राजञ्शैनेयः कृतहस्तवत् ॥१३॥
transliteration: tamāpatantaṁ sahasā ghorarūpaṁ viśāṁ pate ,ciccheda saptadhā rājañśaineyaḥ kṛtahastavat.
english: O King, O lord of the people, Śaineya, like an expert, swiftly cut that dreadful-looking (missile) which was approaching into seven parts.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 42 Verse 13. O King, O lord of the people, Śaineya, like an expert, swiftly cut that dreadful-looking (missile) which was approaching into seven parts.

sanskrit: दृष्ट्वा विनिहितं बाणं शरैः कर्णो विशां पते ।सात्यकिं शरवर्षेण समन्तात्पर्यवारयत् ॥१४॥
transliteration: dṛṣṭvā vinihitaṁ bāṇaṁ śaraiḥ karṇo viśāṁ pate ,sātyakiṁ śaravarṣeṇa samantātparyavārayat.
english: O lord of the people, upon seeing (Saatyaki's) arrow discharged, Karṇa then surrounded Saatyaki from all sides with (his) arrows, (creating) a shower of arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 42 Verse 14. O lord of the people, upon seeing (Saatyaki's) arrow discharged, Karṇa then surrounded Saatyaki from all sides with (his) arrows, (creating) a shower of arrows.

sanskrit: विव्याध चैनं समरे नाराचैस्तत्र सप्तभिः ।तं प्रत्यविध्यच्छैनेयः शरैर्हेमविभूषितैः ॥१५॥
transliteration: vivyādha cainaṁ samare nārācaistatra saptabhiḥ ,taṁ pratyavidhyacchaineyaḥ śarairhemavibhūṣitaiḥ.
english: And there, in that battle, (Karṇa) pierced him (Saatyaki) with seven iron arrows. Then, Śaineya counter-pierced him (Karṇa) with gold-adorned arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 42 Verse 15. And there, in that battle, (Karṇa) pierced him (Saatyaki) with seven iron arrows. Then, Śaineya counter-pierced him (Karṇa) with gold-adorned arrows.

sanskrit: ततो युद्धमतीवासीच्चक्षुःश्रोत्रभयावहम् ।राजन्घोरं च चित्रं च प्रेक्षणीयं समन्ततः ॥१६॥
transliteration: tato yuddhamatīvāsīccakṣuḥśrotrabhayāvaham ,rājanghoraṁ ca citraṁ ca prekṣaṇīyaṁ samantataḥ.
english: Then, O King, the battle became exceedingly terrifying to the eyes and ears; it was dreadful, yet wonderful and spectacular to behold from all sides.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 42 Verse 16. Then, O King, the battle became exceedingly terrifying to the eyes and ears; it was dreadful, yet wonderful and spectacular to behold from all sides.

sanskrit: सर्वेषां तत्र भूतानां लोमहर्षो व्यजायत ।तद्दृष्ट्वा समरे कर्म कर्णशैनेययोर्नृप ॥१७॥
transliteration: sarveṣāṁ tatra bhūtānāṁ lomaharṣo vyajāyata ,taddṛṣṭvā samare karma karṇaśaineyayornṛpa.
english: O King, seeing that deed of Karṇa and Śaineya in battle, a thrill of horripilation arose in all creatures present there.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 42 Verse 17. O King, seeing that deed of Karṇa and Śaineya in battle, a thrill of horripilation arose in all creatures present there.

sanskrit: एतस्मिन्नन्तरे द्रौणिरभ्ययात्सुमहाबलम् ।पार्षतं शत्रुदमनं शत्रुवीर्यासुनाशनम् ॥१८॥
transliteration: etasminnantare drauṇirabhyayātsumahābalam ,pārṣataṁ śatrudamanaṁ śatruvīryāsunāśanam.
english: At this juncture, Droṇi (Aśvatthāman) attacked the exceedingly mighty Pārṣata (Dhṛṣṭadyumna), the subduer of foes and the destroyer of the vital power of enemies.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 42 Verse 18. At this juncture, Droṇi (Aśvatthāman) attacked the exceedingly mighty Pārṣata (Dhṛṣṭadyumna), the subduer of foes and the destroyer of the vital power of enemies.

sanskrit: अभ्यभाषत संक्रुद्धो द्रौणिर्दूरे धनंजये ।तिष्ठ तिष्ठाद्य ब्रह्मघ्न न मे जीवन्विमोक्ष्यसे ॥१९॥
transliteration: abhyabhāṣata saṁkruddho drauṇirdūre dhanaṁjaye ,tiṣṭha tiṣṭhādya brahmaghna na me jīvanvimokṣyase.
english: Enraged, Droṇi (Aśvatthāman) addressed him from a distance, while Dhanañjaya (Arjuna) was far away, saying: "Stop! Stop now, O killer of brahmins! You will not escape from me alive!"
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 42 Verse 19. Enraged, Droṇi (Aśvatthāman) addressed him from a distance, while Dhanañjaya (Arjuna) was far away, saying: "Stop! Stop now, O killer of brahmins! You will not escape from me alive!"

sanskrit: इत्युक्त्वा सुभृशं वीरः शीघ्रकृन्निशितैः शरैः ।पार्षतं छादयामास घोररूपैः सुतेजनैः ।यतमानं परं शक्त्या यतमानो महारथः ॥२०॥
transliteration: ityuktvā subhṛśaṁ vīraḥ śīghrakṛnniśitaiḥ śaraiḥ ,pārṣataṁ chādayāmāsa ghorarūpaiḥ sutejanaiḥ ,yatamānaṁ paraṁ śaktyā yatamāno mahārathaḥ.
english: Having spoken thus, the hero, swift in action, the great charioteer (Droṇi), while exerting himself with great power (śakti), enveloped Pārṣata (Dhṛṣṭadyumna), who was also striving, with dreadful and exceedingly sharp arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 42 Verse 20. Having spoken thus, the hero, swift in action, the great charioteer (Droṇi), while exerting himself with great power (śakti), enveloped Pārṣata (Dhṛṣṭadyumna), who was also striving, with dreadful and exceedingly sharp arrows.

sanskrit: यथा हि समरे द्रौणिः पार्षतं वीक्ष्य मारिष ।तथा द्रौणिं रणे दृष्ट्वा पार्षतः परवीरहा ।नातिहृष्टमना भूत्वा मन्यते मृत्युमात्मनः ॥२१॥
transliteration: yathā hi samare drauṇiḥ pārṣataṁ vīkṣya māriṣa ,tathā drauṇiṁ raṇe dṛṣṭvā pārṣataḥ paravīrahā ,nātihṛṣṭamanā bhūtvā manyate mṛtyumātmanaḥ.
english: O respected one, just as Droṇi (Aśvatthāmā) looked upon Pārṣata (Dhṛṣṭadyumna) in battle, so too Pārṣata, the slayer of enemy heroes, upon seeing Droṇi in battle, was not overly pleased in mind, and instead considered it his own death (ātman).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 42 Verse 21. O respected one, just as Droṇi (Aśvatthāmā) looked upon Pārṣata (Dhṛṣṭadyumna) in battle, so too Pārṣata, the slayer of enemy heroes, upon seeing Droṇi in battle, was not overly pleased in mind, and instead considered it his own death (ātman).

sanskrit: द्रौणिस्तु दृष्ट्वा राजेन्द्र धृष्टद्युम्नं रणे स्थितम् ।क्रोधेन निःश्वसन्वीरः पार्षतं समुपाद्रवत् ।तावन्योन्यं तु दृष्ट्वैव संरम्भं जग्मतुः परम् ॥२२॥
transliteration: drauṇistu dṛṣṭvā rājendra dhṛṣṭadyumnaṁ raṇe sthitam ,krodhena niḥśvasanvīraḥ pārṣataṁ samupādravat ,tāvanyonyaṁ tu dṛṣṭvaiva saṁrambhaṁ jagmatuḥ param.
english: But Droṇi (Aśvatthāmā), O king of kings, seeing Dhṛṣṭadyumna standing in battle, the hero, hissing with anger, rushed upon Pārṣata (Dhṛṣṭadyumna). Then, seeing each other, both of them immediately became filled with extreme fury.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 42 Verse 22. But Droṇi (Aśvatthāmā), O king of kings, seeing Dhṛṣṭadyumna standing in battle, the hero, hissing with anger, rushed upon Pārṣata (Dhṛṣṭadyumna). Then, seeing each other, both of them immediately became filled with extreme fury.

sanskrit: अथाब्रवीन्महाराज द्रोणपुत्रः प्रतापवान् ।धृष्टद्युम्नं समीपस्थं त्वरमाणो विशां पते ।पाञ्चालापसदाद्य त्वां प्रेषयिष्यामि मृत्यवे ॥२३॥
transliteration: athābravīnmahārāja droṇaputraḥ pratāpavān ,dhṛṣṭadyumnaṁ samīpasthaṁ tvaramāṇo viśāṁ pate ,pāñcālāpasadādya tvāṁ preṣayiṣyāmi mṛtyave.
english: Then, O great king, Droṇa's powerful son, hastening towards Dhṛṣṭadyumna who stood nearby, spoke, saying: 'O disgrace of the Pañcālas, today I shall send you to your death.'
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 42 Verse 23. Then, O great king, Droṇa's powerful son, hastening towards Dhṛṣṭadyumna who stood nearby, spoke, saying: 'O disgrace of the Pañcālas, today I shall send you to your death.'

sanskrit: पापं हि यत्त्वया कर्म घ्नता द्रोणं पुरा कृतम् ।अद्य त्वा पत्स्यते तद्वै यथा ह्यकुशलं तथा ॥२४॥
transliteration: pāpaṁ hi yattvayā karma ghnatā droṇaṁ purā kṛtam ,adya tvā patsyate tadvai yathā hyakuśalaṁ tathā.
english: Indeed, that sinful deed (karma) which you committed previously by killing Droṇa, that very deed (karma) will befall you today, just as an inauspicious event inevitably occurs.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 42 Verse 24. Indeed, that sinful deed (karma) which you committed previously by killing Droṇa, that very deed (karma) will befall you today, just as an inauspicious event inevitably occurs.

sanskrit: अरक्ष्यमाणः पार्थेन यदि तिष्ठसि संयुगे ।नापक्रमसि वा मूढ सत्यमेतद्ब्रवीमि ते ॥२५॥
transliteration: arakṣyamāṇaḥ pārthena yadi tiṣṭhasi saṁyuge ,nāpakramasi vā mūḍha satyametadbravīmi te.
english: O foolish one, if you remain in battle unprotected by Arjuna (Pārtha), or if you do not retreat, I tell you this truth.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 42 Verse 25. O foolish one, if you remain in battle unprotected by Arjuna (Pārtha), or if you do not retreat, I tell you this truth.

sanskrit: एवमुक्तः प्रत्युवाच धृष्टद्युम्नः प्रतापवान् ।प्रतिवाक्यं स एवासिर्मामको दास्यते तव ।येनैव ते पितुर्दत्तं यतमानस्य संयुगे ॥२६॥
transliteration: evamuktaḥ pratyuvāca dhṛṣṭadyumnaḥ pratāpavān ,prativākyaṁ sa evāsirmāmako dāsyate tava ,yenaiva te piturdattaṁ yatamānasya saṁyuge.
english: Thus addressed, the mighty Dhṛṣṭadyumna replied: 'My own sword will give you the answer, that very sword by which your father was indeed defeated while striving in battle.'
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 42 Verse 26. Thus addressed, the mighty Dhṛṣṭadyumna replied: 'My own sword will give you the answer, that very sword by which your father was indeed defeated while striving in battle.'

sanskrit: यदि तावन्मया द्रोणो निहतो ब्राह्मणब्रुवः ।त्वामिदानीं कथं युद्धे न हनिष्यामि विक्रमात् ॥२७॥
transliteration: yadi tāvanmayā droṇo nihato brāhmaṇabruvaḥ ,tvāmidānīṁ kathaṁ yuddhe na haniṣyāmi vikramāt.
english: If the so-called Brahmin (brāhmaṇabruva) Droṇa was killed by me, then how will I not kill you now in battle with my valor (vikrama)?
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 42 Verse 27. If the so-called Brahmin (brāhmaṇabruva) Droṇa was killed by me, then how will I not kill you now in battle with my valor (vikrama)?

sanskrit: एवमुक्त्वा महाराज सेनापतिरमर्षणः ।निशितेनाथ बाणेन द्रौणिं विव्याध पार्षतः ॥२८॥
transliteration: evamuktvā mahārāja senāpatiramarṣaṇaḥ ,niśitenātha bāṇena drauṇiṁ vivyādha pārṣataḥ.
english: Having spoken thus, O great king, the indignant commander of the army, Dhṛṣṭadyumna (Pārṣata), then pierced Droṇi with a sharpened arrow.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 42 Verse 28. Having spoken thus, O great king, the indignant commander of the army, Dhṛṣṭadyumna (Pārṣata), then pierced Droṇi with a sharpened arrow.

sanskrit: ततो द्रौणिः सुसंक्रुद्धः शरैः संनतपर्वभिः ।प्राच्छादयद्दिशो राजन्धृष्टद्युम्नस्य संयुगे ॥२९॥
transliteration: tato drauṇiḥ susaṁkruddhaḥ śaraiḥ saṁnataparvabhiḥ ,prācchādayaddiśo rājandhṛṣṭadyumnasya saṁyuge.
english: Then, O King, Droṇa's son (Drauṇiḥ), greatly enraged, covered all directions with his well-jointed arrows in the battle against Dhṛṣṭadyumna.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 42 Verse 29. Then, O King, Droṇa's son (Drauṇiḥ), greatly enraged, covered all directions with his well-jointed arrows in the battle against Dhṛṣṭadyumna.

sanskrit: नैवान्तरिक्षं न दिशो नैव योधाः समन्ततः ।दृश्यन्ते वै महाराज शरैश्छन्नाः सहस्रशः ॥३०॥
transliteration: naivāntarikṣaṁ na diśo naiva yodhāḥ samantataḥ ,dṛśyante vai mahārāja śaraiśchannāḥ sahasraśaḥ.
english: Indeed, O Great King, neither the sky, nor the directions, nor the warriors all around could be seen, as they were covered by thousands of arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 42 Verse 30. Indeed, O Great King, neither the sky, nor the directions, nor the warriors all around could be seen, as they were covered by thousands of arrows.

sanskrit: तथैव पार्षतो राजन्द्रौणिमाहवशोभिनम् ।शरैः संछादयामास सूतपुत्रस्य पश्यतः ॥३१॥
transliteration: tathaiva pārṣato rājandrauṇimāhavaśobhinam ,śaraiḥ saṁchādayāmāsa sūtaputrasya paśyataḥ.
english: Just so, O King, Dhṛṣṭadyumna (Pārṣataḥ) covered Droṇa's son, Aśvatthāmā (Drauṇim), who was splendid in battle, with arrows, as the charioteer's son (Karṇa) watched.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 42 Verse 31. Just so, O King, Dhṛṣṭadyumna (Pārṣataḥ) covered Droṇa's son, Aśvatthāmā (Drauṇim), who was splendid in battle, with arrows, as the charioteer's son (Karṇa) watched.

sanskrit: राधेयोऽपि महाराज पाञ्चालान्सह पाण्डवैः ।द्रौपदेयान्युधामन्युं सात्यकिं च महारथम् ।एकः स वारयामास प्रेक्षणीयः समन्ततः ॥३२॥
transliteration: rādheyo'pi mahārāja pāñcālānsaha pāṇḍavaiḥ ,draupadeyānyudhāmanyuṁ sātyakiṁ ca mahāratham ,ekaḥ sa vārayāmāsa prekṣaṇīyaḥ samantataḥ.
english: O Great King, Rādhā's son (Rādheyaḥ) alone, a spectacle to behold from all sides, also repelled the Pāñcālas along with the Pāṇḍavas, as well as the sons of Draupadī (Draupadeyān), Yudhamanyu, and Sātyaki, the great chariot warrior.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 42 Verse 32. O Great King, Rādhā's son (Rādheyaḥ) alone, a spectacle to behold from all sides, also repelled the Pāñcālas along with the Pāṇḍavas, as well as the sons of Draupadī (Draupadeyān), Yudhamanyu, and Sātyaki, the great chariot warrior.

sanskrit: धृष्टद्युम्नोऽपि समरे द्रौणेश्चिच्छेद कार्मुकम् ।तदपास्य धनुश्छिन्नमन्यदादत्त कार्मुकम् ।वेगवत्समरे घोरं शरांश्चाशीविषोपमान् ॥३३॥
transliteration: dhṛṣṭadyumno'pi samare drauṇeściccheda kārmukam ,tadapāsya dhanuśchinnamanyadādatta kārmukam ,vegavatsamare ghoraṁ śarāṁścāśīviṣopamān.
english: Dhṛṣṭadyumna, in battle, also cut Drauṇi's bow. Having cast aside that broken bow, Drauṇi took up another, and swiftly discharged dreadful arrows in battle, arrows that were like venomous snakes.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 42 Verse 33. Dhṛṣṭadyumna, in battle, also cut Drauṇi's bow. Having cast aside that broken bow, Drauṇi took up another, and swiftly discharged dreadful arrows in battle, arrows that were like venomous snakes.

sanskrit: स पार्षतस्य राजेन्द्र धनुः शक्तिं गदां ध्वजम् ।हयान्सूतं रथं चैव निमेषाद्व्यधमच्छरैः ॥३४॥
transliteration: sa pārṣatasya rājendra dhanuḥ śaktiṁ gadāṁ dhvajam ,hayānsūtaṁ rathaṁ caiva nimeṣādvyadhamaccharaiḥ.
english: O King (Rājendra), in an instant, he (Drauṇi) destroyed Dhṛṣṭadyumna's (Pārṣata's) bow, spear (śakti), mace, banner, horses, charioteer, and chariot with his arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 42 Verse 34. O King (Rājendra), in an instant, he (Drauṇi) destroyed Dhṛṣṭadyumna's (Pārṣata's) bow, spear (śakti), mace, banner, horses, charioteer, and chariot with his arrows.

sanskrit: स छिन्नधन्वा विरथो हताश्वो हतसारथिः ।खड्गमादत्त विपुलं शतचन्द्रं च भानुमत् ॥३५॥
transliteration: sa chinnadhanvā viratho hatāśvo hatasārathiḥ ,khaḍgamādatta vipulaṁ śatacandraṁ ca bhānumat.
english: He (Dhṛṣṭadyumna), with his bow cut, deprived of his chariot, his horses killed, and his charioteer slain, took up a large, radiant sword adorned with a hundred (moon-like) circles.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 42 Verse 35. He (Dhṛṣṭadyumna), with his bow cut, deprived of his chariot, his horses killed, and his charioteer slain, took up a large, radiant sword adorned with a hundred (moon-like) circles.

sanskrit: द्रौणिस्तदपि राजेन्द्र भल्लैः क्षिप्रं महारथः ।चिच्छेद समरे वीरः क्षिप्रहस्तो दृढायुधः ।रथादनवरूढस्य तदद्भुतमिवाभवत् ॥३६॥
transliteration: drauṇistadapi rājendra bhallaiḥ kṣipraṁ mahārathaḥ ,ciccheda samare vīraḥ kṣiprahasto dṛḍhāyudhaḥ ,rathādanavarūḍhasya tadadbhutamivābhavat.
english: O King (Rājendra), the heroic great warrior (mahāratha) Drauṇi, swift-handed and firm in his weapon, quickly cut even that sword in battle with broad-headed arrows (bhallaiḥ). That deed was truly astonishing, especially since it was done while (Dhṛṣṭadyumna) had not yet dismounted from his chariot.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 42 Verse 36. O King (Rājendra), the heroic great warrior (mahāratha) Drauṇi, swift-handed and firm in his weapon, quickly cut even that sword in battle with broad-headed arrows (bhallaiḥ). That deed was truly astonishing, especially since it was done while (Dhṛṣṭadyumna) had not yet dismounted from his chariot.

sanskrit: धृष्टद्युम्नं तु विरथं हताश्वं छिन्नकार्मुकम् ।शरैश्च बहुधा विद्धमस्त्रैश्च शकलीकृतम् ।नातरद्भरतश्रेष्ठ यतमानो महारथः ॥३७॥
transliteration: dhṛṣṭadyumnaṁ tu virathaṁ hatāśvaṁ chinnakārmukam ,śaraiśca bahudhā viddhamastraiśca śakalīkṛtam ,nātaradbharataśreṣṭha yatamāno mahārathaḥ.
english: O best among Bharatas, the great warrior (Aśvatthāmā), though striving, could not overcome Dhṛṣṭadyumna, who was without a chariot, whose horses had been slain, whose bow was broken, repeatedly pierced by arrows, and torn to pieces by weapons.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 42 Verse 37. O best among Bharatas, the great warrior (Aśvatthāmā), though striving, could not overcome Dhṛṣṭadyumna, who was without a chariot, whose horses had been slain, whose bow was broken, repeatedly pierced by arrows, and torn to pieces by weapons.

sanskrit: तस्यान्तमिषुभी राजन्यदा द्रौणिर्न जग्मिवान् ।अथ त्यक्त्वा धनुर्वीरः पार्षतं त्वरितोऽन्वगात् ॥३८॥
transliteration: tasyāntamiṣubhī rājanyadā drauṇirna jagmivān ,atha tyaktvā dhanurvīraḥ pārṣataṁ tvarito'nvagāt.
english: O king, when Droṇa's son (Aśvatthāmā) could not bring about his (Dhṛṣṭadyumna's) end even with arrows, then that hero, quickly abandoning his bow, went after Pārṣata (Dhṛṣṭadyumna).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 42 Verse 38. O king, when Droṇa's son (Aśvatthāmā) could not bring about his (Dhṛṣṭadyumna's) end even with arrows, then that hero, quickly abandoning his bow, went after Pārṣata (Dhṛṣṭadyumna).

sanskrit: आसीदाद्रवतो राजन्वेगस्तस्य महात्मनः ।गरुडस्येव पततो जिघृक्षोः पन्नगोत्तमम् ॥३९॥
transliteration: āsīdādravato rājanvegastasya mahātmanaḥ ,garuḍasyeva patato jighṛkṣoḥ pannagottamam.
english: O king, the great-souled (mahātman) (Aśvatthāmā), as he rushed, exhibited a speed like that of Garuḍa flying to seize the best of serpents.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 42 Verse 39. O king, the great-souled (mahātman) (Aśvatthāmā), as he rushed, exhibited a speed like that of Garuḍa flying to seize the best of serpents.

sanskrit: एतस्मिन्नेव काले तु माधवोऽर्जुनमब्रवीत् ।पश्य पार्थ यथा द्रौणिः पार्षतस्य वधं प्रति ।यत्नं करोति विपुलं हन्याच्चैनमसंशयम् ॥४०॥
transliteration: etasminneva kāle tu mādhavo'rjunamabravīt ,paśya pārtha yathā drauṇiḥ pārṣatasya vadhaṁ prati ,yatnaṁ karoti vipulaṁ hanyāccainamasaṁśayam.
english: Indeed, at that very moment, Mādhava (Krishna) addressed Arjuna: 'O Pārtha, observe how Droṇa's son (Aśvatthāmā) is exerting a tremendous effort towards the slaying of Pārṣata (Dhṛṣṭadyumna); he will undoubtedly kill him.'
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 42 Verse 40. Indeed, at that very moment, Mādhava (Krishna) addressed Arjuna: 'O Pārtha, observe how Droṇa's son (Aśvatthāmā) is exerting a tremendous effort towards the slaying of Pārṣata (Dhṛṣṭadyumna); he will undoubtedly kill him.'

sanskrit: तं मोचय महाबाहो पार्षतं शत्रुतापनम् ।द्रौणेरास्यमनुप्राप्तं मृत्योरास्यगतं यथा ॥४१॥
transliteration: taṁ mocaya mahābāho pārṣataṁ śatrutāpanam ,drauṇerāsyamanuprāptaṁ mṛtyorāsyagataṁ yathā.
english: O mighty-armed one, rescue that son of Pṛṣata (Dhṛṣṭadyumna), the tormentor of enemies, who has fallen into the mouth of Droṇa's son (Drauṇi), just like one who has entered the mouth of death.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 42 Verse 41. O mighty-armed one, rescue that son of Pṛṣata (Dhṛṣṭadyumna), the tormentor of enemies, who has fallen into the mouth of Droṇa's son (Drauṇi), just like one who has entered the mouth of death.

sanskrit: एवमुक्त्वा महाराज वासुदेवः प्रतापवान् ।प्रैषयत्तत्र तुरगान्यत्र द्रौणिर्व्यवस्थितः ॥४२॥
transliteration: evamuktvā mahārāja vāsudevaḥ pratāpavān ,praiṣayattatra turagānyatra drauṇirvyavasthitaḥ.
english: Having spoken thus, O great king, the glorious Vāsudeva (Kṛṣṇa) dispatched the horses to the place where Droṇa's son (Drauṇi) was positioned.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 42 Verse 42. Having spoken thus, O great king, the glorious Vāsudeva (Kṛṣṇa) dispatched the horses to the place where Droṇa's son (Drauṇi) was positioned.

sanskrit: ते हयाश्चन्द्रसंकाशाः केशवेन प्रचोदिताः ।पिबन्त इव तद्व्योम जग्मुर्द्रौणिरथं प्रति ॥४३॥
transliteration: te hayāścandrasaṁkāśāḥ keśavena pracoditāḥ ,pibanta iva tadvyoma jagmurdrauṇirathaṁ prati.
english: Those horses, gleaming like the moon, impelled by Keśava (Kṛṣṇa), sped towards Droṇa's son's (Drauṇi's) chariot, as if drinking up the very sky.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 42 Verse 43. Those horses, gleaming like the moon, impelled by Keśava (Kṛṣṇa), sped towards Droṇa's son's (Drauṇi's) chariot, as if drinking up the very sky.

sanskrit: दृष्ट्वायान्तौ महावीर्यावुभौ कृष्णधनंजयौ ।धृष्टद्युम्नवधे राजंश्चक्रे यत्नं महाबलः ॥४४॥
transliteration: dṛṣṭvāyāntau mahāvīryāvubhau kṛṣṇadhanaṁjayau ,dhṛṣṭadyumnavadhe rājaṁścakre yatnaṁ mahābalaḥ.
english: O king, having seen both Kṛṣṇa and Dhanañjaya (Arjuna), those mighty heroes, approaching, the exceedingly powerful (Drauṇi) made a determined effort to slay Dhṛṣṭadyumna.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 42 Verse 44. O king, having seen both Kṛṣṇa and Dhanañjaya (Arjuna), those mighty heroes, approaching, the exceedingly powerful (Drauṇi) made a determined effort to slay Dhṛṣṭadyumna.

sanskrit: विकृष्यमाणं दृष्ट्वैव धृष्टद्युम्नं जनेश्वर ।शरांश्चिक्षेप वै पार्थो द्रौणिं प्रति महाबलः ॥४५॥
transliteration: vikṛṣyamāṇaṁ dṛṣṭvaiva dhṛṣṭadyumnaṁ janeśvara ,śarāṁścikṣepa vai pārtho drauṇiṁ prati mahābalaḥ.
english: O lord of men, just seeing Dhṛṣṭadyumna being dragged, the mighty Pārtha (Arjuna) indeed shot arrows at Droṇi (Aśvatthāman).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 42 Verse 45. O lord of men, just seeing Dhṛṣṭadyumna being dragged, the mighty Pārtha (Arjuna) indeed shot arrows at Droṇi (Aśvatthāman).

sanskrit: ते शरा हेमविकृता गाण्डीवप्रेषिता भृशम् ।द्रौणिमासाद्य विविशुर्वल्मीकमिव पन्नगाः ॥४६॥
transliteration: te śarā hemavikṛtā gāṇḍīvapreṣitā bhṛśam ,drauṇimāsādya viviśurvalmīkamiva pannagāḥ.
english: Those gold-decorated arrows, intensely shot from the Gaṇḍīva bow, having reached Droṇi (Aśvatthāman), entered him like snakes entering an anthill.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 42 Verse 46. Those gold-decorated arrows, intensely shot from the Gaṇḍīva bow, having reached Droṇi (Aśvatthāman), entered him like snakes entering an anthill.

sanskrit: स विध्वस्तैः शरैर्घोरैर्द्रोणपुत्रः प्रतापवान् ।रथमारुरुहे वीरो धनंजयशरार्दितः ।प्रगृह्य च धनुः श्रेष्ठं पार्थं विव्याध सायकैः ॥४७॥
transliteration: sa vidhvastaiḥ śarairghorairdroṇaputraḥ pratāpavān ,rathamāruruhe vīro dhanaṁjayaśarārditaḥ ,pragṛhya ca dhanuḥ śreṣṭhaṁ pārthaṁ vivyādha sāyakaiḥ.
english: That mighty hero, Droṇa's son (Aśvatthāman), who was afflicted and overcome by terrible arrows from Dhanaṃjaya (Arjuna), mounted his chariot. And, grasping his excellent bow, he pierced Pārtha (Arjuna) with his own arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 42 Verse 47. That mighty hero, Droṇa's son (Aśvatthāman), who was afflicted and overcome by terrible arrows from Dhanaṃjaya (Arjuna), mounted his chariot. And, grasping his excellent bow, he pierced Pārtha (Arjuna) with his own arrows.

sanskrit: एतस्मिन्नन्तरे वीरः सहदेवो जनाधिप ।अपोवाह रथेनाजौ पार्षतं शत्रुतापनम् ॥४८॥
transliteration: etasminnantare vīraḥ sahadevo janādhipa ,apovāha rathenājau pārṣataṁ śatrutāpanam.
english: O lord of men, in the meantime, the hero Sahadeva drove Pārṣata (Dhṛṣṭadyumna), the tormentor of enemies, away in his chariot during the battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 42 Verse 48. O lord of men, in the meantime, the hero Sahadeva drove Pārṣata (Dhṛṣṭadyumna), the tormentor of enemies, away in his chariot during the battle.

sanskrit: अर्जुनोऽपि महाराज द्रौणिं विव्याध पत्रिभिः ।तं द्रोणपुत्रः संक्रुद्धो बाह्वोरुरसि चार्दयत् ॥४९॥
transliteration: arjuno'pi mahārāja drauṇiṁ vivyādha patribhiḥ ,taṁ droṇaputraḥ saṁkruddho bāhvorurasi cārdayat.
english: O great king, Arjuna also pierced Drauṇi (Aśvatthāmā) with arrows. Enraged, Drona's son (Aśvatthāmā) then wounded him (Arjuna) on his arms and chest.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 42 Verse 49. O great king, Arjuna also pierced Drauṇi (Aśvatthāmā) with arrows. Enraged, Drona's son (Aśvatthāmā) then wounded him (Arjuna) on his arms and chest.

sanskrit: क्रोधितस्तु रणे पार्थो नाराचं कालसंमितम् ।द्रोणपुत्राय चिक्षेप कालदण्डमिवापरम् ।स ब्राह्मणस्यांसदेशे निपपात महाद्युतिः ॥५०॥
transliteration: krodhitastu raṇe pārtho nārācaṁ kālasaṁmitam ,droṇaputrāya cikṣepa kāladaṇḍamivāparam ,sa brāhmaṇasyāṁsadeśe nipapāta mahādyutiḥ.
english: But enraged in battle, Arjuna, son of Pṛthā, discharged an iron arrow at Drona's son (Aśvatthāmā). It was comparable to Kala (Death) itself, like another staff of Death. That exceedingly brilliant (arrow) struck the shoulder region of the Brahmin (Aśvatthāmā).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 42 Verse 50. But enraged in battle, Arjuna, son of Pṛthā, discharged an iron arrow at Drona's son (Aśvatthāmā). It was comparable to Kala (Death) itself, like another staff of Death. That exceedingly brilliant (arrow) struck the shoulder region of the Brahmin (Aśvatthāmā).

sanskrit: स विह्वलो महाराज शरवेगेन संयुगे ।निषसाद रथोपस्थे वैक्लव्यं च परं ययौ ॥५१॥
transliteration: sa vihvalo mahārāja śaravegena saṁyuge ,niṣasāda rathopasthe vaiklavyaṁ ca paraṁ yayau.
english: O great king, agitated by the force of the arrow in battle, he (Aśvatthāmā) slumped into his chariot's seat and became exceedingly distressed.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 42 Verse 51. O great king, agitated by the force of the arrow in battle, he (Aśvatthāmā) slumped into his chariot's seat and became exceedingly distressed.

sanskrit: ततः कर्णो महाराज व्याक्षिपद्विजयं धनुः ।अर्जुनं समरे क्रुद्धः प्रेक्षमाणो मुहुर्मुहुः ।द्वैरथं चापि पार्थेन कामयानो महारणे ॥५२॥
transliteration: tataḥ karṇo mahārāja vyākṣipadvijayaṁ dhanuḥ ,arjunaṁ samare kruddhaḥ prekṣamāṇo muhurmuhuḥ ,dvairathaṁ cāpi pārthena kāmayāno mahāraṇe.
english: Then, O great king, the enraged Karna drew his bow named Vijaya. Repeatedly looking at Arjuna in battle, he desired single combat (dvairatham) with Pṛthā's son (Arjuna) in that great war.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 42 Verse 52. Then, O great king, the enraged Karna drew his bow named Vijaya. Repeatedly looking at Arjuna in battle, he desired single combat (dvairatham) with Pṛthā's son (Arjuna) in that great war.

sanskrit: तं तु हित्वा हतं वीरं सारथिः शत्रुकर्शनम् ।अपोवाह रथेनाजौ त्वरमाणो रणाजिरात् ॥५३॥
transliteration: taṁ tu hitvā hataṁ vīraṁ sārathiḥ śatrukarśanam ,apovāha rathenājau tvaramāṇo raṇājirāt.
english: But the charioteer, having abandoned a slain hero, hastily carried away that tormentor of enemies from the battlefield in his chariot.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 42 Verse 53. But the charioteer, having abandoned a slain hero, hastily carried away that tormentor of enemies from the battlefield in his chariot.

sanskrit: अथोत्क्रुष्टं महाराज पाञ्चालैर्जितकाशिभिः ।मोक्षितं पार्षतं दृष्ट्वा द्रोणपुत्रं च पीडितम् ॥५४॥
transliteration: athotkruṣṭaṁ mahārāja pāñcālairjitakāśibhiḥ ,mokṣitaṁ pārṣataṁ dṛṣṭvā droṇaputraṁ ca pīḍitam.
english: O great king, then a shout was raised by the victorious Pañcālas, having seen Dhṛṣṭadyumna released and Aśvatthāmā afflicted.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 42 Verse 54. O great king, then a shout was raised by the victorious Pañcālas, having seen Dhṛṣṭadyumna released and Aśvatthāmā afflicted.

sanskrit: वादित्राणि च दिव्यानि प्रावाद्यन्त सहस्रशः ।सिंहनादश्च संजज्ञे दृष्ट्वा घोरं महाद्भुतम् ॥५५॥
transliteration: vāditrāṇi ca divyāni prāvādyanta sahasraśaḥ ,siṁhanādaśca saṁjajñe dṛṣṭvā ghoraṁ mahādbhutam.
english: And divine musical instruments were played by thousands; and a lion's roar arose, having witnessed that dreadful, great wonder.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 42 Verse 55. And divine musical instruments were played by thousands; and a lion's roar arose, having witnessed that dreadful, great wonder.

sanskrit: एवं कृत्वाब्रवीत्पार्थो वासुदेवं धनंजयः ।याहि संशप्तकान्कृष्ण कार्यमेतत्परं मम ॥५६॥
transliteration: evaṁ kṛtvābravītpārtho vāsudevaṁ dhanaṁjayaḥ ,yāhi saṁśaptakānkṛṣṇa kāryametatparaṁ mama.
english: Having done this, Arjuna (Pārtha), the conqueror of wealth (Dhamañjaya), spoke to Krishna (Vāsudeva): 'O Krishna, go to the Saṃśaptakas! This is my supreme task.'
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 42 Verse 56. Having done this, Arjuna (Pārtha), the conqueror of wealth (Dhamañjaya), spoke to Krishna (Vāsudeva): 'O Krishna, go to the Saṃśaptakas! This is my supreme task.'

sanskrit: ततः प्रयातो दाशार्हः श्रुत्वा पाण्डवभाषितम् ।रथेनातिपताकेन मनोमारुतरंहसा ॥५७॥
transliteration: tataḥ prayāto dāśārhaḥ śrutvā pāṇḍavabhāṣitam ,rathenātipatākena manomārutaraṁhasā.
english: Then, having heard the speech of the Pāṇḍavas, Kṛṣṇa, the descendant of Daśārha, departed by chariot, which had an extraordinary banner and the speed of mind and wind.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 42 Verse 57. Then, having heard the speech of the Pāṇḍavas, Kṛṣṇa, the descendant of Daśārha, departed by chariot, which had an extraordinary banner and the speed of mind and wind.

sanskrit: संजय उवाच ।एतस्मिन्नन्तरे कृष्णः पार्थं वचनमब्रवीत् ।दर्शयन्निव कौन्तेयं धर्मराजं युधिष्ठिरम् ॥१॥
transliteration: saṁjaya uvāca ,etasminnantare kṛṣṇaḥ pārthaṁ vacanamabravīt ,darśayanniva kaunteyaṁ dharmarājaṁ yudhiṣṭhiram.
english: Saṃjaya said: At this moment, Kṛṣṇa spoke these words to Pārtha (Arjuna), as if showing him Yudhiṣṭhira, the son of Kuntī and king of natural law (dharma).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 43 Verse 1. Saṃjaya said: At this moment, Kṛṣṇa spoke these words to Pārtha (Arjuna), as if showing him Yudhiṣṭhira, the son of Kuntī and king of natural law (dharma).

sanskrit: एष पाण्डव ते भ्राता धार्तराष्ट्रैर्महाबलैः ।जिघांसुभिर्महेष्वासैर्द्रुतं पार्थानुसर्यते ॥२॥
transliteration: eṣa pāṇḍava te bhrātā dhārtarāṣṭrairmahābalaiḥ ,jighāṁsubhirmaheṣvāsairdrutaṁ pārthānusaryate.
english: “O Pāṇḍava (Arjuna), this brother of yours is swiftly pursued by the Dhārtarāṣṭras, who are mighty, desirous of killing, and great archers, O Pārtha (Arjuna).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 43 Verse 2. “O Pāṇḍava (Arjuna), this brother of yours is swiftly pursued by the Dhārtarāṣṭras, who are mighty, desirous of killing, and great archers, O Pārtha (Arjuna).

sanskrit: तथानुयान्ति संरब्धाः पाञ्चाला युद्धदुर्मदाः ।युधिष्ठिरं महात्मानं परीप्सन्तो महाजवाः ॥३॥
transliteration: tathānuyānti saṁrabdhāḥ pāñcālā yuddhadurmadāḥ ,yudhiṣṭhiraṁ mahātmānaṁ parīpsanto mahājavāḥ.
english: Similarly, the enraged Pāñcālas, who are fierce in battle and very swift, follow, desiring to protect the great-souled Yudhiṣṭhira.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 43 Verse 3. Similarly, the enraged Pāñcālas, who are fierce in battle and very swift, follow, desiring to protect the great-souled Yudhiṣṭhira.

sanskrit: एष दुर्योधनः पार्थ रथानीकेन दंशितः ।राजा सर्वस्य लोकस्य राजानमनुधावति ॥४॥
transliteration: eṣa duryodhanaḥ pārtha rathānīkena daṁśitaḥ ,rājā sarvasya lokasya rājānamanudhāvati.
english: O Pārtha, this Duryodhana, the king of all people, clad in armor with his chariot army, pursues the (other) king (Yudhiṣṭhira).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 43 Verse 4. O Pārtha, this Duryodhana, the king of all people, clad in armor with his chariot army, pursues the (other) king (Yudhiṣṭhira).

sanskrit: जिघांसुः पुरुषव्याघ्रं भ्रातृभिः सहितो बली ।आशीविषसमस्पर्शैः सर्वयुद्धविशारदैः ॥५॥
transliteration: jighāṁsuḥ puruṣavyāghraṁ bhrātṛbhiḥ sahito balī ,āśīviṣasamasparśaiḥ sarvayuddhaviśāradaiḥ.
english: Powerful, desiring to kill the tiger among men (puruṣavyāghra), he (Duryodhana) is accompanied by his brothers, who are like venomous snakes in their touch and are skilled in all warfare.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 43 Verse 5. Powerful, desiring to kill the tiger among men (puruṣavyāghra), he (Duryodhana) is accompanied by his brothers, who are like venomous snakes in their touch and are skilled in all warfare.

sanskrit: एते जिघृक्षवो यान्ति द्विपाश्वरथपत्तयः ।युधिष्ठिरं धार्तराष्ट्रा रत्नोत्तममिवार्थिनः ॥६॥
transliteration: ete jighṛkṣavo yānti dvipāśvarathapattayaḥ ,yudhiṣṭhiraṁ dhārtarāṣṭrā ratnottamamivārthinaḥ.
english: These sons of Dhṛtarāṣṭra (Dhārtarāṣṭra), desirous of seizing Yudhiṣṭhira, advance with their elephants, horses, chariots, and infantry, just as seekers (desire) a supreme gem.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 43 Verse 6. These sons of Dhṛtarāṣṭra (Dhārtarāṣṭra), desirous of seizing Yudhiṣṭhira, advance with their elephants, horses, chariots, and infantry, just as seekers (desire) a supreme gem.

sanskrit: पश्य सात्वतभीमाभ्यां निरुद्धाधिष्ठितः प्रभुः ।जिहीर्षवोऽमृतं दैत्याः शक्राग्निभ्यामिवावशाः ॥७॥
transliteration: paśya sātvatabhīmābhyāṁ niruddhādhiṣṭhitaḥ prabhuḥ ,jihīrṣavo'mṛtaṁ daityāḥ śakrāgnibhyāmivāvaśāḥ.
english: Behold the lord (Duryodhana), who is blocked and held by Sātvata (Kṛṣṇa) and Bhīma, just like the helpless Daitiyas (demons), desiring to seize nectar, were held by Indra and Agni.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 43 Verse 7. Behold the lord (Duryodhana), who is blocked and held by Sātvata (Kṛṣṇa) and Bhīma, just like the helpless Daitiyas (demons), desiring to seize nectar, were held by Indra and Agni.

sanskrit: एते बहुत्वात्त्वरिताः पुनर्गच्छन्ति पाण्डवम् ।समुद्रमिव वार्योघाः प्रावृट्काले महारथाः ॥८॥
transliteration: ete bahutvāttvaritāḥ punargacchanti pāṇḍavam ,samudramiva vāryoghāḥ prāvṛṭkāle mahārathāḥ.
english: These great charioteers (mahārathas), numerous and swift, repeatedly advance towards the son of Pāṇḍu, just as floods rush into the ocean during the rainy season.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 43 Verse 8. These great charioteers (mahārathas), numerous and swift, repeatedly advance towards the son of Pāṇḍu, just as floods rush into the ocean during the rainy season.

sanskrit: नदन्तः सिंहनादांश्च धमन्तश्चापि वारिजान् ।बलवन्तो महेष्वासा विधुन्वन्तो धनूंषि च ॥९॥
transliteration: nadantaḥ siṁhanādāṁśca dhamantaścāpi vārijān ,balavanto maheṣvāsā vidhunvanto dhanūṁṣi ca.
english: These mighty and great archers (maheṣvāsas) are roaring lion-like cries, blowing their conch-shells, and wielding their bows.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 43 Verse 9. These mighty and great archers (maheṣvāsas) are roaring lion-like cries, blowing their conch-shells, and wielding their bows.

sanskrit: मृत्योर्मुखगतं मन्ये कुन्तीपुत्रं युधिष्ठिरम् ।हुतमग्नौ च भद्रं ते दुर्योधनवशं गतम् ॥१०॥
transliteration: mṛtyormukhagataṁ manye kuntīputraṁ yudhiṣṭhiram ,hutamagnau ca bhadraṁ te duryodhanavaśaṁ gatam.
english: I consider Yudhiṣṭhira, the son of Kuntī, to be in the jaws of Death and offered into the sacrificial fire. May it be well for you, as he has fallen under Duryodhana's control.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 43 Verse 10. I consider Yudhiṣṭhira, the son of Kuntī, to be in the jaws of Death and offered into the sacrificial fire. May it be well for you, as he has fallen under Duryodhana's control.

sanskrit: यथायुक्तमनीकं हि धार्तराष्ट्रस्य पाण्डव ।नास्य शक्रोऽपि मुच्येत संप्राप्तो बाणगोचरम् ॥११॥
transliteration: yathāyuktamanīkaṁ hi dhārtarāṣṭrasya pāṇḍava ,nāsya śakro'pi mucyeta saṁprāpto bāṇagocaram.
english: O son of Pāṇḍu, the army of Dhṛtarāṣṭra (Duryodhana) is indeed so skillfully arrayed that even Indra (Śakra) would not escape it once he entered the range of its arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 43 Verse 11. O son of Pāṇḍu, the army of Dhṛtarāṣṭra (Duryodhana) is indeed so skillfully arrayed that even Indra (Śakra) would not escape it once he entered the range of its arrows.

sanskrit: दुर्योधनस्य शूरस्य द्रौणेः शारद्वतस्य च ।कर्णस्य चेषुवेगो वै पर्वतानपि दारयेत् ॥१२॥
transliteration: duryodhanasya śūrasya drauṇeḥ śāradvatasya ca ,karṇasya ceṣuvego vai parvatānapi dārayet.
english: Indeed, the force of arrows from the brave Duryodhana, Drona's son (Aśvatthāman), Śāradvata's son (Kṛpa), and Karṇa could tear apart even mountains.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 43 Verse 12. Indeed, the force of arrows from the brave Duryodhana, Drona's son (Aśvatthāman), Śāradvata's son (Kṛpa), and Karṇa could tear apart even mountains.

sanskrit: दुर्योधनस्य शूरस्य शरौघाञ्शीघ्रमस्यतः ।संक्रुद्धस्यान्तकस्येव को वेगं संसहेद्रणे ॥१३॥
transliteration: duryodhanasya śūrasya śaraughāñśīghramasyataḥ ,saṁkruddhasyāntakasyeva ko vegaṁ saṁsahedraṇe.
english: In battle, who could endure the impetuosity of the brave Duryodhana, who swiftly discharges torrents of arrows, especially when he is like enraged Death (Antaka)?
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 43 Verse 13. In battle, who could endure the impetuosity of the brave Duryodhana, who swiftly discharges torrents of arrows, especially when he is like enraged Death (Antaka)?

sanskrit: कर्णेन च कृतो राजा विमुखः शत्रुतापनः ।बलवाँल्लघुहस्तश्च कृती युद्धविशारदः ॥१४॥
transliteration: karṇena ca kṛto rājā vimukhaḥ śatrutāpanaḥ ,balavāँllaghuhastaśca kṛtī yuddhaviśāradaḥ.
english: And the king (Duryodhana), whom Karṇa made victorious, is a tormentor of foes, powerful, quick in action, accomplished, and an expert in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 43 Verse 14. And the king (Duryodhana), whom Karṇa made victorious, is a tormentor of foes, powerful, quick in action, accomplished, and an expert in battle.

sanskrit: राधेयः पाण्डवश्रेष्ठं शक्तः पीडयितुं रणे ।सहितो धृतराष्ट्रस्य पुत्रैः शूरो महात्मभिः ॥१५॥
transliteration: rādheyaḥ pāṇḍavaśreṣṭhaṁ śaktaḥ pīḍayituṁ raṇe ,sahito dhṛtarāṣṭrasya putraiḥ śūro mahātmabhiḥ.
english: The brave Karṇa (Rādheya), accompanied by Dhṛtarāṣṭra's great-souled sons, is capable of tormenting the foremost of the Pāṇḍavas (Arjuna) in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 43 Verse 15. The brave Karṇa (Rādheya), accompanied by Dhṛtarāṣṭra's great-souled sons, is capable of tormenting the foremost of the Pāṇḍavas (Arjuna) in battle.

sanskrit: तस्यैवं युध्यमानस्य संग्रामे संयतात्मनः ।अन्यैरपि च पार्थस्य हृतं वर्म महारथैः ॥१६॥
transliteration: tasyaivaṁ yudhyamānasya saṁgrāme saṁyatātmanaḥ ,anyairapi ca pārthasya hṛtaṁ varma mahārathaiḥ.
english: While he, Pārtha, whose self (ātman) was restrained, was fighting in this manner in battle, his armor was also seized by other great charioteers.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 43 Verse 16. While he, Pārtha, whose self (ātman) was restrained, was fighting in this manner in battle, his armor was also seized by other great charioteers.

sanskrit: उपवासकृशो राजा भृशं भरतसत्तम ।ब्राह्मे बले स्थितो ह्येष न क्षत्रेऽतिबले विभो ॥१७॥
transliteration: upavāsakṛśo rājā bhṛśaṁ bharatasattama ,brāhme bale sthito hyeṣa na kṣatre'tibale vibho.
english: O best among the Bharatas, the king is exceedingly emaciated by fasting. Indeed, O lord, he relies on spiritual strength, not on the immense power of a warrior (kṣatra).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 43 Verse 17. O best among the Bharatas, the king is exceedingly emaciated by fasting. Indeed, O lord, he relies on spiritual strength, not on the immense power of a warrior (kṣatra).

sanskrit: न जीवति महाराजो मन्ये पार्थ युधिष्ठिरः ।यद्भीमसेनः सहते सिंहनादममर्षणः ॥१८॥
transliteration: na jīvati mahārājo manye pārtha yudhiṣṭhiraḥ ,yadbhīmasenaḥ sahate siṁhanādamamarṣaṇaḥ.
english: O Pārtha, I believe that the great king Yudhiṣṭhira is not alive, because Bhīmasena, who is typically intolerant, is (now) enduring the lion's roar.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 43 Verse 18. O Pārtha, I believe that the great king Yudhiṣṭhira is not alive, because Bhīmasena, who is typically intolerant, is (now) enduring the lion's roar.

sanskrit: नर्दतां धार्तराष्ट्राणां पुनः पुनररिंदम ।धमतां च महाशङ्खान्संग्रामे जितकाशिनाम् ॥१९॥
transliteration: nardatāṁ dhārtarāṣṭrāṇāṁ punaḥ punarariṁdama ,dhamatāṁ ca mahāśaṅkhānsaṁgrāme jitakāśinām.
english: O subduer of enemies, (he is enduring the roars) of the Dhārtarāṣṭras who are repeatedly roaring and blowing great conch shells in battle, those who are exulting in their victory.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 43 Verse 19. O subduer of enemies, (he is enduring the roars) of the Dhārtarāṣṭras who are repeatedly roaring and blowing great conch shells in battle, those who are exulting in their victory.

sanskrit: युधिष्ठिरं पाण्डवेयं हतेति भरतर्षभ ।संचोदयत्यसौ कर्णो धार्तराष्ट्रान्महाबलान् ॥२०॥
transliteration: yudhiṣṭhiraṁ pāṇḍaveyaṁ hateti bharatarṣabha ,saṁcodayatyasau karṇo dhārtarāṣṭrānmahābalān.
english: O best among the Bhāratas (bharatarṣabha)! This Karṇa is inciting the immensely powerful sons of Dhṛtarāṣṭra, believing that Yudhiṣṭhira, son of Pāṇḍu, has been slain.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 43 Verse 20. O best among the Bhāratas (bharatarṣabha)! This Karṇa is inciting the immensely powerful sons of Dhṛtarāṣṭra, believing that Yudhiṣṭhira, son of Pāṇḍu, has been slain.

sanskrit: स्थूणाकर्णेन्द्रजालेन पार्थ पाशुपतेन च ।प्रच्छादयन्तो राजानमनुयान्ति महारथाः ।आतुरो मे मतो राजा संनिषेव्यश्च भारत ॥२१॥
transliteration: sthūṇākarṇendrajālena pārtha pāśupatena ca ,pracchādayanto rājānamanuyānti mahārathāḥ ,āturo me mato rājā saṁniṣevyaśca bhārata.
english: O Pārtha (Arjuna), the great charioteers are following the king, shielding him with the magic of "sthūṇākarṇa" (pillar-ear illusion) and also with the Pāśupata (weapon). O Bhārata, I consider the king to be distressed and needing protection.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 43 Verse 21. O Pārtha (Arjuna), the great charioteers are following the king, shielding him with the magic of "sthūṇākarṇa" (pillar-ear illusion) and also with the Pāśupata (weapon). O Bhārata, I consider the king to be distressed and needing protection.

sanskrit: यथैनमनुवर्तन्ते पाञ्चालाः सह पाण्डवैः ।त्वरमाणास्त्वराकाले सर्वशस्त्रभृतां वराः ।मज्जन्तमिव पाताले बलिनोऽप्युज्जिहीर्षवः ॥२२॥
transliteration: yathainamanuvartante pāñcālāḥ saha pāṇḍavaiḥ ,tvaramāṇāstvarākāle sarvaśastrabhṛtāṁ varāḥ ,majjantamiva pātāle balino'pyujjihīrṣavaḥ.
english: Just as the Pāñcālas, along with the Pāṇḍavas, who are the foremost among all weapon-bearers, are swiftly following him in this urgent time, desiring to lift him up as if he were sinking into the netherworld (pātāla), even though they themselves are powerful.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 43 Verse 22. Just as the Pāñcālas, along with the Pāṇḍavas, who are the foremost among all weapon-bearers, are swiftly following him in this urgent time, desiring to lift him up as if he were sinking into the netherworld (pātāla), even though they themselves are powerful.

sanskrit: न केतुर्दृश्यते राज्ञः कर्णेन निहतः शरैः ।पश्यतोर्यमयोः पार्थ सात्यकेश्च शिखण्डिनः ॥२३॥
transliteration: na keturdṛśyate rājñaḥ karṇena nihataḥ śaraiḥ ,paśyatoryamayoḥ pārtha sātyakeśca śikhaṇḍinaḥ.
english: O Pārtha (Arjuna), the king's banner is not visible, having been struck down by Karṇa's arrows, even while the two twin brothers (Nākula and Sahadeva), Sātyaki, and Śikhaṇḍin are watching.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 43 Verse 23. O Pārtha (Arjuna), the king's banner is not visible, having been struck down by Karṇa's arrows, even while the two twin brothers (Nākula and Sahadeva), Sātyaki, and Śikhaṇḍin are watching.

sanskrit: धृष्टद्युम्नस्य भीमस्य शतानीकस्य वा विभो ।पाञ्चालानां च सर्वेषां चेदीनां चैव भारत ॥२४॥
transliteration: dhṛṣṭadyumnasya bhīmasya śatānīkasya vā vibho ,pāñcālānāṁ ca sarveṣāṁ cedīnāṁ caiva bhārata.
english: O mighty one, O descendant of Bharata, (this list continues with) of Dhṛṣṭadyumna, Bhīma, or Śatānīka, and of all the Pāñcālas and also the Cedis.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 43 Verse 24. O mighty one, O descendant of Bharata, (this list continues with) of Dhṛṣṭadyumna, Bhīma, or Śatānīka, and of all the Pāñcālas and also the Cedis.

sanskrit: एष कर्णो रणे पार्थ पाण्डवानामनीकिनीम् ।शरैर्विध्वंसयति वै नलिनीमिव कुञ्जरः ॥२५॥
transliteration: eṣa karṇo raṇe pārtha pāṇḍavānāmanīkinīm ,śarairvidhvaṁsayati vai nalinīmiva kuñjaraḥ.
english: O Pārtha, this Karṇa in battle is certainly destroying the army of the Pāṇḍavas with arrows, just as an elephant devastates a lotus pond.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 43 Verse 25. O Pārtha, this Karṇa in battle is certainly destroying the army of the Pāṇḍavas with arrows, just as an elephant devastates a lotus pond.

sanskrit: एते द्रवन्ति रथिनस्त्वदीयाः पाण्डुनन्दन ।पश्य पश्य यथा पार्थ गच्छन्त्येते महारथाः ॥२६॥
transliteration: ete dravanti rathinastvadīyāḥ pāṇḍunandana ,paśya paśya yathā pārtha gacchantyete mahārathāḥ.
english: O son of Pāṇḍu, these charioteers of yours are fleeing. O Pārtha, behold, behold how these great charioteers are retreating!
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 43 Verse 26. O son of Pāṇḍu, these charioteers of yours are fleeing. O Pārtha, behold, behold how these great charioteers are retreating!

sanskrit: एते भारत मातङ्गाः कर्णेनाभिहता रणे ।आर्तनादान्विकुर्वाणा विद्रवन्ति दिशो दश ॥२७॥
transliteration: ete bhārata mātaṅgāḥ karṇenābhihatā raṇe ,ārtanādānvikurvāṇā vidravanti diśo daśa.
english: O Bhārata, these elephants, struck by Karṇa in battle, are uttering cries of distress and fleeing in all ten directions.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 43 Verse 27. O Bhārata, these elephants, struck by Karṇa in battle, are uttering cries of distress and fleeing in all ten directions.

sanskrit: रथानां द्रवतां वृन्दं पश्य पार्थ समन्ततः ।द्राव्यमाणं रणे चैव कर्णेनामित्रकर्शिना ॥२८॥
transliteration: rathānāṁ dravatāṁ vṛndaṁ paśya pārtha samantataḥ ,drāvyamāṇaṁ raṇe caiva karṇenāmitrakarśinā.
english: O Pārtha, behold the multitude of chariots fleeing from all directions, being driven away in battle by Karṇa, the annihilator of foes.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 43 Verse 28. O Pārtha, behold the multitude of chariots fleeing from all directions, being driven away in battle by Karṇa, the annihilator of foes.

sanskrit: हस्तिकक्ष्यां रणे पश्य चरन्तीं तत्र तत्र ह ।रथस्थं सूतपुत्रस्य केतुं केतुमतां वर ॥२९॥
transliteration: hastikakṣyāṁ raṇe paśya carantīṁ tatra tatra ha ,rathasthaṁ sūtaputrasya ketuṁ ketumatāṁ vara.
english: O best among those bearing banners, behold the elephant division moving here and there in the battle. And see the banner of Sūta's son (Karṇa), positioned on his chariot.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 43 Verse 29. O best among those bearing banners, behold the elephant division moving here and there in the battle. And see the banner of Sūta's son (Karṇa), positioned on his chariot.

sanskrit: असौ धावति राधेयो भीमसेनरथं प्रति ।किरञ्शरशतानीव विनिघ्नंस्तव वाहिनीम् ॥३०॥
transliteration: asau dhāvati rādheyo bhīmasenarathaṁ prati ,kirañśaraśatānīva vinighnaṁstava vāhinīm.
english: That Rādheya (Karṇa) is rushing towards Bhīmasena's chariot, showering hundreds of arrows as if in a volley, and annihilating your army.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 43 Verse 30. That Rādheya (Karṇa) is rushing towards Bhīmasena's chariot, showering hundreds of arrows as if in a volley, and annihilating your army.

sanskrit: एतान्पश्य च पाञ्चालान्द्राव्यमाणान्महात्मना ।शक्रेणेव यथा दैत्यान्हन्यमानान्महाहवे ॥३१॥
transliteration: etānpaśya ca pāñcālāndrāvyamāṇānmahātmanā ,śakreṇeva yathā daityānhanyamānānmahāhave.
