english: And behold these Pāñcālas being driven away by the great-souled (Karṇa), just as the Daityas are being slaughtered by Indra in a mighty battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 43 Verse 31. And behold these Pāñcālas being driven away by the great-souled (Karṇa), just as the Daityas are being slaughtered by Indra in a mighty battle.

sanskrit: एष कर्णो रणे जित्वा पाञ्चालान्पाण्डुसृञ्जयान् ।दिशो विप्रेक्षते सर्वास्त्वदर्थमिति मे मतिः ॥३२॥
transliteration: eṣa karṇo raṇe jitvā pāñcālānpāṇḍusṛñjayān ,diśo viprekṣate sarvāstvadarthamiti me matiḥ.
english: Having conquered the Panchalas, the Pandavas, and the Srinjayas in battle, this Karna looks in all directions for your sake; this is my opinion.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 43 Verse 32. Having conquered the Panchalas, the Pandavas, and the Srinjayas in battle, this Karna looks in all directions for your sake; this is my opinion.

sanskrit: पश्य पार्थ धनुः श्रेष्ठं विकर्षन्साधु शोभते ।शत्रूञ्जित्वा यथा शक्रो देवसंघैः समावृतः ॥३३॥
transliteration: paśya pārtha dhanuḥ śreṣṭhaṁ vikarṣansādhu śobhate ,śatrūñjitvā yathā śakro devasaṁghaiḥ samāvṛtaḥ.
english: O Partha, behold how splendidly he shines, skillfully drawing his excellent bow, having conquered the enemies, just like Shakra (Indra) surrounded by hosts of gods.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 43 Verse 33. O Partha, behold how splendidly he shines, skillfully drawing his excellent bow, having conquered the enemies, just like Shakra (Indra) surrounded by hosts of gods.

sanskrit: एते नदन्ति कौरव्या दृष्ट्वा कर्णस्य विक्रमम् ।त्रासयन्तो रणे पार्थान्सृञ्जयांश्च सहस्रशः ॥३४॥
transliteration: ete nadanti kauravyā dṛṣṭvā karṇasya vikramam ,trāsayanto raṇe pārthānsṛñjayāṁśca sahasraśaḥ.
english: These Kurus roar, having witnessed Karna's prowess (vikrama), terrifying thousands of Pandavas and Srinjayas on the battlefield.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 43 Verse 34. These Kurus roar, having witnessed Karna's prowess (vikrama), terrifying thousands of Pandavas and Srinjayas on the battlefield.

sanskrit: एष सर्वात्मना पाण्डूंस्त्रासयित्वा महारणे ।अभिभाषति राधेयः सर्वसैन्यानि मानदः ॥३५॥
transliteration: eṣa sarvātmanā pāṇḍūṁstrāsayitvā mahāraṇe ,abhibhāṣati rādheyaḥ sarvasainyāni mānadaḥ.
english: This son of Radha (Karna), the giver of honor, having terrified the Pandavas with all his might in the great battle, now addresses all the armies.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 43 Verse 35. This son of Radha (Karna), the giver of honor, having terrified the Pandavas with all his might in the great battle, now addresses all the armies.

sanskrit: अभिद्रवत गच्छध्वं द्रुतं द्रवत कौरवाः ।यथा जीवन्न वः कश्चिन्मुच्यते युधि सृञ्जयः ॥३६॥
transliteration: abhidravata gacchadhvaṁ drutaṁ dravata kauravāḥ ,yathā jīvanna vaḥ kaścinmucyate yudhi sṛñjayaḥ.
english: O Kauravas, attack, go forth swiftly, run! So that no Sṛñjaya among them escapes alive from you in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 43 Verse 36. O Kauravas, attack, go forth swiftly, run! So that no Sṛñjaya among them escapes alive from you in battle.

sanskrit: तथा कुरुत संयत्ता वयं यास्याम पृष्ठतः ।एवमुक्त्वा ययावेष पृष्ठतो विकिरञ्शरैः ॥३७॥
transliteration: tathā kuruta saṁyattā vayaṁ yāsyāma pṛṣṭhataḥ ,evamuktvā yayāveṣa pṛṣṭhato vikirañśaraiḥ.
english: Act thus, being prepared; we shall go to the rear. Having spoken thus, he went to the rear, showering arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 43 Verse 37. Act thus, being prepared; we shall go to the rear. Having spoken thus, he went to the rear, showering arrows.

sanskrit: पश्य कर्णं रणे पार्थ श्वेतच्छविविराजितम् ।उदयं पर्वतं यद्वच्छोभयन्वै दिवाकरः ॥३८॥
transliteration: paśya karṇaṁ raṇe pārtha śvetacchavivirājitam ,udayaṁ parvataṁ yadvacchobhayanvai divākaraḥ.
english: O Pārtha, behold Karṇa in battle, radiant with white splendor, just as the sun indeed adorns the rising mountain (Udaya).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 43 Verse 38. O Pārtha, behold Karṇa in battle, radiant with white splendor, just as the sun indeed adorns the rising mountain (Udaya).

sanskrit: पूर्णचन्द्रनिकाशेन मूर्ध्नि छत्रेण भारत ।ध्रियमाणेन समरे तथा शतशलाकिना ॥३९॥
transliteration: pūrṇacandranikāśena mūrdhni chatreṇa bhārata ,dhriyamāṇena samare tathā śataśalākinā.
english: O Bhārata, by the umbrella held above his head in battle, which resembles a full moon and also has a hundred ribs, he [shines].
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 43 Verse 39. O Bhārata, by the umbrella held above his head in battle, which resembles a full moon and also has a hundred ribs, he [shines].

sanskrit: एष त्वां प्रेक्षते कर्णः सकटाक्षो विशां पते ।उत्तमं यत्नमास्थाय ध्रुवमेष्यति संयुगे ॥४०॥
transliteration: eṣa tvāṁ prekṣate karṇaḥ sakaṭākṣo viśāṁ pate ,uttamaṁ yatnamāsthāya dhruvameṣyati saṁyuge.
english: O ruler of the people, Karna is looking at you with a sidelong glance. Having exerted his utmost effort, he will certainly advance into battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 43 Verse 40. O ruler of the people, Karna is looking at you with a sidelong glance. Having exerted his utmost effort, he will certainly advance into battle.

sanskrit: पश्य ह्येनं महाबाहो विधुन्वानं महद्धनुः ।शरांश्चाशीविषाकारान्विसृजन्तं महाबलम् ॥४१॥
transliteration: paśya hyenaṁ mahābāho vidhunvānaṁ mahaddhanuḥ ,śarāṁścāśīviṣākārānvisṛjantaṁ mahābalam.
english: O mighty-armed one, behold him, the exceedingly powerful one, brandishing his great bow and releasing arrows that resemble venomous snakes.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 43 Verse 41. O mighty-armed one, behold him, the exceedingly powerful one, brandishing his great bow and releasing arrows that resemble venomous snakes.

sanskrit: असौ निवृत्तो राधेयो दृश्यते वानरध्वज ।वधाय चात्मनोऽभ्येति दीपस्य शलभो यथा ॥४२॥
transliteration: asau nivṛtto rādheyo dṛśyate vānaradhvaja ,vadhāya cātmano'bhyeti dīpasya śalabho yathā.
english: O Arjuna (vānaradhvaja), that son of Rādhā (Karna) is seen having turned back. He comes for his own destruction (ātman), just as a moth approaches a lamp.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 43 Verse 42. O Arjuna (vānaradhvaja), that son of Rādhā (Karna) is seen having turned back. He comes for his own destruction (ātman), just as a moth approaches a lamp.

sanskrit: कर्णमेकाकिनं दृष्ट्वा रथानीकेन भारत ।रिरक्षिषुः सुसंयत्तो धार्तराष्ट्रोऽभिवर्तते ॥४३॥
transliteration: karṇamekākinaṁ dṛṣṭvā rathānīkena bhārata ,rirakṣiṣuḥ susaṁyatto dhārtarāṣṭro'bhivartate.
english: O descendant of Bharata, seeing Karna all alone, Duryodhana (dhārtarāṣṭraḥ), well-prepared and desiring to protect him, advances with his chariot army.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 43 Verse 43. O descendant of Bharata, seeing Karna all alone, Duryodhana (dhārtarāṣṭraḥ), well-prepared and desiring to protect him, advances with his chariot army.

sanskrit: सार्वैः सहैभिर्दुष्टात्मा वध्य एष प्रयत्नतः ।त्वया यशश्च राज्यं च सुखं चोत्तममिच्छता ॥४४॥
transliteration: sārvaiḥ sahaibhirduṣṭātmā vadhya eṣa prayatnataḥ ,tvayā yaśaśca rājyaṁ ca sukhaṁ cottamamicchatā.
english: You, who desire fame, a kingdom, and supreme happiness, must diligently kill this evil-minded one along with all his associates.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 43 Verse 44. You, who desire fame, a kingdom, and supreme happiness, must diligently kill this evil-minded one along with all his associates.

sanskrit: आत्मानं च कृतात्मानं समीक्ष्य भरतर्षभ ।कृतागसं च राधेयं धर्मात्मनि युधिष्ठिरे ॥४५॥
transliteration: ātmānaṁ ca kṛtātmānaṁ samīkṣya bharatarṣabha ,kṛtāgasaṁ ca rādheyaṁ dharmātmani yudhiṣṭhire.
english: O best of the Bharatas, having considered your own true self (ātman), and the perfected self (ātman), and also Rādhā's son (Karṇa), who has committed offenses against the righteous-souled (dharmātman) Yudhiṣṭhira...
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 43 Verse 45. O best of the Bharatas, having considered your own true self (ātman), and the perfected self (ātman), and also Rādhā's son (Karṇa), who has committed offenses against the righteous-souled (dharmātman) Yudhiṣṭhira...

sanskrit: प्रतिपद्यस्व राधेयं प्राप्तकालमनन्तरम् ।आर्यां युद्धे मतिं कृत्वा प्रत्येहि रथयूथपम् ॥४६॥
transliteration: pratipadyasva rādheyaṁ prāptakālamanantaram ,āryāṁ yuddhe matiṁ kṛtvā pratyehi rathayūthapam.
english: Confront Rādhā's son (Karṇa) immediately, for the opportune moment has arrived. Having formed a noble resolve for battle, go back to engage the commander of the chariots.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 43 Verse 46. Confront Rādhā's son (Karṇa) immediately, for the opportune moment has arrived. Having formed a noble resolve for battle, go back to engage the commander of the chariots.

sanskrit: पञ्च ह्येतानि मुख्यानां रथानां रथसत्तम ।शतान्यायान्ति वेगेन बलिनां भीमतेजसाम् ॥४७॥
transliteration: pañca hyetāni mukhyānāṁ rathānāṁ rathasattama ,śatānyāyānti vegena balināṁ bhīmatejasām.
english: Indeed, O best of charioteers, these five hundred principal chariots, belonging to powerful warriors of dreadful might, are approaching swiftly.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 43 Verse 47. Indeed, O best of charioteers, these five hundred principal chariots, belonging to powerful warriors of dreadful might, are approaching swiftly.

sanskrit: पञ्च नागसहस्राणि द्विगुणा वाजिनस्तथा ।अभिसंहत्य कौन्तेय पदातिप्रयुतानि च ।अन्योन्यरक्षितं वीर बलं त्वामभिवर्तते ॥४८॥
transliteration: pañca nāgasahasrāṇi dviguṇā vājinastathā ,abhisaṁhatya kaunteya padātiprayutāni ca ,anyonyarakṣitaṁ vīra balaṁ tvāmabhivartate.
english: O son of Kunti (kaunteya), O hero (vīra)! A mutually protected force, comprising five thousand elephants, twice that number of horses, and millions of infantry, having assembled, is advancing towards you.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 43 Verse 48. O son of Kunti (kaunteya), O hero (vīra)! A mutually protected force, comprising five thousand elephants, twice that number of horses, and millions of infantry, having assembled, is advancing towards you.

sanskrit: सूतपुत्रे महेष्वासे दर्शयात्मानमात्मना ।उत्तमं यत्नमास्थाय प्रत्येहि भरतर्षभ ॥४९॥
transliteration: sūtaputre maheṣvāse darśayātmānamātmanā ,uttamaṁ yatnamāsthāya pratyehi bharatarṣabha.
english: O son of a charioteer (sūtaputra), O great archer! Demonstrate your true nature (ātman) by your own actions (ātmanā). O best among the Bharatas, having put forth your supreme effort, confront them!
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 43 Verse 49. O son of a charioteer (sūtaputra), O great archer! Demonstrate your true nature (ātman) by your own actions (ātmanā). O best among the Bharatas, having put forth your supreme effort, confront them!

sanskrit: असौ कर्णः सुसंरब्धः पाञ्चालानभिधावति ।केतुमस्य हि पश्यामि धृष्टद्युम्नरथं प्रति ।समुच्छेत्स्यति पाञ्चालानिति मन्ये परंतप ॥५०॥
transliteration: asau karṇaḥ susaṁrabdhaḥ pāñcālānabhidhāvati ,ketumasya hi paśyāmi dhṛṣṭadyumnarathaṁ prati ,samucchetsyati pāñcālāniti manye paraṁtapa.
english: That Karna, highly enraged, is attacking the Pāñcālas. Indeed, I see his banner positioned against Dhṛṣṭadyumna's chariot. O tormentor of enemies (paraṃtapa), I believe he will utterly destroy the Pāñcālas.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 43 Verse 50. That Karna, highly enraged, is attacking the Pāñcālas. Indeed, I see his banner positioned against Dhṛṣṭadyumna's chariot. O tormentor of enemies (paraṃtapa), I believe he will utterly destroy the Pāñcālas.

sanskrit: आचक्षे ते प्रियं पार्थ तदेवं भरतर्षभ ।राजा जीवति कौरव्यो धर्मपुत्रो युधिष्ठिरः ॥५१॥
transliteration: ācakṣe te priyaṁ pārtha tadevaṁ bharatarṣabha ,rājā jīvati kauravyo dharmaputro yudhiṣṭhiraḥ.
english: O Pārtha, O best among the Bharatas! I announce this pleasant news to you: the Kuru king, Yudhiṣṭhira, the son of Dharma (dharma), is alive.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 43 Verse 51. O Pārtha, O best among the Bharatas! I announce this pleasant news to you: the Kuru king, Yudhiṣṭhira, the son of Dharma (dharma), is alive.

sanskrit: असौ भीमो महाबाहुः संनिवृत्तश्चमूमुखे ।वृतः सृञ्जयसैन्येन सात्यकेन च भारत ॥५२॥
transliteration: asau bhīmo mahābāhuḥ saṁnivṛttaścamūmukhe ,vṛtaḥ sṛñjayasainyena sātyakena ca bhārata.
english: O Bharata, this mighty-armed Bhima has returned and stands firm at the front of the army, surrounded by the Srinjaya forces and Satyaki.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 43 Verse 52. O Bharata, this mighty-armed Bhima has returned and stands firm at the front of the army, surrounded by the Srinjaya forces and Satyaki.

sanskrit: वध्यन्त एते समरे कौरवा निशितैः शरैः ।भीमसेनेन कौन्तेय पाञ्चालैश्च महात्मभिः ॥५३॥
transliteration: vadhyanta ete samare kauravā niśitaiḥ śaraiḥ ,bhīmasenena kaunteya pāñcālaiśca mahātmabhiḥ.
english: O son of Kunti, these Kauravas are being killed in battle by sharp arrows, by Bhimasena, and by the great-souled Panchalas.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 43 Verse 53. O son of Kunti, these Kauravas are being killed in battle by sharp arrows, by Bhimasena, and by the great-souled Panchalas.

sanskrit: सेना हि धार्तराष्ट्रस्य विमुखा चाभवद्रणात् ।विप्रधावति वेगेन भीमस्य निहता शरैः ॥५४॥
transliteration: senā hi dhārtarāṣṭrasya vimukhā cābhavadraṇāt ,vipradhāvati vegena bhīmasya nihatā śaraiḥ.
english: Indeed, the army of Dhritarashtra's son (Duryodhana) turned its back on the battle. Struck by Bhima's arrows, it flees rapidly.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 43 Verse 54. Indeed, the army of Dhritarashtra's son (Duryodhana) turned its back on the battle. Struck by Bhima's arrows, it flees rapidly.

sanskrit: विपन्नसस्येव मही रुधिरेण समुक्षिता ।भारती भरतश्रेष्ठ सेना कृपणदर्शना ॥५५॥
transliteration: vipannasasyeva mahī rudhireṇa samukṣitā ,bhāratī bharataśreṣṭha senā kṛpaṇadarśanā.
english: O best among Bharatas, the army of the Bharatas, drenched with blood, presents a pitiable sight, like a land with ruined crops.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 43 Verse 55. O best among Bharatas, the army of the Bharatas, drenched with blood, presents a pitiable sight, like a land with ruined crops.

sanskrit: निवृत्तं पश्य कौन्तेय भीमसेनं युधां पतिम् ।आशीविषमिव क्रुद्धं तस्माद्द्रवति वाहिनी ॥५६॥
transliteration: nivṛttaṁ paśya kaunteya bhīmasenaṁ yudhāṁ patim ,āśīviṣamiva kruddhaṁ tasmāddravati vāhinī.
english: O Kaunteya, behold Bhīmasena, the lord of battles, having turned back and enraged like a venomous snake. Because of him, the army is fleeing.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 43 Verse 56. O Kaunteya, behold Bhīmasena, the lord of battles, having turned back and enraged like a venomous snake. Because of him, the army is fleeing.

sanskrit: पीतरक्तासितसितास्ताराचन्द्रार्कमण्डिताः ।पताका विप्रकीर्यन्ते छत्राण्येतानि चार्जुन ॥५७॥
transliteration: pītaraktāsitasitāstārācandrārkamaṇḍitāḥ ,patākā viprakīryante chatrāṇyetāni cārjuna.
english: O Arjuna, banners of yellow, red, black, and white, adorned with stars, the moon, and the sun, are being scattered, and these umbrellas too.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 43 Verse 57. O Arjuna, banners of yellow, red, black, and white, adorned with stars, the moon, and the sun, are being scattered, and these umbrellas too.

sanskrit: सौवर्णा राजताश्चैव तैजसाश्च पृथग्विधाः ।केतवो विनिपात्यन्ते हस्त्यश्वं विप्रकीर्यते ॥५८॥
transliteration: sauvarṇā rājatāścaiva taijasāśca pṛthagvidhāḥ ,ketavo vinipātyante hastyaśvaṁ viprakīryate.
english: Golden, silver, and other metallic standards of various kinds are being thrown down, and the elephants and horses are being scattered.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 43 Verse 58. Golden, silver, and other metallic standards of various kinds are being thrown down, and the elephants and horses are being scattered.

sanskrit: रथेभ्यः प्रपतन्त्येते रथिनो विगतासवः ।नानावर्णैर्हता बाणैः पाञ्चालैरपलायिभिः ॥५९॥
transliteration: rathebhyaḥ prapatantyete rathino vigatāsavaḥ ,nānāvarṇairhatā bāṇaiḥ pāñcālairapalāyibhiḥ.
english: These lifeless charioteers fall from their chariots, struck by multi-colored arrows from the unwavering Pāñcālas.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 43 Verse 59. These lifeless charioteers fall from their chariots, struck by multi-colored arrows from the unwavering Pāñcālas.

sanskrit: निर्मनुष्यान्गजानश्वान्रथांश्चैव धनंजय ।समाद्रवन्ति पाञ्चाला धार्तराष्ट्रांस्तरस्विनः ॥६०॥
transliteration: nirmanuṣyāngajānaśvānrathāṁścaiva dhanaṁjaya ,samādravanti pāñcālā dhārtarāṣṭrāṁstarasvinaḥ.
english: O Dhanaṃjaya, the mighty Panchalas are charging towards the Dhārtarāṣṭras, attacking their unmanned elephants, horses, and chariots.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 43 Verse 60. O Dhanaṃjaya, the mighty Panchalas are charging towards the Dhārtarāṣṭras, attacking their unmanned elephants, horses, and chariots.

sanskrit: मृद्नन्ति च नरव्याघ्रा भीमसेनव्यपाश्रयात् ।बलं परेषां दुर्धर्षं त्यक्त्वा प्राणानरिंदम ॥६१॥
transliteration: mṛdnanti ca naravyāghrā bhīmasenavyapāśrayāt ,balaṁ pareṣāṁ durdharṣaṁ tyaktvā prāṇānariṁdama.
english: O Subduer of Enemies (ariṃdama), these excellent warriors (naravyāghrāḥ), relying on Bhīmasena's support, are crushing the formidable army of their enemies, having given up their own lives.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 43 Verse 61. O Subduer of Enemies (ariṃdama), these excellent warriors (naravyāghrāḥ), relying on Bhīmasena's support, are crushing the formidable army of their enemies, having given up their own lives.

sanskrit: एते नदन्ति पाञ्चाला धमन्त्यपि च वारिजान् ।अभिद्रवन्ति च रणे निघ्नन्तः सायकैः परान् ॥६२॥
transliteration: ete nadanti pāñcālā dhamantyapi ca vārijān ,abhidravanti ca raṇe nighnantaḥ sāyakaiḥ parān.
english: These Panchalas are roaring and also blowing their conches. And in battle, striking down their enemies with arrows, they are charging forward.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 43 Verse 62. These Panchalas are roaring and also blowing their conches. And in battle, striking down their enemies with arrows, they are charging forward.

sanskrit: पश्य स्वर्गस्य माहात्म्यं पाञ्चाला हि परंतप ।धार्तराष्ट्रान्विनिघ्नन्ति क्रुद्धाः सिंहा इव द्विपान् ॥६३॥
transliteration: paśya svargasya māhātmyaṁ pāñcālā hi paraṁtapa ,dhārtarāṣṭrānvinighnanti kruddhāḥ siṁhā iva dvipān.
english: O Scorcher of Foes (paraṃtapa), behold the glory of heaven! Indeed, the Panchalas are slaying the Dhārtarāṣṭras, just like enraged lions slay elephants.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 43 Verse 63. O Scorcher of Foes (paraṃtapa), behold the glory of heaven! Indeed, the Panchalas are slaying the Dhārtarāṣṭras, just like enraged lions slay elephants.

sanskrit: सर्वतश्चाभिपन्नैषा धार्तराष्ट्री महाचमूः ।पाञ्चालैर्मानसादेत्य हंसैर्गङ्गेव वेगितैः ॥६४॥
transliteration: sarvataścābhipannaiṣā dhārtarāṣṭrī mahācamūḥ ,pāñcālairmānasādetya haṁsairgaṅgeva vegitaiḥ.
english: This great army of the Dhṛtarāṣṭras is being attacked from all sides by the Pañcālas, much like the Gaṅgā river is descended upon by swift swans arriving from Lake Manasa.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 43 Verse 64. This great army of the Dhṛtarāṣṭras is being attacked from all sides by the Pañcālas, much like the Gaṅgā river is descended upon by swift swans arriving from Lake Manasa.

sanskrit: सुभृशं च पराक्रान्ताः पाञ्चालानां निवारणे ।कृपकर्णादयो वीरा ऋषभाणामिवर्षभाः ॥६५॥
transliteration: subhṛśaṁ ca parākrāntāḥ pāñcālānāṁ nivāraṇe ,kṛpakarṇādayo vīrā ṛṣabhāṇāmivarṣabhāḥ.
english: And the heroes like Kṛpa and Karṇa are exceedingly valiant in resisting the Pañcālas, appearing like bulls among other bulls (i.e., supreme among heroes).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 43 Verse 65. And the heroes like Kṛpa and Karṇa are exceedingly valiant in resisting the Pañcālas, appearing like bulls among other bulls (i.e., supreme among heroes).

sanskrit: सुनिमग्नांश्च भीमास्त्रैर्धार्तराष्ट्रान्महारथान् ।धृष्टद्युम्नमुखा वीरा घ्नन्ति शत्रून्सहस्रशः ।विषण्णभूयिष्ठरथा धार्तराष्ट्री महाचमूः ॥६६॥
transliteration: sunimagnāṁśca bhīmāstrairdhārtarāṣṭrānmahārathān ,dhṛṣṭadyumnamukhā vīrā ghnanti śatrūnsahasraśaḥ ,viṣaṇṇabhūyiṣṭharathā dhārtarāṣṭrī mahācamūḥ.
english: And the heroes led by Dhṛṣṭadyumna are slaying thousands of the enemy great charioteers of the Dhṛtarāṣṭras, who are deeply overwhelmed by terrible weapons. The great Dhṛtarāṣṭra army has most of its chariots in a state of distress.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 43 Verse 66. And the heroes led by Dhṛṣṭadyumna are slaying thousands of the enemy great charioteers of the Dhṛtarāṣṭras, who are deeply overwhelmed by terrible weapons. The great Dhṛtarāṣṭra army has most of its chariots in a state of distress.

sanskrit: पश्य भीमेन नाराचैश्छिन्ना नागाः पतन्त्यमी ।वज्रिवज्राहतानीव शिखराणि महीभृताम् ॥६७॥
transliteration: paśya bhīmena nārācaiśchinnā nāgāḥ patantyamī ,vajrivajrāhatānīva śikharāṇi mahībhṛtām.
english: Behold! These elephants, struck and severed by Bhīma's iron arrows, are falling, just like mountain peaks struck by Indra's thunderbolt.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 43 Verse 67. Behold! These elephants, struck and severed by Bhīma's iron arrows, are falling, just like mountain peaks struck by Indra's thunderbolt.

sanskrit: भीमसेनस्य निर्विद्धा बाणैः संनतपर्वभिः ।स्वान्यनीकानि मृद्नन्तो द्रवन्त्येते महागजाः ॥६८॥
transliteration: bhīmasenasya nirviddhā bāṇaiḥ saṁnataparvabhiḥ ,svānyanīkāni mṛdnanto dravantyete mahāgajāḥ.
english: These great elephants, pierced by Bhimasena's well-aimed arrows, are crushing their own troops as they flee.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 43 Verse 68. These great elephants, pierced by Bhimasena's well-aimed arrows, are crushing their own troops as they flee.

sanskrit: नाभिजानासि भीमस्य सिंहनादं दुरुत्सहम् ।नदतोऽर्जुन संग्रामे वीरस्य जितकाशिनः ॥६९॥
transliteration: nābhijānāsi bhīmasya siṁhanādaṁ durutsaham ,nadato'rjuna saṁgrāme vīrasya jitakāśinaḥ.
english: O Arjuna, do you not recognize the unbearable lion's roar of the heroic Bhima, who shines forth victoriously, as he roars in battle?
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 43 Verse 69. O Arjuna, do you not recognize the unbearable lion's roar of the heroic Bhima, who shines forth victoriously, as he roars in battle?

sanskrit: एष नैषादिरभ्येति द्विपमुख्येन पाण्डवम् ।जिघांसुस्तोमरैः क्रुद्धो दण्डपाणिरिवान्तकः ॥७०॥
transliteration: eṣa naiṣādirabhyeti dvipamukhyena pāṇḍavam ,jighāṁsustomaraiḥ kruddho daṇḍapāṇirivāntakaḥ.
english: This Nishada warrior, enraged and eager to kill with his javelins, approaches the Pandava (Arjuna) on his chief elephant, like Death holding his staff.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 43 Verse 70. This Nishada warrior, enraged and eager to kill with his javelins, approaches the Pandava (Arjuna) on his chief elephant, like Death holding his staff.

sanskrit: सतोमरावस्य भुजौ छिन्नौ भीमेन गर्जतः ।तीक्ष्णैरग्निशिखाप्रख्यैर्नाराचैर्दशभिर्हतः ॥७१॥
transliteration: satomarāvasya bhujau chinnau bhīmena garjataḥ ,tīkṣṇairagniśikhāprakhyairnārācairdaśabhirhataḥ.
english: His two arms, along with their javelins, were severed by Bhima while he (the Nishada) was roaring. He was then struck down by ten sharp iron arrows, resembling flames of fire.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 43 Verse 71. His two arms, along with their javelins, were severed by Bhima while he (the Nishada) was roaring. He was then struck down by ten sharp iron arrows, resembling flames of fire.

sanskrit: हत्वैनं पुनरायाति नागानन्यान्प्रहारिणः ।पश्य नीलाम्बुदनिभान्महामात्रैरधिष्ठितान् ।शक्तितोमरसंकाशैर्विनिघ्नन्तं वृकोदरम् ॥७२॥
transliteration: hatvainaṁ punarāyāti nāgānanyānprahāriṇaḥ ,paśya nīlāmbudanibhānmahāmātrairadhiṣṭhitān ,śaktitomarasaṁkāśairvinighnantaṁ vṛkodaram.
english: Having slain one (elephant), it returns again. Behold Vṛkodara (Bhīma), striking down other attacking elephants, which resemble dark blue clouds and are commanded by great mahouts, with (blows like) spears and javelins.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 43 Verse 72. Having slain one (elephant), it returns again. Behold Vṛkodara (Bhīma), striking down other attacking elephants, which resemble dark blue clouds and are commanded by great mahouts, with (blows like) spears and javelins.

sanskrit: सप्त सप्त च नागांस्तान्वैजयन्तीश्च सध्वजाः ।निहत्य निशितैर्बाणैश्छिन्नाः पार्थाग्रजेन ते ।दशभिर्दशभिश्चैको नाराचैर्निहतो गजः ॥७३॥
transliteration: sapta sapta ca nāgāṁstānvaijayantīśca sadhvajāḥ ,nihatya niśitairbāṇaiśchinnāḥ pārthāgrajena te ,daśabhirdaśabhiścaiko nārācairnihato gajaḥ.
english: Having destroyed with sharp arrows those fourteen elephants along with their banner-flags, they were torn apart by Pārtha's elder brother (Bhīma). Furthermore, one elephant was slain with twenty-one iron arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 43 Verse 73. Having destroyed with sharp arrows those fourteen elephants along with their banner-flags, they were torn apart by Pārtha's elder brother (Bhīma). Furthermore, one elephant was slain with twenty-one iron arrows.

sanskrit: न चासौ धार्तराष्ट्राणां श्रूयते निनदस्तथा ।पुरंदरसमे क्रुद्धे निवृत्ते भरतर्षभे ॥७४॥
transliteration: na cāsau dhārtarāṣṭrāṇāṁ śrūyate ninadastathā ,puraṁdarasame kruddhe nivṛtte bharatarṣabhe.
english: And that roar of the sons of Dhṛtarāṣṭra is no longer heard in the same way, (for it is) when the bull among Bhāratas (Bhīma), angered and equal to Indra, has turned back (the enemy).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 43 Verse 74. And that roar of the sons of Dhṛtarāṣṭra is no longer heard in the same way, (for it is) when the bull among Bhāratas (Bhīma), angered and equal to Indra, has turned back (the enemy).

sanskrit: अक्षौहिण्यस्तथा तिस्रो धार्तराष्ट्रस्य संहताः ।क्रुद्धेन नरसिंहेन भीमसेनेन वारिताः ॥७५॥
transliteration: akṣauhiṇyastathā tisro dhārtarāṣṭrasya saṁhatāḥ ,kruddhena narasiṁhena bhīmasenena vāritāḥ.
english: And thus, three assembled divisions (akṣauhiṇīs) of Dhṛtarāṣṭra's army were repelled by the enraged Bhīmasena, the lion among men.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 43 Verse 75. And thus, three assembled divisions (akṣauhiṇīs) of Dhṛtarāṣṭra's army were repelled by the enraged Bhīmasena, the lion among men.

sanskrit: संजय उवाच ।भीमसेनेन तत्कर्म कृतं दृष्ट्वा सुदुष्करम् ।अर्जुनो व्यधमच्छिष्टानहितान्निशितैः शरैः ॥७६॥
transliteration: saṁjaya uvāca ,bhīmasenena tatkarma kṛtaṁ dṛṣṭvā suduṣkaram ,arjuno vyadhamacchiṣṭānahitānniśitaiḥ śaraiḥ.
english: Sañjaya said: Having witnessed that extremely difficult deed accomplished by Bhīmasena, Arjuna dispersed the remaining enemies with sharp arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 43 Verse 76. Sañjaya said: Having witnessed that extremely difficult deed accomplished by Bhīmasena, Arjuna dispersed the remaining enemies with sharp arrows.

sanskrit: ते वध्यमानाः समरे संशप्तकगणाः प्रभो ।शक्रस्यातिथितां गत्वा विशोका ह्यभवन्मुदा ॥७७॥
transliteration: te vadhyamānāḥ samare saṁśaptakagaṇāḥ prabho ,śakrasyātithitāṁ gatvā viśokā hyabhavanmudā.
english: O lord, those groups of Saṃśaptakas, being slain in battle, indeed became joyful (viśokāḥ), having attained the hospitality of Indra.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 43 Verse 77. O lord, those groups of Saṃśaptakas, being slain in battle, indeed became joyful (viśokāḥ), having attained the hospitality of Indra.

sanskrit: पार्थश्च पुरुषव्याघ्रः शरैः संनतपर्वभिः ।जघान धार्तराष्ट्रस्य चतुर्विधबलां चमूम् ॥७८॥
transliteration: pārthaśca puruṣavyāghraḥ śaraiḥ saṁnataparvabhiḥ ,jaghāna dhārtarāṣṭrasya caturvidhabalāṁ camūm.
english: And Pārtha, the tiger among men (puruṣavyāghra), struck down the army of Dhṛtarāṣṭra's sons, which comprised four kinds of forces, with arrows that had well-formed joints.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 43 Verse 78. And Pārtha, the tiger among men (puruṣavyāghra), struck down the army of Dhṛtarāṣṭra's sons, which comprised four kinds of forces, with arrows that had well-formed joints.

sanskrit: धृतराष्ट्र उवाच ।निवृत्ते भीमसेने च पाण्डवे च युधिष्ठिरे ।वध्यमाने बले चापि मामके पाण्डुसृञ्जयैः ॥१॥
transliteration: dhṛtarāṣṭra uvāca ,nivṛtte bhīmasene ca pāṇḍave ca yudhiṣṭhire ,vadhyamāne bale cāpi māmake pāṇḍusṛñjayaiḥ.
english: Dhṛtarāṣṭra said: When Bhīmasena and the Pāṇḍava Yudhiṣṭhira had retreated, and even my own army was being slain by the Pāṇḍavas and Sañjayas...
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 44 Verse 1. Dhṛtarāṣṭra said: When Bhīmasena and the Pāṇḍava Yudhiṣṭhira had retreated, and even my own army was being slain by the Pāṇḍavas and Sañjayas...

sanskrit: द्रवमाणे बलौघे च निराक्रन्दे मुहुर्मुहुः ।किमकुर्वन्त कुरवस्तन्ममाचक्ष्व संजय ॥२॥
transliteration: dravamāṇe balaughe ca nirākrande muhurmuhuḥ ,kimakurvanta kuravastanmamācakṣva saṁjaya.
english: As the vast army was repeatedly fleeing, utterly without an outcry (of resistance or hope), what did the Kurus do then, O Sañjaya? Please tell me.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 44 Verse 2. As the vast army was repeatedly fleeing, utterly without an outcry (of resistance or hope), what did the Kurus do then, O Sañjaya? Please tell me.

sanskrit: संजय उवाच ।दृष्ट्वा भीमं महाबाहुं सूतपुत्रः प्रतापवान् ।क्रोधरक्तेक्षणो राजन्भीमसेनमुपाद्रवत् ॥३॥
transliteration: saṁjaya uvāca ,dṛṣṭvā bhīmaṁ mahābāhuṁ sūtaputraḥ pratāpavān ,krodharaktekṣaṇo rājanbhīmasenamupādravat.
english: Sañjaya said: "O King, upon seeing the mighty-armed Bhīma, the powerful son of Sūta (Karṇa), his eyes red with anger, charged towards Bhīmasena."
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 44 Verse 3. Sañjaya said: "O King, upon seeing the mighty-armed Bhīma, the powerful son of Sūta (Karṇa), his eyes red with anger, charged towards Bhīmasena."

sanskrit: तावकं च बलं दृष्ट्वा भीमसेनात्पराङ्मुखम् ।यत्नेन महता राजन्पर्यवस्थापयद्बली ॥४॥
transliteration: tāvakaṁ ca balaṁ dṛṣṭvā bhīmasenātparāṅmukham ,yatnena mahatā rājanparyavasthāpayadbalī.
english: O King, seeing your army turning away from Bhīmasena and fleeing, that powerful one (Karṇa) with great effort rallied and re-established it.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 44 Verse 4. O King, seeing your army turning away from Bhīmasena and fleeing, that powerful one (Karṇa) with great effort rallied and re-established it.

sanskrit: व्यवस्थाप्य महाबाहुस्तव पुत्रस्य वाहिनीम् ।प्रत्युद्ययौ तदा कर्णः पाण्डवान्युद्धदुर्मदान् ॥५॥
transliteration: vyavasthāpya mahābāhustava putrasya vāhinīm ,pratyudyayau tadā karṇaḥ pāṇḍavānyuddhadurmadān.
english: After re-establishing your son's army, the mighty-armed Karṇa then confronted the Pāṇḍavas, who were maddened by battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 44 Verse 5. After re-establishing your son's army, the mighty-armed Karṇa then confronted the Pāṇḍavas, who were maddened by battle.

sanskrit: प्रत्युद्ययुस्तु राधेयं पाण्डवानां महारथाः ।धुन्वानाः कार्मुकाण्याजौ विक्षिपन्तश्च सायकान् ॥६॥
transliteration: pratyudyayustu rādheyaṁ pāṇḍavānāṁ mahārathāḥ ,dhunvānāḥ kārmukāṇyājau vikṣipantaśca sāyakān.
english: And the great charioteers of the Pandavas advanced to meet Karna (Rādheya), brandishing their bows and discharging arrows in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 44 Verse 6. And the great charioteers of the Pandavas advanced to meet Karna (Rādheya), brandishing their bows and discharging arrows in battle.

sanskrit: भीमसेनः शिनेर्नप्ता शिखण्डी जनमेजयः ।धृष्टद्युम्नश्च बलवान्सर्वे चापि प्रभद्रकाः ॥७॥
transliteration: bhīmasenaḥ śinernaptā śikhaṇḍī janamejayaḥ ,dhṛṣṭadyumnaśca balavānsarve cāpi prabhadrakāḥ.
english: Bhimasena, the grandson of Shini (Sātyaki), Shikhandi, Janamejaya, and the mighty Dhrishtadyumna, and also all the Prabhadrakas (warriors).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 44 Verse 7. Bhimasena, the grandson of Shini (Sātyaki), Shikhandi, Janamejaya, and the mighty Dhrishtadyumna, and also all the Prabhadrakas (warriors).

sanskrit: पाञ्चालाश्च नरव्याघ्राः समन्तात्तव वाहिनीम् ।अभ्यद्रवन्त संक्रुद्धाः समरे जितकाशिनः ॥८॥
transliteration: pāñcālāśca naravyāghrāḥ samantāttava vāhinīm ,abhyadravanta saṁkruddhāḥ samare jitakāśinaḥ.
english: And the tiger-like men (naravyāghrāḥ) among the Panchalas, highly enraged and victorious in battle, rushed towards your army from all sides.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 44 Verse 8. And the tiger-like men (naravyāghrāḥ) among the Panchalas, highly enraged and victorious in battle, rushed towards your army from all sides.

sanskrit: तथैव तावका राजन्पाण्डवानामनीकिनीम् ।अभ्यद्रवन्त त्वरिता जिघांसन्तो महारथाः ॥९॥
transliteration: tathaiva tāvakā rājanpāṇḍavānāmanīkinīm ,abhyadravanta tvaritā jighāṁsanto mahārathāḥ.
english: O King (rājan), similarly, your own swift great charioteers, desiring to kill, rushed towards the army of the Pandavas.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 44 Verse 9. O King (rājan), similarly, your own swift great charioteers, desiring to kill, rushed towards the army of the Pandavas.

sanskrit: रथनागाश्वकलिलं पत्तिध्वजसमाकुलम् ।बभूव पुरुषव्याघ्र सैन्यमद्भुतदर्शनम् ॥१०॥
transliteration: rathanāgāśvakalilaṁ pattidhvajasamākulam ,babhūva puruṣavyāghra sainyamadbhutadarśanam.
english: O tiger among men, the army became an awe-inspiring sight, filled with chariots, elephants, and horses, and crowded with infantry and banners.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 44 Verse 10. O tiger among men, the army became an awe-inspiring sight, filled with chariots, elephants, and horses, and crowded with infantry and banners.

sanskrit: शिखण्डी च ययौ कर्णं धृष्टद्युम्नः सुतं तव ।दुःशासनं महाराज महत्या सेनया वृतम् ॥११॥
transliteration: śikhaṇḍī ca yayau karṇaṁ dhṛṣṭadyumnaḥ sutaṁ tava ,duḥśāsanaṁ mahārāja mahatyā senayā vṛtam.
english: O great king, Śikhaṇḍī went towards Karṇa, and Dhṛṣṭadyumna went towards your son Duḥśāsana, who was surrounded by a mighty army.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 44 Verse 11. O great king, Śikhaṇḍī went towards Karṇa, and Dhṛṣṭadyumna went towards your son Duḥśāsana, who was surrounded by a mighty army.

sanskrit: नकुलो वृषसेनं च चित्रसेनं युधिष्ठिरः ।उलूकं समरे राजन्सहदेवः समभ्ययात् ॥१२॥
transliteration: nakulo vṛṣasenaṁ ca citrasenaṁ yudhiṣṭhiraḥ ,ulūkaṁ samare rājansahadevaḥ samabhyayāt.
english: O king, Nakula advanced against Vṛṣasena, Yudhiṣṭhira advanced against Citrasena, and Sahadeva advanced against Ulūka in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 44 Verse 12. O king, Nakula advanced against Vṛṣasena, Yudhiṣṭhira advanced against Citrasena, and Sahadeva advanced against Ulūka in battle.

sanskrit: सात्यकिः शकुनिं चापि भीमसेनश्च कौरवान् ।अर्जुनं च रणे यत्तं द्रोणपुत्रो महारथः ॥१३॥
transliteration: sātyakiḥ śakuniṁ cāpi bhīmasenaśca kauravān ,arjunaṁ ca raṇe yattaṁ droṇaputro mahārathaḥ.
english: Sātyaki advanced against Śakuni, Bhīmasena against the Kauravas, and Droṇa's son (Aśvatthāman), the great charioteer, advanced against Arjuna, who was ever-prepared in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 44 Verse 13. Sātyaki advanced against Śakuni, Bhīmasena against the Kauravas, and Droṇa's son (Aśvatthāman), the great charioteer, advanced against Arjuna, who was ever-prepared in battle.

sanskrit: युधामन्युं महेष्वासं गौतमोऽभ्यपतद्रणे ।कृतवर्मा च बलवानुत्तमौजसमाद्रवत् ॥१४॥
transliteration: yudhāmanyuṁ maheṣvāsaṁ gautamo'bhyapatadraṇe ,kṛtavarmā ca balavānuttamaujasamādravat.
english: Gautama attacked the great archer Yudhāmanyu in battle. And the mighty Kṛtavarmā charged towards Uttamaujas.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 44 Verse 14. Gautama attacked the great archer Yudhāmanyu in battle. And the mighty Kṛtavarmā charged towards Uttamaujas.

sanskrit: भीमसेनः कुरून्सर्वान्पुत्रांश्च तव मारिष ।सहानीकान्महाबाहुरेक एवाभ्यवारयत् ॥१५॥
transliteration: bhīmasenaḥ kurūnsarvānputrāṁśca tava māriṣa ,sahānīkānmahābāhureka evābhyavārayat.
english: O respected one, Bhīmasena, the mighty-armed one, alone held back all your sons and the Kuru warriors along with their armies.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 44 Verse 15. O respected one, Bhīmasena, the mighty-armed one, alone held back all your sons and the Kuru warriors along with their armies.

sanskrit: शिखण्डी च ततः कर्णं विचरन्तमभीतवत् ।भीष्महन्ता महाराज वारयामास पत्रिभिः ॥१६॥
transliteration: śikhaṇḍī ca tataḥ karṇaṁ vicarantamabhītavat ,bhīṣmahantā mahārāja vārayāmāsa patribhiḥ.
english: O great king, then Śikhaṇḍī, the killer of Bhīṣma, fearlessly checked Karṇa, who was moving about, with his arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 44 Verse 16. O great king, then Śikhaṇḍī, the killer of Bhīṣma, fearlessly checked Karṇa, who was moving about, with his arrows.

sanskrit: प्रतिरब्धस्ततः कर्णो रोषात्प्रस्फुरिताधरः ।शिखण्डिनं त्रिभिर्बाणैर्भ्रुवोर्मध्ये व्यताडयत् ॥१७॥
transliteration: pratirabdhastataḥ karṇo roṣātprasphuritādharaḥ ,śikhaṇḍinaṁ tribhirbāṇairbhruvormadhye vyatāḍayat.
english: Then Karṇa, enraged and with trembling lips, struck Śikhaṇḍī between the eyebrows with three arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 44 Verse 17. Then Karṇa, enraged and with trembling lips, struck Śikhaṇḍī between the eyebrows with three arrows.

sanskrit: धारयंस्तु स तान्बाणाञ्शिखण्डी बह्वशोभत ।राजतः पर्वतो यद्वत्त्रिभिः शृङ्गैः समन्वितः ॥१८॥
transliteration: dhārayaṁstu sa tānbāṇāñśikhaṇḍī bahvaśobhata ,rājataḥ parvato yadvattribhiḥ śṛṅgaiḥ samanvitaḥ.
english: Shikhaṇḍī, holding those arrows, appeared very splendid, just like a silver mountain endowed with three peaks.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 44 Verse 18. Shikhaṇḍī, holding those arrows, appeared very splendid, just like a silver mountain endowed with three peaks.

sanskrit: सोऽतिविद्धो महेष्वासः सूतपुत्रेण संयुगे ।कर्णं विव्याध समरे नवत्या निशितैः शरैः ॥१९॥
transliteration: so'tividdho maheṣvāsaḥ sūtaputreṇa saṁyuge ,karṇaṁ vivyādha samare navatyā niśitaiḥ śaraiḥ.
english: Though deeply wounded by the son of Sūta (Karna) in battle, that great archer (Shikhaṇḍī) in turn struck Karna with ninety sharp arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 44 Verse 19. Though deeply wounded by the son of Sūta (Karna) in battle, that great archer (Shikhaṇḍī) in turn struck Karna with ninety sharp arrows.

sanskrit: तस्य कर्णो हयान्हत्वा सारथिं च त्रिभिः शरैः ।उन्ममाथ ध्वजं चास्य क्षुरप्रेण महारथः ॥२०॥
transliteration: tasya karṇo hayānhatvā sārathiṁ ca tribhiḥ śaraiḥ ,unmamātha dhvajaṁ cāsya kṣurapreṇa mahārathaḥ.
english: Then Karna, the great charioteer, having killed Shikhaṇḍī's horses and charioteer with three arrows, also cut down his banner with a razor-edged arrow.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 44 Verse 20. Then Karna, the great charioteer, having killed Shikhaṇḍī's horses and charioteer with three arrows, also cut down his banner with a razor-edged arrow.

sanskrit: हताश्वात्तु ततो यानादवप्लुत्य महारथः ।शक्तिं चिक्षेप कर्णाय संक्रुद्धः शत्रुतापनः ॥२१॥
transliteration: hatāśvāttu tato yānādavaplutya mahārathaḥ ,śaktiṁ cikṣepa karṇāya saṁkruddhaḥ śatrutāpanaḥ.
english: But then, that great charioteer (Shikhaṇḍī), having jumped down from his chariot whose horses had been killed, greatly enraged and a tormentor of enemies, threw a spear (śakti) at Karna.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 44 Verse 21. But then, that great charioteer (Shikhaṇḍī), having jumped down from his chariot whose horses had been killed, greatly enraged and a tormentor of enemies, threw a spear (śakti) at Karna.

sanskrit: तां छित्त्वा समरे कर्णस्त्रिभिर्भारत सायकैः ।शिखण्डिनमथाविध्यन्नवभिर्निशितैः शरैः ॥२२॥
transliteration: tāṁ chittvā samare karṇastribhirbhārata sāyakaiḥ ,śikhaṇḍinamathāvidhyannavabhirniśitaiḥ śaraiḥ.
english: O Bharata, Karna, having cut her (Shikhandin's bowstring) in battle with three arrows, then pierced Shikhandin with nine sharp arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 44 Verse 22. O Bharata, Karna, having cut her (Shikhandin's bowstring) in battle with three arrows, then pierced Shikhandin with nine sharp arrows.

sanskrit: कर्णचापच्युतान्बाणान्वर्जयंस्तु नरोत्तमः ।अपयातस्ततस्तूर्णं शिखण्डी जयतां वरः ॥२३॥
transliteration: karṇacāpacyutānbāṇānvarjayaṁstu narottamaḥ ,apayātastatastūrṇaṁ śikhaṇḍī jayatāṁ varaḥ.
english: But Shikhandin, that best among men and foremost among victors, evading the arrows released from Karna's bow, swiftly withdrew from there.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 44 Verse 23. But Shikhandin, that best among men and foremost among victors, evading the arrows released from Karna's bow, swiftly withdrew from there.

sanskrit: ततः कर्णो महाराज पाण्डुसैन्यान्यशातयत् ।तूलराशिं समासाद्य यथा वायुर्महाजवः ॥२४॥
transliteration: tataḥ karṇo mahārāja pāṇḍusainyānyaśātayat ,tūlarāśiṁ samāsādya yathā vāyurmahājavaḥ.
english: Then, O great king, Karna destroyed the armies of the Pandavas, just as a powerful wind, having come upon a heap of cotton, scatters it.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 44 Verse 24. Then, O great king, Karna destroyed the armies of the Pandavas, just as a powerful wind, having come upon a heap of cotton, scatters it.

sanskrit: धृष्टद्युम्नो महाराज तव पुत्रेण पीडितः ।दुःशासनं त्रिभिर्बाणैरभ्यविध्यत्स्तनान्तरे ॥२५॥
transliteration: dhṛṣṭadyumno mahārāja tava putreṇa pīḍitaḥ ,duḥśāsanaṁ tribhirbāṇairabhyavidhyatstanāntare.
english: O great king, Dhristadyumna, though harassed by your son Duhshasana, pierced Duhshasana in the chest with three arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 44 Verse 25. O great king, Dhristadyumna, though harassed by your son Duhshasana, pierced Duhshasana in the chest with three arrows.

sanskrit: तस्य दुःशासनो बाहुं सव्यं विव्याध मारिष ।शितेन रुक्मपुङ्खेन भल्लेन नतपर्वणा ॥२६॥
transliteration: tasya duḥśāsano bāhuṁ savyaṁ vivyādha māriṣa ,śitena rukmapuṅkhena bhallena nataparvaṇā.
english: O respected one, Duḥśāsana pierced his left arm with a sharp dart, which had a gold shaft and a bent joint.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 44 Verse 26. O respected one, Duḥśāsana pierced his left arm with a sharp dart, which had a gold shaft and a bent joint.

sanskrit: धृष्टद्युम्नस्तु निर्विद्धः शरं घोरममर्षणः ।दुःशासनाय संक्रुद्धः प्रेषयामास भारत ॥२७॥
transliteration: dhṛṣṭadyumnastu nirviddhaḥ śaraṁ ghoramamarṣaṇaḥ ,duḥśāsanāya saṁkruddhaḥ preṣayāmāsa bhārata.
english: But Dhṛṣṭadyumna, having been pierced, became utterly furious and, unable to tolerate it, O Bhārata, he discharged a terrible arrow at Duḥśāsana.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 44 Verse 27. But Dhṛṣṭadyumna, having been pierced, became utterly furious and, unable to tolerate it, O Bhārata, he discharged a terrible arrow at Duḥśāsana.

sanskrit: आपतन्तं महावेगं धृष्टद्युम्नसमीरितम् ।शरैश्चिच्छेद पुत्रस्ते त्रिभिरेव विशां पते ॥२८॥
transliteration: āpatantaṁ mahāvegaṁ dhṛṣṭadyumnasamīritam ,śaraiściccheda putraste tribhireva viśāṁ pate.
english: O lord of the people, your son cut down that arrow - which was coming with great velocity, propelled by Dhṛṣṭadyumna - with just three of his own arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 44 Verse 28. O lord of the people, your son cut down that arrow - which was coming with great velocity, propelled by Dhṛṣṭadyumna - with just three of his own arrows.

sanskrit: अथापरैः सप्तदशैर्भल्लैः कनकभूषणैः ।धृष्टद्युम्नं समासाद्य बाह्वोरुरसि चार्दयत् ॥२९॥
transliteration: athāparaiḥ saptadaśairbhallaiḥ kanakabhūṣaṇaiḥ ,dhṛṣṭadyumnaṁ samāsādya bāhvorurasi cārdayat.
english: Then, having closed in on Dhṛṣṭadyumna, he wounded him in both arms and the chest with seventeen other gold-adorned darts.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 44 Verse 29. Then, having closed in on Dhṛṣṭadyumna, he wounded him in both arms and the chest with seventeen other gold-adorned darts.

sanskrit: ततः स पार्षतः क्रुद्धो धनुश्चिच्छेद मारिष ।क्षुरप्रेण सुतीक्ष्णेन तत उच्चुक्रुशुर्जनाः ॥३०॥
transliteration: tataḥ sa pārṣataḥ kruddho dhanuściccheda māriṣa ,kṣurapreṇa sutīkṣṇena tata uccukruśurjanāḥ.
english: O respectable one, then that enraged son of Pṛṣata (Dhṛṣṭadyumna) cut his bow with a very sharp, razor-headed arrow. Then the people cried out loudly.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 44 Verse 30. O respectable one, then that enraged son of Pṛṣata (Dhṛṣṭadyumna) cut his bow with a very sharp, razor-headed arrow. Then the people cried out loudly.

sanskrit: अथान्यद्धनुरादाय पुत्रस्ते भरतर्षभ ।धृष्टद्युम्नं शरव्रातैः समन्तात्पर्यवारयत् ॥३१॥
transliteration: athānyaddhanurādāya putraste bharatarṣabha ,dhṛṣṭadyumnaṁ śaravrātaiḥ samantātparyavārayat.
english: O best among the Bhāratas, then your son (Karṇa), having taken another bow, surrounded Dhṛṣṭadyumna from all sides with showers of arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 44 Verse 31. O best among the Bhāratas, then your son (Karṇa), having taken another bow, surrounded Dhṛṣṭadyumna from all sides with showers of arrows.

sanskrit: तव पुत्रस्य ते दृष्ट्वा विक्रमं तं महात्मनः ।व्यहसन्त रणे योधाः सिद्धाश्चाप्सरसां गणाः ॥३२॥
transliteration: tava putrasya te dṛṣṭvā vikramaṁ taṁ mahātmanaḥ ,vyahasanta raṇe yodhāḥ siddhāścāpsarasāṁ gaṇāḥ.
english: Having seen that valor (vikrama) of your son, the great-souled (mahātman) Karṇa, the warriors in the battle, as well as the Siddhas and the hosts of Apsarases, laughed.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 44 Verse 32. Having seen that valor (vikrama) of your son, the great-souled (mahātman) Karṇa, the warriors in the battle, as well as the Siddhas and the hosts of Apsarases, laughed.

sanskrit: ततः प्रववृते युद्धं तावकानां परैः सह ।घोरं प्राणभृतां काले घोररूपं परंतप ॥३३॥
transliteration: tataḥ pravavṛte yuddhaṁ tāvakānāṁ paraiḥ saha ,ghoraṁ prāṇabhṛtāṁ kāle ghorarūpaṁ paraṁtapa.
english: O tormentor of foes, then a terrible battle (yuddha) of dreadful appearance commenced between your kinsmen and the enemies, at a time (kāla) for all living beings (i.e., a time of great destruction or death).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 44 Verse 33. O tormentor of foes, then a terrible battle (yuddha) of dreadful appearance commenced between your kinsmen and the enemies, at a time (kāla) for all living beings (i.e., a time of great destruction or death).

sanskrit: नकुलं वृषसेनस्तु विद्ध्वा पञ्चभिरायसैः ।पितुः समीपे तिष्ठन्तं त्रिभिरन्यैरविध्यत ॥३४॥
transliteration: nakulaṁ vṛṣasenastu viddhvā pañcabhirāyasaiḥ ,pituḥ samīpe tiṣṭhantaṁ tribhiranyairavidhyata.
english: Vṛṣasena, having first struck Nakula with five iron arrows, then pierced him with three other arrows as he stood near his father.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 44 Verse 34. Vṛṣasena, having first struck Nakula with five iron arrows, then pierced him with three other arrows as he stood near his father.

sanskrit: नकुलस्तु ततः क्रुद्धो वृषसेनं स्मयन्निव ।नाराचेन सुतीक्ष्णेन विव्याध हृदये दृढम् ॥३५॥
transliteration: nakulastu tataḥ kruddho vṛṣasenaṁ smayanniva ,nārācena sutīkṣṇena vivyādha hṛdaye dṛḍham.
english: But Nakula, enraged, then smilingly pierced Vṛṣasena firmly in the heart with a very sharp arrow.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 44 Verse 35. But Nakula, enraged, then smilingly pierced Vṛṣasena firmly in the heart with a very sharp arrow.

sanskrit: सोऽतिविद्धो बलवता शत्रुणा शत्रुकर्शनः ।शत्रुं विव्याध विंशत्या स च तं पञ्चभिः शरैः ॥३६॥
transliteration: so'tividdho balavatā śatruṇā śatrukarśanaḥ ,śatruṁ vivyādha viṁśatyā sa ca taṁ pañcabhiḥ śaraiḥ.
english: Though greatly wounded by his strong enemy (Nakula), that tormentor of foes (Vṛṣasena) pierced Nakula with twenty arrows; and Nakula, in turn, struck him with five arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 44 Verse 36. Though greatly wounded by his strong enemy (Nakula), that tormentor of foes (Vṛṣasena) pierced Nakula with twenty arrows; and Nakula, in turn, struck him with five arrows.

sanskrit: ततः शरसहस्रेण तावुभौ पुरुषर्षभौ ।अन्योन्यमाच्छादयतामथाभज्यत वाहिनी ॥३७॥
transliteration: tataḥ śarasahasreṇa tāvubhau puruṣarṣabhau ,anyonyamācchādayatāmathābhajyata vāhinī.
english: Then, with thousands of arrows, those two eminent men (puruṣa) covered each other, and at that point, the army was routed.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 44 Verse 37. Then, with thousands of arrows, those two eminent men (puruṣa) covered each other, and at that point, the army was routed.

sanskrit: दृष्ट्वा तु प्रद्रुतां सेनां धार्तराष्ट्रस्य सूतजः ।निवारयामास बलादनुपत्य विशां पते ।निवृत्ते तु ततः कर्णे नकुलः कौरवान्ययौ ॥३८॥
transliteration: dṛṣṭvā tu pradrutāṁ senāṁ dhārtarāṣṭrasya sūtajaḥ ,nivārayāmāsa balādanupatya viśāṁ pate ,nivṛtte tu tataḥ karṇe nakulaḥ kauravānyayau.
english: Having seen the army of Dhritarashtra's son (Duryodhana/Kauravas) fleeing, Karna (the charioteer's son) pursued them and forcibly stopped them, O lord of men. But then, when Karna retreated, Nakula attacked the Kauravas.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 44 Verse 38. Having seen the army of Dhritarashtra's son (Duryodhana/Kauravas) fleeing, Karna (the charioteer's son) pursued them and forcibly stopped them, O lord of men. But then, when Karna retreated, Nakula attacked the Kauravas.

sanskrit: कर्णपुत्रस्तु समरे हित्वा नकुलमेव तु ।जुगोप चक्रं त्वरितं राधेयस्यैव मारिष ॥३९॥
transliteration: karṇaputrastu samare hitvā nakulameva tu ,jugopa cakraṁ tvaritaṁ rādheyasyaiva māriṣa.
english: But Karna's son, O venerable one, abandoning Nakula in battle, quickly protected Karna's (Rādheya's) chariot wheel.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 44 Verse 39. But Karna's son, O venerable one, abandoning Nakula in battle, quickly protected Karna's (Rādheya's) chariot wheel.

sanskrit: उलूकस्तु रणे क्रुद्धः सहदेवेन वारितः ।तस्याश्वांश्चतुरो हत्वा सहदेवः प्रतापवान् ।सारथिं प्रेषयामास यमस्य सदनं प्रति ॥४०॥
transliteration: ulūkastu raṇe kruddhaḥ sahadevena vāritaḥ ,tasyāśvāṁścaturo hatvā sahadevaḥ pratāpavān ,sārathiṁ preṣayāmāsa yamasya sadanaṁ prati.
english: But Ulooka, enraged in battle, was warded off by Sahadeva. The valiant Sahadeva, having killed his four horses, sent his (Ulooka's) charioteer towards Yama's abode.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 44 Verse 40. But Ulooka, enraged in battle, was warded off by Sahadeva. The valiant Sahadeva, having killed his four horses, sent his (Ulooka's) charioteer towards Yama's abode.

sanskrit: उलूकस्तु ततो यानादवप्लुत्य विशां पते ।त्रिगर्तानां बलं पूर्णं जगाम पितृनन्दनः ॥४१॥
transliteration: ulūkastu tato yānādavaplutya viśāṁ pate ,trigartānāṁ balaṁ pūrṇaṁ jagāma pitṛnandanaḥ.
english: But Ulooka, O lord of men, having jumped down from his chariot, then went to the entire army of the Trigartas, he who was his father's delight.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 44 Verse 41. But Ulooka, O lord of men, having jumped down from his chariot, then went to the entire army of the Trigartas, he who was his father's delight.

sanskrit: सात्यकिः शकुनिं विद्ध्वा विंशत्या निशितैः शरैः ।ध्वजं चिच्छेद भल्लेन सौबलस्य हसन्निव ॥४२॥
transliteration: sātyakiḥ śakuniṁ viddhvā viṁśatyā niśitaiḥ śaraiḥ ,dhvajaṁ ciccheda bhallena saubalasya hasanniva.
english: Satyaki, having struck Shakuni with twenty sharp arrows, cut down Saubala's (Shakuni's) banner with a broad-headed arrow, as if laughing.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 44 Verse 42. Satyaki, having struck Shakuni with twenty sharp arrows, cut down Saubala's (Shakuni's) banner with a broad-headed arrow, as if laughing.

sanskrit: सौबलस्तस्य समरे क्रुद्धो राजन्प्रतापवान् ।विदार्य कवचं भूयो ध्वजं चिच्छेद काञ्चनम् ॥४३॥
transliteration: saubalastasya samare kruddho rājanpratāpavān ,vidārya kavacaṁ bhūyo dhvajaṁ ciccheda kāñcanam.
english: O King, the valorous Saubala (Shakuni), enraged in that battle, after tearing his (Satyaki's) armor, again cut down the golden banner.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 44 Verse 43. O King, the valorous Saubala (Shakuni), enraged in that battle, after tearing his (Satyaki's) armor, again cut down the golden banner.

sanskrit: अथैनं निशितैर्बाणैः सात्यकिः प्रत्यविध्यत ।सारथिं च महाराज त्रिभिरेव समार्दयत् ।अथास्य वाहांस्त्वरितः शरैर्निन्ये यमक्षयम् ॥४४॥
transliteration: athainaṁ niśitairbāṇaiḥ sātyakiḥ pratyavidhyata ,sārathiṁ ca mahārāja tribhireva samārdayat ,athāsya vāhāṁstvaritaḥ śarairninye yamakṣayam.
english: O great King, then Satyaki struck him (Shakuni) back with sharp arrows and wounded his charioteer with just three (arrows). Then, swiftly, he sent his horses to the abode of Yama (the god of death) with (more) arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 44 Verse 44. O great King, then Satyaki struck him (Shakuni) back with sharp arrows and wounded his charioteer with just three (arrows). Then, swiftly, he sent his horses to the abode of Yama (the god of death) with (more) arrows.

sanskrit: ततोऽवप्लुत्य सहसा शकुनिर्भरतर्षभ ।आरुरोह रथं तूर्णमुलूकस्य महारथः ।अपोवाहाथ शीघ्रं स शैनेयाद्युद्धशालिनः ॥४५॥
transliteration: tato'vaplutya sahasā śakunirbharatarṣabha ,āruroha rathaṁ tūrṇamulūkasya mahārathaḥ ,apovāhātha śīghraṁ sa śaineyādyuddhaśālinaḥ.
english: O best of Bharatas, then Shakuni, the great warrior, having suddenly leaped down, quickly mounted Uluka's chariot. Then, Uluka, swiftly drove him (Shakuni) away from Shaineya (Satyaki), who was glorious in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 44 Verse 45. O best of Bharatas, then Shakuni, the great warrior, having suddenly leaped down, quickly mounted Uluka's chariot. Then, Uluka, swiftly drove him (Shakuni) away from Shaineya (Satyaki), who was glorious in battle.

sanskrit: सात्यकिस्तु रणे राजंस्तावकानामनीकिनीम् ।अभिदुद्राव वेगेन ततोऽनीकमभिद्यत ॥४६॥
transliteration: sātyakistu raṇe rājaṁstāvakānāmanīkinīm ,abhidudrāva vegena tato'nīkamabhidyata.
english: O King, Satyaki swiftly charged towards your army in battle. Consequently, that army was shattered.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 44 Verse 46. O King, Satyaki swiftly charged towards your army in battle. Consequently, that army was shattered.

sanskrit: शैनेयशरनुन्नं तु ततः सैन्यं विशां पते ।भेजे दश दिशस्तूर्णं न्यपतच्च गतासुवत् ॥४७॥
transliteration: śaineyaśaranunnaṁ tu tataḥ sainyaṁ viśāṁ pate ,bheje daśa diśastūrṇaṁ nyapatacca gatāsuvat.
english: O Lord of the people, the army, propelled by Shaineya's (Satyaki's) arrows, then swiftly scattered in all ten directions, falling lifelessly like corpses.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 44 Verse 47. O Lord of the people, the army, propelled by Shaineya's (Satyaki's) arrows, then swiftly scattered in all ten directions, falling lifelessly like corpses.

sanskrit: भीमसेनं तव सुतो वारयामास संयुगे ।तं तु भीमो मुहूर्तेन व्यश्वसूतरथध्वजम् ।चक्रे लोकेश्वरं तत्र तेनातुष्यन्त चारणाः ॥४८॥
transliteration: bhīmasenaṁ tava suto vārayāmāsa saṁyuge ,taṁ tu bhīmo muhūrtena vyaśvasūtarathadhvajam ,cakre lokeśvaraṁ tatra tenātuṣyanta cāraṇāḥ.
english: Your son (Duryodhana) attempted to restrain Bhimasena in battle. But Bhima, in an instant, stripped that great king (Duryodhana) of his horses, charioteer, chariot, and banner. At this sight, the Charanans (celestial bards) were greatly pleased.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 44 Verse 48. Your son (Duryodhana) attempted to restrain Bhimasena in battle. But Bhima, in an instant, stripped that great king (Duryodhana) of his horses, charioteer, chariot, and banner. At this sight, the Charanans (celestial bards) were greatly pleased.

sanskrit: ततोऽपायान्नृपस्तत्र भीमसेनस्य गोचरात् ।कुरुसैन्यं ततः सर्वं भीमसेनमुपाद्रवत् ।तत्र रावो महानासीद्भीममेकं जिघांसताम् ॥४९॥
transliteration: tato'pāyānnṛpastatra bhīmasenasya gocarāt ,kurusainyaṁ tataḥ sarvaṁ bhīmasenamupādravat ,tatra rāvo mahānāsīdbhīmamekaṁ jighāṁsatām.
english: Then the king (Duryodhana) departed from Bhimasena's presence. Thereupon, the entire Kuru army rushed towards Bhimasena. A great roar arose from those who sought to kill Bhima, who stood alone.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 44 Verse 49. Then the king (Duryodhana) departed from Bhimasena's presence. Thereupon, the entire Kuru army rushed towards Bhimasena. A great roar arose from those who sought to kill Bhima, who stood alone.

sanskrit: युधामन्युः कृपं विद्ध्वा धनुरस्याशु चिच्छिदे ।अथान्यद्धनुरादाय कृपः शस्त्रभृतां वरः ॥५०॥
transliteration: yudhāmanyuḥ kṛpaṁ viddhvā dhanurasyāśu cicchide ,athānyaddhanurādāya kṛpaḥ śastrabhṛtāṁ varaḥ.
english: Yudhāmanyu, having wounded Kṛpa, swiftly cut his bow. Then Kṛpa, who was the foremost among weapon-bearers, taking up another bow,
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 44 Verse 50. Yudhāmanyu, having wounded Kṛpa, swiftly cut his bow. Then Kṛpa, who was the foremost among weapon-bearers, taking up another bow,

sanskrit: युधामन्योर्ध्वजं सूतं छत्रं चापातयत्क्षितौ ।ततोऽपायाद्रथेनैव युधामन्युर्महारथः ॥५१॥
transliteration: yudhāmanyordhvajaṁ sūtaṁ chatraṁ cāpātayatkṣitau ,tato'pāyādrathenaiva yudhāmanyurmahārathaḥ.
english: [Kṛpa, the best among weapon-bearers,] caused Yudhāmanyu's banner, charioteer, and canopy to fall to the ground. Thereupon, Yudhāmanyu, who was a great chariot-warrior (mahāratha), indeed withdrew with his chariot.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 44 Verse 51. [Kṛpa, the best among weapon-bearers,] caused Yudhāmanyu's banner, charioteer, and canopy to fall to the ground. Thereupon, Yudhāmanyu, who was a great chariot-warrior (mahāratha), indeed withdrew with his chariot.

sanskrit: उत्तमौजास्तु हार्दिक्यं शरैर्भीमपराक्रमम् ।छादयामास सहसा मेघो वृष्ट्या यथाचलम् ॥५२॥
transliteration: uttamaujāstu hārdikyaṁ śarairbhīmaparākramam ,chādayāmāsa sahasā megho vṛṣṭyā yathācalam.
english: But Uttamaujas, with his arrows, suddenly enveloped Hārdikya, who possessed tremendous valor, just as a cloud (megha) covers a mountain with a downpour.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 44 Verse 52. But Uttamaujas, with his arrows, suddenly enveloped Hārdikya, who possessed tremendous valor, just as a cloud (megha) covers a mountain with a downpour.

sanskrit: तद्युद्धं सुमहच्चासीद्घोररूपं परंतप ।यादृशं न मया युद्धं दृष्टपूर्वं विशां पते ॥५३॥
transliteration: tadyuddhaṁ sumahaccāsīdghorarūpaṁ paraṁtapa ,yādṛśaṁ na mayā yuddhaṁ dṛṣṭapūrvaṁ viśāṁ pate.
english: That battle, O scorcher of foes (parantapa), was indeed very great and frightful in its nature. I had never before seen such a battle, O lord of men (viśāṃ pate).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 44 Verse 53. That battle, O scorcher of foes (parantapa), was indeed very great and frightful in its nature. I had never before seen such a battle, O lord of men (viśāṃ pate).

sanskrit: कृतवर्मा ततो राजन्नुत्तमौजसमाहवे ।हृदि विव्याध स तदा रथोपस्थ उपाविशत् ॥५४॥
transliteration: kṛtavarmā tato rājannuttamaujasamāhave ,hṛdi vivyādha sa tadā rathopastha upāviśat.
english: O King, Kṛtavarmā then pierced Uttamaujas in the heart during the battle. At that moment, Uttamaujas collapsed onto his chariot seat.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 44 Verse 54. O King, Kṛtavarmā then pierced Uttamaujas in the heart during the battle. At that moment, Uttamaujas collapsed onto his chariot seat.

sanskrit: सारथिस्तमपोवाह रथेन रथिनां वरम् ।ततस्तु सत्वरं राजन्पाण्डुसैन्यमुपाद्रवत् ॥५५॥
transliteration: sārathistamapovāha rathena rathināṁ varam ,tatastu satvaraṁ rājanpāṇḍusainyamupādravat.
english: O King, his charioteer drove away that finest of charioteers (Uttamaujas) in the chariot. But then, Kṛtavarmā quickly rushed towards the Pāṇḍava army.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 44 Verse 55. O King, his charioteer drove away that finest of charioteers (Uttamaujas) in the chariot. But then, Kṛtavarmā quickly rushed towards the Pāṇḍava army.

sanskrit: संजय उवाच ।द्रौणिस्तु रथवंशेन महता परिवारितः ।आपतत्सहसा राजन्यत्र राजा व्यवस्थितः ॥१॥
transliteration: saṁjaya uvāca ,drauṇistu rathavaṁśena mahatā parivāritaḥ ,āpatatsahasā rājanyatra rājā vyavasthitaḥ.
english: Sañjaya said: O King, Droṇi's son (Aśvatthāman), surrounded by a vast array of chariots, suddenly charged towards the spot where the King (Yudhiṣṭhira) was stationed.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 45 Verse 1. Sañjaya said: O King, Droṇi's son (Aśvatthāman), surrounded by a vast array of chariots, suddenly charged towards the spot where the King (Yudhiṣṭhira) was stationed.

sanskrit: तमापतन्तं सहसा शूरः शौरिसहायवान् ।दधार सहसा पार्थो वेलेव मकरालयम् ॥२॥
transliteration: tamāpatantaṁ sahasā śūraḥ śaurisahāyavān ,dadhāra sahasā pārtho veleva makarālayam.
english: The heroic Pṛthā's son (Arjuna), aided by Śauri (Kṛṣṇa), swiftly checked that approaching foe (Aśvatthāman) just as a shore holds back the ocean (makarālaya).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 45 Verse 2. The heroic Pṛthā's son (Arjuna), aided by Śauri (Kṛṣṇa), swiftly checked that approaching foe (Aśvatthāman) just as a shore holds back the ocean (makarālaya).

sanskrit: ततः क्रुद्धो महाराज द्रोणपुत्रः प्रतापवान् ।अर्जुनं वासुदेवं च छादयामास पत्रिभिः ॥३॥
transliteration: tataḥ kruddho mahārāja droṇaputraḥ pratāpavān ,arjunaṁ vāsudevaṁ ca chādayāmāsa patribhiḥ.
english: Then, O great king, the enraged and mighty son of Droṇa (Aśvatthāmā) covered Arjuna and Kṛṣṇa with arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 45 Verse 3. Then, O great king, the enraged and mighty son of Droṇa (Aśvatthāmā) covered Arjuna and Kṛṣṇa with arrows.

sanskrit: अवच्छन्नौ ततः कृष्णौ दृष्ट्वा तत्र महारथाः ।विस्मयं परमं गत्वा प्रैक्षन्त कुरवस्तदा ॥४॥
transliteration: avacchannau tataḥ kṛṣṇau dṛṣṭvā tatra mahārathāḥ ,vismayaṁ paramaṁ gatvā praikṣanta kuravastadā.
english: Then, seeing the two Kṛṣṇas (Arjuna and Kṛṣṇa) covered there, the great chariot-warriors, including the Kurus, were filled with supreme astonishment and watched.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 45 Verse 4. Then, seeing the two Kṛṣṇas (Arjuna and Kṛṣṇa) covered there, the great chariot-warriors, including the Kurus, were filled with supreme astonishment and watched.

sanskrit: अर्जुनस्तु ततो दिव्यमस्त्रं चक्रे हसन्निव ।तदस्त्रं ब्राह्मणो युद्धे वारयामास भारत ॥५॥
transliteration: arjunastu tato divyamastraṁ cakre hasanniva ,tadastraṁ brāhmaṇo yuddhe vārayāmāsa bhārata.
english: But then Arjuna, as if laughing, employed a divine weapon. O Bhārata, the Brahmin (Aśvatthāmā) repelled that weapon in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 45 Verse 5. But then Arjuna, as if laughing, employed a divine weapon. O Bhārata, the Brahmin (Aśvatthāmā) repelled that weapon in battle.

sanskrit: यद्यद्धि व्याक्षिपद्युद्धे पाण्डवोऽस्त्रं जिघांसया ।तत्तदस्त्रं महेष्वासो द्रोणपुत्रो व्यशातयत् ॥६॥
transliteration: yadyaddhi vyākṣipadyuddhe pāṇḍavo'straṁ jighāṁsayā ,tattadastraṁ maheṣvāso droṇaputro vyaśātayat.
english: Indeed, whatever weapon the Pāṇḍava (Arjuna) discharged in battle with the intention to kill, that very weapon Droṇa's son (Aśvatthāmā), the great archer, shattered.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 45 Verse 6. Indeed, whatever weapon the Pāṇḍava (Arjuna) discharged in battle with the intention to kill, that very weapon Droṇa's son (Aśvatthāmā), the great archer, shattered.

sanskrit: अस्त्रयुद्धे ततो राजन्वर्तमाने भयावहे ।अपश्याम रणे द्रौणिं व्यात्ताननमिवान्तकम् ॥७॥
transliteration: astrayuddhe tato rājanvartamāne bhayāvahe ,apaśyāma raṇe drauṇiṁ vyāttānanamivāntakam.
english: Then, O King, as the terrifying weapon-battle was unfolding, we saw Droṇi (Aśvatthāmā) on the battlefield, appearing like Death (Antaka) with his mouth wide open.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 45 Verse 7. Then, O King, as the terrifying weapon-battle was unfolding, we saw Droṇi (Aśvatthāmā) on the battlefield, appearing like Death (Antaka) with his mouth wide open.

sanskrit: स दिशो विदिशश्चैव छादयित्वा विजिह्मगैः ।वासुदेवं त्रिभिर्बाणैरविध्यद्दक्षिणे भुजे ॥८॥
transliteration: sa diśo vidiśaścaiva chādayitvā vijihmagaiḥ ,vāsudevaṁ tribhirbāṇairavidhyaddakṣiṇe bhuje.
english: He (Droṇi), having covered both the main directions and the intermediate directions with his crooked-flying arrows, then pierced Vāsudeva (Kṛṣṇa) with three arrows in his right arm.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 45 Verse 8. He (Droṇi), having covered both the main directions and the intermediate directions with his crooked-flying arrows, then pierced Vāsudeva (Kṛṣṇa) with three arrows in his right arm.

sanskrit: ततोऽर्जुनो हयान्हत्वा सर्वांस्तस्य महात्मनः ।चकार समरे भूमिं शोणितौघतरङ्गिणीम् ॥९॥
transliteration: tato'rjuno hayānhatvā sarvāṁstasya mahātmanaḥ ,cakāra samare bhūmiṁ śoṇitaughataraṅgiṇīm.
english: Then Arjuna, after killing all the horses of that great-souled (mahātman) Aśvatthāmā, transformed the battlefield into a river surging with torrents of blood.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 45 Verse 9. Then Arjuna, after killing all the horses of that great-souled (mahātman) Aśvatthāmā, transformed the battlefield into a river surging with torrents of blood.

sanskrit: निहता रथिनः पेतुः पार्थचापच्युतैः शरैः ।हयाश्च पर्यधावन्त मुक्तयोक्त्रास्ततस्ततः ॥१०॥
transliteration: nihatā rathinaḥ petuḥ pārthacāpacyutaiḥ śaraiḥ ,hayāśca paryadhāvanta muktayoktrāstatastataḥ.
english: Charioteers, struck down by arrows released from Arjuna's bow, fell to the ground. And the horses, with their harnesses loosened, ran wildly here and there.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 45 Verse 10. Charioteers, struck down by arrows released from Arjuna's bow, fell to the ground. And the horses, with their harnesses loosened, ran wildly here and there.

sanskrit: तद्दृष्ट्वा कर्म पार्थस्य द्रौणिराहवशोभिनः ।अवाकिरद्रणे कृष्णं समन्तान्निशितैः शरैः ॥११॥
transliteration: taddṛṣṭvā karma pārthasya drauṇirāhavaśobhinaḥ ,avākiradraṇe kṛṣṇaṁ samantānniśitaiḥ śaraiḥ.
english: Having witnessed that action (karma) of Partha, who was radiant in battle, Drauni, from all sides, showered Krishna with sharp arrows in the fight.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 45 Verse 11. Having witnessed that action (karma) of Partha, who was radiant in battle, Drauni, from all sides, showered Krishna with sharp arrows in the fight.

sanskrit: ततोऽर्जुनं महाराज द्रौणिरायम्य पत्रिणा ।वक्षोदेशे समासाद्य ताडयामास संयुगे ॥१२॥
transliteration: tato'rjunaṁ mahārāja drauṇirāyamya patriṇā ,vakṣodeśe samāsādya tāḍayāmāsa saṁyuge.
english: Then, O great king, Drauni, having drawn his bow and placed an arrow (patrin), struck Arjuna in the region of the chest (vakṣodeśa) in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 45 Verse 12. Then, O great king, Drauni, having drawn his bow and placed an arrow (patrin), struck Arjuna in the region of the chest (vakṣodeśa) in battle.

sanskrit: सोऽतिविद्धो रणे तेन द्रोणपुत्रेण भारत ।आदत्त परिघं घोरं द्रौणेश्चैनमवाक्षिपत् ॥१३॥
transliteration: so'tividdho raṇe tena droṇaputreṇa bhārata ,ādatta parighaṁ ghoraṁ drauṇeścainamavākṣipat.
english: O Bharata, he (Arjuna), severely wounded in battle by him, by the son of Drona (Drauni), then took up a formidable iron mace and hurled it at Drauni.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 45 Verse 13. O Bharata, he (Arjuna), severely wounded in battle by him, by the son of Drona (Drauni), then took up a formidable iron mace and hurled it at Drauni.

sanskrit: तमापतन्तं परिघं कार्तस्वरविभूषितम् ।द्रौणिश्चिच्छेद सहसा तत उच्चुक्रुशुर्जनाः ॥१४॥
transliteration: tamāpatantaṁ parighaṁ kārtasvaravibhūṣitam ,drauṇiściccheda sahasā tata uccukruśurjanāḥ.
english: Drauni suddenly cut down that rapidly approaching mace, which was adorned with gold (kārtasvara); then, the people cried out loudly.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 45 Verse 14. Drauni suddenly cut down that rapidly approaching mace, which was adorned with gold (kārtasvara); then, the people cried out loudly.

sanskrit: सोऽनेकधापतद्भूमौ भारद्वाजस्य सायकैः ।विशीर्णः पर्वतो राजन्यथा स्यान्मातरिश्वना ॥१५॥
transliteration: so'nekadhāpatadbhūmau bhāradvājasya sāyakaiḥ ,viśīrṇaḥ parvato rājanyathā syānmātariśvanā.
english: O King, he fell to the ground, shattered in many ways by the arrows of Bhāradvāja (Droṇa), just as a mountain might be shattered by the wind.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 45 Verse 15. O King, he fell to the ground, shattered in many ways by the arrows of Bhāradvāja (Droṇa), just as a mountain might be shattered by the wind.

sanskrit: ततोऽर्जुनो रणे द्रौणिं विव्याध दशभिः शरैः ।सारथिं चास्य भल्लेन रथनीडादपाहरत् ॥१६॥
transliteration: tato'rjuno raṇe drauṇiṁ vivyādha daśabhiḥ śaraiḥ ,sārathiṁ cāsya bhallena rathanīḍādapāharat.
english: Then, in battle, Arjuna pierced Droṇa's son (Drauṇi) with ten arrows. He also removed his charioteer from the chariot's seat with a crescent-shaped arrow.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 45 Verse 16. Then, in battle, Arjuna pierced Droṇa's son (Drauṇi) with ten arrows. He also removed his charioteer from the chariot's seat with a crescent-shaped arrow.

sanskrit: स संगृह्य स्वयं वाहान्कृष्णौ प्राच्छादयच्छरैः ।तत्राद्भुतमपश्याम द्रौणेराशु पराक्रमम् ॥१७॥
transliteration: sa saṁgṛhya svayaṁ vāhānkṛṣṇau prācchādayaccharaiḥ ,tatrādbhutamapaśyāma drauṇerāśu parākramam.
english: He (Drauṇi), himself controlling the horses, then covered the two Kṛṣṇas (Arjuna and Kṛṣṇa) with arrows. At that moment, we witnessed the astonishing swift valor (parākrama) of Droṇa's son (Drauṇi).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 45 Verse 17. He (Drauṇi), himself controlling the horses, then covered the two Kṛṣṇas (Arjuna and Kṛṣṇa) with arrows. At that moment, we witnessed the astonishing swift valor (parākrama) of Droṇa's son (Drauṇi).

sanskrit: अयच्छत्तुरगान्यच्च फल्गुनं चाप्ययोधयत् ।तदस्य समरे राजन्सर्वे योधा अपूजयन् ॥१८॥
transliteration: ayacchatturagānyacca phalgunaṁ cāpyayodhayat ,tadasya samare rājansarve yodhā apūjayan.
english: And furthermore, he controlled the horses and also fought Arjuna. Therefore, O King, all the warriors praised this deed of his in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 45 Verse 18. And furthermore, he controlled the horses and also fought Arjuna. Therefore, O King, all the warriors praised this deed of his in battle.

sanskrit: यदा त्वग्रस्यत रणे द्रोणपुत्रेण फल्गुनः ।ततो रश्मीन्रथाश्वानां क्षुरप्रैश्चिच्छिदे जयः ॥१९॥
transliteration: yadā tvagrasyata raṇe droṇaputreṇa phalgunaḥ ,tato raśmīnrathāśvānāṁ kṣurapraiścicchide jayaḥ.
english: When Arjuna was attacked in battle by Dronaputra (Aśvatthāmā), Jaya then severed the reins of the chariot horses with razor-sharp arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 45 Verse 19. When Arjuna was attacked in battle by Dronaputra (Aśvatthāmā), Jaya then severed the reins of the chariot horses with razor-sharp arrows.

sanskrit: प्राद्रवंस्तुरगास्ते तु शरवेगप्रबाधिताः ।ततोऽभून्निनदो भूयस्तव सैन्यस्य भारत ॥२०॥
transliteration: prādravaṁsturagāste tu śaravegaprabādhitāḥ ,tato'bhūnninado bhūyastava sainyasya bhārata.
english: Those horses, indeed, distressed by the speed of the arrows, fled. Then, O Bhārata, a great clamor arose again from your army.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 45 Verse 20. Those horses, indeed, distressed by the speed of the arrows, fled. Then, O Bhārata, a great clamor arose again from your army.

sanskrit: पाण्डवास्तु जयं लब्ध्वा तव सैन्यमुपाद्रवन् ।समन्तान्निशितान्बाणान्विमुञ्चन्तो जयैषिणः ॥२१॥
transliteration: pāṇḍavāstu jayaṁ labdhvā tava sainyamupādravan ,samantānniśitānbāṇānvimuñcanto jayaiṣiṇaḥ.
english: But the Pāṇḍavas, having achieved success, attacked your army, shooting sharp arrows from all sides, as they were intent on victory.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 45 Verse 21. But the Pāṇḍavas, having achieved success, attacked your army, shooting sharp arrows from all sides, as they were intent on victory.

sanskrit: पाण्डवैस्तु महाराज धार्तराष्ट्री महाचमूः ।पुनः पुनरथो वीरैरभज्यत जयोद्धतैः ॥२२॥
transliteration: pāṇḍavaistu mahārāja dhārtarāṣṭrī mahācamūḥ ,punaḥ punaratho vīrairabhajyata jayoddhataiḥ.
english: But, O great king, the great army of the Dhārtarāṣṭras was repeatedly routed by the Pāṇḍavas, who were heroes elated by victory.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 45 Verse 22. But, O great king, the great army of the Dhārtarāṣṭras was repeatedly routed by the Pāṇḍavas, who were heroes elated by victory.

sanskrit: पश्यतां ते महाराज पुत्राणां चित्रयोधिनाम् ।शकुनेः सौबलेयस्य कर्णस्य च महात्मनः ॥२३॥
transliteration: paśyatāṁ te mahārāja putrāṇāṁ citrayodhinām ,śakuneḥ saubaleyasya karṇasya ca mahātmanaḥ.
english: O great king, while your sons, those wondrous fighters, and Śakuni, the son of Subala, and the great-souled (mahātman) Karṇa were observing.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 45 Verse 23. O great king, while your sons, those wondrous fighters, and Śakuni, the son of Subala, and the great-souled (mahātman) Karṇa were observing.

sanskrit: वार्यमाणा महासेना पुत्रैस्तव जनेश्वर ।नावतिष्ठत संग्रामे ताड्यमाना समन्ततः ॥२४॥
transliteration: vāryamāṇā mahāsenā putraistava janeśvara ,nāvatiṣṭhata saṁgrāme tāḍyamānā samantataḥ.
english: O lord of men, your great army, though being restrained by your sons and being assailed from all sides, could not stand firm in the battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 45 Verse 24. O lord of men, your great army, though being restrained by your sons and being assailed from all sides, could not stand firm in the battle.

sanskrit: ततो योधैर्महाराज पलायद्भिस्ततस्ततः ।अभवद्व्याकुलं भीतैः पुत्राणां ते महद्बलम् ॥२५॥
transliteration: tato yodhairmahārāja palāyadbhistatastataḥ ,abhavadvyākulaṁ bhītaiḥ putrāṇāṁ te mahadbalam.
english: Then, O great king, your sons' great army became utterly disordered by the terrified warriors who were fleeing everywhere.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 45 Verse 25. Then, O great king, your sons' great army became utterly disordered by the terrified warriors who were fleeing everywhere.

sanskrit: तिष्ठ तिष्ठेति सततं सूतपुत्रस्य जल्पतः ।नावतिष्ठत सा सेना वध्यमाना महात्मभिः ॥२६॥
transliteration: tiṣṭha tiṣṭheti satataṁ sūtaputrasya jalpataḥ ,nāvatiṣṭhata sā senā vadhyamānā mahātmabhiḥ.
english: Even though the son of Sūta (Karṇa) was constantly crying out, 'Stop! Stop!', that army, being slaughtered by the great-souled ones (mahātman), could not stand firm.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 45 Verse 26. Even though the son of Sūta (Karṇa) was constantly crying out, 'Stop! Stop!', that army, being slaughtered by the great-souled ones (mahātman), could not stand firm.

sanskrit: अथोत्क्रुष्टं महाराज पाण्डवैर्जितकाशिभिः ।धार्तराष्ट्रबलं दृष्ट्वा द्रवमाणं समन्ततः ॥२७॥
transliteration: athotkruṣṭaṁ mahārāja pāṇḍavairjitakāśibhiḥ ,dhārtarāṣṭrabalaṁ dṛṣṭvā dravamāṇaṁ samantataḥ.
english: Then, O great king, after observing Dhritarashtra's army fleeing in all directions, a loud cheer was raised by the Pandavas, who were shining with victory.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 45 Verse 27. Then, O great king, after observing Dhritarashtra's army fleeing in all directions, a loud cheer was raised by the Pandavas, who were shining with victory.

sanskrit: ततो दुर्योधनः कर्णमब्रवीत्प्रणयादिव ।पश्य कर्ण यथा सेना पाण्डवैरर्दिता भृशम् ॥२८॥
transliteration: tato duryodhanaḥ karṇamabravītpraṇayādiva ,paśya karṇa yathā senā pāṇḍavairarditā bhṛśam.
english: Then Duryodhana spoke to Karna, as if with great affection: 'Look, Karna, how severely the army has been afflicted by the Pandavas!'
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 45 Verse 28. Then Duryodhana spoke to Karna, as if with great affection: 'Look, Karna, how severely the army has been afflicted by the Pandavas!'

sanskrit: त्वयि तिष्ठति संत्रासात्पलायति समन्ततः ।एतज्ज्ञात्वा महाबाहो कुरु प्राप्तमरिंदम ॥२९॥
transliteration: tvayi tiṣṭhati saṁtrāsātpalāyati samantataḥ ,etajjñātvā mahābāho kuru prāptamariṁdama.
english: Even with you present, it [the army] is fleeing everywhere out of fear. Knowing this, O mighty-armed one, O vanquisher of foes, perform what is appropriate!
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 45 Verse 29. Even with you present, it [the army] is fleeing everywhere out of fear. Knowing this, O mighty-armed one, O vanquisher of foes, perform what is appropriate!

sanskrit: सहस्राणि च योधानां त्वामेव पुरुषर्षभ ।क्रोशन्ति समरे वीर द्राव्यमाणानि पाण्डवैः ॥३०॥
transliteration: sahasrāṇi ca yodhānāṁ tvāmeva puruṣarṣabha ,krośanti samare vīra drāvyamāṇāni pāṇḍavaiḥ.
english: And thousands of warriors, O best among men, O hero, are calling out to you precisely, while they are being driven away in battle by the Pandavas.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 45 Verse 30. And thousands of warriors, O best among men, O hero, are calling out to you precisely, while they are being driven away in battle by the Pandavas.

sanskrit: एतच्छ्रुत्वा तु राधेयो दुर्योधनवचो महत् ।मद्रराजमिदं वाक्यमब्रवीत्सूतनन्दनः ॥३१॥
transliteration: etacchrutvā tu rādheyo duryodhanavaco mahat ,madrarājamidaṁ vākyamabravītsūtanandanaḥ.
english: Upon hearing these weighty words from Duryodhana, Karna, the son of the charioteer, spoke this statement to the King of Madras.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 45 Verse 31. Upon hearing these weighty words from Duryodhana, Karna, the son of the charioteer, spoke this statement to the King of Madras.

sanskrit: पश्य मे भुजयोर्वीर्यमस्त्राणां च जनेश्वर ।अद्य हन्मि रणे सर्वान्पाञ्चालान्पाण्डुभिः सह ।वाहयाश्वान्नरव्याघ्र भद्रेणैव जनेश्वर ॥३२॥
transliteration: paśya me bhujayorvīryamastrāṇāṁ ca janeśvara ,adya hanmi raṇe sarvānpāñcālānpāṇḍubhiḥ saha ,vāhayāśvānnaravyāghra bhadreṇaiva janeśvara.
english: O Lord of men (janeśvara), behold the might of my arms and my weapons! Today in battle, I shall slay all the Pañcālas together with the Pāṇḍavas. O tiger among men (naravyāghra), drive the horses skillfully, O Lord of men (janeśvara)!
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 45 Verse 32. O Lord of men (janeśvara), behold the might of my arms and my weapons! Today in battle, I shall slay all the Pañcālas together with the Pāṇḍavas. O tiger among men (naravyāghra), drive the horses skillfully, O Lord of men (janeśvara)!

sanskrit: एवमुक्त्वा महाराज सूतपुत्रः प्रतापवान् ।प्रगृह्य विजयं वीरो धनुःश्रेष्ठं पुरातनम् ।सज्यं कृत्वा महाराज संमृज्य च पुनः पुनः ॥३३॥
transliteration: evamuktvā mahārāja sūtaputraḥ pratāpavān ,pragṛhya vijayaṁ vīro dhanuḥśreṣṭhaṁ purātanam ,sajyaṁ kṛtvā mahārāja saṁmṛjya ca punaḥ punaḥ.
english: O great king (mahārāja), having thus spoken, the mighty son of the charioteer, that hero, grasped the ancient and supreme bow called Vijaya, and having strung it and cleaned it again and again, (he did this).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 45 Verse 33. O great king (mahārāja), having thus spoken, the mighty son of the charioteer, that hero, grasped the ancient and supreme bow called Vijaya, and having strung it and cleaned it again and again, (he did this).

sanskrit: संनिवार्य च योधान्स्वान्सत्येन शपथेन च ।प्रायोजयदमेयात्मा भार्गवास्त्रं महाबलः ॥३४॥
transliteration: saṁnivārya ca yodhānsvānsatyena śapathena ca ,prāyojayadameyātmā bhārgavāstraṁ mahābalaḥ.
english: And restraining his own warriors with an oath and by the truth, that one of immeasurable spirit (ātman) and great strength deployed the Bhārgava weapon (astra).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 45 Verse 34. And restraining his own warriors with an oath and by the truth, that one of immeasurable spirit (ātman) and great strength deployed the Bhārgava weapon (astra).

sanskrit: ततो राजन्सहस्राणि प्रयुतान्यर्बुदानि च ।कोटिशश्च शरास्तीक्ष्णा निरगच्छन्महामृधे ॥३५॥
transliteration: tato rājansahasrāṇi prayutānyarbudāni ca ,koṭiśaśca śarāstīkṣṇā niragacchanmahāmṛdhe.
english: Then, O king, thousands, millions, and hundreds of millions of sharp arrows, numbering in the crores, were discharged in that great battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 45 Verse 35. Then, O king, thousands, millions, and hundreds of millions of sharp arrows, numbering in the crores, were discharged in that great battle.

sanskrit: ज्वलितैस्तैर्महाघोरैः कङ्कबर्हिणवाजितैः ।संछन्ना पाण्डवी सेना न प्राज्ञायत किंचन ॥३६॥
