transliteration: jvalitaistairmahāghoraiḥ kaṅkabarhiṇavājitaiḥ ,saṁchannā pāṇḍavī senā na prājñāyata kiṁcana.
english: Completely covered by those blazing, exceedingly dreadful arrows, adorned with the feathers of kites and peacocks, the Pāṇḍava army could not be discerned at all.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 45 Verse 36. Completely covered by those blazing, exceedingly dreadful arrows, adorned with the feathers of kites and peacocks, the Pāṇḍava army could not be discerned at all.

sanskrit: हाहाकारो महानासीत्पाञ्चालानां विशां पते ।पीडितानां बलवता भार्गवास्त्रेण संयुगे ॥३७॥
transliteration: hāhākāro mahānāsītpāñcālānāṁ viśāṁ pate ,pīḍitānāṁ balavatā bhārgavāstreṇa saṁyuge.
english: O lord of the people, a great uproar arose from the Pāñcālas, who were afflicted in the battle by the powerful Bhārgava weapon (astra).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 45 Verse 37. O lord of the people, a great uproar arose from the Pāñcālas, who were afflicted in the battle by the powerful Bhārgava weapon (astra).

sanskrit: निपतद्भिर्गजै राजन्नरैश्चापि सहस्रशः ।रथैश्चापि नरव्याघ्र हयैश्चापि समन्ततः ॥३८॥
transliteration: nipatadbhirgajai rājannaraiścāpi sahasraśaḥ ,rathaiścāpi naravyāghra hayaiścāpi samantataḥ.
english: O king, O tiger among men, by falling elephants, and by thousands of falling men, as well as by chariots and horses everywhere...
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 45 Verse 38. O king, O tiger among men, by falling elephants, and by thousands of falling men, as well as by chariots and horses everywhere...

sanskrit: प्राकम्पत मही राजन्निहतैस्तैस्ततस्ततः ।व्याकुलं सर्वमभवत्पाण्डवानां महद्बलम् ॥३९॥
transliteration: prākampata mahī rājannihataistaistatastataḥ ,vyākulaṁ sarvamabhavatpāṇḍavānāṁ mahadbalam.
english: O King, the earth trembled because of those slain warriors scattered here and there. The entire great army of the Pandavas became agitated.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 45 Verse 39. O King, the earth trembled because of those slain warriors scattered here and there. The entire great army of the Pandavas became agitated.

sanskrit: कर्णस्त्वेको युधां श्रेष्ठो विधूम इव पावकः ।दहञ्शत्रून्नरव्याघ्र शुशुभे स परंतपः ॥४०॥
transliteration: karṇastveko yudhāṁ śreṣṭho vidhūma iva pāvakaḥ ,dahañśatrūnnaravyāghra śuśubhe sa paraṁtapaḥ.
english: O tiger among men, Karna alone, the foremost among warriors, shone brilliantly like a smokeless fire, burning his enemies, for he was indeed the tormentor of foes.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 45 Verse 40. O tiger among men, Karna alone, the foremost among warriors, shone brilliantly like a smokeless fire, burning his enemies, for he was indeed the tormentor of foes.

sanskrit: ते वध्यमानाः कर्णेन पाञ्चालाश्चेदिभिः सह ।तत्र तत्र व्यमुह्यन्त वनदाहे यथा द्विपाः ।चुक्रुशुस्ते नरव्याघ्र यथाप्राग्वा नरोत्तमाः ॥४१॥
transliteration: te vadhyamānāḥ karṇena pāñcālāścedibhiḥ saha ,tatra tatra vyamuhyanta vanadāhe yathā dvipāḥ ,cukruśuste naravyāghra yathāprāgvā narottamāḥ.
english: O tiger among men, those Panchalas, along with the Chedis, being slain by Karna, became utterly bewildered here and there, just like elephants in a forest fire. And those finest of men cried out exactly as they had before.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 45 Verse 41. O tiger among men, those Panchalas, along with the Chedis, being slain by Karna, became utterly bewildered here and there, just like elephants in a forest fire. And those finest of men cried out exactly as they had before.

sanskrit: तेषां तु क्रोशतां श्रुत्वा भीतानां रणमूर्धनि ।धावतां च दिशो राजन्वित्रस्तानां समन्ततः ।आर्तनादो महांस्तत्र प्रेतानामिव संप्लवे ॥४२॥
transliteration: teṣāṁ tu krośatāṁ śrutvā bhītānāṁ raṇamūrdhani ,dhāvatāṁ ca diśo rājanvitrastānāṁ samantataḥ ,ārtanādo mahāṁstatra pretānāmiva saṁplave.
english: O King, having heard the cries of those terrified warriors at the forefront of battle, and of those utterly frightened ones fleeing in all directions, a great cry of distress arose there, like that of departed spirits (preta) amidst a universal dissolution (saṃplava).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 45 Verse 42. O King, having heard the cries of those terrified warriors at the forefront of battle, and of those utterly frightened ones fleeing in all directions, a great cry of distress arose there, like that of departed spirits (preta) amidst a universal dissolution (saṃplava).

sanskrit: वध्यमानांस्तु तान्दृष्ट्वा सूतपुत्रेण मारिष ।वित्रेसुः सर्वभूतानि तिर्यग्योनिगतान्यपि ॥४३॥
transliteration: vadhyamānāṁstu tāndṛṣṭvā sūtaputreṇa māriṣa ,vitresuḥ sarvabhūtāni tiryagyonigatānyapi.
english: O venerable one, seeing them (the warriors) being slain by Karṇa (the son of Sūta), all creatures, even those born in animal wombs, trembled in fear.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 45 Verse 43. O venerable one, seeing them (the warriors) being slain by Karṇa (the son of Sūta), all creatures, even those born in animal wombs, trembled in fear.

sanskrit: ते वध्यमानाः समरे सूतपुत्रेण सृञ्जयाः ।अर्जुनं वासुदेवं च व्याक्रोशन्त मुहुर्मुहुः ।प्रेतराजपुरे यद्वत्प्रेतराजं विचेतसः ॥४४॥
transliteration: te vadhyamānāḥ samare sūtaputreṇa sṛñjayāḥ ,arjunaṁ vāsudevaṁ ca vyākrośanta muhurmuhuḥ ,pretarājapure yadvatpretarājaṁ vicetasaḥ.
english: The Sṛñjayas, being slain in battle by Karṇa (the son of Sūta), repeatedly cried out to Arjuna and Vāsudeva (Kṛṣṇa), just as bewildered people (vicetasaḥ) in the city of the lord of the departed (pretarājapura) cry out to the lord of the departed (pretarāja) himself.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 45 Verse 44. The Sṛñjayas, being slain in battle by Karṇa (the son of Sūta), repeatedly cried out to Arjuna and Vāsudeva (Kṛṣṇa), just as bewildered people (vicetasaḥ) in the city of the lord of the departed (pretarājapura) cry out to the lord of the departed (pretarāja) himself.

sanskrit: अथाब्रवीद्वासुदेवं कुन्तीपुत्रो धनंजयः ।भार्गवास्त्रं महाघोरं दृष्ट्वा तत्र सभीरितम् ॥४५॥
transliteration: athābravīdvāsudevaṁ kuntīputro dhanaṁjayaḥ ,bhārgavāstraṁ mahāghoraṁ dṛṣṭvā tatra sabhīritam.
english: Then, Dhanañjaya, the son of Kuntī (Arjuna), spoke to Vāsudeva (Kṛṣṇa), having seen the extremely dreadful Bhārgava missile (astra) unleashed there.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 45 Verse 45. Then, Dhanañjaya, the son of Kuntī (Arjuna), spoke to Vāsudeva (Kṛṣṇa), having seen the extremely dreadful Bhārgava missile (astra) unleashed there.

sanskrit: पश्य कृष्ण महाबाहो भार्गवास्त्रस्य विक्रमम् ।नैतदस्त्रं हि समरे शक्यं हन्तुं कथंचन ॥४६॥
transliteration: paśya kṛṣṇa mahābāho bhārgavāstrasya vikramam ,naitadastraṁ hi samare śakyaṁ hantuṁ kathaṁcana.
english: O Kṛṣṇa, O mighty-armed one, behold the prowess (vikramam) of the Bhārgava missile (astra)! Indeed, this missile cannot be countered (hantum) in battle by any means whatsoever.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 45 Verse 46. O Kṛṣṇa, O mighty-armed one, behold the prowess (vikramam) of the Bhārgava missile (astra)! Indeed, this missile cannot be countered (hantum) in battle by any means whatsoever.

sanskrit: सूतपुत्रं च संरब्धं पश्य कृष्ण महारणे ।अन्तकप्रतिमं वीरं कुर्वाणं कर्म दारुणम् ॥४७॥
transliteration: sūtaputraṁ ca saṁrabdhaṁ paśya kṛṣṇa mahāraṇe ,antakapratimaṁ vīraṁ kurvāṇaṁ karma dāruṇam.
english: O Kṛṣṇa, behold the enraged son of Sūta (Karṇa) in this great battle, a hero like death's very image (antaka), performing dreadful actions (karma).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 45 Verse 47. O Kṛṣṇa, behold the enraged son of Sūta (Karṇa) in this great battle, a hero like death's very image (antaka), performing dreadful actions (karma).

sanskrit: सुतीक्ष्णं चोदयन्नश्वान्प्रेक्षते मां मुहुर्मुहुः ।न च पश्यामि समरे कर्णस्य प्रपलायितम् ॥४८॥
transliteration: sutīkṣṇaṁ codayannaśvānprekṣate māṁ muhurmuhuḥ ,na ca paśyāmi samare karṇasya prapalāyitam.
english: He, fiercely urging his horses, repeatedly looks at me. And I do not see any fleeing (prapalāyitam) of Karṇa in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 45 Verse 48. He, fiercely urging his horses, repeatedly looks at me. And I do not see any fleeing (prapalāyitam) of Karṇa in battle.

sanskrit: जीवन्प्राप्नोति पुरुषः संख्ये जयपराजयौ ।जितस्य तु हृषीकेश वध एव कुतो जयः ॥४९॥
transliteration: jīvanprāpnoti puruṣaḥ saṁkhye jayaparājayau ,jitasya tu hṛṣīkeśa vadha eva kuto jayaḥ.
english: A living person (puruṣa) achieves both victory and defeat in battle. But for the vanquished, O Hṛṣīkeśa, there is only death - how then can there be victory?
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 45 Verse 49. A living person (puruṣa) achieves both victory and defeat in battle. But for the vanquished, O Hṛṣīkeśa, there is only death - how then can there be victory?

sanskrit: ततो जनार्दनः प्रायाद्द्रष्टुमिच्छन्युधिष्ठिरम् ।श्रमेण ग्राहयिष्यंश्च कर्णं युद्धेन मारिष ॥५०॥
transliteration: tato janārdanaḥ prāyāddraṣṭumicchanyudhiṣṭhiram ,śrameṇa grāhayiṣyaṁśca karṇaṁ yuddhena māriṣa.
english: Then Janārdana (Kṛṣṇa) set forth, desiring to see Yudhiṣṭhira and intending to have Karṇa overcome through battle and his exertion, O venerable one.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 45 Verse 50. Then Janārdana (Kṛṣṇa) set forth, desiring to see Yudhiṣṭhira and intending to have Karṇa overcome through battle and his exertion, O venerable one.

sanskrit: अर्जुनं चाब्रवीत्कृष्णो भृशं राजा परिक्षतः ।तमाश्वास्य कुरुश्रेष्ठ ततः कर्णं हनिष्यसि ॥५१॥
transliteration: arjunaṁ cābravītkṛṣṇo bhṛśaṁ rājā parikṣataḥ ,tamāśvāsya kuruśreṣṭha tataḥ karṇaṁ haniṣyasi.
english: Krishna then spoke to Arjuna: "The king (Yudhiṣṭhira) is gravely wounded. Therefore, O best among the Kurus, after you have comforted him, you will then slay Karṇa."
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 45 Verse 51. Krishna then spoke to Arjuna: "The king (Yudhiṣṭhira) is gravely wounded. Therefore, O best among the Kurus, after you have comforted him, you will then slay Karṇa."

sanskrit: ततो धनंजयो द्रष्टुं राजानं बाणपीडितम् ।रथेन प्रययौ क्षिप्रं संग्रामे केशवाज्ञया ॥५२॥
transliteration: tato dhanaṁjayo draṣṭuṁ rājānaṁ bāṇapīḍitam ,rathena prayayau kṣipraṁ saṁgrāme keśavājñayā.
english: Then Dhanañjaya (Arjuna) quickly went by chariot on the battlefield to see the king, who was afflicted by arrows, following Keśava's (Krishna's) command.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 45 Verse 52. Then Dhanañjaya (Arjuna) quickly went by chariot on the battlefield to see the king, who was afflicted by arrows, following Keśava's (Krishna's) command.

sanskrit: गच्छन्नेव तु कौन्तेयो धर्मराजदिदृक्षया ।सैन्यमालोकयामास नापश्यत्तत्र चाग्रजम् ॥५३॥
transliteration: gacchanneva tu kaunteyo dharmarājadidṛkṣayā ,sainyamālokayāmāsa nāpaśyattatra cāgrajam.
english: As he went, the son of Kunti (Arjuna), eager to see Dharmarāja (Yudhiṣṭhira), indeed surveyed the army, but he did not see his elder brother there.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 45 Verse 53. As he went, the son of Kunti (Arjuna), eager to see Dharmarāja (Yudhiṣṭhira), indeed surveyed the army, but he did not see his elder brother there.

sanskrit: युद्धं कृत्वा तु कौन्तेयो द्रोणपुत्रेण भारत ।दुःसहं वज्रिणा संख्ये पराजिग्ये भृगोः सुतम् ॥५४॥
transliteration: yuddhaṁ kṛtvā tu kaunteyo droṇaputreṇa bhārata ,duḥsahaṁ vajriṇā saṁkhye parājigye bhṛgoḥ sutam.
english: O Bhārata, after having fought a battle with Droṇa's son – a battle that was indeed difficult for even Indra to withstand in combat – the son of Kunti (Arjuna) defeated him, the son of Bhṛgu (Aśvatthāmā).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 45 Verse 54. O Bhārata, after having fought a battle with Droṇa's son – a battle that was indeed difficult for even Indra to withstand in combat – the son of Kunti (Arjuna) defeated him, the son of Bhṛgu (Aśvatthāmā).

sanskrit: द्रौणिं पराजित्य ततोग्रधन्वा कृत्वा महद्दुष्करमार्यकर्म ।आलोकयामास ततः स्वसैन्यं धनंजयः शत्रुभिरप्रधृष्यः ॥५५॥
transliteration: drauṇiṁ parājitya tatogradhanvā; kṛtvā mahadduṣkaramāryakarma ,ālokayāmāsa tataḥ svasainyaṁ; dhanaṁjayaḥ śatrubhirapradhṛṣyaḥ.
english: After defeating Drauni and performing a great, difficult, and noble deed, the fierce-bowed Arjuna (Dhananjaya), unconquerable by enemies, then surveyed his own army.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 45 Verse 55. After defeating Drauni and performing a great, difficult, and noble deed, the fierce-bowed Arjuna (Dhananjaya), unconquerable by enemies, then surveyed his own army.

sanskrit: स युध्यमानः पृतनामुखस्थाञ्शूराञ्शूरो हर्षयन्सव्यसाची ।पूर्वापदानैः प्रथितैः प्रशंसन्स्थिरांश्चकारात्मरथाननीके ॥५६॥
transliteration: sa yudhyamānaḥ pṛtanāmukhasthā;ñśūrāñśūro harṣayansavyasācī ,pūrvāpadānaiḥ prathitaiḥ praśaṁsa;nsthirāṁścakārātmarathānanīke.
english: He, the brave ambidextrous (Arjuna), encouraging the heroes positioned at the vanguard of the army and praising them for their renowned past exploits, made his own forces steadfast in the ranks.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 45 Verse 56. He, the brave ambidextrous (Arjuna), encouraging the heroes positioned at the vanguard of the army and praising them for their renowned past exploits, made his own forces steadfast in the ranks.

sanskrit: अपश्यमानस्तु किरीटमाली युधि ज्येष्ठं भ्रातरमाजमीढम् ।उवाच भीमं तरसाभ्युपेत्य राज्ञः प्रवृत्तिस्त्विह केति राजन् ॥५७॥
transliteration: apaśyamānastu kirīṭamālī; yudhi jyeṣṭhaṁ bhrātaramājamīḍham ,uvāca bhīmaṁ tarasābhyupetya; rājñaḥ pravṛttistviha keti rājan.
english: But Arjuna, wearing his diadem (kirīṭamālī), not seeing his elder brother, Yudhiṣṭhira (the descendant of Ajamiḍha), in the battle, quickly approached Bhima and asked, "O King, what is the situation of the king here?"
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 45 Verse 57. But Arjuna, wearing his diadem (kirīṭamālī), not seeing his elder brother, Yudhiṣṭhira (the descendant of Ajamiḍha), in the battle, quickly approached Bhima and asked, "O King, what is the situation of the king here?"

sanskrit: भीम उवाच ।अपयात इतो राजा धर्मपुत्रो युधिष्ठिरः ।कर्णबाणविभुग्नाङ्गो यदि जीवेत्कथंचन ॥५८॥
transliteration: bhīma uvāca ,apayāta ito rājā dharmaputro yudhiṣṭhiraḥ ,karṇabāṇavibhugnāṅgo yadi jīvetkathaṁcana.
english: Bhima said, "King Yudhiṣṭhira, the son of Dharma (natural law), has departed from here. His body is shattered by Karna's arrows; it is doubtful if he will survive."
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 45 Verse 58. Bhima said, "King Yudhiṣṭhira, the son of Dharma (natural law), has departed from here. His body is shattered by Karna's arrows; it is doubtful if he will survive."

sanskrit: अर्जुन उवाच ।तस्माद्भवाञ्शीघ्रमितः प्रयातु राज्ञः प्रवृत्त्यै कुरुसत्तमस्य ।नूनं हि विद्धोऽतिभृशं पृषत्कैः कर्णेन राजा शिबिरं गतोऽसौ ॥५९॥
transliteration: arjuna uvāca ,tasmādbhavāñśīghramitaḥ prayātu; rājñaḥ pravṛttyai kurusattamasya ,nūnaṁ hi viddho'tibhṛśaṁ pṛṣatkaiḥ; karṇena rājā śibiraṁ gato'sau.
english: Arjuna said: Therefore, you should quickly go from here to ascertain the condition of the king, the best among the Kurus. Indeed, that king, having been struck very severely by Karna with arrows, has surely gone back to the camp.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 45 Verse 59. Arjuna said: Therefore, you should quickly go from here to ascertain the condition of the king, the best among the Kurus. Indeed, that king, having been struck very severely by Karna with arrows, has surely gone back to the camp.

sanskrit: यः संप्रहारे निशि संप्रवृत्ते द्रोणेन विद्धोऽतिभृशं तरस्वी ।तस्थौ च तत्रापि जयप्रतीक्षो द्रोणेन यावन्न हतः किलासीत् ॥६०॥
transliteration: yaḥ saṁprahāre niśi saṁpravṛtte; droṇena viddho'tibhṛśaṁ tarasvī ,tasthau ca tatrāpi jayapratīkṣo; droṇena yāvanna hataḥ kilāsīt.
english: That energetic (tarasvin) king, who, even though very severely struck by Drona during the battle that had commenced at night, remained on the field, awaiting victory, until he was indeed not killed by Drona.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 45 Verse 60. That energetic (tarasvin) king, who, even though very severely struck by Drona during the battle that had commenced at night, remained on the field, awaiting victory, until he was indeed not killed by Drona.

sanskrit: स संशयं गमितः पाण्डवाग्र्यः संख्येऽद्य कर्णेन महानुभावः ।ज्ञातुं प्रयाह्याशु तमद्य भीम स्थास्याम्यहं शत्रुगणान्निरुध्य ॥६१॥
transliteration: sa saṁśayaṁ gamitaḥ pāṇḍavāgryaḥ; saṁkhye'dya karṇena mahānubhāvaḥ ,jñātuṁ prayāhyāśu tamadya bhīma; sthāsyāmyahaṁ śatrugaṇānnirudhya.
english: That great-souled (mahānubhāva), foremost of the Pandavas (Yudhishthira), has been brought into a state of peril (saṃśaya) today in battle by Karna. O Bhima, quickly go today to find out about him. I will remain here, restraining the hordes of enemies.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 45 Verse 61. That great-souled (mahānubhāva), foremost of the Pandavas (Yudhishthira), has been brought into a state of peril (saṃśaya) today in battle by Karna. O Bhima, quickly go today to find out about him. I will remain here, restraining the hordes of enemies.

sanskrit: भीम उवाच ।त्वमेव जानीहि महानुभाव राज्ञः प्रवृत्तिं भरतर्षभस्य ।अहं हि यद्यर्जुन यामि तत्र वक्ष्यन्ति मां भीत इति प्रवीराः ॥६२॥
transliteration: bhīma uvāca ,tvameva jānīhi mahānubhāva; rājñaḥ pravṛttiṁ bharatarṣabhasya ,ahaṁ hi yadyarjuna yāmi tatra; vakṣyanti māṁ bhīta iti pravīrāḥ.
english: Bhima said: O noble (mahānubhāva) one, you yourself should ascertain the condition of the king, the best of the Bharatas. For, O Arjuna, if I go there, the great warriors will indeed say that I am afraid.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 45 Verse 62. Bhima said: O noble (mahānubhāva) one, you yourself should ascertain the condition of the king, the best of the Bharatas. For, O Arjuna, if I go there, the great warriors will indeed say that I am afraid.

sanskrit: ततोऽब्रवीदर्जुनो भीमसेनं संशप्तकाः प्रत्यनीकं स्थिता मे ।एतानहत्वा न मया तु शक्यमितोऽपयातुं रिपुसंघगोष्ठात् ॥६३॥
transliteration: tato'bravīdarjuno bhīmasenaṁ; saṁśaptakāḥ pratyanīkaṁ sthitā me ,etānahatvā na mayā tu śakya;mito'payātuṁ ripusaṁghagoṣṭhāt.
english: Then Arjuna said to Bhimasena, "The Samsaptakas stand directly opposite me. It is not possible for me to leave this assembly of enemies without having killed them."
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 45 Verse 63. Then Arjuna said to Bhimasena, "The Samsaptakas stand directly opposite me. It is not possible for me to leave this assembly of enemies without having killed them."

sanskrit: अथाब्रवीदर्जुनं भीमसेनः स्ववीर्यमाश्रित्य कुरुप्रवीर ।संशप्तकान्प्रतियोत्स्यामि संख्ये सर्वानहं याहि धनंजयेति ॥६४॥
transliteration: athābravīdarjunaṁ bhīmasenaḥ; svavīryamāśritya kurupravīra ,saṁśaptakānpratiyotsyāmi saṁkhye; sarvānahaṁ yāhi dhanaṁjayeti.
english: Then Bhimasena said to Arjuna, "O chief of the Kurus, relying on my own strength, I will fight against all the Samsaptakas in battle. You go, O Dhananjaya!"
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 45 Verse 64. Then Bhimasena said to Arjuna, "O chief of the Kurus, relying on my own strength, I will fight against all the Samsaptakas in battle. You go, O Dhananjaya!"

sanskrit: तद्भीमसेनस्य वचो निशम्य सुदुर्वचं भ्रातुरमित्रमध्ये ।द्रष्टुं कुरुश्रेष्ठमभिप्रयातुं प्रोवाच वृष्णिप्रवरं तदानीम् ॥६५॥
transliteration: tadbhīmasenasya vaco niśamya; sudurvacaṁ bhrāturamitramadhye ,draṣṭuṁ kuruśreṣṭhamabhiprayātuṁ; provāca vṛṣṇipravaraṁ tadānīm.
english: Having heard that statement of Bhimasena – his brother's words, so difficult to utter amidst enemies – Arjuna, desiring to see the chief of the Kurus (Yudhishthira) and go towards him, then spoke to the foremost of the Vrishnis (Krishna).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 45 Verse 65. Having heard that statement of Bhimasena – his brother's words, so difficult to utter amidst enemies – Arjuna, desiring to see the chief of the Kurus (Yudhishthira) and go towards him, then spoke to the foremost of the Vrishnis (Krishna).

sanskrit: चोदयाश्वान्हृषीकेश विगाह्यैतं रथार्णवम् ।अजातशत्रुं राजानं द्रष्टुमिच्छामि केशव ॥६६॥
transliteration: codayāśvānhṛṣīkeśa vigāhyaitaṁ rathārṇavam ,ajātaśatruṁ rājānaṁ draṣṭumicchāmi keśava.
english: “O Hrishikesha, drive the horses! Having penetrated this ocean of chariots, I wish to see King Ajatashatru (Yudhishthira), O Keshava.”
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 45 Verse 66. “O Hrishikesha, drive the horses! Having penetrated this ocean of chariots, I wish to see King Ajatashatru (Yudhishthira), O Keshava.”

sanskrit: ततो हयान्सर्वदाशार्हमुख्यः प्राचोदयद्भीममुवाच चेदम् ।नैतच्चित्रं तव कर्माद्य वीर यास्यामहे जहि भीमारिसंघान् ॥६७॥
transliteration: tato hayānsarvadāśārhamukhyaḥ; prācodayadbhīmamuvāca cedam ,naitaccitraṁ tava karmādya vīra; yāsyāmahe jahi bhīmārisaṁghān.
english: Then, the chief of all Dāśārhas (Krishna) urged the horses and said this to Bhīma: 'O hero, this action (karma) of yours today is not surprising. Let us go; destroy the formidable hosts of enemies!'
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 45 Verse 67. Then, the chief of all Dāśārhas (Krishna) urged the horses and said this to Bhīma: 'O hero, this action (karma) of yours today is not surprising. Let us go; destroy the formidable hosts of enemies!'

sanskrit: ततो ययौ हृषीकेशो यत्र राजा युधिष्ठिरः ।शीघ्राच्छीघ्रतरं राजन्वाजिभिर्गरुडोपमैः ॥६८॥
transliteration: tato yayau hṛṣīkeśo yatra rājā yudhiṣṭhiraḥ ,śīghrācchīghrataraṁ rājanvājibhirgaruḍopamaiḥ.
english: O king, then Hṛṣīkeśa (Krishna) went to where King Yudhiṣṭhira was, more swiftly than swift, with horses resembling Garuḍa.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 45 Verse 68. O king, then Hṛṣīkeśa (Krishna) went to where King Yudhiṣṭhira was, more swiftly than swift, with horses resembling Garuḍa.

sanskrit: प्रत्यनीके व्यवस्थाप्य भीमसेनमरिंदमम् ।संदिश्य चैव राजेन्द्र युद्धं प्रति वृकोदरम् ॥६९॥
transliteration: pratyanīke vyavasthāpya bhīmasenamariṁdamam ,saṁdiśya caiva rājendra yuddhaṁ prati vṛkodaram.
english: O chief of kings (Dhritarashtra), having stationed Bhīmasena, the subduer of enemies, facing the opposing army, and indeed having given instructions to Vṛkodara (Bhīma) concerning the battle...
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 45 Verse 69. O chief of kings (Dhritarashtra), having stationed Bhīmasena, the subduer of enemies, facing the opposing army, and indeed having given instructions to Vṛkodara (Bhīma) concerning the battle...

sanskrit: ततस्तु गत्वा पुरुषप्रवीरौ राजानमासाद्य शयानमेकम् ।रथादुभौ प्रत्यवरुह्य तस्माद्ववन्दतुर्धर्मराजस्य पादौ ॥७०॥
transliteration: tatastu gatvā puruṣapravīrau; rājānamāsādya śayānamekam ,rathādubhau pratyavaruhya tasmā;dvavandaturdharmarājasya pādau.
english: Then, having gone and approached the king (Yudhiṣṭhira), who was lying down alone, the two foremost among men (Krishna and Bhīma) both dismounted from that chariot and bowed down to the feet of the King of Righteousness (Dharmarāja).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 45 Verse 70. Then, having gone and approached the king (Yudhiṣṭhira), who was lying down alone, the two foremost among men (Krishna and Bhīma) both dismounted from that chariot and bowed down to the feet of the King of Righteousness (Dharmarāja).

sanskrit: तौ दृष्ट्वा पुरुषव्याघ्रौ क्षेमिणौ पुरुषर्षभ ।मुदाभ्युपगतौ कृष्णावश्विनाविव वासवम् ॥७१॥
transliteration: tau dṛṣṭvā puruṣavyāghrau kṣemiṇau puruṣarṣabha ,mudābhyupagatau kṛṣṇāvaśvināviva vāsavam.
english: O bull among men (puruṣarṣabha), seeing those two, Kṛṣṇa and Arjuna (the two Kṛṣṇas), who are like tigers among men (puruṣavyāghrau) and safe, they joyfully reunited, just as the Aśvin twins approached Indra (Vāsava).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 45 Verse 71. O bull among men (puruṣarṣabha), seeing those two, Kṛṣṇa and Arjuna (the two Kṛṣṇas), who are like tigers among men (puruṣavyāghrau) and safe, they joyfully reunited, just as the Aśvin twins approached Indra (Vāsava).

sanskrit: तावभ्यनन्दद्राजा हि विवस्वानश्विनाविव ।हते महासुरे जम्भे शक्रविष्णू यथा गुरुः ॥७२॥
transliteration: tāvabhyanandadrājā hi vivasvānaśvināviva ,hate mahāsure jambhe śakraviṣṇū yathā guruḥ.
english: The king indeed welcomed those two, just as Vivasvān welcomed the Aśvin twins, or as Guru (Bṛhaspati) welcomed Indra (Śakra) and Viṣṇu after the great asura Jambha was slain.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 45 Verse 72. The king indeed welcomed those two, just as Vivasvān welcomed the Aśvin twins, or as Guru (Bṛhaspati) welcomed Indra (Śakra) and Viṣṇu after the great asura Jambha was slain.

sanskrit: मन्यमानो हतं कर्णं धर्मराजो युधिष्ठिरः ।हर्षगद्गदया वाचा प्रीतः प्राह परंतपौ ॥७३॥
transliteration: manyamāno hataṁ karṇaṁ dharmarājo yudhiṣṭhiraḥ ,harṣagadgadayā vācā prītaḥ prāha paraṁtapau.
english: Yudhiṣṭhira, the king of righteousness (dharma), considering Karṇa slain, was delighted and spoke with a voice choked with joy to those two tormentors of foes (parantapau).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 45 Verse 73. Yudhiṣṭhira, the king of righteousness (dharma), considering Karṇa slain, was delighted and spoke with a voice choked with joy to those two tormentors of foes (parantapau).

sanskrit: संजय उवाच ।महासत्त्वौ तु तौ दृष्ट्वा सहितौ केशवार्जुनौ ।हतमाधिरथिं मेने संख्ये गाण्डीवधन्वना ॥१॥
transliteration: saṁjaya uvāca ,mahāsattvau tu tau dṛṣṭvā sahitau keśavārjunau ,hatamādhirathiṁ mene saṁkhye gāṇḍīvadhanvanā.
english: Sañjaya said: But seeing those two great-souled ones, Keśava (Kṛṣṇa) and Arjuna, reunited, he (Yudhiṣṭhira, implicitly) understood that Karṇa (Adhirathi's son) had been slain in battle by the wielder of the Gāṇḍīva bow.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 46 Verse 1. Sañjaya said: But seeing those two great-souled ones, Keśava (Kṛṣṇa) and Arjuna, reunited, he (Yudhiṣṭhira, implicitly) understood that Karṇa (Adhirathi's son) had been slain in battle by the wielder of the Gāṇḍīva bow.

sanskrit: तावभ्यनन्दत्कौन्तेयः साम्ना परमवल्गुना ।स्मितपूर्वममित्रघ्नः पूजयन्भरतर्षभ ॥२॥
transliteration: tāvabhyanandatkaunteyaḥ sāmnā paramavalgunā ,smitapūrvamamitraghnaḥ pūjayanbharatarṣabha.
english: O best among Bharatas (Bharatarṣabha), the son of Kunti (Kaunteya), the slayer of enemies (amitraghna), respectfully greeted those two with exceedingly gentle words, preceded by a smile.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 46 Verse 2. O best among Bharatas (Bharatarṣabha), the son of Kunti (Kaunteya), the slayer of enemies (amitraghna), respectfully greeted those two with exceedingly gentle words, preceded by a smile.

sanskrit: युधिष्ठिर उवाच ।स्वागतं देवकीपुत्र स्वागतं ते धनंजय ।प्रियं मे दर्शनं बाढं युवयोरच्युतार्जुनौ ॥३॥
transliteration: yudhiṣṭhira uvāca ,svāgataṁ devakīputra svāgataṁ te dhanaṁjaya ,priyaṁ me darśanaṁ bāḍhaṁ yuvayoracyutārjunau.
english: Yudhiṣṭhira said: Welcome, son of Devakī! Welcome to you, Dhanaṃjaya! The sight of you two is indeed very dear to me, O Acyuta and Arjuna!
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 46 Verse 3. Yudhiṣṭhira said: Welcome, son of Devakī! Welcome to you, Dhanaṃjaya! The sight of you two is indeed very dear to me, O Acyuta and Arjuna!

sanskrit: अक्षताभ्यामरिष्टाभ्यां कथं युध्य महारथम् ।आशीविषसमं युद्धे सर्वशस्त्रविशारदम् ॥४॥
transliteration: akṣatābhyāmariṣṭābhyāṁ kathaṁ yudhya mahāratham ,āśīviṣasamaṁ yuddhe sarvaśastraviśāradam.
english: How are you two, who are unharmed and uninjured, to fight such a great charioteer, who is like a venomous snake in battle and skilled in all weapons?
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 46 Verse 4. How are you two, who are unharmed and uninjured, to fight such a great charioteer, who is like a venomous snake in battle and skilled in all weapons?

sanskrit: अग्रगं धार्तराष्ट्राणां सर्वेषां शर्म वर्म च ।रक्षितं वृषसेनेन सुषेणेन च धन्विना ॥५॥
transliteration: agragaṁ dhārtarāṣṭrāṇāṁ sarveṣāṁ śarma varma ca ,rakṣitaṁ vṛṣasenena suṣeṇena ca dhanvinā.
english: He is the foremost among all the Dhārtarāṣṭras, their protection and their armor, guarded by Vṛṣasena and by the archer Suṣeṇa.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 46 Verse 5. He is the foremost among all the Dhārtarāṣṭras, their protection and their armor, guarded by Vṛṣasena and by the archer Suṣeṇa.

sanskrit: अनुज्ञातं महावीर्यं रामेणास्त्रेषु दुर्जयम् ।त्रातारं धार्तराष्ट्राणां गन्तारं वाहिनीमुखे ॥६॥
transliteration: anujñātaṁ mahāvīryaṁ rāmeṇāstreṣu durjayam ,trātāraṁ dhārtarāṣṭrāṇāṁ gantāraṁ vāhinīmukhe.
english: You have defeated him who was sanctioned (to wield weapons) by Rāma (Paraśurāma), who was of great prowess and unconquerable in missile warfare. He was the protector of the sons of Dhṛtarāṣṭra and always advanced at the forefront of the army.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 46 Verse 6. You have defeated him who was sanctioned (to wield weapons) by Rāma (Paraśurāma), who was of great prowess and unconquerable in missile warfare. He was the protector of the sons of Dhṛtarāṣṭra and always advanced at the forefront of the army.

sanskrit: हन्तारमरिसैन्यानाममित्रगणमर्दनम् ।दुर्योधनहिते युक्तमस्मद्युद्धाय चोद्यतम् ॥७॥
transliteration: hantāramarisainyānāmamitragaṇamardanam ,duryodhanahite yuktamasmadyuddhāya codyatam.
english: He was the slayer of enemy armies, the crusher of enemy hosts, fully dedicated to Duryodhana's welfare, and ever ready for battle against us.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 46 Verse 7. He was the slayer of enemy armies, the crusher of enemy hosts, fully dedicated to Duryodhana's welfare, and ever ready for battle against us.

sanskrit: अप्रधृष्यं महायुद्धे देवैरपि सवासवैः ।अनलानिलयोस्तुल्यं तेजसा च बलेन च ॥८॥
transliteration: apradhṛṣyaṁ mahāyuddhe devairapi savāsavaiḥ ,analānilayostulyaṁ tejasā ca balena ca.
english: He was unassailable even by the gods with Indra in a great battle, and equal to fire and wind in both their fierce energy and immense power.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 46 Verse 8. He was unassailable even by the gods with Indra in a great battle, and equal to fire and wind in both their fierce energy and immense power.

sanskrit: पातालमिव गम्भीरं सुहृदानन्दवर्धनम् ।अन्तकाभममित्राणां कर्णं हत्वा महाहवे ।दिष्ट्या युवामनुप्राप्तौ जित्वासुरमिवामरौ ॥९॥
transliteration: pātālamiva gambhīraṁ suhṛdānandavardhanam ,antakābhamamitrāṇāṁ karṇaṁ hatvā mahāhave ,diṣṭyā yuvāmanuprāptau jitvāsuramivāmarau.
english: He was deep like the netherworld, the enhancer of his friends' joy, and a figure like Death (Antaka) for his foes. By good fortune, you two (Pāṇḍavas), like immortal deities who have vanquished a demon, have returned after slaying Karṇa in this great battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 46 Verse 9. He was deep like the netherworld, the enhancer of his friends' joy, and a figure like Death (Antaka) for his foes. By good fortune, you two (Pāṇḍavas), like immortal deities who have vanquished a demon, have returned after slaying Karṇa in this great battle.

sanskrit: तेन युद्धमदीनेन मया ह्यद्याच्युतार्जुनौ ।कुपितेनान्तकेनेव प्रजाः सर्वा जिघांसता ॥१०॥
transliteration: tena yuddhamadīnena mayā hyadyācyutārjunau ,kupitenāntakeneva prajāḥ sarvā jighāṁsatā.
english: Indeed, today, in my presence, that resolute Karna waged battle against Acyuta (Krishna) and Arjuna, like an enraged god of death (Antaka) who desires to destroy all creatures.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 46 Verse 10. Indeed, today, in my presence, that resolute Karna waged battle against Acyuta (Krishna) and Arjuna, like an enraged god of death (Antaka) who desires to destroy all creatures.

sanskrit: तेन केतुश्च मे छिन्नो हतौ च पार्ष्णिसारथी ।हतवाहः कृतश्चास्मि युयुधानस्य पश्यतः ॥११॥
transliteration: tena ketuśca me chinno hatau ca pārṣṇisārathī ,hatavāhaḥ kṛtaścāsmi yuyudhānasya paśyataḥ.
english: By him (Karna), my banner was cut down, and my charioteer and his protector (pārṣṇisārathī) were both killed. I was also rendered deprived of my vehicle (hatavāhaḥ), while Yuyudhana (Satyaki) was looking on.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 46 Verse 11. By him (Karna), my banner was cut down, and my charioteer and his protector (pārṣṇisārathī) were both killed. I was also rendered deprived of my vehicle (hatavāhaḥ), while Yuyudhana (Satyaki) was looking on.

sanskrit: धृष्टद्युम्नस्य यमयोर्वीरस्य च शिखण्डिनः ।पश्यतां द्रौपदेयानां पाञ्चालानां च सर्वशः ॥१२॥
transliteration: dhṛṣṭadyumnasya yamayorvīrasya ca śikhaṇḍinaḥ ,paśyatāṁ draupadeyānāṁ pāñcālānāṁ ca sarvaśaḥ.
english: While Dhṛṣṭadyumna, the twins (Nakula and Sahadeva), the hero Śikhaṇḍin, and all the sons of Draupadi and the Pāñcālas were looking on...
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 46 Verse 12. While Dhṛṣṭadyumna, the twins (Nakula and Sahadeva), the hero Śikhaṇḍin, and all the sons of Draupadi and the Pāñcālas were looking on...

sanskrit: एताञ्जित्वा महावीर्यान्कर्णः शत्रुगणान्बहून् ।जितवान्मां महाबाहो यतमानं महारणे ॥१३॥
transliteration: etāñjitvā mahāvīryānkarṇaḥ śatrugaṇānbahūn ,jitavānmāṁ mahābāho yatamānaṁ mahāraṇe.
english: O mighty-armed one (Arjuna)! Karna, having conquered these many highly valorous hosts of enemies, then conquered me, even as I was striving in the great battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 46 Verse 13. O mighty-armed one (Arjuna)! Karna, having conquered these many highly valorous hosts of enemies, then conquered me, even as I was striving in the great battle.

sanskrit: अनुसृत्य च मां युद्धे परुषाण्युक्तवान्बहु ।तत्र तत्र युधां श्रेष्ठः परिभूय न संशयः ॥१४॥
transliteration: anusṛtya ca māṁ yuddhe paruṣāṇyuktavānbahu ,tatra tatra yudhāṁ śreṣṭhaḥ paribhūya na saṁśayaḥ.
english: And pursuing me in battle, he spoke many harsh words. There is no doubt that the foremost of warriors (Bhima) repeatedly humiliated me in various encounters.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 46 Verse 14. And pursuing me in battle, he spoke many harsh words. There is no doubt that the foremost of warriors (Bhima) repeatedly humiliated me in various encounters.

sanskrit: भीमसेनप्रभावात्तु यज्जीवामि धनंजय ।बहुनात्र किमुक्तेन नाहं तत्सोढुमुत्सहे ॥१५॥
transliteration: bhīmasenaprabhāvāttu yajjīvāmi dhanaṁjaya ,bahunātra kimuktena nāhaṁ tatsoḍhumutsahe.
english: O Dhananjaya (Arjuna), I survive only by the power of Bhimasena. What more needs to be said on this matter? I am truly unable to endure that (suffering).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 46 Verse 15. O Dhananjaya (Arjuna), I survive only by the power of Bhimasena. What more needs to be said on this matter? I am truly unable to endure that (suffering).

sanskrit: त्रयोदशाहं वर्षाणि यस्माद्भीतो धनंजय ।न स्म निद्रां लभे रात्रौ न चाहनि सुखं क्वचित् ॥१६॥
transliteration: trayodaśāhaṁ varṣāṇi yasmādbhīto dhanaṁjaya ,na sma nidrāṁ labhe rātrau na cāhani sukhaṁ kvacit.
english: O Dhananjaya (Arjuna), for thirteen years, I, fearful of him (Bhima), could not find sleep at night, nor any comfort during the day.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 46 Verse 16. O Dhananjaya (Arjuna), for thirteen years, I, fearful of him (Bhima), could not find sleep at night, nor any comfort during the day.

sanskrit: तस्य द्वेषेण संयुक्तः परिदह्ये धनंजय ।आत्मनो मरणं जानन्वाध्रीणस इव द्विपः ॥१७॥
transliteration: tasya dveṣeṇa saṁyuktaḥ paridahye dhanaṁjaya ,ātmano maraṇaṁ jānanvādhrīṇasa iva dvipaḥ.
english: O Dhananjaya (Arjuna), filled with hatred for him (Bhima), I burn within, knowing the death of my own self (ātman), just like a bound elephant (vādhīṇasa) knowing its end.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 46 Verse 17. O Dhananjaya (Arjuna), filled with hatred for him (Bhima), I burn within, knowing the death of my own self (ātman), just like a bound elephant (vādhīṇasa) knowing its end.

sanskrit: यस्यायमगमत्कालश्चिन्तयानस्य मे विभो ।कथं शक्यो मया कर्णो युद्धे क्षपयितुं भवेत् ॥१८॥
transliteration: yasyāyamagamatkālaścintayānasya me vibho ,kathaṁ śakyo mayā karṇo yuddhe kṣapayituṁ bhavet.
english: O mighty one, all this time has passed while I was contemplating, 'How can Karna possibly be vanquished by me in battle?'
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 46 Verse 18. O mighty one, all this time has passed while I was contemplating, 'How can Karna possibly be vanquished by me in battle?'

sanskrit: जाग्रत्स्वपंश्च कौन्तेय कर्णमेव सदा ह्यहम् ।पश्यामि तत्र तत्रैव कर्णभूतमिदं जगत् ॥१९॥
transliteration: jāgratsvapaṁśca kaunteya karṇameva sadā hyaham ,paśyāmi tatra tatraiva karṇabhūtamidaṁ jagat.
english: O son of Kunti, whether I am awake or asleep, I always see only Karna. Indeed, I perceive this entire world, in every place, as having become Karna.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 46 Verse 19. O son of Kunti, whether I am awake or asleep, I always see only Karna. Indeed, I perceive this entire world, in every place, as having become Karna.

sanskrit: यत्र यत्र हि गच्छामि कर्णाद्भीतो धनंजय ।तत्र तत्र हि पश्यामि कर्णमेवाग्रतः स्थितम् ॥२०॥
transliteration: yatra yatra hi gacchāmi karṇādbhīto dhanaṁjaya ,tatra tatra hi paśyāmi karṇamevāgrataḥ sthitam.
english: O Dhananjaya, wherever I go, fearful of Karna, there I always perceive Karna himself standing right in front of me.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 46 Verse 20. O Dhananjaya, wherever I go, fearful of Karna, there I always perceive Karna himself standing right in front of me.

sanskrit: सोऽहं तेनैव वीरेण समरेष्वपलायिना ।सहयः सरथः पार्थ जित्वा जीवन्विसर्जितः ॥२१॥
transliteration: so'haṁ tenaiva vīreṇa samareṣvapalāyinā ,sahayaḥ sarathaḥ pārtha jitvā jīvanvisarjitaḥ.
english: O son of Pritha, I, this very person, with my horses and chariot, having conquered, was released alive by that very hero (Karna) who never retreats in battles.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 46 Verse 21. O son of Pritha, I, this very person, with my horses and chariot, having conquered, was released alive by that very hero (Karna) who never retreats in battles.

sanskrit: को नु मे जीवितेनार्थो राज्येनार्थोऽथ वा पुनः ।ममैवं धिक्कृतस्येह कर्णेनाहवशोभिना ॥२२॥
transliteration: ko nu me jīvitenārtho rājyenārtho'tha vā punaḥ ,mamaivaṁ dhikkṛtasyeha karṇenāhavaśobhinā.
english: What purpose do I have in life, or what use is there in a kingdom, for me who has been thus disgraced here by Karna, who shines gloriously in battle?
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 46 Verse 22. What purpose do I have in life, or what use is there in a kingdom, for me who has been thus disgraced here by Karna, who shines gloriously in battle?

sanskrit: न प्राप्तपूर्वं यद्भीष्मात्कृपाद्द्रोणाच्च संयुगे ।तत्प्राप्तमद्य मे युद्धे सूतपुत्रान्महारथात् ॥२३॥
transliteration: na prāptapūrvaṁ yadbhīṣmātkṛpāddroṇācca saṁyuge ,tatprāptamadya me yuddhe sūtaputrānmahārathāt.
english: That which I had not previously obtained from Bhishma, Kripa, and Drona in battle, that very thing I have now obtained in battle from Karna, the son of a charioteer and a great warrior.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 46 Verse 23. That which I had not previously obtained from Bhishma, Kripa, and Drona in battle, that very thing I have now obtained in battle from Karna, the son of a charioteer and a great warrior.

sanskrit: तत्त्वा पृच्छामि कौन्तेय यथा ह्यकुशलस्तथा ।तन्ममाचक्ष्व कार्त्स्न्येन यथा कर्णस्त्वया हतः ॥२४॥
transliteration: tattvā pṛcchāmi kaunteya yathā hyakuśalastathā ,tanmamācakṣva kārtsnyena yathā karṇastvayā hataḥ.
english: Therefore, O son of Kunti, I ask you, as if I were truly ignorant or unskilled, explain that to me completely: how was Karna slain by you?
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 46 Verse 24. Therefore, O son of Kunti, I ask you, as if I were truly ignorant or unskilled, explain that to me completely: how was Karna slain by you?

sanskrit: शक्रवीर्यसमो युद्धे यमतुल्यपराक्रमः ।रामतुल्यस्तथास्त्रे यः स कथं वै निषूदितः ॥२५॥
transliteration: śakravīryasamo yuddhe yamatulyaparākramaḥ ,rāmatulyastathāstre yaḥ sa kathaṁ vai niṣūditaḥ.
english: He who was equal to Indra in valor during battle, whose prowess matched Yama's, and who was like Rama in his skill with weapons (astra), how indeed was such a one slain?
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 46 Verse 25. He who was equal to Indra in valor during battle, whose prowess matched Yama's, and who was like Rama in his skill with weapons (astra), how indeed was such a one slain?

sanskrit: महारथः समाख्यातः सर्वयुद्धविशारदः ।धनुर्धराणां प्रवरः सर्वेषामेकपूरुषः ॥२६॥
transliteration: mahārathaḥ samākhyātaḥ sarvayuddhaviśāradaḥ ,dhanurdharāṇāṁ pravaraḥ sarveṣāmekapūruṣaḥ.
english: He was known as a great warrior, an expert in all forms of combat. Among all archers, he was considered the foremost, a unique individual (puruṣa).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 46 Verse 26. He was known as a great warrior, an expert in all forms of combat. Among all archers, he was considered the foremost, a unique individual (puruṣa).

sanskrit: पूजितो धृतराष्ट्रेण सपुत्रेण विशां पते ।सदा त्वदर्थं राधेयः स कथं निहतस्त्वया ॥२७॥
transliteration: pūjito dhṛtarāṣṭreṇa saputreṇa viśāṁ pate ,sadā tvadarthaṁ rādheyaḥ sa kathaṁ nihatastvayā.
english: O lord of men (viśāṃ pate), he (Radheya) was honored by Dhritarashtra along with his son, and he was always your chief adversary. How then was he killed by you?
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 46 Verse 27. O lord of men (viśāṃ pate), he (Radheya) was honored by Dhritarashtra along with his son, and he was always your chief adversary. How then was he killed by you?

sanskrit: धृतराष्ट्रो हि योधेषु सर्वेष्वेव सदार्जुन ।तव मृत्युं रणे कर्णं मन्यते पुरुषर्षभः ॥२८॥
transliteration: dhṛtarāṣṭro hi yodheṣu sarveṣveva sadārjuna ,tava mṛtyuṁ raṇe karṇaṁ manyate puruṣarṣabhaḥ.
english: Indeed, O Arjuna, among all warriors, Dhritarashtra always considered Karna to be the cause of your death in battle, O best of men (puruṣa).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 46 Verse 28. Indeed, O Arjuna, among all warriors, Dhritarashtra always considered Karna to be the cause of your death in battle, O best of men (puruṣa).

sanskrit: स त्वया पुरुषव्याघ्र कथं युद्धे निषूदितः ।तं ममाचक्ष्व बीभत्सो यथा कर्णो हतस्त्वया ॥२९॥
transliteration: sa tvayā puruṣavyāghra kathaṁ yuddhe niṣūditaḥ ,taṁ mamācakṣva bībhatso yathā karṇo hatastvayā.
english: O tiger among men (puruṣa), how was he (Karna) killed by you in battle? O Bibhatsu, tell me exactly how Karna was slain by you.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 46 Verse 29. O tiger among men (puruṣa), how was he (Karna) killed by you in battle? O Bibhatsu, tell me exactly how Karna was slain by you.

sanskrit: सोत्सेधमस्य च शिरः पश्यतां सुहृदां हृतम् ।त्वया पुरुषशार्दूल शार्दूलेन यथा रुरोः ॥३०॥
transliteration: sotsedhamasya ca śiraḥ paśyatāṁ suhṛdāṁ hṛtam ,tvayā puruṣaśārdūla śārdūlena yathā ruroḥ.
english: O tiger among men, his swollen head was severed by you, just as a tiger carries away the head of a deer, while his friends looked on.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 46 Verse 30. O tiger among men, his swollen head was severed by you, just as a tiger carries away the head of a deer, while his friends looked on.

sanskrit: यः पर्युपासीत्प्रदिशो दिशश्च त्वां सूतपुत्रः समरे परीप्सन् ।दित्सुः कर्णः समरे हस्तिपूगं स हीदानीं कङ्कपत्रैः सुतीक्ष्णैः ॥३१॥
transliteration: yaḥ paryupāsītpradiśo diśaśca; tvāṁ sūtaputraḥ samare parīpsan ,ditsuḥ karṇaḥ samare hastipūgaṁ; sa hīdānīṁ kaṅkapatraiḥ sutīkṣṇaiḥ.
english: The son of a charioteer (sūtaputra), Karna, who, desiring to attack you in battle and eager to overwhelm hosts of elephants, attended you in all directions and intermediate quarters – he now [lies pierced] by very sharp, vulture-feathered [arrows].
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 46 Verse 31. The son of a charioteer (sūtaputra), Karna, who, desiring to attack you in battle and eager to overwhelm hosts of elephants, attended you in all directions and intermediate quarters – he now [lies pierced] by very sharp, vulture-feathered [arrows].

sanskrit: त्वया रणे निहतः सूतपुत्रः कच्चिच्छेते भूमितले दुरात्मा ।कच्चित्प्रियं मे परमं त्वयाद्य कृतं रणे सूतपुत्रं निहत्य ॥३२॥
transliteration: tvayā raṇe nihataḥ sūtaputraḥ; kaccicchete bhūmitale durātmā ,kaccitpriyaṁ me paramaṁ tvayādya; kṛtaṁ raṇe sūtaputraṁ nihatya.
english: Hopefully, that wicked-souled son of a charioteer (sūtaputra), slain by you in battle, now lies on the ground? And hopefully, today, by slaying the son of a charioteer (sūtaputra) in battle, you have accomplished what is supremely dear to me.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 46 Verse 32. Hopefully, that wicked-souled son of a charioteer (sūtaputra), slain by you in battle, now lies on the ground? And hopefully, today, by slaying the son of a charioteer (sūtaputra) in battle, you have accomplished what is supremely dear to me.

sanskrit: यः सर्वतः पर्यपतत्त्वदर्थे मदान्वितो गर्वितः सूतपुत्रः ।स शूरमानी समरे समेत्य कच्चित्त्वया निहतः संयुगेऽद्य ॥३३॥
transliteration: yaḥ sarvataḥ paryapatattvadarthe; madānvito garvitaḥ sūtaputraḥ ,sa śūramānī samare sametya; kaccittvayā nihataḥ saṁyuge'dya.
english: Hopefully, that son of a charioteer (sūtaputra), who, filled with arrogance and pride, moved everywhere for your sake, and who considered himself a hero, having confronted you in battle, has he been slain by you today in combat?
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 46 Verse 33. Hopefully, that son of a charioteer (sūtaputra), who, filled with arrogance and pride, moved everywhere for your sake, and who considered himself a hero, having confronted you in battle, has he been slain by you today in combat?

sanskrit: रौक्मं रथं हस्तिवरैश्च युक्तं रथं दित्सुर्यः परेभ्यस्त्वदर्थे ।सदा रणे स्पर्धते यः स पापः कच्चित्त्वया निहतस्तात युद्धे ॥३४॥
transliteration: raukmaṁ rathaṁ hastivaraiśca yuktaṁ; rathaṁ ditsuryaḥ parebhyastvadarthe ,sadā raṇe spardhate yaḥ sa pāpaḥ; kaccittvayā nihatastāta yuddhe.
english: O dear one, I hope that sinner has been killed by you in battle - the one who, on your account, always contends in combat, eagerly wishing to give golden chariots and chariots equipped with excellent elephants to others.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 46 Verse 34. O dear one, I hope that sinner has been killed by you in battle - the one who, on your account, always contends in combat, eagerly wishing to give golden chariots and chariots equipped with excellent elephants to others.

sanskrit: योऽसौ नित्यं शूरमदेन मत्तो विकत्थते संसदि कौरवाणाम् ।प्रियोऽत्यर्थं तस्य सुयोधनस्य कच्चित्स पापो निहतस्त्वयाद्य ॥३५॥
transliteration: yo'sau nityaṁ śūramadena matto; vikatthate saṁsadi kauravāṇām ,priyo'tyarthaṁ tasya suyodhanasya; kaccitsa pāpo nihatastvayādya.
english: I hope, by you today, that sinner has been slain - the one who, intoxicated by the arrogance of a warrior, always boasts in the assembly of the Kauravas and is exceedingly dear to Suyodhana.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 46 Verse 35. I hope, by you today, that sinner has been slain - the one who, intoxicated by the arrogance of a warrior, always boasts in the assembly of the Kauravas and is exceedingly dear to Suyodhana.

sanskrit: कच्चित्समागम्य धनुःप्रमुक्तैस्त्वत्प्रेषितैर्लोहितार्थैर्विहंगैः ।शेतेऽद्य पापः स विभिन्नगात्रः कच्चिद्भग्नो धार्तराष्ट्रस्य बाहुः ॥३६॥
transliteration: kaccitsamāgamya dhanuḥpramuktai;stvatpreṣitairlohitārthairvihaṁgaiḥ ,śete'dya pāpaḥ sa vibhinnagātraḥ; kaccidbhagno dhārtarāṣṭrasya bāhuḥ.
english: I hope that sinner, with his body shattered, lies dead today, having been struck by blood-thirsty 'birds' (arrows) released from your bow and dispatched by you. I also hope the arm of Dhritarashtra's son has been broken.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 46 Verse 36. I hope that sinner, with his body shattered, lies dead today, having been struck by blood-thirsty 'birds' (arrows) released from your bow and dispatched by you. I also hope the arm of Dhritarashtra's son has been broken.

sanskrit: योऽसौ सदा श्लाघते राजमध्ये दुर्योधनं हर्षयन्दर्पपूर्णः ।अहं हन्ता फल्गुनस्येति मोहात्कच्चिद्धतस्तस्य न वै तथा रथः ॥३७॥
transliteration: yo'sau sadā ślāghate rājamadhye; duryodhanaṁ harṣayandarpapūrṇaḥ ,ahaṁ hantā phalgunasyeti mohā;tkacciddhatastasya na vai tathā rathaḥ.
english: I hope that arrogant individual, who constantly boasts among kings, delighting Duryodhana by proclaiming out of delusion, 'I am Arjuna's killer!', has been slain. Surely his chariot, too, has not been spared in that manner?
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 46 Verse 37. I hope that arrogant individual, who constantly boasts among kings, delighting Duryodhana by proclaiming out of delusion, 'I am Arjuna's killer!', has been slain. Surely his chariot, too, has not been spared in that manner?

sanskrit: नाहं पादौ धावयिष्ये कदाचिद्यावत्स्थितः पार्थ इत्यल्पबुद्धिः ।व्रतं तस्यैतत्सर्वदा शक्रसूनो कच्चित्त्वया निहतः सोऽद्य कर्णः ॥३८॥
transliteration: nāhaṁ pādau dhāvayiṣye kadāci;dyāvatsthitaḥ pārtha ityalpabuddhiḥ ,vrataṁ tasyaitatsarvadā śakrasūno; kaccittvayā nihataḥ so'dya karṇaḥ.
english: O son of Indra (Arjuna), I hope that Karṇa, that foolish one, has been killed by you today, he who constantly swore: "I will never wash my feet as long as Pārtha is alive!".
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 46 Verse 38. O son of Indra (Arjuna), I hope that Karṇa, that foolish one, has been killed by you today, he who constantly swore: "I will never wash my feet as long as Pārtha is alive!".

sanskrit: योऽसौ कृष्णामब्रवीद्दुष्टबुद्धिः कर्णः सभायां कुरुवीरमध्ये ।किं पाण्डवांस्त्वं न जहासि कृष्णे सुदुर्बलान्पतितान्हीनसत्त्वान् ॥३९॥
transliteration: yo'sau kṛṣṇāmabravīdduṣṭabuddhiḥ; karṇaḥ sabhāyāṁ kuruvīramadhye ,kiṁ pāṇḍavāṁstvaṁ na jahāsi kṛṣṇe; sudurbalānpatitānhīnasattvān.
english: That evil-minded Karṇa, who addressed Kṛṣṇā (Draupadi) in the assembly amidst the Kuru heroes, saying: "O Kṛṣṇā, why do you not abandon these Pāṇḍavas, who are extremely weak, fallen, and devoid of courage?"
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 46 Verse 39. That evil-minded Karṇa, who addressed Kṛṣṇā (Draupadi) in the assembly amidst the Kuru heroes, saying: "O Kṛṣṇā, why do you not abandon these Pāṇḍavas, who are extremely weak, fallen, and devoid of courage?"

sanskrit: यत्तत्कर्णः प्रत्यजानात्त्वदर्थे नाहत्वाहं सह कृष्णेन पार्थम् ।इहोपयातेति स पापबुद्धिः कच्चिच्छेते शरसंभिन्नगात्रः ॥४०॥
transliteration: yattatkarṇaḥ pratyajānāttvadarthe; nāhatvāhaṁ saha kṛṣṇena pārtham ,ihopayāteti sa pāpabuddhiḥ; kaccicchete śarasaṁbhinnagātraḥ.
english: That which Karṇa vowed concerning you (Draupadi): "I will not approach here unless I have slain Pārtha along with Kṛṣṇa." I hope that evil-minded one, his body pierced by arrows, now lies (dead).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 46 Verse 40. That which Karṇa vowed concerning you (Draupadi): "I will not approach here unless I have slain Pārtha along with Kṛṣṇa." I hope that evil-minded one, his body pierced by arrows, now lies (dead).

sanskrit: कच्चित्संग्रामे विदितो वा तदायं समागमः सृञ्जयकौरवाणाम् ।यत्रावस्थामीदृशीं प्रापितोऽहं कच्चित्त्वया सोऽद्य हतः समेत्य ॥४१॥
transliteration: kaccitsaṁgrāme vidito vā tadāyaṁ; samāgamaḥ sṛñjayakauravāṇām ,yatrāvasthāmīdṛśīṁ prāpito'haṁ; kaccittvayā so'dya hataḥ sametya.
english: I wonder, was the true nature of this gathering of Sṛñjayas and Kurus, which led me to such a deplorable state, understood in battle at that time? I hope that today, having confronted him, Karṇa has been slain by you.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 46 Verse 41. I wonder, was the true nature of this gathering of Sṛñjayas and Kurus, which led me to such a deplorable state, understood in battle at that time? I hope that today, having confronted him, Karṇa has been slain by you.

sanskrit: कच्चित्त्वया तस्य सुमन्दबुद्धेर्गाण्डीवमुक्तैर्विशिखैर्ज्वलद्भिः ।सकुण्डलं भानुमदुत्तमाङ्गं कायात्प्रकृत्तं युधि सव्यसाचिन् ॥४२॥
transliteration: kaccittvayā tasya sumandabuddhe;rgāṇḍīvamuktairviśikhairjvaladbhiḥ ,sakuṇḍalaṁ bhānumaduttamāṅgaṁ; kāyātprakṛttaṁ yudhi savyasācin.
english: O Savyasācin (Arjuna), I hope that in battle you have severed from the body of that extremely dull-witted man his brilliant head, adorned with earrings, by means of the blazing arrows released from your Gaṇḍīva bow.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 46 Verse 42. O Savyasācin (Arjuna), I hope that in battle you have severed from the body of that extremely dull-witted man his brilliant head, adorned with earrings, by means of the blazing arrows released from your Gaṇḍīva bow.

sanskrit: यत्तन्मया बाणसमर्पितेन ध्यातोऽसि कर्णस्य वधाय वीर ।तन्मे त्वया कच्चिदमोघमद्य ध्यातं कृतं कर्णनिपातनेन ॥४३॥
transliteration: yattanmayā bāṇasamarpitena; dhyāto'si karṇasya vadhāya vīra ,tanme tvayā kaccidamoghamadya; dhyātaṁ kṛtaṁ karṇanipātanena.
english: O hero (Arjuna), I hope that the profound meditation (dhyāna) I performed, focused on you and the offering of arrows for Karṇa's destruction, has today been fulfilled by you through the slaying of Karṇa, thus proving it unfailing.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 46 Verse 43. O hero (Arjuna), I hope that the profound meditation (dhyāna) I performed, focused on you and the offering of arrows for Karṇa's destruction, has today been fulfilled by you through the slaying of Karṇa, thus proving it unfailing.

sanskrit: यद्दर्पपूर्णः स सुयोधनोऽस्मानवेक्षते कर्णसमाश्रयेण ।कच्चित्त्वया सोऽद्य समाश्रयोऽस्य भग्नः पराक्रम्य सुयोधनस्य ॥४४॥
transliteration: yaddarpapūrṇaḥ sa suyodhano'smā;navekṣate karṇasamāśrayeṇa ,kaccittvayā so'dya samāśrayo'sya; bhagnaḥ parākramya suyodhanasya.
english: Because that Suyodhana, full of pride, looks down upon us relying on Karṇa, I hope that today you have valiantly shattered that foundation of Suyodhana's confidence.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 46 Verse 44. Because that Suyodhana, full of pride, looks down upon us relying on Karṇa, I hope that today you have valiantly shattered that foundation of Suyodhana's confidence.

sanskrit: यो नः पुरा षण्ढतिलानवोचत्सभामध्ये पार्थिवानां समक्षम् ।स दुर्मतिः कच्चिदुपेत्य संख्ये त्वया हतः सूतपुत्रोऽत्यमर्षी ॥४५॥
transliteration: yo naḥ purā ṣaṇḍhatilānavoca;tsabhāmadhye pārthivānāṁ samakṣam ,sa durmatiḥ kaccidupetya saṁkhye; tvayā hataḥ sūtaputro'tyamarṣī.
english: I hope that the wicked Karṇa, that extremely arrogant son of a charioteer, who formerly, in the assembly and in the presence of kings, called us 'eunuch-sesame seeds' (implying uselessness), has been killed by you today, having faced him in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 46 Verse 45. I hope that the wicked Karṇa, that extremely arrogant son of a charioteer, who formerly, in the assembly and in the presence of kings, called us 'eunuch-sesame seeds' (implying uselessness), has been killed by you today, having faced him in battle.

sanskrit: यः सूतपुत्रः प्रहसन्दुरात्मा पुराब्रवीन्निर्जितां सौबलेन ।स्वयं प्रसह्यानय याज्ञसेनीमपीह कच्चित्स हतस्त्वयाद्य ॥४६॥
transliteration: yaḥ sūtaputraḥ prahasandurātmā; purābravīnnirjitāṁ saubalena ,svayaṁ prasahyānaya yājñasenī;mapīha kaccitsa hatastvayādya.
english: Has that evil-minded son of a charioteer (sūtaputra), who formerly laughed and declared, 'She has been won by the son of Subala; bring Yājñasenī by force yourself!' - has he been killed by you here today?
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 46 Verse 46. Has that evil-minded son of a charioteer (sūtaputra), who formerly laughed and declared, 'She has been won by the son of Subala; bring Yājñasenī by force yourself!' - has he been killed by you here today?

sanskrit: यः शस्त्रभृच्छ्रेष्ठतमं पृथिव्यां पितामहं व्याक्षिपदल्पचेताः ।संख्यायमानोऽर्धरथः स कच्चित्त्वया हतोऽद्याधिरथिर्दुरात्मा ॥४७॥
transliteration: yaḥ śastrabhṛcchreṣṭhatamaṁ pṛthivyāṁ; pitāmahaṁ vyākṣipadalpacetāḥ ,saṁkhyāyamāno'rdharathaḥ sa kacci;ttvayā hato'dyādhirathirdurātmā.
english: Has that evil-minded charioteer's son (adhirathi), who, with his meager intellect (alpacetas), insulted the grandsire (Bhīṣma), the most excellent of all weapon-bearers on earth, and who was himself accounted merely a 'half-chariot warrior' (ardharatha) - has he been killed by you today?
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 46 Verse 47. Has that evil-minded charioteer's son (adhirathi), who, with his meager intellect (alpacetas), insulted the grandsire (Bhīṣma), the most excellent of all weapon-bearers on earth, and who was himself accounted merely a 'half-chariot warrior' (ardharatha) - has he been killed by you today?

sanskrit: अमर्षणं निकृतिसमीरणेरितं हृदि श्रितं ज्वलनमिमं सदा मम ।हतो मया सोऽद्य समेत्य पापधीरिति ब्रुवन्प्रशमय मेऽद्य फल्गुन ॥४८॥
transliteration: amarṣaṇaṁ nikṛtisamīraṇeritaṁ; hṛdi śritaṁ jvalanamimaṁ sadā mama ,hato mayā so'dya sametya pāpadhī;riti bruvanpraśamaya me'dya phalguna.
english: O Phalguna (Arjuna), please quell for me today this unbearable fire (jvalana) that always dwells in my heart, agitated by the wind of deceit (nikṛti-samīraṇa), by saying, 'That evil-minded (pāpadhī) fellow has been killed by me today!'
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 46 Verse 48. O Phalguna (Arjuna), please quell for me today this unbearable fire (jvalana) that always dwells in my heart, agitated by the wind of deceit (nikṛti-samīraṇa), by saying, 'That evil-minded (pāpadhī) fellow has been killed by me today!'

sanskrit: संजय उवाच ।तद्धर्मशीलस्य वचो निशम्य राज्ञः क्रुद्धस्याधिरथौ महात्मा ।उवाच दुर्धर्षमदीनसत्त्वं युधिष्ठिरं जिष्णुरनन्तवीर्यः ॥१॥
transliteration: saṁjaya uvāca ,taddharmaśīlasya vaco niśamya; rājñaḥ kruddhasyādhirathau mahātmā ,uvāca durdharṣamadīnasattvaṁ; yudhiṣṭhiraṁ jiṣṇuranantavīryaḥ.
english: Sañjaya said: Hearing that speech of the king, who was steadfast in righteousness (dharma-śīla) and enraged at the charioteer's son (adhirathi), the great-souled (mahātmā) Jishnu (Arjuna), whose valor (vīrya) is infinite, spoke to Yudhishthira, who was formidable and possessed of unwavering courage (adīnasattva).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 47 Verse 1. Sañjaya said: Hearing that speech of the king, who was steadfast in righteousness (dharma-śīla) and enraged at the charioteer's son (adhirathi), the great-souled (mahātmā) Jishnu (Arjuna), whose valor (vīrya) is infinite, spoke to Yudhishthira, who was formidable and possessed of unwavering courage (adīnasattva).

sanskrit: संशप्तकैर्युध्यमानस्य मेऽद्य सेनाग्रयायी कुरुसैन्यस्य राजन् ।आशीविषाभान्खगमान्प्रमुञ्चन्द्रौणिः पुरस्तात्सहसा व्यतिष्ठत् ॥२॥
transliteration: saṁśaptakairyudhyamānasya me'dya; senāgrayāyī kurusainyasya rājan ,āśīviṣābhānkhagamānpramuñca;ndrauṇiḥ purastātsahasā vyatiṣṭhat.
english: O King, today, while I was fighting with the Samśaptakas, Droṇi (Aśvatthāmā), the leader of the Kuru army, suddenly appeared before me, releasing arrows that resembled venomous serpents.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 47 Verse 2. O King, today, while I was fighting with the Samśaptakas, Droṇi (Aśvatthāmā), the leader of the Kuru army, suddenly appeared before me, releasing arrows that resembled venomous serpents.

sanskrit: दृष्ट्वा रथं मेघनिभं ममेममम्बष्ठसेना मरणे व्यतिष्ठत् ।तेषामहं पञ्च शतानि हत्वा ततो द्रौणिमगमं पार्थिवाग्र्य ॥३॥
transliteration: dṛṣṭvā rathaṁ meghanibhaṁ mamema;mambaṣṭhasenā maraṇe vyatiṣṭhat ,teṣāmahaṁ pañca śatāni hatvā; tato drauṇimagamaṁ pārthivāgrya.
english: O foremost of kings, upon seeing this cloud-like chariot of mine, the Ambashṭha army positioned itself for a fight to the death. Having then slain five hundred of their warriors, I proceeded towards Droṇi.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 47 Verse 3. O foremost of kings, upon seeing this cloud-like chariot of mine, the Ambashṭha army positioned itself for a fight to the death. Having then slain five hundred of their warriors, I proceeded towards Droṇi.

sanskrit: ततोऽपरान्बाणसंघाननेकानाकर्णपूर्णायतविप्रमुक्तान् ।ससर्ज शिक्षास्त्रबलप्रयत्नैस्तथा यथा प्रावृषि कालमेघः ॥४॥
transliteration: tato'parānbāṇasaṁghānanekā;nākarṇapūrṇāyatavipramuktān ,sasarja śikṣāstrabalaprayatnai;stathā yathā prāvṛṣi kālameghaḥ.
english: Then, with efforts born of his training, skill, and power in weaponry, he discharged many other multitudes of arrows, which were released from the bowstring fully drawn up to the ear, just as a dark cloud discharges rain in the monsoon season.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 47 Verse 4. Then, with efforts born of his training, skill, and power in weaponry, he discharged many other multitudes of arrows, which were released from the bowstring fully drawn up to the ear, just as a dark cloud discharges rain in the monsoon season.

sanskrit: नैवाददानं न च संदधानं जानीमहे कतरेणास्यतीति ।वामेन वा यदि वा दक्षिणेन स द्रोणपुत्रः समरे पर्यवर्तत् ॥५॥
transliteration: naivādadānaṁ na ca saṁdadhānaṁ; jānīmahe katareṇāsyatīti ,vāmena vā yadi vā dakṣiṇena; sa droṇaputraḥ samare paryavartat.
english: We could not discern when he was taking up an arrow or when he was fixing it to the string, nor even with which hand he was shooting – whether with his left or with his right. So swiftly did that son of Droṇa (Aśvatthāmā) move about in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 47 Verse 5. We could not discern when he was taking up an arrow or when he was fixing it to the string, nor even with which hand he was shooting – whether with his left or with his right. So swiftly did that son of Droṇa (Aśvatthāmā) move about in battle.

sanskrit: अविध्यन्मां पञ्चभिर्द्रोणपुत्रः शितैः शरैः पञ्चभिर्वासुदेवम् ।अहं तु तं त्रिंशता वज्रकल्पैः समार्दयं निमिषस्यान्तरेण ॥६॥
transliteration: avidhyanmāṁ pañcabhirdroṇaputraḥ; śitaiḥ śaraiḥ pañcabhirvāsudevam ,ahaṁ tu taṁ triṁśatā vajrakalpaiḥ; samārdayaṁ nimiṣasyāntareṇa.
english: Drona's son (Aśvatthāman) pierced me with five sharp arrows and Vasudeva (Kṛṣṇa) with five (arrows). But I, within a moment, struck him (Aśvatthāman) with thirty arrows resembling thunderbolts.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 47 Verse 6. Drona's son (Aśvatthāman) pierced me with five sharp arrows and Vasudeva (Kṛṣṇa) with five (arrows). But I, within a moment, struck him (Aśvatthāman) with thirty arrows resembling thunderbolts.

sanskrit: स विक्षरन्रुधिरं सर्वगात्रै रथानीकं सूतसूनोर्विवेश ।मयाभिभूतः सैनिकानां प्रबर्हानसावपश्यन्रुधिरेण प्रदिग्धान् ॥७॥
transliteration: sa vikṣaranrudhiraṁ sarvagātrai; rathānīkaṁ sūtasūnorviveśa ,mayābhibhūtaḥ sainikānāṁ prabarhā;nasāvapaśyanrudhireṇa pradigdhān.
english: With blood oozing from all his limbs, he (Aśvatthāman) entered the chariot division of Karna. Overwhelmed by me, he (Aśvatthāman), seeing the best of the soldiers smeared with blood,
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 47 Verse 7. With blood oozing from all his limbs, he (Aśvatthāman) entered the chariot division of Karna. Overwhelmed by me, he (Aśvatthāman), seeing the best of the soldiers smeared with blood,

sanskrit: ततोऽभिभूतं युधि वीक्ष्य सैन्यं विध्वस्तयोधं द्रुतवाजिनागम् ।पञ्चाशता रथमुख्यैः समेतः कर्णस्त्वरन्मामुपायात्प्रमाथी ॥८॥
transliteration: tato'bhibhūtaṁ yudhi vīkṣya sainyaṁ; vidhvastayodhaṁ drutavājināgam ,pañcāśatā rathamukhyaiḥ sametaḥ; karṇastvaranmāmupāyātpramāthī.
english: Then, Karna, the destructive one, having seen the army overwhelmed in battle, its warriors destroyed, and its horses and elephants put to flight, hastened and approached me, accompanied by fifty chief charioteers.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 47 Verse 8. Then, Karna, the destructive one, having seen the army overwhelmed in battle, its warriors destroyed, and its horses and elephants put to flight, hastened and approached me, accompanied by fifty chief charioteers.

sanskrit: तान्सूदयित्वाहमपास्य कर्णं द्रष्टुं भवन्तं त्वरयाभियातः ।सर्वे पाञ्चाला ह्युद्विजन्ते स्म कर्णाद्गन्धाद्गावः केसरिणो यथैव ॥९॥
transliteration: tānsūdayitvāhamapāsya karṇaṁ; draṣṭuṁ bhavantaṁ tvarayābhiyātaḥ ,sarve pāñcālā hyudvijante sma karṇā;dgandhādgāvaḥ kesariṇo yathaiva.
english: Having destroyed those (fifty charioteers) and pushed Karna aside, I quickly came to see you. Indeed, all the Panchalas used to tremble from Karna, just as cows (tremble) from the scent of a lion.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 47 Verse 9. Having destroyed those (fifty charioteers) and pushed Karna aside, I quickly came to see you. Indeed, all the Panchalas used to tremble from Karna, just as cows (tremble) from the scent of a lion.

sanskrit: महाझषस्येव मुखं प्रपन्नाः प्रभद्रकाः कर्णमभि द्रवन्ति ।मृत्योरास्यं व्यात्तमिवान्वपद्यन्प्रभद्रकाः कर्णमासाद्य राजन् ॥१०॥
transliteration: mahājhaṣasyeva mukhaṁ prapannāḥ; prabhadrakāḥ karṇamabhi dravanti ,mṛtyorāsyaṁ vyāttamivānvapadya;nprabhadrakāḥ karṇamāsādya rājan.
english: O King, the excellent warriors (prabhadrakāḥ), having reached Karṇa, rush towards him as if they have entered the mouth of a great fish, or fallen into Death's wide-open maw.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 47 Verse 10. O King, the excellent warriors (prabhadrakāḥ), having reached Karṇa, rush towards him as if they have entered the mouth of a great fish, or fallen into Death's wide-open maw.

sanskrit: आयाहि पश्याद्य युयुत्समानं मां सूतपुत्रं च वृतौ जयाय ।षट्साहस्रा भारत राजपुत्राः स्वर्गाय लोकाय रथा निमग्नाः ॥११॥
transliteration: āyāhi paśyādya yuyutsamānaṁ; māṁ sūtaputraṁ ca vṛtau jayāya ,ṣaṭsāhasrā bhārata rājaputrāḥ; svargāya lokāya rathā nimagnāḥ.
english: O Bhārata, come and see today me and the son of Sūta (Karṇa) fighting for victory in this conflict. Six thousand princes, with their chariots destroyed, are now destined for the heavenly world (svarga).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 47 Verse 11. O Bhārata, come and see today me and the son of Sūta (Karṇa) fighting for victory in this conflict. Six thousand princes, with their chariots destroyed, are now destined for the heavenly world (svarga).

sanskrit: समेत्याहं सूतपुत्रेण संख्ये वृत्रेण वज्रीव नरेन्द्रमुख्य ।योत्स्ये भृशं भारत सूतपुत्रमस्मिन्संग्रामे यदि वै दृश्यतेऽद्य ॥१२॥
transliteration: sametyāhaṁ sūtaputreṇa saṁkhye; vṛtreṇa vajrīva narendramukhya ,yotsye bhṛśaṁ bhārata sūtaputra;masminsaṁgrāme yadi vai dṛśyate'dya.
english: O chief of kings (narendramukhya), O Bhārata, if indeed he is seen today in this conflict, I will fight fiercely with the son of Sūta (Karṇa), having met him in battle, just as the wielder of the thunderbolt (Indra) fought with Vṛtra.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 47 Verse 12. O chief of kings (narendramukhya), O Bhārata, if indeed he is seen today in this conflict, I will fight fiercely with the son of Sūta (Karṇa), having met him in battle, just as the wielder of the thunderbolt (Indra) fought with Vṛtra.

sanskrit: कर्णं न चेदद्य निहन्मि राजन्सबान्धवं युध्यमानं प्रसह्य ।प्रतिश्रुत्याकुर्वतां वै गतिर्या कष्टां गच्छेयं तामहं राजसिंह ॥१३॥
transliteration: karṇaṁ na cedadya nihanmi rāja;nsabāndhavaṁ yudhyamānaṁ prasahya ,pratiśrutyākurvatāṁ vai gatiryā; kaṣṭāṁ gaccheyaṁ tāmahaṁ rājasiṁha.
english: O King (rājan), O lion among kings (rājasiṁha), if today I do not forcefully kill Karṇa, along with his kinsmen, while he is fighting, then I shall certainly attain that difficult fate which awaits those who do not fulfill their solemn vows (pratiśrutya).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 47 Verse 13. O King (rājan), O lion among kings (rājasiṁha), if today I do not forcefully kill Karṇa, along with his kinsmen, while he is fighting, then I shall certainly attain that difficult fate which awaits those who do not fulfill their solemn vows (pratiśrutya).

sanskrit: आमन्त्रये त्वां ब्रूहि जयं रणे मे पुरा भीमं धार्तराष्ट्रा ग्रसन्ते ।सौतिं हनिष्यामि नरेन्द्रसिंह सैन्यं तथा शत्रुगणांश्च सर्वान् ॥१४॥
transliteration: āmantraye tvāṁ brūhi jayaṁ raṇe me; purā bhīmaṁ dhārtarāṣṭrā grasante ,sautiṁ haniṣyāmi narendrasiṁha; sainyaṁ tathā śatrugaṇāṁśca sarvān.
english: I appeal to you: assure me of victory in battle before the sons of Dhritarashtra overwhelm Bhima. O lion among kings, I will kill Sauti (Karṇa), along with their entire army and all groups of enemies.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 47 Verse 14. I appeal to you: assure me of victory in battle before the sons of Dhritarashtra overwhelm Bhima. O lion among kings, I will kill Sauti (Karṇa), along with their entire army and all groups of enemies.

sanskrit: संजय उवाच ।श्रुत्वा कर्णं कल्यमुदारवीर्यं क्रुद्धः पार्थः फल्गुनस्यामितौजाः ।धनंजयं वाक्यमुवाच चेदं युधिष्ठिरः कर्णशराभितप्तः ॥१॥
transliteration: saṁjaya uvāca ,śrutvā karṇaṁ kalyamudāravīryaṁ; kruddhaḥ pārthaḥ phalgunasyāmitaujāḥ ,dhanaṁjayaṁ vākyamuvāca cedaṁ; yudhiṣṭhiraḥ karṇaśarābhitaptaḥ.
english: Sanjaya said: Yudhiṣṭhira, tormented by Karṇa's arrows, having heard Karṇa (who was) skillful and of great prowess, then spoke these words to Dhanañjaya (Arjuna), who was angry, a son of Pṛthā (Arjuna), and of immeasurable might.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 48 Verse 1. Sanjaya said: Yudhiṣṭhira, tormented by Karṇa's arrows, having heard Karṇa (who was) skillful and of great prowess, then spoke these words to Dhanañjaya (Arjuna), who was angry, a son of Pṛthā (Arjuna), and of immeasurable might.

sanskrit: इदं यदि द्वैतवने ह्यवक्ष्यः कर्णं योद्धुं न प्रसहे नृपेति ।वयं तदा प्राप्तकालानि सर्वे वृत्तान्युपैष्याम तदैव पार्थ ॥२॥
transliteration: idaṁ yadi dvaitavane hyavakṣyaḥ; karṇaṁ yoddhuṁ na prasahe nṛpeti ,vayaṁ tadā prāptakālāni sarve; vṛttānyupaiṣyāma tadaiva pārtha.
english: If you had indeed said this in Dvitavana – "O King, I cannot bring myself to fight Karṇa" – then, O Pārtha (Arjuna), we would have all undertaken actions suitable for that time.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 48 Verse 2. If you had indeed said this in Dvitavana – "O King, I cannot bring myself to fight Karṇa" – then, O Pārtha (Arjuna), we would have all undertaken actions suitable for that time.

sanskrit: मयि प्रतिश्रुत्य वधं हि तस्य बलस्य चाप्तस्य तथैव वीर ।आनीय नः शत्रुमध्यं स कस्मात्समुत्क्षिप्य स्थण्डिले प्रत्यपिंष्ठाः ॥३॥
transliteration: mayi pratiśrutya vadhaṁ hi tasya; balasya cāptasya tathaiva vīra ,ānīya naḥ śatrumadhyaṁ sa kasmā;tsamutkṣipya sthaṇḍile pratyapiṁṣṭhāḥ.
english: O hero, having indeed promised me his death, and likewise the complete defeat of his strength (or army), why, having brought us into the midst of enemies, did you abandon us on the bare ground, having cast (us) away?
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 48 Verse 3. O hero, having indeed promised me his death, and likewise the complete defeat of his strength (or army), why, having brought us into the midst of enemies, did you abandon us on the bare ground, having cast (us) away?

sanskrit: अन्वाशिष्म वयमर्जुन त्वयि यियासवो बहु कल्याणमिष्टम् ।तन्नः सर्वं विफलं राजपुत्र फलार्थिनां निचुल इवातिपुष्पः ॥४॥
transliteration: anvāśiṣma vayamarjuna tvayi; yiyāsavo bahu kalyāṇamiṣṭam ,tannaḥ sarvaṁ viphalaṁ rājaputra; phalārthināṁ nicula ivātipuṣpaḥ.
english: O Arjuna, we desired much good fortune and prosperity for you, wishing to achieve it. O prince, all those aspirations of ours have proven fruitless, just like an excessively flowering *nicula* tree is useless to those seeking fruit.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 48 Verse 4. O Arjuna, we desired much good fortune and prosperity for you, wishing to achieve it. O prince, all those aspirations of ours have proven fruitless, just like an excessively flowering *nicula* tree is useless to those seeking fruit.

sanskrit: प्रच्छादितं बडिशमिवामिषेण प्रच्छादितो गवय इवापवाचा ।अनर्थकं मे दर्शितवानसि त्वं राज्यार्थिनो राज्यरूपं विनाशम् ॥५॥
transliteration: pracchāditaṁ baḍiśamivāmiṣeṇa; pracchādito gavaya ivāpavācā ,anarthakaṁ me darśitavānasi tvaṁ; rājyārthino rājyarūpaṁ vināśam.
english: You have shown me ruin in the guise of a kingdom - a ruin that is ultimately futile for one desiring a kingdom - just as a fish-hook is concealed by bait, or a wild ox is entrapped by false accusations.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 48 Verse 5. You have shown me ruin in the guise of a kingdom - a ruin that is ultimately futile for one desiring a kingdom - just as a fish-hook is concealed by bait, or a wild ox is entrapped by false accusations.

sanskrit: यत्तत्पृथां वागुवाचान्तरिक्षे सप्ताहजाते त्वयि मन्दबुद्धौ ।जातः पुत्रो वासवविक्रमोऽयं सर्वाञ्शूराञ्शात्रवाञ्जेष्यतीति ॥६॥
transliteration: yattatpṛthāṁ vāguvācāntarikṣe; saptāhajāte tvayi mandabuddhau ,jātaḥ putro vāsavavikramo'yaṁ; sarvāñśūrāñśātravāñjeṣyatīti.
english: That voice which spoke to Pritha from the sky, concerning you, dull-witted, when you were only seven days old, said: 'This son, who is born with the valor of Indra, will conquer all his heroic enemies!'
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 48 Verse 6. That voice which spoke to Pritha from the sky, concerning you, dull-witted, when you were only seven days old, said: 'This son, who is born with the valor of Indra, will conquer all his heroic enemies!'

sanskrit: अयं जेता खाण्डवे देवसंघान्सर्वाणि भूतान्यपि चोत्तमौजाः ।अयं जेता मद्रकलिङ्गकेकयानयं कुरून्हन्ति च राजमध्ये ॥७॥
transliteration: ayaṁ jetā khāṇḍave devasaṁghā;nsarvāṇi bhūtānyapi cottamaujāḥ ,ayaṁ jetā madrakaliṅgakekayā;nayaṁ kurūnhanti ca rājamadhye.
english: Possessing supreme power, this one will conquer the hosts of gods and indeed all beings in Khandava. This one will conquer the Madras, Kalingas, and Kekayas. And he will slay the Kurus in the assembly of kings.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 48 Verse 7. Possessing supreme power, this one will conquer the hosts of gods and indeed all beings in Khandava. This one will conquer the Madras, Kalingas, and Kekayas. And he will slay the Kurus in the assembly of kings.

sanskrit: अस्मात्परो न भविता धनुर्धरो न वै भूतः कश्चन जातु जेता ।इच्छन्नार्यः सर्वभूतानि कुर्याद्वशे वशी सर्वसमाप्तविद्यः ॥८॥
transliteration: asmātparo na bhavitā dhanurdharo; na vai bhūtaḥ kaścana jātu jetā ,icchannāryaḥ sarvabhūtāni kuryā;dvaśe vaśī sarvasamāptavidyaḥ.
english: No archer greater than him will ever exist, nor has any conqueror ever been born. This noble one, a master of all knowledge, is a controller who can bring all beings under his sway if he so desires.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 48 Verse 8. No archer greater than him will ever exist, nor has any conqueror ever been born. This noble one, a master of all knowledge, is a controller who can bring all beings under his sway if he so desires.

sanskrit: कान्त्या शशाङ्कस्य जवेन वायोः स्थैर्येण मेरोः क्षमया पृथिव्याः ।सूर्यस्य भासा धनदस्य लक्ष्म्या शौर्येण शक्रस्य बलेन विष्णोः ॥९॥
transliteration: kāntyā śaśāṅkasya javena vāyoḥ; sthairyeṇa meroḥ kṣamayā pṛthivyāḥ ,sūryasya bhāsā dhanadasya lakṣmyā; śauryeṇa śakrasya balena viṣṇoḥ.
english: Possessing the radiance of the moon, the speed of the wind, the steadiness of Mount Meru, the forbearance of the earth, the brilliance of the sun, the prosperity of Kubera, the valor of Indra, and the strength of Vishnu.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 48 Verse 9. Possessing the radiance of the moon, the speed of the wind, the steadiness of Mount Meru, the forbearance of the earth, the brilliance of the sun, the prosperity of Kubera, the valor of Indra, and the strength of Vishnu.

sanskrit: तुल्यो महात्मा तव कुन्ति पुत्रो जातोऽदितेर्विष्णुरिवारिहन्ता ।स्वेषां जयाय द्विषतां वधाय ख्यातोऽमितौजाः कुलतन्तुकर्ता ॥१०॥
transliteration: tulyo mahātmā tava kunti putro; jāto'diterviṣṇurivārihantā ,sveṣāṁ jayāya dviṣatāṁ vadhāya; khyāto'mitaujāḥ kulatantukartā.
english: O Kunti, your great-souled (mahātman) son, born like Vishnu to Aditi, is a slayer of enemies, equal to all these qualities. Renowned for his immeasurable might, he brings victory to his own people, destruction to his foes, and upholds the family lineage.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 48 Verse 10. O Kunti, your great-souled (mahātman) son, born like Vishnu to Aditi, is a slayer of enemies, equal to all these qualities. Renowned for his immeasurable might, he brings victory to his own people, destruction to his foes, and upholds the family lineage.

sanskrit: इत्यन्तरिक्षे शतशृङ्गमूर्ध्नि तपस्विनां शृण्वतां वागुवाच ।एवंविधं त्वां तच्च नाभूत्तवाद्य देवा हि नूनमनृतं वदन्ति ॥११॥
transliteration: ityantarikṣe śataśṛṅgamūrdhni; tapasvināṁ śṛṇvatāṁ vāguvāca ,evaṁvidhaṁ tvāṁ tacca nābhūttavādya; devā hi nūnamanṛtaṁ vadanti.
english: Thus, on the peak of Śataśṛṅga, a voice spoke in the sky while the ascetics listened. (Kunti then lamented): 'Though you are of such a kind, that (promised glory) is not for you today, for the gods certainly speak untruth.'
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 48 Verse 11. Thus, on the peak of Śataśṛṅga, a voice spoke in the sky while the ascetics listened. (Kunti then lamented): 'Though you are of such a kind, that (promised glory) is not for you today, for the gods certainly speak untruth.'

sanskrit: तथापरेषामृषिसत्तमानां श्रुत्वा गिरं पूजयतां सदैव ।न संनतिं प्रैमि सुयोधनस्य न त्वा जानाम्याधिरथेर्भयार्तम् ॥१२॥
transliteration: tathāpareṣāmṛṣisattamānāṁ; śrutvā giraṁ pūjayatāṁ sadaiva ,na saṁnatiṁ praimi suyodhanasya; na tvā jānāmyādhiratherbhayārtam.
english: Having always listened to the words of other excellent sages (ṛṣisattamas) who honor (truth), I do not accept Duryodhana's submission, nor do I consider you, son of Adhiratha (Karṇa), to be afflicted by fear.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 48 Verse 12. Having always listened to the words of other excellent sages (ṛṣisattamas) who honor (truth), I do not accept Duryodhana's submission, nor do I consider you, son of Adhiratha (Karṇa), to be afflicted by fear.

sanskrit: त्वष्ट्रा कृतं वाहमकूजनाक्षं शुभं समास्थाय कपिध्वजं त्वम् ।खड्गं गृहीत्वा हेमचित्रं समिद्धं धनुश्चेदं गाण्डिवं तालमात्रम् ।स केशवेनोह्यमानः कथं नु कर्णाद्भीतो व्यपयातोऽसि पार्थ ॥१३॥
transliteration: tvaṣṭrā kṛtaṁ vāhamakūjanākṣaṁ; śubhaṁ samāsthāya kapidhvajaṁ tvam ,khaḍgaṁ gṛhītvā hemacitraṁ samiddhaṁ; dhanuścedaṁ gāṇḍivaṁ tālamātram ,sa keśavenohyamānaḥ kathaṁ nu; karṇādbhīto vyapayāto'si pārtha.
english: How could you, O Pārtha (Arjuna), after mounting that auspicious chariot  -  crafted by Tvaṣṭā, with silent wheels, and bearing the monkey banner  -  and having taken this magnificent, gold-adorned sword and this blazing Gāṇḍīva bow, as tall as a palm tree, how could you, being driven by Keśava (Kṛṣṇa) himself, flee, terrified of Karṇa?
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 48 Verse 13. How could you, O Pārtha (Arjuna), after mounting that auspicious chariot  -  crafted by Tvaṣṭā, with silent wheels, and bearing the monkey banner  -  and having taken this magnificent, gold-adorned sword and this blazing Gāṇḍīva bow, as tall as a palm tree, how could you, being driven by Keśava (Kṛṣṇa) himself, flee, terrified of Karṇa?

sanskrit: धनुश्चैतत्केशवाय प्रदाय यन्ताभविष्यस्त्वं रणे चेद्दुरात्मन् ।ततोऽहनिष्यत्केशवः कर्णमुग्रं मरुत्पतिर्वृत्रमिवात्तवज्रः ॥१४॥
transliteration: dhanuścaitatkeśavāya pradāya; yantābhaviṣyastvaṁ raṇe ceddurātman ,tato'haniṣyatkeśavaḥ karṇamugraṁ; marutpatirvṛtramivāttavajraḥ.
english: O evil-minded one, if you had given this bow to Keśava (Kṛṣṇa) and had become the charioteer in battle, then Keśava would have struck down the fierce Karṇa, just as Marutpati (Indra), having seized his thunderbolt, struck down Vṛtra.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 48 Verse 14. O evil-minded one, if you had given this bow to Keśava (Kṛṣṇa) and had become the charioteer in battle, then Keśava would have struck down the fierce Karṇa, just as Marutpati (Indra), having seized his thunderbolt, struck down Vṛtra.

sanskrit: मासेऽपतिष्यः पञ्चमे त्वं प्रकृच्छ्रे न वा गर्भोऽप्यभविष्यः पृथायाः ।तत्ते श्रमो राजपुत्राभविष्यन्न संग्रामादपयातुं दुरात्मन् ॥१५॥
transliteration: māse'patiṣyaḥ pañcame tvaṁ prakṛcchre; na vā garbho'pyabhaviṣyaḥ pṛthāyāḥ ,tatte śramo rājaputrābhaviṣya;nna saṁgrāmādapayātuṁ durātman.
