english: O prince (rājaputra), O evil-minded one (durātman), it would have been better if you had suffered a difficult miscarriage in the fifth month, or perhaps not even been conceived as a fetus in Pṛthā's (Kuntī's) womb at all! Therefore, your effort should not be for fleeing from battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 48 Verse 15. O prince (rājaputra), O evil-minded one (durātman), it would have been better if you had suffered a difficult miscarriage in the fifth month, or perhaps not even been conceived as a fetus in Pṛthā's (Kuntī's) womb at all! Therefore, your effort should not be for fleeing from battle.

sanskrit: संजय उवाच ।युधिष्ठिरेणैवमुक्तः कौन्तेयः श्वेतवाहनः ।असिं जग्राह संक्रुद्धो जिघांसुर्भरतर्षभम् ॥१॥
transliteration: saṁjaya uvāca ,yudhiṣṭhireṇaivamuktaḥ kaunteyaḥ śvetavāhanaḥ ,asiṁ jagrāha saṁkruddho jighāṁsurbharatarṣabham.
english: Sañjaya said: Thus addressed by Yudhiṣṭhira, Kaunteya (Arjuna), the one with white steeds, became greatly enraged. Desirous to kill the best of the Bhāratas (Yudhiṣṭhira), he seized his sword.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 49 Verse 1. Sañjaya said: Thus addressed by Yudhiṣṭhira, Kaunteya (Arjuna), the one with white steeds, became greatly enraged. Desirous to kill the best of the Bhāratas (Yudhiṣṭhira), he seized his sword.

sanskrit: तस्य कोपं समुद्वीक्ष्य चित्तज्ञः केशवस्तदा ।उवाच किमिदं पार्थ गृहीतः खड्ग इत्युत ॥२॥
transliteration: tasya kopaṁ samudvīkṣya cittajñaḥ keśavastadā ,uvāca kimidaṁ pārtha gṛhītaḥ khaḍga ityuta.
english: Then, perceiving his anger, Keśava (Kṛṣṇa), who knew his mind, said: "What is this, O Pārtha? Has a sword been taken up?"
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 49 Verse 2. Then, perceiving his anger, Keśava (Kṛṣṇa), who knew his mind, said: "What is this, O Pārtha? Has a sword been taken up?"

sanskrit: नेह पश्यामि योद्धव्यं तव किंचिद्धनंजय ।ते ध्वस्ता धार्तराष्ट्रा हि सर्वे भीमेन धीमता ॥३॥
transliteration: neha paśyāmi yoddhavyaṁ tava kiṁciddhanaṁjaya ,te dhvastā dhārtarāṣṭrā hi sarve bhīmena dhīmatā.
english: O Dhanañjaya, I do not see anything here that needs to be fought by you. For indeed, all those sons of Dhṛtarāṣṭra have already been destroyed by the intelligent Bhīma.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 49 Verse 3. O Dhanañjaya, I do not see anything here that needs to be fought by you. For indeed, all those sons of Dhṛtarāṣṭra have already been destroyed by the intelligent Bhīma.

sanskrit: अपयातोऽसि कौन्तेय राजा द्रष्टव्य इत्यपि ।स राजा भवता दृष्टः कुशली च युधिष्ठिरः ॥४॥
transliteration: apayāto'si kaunteya rājā draṣṭavya ityapi ,sa rājā bhavatā dṛṣṭaḥ kuśalī ca yudhiṣṭhiraḥ.
english: O Kaunteya, you have already departed from battle with the thought, 'the king must be seen.' That king, Yudhiṣṭhira, has now been seen by you, and he is well.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 49 Verse 4. O Kaunteya, you have already departed from battle with the thought, 'the king must be seen.' That king, Yudhiṣṭhira, has now been seen by you, and he is well.

sanskrit: तं दृष्ट्वा नृपशार्दूलं शार्दूलसमविक्रमम् ।हर्षकाले तु संप्राप्ते कस्मात्त्वा मन्युराविशत् ॥५॥
transliteration: taṁ dṛṣṭvā nṛpaśārdūlaṁ śārdūlasamavikramam ,harṣakāle tu saṁprāpte kasmāttvā manyurāviśat.
english: Having seen that tiger among kings, who possessed valor equal to a tiger, why has anger overcome you when a time for rejoicing has arrived?
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 49 Verse 5. Having seen that tiger among kings, who possessed valor equal to a tiger, why has anger overcome you when a time for rejoicing has arrived?

sanskrit: न तं पश्यामि कौन्तेय यस्ते वध्यो भवेदिह ।कस्माद्भवान्महाखड्गं परिगृह्णाति सत्वरम् ॥६॥
transliteration: na taṁ paśyāmi kaunteya yaste vadhyo bhavediha ,kasmādbhavānmahākhaḍgaṁ parigṛhṇāti satvaram.
english: O son of Kunti, I do not see anyone here who deserves to be slain by you. Why then do you quickly seize this great sword?
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 49 Verse 6. O son of Kunti, I do not see anyone here who deserves to be slain by you. Why then do you quickly seize this great sword?

sanskrit: तत्त्वा पृच्छामि कौन्तेय किमिदं ते चिकीर्षितम् ।परामृशसि यत्क्रुद्धः खड्गमद्भुतविक्रम ॥७॥
transliteration: tattvā pṛcchāmi kaunteya kimidaṁ te cikīrṣitam ,parāmṛśasi yatkruddhaḥ khaḍgamadbhutavikrama.
english: Therefore, O son of Kunti, I ask you, what is this action you intend to undertake? For you are angrily grasping your sword, O one of wonderful valor!
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 49 Verse 7. Therefore, O son of Kunti, I ask you, what is this action you intend to undertake? For you are angrily grasping your sword, O one of wonderful valor!

sanskrit: एवमुक्तस्तु कृष्णेन प्रेक्षमाणो युधिष्ठिरम् ।अर्जुनः प्राह गोविन्दं क्रुद्धः सर्प इव श्वसन् ॥८॥
transliteration: evamuktastu kṛṣṇena prekṣamāṇo yudhiṣṭhiram ,arjunaḥ prāha govindaṁ kruddhaḥ sarpa iva śvasan.
english: Thus addressed by Krishna, Arjuna, looking towards Yudhiṣṭhira, angrily spoke to Govinda, hissing like an enraged serpent.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 49 Verse 8. Thus addressed by Krishna, Arjuna, looking towards Yudhiṣṭhira, angrily spoke to Govinda, hissing like an enraged serpent.

sanskrit: दद गाण्डीवमन्यस्मा इति मां योऽभिचोदयेत् ।छिन्द्यामहं शिरस्तस्य इत्युपांशुव्रतं मम ॥९॥
transliteration: dada gāṇḍīvamanyasmā iti māṁ yo'bhicodayet ,chindyāmahaṁ śirastasya ityupāṁśuvrataṁ mama.
english: If anyone were to incite me, saying, 'Give the Gāṇḍīva (bow) to another,' I would cut off his head; this is my secret vow.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 49 Verse 9. If anyone were to incite me, saying, 'Give the Gāṇḍīva (bow) to another,' I would cut off his head; this is my secret vow.

sanskrit: तदुक्तोऽहमदीनात्मन्राज्ञामितपराक्रम ।समक्षं तव गोविन्द न तत्क्षन्तुमिहोत्सहे ॥१०॥
transliteration: tadukto'hamadīnātmanrājñāmitaparākrama ,samakṣaṁ tava govinda na tatkṣantumihotsahe.
english: Having been addressed in that manner, I - whose spirit is not dejected, and whose valor is boundless among kings - O Govinda, cannot bring myself to tolerate that here in your presence.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 49 Verse 10. Having been addressed in that manner, I - whose spirit is not dejected, and whose valor is boundless among kings - O Govinda, cannot bring myself to tolerate that here in your presence.

sanskrit: तस्मादेनं वधिष्यामि राजानं धर्मभीरुकम् ।प्रतिज्ञां पालयिष्यामि हत्वेमं नरसत्तमम् ।एतदर्थं मया खड्गो गृहीतो यदुनन्दन ॥११॥
transliteration: tasmādenaṁ vadhiṣyāmi rājānaṁ dharmabhīrukam ,pratijñāṁ pālayiṣyāmi hatvemaṁ narasattamam ,etadarthaṁ mayā khaḍgo gṛhīto yadunandana.
english: Therefore, I will kill this king, who is afraid of (natural law) dharma. I will fulfill my vow by slaying this best of men. For this very purpose, O delight of the Yadus (Krishna), this sword has been taken up by me.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 49 Verse 11. Therefore, I will kill this king, who is afraid of (natural law) dharma. I will fulfill my vow by slaying this best of men. For this very purpose, O delight of the Yadus (Krishna), this sword has been taken up by me.

sanskrit: सोऽहं युधिष्ठिरं हत्वा सत्येऽप्यानृण्यतां गतः ।विशोको विज्वरश्चापि भविष्यामि जनार्दन ॥१२॥
transliteration: so'haṁ yudhiṣṭhiraṁ hatvā satye'pyānṛṇyatāṁ gataḥ ,viśoko vijvaraścāpi bhaviṣyāmi janārdana.
english: Thus, having killed Yudhiṣṭhira, I will have attained freedom from my debt concerning the truth (of my vow); I will become free from sorrow and fever, O Janārdana (Krishna).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 49 Verse 12. Thus, having killed Yudhiṣṭhira, I will have attained freedom from my debt concerning the truth (of my vow); I will become free from sorrow and fever, O Janārdana (Krishna).

sanskrit: किं वा त्वं मन्यसे प्राप्तमस्मिन्काले समुत्थिते ।त्वमस्य जगतस्तात वेत्थ सर्वं गतागतम् ।तत्तथा प्रकरिष्यामि यथा मां वक्ष्यते भवान् ॥१३॥
transliteration: kiṁ vā tvaṁ manyase prāptamasminkāle samutthite ,tvamasya jagatastāta vettha sarvaṁ gatāgatam ,tattathā prakariṣyāmi yathā māṁ vakṣyate bhavān.
english: What do you consider appropriate to be done in this arisen time? O father, you know all that has gone and all that has come (the past and future) of this world. Therefore, I will act accordingly, just as you (your honor) will instruct me.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 49 Verse 13. What do you consider appropriate to be done in this arisen time? O father, you know all that has gone and all that has come (the past and future) of this world. Therefore, I will act accordingly, just as you (your honor) will instruct me.

sanskrit: कृष्ण उवाच ।इदानीं पार्थ जानामि न वृद्धाः सेवितास्त्वया ।अकाले पुरुषव्याघ्र संरम्भक्रिययानया ।न हि धर्मविभागज्ञः कुर्यादेवं धनंजय ॥१४॥
transliteration: kṛṣṇa uvāca ,idānīṁ pārtha jānāmi na vṛddhāḥ sevitāstvayā ,akāle puruṣavyāghra saṁrambhakriyayānayā ,na hi dharmavibhāgajñaḥ kuryādevaṁ dhanaṁjaya.
english: Kṛṣṇa said: "Now, O Pārtha, by this action of rash haste at the wrong time, O tiger among men, I understand that you have not served (consulted) the elders. Indeed, O Dhanañjaya, one who understands the distinctions of natural law (dharma) would not act thus."
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 49 Verse 14. Kṛṣṇa said: "Now, O Pārtha, by this action of rash haste at the wrong time, O tiger among men, I understand that you have not served (consulted) the elders. Indeed, O Dhanañjaya, one who understands the distinctions of natural law (dharma) would not act thus."

sanskrit: अकार्याणां च कार्याणां संयोगं यः करोति वै ।कार्याणामक्रियाणां च स पार्थ पुरुषाधमः ॥१५॥
transliteration: akāryāṇāṁ ca kāryāṇāṁ saṁyogaṁ yaḥ karoti vai ,kāryāṇāmakriyāṇāṁ ca sa pārtha puruṣādhamaḥ.
english: Indeed, he who combines what should not be done with what should be done, and what should be done with what should not be done – that man, O Pārtha, is the lowest of men.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 49 Verse 15. Indeed, he who combines what should not be done with what should be done, and what should be done with what should not be done – that man, O Pārtha, is the lowest of men.

sanskrit: अनुसृत्य तु ये धर्मं कवयः समुपस्थिताः ।समासविस्तरविदां न तेषां वेत्थ निश्चयम् ॥१६॥
transliteration: anusṛtya tu ye dharmaṁ kavayaḥ samupasthitāḥ ,samāsavistaravidāṁ na teṣāṁ vettha niścayam.
english: But you do not know the definite conclusion of those sages who, following natural law (dharma), have thoroughly understood, and who are experts in both conciseness and expansion.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 49 Verse 16. But you do not know the definite conclusion of those sages who, following natural law (dharma), have thoroughly understood, and who are experts in both conciseness and expansion.

sanskrit: अनिश्चयज्ञो हि नरः कार्याकार्यविनिश्चये ।अवशो मुह्यते पार्थ यथा त्वं मूढ एव तु ॥१७॥
transliteration: aniścayajño hi naraḥ kāryākāryaviniścaye ,avaśo muhyate pārtha yathā tvaṁ mūḍha eva tu.
english: O Pārtha (Arjuna), indeed, a person who is indecisive regarding the determination of what is proper action and improper action becomes bewildered and helpless, just as you are now utterly deluded.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 49 Verse 17. O Pārtha (Arjuna), indeed, a person who is indecisive regarding the determination of what is proper action and improper action becomes bewildered and helpless, just as you are now utterly deluded.

sanskrit: न हि कार्यमकार्यं वा सुखं ज्ञातुं कथंचन ।श्रुतेन ज्ञायते सर्वं तच्च त्वं नावबुध्यसे ॥१८॥
transliteration: na hi kāryamakāryaṁ vā sukhaṁ jñātuṁ kathaṁcana ,śrutena jñāyate sarvaṁ tacca tvaṁ nāvabudhyase.
english: Indeed, it is by no means easy to discern what is proper action and what is improper action. Everything is known through scripture (śruti) or tradition, and you, however, do not comprehend that.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 49 Verse 18. Indeed, it is by no means easy to discern what is proper action and what is improper action. Everything is known through scripture (śruti) or tradition, and you, however, do not comprehend that.

sanskrit: अविज्ञानाद्भवान्यच्च धर्मं रक्षति धर्मवित् ।प्राणिनां हि वधं पार्थ धार्मिको नावबुध्यते ॥१९॥
transliteration: avijñānādbhavānyacca dharmaṁ rakṣati dharmavit ,prāṇināṁ hi vadhaṁ pārtha dhārmiko nāvabudhyate.
english: O Pārtha (Arjuna), you, a knower of (dharma) (natural law), protect (this) due to a lack of proper understanding, and indeed, a righteous person (dhārmika) does not consider the slaughter of living beings (as consistent with dharma).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 49 Verse 19. O Pārtha (Arjuna), you, a knower of (dharma) (natural law), protect (this) due to a lack of proper understanding, and indeed, a righteous person (dhārmika) does not consider the slaughter of living beings (as consistent with dharma).

sanskrit: प्राणिनामवधस्तात सर्वज्यायान्मतो मम ।अनृतं तु भवेद्वाच्यं न च हिंस्यात्कथंचन ॥२०॥
transliteration: prāṇināmavadhastāta sarvajyāyānmato mama ,anṛtaṁ tu bhavedvācyaṁ na ca hiṁsyātkathaṁcana.
english: O dear one (tāta), the non-killing of living beings is considered by me to be superior to all. However, an untruth may be spoken, but one should never cause harm by any means.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 49 Verse 20. O dear one (tāta), the non-killing of living beings is considered by me to be superior to all. However, an untruth may be spoken, but one should never cause harm by any means.

sanskrit: स कथं भ्रातरं ज्येष्ठं राजानं धर्मकोविदम् ।हन्याद्भवान्नरश्रेष्ठ प्राकृतोऽन्यः पुमानिव ॥२१॥
transliteration: sa kathaṁ bhrātaraṁ jyeṣṭhaṁ rājānaṁ dharmakovidam ,hanyādbhavānnaraśreṣṭha prākṛto'nyaḥ pumāniva.
english: O best among men, how can you kill your elder brother, the king, who is so knowledgeable in the natural law (dharma), as if you were just some ordinary person?
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 49 Verse 21. O best among men, how can you kill your elder brother, the king, who is so knowledgeable in the natural law (dharma), as if you were just some ordinary person?

sanskrit: अयुध्यमानस्य वधस्तथाशस्त्रस्य भारत ।पराङ्मुखस्य द्रवतः शरणं वाभिगच्छतः ।कृताञ्जलेः प्रपन्नस्य न वधः पूज्यते बुधैः ॥२२॥
transliteration: ayudhyamānasya vadhastathāśastrasya bhārata ,parāṅmukhasya dravataḥ śaraṇaṁ vābhigacchataḥ ,kṛtāñjaleḥ prapannasya na vadhaḥ pūjyate budhaiḥ.
english: O Bhārata, the wise do not approve of killing one who is not fighting, or is unarmed, or has turned away, or is fleeing, or is seeking refuge, or has folded hands, or has surrendered.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 49 Verse 22. O Bhārata, the wise do not approve of killing one who is not fighting, or is unarmed, or has turned away, or is fleeing, or is seeking refuge, or has folded hands, or has surrendered.

sanskrit: त्वया चैव व्रतं पार्थ बालेनैव कृतं पुरा ।तस्मादधर्मसंयुक्तं मौढ्यात्कर्म व्यवस्यसि ॥२३॥
transliteration: tvayā caiva vrataṁ pārtha bālenaiva kṛtaṁ purā ,tasmādadharmasaṁyuktaṁ mauḍhyātkarma vyavasyasi.
english: O Pārtha, you made a vow in your childhood. Therefore, it is out of foolishness that you are now undertaking an action connected with unrighteousness (adharma).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 49 Verse 23. O Pārtha, you made a vow in your childhood. Therefore, it is out of foolishness that you are now undertaking an action connected with unrighteousness (adharma).

sanskrit: स गुरुं पार्थ कस्मात्त्वं हन्या धर्ममनुस्मरन् ।असंप्रधार्य धर्माणां गतिं सूक्ष्मां दुरन्वयाम् ॥२४॥
transliteration: sa guruṁ pārtha kasmāttvaṁ hanyā dharmamanusmaran ,asaṁpradhārya dharmāṇāṁ gatiṁ sūkṣmāṁ duranvayām.
english: O Pārtha, remembering your constitutional duty (dharma), why would you kill your teacher, especially without having fully considered the subtle and difficult-to-discern consequences of the natural law (dharma)?
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 49 Verse 24. O Pārtha, remembering your constitutional duty (dharma), why would you kill your teacher, especially without having fully considered the subtle and difficult-to-discern consequences of the natural law (dharma)?

sanskrit: इदं धर्मरहस्यं च वक्ष्यामि भरतर्षभ ।यद्ब्रूयात्तव भीष्मो वा धर्मज्ञो वा युधिष्ठिरः ॥२५॥
transliteration: idaṁ dharmarahasyaṁ ca vakṣyāmi bharatarṣabha ,yadbrūyāttava bhīṣmo vā dharmajño vā yudhiṣṭhiraḥ.
english: O best among the Bharatas (bharatarṣabha), I will now explain to you this profound secret of natural law (dharma), which Bhishma would teach you, or which Yudhishthira, who is a knower of natural law (dharma), would articulate.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 49 Verse 25. O best among the Bharatas (bharatarṣabha), I will now explain to you this profound secret of natural law (dharma), which Bhishma would teach you, or which Yudhishthira, who is a knower of natural law (dharma), would articulate.

sanskrit: विदुरो वा तथा क्षत्ता कुन्ती वापि यशस्विनी ।तत्ते वक्ष्यामि तत्त्वेन तन्निबोध धनंजय ॥२६॥
transliteration: viduro vā tathā kṣattā kuntī vāpi yaśasvinī ,tatte vakṣyāmi tattvena tannibodha dhanaṁjaya.
english: Or similarly, Vidura, the wise administrator, or even the glorious Kunti, would express (this secret). That very truth I will reveal to you in its essence (tattvena), so understand it, O Dhananjaya.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 49 Verse 26. Or similarly, Vidura, the wise administrator, or even the glorious Kunti, would express (this secret). That very truth I will reveal to you in its essence (tattvena), so understand it, O Dhananjaya.

sanskrit: सत्यस्य वचनं साधु न सत्याद्विद्यते परम् ।तत्त्वेनैतत्सुदुर्ज्ञेयं यस्य सत्यमनुष्ठितम् ॥२७॥
transliteration: satyasya vacanaṁ sādhu na satyādvidyate param ,tattvenaitatsudurjñeyaṁ yasya satyamanuṣṭhitam.
english: Speaking the truth is virtuous, and nothing is superior to truth. However, this (secret of natural law (dharma)) is truly very difficult to comprehend, even for one in whom truth (satyam) is firmly established.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 49 Verse 27. Speaking the truth is virtuous, and nothing is superior to truth. However, this (secret of natural law (dharma)) is truly very difficult to comprehend, even for one in whom truth (satyam) is firmly established.

sanskrit: भवेत्सत्यमवक्तव्यं वक्तव्यमनृतं भवेत् ।सर्वस्वस्यापहारे तु वक्तव्यमनृतं भवेत् ॥२८॥
transliteration: bhavetsatyamavaktavyaṁ vaktavyamanṛtaṁ bhavet ,sarvasvasyāpahāre tu vaktavyamanṛtaṁ bhavet.
english: Sometimes, truth (satyam) may need to remain unspoken, and falsehood (anṛtam) may need to be uttered. Indeed, in the event of the complete confiscation of one's possessions (sarvasvasya apahāre), falsehood (anṛtam) should be spoken.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 49 Verse 28. Sometimes, truth (satyam) may need to remain unspoken, and falsehood (anṛtam) may need to be uttered. Indeed, in the event of the complete confiscation of one's possessions (sarvasvasya apahāre), falsehood (anṛtam) should be spoken.

sanskrit: प्राणात्यये विवाहे च वक्तव्यमनृतं भवेत् ।यत्रानृतं भवेत्सत्यं सत्यं चाप्यनृतं भवेत् ॥२९॥
transliteration: prāṇātyaye vivāhe ca vaktavyamanṛtaṁ bhavet ,yatrānṛtaṁ bhavetsatyaṁ satyaṁ cāpyanṛtaṁ bhavet.
english: It is permissible to speak an untruth when one's life is in danger or during a marriage ceremony. This is because there are situations where an untruth can transform into truth, and conversely, truth itself can become an untruth.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 49 Verse 29. It is permissible to speak an untruth when one's life is in danger or during a marriage ceremony. This is because there are situations where an untruth can transform into truth, and conversely, truth itself can become an untruth.

sanskrit: तादृशं पश्यते बालो यस्य सत्यमनुष्ठितम् ।सत्यानृते विनिश्चित्य ततो भवति धर्मवित् ॥३०॥
transliteration: tādṛśaṁ paśyate bālo yasya satyamanuṣṭhitam ,satyānṛte viniścitya tato bhavati dharmavit.
english: An ignorant person (bāla) perceives things in accordance with what they believe to be true. Only after carefully distinguishing between truth and untruth does one become a knower of the intrinsic nature (dharma).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 49 Verse 30. An ignorant person (bāla) perceives things in accordance with what they believe to be true. Only after carefully distinguishing between truth and untruth does one become a knower of the intrinsic nature (dharma).

sanskrit: किमाश्चर्यं कृतप्रज्ञः पुरुषोऽपि सुदारुणः ।सुमहत्प्राप्नुयात्पुण्यं बलाकोऽन्धवधादिव ॥३१॥
transliteration: kimāścaryaṁ kṛtaprajñaḥ puruṣo'pi sudāruṇaḥ ,sumahatprāpnuyātpuṇyaṁ balāko'ndhavadhādiva.
english: It is astonishing how even a person (puruṣa) of great wisdom (kṛta-prajña), though very cruel, might attain immense merit (puṇya), just like Balāka did from the killing of the blind.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 49 Verse 31. It is astonishing how even a person (puruṣa) of great wisdom (kṛta-prajña), though very cruel, might attain immense merit (puṇya), just like Balāka did from the killing of the blind.

sanskrit: किमाश्चर्यं पुनर्मूढो धर्मकामोऽप्यपण्डितः ।सुमहत्प्राप्नुयात्पापमापगामिव कौशिकः ॥३२॥
transliteration: kimāścaryaṁ punarmūḍho dharmakāmo'pyapaṇḍitaḥ ,sumahatprāpnuyātpāpamāpagāmiva kauśikaḥ.
english: What an astonishing thing it is, then, that even a foolish and unlearned person, though desiring to uphold natural law (dharma), may incur immense sin, just as Kauśika did (incurring sin vast as a river).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 49 Verse 32. What an astonishing thing it is, then, that even a foolish and unlearned person, though desiring to uphold natural law (dharma), may incur immense sin, just as Kauśika did (incurring sin vast as a river).

sanskrit: अर्जुन उवाच ।आचक्ष्व भगवन्नेतद्यथा विद्यामहं तथा ।बलाकान्धाभिसंबद्धं नदीनां कौशिकस्य च ॥३३॥
transliteration: arjuna uvāca ,ācakṣva bhagavannetadyathā vidyāmahaṁ tathā ,balākāndhābhisaṁbaddhaṁ nadīnāṁ kauśikasya ca.
english: Arjuna said: "O Revered One, please narrate this to me in such a way that I may understand it – this account connected with Balāka, the blind individuals, the rivers, and Kauśika."
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 49 Verse 33. Arjuna said: "O Revered One, please narrate this to me in such a way that I may understand it – this account connected with Balāka, the blind individuals, the rivers, and Kauśika."

sanskrit: कृष्ण उवाच ।मृगव्याधोऽभवत्कश्चिद्बलाको नाम भारत ।यात्रार्थं पुत्रदारस्य मृगान्हन्ति न कामतः ॥३४॥
transliteration: kṛṣṇa uvāca ,mṛgavyādho'bhavatkaścidbalāko nāma bhārata ,yātrārthaṁ putradārasya mṛgānhanti na kāmataḥ.
english: Kṛṣṇa said: "O descendant of Bharata, there was a certain hunter named Balāka. He used to hunt deer for the livelihood of his son and wife, not out of any personal desire."
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 49 Verse 34. Kṛṣṇa said: "O descendant of Bharata, there was a certain hunter named Balāka. He used to hunt deer for the livelihood of his son and wife, not out of any personal desire."

sanskrit: सोऽन्धौ च मातापितरौ बिभर्त्यन्यांश्च संश्रितान् ।स्वधर्मनिरतो नित्यं सत्यवागनसूयकः ॥३५॥
transliteration: so'ndhau ca mātāpitarau bibhartyanyāṁśca saṁśritān ,svadharmanirato nityaṁ satyavāganasūyakaḥ.
english: He supports his blind mother and father, as well as other dependents. He is always dedicated to his own prescribed duties (sva-dharma), speaks truthfully, and is free from envy.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 49 Verse 35. He supports his blind mother and father, as well as other dependents. He is always dedicated to his own prescribed duties (sva-dharma), speaks truthfully, and is free from envy.

sanskrit: स कदाचिन्मृगाँल्लिप्सुर्नान्वविन्दत्प्रयत्नवान् ।अथापश्यत्स पीतोदं श्वापदं घ्राणचक्षुषम् ॥३६॥
transliteration: sa kadācinmṛgāँllipsurnānvavindatprayatnavān ,athāpaśyatsa pītodaṁ śvāpadaṁ ghrāṇacakṣuṣam.
english: Once, diligently searching for deer, he found none. Then he saw a beast of prey that had drunk water, which relied on its sense of smell for sight.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 49 Verse 36. Once, diligently searching for deer, he found none. Then he saw a beast of prey that had drunk water, which relied on its sense of smell for sight.

sanskrit: अदृष्टपूर्वमपि तत्सत्त्वं तेन हतं तदा ।अन्वेव च ततो व्योम्नः पुष्पवर्षमवापतत् ॥३७॥
transliteration: adṛṣṭapūrvamapi tatsattvaṁ tena hataṁ tadā ,anveva ca tato vyomnaḥ puṣpavarṣamavāpatat.
english: That being, which had never been seen before, was then killed by him. Immediately afterwards, a shower of flowers fell from the sky.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 49 Verse 37. That being, which had never been seen before, was then killed by him. Immediately afterwards, a shower of flowers fell from the sky.

sanskrit: अप्सरोगीतवादित्रैर्नादितं च मनोरमम् ।विमानमागमत्स्वर्गान्मृगव्याधनिनीषया ॥३८॥
transliteration: apsarogītavāditrairnāditaṁ ca manoramam ,vimānamāgamatsvargānmṛgavyādhaninīṣayā.
english: And a charming celestial chariot, resonating with the songs and music of Apsarās, arrived from the heavens, intending to carry the hunter.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 49 Verse 38. And a charming celestial chariot, resonating with the songs and music of Apsarās, arrived from the heavens, intending to carry the hunter.

sanskrit: तद्भूतं सर्वभूतानामभावाय किलार्जुन ।तपस्तप्त्वा वरं प्राप्तं कृतमन्धं स्वयंभुवा ॥३९॥
transliteration: tadbhūtaṁ sarvabhūtānāmabhāvāya kilārjuna ,tapastaptvā varaṁ prāptaṁ kṛtamandhaṁ svayaṁbhuvā.
english: That creature (bhūta), O Arjuna, was indeed destined for the annihilation of all beings. It had obtained a boon after performing severe asceticism (tapas), and was rendered blind by Svayaṃbhū (Brahmā) himself.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 49 Verse 39. That creature (bhūta), O Arjuna, was indeed destined for the annihilation of all beings. It had obtained a boon after performing severe asceticism (tapas), and was rendered blind by Svayaṃbhū (Brahmā) himself.

sanskrit: तद्धत्वा सर्वभूतानामभावकृतनिश्चयम् ।ततो बलाकः स्वरगादेवं धर्मः सुदुर्विदः ॥४०॥
transliteration: taddhatvā sarvabhūtānāmabhāvakṛtaniścayam ,tato balākaḥ svaragādevaṁ dharmaḥ sudurvidaḥ.
english: Having killed that creature, which was resolute in annihilating all beings, Balāka then returned from heaven. Thus, the intrinsic nature (dharma) of events is indeed very difficult to comprehend.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 49 Verse 40. Having killed that creature, which was resolute in annihilating all beings, Balāka then returned from heaven. Thus, the intrinsic nature (dharma) of events is indeed very difficult to comprehend.

sanskrit: कौशिकोऽप्यभवद्विप्रस्तपस्वी न बहुश्रुतः ।नदीनां संगमे ग्रामाददूरे स किलावसत् ॥४१॥
transliteration: kauśiko'pyabhavadviprastapasvī na bahuśrutaḥ ,nadīnāṁ saṁgame grāmādadūre sa kilāvasat.
english: Kaushika became a Brahmin (vipra) and an ascetic (tapasvin), but he was not very learned. He reportedly lived near the confluence of rivers, not far from a village.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 49 Verse 41. Kaushika became a Brahmin (vipra) and an ascetic (tapasvin), but he was not very learned. He reportedly lived near the confluence of rivers, not far from a village.

sanskrit: सत्यं मया सदा वाच्यमिति तस्याभवद्व्रतम् ।सत्यवादीति विख्यातः स तदासीद्धनंजय ॥४२॥
transliteration: satyaṁ mayā sadā vācyamiti tasyābhavadvratam ,satyavādīti vikhyātaḥ sa tadāsīddhanaṁjaya.
english: “The truth (satya) should always be spoken by me” - thus was his vow (vrata). At that time, he was renowned as 'Truth-speaker', O Dhananjaya.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 49 Verse 42. “The truth (satya) should always be spoken by me” - thus was his vow (vrata). At that time, he was renowned as 'Truth-speaker', O Dhananjaya.

sanskrit: अथ दस्युभयात्केचित्तदा तद्वनमाविशन् ।दस्यवोऽपि गताः क्रूरा व्यमार्गन्त प्रयत्नतः ॥४३॥
transliteration: atha dasyubhayātkecittadā tadvanamāviśan ,dasyavo'pi gatāḥ krūrā vyamārganta prayatnataḥ.
english: Then, some people, out of fear of robbers, entered that forest. The cruel robbers, who had also gone, diligently searched (for them).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 49 Verse 43. Then, some people, out of fear of robbers, entered that forest. The cruel robbers, who had also gone, diligently searched (for them).

sanskrit: अथ कौशिकमभ्येत्य प्राहुस्तं सत्यवादिनम् ।कतमेन पथा याता भगवन्बहवो जनाः ।सत्येन पृष्टः प्रब्रूहि यदि तान्वेत्थ शंस नः ॥४४॥
transliteration: atha kauśikamabhyetya prāhustaṁ satyavādinam ,katamena pathā yātā bhagavanbahavo janāḥ ,satyena pṛṣṭaḥ prabrūhi yadi tānvettha śaṁsa naḥ.
english: Then, having approached Kaushika, they said to that truth-speaker: "O revered one (bhagavan), by which path have many people gone? If you know them, being asked truthfully, declare to us and tell us."
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 49 Verse 44. Then, having approached Kaushika, they said to that truth-speaker: "O revered one (bhagavan), by which path have many people gone? If you know them, being asked truthfully, declare to us and tell us."

sanskrit: स पृष्टः कौशिकः सत्यं वचनं तानुवाच ह ।बहुवृक्षलतागुल्ममेतद्वनमुपाश्रिताः ।ततस्ते तान्समासाद्य क्रूरा जघ्नुरिति श्रुतिः ॥४५॥
transliteration: sa pṛṣṭaḥ kauśikaḥ satyaṁ vacanaṁ tānuvāca ha ,bahuvṛkṣalatāgulmametadvanamupāśritāḥ ,tataste tānsamāsādya krūrā jaghnuriti śrutiḥ.
english: He, Kauśika, when questioned, indeed spoke a truthful word to them: '(The beings you seek) are residing in this forest, which is full of many trees, creepers, and bushes.' Thereupon, those (inquirers), having approached them, the cruel ones (meaning the Kṣatriyas themselves, by their act of violence) killed them - this is the traditional account (śruti).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 49 Verse 45. He, Kauśika, when questioned, indeed spoke a truthful word to them: '(The beings you seek) are residing in this forest, which is full of many trees, creepers, and bushes.' Thereupon, those (inquirers), having approached them, the cruel ones (meaning the Kṣatriyas themselves, by their act of violence) killed them - this is the traditional account (śruti).

sanskrit: तेनाधर्मेण महता वाग्दुरुक्तेन कौशिकः ।गतः सुकष्टं नरकं सूक्ष्मधर्मेष्वकोविदः ।अप्रभूतश्रुतो मूढो धर्माणामविभागवित् ॥४६॥
transliteration: tenādharmeṇa mahatā vāgduruktena kauśikaḥ ,gataḥ sukaṣṭaṁ narakaṁ sūkṣmadharmeṣvakovidaḥ ,aprabhūtaśruto mūḍho dharmāṇāmavibhāgavit.
english: Due to that great unrighteous action (adharma) in speaking (truth that led to harm), Kauśika, who was unskilled in subtle matters of natural law (dharma), went to a very difficult hell. He was ignorant, foolish, and unable to discern the proper distinctions of natural laws (dharma).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 49 Verse 46. Due to that great unrighteous action (adharma) in speaking (truth that led to harm), Kauśika, who was unskilled in subtle matters of natural law (dharma), went to a very difficult hell. He was ignorant, foolish, and unable to discern the proper distinctions of natural laws (dharma).

sanskrit: वृद्धानपृष्ट्वा संदेहं महच्छ्वभ्रमितोऽर्हति ।तत्र ते लक्षणोद्देशः कश्चिदेव भविष्यति ॥४७॥
transliteration: vṛddhānapṛṣṭvā saṁdehaṁ mahacchvabhramito'rhati ,tatra te lakṣaṇoddeśaḥ kaścideva bhaviṣyati.
english: One who, without consulting elders regarding a doubt, falls into a great chasm, deserves (his fate). In that regard, there will indeed be some particular distinguishing mark or indication for you.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 49 Verse 47. One who, without consulting elders regarding a doubt, falls into a great chasm, deserves (his fate). In that regard, there will indeed be some particular distinguishing mark or indication for you.

sanskrit: दुष्करं परमज्ञानं तर्केणात्र व्यवस्यति ।श्रुतिर्धर्म इति ह्येके वदन्ति बहवो जनाः ॥४८॥
transliteration: duṣkaraṁ paramajñānaṁ tarkeṇātra vyavasyati ,śrutirdharma iti hyeke vadanti bahavo janāḥ.
english: Here, it is difficult to ascertain the highest knowledge through mere logic. Indeed, some, even many people, declare that sacred tradition (śruti) itself constitutes natural law (dharma).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 49 Verse 48. Here, it is difficult to ascertain the highest knowledge through mere logic. Indeed, some, even many people, declare that sacred tradition (śruti) itself constitutes natural law (dharma).

sanskrit: न त्वेतत्प्रतिसूयामि न हि सर्वं विधीयते ।प्रभवार्थाय भूतानां धर्मप्रवचनं कृतम् ॥४९॥
transliteration: na tvetatpratisūyāmi na hi sarvaṁ vidhīyate ,prabhavārthāya bhūtānāṁ dharmapravacanaṁ kṛtam.
english: Indeed, I do not find fault with this, for not everything is explicitly ordained. The teaching of the natural law (dharma) has been established for the well-being of all beings.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 49 Verse 49. Indeed, I do not find fault with this, for not everything is explicitly ordained. The teaching of the natural law (dharma) has been established for the well-being of all beings.

sanskrit: धारणाद्धर्ममित्याहुर्धर्मो धारयति प्रजाः ।यः स्याद्धारणसंयुक्तः स धर्म इति निश्चयः ॥५०॥
transliteration: dhāraṇāddharmamityāhurdharmo dhārayati prajāḥ ,yaḥ syāddhāraṇasaṁyuktaḥ sa dharma iti niścayaḥ.
english: They say that natural law (dharma) is so called because it upholds; natural law (dharma) sustains all beings. That which is characterized by upholding is indeed natural law (dharma) – this is the certain conclusion.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 49 Verse 50. They say that natural law (dharma) is so called because it upholds; natural law (dharma) sustains all beings. That which is characterized by upholding is indeed natural law (dharma) – this is the certain conclusion.

sanskrit: येऽन्यायेन जिहीर्षन्तो जना इच्छन्ति कर्हिचित् ।अकूजनेन चेन्मोक्षो नात्र कूजेत्कथंचन ॥५१॥
transliteration: ye'nyāyena jihīrṣanto janā icchanti karhicit ,akūjanena cenmokṣo nātra kūjetkathaṁcana.
english: If people who desire to seize something unlawfully (anyāya) at any time can gain impunity by not complaining, then one should certainly never complain here in any way.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 49 Verse 51. If people who desire to seize something unlawfully (anyāya) at any time can gain impunity by not complaining, then one should certainly never complain here in any way.

sanskrit: अवश्यं कूजितव्यं वा शङ्केरन्वाप्यकूजतः ।श्रेयस्तत्रानृतं वक्तुं सत्यादिति विचारितम् ॥५२॥
transliteration: avaśyaṁ kūjitavyaṁ vā śaṅkeranvāpyakūjataḥ ,śreyastatrānṛtaṁ vaktuṁ satyāditi vicāritam.
english: Or one should certainly complain, for they might suspect even one who remains silent. Therefore, in such a situation, it has been considered better to speak untruth (anṛta) than truth.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 49 Verse 52. Or one should certainly complain, for they might suspect even one who remains silent. Therefore, in such a situation, it has been considered better to speak untruth (anṛta) than truth.

sanskrit: प्राणात्यये विवाहे वा सर्वज्ञातिधनक्षये ।नर्मण्यभिप्रवृत्ते वा प्रवक्तव्यं मृषा भवेत् ।अधर्मं नात्र पश्यन्ति धर्मतत्त्वार्थदर्शिनः ॥५३॥
transliteration: prāṇātyaye vivāhe vā sarvajñātidhanakṣaye ,narmaṇyabhipravṛtte vā pravaktavyaṁ mṛṣā bhavet ,adharmaṁ nātra paśyanti dharmatattvārthadarśinaḥ.
english: A falsehood may be spoken when life is at stake, or at a marriage, or to prevent the complete loss of family wealth, or when jesting is taking place. Those who understand the true essence of (natural) law (dharma) do not see unrighteousness (adharma) in such situations.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 49 Verse 53. A falsehood may be spoken when life is at stake, or at a marriage, or to prevent the complete loss of family wealth, or when jesting is taking place. Those who understand the true essence of (natural) law (dharma) do not see unrighteousness (adharma) in such situations.

sanskrit: यः स्तेनैः सह संबन्धान्मुच्यते शपथैरपि ।श्रेयस्तत्रानृतं वक्तुं तत्सत्यमविचारितम् ॥५४॥
transliteration: yaḥ stenaiḥ saha saṁbandhānmucyate śapathairapi ,śreyastatrānṛtaṁ vaktuṁ tatsatyamavicāritam.
english: He who is freed from associations with thieves, even by means of oaths, it is better in that situation to speak a falsehood. Such a falsehood is considered a truth that should not be questioned.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 49 Verse 54. He who is freed from associations with thieves, even by means of oaths, it is better in that situation to speak a falsehood. Such a falsehood is considered a truth that should not be questioned.

sanskrit: न च तेभ्यो धनं देयं शक्ये सति कथंचन ।पापेभ्यो हि धनं दत्तं दातारमपि पीडयेत् ।तस्माद्धर्मार्थमनृतमुक्त्वा नानृतवाग्भवेत् ॥५५॥
transliteration: na ca tebhyo dhanaṁ deyaṁ śakye sati kathaṁcana ,pāpebhyo hi dhanaṁ dattaṁ dātāramapi pīḍayet ,tasmāddharmārthamanṛtamuktvā nānṛtavāgbhavet.
english: And under no circumstances should wealth be given to them (the thieves mentioned in the previous verse) if it is possible to avoid doing so. For wealth given to sinners would indeed harm even the giver. Therefore, having spoken a falsehood for the sake of (natural) law (dharma), one does not become a speaker of untruths.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 49 Verse 55. And under no circumstances should wealth be given to them (the thieves mentioned in the previous verse) if it is possible to avoid doing so. For wealth given to sinners would indeed harm even the giver. Therefore, having spoken a falsehood for the sake of (natural) law (dharma), one does not become a speaker of untruths.

sanskrit: एष ते लक्षणोद्देशः समुद्दिष्टो यथाविधि ।एतच्छ्रुत्वा ब्रूहि पार्थ यदि वध्यो युधिष्ठिरः ॥५६॥
transliteration: eṣa te lakṣaṇoddeśaḥ samuddiṣṭo yathāvidhi ,etacchrutvā brūhi pārtha yadi vadhyo yudhiṣṭhiraḥ.
english: This exposition of the rules has been properly declared to you according to proper procedure. Having heard this, tell me, O Pārtha (Arjuna), whether Yudhishthira is worthy of being killed.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 49 Verse 56. This exposition of the rules has been properly declared to you according to proper procedure. Having heard this, tell me, O Pārtha (Arjuna), whether Yudhishthira is worthy of being killed.

sanskrit: अर्जुन उवाच ।यथा ब्रूयान्महाप्राज्ञो यथा ब्रूयान्महामतिः ।हितं चैव यथास्माकं तथैतद्वचनं तव ॥५७॥
transliteration: arjuna uvāca ,yathā brūyānmahāprājño yathā brūyānmahāmatiḥ ,hitaṁ caiva yathāsmākaṁ tathaitadvacanaṁ tava.
english: Arjuna said: Just as a very wise person or a greatly intelligent person would speak, and just as it would be beneficial for us, so is this statement of yours.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 49 Verse 57. Arjuna said: Just as a very wise person or a greatly intelligent person would speak, and just as it would be beneficial for us, so is this statement of yours.

sanskrit: भवान्मातृसमोऽस्माकं तथा पितृसमोऽपि च ।गतिश्च परमा कृष्ण तेन ते वाक्यमद्भुतम् ॥५८॥
transliteration: bhavānmātṛsamo'smākaṁ tathā pitṛsamo'pi ca ,gatiśca paramā kṛṣṇa tena te vākyamadbhutam.
english: You are like a mother to us, and also like a father. And you, Kṛṣṇa, are our supreme refuge. Therefore, your words are amazing.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 49 Verse 58. You are like a mother to us, and also like a father. And you, Kṛṣṇa, are our supreme refuge. Therefore, your words are amazing.

sanskrit: न हि ते त्रिषु लोकेषु विद्यतेऽविदितं क्वचित् ।तस्माद्भवान्परं धर्मं वेद सर्वं यथातथम् ॥५९॥
transliteration: na hi te triṣu lokeṣu vidyate'viditaṁ kvacit ,tasmādbhavānparaṁ dharmaṁ veda sarvaṁ yathātatham.
english: Indeed, there is nothing unknown to you anywhere in the three worlds. Therefore, you know the supreme natural law (dharma) completely, exactly as it is.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 49 Verse 59. Indeed, there is nothing unknown to you anywhere in the three worlds. Therefore, you know the supreme natural law (dharma) completely, exactly as it is.

sanskrit: अवध्यं पाण्डवं मन्ये धर्मराजं युधिष्ठिरम् ।तस्मिन्समयसंयोगे ब्रूहि किंचिदनुग्रहम् ।इदं चापरमत्रैव शृणु हृत्स्थं विवक्षितम् ॥६०॥
transliteration: avadhyaṁ pāṇḍavaṁ manye dharmarājaṁ yudhiṣṭhiram ,tasminsamayasaṁyoge brūhi kiṁcidanugraham ,idaṁ cāparamatraiva śṛṇu hṛtsthaṁ vivakṣitam.
english: I consider Yudhiṣṭhira, the Pāṇḍava upholding natural law (dharma), to be inviolable. Given this critical juncture, please show some favor. Also, listen here to this other thing that is in my heart and that I wish to express.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 49 Verse 60. I consider Yudhiṣṭhira, the Pāṇḍava upholding natural law (dharma), to be inviolable. Given this critical juncture, please show some favor. Also, listen here to this other thing that is in my heart and that I wish to express.

sanskrit: जानासि दाशार्ह मम व्रतं त्वं यो मां ब्रूयात्कश्चन मानुषेषु ।अन्यस्मै त्वं गाण्डिवं देहि पार्थ यस्त्वत्तोऽस्त्रैर्भविता वा विशिष्टः ॥६१॥
transliteration: jānāsi dāśārha mama vrataṁ tvaṁ; yo māṁ brūyātkaścana mānuṣeṣu ,anyasmai tvaṁ gāṇḍivaṁ dehi pārtha; yastvatto'strairbhavitā vā viśiṣṭaḥ.
english: O Dāśārha (Kṛṣṇa), you know my vow: if any man were to tell me (to give up my bow), I would kill him. (Now I am faced with such a command:) "O Pārtha, you should give your (Gāṇḍīva) bow to someone else who might be superior to you in weapons."
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 49 Verse 61. O Dāśārha (Kṛṣṇa), you know my vow: if any man were to tell me (to give up my bow), I would kill him. (Now I am faced with such a command:) "O Pārtha, you should give your (Gāṇḍīva) bow to someone else who might be superior to you in weapons."

sanskrit: हन्यामहं केशव तं प्रसह्य भीमो हन्यात्तूबरकेति चोक्तः ।तन्मे राजा प्रोक्तवांस्ते समक्षं धनुर्देहीत्यसकृद्वृष्णिसिंह ॥६२॥
transliteration: hanyāmahaṁ keśava taṁ prasahya; bhīmo hanyāttūbaraketi coktaḥ ,tanme rājā proktavāṁste samakṣaṁ; dhanurdehītyasakṛdvṛṣṇisiṁha.
english: O Keśava (Kṛṣṇa), I would forcibly kill that person (who tells me to give up my bow). It was also said (by the king) that "Bhīma should kill the incompetent one (tūbaraka)" – an insult directed at me. That king (Yudhiṣṭhira) repeatedly told me, in your presence, "Give up your bow," O lion among the Vṛṣṇis.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 49 Verse 62. O Keśava (Kṛṣṇa), I would forcibly kill that person (who tells me to give up my bow). It was also said (by the king) that "Bhīma should kill the incompetent one (tūbaraka)" – an insult directed at me. That king (Yudhiṣṭhira) repeatedly told me, in your presence, "Give up your bow," O lion among the Vṛṣṇis.

sanskrit: तं हत्वा चेत्केशव जीवलोके स्थाता कालं नाहमप्यल्पमात्रम् ।सा च प्रतिज्ञा मम लोकप्रबुद्धा भवेत्सत्या धर्मभृतां वरिष्ठ ।यथा जीवेत्पाण्डवोऽहं च कृष्ण तथा बुद्धिं दातुमद्यार्हसि त्वम् ॥६३॥
transliteration: taṁ hatvā cetkeśava jīvaloke; sthātā kālaṁ nāhamapyalpamātram ,sā ca pratijñā mama lokaprabuddhā; bhavetsatyā dharmabhṛtāṁ variṣṭha ,yathā jīvetpāṇḍavo'haṁ ca kṛṣṇa; tathā buddhiṁ dātumadyārhasi tvam.
english: O Keśava (Kṛṣṇa), if I were to kill him (Yudhiṣṭhira), I would not remain in the world of living beings for even a short time. And my vow, renowned in the world, should remain true, O best among the upholders of natural law (dharma). Therefore, O Kṛṣṇa, you ought to give counsel today so that both I and the Pāṇḍava (Yudhiṣṭhira) may live.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 49 Verse 63. O Keśava (Kṛṣṇa), if I were to kill him (Yudhiṣṭhira), I would not remain in the world of living beings for even a short time. And my vow, renowned in the world, should remain true, O best among the upholders of natural law (dharma). Therefore, O Kṛṣṇa, you ought to give counsel today so that both I and the Pāṇḍava (Yudhiṣṭhira) may live.

sanskrit: वासुदेव उवाच ।राजा श्रान्तो जगतो विक्षतश्च कर्णेन संख्ये निशितैर्बाणसंघैः ।तस्मात्पार्थ त्वां परुषाण्यवोचत्कर्णे द्यूतं ह्यद्य रणे निबद्धम् ॥६४॥
transliteration: vāsudeva uvāca ,rājā śrānto jagato vikṣataśca; karṇena saṁkhye niśitairbāṇasaṁghaiḥ ,tasmātpārtha tvāṁ paruṣāṇyavoca;tkarṇe dyūtaṁ hyadya raṇe nibaddham.
english: Vāsudeva (Kṛṣṇa) said: "The king (Yudhiṣṭhira) is weary of the world and wounded by Karṇa's sharp volleys of arrows in battle. Therefore, O Pārtha, he spoke harsh words to you. Indeed, today in battle, the entire struggle (dyūtam) is focused on Karṇa."
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 49 Verse 64. Vāsudeva (Kṛṣṇa) said: "The king (Yudhiṣṭhira) is weary of the world and wounded by Karṇa's sharp volleys of arrows in battle. Therefore, O Pārtha, he spoke harsh words to you. Indeed, today in battle, the entire struggle (dyūtam) is focused on Karṇa."

sanskrit: तस्मिन्हते कुरवो निर्जिताः स्युरेवंबुद्धिः पार्थिवो धर्मपुत्रः ।यदावमानं लभते महान्तं तदा जीवन्मृत इत्युच्यते सः ॥६५॥
transliteration: tasminhate kuravo nirjitāḥ syu;revaṁbuddhiḥ pārthivo dharmaputraḥ ,yadāvamānaṁ labhate mahāntaṁ; tadā jīvanmṛta ityucyate saḥ.
english: The king, son of the intrinsic nature (dharma-putra), holds the belief that if that person is killed, the Kauravas would be conquered. Yet, when such a great individual (referring to Yudhiṣṭhira) suffers immense disrespect, he is then declared to be dead while still alive.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 49 Verse 65. The king, son of the intrinsic nature (dharma-putra), holds the belief that if that person is killed, the Kauravas would be conquered. Yet, when such a great individual (referring to Yudhiṣṭhira) suffers immense disrespect, he is then declared to be dead while still alive.

sanskrit: तन्मानितः पार्थिवोऽयं सदैव त्वया सभीमेन तथा यमाभ्याम् ।वृद्धैश्च लोके पुरुषप्रवीरैस्तस्यावमानं कलया त्वं प्रयुङ्क्ष्व ॥६६॥
transliteration: tanmānitaḥ pārthivo'yaṁ sadaiva; tvayā sabhīmena tathā yamābhyām ,vṛddhaiśca loke puruṣapravīrai;stasyāvamānaṁ kalayā tvaṁ prayuṅkṣva.
english: This king (pārthiva), thus honored (mānita) [as one who takes insult so gravely], is always respected by you, by Bhīma, and by the two Yamas (Nakula and Sahadeva), and also by elders and the chief among men (puruṣapravīra) in the world. Therefore, you must inflict disrespect upon him.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 49 Verse 66. This king (pārthiva), thus honored (mānita) [as one who takes insult so gravely], is always respected by you, by Bhīma, and by the two Yamas (Nakula and Sahadeva), and also by elders and the chief among men (puruṣapravīra) in the world. Therefore, you must inflict disrespect upon him.

sanskrit: त्वमित्यत्रभवन्तं त्वं ब्रूहि पार्थ युधिष्ठिरम् ।त्वमित्युक्तो हि निहतो गुरुर्भवति भारत ॥६७॥
transliteration: tvamityatrabhavantaṁ tvaṁ brūhi pārtha yudhiṣṭhiram ,tvamityukto hi nihato gururbhavati bhārata.
english: O Pārtha (Arjuna), you must address this venerable Yudhiṣṭhira using the word 'you' (tvam). For indeed, O Bhārata (Arjuna), a revered one (guru) who is addressed with 'you' (tvam) becomes as good as dead.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 49 Verse 67. O Pārtha (Arjuna), you must address this venerable Yudhiṣṭhira using the word 'you' (tvam). For indeed, O Bhārata (Arjuna), a revered one (guru) who is addressed with 'you' (tvam) becomes as good as dead.

sanskrit: एवमाचर कौन्तेय धर्मराजे युधिष्ठिरे ।अधर्मयुक्तं संयोगं कुरुष्वैवं कुरूद्वह ॥६८॥
transliteration: evamācara kaunteya dharmarāje yudhiṣṭhire ,adharmayuktaṁ saṁyogaṁ kuruṣvaivaṁ kurūdvaha.
english: O son of Kuntī (Kaunteya), act in this way towards Yudhiṣṭhira, the king of intrinsic nature (dharma-rāja). O upholder of the Kurus (Kurūdvaha), thus perform this unrighteous act.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 49 Verse 68. O son of Kuntī (Kaunteya), act in this way towards Yudhiṣṭhira, the king of intrinsic nature (dharma-rāja). O upholder of the Kurus (Kurūdvaha), thus perform this unrighteous act.

sanskrit: अथर्वाङ्गिरसी ह्येषा श्रुतीनामुत्तमा श्रुतिः ।अविचार्यैव कार्यैषा श्रेयःकामैर्नरैः सदा ॥६९॥
transliteration: atharvāṅgirasī hyeṣā śrutīnāmuttamā śrutiḥ ,avicāryaiva kāryaiṣā śreyaḥkāmairnaraiḥ sadā.
english: Indeed, this Atharvāṅgirasī revealed text (śruti) is the foremost among all revealed texts. It should always be implemented without hesitation by people who desire ultimate good (śreyaḥ).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 49 Verse 69. Indeed, this Atharvāṅgirasī revealed text (śruti) is the foremost among all revealed texts. It should always be implemented without hesitation by people who desire ultimate good (śreyaḥ).

sanskrit: वधो ह्ययं पाण्डव धर्मराज्ञस्त्वत्तो युक्तो वेत्स्यते चैवमेषः ।ततोऽस्य पादावभिवाद्य पश्चाच्छमं ब्रूयाः सान्त्वपूर्वं च पार्थम् ॥७०॥
transliteration: vadho hyayaṁ pāṇḍava dharmarājña;stvatto yukto vetsyate caivameṣaḥ ,tato'sya pādāvabhivādya paścā;cchamaṁ brūyāḥ sāntvapūrvaṁ ca pārtham.
english: Indeed, O Pāṇḍava (Arjuna), this slaying (of Karṇa) performed by you will be deemed fitting by Dharmarāja (Yudhiṣṭhira), and he will understand it as such. Therefore, afterwards, having respectfully bowed at his (Dharmarāja's) feet, you (Arjuna) should speak words of peace and consolation to Pārtha (Yudhiṣṭhira).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 49 Verse 70. Indeed, O Pāṇḍava (Arjuna), this slaying (of Karṇa) performed by you will be deemed fitting by Dharmarāja (Yudhiṣṭhira), and he will understand it as such. Therefore, afterwards, having respectfully bowed at his (Dharmarāja's) feet, you (Arjuna) should speak words of peace and consolation to Pārtha (Yudhiṣṭhira).

sanskrit: भ्राता प्राज्ञस्तव कोपं न जातु कुर्याद्राजा कंचन पाण्डवेयः ।मुक्तोऽनृताद्भ्रातृवधाच्च पार्थ हृष्टः कर्णं त्वं जहि सूतपुत्रम् ॥७१॥
transliteration: bhrātā prājñastava kopaṁ na jātu; kuryādrājā kaṁcana pāṇḍaveyaḥ ,mukto'nṛtādbhrātṛvadhācca pārtha; hṛṣṭaḥ karṇaṁ tvaṁ jahi sūtaputram.
english: O Pārtha (Arjuna), your wise brother, the Pāṇḍava king (Yudhiṣṭhira), would never harbor any anger towards you. Freed from both falsehood and the (potential) sin of slaying a brother (due to Karṇa's true identity), you should joyfully kill Karṇa, the son of the charioteer.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 49 Verse 71. O Pārtha (Arjuna), your wise brother, the Pāṇḍava king (Yudhiṣṭhira), would never harbor any anger towards you. Freed from both falsehood and the (potential) sin of slaying a brother (due to Karṇa's true identity), you should joyfully kill Karṇa, the son of the charioteer.

sanskrit: संजय उवाच ।इत्येवमुक्तस्तु जनार्दनेन पार्थः प्रशस्याथ सुहृद्वधं तम् ।ततोऽब्रवीदर्जुनो धर्मराजमनुक्तपूर्वं परुषं प्रसह्य ॥७२॥
transliteration: saṁjaya uvāca ,ityevamuktastu janārdanena; pārthaḥ praśasyātha suhṛdvadhaṁ tam ,tato'bravīdarjuno dharmarāja;manuktapūrvaṁ paruṣaṁ prasahya.
english: Sañjaya said: Indeed, having thus been addressed by Janārdana (Kṛṣṇa), Pārtha (Arjuna), accepting that slaying of a kinsman (Karṇa), then Arjuna forcefully spoke harsh words, never before uttered, to Dharmarāja (Yudhiṣṭhira).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 49 Verse 72. Sañjaya said: Indeed, having thus been addressed by Janārdana (Kṛṣṇa), Pārtha (Arjuna), accepting that slaying of a kinsman (Karṇa), then Arjuna forcefully spoke harsh words, never before uttered, to Dharmarāja (Yudhiṣṭhira).

sanskrit: मा त्वं राजन्व्याहर व्याहरत्सु न तिष्ठसे क्रोशमात्रे रणार्धे ।भीमस्तु मामर्हति गर्हणाय यो युध्यते सर्वयोधप्रवीरः ॥७३॥
transliteration: mā tvaṁ rājanvyāhara vyāharatsu; na tiṣṭhase krośamātre raṇārdhe ,bhīmastu māmarhati garhaṇāya; yo yudhyate sarvayodhapravīraḥ.
english: O King, do not speak! For you do not stand among those who are fighting, not even half a krosa into the battle. It is Bhima, who fights as the foremost hero among all warriors, who truly deserves my criticism.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 49 Verse 73. O King, do not speak! For you do not stand among those who are fighting, not even half a krosa into the battle. It is Bhima, who fights as the foremost hero among all warriors, who truly deserves my criticism.

sanskrit: काले हि शत्रून्प्रतिपीड्य संख्ये हत्वा च शूरान्पृथिवीपतींस्तान् ।यः कुञ्जराणामधिकं सहस्रं हत्वानदत्तुमुलं सिंहनादम् ॥७४॥
transliteration: kāle hi śatrūnpratipīḍya saṁkhye; hatvā ca śūrānpṛthivīpatīṁstān ,yaḥ kuñjarāṇāmadhikaṁ sahasraṁ; hatvānadattumulaṁ siṁhanādam.
english: For, at the opportune moment, having greatly afflicted the enemies in battle and having slain those valiant kings, he who then, having killed more than a thousand elephants, let out a tremendous lion's roar.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 49 Verse 74. For, at the opportune moment, having greatly afflicted the enemies in battle and having slain those valiant kings, he who then, having killed more than a thousand elephants, let out a tremendous lion's roar.

sanskrit: सुदुष्करं कर्म करोति वीरः कर्तुं यथा नार्हसि त्वं कदाचित् ।रथादवप्लुत्य गदां परामृशंस्तया निहन्त्यश्वनरद्विपान्रणे ॥७५॥
transliteration: suduṣkaraṁ karma karoti vīraḥ; kartuṁ yathā nārhasi tvaṁ kadācit ,rathādavaplutya gadāṁ parāmṛśaṁ;stayā nihantyaśvanaradvipānraṇe.
english: The hero performs extremely difficult deeds, such as you would never be capable of doing. Leaping down from his chariot and seizing his mace, he uses it to strike down horses, men, and elephants in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 49 Verse 75. The hero performs extremely difficult deeds, such as you would never be capable of doing. Leaping down from his chariot and seizing his mace, he uses it to strike down horses, men, and elephants in battle.

sanskrit: वरासिना वाजिरथाश्वकुञ्जरांस्तथा रथाङ्गैर्धनुषा च हन्त्यरीन् ।प्रमृद्य पद्भ्यामहितान्निहन्ति यः पुनश्च दोर्भ्यां शतमन्युविक्रमः ॥७६॥
transliteration: varāsinā vājirathāśvakuñjarāṁ;stathā rathāṅgairdhanuṣā ca hantyarīn ,pramṛdya padbhyāmahitānnihanti yaḥ; punaśca dorbhyāṁ śatamanyuvikramaḥ.
english: He who, with an excellent sword, slays chariot-horses, other horses, and elephants, and also enemies with chariot wheels and his bow; having crushed his foes with his feet, he again slays them with his two arms, possessing the prowess of Indra (Śatamanu).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 49 Verse 76. He who, with an excellent sword, slays chariot-horses, other horses, and elephants, and also enemies with chariot wheels and his bow; having crushed his foes with his feet, he again slays them with his two arms, possessing the prowess of Indra (Śatamanu).

sanskrit: महाबलो वैश्रवणान्तकोपमः प्रसह्य हन्ता द्विषतां यथार्हम् ।स भीमसेनोऽर्हति गर्हणां मे न त्वं नित्यं रक्ष्यसे यः सुहृद्भिः ॥७७॥
transliteration: mahābalo vaiśravaṇāntakopamaḥ; prasahya hantā dviṣatāṁ yathārham ,sa bhīmaseno'rhati garhaṇāṁ me; na tvaṁ nityaṁ rakṣyase yaḥ suhṛdbhiḥ.
english: That Bhimasena, who is exceedingly mighty, comparable to Kubera and Yama, and who forcefully slays enemies as is fitting, he deserves my censure. Not you [do I censure], for you are always protected by your friends.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 49 Verse 77. That Bhimasena, who is exceedingly mighty, comparable to Kubera and Yama, and who forcefully slays enemies as is fitting, he deserves my censure. Not you [do I censure], for you are always protected by your friends.

sanskrit: महारथान्नागवरान्हयांश्च पदातिमुख्यानपि च प्रमथ्य ।एको भीमो धार्तराष्ट्रेषु मग्नः स मामुपालब्धुमरिंदमोऽर्हति ॥७८॥
transliteration: mahārathānnāgavarānhayāṁśca; padātimukhyānapi ca pramathya ,eko bhīmo dhārtarāṣṭreṣu magnaḥ; sa māmupālabdhumariṁdamo'rhati.
english: Having crushed great charioteers, the finest elephants, horses, and even the foremost foot soldiers, that Bhima, the subduer of enemies, became immersed alone among the sons of Dhṛtarāṣṭra. He truly deserves to censure me.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 49 Verse 78. Having crushed great charioteers, the finest elephants, horses, and even the foremost foot soldiers, that Bhima, the subduer of enemies, became immersed alone among the sons of Dhṛtarāṣṭra. He truly deserves to censure me.

sanskrit: कलिङ्गवङ्गाङ्गनिषादमागधान्सदामदान्नीलबलाहकोपमान् ।निहन्ति यः शत्रुगणाननेकशः स माभिवक्तुं प्रभवत्यनागसम् ॥७९॥
transliteration: kaliṅgavaṅgāṅganiṣādamāgadhā;nsadāmadānnīlabalāhakopamān ,nihanti yaḥ śatrugaṇānanekaśaḥ; sa mābhivaktuṁ prabhavatyanāgasam.
english: He who repeatedly slays various hosts of enemies – the Kalingas, Vangas, Angas, Niṣādas, and Māgadhas, who are ever-arrogant and resemble dark storm clouds – that Bhima is able to cast blame upon me, even though I am faultless.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 49 Verse 79. He who repeatedly slays various hosts of enemies – the Kalingas, Vangas, Angas, Niṣādas, and Māgadhas, who are ever-arrogant and resemble dark storm clouds – that Bhima is able to cast blame upon me, even though I am faultless.

sanskrit: सुयुक्तमास्थाय रथं हि काले धनुर्विकर्षञ्शरपूर्णमुष्टिः ।सृजत्यसौ शरवर्षाणि वीरो महाहवे मेघ इवाम्बुधाराः ॥८०॥
transliteration: suyuktamāsthāya rathaṁ hi kāle; dhanurvikarṣañśarapūrṇamuṣṭiḥ ,sṛjatyasau śaravarṣāṇi vīro; mahāhave megha ivāmbudhārāḥ.
english: Indeed, having mounted his well-equipped chariot at the opportune moment, drawing his bow with a hand full of arrows, that hero unleashes showers of arrows in a great battle, just as a cloud releases torrents of water.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 49 Verse 80. Indeed, having mounted his well-equipped chariot at the opportune moment, drawing his bow with a hand full of arrows, that hero unleashes showers of arrows in a great battle, just as a cloud releases torrents of water.

sanskrit: बलं तु वाचि द्विजसत्तमानां क्षात्रं बुधा बाहुबलं वदन्ति ।त्वं वाग्बलो भारत निष्ठुरश्च त्वमेव मां वेत्सि यथाविधोऽहम् ॥८१॥
transliteration: balaṁ tu vāci dvijasattamānāṁ; kṣātraṁ budhā bāhubalaṁ vadanti ,tvaṁ vāgbalo bhārata niṣṭhuraśca; tvameva māṁ vetsi yathāvidho'ham.
english: But the wise declare that the strength (bala) of the best among the twice-born (dvija) lies in speech, while warrior strength (kṣātra) is arm-power. O Bhārata, you are strong in speech and harsh, and you alone know me as I truly am.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 49 Verse 81. But the wise declare that the strength (bala) of the best among the twice-born (dvija) lies in speech, while warrior strength (kṣātra) is arm-power. O Bhārata, you are strong in speech and harsh, and you alone know me as I truly am.

sanskrit: यतामि नित्यं तव कर्तुमिष्टं दारैः सुतैर्जीवितेनात्मना च ।एवं च मां वाग्विशिखैर्निहंसि त्वत्तः सुखं न वयं विद्म किंचित् ॥८२॥
transliteration: yatāmi nityaṁ tava kartumiṣṭaṁ; dāraiḥ sutairjīvitenātmanā ca ,evaṁ ca māṁ vāgviśikhairnihaṁsi; tvattaḥ sukhaṁ na vayaṁ vidma kiṁcit.
english: I constantly strive to do what is pleasing to you, (sacrificing) my wife, sons, life, and even my self (ātman). Yet, in this manner, you kill me with your arrows of speech. We know no happiness whatsoever from you.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 49 Verse 82. I constantly strive to do what is pleasing to you, (sacrificing) my wife, sons, life, and even my self (ātman). Yet, in this manner, you kill me with your arrows of speech. We know no happiness whatsoever from you.

sanskrit: अवामंस्था मां द्रौपदीतल्पसंस्थो महारथान्प्रतिहन्मि त्वदर्थे ।तेनातिशङ्की भारत निष्ठुरोऽसि त्वत्तः सुखं नाभिजानामि किंचित् ॥८३॥
transliteration: avāmaṁsthā māṁ draupadītalpasaṁstho; mahārathānpratihanmi tvadarthe ,tenātiśaṅkī bhārata niṣṭhuro'si; tvattaḥ sukhaṁ nābhijānāmi kiṁcit.
english: You have disrespected me, who, positioned by Draupadī's bed, slays great warriors (mahāratha) for your sake. Therefore, O Bhārata, you are overly suspicious and harsh. I know no happiness whatsoever from you.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 49 Verse 83. You have disrespected me, who, positioned by Draupadī's bed, slays great warriors (mahāratha) for your sake. Therefore, O Bhārata, you are overly suspicious and harsh. I know no happiness whatsoever from you.

sanskrit: प्रोक्तः स्वयं सत्यसंधेन मृत्युस्तव प्रियार्थं नरदेव युद्धे ।वीरः शिखण्डी द्रौपदोऽसौ महात्मा मयाभिगुप्तेन हतश्च तेन ॥८४॥
transliteration: proktaḥ svayaṁ satyasaṁdhena mṛtyu;stava priyārthaṁ naradeva yuddhe ,vīraḥ śikhaṇḍī draupado'sau mahātmā; mayābhiguptena hataśca tena.
english: O king (naradeva), my death for your beloved's sake was declared by the Truth-bound (Satyasaṃdha) himself in battle. And that heroic, great-souled Śikhaṇḍī, son of Drupada – through him, while he is protected by me, I shall be struck down.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 49 Verse 84. O king (naradeva), my death for your beloved's sake was declared by the Truth-bound (Satyasaṃdha) himself in battle. And that heroic, great-souled Śikhaṇḍī, son of Drupada – through him, while he is protected by me, I shall be struck down.

sanskrit: न चाभिनन्दामि तवाधिराज्यं यतस्त्वमक्षेष्वहिताय सक्तः ।स्वयं कृत्वा पापमनार्यजुष्टमेभिर्युद्धे तर्तुमिच्छस्यरींस्तु ॥८५॥
transliteration: na cābhinandāmi tavādhirājyaṁ; yatastvamakṣeṣvahitāya saktaḥ ,svayaṁ kṛtvā pāpamanāryajuṣṭa;mebhiryuddhe tartumicchasyarīṁstu.
english: I do not approve of your dominion (adhirājya) because you are addicted to gambling (akṣeṣu) to your own detriment. Having yourself committed a sinful act (pāpam), one that is practiced by ignoble people, you now wish to overcome these enemies in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 49 Verse 85. I do not approve of your dominion (adhirājya) because you are addicted to gambling (akṣeṣu) to your own detriment. Having yourself committed a sinful act (pāpam), one that is practiced by ignoble people, you now wish to overcome these enemies in battle.

sanskrit: अक्षेषु दोषा बहवो विधर्माः श्रुतास्त्वया सहदेवोऽब्रवीद्यान् ।तान्नैषि संतर्तुमसाधुजुष्टान्येन स्म सर्वे निरयं प्रपन्नाः ॥८६॥
transliteration: akṣeṣu doṣā bahavo vidharmāḥ; śrutāstvayā sahadevo'bravīdyān ,tānnaiṣi saṁtartumasādhujuṣṭā;nyena sma sarve nirayaṁ prapannāḥ.
english: Many faults (doṣāḥ), which are contrary to natural law (dharma) and associated with gambling (akṣeṣu), were recounted by Sahadeva, and you heard them. You do not wish to overcome those faults, which are indulged in by ignoble persons, and because of which (gambling), indeed, all of us have fallen into hell (nirayam).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 49 Verse 86. Many faults (doṣāḥ), which are contrary to natural law (dharma) and associated with gambling (akṣeṣu), were recounted by Sahadeva, and you heard them. You do not wish to overcome those faults, which are indulged in by ignoble persons, and because of which (gambling), indeed, all of us have fallen into hell (nirayam).

sanskrit: त्वं देविता त्वत्कृते राज्यनाशस्त्वत्संभवं व्यसनं नो नरेन्द्र ।मास्मान्क्रूरैर्वाक्प्रतोदैस्तुद त्वं भूयो राजन्कोपयन्नल्पभाग्यान् ॥८७॥
transliteration: tvaṁ devitā tvatkṛte rājyanāśa;stvatsaṁbhavaṁ vyasanaṁ no narendra ,māsmānkrūrairvākpratodaistuda tvaṁ; bhūyo rājankopayannalpabhāgyān.
english: You are the gambler (devitā); because of you, O ruler (narendra), is the destruction of our kingdom, and this misfortune (vyasanam) is born from you. O King, do not torment us again with cruel verbal goads (vākpratodaiḥ), further enraging us, the ill-fated (alpabhāgyān) ones.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 49 Verse 87. You are the gambler (devitā); because of you, O ruler (narendra), is the destruction of our kingdom, and this misfortune (vyasanam) is born from you. O King, do not torment us again with cruel verbal goads (vākpratodaiḥ), further enraging us, the ill-fated (alpabhāgyān) ones.

sanskrit: एता वाचः परुषाः सव्यसाची स्थिरप्रज्ञं श्रावयित्वा ततक्ष ।तदानुतेपे सुरराजपुत्रो विनिःश्वसंश्चाप्यसिमुद्बबर्ह ॥८८॥
transliteration: etā vācaḥ paruṣāḥ savyasācī; sthiraprajñaṁ śrāvayitvā tatakṣa ,tadānutepe surarājaputro; viniḥśvasaṁścāpyasimudbabarha.
english: Savyasacin (Arjuna), after reciting these harsh words to the steadfast-minded (sthiraprajñam) one, then finished his speech. Thereupon, the son of the lord of the gods (surarājaputraḥ), Arjuna, felt regret, and sighing deeply, he also drew his sword.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 49 Verse 88. Savyasacin (Arjuna), after reciting these harsh words to the steadfast-minded (sthiraprajñam) one, then finished his speech. Thereupon, the son of the lord of the gods (surarājaputraḥ), Arjuna, felt regret, and sighing deeply, he also drew his sword.

sanskrit: तमाह कृष्णः किमिदं पुनर्भवान्विकोशमाकाशनिभं करोत्यसिम् ।प्रब्रूहि सत्यं पुनरुत्तरं विधेर्वचः प्रवक्ष्याम्यहमर्थसिद्धये ॥८९॥
transliteration: tamāha kṛṣṇaḥ kimidaṁ punarbhavā;nvikośamākāśanibhaṁ karotyasim ,prabrūhi satyaṁ punaruttaraṁ vidhe;rvacaḥ pravakṣyāmyahamarthasiddhaye.
english: Krishna said to him, "What is this? Why are you again unsheathing a sword that shines like the sky? Speak the truth. Then, I will declare the words of Brahma (Vidhi) as an answer for the accomplishment of your purpose."
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 49 Verse 89. Krishna said to him, "What is this? Why are you again unsheathing a sword that shines like the sky? Speak the truth. Then, I will declare the words of Brahma (Vidhi) as an answer for the accomplishment of your purpose."

sanskrit: इत्येव पृष्टः पुरुषोत्तमेन सुदुःखितः केशवमाह वाक्यम् ।अहं हनिष्ये स्वशरीरमेव प्रसह्य येनाहितमाचरं वै ॥९०॥
transliteration: ityeva pṛṣṭaḥ puruṣottamena; suduḥkhitaḥ keśavamāha vākyam ,ahaṁ haniṣye svaśarīrameva; prasahya yenāhitamācaraṁ vai.
english: Thus questioned by the Supreme Person (Puruṣottama), he, greatly distressed, spoke these words to Keśava: "I will surely kill my own body, because by force I have committed wrong."
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 49 Verse 90. Thus questioned by the Supreme Person (Puruṣottama), he, greatly distressed, spoke these words to Keśava: "I will surely kill my own body, because by force I have committed wrong."

sanskrit: निशम्य तत्पार्थवचोऽब्रवीदिदं धनंजयं धर्मभृतां वरिष्ठः ।प्रब्रूहि पार्थ स्वगुणानिहात्मनस्तथा स्वहार्दं भवतीह सद्यः ॥९१॥
transliteration: niśamya tatpārthavaco'bravīdidaṁ; dhanaṁjayaṁ dharmabhṛtāṁ variṣṭhaḥ ,prabrūhi pārtha svaguṇānihātmana;stathā svahārdaṁ bhavatīha sadyaḥ.
english: Having heard those words of Pārtha, the foremost among the upholders of natural law (dharma) said this to Dhanañjaya: "O Pārtha, speak forth your own qualities (guṇa) of the self (ātman) here, and your inner feeling (hārdam) will immediately become manifest here."
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 49 Verse 91. Having heard those words of Pārtha, the foremost among the upholders of natural law (dharma) said this to Dhanañjaya: "O Pārtha, speak forth your own qualities (guṇa) of the self (ātman) here, and your inner feeling (hārdam) will immediately become manifest here."

sanskrit: तथास्तु कृष्णेत्यभिनन्द्य वाक्यं धनंजयः प्राह धनुर्विनाम्य ।युधिष्ठिरं धर्मभृतां वरिष्ठं शृणुष्व राजन्निति शक्रसूनुः ॥९२॥
transliteration: tathāstu kṛṣṇetyabhinandya vākyaṁ; dhanaṁjayaḥ prāha dhanurvināmya ,yudhiṣṭhiraṁ dharmabhṛtāṁ variṣṭhaṁ; śṛṇuṣva rājanniti śakrasūnuḥ.
english: "So be it, Krishna," thus having approved the words, Dhanañjaya, the son of Indra (Śakra), spoke, bending his bow, to Yudhiṣṭhira, the foremost among the upholders of natural law (dharma): "Listen, O King!"
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 49 Verse 92. "So be it, Krishna," thus having approved the words, Dhanañjaya, the son of Indra (Śakra), spoke, bending his bow, to Yudhiṣṭhira, the foremost among the upholders of natural law (dharma): "Listen, O King!"

sanskrit: न मादृशोऽन्यो नरदेव विद्यते धनुर्धरो देवमृते पिनाकिनम् ।अहं हि तेनानुमतो महात्मना क्षणेन हन्यां सचराचरं जगत् ॥९३॥
transliteration: na mādṛśo'nyo naradeva vidyate; dhanurdharo devamṛte pinākinam ,ahaṁ hi tenānumato mahātmanā; kṣaṇena hanyāṁ sacarācaraṁ jagat.
english: O King, no other archer like me exists, except for the god Pinakin (Śiva). Indeed, being authorized by that great-souled one, I could destroy the entire world, both moving and unmoving, in a single moment.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 49 Verse 93. O King, no other archer like me exists, except for the god Pinakin (Śiva). Indeed, being authorized by that great-souled one, I could destroy the entire world, both moving and unmoving, in a single moment.

sanskrit: मया हि राजन्सदिगीश्वरा दिशो विजित्य सर्वा भवतः कृता वशे ।स राजसूयश्च समाप्तदक्षिणः सभा च दिव्या भवतो ममौजसा ॥९४॥
transliteration: mayā hi rājansadigīśvarā diśo; vijitya sarvā bhavataḥ kṛtā vaśe ,sa rājasūyaśca samāptadakṣiṇaḥ; sabhā ca divyā bhavato mamaujasā.
english: Indeed, O King, by me all directions (diś), along with their lords, were conquered and brought under your control. That Rājasūya Vedic ritual (yajña) with its completed fees, and your divine assembly hall, were also created by my power.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 49 Verse 94. Indeed, O King, by me all directions (diś), along with their lords, were conquered and brought under your control. That Rājasūya Vedic ritual (yajña) with its completed fees, and your divine assembly hall, were also created by my power.

sanskrit: पाणौ पृषत्का लिखिता ममेमे धनुश्च संख्ये विततं सबाणम् ।पादौ च मे सशरौ सहध्वजौ न मादृशं युद्धगतं जयन्ति ॥९५॥
transliteration: pāṇau pṛṣatkā likhitā mameme; dhanuśca saṁkhye vitataṁ sabāṇam ,pādau ca me saśarau sahadhvajau; na mādṛśaṁ yuddhagataṁ jayanti.
english: These arrows in my hand are inscribed with my mark. My bow, strung with arrows, is prepared for battle. My chariot wheels, adorned with arrows and banners, are also ready. No one can conquer a warrior like me when I am engaged in combat.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 49 Verse 95. These arrows in my hand are inscribed with my mark. My bow, strung with arrows, is prepared for battle. My chariot wheels, adorned with arrows and banners, are also ready. No one can conquer a warrior like me when I am engaged in combat.

sanskrit: हता उदीच्या निहताः प्रतीच्याः प्राच्या निरस्ता दाक्षिणात्या विशस्ताः ।संशप्तकानां किंचिदेवावशिष्टं सर्वस्य सैन्यस्य हतं मयार्धम् ॥९६॥
transliteration: hatā udīcyā nihatāḥ pratīcyāḥ; prācyā nirastā dākṣiṇātyā viśastāḥ ,saṁśaptakānāṁ kiṁcidevāvaśiṣṭaṁ; sarvasya sainyasya hataṁ mayārdham.
english: The Northerners were killed, the Westerners were slain, the Easterners were repulsed, and the Southerners were slaughtered. Only a small portion of the Saṃśaptakas (saṃśaptaka) remains, as half of the entire army has been annihilated by me.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 49 Verse 96. The Northerners were killed, the Westerners were slain, the Easterners were repulsed, and the Southerners were slaughtered. Only a small portion of the Saṃśaptakas (saṃśaptaka) remains, as half of the entire army has been annihilated by me.

sanskrit: शेते मया निहता भारती च चमू राजन्देवचमूप्रकाशा ।ये नास्त्रज्ञास्तानहं हन्मि शस्त्रैस्तस्माल्लोकं नेह करोमि भस्मसात् ॥९७॥
transliteration: śete mayā nihatā bhāratī ca; camū rājandevacamūprakāśā ,ye nāstrajñāstānahaṁ hanmi śastrai;stasmāllokaṁ neha karomi bhasmasāt.
english: O King, this Indian army, which shines like an army of gods, lies slain by me. I strike down with my ordinary weapons (śastras) those who do not possess knowledge of divine missiles (astras). Therefore, I do not reduce this entire world to ashes here.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 49 Verse 97. O King, this Indian army, which shines like an army of gods, lies slain by me. I strike down with my ordinary weapons (śastras) those who do not possess knowledge of divine missiles (astras). Therefore, I do not reduce this entire world to ashes here.

sanskrit: इत्येवमुक्त्वा पुनराह पार्थो युधिष्ठिरं धर्मभृतां वरिष्ठम् ।अप्यपुत्रा तेन राधा भवित्री कुन्ती मया वा तदृतं विद्धि राजन् ।प्रसीद राजन्क्षम यन्मयोक्तं काले भवान्वेत्स्यति तन्नमस्ते ॥९८॥
transliteration: ityevamuktvā punarāha pārtho; yudhiṣṭhiraṁ dharmabhṛtāṁ variṣṭham ,apyaputrā tena rādhā bhavitrī; kuntī mayā vā tadṛtaṁ viddhi rājan ,prasīda rājankṣama yanmayoktaṁ; kāle bhavānvetsyati tannamaste.
english: Having spoken thus, Arjuna again said to Yudhiṣṭhira, the foremost among those who uphold natural law (dharma): 'O King, know this truth: Radha will surely be childless because of his death, or rather, Kunti will be childless because of me. O King, be gracious and forgive what I have said; you will understand that in due time. Salutations to you.'
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 49 Verse 98. Having spoken thus, Arjuna again said to Yudhiṣṭhira, the foremost among those who uphold natural law (dharma): 'O King, know this truth: Radha will surely be childless because of his death, or rather, Kunti will be childless because of me. O King, be gracious and forgive what I have said; you will understand that in due time. Salutations to you.'

sanskrit: प्रसाद्य राजानममित्रसाहं स्थितोऽब्रवीच्चैनमभिप्रपन्नः ।याम्येष भीमं समरात्प्रमोक्तुं सर्वात्मना सूतपुत्रं च हन्तुम् ॥९९॥
transliteration: prasādya rājānamamitrasāhaṁ; sthito'bravīccainamabhiprapannaḥ ,yāmyeṣa bhīmaṁ samarātpramoktuṁ; sarvātmanā sūtaputraṁ ca hantum.
english: Having appeased the king, who overcomes enemies, Arjuna stood up and, having approached him reverently, spoke to him: 'Now I go to release Bhima from battle and to wholeheartedly kill Karṇa, the son of the charioteer.'
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 49 Verse 99. Having appeased the king, who overcomes enemies, Arjuna stood up and, having approached him reverently, spoke to him: 'Now I go to release Bhima from battle and to wholeheartedly kill Karṇa, the son of the charioteer.'

sanskrit: तव प्रियार्थं मम जीवितं हि ब्रवीमि सत्यं तदवेहि राजन् ।इति प्रायादुपसंगृह्य पादौ समुत्थितो दीप्ततेजाः किरीटी ।नेदं चिरात्क्षिप्रमिदं भविष्यत्यावर्ततेऽसावभियामि चैनम् ॥१००॥
transliteration: tava priyārthaṁ mama jīvitaṁ hi; bravīmi satyaṁ tadavehi rājan ,iti prāyādupasaṁgṛhya pādau; samutthito dīptatejāḥ kirīṭī ,nedaṁ cirātkṣipramidaṁ bhaviṣya;tyāvartate'sāvabhiyāmi cainam.
english: Indeed, my life is for your sake, O King; know that I speak the truth. Thus, the crowned one (Arjuna), of blazing splendor, having risen, clasped his feet and departed. He declared, 'This will not take long; it will happen swiftly. That one (Karṇa) is returning, and I shall confront him.'
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 49 Verse 100. Indeed, my life is for your sake, O King; know that I speak the truth. Thus, the crowned one (Arjuna), of blazing splendor, having risen, clasped his feet and departed. He declared, 'This will not take long; it will happen swiftly. That one (Karṇa) is returning, and I shall confront him.'

sanskrit: एतच्छ्रुत्वा पाण्डवो धर्मराजो भ्रातुर्वाक्यं परुषं फल्गुनस्य ।उत्थाय तस्माच्छयनादुवाच पार्थं ततो दुःखपरीतचेताः ॥१०१॥
transliteration: etacchrutvā pāṇḍavo dharmarājo; bhrāturvākyaṁ paruṣaṁ phalgunasya ,utthāya tasmācchayanāduvāca; pārthaṁ tato duḥkhaparītacetāḥ.
english: Having heard this harsh speech from his brother Arjuna (phālguna), Yudhiṣṭhira, the Pāṇḍava king (dharmarāja), whose mind was then overwhelmed with sorrow, rose from that bed and spoke to Arjuna (pārtha).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 49 Verse 101. Having heard this harsh speech from his brother Arjuna (phālguna), Yudhiṣṭhira, the Pāṇḍava king (dharmarāja), whose mind was then overwhelmed with sorrow, rose from that bed and spoke to Arjuna (pārtha).

sanskrit: कृतं मया पार्थ यथा न साधु येन प्राप्तं व्यसनं वः सुघोरम् ।तस्माच्छिरश्छिन्द्धि ममेदमद्य कुलान्तकस्याधमपूरुषस्य ॥१०२॥
transliteration: kṛtaṁ mayā pārtha yathā na sādhu; yena prāptaṁ vyasanaṁ vaḥ sughoram ,tasmācchiraśchinddhi mamedamadya; kulāntakasyādhamapūruṣasya.
english: O Arjuna (pārtha), I have done something improper, which has brought upon you all a terrible calamity. Therefore, today, cut off this head of mine, the head of this lowest of men (puruṣa), who has brought ruin upon his family.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 49 Verse 102. O Arjuna (pārtha), I have done something improper, which has brought upon you all a terrible calamity. Therefore, today, cut off this head of mine, the head of this lowest of men (puruṣa), who has brought ruin upon his family.

sanskrit: पापस्य पापव्यसनान्वितस्य विमूढबुद्धेरलसस्य भीरोः ।वृद्धावमन्तुः परुषस्य चैव किं ते चिरं मामनुवृत्य रूक्षम् ॥१०३॥
transliteration: pāpasya pāpavyasanānvitasya; vimūḍhabuddheralasasya bhīroḥ ,vṛddhāvamantuḥ paruṣasya caiva; kiṁ te ciraṁ māmanuvṛtya rūkṣam.
english: Of what use is it to you to continue following me, who am sinful, afflicted by evil habits, deluded in intellect, lazy, fearful, disrespectful of elders, and harsh? What good is it to serve such a wretched person for so long?
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 49 Verse 103. Of what use is it to you to continue following me, who am sinful, afflicted by evil habits, deluded in intellect, lazy, fearful, disrespectful of elders, and harsh? What good is it to serve such a wretched person for so long?

sanskrit: गच्छाम्यहं वनमेवाद्य पापः सुखं भवान्वर्ततां मद्विहीनः ।योग्यो राजा भीमसेनो महात्मा क्लीबस्य वा मम किं राज्यकृत्यम् ॥१०४॥
transliteration: gacchāmyahaṁ vanamevādya pāpaḥ; sukhaṁ bhavānvartatāṁ madvihīnaḥ ,yogyo rājā bhīmaseno mahātmā; klībasya vā mama kiṁ rājyakṛtyam.
english: I, a sinner, will go to the forest today. May you live happily without me. The great-souled Bhīmasena is a suitable king. What use is kingship to me, an effeminate man?
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 49 Verse 104. I, a sinner, will go to the forest today. May you live happily without me. The great-souled Bhīmasena is a suitable king. What use is kingship to me, an effeminate man?

sanskrit: न चास्मि शक्तः परुषाणि सोढुं पुनस्तवेमानि रुषान्वितस्य ।भीमोऽस्तु राजा मम जीवितेन किं कार्यमद्यावमतस्य वीर ॥१०५॥
transliteration: na cāsmi śaktaḥ paruṣāṇi soḍhuṁ; punastavemāni ruṣānvitasya ,bhīmo'stu rājā mama jīvitena; kiṁ kāryamadyāvamatasya vīra.
english: O hero, what is the purpose of my life now that I have been humiliated? I am no longer able to endure these harsh words of yours, which are filled with anger. Let Bhīma indeed be the king.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 49 Verse 105. O hero, what is the purpose of my life now that I have been humiliated? I am no longer able to endure these harsh words of yours, which are filled with anger. Let Bhīma indeed be the king.

sanskrit: इत्येवमुक्त्वा सहसोत्पपात राजा ततस्तच्छयनं विहाय ।इयेष निर्गन्तुमथो वनाय तं वासुदेवः प्रणतोऽभ्युवाच ॥१०६॥
transliteration: ityevamuktvā sahasotpapāta; rājā tatastacchayanaṁ vihāya ,iyeṣa nirgantumatho vanāya; taṁ vāsudevaḥ praṇato'bhyuvāca.
english: Having spoken thus, the king suddenly sprang up. Then, leaving that bed, he desired to depart for the forest. Thereupon, Vāsudeva (Kṛṣṇa), bowing reverently, addressed him.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 49 Verse 106. Having spoken thus, the king suddenly sprang up. Then, leaving that bed, he desired to depart for the forest. Thereupon, Vāsudeva (Kṛṣṇa), bowing reverently, addressed him.

sanskrit: राजन्विदितमेतत्ते यथा गाण्डीवधन्वनः ।प्रतिज्ञा सत्यसंधस्य गाण्डीवं प्रति विश्रुता ॥१०७॥
transliteration: rājanviditametatte yathā gāṇḍīvadhanvanaḥ ,pratijñā satyasaṁdhasya gāṇḍīvaṁ prati viśrutā.
english: O King, it is known to you how well-known the vow is regarding the Gāṇḍīva bow of Arjuna (gāṇḍīvadhanvan), who is true to his promises.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 49 Verse 107. O King, it is known to you how well-known the vow is regarding the Gāṇḍīva bow of Arjuna (gāṇḍīvadhanvan), who is true to his promises.

sanskrit: ब्रूयाद्य एवं गाण्डीवं देह्यन्यस्मै त्वमित्युत ।स वध्योऽस्य पुमाँल्लोके त्वया चोक्तोऽयमीदृशम् ॥१०८॥
transliteration: brūyādya evaṁ gāṇḍīvaṁ dehyanyasmai tvamityuta ,sa vadhyo'sya pumāँlloke tvayā cokto'yamīdṛśam.
english: Indeed, any man who says, 'You give the Gāṇḍīva bow to another,' is to be slain by him (Arjuna) in this world. And you (O King) have just spoken such words.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 49 Verse 108. Indeed, any man who says, 'You give the Gāṇḍīva bow to another,' is to be slain by him (Arjuna) in this world. And you (O King) have just spoken such words.
