sanskrit: अतः सत्यां प्रतिज्ञां तां पार्थेन परिरक्षता ।मच्छन्दादवमानोऽयं कृतस्तव महीपते ।गुरूणामवमानो हि वध इत्यभिधीयते ॥१०९॥
transliteration: ataḥ satyāṁ pratijñāṁ tāṁ pārthena parirakṣatā ,macchandādavamāno'yaṁ kṛtastava mahīpate ,gurūṇāmavamāno hi vadha ityabhidhīyate.
english: Therefore, O King, this insult to you was committed by my will, as Arjuna was protecting that truthful vow. Indeed, disrespect towards elders is considered equivalent to killing.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 49 Verse 109. Therefore, O King, this insult to you was committed by my will, as Arjuna was protecting that truthful vow. Indeed, disrespect towards elders is considered equivalent to killing.

sanskrit: तस्मात्त्वं वै महाबाहो मम पार्थस्य चोभयोः ।व्यतिक्रममिमं राजन्संक्षमस्वार्जुनं प्रति ॥११०॥
transliteration: tasmāttvaṁ vai mahābāho mama pārthasya cobhayoḥ ,vyatikramamimaṁ rājansaṁkṣamasvārjunaṁ prati.
english: Therefore, O mighty-armed King, please forgive this transgression of both myself and Arjuna, particularly with regard to Arjuna.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 49 Verse 110. Therefore, O mighty-armed King, please forgive this transgression of both myself and Arjuna, particularly with regard to Arjuna.

sanskrit: शरणं त्वां महाराज प्रपन्नौ स्व उभावपि ।क्षन्तुमर्हसि मे राजन्प्रणतस्याभियाचतः ॥१११॥
transliteration: śaraṇaṁ tvāṁ mahārāja prapannau sva ubhāvapi ,kṣantumarhasi me rājanpraṇatasyābhiyācataḥ.
english: O great King, both of us have come to you for refuge. O King, you should forgive me, who is humble and imploring.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 49 Verse 111. O great King, both of us have come to you for refuge. O King, you should forgive me, who is humble and imploring.

sanskrit: राधेयस्याद्य पापस्य भूमिः पास्यति शोणितम् ।सत्यं ते प्रतिजानामि हतं विद्ध्यद्य सूतजम् ।यस्येच्छसि वधं तस्य गतमेवाद्य जीवितम् ॥११२॥
transliteration: rādheyasyādya pāpasya bhūmiḥ pāsyati śoṇitam ,satyaṁ te pratijānāmi hataṁ viddhyadya sūtajam ,yasyecchasi vadhaṁ tasya gatamevādya jīvitam.
english: Today, the earth will drink the blood of the sinful Radheya (Karna). I truly promise you: consider the charioteer's son slain today. Indeed, the life of whoever you wish dead is gone today.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 49 Verse 112. Today, the earth will drink the blood of the sinful Radheya (Karna). I truly promise you: consider the charioteer's son slain today. Indeed, the life of whoever you wish dead is gone today.

sanskrit: इति कृष्णवचः श्रुत्वा धर्मराजो युधिष्ठिरः ।ससंभ्रमं हृषीकेशमुत्थाप्य प्रणतं तदा ।कृताञ्जलिमिदं वाक्यमुवाचानन्तरं वचः ॥११३॥
transliteration: iti kṛṣṇavacaḥ śrutvā dharmarājo yudhiṣṭhiraḥ ,sasaṁbhramaṁ hṛṣīkeśamutthāpya praṇataṁ tadā ,kṛtāñjalimidaṁ vākyamuvācānantaraṁ vacaḥ.
english: Having heard these words of Kṛṣṇa, King Yudhiṣṭhira (dharma), then, with great respect, raised Hṛṣīkeśa (who had bowed down), and with folded hands, spoke the following words.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 49 Verse 113. Having heard these words of Kṛṣṇa, King Yudhiṣṭhira (dharma), then, with great respect, raised Hṛṣīkeśa (who had bowed down), and with folded hands, spoke the following words.

sanskrit: एवमेतद्यथात्थ त्वमस्त्येषोऽतिक्रमो मम ।अनुनीतोऽस्मि गोविन्द तारितश्चाद्य माधव ।मोक्षिता व्यसनाद्घोराद्वयमद्य त्वयाच्युत ॥११४॥
transliteration: evametadyathāttha tvamastyeṣo'tikramo mama ,anunīto'smi govinda tāritaścādya mādhava ,mokṣitā vyasanādghorādvayamadya tvayācyuta.
english: Indeed, what you have said is exactly true; this fault is mine. O Govinda, I am now appeased, and today, O Mādhava, I have been delivered. O Acyuta, we have been liberated (mokṣa) today from a terrible calamity by you.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 49 Verse 114. Indeed, what you have said is exactly true; this fault is mine. O Govinda, I am now appeased, and today, O Mādhava, I have been delivered. O Acyuta, we have been liberated (mokṣa) today from a terrible calamity by you.

sanskrit: भवन्तं नाथमासाद्य आवां व्यसनसागरात् ।घोरादद्य समुत्तीर्णावुभावज्ञानमोहितौ ॥११५॥
transliteration: bhavantaṁ nāthamāsādya āvāṁ vyasanasāgarāt ,ghorādadya samuttīrṇāvubhāvajñānamohitau.
english: Having obtained you as our protector, both of us, who were bewildered by ignorance, have today emerged from the terrible ocean of calamity.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 49 Verse 115. Having obtained you as our protector, both of us, who were bewildered by ignorance, have today emerged from the terrible ocean of calamity.

sanskrit: त्वद्बुद्धिप्लवमासाद्य दुःखशोकार्णवाद्वयम् ।समुत्तीर्णाः सहामात्याः सनाथाः स्म त्वयाच्युत ॥११६॥
transliteration: tvadbuddhiplavamāsādya duḥkhaśokārṇavādvayam ,samuttīrṇāḥ sahāmātyāḥ sanāthāḥ sma tvayācyuta.
english: O Acyuta, having found refuge in the boat of your wisdom, we, along with our ministers, have been protected by you and have crossed over the ocean of sorrow and grief.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 49 Verse 116. O Acyuta, having found refuge in the boat of your wisdom, we, along with our ministers, have been protected by you and have crossed over the ocean of sorrow and grief.

sanskrit: संजय उवाच ।इति स्म कृष्णवचनात्प्रत्युच्चार्य युधिष्ठिरम् ।बभूव विमनाः पार्थः किंचित्कृत्वेव पातकम् ॥१॥
transliteration: saṁjaya uvāca ,iti sma kṛṣṇavacanātpratyuccārya yudhiṣṭhiram ,babhūva vimanāḥ pārthaḥ kiṁcitkṛtveva pātakam.
english: Sanjaya said: Thus, having addressed Yudhishthira in response, influenced by Krishna's words, Arjuna (Pārtha) became disheartened, as if he had committed some sin.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 50 Verse 1. Sanjaya said: Thus, having addressed Yudhishthira in response, influenced by Krishna's words, Arjuna (Pārtha) became disheartened, as if he had committed some sin.

sanskrit: ततोऽब्रवीद्वासुदेवः प्रहसन्निव पाण्डवम् ।कथं नाम भवेदेतद्यदि त्वं पार्थ धर्मजम् ।असिना तीक्ष्णधारेण हन्या धर्मे व्यवस्थितम् ॥२॥
transliteration: tato'bravīdvāsudevaḥ prahasanniva pāṇḍavam ,kathaṁ nāma bhavedetadyadi tvaṁ pārtha dharmajam ,asinā tīkṣṇadhāreṇa hanyā dharme vyavasthitam.
english: Then Vasudeva (Krishna), as if smiling, said to Arjuna (Pāṇḍava): "How could this possibly happen, if you, Arjuna (Pārtha), were to strike down Yudhishthira (Dharmaja), who is established in righteousness (dharma), with a sharp-edged sword?"
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 50 Verse 2. Then Vasudeva (Krishna), as if smiling, said to Arjuna (Pāṇḍava): "How could this possibly happen, if you, Arjuna (Pārtha), were to strike down Yudhishthira (Dharmaja), who is established in righteousness (dharma), with a sharp-edged sword?"

sanskrit: त्वमित्युक्त्वैव राजानमेवं कश्मलमाविशः ।हत्वा तु नृपतिं पार्थ अकरिष्यः किमुत्तरम् ।एवं सुदुर्विदो धर्मो मन्दप्रज्ञैर्विशेषतः ॥३॥
transliteration: tvamityuktvaiva rājānamevaṁ kaśmalamāviśaḥ ,hatvā tu nṛpatiṁ pārtha akariṣyaḥ kimuttaram ,evaṁ sudurvido dharmo mandaprajñairviśeṣataḥ.
english: Having thus said 'you' to the king, you (Arjuna) entered into such a state of despondency. But if you, Arjuna (Pārtha), were to kill the king, what would be the consequence? Indeed, the principle of natural law (dharma) is very difficult to know, especially for those of dull intellect.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 50 Verse 3. Having thus said 'you' to the king, you (Arjuna) entered into such a state of despondency. But if you, Arjuna (Pārtha), were to kill the king, what would be the consequence? Indeed, the principle of natural law (dharma) is very difficult to know, especially for those of dull intellect.

sanskrit: स भवान्धर्मभीरुत्वाद्ध्रुवमैष्यन्महत्तमः ।नरकं घोररूपं च भ्रातुर्ज्येष्ठस्य वै वधात् ॥४॥
transliteration: sa bhavāndharmabhīrutvāddhruvamaiṣyanmahattamaḥ ,narakaṁ ghorarūpaṁ ca bhrāturjyeṣṭhasya vai vadhāt.
english: You, sir, being afraid of transgressing natural law (dharma), would certainly attain the most terrible, dreadful hell by killing your eldest brother.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 50 Verse 4. You, sir, being afraid of transgressing natural law (dharma), would certainly attain the most terrible, dreadful hell by killing your eldest brother.

sanskrit: स त्वं धर्मभृतां श्रेष्ठं राजानं धर्मसंहितम् ।प्रसादय कुरुश्रेष्ठमेतदत्र मतं मम ॥५॥
transliteration: sa tvaṁ dharmabhṛtāṁ śreṣṭhaṁ rājānaṁ dharmasaṁhitam ,prasādaya kuruśreṣṭhametadatra mataṁ mama.
english: O best of the Kurus, you should propitiate that king, who is the foremost among the upholders of natural law (dharma) and is himself righteous (dharmasaṃhitam). This is my opinion on this matter.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 50 Verse 5. O best of the Kurus, you should propitiate that king, who is the foremost among the upholders of natural law (dharma) and is himself righteous (dharmasaṃhitam). This is my opinion on this matter.

sanskrit: प्रसाद्य भक्त्या राजानं प्रीतं चैव युधिष्ठिरम् ।प्रयामस्त्वरिता योद्धुं सूतपुत्ररथं प्रति ॥६॥
transliteration: prasādya bhaktyā rājānaṁ prītaṁ caiva yudhiṣṭhiram ,prayāmastvaritā yoddhuṁ sūtaputrarathaṁ prati.
english: Having propitiated King Yudhiṣṭhira with devotion (bhakti) and made him pleased, let us quickly proceed to fight towards the chariot of the son of Sūta (Karṇa).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 50 Verse 6. Having propitiated King Yudhiṣṭhira with devotion (bhakti) and made him pleased, let us quickly proceed to fight towards the chariot of the son of Sūta (Karṇa).

sanskrit: हत्वा सुदुर्जयं कर्णं त्वमद्य निशितैः शरैः ।विपुलां प्रीतिमाधत्स्व धर्मपुत्रस्य मानद ॥७॥
transliteration: hatvā sudurjayaṁ karṇaṁ tvamadya niśitaiḥ śaraiḥ ,vipulāṁ prītimādhatsva dharmaputrasya mānada.
english: O giver of honor (mānada), having killed Karṇa, who is very difficult to conquer, with your sharp arrows today, you should bring great joy to the son of Dharma (natural law) (dharmaputra).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 50 Verse 7. O giver of honor (mānada), having killed Karṇa, who is very difficult to conquer, with your sharp arrows today, you should bring great joy to the son of Dharma (natural law) (dharmaputra).

sanskrit: एतदत्र महाबाहो प्राप्तकालं मतं मम ।एवं कृते कृतं चैव तव कार्यं भविष्यति ॥८॥
transliteration: etadatra mahābāho prāptakālaṁ mataṁ mama ,evaṁ kṛte kṛtaṁ caiva tava kāryaṁ bhaviṣyati.
english: O mighty-armed one (mahābāho), this opinion of mine is timely here. When this is done, your objective will indeed be accomplished.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 50 Verse 8. O mighty-armed one (mahābāho), this opinion of mine is timely here. When this is done, your objective will indeed be accomplished.

sanskrit: ततोऽर्जुनो महाराज लज्जया वै समन्वितः ।धर्मराजस्य चरणौ प्रपेदे शिरसानघ ॥९॥
transliteration: tato'rjuno mahārāja lajjayā vai samanvitaḥ ,dharmarājasya caraṇau prapede śirasānagha.
english: Then, O great king, O sinless one, Arjuna, filled with shame, indeed bowed his head at the feet of the king of natural law (dharma).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 50 Verse 9. Then, O great king, O sinless one, Arjuna, filled with shame, indeed bowed his head at the feet of the king of natural law (dharma).

sanskrit: उवाच भरतश्रेष्ठ प्रसीदेति पुनः पुनः ।क्षमस्व राजन्यत्प्रोक्तं धर्मकामेन भीरुणा ॥१०॥
transliteration: uvāca bharataśreṣṭha prasīdeti punaḥ punaḥ ,kṣamasva rājanyatproktaṁ dharmakāmena bhīruṇā.
english: O best among the Bhāratas, he (Arjuna) said again and again, 'Be gracious! O king, forgive what was spoken by a fearful person desiring natural law (dharma).'
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 50 Verse 10. O best among the Bhāratas, he (Arjuna) said again and again, 'Be gracious! O king, forgive what was spoken by a fearful person desiring natural law (dharma).'

sanskrit: पादयोः पतितं दृष्ट्वा धर्मराजो युधिष्ठिरः ।धनंजयममित्रघ्नं रुदन्तं भरतर्षभ ॥११॥
transliteration: pādayoḥ patitaṁ dṛṣṭvā dharmarājo yudhiṣṭhiraḥ ,dhanaṁjayamamitraghnaṁ rudantaṁ bharatarṣabha.
english: O best among the Bhāratas, upon seeing Dhananjaya, the slayer of foes, the king of natural law (dharma) Yudhiṣṭhira, weeping and fallen at his feet -
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 50 Verse 11. O best among the Bhāratas, upon seeing Dhananjaya, the slayer of foes, the king of natural law (dharma) Yudhiṣṭhira, weeping and fallen at his feet -

sanskrit: उत्थाप्य भ्रातरं राजा धर्मराजो धनंजयम् ।समाश्लिष्य च सस्नेहं प्ररुरोद महीपतिः ॥१२॥
transliteration: utthāpya bhrātaraṁ rājā dharmarājo dhanaṁjayam ,samāśliṣya ca sasnehaṁ praruroda mahīpatiḥ.
english: The king, Yudhiṣṭhira, the king of natural law (dharma), having lifted up his brother Dhananjaya and affectionately embraced him, that lord of the earth wept aloud.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 50 Verse 12. The king, Yudhiṣṭhira, the king of natural law (dharma), having lifted up his brother Dhananjaya and affectionately embraced him, that lord of the earth wept aloud.

sanskrit: रुदित्वा तु चिरं कालं भ्रातरौ सुमहाद्युती ।कृतशौचौ नरव्याघ्रौ प्रीतिमन्तौ बभूवतुः ॥१३॥
transliteration: ruditvā tu ciraṁ kālaṁ bhrātarau sumahādyutī ,kṛtaśaucau naravyāghrau prītimantau babhūvatuḥ.
english: After weeping for a long time, the two extremely glorious brothers, these tigers among men, having performed the purification rites, became filled with affection.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 50 Verse 13. After weeping for a long time, the two extremely glorious brothers, these tigers among men, having performed the purification rites, became filled with affection.

sanskrit: तत आश्लिष्य स प्रेम्णा मूर्ध्नि चाघ्राय पाण्डवम् ।प्रीत्या परमया युक्तः प्रस्मयंश्चाब्रवीज्जयम् ॥१४॥
transliteration: tata āśliṣya sa premṇā mūrdhni cāghrāya pāṇḍavam ,prītyā paramayā yuktaḥ prasmayaṁścābravījjayam.
english: Then he, with love, embraced the Pāṇḍava and kissed him on the head. Full of supreme affection and smiling, he spoke to Jaya.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 50 Verse 14. Then he, with love, embraced the Pāṇḍava and kissed him on the head. Full of supreme affection and smiling, he spoke to Jaya.

sanskrit: कर्णेन मे महाबाहो सर्वसैन्यस्य पश्यतः ।कवचं च ध्वजश्चैव धनुः शक्तिर्हया गदा ।शरैः कृत्ता महेष्वास यतमानस्य संयुगे ॥१५॥
transliteration: karṇena me mahābāho sarvasainyasya paśyataḥ ,kavacaṁ ca dhvajaścaiva dhanuḥ śaktirhayā gadā ,śaraiḥ kṛttā maheṣvāsa yatamānasya saṁyuge.
english: O mighty-armed one (mahābāho), by Karṇa, before the eyes of my entire army, my armor, banner, bow, spear, horses, and mace were cut by arrows, even as I was striving in battle, O great archer (maheṣvāsa).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 50 Verse 15. O mighty-armed one (mahābāho), by Karṇa, before the eyes of my entire army, my armor, banner, bow, spear, horses, and mace were cut by arrows, even as I was striving in battle, O great archer (maheṣvāsa).

sanskrit: सोऽहं ज्ञात्वा रणे तस्य कर्म दृष्ट्वा च फल्गुन ।व्यवसीदामि दुःखेन न च मे जीवितं प्रियम् ॥१६॥
transliteration: so'haṁ jñātvā raṇe tasya karma dṛṣṭvā ca phalguna ,vyavasīdāmi duḥkhena na ca me jīvitaṁ priyam.
english: Having known and seen his action (karma) in battle, O Phalguna, I am overcome with sorrow, and my life is no longer dear to me.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 50 Verse 16. Having known and seen his action (karma) in battle, O Phalguna, I am overcome with sorrow, and my life is no longer dear to me.

sanskrit: तमद्य यदि वै वीर न हनिष्यसि सूतजम् ।प्राणानेव परित्यक्ष्ये जीवितार्थो हि को मम ॥१७॥
transliteration: tamadya yadi vai vīra na haniṣyasi sūtajam ,prāṇāneva parityakṣye jīvitārtho hi ko mama.
english: O hero, if you do not kill that son of Sūta today, I will surely abandon my life. For what purpose would my life then serve?
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 50 Verse 17. O hero, if you do not kill that son of Sūta today, I will surely abandon my life. For what purpose would my life then serve?

sanskrit: एवमुक्तः प्रत्युवाच विजयो भरतर्षभ ।सत्येन ते शपे राजन्प्रसादेन तवैव च ।भीमेन च नरश्रेष्ठ यमाभ्यां च महीपते ॥१८॥
transliteration: evamuktaḥ pratyuvāca vijayo bharatarṣabha ,satyena te śape rājanprasādena tavaiva ca ,bhīmena ca naraśreṣṭha yamābhyāṁ ca mahīpate.
english: Addressed thus, Arjuna (Vijaya), O best of Bharatas, replied: 'O King, I swear to you by truth, and by your grace, and by Bhīma, O best among men, and by the two Yamas, O lord of the earth.'
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 50 Verse 18. Addressed thus, Arjuna (Vijaya), O best of Bharatas, replied: 'O King, I swear to you by truth, and by your grace, and by Bhīma, O best among men, and by the two Yamas, O lord of the earth.'

sanskrit: यथाद्य समरे कर्णं हनिष्यामि हतोऽथ वा ।महीतले पतिष्यामि सत्येनायुधमालभे ॥१९॥
transliteration: yathādya samare karṇaṁ haniṣyāmi hato'tha vā ,mahītale patiṣyāmi satyenāyudhamālabhe.
english: Today in battle, I shall either slay Karṇa, or I shall fall dead upon the earth. I take up my weapon by this oath of truth.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 50 Verse 19. Today in battle, I shall either slay Karṇa, or I shall fall dead upon the earth. I take up my weapon by this oath of truth.

sanskrit: एवमाभाष्य राजानमब्रवीन्माधवं वचः ।अद्य कर्णं रणे कृष्ण सूदयिष्ये न संशयः ।तदनुध्याहि भद्रं ते वधं तस्य दुरात्मनः ॥२०॥
transliteration: evamābhāṣya rājānamabravīnmādhavaṁ vacaḥ ,adya karṇaṁ raṇe kṛṣṇa sūdayiṣye na saṁśayaḥ ,tadanudhyāhi bhadraṁ te vadhaṁ tasya durātmanaḥ.
english: Having thus addressed the king, he spoke these words to Kṛṣṇa (Mādhava): 'Today, O Kṛṣṇa, I shall certainly slay Karṇa in battle; there is no doubt about it. Therefore, contemplate this  -  may good fortune be yours  -  the slaying of that evil-souled (ātman) one.'
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 50 Verse 20. Having thus addressed the king, he spoke these words to Kṛṣṇa (Mādhava): 'Today, O Kṛṣṇa, I shall certainly slay Karṇa in battle; there is no doubt about it. Therefore, contemplate this  -  may good fortune be yours  -  the slaying of that evil-souled (ātman) one.'

sanskrit: एवमुक्तोऽब्रवीत्पार्थं केशवो राजसत्तम ।शक्तोऽस्मि भरतश्रेष्ठ यत्नं कर्तुं यथाबलम् ॥२१॥
transliteration: evamukto'bravītpārthaṁ keśavo rājasattama ,śakto'smi bharataśreṣṭha yatnaṁ kartuṁ yathābalam.
english: Thus addressed, Keśava (Krishna), O foremost of kings, said to Pārtha (Arjuna): "O best among the Bhāratas, I am able to make an effort to the best of my ability."
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 50 Verse 21. Thus addressed, Keśava (Krishna), O foremost of kings, said to Pārtha (Arjuna): "O best among the Bhāratas, I am able to make an effort to the best of my ability."

sanskrit: एवं चापि हि मे कामो नित्यमेव महारथ ।कथं भवान्रणे कर्णं निहन्यादिति मे मतिः ॥२२॥
transliteration: evaṁ cāpi hi me kāmo nityameva mahāratha ,kathaṁ bhavānraṇe karṇaṁ nihanyāditi me matiḥ.
english: And indeed, O great warrior, my constant desire is this: 'How will you slay Karṇa in battle?' Such is my conviction.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 50 Verse 22. And indeed, O great warrior, my constant desire is this: 'How will you slay Karṇa in battle?' Such is my conviction.

sanskrit: भूयश्चोवाच मतिमान्माधवो धर्मनन्दनम् ।युधिष्ठिरेमं बीभत्सुं त्वं सान्त्वयितुमर्हसि ।अनुज्ञातुं च कर्णस्य वधायाद्य दुरात्मनः ॥२३॥
transliteration: bhūyaścovāca matimānmādhavo dharmanandanam ,yudhiṣṭhiremaṁ bībhatsuṁ tvaṁ sāntvayitumarhasi ,anujñātuṁ ca karṇasya vadhāyādya durātmanaḥ.
english: Furthermore, the intelligent Mādhava (Krishna) said to the son of (dharma) (Yudhiṣṭhira): "O Yudhiṣṭhira, you should pacify this Bibhatsu (Arjuna). And you should grant permission today for the slaying of the wicked Karṇa."
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 50 Verse 23. Furthermore, the intelligent Mādhava (Krishna) said to the son of (dharma) (Yudhiṣṭhira): "O Yudhiṣṭhira, you should pacify this Bibhatsu (Arjuna). And you should grant permission today for the slaying of the wicked Karṇa."

sanskrit: श्रुत्वा ह्ययमहं चैव त्वां कर्णशरपीडितम् ।प्रवृत्तिं ज्ञातुमायाताविह पाण्डवनन्दन ॥२४॥
transliteration: śrutvā hyayamahaṁ caiva tvāṁ karṇaśarapīḍitam ,pravṛttiṁ jñātumāyātāviha pāṇḍavanandana.
english: For, O son of Pāṇḍu, having heard that you were afflicted by Karṇa's arrows, both I and this (Arjuna) have come here to ascertain the situation.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 50 Verse 24. For, O son of Pāṇḍu, having heard that you were afflicted by Karṇa's arrows, both I and this (Arjuna) have come here to ascertain the situation.

sanskrit: दिष्ट्यासि राजन्निरुजो दिष्ट्या न ग्रहणं गतः ।परिसान्त्वय बीभत्सुं जयमाशाधि चानघ ॥२५॥
transliteration: diṣṭyāsi rājannirujo diṣṭyā na grahaṇaṁ gataḥ ,parisāntvaya bībhatsuṁ jayamāśādhi cānagha.
english: Fortunately, O King, you are uninjured, and fortunately, you were not captured. Console Bhībhatsu (Arjuna), O sinless one, and seek victory.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 50 Verse 25. Fortunately, O King, you are uninjured, and fortunately, you were not captured. Console Bhībhatsu (Arjuna), O sinless one, and seek victory.

sanskrit: युधिष्ठिर उवाच ।एह्येहि पार्थ बीभत्सो मां परिष्वज पाण्डव ।वक्तव्यमुक्तोऽस्म्यहितं त्वया क्षान्तं च तन्मया ॥२६॥
transliteration: yudhiṣṭhira uvāca ,ehyehi pārtha bībhatso māṁ pariṣvaja pāṇḍava ,vaktavyamukto'smyahitaṁ tvayā kṣāntaṁ ca tanmayā.
english: Yudhiṣṭhira said: 'Come, come, O Pārtha (Arjuna), O Bhībhatsu (Arjuna)! Embrace me, O Pāṇḍava (Arjuna)! I have spoken harmful words, which were intended to be said. And that has been forgiven by you, and by me too.'
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 50 Verse 26. Yudhiṣṭhira said: 'Come, come, O Pārtha (Arjuna), O Bhībhatsu (Arjuna)! Embrace me, O Pāṇḍava (Arjuna)! I have spoken harmful words, which were intended to be said. And that has been forgiven by you, and by me too.'

sanskrit: अहं त्वामनुजानामि जहि कर्णं धनंजय ।मन्युं च मा कृथाः पार्थ यन्मयोक्तोऽसि दारुणम् ॥२७॥
transliteration: ahaṁ tvāmanujānāmi jahi karṇaṁ dhanaṁjaya ,manyuṁ ca mā kṛthāḥ pārtha yanmayokto'si dāruṇam.
english: I permit you, O Dhanañjaya (Arjuna), to kill Karṇa. And do not harbor anger (manyu), O Pārtha (Arjuna), for the harsh words which you were spoken by me.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 50 Verse 27. I permit you, O Dhanañjaya (Arjuna), to kill Karṇa. And do not harbor anger (manyu), O Pārtha (Arjuna), for the harsh words which you were spoken by me.

sanskrit: संजय उवाच ।ततो धनंजयो राजञ्शिरसा प्रणतस्तदा ।पादौ जग्राह पाणिभ्यां भ्रातुर्ज्येष्ठस्य मारिष ॥२८॥
transliteration: saṁjaya uvāca ,tato dhanaṁjayo rājañśirasā praṇatastadā ,pādau jagrāha pāṇibhyāṁ bhrāturjyeṣṭhasya māriṣa.
english: Saṃjaya said: 'Then, O King (Dhṛtarāṣṭra), Dhanañjaya (Arjuna) bowed with his head at that time and grasped the feet of his elder brother (Yudhiṣṭhira) with both hands, O respected one.'
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 50 Verse 28. Saṃjaya said: 'Then, O King (Dhṛtarāṣṭra), Dhanañjaya (Arjuna) bowed with his head at that time and grasped the feet of his elder brother (Yudhiṣṭhira) with both hands, O respected one.'

sanskrit: समुत्थाप्य ततो राजा परिष्वज्य च पीडितम् ।मूर्ध्न्युपाघ्राय चैवैनमिदं पुनरुवाच ह ॥२९॥
transliteration: samutthāpya tato rājā pariṣvajya ca pīḍitam ,mūrdhnyupāghrāya caivainamidaṁ punaruvāca ha.
english: Then the king, having raised him up, embraced the distressed one firmly, smelled his head, and again spoke these words to him.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 50 Verse 29. Then the king, having raised him up, embraced the distressed one firmly, smelled his head, and again spoke these words to him.

sanskrit: धनंजय महाबाहो मानितोऽस्मि दृढं त्वया ।माहात्म्यं विजयं चैव भूयः प्राप्नुहि शाश्वतम् ॥३०॥
transliteration: dhanaṁjaya mahābāho mānito'smi dṛḍhaṁ tvayā ,māhātmyaṁ vijayaṁ caiva bhūyaḥ prāpnuhi śāśvatam.
english: O Dhananjaya, O mighty-armed one, I have been greatly honored by you. May you again attain eternal greatness and victory.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 50 Verse 30. O Dhananjaya, O mighty-armed one, I have been greatly honored by you. May you again attain eternal greatness and victory.

sanskrit: अर्जुन उवाच ।अद्य तं पापकर्माणं सानुबन्धं रणे शरैः ।नयाम्यन्तं समासाद्य राधेयं बलगर्वितम् ॥३१॥
transliteration: arjuna uvāca ,adya taṁ pāpakarmāṇaṁ sānubandhaṁ raṇe śaraiḥ ,nayāmyantaṁ samāsādya rādheyaṁ balagarvitam.
english: Arjuna said: "Today, having reached that evil-doer, Radheya (Karna), who is proud of his strength, I will bring him and his followers to their end in battle with arrows."
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 50 Verse 31. Arjuna said: "Today, having reached that evil-doer, Radheya (Karna), who is proud of his strength, I will bring him and his followers to their end in battle with arrows."

sanskrit: येन त्वं पीडितो बाणैर्दृढमायम्य कार्मुकम् ।तस्याद्य कर्मणः कर्णः फलं प्राप्स्यति दारुणम् ॥३२॥
transliteration: yena tvaṁ pīḍito bāṇairdṛḍhamāyamya kārmukam ,tasyādya karmaṇaḥ karṇaḥ phalaṁ prāpsyati dāruṇam.
english: For that deed by which you were tormented by arrows, having firmly drawn his bow, Karna will today obtain a terrible consequence.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 50 Verse 32. For that deed by which you were tormented by arrows, having firmly drawn his bow, Karna will today obtain a terrible consequence.

sanskrit: अद्य त्वामहमेष्यामि कर्णं हत्वा महीपते ।सभाजयितुमाक्रन्दादिति सत्यं ब्रवीमि ते ॥३३॥
transliteration: adya tvāmahameṣyāmi karṇaṁ hatvā mahīpate ,sabhājayitumākrandāditi satyaṁ bravīmi te.
english: O king, today, having killed Karna, I will come to you to honor you, freeing you from lamentation. I tell you this truth.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 50 Verse 33. O king, today, having killed Karna, I will come to you to honor you, freeing you from lamentation. I tell you this truth.

sanskrit: नाहत्वा विनिवर्तेऽहं कर्णमद्य रणाजिरात् ।इति सत्येन ते पादौ स्पृशामि जगतीपते ॥३४॥
transliteration: nāhatvā vinivarte'haṁ karṇamadya raṇājirāt ,iti satyena te pādau spṛśāmi jagatīpate.
english: O lord of the earth, I will not return from the battlefield today without having killed Karna. By this truth, I touch your feet.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 50 Verse 34. O lord of the earth, I will not return from the battlefield today without having killed Karna. By this truth, I touch your feet.

sanskrit: संजय उवाच ।प्रसाद्य धर्मराजानं प्रहृष्टेनान्तरात्मना ।पार्थः प्रोवाच गोविन्दं सूतपुत्रवधोद्यतः ॥३५॥
transliteration: saṁjaya uvāca ,prasādya dharmarājānaṁ prahṛṣṭenāntarātmanā ,pārthaḥ provāca govindaṁ sūtaputravadhodyataḥ.
english: Sanjaya said: Having appeased Dharmaraja (Yudhishthira) with a delighted inner self (ātman), Partha (Arjuna), intent on slaying the son of Sūta (Karna), spoke to Govinda (Krishna).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 50 Verse 35. Sanjaya said: Having appeased Dharmaraja (Yudhishthira) with a delighted inner self (ātman), Partha (Arjuna), intent on slaying the son of Sūta (Karna), spoke to Govinda (Krishna).

sanskrit: कल्प्यतां च रथो भूयो युज्यन्तां च हयोत्तमाः ।आयुधानि च सर्वाणि सज्ज्यन्तां वै महारथे ॥३६॥
transliteration: kalpyatāṁ ca ratho bhūyo yujyantāṁ ca hayottamāḥ ,āyudhāni ca sarvāṇi sajjyantāṁ vai mahārathe.
english: Let the chariot be prepared again, and let the excellent horses be yoked. And indeed, let all weapons be made ready in the great chariot.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 50 Verse 36. Let the chariot be prepared again, and let the excellent horses be yoked. And indeed, let all weapons be made ready in the great chariot.

sanskrit: उपावृत्ताश्च तुरगाः शिक्षिताश्चाश्वसादिनः ।रथोपकरणैः सर्वैरुपायान्तु त्वरान्विताः ॥३७॥
transliteration: upāvṛttāśca turagāḥ śikṣitāścāśvasādinaḥ ,rathopakaraṇaiḥ sarvairupāyāntu tvarānvitāḥ.
english: Let the horses be brought back, and the horsemen be instructed. They should all come swiftly, equipped with all the chariot's apparatus.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 50 Verse 37. Let the horses be brought back, and the horsemen be instructed. They should all come swiftly, equipped with all the chariot's apparatus.

sanskrit: एवमुक्ते महाराज फल्गुनेन महात्मना ।उवाच दारुकं कृष्णः कुरु सर्वं यथाब्रवीत् ।अर्जुनो भरतश्रेष्ठः श्रेष्ठः सर्वधनुष्मताम् ॥३८॥
transliteration: evamukte mahārāja phalgunena mahātmanā ,uvāca dārukaṁ kṛṣṇaḥ kuru sarvaṁ yathābravīt ,arjuno bharataśreṣṭhaḥ śreṣṭhaḥ sarvadhanuṣmatām.
english: O great king, when the magnanimous Arjuna (phalguena) had spoken thus, Kṛṣṇa said to Dāruka, 'Do everything as he has instructed. Arjuna, O best of the Bhāratas, is indeed the foremost of all wielders of bows.'
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 50 Verse 38. O great king, when the magnanimous Arjuna (phalguena) had spoken thus, Kṛṣṇa said to Dāruka, 'Do everything as he has instructed. Arjuna, O best of the Bhāratas, is indeed the foremost of all wielders of bows.'

sanskrit: आज्ञप्तस्त्वथ कृष्णेन दारुको राजसत्तम ।योजयामास स रथं वैयाघ्रं शत्रुतापनम् ॥३९॥
transliteration: ājñaptastvatha kṛṣṇena dāruko rājasattama ,yojayāmāsa sa rathaṁ vaiyāghraṁ śatrutāpanam.
english: Then, O best among kings, Dāruka, having been commanded by Kṛṣṇa, harnessed that tiger-skinned chariot, which was terrifying to foes.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 50 Verse 39. Then, O best among kings, Dāruka, having been commanded by Kṛṣṇa, harnessed that tiger-skinned chariot, which was terrifying to foes.

sanskrit: युक्तं तु रथमास्थाय दारुकेण महात्मना ।आपृच्छ्य धर्मराजानं ब्राह्मणान्स्वस्ति वाच्य च ।समङ्गलस्वस्त्ययनमारुरोह रथोत्तमम् ॥४०॥
transliteration: yuktaṁ tu rathamāsthāya dārukeṇa mahātmanā ,āpṛcchya dharmarājānaṁ brāhmaṇānsvasti vācya ca ,samaṅgalasvastyayanamāruroha rathottamam.
english: Indeed, having mounted the harnessed chariot with the magnanimous Dāruka, and having sought permission from King Yudhiṣṭhira (dharmarājan) and made the brāhmaṇas recite blessings, he (Arjuna) ascended that excellent chariot, which was accompanied by auspicious benedictions.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 50 Verse 40. Indeed, having mounted the harnessed chariot with the magnanimous Dāruka, and having sought permission from King Yudhiṣṭhira (dharmarājan) and made the brāhmaṇas recite blessings, he (Arjuna) ascended that excellent chariot, which was accompanied by auspicious benedictions.

sanskrit: तस्य राजा महाप्राज्ञो धर्मराजो युधिष्ठिरः ।आशिषोऽयुङ्क्त परमा युक्ताः कर्णवधं प्रति ॥४१॥
transliteration: tasya rājā mahāprājño dharmarājo yudhiṣṭhiraḥ ,āśiṣo'yuṅkta paramā yuktāḥ karṇavadhaṁ prati.
english: The very wise King Yudhishthira, the king of righteousness (dharma), offered to him (Arjuna) supreme and appropriate blessings regarding the slaying of Karna.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 50 Verse 41. The very wise King Yudhishthira, the king of righteousness (dharma), offered to him (Arjuna) supreme and appropriate blessings regarding the slaying of Karna.

sanskrit: तं प्रयान्तं महेष्वासं दृष्ट्वा भूतानि भारत ।निहतं मेनिरे कर्णं पाण्डवेन महात्मना ॥४२॥
transliteration: taṁ prayāntaṁ maheṣvāsaṁ dṛṣṭvā bhūtāni bhārata ,nihataṁ menire karṇaṁ pāṇḍavena mahātmanā.
english: O Bharata, seeing him, the great archer, go forth, all beings considered Karna already slain by the great-souled Pandava (Arjuna).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 50 Verse 42. O Bharata, seeing him, the great archer, go forth, all beings considered Karna already slain by the great-souled Pandava (Arjuna).

sanskrit: बभूवुर्विमलाः सर्वा दिशो राजन्समन्ततः ।चाषाश्च शतपत्राश्च क्रौञ्चाश्चैव जनेश्वर ।प्रदक्षिणमकुर्वन्त तदा वै पाण्डुनन्दनम् ॥४३॥
transliteration: babhūvurvimalāḥ sarvā diśo rājansamantataḥ ,cāṣāśca śatapatrāśca krauñcāścaiva janeśvara ,pradakṣiṇamakurvanta tadā vai pāṇḍunandanam.
english: O King, O Lord of men, all directions everywhere became clear and pure. Then, kingfishers, cuckoos, and cranes indeed circumambulated the son of Pandu (Arjuna) clockwise.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 50 Verse 43. O King, O Lord of men, all directions everywhere became clear and pure. Then, kingfishers, cuckoos, and cranes indeed circumambulated the son of Pandu (Arjuna) clockwise.

sanskrit: बहवः पक्षिणो राजन्पुंनामानः शुभाः शिवाः ।त्वरयन्तोऽर्जुनं युद्धे हृष्टरूपा ववाशिरे ॥४४॥
transliteration: bahavaḥ pakṣiṇo rājanpuṁnāmānaḥ śubhāḥ śivāḥ ,tvarayanto'rjunaṁ yuddhe hṛṣṭarūpā vavāśire.
english: O King, many auspicious and benevolent male birds, with joyful forms, cried out, encouraging Arjuna in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 50 Verse 44. O King, many auspicious and benevolent male birds, with joyful forms, cried out, encouraging Arjuna in battle.

sanskrit: कङ्का गृध्रा वडाश्चैव वायसाश्च विशां पते ।अग्रतस्तस्य गच्छन्ति भक्ष्यहेतोर्भयानकाः ॥४५॥
transliteration: kaṅkā gṛdhrā vaḍāścaiva vāyasāśca viśāṁ pate ,agratastasya gacchanti bhakṣyahetorbhayānakāḥ.
english: O lord of the people, terrifying herons, vultures, and crows went before him (Karna), seeking food.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 50 Verse 45. O lord of the people, terrifying herons, vultures, and crows went before him (Karna), seeking food.

sanskrit: निमित्तानि च धन्यानि पार्थस्य प्रशशंसिरे ।विनाशमरिसैन्यानां कर्णस्य च वधं तथा ॥४६॥
transliteration: nimittāni ca dhanyāni pārthasya praśaśaṁsire ,vināśamarisainyānāṁ karṇasya ca vadhaṁ tathā.
english: And auspicious omens portended good fortune for Pārtha (Arjuna), foretelling the destruction of the enemy armies and the death of Karṇa.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 50 Verse 46. And auspicious omens portended good fortune for Pārtha (Arjuna), foretelling the destruction of the enemy armies and the death of Karṇa.

sanskrit: प्रयातस्याथ पार्थस्य महान्स्वेदो व्यजायत ।चिन्ता च विपुला जज्ञे कथं न्वेतद्भविष्यति ॥४७॥
transliteration: prayātasyātha pārthasya mahānsvedo vyajāyata ,cintā ca vipulā jajñe kathaṁ nvetadbhaviṣyati.
english: Then, as Pārtha (Arjuna) set out, a great sweat broke out on him, and a profound anxiety arose, [thinking,] 'How indeed will this come to pass?'
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 50 Verse 47. Then, as Pārtha (Arjuna) set out, a great sweat broke out on him, and a profound anxiety arose, [thinking,] 'How indeed will this come to pass?'

sanskrit: ततो गाण्डीवधन्वानमब्रवीन्मधुसूदनः ।दृष्ट्वा पार्थं तदायस्तं चिन्तापरिगतं तदा ॥४८॥
transliteration: tato gāṇḍīvadhanvānamabravīnmadhusūdanaḥ ,dṛṣṭvā pārthaṁ tadāyastaṁ cintāparigataṁ tadā.
english: Then Kṛṣṇa, the slayer of Madhu (Madhusūdana), spoke to the wielder of the Gāṇḍīva bow (Arjuna), having seen Pārtha troubled and overwhelmed by anxiety at that moment.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 50 Verse 48. Then Kṛṣṇa, the slayer of Madhu (Madhusūdana), spoke to the wielder of the Gāṇḍīva bow (Arjuna), having seen Pārtha troubled and overwhelmed by anxiety at that moment.

sanskrit: गाण्डीवधन्वन्संग्रामे ये त्वया धनुषा जिताः ।न तेषां मानुषो जेता त्वदन्य इह विद्यते ॥४९॥
transliteration: gāṇḍīvadhanvansaṁgrāme ye tvayā dhanuṣā jitāḥ ,na teṣāṁ mānuṣo jetā tvadanya iha vidyate.
english: O wielder of the Gāṇḍīva bow, there is no human conqueror in this world other than you who could have vanquished those whom you defeated with your bow in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 50 Verse 49. O wielder of the Gāṇḍīva bow, there is no human conqueror in this world other than you who could have vanquished those whom you defeated with your bow in battle.

sanskrit: दृष्टा हि बहवः शूराः शक्रतुल्यपराक्रमाः ।त्वां प्राप्य समरे वीरं ये गताः परमां गतिम् ॥५०॥
transliteration: dṛṣṭā hi bahavaḥ śūrāḥ śakratulyaparākramāḥ ,tvāṁ prāpya samare vīraṁ ye gatāḥ paramāṁ gatim.
english: Indeed, many brave warriors, whose prowess was equal to that of Indra, having encountered you, the hero, in battle, attained the supreme state.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 50 Verse 50. Indeed, many brave warriors, whose prowess was equal to that of Indra, having encountered you, the hero, in battle, attained the supreme state.

sanskrit: को हि द्रोणं च भीष्मं च भगदत्तं च मारिष ।विन्दानुविन्दावावन्त्यौ काम्बोजं च सुदक्षिणम् ॥५१॥
transliteration: ko hi droṇaṁ ca bhīṣmaṁ ca bhagadattaṁ ca māriṣa ,vindānuvindāvāvantyau kāmbojaṁ ca sudakṣiṇam.
english: O respected one, who indeed could face Droṇa, and Bhīṣma, and Bhagadatta, and the two princes of Avanti (Vinda and Anuvinda), and Sudakṣiṇa of Kāmboja...
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 50 Verse 51. O respected one, who indeed could face Droṇa, and Bhīṣma, and Bhagadatta, and the two princes of Avanti (Vinda and Anuvinda), and Sudakṣiṇa of Kāmboja...

sanskrit: श्रुतायुषं महावीर्यमच्युतायुषमेव च ।प्रत्युद्गम्य भवेत्क्षेमी यो न स्यात्त्वमिव क्षमी ॥५२॥
transliteration: śrutāyuṣaṁ mahāvīryamacyutāyuṣameva ca ,pratyudgamya bhavetkṣemī yo na syāttvamiva kṣamī.
english: ...and Śrutāyuṣa of great valor, and Acyutāyuṣa? Who, indeed, having confronted them, would be safe, unless he were as capable as you?
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 50 Verse 52. ...and Śrutāyuṣa of great valor, and Acyutāyuṣa? Who, indeed, having confronted them, would be safe, unless he were as capable as you?

sanskrit: तव ह्यस्त्राणि दिव्यानि लाघवं बलमेव च ।वेधः पातश्च लक्षश्च योगश्चैव तवार्जुन ।असंमोहश्च युद्धेषु विज्ञानस्य च संनतिः ॥५३॥
transliteration: tava hyastrāṇi divyāni lāghavaṁ balameva ca ,vedhaḥ pātaśca lakṣaśca yogaścaiva tavārjuna ,asaṁmohaśca yuddheṣu vijñānasya ca saṁnatiḥ.
english: Indeed, Arjuna, you possess divine weapons, swiftness, and strength. Yours also are the ability to pierce, strike, and hit the target, as well as the skill of yogic (yoga) discipline. Furthermore, you demonstrate unfailing composure in battles and a profound humility born of wisdom.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 50 Verse 53. Indeed, Arjuna, you possess divine weapons, swiftness, and strength. Yours also are the ability to pierce, strike, and hit the target, as well as the skill of yogic (yoga) discipline. Furthermore, you demonstrate unfailing composure in battles and a profound humility born of wisdom.

sanskrit: भवान्देवासुरान्सर्वान्हन्यात्सहचराचरान् ।पृथिव्यां हि रणे पार्थ न योद्धा त्वत्समः पुमान् ॥५४॥
transliteration: bhavāndevāsurānsarvānhanyātsahacarācarān ,pṛthivyāṁ hi raṇe pārtha na yoddhā tvatsamaḥ pumān.
english: You, O son of Pṛthā, could indeed slay all gods and demons, along with all beings, moving and unmoving. Truly, on earth, there is no man, no warrior, equal to you in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 50 Verse 54. You, O son of Pṛthā, could indeed slay all gods and demons, along with all beings, moving and unmoving. Truly, on earth, there is no man, no warrior, equal to you in battle.

sanskrit: धनुर्ग्रहा हि ये केचित्क्षत्रिया युद्धदुर्मदाः ।आ देवात्त्वत्समं तेषां न पश्यामि शृणोमि वा ॥५५॥
transliteration: dhanurgrahā hi ye kecitkṣatriyā yuddhadurmadāḥ ,ā devāttvatsamaṁ teṣāṁ na paśyāmi śṛṇomi vā.
english: Indeed, among all those kshatriyas (kṣatriya) who wield bows and are arrogant in battle, even up to the gods, I neither see nor hear of anyone equal to you.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 50 Verse 55. Indeed, among all those kshatriyas (kṣatriya) who wield bows and are arrogant in battle, even up to the gods, I neither see nor hear of anyone equal to you.

sanskrit: ब्रह्मणा च प्रजाः सृष्टा गाण्डीवं च महाद्भुतम् ।येन त्वं युध्यसे पार्थ तस्मान्नास्ति त्वया समः ॥५६॥
transliteration: brahmaṇā ca prajāḥ sṛṣṭā gāṇḍīvaṁ ca mahādbhutam ,yena tvaṁ yudhyase pārtha tasmānnāsti tvayā samaḥ.
english: Just as Brahmā created beings (prajā), so too was the exceedingly wondrous Gāṇḍīva bow created. Because you fight with it, O son of Pṛthā, there is no one equal to you.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 50 Verse 56. Just as Brahmā created beings (prajā), so too was the exceedingly wondrous Gāṇḍīva bow created. Because you fight with it, O son of Pṛthā, there is no one equal to you.

sanskrit: अवश्यं तु मया वाच्यं यत्पथ्यं तव पाण्डव ।मावमंस्था महाबाहो कर्णमाहवशोभिनम् ॥५७॥
transliteration: avaśyaṁ tu mayā vācyaṁ yatpathyaṁ tava pāṇḍava ,māvamaṁsthā mahābāho karṇamāhavaśobhinam.
english: Indeed, O Pāṇḍava, I must certainly tell you what is beneficial. O mighty-armed one, do not disrespect Karna, who shines brilliantly in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 50 Verse 57. Indeed, O Pāṇḍava, I must certainly tell you what is beneficial. O mighty-armed one, do not disrespect Karna, who shines brilliantly in battle.

sanskrit: कर्णो हि बलवान्धृष्टः कृतास्त्रश्च महारथः ।कृती च चित्रयोधी च देशे काले च कोविदः ॥५८॥
transliteration: karṇo hi balavāndhṛṣṭaḥ kṛtāstraśca mahārathaḥ ,kṛtī ca citrayodhī ca deśe kāle ca kovidaḥ.
english: Indeed, Karna is mighty, fearless, skilled in weaponry, and a great warrior. He is accomplished, a versatile fighter, and knowledgeable regarding proper place and time.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 50 Verse 58. Indeed, Karna is mighty, fearless, skilled in weaponry, and a great warrior. He is accomplished, a versatile fighter, and knowledgeable regarding proper place and time.

sanskrit: तेजसा वह्निसदृशो वायुवेगसमो जवे ।अन्तकप्रतिमः क्रोधे सिंहसंहननो बली ॥५९॥
transliteration: tejasā vahnisadṛśo vāyuvegasamo jave ,antakapratimaḥ krodhe siṁhasaṁhanano balī.
english: In his splendor, he is like fire; in swiftness, he is equal to the speed of the wind. In anger, he is like Yama, the Destroyer, possessing the build of a lion and being mighty.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 50 Verse 59. In his splendor, he is like fire; in swiftness, he is equal to the speed of the wind. In anger, he is like Yama, the Destroyer, possessing the build of a lion and being mighty.

sanskrit: अयोरत्निर्महाबाहुर्व्यूढोरस्कः सुदुर्जयः ।अतिमानी च शूरश्च प्रवीरः प्रियदर्शनः ॥६०॥
transliteration: ayoratnirmahābāhurvyūḍhoraskaḥ sudurjayaḥ ,atimānī ca śūraśca pravīraḥ priyadarśanaḥ.
english: He has forearms like iron, is mighty-armed, broad-chested, and very difficult to defeat. He is exceedingly proud, heroic, a great champion, and of pleasing appearance.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 50 Verse 60. He has forearms like iron, is mighty-armed, broad-chested, and very difficult to defeat. He is exceedingly proud, heroic, a great champion, and of pleasing appearance.

sanskrit: सर्वैर्योधगुणैर्युक्तो मित्राणामभयंकरः ।सततं पाण्डवद्वेषी धार्तराष्ट्रहिते रतः ॥६१॥
transliteration: sarvairyodhaguṇairyukto mitrāṇāmabhayaṁkaraḥ ,satataṁ pāṇḍavadveṣī dhārtarāṣṭrahite rataḥ.
english: He is endowed with all warrior qualities, a protector of his friends, constantly hostile to the Pāṇḍavas, and devoted to the welfare of the sons of Dhṛtarāṣṭra.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 50 Verse 61. He is endowed with all warrior qualities, a protector of his friends, constantly hostile to the Pāṇḍavas, and devoted to the welfare of the sons of Dhṛtarāṣṭra.

sanskrit: सर्वैरवध्यो राधेयो देवैरपि सवासवैः ।ऋते त्वामिति मे बुद्धिस्त्वमद्य जहि सूतजम् ॥६२॥
transliteration: sarvairavadhyo rādheyo devairapi savāsavaiḥ ,ṛte tvāmiti me buddhistvamadya jahi sūtajam.
english: The son of Rādhā (rādheya) is invulnerable to all, even to the gods along with Indra (vāsava), except for you - this is my conviction. Therefore, you must slay the son of the charioteer (sūta-ja) today.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 50 Verse 62. The son of Rādhā (rādheya) is invulnerable to all, even to the gods along with Indra (vāsava), except for you - this is my conviction. Therefore, you must slay the son of the charioteer (sūta-ja) today.

sanskrit: देवैरपि हि संयत्तैर्बिभ्रद्भिर्मांसशोणितम् ।अशक्यः समरे जेतुं सर्वैरपि युयुत्सुभिः ॥६३॥
transliteration: devairapi hi saṁyattairbibhradbhirmāṁsaśoṇitam ,aśakyaḥ samare jetuṁ sarvairapi yuyutsubhiḥ.
english: Indeed, even by the well-prepared gods, though they bear physical bodies (māṃsa-śoṇitam), he is impossible to conquer in battle, even by all those who wish to fight.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 50 Verse 63. Indeed, even by the well-prepared gods, though they bear physical bodies (māṃsa-śoṇitam), he is impossible to conquer in battle, even by all those who wish to fight.

sanskrit: दुरात्मानं पापमतिं नृशंसं दुष्टप्रज्ञं पाण्डवेयेषु नित्यम् ।हीनस्वार्थं पाण्डवेयैर्विरोधे हत्वा कर्णं धिष्ठितार्थो भवाद्य ॥६४॥
transliteration: durātmānaṁ pāpamatiṁ nṛśaṁsaṁ; duṣṭaprajñaṁ pāṇḍaveyeṣu nityam ,hīnasvārthaṁ pāṇḍaveyairvirodhe; hatvā karṇaṁ dhiṣṭhitārtho bhavādya.
english: Today, having slain Karṇa - who is wicked-souled, evil-minded, cruel, constantly ill-disposed towards the Pāṇḍavas, and whose own welfare is diminished by his enmity with the Pāṇḍavas - become one who has accomplished his purpose.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 50 Verse 64. Today, having slain Karṇa - who is wicked-souled, evil-minded, cruel, constantly ill-disposed towards the Pāṇḍavas, and whose own welfare is diminished by his enmity with the Pāṇḍavas - become one who has accomplished his purpose.

sanskrit: वीरं मन्यत आत्मानं येन पापः सुयोधनः ।तमद्य मूलं पापानां जय सौतिं धनंजय ॥६५॥
transliteration: vīraṁ manyata ātmānaṁ yena pāpaḥ suyodhanaḥ ,tamadya mūlaṁ pāpānāṁ jaya sautiṁ dhanaṁjaya.
english: The wicked Duryodhana considers himself a hero (ātman) because of whom. Today, O Arjuna, conquer that son of Suta (Karṇa), who is the root of all evils.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 50 Verse 65. The wicked Duryodhana considers himself a hero (ātman) because of whom. Today, O Arjuna, conquer that son of Suta (Karṇa), who is the root of all evils.

sanskrit: संजय उवाच ।ततः पुनरमेयात्मा केशवोऽर्जुनमब्रवीत् ।कृतसंकल्पमायस्तं वधे कर्णस्य सर्वशः ॥१॥
transliteration: saṁjaya uvāca ,tataḥ punarameyātmā keśavo'rjunamabravīt ,kṛtasaṁkalpamāyastaṁ vadhe karṇasya sarvaśaḥ.
english: Sañjaya said: Then, Keśava, whose nature (ātman) is immeasurable, spoke again to Arjuna, who was resolute and exhausted in every way concerning Karṇa's death.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 51 Verse 1. Sañjaya said: Then, Keśava, whose nature (ātman) is immeasurable, spoke again to Arjuna, who was resolute and exhausted in every way concerning Karṇa's death.

sanskrit: अद्य सप्तदशाहानि वर्तमानस्य भारत ।विनाशस्यातिघोरस्य नरवारणवाजिनाम् ॥२॥
transliteration: adya saptadaśāhāni vartamānasya bhārata ,vināśasyātighorasya naravāraṇavājinām.
english: Today, O Dhṛtarāṣṭra, it is the seventeenth day of this exceedingly dreadful destruction of men, elephants, and horses.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 51 Verse 2. Today, O Dhṛtarāṣṭra, it is the seventeenth day of this exceedingly dreadful destruction of men, elephants, and horses.

sanskrit: भूत्वा हि विपुला सेना तावकानां परैः सह ।अन्योन्यं समरे प्राप्य किंचिच्छेषा विशां पते ॥३॥
transliteration: bhūtvā hi vipulā senā tāvakānāṁ paraiḥ saha ,anyonyaṁ samare prāpya kiṁciccheṣā viśāṁ pate.
english: For indeed, O lord of men, your vast army, along with the enemies, having met each other in battle, has become only a small remnant.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 51 Verse 3. For indeed, O lord of men, your vast army, along with the enemies, having met each other in battle, has become only a small remnant.

sanskrit: भूत्वा हि कौरवाः पार्थ प्रभूतगजवाजिनः ।त्वां वै शत्रुं समासाद्य विनष्टा रणमूर्धनि ॥४॥
transliteration: bhūtvā hi kauravāḥ pārtha prabhūtagajavājinaḥ ,tvāṁ vai śatruṁ samāsādya vinaṣṭā raṇamūrdhani.
english: Indeed, O Pārtha, the Kauravas, despite possessing abundant elephants and horses, perished on the battlefield after encountering you as an enemy.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 51 Verse 4. Indeed, O Pārtha, the Kauravas, despite possessing abundant elephants and horses, perished on the battlefield after encountering you as an enemy.

sanskrit: एते च सर्वे पाञ्चालाः सृञ्जयाश्च सहान्वयाः ।त्वां समासाद्य दुर्धर्षं पाण्डवाश्च व्यवस्थिताः ॥५॥
transliteration: ete ca sarve pāñcālāḥ sṛñjayāśca sahānvayāḥ ,tvāṁ samāsādya durdharṣaṁ pāṇḍavāśca vyavasthitāḥ.
english: And all these Pāñcālas, along with the Sṛñjayas and their followers, and the Pāṇḍavas, having approached you, the formidable one, remained steadfast.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 51 Verse 5. And all these Pāñcālas, along with the Sṛñjayas and their followers, and the Pāṇḍavas, having approached you, the formidable one, remained steadfast.

sanskrit: पाञ्चालैः पाण्डवैर्मत्स्यैः कारूषैश्चेदिकेकयैः ।त्वया गुप्तैरमित्रघ्न कृतः शत्रुगणक्षयः ॥६॥
transliteration: pāñcālaiḥ pāṇḍavairmatsyaiḥ kārūṣaiścedikekayaiḥ ,tvayā guptairamitraghna kṛtaḥ śatrugaṇakṣayaḥ.
english: O slayer of enemies (amitraghna), the destruction of the enemy hosts was accomplished by the Pāñcālas, Pāṇḍavas, Matsyas, Kārūṣas, and the Cedis and Kekayas, who were all protected by you.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 51 Verse 6. O slayer of enemies (amitraghna), the destruction of the enemy hosts was accomplished by the Pāñcālas, Pāṇḍavas, Matsyas, Kārūṣas, and the Cedis and Kekayas, who were all protected by you.

sanskrit: को हि शक्तो रणे जेतुं कौरवांस्तात संगतान् ।अन्यत्र पाण्डवान्युद्धे त्वया गुप्तान्महारथान् ॥७॥
transliteration: ko hi śakto raṇe jetuṁ kauravāṁstāta saṁgatān ,anyatra pāṇḍavānyuddhe tvayā guptānmahārathān.
english: Indeed, dear one (tāta), who would be capable of conquering the assembled Kauravas in battle, except the great charioteers (mahārathān), the Pāṇḍavas, when protected by you in war?
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 51 Verse 7. Indeed, dear one (tāta), who would be capable of conquering the assembled Kauravas in battle, except the great charioteers (mahārathān), the Pāṇḍavas, when protected by you in war?

sanskrit: त्वं हि शक्तो रणे जेतुं ससुरासुरमानुषान् ।त्रीँल्लोकान्सममुद्युक्तान्किं पुनः कौरवं बलम् ॥८॥
transliteration: tvaṁ hi śakto raṇe jetuṁ sasurāsuramānuṣān ,trīँllokānsamamudyuktānkiṁ punaḥ kauravaṁ balam.
english: Indeed, you are capable of conquering in battle the three worlds, even if they were united with gods, asuras, and humans and fully arrayed for battle. What then (is the challenge of) the Kuru army?
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 51 Verse 8. Indeed, you are capable of conquering in battle the three worlds, even if they were united with gods, asuras, and humans and fully arrayed for battle. What then (is the challenge of) the Kuru army?

sanskrit: भगदत्तं हि राजानं कोऽन्यः शक्तस्त्वया विना ।जेतुं पुरुषशार्दूल योऽपि स्याद्वासवोपमः ॥९॥
transliteration: bhagadattaṁ hi rājānaṁ ko'nyaḥ śaktastvayā vinā ,jetuṁ puruṣaśārdūla yo'pi syādvāsavopamaḥ.
english: O best among men, indeed, who else apart from you would be capable of conquering King Bhagadatta, even if that person were equal to Indra (Vasava)?
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 51 Verse 9. O best among men, indeed, who else apart from you would be capable of conquering King Bhagadatta, even if that person were equal to Indra (Vasava)?

sanskrit: तथेमां विपुलां सेनां गुप्तां पार्थ त्वयानघ ।न शेकुः पार्थिवाः सर्वे चक्षुर्भिरभिवीक्षितुम् ॥१०॥
transliteration: tathemāṁ vipulāṁ senāṁ guptāṁ pārtha tvayānagha ,na śekuḥ pārthivāḥ sarve cakṣurbhirabhivīkṣitum.
english: O sinless Partha (Arjuna), thus, all the kings were not able to even gaze upon this vast army, which was protected by you.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 51 Verse 10. O sinless Partha (Arjuna), thus, all the kings were not able to even gaze upon this vast army, which was protected by you.

sanskrit: तथैव सततं पार्थ रक्षिताभ्यां त्वया रणे ।धृष्टद्युम्नशिखण्डिभ्यां भीष्मद्रोणौ निपातितौ ॥११॥
transliteration: tathaiva satataṁ pārtha rakṣitābhyāṁ tvayā raṇe ,dhṛṣṭadyumnaśikhaṇḍibhyāṁ bhīṣmadroṇau nipātitau.
english: O Partha, in the same way, Bhishma and Drona were struck down in battle by Dhṛṣṭadyumna and Śikhaṇḍin, who were constantly protected by you.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 51 Verse 11. O Partha, in the same way, Bhishma and Drona were struck down in battle by Dhṛṣṭadyumna and Śikhaṇḍin, who were constantly protected by you.

sanskrit: को हि शक्तो रणे पार्थ पाञ्चालानां महारथौ ।भीष्मद्रोणौ युधा जेतुं शक्रतुल्यपराक्रमौ ॥१२॥
transliteration: ko hi śakto raṇe pārtha pāñcālānāṁ mahārathau ,bhīṣmadroṇau yudhā jetuṁ śakratulyaparākramau.
english: O Pārtha, who indeed among the Pāñcālas is capable of defeating in battle Bhīṣma and Droṇa, the two great charioteers whose valor equals that of Indra?
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 51 Verse 12. O Pārtha, who indeed among the Pāñcālas is capable of defeating in battle Bhīṣma and Droṇa, the two great charioteers whose valor equals that of Indra?

sanskrit: को हि शांतनवं संख्ये द्रोणं वैकर्तनं कृपम् ।द्रौणिं च सौमदत्तिं च कृतवर्माणमेव च ।सैन्धवं मद्रराजं च राजानं च सुयोधनम् ॥१३॥
transliteration: ko hi śāṁtanavaṁ saṁkhye droṇaṁ vaikartanaṁ kṛpam ,drauṇiṁ ca saumadattiṁ ca kṛtavarmāṇameva ca ,saindhavaṁ madrarājaṁ ca rājānaṁ ca suyodhanam.
english: Who indeed in battle [can conquer] the son of Śāntanu (Bhīṣma), Droṇa, the son of Vikartana (Karṇa), Kṛpa; and Drauṇi (Aśvatthāmā), and Saumadatti (Bhūriśravas), and also Kṛtavarmā; and Saindhava (Jayadratha), and the King of Madras (Śalya), and King Suyodhana (Duryodhana)?
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 51 Verse 13. Who indeed in battle [can conquer] the son of Śāntanu (Bhīṣma), Droṇa, the son of Vikartana (Karṇa), Kṛpa; and Drauṇi (Aśvatthāmā), and Saumadatti (Bhūriśravas), and also Kṛtavarmā; and Saindhava (Jayadratha), and the King of Madras (Śalya), and King Suyodhana (Duryodhana)?

sanskrit: वीरान्कृतास्त्रान्समरे सर्वानेवानुवर्तिनः ।अक्षौहिणीपतीनुग्रान्संरब्धान्युद्धदुर्मदान् ॥१४॥
transliteration: vīrānkṛtāstrānsamare sarvānevānuvartinaḥ ,akṣauhiṇīpatīnugrānsaṁrabdhānyuddhadurmadān.
english: [Who can conquer these] heroes, skilled in weaponry, all loyal followers in battle; the formidable commanders of akṣauhiṇī divisions, enraged and arrogant with the frenzy of war?
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 51 Verse 14. [Who can conquer these] heroes, skilled in weaponry, all loyal followers in battle; the formidable commanders of akṣauhiṇī divisions, enraged and arrogant with the frenzy of war?

sanskrit: श्रेण्यश्च बहुलाः क्षीणाः प्रदीर्णाश्वरथद्विपाः ।नानाजनपदाश्चोग्राः क्षत्रियाणाममर्षिणाम् ॥१५॥
transliteration: śreṇyaśca bahulāḥ kṣīṇāḥ pradīrṇāśvarathadvipāḥ ,nānājanapadāścogrāḥ kṣatriyāṇāmamarṣiṇām.
english: And there are also numerous depleted ranks, whose horses, chariots, and elephants are scattered; and fierce warriors from various regions, belonging to the angry Kṣatriyas.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 51 Verse 15. And there are also numerous depleted ranks, whose horses, chariots, and elephants are scattered; and fierce warriors from various regions, belonging to the angry Kṣatriyas.

sanskrit: गोवासदासमीयानां वसातीनां च भारत ।व्रात्यानां वाटधानानां भोजानां चापि मानिनाम् ॥१६॥
transliteration: govāsadāsamīyānāṁ vasātīnāṁ ca bhārata ,vrātyānāṁ vāṭadhānānāṁ bhojānāṁ cāpi māninām.
english: O Bhārata, (among those who met destruction were) also the Govāsadasamīyas, the Vasātis, the Vrātyas, the Vāṭadhānas, and the proud Bhojas.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 51 Verse 16. O Bhārata, (among those who met destruction were) also the Govāsadasamīyas, the Vasātis, the Vrātyas, the Vāṭadhānas, and the proud Bhojas.

sanskrit: उदीर्णाश्च महासेना ब्रह्मक्षत्रस्य भारत ।त्वां समासाद्य निधनं गताः साश्वरथद्विपाः ॥१७॥
transliteration: udīrṇāśca mahāsenā brahmakṣatrasya bhārata ,tvāṁ samāsādya nidhanaṁ gatāḥ sāśvarathadvipāḥ.
english: O Bhārata, immense armies, even those consisting of Brahmins and Kṣatriyas, that had risen, found their end upon encountering you, along with their horses, chariots, and elephants.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 51 Verse 17. O Bhārata, immense armies, even those consisting of Brahmins and Kṣatriyas, that had risen, found their end upon encountering you, along with their horses, chariots, and elephants.

sanskrit: उग्राश्च क्रूरकर्माणस्तुखारा यवनाः खशाः ।दार्वाभिसारा दरदाः शका रमठतङ्गणाः ॥१८॥
transliteration: ugrāśca krūrakarmāṇastukhārā yavanāḥ khaśāḥ ,dārvābhisārā daradāḥ śakā ramaṭhataṅgaṇāḥ.
english: Among them were also the fierce and cruel-acting Tukhāras, Yavanas, Khaśas, Dārvābhisāras, Daradas, Śakas, Ramaṭhas, and Taṅgaṇas.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 51 Verse 18. Among them were also the fierce and cruel-acting Tukhāras, Yavanas, Khaśas, Dārvābhisāras, Daradas, Śakas, Ramaṭhas, and Taṅgaṇas.

sanskrit: अन्ध्रकाश्च पुलिन्दाश्च किराताश्चोग्रविक्रमाः ।म्लेच्छाश्च पार्वतीयाश्च सागरानूपवासिनः ।संरम्भिणो युद्धशौण्डा बलिनो दृब्धपाणयः ॥१९॥
transliteration: andhrakāśca pulindāśca kirātāścogravikramāḥ ,mlecchāśca pārvatīyāśca sāgarānūpavāsinaḥ ,saṁrambhiṇo yuddhaśauṇḍā balino dṛbdhapāṇayaḥ.
english: Also, the Andhrakas, Pulindas, and Kirātas, who were of terrible prowess; and the Mlecchas, the mountain dwellers, and those residing in the coastal and marshy regions; all of them were wrathful, eager for battle, mighty, and held their weapons with a firm grip.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 51 Verse 19. Also, the Andhrakas, Pulindas, and Kirātas, who were of terrible prowess; and the Mlecchas, the mountain dwellers, and those residing in the coastal and marshy regions; all of them were wrathful, eager for battle, mighty, and held their weapons with a firm grip.

sanskrit: एते सुयोधनस्यार्थे संरब्धाः कुरुभिः सह ।न शक्या युधि निर्जेतुं त्वदन्येन परंतप ॥२०॥
transliteration: ete suyodhanasyārthe saṁrabdhāḥ kurubhiḥ saha ,na śakyā yudhi nirjetuṁ tvadanyena paraṁtapa.
english: O scorcher of foes, these warriors, who are fiercely resolute on behalf of Suyodhana and allied with the Kurus, cannot be defeated in battle by anyone other than you.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 51 Verse 20. O scorcher of foes, these warriors, who are fiercely resolute on behalf of Suyodhana and allied with the Kurus, cannot be defeated in battle by anyone other than you.

sanskrit: धार्तराष्ट्रमुदग्रं हि व्यूढं दृष्ट्वा महाबलम् ।यस्य त्वं न भवेस्त्राता प्रतीयात्को नु मानवः ॥२१॥
transliteration: dhārtarāṣṭramudagraṁ hi vyūḍhaṁ dṛṣṭvā mahābalam ,yasya tvaṁ na bhavestrātā pratīyātko nu mānavaḥ.
english: Indeed, seeing the formidable and mighty army of Dhritarashtra's son arrayed, what human being would place faith (in victory) if you were not their protector?
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 51 Verse 21. Indeed, seeing the formidable and mighty army of Dhritarashtra's son arrayed, what human being would place faith (in victory) if you were not their protector?

sanskrit: तत्सागरमिवोद्धूतं रजसा संवृतं बलम् ।विदार्य पाण्डवैः क्रुद्धैस्त्वया गुप्तैर्हतं विभो ॥२२॥
transliteration: tatsāgaramivoddhūtaṁ rajasā saṁvṛtaṁ balam ,vidārya pāṇḍavaiḥ kruddhaistvayā guptairhataṁ vibho.
english: O lord, that army, agitated like an ocean and enveloped by dust, was first shattered and then destroyed by the furious Pāṇḍavas, who were protected by you.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 51 Verse 22. O lord, that army, agitated like an ocean and enveloped by dust, was first shattered and then destroyed by the furious Pāṇḍavas, who were protected by you.

sanskrit: मागधानामधिपतिर्जयत्सेनो महाबलः ।अद्य सप्तैव चाहानि हतः संख्येऽभिमन्युना ॥२३॥
transliteration: māgadhānāmadhipatirjayatseno mahābalaḥ ,adya saptaiva cāhāni hataḥ saṁkhye'bhimanyunā.
english: Jayatsena, the mighty ruler of the Magadhas, was slain in battle by Abhimanyu; and it has been only seven days (since that event).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 51 Verse 23. Jayatsena, the mighty ruler of the Magadhas, was slain in battle by Abhimanyu; and it has been only seven days (since that event).

sanskrit: ततो दश सहस्राणि गजानां भीमकर्मणाम् ।जघान गदया भीमस्तस्य राज्ञः परिच्छदम् ।ततोऽन्येऽपि हता नागा रथाश्च शतशो बलात् ॥२४॥
transliteration: tato daśa sahasrāṇi gajānāṁ bhīmakarmaṇām ,jaghāna gadayā bhīmastasya rājñaḥ paricchadam ,tato'nye'pi hatā nāgā rathāśca śataśo balāt.
english: Bhīma then, with his mace, killed ten thousand elephants of formidable power, which were part of that king's retinue. Subsequently, other elephants and hundreds of chariots were also forcibly destroyed.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 51 Verse 24. Bhīma then, with his mace, killed ten thousand elephants of formidable power, which were part of that king's retinue. Subsequently, other elephants and hundreds of chariots were also forcibly destroyed.

sanskrit: तदेवं समरे तात वर्तमाने महाभये ।भीमसेनं समासाद्य त्वां च पाण्डव कौरवाः ।सवाजिरथनागाश्च मृत्युलोकमितो गताः ॥२५॥
transliteration: tadevaṁ samare tāta vartamāne mahābhaye ,bhīmasenaṁ samāsādya tvāṁ ca pāṇḍava kauravāḥ ,savājirathanāgāśca mṛtyulokamito gatāḥ.
english: O dear father (tāta), while such a terribly frightening battle (samare mahābhayāvartamāne) was raging, the Kauravas, having confronted Bhīmasena and you (O Pāṇḍava), along with their horses, chariots, and elephants, departed from this world to the realm of death.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 51 Verse 25. O dear father (tāta), while such a terribly frightening battle (samare mahābhayāvartamāne) was raging, the Kauravas, having confronted Bhīmasena and you (O Pāṇḍava), along with their horses, chariots, and elephants, departed from this world to the realm of death.

sanskrit: तथा सेनामुखे तत्र निहते पार्थ पाण्डवैः ।भीष्मः प्रासृजदुग्राणि शरवर्षाणि मारिष ॥२६॥
transliteration: tathā senāmukhe tatra nihate pārtha pāṇḍavaiḥ ,bhīṣmaḥ prāsṛjadugrāṇi śaravarṣāṇi māriṣa.
english: O Pārtha (Prince), O respected one, when the vanguard of the army was thus destroyed there by the Pāṇḍavas, Bhīṣma unleashed fierce torrents of arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 51 Verse 26. O Pārtha (Prince), O respected one, when the vanguard of the army was thus destroyed there by the Pāṇḍavas, Bhīṣma unleashed fierce torrents of arrows.

sanskrit: स चेदिकाशिपाञ्चालान्करूषान्मत्स्यकेकयान् ।शरैः प्रच्छाद्य निधनमनयत्परुषास्त्रवित् ॥२७॥
transliteration: sa cedikāśipāñcālānkarūṣānmatsyakekayān ,śaraiḥ pracchādya nidhanamanayatparuṣāstravit.
english: He (Bhīṣma), skilled in fierce weaponry, overwhelmed the Cedis, Kāśis, Pāñcālas, Karūṣas, Matsyas, and Kekayas with his arrows, leading them to their demise.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 51 Verse 27. He (Bhīṣma), skilled in fierce weaponry, overwhelmed the Cedis, Kāśis, Pāñcālas, Karūṣas, Matsyas, and Kekayas with his arrows, leading them to their demise.

sanskrit: तस्य चापच्युतैर्बाणैः परदेहविदारणैः ।पूर्णमाकाशमभवद्रुक्मपुङ्खैरजिह्मगैः ॥२८॥
transliteration: tasya cāpacyutairbāṇaiḥ paradehavidāraṇaiḥ ,pūrṇamākāśamabhavadrukmapuṅkhairajihmagaiḥ.
english: And the sky became filled with his arrows, which, shot from his bow, were piercing the bodies of foes, had golden shafts, and flew straight.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 51 Verse 28. And the sky became filled with his arrows, which, shot from his bow, were piercing the bodies of foes, had golden shafts, and flew straight.

sanskrit: गत्या दशम्या ते गत्वा जघ्नुर्वाजिरथद्विपान् ।हित्वा नव गतीर्दुष्टाः स बाणान्व्यायतोऽमुचत् ॥२९॥
transliteration: gatyā daśamyā te gatvā jaghnurvājirathadvipān ,hitvā nava gatīrduṣṭāḥ sa bāṇānvyāyato'mucat.
english: They, advancing with the tenth strategy, killed the horses, chariots, and elephants. He, having abandoned the nine faulty approaches, released arrows after drawing his bow.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 51 Verse 29. They, advancing with the tenth strategy, killed the horses, chariots, and elephants. He, having abandoned the nine faulty approaches, released arrows after drawing his bow.

sanskrit: दिनानि दश भीष्मेण निघ्नता तावकं बलम् ।शून्याः कृता रथोपस्था हताश्च गजवाजिनः ॥३०॥
transliteration: dināni daśa bhīṣmeṇa nighnatā tāvakaṁ balam ,śūnyāḥ kṛtā rathopasthā hatāśca gajavājinaḥ.
english: For ten days, as Bhishma was striking down your army, the chariot warriors were annihilated, and the elephants and horses were killed.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 51 Verse 30. For ten days, as Bhishma was striking down your army, the chariot warriors were annihilated, and the elephants and horses were killed.

sanskrit: दर्शयित्वात्मनो रूपं रुद्रोपेन्द्रसमं युधि ।पाण्डवानामनीकानि प्रविगाह्य व्यशातयत् ॥३१॥
transliteration: darśayitvātmano rūpaṁ rudropendrasamaṁ yudhi ,pāṇḍavānāmanīkāni pravigāhya vyaśātayat.
english: After displaying his own form (ātman), which was like that of Rudra and Upendra, in battle, he, having deeply penetrated the armies of the Pandavas, utterly scattered them.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 51 Verse 31. After displaying his own form (ātman), which was like that of Rudra and Upendra, in battle, he, having deeply penetrated the armies of the Pandavas, utterly scattered them.

sanskrit: विनिघ्नन्पृथिवीपालांश्चेदिपाञ्चालकेकयान् ।व्यदहत्पाण्डवीं सेनां नराश्वगजसंकुलाम् ॥३२॥
transliteration: vinighnanpṛthivīpālāṁścedipāñcālakekayān ,vyadahatpāṇḍavīṁ senāṁ narāśvagajasaṁkulām.
english: He struck down the kings of Chedi, Pañcāla, and Kekaya, and scorched the Pāṇḍava army, which was teeming with men, horses, and elephants.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 51 Verse 32. He struck down the kings of Chedi, Pañcāla, and Kekaya, and scorched the Pāṇḍava army, which was teeming with men, horses, and elephants.

sanskrit: मज्जन्तमप्लवे मन्दमुज्जिहीर्षुः सुयोधनम् ।तथा चरन्तं समरे तपन्तमिव भास्करम् ।न शेकुः सृञ्जया द्रष्टुं तथैवान्ये महीक्षितः ॥३३॥
transliteration: majjantamaplave mandamujjihīrṣuḥ suyodhanam ,tathā carantaṁ samare tapantamiva bhāskaram ,na śekuḥ sṛñjayā draṣṭuṁ tathaivānye mahīkṣitaḥ.
english: Desiring to rescue the disheartened Suyodhana, who was drowning in helplessness, and acting thus in battle, blazing like the sun, neither the Srinjayas nor other kings were able to look at him.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 51 Verse 33. Desiring to rescue the disheartened Suyodhana, who was drowning in helplessness, and acting thus in battle, blazing like the sun, neither the Srinjayas nor other kings were able to look at him.

sanskrit: विचरन्तं तथा तं तु संग्रामे जितकाशिनम् ।सर्वोद्योगेन सहसा पाण्डवाः समुपाद्रवन् ॥३४॥
transliteration: vicarantaṁ tathā taṁ tu saṁgrāme jitakāśinam ,sarvodyogena sahasā pāṇḍavāḥ samupādravan.
english: But the Pāṇḍavas, with their full force, suddenly attacked him, who was thus moving about in battle and shining victoriously.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 51 Verse 34. But the Pāṇḍavas, with their full force, suddenly attacked him, who was thus moving about in battle and shining victoriously.

sanskrit: स तु विद्राव्य समरे पाण्डवान्सृञ्जयानपि ।एक एव रणे भीष्म एकवीरत्वमागतः ॥३५॥
transliteration: sa tu vidrāvya samare pāṇḍavānsṛñjayānapi ,eka eva raṇe bhīṣma ekavīratvamāgataḥ.
english: But Bhishma, having routed the Pāṇḍavas and even the Srinjayas in battle, alone attained supreme heroism (ekavīratvam) in the fight.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 51 Verse 35. But Bhishma, having routed the Pāṇḍavas and even the Srinjayas in battle, alone attained supreme heroism (ekavīratvam) in the fight.

sanskrit: तं शिखण्डी समासाद्य त्वया गुप्तो महारथम् ।जघान पुरुषव्याघ्रं शरैः संनतपर्वभिः ॥३६॥
transliteration: taṁ śikhaṇḍī samāsādya tvayā gupto mahāratham ,jaghāna puruṣavyāghraṁ śaraiḥ saṁnataparvabhiḥ.
english: Shikhandi, protected by you, having approached that great chariot-warrior (mahāratha), the tiger among men, struck him with arrows having well-aimed shafts.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 51 Verse 36. Shikhandi, protected by you, having approached that great chariot-warrior (mahāratha), the tiger among men, struck him with arrows having well-aimed shafts.

sanskrit: स एष पतितः शेते शरतल्पे पितामहः ।त्वां प्राप्य पुरुषव्याघ्र गृध्रः प्राप्येव वायसम् ॥३७॥
transliteration: sa eṣa patitaḥ śete śaratalpe pitāmahaḥ ,tvāṁ prāpya puruṣavyāghra gṛdhraḥ prāpyeva vāyasam.
english: That grandfather (Bhishma) lies fallen on a bed of arrows. O tiger among men, having encountered you, he is like a crow caught by a vulture.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 51 Verse 37. That grandfather (Bhishma) lies fallen on a bed of arrows. O tiger among men, having encountered you, he is like a crow caught by a vulture.

sanskrit: द्रोणः पञ्च दिनान्युग्रो विधम्य रिपुवाहिनीः ।कृत्वा व्यूहं महायुद्धे पातयित्वा महारथान् ॥३८॥
transliteration: droṇaḥ pañca dinānyugro vidhamya ripuvāhinīḥ ,kṛtvā vyūhaṁ mahāyuddhe pātayitvā mahārathān.
english: Fierce Drona, for five days, having scattered the enemy armies, having formed battle arrays in the great battle, and having caused great chariot-warriors to fall...
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 51 Verse 38. Fierce Drona, for five days, having scattered the enemy armies, having formed battle arrays in the great battle, and having caused great chariot-warriors to fall...

sanskrit: जयद्रथस्य समरे कृत्वा रक्षां महारथः ।अन्तकप्रतिमश्चोग्रां रात्रिं युद्ध्वादहत्प्रजाः ॥३९॥
transliteration: jayadrathasya samare kṛtvā rakṣāṁ mahārathaḥ ,antakapratimaścogrāṁ rātriṁ yuddhvādahatprajāḥ.
english: The great chariot-warrior (Drona), having provided protection for Jayadratha in battle, and fierce like the god of death (Antaka), having fought through the night, annihilated many warriors.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 51 Verse 39. The great chariot-warrior (Drona), having provided protection for Jayadratha in battle, and fierce like the god of death (Antaka), having fought through the night, annihilated many warriors.

sanskrit: अद्येति द्वे दिने वीरो भारद्वाजः प्रतापवान् ।धृष्टद्युम्नं समासाद्य स गतः परमां गतिम् ॥४०॥
transliteration: adyeti dve dine vīro bhāradvājaḥ pratāpavān ,dhṛṣṭadyumnaṁ samāsādya sa gataḥ paramāṁ gatim.
english: Thus, for two days, the valiant and mighty Bharadvāja, Drona, after encountering Dhṛṣṭadyumna, attained the supreme state (paramāṃ gatim).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 51 Verse 40. Thus, for two days, the valiant and mighty Bharadvāja, Drona, after encountering Dhṛṣṭadyumna, attained the supreme state (paramāṃ gatim).

sanskrit: यदि चैव परान्युद्धे सूतपुत्रमुखान्रथान् ।नावारयिष्यः संग्रामे न स्म द्रोणो व्यनङ्क्ष्यत ॥४१॥
transliteration: yadi caiva parānyuddhe sūtaputramukhānrathān ,nāvārayiṣyaḥ saṁgrāme na sma droṇo vyanaṅkṣyata.
english: If indeed you had not held back the enemy chariots, led by Karṇa, in battle, Drona would not have been killed in the war.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 51 Verse 41. If indeed you had not held back the enemy chariots, led by Karṇa, in battle, Drona would not have been killed in the war.

sanskrit: भवता तु बलं सर्वं धार्तराष्ट्रस्य वारितम् ।ततो द्रोणो हतो युद्धे पार्षतेन धनंजय ॥४२॥
transliteration: bhavatā tu balaṁ sarvaṁ dhārtarāṣṭrasya vāritam ,tato droṇo hato yuddhe pārṣatena dhanaṁjaya.
english: But by you, the entire army of Dhṛtarāṣṭra's sons was held back. Therefore, Drona was killed in battle by Dhṛṣṭadyumna, O Dhanañjaya.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 51 Verse 42. But by you, the entire army of Dhṛtarāṣṭra's sons was held back. Therefore, Drona was killed in battle by Dhṛṣṭadyumna, O Dhanañjaya.

sanskrit: क इवान्यो रणे कुर्यात्त्वदन्यः क्षत्रियो युधि ।यादृशं ते कृतं पार्थ जयद्रथवधं प्रति ॥४३॥
transliteration: ka ivānyo raṇe kuryāttvadanyaḥ kṣatriyo yudhi ,yādṛśaṁ te kṛtaṁ pārtha jayadrathavadhaṁ prati.
english: What other warrior in battle, besides you, O Pārtha, could perform such an act as you did concerning the killing of Jayadratha?
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 51 Verse 43. What other warrior in battle, besides you, O Pārtha, could perform such an act as you did concerning the killing of Jayadratha?

sanskrit: निवार्य सेनां महतीं हत्वा शूरांश्च पार्थिवान् ।निहतः सैन्धवो राजा त्वयास्त्रबलतेजसा ॥४४॥
transliteration: nivārya senāṁ mahatīṁ hatvā śūrāṁśca pārthivān ,nihataḥ saindhavo rājā tvayāstrabalatejasā.
english: After checking the mighty army and killing the heroic kings, the king of Sindhu (Jayadratha) was slain by you through the power and brilliance of your weapons.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 51 Verse 44. After checking the mighty army and killing the heroic kings, the king of Sindhu (Jayadratha) was slain by you through the power and brilliance of your weapons.

sanskrit: आश्चर्यं सिन्धुराजस्य वधं जानन्ति पार्थिवाः ।अनाश्चर्यं हि तत्त्वत्तस्त्वं हि पार्थ महारथः ॥४५॥
transliteration: āścaryaṁ sindhurājasya vadhaṁ jānanti pārthivāḥ ,anāścaryaṁ hi tattvattastvaṁ hi pārtha mahārathaḥ.
english: Kings consider the slaying of the king of Sindhu to be a wonder. But that, from you, O Pārtha (Arjuna), is certainly not a wonder, for you are indeed a great warrior.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 51 Verse 45. Kings consider the slaying of the king of Sindhu to be a wonder. But that, from you, O Pārtha (Arjuna), is certainly not a wonder, for you are indeed a great warrior.

sanskrit: त्वां हि प्राप्य रणे क्षत्रमेकाहादिति भारत ।तप्यमानमसंयुक्तं न भवेदिति मे मतिः ॥४६॥
transliteration: tvāṁ hi prāpya raṇe kṣatramekāhāditi bhārata ,tapyamānamasaṁyuktaṁ na bhavediti me matiḥ.
english: Indeed, my opinion, O Bhārata (Arjuna), is that having confronted you in battle, the warrior class (kṣatram) would not remain suffering and disunited for even a single day.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 51 Verse 46. Indeed, my opinion, O Bhārata (Arjuna), is that having confronted you in battle, the warrior class (kṣatram) would not remain suffering and disunited for even a single day.

sanskrit: सेयं पार्थ चमूर्घोरा धार्तराष्ट्रस्य संयुगे ।हता ससर्ववीरा हि भीष्मद्रोणौ यदा हतौ ॥४७॥
transliteration: seyaṁ pārtha camūrghorā dhārtarāṣṭrasya saṁyuge ,hatā sasarvavīrā hi bhīṣmadroṇau yadā hatau.
english: O Pārtha (Arjuna), this dreadful army of Dhṛtarāṣṭra's sons was indeed destroyed along with all its warriors in battle, when Bhīṣma and Droṇa were slain.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 51 Verse 47. O Pārtha (Arjuna), this dreadful army of Dhṛtarāṣṭra's sons was indeed destroyed along with all its warriors in battle, when Bhīṣma and Droṇa were slain.

sanskrit: शीर्णप्रवरयोधाद्य हतवाजिनरद्विपा ।हीना सूर्येन्दुनक्षत्रैर्द्यौरिवाभाति भारती ॥४८॥
transliteration: śīrṇapravarayodhādya hatavājinaradvipā ,hīnā sūryendunakṣatrairdyaurivābhāti bhāratī.
english: The Bhārata army, with its foremost warriors shattered and its horses, men, and elephants slain, appears like a sky devoid of the sun, moon, and stars.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 51 Verse 48. The Bhārata army, with its foremost warriors shattered and its horses, men, and elephants slain, appears like a sky devoid of the sun, moon, and stars.

sanskrit: विध्वस्ता हि रणे पार्थ सेनेयं भीमविक्रमात् ।आसुरीव पुरा सेना शक्रस्येव पराक्रमैः ॥४९॥
transliteration: vidhvastā hi raṇe pārtha seneyaṁ bhīmavikramāt ,āsurīva purā senā śakrasyeva parākramaiḥ.
english: Indeed, O Pārtha, this army has been completely destroyed in battle by Bhīma's formidable valor, just as the asura army was formerly shattered by Indra's might.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 51 Verse 49. Indeed, O Pārtha, this army has been completely destroyed in battle by Bhīma's formidable valor, just as the asura army was formerly shattered by Indra's might.

sanskrit: तेषां हतावशिष्टास्तु पञ्च सन्ति महारथाः ।अश्वत्थामा कृतवर्मा कर्णो मद्राधिपः कृपः ॥५०॥
transliteration: teṣāṁ hatāvaśiṣṭāstu pañca santi mahārathāḥ ,aśvatthāmā kṛtavarmā karṇo madrādhipaḥ kṛpaḥ.
english: But among them, there are five surviving great charioteers: Ashwatthama, Kṛtavarmā, Karṇa, the king of Madra, and Kṛpa.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 51 Verse 50. But among them, there are five surviving great charioteers: Ashwatthama, Kṛtavarmā, Karṇa, the king of Madra, and Kṛpa.

sanskrit: तांस्त्वमद्य नरव्याघ्र हत्वा पञ्च महारथान् ।हतामित्रः प्रयच्छोर्वीं राज्ञः सद्वीपपत्तनाम् ॥५१॥
transliteration: tāṁstvamadya naravyāghra hatvā pañca mahārathān ,hatāmitraḥ prayacchorvīṁ rājñaḥ sadvīpapattanām.
english: O tiger among men (naravyāghra), after you have slain these five great charioteers today, grant the earth - including its islands and cities - to the king, your enemies having been vanquished.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 51 Verse 51. O tiger among men (naravyāghra), after you have slain these five great charioteers today, grant the earth - including its islands and cities - to the king, your enemies having been vanquished.

sanskrit: साकाशजलपातालां सपर्वतमहावनाम् ।प्राप्नोत्वमितवीर्यश्रीरद्य पार्थो वसुंधराम् ॥५२॥
transliteration: sākāśajalapātālāṁ saparvatamahāvanām ,prāpnotvamitavīryaśrīradya pārtho vasuṁdharām.
english: May Arjuna, possessing immeasurable strength and splendor, today obtain the earth, along with its sky, waters, netherworlds, mountains, and vast forests.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 51 Verse 52. May Arjuna, possessing immeasurable strength and splendor, today obtain the earth, along with its sky, waters, netherworlds, mountains, and vast forests.

sanskrit: एतां पुरा विष्णुरिव हत्वा दैतेयदानवान् ।प्रयच्छ मेदिनीं राज्ञे शक्रायेव यथा हरिः ॥५३॥
transliteration: etāṁ purā viṣṇuriva hatvā daiteyadānavān ,prayaccha medinīṁ rājñe śakrāyeva yathā hariḥ.
english: Just as Hari (Viṣṇu), formerly having slain the Daitya and Danava demons, gave this earth to Indra (Śakra), so should you bestow this land upon the king.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 51 Verse 53. Just as Hari (Viṣṇu), formerly having slain the Daitya and Danava demons, gave this earth to Indra (Śakra), so should you bestow this land upon the king.

sanskrit: अद्य मोदन्तु पाञ्चाला निहतेष्वरिषु त्वया ।विष्णुना निहतेष्वेव दानवेयेषु देवताः ॥५४॥
transliteration: adya modantu pāñcālā nihateṣvariṣu tvayā ,viṣṇunā nihateṣveva dānaveyeṣu devatāḥ.
english: Today, may the Panchalas rejoice when the enemies are slain by you, just as the gods rejoiced when the Danaveya demons were slain by Viṣṇu.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 51 Verse 54. Today, may the Panchalas rejoice when the enemies are slain by you, just as the gods rejoiced when the Danaveya demons were slain by Viṣṇu.

sanskrit: यदि वा द्विपदां श्रेष्ठ द्रोणं मानयतो गुरुम् ।अश्वत्थाम्नि कृपा तेऽस्ति कृपे चाचार्यगौरवात् ॥५५॥
transliteration: yadi vā dvipadāṁ śreṣṭha droṇaṁ mānayato gurum ,aśvatthāmni kṛpā te'sti kṛpe cācāryagauravāt.
english: If, O best among two-footed beings, your two compassions  -  one for Droṇa, the revered (guru) whom you honor, and the other for Ashvatthaman  -  exist, and these (compassions) are based on reverence for the preceptor (guru).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 51 Verse 55. If, O best among two-footed beings, your two compassions  -  one for Droṇa, the revered (guru) whom you honor, and the other for Ashvatthaman  -  exist, and these (compassions) are based on reverence for the preceptor (guru).

sanskrit: अत्यन्तोपचितान्वा त्वं मानयन्भ्रातृबान्धवान् ।कृतवर्माणमासाद्य न नेष्यसि यमक्षयम् ॥५६॥
transliteration: atyantopacitānvā tvaṁ mānayanbhrātṛbāndhavān ,kṛtavarmāṇamāsādya na neṣyasi yamakṣayam.
english: Even if you honor your exceedingly powerful brothers and kinsmen, or resort to Kṛtavarman, you will still be led to the abode of Yama (destruction).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 51 Verse 56. Even if you honor your exceedingly powerful brothers and kinsmen, or resort to Kṛtavarman, you will still be led to the abode of Yama (destruction).

sanskrit: भ्रातरं मातुरासाद्य शल्यं मद्रजनाधिपम् ।यदि त्वमरविन्दाक्ष दयावान्न जिघांससि ॥५७॥
transliteration: bhrātaraṁ māturāsādya śalyaṁ madrajanādhipam ,yadi tvamaravindākṣa dayāvānna jighāṁsasi.
english: O lotus-eyed one, if you, being compassionate, do not wish to kill your maternal uncle Śalya, the lord of the Madras, even after having obtained him [as an ally]...
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 51 Verse 57. O lotus-eyed one, if you, being compassionate, do not wish to kill your maternal uncle Śalya, the lord of the Madras, even after having obtained him [as an ally]...
