sanskrit: इमं पापमतिं क्षुद्रमत्यन्तं पाण्डवान्प्रति ।कर्णमद्य नरश्रेष्ठ जह्याशु निशितैः शरैः ॥५८॥
transliteration: imaṁ pāpamatiṁ kṣudramatyantaṁ pāṇḍavānprati ,karṇamadya naraśreṣṭha jahyāśu niśitaiḥ śaraiḥ.
english: O best of men, today quickly strike down with sharp arrows this Karṇa, who is exceedingly evil-minded and mean towards the Pāṇḍavas.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 51 Verse 58. O best of men, today quickly strike down with sharp arrows this Karṇa, who is exceedingly evil-minded and mean towards the Pāṇḍavas.

sanskrit: एतत्ते सुकृतं कर्म नात्र किंचिन्न युज्यते ।वयमप्यत्र जानीमो नात्र दोषोऽस्ति कश्चन ॥५९॥
transliteration: etatte sukṛtaṁ karma nātra kiṁcinna yujyate ,vayamapyatra jānīmo nātra doṣo'sti kaścana.
english: This would be a virtuous action (karma) for you; nothing here is inappropriate. We too know that there is no fault (doṣa) whatsoever in this matter.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 51 Verse 59. This would be a virtuous action (karma) for you; nothing here is inappropriate. We too know that there is no fault (doṣa) whatsoever in this matter.

sanskrit: दहने यत्सपुत्राया निशि मातुस्तवानघ ।द्यूतार्थे यच्च युष्मासु प्रावर्तत सुयोधनः ।तत्र सर्वत्र दुष्टात्मा कर्णो मूलमिहार्जुन ॥६०॥
transliteration: dahane yatsaputrāyā niśi mātustavānagha ,dyūtārthe yacca yuṣmāsu prāvartata suyodhanaḥ ,tatra sarvatra duṣṭātmā karṇo mūlamihārjuna.
english: O sinless Arjuna, regarding the incident of setting ablaze your mother (Kunti) and her sons at night, and what Duryodhana initiated among you all for the sake of gambling, in all these instances, the evil-minded Karna is the root cause here.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 51 Verse 60. O sinless Arjuna, regarding the incident of setting ablaze your mother (Kunti) and her sons at night, and what Duryodhana initiated among you all for the sake of gambling, in all these instances, the evil-minded Karna is the root cause here.

sanskrit: कर्णाद्धि मन्यते त्राणं नित्यमेव सुयोधनः ।ततो मामपि संरब्धो निग्रहीतुं प्रचक्रमे ॥६१॥
transliteration: karṇāddhi manyate trāṇaṁ nityameva suyodhanaḥ ,tato māmapi saṁrabdho nigrahītuṁ pracakrame.
english: Duryodhana indeed always considers Karna his sole protection. Therefore, enraged, he even began to seize me.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 51 Verse 61. Duryodhana indeed always considers Karna his sole protection. Therefore, enraged, he even began to seize me.

sanskrit: स्थिरा बुद्धिर्नरेन्द्रस्य धार्तराष्ट्रस्य मानद ।कर्णः पार्थान्रणे सर्वान्विजेष्यति न संशयः ॥६२॥
transliteration: sthirā buddhirnarendrasya dhārtarāṣṭrasya mānada ,karṇaḥ pārthānraṇe sarvānvijeṣyati na saṁśayaḥ.
english: O giver of honor (mānada), the resolve (buddhi) of Dhṛtarāṣṭra's son (Duryodhana) is firm, thinking: 'Karna will certainly conquer all the Pārthas (Pandavas) in battle, there is no doubt.'
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 51 Verse 62. O giver of honor (mānada), the resolve (buddhi) of Dhṛtarāṣṭra's son (Duryodhana) is firm, thinking: 'Karna will certainly conquer all the Pārthas (Pandavas) in battle, there is no doubt.'

sanskrit: कर्णमाश्रित्य कौन्तेय धार्तराष्ट्रेण विग्रहः ।रोचितो भवता सार्धं जानतापि बलं तव ॥६३॥
transliteration: karṇamāśritya kaunteya dhārtarāṣṭreṇa vigrahaḥ ,rocito bhavatā sārdhaṁ jānatāpi balaṁ tava.
english: O son of Kunti (Kaunteya), Dhṛtarāṣṭra's son (Duryodhana), relying on Karna, initiated this conflict, even though he (Duryodhana) knew your (Arjuna's) strength, and did so in your (Krishna's) presence.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 51 Verse 63. O son of Kunti (Kaunteya), Dhṛtarāṣṭra's son (Duryodhana), relying on Karna, initiated this conflict, even though he (Duryodhana) knew your (Arjuna's) strength, and did so in your (Krishna's) presence.

sanskrit: कर्णो हि भाषते नित्यमहं पार्थान्समागतान् ।वासुदेवं सराजानं विजेष्यामि महारणे ॥६४॥
transliteration: karṇo hi bhāṣate nityamahaṁ pārthānsamāgatān ,vāsudevaṁ sarājānaṁ vijeṣyāmi mahāraṇe.
english: Indeed, Karna constantly boasts, saying, 'I will conquer the assembled Pārthas, along with Vāsudeva and their kings, in a great battle.'
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 51 Verse 64. Indeed, Karna constantly boasts, saying, 'I will conquer the assembled Pārthas, along with Vāsudeva and their kings, in a great battle.'

sanskrit: प्रोत्साहयन्दुरात्मानं धार्तराष्ट्रं सुदुर्मतिः ।समितौ गर्जते कर्णस्तमद्य जहि भारत ॥६५॥
transliteration: protsāhayandurātmānaṁ dhārtarāṣṭraṁ sudurmatiḥ ,samitau garjate karṇastamadya jahi bhārata.
english: Encouraging the wicked Dhārtarāṣṭra (Duryodhana), Karna, who is exceedingly ill-minded, roars in the assembly. Therefore, O Bhārata (Arjuna), slay him today!
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 51 Verse 65. Encouraging the wicked Dhārtarāṣṭra (Duryodhana), Karna, who is exceedingly ill-minded, roars in the assembly. Therefore, O Bhārata (Arjuna), slay him today!

sanskrit: यच्च युष्मासु पापं वै धार्तराष्ट्रः प्रयुक्तवान् ।तत्र सर्वत्र दुष्टात्मा कर्णः पापमतिर्मुखम् ॥६६॥
transliteration: yacca yuṣmāsu pāpaṁ vai dhārtarāṣṭraḥ prayuktavān ,tatra sarvatra duṣṭātmā karṇaḥ pāpamatirmukham.
english: And whatever wrong the Dhārtarāṣṭra (Duryodhana) has indeed inflicted upon you, in all those matters, the wicked-minded Karna, having evil intentions, was the principal figure.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 51 Verse 66. And whatever wrong the Dhārtarāṣṭra (Duryodhana) has indeed inflicted upon you, in all those matters, the wicked-minded Karna, having evil intentions, was the principal figure.

sanskrit: यच्च तद्धार्तराष्ट्राणां क्रूरैः षड्भिर्महारथैः ।अपश्यं निहतं वीरं सौभद्रमृषभेक्षणम् ॥६७॥
transliteration: yacca taddhārtarāṣṭrāṇāṁ krūraiḥ ṣaḍbhirmahārathaiḥ ,apaśyaṁ nihataṁ vīraṁ saubhadramṛṣabhekṣaṇam.
english: And I saw that valiant hero, Abhimanyu (son of Subhadrā), whose eyes were like a bull's, slain by those six cruel great charioteers belonging to the Dhārtarāṣṭras.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 51 Verse 67. And I saw that valiant hero, Abhimanyu (son of Subhadrā), whose eyes were like a bull's, slain by those six cruel great charioteers belonging to the Dhārtarāṣṭras.

sanskrit: द्रोणद्रौणिकृपान्वीरान्कम्पयन्तो महारथान् ।निर्मनुष्यांश्च मातङ्गान्विरथांश्च महारथान् ॥६८॥
transliteration: droṇadrauṇikṛpānvīrānkampayanto mahārathān ,nirmanuṣyāṁśca mātaṅgānvirathāṁśca mahārathān.
english: Making Drona, Ashvatthama, Kripa, and other great charioteer heroes tremble; rendering great charioteers chariot-less; and causing elephants to be without riders.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 51 Verse 68. Making Drona, Ashvatthama, Kripa, and other great charioteer heroes tremble; rendering great charioteers chariot-less; and causing elephants to be without riders.

sanskrit: व्यश्वारोहांश्च तुरगान्पत्तीन्व्यायुधजीवितान् ।कुर्वन्तमृषभस्कन्धं कुरुवृष्णियशस्करम् ॥६९॥
transliteration: vyaśvārohāṁśca turagānpattīnvyāyudhajīvitān ,kurvantamṛṣabhaskandhaṁ kuruvṛṣṇiyaśaskaram.
english: Causing horses to be without horsemen, and foot-soldiers to be deprived of their weapons and lives; (that) bull-shouldered (warrior) who brings fame to the Kurus and Vrishnis.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 51 Verse 69. Causing horses to be without horsemen, and foot-soldiers to be deprived of their weapons and lives; (that) bull-shouldered (warrior) who brings fame to the Kurus and Vrishnis.

sanskrit: विधमन्तमनीकानि व्यथयन्तं महारथान् ।मनुष्यवाजिमातङ्गान्प्रहिण्वन्तं यमक्षयम् ॥७०॥
transliteration: vidhamantamanīkāni vyathayantaṁ mahārathān ,manuṣyavājimātaṅgānprahiṇvantaṁ yamakṣayam.
english: Scattering armies and afflicting great charioteers; sending men, horses, and elephants to the abode of Yama (the god of death).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 51 Verse 70. Scattering armies and afflicting great charioteers; sending men, horses, and elephants to the abode of Yama (the god of death).

sanskrit: शरैः सौभद्रमायस्तं दहन्तमिव वाहिनीम् ।तन्मे दहति गात्राणि सखे सत्येन ते शपे ॥७१॥
transliteration: śaraiḥ saubhadramāyastaṁ dahantamiva vāhinīm ,tanme dahati gātrāṇi sakhe satyena te śape.
english: That very son of Subhadra (Abhimanyu), intense and as if burning the entire army with his arrows - that spectacle burns my own limbs, O friend; I swear to you by the truth.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 51 Verse 71. That very son of Subhadra (Abhimanyu), intense and as if burning the entire army with his arrows - that spectacle burns my own limbs, O friend; I swear to you by the truth.

sanskrit: यत्तत्रापि च दुष्टात्मा कर्णोऽभ्यद्रुह्यत प्रभो ।अशक्नुवंश्चाभिमन्योः कर्णः स्थातुं रणेऽग्रतः ॥७२॥
transliteration: yattatrāpi ca duṣṭātmā karṇo'bhyadruhyata prabho ,aśaknuvaṁścābhimanyoḥ karṇaḥ sthātuṁ raṇe'grataḥ.
english: O Lord, the evil-minded (ātman) Karṇa also attacked there. Yet Karṇa, being unable to stand in front of Abhimanyu in battle,
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 51 Verse 72. O Lord, the evil-minded (ātman) Karṇa also attacked there. Yet Karṇa, being unable to stand in front of Abhimanyu in battle,

sanskrit: सौभद्रशरनिर्भिन्नो विसंज्ञः शोणितोक्षितः ।निःश्वसन्क्रोधसंदीप्तो विमुखः सायकार्दितः ॥७३॥
transliteration: saubhadraśaranirbhinno visaṁjñaḥ śoṇitokṣitaḥ ,niḥśvasankrodhasaṁdīpto vimukhaḥ sāyakārditaḥ.
english: Pierced by Abhimanyu's arrows, he became unconscious, drenched in blood. Sighing, ablaze with anger, his face averted, and afflicted by arrows,
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 51 Verse 73. Pierced by Abhimanyu's arrows, he became unconscious, drenched in blood. Sighing, ablaze with anger, his face averted, and afflicted by arrows,

sanskrit: अपयानकृतोत्साहो निराशश्चापि जीविते ।तस्थौ सुविह्वलः संख्ये प्रहारजनितश्रमः ॥७४॥
transliteration: apayānakṛtotsāho nirāśaścāpi jīvite ,tasthau suvihvalaḥ saṁkhye prahārajanitaśramaḥ.
english: With his resolve set on retreating, and despairing even of his life, he stood utterly bewildered on the battlefield, exhausted by the blows.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 51 Verse 74. With his resolve set on retreating, and despairing even of his life, he stood utterly bewildered on the battlefield, exhausted by the blows.

sanskrit: अथ द्रोणस्य समरे तत्कालसदृशं तदा ।श्रुत्वा कर्णो वचः क्रूरं ततश्चिच्छेद कार्मुकम् ॥७५॥
transliteration: atha droṇasya samare tatkālasadṛśaṁ tadā ,śrutvā karṇo vacaḥ krūraṁ tataściccheda kārmukam.
english: Then, having heard Droṇa's cruel words - words that were appropriate for that very moment in battle - Karṇa then cut his bow (string).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 51 Verse 75. Then, having heard Droṇa's cruel words - words that were appropriate for that very moment in battle - Karṇa then cut his bow (string).

sanskrit: ततश्छिन्नायुधं तेन रणे पञ्च महारथाः ।स चैव निकृतिप्रज्ञः प्रावधीच्छरवृष्टिभिः ॥७६॥
transliteration: tataśchinnāyudhaṁ tena raṇe pañca mahārathāḥ ,sa caiva nikṛtiprajñaḥ prāvadhīccharavṛṣṭibhiḥ.
english: Then, in battle, five great charioteers, whose weapons had been severed by him, were struck down by that very person (Karṇa), who had a deceitful intellect, with showers of arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 51 Verse 76. Then, in battle, five great charioteers, whose weapons had been severed by him, were struck down by that very person (Karṇa), who had a deceitful intellect, with showers of arrows.

sanskrit: यच्च कर्णोऽब्रवीत्कृष्णां सभायां परुषं वचः ।प्रमुखे पाण्डवेयानां कुरूणां च नृशंसवत् ॥७७॥
transliteration: yacca karṇo'bravītkṛṣṇāṁ sabhāyāṁ paruṣaṁ vacaḥ ,pramukhe pāṇḍaveyānāṁ kurūṇāṁ ca nṛśaṁsavat.
english: And what harsh words Karṇa spoke to Kṛṣṇā (Draupadī) in the assembly, in the presence of the Pāṇḍavas and the Kurus, in a cruel manner.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 51 Verse 77. And what harsh words Karṇa spoke to Kṛṣṇā (Draupadī) in the assembly, in the presence of the Pāṇḍavas and the Kurus, in a cruel manner.

sanskrit: विनष्टाः पाण्डवाः कृष्णे शाश्वतं नरकं गताः ।पतिमन्यं पृथुश्रोणि वृणीष्व मितभाषिणि ॥७८॥
transliteration: vinaṣṭāḥ pāṇḍavāḥ kṛṣṇe śāśvataṁ narakaṁ gatāḥ ,patimanyaṁ pṛthuśroṇi vṛṇīṣva mitabhāṣiṇi.
english: O Kṛṣṇā (Draupadī), the Pāṇḍavas are destroyed, having gone to eternal hell. O broad-hipped one, O soft-spoken one, choose another husband.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 51 Verse 78. O Kṛṣṇā (Draupadī), the Pāṇḍavas are destroyed, having gone to eternal hell. O broad-hipped one, O soft-spoken one, choose another husband.

sanskrit: लेखाभ्रु धृतराष्ट्रस्य दासी भूत्वा निवेशनम् ।प्रविशारालपक्ष्माक्षि न सन्ति पतयस्तव ॥७९॥
transliteration: lekhābhru dhṛtarāṣṭrasya dāsī bhūtvā niveśanam ,praviśārālapakṣmākṣi na santi patayastava.
english: O beautifully-browed one, having become Dhṛtarāṣṭra's maidservant, enter his dwelling, O one with straight eyelashes. You have no husbands.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 51 Verse 79. O beautifully-browed one, having become Dhṛtarāṣṭra's maidservant, enter his dwelling, O one with straight eyelashes. You have no husbands.

sanskrit: इत्युक्तवानधर्मज्ञस्तदा परमदुर्मतिः ।पापः पापं वचः कर्णः शृण्वतस्तव भारत ॥८०॥
transliteration: ityuktavānadharmajñastadā paramadurmatiḥ ,pāpaḥ pāpaṁ vacaḥ karṇaḥ śṛṇvatastava bhārata.
english: O descendant of Bharata, at that time, Karna, the sinful one, extremely evil-minded and ignorant of natural law (dharma), spoke such evil words, while you were listening.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 51 Verse 80. O descendant of Bharata, at that time, Karna, the sinful one, extremely evil-minded and ignorant of natural law (dharma), spoke such evil words, while you were listening.

sanskrit: तस्य पापस्य तद्वाक्यं सुवर्णविकृताः शराः ।शमयन्तु शिलाधौतास्त्वयास्ता जीवितच्छिदः ॥८१॥
transliteration: tasya pāpasya tadvākyaṁ suvarṇavikṛtāḥ śarāḥ ,śamayantu śilādhautāstvayāstā jīvitacchidaḥ.
english: May those life-destroying arrows, shot by you, polished on stone and adorned with gold, silence that sinful one's words.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 51 Verse 81. May those life-destroying arrows, shot by you, polished on stone and adorned with gold, silence that sinful one's words.

sanskrit: यानि चान्यानि दुष्टात्मा पापानि कृतवांस्त्वयि ।तान्यद्य जीवितं चास्य शमयन्तु शरास्तव ॥८२॥
transliteration: yāni cānyāni duṣṭātmā pāpāni kṛtavāṁstvayi ,tānyadya jīvitaṁ cāsya śamayantu śarāstava.
english: And whatever other evil deeds that wicked person committed against you, may your arrows destroy both those actions and his life (jīvita) today.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 51 Verse 82. And whatever other evil deeds that wicked person committed against you, may your arrows destroy both those actions and his life (jīvita) today.

sanskrit: गाण्डीवप्रहितान्घोरानद्य गात्रैः स्पृशञ्शरान् ।कर्णः स्मरतु दुष्टात्मा वचनं द्रोणभीष्मयोः ॥८३॥
transliteration: gāṇḍīvaprahitānghorānadya gātraiḥ spṛśañśarān ,karṇaḥ smaratu duṣṭātmā vacanaṁ droṇabhīṣmayoḥ.
english: May that wicked person, Karna, as he feels the dreadful arrows shot from the Gaṇḍīva bow with his own body today, remember the counsel (vacana) of Drona and Bhishma.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 51 Verse 83. May that wicked person, Karna, as he feels the dreadful arrows shot from the Gaṇḍīva bow with his own body today, remember the counsel (vacana) of Drona and Bhishma.

sanskrit: सुवर्णपुङ्खा नाराचाः शत्रुघ्ना वैद्युतप्रभाः ।त्वयास्तास्तस्य मर्माणि भित्त्वा पास्यन्ति शोणितम् ॥८४॥
transliteration: suvarṇapuṅkhā nārācāḥ śatrughnā vaidyutaprabhāḥ ,tvayāstāstasya marmāṇi bhittvā pāsyanti śoṇitam.
english: The golden-shafted iron arrows, enemy-killing and as brilliant as lightning, shot by you, will pierce his vital spots and drink his blood.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 51 Verse 84. The golden-shafted iron arrows, enemy-killing and as brilliant as lightning, shot by you, will pierce his vital spots and drink his blood.

sanskrit: उग्रास्त्वद्भुजनिर्मुक्ता मर्म भित्त्वा शिताः शराः ।अद्य कर्णं महावेगाः प्रेषयन्तु यमक्षयम् ॥८५॥
transliteration: ugrāstvadbhujanirmuktā marma bhittvā śitāḥ śarāḥ ,adya karṇaṁ mahāvegāḥ preṣayantu yamakṣayam.
english: Today, may these terrible, sharp, swift arrows, released from your arm, pierce Karna's vital spot and send him to the abode of Yama.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 51 Verse 85. Today, may these terrible, sharp, swift arrows, released from your arm, pierce Karna's vital spot and send him to the abode of Yama.

sanskrit: अद्य हाहाकृता दीना विषण्णास्त्वच्छरार्दिताः ।प्रपतन्तं रथात्कर्णं पश्यन्तु वसुधाधिपाः ॥८६॥
transliteration: adya hāhākṛtā dīnā viṣaṇṇāstvaccharārditāḥ ,prapatantaṁ rathātkarṇaṁ paśyantu vasudhādhipāḥ.
english: Today, may the kings, crying out 'hā hā' in distress, miserable, dejected, and tormented by your arrows, see Karna falling from his chariot.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 51 Verse 86. Today, may the kings, crying out 'hā hā' in distress, miserable, dejected, and tormented by your arrows, see Karna falling from his chariot.

sanskrit: अद्य स्वशोणिते मग्नं शयानं पतितं भुवि ।अपविद्धायुधं कर्णं पश्यन्तु सुहृदो निजाः ॥८७॥
transliteration: adya svaśoṇite magnaṁ śayānaṁ patitaṁ bhuvi ,apaviddhāyudhaṁ karṇaṁ paśyantu suhṛdo nijāḥ.
english: Today, may Karna's own friends see him lying fallen on the ground, immersed in his own blood, and with his weapons cast aside.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 51 Verse 87. Today, may Karna's own friends see him lying fallen on the ground, immersed in his own blood, and with his weapons cast aside.

sanskrit: हस्तिकक्ष्यो महानस्य भल्लेनोन्मथितस्त्वया ।प्रकम्पमानः पततु भूमावाधिरथेर्ध्वजः ॥८८॥
transliteration: hastikakṣyo mahānasya bhallenonmathitastvayā ,prakampamānaḥ patatu bhūmāvādhiratherdhvajaḥ.
english: Let that great elephant banner of Adhiratha's son (Karṇa), shattered by your spear, fall trembling to the ground.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 51 Verse 88. Let that great elephant banner of Adhiratha's son (Karṇa), shattered by your spear, fall trembling to the ground.

sanskrit: त्वया शरशतैश्छिन्नं रथं हेमविभूषितम् ।हतयोधं समुत्सृज्य भीतः शल्यः पलायताम् ॥८९॥
transliteration: tvayā śaraśataiśchinnaṁ rathaṁ hemavibhūṣitam ,hatayodhaṁ samutsṛjya bhītaḥ śalyaḥ palāyatām.
english: Let the frightened Śalya abandon the gold-adorned chariot, its warrior slain and shattered by hundreds of your arrows, and let him flee.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 51 Verse 89. Let the frightened Śalya abandon the gold-adorned chariot, its warrior slain and shattered by hundreds of your arrows, and let him flee.

sanskrit: ततः सुयोधनो दृष्ट्वा हतमाधिरथिं त्वया ।निराशो जीविते त्वद्य राज्ये चैव धनंजय ॥९०॥
transliteration: tataḥ suyodhano dṛṣṭvā hatamādhirathiṁ tvayā ,nirāśo jīvite tvadya rājye caiva dhanaṁjaya.
english: Then, O Dhanañjaya (Arjuna), after seeing Adhiratha's son (Karṇa) killed by you, Duryodhana will be without hope for his life and indeed for his kingdom now.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 51 Verse 90. Then, O Dhanañjaya (Arjuna), after seeing Adhiratha's son (Karṇa) killed by you, Duryodhana will be without hope for his life and indeed for his kingdom now.

sanskrit: एते द्रवन्ति पाञ्चाला वध्यमानाः शितैः शरैः ।कर्णेन भरतश्रेष्ठ पाण्डवानुज्जिहीर्षवः ॥९१॥
transliteration: ete dravanti pāñcālā vadhyamānāḥ śitaiḥ śaraiḥ ,karṇena bharataśreṣṭha pāṇḍavānujjihīrṣavaḥ.
english: O best of Bhāratas (Arjuna), these Pañcālas, desiring to rescue the Pāṇḍavas, are fleeing as they are being struck down by Karṇa with sharp arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 51 Verse 91. O best of Bhāratas (Arjuna), these Pañcālas, desiring to rescue the Pāṇḍavas, are fleeing as they are being struck down by Karṇa with sharp arrows.

sanskrit: पाञ्चालान्द्रौपदेयांश्च धृष्टद्युम्नशिखण्डिनौ ।धृष्टद्युम्नतनूजांश्च शतानीकं च नाकुलिम् ॥९२॥
transliteration: pāñcālāndraupadeyāṁśca dhṛṣṭadyumnaśikhaṇḍinau ,dhṛṣṭadyumnatanūjāṁśca śatānīkaṁ ca nākulim.
english: The Pañcālas, the sons of Draupadī, Dhṛṣṭadyumna and Śikhaṇḍin, the sons of Dhṛṣṭadyumna, and Śatānīka, who is Nakula's son.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 51 Verse 92. The Pañcālas, the sons of Draupadī, Dhṛṣṭadyumna and Śikhaṇḍin, the sons of Dhṛṣṭadyumna, and Śatānīka, who is Nakula's son.

sanskrit: नकुलं सहदेवं च दुर्मुखं जनमेजयम् ।सुवर्माणं सात्यकिं च विद्धि कर्णवशं गतान् ॥९३॥
transliteration: nakulaṁ sahadevaṁ ca durmukhaṁ janamejayam ,suvarmāṇaṁ sātyakiṁ ca viddhi karṇavaśaṁ gatān.
english: Know that Nakula, Sahadeva, Durmukha, Janamejaya, Suvarman, and Sātyaki have all fallen under Karṇa's control.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 51 Verse 93. Know that Nakula, Sahadeva, Durmukha, Janamejaya, Suvarman, and Sātyaki have all fallen under Karṇa's control.

sanskrit: अभ्याहतानां कर्णेन पाञ्चालानां महारणे ।श्रूयते निनदो घोरस्त्वद्बन्धूनां परंतप ॥९४॥
transliteration: abhyāhatānāṁ karṇena pāñcālānāṁ mahāraṇe ,śrūyate ninado ghorastvadbandhūnāṁ paraṁtapa.
english: O tormentor of foes, a terrible cry is heard from your kinsmen, the Pañcālas, who have been assailed by Karṇa in the great battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 51 Verse 94. O tormentor of foes, a terrible cry is heard from your kinsmen, the Pañcālas, who have been assailed by Karṇa in the great battle.

sanskrit: न त्वेव भीताः पाञ्चालाः कथंचित्स्युः पराङ्मुखाः ।न हि मृत्युं महेष्वासा गणयन्ति महारथाः ॥९५॥
transliteration: na tveva bhītāḥ pāñcālāḥ kathaṁcitsyuḥ parāṅmukhāḥ ,na hi mṛtyuṁ maheṣvāsā gaṇayanti mahārathāḥ.
english: However, even if frightened, the Pañcālas would certainly not turn their backs in any way. For truly, great archers and great charioteers do not regard death.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 51 Verse 95. However, even if frightened, the Pañcālas would certainly not turn their backs in any way. For truly, great archers and great charioteers do not regard death.

sanskrit: य एकः पाण्डवीं सेनां शरौघैः समवेष्टयत् ।तं समासाद्य पाञ्चाला भीष्मं नासन्पराङ्मुखाः ॥९६॥
transliteration: ya ekaḥ pāṇḍavīṁ senāṁ śaraughaiḥ samaveṣṭayat ,taṁ samāsādya pāñcālā bhīṣmaṁ nāsanparāṅmukhāḥ.
english: The Panchalas, upon confronting Bhishma – who single-handedly enveloped the Pandava army with torrents of arrows – did not turn away.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 51 Verse 96. The Panchalas, upon confronting Bhishma – who single-handedly enveloped the Pandava army with torrents of arrows – did not turn away.

sanskrit: तथा ज्वलन्तमस्त्राग्निं गुरुं सर्वधनुष्मताम् ।निर्दहन्तं समारोहन्दुर्धर्षं द्रोणमोजसा ॥९७॥
transliteration: tathā jvalantamastrāgniṁ guruṁ sarvadhanuṣmatām ,nirdahantaṁ samārohandurdharṣaṁ droṇamojasā.
english: Similarly, with their might, they assailed the invincible Drona, the (guru) teacher of all archers, who was blazing forth like a fire of weapons and consuming (all foes).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 51 Verse 97. Similarly, with their might, they assailed the invincible Drona, the (guru) teacher of all archers, who was blazing forth like a fire of weapons and consuming (all foes).

sanskrit: ते नित्यमुदिता जेतुं युद्धे शत्रूनरिंदमाः ।न जात्वाधिरथेर्भीताः पाञ्चालाः स्युः पराङ्मुखाः ॥९८॥
transliteration: te nityamuditā jetuṁ yuddhe śatrūnariṁdamāḥ ,na jātvādhiratherbhītāḥ pāñcālāḥ syuḥ parāṅmukhāḥ.
english: Those Panchalas, always eager to conquer their enemies in battle and renowned as subduers of foes, would certainly never be afraid of Adhiratha's son (Karṇa) and turn away from battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 51 Verse 98. Those Panchalas, always eager to conquer their enemies in battle and renowned as subduers of foes, would certainly never be afraid of Adhiratha's son (Karṇa) and turn away from battle.

sanskrit: तेषामापततां शूरः पाञ्चालानां तरस्विनाम् ।आदत्तेऽसूञ्शरैः कर्णः पतंगानामिवानलः ॥९९॥
transliteration: teṣāmāpatatāṁ śūraḥ pāñcālānāṁ tarasvinām ,ādatte'sūñśaraiḥ karṇaḥ pataṁgānāmivānalaḥ.
english: Just as fire consumes the lives (asu) of moths, so brave Karṇa, with his arrows, took away the lives of those mighty Panchalas who were advancing.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 51 Verse 99. Just as fire consumes the lives (asu) of moths, so brave Karṇa, with his arrows, took away the lives of those mighty Panchalas who were advancing.

sanskrit: तांस्तथाभिमुखान्वीरान्मित्रार्थे त्यक्तजीवितान् ।क्षयं नयति राधेयः पाञ्चालाञ्शतशो रणे ॥१००॥
transliteration: tāṁstathābhimukhānvīrānmitrārthe tyaktajīvitān ,kṣayaṁ nayati rādheyaḥ pāñcālāñśataśo raṇe.
english: The son of Rādhā (Rādheya) brings to ruin those brave heroes who stand facing him and have abandoned their lives for the sake of their friend, slaying the Pañcālas in hundreds in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 51 Verse 100. The son of Rādhā (Rādheya) brings to ruin those brave heroes who stand facing him and have abandoned their lives for the sake of their friend, slaying the Pañcālas in hundreds in battle.

sanskrit: अस्त्रं हि रामात्कर्णेन भार्गवादृषिसत्तमात् ।यदुपात्तं पुरा घोरं तस्य रूपमुदीर्यते ॥१०१॥
transliteration: astraṁ hi rāmātkarṇena bhārgavādṛṣisattamāt ,yadupāttaṁ purā ghoraṁ tasya rūpamudīryate.
english: Indeed, the dreadful weapon which was formerly acquired by Karna from Rāma (Bhārgava), the foremost of sages, its form is now being described.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 51 Verse 101. Indeed, the dreadful weapon which was formerly acquired by Karna from Rāma (Bhārgava), the foremost of sages, its form is now being described.

sanskrit: तापनं सर्वसैन्यानां घोररूपं सुदारुणम् ।समावृत्य महासेनां ज्वलति स्वेन तेजसा ॥१०२॥
transliteration: tāpanaṁ sarvasainyānāṁ ghorarūpaṁ sudāruṇam ,samāvṛtya mahāsenāṁ jvalati svena tejasā.
english: Scorching all armies, dreadful in form, and exceedingly terrible, it blazes with its own radiance, having surrounded the great army.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 51 Verse 102. Scorching all armies, dreadful in form, and exceedingly terrible, it blazes with its own radiance, having surrounded the great army.

sanskrit: एते चरन्ति संग्रामे कर्णचापच्युताः शराः ।भ्रमराणामिव व्रातास्तापयन्तः स्म तावकान् ॥१०३॥
transliteration: ete caranti saṁgrāme karṇacāpacyutāḥ śarāḥ ,bhramarāṇāmiva vrātāstāpayantaḥ sma tāvakān.
english: These arrows, released from Karna's bow, move about in battle like swarms of bees, continually scorching your men.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 51 Verse 103. These arrows, released from Karna's bow, move about in battle like swarms of bees, continually scorching your men.

sanskrit: एते चरन्ति पाञ्चाला दिक्षु सर्वासु भारत ।कर्णास्त्रं समरे प्राप्य दुर्निवारमनात्मभिः ॥१०४॥
transliteration: ete caranti pāñcālā dikṣu sarvāsu bhārata ,karṇāstraṁ samare prāpya durnivāramanātmabhiḥ.
english: O Bhārata, these Pañcālas wander in all directions, as Karṇa's weapon, having been obtained in battle, is irresistible by those lacking self-control (ātman).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 51 Verse 104. O Bhārata, these Pañcālas wander in all directions, as Karṇa's weapon, having been obtained in battle, is irresistible by those lacking self-control (ātman).

sanskrit: एष भीमो दृढक्रोधो वृतः पार्थ समन्ततः ।सृञ्जयैर्योधयन्कर्णं पीड्यते स्म शितैः शरैः ॥१०५॥
transliteration: eṣa bhīmo dṛḍhakrodho vṛtaḥ pārtha samantataḥ ,sṛñjayairyodhayankarṇaṁ pīḍyate sma śitaiḥ śaraiḥ.
english: O Pārtha, this Bhīma, firm in anger, is surrounded on all sides. While fighting Karṇa with the Sṛñjayas, he is being tormented by sharp arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 51 Verse 105. O Pārtha, this Bhīma, firm in anger, is surrounded on all sides. While fighting Karṇa with the Sṛñjayas, he is being tormented by sharp arrows.

sanskrit: पाण्डवान्सृञ्जयांश्चैव पाञ्चालांश्चैव भारत ।हन्यादुपेक्षितः कर्णो रोगो देहमिवाततः ॥१०६॥
transliteration: pāṇḍavānsṛñjayāṁścaiva pāñcālāṁścaiva bhārata ,hanyādupekṣitaḥ karṇo rogo dehamivātataḥ.
english: O Bhārata, if Karṇa is neglected, he would destroy the Pāṇḍavas, the Sṛñjayas, and the Pañcālas, just as an unchecked disease destroys the body.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 51 Verse 106. O Bhārata, if Karṇa is neglected, he would destroy the Pāṇḍavas, the Sṛñjayas, and the Pañcālas, just as an unchecked disease destroys the body.

sanskrit: नान्यं त्वत्तोऽभिपश्यामि योधं यौधिष्ठिरे बले ।यः समासाद्य राधेयं स्वस्तिमानाव्रजेद्गृहम् ॥१०७॥
transliteration: nānyaṁ tvatto'bhipaśyāmi yodhaṁ yaudhiṣṭhire bale ,yaḥ samāsādya rādheyaṁ svastimānāvrajedgṛham.
english: In Yudhiṣṭhira's army, I do not see any other warrior besides you who, having encountered Rādhā's son (Karṇa), would return home safely.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 51 Verse 107. In Yudhiṣṭhira's army, I do not see any other warrior besides you who, having encountered Rādhā's son (Karṇa), would return home safely.

sanskrit: तमद्य निशितैर्बाणैर्निहत्य भरतर्षभ ।यथाप्रतिज्ञं पार्थ त्वं कृत्वा कीर्तिमवाप्नुहि ॥१०८॥
transliteration: tamadya niśitairbāṇairnihatya bharatarṣabha ,yathāpratijñaṁ pārtha tvaṁ kṛtvā kīrtimavāpnuhi.
english: O best among the Bharatas (bharatarṣabha), O son of Pṛthā (pārtha), having killed him today with sharp arrows and having fulfilled your vow, may you achieve great renown.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 51 Verse 108. O best among the Bharatas (bharatarṣabha), O son of Pṛthā (pārtha), having killed him today with sharp arrows and having fulfilled your vow, may you achieve great renown.

sanskrit: त्वं हि शक्तो रणे जेतुं सकर्णानपि कौरवान् ।नान्यो युधि युधां श्रेष्ठ सत्यमेतद्ब्रवीमि ते ॥१०९॥
transliteration: tvaṁ hi śakto raṇe jetuṁ sakarṇānapi kauravān ,nānyo yudhi yudhāṁ śreṣṭha satyametadbravīmi te.
english: Indeed, you are capable of conquering the Kauravas, even including Karna, in battle. There is no other warrior in battle (yudhi yudhām śreṣṭha), O best of warriors; I tell you this truth.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 51 Verse 109. Indeed, you are capable of conquering the Kauravas, even including Karna, in battle. There is no other warrior in battle (yudhi yudhām śreṣṭha), O best of warriors; I tell you this truth.

sanskrit: एतत्कृत्वा महत्कर्म हत्वा कर्णं महारथम् ।कृतार्थः सफलः पार्थ सुखी भव नरोत्तम ॥११०॥
transliteration: etatkṛtvā mahatkarma hatvā karṇaṁ mahāratham ,kṛtārthaḥ saphalaḥ pārtha sukhī bhava narottama.
english: Having performed this great action (karma) by killing Karna, the great warrior, O son of Pṛthā (pārtha), be successful, fulfilled, and happy, O best among men (narottama).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 51 Verse 110. Having performed this great action (karma) by killing Karna, the great warrior, O son of Pṛthā (pārtha), be successful, fulfilled, and happy, O best among men (narottama).

sanskrit: संजय उवाच ।स केशवस्य बीभत्सुः श्रुत्वा भारत भाषितम् ।विशोकः संप्रहृष्टश्च क्षणेन समपद्यत ॥१॥
transliteration: saṁjaya uvāca ,sa keśavasya bībhatsuḥ śrutvā bhārata bhāṣitam ,viśokaḥ saṁprahṛṣṭaśca kṣaṇena samapadyata.
english: Sañjaya said: O descendant of Bharata (bhārata), having heard Keśava's words, Arjuna (bībhatsu) immediately became free from sorrow and greatly delighted.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 52 Verse 1. Sañjaya said: O descendant of Bharata (bhārata), having heard Keśava's words, Arjuna (bībhatsu) immediately became free from sorrow and greatly delighted.

sanskrit: ततो ज्यामनुमृज्याशु व्याक्षिपद्गाण्डिवं धनुः ।दध्रे कर्णविनाशाय केशवं चाभ्यभाषत ॥२॥
transliteration: tato jyāmanumṛjyāśu vyākṣipadgāṇḍivaṁ dhanuḥ ,dadhre karṇavināśāya keśavaṁ cābhyabhāṣata.
english: Then, quickly wiping his bowstring, he twanged his Gandiva bow. He held it ready for the destruction of Karna and addressed Keshava (Krishna).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 52 Verse 2. Then, quickly wiping his bowstring, he twanged his Gandiva bow. He held it ready for the destruction of Karna and addressed Keshava (Krishna).

sanskrit: त्वया नाथेन गोविन्द ध्रुव एष जयो मम ।प्रसन्नो यस्य मेऽद्य त्वं भूतभव्यभवत्प्रभुः ॥३॥
transliteration: tvayā nāthena govinda dhruva eṣa jayo mama ,prasanno yasya me'dya tvaṁ bhūtabhavyabhavatprabhuḥ.
english: Govinda, with you as my protector, this victory of mine is certain. For you, the lord of past, future, and present, are gracious to me today.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 52 Verse 3. Govinda, with you as my protector, this victory of mine is certain. For you, the lord of past, future, and present, are gracious to me today.

sanskrit: त्वत्सहायो ह्यहं कृष्ण त्रीँल्लोकान्वै समागतान् ।प्रापयेयं परं लोकं किमु कर्णं महारणे ॥४॥
transliteration: tvatsahāyo hyahaṁ kṛṣṇa trīँllokānvai samāgatān ,prāpayeyaṁ paraṁ lokaṁ kimu karṇaṁ mahāraṇe.
english: Indeed, O Krishna, supported by you, I could send even the three assembled worlds to the supreme realm (of death). How much more easily then can I defeat Karna in this great battle?
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 52 Verse 4. Indeed, O Krishna, supported by you, I could send even the three assembled worlds to the supreme realm (of death). How much more easily then can I defeat Karna in this great battle?

sanskrit: पश्यामि द्रवतीं सेनां पाञ्चालानां जनार्दन ।पश्यामि कर्णं समरे विचरन्तमभीतवत् ॥५॥
transliteration: paśyāmi dravatīṁ senāṁ pāñcālānāṁ janārdana ,paśyāmi karṇaṁ samare vicarantamabhītavat.
english: O Janardana, I see the army of the Panchalas fleeing. I also see Karna moving about fearlessly in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 52 Verse 5. O Janardana, I see the army of the Panchalas fleeing. I also see Karna moving about fearlessly in battle.

sanskrit: भार्गवास्त्रं च पश्यामि विचरन्तं समन्ततः ।सृष्टं कर्णेन वार्ष्णेय शक्रेणेव महाशनिम् ॥६॥
transliteration: bhārgavāstraṁ ca paśyāmi vicarantaṁ samantataḥ ,sṛṣṭaṁ karṇena vārṣṇeya śakreṇeva mahāśanim.
english: O Vārṣṇeya (Krishna), I behold Bhargava's weapon (Bhārgavāstra) ranging everywhere, unleashed by Karna, just as a great thunderbolt is hurled by Indra.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 52 Verse 6. O Vārṣṇeya (Krishna), I behold Bhargava's weapon (Bhārgavāstra) ranging everywhere, unleashed by Karna, just as a great thunderbolt is hurled by Indra.

sanskrit: अयं खलु स संग्रामो यत्र कृष्ण मया कृतम् ।कथयिष्यन्ति भूतानि यावद्भूमिर्धरिष्यति ॥७॥
transliteration: ayaṁ khalu sa saṁgrāmo yatra kṛṣṇa mayā kṛtam ,kathayiṣyanti bhūtāni yāvadbhūmirdhariṣyati.
english: Indeed, O Krishna, this is that battle where the deeds performed by me will be recounted by all beings as long as the earth (bhūmi) endures.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 52 Verse 7. Indeed, O Krishna, this is that battle where the deeds performed by me will be recounted by all beings as long as the earth (bhūmi) endures.

sanskrit: अद्य कृष्ण विकर्णा मे कर्णं नेष्यन्ति मृत्यवे ।गाण्डीवमुक्ताः क्षिण्वन्तो मम हस्तप्रचोदिताः ॥८॥
transliteration: adya kṛṣṇa vikarṇā me karṇaṁ neṣyanti mṛtyave ,gāṇḍīvamuktāḥ kṣiṇvanto mama hastapracoditāḥ.
english: Today, O Krishna, my unerring arrows, released from the Gaṇḍīva, striking down (enemies) and impelled by my hand, will lead Karna to his death.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 52 Verse 8. Today, O Krishna, my unerring arrows, released from the Gaṇḍīva, striking down (enemies) and impelled by my hand, will lead Karna to his death.

sanskrit: अद्य राजा धृतराष्ट्रः स्वां बुद्धिमवमंस्यते ।दुर्योधनमराज्यार्हं यया राज्येऽभ्यषेचयत् ॥९॥
transliteration: adya rājā dhṛtarāṣṭraḥ svāṁ buddhimavamaṁsyate ,duryodhanamarājyārhaṁ yayā rājye'bhyaṣecayat.
english: Today, King Dhritarashtra will regret his own judgment (buddhi), by which he consecrated Duryodhana, (though he was) unworthy of kingship, to the kingdom.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 52 Verse 9. Today, King Dhritarashtra will regret his own judgment (buddhi), by which he consecrated Duryodhana, (though he was) unworthy of kingship, to the kingdom.

sanskrit: अद्य राज्यात्सुखाच्चैव श्रियो राष्ट्रात्तथा पुरात् ।पुत्रेभ्यश्च महाबाहो धृतराष्ट्रो वियोक्ष्यते ॥१०॥
transliteration: adya rājyātsukhāccaiva śriyo rāṣṭrāttathā purāt ,putrebhyaśca mahābāho dhṛtarāṣṭro viyokṣyate.
english: Today, O mighty-armed one, Dhritarashtra will be deprived of his kingdom, happiness, prosperity, country, city, and even his sons.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 52 Verse 10. Today, O mighty-armed one, Dhritarashtra will be deprived of his kingdom, happiness, prosperity, country, city, and even his sons.

sanskrit: अद्य दुर्योधनो राजा जीविताच्च निराशकः ।भविष्यति हते कर्णे कृष्ण सत्यं ब्रवीमि ते ॥११॥
transliteration: adya duryodhano rājā jīvitācca nirāśakaḥ ,bhaviṣyati hate karṇe kṛṣṇa satyaṁ bravīmi te.
english: Today, O Krishna, I tell you the truth: King Duryodhana will lose all hope for his life once Karna is slain.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 52 Verse 11. Today, O Krishna, I tell you the truth: King Duryodhana will lose all hope for his life once Karna is slain.

sanskrit: अद्य दृष्ट्वा मया कर्णं शरैर्विशकलीकृतम् ।स्मरतां तव वाक्यानि शमं प्रति जनेश्वरः ॥१२॥
transliteration: adya dṛṣṭvā mayā karṇaṁ śarairviśakalīkṛtam ,smaratāṁ tava vākyāni śamaṁ prati janeśvaraḥ.
english: Today, after I (Sanjaya) have seen Karna torn to pieces by arrows, the king of men (Dhritarashtra) will reflect upon your (Krishna's) words concerning peace.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 52 Verse 12. Today, after I (Sanjaya) have seen Karna torn to pieces by arrows, the king of men (Dhritarashtra) will reflect upon your (Krishna's) words concerning peace.

sanskrit: अद्यासौ सौबलः कृष्ण ग्लहं जानातु वै शरान् ।दुरोदरं च गाण्डीवं मण्डलं च रथं मम ॥१३॥
transliteration: adyāsau saubalaḥ kṛṣṇa glahaṁ jānātu vai śarān ,durodaraṁ ca gāṇḍīvaṁ maṇḍalaṁ ca rathaṁ mama.
english: Today, O Krishna, let that son of Subala (Śakuni) truly understand: my arrows are the wager, my Gaṇḍīva bow is the game of dice, and my chariot is the gambling hall.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 52 Verse 13. Today, O Krishna, let that son of Subala (Śakuni) truly understand: my arrows are the wager, my Gaṇḍīva bow is the game of dice, and my chariot is the gambling hall.

sanskrit: योऽसौ रणे नरं नान्यं पृथिव्यामभिमन्यते ।तस्याद्य सूतपुत्रस्य भूमिः पास्यति शोणितम् ।गाण्डीवसृष्टा दास्यन्ति कर्णस्य परमां गतिम् ॥१४॥
transliteration: yo'sau raṇe naraṁ nānyaṁ pṛthivyāmabhimanyate ,tasyādya sūtaputrasya bhūmiḥ pāsyati śoṇitam ,gāṇḍīvasṛṣṭā dāsyanti karṇasya paramāṁ gatim.
english: He who considers no other man on earth his equal in battle – today the earth will drink the blood of that son of a charioteer (sūtaputra). The arrows released from Gāṇḍīva will grant Karṇa his ultimate fate.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 52 Verse 14. He who considers no other man on earth his equal in battle – today the earth will drink the blood of that son of a charioteer (sūtaputra). The arrows released from Gāṇḍīva will grant Karṇa his ultimate fate.

sanskrit: अद्य तप्स्यति राधेयः पाञ्चालीं यत्तदाब्रवीत् ।सभामध्ये वचः क्रूरं कुत्सयन्पाण्डवान्प्रति ॥१५॥
transliteration: adya tapsyati rādheyaḥ pāñcālīṁ yattadābravīt ,sabhāmadhye vacaḥ krūraṁ kutsayanpāṇḍavānprati.
english: Today, Rādhya (Karṇa) will suffer for the cruel words he uttered to Pāñcālī (Draupadī) in the midst of the assembly, slandering the Pāṇḍavas.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 52 Verse 15. Today, Rādhya (Karṇa) will suffer for the cruel words he uttered to Pāñcālī (Draupadī) in the midst of the assembly, slandering the Pāṇḍavas.

sanskrit: ये वै षण्ढतिलास्तत्र भवितारोऽद्य ते तिलाः ।हते वैकर्तने कर्णे सूतपुत्रे दुरात्मनि ॥१६॥
transliteration: ye vai ṣaṇḍhatilāstatra bhavitāro'dya te tilāḥ ,hate vaikartane karṇe sūtaputre durātmani.
english: When Vaikartana Karṇa, that evil-minded son of a charioteer (sūtaputra), is killed, those who were barren sesame seeds (worthless) there shall today truly become sesame seeds (offered in funeral rites).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 52 Verse 16. When Vaikartana Karṇa, that evil-minded son of a charioteer (sūtaputra), is killed, those who were barren sesame seeds (worthless) there shall today truly become sesame seeds (offered in funeral rites).

sanskrit: अहं वः पाण्डुपुत्रेभ्यस्त्रास्यामीति यदब्रवीत् ।अनृतं तत्करिष्यन्ति मामका निशिताः शराः ॥१७॥
transliteration: ahaṁ vaḥ pāṇḍuputrebhyastrāsyāmīti yadabravīt ,anṛtaṁ tatkariṣyanti māmakā niśitāḥ śarāḥ.
english: What he said, 'I will protect you from the sons of Pāṇḍu,' my sharpened arrows will make that a lie.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 52 Verse 17. What he said, 'I will protect you from the sons of Pāṇḍu,' my sharpened arrows will make that a lie.

sanskrit: हन्ताहं पाण्डवान्सर्वान्सपुत्रानिति योऽब्रवीत् ।तमद्य कर्णं हन्तास्मि मिषतां सर्वधन्विनाम् ॥१८॥
transliteration: hantāhaṁ pāṇḍavānsarvānsaputrāniti yo'bravīt ,tamadya karṇaṁ hantāsmi miṣatāṁ sarvadhanvinām.
english: Today I will kill Karna, the one who declared, 'I will slay all the Pāṇḍavas with their sons,' while all the archers witness it.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 52 Verse 18. Today I will kill Karna, the one who declared, 'I will slay all the Pāṇḍavas with their sons,' while all the archers witness it.

sanskrit: यस्य वीर्ये समाश्वस्य धार्तराष्ट्रो बृहन्मनाः ।अवामन्यत दुर्बुद्धिर्नित्यमस्मान्दुरात्मवान् ।तमद्य कर्णं राधेयं हन्तास्मि मधुसूदन ॥१९॥
transliteration: yasya vīrye samāśvasya dhārtarāṣṭro bṛhanmanāḥ ,avāmanyata durbuddhirnityamasmāndurātmavān ,tamadya karṇaṁ rādheyaṁ hantāsmi madhusūdana.
english: O Madhusūdana, today I will kill that Karna, son of Rādhā, by relying on whose valor the large-minded Dhārtarāṣṭra (Duryodhana), that evil-minded, wicked one, constantly disrespected us.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 52 Verse 19. O Madhusūdana, today I will kill that Karna, son of Rādhā, by relying on whose valor the large-minded Dhārtarāṣṭra (Duryodhana), that evil-minded, wicked one, constantly disrespected us.

sanskrit: अद्य कर्णे हते कृष्ण धार्तराष्ट्राः सराजकाः ।विद्रवन्तु दिशो भीताः सिंहत्रस्ता मृगा इव ॥२०॥
transliteration: adya karṇe hate kṛṣṇa dhārtarāṣṭrāḥ sarājakāḥ ,vidravantu diśo bhītāḥ siṁhatrastā mṛgā iva.
english: O Kṛṣṇa, today, when Karna is slain, may the sons of Dhṛtarāṣṭra, along with their king, flee terrified in all directions, just like deer frightened by a lion.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 52 Verse 20. O Kṛṣṇa, today, when Karna is slain, may the sons of Dhṛtarāṣṭra, along with their king, flee terrified in all directions, just like deer frightened by a lion.

sanskrit: अद्य दुर्योधनो राजा पृथिवीमन्ववेक्षताम् ।हते कर्णे मया संख्ये सपुत्रे ससुहृज्जने ॥२१॥
transliteration: adya duryodhano rājā pṛthivīmanvavekṣatām ,hate karṇe mayā saṁkhye saputre sasuhṛjjane.
english: Today, once Karna is slain by me in battle, may King Duryodhana look upon the earth, (having lost) his sons and friends (as well).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 52 Verse 21. Today, once Karna is slain by me in battle, may King Duryodhana look upon the earth, (having lost) his sons and friends (as well).

sanskrit: अद्य कर्णं हतं दृष्ट्वा धार्तराष्ट्रोऽत्यमर्षणः ।जानातु मां रणे कृष्ण प्रवरं सर्वधन्विनाम् ॥२२॥
transliteration: adya karṇaṁ hataṁ dṛṣṭvā dhārtarāṣṭro'tyamarṣaṇaḥ ,jānātu māṁ raṇe kṛṣṇa pravaraṁ sarvadhanvinām.
english: O Krishna, today, after seeing Karna slain, let that exceedingly furious son of Dhritarashtra realize that I am the foremost among all archers in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 52 Verse 22. O Krishna, today, after seeing Karna slain, let that exceedingly furious son of Dhritarashtra realize that I am the foremost among all archers in battle.

sanskrit: अद्याहमनृणः कृष्ण भविष्यामि धनुर्भृताम् ।क्रोधस्य च कुरूणां च शराणां गाण्डिवस्य च ॥२३॥
transliteration: adyāhamanṛṇaḥ kṛṣṇa bhaviṣyāmi dhanurbhṛtām ,krodhasya ca kurūṇāṁ ca śarāṇāṁ gāṇḍivasya ca.
english: O Krishna, today I will be free of my obligations (anṛṇaḥ) to the archers. I shall also fulfill the demands of my wrath, the Kurus, my arrows, and my Gaṇḍīva bow.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 52 Verse 23. O Krishna, today I will be free of my obligations (anṛṇaḥ) to the archers. I shall also fulfill the demands of my wrath, the Kurus, my arrows, and my Gaṇḍīva bow.

sanskrit: अद्य दुःखमहं मोक्ष्ये त्रयोदशसमार्जितम् ।हत्वा कर्णं रणे कृष्ण शम्बरं मघवानिव ॥२४॥
transliteration: adya duḥkhamahaṁ mokṣye trayodaśasamārjitam ,hatvā karṇaṁ raṇe kṛṣṇa śambaraṁ maghavāniva.
english: O Krishna, today I will cast off the sorrow accumulated over thirteen years, having slain Karna in battle, just as Indra slew the demon Shambara.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 52 Verse 24. O Krishna, today I will cast off the sorrow accumulated over thirteen years, having slain Karna in battle, just as Indra slew the demon Shambara.

sanskrit: अद्य कर्णे हते युद्धे सोमकानां महारथाः ।कृतं कार्यं च मन्यन्तां मित्रकार्येप्सवो युधि ॥२५॥
transliteration: adya karṇe hate yuddhe somakānāṁ mahārathāḥ ,kṛtaṁ kāryaṁ ca manyantāṁ mitrakāryepsavo yudhi.
english: Today, with Karna slain in battle, let the great charioteers among the Somakas, who are eager to fulfill their friend's purpose, consider their task accomplished in this war.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 52 Verse 25. Today, with Karna slain in battle, let the great charioteers among the Somakas, who are eager to fulfill their friend's purpose, consider their task accomplished in this war.

sanskrit: न जाने च कथं प्रीतिः शैनेयस्याद्य माधव ।भविष्यति हते कर्णे मयि चापि जयाधिके ॥२६॥
transliteration: na jāne ca kathaṁ prītiḥ śaineyasyādya mādhava ,bhaviṣyati hate karṇe mayi cāpi jayādhike.
english: O Mādhava, I do not know how great Satyaki's joy will be today, with Karṇa slain and me having achieved a decisive victory.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 52 Verse 26. O Mādhava, I do not know how great Satyaki's joy will be today, with Karṇa slain and me having achieved a decisive victory.

sanskrit: अहं हत्वा रणे कर्णं पुत्रं चास्य महारथम् ।प्रीतिं दास्यामि भीमस्य यमयोः सात्यकेरपि ॥२७॥
transliteration: ahaṁ hatvā raṇe karṇaṁ putraṁ cāsya mahāratham ,prītiṁ dāsyāmi bhīmasya yamayoḥ sātyakerapi.
english: Having slain Karṇa and his great chariot-warrior son in battle, I will bring joy to Bhīma, to the twins (Nakula and Sahadeva), and also to Satyaki.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 52 Verse 27. Having slain Karṇa and his great chariot-warrior son in battle, I will bring joy to Bhīma, to the twins (Nakula and Sahadeva), and also to Satyaki.

sanskrit: धृष्टद्युम्नशिखण्डिभ्यां पाञ्चालानां च माधव ।अध्यानृण्यं गमिष्यामि हत्वा कर्णं महारणे ॥२८॥
transliteration: dhṛṣṭadyumnaśikhaṇḍibhyāṁ pāñcālānāṁ ca mādhava ,adhyānṛṇyaṁ gamiṣyāmi hatvā karṇaṁ mahāraṇe.
english: O Mādhava, today, by slaying Karṇa in the great battle, I will fulfill my obligation to Dhṛṣṭadyumna, Śikhaṇḍin, and the Pāñcālas.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 52 Verse 28. O Mādhava, today, by slaying Karṇa in the great battle, I will fulfill my obligation to Dhṛṣṭadyumna, Śikhaṇḍin, and the Pāñcālas.

sanskrit: अद्य पश्यन्तु संग्रामे धनंजयममर्षणम् ।युध्यन्तं कौरवान्संख्ये पातयन्तं च सूतजम् ।भवत्सकाशे वक्ष्ये च पुनरेवात्मसंस्तवम् ॥२९॥
transliteration: adya paśyantu saṁgrāme dhanaṁjayamamarṣaṇam ,yudhyantaṁ kauravānsaṁkhye pātayantaṁ ca sūtajam ,bhavatsakāśe vakṣye ca punarevātmasaṁstavam.
english: Today, let them witness Dhanaṃjaya, intolerant of enemies, fighting the Kauravas in battle and felling Karṇa, the son of Sūta. And in your presence, I will again declare my own (ātman) prowess.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 52 Verse 29. Today, let them witness Dhanaṃjaya, intolerant of enemies, fighting the Kauravas in battle and felling Karṇa, the son of Sūta. And in your presence, I will again declare my own (ātman) prowess.

sanskrit: धनुर्वेदे मत्समो नास्ति लोके पराक्रमे वा मम कोऽस्ति तुल्यः ।को वाप्यन्यो मत्समोऽस्ति क्षमायां तथा क्रोधे सदृशोऽन्यो न मेऽस्ति ॥३०॥
transliteration: dhanurvede matsamo nāsti loke; parākrame vā mama ko'sti tulyaḥ ,ko vāpyanyo matsamo'sti kṣamāyāṁ; tathā krodhe sadṛśo'nyo na me'sti.
english: In the science of archery, there is no one in the world equal to me. Or who can compare to me in valor? Who else is my equal in forbearance? Similarly, there is no one else like me in anger.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 52 Verse 30. In the science of archery, there is no one in the world equal to me. Or who can compare to me in valor? Who else is my equal in forbearance? Similarly, there is no one else like me in anger.

sanskrit: अहं धनुष्मानसुरान्सुरांश्च सर्वाणि भूतानि च संगतानि ।स्वबाहुवीर्याद्गमये पराभवं मत्पौरुषं विद्धि परः परेभ्यः ॥३१॥
transliteration: ahaṁ dhanuṣmānasurānsurāṁśca; sarvāṇi bhūtāni ca saṁgatāni ,svabāhuvīryādgamaye parābhavaṁ; matpauruṣaṁ viddhi paraḥ parebhyaḥ.
english: I, armed with a bow, can bring about the defeat of demons (asuras), gods (suras), and all assembled beings through the might of my own arms. Understand that my prowess is superior to all others.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 52 Verse 31. I, armed with a bow, can bring about the defeat of demons (asuras), gods (suras), and all assembled beings through the might of my own arms. Understand that my prowess is superior to all others.

sanskrit: शरार्चिषा गाण्डिवेनाहमेकः सर्वान्कुरून्बाह्लिकांश्चाभिपत्य ।हिमात्यये कक्षगतो यथाग्निस्तहा दहेयं सगणान्प्रसह्य ॥३२॥
transliteration: śarārciṣā gāṇḍivenāhamekaḥ; sarvānkurūnbāhlikāṁścābhipatya ,himātyaye kakṣagato yathāgni;stahā daheyaṁ sagaṇānprasahya.
english: I alone, with my Gāṇḍīva bow, which has the brilliance of arrows, after assailing all the Kurus and Bāhlīkas, would forcibly burn them along with their retinues, just as a fire consumes dry grass at the end of winter.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 52 Verse 32. I alone, with my Gāṇḍīva bow, which has the brilliance of arrows, after assailing all the Kurus and Bāhlīkas, would forcibly burn them along with their retinues, just as a fire consumes dry grass at the end of winter.

sanskrit: पाणौ पृषत्का लिखिता ममैते धनुश्च सव्ये निहितं सबाणम् ।पादौ च मे सरथौ सध्वजौ च न मादृशं युद्धगतं जयन्ति ॥३३॥
transliteration: pāṇau pṛṣatkā likhitā mamaite; dhanuśca savye nihitaṁ sabāṇam ,pādau ca me sarathau sadhvajau ca; na mādṛśaṁ yuddhagataṁ jayanti.
english: These arrows are marked in my hand, and my bow, along with its arrows, is positioned on my left. My feet are ready with my chariot and banner. Those who face one like me in battle cannot conquer (me).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 52 Verse 33. These arrows are marked in my hand, and my bow, along with its arrows, is positioned on my left. My feet are ready with my chariot and banner. Those who face one like me in battle cannot conquer (me).

sanskrit: संजय उवाच ।तेषामनीकानि बृहद्ध्वजानि रणे समृद्धानि समागतानि ।गर्जन्ति भेरीनिनदोन्मुखानि मेघैर्यथा मेघगणास्तपान्ते ॥१॥
transliteration: saṁjaya uvāca ,teṣāmanīkāni bṛhaddhvajāni; raṇe samṛddhāni samāgatāni ,garjanti bherīninadonmukhāni; meghairyathā meghagaṇāstapānte.
english: Sañjaya said: Their assembled armies, mighty in battle and bearing great banners, roared, facing the sound of the war drums, just as masses of clouds roar with other clouds at the end of the summer season.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 53 Verse 1. Sañjaya said: Their assembled armies, mighty in battle and bearing great banners, roared, facing the sound of the war drums, just as masses of clouds roar with other clouds at the end of the summer season.

sanskrit: महागजाभ्राकुलमस्त्रतोयं वादित्रनेमीतलशब्दवच्च ।हिरण्यचित्रायुधवैद्युतं च महारथैरावृतशब्दवच्च ॥२॥
transliteration: mahāgajābhrākulamastratoyaṁ; vāditranemītalaśabdavacca ,hiraṇyacitrāyudhavaidyutaṁ ca; mahārathairāvṛtaśabdavacca.
english: It was agitated by great elephants like masses of clouds, having weapons like pouring rain, and resounding with the noise of musical instruments and chariot wheels. It glittered with brilliant, lightning-like weapons, and echoed with the sounds of great chariots.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 53 Verse 2. It was agitated by great elephants like masses of clouds, having weapons like pouring rain, and resounding with the noise of musical instruments and chariot wheels. It glittered with brilliant, lightning-like weapons, and echoed with the sounds of great chariots.

sanskrit: तद्भीमवेगं रुधिरौघवाहि खड्गाकुलं क्षत्रियजीववाहि ।अनार्तवं क्रूरमनिष्टवर्षं बभूव तत्संहरणं प्रजानाम् ॥३॥
transliteration: tadbhīmavegaṁ rudhiraughavāhi; khaḍgākulaṁ kṣatriyajīvavāhi ,anārtavaṁ krūramaniṣṭavarṣaṁ; babhūva tatsaṁharaṇaṁ prajānām.
english: That (battle), with its terrible speed, carrying streams of blood and agitated by swords, taking the lives of warriors (kṣatriyas), became an untimely, cruel, and inauspicious shower, a destruction of all people.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 53 Verse 3. That (battle), with its terrible speed, carrying streams of blood and agitated by swords, taking the lives of warriors (kṣatriyas), became an untimely, cruel, and inauspicious shower, a destruction of all people.

sanskrit: रथान्ससूतान्सहयान्गजांश्च सर्वानरीन्मृत्युवशं शरौघैः ।निन्ये हयांश्चैव तथा ससादीन्पदातिसंघांश्च तथैव पार्थः ॥४॥
transliteration: rathānsasūtānsahayāngajāṁśca; sarvānarīnmṛtyuvaśaṁ śaraughaiḥ ,ninye hayāṁścaiva tathā sasādī;npadātisaṁghāṁśca tathaiva pārthaḥ.
english: And Pārtha (Arjuna), with showers of arrows, brought all enemies - chariots with their charioteers and horses, elephants, and also horses with their riders, and similarly masses of foot-soldiers - under the sway of death.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 53 Verse 4. And Pārtha (Arjuna), with showers of arrows, brought all enemies - chariots with their charioteers and horses, elephants, and also horses with their riders, and similarly masses of foot-soldiers - under the sway of death.

sanskrit: कृपः शिखण्डी च रणे समेतौ दुर्योधनं सात्यकिरभ्यगच्छत ।श्रुतश्रवा द्रोणसुतेन सार्धं युधामन्युश्चित्रसेनेन चापि ॥५॥
transliteration: kṛpaḥ śikhaṇḍī ca raṇe sametau; duryodhanaṁ sātyakirabhyagacchata ,śrutaśravā droṇasutena sārdhaṁ; yudhāmanyuścitrasenena cāpi.
english: Kripa and Shikhandi met in battle. Satyaki approached Duryodhana. Shrutashrava fought alongside Drona's son, and Yudhamanyu also engaged Chitrasena.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 53 Verse 5. Kripa and Shikhandi met in battle. Satyaki approached Duryodhana. Shrutashrava fought alongside Drona's son, and Yudhamanyu also engaged Chitrasena.

sanskrit: कर्णस्य पुत्रस्तु रथी सुषेणं समागतः सृञ्जयांश्चोत्तमौजाः ।गान्धारराजं सहदेवः क्षुधार्तो महर्षभं सिंह इवाभ्यधावत् ॥६॥
transliteration: karṇasya putrastu rathī suṣeṇaṁ; samāgataḥ sṛñjayāṁścottamaujāḥ ,gāndhārarājaṁ sahadevaḥ kṣudhārto; maharṣabhaṁ siṁha ivābhyadhāvat.
english: Karna's son, who was a charioteer, indeed met Sushena; and Uttamaujas engaged the Srinjayas. Sahadeva, as if famished, charged the king of Gandhara like a lion rushing upon a great bull.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 53 Verse 6. Karna's son, who was a charioteer, indeed met Sushena; and Uttamaujas engaged the Srinjayas. Sahadeva, as if famished, charged the king of Gandhara like a lion rushing upon a great bull.

sanskrit: शतानीको नाकुलिः कर्णपुत्रं युवा युवानं वृषसेनं शरौघैः ।समार्दयत्कर्णसुतश्च वीरः पाञ्चालेयं शरवर्षैरनेकैः ॥७॥
transliteration: śatānīko nākuliḥ karṇaputraṁ; yuvā yuvānaṁ vṛṣasenaṁ śaraughaiḥ ,samārdayatkarṇasutaśca vīraḥ; pāñcāleyaṁ śaravarṣairanekaiḥ.
english: Nakula's young son, Satanika, assailed Karna's young son, Vrishasena, with a torrent of arrows. And Karna's heroic son, in turn, tormented the Panchala prince with numerous showers of arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 53 Verse 7. Nakula's young son, Satanika, assailed Karna's young son, Vrishasena, with a torrent of arrows. And Karna's heroic son, in turn, tormented the Panchala prince with numerous showers of arrows.

sanskrit: रथर्षभः कृतवर्माणमार्च्छन्माद्रीपुत्रो नकुलश्चित्रयोधी ।पाञ्चालानामधिपो याज्ञसेनिः सेनापतिं कर्णमार्च्छत्ससैन्यम् ॥८॥
transliteration: ratharṣabhaḥ kṛtavarmāṇamārccha;nmādrīputro nakulaścitrayodhī ,pāñcālānāmadhipo yājñaseniḥ; senāpatiṁ karṇamārcchatsasainyam.
english: Nakula, Madri's son, who was a superb charioteer and skillful warrior, attacked Kritavarma. Dhrishtadyumna, the chief of the Panchalas, assailed Commander Karna, along with his forces.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 53 Verse 8. Nakula, Madri's son, who was a superb charioteer and skillful warrior, attacked Kritavarma. Dhrishtadyumna, the chief of the Panchalas, assailed Commander Karna, along with his forces.

sanskrit: दुःशासनो भारत भारती च संशप्तकानां पृतना समृद्धा ।भीमं रणे शस्त्रभृतां वरिष्ठं तदा समार्च्छत्तमसह्यवेगम् ॥९॥
transliteration: duḥśāsano bhārata bhāratī ca; saṁśaptakānāṁ pṛtanā samṛddhā ,bhīmaṁ raṇe śastrabhṛtāṁ variṣṭhaṁ; tadā samārcchattamasahyavegam.
english: O descendant of Bharata, Duḥśāsana and the formidable army of the Saṃśaptakas then attacked Bhīma in battle, who was the foremost among weapon-bearers and of irresistible might.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 53 Verse 9. O descendant of Bharata, Duḥśāsana and the formidable army of the Saṃśaptakas then attacked Bhīma in battle, who was the foremost among weapon-bearers and of irresistible might.

sanskrit: कर्णात्मजं तत्र जघान शूरस्तथाच्छिनच्चोत्तमौजाः प्रसह्य ।तस्योत्तमाङ्गं निपपात भूमौ निनादयद्गां निनदेन खं च ॥१०॥
transliteration: karṇātmajaṁ tatra jaghāna śūra;stathācchinaccottamaujāḥ prasahya ,tasyottamāṅgaṁ nipapāta bhūmau; ninādayadgāṁ ninadena khaṁ ca.
english: There, the brave Uttamaujas forcibly killed Karṇa's son and cut him down. His head fell to the ground, making the earth and sky resound with its noise.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 53 Verse 10. There, the brave Uttamaujas forcibly killed Karṇa's son and cut him down. His head fell to the ground, making the earth and sky resound with its noise.

sanskrit: सुषेणशीर्षं पतितं पृथिव्यां विलोक्य कर्णोऽथ तदार्तरूपः ।क्रोधाद्धयांस्तस्य रथं ध्वजं च बाणैः सुधारैर्निशितैर्न्यकृन्तत् ॥११॥
transliteration: suṣeṇaśīrṣaṁ patitaṁ pṛthivyāṁ; vilokya karṇo'tha tadārtarūpaḥ ,krodhāddhayāṁstasya rathaṁ dhvajaṁ ca; bāṇaiḥ sudhārairniśitairnyakṛntat.
english: Seeing Suṣeṇa's head fallen on the earth, Karṇa, then distressed by that event, angrily cut down his (Uttamaujas's) horses, chariot, and banner with very sharp, excellent arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 53 Verse 11. Seeing Suṣeṇa's head fallen on the earth, Karṇa, then distressed by that event, angrily cut down his (Uttamaujas's) horses, chariot, and banner with very sharp, excellent arrows.

sanskrit: स तूत्तमौजा निशितैः पृषत्कैर्विव्याध खड्गेन च भास्वरेण ।पार्ष्णिं हयांश्चैव कृपस्य हत्वा शिखण्डिवाहं स ततोऽभ्यरोहत् ॥१२॥
transliteration: sa tūttamaujā niśitaiḥ pṛṣatkai;rvivyādha khaḍgena ca bhāsvareṇa ,pārṣṇiṁ hayāṁścaiva kṛpasya hatvā; śikhaṇḍivāhaṁ sa tato'bhyarohat.
english: But that Uttamaujas, having struck with sharp arrows and with a shining sword, and having killed Kṛpa's charioteer and horses, then mounted Śikhaṇḍin's chariot.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 53 Verse 12. But that Uttamaujas, having struck with sharp arrows and with a shining sword, and having killed Kṛpa's charioteer and horses, then mounted Śikhaṇḍin's chariot.

sanskrit: कृपं तु दृष्ट्वा विरथं रथस्थो नैच्छच्छरैस्ताडयितुं शिखण्डी ।तं द्रौणिरावार्य रथं कृपं स्म समुज्जह्रे पङ्कगतां यथा गाम् ॥१३॥
transliteration: kṛpaṁ tu dṛṣṭvā virathaṁ rathastho; naicchaccharaistāḍayituṁ śikhaṇḍī ,taṁ drauṇirāvārya rathaṁ kṛpaṁ sma; samujjahre paṅkagatāṁ yathā gām.
english: Seeing Kṛpa dismounted from his chariot, Śikhaṇḍī, who was still on his chariot, did not wish to strike him with arrows. Then, Drauṇi, bringing his chariot to Kṛpa's aid and protecting him, lifted Kṛpa up, just as one would lift a cow that has become stuck in mud.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 53 Verse 13. Seeing Kṛpa dismounted from his chariot, Śikhaṇḍī, who was still on his chariot, did not wish to strike him with arrows. Then, Drauṇi, bringing his chariot to Kṛpa's aid and protecting him, lifted Kṛpa up, just as one would lift a cow that has become stuck in mud.

sanskrit: हिरण्यवर्मा निशितैः पृषत्कैस्तवात्मजानामनिलात्मजो वै ।अतापयत्सैन्यमतीव भीमः काले शुचौ मध्यगतो यथार्कः ॥१४॥
transliteration: hiraṇyavarmā niśitaiḥ pṛṣatkai;stavātmajānāmanilātmajo vai ,atāpayatsainyamatīva bhīmaḥ; kāle śucau madhyagato yathārkaḥ.
english: Wearing golden armor, Bhīma, the son of the wind god, truly terrible, with sharpened arrows, scorched the army of your sons, just as the sun positioned in the middle of a scorching season (summer) [scorches the earth].
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 53 Verse 14. Wearing golden armor, Bhīma, the son of the wind god, truly terrible, with sharpened arrows, scorched the army of your sons, just as the sun positioned in the middle of a scorching season (summer) [scorches the earth].

sanskrit: संजय उवाच ।अथ त्विदानीं तुमुले विमर्दे द्विषद्भिरेको बहुभिः समावृतः ।महाभये सारथिमित्युवाच भीमश्चमूं वारयन्धार्तराष्ट्रीम् ।त्वं सारथे याहि जवेन वाहैर्नयाम्येतान्धार्तराष्ट्रान्यमाय ॥१॥
transliteration: saṁjaya uvāca ,atha tvidānīṁ tumule vimarde; dviṣadbhireko bahubhiḥ samāvṛtaḥ ,mahābhaye sārathimityuvāca; bhīmaścamūṁ vārayandhārtarāṣṭrīm ,tvaṁ sārathe yāhi javena vāhai;rnayāmyetāndhārtarāṣṭrānyamāya.
english: Saṃjaya said: Now, at that very moment, in the midst of that tumultuous battle, Bhīma, standing alone and surrounded by numerous enemies, in a moment of great peril, addressed his charioteer, saying: "O charioteer, drive swiftly with the horses! I shall deliver these sons of Dhṛtarāṣṭra (Kauravas) to the abode of Yama (the god of death)."
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 54 Verse 1. Saṃjaya said: Now, at that very moment, in the midst of that tumultuous battle, Bhīma, standing alone and surrounded by numerous enemies, in a moment of great peril, addressed his charioteer, saying: "O charioteer, drive swiftly with the horses! I shall deliver these sons of Dhṛtarāṣṭra (Kauravas) to the abode of Yama (the god of death)."

sanskrit: संचोदितो भीमसेनेन चैवं स सारथिः पुत्रबलं त्वदीयम् ।प्रायात्ततः सारथिरुग्रवेगो यतो भीमस्तद्बलं गन्तुमैच्छत् ॥२॥
transliteration: saṁcodito bhīmasenena caivaṁ; sa sārathiḥ putrabalaṁ tvadīyam ,prāyāttataḥ sārathirugravego; yato bhīmastadbalaṁ gantumaicchat.
english: Thus urged by Bhīmasena, that charioteer of yours, exceedingly swift, then drove towards your sons' army, to the very place where Bhīma desired to proceed to that force.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 54 Verse 2. Thus urged by Bhīmasena, that charioteer of yours, exceedingly swift, then drove towards your sons' army, to the very place where Bhīma desired to proceed to that force.

sanskrit: ततोऽपरे नागरथाश्वपत्तिभिः प्रत्युद्ययुः कुरवस्तं समन्तात् ।भीमस्य वाहाग्र्यमुदारवेगं समन्ततो बाणगणैर्निजघ्नुः ॥३॥
transliteration: tato'pare nāgarathāśvapattibhiḥ; pratyudyayuḥ kuravastaṁ samantāt ,bhīmasya vāhāgryamudāravegaṁ; samantato bāṇagaṇairnijaghnuḥ.
english: Then, other Kuru warriors, accompanied by elephants, chariots, horses, and foot soldiers, advanced against him (Bhīma) from all sides. They struck Bhīma's excellent horse, which was of great speed, from all directions with multitudes of arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 54 Verse 3. Then, other Kuru warriors, accompanied by elephants, chariots, horses, and foot soldiers, advanced against him (Bhīma) from all sides. They struck Bhīma's excellent horse, which was of great speed, from all directions with multitudes of arrows.

sanskrit: ततः शरानापततो महात्मा चिच्छेद बाणैस्तपनीयपुङ्खैः ।ते वै निपेतुस्तपनीयपुङ्खा द्विधा त्रिधा भीमशरैर्निकृत्ताः ॥४॥
transliteration: tataḥ śarānāpatato mahātmā; ciccheda bāṇaistapanīyapuṅkhaiḥ ,te vai nipetustapanīyapuṅkhā; dvidhā tridhā bhīmaśarairnikṛttāḥ.
english: Then, the great-souled Bhīma cut down the incoming arrows with his own arrows, which had golden shafts. Indeed, those very golden-shafted arrows (of the enemies) fell, cut into two or three pieces by Bhīma's arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 54 Verse 4. Then, the great-souled Bhīma cut down the incoming arrows with his own arrows, which had golden shafts. Indeed, those very golden-shafted arrows (of the enemies) fell, cut into two or three pieces by Bhīma's arrows.

sanskrit: ततो राजन्नागरथाश्वयूनां भीमाहतानां तव राजमध्ये ।घोरो निनादः प्रबभौ नरेन्द्र वज्राहतानामिव पर्वतानाम् ॥५॥
transliteration: tato rājannāgarathāśvayūnāṁ; bhīmāhatānāṁ tava rājamadhye ,ghoro ninādaḥ prababhau narendra; vajrāhatānāmiva parvatānām.
english: Then, O King (narendra), a terrible roar resounded in the midst of your kings, emanating from the masses of elephants, chariots, and horses that had been struck by Bhīma, just like the sound of mountains being struck by a thunderbolt (vajra).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 54 Verse 5. Then, O King (narendra), a terrible roar resounded in the midst of your kings, emanating from the masses of elephants, chariots, and horses that had been struck by Bhīma, just like the sound of mountains being struck by a thunderbolt (vajra).

sanskrit: ते वध्यमानाश्च नरेन्द्रमुख्या निर्भिन्ना वै भीमसेनप्रवेकैः ।भीमं समन्तात्समरेऽध्यरोहन्वृक्षं शकुन्ता इव पुष्पहेतोः ॥६॥
transliteration: te vadhyamānāśca narendramukhyā; nirbhinnā vai bhīmasenapravekaiḥ ,bhīmaṁ samantātsamare'dhyaroha;nvṛkṣaṁ śakuntā iva puṣpahetoḥ.
english: Even while being struck down and indeed pierced by the excellent arrows of Bhīmasena, those principal kings swarmed Bhīma from all sides in battle, just as birds ascend a tree for the sake of its flowers.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 54 Verse 6. Even while being struck down and indeed pierced by the excellent arrows of Bhīmasena, those principal kings swarmed Bhīma from all sides in battle, just as birds ascend a tree for the sake of its flowers.

sanskrit: ततोऽभिपातं तव सैन्यमध्ये प्रादुश्चक्रे वेगमिवात्तवेगः ।यथान्तकाले क्षपयन्दिधक्षुर्भूतान्तकृत्काल इवात्तदण्डः ॥७॥
transliteration: tato'bhipātaṁ tava sainyamadhye; prāduścakre vegamivāttavegaḥ ,yathāntakāle kṣapayandidhakṣu;rbhūtāntakṛtkāla ivāttadaṇḍaḥ.
english: Then, in the midst of your army, he launched an attack with tremendous speed, just like Time (kāla), the annihilator of all beings, eager to consume everything, wielding his staff of punishment at the time of the world's dissolution.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 54 Verse 7. Then, in the midst of your army, he launched an attack with tremendous speed, just like Time (kāla), the annihilator of all beings, eager to consume everything, wielding his staff of punishment at the time of the world's dissolution.

sanskrit: तस्यातिवेगस्य रणेऽतिवेगं नाशक्नुवन्धारयितुं त्वदीयाः ।व्यात्ताननस्यापततो यथैव कालस्य काले हरतः प्रजा वै ॥८॥
transliteration: tasyātivegasya raṇe'tivegaṁ; nāśaknuvandhārayituṁ tvadīyāḥ ,vyāttānanasyāpatato yathaiva; kālasya kāle harataḥ prajā vai.
english: Your warriors were unable to withstand his extreme speed in battle, just as people (prajā) cannot bear the onslaught of Time (kāla) with its gaping mouth, sweeping away all beings at the hour of death.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 54 Verse 8. Your warriors were unable to withstand his extreme speed in battle, just as people (prajā) cannot bear the onslaught of Time (kāla) with its gaping mouth, sweeping away all beings at the hour of death.

sanskrit: ततो बलं भारत भारतानां प्रदह्यमानं समरे महात्मन् ।भीतं दिशोऽकीर्यत भीमनुन्नं महानिलेनाभ्रगणो यथैव ॥९॥
transliteration: tato balaṁ bhārata bhāratānāṁ; pradahyamānaṁ samare mahātman ,bhītaṁ diśo'kīryata bhīmanunnaṁ; mahānilenābhragaṇo yathaiva.
english: Then, O descendant of Bhārata, O great-souled one, the army of the Bhāratas, being overwhelmed in battle, became terrified and was scattered in all directions, driven by Bhīma, just as a mass of clouds is dispersed by a mighty wind.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 54 Verse 9. Then, O descendant of Bhārata, O great-souled one, the army of the Bhāratas, being overwhelmed in battle, became terrified and was scattered in all directions, driven by Bhīma, just as a mass of clouds is dispersed by a mighty wind.

sanskrit: ततो धीमान्सारथिमब्रवीद्बली स भीमसेनः पुनरेव हृष्टः ।सूताभिजानीहि परान्स्वकान्वा रथान्ध्वजांश्चापततः समेतान् ।युध्यन्नहं नाभिजानामि किंचिन्मा सैन्यं स्वं छादयिष्ये पृषत्कैः ॥१०॥
transliteration: tato dhīmānsārathimabravīdbalī; sa bhīmasenaḥ punareva hṛṣṭaḥ ,sūtābhijānīhi parānsvakānvā; rathāndhvajāṁścāpatataḥ sametān ,yudhyannahaṁ nābhijānāmi kiṁci;nmā sainyaṁ svaṁ chādayiṣye pṛṣatkaiḥ.
english: Then, that mighty and intelligent Bhīmasena, again exhilarated, said to his charioteer: 'O charioteer, discern who are the enemies and who are our own among these chariots and banners rushing together! As I fight, I cannot distinguish anything, and I do not wish to cover our own army with my arrows.'
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 54 Verse 10. Then, that mighty and intelligent Bhīmasena, again exhilarated, said to his charioteer: 'O charioteer, discern who are the enemies and who are our own among these chariots and banners rushing together! As I fight, I cannot distinguish anything, and I do not wish to cover our own army with my arrows.'

sanskrit: अरीन्विशोकाभिनिरीक्ष्य सर्वतो मनस्तु चिन्ता प्रदुनोति मे भृशम् ।राजातुरो नागमद्यत्किरीटी बहूनि दुःखान्यभिजातोऽस्मि सूत ॥११॥
transliteration: arīnviśokābhinirīkṣya sarvato; manastu cintā pradunoti me bhṛśam ,rājāturo nāgamadyatkirīṭī; bahūni duḥkhānyabhijāto'smi sūta.
english: Having observed the enemies on all sides, fearless, my mind is greatly tormented by anxiety. O charioteer (Sūta), because the king (Yudhiṣṭhira) did not come, being distressed, I, the crowned one, have encountered many sorrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 54 Verse 11. Having observed the enemies on all sides, fearless, my mind is greatly tormented by anxiety. O charioteer (Sūta), because the king (Yudhiṣṭhira) did not come, being distressed, I, the crowned one, have encountered many sorrows.

sanskrit: एतद्दुःखं सारथे धर्मराजो यन्मां हित्वा यातवाञ्शत्रुमध्ये ।नैनं जीवन्नापि जानाम्यजीवन्बीभत्सुं वा तन्ममाद्यातिदुःखम् ॥१२॥
transliteration: etadduḥkhaṁ sārathe dharmarājo; yanmāṁ hitvā yātavāñśatrumadhye ,nainaṁ jīvannāpi jānāmyajīva;nbībhatsuṁ vā tanmamādyātiduḥkham.
english: O charioteer (Sūta), this sorrow is that King Yudhiṣṭhira (Dharmarāja), having abandoned me, went into the midst of enemies. I do not know him, whether alive or not alive, nor do I know myself (Bībhatsu) whether I am alive or not. That is my extreme sorrow today.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 54 Verse 12. O charioteer (Sūta), this sorrow is that King Yudhiṣṭhira (Dharmarāja), having abandoned me, went into the midst of enemies. I do not know him, whether alive or not alive, nor do I know myself (Bībhatsu) whether I am alive or not. That is my extreme sorrow today.

sanskrit: सोऽहं द्विषत्सैन्यमुदग्रकल्पं विनाशयिष्ये परमप्रतीतः ।एतान्निहत्याजिमध्ये समेतान्प्रीतो भविष्यामि सह त्वयाद्य ॥१३॥
transliteration: so'haṁ dviṣatsainyamudagrakalpaṁ; vināśayiṣye paramapratītaḥ ,etānnihatyājimadhye sametā;nprīto bhaviṣyāmi saha tvayādya.
english: Therefore, I will destroy this formidable enemy army, being supremely content. Having slain these who are assembled in the midst of battle, I shall be content, along with you, today.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 54 Verse 13. Therefore, I will destroy this formidable enemy army, being supremely content. Having slain these who are assembled in the midst of battle, I shall be content, along with you, today.

sanskrit: सर्वांस्तूणीरान्मार्गणान्वान्ववेक्ष्य किं शिष्टं स्यात्सायकानां रथे मे ।का वा जातिः किं प्रमाणं च तेषां ज्ञात्वा व्यक्तं तन्ममाचक्ष्व सूत ॥१४॥
transliteration: sarvāṁstūṇīrānmārgaṇānvānvavekṣya; kiṁ śiṣṭaṁ syātsāyakānāṁ rathe me ,kā vā jātiḥ kiṁ pramāṇaṁ ca teṣāṁ; jñātvā vyaktaṁ tanmamācakṣva sūta.
english: O charioteer (Sūta), having surveyed all the quivers and arrows, tell me clearly what quantity of arrows remains in my chariot. What is their type and what is their measure? Having ascertained that, report it to me.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 54 Verse 14. O charioteer (Sūta), having surveyed all the quivers and arrows, tell me clearly what quantity of arrows remains in my chariot. What is their type and what is their measure? Having ascertained that, report it to me.

sanskrit: विशोक उवाच ।षण्मार्गणानामयुतानि वीर क्षुराश्च भल्लाश्च तथायुताख्याः ।नाराचानां द्वे सहस्रे तु वीर त्रीण्येव च प्रदराणां च पार्थ ॥१५॥
transliteration: viśoka uvāca ,ṣaṇmārgaṇānāmayutāni vīra; kṣurāśca bhallāśca tathāyutākhyāḥ ,nārācānāṁ dve sahasre tu vīra; trīṇyeva ca pradarāṇāṁ ca pārtha.
english: Viśoka said: O hero, O Pārtha, there are sixty thousand arrows, and also ten thousand razor-sharp arrows and lances, and two thousand iron arrows, and indeed three thousand broad-headed arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 54 Verse 15. Viśoka said: O hero, O Pārtha, there are sixty thousand arrows, and also ten thousand razor-sharp arrows and lances, and two thousand iron arrows, and indeed three thousand broad-headed arrows.

sanskrit: अस्त्यायुधं पाण्डवेयावशिष्टं न यद्वहेच्छकटं षड्गवीयम् ।एतद्विद्वन्मुञ्च सहस्रशोऽपि गदासिबाहुद्रविणं च तेऽस्ति ॥१६॥
transliteration: astyāyudhaṁ pāṇḍaveyāvaśiṣṭaṁ; na yadvahecchakaṭaṁ ṣaḍgavīyam ,etadvidvanmuñca sahasraśo'pi; gadāsibāhudraviṇaṁ ca te'sti.
english: There remains a weapon (arsenal) from the Pāṇḍavas which a six-ox cart could not carry. O wise one, unleash these by the thousands! And you also possess the wealth of maces, swords, and the might of your arms.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 54 Verse 16. There remains a weapon (arsenal) from the Pāṇḍavas which a six-ox cart could not carry. O wise one, unleash these by the thousands! And you also possess the wealth of maces, swords, and the might of your arms.

sanskrit: भीम उवाच ।सूताद्येमं पश्य भीमप्रमुक्तैः संभिन्दद्भिः पार्थिवानाशुवेगैः ।उग्रैर्बाणैराहवं घोररूपं नष्टादित्यं मृत्युलोकेन तुल्यम् ॥१७॥
transliteration: bhīma uvāca ,sūtādyemaṁ paśya bhīmapramuktaiḥ; saṁbhindadbhiḥ pārthivānāśuvegaiḥ ,ugrairbāṇairāhavaṁ ghorarūpaṁ; naṣṭādityaṁ mṛtyulokena tulyam.
english: Bhīma said: O charioteer, today behold this terrifying battle, which resembles the world of death (mṛtyuloka), with the sun obscured by the fierce, swift-moving arrows released by Bhīma, shattering the kings.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 54 Verse 17. Bhīma said: O charioteer, today behold this terrifying battle, which resembles the world of death (mṛtyuloka), with the sun obscured by the fierce, swift-moving arrows released by Bhīma, shattering the kings.

sanskrit: अद्यैव तद्विदितं पार्थिवानां भविष्यति आकुमारं च सूत ।निमग्नो वा समरे भीमसेन एकः कुरून्वा समरे विजेता ॥१८॥
transliteration: adyaiva tadviditaṁ pārthivānāṁ; bhaviṣyati ākumāraṁ ca sūta ,nimagno vā samare bhīmasena; ekaḥ kurūnvā samare vijetā.
english: O charioteer, today itself this will become known to the kings and even to the children: either Bhīmasena will be immersed alone in battle, or he alone will conquer the Kurus in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 54 Verse 18. O charioteer, today itself this will become known to the kings and even to the children: either Bhīmasena will be immersed alone in battle, or he alone will conquer the Kurus in battle.

sanskrit: सर्वे संख्ये कुरवो निष्पतन्तु मां वा लोकाः कीर्तयन्त्वाकुमारम् ।सर्वानेकस्तानहं पातयिष्ये ते वा सर्वे भीमसेनं तुदन्तु ॥१९॥
transliteration: sarve saṁkhye kuravo niṣpatantu; māṁ vā lokāḥ kīrtayantvākumāram ,sarvānekastānahaṁ pātayiṣye; te vā sarve bhīmasenaṁ tudantu.
english: Let all the Kauravas be struck down in battle, or let people proclaim my fame from childhood onwards. I shall fell all those united ones. Alternatively, let all of them torment Bhimasena.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 54 Verse 19. Let all the Kauravas be struck down in battle, or let people proclaim my fame from childhood onwards. I shall fell all those united ones. Alternatively, let all of them torment Bhimasena.

sanskrit: आशास्तारः कर्म चाप्युत्तमं वा तन्मे देवाः केवलं साधयन्तु ।आयात्विहाद्यार्जुनः शत्रुघाती शक्रस्तूर्णं यज्ञ इवोपहूतः ॥२०॥
transliteration: āśāstāraḥ karma cāpyuttamaṁ vā; tanme devāḥ kevalaṁ sādhayantu ,āyātvihādyārjunaḥ śatrughātī; śakrastūrṇaṁ yajña ivopahūtaḥ.
english: May the gods fulfill for me that excellent deed or wish that I desire. Let Arjuna, the slayer of enemies, come here today, quickly like Indra, who is invited to a Vedic ritual (yajña).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 54 Verse 20. May the gods fulfill for me that excellent deed or wish that I desire. Let Arjuna, the slayer of enemies, come here today, quickly like Indra, who is invited to a Vedic ritual (yajña).

sanskrit: ईक्षस्वैतां भारतीं दीर्यमाणामेते कस्माद्विद्रवन्ते नरेन्द्राः ।व्यक्तं धीमान्सव्यसाची नराग्र्यः सैन्यं ह्येतच्छादयत्याशु बाणैः ॥२१॥
transliteration: īkṣasvaitāṁ bhāratīṁ dīryamāṇā;mete kasmādvidravante narendrāḥ ,vyaktaṁ dhīmānsavyasācī narāgryaḥ; sainyaṁ hyetacchādayatyāśu bāṇaiḥ.
english: Observe this Bharata army (bhāratī) being torn asunder! Why do these kings flee? Clearly, the intelligent Savyasacin, preeminent among men, is swiftly covering this army with arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 54 Verse 21. Observe this Bharata army (bhāratī) being torn asunder! Why do these kings flee? Clearly, the intelligent Savyasacin, preeminent among men, is swiftly covering this army with arrows.

sanskrit: पश्य ध्वजांश्च द्रवतो विशोक नागान्हयान्पत्तिसंघांश्च संख्ये ।रथान्विशीर्णाञ्शरशक्तिताडितान्पश्यस्वैतान्रथिनश्चैव सूत ॥२२॥
transliteration: paśya dhvajāṁśca dravato viśoka; nāgānhayānpattisaṁghāṁśca saṁkhye ,rathānviśīrṇāñśaraśaktitāḍitā;npaśyasvaitānrathinaścaiva sūta.
english: O Viśoka, behold in battle (saṅkhye) the standards, and the fleeing elephants, horses, and throngs of infantry! And, O Charioteer (Sūta), behold these chariots, shattered and struck by arrows and spears, and also the charioteers themselves.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 54 Verse 22. O Viśoka, behold in battle (saṅkhye) the standards, and the fleeing elephants, horses, and throngs of infantry! And, O Charioteer (Sūta), behold these chariots, shattered and struck by arrows and spears, and also the charioteers themselves.

sanskrit: आपूर्यते कौरवी चाप्यभीक्ष्णं सेना ह्यसौ सुभृशं हन्यमाना ।धनंजयस्याशनितुल्यवेगैर्ग्रस्ता शरैर्बर्हिसुवर्णवाजैः ॥२३॥
transliteration: āpūryate kauravī cāpyabhīkṣṇaṁ; senā hyasau subhṛśaṁ hanyamānā ,dhanaṁjayasyāśanitulyavegai;rgrastā śarairbarhisuvarṇavājaiḥ.
english: Indeed, this Kaurava army, though constantly reinforced, is being severely struck and consumed by Dhanañjaya's lightning-fast arrows, which are adorned with peacock and golden feathers.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 54 Verse 23. Indeed, this Kaurava army, though constantly reinforced, is being severely struck and consumed by Dhanañjaya's lightning-fast arrows, which are adorned with peacock and golden feathers.

sanskrit: एते द्रवन्ति स्म रथाश्वनागाः पदातिसंघानवमर्दयन्तः ।संमुह्यमानाः कौरवाः सर्व एव द्रवन्ति नागा इव दावभीताः ।हाहाकृताश्चैव रणे विशोक मुञ्चन्ति नादान्विपुलान्गजेन्द्राः ॥२४॥
transliteration: ete dravanti sma rathāśvanāgāḥ; padātisaṁghānavamardayantaḥ ,saṁmuhyamānāḥ kauravāḥ sarva eva; dravanti nāgā iva dāvabhītāḥ ,hāhākṛtāścaiva raṇe viśoka; muñcanti nādānvipulāngajendrāḥ.
english: These chariots, horses, and elephants ran, trampling down the infantry formations. All the Kauravas, utterly bewildered, fled like elephants terrified by a forest fire. And, O Visoka, the great elephants in the battle loudly roared, crying 'hāhā'.
