search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 54 Verse 24. These chariots, horses, and elephants ran, trampling down the infantry formations. All the Kauravas, utterly bewildered, fled like elephants terrified by a forest fire. And, O Visoka, the great elephants in the battle loudly roared, crying 'hāhā'.

sanskrit: विशोक उवाच ।सर्वे कामाः पाण्डव ते समृद्धाः कपिध्वजो दृश्यते हस्तिसैन्ये ।नीलाद्धनाद्विद्युतमुच्चरन्तीं तथापश्यं विस्फुरद्वै धनुस्तत् ॥२५॥
transliteration: viśoka uvāca ,sarve kāmāḥ pāṇḍava te samṛddhāḥ; kapidhvajo dṛśyate hastisainye ,nīlāddhanādvidyutamuccarantīṁ; tathāpaśyaṁ visphuradvai dhanustat.
english: Visoka said: 'O Pāṇḍava, all your aspirations are being realized. Arjuna, whose standard bears the monkey (Hanumān), is visible among the elephant division. And I saw that very bow, flashing like a bolt of lightning emerging from a dark blue cloud.'
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 54 Verse 25. Visoka said: 'O Pāṇḍava, all your aspirations are being realized. Arjuna, whose standard bears the monkey (Hanumān), is visible among the elephant division. And I saw that very bow, flashing like a bolt of lightning emerging from a dark blue cloud.'

sanskrit: कपिर्ह्यसौ वीक्ष्यते सर्वतो वै ध्वजाग्रमारुह्य धनंजयस्य ।दिवाकराभो मणिरेष दिव्यो विभ्राजते चैव किरीटसंस्थः ॥२६॥
transliteration: kapirhyasau vīkṣyate sarvato vai; dhvajāgramāruhya dhanaṁjayasya ,divākarābho maṇireṣa divyo; vibhrājate caiva kirīṭasaṁsthaḥ.
english: Indeed, this monkey (Hanumān) is seen everywhere, having ascended to the top of Dhanañjaya's (Arjuna's) banner. And this divine jewel, radiant like the sun, shines brightly as it is placed in his crown.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 54 Verse 26. Indeed, this monkey (Hanumān) is seen everywhere, having ascended to the top of Dhanañjaya's (Arjuna's) banner. And this divine jewel, radiant like the sun, shines brightly as it is placed in his crown.

sanskrit: पार्श्वे भीमं पाण्डुराभ्रप्रकाशं पश्येमं त्वं देवदत्तं सुघोषम् ।अभीशुहस्तस्य जनार्दनस्य विगाहमानस्य चमूं परेषाम् ॥२७॥
transliteration: pārśve bhīmaṁ pāṇḍurābhraprakāśaṁ; paśyemaṁ tvaṁ devadattaṁ sughoṣam ,abhīśuhastasya janārdanasya; vigāhamānasya camūṁ pareṣām.
english: You, behold this formidable Devadatta (conch), shining like a white cloud and producing a great sound, positioned beside Janārdana (Kṛṣṇa), who, holding the reins, is penetrating the army of the enemies.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 54 Verse 27. You, behold this formidable Devadatta (conch), shining like a white cloud and producing a great sound, positioned beside Janārdana (Kṛṣṇa), who, holding the reins, is penetrating the army of the enemies.

sanskrit: रविप्रभं वज्रनाभं क्षुरान्तं पार्श्वे स्थितं पश्य जनार्दनस्य ।चक्रं यशो वर्धयत्केशवस्य सदार्चितं यदुभिः पश्य वीर ॥२८॥
transliteration: raviprabhaṁ vajranābhaṁ kṣurāntaṁ; pārśve sthitaṁ paśya janārdanasya ,cakraṁ yaśo vardhayatkeśavasya; sadārcitaṁ yadubhiḥ paśya vīra.
english: O hero, behold the discus (cakra) of Janārdana (Kṛṣṇa), situated by his side, shining like the sun, having a thunderbolt (vajra) at its hub, and sharp edges. See how it, always worshipped by the Yadus, increases the glory of Keśava (Kṛṣṇa).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 54 Verse 28. O hero, behold the discus (cakra) of Janārdana (Kṛṣṇa), situated by his side, shining like the sun, having a thunderbolt (vajra) at its hub, and sharp edges. See how it, always worshipped by the Yadus, increases the glory of Keśava (Kṛṣṇa).

sanskrit: भीम उवाच ।ददामि ते ग्रामवरांश्चतुर्दश प्रियाख्याने सारथे सुप्रसन्नः ।दसीशतं चापि रथांश्च विंशतिं यदर्जुनं वेदयसे विशोक ॥२९॥
transliteration: bhīma uvāca ,dadāmi te grāmavarāṁścaturdaśa; priyākhyāne sārathe suprasannaḥ ,dasīśataṁ cāpi rathāṁśca viṁśatiṁ; yadarjunaṁ vedayase viśoka.
english: Bhīma said: "O charioteer, I am very pleased, and I will give you fourteen excellent villages, a hundred female attendants, and twenty chariots, for bringing the good news that Arjuna is free from sorrow."
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 54 Verse 29. Bhīma said: "O charioteer, I am very pleased, and I will give you fourteen excellent villages, a hundred female attendants, and twenty chariots, for bringing the good news that Arjuna is free from sorrow."

sanskrit: संजय उवाच ।श्रुत्वा च रथनिर्घोषं सिंहनादं च संयुगे ।अर्जुनः प्राह गोविन्दं शीघ्रं चोदय वाजिनः ॥१॥
transliteration: saṁjaya uvāca ,śrutvā ca rathanirghoṣaṁ siṁhanādaṁ ca saṁyuge ,arjunaḥ prāha govindaṁ śīghraṁ codaya vājinaḥ.
english: Sañjaya said: "Having heard the roar of chariots and the lion's roar in battle, Arjuna spoke to Govinda (Kṛṣṇa): 'Quickly, drive the horses!'"
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 55 Verse 1. Sañjaya said: "Having heard the roar of chariots and the lion's roar in battle, Arjuna spoke to Govinda (Kṛṣṇa): 'Quickly, drive the horses!'"

sanskrit: अर्जुनस्य वचः श्रुत्वा गोविन्दोऽर्जुनमब्रवीत् ।एष गच्छामि सुक्षिप्रं यत्र भीमो व्यवस्थितः ॥२॥
transliteration: arjunasya vacaḥ śrutvā govindo'rjunamabravīt ,eṣa gacchāmi sukṣipraṁ yatra bhīmo vyavasthitaḥ.
english: Hearing Arjuna's words, Govinda (Krishna) replied to Arjuna, 'I am going there very quickly, to where Bhima is stationed.'
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 55 Verse 2. Hearing Arjuna's words, Govinda (Krishna) replied to Arjuna, 'I am going there very quickly, to where Bhima is stationed.'

sanskrit: आयान्तमश्वैर्हिमशङ्खवर्णैः सुवर्णमुक्तामणिजालनद्धैः ।जम्भं जिघांसुं प्रगृहीतवज्रं जयाय देवेन्द्रमिवोग्रमन्युम् ॥३॥
transliteration: āyāntamaśvairhimaśaṅkhavarṇaiḥ; suvarṇamuktāmaṇijālanaddhaiḥ ,jambhaṁ jighāṁsuṁ pragṛhītavajraṁ; jayāya devendramivogramanyum.
english: He was approaching with horses white as snow and conch shells, adorned with nets of gold, pearls, and jewels, just like the lord of gods Indra, fiercely enraged, holding his thunderbolt, eager to slay the demon Jambha, for the sake of victory.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 55 Verse 3. He was approaching with horses white as snow and conch shells, adorned with nets of gold, pearls, and jewels, just like the lord of gods Indra, fiercely enraged, holding his thunderbolt, eager to slay the demon Jambha, for the sake of victory.

sanskrit: रथाश्वमातङ्गपदातिसंघा बाणस्वनैर्नेमिखुरस्वनैश्च ।संनादयन्तो वसुधां दिशश्च क्रुद्धा नृसिंहा जयमभ्युदीयुः ॥४॥
transliteration: rathāśvamātaṅgapadātisaṁghā; bāṇasvanairnemikhurasvanaiśca ,saṁnādayanto vasudhāṁ diśaśca; kruddhā nṛsiṁhā jayamabhyudīyuḥ.
english: Enraged masses of chariots, horses, elephants, and infantry - like lions among men - made the earth and the directions reverberate with the sounds of arrows and the clatter of wheels and hooves, as they advanced towards victory.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 55 Verse 4. Enraged masses of chariots, horses, elephants, and infantry - like lions among men - made the earth and the directions reverberate with the sounds of arrows and the clatter of wheels and hooves, as they advanced towards victory.

sanskrit: तेषां च पार्थस्य महत्तदासीद्देहासुपाप्मक्षपणं सुयुद्धम् ।त्रैलोक्यहेतोरसुरैर्यथासीद्देवस्य विष्णोर्जयतां वरस्य ॥५॥
transliteration: teṣāṁ ca pārthasya mahattadāsī;ddehāsupāpmakṣapaṇaṁ suyuddham ,trailokyahetorasurairyathāsī;ddevasya viṣṇorjayatāṁ varasya.
english: And then, their great battle, along with Partha (Arjuna), was a destruction of bodies, life-breaths, and sins (dehāsupāpmakṣapaṇam), just as the supreme god (deva) Vishnu, the best among conquerors, fought for the sake of the three worlds against the asuras.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 55 Verse 5. And then, their great battle, along with Partha (Arjuna), was a destruction of bodies, life-breaths, and sins (dehāsupāpmakṣapaṇam), just as the supreme god (deva) Vishnu, the best among conquerors, fought for the sake of the three worlds against the asuras.

sanskrit: तैरस्तमुच्चावचमायुधौघमेकः प्रचिच्छेद किरीटमाली ।क्षुरार्धचन्द्रैर्निशितैश्च बाणैः शिरांसि तेषां बहुधा च बाहून् ॥६॥
transliteration: tairastamuccāvacamāyudhaugha;mekaḥ praciccheda kirīṭamālī ,kṣurārdhacandrairniśitaiśca bāṇaiḥ; śirāṁsi teṣāṁ bahudhā ca bāhūn.
english: The one adorned with a diadem (kirīṭa) single-handedly cut down the diverse barrage of weapons thrown by them. With sharp, crescent-shaped, razor-edged arrows, he repeatedly severed their heads and arms.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 55 Verse 6. The one adorned with a diadem (kirīṭa) single-handedly cut down the diverse barrage of weapons thrown by them. With sharp, crescent-shaped, razor-edged arrows, he repeatedly severed their heads and arms.

sanskrit: छत्राणि वालव्यजनानि केतूनश्वान्रथान्पत्तिगणान्द्विपांश्च ।ते पेतुरुर्व्यां बहुधा विरूपा वातप्रभग्नानि यथा वनानि ॥७॥
transliteration: chatrāṇi vālavyajanāni ketū;naśvānrathānpattigaṇāndvipāṁśca ,te petururvyāṁ bahudhā virūpā; vātaprabhagnāni yathā vanāni.
english: Parasols, yak-tail whisks, banners, horses, chariots, infantry units, and elephants - all these fell to the earth, disfigured and scattered, just like trees in a forest shattered by a storm.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 55 Verse 7. Parasols, yak-tail whisks, banners, horses, chariots, infantry units, and elephants - all these fell to the earth, disfigured and scattered, just like trees in a forest shattered by a storm.

sanskrit: सुवर्णजालावतता महागजाः सवैजयन्तीध्वजयोधकल्पिताः ।सुवर्णपुङ्खैरिषुभिः समाचिताश्चकाशिरे प्रज्वलिता यथाचलाः ॥८॥
transliteration: suvarṇajālāvatatā mahāgajāḥ; savaijayantīdhvajayodhakalpitāḥ ,suvarṇapuṅkhairiṣubhiḥ samācitā;ścakāśire prajvalitā yathācalāḥ.
english: The mighty elephants, adorned with golden nets, equipped with banners, flags, and warriors, and thoroughly pierced by arrows with golden shafts, gleamed like blazing mountains.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 55 Verse 8. The mighty elephants, adorned with golden nets, equipped with banners, flags, and warriors, and thoroughly pierced by arrows with golden shafts, gleamed like blazing mountains.

sanskrit: विदार्य नागांश्च रथांश्च वाजिनः शरोत्तमैर्वासववज्रसंनिभैः ।द्रुतं ययौ कर्णजिघांसया तथा यथा मरुत्वान्बलभेदने पुरा ॥९॥
transliteration: vidārya nāgāṁśca rathāṁśca vājinaḥ; śarottamairvāsavavajrasaṁnibhaiḥ ,drutaṁ yayau karṇajighāṁsayā tathā; yathā marutvānbalabhedane purā.
english: Having ripped apart the elephants, chariots, and horses with superb arrows comparable to Indra's (Vāsava) thunderbolt (vajra), he swiftly advanced with the intent of slaying Karṇa, just as Indra (Marutvān) did in the past (pura) when he destroyed the Asura Bala.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 55 Verse 9. Having ripped apart the elephants, chariots, and horses with superb arrows comparable to Indra's (Vāsava) thunderbolt (vajra), he swiftly advanced with the intent of slaying Karṇa, just as Indra (Marutvān) did in the past (pura) when he destroyed the Asura Bala.

sanskrit: ततः स पुरुषव्याघ्रः सूतसैन्यमरिंदम ।प्रविवेश महाबाहुर्मकरः सागरं यथा ॥१०॥
transliteration: tataḥ sa puruṣavyāghraḥ sūtasainyamariṁdama ,praviveśa mahābāhurmakaraḥ sāgaraṁ yathā.
english: Then, that mighty-armed (mahābāhu) tiger among men (puruṣavyāghra), the subduer of enemies (ariṃdama), entered the army led by Karna, just as a great sea-monster (makara) enters the ocean.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 55 Verse 10. Then, that mighty-armed (mahābāhu) tiger among men (puruṣavyāghra), the subduer of enemies (ariṃdama), entered the army led by Karna, just as a great sea-monster (makara) enters the ocean.

sanskrit: तं दृष्ट्वा तावका राजन्रथपत्तिसमन्विताः ।गजाश्वसादिबहुलाः पाण्डवं समुपाद्रवन् ॥११॥
transliteration: taṁ dṛṣṭvā tāvakā rājanrathapattisamanvitāḥ ,gajāśvasādibahulāḥ pāṇḍavaṁ samupādravan.
english: O King (rājan), upon seeing him, your warriors, accompanied by chariot-chiefs and abundant with elephants, horses, and riders, charged towards the son of Pāṇḍu (pāṇḍava).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 55 Verse 11. O King (rājan), upon seeing him, your warriors, accompanied by chariot-chiefs and abundant with elephants, horses, and riders, charged towards the son of Pāṇḍu (pāṇḍava).

sanskrit: तत्राभिद्रवतां पार्थमारावः सुमहानभूत् ।सागरस्येव मत्तस्य यथा स्यात्सलिलस्वनः ॥१२॥
transliteration: tatrābhidravatāṁ pārthamārāvaḥ sumahānabhūt ,sāgarasyeva mattasya yathā syātsalilasvanaḥ.
english: Then, as they charged towards Pārtha (Arjuna), a tremendous roar arose, just like the sound of agitated ocean waters.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 55 Verse 12. Then, as they charged towards Pārtha (Arjuna), a tremendous roar arose, just like the sound of agitated ocean waters.

sanskrit: ते तु तं पुरुषव्याघ्रं व्याघ्रा इव महारथाः ।अभ्यद्रवन्त संग्रामे त्यक्त्वा प्राणकृतं भयम् ॥१३॥
transliteration: te tu taṁ puruṣavyāghraṁ vyāghrā iva mahārathāḥ ,abhyadravanta saṁgrāme tyaktvā prāṇakṛtaṁ bhayam.
english: Indeed, those great chariot-warriors (mahārathāḥ), like tigers, charged towards that tiger among men (puruṣavyāghra) in battle, having abandoned the fear caused by the thought of losing their lives.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 55 Verse 13. Indeed, those great chariot-warriors (mahārathāḥ), like tigers, charged towards that tiger among men (puruṣavyāghra) in battle, having abandoned the fear caused by the thought of losing their lives.

sanskrit: तेषामापततां तत्र शरवर्षाणि मुञ्चताम् ।अर्जुनो व्यधमत्सैन्यं महावातो घनानिव ॥१४॥
transliteration: teṣāmāpatatāṁ tatra śaravarṣāṇi muñcatām ,arjuno vyadhamatsainyaṁ mahāvāto ghanāniva.
english: Just as a strong wind scatters clouds, Arjuna dispersed that army of theirs, which was rushing forward there and unleashing showers of arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 55 Verse 14. Just as a strong wind scatters clouds, Arjuna dispersed that army of theirs, which was rushing forward there and unleashing showers of arrows.

sanskrit: तेऽर्जुनं सहिता भूत्वा रथवंशैः प्रहारिणः ।अभियाय महेष्वासा विव्यधुर्निशितैः शरैः ॥१५॥
transliteration: te'rjunaṁ sahitā bhūtvā rathavaṁśaiḥ prahāriṇaḥ ,abhiyāya maheṣvāsā vivyadhurniśitaiḥ śaraiḥ.
english: Having assembled, those great archers, who were also skilled chariot-fighters, approached Arjuna and pierced him with sharp arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 55 Verse 15. Having assembled, those great archers, who were also skilled chariot-fighters, approached Arjuna and pierced him with sharp arrows.

sanskrit: ततोऽर्जुनः सहस्राणि रथवारणवाजिनाम् ।प्रेषयामास विशिखैर्यमस्य सदनं प्रति ॥१६॥
transliteration: tato'rjunaḥ sahasrāṇi rathavāraṇavājinām ,preṣayāmāsa viśikhairyamasya sadanaṁ prati.
english: Then Arjuna, using his arrows, dispatched thousands of warriors on chariots, elephants, and horses to the abode of Yama, the god of death.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 55 Verse 16. Then Arjuna, using his arrows, dispatched thousands of warriors on chariots, elephants, and horses to the abode of Yama, the god of death.

sanskrit: ते वध्यमानाः समरे पार्थचापच्युतैः शरैः ।तत्र तत्र स्म लीयन्ते भये जाते महारथाः ॥१७॥
transliteration: te vadhyamānāḥ samare pārthacāpacyutaiḥ śaraiḥ ,tatra tatra sma līyante bhaye jāte mahārathāḥ.
english: As fear arose among them, those great chariot-warriors, being slain in battle by arrows loosed from Pārtha's (Arjuna's) bow, scattered and hid themselves here and there.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 55 Verse 17. As fear arose among them, those great chariot-warriors, being slain in battle by arrows loosed from Pārtha's (Arjuna's) bow, scattered and hid themselves here and there.

sanskrit: तेषां चतुःशतान्वीरान्यतमानान्महारथान् ।अर्जुनो निशितैर्बाणैरनयद्यमसादनम् ॥१८॥
transliteration: teṣāṁ catuḥśatānvīrānyatamānānmahārathān ,arjuno niśitairbāṇairanayadyamasādanam.
english: Arjuna, with his sharp arrows, sent those four hundred struggling heroic great charioteers to the abode of Yama (the god of death).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 55 Verse 18. Arjuna, with his sharp arrows, sent those four hundred struggling heroic great charioteers to the abode of Yama (the god of death).

sanskrit: ते वध्यमानाः समरे नानालिङ्गैः शितैः शरैः ।अर्जुनं समभित्यज्य दुद्रुवुर्वै दिशो भयात् ॥१९॥
transliteration: te vadhyamānāḥ samare nānāliṅgaiḥ śitaiḥ śaraiḥ ,arjunaṁ samabhityajya dudruvurvai diśo bhayāt.
english: Being struck down in battle by various kinds of sharp arrows, they completely abandoned Arjuna and indeed fled in fear in all directions.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 55 Verse 19. Being struck down in battle by various kinds of sharp arrows, they completely abandoned Arjuna and indeed fled in fear in all directions.

sanskrit: तेषां शब्दो महानासीद्द्रवतां वाहिनीमुखे ।महौघस्येव भद्रं ते गिरिमासाद्य दीर्यतः ॥२०॥
transliteration: teṣāṁ śabdo mahānāsīddravatāṁ vāhinīmukhe ,mahaughasyeva bhadraṁ te girimāsādya dīryataḥ.
english: A great clamor arose from them as they fled from the front of the army, like a mighty torrent that bursts apart after striking a mountain. May you be well!
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 55 Verse 20. A great clamor arose from them as they fled from the front of the army, like a mighty torrent that bursts apart after striking a mountain. May you be well!

sanskrit: तां तु सेनां भृशं विद्ध्वा द्रावयित्वार्जुनः शरैः ।प्रायादभिमुखः पार्थः सूतानीकानि मारिष ॥२१॥
transliteration: tāṁ tu senāṁ bhṛśaṁ viddhvā drāvayitvārjunaḥ śaraiḥ ,prāyādabhimukhaḥ pārthaḥ sūtānīkāni māriṣa.
english: But Arjuna, the son of Pṛthā, having severely pierced that army with arrows and put it to flight, advanced, O respected sir, towards the charioteer divisions.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 55 Verse 21. But Arjuna, the son of Pṛthā, having severely pierced that army with arrows and put it to flight, advanced, O respected sir, towards the charioteer divisions.

sanskrit: तस्य शब्दो महानासीत्परानभिमुखस्य वै ।गरुडस्येव पततः पन्नगार्थे यथा पुरा ॥२२॥
transliteration: tasya śabdo mahānāsītparānabhimukhasya vai ,garuḍasyeva patataḥ pannagārthe yathā purā.
english: His mighty roar was heard as he faced the enemies, just like Garuḍa swooping down for serpents, as he did in ancient times.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 55 Verse 22. His mighty roar was heard as he faced the enemies, just like Garuḍa swooping down for serpents, as he did in ancient times.

sanskrit: तं तु शब्दमभिश्रुत्य भीमसेनो महाबलः ।बभूव परमप्रीतः पार्थदर्शनलालसः ॥२३॥
transliteration: taṁ tu śabdamabhiśrutya bhīmaseno mahābalaḥ ,babhūva paramaprītaḥ pārthadarśanalālasaḥ.
english: Indeed, upon hearing that sound, the mighty Bhīmasena became extremely pleased and eager to see Pārtha.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 55 Verse 23. Indeed, upon hearing that sound, the mighty Bhīmasena became extremely pleased and eager to see Pārtha.

sanskrit: श्रुत्वैव पार्थमायान्तं भीमसेनः प्रतापवान् ।त्यक्त्वा प्राणान्महाराज सेनां तव ममर्द ह ॥२४॥
transliteration: śrutvaiva pārthamāyāntaṁ bhīmasenaḥ pratāpavān ,tyaktvā prāṇānmahārāja senāṁ tava mamarda ha.
english: O great king, as soon as the mighty Bhīmasena heard Pārtha (Arjuna) approaching, he crushed your army, as if heedless of his own life.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 55 Verse 24. O great king, as soon as the mighty Bhīmasena heard Pārtha (Arjuna) approaching, he crushed your army, as if heedless of his own life.

sanskrit: स वायुवेगप्रतिमो वायुवेगसमो जवे ।वायुवद्व्यचरद्भीमो वायुपुत्रः प्रतापवान् ॥२५॥
transliteration: sa vāyuvegapratimo vāyuvegasamo jave ,vāyuvadvyacaradbhīmo vāyuputraḥ pratāpavān.
english: That mighty Bhīma, the son of Vāyu, was swift as the wind, equal to its speed, and moved about just like the wind.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 55 Verse 25. That mighty Bhīma, the son of Vāyu, was swift as the wind, equal to its speed, and moved about just like the wind.

sanskrit: तेनार्द्यमाना राजेन्द्र सेना तव विशां पते ।व्यभ्राम्यत महाराज भिन्ना नौरिव सागरे ॥२६॥
transliteration: tenārdyamānā rājendra senā tava viśāṁ pate ,vyabhrāmyata mahārāja bhinnā nauriva sāgare.
english: O King of kings, O lord of the people, your army, being tormented by him, was scattered and bewildered, O great king, just like a broken ship upon the ocean.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 55 Verse 26. O King of kings, O lord of the people, your army, being tormented by him, was scattered and bewildered, O great king, just like a broken ship upon the ocean.

sanskrit: तां तु सेनां तदा भीमो दर्शयन्पाणिलाघवम् ।शरैरवचकर्तोग्रैः प्रेषयिष्यन्यमक्षयम् ॥२७॥
transliteration: tāṁ tu senāṁ tadā bhīmo darśayanpāṇilāghavam ,śarairavacakartograiḥ preṣayiṣyanyamakṣayam.
english: Then, Bhima, displaying his dexterity, cut apart that army with fierce arrows, intending to send them to the abode of Yama (yamakṣayam).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 55 Verse 27. Then, Bhima, displaying his dexterity, cut apart that army with fierce arrows, intending to send them to the abode of Yama (yamakṣayam).

sanskrit: तत्र भारत भीमस्य बलं दृष्ट्वातिमानुषम् ।व्यत्रस्यन्त रणे योधाः कालस्येव युगक्षये ॥२८॥
transliteration: tatra bhārata bhīmasya balaṁ dṛṣṭvātimānuṣam ,vyatrasyanta raṇe yodhāḥ kālasyeva yugakṣaye.
english: Then, O descendant of Bharata, seeing Bhima's superhuman strength, the warriors on the battlefield trembled and scattered, as if at the end of an age (yugakṣaya) due to the power of Time (kāla).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 55 Verse 28. Then, O descendant of Bharata, seeing Bhima's superhuman strength, the warriors on the battlefield trembled and scattered, as if at the end of an age (yugakṣaya) due to the power of Time (kāla).

sanskrit: तथार्दितान्भीमबलान्भीमसेनेन भारत ।दृष्ट्वा दुर्योधनो राजा इदं वचनमब्रवीत् ॥२९॥
transliteration: tathārditānbhīmabalānbhīmasenena bhārata ,dṛṣṭvā duryodhano rājā idaṁ vacanamabravīt.
english: Then, O descendant of Bharata, King Duryodhana, upon seeing those powerful warriors thus afflicted by Bhimasena, spoke these words.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 55 Verse 29. Then, O descendant of Bharata, King Duryodhana, upon seeing those powerful warriors thus afflicted by Bhimasena, spoke these words.

sanskrit: सैनिकान्स महेष्वासो योधांश्च भरतर्षभ ।समादिशद्रणे सर्वान्हत भीममिति स्म ह ।तस्मिन्हते हतं मन्ये सर्वसैन्यमशेषतः ॥३०॥
transliteration: sainikānsa maheṣvāso yodhāṁśca bharatarṣabha ,samādiśadraṇe sarvānhata bhīmamiti sma ha ,tasminhate hataṁ manye sarvasainyamaśeṣataḥ.
english: O best among the Bhāratas, the great archer (Duryodhana) commanded all his soldiers and warriors in battle, 'Kill Bhīma!' Indeed, if he (Bhīma) is slain, I consider our entire army to be utterly destroyed.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 55 Verse 30. O best among the Bhāratas, the great archer (Duryodhana) commanded all his soldiers and warriors in battle, 'Kill Bhīma!' Indeed, if he (Bhīma) is slain, I consider our entire army to be utterly destroyed.

sanskrit: प्रतिगृह्य च तामाज्ञां तव पुत्रस्य पार्थिवाः ।भीमं प्रच्छादयामासुः शरवर्षैः समन्ततः ॥३१॥
transliteration: pratigṛhya ca tāmājñāṁ tava putrasya pārthivāḥ ,bhīmaṁ pracchādayāmāsuḥ śaravarṣaiḥ samantataḥ.
english: And having accepted that command of your son, the kings (pārthivāḥ) completely enveloped Bhīma from all sides with showers of arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 55 Verse 31. And having accepted that command of your son, the kings (pārthivāḥ) completely enveloped Bhīma from all sides with showers of arrows.

sanskrit: गजाश्च बहुला राजन्नराश्च जयगृद्धिनः ।रथा हयाश्च राजेन्द्र परिवव्रुर्वृकोदरम् ॥३२॥
transliteration: gajāśca bahulā rājannarāśca jayagṛddhinaḥ ,rathā hayāśca rājendra parivavrurvṛkodaram.
english: O King, O king of kings, numerous elephants, and men eager for victory, along with chariots and horses, surrounded Vṛkodara (Bhīma).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 55 Verse 32. O King, O king of kings, numerous elephants, and men eager for victory, along with chariots and horses, surrounded Vṛkodara (Bhīma).

sanskrit: स तैः परिवृतः शूरैः शूरो राजन्समन्ततः ।शुशुभे भरतश्रेष्ठ नक्षत्रैरिव चन्द्रमाः ॥३३॥
transliteration: sa taiḥ parivṛtaḥ śūraiḥ śūro rājansamantataḥ ,śuśubhe bharataśreṣṭha nakṣatrairiva candramāḥ.
english: O King, O best among the Bhāratas, that hero (Bhīma), surrounded on all sides by those brave warriors, shone splendidly, just like the moon among the constellations.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 55 Verse 33. O King, O best among the Bhāratas, that hero (Bhīma), surrounded on all sides by those brave warriors, shone splendidly, just like the moon among the constellations.

sanskrit: स रराज तथा संख्ये दर्शनीयो नरोत्तमः ।निर्विशेषं महाराज यथा हि विजयस्तथा ॥३४॥
transliteration: sa rarāja tathā saṁkhye darśanīyo narottamaḥ ,nirviśeṣaṁ mahārāja yathā hi vijayastathā.
english: O great king, that best among men (narottama) shone splendidly in battle, a sight to behold, just as Vijaya (Arjuna) himself, without any difference.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 55 Verse 34. O great king, that best among men (narottama) shone splendidly in battle, a sight to behold, just as Vijaya (Arjuna) himself, without any difference.

sanskrit: तत्र ते पार्थिवाः सर्वे शरवृष्टीः समासृजन् ।क्रोधरक्तेक्षणाः क्रूरा हन्तुकामा वृकोदरम् ॥३५॥
transliteration: tatra te pārthivāḥ sarve śaravṛṣṭīḥ samāsṛjan ,krodharaktekṣaṇāḥ krūrā hantukāmā vṛkodaram.
english: There, all those kings, fierce and with eyes reddened by anger, desirous to kill Vrikodara (Bhīma), showered him with volleys of arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 55 Verse 35. There, all those kings, fierce and with eyes reddened by anger, desirous to kill Vrikodara (Bhīma), showered him with volleys of arrows.

sanskrit: स विदार्य महासेनां शरैः संनतपर्वभिः ।निश्चक्राम रणाद्भीमो मत्स्यो जालादिवाम्भसि ॥३६॥
transliteration: sa vidārya mahāsenāṁ śaraiḥ saṁnataparvabhiḥ ,niścakrāma raṇādbhīmo matsyo jālādivāmbhasi.
english: Having torn apart the great army with his well-aimed arrows, Bhīma emerged from the battle, just as a fish escapes from a net in the water.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 55 Verse 36. Having torn apart the great army with his well-aimed arrows, Bhīma emerged from the battle, just as a fish escapes from a net in the water.

sanskrit: हत्वा दश सहस्राणि गजानामनिवर्तिनाम् ।नृणां शतसहस्रे द्वे द्वे शते चैव भारत ॥३७॥
transliteration: hatvā daśa sahasrāṇi gajānāmanivartinām ,nṛṇāṁ śatasahasre dve dve śate caiva bhārata.
english: O Bharata, having indeed killed ten thousand unswerving elephants, and two hundred thousand and two hundred men, (Bhīma emerged).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 55 Verse 37. O Bharata, having indeed killed ten thousand unswerving elephants, and two hundred thousand and two hundred men, (Bhīma emerged).

sanskrit: पञ्च चाश्वसहस्राणि रथानां शतमेव च ।हत्वा प्रास्यन्दयद्भीमो नदीं शोणितकर्दमाम् ॥३८॥
transliteration: pañca cāśvasahasrāṇi rathānāṁ śatameva ca ,hatvā prāsyandayadbhīmo nadīṁ śoṇitakardamām.
english: Having killed five thousand horses and one hundred chariots as well, Bhima caused a river, thick with blood-mud, to flow forth.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 55 Verse 38. Having killed five thousand horses and one hundred chariots as well, Bhima caused a river, thick with blood-mud, to flow forth.

sanskrit: शोणितोदां रथावर्तां हस्तिग्राहसमाकुलाम् ।नरमीनामश्वनक्रां केशशैवलशाद्वलाम् ॥३९॥
transliteration: śoṇitodāṁ rathāvartāṁ hastigrāhasamākulām ,naramīnāmaśvanakrāṁ keśaśaivalaśādvalām.
english: It had blood for its water, chariots for its whirlpools, was agitated by elephant-like crocodiles, had men as its fish, horses as its crocodiles, and hair as its moss and fresh grass.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 55 Verse 39. It had blood for its water, chariots for its whirlpools, was agitated by elephant-like crocodiles, had men as its fish, horses as its crocodiles, and hair as its moss and fresh grass.

sanskrit: संछिन्नभुजनागेन्द्रां बहुरत्नापहारिणीम् ।ऊरुग्राहां मज्जपङ्कां शीर्षोपलसमाकुलाम् ॥४०॥
transliteration: saṁchinnabhujanāgendrāṁ bahuratnāpahāriṇīm ,ūrugrāhāṁ majjapaṅkāṁ śīrṣopalasamākulām.
english: It had mighty elephant-like arms completely severed, was carrying away many valuable jewels, had thighs as its alligators, marrow as its mud, and was agitated by heads like stones.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 55 Verse 40. It had mighty elephant-like arms completely severed, was carrying away many valuable jewels, had thighs as its alligators, marrow as its mud, and was agitated by heads like stones.

sanskrit: धनुष्काशां शरावापां गदापरिघकेतनाम् ।योधव्रातवतीं संख्ये वहन्तीं यमसादनम् ॥४१॥
transliteration: dhanuṣkāśāṁ śarāvāpāṁ gadāparighaketanām ,yodhavrātavatīṁ saṁkhye vahantīṁ yamasādanam.
english: It had bows for its reeds, quivers for its containers, maces and iron bars for its banners, was possessing multitudes of warriors, and, in battle, carried them to the abode of Yama.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 55 Verse 41. It had bows for its reeds, quivers for its containers, maces and iron bars for its banners, was possessing multitudes of warriors, and, in battle, carried them to the abode of Yama.

sanskrit: क्षणेन पुरुषव्याघ्रः प्रावर्तयत निम्नगाम् ।यथा वैतरणीमुग्रां दुस्तरामकृतात्मभिः ॥४२॥
transliteration: kṣaṇena puruṣavyāghraḥ prāvartayata nimnagām ,yathā vaitaraṇīmugrāṁ dustarāmakṛtātmabhiḥ.
english: In an instant, that tiger among men (Bhīmasena) caused a river to flow, just like the formidable Vaitaraṇī, which is difficult for those lacking self-discipline (akṛtātman) to cross.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 55 Verse 42. In an instant, that tiger among men (Bhīmasena) caused a river to flow, just like the formidable Vaitaraṇī, which is difficult for those lacking self-discipline (akṛtātman) to cross.

sanskrit: यतो यतः पाण्डवेयः प्रवृत्तो रथसत्तमः ।ततस्ततोऽपातयत योधाञ्शतसहस्रशः ॥४३॥
transliteration: yato yataḥ pāṇḍaveyaḥ pravṛtto rathasattamaḥ ,tatastato'pātayata yodhāñśatasahasraśaḥ.
english: Wherever that best of charioteers, the son of Pāṇḍu (Bhīmasena), advanced, from there he struck down warriors by the hundreds of thousands.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 55 Verse 43. Wherever that best of charioteers, the son of Pāṇḍu (Bhīmasena), advanced, from there he struck down warriors by the hundreds of thousands.

sanskrit: एवं दृष्ट्वा कृतं कर्म भीमसेनेन संयुगे ।दुर्योधनो महाराज शकुनिं वाक्यमब्रवीत् ॥४४॥
transliteration: evaṁ dṛṣṭvā kṛtaṁ karma bhīmasenena saṁyuge ,duryodhano mahārāja śakuniṁ vākyamabravīt.
english: O great king, having thus seen the deeds (karma) performed by Bhīmasena in battle, Duryodhana spoke these words to Śakuni.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 55 Verse 44. O great king, having thus seen the deeds (karma) performed by Bhīmasena in battle, Duryodhana spoke these words to Śakuni.

sanskrit: जय मातुल संग्रामे भीमसेनं महाबलम् ।अस्मिञ्जिते जितं मन्ये पाण्डवेयं महाबलम् ॥४५॥
transliteration: jaya mātula saṁgrāme bhīmasenaṁ mahābalam ,asmiñjite jitaṁ manye pāṇḍaveyaṁ mahābalam.
english: O maternal uncle, conquer the mighty Bhīmasena in battle! I believe that once he is vanquished, the mighty Pāṇḍava (side) will be considered completely won.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 55 Verse 45. O maternal uncle, conquer the mighty Bhīmasena in battle! I believe that once he is vanquished, the mighty Pāṇḍava (side) will be considered completely won.

sanskrit: ततः प्रायान्महाराज सौबलेयः प्रतापवान् ।रणाय महते युक्तो भ्रातृभिः परिवारितः ॥४६॥
transliteration: tataḥ prāyānmahārāja saubaleyaḥ pratāpavān ,raṇāya mahate yukto bhrātṛbhiḥ parivāritaḥ.
english: Then, O great king (Maharaja), the powerful son of Subala (Śakuni), surrounded by his brothers, advanced, prepared for a mighty battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 55 Verse 46. Then, O great king (Maharaja), the powerful son of Subala (Śakuni), surrounded by his brothers, advanced, prepared for a mighty battle.

sanskrit: स समासाद्य संग्रामे भीमं भीमपराक्रमम् ।वारयामास तं वीरो वेलेव मकरालयम् ।स न्यवर्तत तं भीमो वार्यमाणः शितैः शरैः ॥४७॥
transliteration: sa samāsādya saṁgrāme bhīmaṁ bhīmaparākramam ,vārayāmāsa taṁ vīro veleva makarālayam ,sa nyavartata taṁ bhīmo vāryamāṇaḥ śitaiḥ śaraiḥ.
english: Having encountered Bhīma, who possessed formidable prowess (Bhīmaparākrama), in battle, that hero (Śakuni) checked him, just as a shore restrains the ocean (makarālaya). Being held back by sharp arrows, Bhīma then turned away from him.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 55 Verse 47. Having encountered Bhīma, who possessed formidable prowess (Bhīmaparākrama), in battle, that hero (Śakuni) checked him, just as a shore restrains the ocean (makarālaya). Being held back by sharp arrows, Bhīma then turned away from him.

sanskrit: शकुनिस्तस्य राजेन्द्र वामे पार्श्वे स्तनान्तरे ।प्रेषयामास नाराचान्रुक्मपुङ्खाञ्शिलाशितान् ॥४८॥
transliteration: śakunistasya rājendra vāme pārśve stanāntare ,preṣayāmāsa nārācānrukmapuṅkhāñśilāśitān.
english: O king among kings (Rajendra), Śakuni dispatched gold-feathered, stone-sharpened arrows at his left flank, directly between his breasts.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 55 Verse 48. O king among kings (Rajendra), Śakuni dispatched gold-feathered, stone-sharpened arrows at his left flank, directly between his breasts.

sanskrit: वर्म भित्त्वा तु सौवर्णं बाणास्तस्य महात्मनः ।न्यमज्जन्त महाराज कङ्कबर्हिणवाससः ॥४९॥
transliteration: varma bhittvā tu sauvarṇaṁ bāṇāstasya mahātmanaḥ ,nyamajjanta mahārāja kaṅkabarhiṇavāsasaḥ.
english: But, O great king (Maharaja), having pierced the golden armor of that great-souled one, his arrows, adorned with feathers of kites and peacocks, sank deeply into his body.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 55 Verse 49. But, O great king (Maharaja), having pierced the golden armor of that great-souled one, his arrows, adorned with feathers of kites and peacocks, sank deeply into his body.

sanskrit: सोऽतिविद्धो रणे भीमः शरं हेमविभूषितम् ।प्रेषयामास सहसा सौबलं प्रति भारत ॥५०॥
transliteration: so'tividdho raṇe bhīmaḥ śaraṁ hemavibhūṣitam ,preṣayāmāsa sahasā saubalaṁ prati bhārata.
english: O Bharata, Bhima, though deeply wounded in battle, swiftly sent a gold-adorned arrow towards Saubala (Shakuni).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 55 Verse 50. O Bharata, Bhima, though deeply wounded in battle, swiftly sent a gold-adorned arrow towards Saubala (Shakuni).

sanskrit: तमायान्तं शरं घोरं शकुनिः शत्रुतापनः ।चिच्छेद शतधा राजन्कृतहस्तो महाबलः ॥५१॥
transliteration: tamāyāntaṁ śaraṁ ghoraṁ śakuniḥ śatrutāpanaḥ ,ciccheda śatadhā rājankṛtahasto mahābalaḥ.
english: O King, that mighty and skilled Shakuni, the tormentor of his enemies, cut that fierce incoming arrow into a hundred pieces.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 55 Verse 51. O King, that mighty and skilled Shakuni, the tormentor of his enemies, cut that fierce incoming arrow into a hundred pieces.

sanskrit: तस्मिन्निपतिते भूमौ भीमः क्रुद्धो विशां पते ।धनुश्चिच्छेद भल्लेन सौबलस्य हसन्निव ॥५२॥
transliteration: tasminnipatite bhūmau bhīmaḥ kruddho viśāṁ pate ,dhanuściccheda bhallena saubalasya hasanniva.
english: O Lord of the people, when that (arrow) had fallen to the ground, enraged Bhima, as if laughing, cut Saubala's (Shakuni's) bow with a special bhalla arrow.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 55 Verse 52. O Lord of the people, when that (arrow) had fallen to the ground, enraged Bhima, as if laughing, cut Saubala's (Shakuni's) bow with a special bhalla arrow.

sanskrit: तदपास्य धनुश्छिन्नं सौबलेयः प्रतापवान् ।अन्यदादत्त वेगेन धनुर्भल्लांश्च षोडश ॥५३॥
transliteration: tadapāsya dhanuśchinnaṁ saubaleyaḥ pratāpavān ,anyadādatta vegena dhanurbhallāṁśca ṣoḍaśa.
english: Having cast aside that broken bow, the mighty son of Subala (Shakuni) swiftly took another bow and sixteen bhalla arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 55 Verse 53. Having cast aside that broken bow, the mighty son of Subala (Shakuni) swiftly took another bow and sixteen bhalla arrows.

sanskrit: तैस्तस्य तु महाराज भल्लैः संनतपर्वभिः ।चतुर्भिः सारथिं ह्यार्च्छद्भीमं पञ्चभिरेव च ॥५४॥
transliteration: taistasya tu mahārāja bhallaiḥ saṁnataparvabhiḥ ,caturbhiḥ sārathiṁ hyārcchadbhīmaṁ pañcabhireva ca.
english: O great king, with those four broad-headed arrows, which had bent joints, he (Shakuni) indeed struck Bhima's charioteer, and also Bhima himself with five (such arrows).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 55 Verse 54. O great king, with those four broad-headed arrows, which had bent joints, he (Shakuni) indeed struck Bhima's charioteer, and also Bhima himself with five (such arrows).

sanskrit: ध्वजमेकेन चिच्छेद छत्रं द्वाभ्यां विशां पते ।चतुर्भिश्चतुरो वाहान्विव्याध सुबलात्मजः ॥५५॥
transliteration: dhvajamekena ciccheda chatraṁ dvābhyāṁ viśāṁ pate ,caturbhiścaturo vāhānvivyādha subalātmajaḥ.
english: O lord of the people, with one (arrow), he cut down the banner, and with two (arrows), the umbrella. The son of Subala (Shakuni) then pierced the four horses with four (other) arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 55 Verse 55. O lord of the people, with one (arrow), he cut down the banner, and with two (arrows), the umbrella. The son of Subala (Shakuni) then pierced the four horses with four (other) arrows.

sanskrit: ततः क्रुद्धो महाराज भीमसेनः प्रतापवान् ।शक्तिं चिक्षेप समरे रुक्मदण्डामयस्मयीम् ॥५६॥
transliteration: tataḥ kruddho mahārāja bhīmasenaḥ pratāpavān ,śaktiṁ cikṣepa samare rukmadaṇḍāmayasmayīm.
english: O great king, then the powerful Bhimasena, enraged, hurled an iron dart (śakti) with a golden shaft into the battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 55 Verse 56. O great king, then the powerful Bhimasena, enraged, hurled an iron dart (śakti) with a golden shaft into the battle.

sanskrit: सा भीमभुजनिर्मुक्ता नागजिह्वेव चञ्चला ।निपपात रथे तूर्णं सौबलस्य महात्मनः ॥५७॥
transliteration: sā bhīmabhujanirmuktā nāgajihveva cañcalā ,nipapāta rathe tūrṇaṁ saubalasya mahātmanaḥ.
english: That (śakti), released from Bhima's arm, flickering like a serpent's tongue, swiftly fell upon the chariot of the great-souled son of Subala (Shakuni).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 55 Verse 57. That (śakti), released from Bhima's arm, flickering like a serpent's tongue, swiftly fell upon the chariot of the great-souled son of Subala (Shakuni).

sanskrit: ततस्तामेव संगृह्य शक्तिं कनकभूषणाम् ।भीमसेनाय चिक्षेप क्रुद्धरूपो विशां पते ॥५८॥
transliteration: tatastāmeva saṁgṛhya śaktiṁ kanakabhūṣaṇām ,bhīmasenāya cikṣepa kruddharūpo viśāṁ pate.
english: O lord of people, then that one, whose appearance was wrathful, seized that very gold-adorned spear and threw it at Bhimasena.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 55 Verse 58. O lord of people, then that one, whose appearance was wrathful, seized that very gold-adorned spear and threw it at Bhimasena.

sanskrit: सा निर्भिद्य भुजं सव्यं पाण्डवस्य महात्मनः ।पपात च ततो भूमौ यथा विद्युन्नभश्च्युता ॥५९॥
transliteration: sā nirbhidya bhujaṁ savyaṁ pāṇḍavasya mahātmanaḥ ,papāta ca tato bhūmau yathā vidyunnabhaścyutā.
english: Having pierced the left arm of the great-souled Pandava, it then fell to the ground, just like lightning falling from the sky.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 55 Verse 59. Having pierced the left arm of the great-souled Pandava, it then fell to the ground, just like lightning falling from the sky.

sanskrit: अथोत्क्रुष्टं महाराज धार्तराष्ट्रैः समन्ततः ।न तु तं ममृषे भीमः सिंहनादं तरस्विनाम् ॥६०॥
transliteration: athotkruṣṭaṁ mahārāja dhārtarāṣṭraiḥ samantataḥ ,na tu taṁ mamṛṣe bhīmaḥ siṁhanādaṁ tarasvinām.
english: Then, O great king, the Dhārtarāṣṭras raised a great shout from all sides. But Bhima did not tolerate that lion's roar of the mighty ones.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 55 Verse 60. Then, O great king, the Dhārtarāṣṭras raised a great shout from all sides. But Bhima did not tolerate that lion's roar of the mighty ones.

sanskrit: स संगृह्य धनुः सज्यं त्वरमाणो महारथः ।मुहूर्तादिव राजेन्द्र छादयामास सायकैः ।सौबलस्य बलं संख्ये त्यक्त्वात्मानं महाबलः ॥६१॥
transliteration: sa saṁgṛhya dhanuḥ sajyaṁ tvaramāṇo mahārathaḥ ,muhūrtādiva rājendra chādayāmāsa sāyakaiḥ ,saubalasya balaṁ saṁkhye tyaktvātmānaṁ mahābalaḥ.
english: O lord of kings, that great and mighty charioteer, rushing swiftly, seized his strung bow and, as if in an instant, covered Saubala's army in battle with arrows, completely disregarding his own life (ātman).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 55 Verse 61. O lord of kings, that great and mighty charioteer, rushing swiftly, seized his strung bow and, as if in an instant, covered Saubala's army in battle with arrows, completely disregarding his own life (ātman).

sanskrit: तस्याश्वांश्चतुरो हत्वा सूतं चैव विशां पते ।ध्वजं चिच्छेद भल्लेन त्वरमाणः पराक्रमी ॥६२॥
transliteration: tasyāśvāṁścaturo hatvā sūtaṁ caiva viśāṁ pate ,dhvajaṁ ciccheda bhallena tvaramāṇaḥ parākramī.
english: O lord of the people, that mighty and swiftly acting warrior, having killed his four horses and also the charioteer, cut down his banner with a *bhalla* arrow.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 55 Verse 62. O lord of the people, that mighty and swiftly acting warrior, having killed his four horses and also the charioteer, cut down his banner with a *bhalla* arrow.

sanskrit: हताश्वं रथमुत्सृज्य त्वरमाणो नरोत्तमः ।तस्थौ विस्फारयंश्चापं क्रोधरक्तेक्षणः श्वसन् ।शरैश्च बहुधा राजन्भीममार्च्छत्समन्ततः ॥६३॥
transliteration: hatāśvaṁ rathamutsṛjya tvaramāṇo narottamaḥ ,tasthau visphārayaṁścāpaṁ krodharaktekṣaṇaḥ śvasan ,śaraiśca bahudhā rājanbhīmamārcchatsamantataḥ.
english: O King, that swiftly acting best among men, having abandoned his chariot whose horses were killed, stood stretching his bow, his eyes red with anger, breathing heavily. He then repeatedly pierced Bhīma with arrows from all sides.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 55 Verse 63. O King, that swiftly acting best among men, having abandoned his chariot whose horses were killed, stood stretching his bow, his eyes red with anger, breathing heavily. He then repeatedly pierced Bhīma with arrows from all sides.

sanskrit: प्रतिहत्य तु वेगेन भीमसेनः प्रतापवान् ।धनुश्चिच्छेद संक्रुद्धो विव्याध च शितैः शरैः ॥६४॥
transliteration: pratihatya tu vegena bhīmasenaḥ pratāpavān ,dhanuściccheda saṁkruddho vivyādha ca śitaiḥ śaraiḥ.
english: But the powerful Bhīmasena, having swiftly countered, became enraged and cut his (opponent's) bow, then pierced him with sharp arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 55 Verse 64. But the powerful Bhīmasena, having swiftly countered, became enraged and cut his (opponent's) bow, then pierced him with sharp arrows.

sanskrit: सोऽतिविद्धो बलवता शत्रुणा शत्रुकर्शनः ।निपपात ततो भूमौ किंचित्प्राणो नराधिप ॥६५॥
transliteration: so'tividdho balavatā śatruṇā śatrukarśanaḥ ,nipapāta tato bhūmau kiṁcitprāṇo narādhipa.
english: O lord of men, that tormentor of enemies, severely wounded by the powerful adversary, then fell to the ground, nearly lifeless.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 55 Verse 65. O lord of men, that tormentor of enemies, severely wounded by the powerful adversary, then fell to the ground, nearly lifeless.

sanskrit: ततस्तं विह्वलं ज्ञात्वा पुत्रस्तव विशां पते ।अपोवाह रथेनाजौ भीमसेनस्य पश्यतः ॥६६॥
transliteration: tatastaṁ vihvalaṁ jñātvā putrastava viśāṁ pate ,apovāha rathenājau bhīmasenasya paśyataḥ.
english: Then, knowing him to be bewildered, your son, O lord of men, carried him away in a chariot from the battle, while Bhīmasena watched.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 55 Verse 66. Then, knowing him to be bewildered, your son, O lord of men, carried him away in a chariot from the battle, while Bhīmasena watched.

sanskrit: रथस्थे तु नरव्याघ्रे धार्तराष्ट्राः पराङ्मुखाः ।प्रदुद्रुवुर्दिशो भीता भीमाज्जाते महाभये ॥६७॥
transliteration: rathasthe tu naravyāghre dhārtarāṣṭrāḥ parāṅmukhāḥ ,pradudruvurdiśo bhītā bhīmājjāte mahābhaye.
english: But when that man-tiger (Bhīma) was on his chariot, the Dhārtarāṣṭras, terrified, turned their backs and fled in all directions, as great fear arose because of Bhīma.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 55 Verse 67. But when that man-tiger (Bhīma) was on his chariot, the Dhārtarāṣṭras, terrified, turned their backs and fled in all directions, as great fear arose because of Bhīma.

sanskrit: सौबले निर्जिते राजन्भीमसेनेन धन्विना ।भयेन महता भग्नः पुत्रो दुर्योधनस्तव ।अपायाज्जवनैरश्वैः सापेक्षो मातुलं प्रति ॥६८॥
transliteration: saubale nirjite rājanbhīmasenena dhanvinā ,bhayena mahatā bhagnaḥ putro duryodhanastava ,apāyājjavanairaśvaiḥ sāpekṣo mātulaṁ prati.
english: O King, when Subala's son (Śakuni) was defeated by the archer Bhīmasena, your son Duryodhana, overwhelmed by great fear, fled on swift horses, constantly looking back towards his maternal uncle.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 55 Verse 68. O King, when Subala's son (Śakuni) was defeated by the archer Bhīmasena, your son Duryodhana, overwhelmed by great fear, fled on swift horses, constantly looking back towards his maternal uncle.

sanskrit: पराङ्मुखं तु राजानं दृष्ट्वा सैन्यानि भारत ।विप्रजग्मुः समुत्सृज्य द्वैरथानि समन्ततः ॥६९॥
transliteration: parāṅmukhaṁ tu rājānaṁ dṛṣṭvā sainyāni bhārata ,viprajagmuḥ samutsṛjya dvairathāni samantataḥ.
english: O Bhārata, seeing the king retreating, the armies dispersed, abandoning the chariot duels everywhere.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 55 Verse 69. O Bhārata, seeing the king retreating, the armies dispersed, abandoning the chariot duels everywhere.

sanskrit: तान्दृष्ट्वातिरथान्सर्वान्धार्तराष्ट्रान्पराङ्मुखान् ।जवेनाभ्यपतद्भीमः किरञ्शरशतान्बहून् ॥७०॥
transliteration: tāndṛṣṭvātirathānsarvāndhārtarāṣṭrānparāṅmukhān ,javenābhyapatadbhīmaḥ kirañśaraśatānbahūn.
english: Seeing all those great charioteers among the Dhārtarāṣṭras (Kauravas) turning their backs in retreat, Bhīma swiftly charged at them, showering countless hundreds of arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 55 Verse 70. Seeing all those great charioteers among the Dhārtarāṣṭras (Kauravas) turning their backs in retreat, Bhīma swiftly charged at them, showering countless hundreds of arrows.

sanskrit: ते वध्यमाना भीमेन धार्तराष्ट्राः पराङ्मुखाः ।कर्णमासाद्य समरे स्थिता राजन्समन्ततः ।स हि तेषां महावीर्यो द्वीपोऽभूत्सुमहाबलः ॥७१॥
transliteration: te vadhyamānā bhīmena dhārtarāṣṭrāḥ parāṅmukhāḥ ,karṇamāsādya samare sthitā rājansamantataḥ ,sa hi teṣāṁ mahāvīryo dvīpo'bhūtsumahābalaḥ.
english: O King, those Dhārtarāṣṭras (Kauravas), who were retreating and being assailed by Bhīma, having reached Karṇa in battle, took their stand around him from all sides. For indeed, that exceedingly mighty and greatly valorous Karṇa became a refuge (dvīpa) for them.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 55 Verse 71. O King, those Dhārtarāṣṭras (Kauravas), who were retreating and being assailed by Bhīma, having reached Karṇa in battle, took their stand around him from all sides. For indeed, that exceedingly mighty and greatly valorous Karṇa became a refuge (dvīpa) for them.

sanskrit: भिन्ननौका यथा राजन्द्वीपमासाद्य निर्वृताः ।भवन्ति पुरुषव्याघ्र नाविकाः कालपर्यये ॥७२॥
transliteration: bhinnanaukā yathā rājandvīpamāsādya nirvṛtāḥ ,bhavanti puruṣavyāghra nāvikāḥ kālaparyaye.
english: Just as, O King, O tiger among men, shipwrecked sailors, having reached an island, become relieved and safe in times of distress,
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 55 Verse 72. Just as, O King, O tiger among men, shipwrecked sailors, having reached an island, become relieved and safe in times of distress,

sanskrit: तथा कर्णं समासाद्य तावका भरतर्षभ ।समाश्वस्ताः स्थिता राजन्संप्रहृष्टाः परस्परम् ।समाजग्मुश्च युद्धाय मृत्युं कृत्वा निवर्तनम् ॥७३॥
transliteration: tathā karṇaṁ samāsādya tāvakā bharatarṣabha ,samāśvastāḥ sthitā rājansaṁprahṛṣṭāḥ parasparam ,samājagmuśca yuddhāya mṛtyuṁ kṛtvā nivartanam.
english: Similarly, O best of Bharatas, O King, your warriors (the Kauravas), having approached Karṇa, stood greatly reassured and rejoiced amongst themselves. And they rallied for battle, having resolved to fight to the death and never retreat.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 55 Verse 73. Similarly, O best of Bharatas, O King, your warriors (the Kauravas), having approached Karṇa, stood greatly reassured and rejoiced amongst themselves. And they rallied for battle, having resolved to fight to the death and never retreat.

sanskrit: धृतराष्ट्र उवाच ।ततो भग्नेषु सैन्येषु भीमसेनेन संयुगे ।दुर्योधनोऽब्रवीत्किं नु सौबलो वापि संजय ॥१॥
transliteration: dhṛtarāṣṭra uvāca ,tato bhagneṣu sainyeṣu bhīmasenena saṁyuge ,duryodhano'bravītkiṁ nu saubalo vāpi saṁjaya.
english: Dhritarashtra said: "Then, when the armies were shattered by Bhimasena in battle, what indeed did Duryodhana say, Sanjaya? Or (did he speak) to Saubala (Śakuni)?"
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 56 Verse 1. Dhritarashtra said: "Then, when the armies were shattered by Bhimasena in battle, what indeed did Duryodhana say, Sanjaya? Or (did he speak) to Saubala (Śakuni)?"

sanskrit: कर्णो वा जयतां श्रेष्ठो योधा वा मामका युधि ।कृपो वा कृतवर्मा च द्रौणिर्दुःशासनोऽपि वा ॥२॥
transliteration: karṇo vā jayatāṁ śreṣṭho yodhā vā māmakā yudhi ,kṛpo vā kṛtavarmā ca drauṇirduḥśāsano'pi vā.
english: (Did he speak to) Karṇa, the best among conquerors? Or my warriors in battle? Or Kṛpa, or Kṛtavarmā, and Drauṇi (Aśvatthāman), or even Duḥśāsana?
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 56 Verse 2. (Did he speak to) Karṇa, the best among conquerors? Or my warriors in battle? Or Kṛpa, or Kṛtavarmā, and Drauṇi (Aśvatthāman), or even Duḥśāsana?

sanskrit: अत्यद्भुतमिदं मन्ये पाण्डवेयस्य विक्रमम् ।यथाप्रतिज्ञं योधानां राधेयः कृतवानपि ॥३॥
transliteration: atyadbhutamidaṁ manye pāṇḍaveyasya vikramam ,yathāpratijñaṁ yodhānāṁ rādheyaḥ kṛtavānapi.
english: I consider this valor of the Pāṇḍava to be exceedingly marvelous, even though Rādheya (Karṇa) also acted in accordance with the warriors' (Kaurava) vows.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 56 Verse 3. I consider this valor of the Pāṇḍava to be exceedingly marvelous, even though Rādheya (Karṇa) also acted in accordance with the warriors' (Kaurava) vows.

sanskrit: कुरूणामपि सर्वेषां कर्णः शत्रुनिषूदनः ।शर्म वर्म प्रतिष्ठा च जीविताशा च संजय ॥४॥
transliteration: kurūṇāmapi sarveṣāṁ karṇaḥ śatruniṣūdanaḥ ,śarma varma pratiṣṭhā ca jīvitāśā ca saṁjaya.
english: For all the Kurus, O Sañjaya, Karṇa, the destroyer of enemies, was indeed their protection, their armor, their prestige, and their hope for life.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 56 Verse 4. For all the Kurus, O Sañjaya, Karṇa, the destroyer of enemies, was indeed their protection, their armor, their prestige, and their hope for life.

sanskrit: तत्प्रभग्नं बलं दृष्ट्वा कौन्तेयेनामितौजसा ।राधेयानामधिरथः कर्णः किमकरोद्युधि ॥५॥
transliteration: tatprabhagnaṁ balaṁ dṛṣṭvā kaunteyenāmitaujasā ,rādheyānāmadhirathaḥ karṇaḥ kimakarodyudhi.
english: Seeing that army completely shattered by the immensely powerful son of Kunti (Arjuna), what did Karna, the chief charioteer among the Radheyas, do in battle?
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 56 Verse 5. Seeing that army completely shattered by the immensely powerful son of Kunti (Arjuna), what did Karna, the chief charioteer among the Radheyas, do in battle?

sanskrit: पुत्रा वा मम दुर्धर्षा राजानो वा महारथाः ।एतन्मे सर्वमाचक्ष्व कुशलो ह्यसि संजय ॥६॥
transliteration: putrā vā mama durdharṣā rājāno vā mahārathāḥ ,etanme sarvamācakṣva kuśalo hyasi saṁjaya.
english: O Sañjaya, tell me all about my invincible sons, and the kings who are great charioteers, for you are skilled.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 56 Verse 6. O Sañjaya, tell me all about my invincible sons, and the kings who are great charioteers, for you are skilled.

sanskrit: संजय उवाच ।अपराह्णे महाराज सूतपुत्रः प्रतापवान् ।जघान सोमकान्सर्वान्भीमसेनस्य पश्यतः ।भीमोऽप्यतिबलः सैन्यं धार्तराष्ट्रं व्यपोथयत् ॥७॥
transliteration: saṁjaya uvāca ,aparāhṇe mahārāja sūtaputraḥ pratāpavān ,jaghāna somakānsarvānbhīmasenasya paśyataḥ ,bhīmo'pyatibalaḥ sainyaṁ dhārtarāṣṭraṁ vyapothayat.
english: Sañjaya said: "O great king, in the afternoon, the glorious son of the charioteer (Karna) killed all the Somakas while Bhīmasena was watching. And Bhīma, exceedingly strong, crushed the Dhārtarāṣṭra army."
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 56 Verse 7. Sañjaya said: "O great king, in the afternoon, the glorious son of the charioteer (Karna) killed all the Somakas while Bhīmasena was watching. And Bhīma, exceedingly strong, crushed the Dhārtarāṣṭra army."

sanskrit: द्राव्यमाणं बलं दृष्ट्वा भीमसेनेन धीमता ।यन्तारमब्रवीत्कर्णः पाञ्चालानेव मा वह ॥८॥
transliteration: drāvyamāṇaṁ balaṁ dṛṣṭvā bhīmasenena dhīmatā ,yantāramabravītkarṇaḥ pāñcālāneva mā vaha.
english: Seeing his army being driven away by the intelligent Bhīmasena, Karna said to his charioteer: "Drive me only to the Pāñcālas."
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 56 Verse 8. Seeing his army being driven away by the intelligent Bhīmasena, Karna said to his charioteer: "Drive me only to the Pāñcālas."

sanskrit: मद्रराजस्ततः शल्यः श्वेतानश्वान्महाजवान् ।प्राहिणोच्चेदिपाञ्चालान्करूषांश्च महाबलः ॥९॥
transliteration: madrarājastataḥ śalyaḥ śvetānaśvānmahājavān ,prāhiṇoccedipāñcālānkarūṣāṁśca mahābalaḥ.
english: Then, the mighty Shalya, king of Madras, dispatched his swift white horses towards the Chedis, Panchalas, and Karushas.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 56 Verse 9. Then, the mighty Shalya, king of Madras, dispatched his swift white horses towards the Chedis, Panchalas, and Karushas.

sanskrit: प्रविश्य च स तां सेनां शल्यः परबलार्दनः ।न्ययच्छत्तुरगान्हृष्टो यत्र यत्रैच्छदग्रणीः ॥१०॥
transliteration: praviśya ca sa tāṁ senāṁ śalyaḥ parabalārdanaḥ ,nyayacchatturagānhṛṣṭo yatra yatraicchadagraṇīḥ.
english: Having entered that army, the leader Shalya, the tormentor of enemy forces, delighted, steered his horses wherever he wished.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 56 Verse 10. Having entered that army, the leader Shalya, the tormentor of enemy forces, delighted, steered his horses wherever he wished.

sanskrit: तं रथं मेघसंकाशं वैयाघ्रपरिवारणम् ।संदृश्य पाण्डुपाञ्चालास्त्रस्ता आसन्विशां पते ॥११॥
transliteration: taṁ rathaṁ meghasaṁkāśaṁ vaiyāghraparivāraṇam ,saṁdṛśya pāṇḍupāñcālāstrastā āsanviśāṁ pate.
english: O Lord of people, when the Pandavas and Panchalas saw that chariot, which resembled a cloud and was covered with tiger skins, they became terrified.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 56 Verse 11. O Lord of people, when the Pandavas and Panchalas saw that chariot, which resembled a cloud and was covered with tiger skins, they became terrified.

sanskrit: ततो रथस्य निनदः प्रादुरासीन्महारणे ।पर्जन्यसमनिर्घोषः पर्वतस्येव दीर्यतः ॥१२॥
transliteration: tato rathasya ninadaḥ prādurāsīnmahāraṇe ,parjanyasamanirghoṣaḥ parvatasyeva dīryataḥ.
english: Then, in that great battle, the sound of the chariot arose, a roar like a thundercloud or a mountain splitting apart.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 56 Verse 12. Then, in that great battle, the sound of the chariot arose, a roar like a thundercloud or a mountain splitting apart.

sanskrit: ततः शरशतैस्तीक्ष्णैः कर्णोऽप्याकर्णनिःसृतैः ।जघान पाण्डवबलं शतशोऽथ सहस्रशः ॥१३॥
transliteration: tataḥ śaraśataistīkṣṇaiḥ karṇo'pyākarṇaniḥsṛtaiḥ ,jaghāna pāṇḍavabalaṁ śataśo'tha sahasraśaḥ.
english: Then, Karṇa also, with sharp arrows released up to the ear, struck the Pāṇḍava army by hundreds and by thousands.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 56 Verse 13. Then, Karṇa also, with sharp arrows released up to the ear, struck the Pāṇḍava army by hundreds and by thousands.

sanskrit: तं तथा समरे कर्म कुर्वाणमतिमानुषम् ।परिवव्रुर्महेष्वासाः पाण्डवानां महारथाः ॥१४॥
transliteration: taṁ tathā samare karma kurvāṇamatimānuṣam ,parivavrurmaheṣvāsāḥ pāṇḍavānāṁ mahārathāḥ.
english: The great archers and great charioteers (mahārathas) of the Pāṇḍavas surrounded him as he performed such a superhuman deed (karma) in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 56 Verse 14. The great archers and great charioteers (mahārathas) of the Pāṇḍavas surrounded him as he performed such a superhuman deed (karma) in battle.

sanskrit: तं शिखण्डी च भीमश्च धृष्टद्युम्नश्च पार्षतः ।नकुलः सहदेवश्च द्रौपदेयाः ससात्यकाः ।परिवव्रुर्जिघांसन्तो राधेयं शरवृष्टिभिः ॥१५॥
transliteration: taṁ śikhaṇḍī ca bhīmaśca dhṛṣṭadyumnaśca pārṣataḥ ,nakulaḥ sahadevaśca draupadeyāḥ sasātyakāḥ ,parivavrurjighāṁsanto rādheyaṁ śaravṛṣṭibhiḥ.
english: Śikhaṇḍī, Bhīma, Dhṛṣṭadyumna (the descendant of Pṛṣata), Nakula, Sahadeva, and the sons of Draupadī, along with Sātyaki, surrounded Rādhā's son (Karṇa) with showers of arrows, intending to kill him.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 56 Verse 15. Śikhaṇḍī, Bhīma, Dhṛṣṭadyumna (the descendant of Pṛṣata), Nakula, Sahadeva, and the sons of Draupadī, along with Sātyaki, surrounded Rādhā's son (Karṇa) with showers of arrows, intending to kill him.

sanskrit: सात्यकिस्तु ततः कर्णं विंशत्या निशितैः शरैः ।अताडयद्रणे शूरो जत्रुदेशे नरोत्तमः ॥१६॥
transliteration: sātyakistu tataḥ karṇaṁ viṁśatyā niśitaiḥ śaraiḥ ,atāḍayadraṇe śūro jatrudeśe narottamaḥ.
english: But then, the hero, the best of men, Sātyaki, struck Karṇa in battle in the region of the collarbone with twenty sharp arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 56 Verse 16. But then, the hero, the best of men, Sātyaki, struck Karṇa in battle in the region of the collarbone with twenty sharp arrows.

sanskrit: शिखण्डी पञ्चविंशत्या धृष्टद्युम्नश्च पञ्चभिः ।द्रौपदेयाश्चतुःषष्ट्या सहदेवश्च सप्तभिः ।नकुलश्च शतेनाजौ कर्णं विव्याध सायकैः ॥१७॥
transliteration: śikhaṇḍī pañcaviṁśatyā dhṛṣṭadyumnaśca pañcabhiḥ ,draupadeyāścatuḥṣaṣṭyā sahadevaśca saptabhiḥ ,nakulaśca śatenājau karṇaṁ vivyādha sāyakaiḥ.
english: Shikhandi pierced (Karna) with twenty-five (arrows), Dhṛṣṭadyumna with five, the sons of Draupadi with sixty-four, Sahadeva with seven, and Nakula with a hundred arrows; all struck Karna in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 56 Verse 17. Shikhandi pierced (Karna) with twenty-five (arrows), Dhṛṣṭadyumna with five, the sons of Draupadi with sixty-four, Sahadeva with seven, and Nakula with a hundred arrows; all struck Karna in battle.

sanskrit: भीमसेनस्तु राधेयं नवत्या नतपर्वणाम् ।विव्याध समरे क्रुद्धो जत्रुदेशे महाबलः ॥१८॥
transliteration: bhīmasenastu rādheyaṁ navatyā nataparvaṇām ,vivyādha samare kruddho jatrudeśe mahābalaḥ.
english: But the mighty Bhimasena, enraged in battle, pierced Karna (Radha's son) in the region of the collarbone with ninety well-crafted arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 56 Verse 18. But the mighty Bhimasena, enraged in battle, pierced Karna (Radha's son) in the region of the collarbone with ninety well-crafted arrows.

sanskrit: ततः प्रहस्याधिरथिर्विक्षिपन्धनुरुत्तमम् ।मुमोच निशितान्बाणान्पीडयन्सुमहाबलः ।तान्प्रत्यविध्यद्राधेयः पञ्चभिः पञ्चभिः शरैः ॥१९॥
transliteration: tataḥ prahasyādhirathirvikṣipandhanuruttamam ,mumoca niśitānbāṇānpīḍayansumahābalaḥ ,tānpratyavidhyadrādheyaḥ pañcabhiḥ pañcabhiḥ śaraiḥ.
english: Then, the extremely mighty Karna (Adhirathi), after laughing, drew his excellent bow and released sharp arrows, harassing (his opponents). Karna (Radha's son) in turn countered those (incoming arrows) by piercing them with five arrows each.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 56 Verse 19. Then, the extremely mighty Karna (Adhirathi), after laughing, drew his excellent bow and released sharp arrows, harassing (his opponents). Karna (Radha's son) in turn countered those (incoming arrows) by piercing them with five arrows each.

sanskrit: सात्यकेस्तु धनुश्छित्त्वा ध्वजं च पुरुषर्षभः ।अथैनं नवभिर्बाणैराजघान स्तनान्तरे ॥२०॥
transliteration: sātyakestu dhanuśchittvā dhvajaṁ ca puruṣarṣabhaḥ ,athainaṁ navabhirbāṇairājaghāna stanāntare.
english: But the bull among men (Karna), having cut Satyaki's bow and banner, then struck him in the middle of the chest with nine arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 56 Verse 20. But the bull among men (Karna), having cut Satyaki's bow and banner, then struck him in the middle of the chest with nine arrows.

sanskrit: भीमसेनस्तु तं क्रुद्धो विव्याध त्रिंशता शरैः ।सारथिं च त्रिभिर्बाणैराजघान परंतपः ॥२१॥
transliteration: bhīmasenastu taṁ kruddho vivyādha triṁśatā śaraiḥ ,sārathiṁ ca tribhirbāṇairājaghāna paraṁtapaḥ.
english: But Bhimasena, enraged, pierced him with thirty arrows. And that tormentor of foes also struck the charioteer with three arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 56 Verse 21. But Bhimasena, enraged, pierced him with thirty arrows. And that tormentor of foes also struck the charioteer with three arrows.

sanskrit: विरथान्द्रौपदेयांश्च चकार पुरुषर्षभः ।अक्ष्णोर्निमेषमात्रेण तदद्भुतमिवाभवत् ॥२२॥
transliteration: virathāndraupadeyāṁśca cakāra puruṣarṣabhaḥ ,akṣṇornimeṣamātreṇa tadadbhutamivābhavat.
english: And that best among men (Bhimasena) made the sons of Draupadi chariotless. That incident was as if a wonder, occurring in the mere twinkling of an eye.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 56 Verse 22. And that best among men (Bhimasena) made the sons of Draupadi chariotless. That incident was as if a wonder, occurring in the mere twinkling of an eye.

sanskrit: विमुखीकृत्य तान्सर्वाञ्शरैः संनतपर्वभिः ।पाञ्चालानहनच्छूरश्चेदीनां च महारथान् ॥२३॥
transliteration: vimukhīkṛtya tānsarvāñśaraiḥ saṁnataparvabhiḥ ,pāñcālānahanacchūraścedīnāṁ ca mahārathān.
english: Having turned all of them away with arrows having bent joints, that hero (Bhimasena) killed the Panchalas and the great charioteers of the Chedis.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 56 Verse 23. Having turned all of them away with arrows having bent joints, that hero (Bhimasena) killed the Panchalas and the great charioteers of the Chedis.

sanskrit: ते वध्यमानाः समरे चेदिमत्स्या विशां पते ।कर्णमेकमभिद्रुत्य शरसंघैः समार्दयन् ।ताञ्जघान शितैर्बाणैः सूतपुत्रो महारथः ॥२४॥
transliteration: te vadhyamānāḥ samare cedimatsyā viśāṁ pate ,karṇamekamabhidrutya śarasaṁghaiḥ samārdayan ,tāñjaghāna śitairbāṇaiḥ sūtaputro mahārathaḥ.
english: O lord of men, while they, the Chedis and Matsyas, were being slain in battle, they rushed towards Karna alone and tormented him with showers of arrows. But the great charioteer, the son of Suta, killed them with sharp arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 56 Verse 24. O lord of men, while they, the Chedis and Matsyas, were being slain in battle, they rushed towards Karna alone and tormented him with showers of arrows. But the great charioteer, the son of Suta, killed them with sharp arrows.

sanskrit: एतदत्यद्भुतं कर्णे दृष्टवानस्मि भारत ।यदेकः समरे शूरान्सूतपुत्रः प्रतापवान् ॥२५॥
transliteration: etadatyadbhutaṁ karṇe dṛṣṭavānasmi bhārata ,yadekaḥ samare śūrānsūtaputraḥ pratāpavān.
english: O Bhārata, I have witnessed this exceedingly astonishing thing regarding Karna, that the mighty son of Sūta, alone in battle, (faced) the brave warriors...
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 56 Verse 25. O Bhārata, I have witnessed this exceedingly astonishing thing regarding Karna, that the mighty son of Sūta, alone in battle, (faced) the brave warriors...

sanskrit: यतमानान्परं शक्त्यायोधयत्तांश्च धन्विनः ।पाण्डवेयान्महाराज शरैर्वारितवान्रणे ॥२६॥
transliteration: yatamānānparaṁ śaktyāyodhayattāṁśca dhanvinaḥ ,pāṇḍaveyānmahārāja śarairvāritavānraṇe.
english: ...(that Karna) fought against those striving archers, the Pāṇḍavas, with utmost strength (śakti), and repelled them with his arrows, O great king.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 56 Verse 26. ...(that Karna) fought against those striving archers, the Pāṇḍavas, with utmost strength (śakti), and repelled them with his arrows, O great king.

sanskrit: तत्र भारत कर्णस्य लाघवेन महात्मनः ।तुतुषुर्देवताः सर्वाः सिद्धाश्च परमर्षयः ॥२७॥
transliteration: tatra bhārata karṇasya lāghavena mahātmanaḥ ,tutuṣurdevatāḥ sarvāḥ siddhāśca paramarṣayaḥ.
english: O Bhārata, at that time, all the deities, along with the Siddhas (celestial beings) and the great sages, were pleased by the dexterity of the great-souled (ātman) Karna.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 56 Verse 27. O Bhārata, at that time, all the deities, along with the Siddhas (celestial beings) and the great sages, were pleased by the dexterity of the great-souled (ātman) Karna.

sanskrit: अपूजयन्महेष्वासा धार्तराष्ट्रा नरोत्तमम् ।कर्णं रथवरश्रेष्ठं श्रेष्ठं सर्वधनुष्मताम् ॥२८॥
transliteration: apūjayanmaheṣvāsā dhārtarāṣṭrā narottamam ,karṇaṁ rathavaraśreṣṭhaṁ śreṣṭhaṁ sarvadhanuṣmatām.
english: The Dhārtarāṣṭras (Kauravas), who were great archers themselves, honored Karna, that best of men, the foremost of excellent charioteers, and the best of all archers.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 56 Verse 28. The Dhārtarāṣṭras (Kauravas), who were great archers themselves, honored Karna, that best of men, the foremost of excellent charioteers, and the best of all archers.

sanskrit: ततः कर्णो महाराज ददाह रिपुवाहिनीम् ।कक्षमिद्धो यथा वह्निर्निदाघे ज्वलितो महान् ॥२९॥
transliteration: tataḥ karṇo mahārāja dadāha ripuvāhinīm ,kakṣamiddho yathā vahnirnidāghe jvalito mahān.
english: Then, O great king, Karna consumed the enemy army, just as a huge fire, blazing in the summer, engulfs a dry forest.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 56 Verse 29. Then, O great king, Karna consumed the enemy army, just as a huge fire, blazing in the summer, engulfs a dry forest.

sanskrit: ते वध्यमानाः कर्णेन पाण्डवेयास्ततस्ततः ।प्राद्रवन्त रणे भीताः कर्णं दृष्ट्वा महाबलम् ॥३०॥
transliteration: te vadhyamānāḥ karṇena pāṇḍaveyāstatastataḥ ,prādravanta raṇe bhītāḥ karṇaṁ dṛṣṭvā mahābalam.
english: The Pāṇḍavas, being struck down by Karna, fled terrified in all directions on the battlefield after seeing the mighty Karna.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 56 Verse 30. The Pāṇḍavas, being struck down by Karna, fled terrified in all directions on the battlefield after seeing the mighty Karna.

sanskrit: तत्राक्रन्दो महानासीत्पाञ्चालानां महारणे ।वध्यतां सायकैस्तीक्ष्णैः कर्णचापवरच्युतैः ॥३१॥
transliteration: tatrākrando mahānāsītpāñcālānāṁ mahāraṇe ,vadhyatāṁ sāyakaistīkṣṇaiḥ karṇacāpavaracyutaiḥ.
english: There was a great lamentation (ākranda) among the Pāñcālas in that immense battle, as they were being slaughtered by the sharp arrows released from Karna's excellent bow.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 56 Verse 31. There was a great lamentation (ākranda) among the Pāñcālas in that immense battle, as they were being slaughtered by the sharp arrows released from Karna's excellent bow.

sanskrit: तेन शब्देन वित्रस्ता पाण्डवानां महाचमूः ।कर्णमेकं रणे योधं मेनिरे तत्र शात्रवाः ॥३२॥
transliteration: tena śabdena vitrastā pāṇḍavānāṁ mahācamūḥ ,karṇamekaṁ raṇe yodhaṁ menire tatra śātravāḥ.
english: Greatly terrified by that sound, the vast army of the Pāṇḍavas, themselves the adversaries (śātravāḥ), then considered Karna alone to be the true warrior (yodha) on the battlefield.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 56 Verse 32. Greatly terrified by that sound, the vast army of the Pāṇḍavas, themselves the adversaries (śātravāḥ), then considered Karna alone to be the true warrior (yodha) on the battlefield.

sanskrit: तत्राद्भुतं परं चक्रे राधेयः शत्रुकर्शनः ।यदेकं पाण्डवाः सर्वे न शेकुरभिवीक्षितुम् ॥३३॥
transliteration: tatrādbhutaṁ paraṁ cakre rādheyaḥ śatrukarśanaḥ ,yadekaṁ pāṇḍavāḥ sarve na śekurabhivīkṣitum.
english: At that moment, Karna, the son of Radha and tormentor of enemies, performed an extraordinary feat that all the Pandavas together were unable to face or even properly witness.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 56 Verse 33. At that moment, Karna, the son of Radha and tormentor of enemies, performed an extraordinary feat that all the Pandavas together were unable to face or even properly witness.

sanskrit: यथौघः पर्वतश्रेष्ठमासाद्याभिप्रदीर्यते ।तथा तत्पाण्डवं सैन्यं कर्णमासाद्य दीर्यते ॥३४॥
transliteration: yathaughaḥ parvataśreṣṭhamāsādyābhipradīryate ,tathā tatpāṇḍavaṁ sainyaṁ karṇamāsādya dīryate.
english: Just as a massive flood, upon reaching a great mountain, is shattered, similarly, the Pandava army, upon encountering Karna, was being broken apart.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 56 Verse 34. Just as a massive flood, upon reaching a great mountain, is shattered, similarly, the Pandava army, upon encountering Karna, was being broken apart.

sanskrit: कर्णोऽपि समरे राजन्विधूमोऽग्निरिव ज्वलन् ।दहंस्तस्थौ महाबाहुः पाण्डवानां महाचमूम् ॥३५॥
transliteration: karṇo'pi samare rājanvidhūmo'gniriva jvalan ,dahaṁstasthau mahābāhuḥ pāṇḍavānāṁ mahācamūm.
english: O King, in that battle, Karna, the mighty-armed warrior, blazing like a smokeless fire, stood there consuming the great army of the Pandavas.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 56 Verse 35. O King, in that battle, Karna, the mighty-armed warrior, blazing like a smokeless fire, stood there consuming the great army of the Pandavas.

sanskrit: शिरांसि च महाराज कर्णांश्चञ्चलकुण्डलान् ।बाहूंश्च वीरो वीराणां चिच्छेद लघु चेषुभिः ॥३६॥
transliteration: śirāṁsi ca mahārāja karṇāṁścañcalakuṇḍalān ,bāhūṁśca vīro vīrāṇāṁ ciccheda laghu ceṣubhiḥ.
english: O Great King, that hero (Karna) quickly cut off with his arrows the heads, the ears adorned with swinging earrings, and the arms of those warriors.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 56 Verse 36. O Great King, that hero (Karna) quickly cut off with his arrows the heads, the ears adorned with swinging earrings, and the arms of those warriors.

sanskrit: हस्तिदन्तान्त्सरून्खड्गान्ध्वजाञ्शक्तीर्हयान्गजान् ।रथांश्च विविधान्राजन्पताका व्यजनानि च ॥३७॥
transliteration: hastidantāntsarūnkhaḍgāndhvajāñśaktīrhayāngajān ,rathāṁśca vividhānrājanpatākā vyajanāni ca.
english: O King, he cut down elephant tusks, sword hilts, swords, banners, spears, horses, elephants, various chariots, flags, and fans.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 56 Verse 37. O King, he cut down elephant tusks, sword hilts, swords, banners, spears, horses, elephants, various chariots, flags, and fans.

sanskrit: अक्षेषायुगयोक्त्राणि चक्राणि विविधानि च ।चिच्छेद शतधा कर्णो योधव्रतमनुष्ठितः ॥३८॥
transliteration: akṣeṣāyugayoktrāṇi cakrāṇi vividhāni ca ,ciccheda śatadhā karṇo yodhavratamanuṣṭhitaḥ.
english: Karna, having observed the warrior's vow (yodhavrata), cut into a hundred pieces the chariot axles and poles, yokes, harnesses, and various wheels.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 56 Verse 38. Karna, having observed the warrior's vow (yodhavrata), cut into a hundred pieces the chariot axles and poles, yokes, harnesses, and various wheels.

sanskrit: तत्र भारत कर्णेन निहतैर्गजवाजिभिः ।अगम्यरूपा पृथिवी मांसशोणितकर्दमा ॥३९॥
transliteration: tatra bhārata karṇena nihatairgajavājibhiḥ ,agamyarūpā pṛthivī māṁsaśoṇitakardamā.
english: There, O Bharata, the earth became impassable, its mud consisting of flesh and blood, due to the elephants and horses slain by Karna.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 56 Verse 39. There, O Bharata, the earth became impassable, its mud consisting of flesh and blood, due to the elephants and horses slain by Karna.

sanskrit: विषमं च समं चैव हतैरश्वपदातिभिः ।रथैश्च कुञ्जरैश्चैव न प्राज्ञायत किंचन ॥४०॥
transliteration: viṣamaṁ ca samaṁ caiva hatairaśvapadātibhiḥ ,rathaiśca kuñjaraiścaiva na prājñāyata kiṁcana.
english: Neither the uneven nor the even ground could be distinguished due to the slain horses, foot soldiers, chariots, and elephants.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 56 Verse 40. Neither the uneven nor the even ground could be distinguished due to the slain horses, foot soldiers, chariots, and elephants.

sanskrit: नापि स्वे न परे योधाः प्राज्ञायन्त परस्परम् ।घोरे शरान्धकारे तु कर्णास्त्रे च विजृम्भिते ॥४१॥
transliteration: nāpi sve na pare yodhāḥ prājñāyanta parasparam ,ghore śarāndhakāre tu karṇāstre ca vijṛmbhite.
english: Neither their own warriors nor the enemy warriors could discern each other, especially in the terrible darkness created by arrows, and with Karṇa's missile (astra) being manifested.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 56 Verse 41. Neither their own warriors nor the enemy warriors could discern each other, especially in the terrible darkness created by arrows, and with Karṇa's missile (astra) being manifested.

sanskrit: राधेयचापनिर्मुक्तैः शरैः काञ्चनभूषितैः ।संछादिता महाराज यतमाना महारथाः ॥४२॥
transliteration: rādheyacāpanirmuktaiḥ śaraiḥ kāñcanabhūṣitaiḥ ,saṁchāditā mahārāja yatamānā mahārathāḥ.
english: O great king, the great charioteers, despite their efforts, were completely covered by the gold-adorned arrows released from the bow of Karṇa, son of Rādhā.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 56 Verse 42. O great king, the great charioteers, despite their efforts, were completely covered by the gold-adorned arrows released from the bow of Karṇa, son of Rādhā.

sanskrit: ते पाण्डवेयाः समरे कर्णेन स्म पुनः पुनः ।अभज्यन्त महाराज यतमाना महारथाः ॥४३॥
transliteration: te pāṇḍaveyāḥ samare karṇena sma punaḥ punaḥ ,abhajyanta mahārāja yatamānā mahārathāḥ.
english: O great king, those great Pāṇḍava charioteers, despite their efforts, were repeatedly broken (defeated) by Karṇa in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 56 Verse 43. O great king, those great Pāṇḍava charioteers, despite their efforts, were repeatedly broken (defeated) by Karṇa in battle.

sanskrit: मृगसंघान्यथा क्रुद्धः सिंहो द्रावयते वने ।कर्णस्तु समरे योधांस्तत्र तत्र महायशाः ।कालयामास तत्सैन्यं यथा पशुगणान्वृकः ॥४४॥
transliteration: mṛgasaṁghānyathā kruddhaḥ siṁho drāvayate vane ,karṇastu samare yodhāṁstatra tatra mahāyaśāḥ ,kālayāmāsa tatsainyaṁ yathā paśugaṇānvṛkaḥ.
english: Just as an enraged lion drives away herds of deer in the forest, so too did the greatly renowned Karṇa scatter the warriors and that army in battle here and there, just like a wolf scatters herds of animals.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 56 Verse 44. Just as an enraged lion drives away herds of deer in the forest, so too did the greatly renowned Karṇa scatter the warriors and that army in battle here and there, just like a wolf scatters herds of animals.

sanskrit: दृष्ट्वा तु पाण्डवीं सेनां धार्तराष्ट्राः पराङ्मुखीम् ।अभिजग्मुर्महेष्वासा रुवन्तो भैरवान्रवान् ॥४५॥
transliteration: dṛṣṭvā tu pāṇḍavīṁ senāṁ dhārtarāṣṭrāḥ parāṅmukhīm ,abhijagmurmaheṣvāsā ruvanto bhairavānravān.
english: Seeing the Pāṇḍava army turning away, the sons of Dhṛtarāṣṭra, who were great archers, advanced, roaring dreadful roars.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 56 Verse 45. Seeing the Pāṇḍava army turning away, the sons of Dhṛtarāṣṭra, who were great archers, advanced, roaring dreadful roars.

sanskrit: दुर्योधनो हि राजेन्द्र मुदा परमया युतः ।वादयामास संहृष्टो नानावाद्यानि सर्वशः ॥४६॥
transliteration: duryodhano hi rājendra mudā paramayā yutaḥ ,vādayāmāsa saṁhṛṣṭo nānāvādyāni sarvaśaḥ.
english: O King (rāja-indra)! Duryodhana, indeed, filled with supreme joy, highly delighted, caused various musical instruments to be played everywhere.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 56 Verse 46. O King (rāja-indra)! Duryodhana, indeed, filled with supreme joy, highly delighted, caused various musical instruments to be played everywhere.

sanskrit: पाञ्चालापि महेष्वासा भग्ना भग्ना नरोत्तमाः ।न्यवर्तन्त यथा शूरा मृत्युं कृत्वा निवर्तनम् ॥४७॥
transliteration: pāñcālāpi maheṣvāsā bhagnā bhagnā narottamāḥ ,nyavartanta yathā śūrā mṛtyuṁ kṛtvā nivartanam.
english: Even the Pāñcālas, who were great archers and best among men, though broken again and again, returned like heroes, having accepted death as their retreat.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 56 Verse 47. Even the Pāñcālas, who were great archers and best among men, though broken again and again, returned like heroes, having accepted death as their retreat.

sanskrit: तान्निवृत्तान्रणे शूरान्राधेयः शत्रुतापनः ।अनेकशो महाराज बभञ्ज पुरुषर्षभः ॥४८॥
transliteration: tānnivṛttānraṇe śūrānrādheyaḥ śatrutāpanaḥ ,anekaśo mahārāja babhañja puruṣarṣabhaḥ.
english: O Great King (mahā-rāja)! The son of Rādhā (rādheya), tormentor of foes (śatru-tāpana), and foremost among men (puruṣa-ṛṣabhaḥ), broke those heroes who had retreated from the battle, many times over.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 56 Verse 48. O Great King (mahā-rāja)! The son of Rādhā (rādheya), tormentor of foes (śatru-tāpana), and foremost among men (puruṣa-ṛṣabhaḥ), broke those heroes who had retreated from the battle, many times over.

sanskrit: तत्र भारत कर्णेन पाञ्चाला विंशती रथाः ।निहताः सादयः क्रोधाच्चेदयश्च परःशताः ॥४९॥
transliteration: tatra bhārata karṇena pāñcālā viṁśatī rathāḥ ,nihatāḥ sādayaḥ krodhāccedayaśca paraḥśatāḥ.
english: O Bhārata, there, Karna, in his rage, killed twenty Pañcāla charioteers along with their mounts, and hundreds of Cedis as well.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 56 Verse 49. O Bhārata, there, Karna, in his rage, killed twenty Pañcāla charioteers along with their mounts, and hundreds of Cedis as well.

sanskrit: कृत्वा शून्यान्रथोपस्थान्वाजिपृष्ठांश्च भारत ।निर्मनुष्यान्गजस्कन्धान्पादातांश्चैव विद्रुतान् ॥५०॥
transliteration: kṛtvā śūnyānrathopasthānvājipṛṣṭhāṁśca bhārata ,nirmanuṣyāngajaskandhānpādātāṁścaiva vidrutān.
english: O Bhārata, having made the chariot seats and horses' backs empty, and the elephant shoulders devoid of men, he also routed the foot soldiers.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 56 Verse 50. O Bhārata, having made the chariot seats and horses' backs empty, and the elephant shoulders devoid of men, he also routed the foot soldiers.

sanskrit: आदित्य इव मध्याह्ने दुर्निरीक्ष्यः परंतपः ।कालान्तकवपुः क्रूरः सूतपुत्रश्चचार ह ॥५१॥
transliteration: āditya iva madhyāhne durnirīkṣyaḥ paraṁtapaḥ ,kālāntakavapuḥ krūraḥ sūtaputraścacāra ha.
english: Like the midday sun, formidable to behold, the tormentor of enemies, Karṇa, the son of Sūta, moved about fiercely, embodying the Destroyer (Kāla).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 56 Verse 51. Like the midday sun, formidable to behold, the tormentor of enemies, Karṇa, the son of Sūta, moved about fiercely, embodying the Destroyer (Kāla).

sanskrit: एवमेतान्महाराज नरवाजिरथद्विपान् ।हत्वा तस्थौ महेष्वासः कर्णोऽरिगणसूदनः ॥५२॥
transliteration: evametānmahārāja naravājirathadvipān ,hatvā tasthau maheṣvāsaḥ karṇo'rigaṇasūdanaḥ.
english: Thus, O great king, having slaughtered these divisions of men, horses, chariots, and elephants, Karṇa, the great archer and annihilator of enemy throngs, stood his ground.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 56 Verse 52. Thus, O great king, having slaughtered these divisions of men, horses, chariots, and elephants, Karṇa, the great archer and annihilator of enemy throngs, stood his ground.

sanskrit: यथा भूतगणान्हत्वा कालस्तिष्ठेन्महाबलः ।तथा स सोमकान्हत्वा तस्थावेको महारथः ॥५३॥
transliteration: yathā bhūtagaṇānhatvā kālastiṣṭhenmahābalaḥ ,tathā sa somakānhatvā tasthāveko mahārathaḥ.
english: Just as all-powerful Time (kāla) remains standing after destroying countless hosts of beings, so too that solitary great charioteer (mahāratha) stood firm after slaying the Somakas.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 56 Verse 53. Just as all-powerful Time (kāla) remains standing after destroying countless hosts of beings, so too that solitary great charioteer (mahāratha) stood firm after slaying the Somakas.

sanskrit: तत्राद्भुतमपश्याम पाञ्चालानां पराक्रमम् ।वध्यमानापि कर्णेन नाजहू रणमूर्धनि ॥५४॥
transliteration: tatrādbhutamapaśyāma pāñcālānāṁ parākramam ,vadhyamānāpi karṇena nājahū raṇamūrdhani.
english: There we witnessed the astonishing valor of the Pāñcālas; even while being slaughtered by Karṇa, they did not abandon the forefront of the battle.
