search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 56 Verse 54. There we witnessed the astonishing valor of the Pāñcālas; even while being slaughtered by Karṇa, they did not abandon the forefront of the battle.

sanskrit: राजा दुःशासनश्चैव कृपः शारद्वतस्तथा ।अश्वत्थामा कृतवर्मा शकुनिश्चापि सौबलः ।न्यहनन्पाण्डवीं सेनां शतशोऽथ सहस्रशः ॥५५॥
transliteration: rājā duḥśāsanaścaiva kṛpaḥ śāradvatastathā ,aśvatthāmā kṛtavarmā śakuniścāpi saubalaḥ ,nyahananpāṇḍavīṁ senāṁ śataśo'tha sahasraśaḥ.
english: King Duḥśāsana, along with Kṛpa, son of Śaradvan, and also Aśvatthāmā, Kṛtavarmā, and Śakuni, son of Subala, struck down the Pāṇḍava army by hundreds and then by thousands.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 56 Verse 55. King Duḥśāsana, along with Kṛpa, son of Śaradvan, and also Aśvatthāmā, Kṛtavarmā, and Śakuni, son of Subala, struck down the Pāṇḍava army by hundreds and then by thousands.

sanskrit: कर्णपुत्रौ च राजेन्द्र भ्रातरौ सत्यविक्रमौ ।अनाशयेतां बलिनः पाञ्चालान्वै ततस्ततः ।तत्र युद्धं तदा ह्यासीत्क्रूरं विशसनं महत् ॥५६॥
transliteration: karṇaputrau ca rājendra bhrātarau satyavikramau ,anāśayetāṁ balinaḥ pāñcālānvai tatastataḥ ,tatra yuddhaṁ tadā hyāsītkrūraṁ viśasanaṁ mahat.
english: And, O king of kings, the two sons of Karṇa, who were brothers of true valor, certainly caused the powerful Pāñcālas to perish everywhere. At that time, indeed, there was a great, cruel, and destructive battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 56 Verse 56. And, O king of kings, the two sons of Karṇa, who were brothers of true valor, certainly caused the powerful Pāñcālas to perish everywhere. At that time, indeed, there was a great, cruel, and destructive battle.

sanskrit: तथैव पाण्डवाः शूरा धृष्टद्युम्नशिखण्डिनौ ।द्रौपदेयाश्च संक्रुद्धा अभ्यघ्नंस्तावकं बलम् ॥५७॥
transliteration: tathaiva pāṇḍavāḥ śūrā dhṛṣṭadyumnaśikhaṇḍinau ,draupadeyāśca saṁkruddhā abhyaghnaṁstāvakaṁ balam.
english: Similarly, the valiant Pāṇḍavas, along with Dhṛṣṭadyumna, Śikhaṇḍin, and the enraged sons of Draupadī, attacked your army.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 56 Verse 57. Similarly, the valiant Pāṇḍavas, along with Dhṛṣṭadyumna, Śikhaṇḍin, and the enraged sons of Draupadī, attacked your army.

sanskrit: एवमेष क्षयो वृत्तः पाण्डवानां ततस्ततः ।तावकानामपि रणे भीमं प्राप्य महाबलम् ॥५८॥
transliteration: evameṣa kṣayo vṛttaḥ pāṇḍavānāṁ tatastataḥ ,tāvakānāmapi raṇe bhīmaṁ prāpya mahābalam.
english: Thus, this destruction occurred everywhere among the Pāṇḍavas, and also among your own people in battle, when they encountered the immensely powerful Bhīma.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 56 Verse 58. Thus, this destruction occurred everywhere among the Pāṇḍavas, and also among your own people in battle, when they encountered the immensely powerful Bhīma.

sanskrit: संजय उवाच ।अर्जुनस्तु महाराज कृत्वा सैन्यं पृथग्विधम् ।सूतपुत्रं सुसंरब्धं दृष्ट्वा चैव महारणे ॥१॥
transliteration: saṁjaya uvāca ,arjunastu mahārāja kṛtvā sainyaṁ pṛthagvidham ,sūtaputraṁ susaṁrabdhaṁ dṛṣṭvā caiva mahāraṇe.
english: Sañjaya said: But Arjuna, O great king, after deploying his army in various formations, and having seen the fiercely enraged son of Sūta (Karṇa) in the mighty battle -
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 57 Verse 1. Sañjaya said: But Arjuna, O great king, after deploying his army in various formations, and having seen the fiercely enraged son of Sūta (Karṇa) in the mighty battle -

sanskrit: शोणितोदां महीं कृत्वा मांसमज्जास्थिवाहिनीम् ।वासुदेवमिदं वाक्यमब्रवीत्पुरुषर्षभ ॥२॥
transliteration: śoṇitodāṁ mahīṁ kṛtvā māṁsamajjāsthivāhinīm ,vāsudevamidaṁ vākyamabravītpuruṣarṣabha.
english: ...having made the earth (mahī) flow with rivers of blood, flesh, marrow, and bones, that best among men (Arjuna) spoke these words to Vāsudeva (Kṛṣṇa).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 57 Verse 2. ...having made the earth (mahī) flow with rivers of blood, flesh, marrow, and bones, that best among men (Arjuna) spoke these words to Vāsudeva (Kṛṣṇa).

sanskrit: एष केतू रणे कृष्ण सूतपुत्रस्य दृश्यते ।भीमसेनादयश्चैते योधयन्ति महारथान् ।एते द्रवन्ति पाञ्चालाः कर्णात्त्रस्ता जनार्दन ॥३॥
transliteration: eṣa ketū raṇe kṛṣṇa sūtaputrasya dṛśyate ,bhīmasenādayaścaite yodhayanti mahārathān ,ete dravanti pāñcālāḥ karṇāttrastā janārdana.
english: O Krishna, this banner of Karṇa (sūtaputra) is seen in battle. And these Bhīmasena and others are fighting the great charioteers. O Janārdana, these Pāñcālas are fleeing, terrified by Karṇa.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 57 Verse 3. O Krishna, this banner of Karṇa (sūtaputra) is seen in battle. And these Bhīmasena and others are fighting the great charioteers. O Janārdana, these Pāñcālas are fleeing, terrified by Karṇa.

sanskrit: एष दुर्योधनो राजा श्वेतच्छत्रेण भास्वता ।कर्णेन भग्नान्पाञ्चालान्द्रावयन्बहु शोभते ॥४॥
transliteration: eṣa duryodhano rājā śvetacchatreṇa bhāsvatā ,karṇena bhagnānpāñcālāndrāvayanbahu śobhate.
english: This King Duryodhana, with his brilliant white umbrella, shines splendidly as he puts to flight the Pāñcālas, who have been routed by Karṇa.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 57 Verse 4. This King Duryodhana, with his brilliant white umbrella, shines splendidly as he puts to flight the Pāñcālas, who have been routed by Karṇa.

sanskrit: कृपश्च कृतवर्मा च द्रौणिश्चैव महाबलः ।एते रक्षन्ति राजानं सूतपुत्रेण रक्षिताः ।अवध्यमानास्तेऽस्माभिर्घातयिष्यन्ति सोमकान् ॥५॥
transliteration: kṛpaśca kṛtavarmā ca drauṇiścaiva mahābalaḥ ,ete rakṣanti rājānaṁ sūtaputreṇa rakṣitāḥ ,avadhyamānāste'smābhirghātayiṣyanti somakān.
english: Kṛpa, Kṛtavarmā, and also the exceedingly mighty Droṇi (Aśvatthāmā) – these, protected by Karṇa (sūtaputra), are guarding the king. Even though they are not being slain by us, they will destroy the Somakas.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 57 Verse 5. Kṛpa, Kṛtavarmā, and also the exceedingly mighty Droṇi (Aśvatthāmā) – these, protected by Karṇa (sūtaputra), are guarding the king. Even though they are not being slain by us, they will destroy the Somakas.

sanskrit: एष शल्यो रथोपस्थे रश्मिसंचारकोविदः ।सूतपुत्ररथं कृष्ण वाहयन्बहु शोभते ॥६॥
transliteration: eṣa śalyo rathopasthe raśmisaṁcārakovidaḥ ,sūtaputrarathaṁ kṛṣṇa vāhayanbahu śobhate.
english: O Krishna, this Śalya, from his position on the chariot, expert in handling the reins, shines splendidly as he drives the chariot of Karṇa (sūtaputra).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 57 Verse 6. O Krishna, this Śalya, from his position on the chariot, expert in handling the reins, shines splendidly as he drives the chariot of Karṇa (sūtaputra).

sanskrit: तत्र मे बुद्धिरुत्पन्ना वाहयात्र महारथम् ।नाहत्वा समरे कर्णं निवर्तिष्ये कथंचन ॥७॥
transliteration: tatra me buddhirutpannā vāhayātra mahāratham ,nāhatvā samare karṇaṁ nivartiṣye kathaṁcana.
english: A thought then arose in me: "Drive the great chariot here! I will not return from battle by any means without having killed Karna."
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 57 Verse 7. A thought then arose in me: "Drive the great chariot here! I will not return from battle by any means without having killed Karna."

sanskrit: राधेयोऽप्यन्यथा पार्थान्सृञ्जयांश्च महारथान् ।निःशेषान्समरे कुर्यात्पश्यतोर्नौ जनार्दन ॥८॥
transliteration: rādheyo'pyanyathā pārthānsṛñjayāṁśca mahārathān ,niḥśeṣānsamare kuryātpaśyatornau janārdana.
english: O Janardana, otherwise, while we two are watching, the son of Radha (Karna) would utterly annihilate the Pandavas and the great warrior Srnjayas in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 57 Verse 8. O Janardana, otherwise, while we two are watching, the son of Radha (Karna) would utterly annihilate the Pandavas and the great warrior Srnjayas in battle.

sanskrit: ततः प्रायाद्रथेनाशु केशवस्तव वाहिनीम् ।कर्णं प्रति महेष्वासं द्वैरथे सव्यसाचिना ॥९॥
transliteration: tataḥ prāyādrathenāśu keśavastava vāhinīm ,karṇaṁ prati maheṣvāsaṁ dvairathe savyasācinā.
english: Then, Kesava (Krishna) swiftly drove his chariot towards your army, specifically towards Karna, the great archer, for his duel (dvairatha) with Arjuna (Savyasacin).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 57 Verse 9. Then, Kesava (Krishna) swiftly drove his chariot towards your army, specifically towards Karna, the great archer, for his duel (dvairatha) with Arjuna (Savyasacin).

sanskrit: प्रयातश्च महाबाहुः पाण्डवानुज्ञया हरिः ।आश्वासयन्रथेनैव पाण्डुसैन्यानि सर्वशः ॥१०॥
transliteration: prayātaśca mahābāhuḥ pāṇḍavānujñayā hariḥ ,āśvāsayanrathenaiva pāṇḍusainyāni sarvaśaḥ.
english: And the mighty-armed Hari (Krishna), having set forth with the permission of the Pandavas, then reassured all the Pandava armies completely, even from his chariot.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 57 Verse 10. And the mighty-armed Hari (Krishna), having set forth with the permission of the Pandavas, then reassured all the Pandava armies completely, even from his chariot.

sanskrit: रथघोषः स संग्रामे पाण्डवेयस्य संबभौ ।वासवाशनितुल्यस्य महौघस्येव मारिष ॥११॥
transliteration: rathaghoṣaḥ sa saṁgrāme pāṇḍaveyasya saṁbabhau ,vāsavāśanitulyasya mahaughasyeva māriṣa.
english: O respected one, the roar of that chariot of the son of Pāṇḍu (Arjuna) in battle resounded, resembling a mighty flood and equal to Indra's thunderbolt.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 57 Verse 11. O respected one, the roar of that chariot of the son of Pāṇḍu (Arjuna) in battle resounded, resembling a mighty flood and equal to Indra's thunderbolt.

sanskrit: महता रथघोषेण पाण्डवः सत्यविक्रमः ।अभ्ययादप्रमेयात्मा विजयस्तव वाहिनीम् ॥१२॥
transliteration: mahatā rathaghoṣeṇa pāṇḍavaḥ satyavikramaḥ ,abhyayādaprameyātmā vijayastava vāhinīm.
english: With a tremendous roar from his chariot, the Pāṇḍava (Arjuna), whose valor is unwavering and whose spirit (ātman) is immeasurable, advanced victoriously towards your army.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 57 Verse 12. With a tremendous roar from his chariot, the Pāṇḍava (Arjuna), whose valor is unwavering and whose spirit (ātman) is immeasurable, advanced victoriously towards your army.

sanskrit: तमायान्तं समीक्ष्यैव श्वेताश्वं कृष्णसारथिम् ।मद्रराजोऽब्रवीत्कर्णं केतुं दृष्ट्वा महात्मनः ॥१३॥
transliteration: tamāyāntaṁ samīkṣyaiva śvetāśvaṁ kṛṣṇasārathim ,madrarājo'bravītkarṇaṁ ketuṁ dṛṣṭvā mahātmanaḥ.
english: Just after seeing him approaching - the one with white horses and Kṛṣṇa as his charioteer - and having seen the banner of that great-souled one (mahātman), the king of Madras (Śalya) spoke to Karṇa.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 57 Verse 13. Just after seeing him approaching - the one with white horses and Kṛṣṇa as his charioteer - and having seen the banner of that great-souled one (mahātman), the king of Madras (Śalya) spoke to Karṇa.

sanskrit: अयं स रथ आयाति श्वेताश्वः कृष्णसारथिः ।निघ्नन्नमित्रान्समरे यं कर्ण परिपृच्छसि ॥१४॥
transliteration: ayaṁ sa ratha āyāti śvetāśvaḥ kṛṣṇasārathiḥ ,nighnannamitrānsamare yaṁ karṇa paripṛcchasi.
english: O Karṇa, this is that very chariot, drawn by white horses and with Kṛṣṇa as its charioteer, which is approaching, striking down enemies in battle - the one about whom you keep asking.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 57 Verse 14. O Karṇa, this is that very chariot, drawn by white horses and with Kṛṣṇa as its charioteer, which is approaching, striking down enemies in battle - the one about whom you keep asking.

sanskrit: एष तिष्ठति कौन्तेयः संस्पृशन्गाण्डिवं धनुः ।तं हनिष्यसि चेदद्य तन्नः श्रेयो भविष्यति ॥१५॥
transliteration: eṣa tiṣṭhati kaunteyaḥ saṁspṛśangāṇḍivaṁ dhanuḥ ,taṁ haniṣyasi cedadya tannaḥ śreyo bhaviṣyati.
english: Kaunteya stands here, grasping his Gaṇḍīva bow. If you can kill him today, that will certainly be auspicious for us.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 57 Verse 15. Kaunteya stands here, grasping his Gaṇḍīva bow. If you can kill him today, that will certainly be auspicious for us.

sanskrit: एषा विदीर्यते सेना धार्तराष्ट्री समन्ततः ।अर्जुनस्य भयात्तूर्णं निघ्नतः शात्रवान्बहून् ॥१६॥
transliteration: eṣā vidīryate senā dhārtarāṣṭrī samantataḥ ,arjunasya bhayāttūrṇaṁ nighnataḥ śātravānbahūn.
english: This army of Dhritarashtra's sons is rapidly breaking apart on all sides, out of fear of Arjuna, who is swiftly striking down many enemies.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 57 Verse 16. This army of Dhritarashtra's sons is rapidly breaking apart on all sides, out of fear of Arjuna, who is swiftly striking down many enemies.

sanskrit: वर्जयन्सर्वसैन्यानि त्वरते हि धनंजयः ।त्वदर्थमिति मन्येऽहं यथास्योदीर्यते वपुः ॥१७॥
transliteration: varjayansarvasainyāni tvarate hi dhanaṁjayaḥ ,tvadarthamiti manye'haṁ yathāsyodīryate vapuḥ.
english: Dhananjaya is indeed rushing forward, bypassing all the other armies. I believe this is specifically for your sake, judging by the way his form is displayed.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 57 Verse 17. Dhananjaya is indeed rushing forward, bypassing all the other armies. I believe this is specifically for your sake, judging by the way his form is displayed.

sanskrit: न ह्यवस्थाप्यते पार्थो युयुत्सुः केनचित्सह ।त्वामृते क्रोधदीप्तो हि पीड्यमाने वृकोदरे ॥१८॥
transliteration: na hyavasthāpyate pārtho yuyutsuḥ kenacitsaha ,tvāmṛte krodhadīpto hi pīḍyamāne vṛkodare.
english: Indeed, Partha, who is eager to fight, cannot be held back by anyone except you. For he is blazing with anger because Bhima (Vṛkodara) is being tormented.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 57 Verse 18. Indeed, Partha, who is eager to fight, cannot be held back by anyone except you. For he is blazing with anger because Bhima (Vṛkodara) is being tormented.

sanskrit: विरथं धर्मराजं च दृष्ट्वा सुदृढविक्षतम् ।शिखण्डिनं सात्यकिं च धृष्टद्युम्नं च पार्षतम् ॥१९॥
transliteration: virathaṁ dharmarājaṁ ca dṛṣṭvā sudṛḍhavikṣatam ,śikhaṇḍinaṁ sātyakiṁ ca dhṛṣṭadyumnaṁ ca pārṣatam.
english: Seeing Dharmarāja (Yudhishthira) without his chariot and severely wounded, and also Shikhandin, Satyaki, and Dhrishtadyumna, the son of Prishata.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 57 Verse 19. Seeing Dharmarāja (Yudhishthira) without his chariot and severely wounded, and also Shikhandin, Satyaki, and Dhrishtadyumna, the son of Prishata.

sanskrit: द्रौपदेयान्युधामन्युमुत्तमौजसमेव च ।नकुलं सहदेवं च भ्रातरौ द्वौ समीक्ष्य च ॥२०॥
transliteration: draupadeyānyudhāmanyumuttamaujasameva ca ,nakulaṁ sahadevaṁ ca bhrātarau dvau samīkṣya ca.
english: ...and the sons of Draupadi, Yudhāmanyu, and Uttamaujas, and also observing Nakula and Sahadeva, the two brothers.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 57 Verse 20. ...and the sons of Draupadi, Yudhāmanyu, and Uttamaujas, and also observing Nakula and Sahadeva, the two brothers.

sanskrit: सहसैकरथः पार्थस्त्वामभ्येति परंतप ।क्रोधरक्तेक्षणः क्रुद्धो जिघांसुः सर्वधन्विनाम् ॥२१॥
transliteration: sahasaikarathaḥ pārthastvāmabhyeti paraṁtapa ,krodharaktekṣaṇaḥ kruddho jighāṁsuḥ sarvadhanvinām.
english: O tormentor of foes, Arjuna (Pārtha), alone on his chariot, suddenly comes towards you. Enraged, with eyes red from anger, he desires to kill all archers.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 57 Verse 21. O tormentor of foes, Arjuna (Pārtha), alone on his chariot, suddenly comes towards you. Enraged, with eyes red from anger, he desires to kill all archers.

sanskrit: त्वरितोऽभिपतत्यस्मांस्त्यक्त्वा सैन्यान्यसंशयम् ।त्वं कर्ण प्रतियाह्येनं नास्त्यन्यो हि धनुर्धरः ॥२२॥
transliteration: tvarito'bhipatatyasmāṁstyaktvā sainyānyasaṁśayam ,tvaṁ karṇa pratiyāhyenaṁ nāstyanyo hi dhanurdharaḥ.
english: He quickly attacks us, having undoubtedly abandoned his armies. O Karṇa, you must go to meet him, for indeed, there is no other archer [who can match him].
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 57 Verse 22. He quickly attacks us, having undoubtedly abandoned his armies. O Karṇa, you must go to meet him, for indeed, there is no other archer [who can match him].

sanskrit: न तं पश्यामि लोकेऽस्मिंस्त्वत्तोऽप्यन्यं धनुर्धरम् ।अर्जुनं समरे क्रुद्धं यो वेलामिव धारयेत् ॥२३॥
transliteration: na taṁ paśyāmi loke'smiṁstvatto'pyanyaṁ dhanurdharam ,arjunaṁ samare kruddhaṁ yo velāmiva dhārayet.
english: I do not see anyone else in this world, besides you, who is such a bowman, capable of holding back an enraged Arjuna in battle, just as the shore holds back the ocean.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 57 Verse 23. I do not see anyone else in this world, besides you, who is such a bowman, capable of holding back an enraged Arjuna in battle, just as the shore holds back the ocean.

sanskrit: न चास्य रक्षां पश्यामि पृष्ठतो न च पार्श्वतः ।एक एवाभियाति त्वां पश्य साफल्यमात्मनः ॥२४॥
transliteration: na cāsya rakṣāṁ paśyāmi pṛṣṭhato na ca pārśvataḥ ,eka evābhiyāti tvāṁ paśya sāphalyamātmanaḥ.
english: And I see no protection for him, neither from behind nor from his sides. He alone approaches you. Behold, therefore, the success (sāphalya) of your own self (ātman).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 57 Verse 24. And I see no protection for him, neither from behind nor from his sides. He alone approaches you. Behold, therefore, the success (sāphalya) of your own self (ātman).

sanskrit: त्वं हि कृष्णौ रणे शक्तः संसाधयितुमाहवे ।तवैष भारो राधेय प्रत्युद्याहि धनंजयम् ॥२५॥
transliteration: tvaṁ hi kṛṣṇau raṇe śaktaḥ saṁsādhayitumāhave ,tavaiṣa bhāro rādheya pratyudyāhi dhanaṁjayam.
english: Indeed, you, O son of Radha, are capable of overcoming the two Krishnas (Kṛṣṇa and Arjuna) in battle and combat. This burden is yours; confront Dhananjaya (Arjuna)!
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 57 Verse 25. Indeed, you, O son of Radha, are capable of overcoming the two Krishnas (Kṛṣṇa and Arjuna) in battle and combat. This burden is yours; confront Dhananjaya (Arjuna)!

sanskrit: त्वं कृतो ह्येव भीष्मेण द्रोणद्रौणिकृपैरपि ।सव्यसाचिप्रतिरथस्तं निवर्तय पाण्डवम् ॥२६॥
transliteration: tvaṁ kṛto hyeva bhīṣmeṇa droṇadrauṇikṛpairapi ,savyasācipratirathastaṁ nivartaya pāṇḍavam.
english: Indeed, you have been designated by Bhishma, Drona, Dronaputra (Ashvatthama), and Kripa as the equal challenger to Savyasaci (Arjuna). Therefore, stop that Pandava!
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 57 Verse 26. Indeed, you have been designated by Bhishma, Drona, Dronaputra (Ashvatthama), and Kripa as the equal challenger to Savyasaci (Arjuna). Therefore, stop that Pandava!

sanskrit: लेलिहानं यथा सर्पं गर्जन्तमृषभं यथा ।लयस्थितं यथा व्याघ्रं जहि कर्ण धनंजयम् ॥२७॥
transliteration: lelihānaṁ yathā sarpaṁ garjantamṛṣabhaṁ yathā ,layasthitaṁ yathā vyāghraṁ jahi karṇa dhanaṁjayam.
english: O Karṇa, kill Dhanaṃjaya (Arjuna), who is like a licking snake, a roaring bull, or a tiger poised for attack.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 57 Verse 27. O Karṇa, kill Dhanaṃjaya (Arjuna), who is like a licking snake, a roaring bull, or a tiger poised for attack.

sanskrit: एते द्रवन्ति समरे धार्तराष्ट्रा महारथाः ।अर्जुनस्य भयात्तूर्णं निरपेक्षा जनाधिपाः ॥२८॥
transliteration: ete dravanti samare dhārtarāṣṭrā mahārathāḥ ,arjunasya bhayāttūrṇaṁ nirapekṣā janādhipāḥ.
english: These great warriors (maharathas) who are the Dhārtarāṣṭras (sons of Dhṛtarāṣṭra), these lords of men (janādhipas), are fleeing quickly in battle out of fear of Arjuna, utterly disregardful of their lives (nirapekṣāḥ).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 57 Verse 28. These great warriors (maharathas) who are the Dhārtarāṣṭras (sons of Dhṛtarāṣṭra), these lords of men (janādhipas), are fleeing quickly in battle out of fear of Arjuna, utterly disregardful of their lives (nirapekṣāḥ).

sanskrit: द्रवतामथ तेषां तु युधि नान्योऽस्ति मानवः ।भयहा यो भवेद्वीर त्वामृते सूतनन्दन ॥२९॥
transliteration: dravatāmatha teṣāṁ tu yudhi nānyo'sti mānavaḥ ,bhayahā yo bhavedvīra tvāmṛte sūtanandana.
english: Indeed, among those who are fleeing in battle, O son of Sūta (Sūtanandana), O hero (vīra), there is no other man who could be a dispeller of fear except for you.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 57 Verse 29. Indeed, among those who are fleeing in battle, O son of Sūta (Sūtanandana), O hero (vīra), there is no other man who could be a dispeller of fear except for you.

sanskrit: एते त्वां कुरवः सर्वे द्वीपमासाद्य संयुगे ।विष्ठिताः पुरुषव्याघ्र त्वत्तः शरणकाङ्क्षिणः ॥३०॥
transliteration: ete tvāṁ kuravaḥ sarve dvīpamāsādya saṁyuge ,viṣṭhitāḥ puruṣavyāghra tvattaḥ śaraṇakāṅkṣiṇaḥ.
english: O tiger among men (puruṣavyāghra), all these Kauravas, having taken refuge in you as if you were an island in battle, are standing here, desiring protection from you.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 57 Verse 30. O tiger among men (puruṣavyāghra), all these Kauravas, having taken refuge in you as if you were an island in battle, are standing here, desiring protection from you.

sanskrit: वैदेहाम्बष्ठकाम्बोजास्तथा नग्नजितस्त्वया ।गान्धाराश्च यया धृत्या जिताः संख्ये सुदुर्जयाः ॥३१॥
transliteration: vaidehāmbaṣṭhakāmbojāstathā nagnajitastvayā ,gāndhārāśca yayā dhṛtyā jitāḥ saṁkhye sudurjayāḥ.
english: The Vaidehas, Ambaṣṭhas, Kāmbojas, Nagnajitas, and Gandharas - who were extremely difficult to conquer in battle - were all defeated by you with that very same resolve (dhṛti).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 57 Verse 31. The Vaidehas, Ambaṣṭhas, Kāmbojas, Nagnajitas, and Gandharas - who were extremely difficult to conquer in battle - were all defeated by you with that very same resolve (dhṛti).

sanskrit: तां धृतिं कुरु राधेय ततः प्रत्येहि पाण्डवम् ।वासुदेवं च वार्ष्णेयं प्रीयमाणं किरीटिना ॥३२॥
transliteration: tāṁ dhṛtiṁ kuru rādheya tataḥ pratyehi pāṇḍavam ,vāsudevaṁ ca vārṣṇeyaṁ prīyamāṇaṁ kirīṭinā.
english: O son of Rādhā (Karna), cultivate that same resolve (dhṛti). Then, approach the son of Pāṇḍu (Arjuna) and Vāsudeva (Krishna), the scion of Vṛṣṇi, who is pleased by Kirīṭin (Arjuna).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 57 Verse 32. O son of Rādhā (Karna), cultivate that same resolve (dhṛti). Then, approach the son of Pāṇḍu (Arjuna) and Vāsudeva (Krishna), the scion of Vṛṣṇi, who is pleased by Kirīṭin (Arjuna).

sanskrit: कर्ण उवाच ।प्रकृतिस्थो हि मे शल्य इदानीं संमतस्तथा ।प्रतिभासि महाबाहो विभीश्चैव धनंजयात् ॥३३॥
transliteration: karṇa uvāca ,prakṛtistho hi me śalya idānīṁ saṁmatastathā ,pratibhāsi mahābāho vibhīścaiva dhanaṁjayāt.
english: Karna said: 'O mighty-armed Śalya, you now appear to me to be truly in your proper disposition (prakṛti), agreeable, and indeed fearful of Dhanañjaya (Arjuna).'
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 57 Verse 33. Karna said: 'O mighty-armed Śalya, you now appear to me to be truly in your proper disposition (prakṛti), agreeable, and indeed fearful of Dhanañjaya (Arjuna).'

sanskrit: पश्य बाह्वोर्बलं मेऽद्य शिक्षितस्य च पश्य मे ।एकोऽद्य निहनिष्यामि पाण्डवानां महाचमूम् ॥३४॥
transliteration: paśya bāhvorbalaṁ me'dya śikṣitasya ca paśya me ,eko'dya nihaniṣyāmi pāṇḍavānāṁ mahācamūm.
english: Today, behold the might of my arms, and behold me, the trained warrior! Today, I alone will destroy the vast army of the Pāṇḍavas.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 57 Verse 34. Today, behold the might of my arms, and behold me, the trained warrior! Today, I alone will destroy the vast army of the Pāṇḍavas.

sanskrit: कृष्णौ च पुरुषव्याघ्रौ तच्च सत्यं ब्रवीमि ते ।नाहत्वा युधि तौ वीरावपयास्ये कथंचन ॥३५॥
transliteration: kṛṣṇau ca puruṣavyāghrau tacca satyaṁ bravīmi te ,nāhatvā yudhi tau vīrāvapayāsye kathaṁcana.
english: I tell you this truth: I will certainly not retreat from battle by any means without having killed those two heroes, the two Krishnas, who are like tigers among men.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 57 Verse 35. I tell you this truth: I will certainly not retreat from battle by any means without having killed those two heroes, the two Krishnas, who are like tigers among men.

sanskrit: स्वप्स्ये वा निहतस्ताभ्यामसत्यो हि रणे जयः ।कृतार्थो वा भविष्यामि हत्वा तावथ वा हतः ॥३६॥
transliteration: svapsye vā nihatastābhyāmasatyo hi raṇe jayaḥ ,kṛtārtho vā bhaviṣyāmi hatvā tāvatha vā hataḥ.
english: Alternatively, I will die, slain by them; for indeed, victory (achieved by retreating) is meaningless in battle. Or, having slain those two, or having been slain myself, I will achieve my purpose.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 57 Verse 36. Alternatively, I will die, slain by them; for indeed, victory (achieved by retreating) is meaningless in battle. Or, having slain those two, or having been slain myself, I will achieve my purpose.

sanskrit: नैतादृशो जातु बभूव लोके रथोत्तमो यावदनुश्रुतं नः ।तमीदृशं प्रतियोत्स्यामि पार्थं महाहवे पश्य च पौरुषं मे ॥३७॥
transliteration: naitādṛśo jātu babhūva loke; rathottamo yāvadanuśrutaṁ naḥ ,tamīdṛśaṁ pratiyotsyāmi pārthaṁ; mahāhave paśya ca pauruṣaṁ me.
english: As far as we have heard, there has never existed in the world such an excellent charioteer. I will fight against that Arjuna, who is of such caliber, in this great battle. And behold my masculine prowess (puruṣa)!
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 57 Verse 37. As far as we have heard, there has never existed in the world such an excellent charioteer. I will fight against that Arjuna, who is of such caliber, in this great battle. And behold my masculine prowess (puruṣa)!

sanskrit: रथे चरत्येष रथप्रवीरः शीघ्रैर्हयैः कौरवराजपुत्रः ।स वाद्य मां नेष्यति कृच्छ्रमेतत्कर्णस्यान्तादेतदन्ताः स्थ सर्वे ॥३८॥
transliteration: rathe caratyeṣa rathapravīraḥ; śīghrairhayaiḥ kauravarājaputraḥ ,sa vādya māṁ neṣyati kṛcchrameta;tkarṇasyāntādetadantāḥ stha sarve.
english: This chief charioteer, the son of the Kuru king (Arjuna), moves in his chariot with swift horses. Indeed, today he will bring me to this great difficulty. (But beware!) all of you will meet your end after Karna's demise.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 57 Verse 38. This chief charioteer, the son of the Kuru king (Arjuna), moves in his chariot with swift horses. Indeed, today he will bring me to this great difficulty. (But beware!) all of you will meet your end after Karna's demise.

sanskrit: तमीदृशं वीर्यगुणोपपन्नं कृष्णद्वितीयं वरये रणाय ।अनन्तवीर्येण च केशवेन नारायणेनाप्रतिमेन गुप्तम् ॥४७॥
transliteration: tamīdṛśaṁ vīryaguṇopapannaṁ; kṛṣṇadvitīyaṁ varaye raṇāya ,anantavīryeṇa ca keśavena; nārāyaṇenāpratimena guptam.
english: I choose him for battle - such a one, endowed with valorous qualities, and accompanied by Kṛṣṇa. He is protected by the unrivaled Nārāyaṇa, who is Keśava and possesses infinite valor.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 57 Verse 47. I choose him for battle - such a one, endowed with valorous qualities, and accompanied by Kṛṣṇa. He is protected by the unrivaled Nārāyaṇa, who is Keśava and possesses infinite valor.

sanskrit: वर्षायुतैर्यस्य गुणा न शक्या वक्तुं समेतैरपि सर्वलोकैः ।महात्मनः शङ्खचक्रासिपाणेर्विष्णोर्जिष्णोर्वसुदेवात्मजस्य ।भयं मे वै जायते साध्वसं च दृष्ट्वा कृष्णावेकरथे समेतौ ॥४८॥
transliteration: varṣāyutairyasya guṇā na śakyā; vaktuṁ sametairapi sarvalokaiḥ ,mahātmanaḥ śaṅkhacakrāsipāṇe;rviṣṇorjiṣṇorvasudevātmajasya ,bhayaṁ me vai jāyate sādhvasaṁ ca; dṛṣṭvā kṛṣṇāvekarathe sametau.
english: Whose qualities cannot be fully described, even if all beings were to assemble and speak for tens of thousands of years - the qualities of the great-souled (mahātman), victorious (jiṣṇu) Viṣṇu, son of Vasudeva, who holds the conch, discus, and sword in his hand. Indeed, fear and terror arise in me upon seeing the two Kṛṣṇas (Kṛṣṇa and Arjuna) together in a single chariot.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 57 Verse 48. Whose qualities cannot be fully described, even if all beings were to assemble and speak for tens of thousands of years - the qualities of the great-souled (mahātman), victorious (jiṣṇu) Viṣṇu, son of Vasudeva, who holds the conch, discus, and sword in his hand. Indeed, fear and terror arise in me upon seeing the two Kṛṣṇas (Kṛṣṇa and Arjuna) together in a single chariot.

sanskrit: उभौ हि शूरौ कृतिनौ दृढास्त्रौ महारथौ संहननोपपन्नौ ।एतादृशौ फल्गुनवासुदेवौ कोऽन्यः प्रतीयान्मदृते नु शल्य ॥४९॥
transliteration: ubhau hi śūrau kṛtinau dṛḍhāstrau; mahārathau saṁhananopapannau ,etādṛśau phalgunavāsudevau; ko'nyaḥ pratīyānmadṛte nu śalya.
english: Indeed, both of them are heroes, skilled, firm in wielding weapons, great charioteers, and endowed with great strength. O Śalya, who else besides me would dare to confront such an Arjuna and Vāsudeva?
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 57 Verse 49. Indeed, both of them are heroes, skilled, firm in wielding weapons, great charioteers, and endowed with great strength. O Śalya, who else besides me would dare to confront such an Arjuna and Vāsudeva?

sanskrit: एतावहं युधि वा पातयिष्ये मां वा कृष्णौ निहनिष्यतोऽद्य ।इति ब्रुवञ्शल्यममित्रहन्ता कर्णो रणे मेघ इवोन्ननाद ॥५०॥
transliteration: etāvahaṁ yudhi vā pātayiṣye; māṁ vā kṛṣṇau nihaniṣyato'dya ,iti bruvañśalyamamitrahantā; karṇo raṇe megha ivonnanāda.
english: "Either I will fell these two in battle, or the two Kṛṣṇas will strike me down today!" Thus speaking to Śalya, Karṇa, the destroyer of enemies, roared in battle like a cloud.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 57 Verse 50. "Either I will fell these two in battle, or the two Kṛṣṇas will strike me down today!" Thus speaking to Śalya, Karṇa, the destroyer of enemies, roared in battle like a cloud.

sanskrit: ततोऽभिजघ्नुः कुपिताः परस्परं शरैस्तदाञ्जोगतिभिः सुतेजनैः ।कुरुप्रवीराः सह सृञ्जयैर्यथासुराः पुरा देववरैरयोधयन् ॥६७॥
transliteration: tato'bhijaghnuḥ kupitāḥ parasparaṁ; śaraistadāñjogatibhiḥ sutejanaiḥ ,kurupravīrāḥ saha sṛñjayairyathā;surāḥ purā devavarairayodhayan.
english: Then, enraged, the great warriors of the Kurus, along with the Sañjayas, struck each other with swiftly moving, well-sharpened arrows, just as the asuras formerly fought with the best of the gods.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 57 Verse 67. Then, enraged, the great warriors of the Kurus, along with the Sañjayas, struck each other with swiftly moving, well-sharpened arrows, just as the asuras formerly fought with the best of the gods.

sanskrit: जयेप्सवः स्वर्गमनाय चोत्सुकाः पतन्ति नागाश्वरथाः परंतप ।जगर्जुरुच्चैर्बलवच्च विव्यधुः शरैः सुमुक्तैरितरेतरं पृथक् ॥६८॥
transliteration: jayepsavaḥ svargamanāya cotsukāḥ; patanti nāgāśvarathāḥ paraṁtapa ,jagarjuruccairbalavacca vivyadhuḥ; śaraiḥ sumuktairitaretaraṁ pṛthak.
english: O scorcher of foes, desirous of victory and eager for attaining heaven, elephants, horses, and chariots fall. They roared loudly and powerfully, and pierced each other repeatedly with well-shot arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 57 Verse 68. O scorcher of foes, desirous of victory and eager for attaining heaven, elephants, horses, and chariots fall. They roared loudly and powerfully, and pierced each other repeatedly with well-shot arrows.

sanskrit: शरान्धकारे तु महात्मभिः कृते महामृधे योधवरैः परस्परम् ।बभुर्दशाशा न दिवं च पार्थिव प्रभा च सूर्यस्य तमोवृताभवत् ॥६९॥
transliteration: śarāndhakāre tu mahātmabhiḥ kṛte; mahāmṛdhe yodhavaraiḥ parasparam ,babhurdaśāśā na divaṁ ca pārthiva; prabhā ca sūryasya tamovṛtābhavat.
english: O King, in that great battle, when a darkness of arrows was created by the great-souled ones and the best of warriors striking each other, the ten directions did not shine, nor did the sky, and the light of the sun became covered with darkness.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 57 Verse 69. O King, in that great battle, when a darkness of arrows was created by the great-souled ones and the best of warriors striking each other, the ten directions did not shine, nor did the sky, and the light of the sun became covered with darkness.

sanskrit: संजय उवाच ।राजन्कुरूणां प्रवरैर्बलैर्भीममभिद्रुतम् ।मज्जन्तमिव कौन्तेयमुज्जिहीर्षुर्धनंजयः ॥१॥
transliteration: saṁjaya uvāca ,rājankurūṇāṁ pravarairbalairbhīmamabhidrutam ,majjantamiva kaunteyamujjihīrṣurdhanaṁjayaḥ.
english: Sañjaya said: O King, Dhanañjaya (Arjuna), desiring to rescue the son of Kunti (Bhīma), who was attacked by the best of the Kuru warriors and seemed to be sinking (into distress).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 58 Verse 1. Sañjaya said: O King, Dhanañjaya (Arjuna), desiring to rescue the son of Kunti (Bhīma), who was attacked by the best of the Kuru warriors and seemed to be sinking (into distress).

sanskrit: विमृद्य सूतपुत्रस्य सेनां भारत सायकैः ।प्राहिणोन्मृत्युलोकाय परवीरान्धनंजयः ॥२॥
transliteration: vimṛdya sūtaputrasya senāṁ bhārata sāyakaiḥ ,prāhiṇonmṛtyulokāya paravīrāndhanaṁjayaḥ.
english: O Bhārata, after crushing the army of Sūta's son (Karṇa) with his arrows, Dhanaṃjaya (Arjuna) sent the enemy heroes to the world of death.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 58 Verse 2. O Bhārata, after crushing the army of Sūta's son (Karṇa) with his arrows, Dhanaṃjaya (Arjuna) sent the enemy heroes to the world of death.

sanskrit: ततोऽस्याम्बरमावृत्य शरजालानि भागशः ।अदृश्यन्त तथान्ये च निघ्नन्तस्तव वाहिनीम् ॥३॥
transliteration: tato'syāmbaramāvṛtya śarajālāni bhāgaśaḥ ,adṛśyanta tathānye ca nighnantastava vāhinīm.
english: Then, his (Arjuna's) barrages of arrows were seen covering the sky in all directions; and similarly, other warriors were also seen striking down your army.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 58 Verse 3. Then, his (Arjuna's) barrages of arrows were seen covering the sky in all directions; and similarly, other warriors were also seen striking down your army.

sanskrit: स पक्षिसंघाचरितमाकाशं पूरयञ्शरैः ।धनंजयो महाराज कुरूणामन्तकोऽभवत् ॥४॥
transliteration: sa pakṣisaṁghācaritamākāśaṁ pūrayañśaraiḥ ,dhanaṁjayo mahārāja kurūṇāmantako'bhavat.
english: O great king, that Dhanaṃjaya (Arjuna), filling with arrows the sky - which is naturally traversed by flocks of birds - became the destroyer of the Kurus.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 58 Verse 4. O great king, that Dhanaṃjaya (Arjuna), filling with arrows the sky - which is naturally traversed by flocks of birds - became the destroyer of the Kurus.

sanskrit: ततो भल्लैः क्षुरप्रैश्च नाराचैर्निर्मलैरपि ।गात्राणि प्राक्षिणोत्पार्थः शिरांसि च चकर्त ह ॥५॥
transliteration: tato bhallaiḥ kṣurapraiśca nārācairnirmalairapi ,gātrāṇi prākṣiṇotpārthaḥ śirāṁsi ca cakarta ha.
english: Then, Pārtha (Arjuna) pierced bodies with broad-headed arrows, razor-sharp arrows, and even gleaming iron arrows; and indeed, he severed heads.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 58 Verse 5. Then, Pārtha (Arjuna) pierced bodies with broad-headed arrows, razor-sharp arrows, and even gleaming iron arrows; and indeed, he severed heads.

sanskrit: छिन्नगात्रैर्विकवचैर्विशिरस्कैः समन्ततः ।पतितैश्च पतद्भिश्च योधैरासीत्समावृतम् ॥६॥
transliteration: chinnagātrairvikavacairviśiraskaiḥ samantataḥ ,patitaiśca patadbhiśca yodhairāsītsamāvṛtam.
english: It was covered all around by warriors, both fallen and falling, with their limbs severed, armors broken, and heads detached.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 58 Verse 6. It was covered all around by warriors, both fallen and falling, with their limbs severed, armors broken, and heads detached.

sanskrit: धनंजयशराभ्यस्तैः स्यन्दनाश्वनरद्विपैः ।रणभूमिरभूद्राजन्महावैतरणी यथा ॥७॥
transliteration: dhanaṁjayaśarābhyastaiḥ syandanāśvanaradvipaiḥ ,raṇabhūmirabhūdrājanmahāvaitaraṇī yathā.
english: O King, the battlefield became like the great river Vaitaraṇī, filled with chariots, horses, men, and elephants assailed by Arjuna's (dhanañjaya) arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 58 Verse 7. O King, the battlefield became like the great river Vaitaraṇī, filled with chariots, horses, men, and elephants assailed by Arjuna's (dhanañjaya) arrows.

sanskrit: ईषाचक्राक्षभङ्गैश्च व्यश्वैः साश्वैश्च युध्यताम् ।ससूतैर्हतसूतैश्च रथैः स्तीर्णाभवन्मही ॥८॥
transliteration: īṣācakrākṣabhaṅgaiśca vyaśvaiḥ sāśvaiśca yudhyatām ,sasūtairhatasūtaiśca rathaiḥ stīrṇābhavanmahī.
english: The earth became strewn with chariots of those fighting – some with broken poles, wheels, and axles, some without horses, some still with horses, some with charioteers (sūta), and some with their charioteers (sūta) slain.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 58 Verse 8. The earth became strewn with chariots of those fighting – some with broken poles, wheels, and axles, some without horses, some still with horses, some with charioteers (sūta), and some with their charioteers (sūta) slain.

sanskrit: सुवर्णवर्मसंनाहैर्योधैः कनकभूषणैः ।आस्थिताः कृतवर्माणो भद्रा नित्यमदा द्विपाः ।क्रुद्धाः क्रुद्धैर्महामात्रैः प्रेषितार्जुनमभ्ययुः ॥९॥
transliteration: suvarṇavarmasaṁnāhairyodhaiḥ kanakabhūṣaṇaiḥ ,āsthitāḥ kṛtavarmāṇo bhadrā nityamadā dvipāḥ ,kruddhāḥ kruddhairmahāmātraiḥ preṣitārjunamabhyayuḥ.
english: Armored, noble, and ever-rutting elephants (dvipa), mounted by warriors adorned with golden armor and golden ornaments, were enraged and, impelled by their equally enraged mahouts, charged towards Arjuna.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 58 Verse 9. Armored, noble, and ever-rutting elephants (dvipa), mounted by warriors adorned with golden armor and golden ornaments, were enraged and, impelled by their equally enraged mahouts, charged towards Arjuna.

sanskrit: चतुःशताः शरवर्षैर्हताः पेतुः किरीटिना ।पर्यस्तानीव शृङ्गाणि ससत्त्वानि महागिरेः ॥१०॥
transliteration: catuḥśatāḥ śaravarṣairhatāḥ petuḥ kirīṭinā ,paryastānīva śṛṅgāṇi sasattvāni mahāgireḥ.
english: Four hundred [warriors], struck by showers of arrows from the crowned one (Arjuna), fell, just as the peaks of a great mountain, complete with their living beings, are overthrown.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 58 Verse 10. Four hundred [warriors], struck by showers of arrows from the crowned one (Arjuna), fell, just as the peaks of a great mountain, complete with their living beings, are overthrown.

sanskrit: धनंजयशराभ्यस्तैः स्तीर्णा भूर्वरवारणैः ।अभिपेदेऽर्जुनरथो घनान्भिन्दन्निवांशुमान् ॥११॥
transliteration: dhanaṁjayaśarābhyastaiḥ stīrṇā bhūrvaravāraṇaiḥ ,abhipede'rjunaratho ghanānbhindannivāṁśumān.
english: The earth was covered with excellent elephants that had been struck by Dhananjaya's (Arjuna's) arrows. Arjuna's chariot advanced, like the sun piercing through clouds.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 58 Verse 11. The earth was covered with excellent elephants that had been struck by Dhananjaya's (Arjuna's) arrows. Arjuna's chariot advanced, like the sun piercing through clouds.

sanskrit: हतैर्गजमनुष्याश्वैर्भग्नैश्च बहुधा रथैः ।विशस्त्रपत्रकवचैर्युद्धशौण्डैर्गतासुभिः ।अपविद्धायुधैर्मार्गः स्तीर्णोऽभूत्फल्गुनेन वै ॥१२॥
transliteration: hatairgajamanuṣyāśvairbhagnaiśca bahudhā rathaiḥ ,viśastrapatrakavacairyuddhaśauṇḍairgatāsubhiḥ ,apaviddhāyudhairmārgaḥ stīrṇo'bhūtphalgunena vai.
english: Indeed, the path was strewn by Phalguna (Arjuna) with slain elephants, men, and horses; with chariots shattered in various ways; and with battle-hardened warriors who were disarmed, whose armor-plates and armors were destroyed, whose lives had departed, and who had cast aside their weapons.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 58 Verse 12. Indeed, the path was strewn by Phalguna (Arjuna) with slain elephants, men, and horses; with chariots shattered in various ways; and with battle-hardened warriors who were disarmed, whose armor-plates and armors were destroyed, whose lives had departed, and who had cast aside their weapons.

sanskrit: व्यस्फूर्जयच्च गाण्डीवं सुमहद्भैरवस्वनम् ।घोरो वज्रविनिष्पेषः स्तनयित्नोरिवाम्बरे ॥१३॥
transliteration: vyasphūrjayacca gāṇḍīvaṁ sumahadbhairavasvanam ,ghoro vajraviniṣpeṣaḥ stanayitnorivāmbare.
english: And he twanged the Gāṇḍīva (Arjuna's bow), which was exceedingly great and produced a terrifying sound, like the dreadful crash of a thunderbolt (vajra) or the roar of thunder in the sky.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 58 Verse 13. And he twanged the Gāṇḍīva (Arjuna's bow), which was exceedingly great and produced a terrifying sound, like the dreadful crash of a thunderbolt (vajra) or the roar of thunder in the sky.

sanskrit: ततः प्रादीर्यत चमूर्धनंजयशराहता ।महावातसमाविद्धा महानौरिव सागरे ॥१४॥
transliteration: tataḥ prādīryata camūrdhanaṁjayaśarāhatā ,mahāvātasamāviddhā mahānauriva sāgare.
english: Then, struck by Arjuna's arrows, the army was scattered, just like a great ship in the ocean is tossed by a strong wind.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 58 Verse 14. Then, struck by Arjuna's arrows, the army was scattered, just like a great ship in the ocean is tossed by a strong wind.

sanskrit: नानारूपाः प्रहरणाः शरा गाण्डीवचोदिताः ।अलातोल्काशनिप्रख्यास्तव सैन्यं विनिर्दहन् ॥१५॥
transliteration: nānārūpāḥ praharaṇāḥ śarā gāṇḍīvacoditāḥ ,alātolkāśaniprakhyāstava sainyaṁ vinirdahan.
english: The arrows, released from the Gāṇḍīva bow, were of various forms – resembling firebrands, meteors, and thunderbolts – completely burning up your army.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 58 Verse 15. The arrows, released from the Gāṇḍīva bow, were of various forms – resembling firebrands, meteors, and thunderbolts – completely burning up your army.

sanskrit: महागिरौ वेणुवनं निशि प्रज्वलितं यथा ।तथा तव महत्सैन्यं प्रास्फुरच्छरपीडितम् ॥१६॥
transliteration: mahāgirau veṇuvanaṁ niśi prajvalitaṁ yathā ,tathā tava mahatsainyaṁ prāsphuraccharapīḍitam.
english: Just as a bamboo forest blazes on a great mountain at night, so too your vast army throbbed and quivered, afflicted by arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 58 Verse 16. Just as a bamboo forest blazes on a great mountain at night, so too your vast army throbbed and quivered, afflicted by arrows.

sanskrit: संपिष्टदग्धविध्वस्तं तव सैन्यं किरीटिना ।हतं प्रविहतं बाणैः सर्वतः प्रद्रुतं दिशः ॥१७॥
transliteration: saṁpiṣṭadagdhavidhvastaṁ tava sainyaṁ kirīṭinā ,hataṁ pravihataṁ bāṇaiḥ sarvataḥ pradrutaṁ diśaḥ.
english: Your army, crushed, burned, and utterly destroyed by Arjuna (kirīṭin), was struck down and completely routed by his arrows, fleeing in all directions.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 58 Verse 17. Your army, crushed, burned, and utterly destroyed by Arjuna (kirīṭin), was struck down and completely routed by his arrows, fleeing in all directions.

sanskrit: महावने मृगगणा दावाग्निग्रसिता यथा ।कुरवः पर्यवर्तन्त निर्दग्धाः सव्यसाचिना ॥१८॥
transliteration: mahāvane mṛgagaṇā dāvāgnigrasitā yathā ,kuravaḥ paryavartanta nirdagdhāḥ savyasācinā.
english: Just as herds of deer in a great forest are consumed by a forest fire, so the Kurus were routed, utterly destroyed by Savyasācin (Arjuna).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 58 Verse 18. Just as herds of deer in a great forest are consumed by a forest fire, so the Kurus were routed, utterly destroyed by Savyasācin (Arjuna).

sanskrit: उत्सृज्य हि महाबाहुं भीमसेनं तदा रणे ।बलं कुरूणामुद्विग्नं सर्वमासीत्पराङ्मुखम् ॥१९॥
transliteration: utsṛjya hi mahābāhuṁ bhīmasenaṁ tadā raṇe ,balaṁ kurūṇāmudvignaṁ sarvamāsītparāṅmukham.
english: Indeed, after abandoning the mighty-armed Bhīmasena in that battle, the entire army of the Kurus became agitated and turned away in retreat.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 58 Verse 19. Indeed, after abandoning the mighty-armed Bhīmasena in that battle, the entire army of the Kurus became agitated and turned away in retreat.

sanskrit: ततः कुरुषु भग्नेषु बीभत्सुरपराजितः ।भीमसेनं समासाद्य मुहूर्तं सोऽभ्यवर्तत ॥२०॥
transliteration: tataḥ kuruṣu bhagneṣu bībhatsuraparājitaḥ ,bhīmasenaṁ samāsādya muhūrtaṁ so'bhyavartata.
english: Then, when the Kurus were defeated, the undefeated Bibhatsu (Arjuna), having approached Bhīmasena, returned to him for a moment.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 58 Verse 20. Then, when the Kurus were defeated, the undefeated Bibhatsu (Arjuna), having approached Bhīmasena, returned to him for a moment.

sanskrit: समागम्य स भीमेन मन्त्रयित्वा च फल्गुनः ।विशल्यमरुजं चास्मै कथयित्वा युधिष्ठिरम् ॥२१॥
transliteration: samāgamya sa bhīmena mantrayitvā ca phalgunaḥ ,viśalyamarujaṁ cāsmai kathayitvā yudhiṣṭhiram.
english: Having met with Bhīmasena and consulted with him, Phalguna (Arjuna) then informed him (Bhīmasena) about Yudhiṣṭhira, who was free of arrows (unwounded) and free of pain (healthy).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 58 Verse 21. Having met with Bhīmasena and consulted with him, Phalguna (Arjuna) then informed him (Bhīmasena) about Yudhiṣṭhira, who was free of arrows (unwounded) and free of pain (healthy).

sanskrit: भीमसेनाभ्यनुज्ञातस्ततः प्रायाद्धनंजयः ।नादयन्रथघोषेण पृथिवीं द्यां च भारत ॥२२॥
transliteration: bhīmasenābhyanujñātastataḥ prāyāddhanaṁjayaḥ ,nādayanrathaghoṣeṇa pṛthivīṁ dyāṁ ca bhārata.
english: O Bhārata, having been granted permission by Bhimasena, Dhananjaya then proceeded, making the earth and the sky resound with the roar of his chariot.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 58 Verse 22. O Bhārata, having been granted permission by Bhimasena, Dhananjaya then proceeded, making the earth and the sky resound with the roar of his chariot.

sanskrit: ततः परिवृतो भीमैर्दशभिः शत्रुपुंगवैः ।दुःशासनादवरजैस्तव पुत्रैर्धनंजयः ॥२३॥
transliteration: tataḥ parivṛto bhīmairdaśabhiḥ śatrupuṁgavaiḥ ,duḥśāsanādavarajaistava putrairdhanaṁjayaḥ.
english: Then Dhananjaya was surrounded by your ten formidable sons, the best among enemies, who were younger than Duḥśāsana.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 58 Verse 23. Then Dhananjaya was surrounded by your ten formidable sons, the best among enemies, who were younger than Duḥśāsana.

sanskrit: ते तमभ्यर्दयन्बाणैरुल्काभिरिव कुञ्जरम् ।आततेष्वसनाः क्रूरा नृत्यन्त इव भारत ॥२४॥
transliteration: te tamabhyardayanbāṇairulkābhiriva kuñjaram ,ātateṣvasanāḥ krūrā nṛtyanta iva bhārata.
english: O Bhārata, those cruel ones, with their bows strung, attacked him with arrows as if (they were attacking) an elephant with flaming torches, appearing as if they were dancing.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 58 Verse 24. O Bhārata, those cruel ones, with their bows strung, attacked him with arrows as if (they were attacking) an elephant with flaming torches, appearing as if they were dancing.

sanskrit: अपसव्यांस्तु तांश्चक्रे रथेन मधुसूदनः ।ततस्ते प्राद्रवञ्शूराः पराङ्मुखरथेऽर्जुने ॥२५॥
transliteration: apasavyāṁstu tāṁścakre rathena madhusūdanaḥ ,tataste prādravañśūrāḥ parāṅmukharathe'rjune.
english: But Madhusudana (Krishna) drove his chariot around them, leaving them on his left side. Then, as Arjuna's chariot turned away, those heroes (the Kauravas) fled.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 58 Verse 25. But Madhusudana (Krishna) drove his chariot around them, leaving them on his left side. Then, as Arjuna's chariot turned away, those heroes (the Kauravas) fled.

sanskrit: तेषामापततां केतून्रथांश्चापानि सायकान् ।नाराचैरर्धचन्द्रैश्च क्षिप्रं पार्थो न्यपातयत् ॥२६॥
transliteration: teṣāmāpatatāṁ ketūnrathāṁścāpāni sāyakān ,nārācairardhacandraiśca kṣipraṁ pārtho nyapātayat.
english: Arjuna (Pārtha) swiftly struck down the banners, chariots, bows, and arrows of those who were attacking, using both iron-tipped and crescent-shaped arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 58 Verse 26. Arjuna (Pārtha) swiftly struck down the banners, chariots, bows, and arrows of those who were attacking, using both iron-tipped and crescent-shaped arrows.

sanskrit: अथान्यैर्दशभिर्भल्लैः शिरांस्येषां न्यपातयत् ।रोषसंरक्तनेत्राणि संदष्टौष्ठानि भूतले ।तानि वक्त्राणि विबभुर्व्योम्नि तारागणा इव ॥२७॥
transliteration: athānyairdaśabhirbhallaiḥ śirāṁsyeṣāṁ nyapātayat ,roṣasaṁraktanetrāṇi saṁdaṣṭauṣṭhāni bhūtale ,tāni vaktrāṇi vibabhurvyomni tārāgaṇā iva.
english: Then, with ten more broad-headed arrows, he struck down their heads onto the ground – heads that had eyes reddened with anger and lips bitten. Those faces, fallen on the ground, shone like clusters of stars in the sky.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 58 Verse 27. Then, with ten more broad-headed arrows, he struck down their heads onto the ground – heads that had eyes reddened with anger and lips bitten. Those faces, fallen on the ground, shone like clusters of stars in the sky.

sanskrit: तांस्तु भल्लैर्महावेगैर्दशभिर्दश कौरवान् ।रुक्माङ्गदान्रुक्मपुङ्खैर्विद्ध्वा प्रायादमित्रहा ॥२८॥
transliteration: tāṁstu bhallairmahāvegairdaśabhirdaśa kauravān ,rukmāṅgadānrukmapuṅkhairviddhvā prāyādamitrahā.
english: But Arjuna, the slayer of enemies (amitra-hā), having pierced those ten Kauravas, who were wearing golden armlets, with ten exceedingly swift, broad-headed arrows that had golden fletching, then advanced.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 58 Verse 28. But Arjuna, the slayer of enemies (amitra-hā), having pierced those ten Kauravas, who were wearing golden armlets, with ten exceedingly swift, broad-headed arrows that had golden fletching, then advanced.

sanskrit: संजय उवाच ।तं तु यान्तं महावेगैरश्वैः कपिवरध्वजम् ।युद्धायाभ्यद्रवन्वीराः कुरूणां नवती रथाः ।परिवव्रुर्नरव्याघ्रा नरव्याघ्रं रणेऽर्जुनम् ॥१॥
transliteration: saṁjaya uvāca ,taṁ tu yāntaṁ mahāvegairaśvaiḥ kapivaradhvajam ,yuddhāyābhyadravanvīrāḥ kurūṇāṁ navatī rathāḥ ,parivavrurnaravyāghrā naravyāghraṁ raṇe'rjunam.
english: Sañjaya said: Then, ninety valiant charioteers of the Kurus, those tiger-like men (nara-vyāghrāḥ), rushed towards Arjuna, whose banner bore the best of monkeys (kapi-vara-dhvajam), as he advanced swiftly with his horses for battle. These tiger-like men (nara-vyāghrāḥ) then surrounded that tiger-like man (nara-vyāghram), Arjuna, in the fight.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 59 Verse 1. Sañjaya said: Then, ninety valiant charioteers of the Kurus, those tiger-like men (nara-vyāghrāḥ), rushed towards Arjuna, whose banner bore the best of monkeys (kapi-vara-dhvajam), as he advanced swiftly with his horses for battle. These tiger-like men (nara-vyāghrāḥ) then surrounded that tiger-like man (nara-vyāghram), Arjuna, in the fight.

sanskrit: कृष्णः श्वेतान्महावेगानश्वान्कनकभूषणान् ।मुक्ताजालप्रतिच्छन्नान्प्रैषीत्कर्णरथं प्रति ॥२॥
transliteration: kṛṣṇaḥ śvetānmahāvegānaśvānkanakabhūṣaṇān ,muktājālapraticchannānpraiṣītkarṇarathaṁ prati.
english: Krishna sent the very swift, white horses, adorned with golden ornaments and covered with nets of pearls, towards Karna's chariot.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 59 Verse 2. Krishna sent the very swift, white horses, adorned with golden ornaments and covered with nets of pearls, towards Karna's chariot.

sanskrit: ततः कर्णरथं यान्तमरीन्घ्नन्तं धनंजयम् ।बाणवर्षैरभिघ्नन्तः संशप्तकरथा ययुः ॥३॥
transliteration: tataḥ karṇarathaṁ yāntamarīnghnantaṁ dhanaṁjayam ,bāṇavarṣairabhighnantaḥ saṁśaptakarathā yayuḥ.
english: Then, the Saṃśaptaka warriors, striking with showers of arrows, advanced towards Karna's chariot, attacking Dhananjaya, who was himself proceeding there and slaying enemies.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 59 Verse 3. Then, the Saṃśaptaka warriors, striking with showers of arrows, advanced towards Karna's chariot, attacking Dhananjaya, who was himself proceeding there and slaying enemies.

sanskrit: त्वरमाणांस्तु तान्सर्वान्ससूतेष्वसनध्वजान् ।जघान नवतिं वीरानर्जुनो निशितैः शरैः ॥४॥
transliteration: tvaramāṇāṁstu tānsarvānsasūteṣvasanadhvajān ,jaghāna navatiṁ vīrānarjuno niśitaiḥ śaraiḥ.
english: But Arjuna, with sharp arrows, killed ninety of all those swift warriors who were equipped with charioteers, bows, and banners.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 59 Verse 4. But Arjuna, with sharp arrows, killed ninety of all those swift warriors who were equipped with charioteers, bows, and banners.

sanskrit: तेऽपतन्त हता बाणैर्नानारूपैः किरीटिना ।सविमाना यथा सिद्धाः स्वर्गात्पुण्यक्षये तथा ॥५॥
transliteration: te'patanta hatā bāṇairnānārūpaiḥ kirīṭinā ,savimānā yathā siddhāḥ svargātpuṇyakṣaye tathā.
english: They fell, slain by Arjuna, the wearer of the diadem, with arrows of various types, just as perfected beings (siddhas) fall from heaven with their celestial chariots upon the exhaustion of their merit.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 59 Verse 5. They fell, slain by Arjuna, the wearer of the diadem, with arrows of various types, just as perfected beings (siddhas) fall from heaven with their celestial chariots upon the exhaustion of their merit.

sanskrit: ततः सरथनागाश्वाः कुरवः कुरुसत्तम ।निर्भया भरतश्रेष्ठमभ्यवर्तन्त फल्गुनम् ॥६॥
transliteration: tataḥ sarathanāgāśvāḥ kuravaḥ kurusattama ,nirbhayā bharataśreṣṭhamabhyavartanta phalgunam.
english: O best of the Kurus, then the fearless Kurus, with their chariots, elephants, and horses, advanced upon Arjuna (Phalguna), the best of the Bharatas.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 59 Verse 6. O best of the Kurus, then the fearless Kurus, with their chariots, elephants, and horses, advanced upon Arjuna (Phalguna), the best of the Bharatas.

sanskrit: तदायस्तममुक्तास्त्रमुदीर्णवरवारणम् ।पुत्राणां ते महत्सैन्यं समरौत्सीद्धनंजयः ॥७॥
transliteration: tadāyastamamuktāstramudīrṇavaravāraṇam ,putrāṇāṁ te mahatsainyaṁ samarautsīddhanaṁjayaḥ.
english: Dhananjaya (Arjuna) then completely checked that great army of your sons, an army which was mighty, had excellent, aroused elephants, and had not yet released its weapons.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 59 Verse 7. Dhananjaya (Arjuna) then completely checked that great army of your sons, an army which was mighty, had excellent, aroused elephants, and had not yet released its weapons.

sanskrit: शक्त्यृष्टितोमरप्रासैर्गदानिस्त्रिंशसायकैः ।प्राच्छादयन्महेष्वासाः कुरवः कुरुनन्दनम् ॥८॥
transliteration: śaktyṛṣṭitomaraprāsairgadānistriṁśasāyakaiḥ ,prācchādayanmaheṣvāsāḥ kuravaḥ kurunandanam.
english: The great archer Kurus, using spears, javelins, tomara lances, barbed missiles, maces, swords, and arrows, covered (overwhelmed) Arjuna (Kuru's delight).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 59 Verse 8. The great archer Kurus, using spears, javelins, tomara lances, barbed missiles, maces, swords, and arrows, covered (overwhelmed) Arjuna (Kuru's delight).

sanskrit: तां कुरूणां प्रविततां शस्त्रवृष्टिं समुद्यताम् ।व्यधमत्पाण्डवो बाणैस्तमः सूर्य इवांशुभिः ॥९॥
transliteration: tāṁ kurūṇāṁ pravitatāṁ śastravṛṣṭiṁ samudyatām ,vyadhamatpāṇḍavo bāṇaistamaḥ sūrya ivāṁśubhiḥ.
english: The son of Pandu (Arjuna) dispelled that vast shower of weapons, which the Kurus had unleashed, with his arrows, just as the sun dispels darkness with its rays.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 59 Verse 9. The son of Pandu (Arjuna) dispelled that vast shower of weapons, which the Kurus had unleashed, with his arrows, just as the sun dispels darkness with its rays.

sanskrit: ततो म्लेच्छाः स्थितैर्मत्तैस्त्रयोदशशतैर्गजैः ।पार्श्वतोऽभ्यहनन्पार्थं तव पुत्रस्य शासनात् ॥१०॥
transliteration: tato mlecchāḥ sthitairmattaistrayodaśaśatairgajaiḥ ,pārśvato'bhyahananpārthaṁ tava putrasya śāsanāt.
english: Then, by your son's command, the Mlecchas, along with thirteen hundred maddened elephants, attacked Arjuna (pārtham) from the side.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 59 Verse 10. Then, by your son's command, the Mlecchas, along with thirteen hundred maddened elephants, attacked Arjuna (pārtham) from the side.

sanskrit: कर्णिनालीकनाराचैस्तोमरैः प्रासशक्तिभिः ।कम्पनैर्भिण्डिपालैश्च रथस्थं पार्थमार्दयन् ॥११॥
transliteration: karṇinālīkanārācaistomaraiḥ prāsaśaktibhiḥ ,kampanairbhiṇḍipālaiśca rathasthaṁ pārthamārdayan.
english: They tormented Arjuna (pārtham), who was standing on his chariot, with sharp-pointed arrows, hollow arrows, iron arrows, javelins, spears, lances, Kampanas (missiles), and Bhindipalas (projectiles).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 59 Verse 11. They tormented Arjuna (pārtham), who was standing on his chariot, with sharp-pointed arrows, hollow arrows, iron arrows, javelins, spears, lances, Kampanas (missiles), and Bhindipalas (projectiles).

sanskrit: तामस्त्रवृष्टिं प्रहितां द्विपस्थैर्यवनैः स्मयन् ।चिच्छेद निशितैर्भल्लैरर्धचन्द्रैश्च फल्गुनः ॥१२॥
transliteration: tāmastravṛṣṭiṁ prahitāṁ dvipasthairyavanaiḥ smayan ,ciccheda niśitairbhallairardhacandraiśca phalgunaḥ.
english: Smiling, Arjuna (phalgunaḥ) cut down that shower of weapons (astravṛṣṭim) which had been dispatched by the Yavanas positioned on elephants, using sharp arrows and crescent-shaped arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 59 Verse 12. Smiling, Arjuna (phalgunaḥ) cut down that shower of weapons (astravṛṣṭim) which had been dispatched by the Yavanas positioned on elephants, using sharp arrows and crescent-shaped arrows.

sanskrit: अथ तान्द्विरदान्सर्वान्नानालिङ्गैर्महाशरैः ।सपताकान्सहारोहान्गिरीन्वज्रैरिवाभिनत् ॥१३॥
transliteration: atha tāndviradānsarvānnānāliṅgairmahāśaraiḥ ,sapatākānsahārohāngirīnvajrairivābhinat.
english: Then, with mighty arrows of various kinds, he shattered all those elephants, along with their banners and riders, just as (Indra) would split mountains with thunderbolts.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 59 Verse 13. Then, with mighty arrows of various kinds, he shattered all those elephants, along with their banners and riders, just as (Indra) would split mountains with thunderbolts.

sanskrit: ते हेमपुङ्खैरिषुभिराचिता हेममालिनः ।हताः पेतुर्महानागाः साग्निज्वाला इवाद्रयः ॥१४॥
transliteration: te hemapuṅkhairiṣubhirācitā hemamālinaḥ ,hatāḥ peturmahānāgāḥ sāgnijvālā ivādrayaḥ.
english: Pierced by golden-shafted arrows and adorned with golden garlands, those great elephants fell, just like mountains ablaze with fire.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 59 Verse 14. Pierced by golden-shafted arrows and adorned with golden garlands, those great elephants fell, just like mountains ablaze with fire.

sanskrit: ततो गाण्डीवनिर्घोषो महानासीद्विशां पते ।स्तनतां कूजतां चैव मनुष्यगजवाजिनाम् ॥१५॥
transliteration: tato gāṇḍīvanirghoṣo mahānāsīdviśāṁ pate ,stanatāṁ kūjatāṁ caiva manuṣyagajavājinām.
english: Then, O Lord of the people, there was a great roar from Gaṇḍīva (Arjuna's bow), amidst the trumpeting and cries of men, elephants, and horses.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 59 Verse 15. Then, O Lord of the people, there was a great roar from Gaṇḍīva (Arjuna's bow), amidst the trumpeting and cries of men, elephants, and horses.

sanskrit: कुञ्जराश्च हता राजन्प्राद्रवंस्ते समन्ततः ।अश्वाश्च पर्यधावन्त हतारोहा दिशो दश ॥१६॥
transliteration: kuñjarāśca hatā rājanprādravaṁste samantataḥ ,aśvāśca paryadhāvanta hatārohā diśo daśa.
english: O King, the struck-down elephants fled in all directions. And the horses, whose riders had been killed, ran about wildly in all ten directions.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 59 Verse 16. O King, the struck-down elephants fled in all directions. And the horses, whose riders had been killed, ran about wildly in all ten directions.

sanskrit: रथा हीना महाराज रथिभिर्वाजिभिस्तथा ।गन्धर्वनगराकारा दृश्यन्ते स्म सहस्रशः ॥१७॥
transliteration: rathā hīnā mahārāja rathibhirvājibhistathā ,gandharvanagarākārā dṛśyante sma sahasraśaḥ.
english: O great king, thousands of chariots, abandoned by their charioteers and horses, were seen appearing like illusory cities of Gandharvas (mirages).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 59 Verse 17. O great king, thousands of chariots, abandoned by their charioteers and horses, were seen appearing like illusory cities of Gandharvas (mirages).

sanskrit: अश्वारोहा महाराज धावमानास्ततस्ततः ।तत्र तत्रैव दृश्यन्ते पतिताः पार्थसायकैः ॥१८॥
transliteration: aśvārohā mahārāja dhāvamānāstatastataḥ ,tatra tatraiva dṛśyante patitāḥ pārthasāyakaiḥ.
english: O great king, horsemen galloping here and there were seen fallen in those very spots by Arjuna's arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 59 Verse 18. O great king, horsemen galloping here and there were seen fallen in those very spots by Arjuna's arrows.

sanskrit: तस्मिन्क्षणे पाण्डवस्य बाह्वोर्बलमदृश्यत ।यत्सादिनो वारणांश्च रथांश्चैकोऽजयद्युधि ॥१९॥
transliteration: tasminkṣaṇe pāṇḍavasya bāhvorbalamadṛśyata ,yatsādino vāraṇāṁśca rathāṁścaiko'jayadyudhi.
english: At that very moment, the strength of the Pāṇḍava's arms became visible, for he alone conquered horsemen, elephants, and chariots in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 59 Verse 19. At that very moment, the strength of the Pāṇḍava's arms became visible, for he alone conquered horsemen, elephants, and chariots in battle.

sanskrit: ततस्त्र्यङ्गेण महता बलेन भरतर्षभ ।दृष्ट्वा परिवृतं राजन्भीमसेनः किरीटिनम् ॥२०॥
transliteration: tatastryaṅgeṇa mahatā balena bharatarṣabha ,dṛṣṭvā parivṛtaṁ rājanbhīmasenaḥ kirīṭinam.
english: Then, O best of Bharatas, O king, having seen the crowned one (Arjuna) surrounded by a great three-limbed army, Bhīmasena (did something, continued in next verse)...
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 59 Verse 20. Then, O best of Bharatas, O king, having seen the crowned one (Arjuna) surrounded by a great three-limbed army, Bhīmasena (did something, continued in next verse)...

sanskrit: हतावशेषानुत्सृज्य त्वदीयान्कतिचिद्रथान् ।जवेनाभ्यद्रवद्राजन्धनंजयरथं प्रति ॥२१॥
transliteration: hatāvaśeṣānutsṛjya tvadīyānkaticidrathān ,javenābhyadravadrājandhanaṁjayarathaṁ prati.
english: O king, Bhīmasena, abandoning the few remaining chariots that belonged to your side (which had survived the slaughter), swiftly rushed towards Dhananjaya's (Arjuna's) chariot.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 59 Verse 21. O king, Bhīmasena, abandoning the few remaining chariots that belonged to your side (which had survived the slaughter), swiftly rushed towards Dhananjaya's (Arjuna's) chariot.

sanskrit: ततस्तत्प्राद्रवत्सैन्यं हतभूयिष्ठमातुरम् ।दृष्ट्वा यदर्जुनं भीमो जगाम भ्रातरं प्रति ॥२२॥
transliteration: tatastatprādravatsainyaṁ hatabhūyiṣṭhamāturam ,dṛṣṭvā yadarjunaṁ bhīmo jagāma bhrātaraṁ prati.
english: Having seen that distressed army, which was mostly destroyed, fleeing because of Arjuna's (Arjuna) presence, Bhima (Bhīma) went towards his brother.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 59 Verse 22. Having seen that distressed army, which was mostly destroyed, fleeing because of Arjuna's (Arjuna) presence, Bhima (Bhīma) went towards his brother.

sanskrit: हतावशिष्टांस्तुरगानर्जुनेन महाजवान् ।भीमो व्यधमदभ्रान्तो गदापाणिर्महाहवे ॥२३॥
transliteration: hatāvaśiṣṭāṁsturagānarjunena mahājavān ,bhīmo vyadhamadabhrānto gadāpāṇirmahāhave.
english: Tireless and mace-in-hand, Bhima (Bhīma) scattered the very swift horses in the great battle, which had survived from the slaughter (hatāvaśiṣṭa) by Arjuna (Arjuna).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 59 Verse 23. Tireless and mace-in-hand, Bhima (Bhīma) scattered the very swift horses in the great battle, which had survived from the slaughter (hatāvaśiṣṭa) by Arjuna (Arjuna).

sanskrit: कालरात्रिमिवात्युग्रां नरनागाश्वभोजनाम् ।प्राकाराट्टपुरद्वारदारणीमतिदारुणाम् ॥२४॥
transliteration: kālarātrimivātyugrāṁ naranāgāśvabhojanām ,prākārāṭṭapuradvāradāraṇīmatidāruṇām.
english: (She was) like the extremely fierce night of destruction (kālarātri), devouring men, elephants, and horses, and tearing down battlements, watchtowers, and city gates; exceedingly dreadful.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 59 Verse 24. (She was) like the extremely fierce night of destruction (kālarātri), devouring men, elephants, and horses, and tearing down battlements, watchtowers, and city gates; exceedingly dreadful.

sanskrit: ततो गदां नृनागाश्वेष्वाशु भीमो व्यवासृजत् ।सा जघान बहूनश्वानश्वारोहांश्च मारिष ॥२५॥
transliteration: tato gadāṁ nṛnāgāśveṣvāśu bhīmo vyavāsṛjat ,sā jaghāna bahūnaśvānaśvārohāṁśca māriṣa.
english: Then Bhima (Bhīma) quickly hurled the mace (gadā) amidst the men, elephants, and horses. And, O venerable one (māriṣa), she (the mace) struck down many horses and horsemen.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 59 Verse 25. Then Bhima (Bhīma) quickly hurled the mace (gadā) amidst the men, elephants, and horses. And, O venerable one (māriṣa), she (the mace) struck down many horses and horsemen.

sanskrit: कांस्यायसतनुत्रांस्तान्नरानश्वांश्च पाण्डवः ।पोथयामास गदया सशब्दं तेऽपतन्हताः ॥२६॥
transliteration: kāṁsyāyasatanutrāṁstānnarānaśvāṁśca pāṇḍavaḥ ,pothayāmāsa gadayā saśabdaṁ te'patanhatāḥ.
english: The Pāṇḍava (Bhīma) struck down with his mace, making a loud sound, those men and horses who were clad in bronze and iron armors. They fell, slain.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 59 Verse 26. The Pāṇḍava (Bhīma) struck down with his mace, making a loud sound, those men and horses who were clad in bronze and iron armors. They fell, slain.

sanskrit: हत्वा तु तद्गजानीकं भीमसेनो महाबलः ।पुनः स्वरथमास्थाय पृष्ठतोऽर्जुनमन्वगात् ॥२७॥
transliteration: hatvā tu tadgajānīkaṁ bhīmaseno mahābalaḥ ,punaḥ svarathamāsthāya pṛṣṭhato'rjunamanvagāt.
english: Having indeed destroyed that elephant army, the mighty Bhīmasena again mounted his own chariot and followed Arjuna from behind.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 59 Verse 27. Having indeed destroyed that elephant army, the mighty Bhīmasena again mounted his own chariot and followed Arjuna from behind.

sanskrit: हतं पराङ्मुखप्रायं निरुत्साहं परं बलम् ।व्यालम्बत महाराज प्रायशः शस्त्रवेष्टितम् ॥२८॥
transliteration: hataṁ parāṅmukhaprāyaṁ nirutsāhaṁ paraṁ balam ,vyālambata mahārāja prāyaśaḥ śastraveṣṭitam.
english: O great king, the enemy army, mostly turning away, dispirited, covered with weapons, and largely destroyed, was wavering.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 59 Verse 28. O great king, the enemy army, mostly turning away, dispirited, covered with weapons, and largely destroyed, was wavering.

sanskrit: विलम्बमानं तत्सैन्यमप्रगल्भमवस्थितम् ।दृष्ट्वा प्राच्छादयद्बाणैरर्जुनः प्राणतापनैः ॥२९॥
transliteration: vilambamānaṁ tatsainyamapragalbhamavasthitam ,dṛṣṭvā prācchādayadbāṇairarjunaḥ prāṇatāpanaiḥ.
english: Having seen that hesitant, irresolute, and standing army, Arjuna overwhelmed it with his life-scorching arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 59 Verse 29. Having seen that hesitant, irresolute, and standing army, Arjuna overwhelmed it with his life-scorching arrows.

sanskrit: ततः कुरूणामभवदार्तनादो महामृधे ।रथाश्वनागासुहरैर्वध्यतामर्जुनेषुभिः ॥३०॥
transliteration: tataḥ kurūṇāmabhavadārtanādo mahāmṛdhe ,rathāśvanāgāsuharairvadhyatāmarjuneṣubhiḥ.
english: Then, in that great battle, a great cry of anguish arose from the Kurus. There was great slaughter by Arjuna's arrows, which were life-takers of chariots, horses, and elephants.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 59 Verse 30. Then, in that great battle, a great cry of anguish arose from the Kurus. There was great slaughter by Arjuna's arrows, which were life-takers of chariots, horses, and elephants.

sanskrit: हाहाकृतं भृशं तस्थौ लीयमानं परस्परम् ।अलातचक्रवत्सैन्यं तदाभ्रमत तावकम् ॥३१॥
transliteration: hāhākṛtaṁ bhṛśaṁ tasthau līyamānaṁ parasparam ,alātacakravatsainyaṁ tadābhramata tāvakam.
english: Your army then stood, crying out intensely in distress, clinging to one another, and whirled like a firebrand circle.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 59 Verse 31. Your army then stood, crying out intensely in distress, clinging to one another, and whirled like a firebrand circle.

sanskrit: आदीप्तं तव तत्सैन्यं शरैश्छिन्नतनुच्छदम् ।आसीत्स्वशोणितक्लिन्नं फुल्लाशोकवनं यथा ॥३२॥
transliteration: ādīptaṁ tava tatsainyaṁ śaraiśchinnatanucchadam ,āsītsvaśoṇitaklinnaṁ phullāśokavanaṁ yathā.
english: Your army, inflamed by arrows and with its armor shattered, became drenched in its own blood, like a blossoming Ashoka forest.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 59 Verse 32. Your army, inflamed by arrows and with its armor shattered, became drenched in its own blood, like a blossoming Ashoka forest.

sanskrit: तद्दृष्ट्वा कुरवस्तत्र विक्रान्तं सव्यसाचिनः ।निराशाः समपद्यन्त सर्वे कर्णस्य जीविते ॥३३॥
transliteration: taddṛṣṭvā kuravastatra vikrāntaṁ savyasācinaḥ ,nirāśāḥ samapadyanta sarve karṇasya jīvite.
english: Seeing that heroic deed of Arjuna, the left-handed warrior, there, all the Kurus became hopeless regarding Karṇa's life.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 59 Verse 33. Seeing that heroic deed of Arjuna, the left-handed warrior, there, all the Kurus became hopeless regarding Karṇa's life.

sanskrit: अविषह्यं तु पार्थस्य शरसंपातमाहवे ।मत्वा न्यवर्तन्कुरवो जिता गाण्डीवधन्वना ॥३४॥
transliteration: aviṣahyaṁ tu pārthasya śarasaṁpātamāhave ,matvā nyavartankuravo jitā gāṇḍīvadhanvanā.
english: Having realized the irresistible shower of arrows from Arjuna (Pārtha) in battle, the Kurus, vanquished by the wielder of the Gāṇḍīva bow, retreated.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 59 Verse 34. Having realized the irresistible shower of arrows from Arjuna (Pārtha) in battle, the Kurus, vanquished by the wielder of the Gāṇḍīva bow, retreated.

sanskrit: ते हित्वा समरे पार्थं वध्यमानाश्च सायकैः ।प्रदुद्रुवुर्दिशो भीताश्चुक्रुशुश्चापि सूतजम् ॥३५॥
transliteration: te hitvā samare pārthaṁ vadhyamānāśca sāyakaiḥ ,pradudruvurdiśo bhītāścukruśuścāpi sūtajam.
english: And they, terrified, abandoned Arjuna (Pārtha) in battle, and being struck by arrows, they fled in all directions and also cried out to the son of the charioteer (Karṇa).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 59 Verse 35. And they, terrified, abandoned Arjuna (Pārtha) in battle, and being struck by arrows, they fled in all directions and also cried out to the son of the charioteer (Karṇa).

sanskrit: अभ्यद्रवत तान्पार्थः किरञ्शरशतान्बहून् ।हर्षयन्पाण्डवान्योधान्भीमसेनपुरोगमान् ॥३६॥
transliteration: abhyadravata tānpārthaḥ kirañśaraśatānbahūn ,harṣayanpāṇḍavānyodhānbhīmasenapurogamān.
english: Arjuna (Pārtha) rushed towards them, showering many hundreds of arrows, thereby gladdening the Pāṇḍava warriors, with Bhīmasena at their forefront.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 59 Verse 36. Arjuna (Pārtha) rushed towards them, showering many hundreds of arrows, thereby gladdening the Pāṇḍava warriors, with Bhīmasena at their forefront.

sanskrit: पुत्रास्तु ते महाराज जग्मुः कर्णरथं प्रति ।अगाधे मज्जतां तेषां द्वीपः कर्णोऽभवत्तदा ॥३७॥
transliteration: putrāstu te mahārāja jagmuḥ karṇarathaṁ prati ,agādhe majjatāṁ teṣāṁ dvīpaḥ karṇo'bhavattadā.
english: But your sons, O great king, went towards Karṇa's chariot; for them, sinking in the unfathomable (depths of battle), Karṇa became an island of refuge at that time.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 59 Verse 37. But your sons, O great king, went towards Karṇa's chariot; for them, sinking in the unfathomable (depths of battle), Karṇa became an island of refuge at that time.

sanskrit: कुरवो हि महाराज निर्विषाः पन्नगा इव ।कर्णमेवोपलीयन्त भयाद्गाण्डीवधन्वनः ॥३८॥
transliteration: kuravo hi mahārāja nirviṣāḥ pannagā iva ,karṇamevopalīyanta bhayādgāṇḍīvadhanvanaḥ.
english: Indeed, O great king, the Kurus were like venomless snakes. They sought refuge solely in Karna, out of fear of Arjuna, the wielder of the Gāṇḍīva bow.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 59 Verse 38. Indeed, O great king, the Kurus were like venomless snakes. They sought refuge solely in Karna, out of fear of Arjuna, the wielder of the Gāṇḍīva bow.

sanskrit: यथा सर्वाणि भूतानि मृत्योर्भीतानि भारत ।धर्ममेवोपलीयन्ते कर्मवन्ति हि यानि च ॥३९॥
transliteration: yathā sarvāṇi bhūtāni mṛtyorbhītāni bhārata ,dharmamevopalīyante karmavanti hi yāni ca.
english: Just as all beings, O Bhārata, those indeed that are endowed with action (karma), being terrified of death, seek refuge solely in natural law (dharma).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 59 Verse 39. Just as all beings, O Bhārata, those indeed that are endowed with action (karma), being terrified of death, seek refuge solely in natural law (dharma).

sanskrit: तथा कर्णं महेष्वासं पुत्रास्तव नराधिप ।उपालीयन्त संत्रासात्पाण्डवस्य महात्मनः ॥४०॥
transliteration: tathā karṇaṁ maheṣvāsaṁ putrāstava narādhipa ,upālīyanta saṁtrāsātpāṇḍavasya mahātmanaḥ.
english: Thus, O king of men, your sons sought refuge in Karna, the great archer, out of great terror of the magnanimous Pāṇḍava (Arjuna).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 59 Verse 40. Thus, O king of men, your sons sought refuge in Karna, the great archer, out of great terror of the magnanimous Pāṇḍava (Arjuna).

sanskrit: ताञ्शोणितपरिक्लिन्नान्विषमस्थाञ्शरातुरान् ।मा भैष्टेत्यब्रवीत्कर्णो ह्यभितो मामितेति च ॥४१॥
transliteration: tāñśoṇitapariklinnānviṣamasthāñśarāturān ,mā bhaiṣṭetyabravītkarṇo hyabhito māmiteti ca.
english: To those who were drenched in blood, in a precarious situation, and tormented by arrows, Karna said, "Do not fear! I am here, surrounding you on all sides."
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 59 Verse 41. To those who were drenched in blood, in a precarious situation, and tormented by arrows, Karna said, "Do not fear! I am here, surrounding you on all sides."

sanskrit: संभग्नं हि बलं दृष्ट्वा बलात्पार्थेन तावकम् ।धनुर्विस्फारयन्कर्णस्तस्थौ शत्रुजिघांसया ।पाञ्चालान्पुनराधावत्पश्यतः सव्यसाचिनः ॥४२॥
transliteration: saṁbhagnaṁ hi balaṁ dṛṣṭvā balātpārthena tāvakam ,dhanurvisphārayankarṇastasthau śatrujighāṁsayā ,pāñcālānpunarādhāvatpaśyataḥ savyasācinaḥ.
english: Indeed, having seen your army shattered by Arjuna's might, Karṇa stood, stretching his bow with the desire to kill the enemies. He then again rushed towards the Pāñcālas, while Savyasācin (Arjuna) watched.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 59 Verse 42. Indeed, having seen your army shattered by Arjuna's might, Karṇa stood, stretching his bow with the desire to kill the enemies. He then again rushed towards the Pāñcālas, while Savyasācin (Arjuna) watched.

sanskrit: ततः क्षणेन क्षितिपाः क्षतजप्रतिमेक्षणाः ।कर्णं ववर्षुर्बाणौघैर्यथा मेघा महीधरम् ॥४३॥
transliteration: tataḥ kṣaṇena kṣitipāḥ kṣatajapratimekṣaṇāḥ ,karṇaṁ vavarṣurbāṇaughairyathā meghā mahīdharam.
english: Then, in an instant, the kings, with eyes resembling blood, showered Karṇa with floods of arrows, just as clouds shower a mountain.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 59 Verse 43. Then, in an instant, the kings, with eyes resembling blood, showered Karṇa with floods of arrows, just as clouds shower a mountain.

sanskrit: ततः शरसहस्राणि कर्णमुक्तानि मारिष ।व्ययोजयन्त पाञ्चालान्प्राणैः प्राणभृतां वर ॥४४॥
transliteration: tataḥ śarasahasrāṇi karṇamuktāni māriṣa ,vyayojayanta pāñcālānprāṇaiḥ prāṇabhṛtāṁ vara.
english: Then, O respected and best among living beings, thousands of arrows released by Karṇa separated the Pāñcālas from their vital breaths (prāṇa).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 59 Verse 44. Then, O respected and best among living beings, thousands of arrows released by Karṇa separated the Pāñcālas from their vital breaths (prāṇa).

sanskrit: ततो रणो महानासीत्पाञ्चालानां विशां पते ।वध्यतां सूतपुत्रेण मित्रार्थेऽमित्रघातिनाम् ॥४५॥
transliteration: tato raṇo mahānāsītpāñcālānāṁ viśāṁ pate ,vadhyatāṁ sūtaputreṇa mitrārthe'mitraghātinām.
english: Then, O lord of the people, a great battle ensued, involving the Pāñcālas - those slayers of enemies - who were being slain by the son of a charioteer (Karṇa) for his friend's sake.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 59 Verse 45. Then, O lord of the people, a great battle ensued, involving the Pāñcālas - those slayers of enemies - who were being slain by the son of a charioteer (Karṇa) for his friend's sake.

sanskrit: संजय उवाच ।ततः कर्णः कुरुषु प्रद्रुतेषु वरूथिना श्वेतहयेन राजन् ।पाञ्चालपुत्रान्व्यधमत्सूतपुत्रो महेषुभिर्वात इवाभ्रसंघान् ॥१॥
transliteration: saṁjaya uvāca ,tataḥ karṇaḥ kuruṣu pradruteṣu; varūthinā śvetahayena rājan ,pāñcālaputrānvyadhamatsūtaputro; maheṣubhirvāta ivābhrasaṁghān.
english: Saṃjaya said: "Then, O King, when the Kauravas were put to flight by the charioteer with white horses (Arjuna), Karṇa, the son of the charioteer, scattered the sons of Pañcāla with his great arrows, just as the wind disperses masses of clouds."
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 60 Verse 1. Saṃjaya said: "Then, O King, when the Kauravas were put to flight by the charioteer with white horses (Arjuna), Karṇa, the son of the charioteer, scattered the sons of Pañcāla with his great arrows, just as the wind disperses masses of clouds."

sanskrit: सूतं रथादञ्जलिकेन पात्य जघान चाश्वाञ्जनमेजयस्य ।शतानीकं सुतसोमं च भल्लैरवाकिरद्धनुषी चाप्यकृन्तत् ॥२॥
transliteration: sūtaṁ rathādañjalikena pātya; jaghāna cāśvāñjanamejayasya ,śatānīkaṁ sutasomaṁ ca bhallai;ravākiraddhanuṣī cāpyakṛntat.
english: With an Añjalika arrow, he struck down (Janamejaya's) charioteer from his chariot and killed Janamejaya's horses. He then overwhelmed Śatānīka and Sutasoma with bhalla arrows and also severed their two bows.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 60 Verse 2. With an Añjalika arrow, he struck down (Janamejaya's) charioteer from his chariot and killed Janamejaya's horses. He then overwhelmed Śatānīka and Sutasoma with bhalla arrows and also severed their two bows.

sanskrit: धृष्टद्युम्नं निर्बिभेदाथ षड्भिर्जघान चाश्वं दक्षिणं तस्य संख्ये ।हत्वा चाश्वान्सात्यकेः सूतपुत्रः कैकेयपुत्रं न्यवधीद्विशोकम् ॥३॥
transliteration: dhṛṣṭadyumnaṁ nirbibhedātha ṣaḍbhi;rjaghāna cāśvaṁ dakṣiṇaṁ tasya saṁkhye ,hatvā cāśvānsātyakeḥ sūtaputraḥ; kaikeyaputraṁ nyavadhīdviśokam.
english: Then, with six (arrows), he pierced Dhṛṣṭadyumna and killed his right horse in battle. After killing Sātyaki's horses, Karṇa, the son of the charioteer, then struck down Viśoka, the son of Kaikeya.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 60 Verse 3. Then, with six (arrows), he pierced Dhṛṣṭadyumna and killed his right horse in battle. After killing Sātyaki's horses, Karṇa, the son of the charioteer, then struck down Viśoka, the son of Kaikeya.

sanskrit: तमभ्यधावन्निहते कुमारे कैकेयसेनापतिरुग्रधन्वा ।शरैर्विभिन्नं भृशमुग्रवेगैः कर्णात्मजं सोऽभ्यहनत्सुषेणम् ॥४॥
transliteration: tamabhyadhāvannihate kumāre; kaikeyasenāpatirugradhanvā ,śarairvibhinnaṁ bhṛśamugravegaiḥ; karṇātmajaṁ so'bhyahanatsuṣeṇam.
english: When that prince (Viśoka) was killed, the fierce-bowed commander of the Kekaya army, along with his forces, rushed towards him (Karṇa). He then struck Karṇa's son, Suṣeṇa, who was greatly pierced by fiercely swift arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 60 Verse 4. When that prince (Viśoka) was killed, the fierce-bowed commander of the Kekaya army, along with his forces, rushed towards him (Karṇa). He then struck Karṇa's son, Suṣeṇa, who was greatly pierced by fiercely swift arrows.

sanskrit: तस्यार्धचन्द्रैस्त्रिभिरुच्चकर्त प्रसह्य बाहू च शिरश्च कर्णः ।स स्यन्दनाद्गामपतद्गतासुः परश्वधैः शाल इवावरुग्णः ॥५॥
transliteration: tasyārdhacandraistribhiruccakarta; prasahya bāhū ca śiraśca karṇaḥ ,sa syandanādgāmapatadgatāsuḥ; paraśvadhaiḥ śāla ivāvarugṇaḥ.
english: Karna, with three crescent-shaped arrows, forcefully severed his (Suṣeṇa's) arms and head. Lifeless, he (Suṣeṇa) fell from his chariot to the ground, like a Sal tree cut down by axes.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 60 Verse 5. Karna, with three crescent-shaped arrows, forcefully severed his (Suṣeṇa's) arms and head. Lifeless, he (Suṣeṇa) fell from his chariot to the ground, like a Sal tree cut down by axes.

sanskrit: हताश्वमञ्जोगतिभिः सुषेणः शिनिप्रवीरं निशितैः पृषत्कैः ।प्रच्छाद्य नृत्यन्निव सौतिपुत्रः शैनेयबाणाभिहतः पपात ॥६॥
transliteration: hatāśvamañjogatibhiḥ suṣeṇaḥ; śinipravīraṁ niśitaiḥ pṛṣatkaiḥ ,pracchādya nṛtyanniva sautiputraḥ; śaineyabāṇābhihataḥ papāta.
english: The son of Suta (Karna), having overwhelmed Suṣeṇa - the hero of the Śinis, whose horses had been slain - with sharp, swiftly-moving arrows; then, struck by Śaineya's arrows, he (Karna) fell as if dancing.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 60 Verse 6. The son of Suta (Karna), having overwhelmed Suṣeṇa - the hero of the Śinis, whose horses had been slain - with sharp, swiftly-moving arrows; then, struck by Śaineya's arrows, he (Karna) fell as if dancing.

sanskrit: पुत्रे हते क्रोधपरीतचेताः कर्णः शिनीनामृषभं जिघांसुः ।हतोऽसि शैनेय इति ब्रुवन्स व्यवासृजद्बाणममित्रसाहम् ॥७॥
