transliteration: putre hate krodhaparītacetāḥ; karṇaḥ śinīnāmṛṣabhaṁ jighāṁsuḥ ,hato'si śaineya iti bruvansa; vyavāsṛjadbāṇamamitrasāham.
english: With his son slain, Karna's mind was overcome with rage. Desiring to kill Satyaki, the foremost of the Śinis, he exclaimed, "O Śaineya, you are dead!" and unleashed an arrow capable of conquering enemies.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 60 Verse 7. With his son slain, Karna's mind was overcome with rage. Desiring to kill Satyaki, the foremost of the Śinis, he exclaimed, "O Śaineya, you are dead!" and unleashed an arrow capable of conquering enemies.

sanskrit: स तस्य चिच्छेद शरं शिखण्डी त्रिभिस्त्रिभिश्च प्रतुतोद कर्णम् ।शिखण्डिनः कार्मुकं स ध्वजं च च्छित्त्वा शराभ्यामहनत्सुजातम् ॥८॥
transliteration: sa tasya ciccheda śaraṁ śikhaṇḍī; tribhistribhiśca pratutoda karṇam ,śikhaṇḍinaḥ kārmukaṁ sa dhvajaṁ ca; cchittvā śarābhyāmahanatsujātam.
english: Shikhaṇḍī cut his (Karna's) arrow, and with three arrows, and then three more, he pierced Karna. He (Karna), having severed Shikhaṇḍī's bow and (his) banner with two arrows, struck the well-born (Shikhaṇḍī).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 60 Verse 8. Shikhaṇḍī cut his (Karna's) arrow, and with three arrows, and then three more, he pierced Karna. He (Karna), having severed Shikhaṇḍī's bow and (his) banner with two arrows, struck the well-born (Shikhaṇḍī).

sanskrit: शिखण्डिनं षड्भिरविध्यदुग्रो दान्तो धार्ष्टद्युम्नशिरश्चकर्त ।अथाभिनत्सुतसोमं शरेण स संशितेनाधिरथिर्महात्मा ॥९॥
transliteration: śikhaṇḍinaṁ ṣaḍbhiravidhyadugro; dānto dhārṣṭadyumnaśiraścakarta ,athābhinatsutasomaṁ śareṇa; sa saṁśitenādhirathirmahātmā.
english: The mighty (ugraḥ) and self-controlled (dāntaḥ) great-souled (mahātmā) Adhirathi (Karna) pierced Shikhandin with six [arrows] and severed Dhrishtadyumna's head. Then, he struck Sutasoma with a sharpened arrow.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 60 Verse 9. The mighty (ugraḥ) and self-controlled (dāntaḥ) great-souled (mahātmā) Adhirathi (Karna) pierced Shikhandin with six [arrows] and severed Dhrishtadyumna's head. Then, he struck Sutasoma with a sharpened arrow.

sanskrit: अथाक्रन्दे तुमुले वर्तमाने धार्ष्टद्युम्ने निहते तत्र कृष्णः ।अपाञ्चाल्यं क्रियते याहि पार्थ कर्णं जहीत्यब्रवीद्राजसिंह ॥१०॥
transliteration: athākrande tumule vartamāne; dhārṣṭadyumne nihate tatra kṛṣṇaḥ ,apāñcālyaṁ kriyate yāhi pārtha; karṇaṁ jahītyabravīdrājasiṁha.
english: Then, while a tumultuous uproar was ongoing and Dhrishtadyumna had been killed there, Krishna, that lion among kings, said: 'The extermination of the Panchalas is being carried out! Go, Partha, and kill Karna!'
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 60 Verse 10. Then, while a tumultuous uproar was ongoing and Dhrishtadyumna had been killed there, Krishna, that lion among kings, said: 'The extermination of the Panchalas is being carried out! Go, Partha, and kill Karna!'

sanskrit: ततः प्रहस्याशु नरप्रवीरो रथं रथेनाधिरथेर्जगाम ।भये तेषां त्राणमिच्छन्सुबाहुरभ्याहतानां रथयूथपेन ॥११॥
transliteration: tataḥ prahasyāśu narapravīro; rathaṁ rathenādhiratherjagāma ,bhaye teṣāṁ trāṇamicchansubāhu;rabhyāhatānāṁ rathayūthapena.
english: Then, that hero among men, the mighty-armed (Arjuna), quickly laughed and, desiring protection for those who were assailed in fear by the commander of the chariot division (Karna), he advanced with his chariot toward Adhirathi's (Karna's) chariot.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 60 Verse 11. Then, that hero among men, the mighty-armed (Arjuna), quickly laughed and, desiring protection for those who were assailed in fear by the commander of the chariot division (Karna), he advanced with his chariot toward Adhirathi's (Karna's) chariot.

sanskrit: विस्फार्य गाण्डीवमथोग्रघोषं ज्यया समाहत्य तले भृशं च ।बाणान्धकारं सहसैव कृत्वा जघान नागाश्वरथान्नरांश्च ॥१२॥
transliteration: visphārya gāṇḍīvamathograghoṣaṁ; jyayā samāhatya tale bhṛśaṁ ca ,bāṇāndhakāraṁ sahasaiva kṛtvā; jaghāna nāgāśvarathānnarāṁśca.
english: Having stretched the terrible-sounding Gandiva bow, and then fiercely striking the bowstring against his palm, and suddenly creating a darkness of arrows, he slew elephants, horses, chariots, and men.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 60 Verse 12. Having stretched the terrible-sounding Gandiva bow, and then fiercely striking the bowstring against his palm, and suddenly creating a darkness of arrows, he slew elephants, horses, chariots, and men.

sanskrit: तं भीमसेनोऽनु ययौ रथेन पृष्ठे रक्षन्पाण्डवमेकवीरम् ।तौ राजपुत्रौ त्वरितौ रथाभ्यां कर्णाय यातावरिभिर्विमुक्तौ ॥१३॥
transliteration: taṁ bhīmaseno'nu yayau rathena; pṛṣṭhe rakṣanpāṇḍavamekavīram ,tau rājaputrau tvaritau rathābhyāṁ; karṇāya yātāvaribhirvimuktau.
english: Bhīmasena followed him (Arjuna), protecting that lone hero, the Pāṇḍava, from behind on his chariot. Meanwhile, those two swift princes, having departed in their chariots towards Karṇa, were left unhindered by their enemies.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 60 Verse 13. Bhīmasena followed him (Arjuna), protecting that lone hero, the Pāṇḍava, from behind on his chariot. Meanwhile, those two swift princes, having departed in their chariots towards Karṇa, were left unhindered by their enemies.

sanskrit: अत्रान्तरे सुमहत्सूतपुत्रश्चक्रे युद्धं सोमकान्संप्रमृद्नन् ।रथाश्वमातङ्गगणाञ्जघान प्रच्छादयामास दिशः शरैश्च ॥१४॥
transliteration: atrāntare sumahatsūtaputra;ścakre yuddhaṁ somakānsaṁpramṛdnan ,rathāśvamātaṅgagaṇāñjaghāna; pracchādayāmāsa diśaḥ śaraiśca.
english: In the interim, the son of Sūta (Karṇa) engaged in a very intense battle, utterly crushing the Somakas. He destroyed hosts of chariots, horses, and elephants, and moreover, completely obscured the directions with his arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 60 Verse 14. In the interim, the son of Sūta (Karṇa) engaged in a very intense battle, utterly crushing the Somakas. He destroyed hosts of chariots, horses, and elephants, and moreover, completely obscured the directions with his arrows.

sanskrit: तमुत्तमौजा जनमेजयश्च क्रुद्धौ युधामन्युशिखण्डिनौ च ।कर्णं विनेदुः सहिताः पृषत्कैः संमर्दमानाः सह पार्षतेन ॥१५॥
transliteration: tamuttamaujā janamejayaśca; kruddhau yudhāmanyuśikhaṇḍinau ca ,karṇaṁ vineduḥ sahitāḥ pṛṣatkaiḥ; saṁmardamānāḥ saha pārṣatena.
english: Uttamaujas and Janamejaya, along with the enraged Yudhamanyu and Śikhaṇḍin, united with the son of Pṛṣata (Dhṛṣṭadyumna), attacked Karṇa with arrows, causing great agitation.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 60 Verse 15. Uttamaujas and Janamejaya, along with the enraged Yudhamanyu and Śikhaṇḍin, united with the son of Pṛṣata (Dhṛṣṭadyumna), attacked Karṇa with arrows, causing great agitation.

sanskrit: ते पञ्च पाञ्चालरथाः सुरूपैर्वैकर्तनं कर्णमभिद्रवन्तः ।तस्माद्रथाच्च्यावयितुं न शेकुर्धैर्यात्कृतात्मानमिवेन्द्रियाणि ॥१६॥
transliteration: te pañca pāñcālarathāḥ surūpai;rvaikartanaṁ karṇamabhidravantaḥ ,tasmādrathāccyāvayituṁ na śeku;rdhairyātkṛtātmānamivendriyāṇi.
english: Those five charioteers of the Pañcālas, appearing splendidly, charged at Karṇa, the son of Vikartana. They were unable to dislodge him from his chariot, just as the senses cannot dislodge a resolute (kṛtātman) person from their courage.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 60 Verse 16. Those five charioteers of the Pañcālas, appearing splendidly, charged at Karṇa, the son of Vikartana. They were unable to dislodge him from his chariot, just as the senses cannot dislodge a resolute (kṛtātman) person from their courage.

sanskrit: तेषां धनूंषि ध्वजवाजिसूतांस्तूणं पताकाश्च निकृत्य बाणैः ।तान्पञ्चभिः स त्वहनत्पृषत्कैः कर्णस्ततः सिंह इवोन्ननाद ॥१७॥
transliteration: teṣāṁ dhanūṁṣi dhvajavājisūtāṁ;stūṇaṁ patākāśca nikṛtya bāṇaiḥ ,tānpañcabhiḥ sa tvahanatpṛṣatkaiḥ; karṇastataḥ siṁha ivonnanāda.
english: Cutting down their bows, flags, horses, charioteers, quivers, and banners with his arrows, Karna then struck those warriors with five more arrows. Following this, Karna roared like a lion (siṃha).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 60 Verse 17. Cutting down their bows, flags, horses, charioteers, quivers, and banners with his arrows, Karna then struck those warriors with five more arrows. Following this, Karna roared like a lion (siṃha).

sanskrit: तस्यास्यतस्तानभिनिघ्नतश्च ज्याबाणहस्तस्य धनुःस्वनेन ।साद्रिद्रुमा स्यात्पृथिवी विशीर्णा इत्येव मत्वा जनता व्यषीदत् ॥१८॥
transliteration: tasyāsyatastānabhinighnataśca; jyābāṇahastasya dhanuḥsvanena ,sādridrumā syātpṛthivī viśīrṇā; ityeva matvā janatā vyaṣīdat.
english: As he (Karna) shot and struck them with arrows held in his hands, and from the sound of his bow, the people became dejected, thinking, 'The earth (pṛthivī), along with its mountains and trees, will surely be shattered!'
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 60 Verse 18. As he (Karna) shot and struck them with arrows held in his hands, and from the sound of his bow, the people became dejected, thinking, 'The earth (pṛthivī), along with its mountains and trees, will surely be shattered!'

sanskrit: स शक्रचापप्रतिमेन धन्वना भृशाततेनाधिरथिः शरान्सृजन् ।बभौ रणे दीप्तमरीचिमण्डलो यथांशुमाली परिवेषवांस्तथा ॥१९॥
transliteration: sa śakracāpapratimena dhanvanā; bhṛśātatenādhirathiḥ śarānsṛjan ,babhau raṇe dīptamarīcimaṇḍalo; yathāṁśumālī pariveṣavāṁstathā.
english: As Adhirathi (Karna), releasing arrows with his greatly drawn bow that resembled Indra's bow, he shone in battle like the sun (aṃśumālī) enveloped in a halo, with a brilliant circle of rays around him.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 60 Verse 19. As Adhirathi (Karna), releasing arrows with his greatly drawn bow that resembled Indra's bow, he shone in battle like the sun (aṃśumālī) enveloped in a halo, with a brilliant circle of rays around him.

sanskrit: शिखण्डिनं द्वादशभिः पराभिनच्छितैः शरैः षड्भिरथोत्तमौजसम् ।त्रिभिर्युधामन्युमविध्यदाशुगैस्त्रिभिस्त्रिभिः सोमकपार्षतात्मजौ ॥२०॥
transliteration: śikhaṇḍinaṁ dvādaśabhiḥ parābhina;cchitaiḥ śaraiḥ ṣaḍbhirathottamaujasam ,tribhiryudhāmanyumavidhyadāśugai;stribhistribhiḥ somakapārṣatātmajau.
english: Then, he struck Shikhaṇḍin with twelve sharp arrows, and Uttamaujas with six. He pierced Yudhamanyu with three swift arrows. And with three arrows each, (he struck) the two sons of Somaka and Pārṣata.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 60 Verse 20. Then, he struck Shikhaṇḍin with twelve sharp arrows, and Uttamaujas with six. He pierced Yudhamanyu with three swift arrows. And with three arrows each, (he struck) the two sons of Somaka and Pārṣata.

sanskrit: पराजिताः पञ्च महारथास्तु ते महाहवे सूतसुतेन मारिष ।निरुद्यमास्तस्थुरमित्रमर्दना यथेन्द्रियार्थात्मवता पराजिताः ॥२१॥
transliteration: parājitāḥ pañca mahārathāstu te; mahāhave sūtasutena māriṣa ,nirudyamāstasthuramitramardanā; yathendriyārthātmavatā parājitāḥ.
english: O venerable one, those five great charioteers, though they were crushers of enemies, were defeated in that great battle by the son of the charioteer (Karṇa). They stood there without spirit, just as sense objects are overcome by a self-controlled person (ātman).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 60 Verse 21. O venerable one, those five great charioteers, though they were crushers of enemies, were defeated in that great battle by the son of the charioteer (Karṇa). They stood there without spirit, just as sense objects are overcome by a self-controlled person (ātman).

sanskrit: निमज्जतस्तानथ कर्णसागरे विपन्ननावो वणिजो यथार्णवे ।उद्दध्रिरे नौभिरिवार्णवाद्रथैः सुकल्पितैर्द्रौपदिजाः स्वमातुलान् ॥२२॥
transliteration: nimajjatastānatha karṇasāgare; vipannanāvo vaṇijo yathārṇave ,uddadhrire naubhirivārṇavādrathaiḥ; sukalpitairdraupadijāḥ svamātulān.
english: Then, Draupadī's sons, using their well-equipped chariots like boats, rescued their maternal uncles, who were sinking in the ocean of Karṇa's assault, just as merchants whose ships have capsized in the ocean are rescued from the sea.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 60 Verse 22. Then, Draupadī's sons, using their well-equipped chariots like boats, rescued their maternal uncles, who were sinking in the ocean of Karṇa's assault, just as merchants whose ships have capsized in the ocean are rescued from the sea.

sanskrit: ततः शिनीनामृषभः शितैः शरैर्निकृत्य कर्णप्रहितानिषून्बहून् ।विदार्य कर्णं निशितैरयस्मयैस्तवात्मजं ज्येष्ठमविध्यदष्टभिः ॥२३॥
transliteration: tataḥ śinīnāmṛṣabhaḥ śitaiḥ śarai;rnikṛtya karṇaprahitāniṣūnbahūn ,vidārya karṇaṁ niśitairayasmayai;stavātmajaṁ jyeṣṭhamavidhyadaṣṭabhiḥ.
english: Then, the foremost warrior of the Śini clan (Sātyaki), after cutting down many arrows that Karṇa had launched with his sharp arrows, pierced Karṇa and then struck your eldest son (Duryodhana) with eight keen, iron arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 60 Verse 23. Then, the foremost warrior of the Śini clan (Sātyaki), after cutting down many arrows that Karṇa had launched with his sharp arrows, pierced Karṇa and then struck your eldest son (Duryodhana) with eight keen, iron arrows.

sanskrit: कृपोऽथ भोजश्च तवात्मजस्तथा स्वयं च कर्णो निशितैरताडयत् ।स तैश्चतुर्भिर्युयुधे यदूत्तमो दिगीश्वरैर्दैत्यपतिर्यथा तथा ॥२४॥
transliteration: kṛpo'tha bhojaśca tavātmajastathā; svayaṁ ca karṇo niśitairatāḍayat ,sa taiścaturbhiryuyudhe yadūttamo; digīśvarairdaityapatiryathā tathā.
english: Then, Kṛpa, Bhoja (Kṛtavarman), your son (Duryodhana), and Karṇa himself attacked him (Sātyaki) with sharp weapons. That excellent warrior of the Yadu clan (Sātyaki) fought with those four, just as a lord of demons might contend with the guardians of the directions.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 60 Verse 24. Then, Kṛpa, Bhoja (Kṛtavarman), your son (Duryodhana), and Karṇa himself attacked him (Sātyaki) with sharp weapons. That excellent warrior of the Yadu clan (Sātyaki) fought with those four, just as a lord of demons might contend with the guardians of the directions.

sanskrit: समानतेनेष्वसनेन कूजता भृशाततेनामितबाणवर्षिणा ।बभूव दुर्धर्षतरः स सात्यकिः शरन्नभोमध्यगतो यथा रविः ॥२५॥
transliteration: samānateneṣvasanena kūjatā; bhṛśātatenāmitabāṇavarṣiṇā ,babhūva durdharṣataraḥ sa sātyakiḥ; śarannabhomadhyagato yathā raviḥ.
english: That Satyaki, with his greatly taut bow equally stretched, twanging and showering countless arrows, became even more formidable, just like the sun positioned in the middle of the autumn sky.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 60 Verse 25. That Satyaki, with his greatly taut bow equally stretched, twanging and showering countless arrows, became even more formidable, just like the sun positioned in the middle of the autumn sky.

sanskrit: पुनः समासाद्य रथान्सुदंशिताः शिनिप्रवीरं जुगुपुः परंतपाः ।समेत्य पाञ्चालरथा महारणे मरुद्गणाः शक्रमिवारिनिग्रहे ॥२६॥
transliteration: punaḥ samāsādya rathānsudaṁśitāḥ; śinipravīraṁ jugupuḥ paraṁtapāḥ ,sametya pāñcālarathā mahāraṇe; marudgaṇāḥ śakramivārinigrahe.
english: Again, the well-armored vanquishers of foes, the charioteers of the Panchalas, having assembled and regained their chariots in the great battle, protected the great hero of the Shinis (Satyaki), just as the hosts of Maruts protect Indra in the destruction of enemies.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 60 Verse 26. Again, the well-armored vanquishers of foes, the charioteers of the Panchalas, having assembled and regained their chariots in the great battle, protected the great hero of the Shinis (Satyaki), just as the hosts of Maruts protect Indra in the destruction of enemies.

sanskrit: ततोऽभवद्युद्धमतीव दारुणं तवाहितानां तव सैनिकैः सह ।रथाश्वमातङ्गविनाशनं तथा यथा सुराणामसुरैः पुराभवत् ॥२७॥
transliteration: tato'bhavadyuddhamatīva dāruṇaṁ; tavāhitānāṁ tava sainikaiḥ saha ,rathāśvamātaṅgavināśanaṁ tathā; yathā surāṇāmasuraiḥ purābhavat.
english: Then, an extremely dreadful battle took place between your enemies and your soldiers, a battle that caused the destruction of chariots, horses, and elephants, just as such a battle occurred formerly between the gods and the asuras.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 60 Verse 27. Then, an extremely dreadful battle took place between your enemies and your soldiers, a battle that caused the destruction of chariots, horses, and elephants, just as such a battle occurred formerly between the gods and the asuras.

sanskrit: रथद्विपा वाजिपदातयोऽपि वा भ्रमन्ति नानाविधशस्त्रवेष्टिताः ।परस्परेणाभिहताश्च चस्खलुर्विनेदुरार्ता व्यसवोऽपतन्त च ॥२८॥
transliteration: rathadvipā vājipadātayo'pi vā; bhramanti nānāvidhaśastraveṣṭitāḥ ,paraspareṇābhihatāśca caskhalu;rvinedurārtā vyasavo'patanta ca.
english: Chariots, elephants, horses, and foot soldiers, surrounded by various kinds of weapons, reeled. Struck by one another, they stumbled and cried out in distress; and then, lifeless, they fell.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 60 Verse 28. Chariots, elephants, horses, and foot soldiers, surrounded by various kinds of weapons, reeled. Struck by one another, they stumbled and cried out in distress; and then, lifeless, they fell.

sanskrit: तथा गते भीममभीस्तवात्मजः ससार राजावरजः किरञ्शरैः ।तमभ्यधावत्त्वरितो वृकोदरो महारुरुं सिंह इवाभिपेतिवान् ॥२९॥
transliteration: tathā gate bhīmamabhīstavātmajaḥ; sasāra rājāvarajaḥ kirañśaraiḥ ,tamabhyadhāvattvarito vṛkodaro; mahāruruṁ siṁha ivābhipetivān.
english: When that happened, your son (Duryodhana), fearless, rushed towards Bhima. Meanwhile, the king's younger brother (Bhima) was showering arrows. Then, Vṛkodara (Bhima) swiftly charged at him (Duryodhana), like a lion pouncing upon a great deer.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 60 Verse 29. When that happened, your son (Duryodhana), fearless, rushed towards Bhima. Meanwhile, the king's younger brother (Bhima) was showering arrows. Then, Vṛkodara (Bhima) swiftly charged at him (Duryodhana), like a lion pouncing upon a great deer.

sanskrit: ततस्तयोर्युद्धमतीतमानुषं प्रदीव्यतोः प्राणदुरोदरेऽभवत् ।परस्परेणाभिनिविष्टरोषयोरुदग्रयोः शम्बरशक्रयोर्यथा ॥३०॥
transliteration: tatastayoryuddhamatītamānuṣaṁ; pradīvyatoḥ prāṇadurodare'bhavat ,paraspareṇābhiniviṣṭaroṣayo;rudagrayoḥ śambaraśakrayoryathā.
english: Then, a battle surpassing human (limits) occurred between the two (Duryodhana and Bhima), who were gambling their lives as if in a game of dice. It was like the battle between Śambara and Śakra (Indra), both fiercely resolute and filled with mutual anger.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 60 Verse 30. Then, a battle surpassing human (limits) occurred between the two (Duryodhana and Bhima), who were gambling their lives as if in a game of dice. It was like the battle between Śambara and Śakra (Indra), both fiercely resolute and filled with mutual anger.

sanskrit: शरैः शरीरान्तकरैः सुतेजनैर्निजघ्नतुस्तावितरेतरं भृशम् ।सकृत्प्रभिन्नाविव वाशितान्तरे महागजौ मन्मथसक्तचेतसौ ॥३१॥
transliteration: śaraiḥ śarīrāntakaraiḥ sutejanai;rnijaghnatustāvitaretaraṁ bhṛśam ,sakṛtprabhinnāviva vāśitāntare; mahāgajau manmathasaktacetasau.
english: With lethal and very sharp arrows, those two (Bhima and Duryodhana) fiercely struck each other, just like two great elephants, once in rut and with their minds captivated by the god of love (manmatha), in the presence of a female elephant.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 60 Verse 31. With lethal and very sharp arrows, those two (Bhima and Duryodhana) fiercely struck each other, just like two great elephants, once in rut and with their minds captivated by the god of love (manmatha), in the presence of a female elephant.

sanskrit: तवात्मजस्याथ वृकोदरस्त्वरन्धनुः क्षुराभ्यां ध्वजमेव चाच्छिनत् ।ललाटमप्यस्य बिभेद पत्रिणा शिरश्च कायात्प्रजहार सारथेः ॥३२॥
transliteration: tavātmajasyātha vṛkodarastvara;ndhanuḥ kṣurābhyāṁ dhvajameva cācchinat ,lalāṭamapyasya bibheda patriṇā; śiraśca kāyātprajahāra sāratheḥ.
english: Then, Vṛkodara (Bhima), swiftly, severed your son's (Duryodhana's) bow and banner with two razor-sharp arrows. He also pierced Duryodhana's forehead with a feathered arrow and struck the charioteer's head from his body.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 60 Verse 32. Then, Vṛkodara (Bhima), swiftly, severed your son's (Duryodhana's) bow and banner with two razor-sharp arrows. He also pierced Duryodhana's forehead with a feathered arrow and struck the charioteer's head from his body.

sanskrit: स राजपुत्रोऽन्यदवाप्य कार्मुकं वृकोदरं द्वादशभिः पराभिनत् ।स्वयं नियच्छंस्तुरगानजिह्मगैः शरैश्च भीमं पुनरभ्यवीवृषत् ॥३३॥
transliteration: sa rājaputro'nyadavāpya kārmukaṁ; vṛkodaraṁ dvādaśabhiḥ parābhinat ,svayaṁ niyacchaṁsturagānajihmagaiḥ; śaraiśca bhīmaṁ punarabhyavīvṛṣat.
english: Having obtained another bow, that prince struck Bhima (Vṛkodara) with twelve arrows. While personally controlling his horses, he again showered Bhima with straight-flying arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 60 Verse 33. Having obtained another bow, that prince struck Bhima (Vṛkodara) with twelve arrows. While personally controlling his horses, he again showered Bhima with straight-flying arrows.

sanskrit: संजय उवाच ।तत्राकरोद्दुष्करं राजपुत्रो दुःशासनस्तुमुले युध्यमानः ।चिच्छेद भीमस्य धनुः क्षुरेण षड्भिः शरैः सारथिमप्यविध्यत् ॥१॥
transliteration: saṁjaya uvāca ,tatrākarodduṣkaraṁ rājaputro; duḥśāsanastumule yudhyamānaḥ ,ciccheda bhīmasya dhanuḥ kṣureṇa; ṣaḍbhiḥ śaraiḥ sārathimapyavidhyat.
english: Sañjaya said: In that fierce battle, Prince Duḥśāsana, fighting valiantly, accomplished a formidable task. He cut Bhima's bow with a razor-sharp arrow and also pierced his charioteer with six arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 61 Verse 1. Sañjaya said: In that fierce battle, Prince Duḥśāsana, fighting valiantly, accomplished a formidable task. He cut Bhima's bow with a razor-sharp arrow and also pierced his charioteer with six arrows.

sanskrit: ततोऽभिनद्बहुभिः क्षिप्रमेव वरेषुभिर्भीमसेनं महात्मा ।स विक्षरन्नाग इव प्रभिन्नो गदामस्मै तुमुले प्राहिणोद्वै ॥२॥
transliteration: tato'bhinadbahubhiḥ kṣiprameva; vareṣubhirbhīmasenaṁ mahātmā ,sa vikṣarannāga iva prabhinno; gadāmasmai tumule prāhiṇodvai.
english: Then, the great-souled (mahātmā) prince swiftly pierced Bhimasena with many excellent arrows. Bhima, profusely bleeding like an elephant in rut, then hurled a mace at him in that fierce battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 61 Verse 2. Then, the great-souled (mahātmā) prince swiftly pierced Bhimasena with many excellent arrows. Bhima, profusely bleeding like an elephant in rut, then hurled a mace at him in that fierce battle.

sanskrit: तयाहरद्दश धन्वन्तराणि दुःशासनं भीमसेनः प्रसह्य ।तया हतः पतितो वेपमानो दुःशासनो गदया वेगवत्या ॥३॥
transliteration: tayāharaddaśa dhanvantarāṇi; duḥśāsanaṁ bhīmasenaḥ prasahya ,tayā hataḥ patito vepamāno; duḥśāsano gadayā vegavatyā.
english: With that (mace), Bhimasena forcibly drove Duḥśāsana back ten bow-lengths. Struck by that swift mace, Duḥśāsana fell, trembling.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 61 Verse 3. With that (mace), Bhimasena forcibly drove Duḥśāsana back ten bow-lengths. Struck by that swift mace, Duḥśāsana fell, trembling.

sanskrit: हयाः ससूताश्च हता नरेन्द्र चूर्णीकृतश्चास्य रथः पतन्त्या ।विध्वस्तवर्माभरणाम्बरस्रग्विचेष्टमानो भृशवेदनार्तः ॥४॥
transliteration: hayāḥ sasūtāśca hatā narendra; cūrṇīkṛtaścāsya rathaḥ patantyā ,vidhvastavarmābharaṇāmbarasra;gviceṣṭamāno bhṛśavedanārtaḥ.
english: O king, his horses, along with their charioteer, were slain, and his chariot was utterly crushed. He, with his armor, ornaments, garments, and garland shattered, lay on the ground, struggling and intensely afflicted by pain.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 61 Verse 4. O king, his horses, along with their charioteer, were slain, and his chariot was utterly crushed. He, with his armor, ornaments, garments, and garland shattered, lay on the ground, struggling and intensely afflicted by pain.

sanskrit: ततः स्मृत्वा भीमसेनस्तरस्वी सापत्नकं यत्प्रयुक्तं सुतैस्ते ।रथादवप्लुत्य गतः स भूमौ यत्नेन तस्मिन्प्रणिधाय चक्षुः ॥५॥
transliteration: tataḥ smṛtvā bhīmasenastarasvī; sāpatnakaṁ yatprayuktaṁ sutaiste ,rathādavaplutya gataḥ sa bhūmau; yatnena tasminpraṇidhāya cakṣuḥ.
english: Then, the energetic Bhimasena, recalling the hostility (sāpatnaka) perpetrated by your (Dhritarashtra's) sons, leaped from his chariot. He went to the ground, fixing his gaze upon Jayadratha with great effort.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 61 Verse 5. Then, the energetic Bhimasena, recalling the hostility (sāpatnaka) perpetrated by your (Dhritarashtra's) sons, leaped from his chariot. He went to the ground, fixing his gaze upon Jayadratha with great effort.

sanskrit: असिं समुद्धृत्य शितं सुधारं कण्ठे समाक्रम्य च वेपमानम् ।उत्कृत्य वक्षः पतितस्य भूमावथापिबच्छोणितमस्य कोष्णम् ।आस्वाद्य चास्वाद्य च वीक्षमाणः क्रुद्धोऽतिवेलं प्रजगाद वाक्यम् ॥६॥
transliteration: asiṁ samuddhṛtya śitaṁ sudhāraṁ; kaṇṭhe samākramya ca vepamānam ,utkṛtya vakṣaḥ patitasya bhūmā;vathāpibacchoṇitamasya koṣṇam ,āsvādya cāsvādya ca vīkṣamāṇaḥ; kruddho'tivelaṁ prajagāda vākyam.
english: Drawing his sharpened, very keen-edged sword, and stepping on the neck of the trembling Jayadratha, Bhimasena then tore open the chest of the one fallen on the ground. He drank Jayadratha's lukewarm blood, tasting it repeatedly. As he watched, enraged, he spoke these words with great intensity.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 61 Verse 6. Drawing his sharpened, very keen-edged sword, and stepping on the neck of the trembling Jayadratha, Bhimasena then tore open the chest of the one fallen on the ground. He drank Jayadratha's lukewarm blood, tasting it repeatedly. As he watched, enraged, he spoke these words with great intensity.

sanskrit: स्तन्यस्य मातुर्मधुसर्पिषो वा माध्वीकपानस्य च सत्कृतस्य ।दिव्यस्य वा तोयरसस्य पानात्पयोदधिभ्यां मथिताच्च मुख्यात् ।सर्वेभ्य एवाभ्यधिको रसोऽयं मतो ममाद्याहितलोहितस्य ॥७॥
transliteration: stanyasya māturmadhusarpiṣo vā; mādhvīkapānasya ca satkṛtasya ,divyasya vā toyarasasya pānā;tpayodadhibhyāṁ mathitācca mukhyāt ,sarvebhya evābhyadhiko raso'yaṁ; mato mamādyāhitalohitasya.
english: This taste (rasa) is considered by me, who today has spilled this blood, to be superior to all other tastes: a mother's breast milk, or honey and ghee, or the drinking of revered mead, or even the drinking of divine refreshing water, or the finest essence churned from milk and curds.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 61 Verse 7. This taste (rasa) is considered by me, who today has spilled this blood, to be superior to all other tastes: a mother's breast milk, or honey and ghee, or the drinking of revered mead, or even the drinking of divine refreshing water, or the finest essence churned from milk and curds.

sanskrit: एवं ब्रुवाणं पुनराद्रवन्तमास्वाद्य वल्गन्तमतिप्रहृष्टम् ।ये भीमसेनं ददृशुस्तदानीं भयेन तेऽपि व्यथिता निपेतुः ॥८॥
transliteration: evaṁ bruvāṇaṁ punarādravanta;māsvādya valgantamatiprahṛṣṭam ,ye bhīmasenaṁ dadṛśustadānīṁ; bhayena te'pi vyathitā nipetuḥ.
english: Those who at that time saw Bhīmasena, speaking thus, rushing forward again, having tasted (blood), leaping, and exceedingly delighted, even they fell down, distressed by fear.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 61 Verse 8. Those who at that time saw Bhīmasena, speaking thus, rushing forward again, having tasted (blood), leaping, and exceedingly delighted, even they fell down, distressed by fear.

sanskrit: ये चापि तत्रापतिता मनुष्यास्तेषां करेभ्यः पतितं च शस्त्रम् ।भयाच्च संचुक्रुशुरुच्चकैस्ते निमीलिताक्षा ददृशुश्च तन्न ॥९॥
transliteration: ye cāpi tatrāpatitā manuṣyā;steṣāṁ karebhyaḥ patitaṁ ca śastram ,bhayācca saṁcukruśuruccakaiste; nimīlitākṣā dadṛśuśca tanna.
english: And those people who had fallen there, their weapons also fell from their hands. And they cried out loudly from fear, and with closed eyes, they did not see that.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 61 Verse 9. And those people who had fallen there, their weapons also fell from their hands. And they cried out loudly from fear, and with closed eyes, they did not see that.

sanskrit: ये तत्र भीमं ददृशुः समन्ताद्दौःशासनं तद्रुधिरं पिबन्तम् ।सर्वे पलायन्त भयाभिपन्ना नायं मनुष्य इति भाषमाणाः ॥१०॥
transliteration: ye tatra bhīmaṁ dadṛśuḥ samantā;ddauḥśāsanaṁ tadrudhiraṁ pibantam ,sarve palāyanta bhayābhipannā; nāyaṁ manuṣya iti bhāṣamāṇāḥ.
english: Those who saw Bhīma there, from all sides, drinking the blood of Duḥśāsana, all fled, overcome by fear, saying, "This is not a human being!"
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 61 Verse 10. Those who saw Bhīma there, from all sides, drinking the blood of Duḥśāsana, all fled, overcome by fear, saying, "This is not a human being!"

sanskrit: शृण्वतां लोकवीराणामिदं वचनमब्रवीत् ।एष ते रुधिरं कण्ठात्पिबामि पुरुषाधम ।ब्रूहीदानीं सुसंरब्धः पुनर्गौरिति गौरिति ॥११॥
transliteration: śṛṇvatāṁ lokavīrāṇāmidaṁ vacanamabravīt ,eṣa te rudhiraṁ kaṇṭhātpibāmi puruṣādhama ,brūhīdānīṁ susaṁrabdhaḥ punargauriti gauriti.
english: To the heroes among the people who were listening, he spoke these words: "O vilest of men! I drink your blood from your throat. Now speak, if you are so fiercely provoked, 'cow, cow' again, 'cow, cow'!".
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 61 Verse 11. To the heroes among the people who were listening, he spoke these words: "O vilest of men! I drink your blood from your throat. Now speak, if you are so fiercely provoked, 'cow, cow' again, 'cow, cow'!".

sanskrit: प्रमाणकोट्यां शयनं कालकूटस्य भोजनम् ।दशनं चाहिभिः कष्टं दाहं च जतुवेश्मनि ॥१२॥
transliteration: pramāṇakoṭyāṁ śayanaṁ kālakūṭasya bhojanam ,daśanaṁ cāhibhiḥ kaṣṭaṁ dāhaṁ ca jatuveśmani.
english: We experienced lying in an extremely precarious situation (like on the tip of a scale), consuming the Kālakūṭa poison, suffering painful bites from serpents, and enduring the burning in the lac-house.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 61 Verse 12. We experienced lying in an extremely precarious situation (like on the tip of a scale), consuming the Kālakūṭa poison, suffering painful bites from serpents, and enduring the burning in the lac-house.

sanskrit: द्यूतेन राज्यहरणमरण्ये वसतिश्च या ।इष्वस्त्राणि च संग्रामेष्वसुखानि च वेश्मनि ॥१३॥
transliteration: dyūtena rājyaharaṇamaraṇye vasatiśca yā ,iṣvastrāṇi ca saṁgrāmeṣvasukhāni ca veśmani.
english: The loss of the kingdom through gambling, the dwelling in the forest, the arrows and weapons in battles, and the unhappiness in our home.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 61 Verse 13. The loss of the kingdom through gambling, the dwelling in the forest, the arrows and weapons in battles, and the unhappiness in our home.

sanskrit: दुःखान्येतानि जानीमो न सुखानि कदाचन ।धृतराष्ट्रस्य दौरात्म्यात्सपुत्रस्य सदा वयम् ॥१४॥
transliteration: duḥkhānyetāni jānīmo na sukhāni kadācana ,dhṛtarāṣṭrasya daurātmyātsaputrasya sadā vayam.
english: We have always experienced these sufferings and never any happiness, all due to the wickedness of Dhṛtarāṣṭra and his sons.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 61 Verse 14. We have always experienced these sufferings and never any happiness, all due to the wickedness of Dhṛtarāṣṭra and his sons.

sanskrit: इत्युक्त्वा वचनं राजञ्जयं प्राप्य वृकोदरः ।पुनराह महाराज स्मयंस्तौ केशवार्जुनौ ॥१५॥
transliteration: ityuktvā vacanaṁ rājañjayaṁ prāpya vṛkodaraḥ ,punarāha mahārāja smayaṁstau keśavārjunau.
english: Having spoken thus, O King, and having achieved victory, Bhima (Vṛkodara), smiling, spoke again to those two, Kṛṣṇa (Keśava) and Arjuna.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 61 Verse 15. Having spoken thus, O King, and having achieved victory, Bhima (Vṛkodara), smiling, spoke again to those two, Kṛṣṇa (Keśava) and Arjuna.

sanskrit: दुःशासने यद्रणे संश्रुतं मे तद्वै सर्वं कृतमद्येह वीरौ ।अद्यैव दास्याम्यपरं द्वितीयं दुर्योधनं यज्ञपशुं विशस्य ।शिरो मृदित्वा च पदा दुरात्मनः शान्तिं लप्स्ये कौरवाणां समक्षम् ॥१६॥
transliteration: duḥśāsane yadraṇe saṁśrutaṁ me; tadvai sarvaṁ kṛtamadyeha vīrau ,adyaiva dāsyāmyaparaṁ dvitīyaṁ; duryodhanaṁ yajñapaśuṁ viśasya ,śiro mṛditvā ca padā durātmanaḥ; śāntiṁ lapsye kauravāṇāṁ samakṣam.
english: O heroes, what I swore regarding Duḥśāsana in battle, all of that has indeed been accomplished here today. Today itself, having slain this other, second Duryodhana, as if he were an animal for a Vedic ritual (yajña), I shall make an offering of him; and having crushed the head of that evil-minded one with my foot, I shall attain peace (śānti) in the presence of the Kauravas.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 61 Verse 16. O heroes, what I swore regarding Duḥśāsana in battle, all of that has indeed been accomplished here today. Today itself, having slain this other, second Duryodhana, as if he were an animal for a Vedic ritual (yajña), I shall make an offering of him; and having crushed the head of that evil-minded one with my foot, I shall attain peace (śānti) in the presence of the Kauravas.

sanskrit: एतावदुक्त्वा वचनं प्रहृष्टो ननाद चोच्चै रुधिरार्द्रगात्रः ।ननर्त चैवातिबलो महात्मा वृत्रं निहत्येव सहस्रनेत्रः ॥१७॥
transliteration: etāvaduktvā vacanaṁ prahṛṣṭo; nanāda coccai rudhirārdragātraḥ ,nanarta caivātibalo mahātmā; vṛtraṁ nihatyeva sahasranetraḥ.
english: Having spoken these words, exceedingly delighted and with his body drenched in blood, he roared loudly. And that exceedingly mighty great soul (mahatma) danced, just like Indra (sahasranetras) having slain Vṛtra.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 61 Verse 17. Having spoken these words, exceedingly delighted and with his body drenched in blood, he roared loudly. And that exceedingly mighty great soul (mahatma) danced, just like Indra (sahasranetras) having slain Vṛtra.

sanskrit: संजय उवाच ।दुःशासने तु निहते पुत्रास्तव महारथाः ।महाक्रोधविषा वीराः समरेष्वपलायिनः ।दश राजन्महावीर्यो भीमं प्राच्छादयञ्शरैः ॥१॥
transliteration: saṁjaya uvāca ,duḥśāsane tu nihate putrāstava mahārathāḥ ,mahākrodhaviṣā vīrāḥ samareṣvapalāyinaḥ ,daśa rājanmahāvīryo bhīmaṁ prācchādayañśaraiḥ.
english: Sañjaya said: O King, when Duḥśāsana had been slain, your ten great chariot-warrior sons - those heroes (vīra) of great valor, whose poison was immense anger, and who never fled from battles - covered Bhīma with arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 62 Verse 1. Sañjaya said: O King, when Duḥśāsana had been slain, your ten great chariot-warrior sons - those heroes (vīra) of great valor, whose poison was immense anger, and who never fled from battles - covered Bhīma with arrows.

sanskrit: कवची निषङ्गी पाशी दण्डधारो धनुर्धरः ।अलोलुपः शलः संधो वातवेगसुवर्चसौ ॥२॥
transliteration: kavacī niṣaṅgī pāśī daṇḍadhāro dhanurdharaḥ ,alolupaḥ śalaḥ saṁdho vātavegasuvarcasau.
english: Armored, quiver-bearing, noose-wielding, staff-bearing, bow-wielding, and fearless was Śala; also Sandha, and Vātavega and Suvarcasa.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 62 Verse 2. Armored, quiver-bearing, noose-wielding, staff-bearing, bow-wielding, and fearless was Śala; also Sandha, and Vātavega and Suvarcasa.

sanskrit: एते समेत्य सहिता भ्रातृव्यसनकर्शिताः ।भीमसेनं महाबाहुं मार्गणैः समवारयन् ॥३॥
transliteration: ete sametya sahitā bhrātṛvyasanakarśitāḥ ,bhīmasenaṁ mahābāhuṁ mārgaṇaiḥ samavārayan.
english: Weakened by the grief over their brother's misfortune, these warriors united and came together. With their arrows, they completely surrounded and halted the mighty-armed Bhīmasena.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 62 Verse 3. Weakened by the grief over their brother's misfortune, these warriors united and came together. With their arrows, they completely surrounded and halted the mighty-armed Bhīmasena.

sanskrit: स वार्यमाणो विशिखैः समन्तात्तैर्महारथैः ।भीमः क्रोधाभिरक्ताक्षः क्रुद्धः काल इवाबभौ ॥४॥
transliteration: sa vāryamāṇo viśikhaiḥ samantāttairmahārathaiḥ ,bhīmaḥ krodhābhiraktākṣaḥ kruddhaḥ kāla ivābabhau.
english: Obstructed on all sides by the arrows of those great charioteers, Bhīma became enraged. With his eyes red from fury, he shone like destructive Time (kāla).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 62 Verse 4. Obstructed on all sides by the arrows of those great charioteers, Bhīma became enraged. With his eyes red from fury, he shone like destructive Time (kāla).

sanskrit: तांस्तु भल्लैर्महावेगैर्दशभिर्दशभिः शितैः ।रुक्माङ्गदो रुक्मपुङ्खैः पार्थो निन्ये यमक्षयम् ॥५॥
transliteration: tāṁstu bhallairmahāvegairdaśabhirdaśabhiḥ śitaiḥ ,rukmāṅgado rukmapuṅkhaiḥ pārtho ninye yamakṣayam.
english: But Pārtha (Arjuna), using ten swift and sharp broad-headed arrows each, adorned with gold and having golden feathers, dispatched those (enemies) to the abode of Yama.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 62 Verse 5. But Pārtha (Arjuna), using ten swift and sharp broad-headed arrows each, adorned with gold and having golden feathers, dispatched those (enemies) to the abode of Yama.

sanskrit: हतेषु तेषु वीरेषु प्रदुद्राव बलं तव ।पश्यतः सूतपुत्रस्य पाण्डवस्य भयार्दितम् ॥६॥
transliteration: hateṣu teṣu vīreṣu pradudrāva balaṁ tava ,paśyataḥ sūtaputrasya pāṇḍavasya bhayārditam.
english: When those heroes were slain, your army, tormented by fear of the Pāṇḍava, fled while the son of Sūta (Karṇa) watched.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 62 Verse 6. When those heroes were slain, your army, tormented by fear of the Pāṇḍava, fled while the son of Sūta (Karṇa) watched.

sanskrit: ततः कर्णो महाराज प्रविवेश महारणम् ।दृष्ट्वा भीमस्य विक्रान्तमन्तकस्य प्रजास्विव ॥७॥
transliteration: tataḥ karṇo mahārāja praviveśa mahāraṇam ,dṛṣṭvā bhīmasya vikrāntamantakasya prajāsviva.
english: Then, O great king, Karṇa entered the great battle, having witnessed Bhīma's fierce valor, which was like that of Death (Antaka) among his subjects.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 62 Verse 7. Then, O great king, Karṇa entered the great battle, having witnessed Bhīma's fierce valor, which was like that of Death (Antaka) among his subjects.

sanskrit: तस्य त्वाकारभावज्ञः शल्यः समितिशोभनः ।उवाच वचनं कर्णं प्राप्तकालमरिंदम ।मा व्यथां कुरु राधेय नैतत्त्वय्युपपद्यते ॥८॥
transliteration: tasya tvākārabhāvajñaḥ śalyaḥ samitiśobhanaḥ ,uvāca vacanaṁ karṇaṁ prāptakālamariṁdama ,mā vyathāṁ kuru rādheya naitattvayyupapadyate.
english: However, Śalya, who understood Karṇa's outward demeanor and inner feelings, and who was illustrious in assemblies, addressed timely words to Karṇa, saying, 'O subduer of enemies (ariṁdama), do not be disheartened, O son of Rādhā (Rādheya); this kind of behavior does not befit you.'
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 62 Verse 8. However, Śalya, who understood Karṇa's outward demeanor and inner feelings, and who was illustrious in assemblies, addressed timely words to Karṇa, saying, 'O subduer of enemies (ariṁdama), do not be disheartened, O son of Rādhā (Rādheya); this kind of behavior does not befit you.'

sanskrit: एते द्रवन्ति राजानो भीमसेनभयार्दिताः ।दुर्योधनश्च संमूढो भ्रातृव्यसनदुःखितः ॥९॥
transliteration: ete dravanti rājāno bhīmasenabhayārditāḥ ,duryodhanaśca saṁmūḍho bhrātṛvyasanaduḥkhitaḥ.
english: These kings are fleeing, distressed by their fear of Bhīmasena. Duryodhana, too, is utterly bewildered and afflicted with sorrow due to the calamity that befell his brothers.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 62 Verse 9. These kings are fleeing, distressed by their fear of Bhīmasena. Duryodhana, too, is utterly bewildered and afflicted with sorrow due to the calamity that befell his brothers.

sanskrit: दुःशासनस्य रुधिरे पीयमाने महात्मना ।व्यापन्नचेतसश्चैव शोकोपहतमन्यवः ॥१०॥
transliteration: duḥśāsanasya rudhire pīyamāne mahātmanā ,vyāpannacetasaścaiva śokopahatamanyavaḥ.
english: As the great-souled (mahātman) Bhīma drank Duḥśāsana's blood, their minds (referring to the kings) became bewildered, and their anger was overwhelmed by grief.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 62 Verse 10. As the great-souled (mahātman) Bhīma drank Duḥśāsana's blood, their minds (referring to the kings) became bewildered, and their anger was overwhelmed by grief.

sanskrit: दुर्योधनमुपासन्ते परिवार्य समन्ततः ।कृपप्रभृतयः कर्ण हतशेषाश्च सोदराः ॥११॥
transliteration: duryodhanamupāsante parivārya samantataḥ ,kṛpaprabhṛtayaḥ karṇa hataśeṣāśca sodarāḥ.
english: O Karṇa, Kṛpa and others, as well as Duryodhana's surviving brothers, are attending upon Duryodhana, surrounding him from all sides.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 62 Verse 11. O Karṇa, Kṛpa and others, as well as Duryodhana's surviving brothers, are attending upon Duryodhana, surrounding him from all sides.

sanskrit: पाण्डवा लब्धलक्षाश्च धनंजयपुरोगमाः ।त्वामेवाभिमुखाः शूरा युद्धाय समुपास्थिताः ॥१२॥
transliteration: pāṇḍavā labdhalakṣāśca dhanaṁjayapurogamāḥ ,tvāmevābhimukhāḥ śūrā yuddhāya samupāsthitāḥ.
english: And the Pāṇḍavas, who have achieved their objective, with Arjuna (dhanaṃjaya) at their forefront, those brave warriors are arrayed facing you for battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 62 Verse 12. And the Pāṇḍavas, who have achieved their objective, with Arjuna (dhanaṃjaya) at their forefront, those brave warriors are arrayed facing you for battle.

sanskrit: स त्वं पुरुषशार्दूल पौरुषे महति स्थितः ।क्षत्रधर्मं पुरस्कृत्य प्रत्युद्याहि धनंजयम् ॥१३॥
transliteration: sa tvaṁ puruṣaśārdūla pauruṣe mahati sthitaḥ ,kṣatradharmaṁ puraskṛtya pratyudyāhi dhanaṁjayam.
english: O tiger among men (puruṣaśārdūla), you, who are established in great valor, honoring the natural law (dharma) of a warrior (kṣatra), go forth and confront Arjuna (dhanaṃjaya)!
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 62 Verse 13. O tiger among men (puruṣaśārdūla), you, who are established in great valor, honoring the natural law (dharma) of a warrior (kṣatra), go forth and confront Arjuna (dhanaṃjaya)!

sanskrit: भारो हि धार्तराष्ट्रेण त्वयि सर्वः समर्पितः ।तमुद्वह महाबाहो यथाशक्ति यथाबलम् ।जये स्याद्विपुला कीर्तिर्ध्रुवः स्वर्गः पराजये ॥१४॥
transliteration: bhāro hi dhārtarāṣṭreṇa tvayi sarvaḥ samarpitaḥ ,tamudvaha mahābāho yathāśakti yathābalam ,jaye syādvipulā kīrtirdhruvaḥ svargaḥ parājaye.
english: Indeed, the entire burden has been entrusted to you by Duryodhana (dhārtarāṣṭra). O mighty-armed one, bear that burden according to your capacity and strength. In victory, there will be great fame, and in defeat, heaven (svarga) is certain.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 62 Verse 14. Indeed, the entire burden has been entrusted to you by Duryodhana (dhārtarāṣṭra). O mighty-armed one, bear that burden according to your capacity and strength. In victory, there will be great fame, and in defeat, heaven (svarga) is certain.

sanskrit: वृषसेनश्च राधेय संक्रुद्धस्तनयस्तव ।त्वयि मोहसमापन्ने पाण्डवानभिधावति ॥१५॥
transliteration: vṛṣasenaśca rādheya saṁkruddhastanayastava ,tvayi mohasamāpanne pāṇḍavānabhidhāvati.
english: O son of Rādhā, your son Vṛṣasena, greatly enraged as you have fallen into delusion, is rushing towards the Pāṇḍavas.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 62 Verse 15. O son of Rādhā, your son Vṛṣasena, greatly enraged as you have fallen into delusion, is rushing towards the Pāṇḍavas.

sanskrit: एतच्छ्रुत्वा तु वचनं शल्यस्यामिततेजसः ।हृदि मानुष्यकं भावं चक्रे युद्धाय सुस्थिरम् ॥१६॥
transliteration: etacchrutvā tu vacanaṁ śalyasyāmitatejasaḥ ,hṛdi mānuṣyakaṁ bhāvaṁ cakre yuddhāya susthiram.
english: Having heard these words from Śalya, who possessed immense prowess, he then firmly established his human resolve for battle in his heart.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 62 Verse 16. Having heard these words from Śalya, who possessed immense prowess, he then firmly established his human resolve for battle in his heart.

sanskrit: ततः क्रुद्धो वृषसेनोऽभ्यधावदातस्थिवांसं स्वरथं हतारिम् ।वृकोदरं कालमिवात्तदण्डं गदाहस्तं पोथमानं त्वदीयान् ॥१७॥
transliteration: tataḥ kruddho vṛṣaseno'bhyadhāva;dātasthivāṁsaṁ svarathaṁ hatārim ,vṛkodaraṁ kālamivāttadaṇḍaṁ; gadāhastaṁ pothamānaṁ tvadīyān.
english: Then, the enraged Vṛṣasena rushed towards Vṛkodara (Bhīma), who stood upon his own chariot, having slain his enemies. Bhīma was like Death (Kāla) holding his rod, with a mace in his hand, crushing your warriors.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 62 Verse 17. Then, the enraged Vṛṣasena rushed towards Vṛkodara (Bhīma), who stood upon his own chariot, having slain his enemies. Bhīma was like Death (Kāla) holding his rod, with a mace in his hand, crushing your warriors.

sanskrit: तमभ्यधावन्नकुलः प्रवीरो रोषादमित्रं प्रतुदन्पृषत्कैः ।कर्णस्य पुत्रं समरे प्रहृष्टं जिष्णुर्जिघांसुर्मघवेव जम्भम् ॥१८॥
transliteration: tamabhyadhāvannakulaḥ pravīro; roṣādamitraṁ pratudanpṛṣatkaiḥ ,karṇasya putraṁ samare prahṛṣṭaṁ; jiṣṇurjighāṁsurmaghaveva jambham.
english: The mighty warrior Nakula, enraged, rushed towards him, Karṇa's son, who was exulting in battle, striking the enemy with arrows. He was like the victorious (Jiṣṇu) Indra (Maghavan), desirous of slaying Jambha.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 62 Verse 18. The mighty warrior Nakula, enraged, rushed towards him, Karṇa's son, who was exulting in battle, striking the enemy with arrows. He was like the victorious (Jiṣṇu) Indra (Maghavan), desirous of slaying Jambha.

sanskrit: ततो ध्वजं स्फाटिकचित्रकम्बुं चिच्छेद वीरो नकुलः क्षुरेण ।कर्णात्मजस्येष्वसनं च चित्रं भल्लेन जाम्बूनदपट्टनद्धम् ॥१९॥
transliteration: tato dhvajaṁ sphāṭikacitrakambuṁ; ciccheda vīro nakulaḥ kṣureṇa ,karṇātmajasyeṣvasanaṁ ca citraṁ; bhallena jāmbūnadapaṭṭanaddham.
english: Then, the heroic Nakula severed Karna's son's banner, which bore a crystal-painted conch shell, with a razor-sharp arrow. And he also cut his beautiful bow, which was bound with golden strips, with a bhalla arrow.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 62 Verse 19. Then, the heroic Nakula severed Karna's son's banner, which bore a crystal-painted conch shell, with a razor-sharp arrow. And he also cut his beautiful bow, which was bound with golden strips, with a bhalla arrow.

sanskrit: अथान्यदादाय धनुः सुशीघ्रं कर्णात्मजः पाण्डवमभ्यविध्यत् ।दिव्यैर्महास्त्रैर्नकुलं महास्त्रो दुःशासनस्यापचितिं यियासुः ॥२०॥
transliteration: athānyadādāya dhanuḥ suśīghraṁ; karṇātmajaḥ pāṇḍavamabhyavidhyat ,divyairmahāstrairnakulaṁ mahāstro; duḥśāsanasyāpacitiṁ yiyāsuḥ.
english: Then, Karna's son (Vṛṣasena), a master of great weapons, swiftly took another bow and struck the Pāṇḍava (Nakula) with divine, mighty arrows, desiring to avenge Duḥśāsana.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 62 Verse 20. Then, Karna's son (Vṛṣasena), a master of great weapons, swiftly took another bow and struck the Pāṇḍava (Nakula) with divine, mighty arrows, desiring to avenge Duḥśāsana.

sanskrit: ततः क्रुद्धो नकुलस्तं महात्मा शरैर्महोल्काप्रतिमैरविध्यत् ।दिव्यैरस्त्रैरभ्यविध्यच्च सोऽपि कर्णस्य पुत्रो नकुलं कृतास्त्रः ॥२१॥
transliteration: tataḥ kruddho nakulastaṁ mahātmā; śarairmaholkāpratimairavidhyat ,divyairastrairabhyavidhyacca so'pi; karṇasya putro nakulaṁ kṛtāstraḥ.
english: Then, the great-souled (mahātman) Nakula, enraged, struck Karna's son with arrows like great meteors. And Karna's son, who was skilled in weapons, also struck Nakula with divine arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 62 Verse 21. Then, the great-souled (mahātman) Nakula, enraged, struck Karna's son with arrows like great meteors. And Karna's son, who was skilled in weapons, also struck Nakula with divine arrows.

sanskrit: कर्णस्य पुत्रो नकुलस्य राजन्सर्वानश्वानक्षिणोदुत्तमास्त्रैः ।वनायुजान्सुकुमारस्य शुभ्रानलंकृताञ्जातरूपेण शीघ्रान् ॥२२॥
transliteration: karṇasya putro nakulasya rāja;nsarvānaśvānakṣiṇoduttamāstraiḥ ,vanāyujānsukumārasya śubhrā;nalaṁkṛtāñjātarūpeṇa śīghrān.
english: O King, Karna's son destroyed all of Nakula's horses - those horses of the delicate Nakula that were born in Vanāyu, splendid, adorned with gold, and swift - with his excellent weapons.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 62 Verse 22. O King, Karna's son destroyed all of Nakula's horses - those horses of the delicate Nakula that were born in Vanāyu, splendid, adorned with gold, and swift - with his excellent weapons.

sanskrit: ततो हताश्वादवरुह्य यानादादाय चर्म रुचिरं चाष्टचन्द्रम् ।आकाशसंकाशमसिं गृहीत्वा पोप्लूयमानः खगवच्चचार ॥२३॥
transliteration: tato hatāśvādavaruhya yānā;dādāya carma ruciraṁ cāṣṭacandram ,ākāśasaṁkāśamasiṁ gṛhītvā; poplūyamānaḥ khagavaccacāra.
english: Then, descending from the chariot whose horses had been slain, and taking up a beautiful shield adorned with eight moons, and holding a sword that gleamed like the sky, he moved about, leaping repeatedly like a bird.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 62 Verse 23. Then, descending from the chariot whose horses had been slain, and taking up a beautiful shield adorned with eight moons, and holding a sword that gleamed like the sky, he moved about, leaping repeatedly like a bird.

sanskrit: ततोऽन्तरिक्षे नृवराश्वनागांश्चिच्छेद मार्गान्विचरन्विचित्रान् ।ते प्रापतन्नसिना गां विशस्ता यथाश्वमेधे पशवः शमित्रा ॥२४॥
transliteration: tato'ntarikṣe nṛvarāśvanāgāṁ;ściccheda mārgānvicaranvicitrān ,te prāpatannasinā gāṁ viśastā; yathāśvamedhe paśavaḥ śamitrā.
english: Then, roaming in the sky, he cut off the various paths of eminent men, horses, and elephants. They fell to the ground, cut down by his sword, just as animals are slaughtered by the butcher in an aśvamedha (Vedic ritual).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 62 Verse 24. Then, roaming in the sky, he cut off the various paths of eminent men, horses, and elephants. They fell to the ground, cut down by his sword, just as animals are slaughtered by the butcher in an aśvamedha (Vedic ritual).

sanskrit: द्विसाहस्रा विदिता युद्धशौण्डा नानादेश्याः सुभृताः सत्यसंधाः ।एकेन शीघ्रं नकुलेन कृत्ताः सारेप्सुनेवोत्तमचन्दनास्ते ॥२५॥
transliteration: dvisāhasrā viditā yuddhaśauṇḍā; nānādeśyāḥ subhṛtāḥ satyasaṁdhāḥ ,ekena śīghraṁ nakulena kṛttāḥ; sārepsunevottamacandanāste.
english: Two thousand renowned warriors, skilled in battle, from various countries, well-maintained, and faithful to their vows, were swiftly cut down by one Nakula, just as excellent sandalwood trees are cut down by someone desiring their essence.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 62 Verse 25. Two thousand renowned warriors, skilled in battle, from various countries, well-maintained, and faithful to their vows, were swiftly cut down by one Nakula, just as excellent sandalwood trees are cut down by someone desiring their essence.

sanskrit: तमापतन्तं नकुलं सोऽभिपत्य समन्ततः सायकैरभ्यविध्यत् ।स तुद्यमानो नकुलः पृषत्कैर्विव्याध वीरं स चुकोप विद्धः ॥२६॥
transliteration: tamāpatantaṁ nakulaṁ so'bhipatya; samantataḥ sāyakairabhyavidhyat ,sa tudyamāno nakulaḥ pṛṣatkai;rvivyādha vīraṁ sa cukopa viddhaḥ.
english: Having rushed towards that approaching Nakula, he pierced him from all sides with arrows. Nakula, being wounded, in turn pierced the hero with his arrows. The hero, thus wounded, became enraged.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 62 Verse 26. Having rushed towards that approaching Nakula, he pierced him from all sides with arrows. Nakula, being wounded, in turn pierced the hero with his arrows. The hero, thus wounded, became enraged.

sanskrit: तं कर्णपुत्रो विधमन्तमेकं नराश्वमातङ्गरथप्रवेकान् ।क्रीडन्तमष्टादशभिः पृषत्कैर्विव्याध वीरं स चुकोप विद्धः ॥२७॥
transliteration: taṁ karṇaputro vidhamantamekaṁ; narāśvamātaṅgarathapravekān ,krīḍantamaṣṭādaśabhiḥ pṛṣatkai;rvivyādha vīraṁ sa cukopa viddhaḥ.
english: Karna's son pierced that single hero who was destroying the best of men, horses, elephants, and chariots, as if he were merely playing, with eighteen arrows. Being pierced, that hero became enraged.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 62 Verse 27. Karna's son pierced that single hero who was destroying the best of men, horses, elephants, and chariots, as if he were merely playing, with eighteen arrows. Being pierced, that hero became enraged.

sanskrit: ततोऽभ्यधावत्समरे जिघांसुः कर्णात्मजं पाण्डुसुतो नृवीरः ।तस्येषुभिर्व्यधमत्कर्णपुत्रो महारणे चर्म सहस्रतारम् ॥२८॥
transliteration: tato'bhyadhāvatsamare jighāṁsuḥ; karṇātmajaṁ pāṇḍusuto nṛvīraḥ ,tasyeṣubhirvyadhamatkarṇaputro; mahāraṇe carma sahasratāram.
english: Then, Pandu's heroic son, desiring to kill Karna's son, rushed forward in battle. In that great battle, Karna's son shattered his shield, which was marked with a thousand stars, with arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 62 Verse 28. Then, Pandu's heroic son, desiring to kill Karna's son, rushed forward in battle. In that great battle, Karna's son shattered his shield, which was marked with a thousand stars, with arrows.

sanskrit: तस्यायसं निशितं तीक्ष्णधारमसिं विकोशं गुरुभारसाहम् ।द्विषच्छरीरापहरं सुघोरमाधुन्वतः सर्पमिवोग्ररूपम् ॥२९॥
transliteration: tasyāyasaṁ niśitaṁ tīkṣṇadhāra;masiṁ vikośaṁ gurubhārasāham ,dviṣaccharīrāpaharaṁ sughora;mādhunvataḥ sarpamivograrūpam.
english: He was brandishing his sharp-edged iron sword, which was sharpened and drawn from its scabbard, capable of bearing great weight, snatching away the bodies of enemies, exceedingly dreadful, and resembled a serpent of fierce form.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 62 Verse 29. He was brandishing his sharp-edged iron sword, which was sharpened and drawn from its scabbard, capable of bearing great weight, snatching away the bodies of enemies, exceedingly dreadful, and resembled a serpent of fierce form.

sanskrit: क्षिप्रं शरैः षड्भिरमित्रसाहश्चकर्त खड्गं निशितैः सुधारैः ।पुनश्च पीतैर्निशितैः पृषत्कैः स्तनान्तरे गाढमथाभ्यविध्यत् ॥३०॥
transliteration: kṣipraṁ śaraiḥ ṣaḍbhiramitrasāha;ścakarta khaḍgaṁ niśitaiḥ sudhāraiḥ ,punaśca pītairniśitaiḥ pṛṣatkaiḥ; stanāntare gāḍhamathābhyavidhyat.
english: Swiftly, the one who overcame enemies cut off the sword with six sharpened, very sharp arrows. Then, with yellow, sharpened arrows, he deeply pierced him in the middle of the chest.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 62 Verse 30. Swiftly, the one who overcame enemies cut off the sword with six sharpened, very sharp arrows. Then, with yellow, sharpened arrows, he deeply pierced him in the middle of the chest.

sanskrit: स भीमसेनस्य रथं हताश्वो माद्रीसुतः कर्णसुताभितप्तः ।आपुप्लुवे सिंह इवाचलाग्रं संप्रेक्षमाणस्य धनंजयस्य ॥३१॥
transliteration: sa bhīmasenasya rathaṁ hatāśvo; mādrīsutaḥ karṇasutābhitaptaḥ ,āpupluve siṁha ivācalāgraṁ; saṁprekṣamāṇasya dhanaṁjayasya.
english: With his horses slain, and tormented by Karna's son, Madri's son (Nakula) leapt onto Bhimasena's chariot, just as a lion springs upon a mountain peak, while Dhanañjaya (Arjuna) looked on.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 62 Verse 31. With his horses slain, and tormented by Karna's son, Madri's son (Nakula) leapt onto Bhimasena's chariot, just as a lion springs upon a mountain peak, while Dhanañjaya (Arjuna) looked on.

sanskrit: नकुलमथ विदित्वा छिन्नबाणासनासिं विरथमरिशरार्तं कर्णपुत्रास्त्रभग्नम् ।पवनधुतपताका ह्रादिनो वल्गिताश्वा वरपुरुषनियत्तास्ते रथाः शीघ्रमीयुः ॥३२॥
transliteration: nakulamatha viditvā chinnabāṇāsanāsiṁ; virathamariśarārtaṁ karṇaputrāstrabhagnam ,pavanadhutapatākā hrādino valgitāśvā; varapuruṣaniyattāste rathāḥ śīghramīyuḥ.
english: Then, seeing Nakula - his bow and sword broken, chariotiess, afflicted by enemy arrows, and shattered by the weapons of Karna's son - those chariots, with their flags flapping in the wind, making noise, their horses prancing, and guided by excellent men, quickly advanced.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 62 Verse 32. Then, seeing Nakula - his bow and sword broken, chariotiess, afflicted by enemy arrows, and shattered by the weapons of Karna's son - those chariots, with their flags flapping in the wind, making noise, their horses prancing, and guided by excellent men, quickly advanced.

sanskrit: द्रुपदसुतवरिष्ठाः पञ्च शैनेयषष्ठा द्रुपददुहितृपुत्राः पञ्च चामित्रसाहाः ।द्विरदरथनराश्वान्सूदयन्तस्त्वदीयान्भुजगपतिनिकाशैर्मार्गणैरात्तशस्त्राः ॥३३॥
transliteration: drupadasutavariṣṭhāḥ pañca śaineyaṣaṣṭhā; drupadaduhitṛputrāḥ pañca cāmitrasāhāḥ ,dviradarathanarāśvānsūdayantastvadīyā;nbhujagapatinikāśairmārgaṇairāttaśastrāḥ.
english: The five foremost sons of Drupada, together with Śaineya (Satyaki) as the sixth, and the five sons of Drupada's daughter (Draupadi), all conquerors of enemies, armed with arrows like lordly serpents, were destroying your elephants, chariots, men, and horses.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 62 Verse 33. The five foremost sons of Drupada, together with Śaineya (Satyaki) as the sixth, and the five sons of Drupada's daughter (Draupadi), all conquerors of enemies, armed with arrows like lordly serpents, were destroying your elephants, chariots, men, and horses.

sanskrit: अथ तव रथमुख्यास्तान्प्रतीयुस्त्वरन्तो हृदिकसुतकृपौ च द्रौणिदुर्योधनौ च ।शकुनिशुकवृकाश्च क्राथदेवावृधौ च द्विरदजलदघोषैः स्यन्दनैः कार्मुकैश्च ॥३४॥
transliteration: atha tava rathamukhyāstānpratīyustvaranto; hṛdikasutakṛpau ca drauṇiduryodhanau ca ,śakuniśukavṛkāśca krāthadevāvṛdhau ca; dviradajaladaghoṣaiḥ syandanaiḥ kārmukaiśca.
english: Then, your leading charioteers - Kṛtavarman (Hṛdika's son) and Kṛpa, Droṇa's son (Aśvatthāman) and Duryodhana, Śakuni, Śuka, and Vṛka, and also Krātha and Devāvṛdha - hurrying, advanced against them with chariots roaring like elephants and thunderclouds, and with their bows.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 62 Verse 34. Then, your leading charioteers - Kṛtavarman (Hṛdika's son) and Kṛpa, Droṇa's son (Aśvatthāman) and Duryodhana, Śakuni, Śuka, and Vṛka, and also Krātha and Devāvṛdha - hurrying, advanced against them with chariots roaring like elephants and thunderclouds, and with their bows.

sanskrit: तव नरवरवर्यास्तान्दशैकं च वीरान्प्रवरशरवराग्र्यैस्ताडयन्तोऽभ्यरुन्धन् ।नवजलदसवर्णैर्हस्तिभिस्तानुदीयुर्गिरिशिखरनिकाशैर्भीमवेगैः कुणिन्दाः ॥३५॥
transliteration: tava naravaravaryāstāndaśaikaṁ ca vīrā;npravaraśaravarāgryaistāḍayanto'bhyarundhan ,navajaladasavarṇairhastibhistānudīyu;rgiriśikharanikāśairbhīmavegaiḥ kuṇindāḥ.
english: Your eleven most distinguished heroes, striking (the enemy) with the best of excellent arrows, besieged them. Then, the Kuṇindas, with elephants dark as fresh clouds, resembling mountain peaks, and possessing terrible speed, advanced against them.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 62 Verse 35. Your eleven most distinguished heroes, striking (the enemy) with the best of excellent arrows, besieged them. Then, the Kuṇindas, with elephants dark as fresh clouds, resembling mountain peaks, and possessing terrible speed, advanced against them.

sanskrit: सुकल्पिता हैमवता मदोत्कटा रणाभिकामैः कृतिभिः समास्थिताः ।सुवर्णजालावतता बभुर्गजास्तथा यथा वै जलदाः सविद्युतः ॥३६॥
transliteration: sukalpitā haimavatā madotkaṭā; raṇābhikāmaiḥ kṛtibhiḥ samāsthitāḥ ,suvarṇajālāvatatā babhurgajā;stathā yathā vai jaladāḥ savidyutaḥ.
english: Well-trained, Himalayan elephants, furious with rut, and mounted by skillful warriors eager for battle, shone forth, covered with golden nets, just like clouds accompanied by lightning.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 62 Verse 36. Well-trained, Himalayan elephants, furious with rut, and mounted by skillful warriors eager for battle, shone forth, covered with golden nets, just like clouds accompanied by lightning.

sanskrit: कुणिन्दपुत्रो दशभिर्महायसैः कृपं ससूताश्वमपीडयद्भृशम् ।ततः शरद्वत्सुतसायकैर्हतः सहैव नागेन पपात भूतले ॥३७॥
transliteration: kuṇindaputro daśabhirmahāyasaiḥ; kṛpaṁ sasūtāśvamapīḍayadbhṛśam ,tataḥ śaradvatsutasāyakairhataḥ; sahaiva nāgena papāta bhūtale.
english: The son of the Kuṇinda, with ten great iron arrows, severely tormented Kṛpa, along with his charioteer and horses. Then, (the Kuṇinda's son), struck down by the arrows of Śaradvat's son (Kṛpa), fell to the ground along with his elephant.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 62 Verse 37. The son of the Kuṇinda, with ten great iron arrows, severely tormented Kṛpa, along with his charioteer and horses. Then, (the Kuṇinda's son), struck down by the arrows of Śaradvat's son (Kṛpa), fell to the ground along with his elephant.

sanskrit: कुणिन्दपुत्रावरजस्तु तोमरैर्दिवाकरांशुप्रतिमैरयस्मयैः ।रथं च विक्षोभ्य ननाद नर्दतस्ततोऽस्य गान्धारपतिः शिरोऽहरत् ॥३८॥
transliteration: kuṇindaputrāvarajastu tomarai;rdivākarāṁśupratimairayasmayaiḥ ,rathaṁ ca vikṣobhya nanāda nardata;stato'sya gāndhārapatiḥ śiro'harat.
english: But the younger brother of the Kuṇinda's son, with iron javelins resembling sunbeams, agitated (Kṛpa's) chariot and roared. As he was roaring, the Lord of Gandhāra (Śakuni) then cut off his head.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 62 Verse 38. But the younger brother of the Kuṇinda's son, with iron javelins resembling sunbeams, agitated (Kṛpa's) chariot and roared. As he was roaring, the Lord of Gandhāra (Śakuni) then cut off his head.

sanskrit: ततः कुणिन्देषु हतेषु तेष्वथ प्रहृष्टरूपास्तव ते महारथाः ।भृशं प्रदध्मुर्लवणाम्बुसंभवान्परांश्च बाणासनपाणयोऽभ्ययुः ॥३९॥
transliteration: tataḥ kuṇindeṣu hateṣu teṣvatha; prahṛṣṭarūpāstava te mahārathāḥ ,bhṛśaṁ pradadhmurlavaṇāmbusaṁbhavā;nparāṁśca bāṇāsanapāṇayo'bhyayuḥ.
english: Then, when those Kuṇindas had been killed, your great charioteers, with joyful expressions and bows in hand, loudly blew their conches (born from saltwater) and advanced against the others (enemies).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 62 Verse 39. Then, when those Kuṇindas had been killed, your great charioteers, with joyful expressions and bows in hand, loudly blew their conches (born from saltwater) and advanced against the others (enemies).

sanskrit: अथाभवद्युद्धमतीव दारुणं पुनः कुरूणां सह पाण्डुसृञ्जयैः ।शरासिशक्त्यृष्टिगदापरश्वधैर्नराश्वनागासुहरं भृशाकुलम् ॥४०॥
transliteration: athābhavadyuddhamatīva dāruṇaṁ; punaḥ kurūṇāṁ saha pāṇḍusṛñjayaiḥ ,śarāsiśaktyṛṣṭigadāparaśvadhai;rnarāśvanāgāsuharaṁ bhṛśākulam.
english: Then again, an exceedingly dreadful and intensely chaotic battle occurred between the Kurus and the Pāṇḍavas and Sr̥ñjayas. It destroyed the lives of men, horses, and elephants, (fought) with arrows, swords, spears, lances, maces, and axes.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 62 Verse 40. Then again, an exceedingly dreadful and intensely chaotic battle occurred between the Kurus and the Pāṇḍavas and Sr̥ñjayas. It destroyed the lives of men, horses, and elephants, (fought) with arrows, swords, spears, lances, maces, and axes.

sanskrit: रथाश्वमातङ्गपदातिभिस्ततः परस्परं विप्रहतापतन्क्षितौ ।यथा सविद्युत्स्तनिता बलाहकाः समास्थिता दिग्भ्य इवोग्रमारुतैः ॥४१॥
transliteration: rathāśvamātaṅgapadātibhistataḥ; parasparaṁ viprahatāpatankṣitau ,yathā savidyutstanitā balāhakāḥ; samāsthitā digbhya ivogramārutaiḥ.
english: Then, (warriors) along with their chariots, horses, elephants, and foot soldiers, having struck one another, fell to the earth, just as clouds, accompanied by lightning and thunder, gather from all directions as if driven by fierce winds.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 62 Verse 41. Then, (warriors) along with their chariots, horses, elephants, and foot soldiers, having struck one another, fell to the earth, just as clouds, accompanied by lightning and thunder, gather from all directions as if driven by fierce winds.

sanskrit: ततः शतानीकहतान्महागजांस्तथा रथान्पत्तिगणांश्च तावकान् ।जघान भोजश्च हयानथापतन्विशस्त्रकृत्ताः कृतवर्मणा द्विपाः ॥४२॥
transliteration: tataḥ śatānīkahatānmahāgajāṁ;stathā rathānpattigaṇāṁśca tāvakān ,jaghāna bhojaśca hayānathāpata;nviśastrakṛttāḥ kṛtavarmaṇā dvipāḥ.
english: Then, Bhoja killed your great elephants, as well as your chariots, infantry troops, and horses, (all of them having been previously) struck down by Śatānīka. And then, elephants, cut down and disarmed by Kr̥tavarmā, fell.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 62 Verse 42. Then, Bhoja killed your great elephants, as well as your chariots, infantry troops, and horses, (all of them having been previously) struck down by Śatānīka. And then, elephants, cut down and disarmed by Kr̥tavarmā, fell.

sanskrit: अथापरे द्रौणिशराहता द्विपास्त्रयः ससर्वायुधयोधकेतवः ।निपेतुरुर्व्यां व्यसवः प्रपातितास्तथा यथा वज्रहता महाचलाः ॥४३॥
transliteration: athāpare drauṇiśarāhatā dvipā;strayaḥ sasarvāyudhayodhaketavaḥ ,nipetururvyāṁ vyasavaḥ prapātitā;stathā yathā vajrahatā mahācalāḥ.
english: Then, three other elephants, struck by the arrows of Droṇa's son (Aśvatthāmā), fell lifeless upon the earth along with their weapons, warriors, and banners, just like mighty mountains struck down by a thunderbolt (vajra).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 62 Verse 43. Then, three other elephants, struck by the arrows of Droṇa's son (Aśvatthāmā), fell lifeless upon the earth along with their weapons, warriors, and banners, just like mighty mountains struck down by a thunderbolt (vajra).

sanskrit: कुणिन्दराजावरजादनन्तरः स्तनान्तरे पत्रिवरैरताडयत् ।तवात्मजं तस्य तवात्मजः शरैः शितैः शरीरं बिभिदे द्विपं च तम् ॥४४॥
transliteration: kuṇindarājāvarajādanantaraḥ; stanāntare patrivarairatāḍayat ,tavātmajaṁ tasya tavātmajaḥ śaraiḥ; śitaiḥ śarīraṁ bibhide dvipaṁ ca tam.
english: Immediately after, the younger brother of the Kuṇinda king struck your son in the chest with his excellent arrows. Then, your son (referring to a Kuru prince) pierced both his (the Kuṇinda prince's) body and his elephant with sharp arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 62 Verse 44. Immediately after, the younger brother of the Kuṇinda king struck your son in the chest with his excellent arrows. Then, your son (referring to a Kuru prince) pierced both his (the Kuṇinda prince's) body and his elephant with sharp arrows.

sanskrit: स नागराजः सह राजसूनुना पपात रक्तं बहु सर्वतः क्षरन् ।शचीशवज्रप्रहतोऽम्बुदागमे यथा जलं गैरिकपर्वतस्तथा ॥४५॥
transliteration: sa nāgarājaḥ saha rājasūnunā; papāta raktaṁ bahu sarvataḥ kṣaran ,śacīśavajraprahato'mbudāgame; yathā jalaṁ gairikaparvatastathā.
english: That chief elephant (nāgarāja), along with the prince, fell, profusely shedding blood from all sides, just as a mountain of red ochre (gairika-parvata), struck by Indra's (Śacīśa's) thunderbolt (vajra) at the onset of the rainy season, pours forth water.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 62 Verse 45. That chief elephant (nāgarāja), along with the prince, fell, profusely shedding blood from all sides, just as a mountain of red ochre (gairika-parvata), struck by Indra's (Śacīśa's) thunderbolt (vajra) at the onset of the rainy season, pours forth water.

sanskrit: कुणिन्दपुत्रप्रहितोऽपरद्विपः शुकं ससूताश्वरथं व्यपोथयत् ।ततोऽपतत्क्राथशराभिदारितः सहेश्वरो वज्रहतो यथा गिरिः ॥४६॥
transliteration: kuṇindaputraprahito'paradvipaḥ; śukaṁ sasūtāśvarathaṁ vyapothayat ,tato'patatkrāthaśarābhidāritaḥ; saheśvaro vajrahato yathā giriḥ.
english: Another elephant, dispatched by the Kuṇinda prince, crushed Śuka along with his charioteer, horses, and chariot. Then, Śuka, torn apart by Krātha's arrows, fell along with his lord, just like a mountain struck by a thunderbolt (vajra).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 62 Verse 46. Another elephant, dispatched by the Kuṇinda prince, crushed Śuka along with his charioteer, horses, and chariot. Then, Śuka, torn apart by Krātha's arrows, fell along with his lord, just like a mountain struck by a thunderbolt (vajra).

sanskrit: रथी द्विपस्थेन हतोऽपतच्छरैः क्राथाधिपः पर्वतजेन दुर्जयः ।सवाजिसूतेष्वसनस्तथापतद्यथा महावातहतो महाद्रुमः ॥४७॥
transliteration: rathī dvipasthena hato'pataccharaiḥ; krāthādhipaḥ parvatajena durjayaḥ ,savājisūteṣvasanastathāpata;dyathā mahāvātahato mahādrumaḥ.
english: The unconquerable lord of Krātha, fighting from a chariot, was struck by arrows by Parvataja, who was mounted on an elephant, and fell. He collapsed along with his horses, charioteer, arrows, and bow, just like a mighty tree uprooted by a fierce gale.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 62 Verse 47. The unconquerable lord of Krātha, fighting from a chariot, was struck by arrows by Parvataja, who was mounted on an elephant, and fell. He collapsed along with his horses, charioteer, arrows, and bow, just like a mighty tree uprooted by a fierce gale.

sanskrit: वृको द्विपस्थं गिरिराजवासिनं भृशं शरैर्द्वादशभिः पराभिनत् ।ततो वृकं साश्वरथं महाजवं त्वरंश्चतुर्भिश्चरणे व्यपोथयत् ॥४८॥
transliteration: vṛko dvipasthaṁ girirājavāsinaṁ; bhṛśaṁ śarairdvādaśabhiḥ parābhinat ,tato vṛkaṁ sāśvarathaṁ mahājavaṁ; tvaraṁścaturbhiścaraṇe vyapothayat.
english: Vṛka intensely pierced the elephant-mounted dweller of the mountain region with twelve arrows. Then, the elephant rider, hastening, crushed Vṛka, who was equipped with swift horses and a chariot, with his four feet.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 62 Verse 48. Vṛka intensely pierced the elephant-mounted dweller of the mountain region with twelve arrows. Then, the elephant rider, hastening, crushed Vṛka, who was equipped with swift horses and a chariot, with his four feet.

sanskrit: स नागराजः सनियन्तृकोऽपतत्पराहतो बभ्रुसुतेषुभिर्भृशम् ।स चापि देवावृधसूनुरर्दितः पपात नुन्नः सहदेवसूनुना ॥४९॥
transliteration: sa nāgarājaḥ saniyantṛko'pata;tparāhato babhrusuteṣubhirbhṛśam ,sa cāpi devāvṛdhasūnurarditaḥ; papāta nunnaḥ sahadevasūnunā.
english: That great elephant (nāgarāja), along with its driver, fell, having been severely struck by numerous arrows from Babhru's son. And Devāvṛdha's son, also afflicted, fell, having been pushed down by Sahadeva's son.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 62 Verse 49. That great elephant (nāgarāja), along with its driver, fell, having been severely struck by numerous arrows from Babhru's son. And Devāvṛdha's son, also afflicted, fell, having been pushed down by Sahadeva's son.

sanskrit: विषाणपोत्रापरगात्रघातिना गजेन हन्तुं शकुनेः कुणिन्दजः ।जगाम वेगेन भृशार्दयंश्च तं ततोऽस्य गान्धारपतिः शिरोऽहरत् ॥५०॥
transliteration: viṣāṇapotrāparagātraghātinā; gajena hantuṁ śakuneḥ kuṇindajaḥ ,jagāma vegena bhṛśārdayaṁśca taṁ; tato'sya gāndhārapatiḥ śiro'harat.
english: The son of Kuṇinda advanced swiftly with an elephant skilled at striking other limbs with its tusks and trunk, intending to kill Śakuni and fiercely assailing him. Then, the lord of Gandhāra cut off his (Kuṇindaja's) head.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 62 Verse 50. The son of Kuṇinda advanced swiftly with an elephant skilled at striking other limbs with its tusks and trunk, intending to kill Śakuni and fiercely assailing him. Then, the lord of Gandhāra cut off his (Kuṇindaja's) head.

sanskrit: ततः शतानीकहता महागजा हया रथाः पत्तिगणाश्च तावकाः ।सुपर्णवातप्रहता यथा नगास्तथा गता गामवशा विचूर्णिताः ॥५१॥
transliteration: tataḥ śatānīkahatā mahāgajā; hayā rathāḥ pattigaṇāśca tāvakāḥ ,suparṇavātaprahatā yathā nagā;stathā gatā gāmavaśā vicūrṇitāḥ.
english: Then your great elephants, horses, chariots, and infantry forces, struck down by Śatānīka, fell helplessly to the ground, shattered, just like trees struck by a fierce gale.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 62 Verse 51. Then your great elephants, horses, chariots, and infantry forces, struck down by Śatānīka, fell helplessly to the ground, shattered, just like trees struck by a fierce gale.

sanskrit: ततोऽभ्यविध्यद्बहुभिः शितैः शरैः कुणिन्दपुत्रो नकुलात्मजं स्मयन् ।ततोऽस्य कायान्निचकर्त नाकुलिः शिरः क्षुरेणाम्बुजसंनिभाननम् ॥५२॥
transliteration: tato'bhyavidhyadbahubhiḥ śitaiḥ śaraiḥ; kuṇindaputro nakulātmajaṁ smayan ,tato'sya kāyānnicakarta nākuliḥ; śiraḥ kṣureṇāmbujasaṁnibhānanam.
english: Then the son of Kuṇinda (Dhṛṣṭadyumna), smiling, pierced the son of Nakula (Śatānīka) with many sharp arrows. Thereupon, Nakuli (Śatānīka) cut off his head - whose face resembled a lotus - from his body with a razor-sharp arrow.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 62 Verse 52. Then the son of Kuṇinda (Dhṛṣṭadyumna), smiling, pierced the son of Nakula (Śatānīka) with many sharp arrows. Thereupon, Nakuli (Śatānīka) cut off his head - whose face resembled a lotus - from his body with a razor-sharp arrow.

sanskrit: ततः शतानीकमविध्यदाशुगैस्त्रिभिः शितैः कर्णसुतोऽर्जुनं त्रिभिः ।त्रिभिश्च भीमं नकुलं च सप्तभिर्जनार्दनं द्वादशभिश्च सायकैः ॥५३॥
transliteration: tataḥ śatānīkamavidhyadāśugai;stribhiḥ śitaiḥ karṇasuto'rjunaṁ tribhiḥ ,tribhiśca bhīmaṁ nakulaṁ ca saptabhi;rjanārdanaṁ dvādaśabhiśca sāyakaiḥ.
english: Then Karna's son (Vṛṣasena) pierced Śatānīka with three sharp, swift arrows, Arjuna with three, Bhīma with three, Nakula with seven, and Janārdana (Kṛṣṇa) with twelve arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 62 Verse 53. Then Karna's son (Vṛṣasena) pierced Śatānīka with three sharp, swift arrows, Arjuna with three, Bhīma with three, Nakula with seven, and Janārdana (Kṛṣṇa) with twelve arrows.

sanskrit: तदस्य कर्मातिमनुष्यकर्मणः समीक्ष्य हृष्टाः कुरवोऽभ्यपूजयन् ।पराक्रमज्ञास्तु धनंजयस्य ते हुतोऽयमग्नाविति तं तु मेनिरे ॥५४॥
transliteration: tadasya karmātimanuṣyakarmaṇaḥ; samīkṣya hṛṣṭāḥ kuravo'bhyapūjayan ,parākramajñāstu dhanaṁjayasya te; huto'yamagnāviti taṁ tu menire.
english: Observing that act of his (Vṛṣasena's), which was a superhuman deed (karma), the Kurus were delighted and cheered. But those who understood Dhanaṃjaya's (Arjuna's) valor considered him (Vṛṣasena) as if he had been offered into the fire, meaning doomed.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 62 Verse 54. Observing that act of his (Vṛṣasena's), which was a superhuman deed (karma), the Kurus were delighted and cheered. But those who understood Dhanaṃjaya's (Arjuna's) valor considered him (Vṛṣasena) as if he had been offered into the fire, meaning doomed.

sanskrit: ततः किरीटी परवीरघाती हताश्वमालोक्य नरप्रवीरम् ।तमभ्यधावद्वृषसेनमाहवे स सूतजस्य प्रमुखे स्थितं तदा ॥५५॥
transliteration: tataḥ kirīṭī paravīraghātī; hatāśvamālokya narapravīram ,tamabhyadhāvadvṛṣasenamāhave; sa sūtajasya pramukhe sthitaṁ tadā.
english: Then, Kirīṭī (Arjuna), the slayer of enemy heroes, seeing that foremost among men (Vṛṣasena) whose horses were slain, rushed towards him in battle, as he stood before Karṇa, the son of the charioteer.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 62 Verse 55. Then, Kirīṭī (Arjuna), the slayer of enemy heroes, seeing that foremost among men (Vṛṣasena) whose horses were slain, rushed towards him in battle, as he stood before Karṇa, the son of the charioteer.

sanskrit: तमापतन्तं नरवीरमुग्रं महाहवे बाणसहस्रधारिणम् ।अभ्यापतत्कर्णसुतो महारथो यथैव चेन्द्रं नमुचिः पुरातने ॥५६॥
transliteration: tamāpatantaṁ naravīramugraṁ; mahāhave bāṇasahasradhāriṇam ,abhyāpatatkarṇasuto mahāratho; yathaiva cendraṁ namuciḥ purātane.
english: Towards that fierce hero among men (Arjuna), who was rushing in the great battle, holding thousands of arrows, Karṇa's son, the great charioteer (Vṛṣasena), rushed, just as Namuci (the demon) had rushed towards Indra in ancient times.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 62 Verse 56. Towards that fierce hero among men (Arjuna), who was rushing in the great battle, holding thousands of arrows, Karṇa's son, the great charioteer (Vṛṣasena), rushed, just as Namuci (the demon) had rushed towards Indra in ancient times.

sanskrit: ततोऽद्भुतेनैकशतेन पार्थं शरैर्विद्ध्वा सूतपुत्रस्य पुत्रः ।ननाद नादं सुमहानुभावो विद्ध्वेव शक्रं नमुचिः पुरा वै ॥५७॥
transliteration: tato'dbhutenaikaśatena pārthaṁ; śarairviddhvā sūtaputrasya putraḥ ,nanāda nādaṁ sumahānubhāvo; viddhveva śakraṁ namuciḥ purā vai.
english: Then, the son of Karṇa (Vṛṣasena), possessing great prowess, having pierced Pārtha (Arjuna) with a wonderful hundred arrows, roared loudly, just as Namuci had indeed roared after piercing Indra in ancient times.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 62 Verse 57. Then, the son of Karṇa (Vṛṣasena), possessing great prowess, having pierced Pārtha (Arjuna) with a wonderful hundred arrows, roared loudly, just as Namuci had indeed roared after piercing Indra in ancient times.

sanskrit: पुनः स पार्थं वृषसेन उग्रैर्बाणैरविध्यद्भुजमूलमध्ये ।तथैव कृष्णं नवभिः समार्दयत्पुनश्च पार्थं दशभिः शिताग्रैः ॥५८॥
transliteration: punaḥ sa pārthaṁ vṛṣasena ugrai;rbāṇairavidhyadbhujamūlamadhye ,tathaiva kṛṣṇaṁ navabhiḥ samārdaya;tpunaśca pārthaṁ daśabhiḥ śitāgraiḥ.
english: Again, that Vṛṣasena pierced Pārtha (Arjuna) with fierce arrows in the shoulder region. Similarly, he wounded Kṛṣṇa with nine (arrows), and again Pārtha with ten sharp-pointed (arrows).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 62 Verse 58. Again, that Vṛṣasena pierced Pārtha (Arjuna) with fierce arrows in the shoulder region. Similarly, he wounded Kṛṣṇa with nine (arrows), and again Pārtha with ten sharp-pointed (arrows).

sanskrit: ततः किरीटी रणमूर्ध्नि कोपात्कृत्वा त्रिशाखां भ्रुकुटिं ललाटे ।मुमोच बाणान्विशिखान्महात्मा वधाय राजन्सूतपुत्रस्य संख्ये ॥५९॥
transliteration: tataḥ kirīṭī raṇamūrdhni kopā;tkṛtvā triśākhāṁ bhrukuṭiṁ lalāṭe ,mumoca bāṇānviśikhānmahātmā; vadhāya rājansūtaputrasya saṁkhye.
english: O King, then, the great-souled (mahātman) Arjuna, the crowned one, with a three-pronged frown on his forehead born of anger on the battlefield, released sharp arrows to slay Karṇa, the son of the charioteer, in that combat.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 62 Verse 59. O King, then, the great-souled (mahātman) Arjuna, the crowned one, with a three-pronged frown on his forehead born of anger on the battlefield, released sharp arrows to slay Karṇa, the son of the charioteer, in that combat.

sanskrit: विव्याध चैनं दशभिः पृषत्कैर्मर्मस्वसक्तं प्रसभं किरीटी ।चिच्छेद चास्येष्वसनं भुजौ च क्षुरैश्चतुर्भिः शिर एव चोग्रैः ॥६०॥
transliteration: vivyādha cainaṁ daśabhiḥ pṛṣatkai;rmarmasvasaktaṁ prasabhaṁ kirīṭī ,ciccheda cāsyeṣvasanaṁ bhujau ca; kṣuraiścaturbhiḥ śira eva cograiḥ.
english: The crowned Arjuna (kirīṭī) forcibly pierced him (Karṇa) with ten arrows, unerringly striking his vital spots (marmasu). With four fierce, razor-sharp arrows, he also severed Karṇa's bow, his two arms, and indeed his head.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 62 Verse 60. The crowned Arjuna (kirīṭī) forcibly pierced him (Karṇa) with ten arrows, unerringly striking his vital spots (marmasu). With four fierce, razor-sharp arrows, he also severed Karṇa's bow, his two arms, and indeed his head.

sanskrit: स पार्थबाणाभिहतः पपात रथाद्विबाहुर्विशिरा धरायाम् ।सुपुष्पितः पर्णधरोऽतिकायो वातेरितः शाल इवाद्रिशृङ्गात् ॥६१॥
transliteration: sa pārthabāṇābhihataḥ papāta; rathādvibāhurviśirā dharāyām ,supuṣpitaḥ parṇadharo'tikāyo; vāteritaḥ śāla ivādriśṛṅgāt.
english: Struck by Arjuna's arrows (pārthabāṇābhihata), he (Karṇa), armless and headless, fell from his chariot to the ground. He was like a mighty, well-blossomed, leafy Sāla tree (śāla) with a huge trunk (atikāya) uprooted by the wind from a mountain peak.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 62 Verse 61. Struck by Arjuna's arrows (pārthabāṇābhihata), he (Karṇa), armless and headless, fell from his chariot to the ground. He was like a mighty, well-blossomed, leafy Sāla tree (śāla) with a huge trunk (atikāya) uprooted by the wind from a mountain peak.

sanskrit: तं प्रेक्ष्य बाणाभिहतं पतन्तं रथात्सुतं सूतजः क्षिप्रकारी ।रथं रथेनाशु जगाम वेगात्किरीटिनः पुत्रवधाभितप्तः ॥६२॥
transliteration: taṁ prekṣya bāṇābhihataṁ patantaṁ; rathātsutaṁ sūtajaḥ kṣiprakārī ,rathaṁ rathenāśu jagāma vegā;tkirīṭinaḥ putravadhābhitaptaḥ.
english: Seeing that son (Vṛṣasena) of his, struck by arrows and falling from the chariot, the swift-acting Karṇa, the son of the charioteer, tormented by the death of his son, swiftly rushed with his own chariot towards Arjuna's (kirīṭī) chariot.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 62 Verse 62. Seeing that son (Vṛṣasena) of his, struck by arrows and falling from the chariot, the swift-acting Karṇa, the son of the charioteer, tormented by the death of his son, swiftly rushed with his own chariot towards Arjuna's (kirīṭī) chariot.

sanskrit: संजय उवाच ।वृषसेनं हतं दृष्ट्वा शोकामर्षसमन्वितः ।मुक्त्वा शोकोद्भवं वारि नेत्राभ्यां सहसा वृषः ॥१॥
transliteration: saṁjaya uvāca ,vṛṣasenaṁ hataṁ dṛṣṭvā śokāmarṣasamanvitaḥ ,muktvā śokodbhavaṁ vāri netrābhyāṁ sahasā vṛṣaḥ.
