english: Sañjaya said: Having seen Vṛṣasena killed, Vṛṣa (Karṇa), filled with sorrow and indignation, suddenly shed tears born of grief from his eyes.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 63 Verse 1. Sañjaya said: Having seen Vṛṣasena killed, Vṛṣa (Karṇa), filled with sorrow and indignation, suddenly shed tears born of grief from his eyes.

sanskrit: रथेन कर्णस्तेजस्वी जगामाभिमुखो रिपून् ।युद्धायामर्षताम्राक्षः समाहूय धनंजयम् ॥२॥
transliteration: rathena karṇastejasvī jagāmābhimukho ripūn ,yuddhāyāmarṣatāmrākṣaḥ samāhūya dhanaṁjayam.
english: The splendid Karṇa, whose eyes were reddish from indignation for battle, went towards the enemies in his chariot, having challenged Dhanañjaya (Arjuna).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 63 Verse 2. The splendid Karṇa, whose eyes were reddish from indignation for battle, went towards the enemies in his chariot, having challenged Dhanañjaya (Arjuna).

sanskrit: तौ रथौ सूर्यसंकाशौ वैयाघ्रपरिवारणौ ।समेतौ ददृशुस्तत्र द्वाविवार्कौ समागतौ ॥३॥
transliteration: tau rathau sūryasaṁkāśau vaiyāghraparivāraṇau ,sametau dadṛśustatra dvāvivārkau samāgatau.
english: Those two chariots, resembling the sun and covered with tiger skins, were seen there, like two suns that have come together.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 63 Verse 3. Those two chariots, resembling the sun and covered with tiger skins, were seen there, like two suns that have come together.

sanskrit: श्वेताश्वौ पुरुषादित्यावास्थितावरिमर्दनौ ।शुशुभाते महात्मानौ चन्द्रादित्यौ यथा दिवि ॥४॥
transliteration: śvetāśvau puruṣādityāvāsthitāvarimardanau ,śuśubhāte mahātmānau candrādityau yathā divi.
english: The two great-souled (mahātman) ones, riding white horses and destroying enemies, shone like the moon and the sun in the sky.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 63 Verse 4. The two great-souled (mahātman) ones, riding white horses and destroying enemies, shone like the moon and the sun in the sky.

sanskrit: तौ दृष्ट्वा विस्मयं जग्मुः सर्वभूतानि मारिष ।त्रैलोक्यविजये यत्ताविन्द्रवैरोचनाविव ॥५॥
transliteration: tau dṛṣṭvā vismayaṁ jagmuḥ sarvabhūtāni māriṣa ,trailokyavijaye yattāvindravairocanāviva.
english: O respected one, upon seeing those two, all beings were filled with wonder, for they were striving for victory as fiercely as Indra and Virocana's son (Bali) once did in their conquest of the three worlds.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 63 Verse 5. O respected one, upon seeing those two, all beings were filled with wonder, for they were striving for victory as fiercely as Indra and Virocana's son (Bali) once did in their conquest of the three worlds.

sanskrit: रथज्यातलनिर्ह्रादैर्बाणशङ्खरवैरपि ।तौ रथावभिधावन्तौ समालोक्य महीक्षिताम् ॥६॥
transliteration: rathajyātalanirhrādairbāṇaśaṅkharavairapi ,tau rathāvabhidhāvantau samālokya mahīkṣitām.
english: Having thoroughly observed those two chariots of the kings, rushing forward with the mighty sounds of bowstrings, hand-slaps, arrows, and conches...
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 63 Verse 6. Having thoroughly observed those two chariots of the kings, rushing forward with the mighty sounds of bowstrings, hand-slaps, arrows, and conches...

sanskrit: ध्वजौ च दृष्ट्वा संसक्तौ विस्मयः समपद्यत ।हस्तिकक्ष्यां च कर्णस्य वानरं च किरीटिनः ॥७॥
transliteration: dhvajau ca dṛṣṭvā saṁsaktau vismayaḥ samapadyata ,hastikakṣyāṁ ca karṇasya vānaraṁ ca kirīṭinaḥ.
english: And when they observed the two banners, Karna's (displaying) the elephant emblem and the diadem-wearer's (Arjuna's) (displaying) the monkey, in close contact, astonishment arose.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 63 Verse 7. And when they observed the two banners, Karna's (displaying) the elephant emblem and the diadem-wearer's (Arjuna's) (displaying) the monkey, in close contact, astonishment arose.

sanskrit: तौ रथौ संप्रसक्तौ च दृष्ट्वा भारत पार्थिवाः ।सिंहनादरवांश्चक्रुः साधुवादांश्च पुष्कलान् ॥८॥
transliteration: tau rathau saṁprasaktau ca dṛṣṭvā bhārata pārthivāḥ ,siṁhanādaravāṁścakruḥ sādhuvādāṁśca puṣkalān.
english: O descendant of Bharata (bhārata), having seen those two chariots closely engaged, the kings let out lion-like roars and abundant shouts of approval.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 63 Verse 8. O descendant of Bharata (bhārata), having seen those two chariots closely engaged, the kings let out lion-like roars and abundant shouts of approval.

sanskrit: श्रुत्वा तु द्वैरथं ताभ्यां तत्र योधाः समन्ततः ।चक्रुर्बाहुवलं चैव तथा चेलवलं महत् ॥९॥
transliteration: śrutvā tu dvairathaṁ tābhyāṁ tatra yodhāḥ samantataḥ ,cakrurbāhuvalaṁ caiva tathā celavalaṁ mahat.
english: Upon hearing about the chariot duel between those two, warriors from all sides then displayed their physical strength and also created a great uproar.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 63 Verse 9. Upon hearing about the chariot duel between those two, warriors from all sides then displayed their physical strength and also created a great uproar.

sanskrit: आजग्मुः कुरवस्तत्र वादित्रानुगतास्तदा ।कर्णं प्रहर्षयन्तश्च शङ्खान्दध्मुश्च पुष्कलान् ॥१०॥
transliteration: ājagmuḥ kuravastatra vāditrānugatāstadā ,karṇaṁ praharṣayantaśca śaṅkhāndadhmuśca puṣkalān.
english: Then, the Kurus arrived there, accompanied by musical instruments, delighting Karṇa, and they blew many loud conches.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 63 Verse 10. Then, the Kurus arrived there, accompanied by musical instruments, delighting Karṇa, and they blew many loud conches.

sanskrit: तथैव पाण्डवाः सर्वे हर्षयन्तो धनंजयम् ।तूर्यशङ्खनिनादेन दिशः सर्वा व्यनादयन् ॥११॥
transliteration: tathaiva pāṇḍavāḥ sarve harṣayanto dhanaṁjayam ,tūryaśaṅkhaninādena diśaḥ sarvā vyanādayan.
english: In the same way, all the Pāṇḍavas, encouraging Dhanañjaya (Arjuna), made all directions resound with the sound of trumpets and conches.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 63 Verse 11. In the same way, all the Pāṇḍavas, encouraging Dhanañjaya (Arjuna), made all directions resound with the sound of trumpets and conches.

sanskrit: क्ष्वेडितास्फोटितोत्क्रुष्टैस्तुमुलं सर्वतोऽभवत् ।बाहुघोषाश्च वीराणां कर्णार्जुनसमागमे ॥१२॥
transliteration: kṣveḍitāsphoṭitotkruṣṭaistumulaṁ sarvato'bhavat ,bāhughoṣāśca vīrāṇāṁ karṇārjunasamāgame.
english: From all sides, there arose a great tumult from shouts, the slapping of arms, and loud cries. There were also the roars of the heroes' arms during the encounter between Karṇa and Arjuna.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 63 Verse 12. From all sides, there arose a great tumult from shouts, the slapping of arms, and loud cries. There were also the roars of the heroes' arms during the encounter between Karṇa and Arjuna.

sanskrit: तौ दृष्ट्वा पुरुषव्याघ्रौ रथस्थौ रथिनां वरौ ।प्रगृहीतमहाचापौ शरशक्तिगदायुधौ ॥१३॥
transliteration: tau dṛṣṭvā puruṣavyāghrau rathasthau rathināṁ varau ,pragṛhītamahācāpau śaraśaktigadāyudhau.
english: Having seen those two, who were like tigers among men (puruṣavyāghrau), the best among charioteers, standing on their chariots, holding their great bows, and armed with arrows, spears, and maces.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 63 Verse 13. Having seen those two, who were like tigers among men (puruṣavyāghrau), the best among charioteers, standing on their chariots, holding their great bows, and armed with arrows, spears, and maces.

sanskrit: वर्मिणौ बद्धनिस्त्रिंशौ श्वेताश्वौ शङ्खशोभिनौ ।तूणीरवरसंपन्नौ द्वावपि स्म सुदर्शनौ ॥१४॥
transliteration: varmiṇau baddhanistriṁśau śvetāśvau śaṅkhaśobhinau ,tūṇīravarasaṁpannau dvāvapi sma sudarśanau.
english: Both of them were indeed handsome (sudarśanau), armored, with swords fastened, mounted on white horses, adorned with conches, and equipped with excellent quivers.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 63 Verse 14. Both of them were indeed handsome (sudarśanau), armored, with swords fastened, mounted on white horses, adorned with conches, and equipped with excellent quivers.

sanskrit: रक्तचन्दनदिग्धाङ्गौ समदौ वृषभाविव ।आशीविषसमप्रख्यौ यमकालान्तकोपमौ ॥१५॥
transliteration: raktacandanadigdhāṅgau samadau vṛṣabhāviva ,āśīviṣasamaprakhyau yamakālāntakopamau.
english: They were like two intoxicated bulls (vṛṣabhau) with limbs smeared with red sandalwood paste, comparable to venomous serpents, and resembling Yama, Time, and Antaka (the Destroyer).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 63 Verse 15. They were like two intoxicated bulls (vṛṣabhau) with limbs smeared with red sandalwood paste, comparable to venomous serpents, and resembling Yama, Time, and Antaka (the Destroyer).

sanskrit: इन्द्रवृत्राविव क्रुद्धौ सूर्याचन्द्रमसप्रभौ ।महाग्रहाविव क्रूरौ युगान्ते समुपस्थितौ ॥१६॥
transliteration: indravṛtrāviva kruddhau sūryācandramasaprabhau ,mahāgrahāviva krūrau yugānte samupasthitau.
english: They were enraged like Indra and Vṛtra (indravṛtrāviva), shining with the splendor of the sun and moon, and fierce like two great planets (mahāgrahau) present at the end of a cosmic age (yugānte).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 63 Verse 16. They were enraged like Indra and Vṛtra (indravṛtrāviva), shining with the splendor of the sun and moon, and fierce like two great planets (mahāgrahau) present at the end of a cosmic age (yugānte).

sanskrit: देवगर्भौ देवसमौ देवतुल्यौ च रूपतः ।समेतौ पुरुषव्याघ्रौ प्रेक्ष्य कर्णधनंजयौ ॥१७॥
transliteration: devagarbhau devasamau devatulyau ca rūpataḥ ,sametau puruṣavyāghrau prekṣya karṇadhanaṁjayau.
english: Observing Karna and Dhananjaya, those tigers among men (puruṣa), who were of divine birth, equal to the gods, and similar to them in form, having met...
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 63 Verse 17. Observing Karna and Dhananjaya, those tigers among men (puruṣa), who were of divine birth, equal to the gods, and similar to them in form, having met...

sanskrit: उभौ वरायुधधरावुभौ रणकृतश्रमौ ।उभौ च बाहुशब्देन नादयन्तौ नभस्तलम् ॥१८॥
transliteration: ubhau varāyudhadharāvubhau raṇakṛtaśramau ,ubhau ca bāhuśabdena nādayantau nabhastalam.
english: Both were bearing excellent weapons, both had exerted themselves in battle, and both were making the sky resound with the sound of their arms.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 63 Verse 18. Both were bearing excellent weapons, both had exerted themselves in battle, and both were making the sky resound with the sound of their arms.

sanskrit: उभौ विश्रुतकर्माणौ पौरुषेण बलेन च ।उभौ च सदृशौ युद्धे शम्बरामरराजयोः ॥१९॥
transliteration: ubhau viśrutakarmāṇau pauruṣeṇa balena ca ,ubhau ca sadṛśau yuddhe śambarāmararājayoḥ.
english: Both were renowned for their deeds (karma), valor, and strength; and both were equal in battle to Shambara and the king of the gods.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 63 Verse 19. Both were renowned for their deeds (karma), valor, and strength; and both were equal in battle to Shambara and the king of the gods.

sanskrit: कार्तवीर्यसमौ युद्धे तथा दाशरथेः समौ ।विष्णुवीर्यसमौ वीर्ये तथा भवसमौ युधि ॥२०॥
transliteration: kārtavīryasamau yuddhe tathā dāśaratheḥ samau ,viṣṇuvīryasamau vīrye tathā bhavasamau yudhi.
english: They were equal to Kartavirya in battle, and similarly equal to Rama, the son of Dasharatha. They were equal to Vishnu in might (vīrya), and likewise equal to Bhava (Shiva) in combat.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 63 Verse 20. They were equal to Kartavirya in battle, and similarly equal to Rama, the son of Dasharatha. They were equal to Vishnu in might (vīrya), and likewise equal to Bhava (Shiva) in combat.

sanskrit: उभौ श्वेतहयौ राजन्रथप्रवरवाहिनौ ।सारथी प्रवरौ चैव तयोरास्तां महाबलौ ॥२१॥
transliteration: ubhau śvetahayau rājanrathapravaravāhinau ,sārathī pravarau caiva tayorāstāṁ mahābalau.
english: O King, both (Arjuna and Krishna) were present, (they who were) associated with white horses and were drivers of excellent chariots. And indeed, they themselves were excellent and exceedingly mighty charioteers.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 63 Verse 21. O King, both (Arjuna and Krishna) were present, (they who were) associated with white horses and were drivers of excellent chariots. And indeed, they themselves were excellent and exceedingly mighty charioteers.

sanskrit: तौ तु दृष्ट्वा महाराज राजमानौ महारथौ ।सिद्धचारणसंघानां विस्मयः समपद्यत ॥२२॥
transliteration: tau tu dṛṣṭvā mahārāja rājamānau mahārathau ,siddhacāraṇasaṁghānāṁ vismayaḥ samapadyata.
english: But, O great king, when (the celestial beings) saw those two (Arjuna and Krishna), shining and great warriors (mahārathau), astonishment arose among the hosts of Siddhas and Caraṇas.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 63 Verse 22. But, O great king, when (the celestial beings) saw those two (Arjuna and Krishna), shining and great warriors (mahārathau), astonishment arose among the hosts of Siddhas and Caraṇas.

sanskrit: धार्तराष्ट्रास्ततः कर्णं सबला भरतर्षभ ।परिवव्रुर्महात्मानं क्षिप्रमाहवशोभिनम् ॥२३॥
transliteration: dhārtarāṣṭrāstataḥ karṇaṁ sabalā bharatarṣabha ,parivavrurmahātmānaṁ kṣipramāhavaśobhinam.
english: O best of Bhāratas, then the sons of Dhṛtarāṣṭra, along with their forces, swiftly surrounded the great-souled (ātman) Karṇa, who was resplendent (śobhinam) in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 63 Verse 23. O best of Bhāratas, then the sons of Dhṛtarāṣṭra, along with their forces, swiftly surrounded the great-souled (ātman) Karṇa, who was resplendent (śobhinam) in battle.

sanskrit: तथैव पाण्डवा हृष्टा धृष्टद्युम्नपुरोगमाः ।परिवव्रुर्महात्मानं पार्थमप्रतिमं युधि ॥२४॥
transliteration: tathaiva pāṇḍavā hṛṣṭā dhṛṣṭadyumnapurogamāḥ ,parivavrurmahātmānaṁ pārthamapratimaṁ yudhi.
english: Similarly, the joyful Pāṇḍavas, with Dhṛṣṭadyumna as their leader, surrounded the great-souled (ātman) Pārtha (Arjuna), who was unequalled in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 63 Verse 24. Similarly, the joyful Pāṇḍavas, with Dhṛṣṭadyumna as their leader, surrounded the great-souled (ātman) Pārtha (Arjuna), who was unequalled in battle.

sanskrit: तावकानां रणे कर्णो ग्लह आसीद्विशां पते ।तथैव पाण्डवेयानां ग्लहः पार्थोऽभवद्युधि ॥२५॥
transliteration: tāvakānāṁ raṇe karṇo glaha āsīdviśāṁ pate ,tathaiva pāṇḍaveyānāṁ glahaḥ pārtho'bhavadyudhi.
english: O lord of men, Karna was the main stake in battle for your (Kaurava) side. Similarly, Arjuna became the stake for the Pandavas in the fight.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 63 Verse 25. O lord of men, Karna was the main stake in battle for your (Kaurava) side. Similarly, Arjuna became the stake for the Pandavas in the fight.

sanskrit: त एव सभ्यास्तत्रासन्प्रेक्षकाश्चाभवन्स्म ते ।तत्रैषां ग्लहमानानां ध्रुवौ जयपराजयौ ॥२६॥
transliteration: ta eva sabhyāstatrāsanprekṣakāścābhavansma te ,tatraiṣāṁ glahamānānāṁ dhruvau jayaparājayau.
english: Those very (kings and warriors) were the assembly members there, and they also served as spectators. For these who were engaged in this high-stakes game, victory and defeat were certain.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 63 Verse 26. Those very (kings and warriors) were the assembly members there, and they also served as spectators. For these who were engaged in this high-stakes game, victory and defeat were certain.

sanskrit: ताभ्यां द्यूतं समायत्तं विजयायेतराय वा ।अस्माकं पाण्डवानां च स्थितानां रणमूर्धनि ॥२७॥
transliteration: tābhyāṁ dyūtaṁ samāyattaṁ vijayāyetarāya vā ,asmākaṁ pāṇḍavānāṁ ca sthitānāṁ raṇamūrdhani.
english: By those two (Karna and Arjuna), the entire gamble (of war) depended, whether for victory or for defeat, for us (Kauravas) and for the Pandavas, who stand at the forefront of battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 63 Verse 27. By those two (Karna and Arjuna), the entire gamble (of war) depended, whether for victory or for defeat, for us (Kauravas) and for the Pandavas, who stand at the forefront of battle.

sanskrit: तौ तु स्थितौ महाराज समरे युद्धशालिनौ ।अन्योन्यं प्रतिसंरब्धावन्योन्यस्य जयैषिणौ ॥२८॥
transliteration: tau tu sthitau mahārāja samare yuddhaśālinau ,anyonyaṁ pratisaṁrabdhāvanyonyasya jayaiṣiṇau.
english: But, O great king, those two (Karna and Arjuna) stood in battle, glorious in warfare, fiercely enraged at each other, and both desiring victory over the other.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 63 Verse 28. But, O great king, those two (Karna and Arjuna) stood in battle, glorious in warfare, fiercely enraged at each other, and both desiring victory over the other.

sanskrit: तावुभौ प्रजिहीर्षेतामिन्द्रवृत्राविवाभितः ।भीमरूपधरावास्तां महाधूमाविव ग्रहौ ॥२९॥
transliteration: tāvubhau prajihīrṣetāmindravṛtrāvivābhitaḥ ,bhīmarūpadharāvāstāṁ mahādhūmāviva grahau.
english: Those two desired to conquer each other from all sides, just like Indra and Vṛtra. They stood, having assumed terrifying forms, resembling two great smoky planets (graha).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 63 Verse 29. Those two desired to conquer each other from all sides, just like Indra and Vṛtra. They stood, having assumed terrifying forms, resembling two great smoky planets (graha).

sanskrit: ततोऽन्तरिक्षे साक्षेपा विवादा भरतर्षभ ।मिथो भेदाश्च भूतानामासन्कर्णार्जुनान्तरे ।व्याश्रयन्त दिशो भिन्नाः सर्वलोकाश्च मारिष ॥३०॥
transliteration: tato'ntarikṣe sākṣepā vivādā bharatarṣabha ,mitho bhedāśca bhūtānāmāsankarṇārjunāntare ,vyāśrayanta diśo bhinnāḥ sarvalokāśca māriṣa.
english: Then, O best of Bharatas, O respected one, in the sky, disputes filled with accusations and mutual divisions among all beings arose between Karṇa and Arjuna. The different directions and all the worlds took opposing sides.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 63 Verse 30. Then, O best of Bharatas, O respected one, in the sky, disputes filled with accusations and mutual divisions among all beings arose between Karṇa and Arjuna. The different directions and all the worlds took opposing sides.

sanskrit: देवदानवगन्धर्वाः पिशाचोरगराक्षसाः ।प्रतिपक्षग्रहं चक्रुः कर्णार्जुनसमागमे ॥३१॥
transliteration: devadānavagandharvāḥ piśācoragarākṣasāḥ ,pratipakṣagrahaṁ cakruḥ karṇārjunasamāgame.
english: The Devas, Danavas, Gandharvas, Piśācas, Uragas, and Rākṣasas all took opposing sides at the encounter between Karṇa and Arjuna.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 63 Verse 31. The Devas, Danavas, Gandharvas, Piśācas, Uragas, and Rākṣasas all took opposing sides at the encounter between Karṇa and Arjuna.

sanskrit: द्यौरासीत्कर्णतो व्यग्रा सनक्षत्रा विशां पते ।भूमिर्विशाला पार्थस्य माता पुत्रस्य भारत ॥३२॥
transliteration: dyaurāsītkarṇato vyagrā sanakṣatrā viśāṁ pate ,bhūmirviśālā pārthasya mātā putrasya bhārata.
english: O lord of men, the sky, with its stars, was agitated on Karṇa's side. O Bhārata, the vast earth was as a mother to Pārtha (Arjuna), her son.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 63 Verse 32. O lord of men, the sky, with its stars, was agitated on Karṇa's side. O Bhārata, the vast earth was as a mother to Pārtha (Arjuna), her son.

sanskrit: सरितः सागराश्चैव गिरयश्च नरोत्तम ।वृक्षाश्चौषधयस्तत्र व्याश्रयन्त किरीटिनम् ॥३३॥
transliteration: saritaḥ sāgarāścaiva girayaśca narottama ,vṛkṣāścauṣadhayastatra vyāśrayanta kirīṭinam.
english: O best of men, rivers, oceans, mountains, trees, and medicinal plants all took refuge in the crowned one (Arjuna).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 63 Verse 33. O best of men, rivers, oceans, mountains, trees, and medicinal plants all took refuge in the crowned one (Arjuna).

sanskrit: असुरा यातुधानाश्च गुह्यकाश्च परंतप ।कर्णतः समपद्यन्त खेचराणि वयांसि च ॥३४॥
transliteration: asurā yātudhānāśca guhyakāśca paraṁtapa ,karṇataḥ samapadyanta khecarāṇi vayāṁsi ca.
english: O tormentor of foes, Asuras, Rākṣasas, Guhyakas, and birds (flying creatures) emerged from the ear.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 63 Verse 34. O tormentor of foes, Asuras, Rākṣasas, Guhyakas, and birds (flying creatures) emerged from the ear.

sanskrit: रत्नानि निधयः सर्वे वेदाश्चाख्यानपञ्चमाः ।सोपवेदोपनिषदः सरहस्याः ससंग्रहाः ॥३५॥
transliteration: ratnāni nidhayaḥ sarve vedāścākhyānapañcamāḥ ,sopavedopaniṣadaḥ sarahasyāḥ sasaṁgrahāḥ.
english: All jewels and treasures, along with the Vedas  -  which have narratives (ākhyāna) as their fifth component, and are accompanied by the Upavedas, Upaniṣads, secret teachings, and various collections.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 63 Verse 35. All jewels and treasures, along with the Vedas  -  which have narratives (ākhyāna) as their fifth component, and are accompanied by the Upavedas, Upaniṣads, secret teachings, and various collections.

sanskrit: वासुकिश्चित्रसेनश्च तक्षकश्चोपतक्षकः ।पर्वताश्च तथा सर्वे काद्रवेयाश्च सान्वयाः ।विषवन्तो महारोषा नागाश्चार्जुनतोऽभवन् ॥३६॥
transliteration: vāsukiścitrasenaśca takṣakaścopatakṣakaḥ ,parvatāśca tathā sarve kādraveyāśca sānvayāḥ ,viṣavanto mahāroṣā nāgāścārjunato'bhavan.
english: Vāsuki, Citrasena, Takṣaka, and Upatakṣaka, as well as all the mountains, and all the Kādraveya Nagas (serpents) along with their families – venomous and extremely wrathful – all came into being from Arjuna.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 63 Verse 36. Vāsuki, Citrasena, Takṣaka, and Upatakṣaka, as well as all the mountains, and all the Kādraveya Nagas (serpents) along with their families – venomous and extremely wrathful – all came into being from Arjuna.

sanskrit: ऐरावताः सौरभेया वैशालेयाश्च भोगिनः ।एतेऽभवन्नर्जुनतः क्षुद्रसर्पास्तु कर्णतः ॥३७॥
transliteration: airāvatāḥ saurabheyā vaiśāleyāśca bhoginaḥ ,ete'bhavannarjunataḥ kṣudrasarpāstu karṇataḥ.
english: The descendants of Airāvata, Saurabheya, and Vaiśāleya, as well as the Bhogins (great serpents), all originated from Arjuna. However, the minor serpents originated from Karna.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 63 Verse 37. The descendants of Airāvata, Saurabheya, and Vaiśāleya, as well as the Bhogins (great serpents), all originated from Arjuna. However, the minor serpents originated from Karna.

sanskrit: ईहामृगा व्याडमृगा मङ्गल्याश्च मृगद्विजाः ।पार्थस्य विजयं राजन्सर्व एवाभिसंश्रिताः ॥३८॥
transliteration: īhāmṛgā vyāḍamṛgā maṅgalyāśca mṛgadvijāḥ ,pārthasya vijayaṁ rājansarva evābhisaṁśritāḥ.
english: O King, the desirable animals, fierce beasts, and auspicious animals and birds, all indeed allied themselves with Partha (Arjuna) for his victory.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 63 Verse 38. O King, the desirable animals, fierce beasts, and auspicious animals and birds, all indeed allied themselves with Partha (Arjuna) for his victory.

sanskrit: वसवो मरुतः साध्या रुद्रा विश्वेऽश्विनौ तथा ।अग्निरिन्द्रश्च सोमश्च पवनश्च दिशो दश ।धनंजयमुपाजग्मुरादित्याः कर्णतोऽभवन् ॥३९॥
transliteration: vasavo marutaḥ sādhyā rudrā viśve'śvinau tathā ,agnirindraśca somaśca pavanaśca diśo daśa ,dhanaṁjayamupājagmurādityāḥ karṇato'bhavan.
english: The Vasus, Maruts, Sadhyas, Rudras, Viśve Devas, and the two Ashvins, along with Agni, Indra, Soma, Pavana, and the deities of the ten directions, all approached Dhanañjaya (Arjuna). The Adityas, however, originated from Karna.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 63 Verse 39. The Vasus, Maruts, Sadhyas, Rudras, Viśve Devas, and the two Ashvins, along with Agni, Indra, Soma, Pavana, and the deities of the ten directions, all approached Dhanañjaya (Arjuna). The Adityas, however, originated from Karna.

sanskrit: देवास्तु पितृभिः सार्धं सगणार्जुनतोऽभवन् ।यमो वैश्रवणश्चैव वरुणश्च यतोऽर्जुनः ॥४०॥
transliteration: devāstu pitṛbhiḥ sārdhaṁ sagaṇārjunato'bhavan ,yamo vaiśravaṇaścaiva varuṇaśca yato'rjunaḥ.
english: But the gods, together with the ancestors and their retinues, originated from Arjuna. Yama, Vaiśravaṇa (Kubera), and Varuna also aligned with Arjuna.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 63 Verse 40. But the gods, together with the ancestors and their retinues, originated from Arjuna. Yama, Vaiśravaṇa (Kubera), and Varuna also aligned with Arjuna.

sanskrit: देवब्रह्मनृपर्षीणां गणाः पाण्डवतोऽभवन् ।तुम्बुरुप्रमुखा राजन्गन्धर्वाश्च यतोऽर्जुनः ॥४१॥
transliteration: devabrahmanṛparṣīṇāṁ gaṇāḥ pāṇḍavato'bhavan ,tumburupramukhā rājangandharvāśca yato'rjunaḥ.
english: O King, groups of gods, brahmins, and royal sages (nṛparṣi) manifested from the Pandava (Arjuna). And where Arjuna is, there too are the Gandharvas, led by Tumburu.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 63 Verse 41. O King, groups of gods, brahmins, and royal sages (nṛparṣi) manifested from the Pandava (Arjuna). And where Arjuna is, there too are the Gandharvas, led by Tumburu.

sanskrit: प्रावेयाः सह मौनेयैर्गन्धर्वाप्सरसां गणाः ।ईहामृगव्याडमृगैर्द्विपाश्च रथपत्तिभिः ॥४२॥
transliteration: prāveyāḥ saha mauneyairgandharvāpsarasāṁ gaṇāḥ ,īhāmṛgavyāḍamṛgairdvipāśca rathapattibhiḥ.
english: Groups of Gandharvas and Apsarases, along with the Mauneyas, were visible. Also [visible were] elephants, accompanied by predatory animals and dangerous wild beasts, and by chariot warriors and foot soldiers.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 63 Verse 42. Groups of Gandharvas and Apsarases, along with the Mauneyas, were visible. Also [visible were] elephants, accompanied by predatory animals and dangerous wild beasts, and by chariot warriors and foot soldiers.

sanskrit: उह्यमानास्तथा मेघैर्वायुना च मनीषिणः ।दिदृक्षवः समाजग्मुः कर्णार्जुनसमागमम् ॥४३॥
transliteration: uhyamānāstathā meghairvāyunā ca manīṣiṇaḥ ,didṛkṣavaḥ samājagmuḥ karṇārjunasamāgamam.
english: And similarly, wise persons (manīṣiṇa) being carried by clouds and by the wind, desirous of witnessing, assembled for the encounter between Karna and Arjuna.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 63 Verse 43. And similarly, wise persons (manīṣiṇa) being carried by clouds and by the wind, desirous of witnessing, assembled for the encounter between Karna and Arjuna.

sanskrit: देवदानवगन्धर्वा नागा यक्षाः पतत्रिणः ।महर्षयो वेदविदः पितरश्च स्वधाभुजः ॥४४॥
transliteration: devadānavagandharvā nāgā yakṣāḥ patatriṇaḥ ,maharṣayo vedavidaḥ pitaraśca svadhābhujaḥ.
english: Gods, Danavas, Gandharvas, Nagas, Yakshas, birds (patatrin), great sages (maharṣi) who are knowers of the Vedas (vedavid), and the ancestors (pitṛ) who partake of the svadhā offering [were also present].
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 63 Verse 44. Gods, Danavas, Gandharvas, Nagas, Yakshas, birds (patatrin), great sages (maharṣi) who are knowers of the Vedas (vedavid), and the ancestors (pitṛ) who partake of the svadhā offering [were also present].

sanskrit: तपो विद्यास्तथौषध्यो नानारूपाम्बरत्विषः ।अन्तरिक्षे महाराज विनदन्तोऽवतस्थिरे ॥४५॥
transliteration: tapo vidyāstathauṣadhyo nānārūpāmbaratviṣaḥ ,antarikṣe mahārāja vinadanto'vatasthire.
english: O great king, ascetic practices (tapas), various branches of knowledge (vidyā), and medicinal herbs, shining with diverse appearances and celestial radiance, stood resounding in the sky.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 63 Verse 45. O great king, ascetic practices (tapas), various branches of knowledge (vidyā), and medicinal herbs, shining with diverse appearances and celestial radiance, stood resounding in the sky.

sanskrit: ब्रह्मा ब्रह्मर्षिभिः सार्धं प्रजापतिभिरेव च ।भवेनावस्थितो यानं दिव्यं तं देशमभ्ययात् ॥४६॥
transliteration: brahmā brahmarṣibhiḥ sārdhaṁ prajāpatibhireva ca ,bhavenāvasthito yānaṁ divyaṁ taṁ deśamabhyayāt.
english: Brahmā, accompanied by the Brahmana sages (brahmarṣi) and also the progenitors (prajāpati), and being present with Bhava (Śiva), proceeded to that divine region by means of a celestial vehicle.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 63 Verse 46. Brahmā, accompanied by the Brahmana sages (brahmarṣi) and also the progenitors (prajāpati), and being present with Bhava (Śiva), proceeded to that divine region by means of a celestial vehicle.

sanskrit: दृष्ट्वा प्रजापतिं देवाः स्वयंभुवमुपागमन् ।समोऽस्तु देव विजय एतयोर्नरसिंहयोः ॥४७॥
transliteration: dṛṣṭvā prajāpatiṁ devāḥ svayaṁbhuvamupāgaman ,samo'stu deva vijaya etayornarasiṁhayoḥ.
english: Having seen the progenitor (prajāpati), the gods approached the self-existent one (svayambhu, i.e., Brahmā). They said: 'O god, may there be an equal victory for these two lion-like men.'
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 63 Verse 47. Having seen the progenitor (prajāpati), the gods approached the self-existent one (svayambhu, i.e., Brahmā). They said: 'O god, may there be an equal victory for these two lion-like men.'

sanskrit: तदुपश्रुत्य मघवा प्रणिपत्य पितामहम् ।कर्णार्जुनविनाशेन मा नश्यत्वखिलं जगत् ॥४८॥
transliteration: tadupaśrutya maghavā praṇipatya pitāmaham ,karṇārjunavināśena mā naśyatvakhilaṁ jagat.
english: Having heard that, Maghavā (Indra), prostrating before the Grandfather (pitāmaha, i.e., Brahmā), pleaded: 'May the entire world not perish due to the destruction of Karna and Arjuna.'
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 63 Verse 48. Having heard that, Maghavā (Indra), prostrating before the Grandfather (pitāmaha, i.e., Brahmā), pleaded: 'May the entire world not perish due to the destruction of Karna and Arjuna.'

sanskrit: स्वयंभो ब्रूहि तद्वाक्यं समोऽस्तु विजयोऽनयोः ।तत्तथास्तु नमस्तेऽस्तु प्रसीद भगवन्मम ॥४९॥
transliteration: svayaṁbho brūhi tadvākyaṁ samo'stu vijayo'nayoḥ ,tattathāstu namaste'stu prasīda bhagavanmama.
english: O Self-born one (svayaṃbhu), declare that these two should have an equal victory. Let it be so; salutations to you. O Lord (bhagavan), be gracious to me.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 63 Verse 49. O Self-born one (svayaṃbhu), declare that these two should have an equal victory. Let it be so; salutations to you. O Lord (bhagavan), be gracious to me.

sanskrit: ब्रह्मेशानावथो वाक्यमूचतुस्त्रिदशेश्वरम् ।विजयो ध्रुव एवास्तु विजयस्य महात्मनः ॥५०॥
transliteration: brahmeśānāvatho vākyamūcatustridaśeśvaram ,vijayo dhruva evāstu vijayasya mahātmanaḥ.
english: Brahmā and Īśāna (Śiva) then spoke this statement to the lord of the gods (Indra): 'Let there indeed be certain victory for the great-souled Vijaya.'
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 63 Verse 50. Brahmā and Īśāna (Śiva) then spoke this statement to the lord of the gods (Indra): 'Let there indeed be certain victory for the great-souled Vijaya.'

sanskrit: मनस्वी बलवाञ्शूरः कृतास्त्रश्च तपोधनः ।बिभर्ति च महातेजा धनुर्वेदमशेषतः ॥५१॥
transliteration: manasvī balavāñśūraḥ kṛtāstraśca tapodhanaḥ ,bibharti ca mahātejā dhanurvedamaśeṣataḥ.
english: He (Vijaya) is high-minded, strong, brave, skilled in weaponry, and rich in asceticism (tapas). And, being greatly radiant, he thoroughly comprehends the entire science of archery.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 63 Verse 51. He (Vijaya) is high-minded, strong, brave, skilled in weaponry, and rich in asceticism (tapas). And, being greatly radiant, he thoroughly comprehends the entire science of archery.

sanskrit: अतिक्रमेच्च माहात्म्याद्दिष्टमेतस्य पर्ययात् ।अतिक्रान्ते च लोकानामभावो नियतो भवेत् ॥५२॥
transliteration: atikramecca māhātmyāddiṣṭametasya paryayāt ,atikrānte ca lokānāmabhāvo niyato bhavet.
english: And he, by his greatness, might even overcome destiny (diṣṭa); but should there be a reversal of this (destiny), then if it (destiny/order) is transgressed, the certain destruction of the worlds would occur.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 63 Verse 52. And he, by his greatness, might even overcome destiny (diṣṭa); but should there be a reversal of this (destiny), then if it (destiny/order) is transgressed, the certain destruction of the worlds would occur.

sanskrit: न विद्यते व्यवस्थानं कृष्णयोः क्रुद्धयोः क्वचित् ।स्रष्टारौ ह्यसतश्चोभौ सतश्च पुरुषर्षभौ ॥५३॥
transliteration: na vidyate vyavasthānaṁ kṛṣṇayoḥ kruddhayoḥ kvacit ,sraṣṭārau hyasataścobhau sataśca puruṣarṣabhau.
english: When the two Krishnas (Arjuna and Vāsudeva), both best among men (puruṣa-ṛṣabha), become enraged, no stable situation or resolution can be found anywhere. For indeed, both of them are the creators of both the existent and the non-existent.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 63 Verse 53. When the two Krishnas (Arjuna and Vāsudeva), both best among men (puruṣa-ṛṣabha), become enraged, no stable situation or resolution can be found anywhere. For indeed, both of them are the creators of both the existent and the non-existent.

sanskrit: नरनारायणावेतौ पुराणावृषिसत्तमौ ।अनियत्तौ नियन्तारावभीतौ स्म परंतपौ ॥५४॥
transliteration: naranārāyaṇāvetau purāṇāvṛṣisattamau ,aniyattau niyantārāvabhītau sma paraṁtapau.
english: These two, Nara and Narayana, are the ancient and foremost among sages. They are unrestrained yet ultimate controllers, fearless, and tormentors of enemies.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 63 Verse 54. These two, Nara and Narayana, are the ancient and foremost among sages. They are unrestrained yet ultimate controllers, fearless, and tormentors of enemies.

sanskrit: कर्णो लोकानयं मुख्यान्प्राप्नोतु पुरुषर्षभः ।वीरो वैकर्तनः शूरो विजयस्त्वस्तु कृष्णयोः ॥५५॥
transliteration: karṇo lokānayaṁ mukhyānprāpnotu puruṣarṣabhaḥ ,vīro vaikartanaḥ śūro vijayastvastu kṛṣṇayoḥ.
english: May this Karna, the best among men (puruṣa-ṛṣabha), attain the principal worlds. But may victory belong to the two Krishnas (Arjuna and Vāsudeva) against the heroic, brave son of Vīkartana.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 63 Verse 55. May this Karna, the best among men (puruṣa-ṛṣabha), attain the principal worlds. But may victory belong to the two Krishnas (Arjuna and Vāsudeva) against the heroic, brave son of Vīkartana.

sanskrit: वसूनां च सलोकत्वं मरुतां वा समाप्नुयात् ।सहितो द्रोणभीष्माभ्यां नाकलोके महीयताम् ॥५६॥
transliteration: vasūnāṁ ca salokatvaṁ marutāṁ vā samāpnuyāt ,sahito droṇabhīṣmābhyāṁ nākaloke mahīyatām.
english: And may he attain the same realm as the Vasus or the Maruts. May he be glorified in the celestial world (nākaloka), accompanied by Drona and Bhishma.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 63 Verse 56. And may he attain the same realm as the Vasus or the Maruts. May he be glorified in the celestial world (nākaloka), accompanied by Drona and Bhishma.

sanskrit: इत्युक्तो देवदेवाभ्यां सहस्राक्षोऽब्रवीद्वचः ।आमन्त्र्य सर्वभूतानि ब्रह्मेशानानुशासनात् ॥५७॥
transliteration: ityukto devadevābhyāṁ sahasrākṣo'bravīdvacaḥ ,āmantrya sarvabhūtāni brahmeśānānuśāsanāt.
english: Having thus been spoken to by the two lords of gods (Brahmā and Īśāna), the thousand-eyed Indra spoke these words, addressing all beings in accordance with the command of Brahmā and Īśāna.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 63 Verse 57. Having thus been spoken to by the two lords of gods (Brahmā and Īśāna), the thousand-eyed Indra spoke these words, addressing all beings in accordance with the command of Brahmā and Īśāna.

sanskrit: श्रुतं भवद्भिर्यत्प्रोक्तं भगवद्भ्यां जगद्धितम् ।तत्तथा नान्यथा तद्धि तिष्ठध्वं गतमन्यवः ॥५८॥
transliteration: śrutaṁ bhavadbhiryatproktaṁ bhagavadbhyāṁ jagaddhitam ,tattathā nānyathā taddhi tiṣṭhadhvaṁ gatamanyavaḥ.
english: That which has been spoken by the two revered lords for the welfare of the world has been heard by you all. Indeed, it is so and cannot be otherwise. Therefore, remain, having set aside your anger.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 63 Verse 58. That which has been spoken by the two revered lords for the welfare of the world has been heard by you all. Indeed, it is so and cannot be otherwise. Therefore, remain, having set aside your anger.

sanskrit: इति श्रुत्वेन्द्रवचनं सर्वभूतानि मारिष ।विस्मितान्यभवन्राजन्पूजयां चक्रिरे च तत् ॥५९॥
transliteration: iti śrutvendravacanaṁ sarvabhūtāni māriṣa ,vismitānyabhavanrājanpūjayāṁ cakrire ca tat.
english: O venerable Māriṣa, O King, upon hearing these words of Indra, all beings became astonished and offered him homage.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 63 Verse 59. O venerable Māriṣa, O King, upon hearing these words of Indra, all beings became astonished and offered him homage.

sanskrit: व्यसृजंश्च सुगन्धीनि नानारूपाणि खात्तथा ।पुष्पवर्षाणि विबुधा देवतूर्याण्यवादयन् ॥६०॥
transliteration: vyasṛjaṁśca sugandhīni nānārūpāṇi khāttathā ,puṣpavarṣāṇi vibudhā devatūryāṇyavādayan.
english: And from the sky, they showered fragrant objects of various forms. The gods also rained down flowers and played divine musical instruments.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 63 Verse 60. And from the sky, they showered fragrant objects of various forms. The gods also rained down flowers and played divine musical instruments.

sanskrit: दिदृक्षवश्चाप्रतिमं द्वैरथं नरसिंहयोः ।देवदानवगन्धर्वाः सर्व एवावतस्थिरे ।रथौ च तौ श्वेतहयौ युक्तकेतू महास्वनौ ॥६१॥
transliteration: didṛkṣavaścāpratimaṁ dvairathaṁ narasiṁhayoḥ ,devadānavagandharvāḥ sarva evāvatasthire ,rathau ca tau śvetahayau yuktaketū mahāsvanau.
english: All the gods, demons, and Gandharvas gathered, eager to witness the unparalleled chariot duel between the two lion-like men (Arjuna and Karṇa). And those two chariots, with their white horses, matching banners, and mighty roars, were also positioned.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 63 Verse 61. All the gods, demons, and Gandharvas gathered, eager to witness the unparalleled chariot duel between the two lion-like men (Arjuna and Karṇa). And those two chariots, with their white horses, matching banners, and mighty roars, were also positioned.

sanskrit: समागता लोकवीराः शङ्खान्दध्मुः पृथक्पृथक् ।वासुदेवार्जुनौ वीरौ कर्णशल्यौ च भारत ॥६२॥
transliteration: samāgatā lokavīrāḥ śaṅkhāndadhmuḥ pṛthakpṛthak ,vāsudevārjunau vīrau karṇaśalyau ca bhārata.
english: O descendant of Bharata, the gathered heroes of the world, including the valiant Vāsudeva (Krishna) and Arjuna, as well as Karṇa and Śalya, each blew their conch shells separately.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 63 Verse 62. O descendant of Bharata, the gathered heroes of the world, including the valiant Vāsudeva (Krishna) and Arjuna, as well as Karṇa and Śalya, each blew their conch shells separately.

sanskrit: तद्भीरुसंत्रासकरं युद्धं समभवत्तदा ।अन्योन्यस्पर्धिनोर्वीर्ये शक्रशम्बरयोरिव ॥६३॥
transliteration: tadbhīrusaṁtrāsakaraṁ yuddhaṁ samabhavattadā ,anyonyaspardhinorvīrye śakraśambarayoriva.
english: Then that battle, terrifying to the faint-hearted, began. It was like the conflict between Śakra (Indra) and Śambara, who were rivals in prowess.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 63 Verse 63. Then that battle, terrifying to the faint-hearted, began. It was like the conflict between Śakra (Indra) and Śambara, who were rivals in prowess.

sanskrit: तयोर्ध्वजौ वीतमालौ शुशुभाते रथस्थितौ ।पृथग्रूपौ समार्छन्तौ क्रोधं युद्धे परस्परम् ॥६४॥
transliteration: tayordhvajau vītamālau śuśubhāte rathasthitau ,pṛthagrūpau samārchantau krodhaṁ yuddhe parasparam.
english: The two distinct banners of those warriors, standing on their chariots and stripped of their garlands, shone brightly, manifesting their mutual anger in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 63 Verse 64. The two distinct banners of those warriors, standing on their chariots and stripped of their garlands, shone brightly, manifesting their mutual anger in battle.

sanskrit: कर्णस्याशीविषनिभा रत्नसारवती दृढा ।पुरंदरधनुःप्रख्या हस्तिकक्ष्या व्यराजत ॥६५॥
transliteration: karṇasyāśīviṣanibhā ratnasāravatī dṛḍhā ,puraṁdaradhanuḥprakhyā hastikakṣyā vyarājata.
english: Karna's elephant girth (kakṣyā), which was strong, adorned with excellent jewels, and resembled a venomous snake, shone brightly, like Indra's bow.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 63 Verse 65. Karna's elephant girth (kakṣyā), which was strong, adorned with excellent jewels, and resembled a venomous snake, shone brightly, like Indra's bow.

sanskrit: कपिश्रेष्ठस्तु पार्थस्य व्यादितास्यो भयंकरः ।भीषयन्नेव दंष्ट्राभिर्दुर्निरीक्ष्यो रविर्यथा ॥६६॥
transliteration: kapiśreṣṭhastu pārthasya vyāditāsyo bhayaṁkaraḥ ,bhīṣayanneva daṁṣṭrābhirdurnirīkṣyo raviryathā.
english: Arjuna's chief of monkeys, terrifying with his wide-open mouth, and indeed terrifying with his fangs, was difficult to behold, just like the sun.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 63 Verse 66. Arjuna's chief of monkeys, terrifying with his wide-open mouth, and indeed terrifying with his fangs, was difficult to behold, just like the sun.

sanskrit: युद्धाभिलाषुको भूत्वा ध्वजो गाण्डीवधन्वनः ।कर्णध्वजमुपातिष्ठत्सोऽवदीदभिनर्दयन् ॥६७॥
transliteration: yuddhābhilāṣuko bhūtvā dhvajo gāṇḍīvadhanvanaḥ ,karṇadhvajamupātiṣṭhatso'vadīdabhinardayan.
english: Having become eager for battle, the banner of Arjuna (the wielder of the Gaṇḍīva bow) approached Karna's banner. Roaring, he (the monkey) cried out.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 63 Verse 67. Having become eager for battle, the banner of Arjuna (the wielder of the Gaṇḍīva bow) approached Karna's banner. Roaring, he (the monkey) cried out.

sanskrit: उत्पत्य च महावेगः कक्ष्यामभ्यहनत्कपिः ।नखैश्च दशनैश्चैव गरुडः पन्नगं यथा ॥६८॥
transliteration: utpatya ca mahāvegaḥ kakṣyāmabhyahanatkapiḥ ,nakhaiśca daśanaiścaiva garuḍaḥ pannagaṁ yathā.
english: And the very swift monkey, having leaped up, struck the girth (kakṣyā) with his claws and teeth, just as Garuda strikes a serpent.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 63 Verse 68. And the very swift monkey, having leaped up, struck the girth (kakṣyā) with his claws and teeth, just as Garuda strikes a serpent.

sanskrit: सुकिङ्किणीकाभरणा कालपाशोपमायसी ।अभ्यद्रवत्सुसंक्रुद्धा नागकक्ष्या महाकपिम् ॥६९॥
transliteration: sukiṅkiṇīkābharaṇā kālapāśopamāyasī ,abhyadravatsusaṁkruddhā nāgakakṣyā mahākapim.
english: Adorned with small bells, iron-like, and resembling the noose of Death, Nāgakakṣyā, extremely enraged, rushed towards the great monkey.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 63 Verse 69. Adorned with small bells, iron-like, and resembling the noose of Death, Nāgakakṣyā, extremely enraged, rushed towards the great monkey.

sanskrit: उभयोरुत्तमे युद्धे द्वैरथे द्यूत आहृते ।प्रकुर्वाते ध्वजौ युद्धं प्रत्यहेषन्हयान्हयाः ॥७०॥
transliteration: ubhayoruttame yuddhe dvairathe dyūta āhṛte ,prakurvāte dhvajau yuddhaṁ pratyaheṣanhayānhayāḥ.
english: As an excellent chariot duel, resembling a game of dice, commenced between the two (warriors), their two banners engaged in battle, and the horses neighed back and forth against each other's horses.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 63 Verse 70. As an excellent chariot duel, resembling a game of dice, commenced between the two (warriors), their two banners engaged in battle, and the horses neighed back and forth against each other's horses.

sanskrit: अविध्यत्पुण्डरीकाक्षः शल्यं नयनसायकैः ।स चापि पुण्डरीकाक्षं तथैवाभिसमैक्षत ॥७१॥
transliteration: avidhyatpuṇḍarīkākṣaḥ śalyaṁ nayanasāyakaiḥ ,sa cāpi puṇḍarīkākṣaṁ tathaivābhisamaikṣata.
english: The lotus-eyed one (Kṛṣṇa) struck Śalya with his eye-arrows (glances), and Śalya, in turn, looked back at the lotus-eyed one in the same manner.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 63 Verse 71. The lotus-eyed one (Kṛṣṇa) struck Śalya with his eye-arrows (glances), and Śalya, in turn, looked back at the lotus-eyed one in the same manner.

sanskrit: तत्राजयद्वासुदेवः शल्यं नयनसायकैः ।कर्णं चाप्यजयद्दृष्ट्या कुन्तीपुत्रो धनंजयः ॥७२॥
transliteration: tatrājayadvāsudevaḥ śalyaṁ nayanasāyakaiḥ ,karṇaṁ cāpyajayaddṛṣṭyā kuntīputro dhanaṁjayaḥ.
english: There, Vāsudeva (Kṛṣṇa) defeated Śalya with his eye-arrows (glances), and the son of Kuntī, Dhanañjaya (Arjuna), also defeated Karṇa with a glance.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 63 Verse 72. There, Vāsudeva (Kṛṣṇa) defeated Śalya with his eye-arrows (glances), and the son of Kuntī, Dhanañjaya (Arjuna), also defeated Karṇa with a glance.

sanskrit: अथाब्रवीत्सूतपुत्रः शल्यमाभाष्य सस्मितम् ।यदि पार्थो रणे हन्यादद्य मामिह कर्हिचित् ।किमुत्तरं तदा ते स्यात्सखे सत्यं ब्रवीहि मे ॥७३॥
transliteration: athābravītsūtaputraḥ śalyamābhāṣya sasmitam ,yadi pārtho raṇe hanyādadya māmiha karhicit ,kimuttaraṁ tadā te syātsakhe satyaṁ bravīhi me.
english: Then, Karṇa, the son of Sūta, addressed Śalya with a smile and said, "If Pārtha were to kill me here in battle today, what would be your reply then, O friend? Tell me the truth."
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 63 Verse 73. Then, Karṇa, the son of Sūta, addressed Śalya with a smile and said, "If Pārtha were to kill me here in battle today, what would be your reply then, O friend? Tell me the truth."

sanskrit: शल्य उवाच ।यदि कर्ण रणे हन्यादद्य त्वां श्वेतवाहनः ।उभावेकरथेनाहं हन्यां माधवपाण्डवौ ॥७४॥
transliteration: śalya uvāca ,yadi karṇa raṇe hanyādadya tvāṁ śvetavāhanaḥ ,ubhāvekarathenāhaṁ hanyāṁ mādhavapāṇḍavau.
english: Śalya replied, "O Karṇa, if Arjuna, the one with white steeds, were to kill you in battle today, then I would slay both Mādhava (Kṛṣṇa) and the Pāṇḍava (Arjuna) with a single chariot."
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 63 Verse 74. Śalya replied, "O Karṇa, if Arjuna, the one with white steeds, were to kill you in battle today, then I would slay both Mādhava (Kṛṣṇa) and the Pāṇḍava (Arjuna) with a single chariot."

sanskrit: संजय उवाच ।एवमेव तु गोविंदमर्जुनः प्रत्यभाषत ।तं प्रहस्याब्रवीत्कृष्णः पार्थं परमिदं वचः ॥७५॥
transliteration: saṁjaya uvāca ,evameva tu goviṁdamarjunaḥ pratyabhāṣata ,taṁ prahasyābravītkṛṣṇaḥ pārthaṁ paramidaṁ vacaḥ.
english: Sañjaya said, "Arjuna, indeed, replied to Govinda (Kṛṣṇa) in exactly the same manner. Then, Kṛṣṇa, smiling, spoke these most excellent words to Pārtha (Arjuna)."
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 63 Verse 75. Sañjaya said, "Arjuna, indeed, replied to Govinda (Kṛṣṇa) in exactly the same manner. Then, Kṛṣṇa, smiling, spoke these most excellent words to Pārtha (Arjuna)."

sanskrit: पतेद्दिवाकरः स्थानाच्छीर्येतानेकधा क्षितिः ।शैत्यमग्निरियान्न त्वा कर्णो हन्याद्धनंजयम् ॥७६॥
transliteration: pateddivākaraḥ sthānācchīryetānekadhā kṣitiḥ ,śaityamagniriyānna tvā karṇo hanyāddhanaṁjayam.
english: "The sun might fall from its orbit, the earth might shatter into countless fragments, and fire might become cold; but Karṇa will certainly not kill Dhananjaya (Arjuna)."
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 63 Verse 76. "The sun might fall from its orbit, the earth might shatter into countless fragments, and fire might become cold; but Karṇa will certainly not kill Dhananjaya (Arjuna)."

sanskrit: यदि त्वेवं कथंचित्स्याल्लोकपर्यसनं यथा ।हन्यां कर्णं तथा शल्यं बाहुभ्यामेव संयुगे ॥७७॥
transliteration: yadi tvevaṁ kathaṁcitsyāllokaparyasanaṁ yathā ,hanyāṁ karṇaṁ tathā śalyaṁ bāhubhyāmeva saṁyuge.
english: If, by some means, the destruction of the world were to happen in such a way, then I would slay Karṇa and Śalya in battle with just my two arms.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 63 Verse 77. If, by some means, the destruction of the world were to happen in such a way, then I would slay Karṇa and Śalya in battle with just my two arms.

sanskrit: इति कृष्णवचः श्रुत्वा प्रहसन्कपिकेतनः ।अर्जुनः प्रत्युवाचेदं कृष्णमक्लिष्टकारिणम् ।ममाप्येतावपर्याप्तौ कर्णशल्यौ जनार्दन ॥७८॥
transliteration: iti kṛṣṇavacaḥ śrutvā prahasankapiketanaḥ ,arjunaḥ pratyuvācedaṁ kṛṣṇamakliṣṭakāriṇam ,mamāpyetāvaparyāptau karṇaśalyau janārdana.
english: Thus, hearing these words of Krishna, Arjuna, whose banner bears the monkey (Hanumān), laughing, replied this to Krishna, the performer of effortless deeds: "O Janārdana, for me, these two, Karṇa and Śalya, are also not enough (to satisfy my prowess)!"
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 63 Verse 78. Thus, hearing these words of Krishna, Arjuna, whose banner bears the monkey (Hanumān), laughing, replied this to Krishna, the performer of effortless deeds: "O Janārdana, for me, these two, Karṇa and Śalya, are also not enough (to satisfy my prowess)!"

sanskrit: सपताकाध्वजं कर्णं सशल्यरथवाजिनम् ।सच्छत्रकवचं चैव सशक्तिशरकार्मुकम् ॥७९॥
transliteration: sapatākādhvajaṁ karṇaṁ saśalyarathavājinam ,sacchatrakavacaṁ caiva saśaktiśarakārmukam.
english: (You will see) Karṇa, along with his banner and flag, along with Śalya (his charioteer), his chariot, and horses, and also with his umbrella and armor, and with his spear, arrows, and bow.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 63 Verse 79. (You will see) Karṇa, along with his banner and flag, along with Śalya (his charioteer), his chariot, and horses, and also with his umbrella and armor, and with his spear, arrows, and bow.

sanskrit: द्रष्टास्यद्य शरैः कर्णं रणे कृत्तमनेकधा ।अद्यैनं सरथं साश्वं सशक्तिकवचायुधम् ।न हि मे शाम्यते वैरं कृष्णां यत्प्राहसत्पुरा ॥८०॥
transliteration: draṣṭāsyadya śaraiḥ karṇaṁ raṇe kṛttamanekadhā ,adyainaṁ sarathaṁ sāśvaṁ saśaktikavacāyudham ,na hi me śāmyate vairaṁ kṛṣṇāṁ yatprāhasatpurā.
english: Today, you will see Karṇa – along with his chariot, horses, spear, armor, and weapons – cut into many pieces by my arrows in battle. Indeed, my animosity (vaira) will not subside, because he laughed at Kṛṣṇā (Draupadī) long ago.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 63 Verse 80. Today, you will see Karṇa – along with his chariot, horses, spear, armor, and weapons – cut into many pieces by my arrows in battle. Indeed, my animosity (vaira) will not subside, because he laughed at Kṛṣṇā (Draupadī) long ago.

sanskrit: अद्य द्रष्टासि गोविन्द कर्णमुन्मथितं मया ।वारणेनेव मत्तेन पुष्पितं जगतीरुहम् ॥८१॥
transliteration: adya draṣṭāsi govinda karṇamunmathitaṁ mayā ,vāraṇeneva mattena puṣpitaṁ jagatīruham.
english: Today, O Govinda, you will see Karna annihilated by me, just as a flowering tree is uprooted by a maddened elephant.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 63 Verse 81. Today, O Govinda, you will see Karna annihilated by me, just as a flowering tree is uprooted by a maddened elephant.

sanskrit: अद्य ता मधुरा वाचः श्रोतासि मधुसूदन ।अद्याभिमन्युजननीमनृणः सान्त्वयिष्यसि ।कुन्तीं पितृष्वसारं च संप्रहृष्टो जनार्दन ॥८२॥
transliteration: adya tā madhurā vācaḥ śrotāsi madhusūdana ,adyābhimanyujananīmanṛṇaḥ sāntvayiṣyasi ,kuntīṁ pitṛṣvasāraṁ ca saṁprahṛṣṭo janārdana.
english: Today, O Madhusudana, you will hear those sweet words. Today, having discharged your duty (anṛṇaḥ), you will console Abhimanyu's mother. And, O Janardana, greatly delighted, you will also console Kunti, your paternal aunt.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 63 Verse 82. Today, O Madhusudana, you will hear those sweet words. Today, having discharged your duty (anṛṇaḥ), you will console Abhimanyu's mother. And, O Janardana, greatly delighted, you will also console Kunti, your paternal aunt.

sanskrit: अद्य बाष्पमुखीं कृष्णां सान्त्वयिष्यसि माधव ।वाग्भिश्चामृतकल्पाभिर्धर्मराजं युधिष्ठिरम् ॥८३॥
transliteration: adya bāṣpamukhīṁ kṛṣṇāṁ sāntvayiṣyasi mādhava ,vāgbhiścāmṛtakalpābhirdharmarājaṁ yudhiṣṭhiram.
english: Today, O Madhava, you will console the tear-faced Draupadi, and with words like nectar, you will console Dharmaraja Yudhishthira.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 63 Verse 83. Today, O Madhava, you will console the tear-faced Draupadi, and with words like nectar, you will console Dharmaraja Yudhishthira.

sanskrit: संजय उवाच ।तद्देवनागासुरसिद्धसंघैर्गन्धर्वयक्षाप्सरसां च संघैः ।ब्रह्मर्षिराजर्षिसुपर्णजुष्टं बभौ वियद्विस्मयनीयरूपम् ॥१॥
transliteration: saṁjaya uvāca ,taddevanāgāsurasiddhasaṁghai;rgandharvayakṣāpsarasāṁ ca saṁghaiḥ ,brahmarṣirājarṣisuparṇajuṣṭaṁ; babhau viyadvismayanīyarūpam.
english: Sanjaya said: That sky, frequented by throngs of gods, nāgas, asuras, and siddhas, and by hosts of gandharvas, yakṣas, and apsarases, as well as by Brahma-rishis, Raja-rishis, and Suparnas, shone with an astonishing appearance.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 64 Verse 1. Sanjaya said: That sky, frequented by throngs of gods, nāgas, asuras, and siddhas, and by hosts of gandharvas, yakṣas, and apsarases, as well as by Brahma-rishis, Raja-rishis, and Suparnas, shone with an astonishing appearance.

sanskrit: नानद्यमानं निनदैर्मनोज्ञैर्वादित्रगीतस्तुतिभिश्च नृत्तैः ।सर्वेऽन्तरिक्षे ददृशुर्मनुष्याः खस्थांश्च तान्विस्मयनीयरूपान् ॥२॥
transliteration: nānadyamānaṁ ninadairmanojñai;rvāditragītastutibhiśca nṛttaiḥ ,sarve'ntarikṣe dadṛśurmanuṣyāḥ; khasthāṁśca tānvismayanīyarūpān.
english: All the humans saw in the sky those wondrously formed beings standing there, being greatly celebrated with charming sounds, as well as with instrumental music, songs, praises, and dances.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 64 Verse 2. All the humans saw in the sky those wondrously formed beings standing there, being greatly celebrated with charming sounds, as well as with instrumental music, songs, praises, and dances.

sanskrit: ततः प्रहृष्टाः कुरुपाण्डुयोधा वादित्रपत्रायुधसिंहनादैः ।निनादयन्तो वसुधां दिशश्च स्वनेन सर्वे द्विषतो निजघ्नुः ॥३॥
transliteration: tataḥ prahṛṣṭāḥ kurupāṇḍuyodhā; vāditrapatrāyudhasiṁhanādaiḥ ,ninādayanto vasudhāṁ diśaśca; svanena sarve dviṣato nijaghnuḥ.
english: Then, all the greatly delighted warriors of the Kurus and Pandavas, making the earth and the directions resound with the clamor of their instrumental music, the clanging of their weapons, and their lion-like roars, struck down their enemies.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 64 Verse 3. Then, all the greatly delighted warriors of the Kurus and Pandavas, making the earth and the directions resound with the clamor of their instrumental music, the clanging of their weapons, and their lion-like roars, struck down their enemies.

sanskrit: नानाश्वमातङ्गरथायुताकुलं वरासिशक्त्यृष्टिनिपातदुःसहम् ।अभीरुजुष्टं हतदेहसंकुलं रणाजिरं लोहितरक्तमाबभौ ॥४॥
transliteration: nānāśvamātaṅgarathāyutākulaṁ; varāsiśaktyṛṣṭinipātaduḥsaham ,abhīrujuṣṭaṁ hatadehasaṁkulaṁ; raṇājiraṁ lohitaraktamābabhau.
english: The battlefield (raṇājiram) was covered in blood-red hues, crowded with tens of thousands of various horses, elephants, and chariots. It was rendered unbearable by the descent of excellent swords, spears (śakti), and javelins (ṛṣṭi), yet it was thronged by the fearless and filled with strewn dead bodies.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 64 Verse 4. The battlefield (raṇājiram) was covered in blood-red hues, crowded with tens of thousands of various horses, elephants, and chariots. It was rendered unbearable by the descent of excellent swords, spears (śakti), and javelins (ṛṣṭi), yet it was thronged by the fearless and filled with strewn dead bodies.

sanskrit: तथा प्रवृत्तेऽस्त्रभृतां पराभवे धनंजयश्चाधिरथिश्च सायकैः ।दिशश्च सैन्यं च शितैरजिह्मगैः परस्परं प्रोर्णुवतुः स्म दंशितौ ॥५॥
transliteration: tathā pravṛtte'strabhṛtāṁ parābhave; dhanaṁjayaścādhirathiśca sāyakaiḥ ,diśaśca sainyaṁ ca śitairajihmagaiḥ; parasparaṁ prorṇuvatuḥ sma daṁśitau.
english: Thus, as the defeat of the weapon-bearers began, Dhananjaya (Arjuna) and Adhirathi (Karṇa), both fully armored, engaged each other, covering the directions and the opposing army with sharp, straight-flying arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 64 Verse 5. Thus, as the defeat of the weapon-bearers began, Dhananjaya (Arjuna) and Adhirathi (Karṇa), both fully armored, engaged each other, covering the directions and the opposing army with sharp, straight-flying arrows.

sanskrit: ततस्त्वदीयाश्च परे च सायकैः कृतेऽन्धकारे विविदुर्न किंचन ।भयात्तु तावेव रथौ समाश्रयंस्तमोनुदौ खे प्रसृता इवांशवः ॥६॥
transliteration: tatastvadīyāśca pare ca sāyakaiḥ; kṛte'ndhakāre vividurna kiṁcana ,bhayāttu tāveva rathau samāśrayaṁ;stamonudau khe prasṛtā ivāṁśavaḥ.
english: Then, as a dense darkness was created by the arrows, neither your warriors nor the enemy warriors could perceive anything. But out of fear, they sought refuge in those very two chariots, which were like rays of light spreading across the sky, dispelling the gloom.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 64 Verse 6. Then, as a dense darkness was created by the arrows, neither your warriors nor the enemy warriors could perceive anything. But out of fear, they sought refuge in those very two chariots, which were like rays of light spreading across the sky, dispelling the gloom.

sanskrit: ततोऽस्त्रमस्त्रेण परस्परस्य तौ विधूय वाताविव पूर्वपश्चिमौ ।घनान्धकारे वितते तमोनुदौ यथोदितौ तद्वदतीव रेजतुः ॥७॥
transliteration: tato'stramastreṇa parasparasya tau; vidhūya vātāviva pūrvapaścimau ,ghanāndhakāre vitate tamonudau; yathoditau tadvadatīva rejatuḥ.
english: Then, those two, countering each other's weapons with their own, dispelled the dense darkness. They shone exceedingly like the two opposing winds, East and West, that have arisen, and just like dispellers of darkness.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 64 Verse 7. Then, those two, countering each other's weapons with their own, dispelled the dense darkness. They shone exceedingly like the two opposing winds, East and West, that have arisen, and just like dispellers of darkness.

sanskrit: न चाभिमन्तव्यमिति प्रचोदिताः परे त्वदीयाश्च तदावतस्थिरे ।महारथौ तौ परिवार्य सर्वतः सुरासुरा वासवशम्बराविव ॥८॥
transliteration: na cābhimantavyamiti pracoditāḥ; pare tvadīyāśca tadāvatasthire ,mahārathau tau parivārya sarvataḥ; surāsurā vāsavaśambarāviva.
english: And then, as if instructed not to attack, both your warriors and the enemy warriors stood still. They had surrounded those two great charioteers from all sides, just as gods and demons might surround Indra and Śambara.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 64 Verse 8. And then, as if instructed not to attack, both your warriors and the enemy warriors stood still. They had surrounded those two great charioteers from all sides, just as gods and demons might surround Indra and Śambara.

sanskrit: मृदङ्गभेरीपणवानकस्वनैर्निनादिते भारत शङ्खनिस्वनैः ।ससिंहनादौ बभतुर्नरोत्तमौ शशाङ्कसूर्याविव मेघसंप्लवे ॥९॥
transliteration: mṛdaṅgabherīpaṇavānakasvanai;rninādite bhārata śaṅkhanisvanaiḥ ,sasiṁhanādau babhaturnarottamau; śaśāṅkasūryāviva meghasaṁplave.
english: O Bhārata, as the battlefield resounded with the sounds of mṛdaṅga, bherī, paṇava, and anaka drums, and the clamor of conches, those two supreme heroes, uttering their lion-roars, shone brilliantly like the moon and the sun amidst a cloudburst.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 64 Verse 9. O Bhārata, as the battlefield resounded with the sounds of mṛdaṅga, bherī, paṇava, and anaka drums, and the clamor of conches, those two supreme heroes, uttering their lion-roars, shone brilliantly like the moon and the sun amidst a cloudburst.

sanskrit: महाधनुर्मण्डलमध्यगावुभौ सुवर्चसौ बाणसहस्ररश्मिनौ ।दिधक्षमाणौ सचराचरं जगद्युगास्तसूर्याविव दुःसहौ रणे ॥१०॥
transliteration: mahādhanurmaṇḍalamadhyagāvubhau; suvarcasau bāṇasahasraraśminau ,didhakṣamāṇau sacarācaraṁ jaga;dyugāstasūryāviva duḥsahau raṇe.
english: Both of them, standing in the center of their great bows' circles, were resplendent, emitting thousands of arrows like rays of light. Eager to consume the entire world, both the moving and the non-moving, they were unbearable in battle, like two suns at the end of an epoch.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 64 Verse 10. Both of them, standing in the center of their great bows' circles, were resplendent, emitting thousands of arrows like rays of light. Eager to consume the entire world, both the moving and the non-moving, they were unbearable in battle, like two suns at the end of an epoch.

sanskrit: उभावजेयावहितान्तकावुभौ जिघांसतुस्तौ कृतिनौ परस्परम् ।महाहवे वीरवरौ समीयतुर्यथेन्द्रजम्भाविव कर्णपाण्डवौ ॥११॥
transliteration: ubhāvajeyāvahitāntakāvubhau; jighāṁsatustau kṛtinau parasparam ,mahāhave vīravarau samīyatu;ryathendrajambhāviva karṇapāṇḍavau.
english: Both were unconquerable, both were the destroyers of their enemies. Those two accomplished and skilled warriors sought to kill each other. In that great battle, the two supreme heroes, Karṇa and Arjuna (Pāṇḍava), met like Indra and Jambha.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 64 Verse 11. Both were unconquerable, both were the destroyers of their enemies. Those two accomplished and skilled warriors sought to kill each other. In that great battle, the two supreme heroes, Karṇa and Arjuna (Pāṇḍava), met like Indra and Jambha.

sanskrit: ततो महास्त्राणि महाधनुर्धरौ विमुञ्चमानाविषुभिर्भयानकैः ।नराश्वनागानमितौ निजघ्नतुः परस्परं जघ्नतुरुत्तमेषुभिः ॥१२॥
transliteration: tato mahāstrāṇi mahādhanurdharau; vimuñcamānāviṣubhirbhayānakaiḥ ,narāśvanāgānamitau nijaghnatuḥ; parasparaṁ jaghnaturuttameṣubhiḥ.
english: Then, the two unrivaled great bow-wielders, releasing terrifying arrows, struck down men, horses, and elephants. With their excellent arrows, they also struck each other.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 64 Verse 12. Then, the two unrivaled great bow-wielders, releasing terrifying arrows, struck down men, horses, and elephants. With their excellent arrows, they also struck each other.

sanskrit: ततो विसस्रुः पुनरर्दिताः शरैर्नरोत्तमाभ्यां कुरुपाण्डवाश्रयाः ।सनागपत्त्यश्वरथा दिशो गतास्तथा यथा सिंहभयाद्वनौकसः ॥१३॥
transliteration: tato visasruḥ punararditāḥ śarai;rnarottamābhyāṁ kurupāṇḍavāśrayāḥ ,sanāgapattyaśvarathā diśo gatā;stathā yathā siṁhabhayādvanaukasaḥ.
english: Then, afflicted again by the arrows of those two supreme warriors, the soldiers of the Kurus and Pāṇḍavas, including their elephants, foot soldiers, horses, and chariots, fled in all directions, just as forest dwellers scatter from fear of a lion.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 64 Verse 13. Then, afflicted again by the arrows of those two supreme warriors, the soldiers of the Kurus and Pāṇḍavas, including their elephants, foot soldiers, horses, and chariots, fled in all directions, just as forest dwellers scatter from fear of a lion.

sanskrit: ततस्तु दुर्योधनभोजसौबलाः कृपश्च शारद्वतसूनुना सह ।महारथाः पञ्च धनंजयाच्युतौ शरैः शरीरान्तकरैरताडयन् ॥१४॥
transliteration: tatastu duryodhanabhojasaubalāḥ; kṛpaśca śāradvatasūnunā saha ,mahārathāḥ pañca dhanaṁjayācyutau; śaraiḥ śarīrāntakarairatāḍayan.
english: Then, Duryodhana, Kṛtavarman (Bhoja), Śakuni (Saubala), Kṛpa, and Aśvatthāmā (the son of Śaradvat) - these five great charioteers - struck Dhanaṃjaya and Acyuta with arrows that could destroy bodies.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 64 Verse 14. Then, Duryodhana, Kṛtavarman (Bhoja), Śakuni (Saubala), Kṛpa, and Aśvatthāmā (the son of Śaradvat) - these five great charioteers - struck Dhanaṃjaya and Acyuta with arrows that could destroy bodies.

sanskrit: धनूंषि तेषामिषुधीन्हयान्ध्वजान्रथांश्च सूतांश्च धनंजयः शरैः ।समं च चिच्छेद पराभिनच्च ताञ्शरोत्तमैर्द्वादशभिश्च सूतजम् ॥१५॥
transliteration: dhanūṁṣi teṣāmiṣudhīnhayāndhvajā;nrathāṁśca sūtāṁśca dhanaṁjayaḥ śaraiḥ ,samaṁ ca ciccheda parābhinacca tā;ñśarottamairdvādaśabhiśca sūtajam.
english: Dhanaṃjaya (Arjuna), with his arrows, simultaneously cut down their bows, quivers, horses, banners, and chariots, as well as their charioteers. Moreover, with twelve excellent arrows, he pierced them and also the son of the charioteer (Karṇa).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 64 Verse 15. Dhanaṃjaya (Arjuna), with his arrows, simultaneously cut down their bows, quivers, horses, banners, and chariots, as well as their charioteers. Moreover, with twelve excellent arrows, he pierced them and also the son of the charioteer (Karṇa).

sanskrit: अथाभ्यधावंस्त्वरिताः शतं रथाः शतं च नागार्जुनमाततायिनः ।शकास्तुखारा यवनाश्च सादिनः सहैव काम्बोजवरैर्जिघांसवः ॥१६॥
transliteration: athābhyadhāvaṁstvaritāḥ śataṁ rathāḥ; śataṁ ca nāgārjunamātatāyinaḥ ,śakāstukhārā yavanāśca sādinaḥ; sahaiva kāmbojavarairjighāṁsavaḥ.
english: Then, a hundred swift charioteers and a hundred elephant warriors, who were aggressors, rushed towards Arjuna. The Śakas, Tukhāras, and Yavanas, along with the finest Kamboja horsemen, all eager to slay, also attacked.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 64 Verse 16. Then, a hundred swift charioteers and a hundred elephant warriors, who were aggressors, rushed towards Arjuna. The Śakas, Tukhāras, and Yavanas, along with the finest Kamboja horsemen, all eager to slay, also attacked.

sanskrit: वरायुधान्पाणिगतान्करैः सह क्षुरैर्न्यकृन्तंस्त्वरिताः शिरांसि च ।हयांश्च नागांश्च रथांश्च युध्यतां धनंजयः शत्रुगणं तमक्षिणोत् ॥१७॥
transliteration: varāyudhānpāṇigatānkaraiḥ saha; kṣurairnyakṛntaṁstvaritāḥ śirāṁsi ca ,hayāṁśca nāgāṁśca rathāṁśca yudhyatāṁ; dhanaṁjayaḥ śatrugaṇaṁ tamakṣiṇot.
english: Dhanaṃjaya (Arjuna), swiftly and with razor-sharp arrows, cut off the excellent weapons held in their hands, along with their hands and heads. He then completely destroyed that entire host of fighting enemies, including their horses, elephants, and chariots.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 64 Verse 17. Dhanaṃjaya (Arjuna), swiftly and with razor-sharp arrows, cut off the excellent weapons held in their hands, along with their hands and heads. He then completely destroyed that entire host of fighting enemies, including their horses, elephants, and chariots.

sanskrit: ततोऽन्तरिक्षे सुरतूर्यनिस्वनाः ससाधुवादा हृषितैः समीरिताः ।निपेतुरप्युत्तमपुष्पवृष्टयः सुरूपगन्धाः पवनेरिताः शिवाः ॥१८॥
transliteration: tato'ntarikṣe suratūryanisvanāḥ; sasādhuvādā hṛṣitaiḥ samīritāḥ ,nipeturapyuttamapuṣpavṛṣṭayaḥ; surūpagandhāḥ pavaneritāḥ śivāḥ.
english: Then, in the sky, the delighted celestials sounded divine trumpets accompanied by acclamations. Auspicious showers of excellent, fragrant flowers, beautiful in form and stirred by the wind, also fell down.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 64 Verse 18. Then, in the sky, the delighted celestials sounded divine trumpets accompanied by acclamations. Auspicious showers of excellent, fragrant flowers, beautiful in form and stirred by the wind, also fell down.

sanskrit: तदद्भुतं देवमनुष्यसाक्षिकं समीक्ष्य भूतानि विसिष्मियुर्नृप ।तवात्मजः सूतसुतश्च न व्यथां न विस्मयं जग्मतुरेकनिश्चयौ ॥१९॥
transliteration: tadadbhutaṁ devamanuṣyasākṣikaṁ; samīkṣya bhūtāni visiṣmiyurnṛpa ,tavātmajaḥ sūtasutaśca na vyathāṁ; na vismayaṁ jagmaturekaniścayau.
english: O King, observing that marvel, which was witnessed by both gods and men, all beings were greatly astonished. However, your son (Duryodhana) and the charioteer's son (Karṇa), resolute in their single determination, experienced neither distress nor astonishment.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 64 Verse 19. O King, observing that marvel, which was witnessed by both gods and men, all beings were greatly astonished. However, your son (Duryodhana) and the charioteer's son (Karṇa), resolute in their single determination, experienced neither distress nor astonishment.

sanskrit: अथाब्रवीद्द्रोणसुतस्तवात्मजं करं करेण प्रतिपीड्य सान्त्वयन् ।प्रसीद दुर्योधन शाम्य पाण्डवैरलं विरोधेन धिगस्तु विग्रहम् ॥२०॥
transliteration: athābravīddroṇasutastavātmajaṁ; karaṁ kareṇa pratipīḍya sāntvayan ,prasīda duryodhana śāmya pāṇḍavai;ralaṁ virodhena dhigastu vigraham.
english: Then Drona's son (Ashvatthama), consoling your son (Duryodhana) while pressing his hand, said: "O Duryodhana, please calm down and cease your hostility with the Pandavas. Enough of this conflict! Let war be condemned!"
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 64 Verse 20. Then Drona's son (Ashvatthama), consoling your son (Duryodhana) while pressing his hand, said: "O Duryodhana, please calm down and cease your hostility with the Pandavas. Enough of this conflict! Let war be condemned!"

sanskrit: हतो गुरुर्ब्रह्मसमो महास्त्रवित्तथैव भीष्मप्रमुखा नरर्षभाः ।अहं त्ववध्यो मम चापि मातुलः प्रशाधि राज्यं सह पाण्डवैश्चिरम् ॥२१॥
transliteration: hato gururbrahmasamo mahāstravi;ttathaiva bhīṣmapramukhā nararṣabhāḥ ,ahaṁ tvavadhyo mama cāpi mātulaḥ; praśādhi rājyaṁ saha pāṇḍavaiściram.
english: The teacher (guru) (Drona), who was like the supreme spiritual reality (brahman) and a knower of great weapons, has been killed. Similarly, chief among men like Bhishma have also been killed. But I am not to be killed, and neither is my maternal uncle (Shakuni). Therefore, rule the kingdom together with the Pandavas for a long time.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 64 Verse 21. The teacher (guru) (Drona), who was like the supreme spiritual reality (brahman) and a knower of great weapons, has been killed. Similarly, chief among men like Bhishma have also been killed. But I am not to be killed, and neither is my maternal uncle (Shakuni). Therefore, rule the kingdom together with the Pandavas for a long time.

sanskrit: धनंजयः स्थास्यति वारितो मया जनार्दनो नैव विरोधमिच्छति ।युधिष्ठिरो भूतहिते सदा रतो वृकोदरस्तद्वशगस्तथा यमौ ॥२२॥
transliteration: dhanaṁjayaḥ sthāsyati vārito mayā; janārdano naiva virodhamicchati ,yudhiṣṭhiro bhūtahite sadā rato; vṛkodarastadvaśagastathā yamau.
english: Arjuna (Dhanañjaya) will be stopped by me. Kṛṣṇa (Janārdana) certainly does not desire conflict. Yudhiṣṭhira is always dedicated to the welfare of all beings, and Bhīma, as well as the twins (Nakula and Sahadeva), are subservient to him.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 64 Verse 22. Arjuna (Dhanañjaya) will be stopped by me. Kṛṣṇa (Janārdana) certainly does not desire conflict. Yudhiṣṭhira is always dedicated to the welfare of all beings, and Bhīma, as well as the twins (Nakula and Sahadeva), are subservient to him.

sanskrit: त्वया च पार्थैश्च परस्परेण प्रजाः शिवं प्राप्नुयुरिच्छति त्वयि ।व्रजन्तु शेषाः स्वपुराणि पार्थिवा निवृत्तवैराश्च भवन्तु सैनिकाः ॥२३॥
transliteration: tvayā ca pārthaiśca paraspareṇa; prajāḥ śivaṁ prāpnuyuricchati tvayi ,vrajantu śeṣāḥ svapurāṇi pārthivā; nivṛttavairāśca bhavantu sainikāḥ.
english: And through you and the Pāṇḍavas, by mutual agreement, the people may attain welfare if you (Duryodhana) wish it. Let the remaining kings return to their own cities, and let the soldiers be free from hostility.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 64 Verse 23. And through you and the Pāṇḍavas, by mutual agreement, the people may attain welfare if you (Duryodhana) wish it. Let the remaining kings return to their own cities, and let the soldiers be free from hostility.

sanskrit: न चेद्वचः श्रोष्यसि मे नराधिप ध्रुवं प्रतप्तासि हतोऽरिभिर्युधि ।इदं च दृष्टं जगता सह त्वया कृतं यदेकेन किरीटमालिना ।यथा न कुर्याद्बलभिन्न चान्तको न च प्रचेता भगवान्न यक्षराट् ॥२४॥
transliteration: na cedvacaḥ śroṣyasi me narādhipa; dhruvaṁ prataptāsi hato'ribhiryudhi ,idaṁ ca dṛṣṭaṁ jagatā saha tvayā; kṛtaṁ yadekena kirīṭamālinā ,yathā na kuryādbalabhinna cāntako; na ca pracetā bhagavānna yakṣarāṭ.
english: O king of men, if you do not listen to my words, you will surely be tormented and slain by your enemies in battle. And the world, along with you, has witnessed what was accomplished by that single crowned one (Arjuna) – a feat that neither Indra (Balabhit), nor Yama (Antaka), nor the revered Varuṇa (Pracetas), nor Kubera (Yakṣarāṭ) would perform.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 64 Verse 24. O king of men, if you do not listen to my words, you will surely be tormented and slain by your enemies in battle. And the world, along with you, has witnessed what was accomplished by that single crowned one (Arjuna) – a feat that neither Indra (Balabhit), nor Yama (Antaka), nor the revered Varuṇa (Pracetas), nor Kubera (Yakṣarāṭ) would perform.

sanskrit: अतोऽपि भूयांश्च गुणैर्धनंजयः स चाभिपत्स्यत्यखिलं वचो मम ।तवानुयात्रां च तथा करिष्यति प्रसीद राजञ्जगतः शमाय वै ॥२५॥
transliteration: ato'pi bhūyāṁśca guṇairdhanaṁjayaḥ; sa cābhipatsyatyakhilaṁ vaco mama ,tavānuyātrāṁ ca tathā kariṣyati; prasīda rājañjagataḥ śamāya vai.
english: Moreover, Arjuna (Dhanañjaya) is even greater in his qualities, and he will fully comply with all my words. He will also, in that way, act in accordance with your wishes. O King, be gracious for the peace of the world.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 64 Verse 25. Moreover, Arjuna (Dhanañjaya) is even greater in his qualities, and he will fully comply with all my words. He will also, in that way, act in accordance with your wishes. O King, be gracious for the peace of the world.

sanskrit: ममापि मानः परमः सदा त्वयि ब्रवीम्यतस्त्वां परमाच्च सौहृदात् ।निवारयिष्यामि हि कर्णमप्यहं यदा भवान्सप्रणयो भविष्यति ॥२६॥
transliteration: mamāpi mānaḥ paramaḥ sadā tvayi; bravīmyatastvāṁ paramācca sauhṛdāt ,nivārayiṣyāmi hi karṇamapyahaṁ; yadā bhavānsapraṇayo bhaviṣyati.
english: My highest respect (māna) is always for you. Therefore, I speak to you out of extreme friendship. Indeed, I will restrain even Karna, provided you become favorably inclined.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 64 Verse 26. My highest respect (māna) is always for you. Therefore, I speak to you out of extreme friendship. Indeed, I will restrain even Karna, provided you become favorably inclined.

sanskrit: वदन्ति मित्रं सहजं विचक्षणास्तथैव साम्ना च धनेन चार्जितम् ।प्रतापतश्चोपनतं चतुर्विधं तदस्ति सर्वं त्वयि पाण्डवेषु च ॥२७॥
transliteration: vadanti mitraṁ sahajaṁ vicakṣaṇā;stathaiva sāmnā ca dhanena cārjitam ,pratāpataścopanataṁ caturvidhaṁ; tadasti sarvaṁ tvayi pāṇḍaveṣu ca.
english: The wise declare four kinds of friendship: natural, that acquired through conciliation (sāman) and wealth, and that gained by prowess. All these types exist in both you and the Pandavas.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 64 Verse 27. The wise declare four kinds of friendship: natural, that acquired through conciliation (sāman) and wealth, and that gained by prowess. All these types exist in both you and the Pandavas.

sanskrit: निसर्गतस्ते तव वीर बान्धवाः पुनश्च साम्ना च समाप्नुहि स्थिरम् ।त्वयि प्रसन्ने यदि मित्रतामियुर्ध्रुवं नरेन्द्रेन्द्र तथा त्वमाचर ॥२८॥
transliteration: nisargataste tava vīra bāndhavāḥ; punaśca sāmnā ca samāpnuhi sthiram ,tvayi prasanne yadi mitratāmiyu;rdhruvaṁ narendrendra tathā tvamācara.
english: O hero, by their very nature, these are your kinsmen. Moreover, through conciliation (sāman), you should attain a stable peace. If you become pleased, they will surely achieve friendship (mitratā). Therefore, O chief of kings (narendrendra), you must act in that manner.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 64 Verse 28. O hero, by their very nature, these are your kinsmen. Moreover, through conciliation (sāman), you should attain a stable peace. If you become pleased, they will surely achieve friendship (mitratā). Therefore, O chief of kings (narendrendra), you must act in that manner.

sanskrit: स एवमुक्तः सुहृदा वचो हितं विचिन्त्य निःश्वस्य च दुर्मनाब्रवीत् ।यथा भवानाह सखे तथैव तन्ममापि च ज्ञापयतो वचः शृणु ॥२९॥
transliteration: sa evamuktaḥ suhṛdā vaco hitaṁ; vicintya niḥśvasya ca durmanābravīt ,yathā bhavānāha sakhe tathaiva ta;nmamāpi ca jñāpayato vacaḥ śṛṇu.
english: Thus addressed by his friend with beneficial words, he reflected, sighed, and spoke with a heavy heart. 'O friend, just as you have said, listen now to my words, which also convey my thoughts to you.'
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 64 Verse 29. Thus addressed by his friend with beneficial words, he reflected, sighed, and spoke with a heavy heart. 'O friend, just as you have said, listen now to my words, which also convey my thoughts to you.'

sanskrit: निहत्य दुःशासनमुक्तवान्बहु प्रसह्य शार्दूलवदेष दुर्मतिः ।वृकोदरस्तद्धृदये मम स्थितं न तत्परोक्षं भवतः कुतः शमः ॥३०॥
transliteration: nihatya duḥśāsanamuktavānbahu; prasahya śārdūlavadeṣa durmatiḥ ,vṛkodarastaddhṛdaye mama sthitaṁ; na tatparokṣaṁ bhavataḥ kutaḥ śamaḥ.
english: This wicked Bhīma (vṛkodaraḥ), having killed Duḥśāsana, forcibly released a great deal (of blood) like a tiger. That (deed) remains fixed in my heart. It is not hidden from you. From where, then, can there be peace?
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 64 Verse 30. This wicked Bhīma (vṛkodaraḥ), having killed Duḥśāsana, forcibly released a great deal (of blood) like a tiger. That (deed) remains fixed in my heart. It is not hidden from you. From where, then, can there be peace?

sanskrit: न चापि कर्णं गुरुपुत्र संस्तवादुपारमेत्यर्हसि वक्तुमच्युत ।श्रमेण युक्तो महताद्य फल्गुनस्तमेष कर्णः प्रसभं हनिष्यति ॥३१॥
transliteration: na cāpi karṇaṁ guruputra saṁstavā;dupārametyarhasi vaktumacyuta ,śrameṇa yukto mahatādya phalguna;stameṣa karṇaḥ prasabhaṁ haniṣyati.
english: O Acyuta (Kṛṣṇa), you should not speak to stop (the fight with) Karṇa on account of praising the preceptor's son (Aśvatthāman). For today, Arjuna (phālgunaḥ), afflicted with great weariness, this Karṇa will forcibly kill him.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 64 Verse 31. O Acyuta (Kṛṣṇa), you should not speak to stop (the fight with) Karṇa on account of praising the preceptor's son (Aśvatthāman). For today, Arjuna (phālgunaḥ), afflicted with great weariness, this Karṇa will forcibly kill him.

sanskrit: तमेवमुक्त्वाभ्यनुनीय चासकृत्तवात्मजः स्वाननुशास्ति सैनिकान् ।समाघ्नताभिद्रवताहितानिमान्सबाणशब्दान्किमु जोषमास्यते ॥३२॥
transliteration: tamevamuktvābhyanunīya cāsakṛ;ttavātmajaḥ svānanuśāsti sainikān ,samāghnatābhidravatāhitānimā;nsabāṇaśabdānkimu joṣamāsyate.
english: Having thus spoken to him and repeatedly conciliated him, your son (Duryodhana) instructs his own soldiers: 'Why are you sitting silently while these enemies, with their showering arrows, are striking and assailing?'
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 64 Verse 32. Having thus spoken to him and repeatedly conciliated him, your son (Duryodhana) instructs his own soldiers: 'Why are you sitting silently while these enemies, with their showering arrows, are striking and assailing?'

sanskrit: संजय उवाच ।तौ शङ्खभेरीनिनदे समृद्धे समीयतुः श्वेतहयौ नराग्र्यौ ।वैकर्तनः सूतपुत्रोऽर्जुनश्च दुर्मन्त्रिते तव पुत्रस्य राजन् ॥१॥
transliteration: saṁjaya uvāca ,tau śaṅkhabherīninade samṛddhe; samīyatuḥ śvetahayau narāgryau ,vaikartanaḥ sūtaputro'rjunaśca; durmantrite tava putrasya rājan.
english: Sañjaya said: O King, those two foremost of men, riding white horses, met amidst the mighty roar of conches and kettledrums. They were Vaikartana (Karṇa), the son of Sūta, and Arjuna, (meeting) due to the evil counsel of your son.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 65 Verse 1. Sañjaya said: O King, those two foremost of men, riding white horses, met amidst the mighty roar of conches and kettledrums. They were Vaikartana (Karṇa), the son of Sūta, and Arjuna, (meeting) due to the evil counsel of your son.

sanskrit: यथा गजौ हैमवतौ प्रभिन्नौ प्रगृह्य दन्ताविव वाशितार्थे ।तथा समाजग्मतुरुग्रवेगौ धनंजयश्चाधिरथिश्च वीरौ ॥२॥
transliteration: yathā gajau haimavatau prabhinnau; pragṛhya dantāviva vāśitārthe ,tathā samājagmaturugravegau; dhanaṁjayaścādhirathiśca vīrau.
english: Just as two infuriated Himalayan elephants, in rut, clash by seizing each other with their tusks, vying for a female, so too did the two intensely swift heroes, Dhanañjaya (Arjuna) and Adhirathi (Karṇa), confront each other.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 65 Verse 2. Just as two infuriated Himalayan elephants, in rut, clash by seizing each other with their tusks, vying for a female, so too did the two intensely swift heroes, Dhanañjaya (Arjuna) and Adhirathi (Karṇa), confront each other.

sanskrit: बलाहकेनेव यथा बलाहको यदृच्छया वा गिरिणा गिरिर्यथा ।तथा धनुर्ज्यातलनेमिनिस्वनौ समीयतुस्ताविषुवर्षवर्षिणौ ॥३॥
transliteration: balāhakeneva yathā balāhako; yadṛcchayā vā giriṇā giriryathā ,tathā dhanurjyātalaneminisvanau; samīyatustāviṣuvarṣavarṣiṇau.
english: Just as one cloud clashes with another cloud, or by chance one mountain clashes with another, similarly, those two, showering torrents of arrows and producing the sounds of bows, bowstrings, and the impact of sword-hilts (or hand-guards), came together.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 65 Verse 3. Just as one cloud clashes with another cloud, or by chance one mountain clashes with another, similarly, those two, showering torrents of arrows and producing the sounds of bows, bowstrings, and the impact of sword-hilts (or hand-guards), came together.

sanskrit: प्रवृद्धशृङ्गद्रुमवीरुदोषधी प्रवृद्धनानाविधपर्वतौकसौ ।यथाचलौ वा गलितौ महाबलौ तथा महास्त्रैरितरेतरं घ्नतः ॥४॥
transliteration: pravṛddhaśṛṅgadrumavīrudoṣadhī; pravṛddhanānāvidhaparvataukasau ,yathācalau vā galitau mahābalau; tathā mahāstrairitaretaraṁ ghnataḥ.
