english: Just as two mighty mountains, with their peaks, trees, creepers, and herbs having grown abundantly, and housing numerous kinds of mountain dwellers, might be dislodged and collide, similarly, they (the two heroes) struck each other with great weapons.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 65 Verse 4. Just as two mighty mountains, with their peaks, trees, creepers, and herbs having grown abundantly, and housing numerous kinds of mountain dwellers, might be dislodged and collide, similarly, they (the two heroes) struck each other with great weapons.

sanskrit: स संनिपातस्तु तयोर्महानभूत्सुरेशवैरोचनयोर्यथा पुरा ।शरैर्विभुग्नाङ्गनियन्तृवाहनः सुदुःसहोऽन्यैः पटुशोणितोदकः ॥५॥
transliteration: sa saṁnipātastu tayormahānabhū;tsureśavairocanayoryathā purā ,śarairvibhugnāṅganiyantṛvāhanaḥ; suduḥsaho'nyaiḥ paṭuśoṇitodakaḥ.
english: And that great clash between them was like the one of old between Indra (Sureśa) and Virocana, characterized by bodies, charioteers, and steeds shattered by arrows, very difficult for others to endure, and marked by flowing, intense blood.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 65 Verse 5. And that great clash between them was like the one of old between Indra (Sureśa) and Virocana, characterized by bodies, charioteers, and steeds shattered by arrows, very difficult for others to endure, and marked by flowing, intense blood.

sanskrit: प्रभूतपद्मोत्पलमत्स्यकच्छपौ महाह्रदौ पक्षिगणानुनादितौ ।सुसंनिकृष्टावनिलोद्धतौ यथा तथा रथौ तौ ध्वजिनौ समीयतुः ॥६॥
transliteration: prabhūtapadmotpalamatsyakacchapau; mahāhradau pakṣigaṇānunāditau ,susaṁnikṛṣṭāvaniloddhatau yathā; tathā rathau tau dhvajinau samīyatuḥ.
english: Just as two great lakes, abundant with lotuses, water lilies, fish, and tortoises, resounding with flocks of birds, and agitated by the wind, draw very close, so did those two banner-bearing chariots approach each other.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 65 Verse 6. Just as two great lakes, abundant with lotuses, water lilies, fish, and tortoises, resounding with flocks of birds, and agitated by the wind, draw very close, so did those two banner-bearing chariots approach each other.

sanskrit: उभौ महेन्द्रस्य समानविक्रमावुभौ महेन्द्रप्रतिमौ महारथौ ।महेन्द्रवज्रप्रतिमैश्च सायकैर्महेन्द्रवृत्राविव संप्रजह्रतुः ॥७॥
transliteration: ubhau mahendrasya samānavikramā;vubhau mahendrapratimau mahārathau ,mahendravajrapratimaiśca sāyakai;rmahendravṛtrāviva saṁprajahratuḥ.
english: Both of them, two great chariot-warriors, possessed valor equal to that of the great Indra and both were comparable to the great Indra himself. With arrows resembling Indra's thunderbolt (vajra), they attacked each other just like Indra and Vṛtra.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 65 Verse 7. Both of them, two great chariot-warriors, possessed valor equal to that of the great Indra and both were comparable to the great Indra himself. With arrows resembling Indra's thunderbolt (vajra), they attacked each other just like Indra and Vṛtra.

sanskrit: सनागपत्त्यश्वरथे उभे बले विचित्रवर्णाभरणाम्बरस्रजे ।चकम्पतुश्चोन्नमतः स्म विस्मयाद्वियद्गताश्चार्जुनकर्णसंयुगे ॥८॥
transliteration: sanāgapattyaśvarathe ubhe bale; vicitravarṇābharaṇāmbarasraje ,cakampatuśconnamataḥ sma vismayā;dviyadgatāścārjunakarṇasaṁyuge.
english: Both armies, complete with elephants, infantry, horses, and chariots, and adorned with multi-colored ornaments, garments, and garlands, trembled. And the celestials in the sky, rising up in astonishment at the battle of Arjuna and Karṇa, also observed.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 65 Verse 8. Both armies, complete with elephants, infantry, horses, and chariots, and adorned with multi-colored ornaments, garments, and garlands, trembled. And the celestials in the sky, rising up in astonishment at the battle of Arjuna and Karṇa, also observed.

sanskrit: भुजाः सवज्राङ्गुलयः समुच्छ्रिताः ससिंहनादा हृषितैर्दिदृक्षुभिः ।यदार्जुनं मत्तमिव द्विपो द्विपं समभ्ययादाधिरथिर्जिघांसया ॥९॥
transliteration: bhujāḥ savajrāṅgulayaḥ samucchritāḥ; sasiṁhanādā hṛṣitairdidṛkṣubhiḥ ,yadārjunaṁ mattamiva dvipo dvipaṁ; samabhyayādādhirathirjighāṁsayā.
english: The onlookers, thrilled and eager to witness the fight, raised their arms, which were like thunderbolt (vajra)-like fingers, and uttered lion-like roars, as Adhirathi's son (Karṇa), with a fierce desire to kill, advanced towards Arjuna, just as one intoxicated elephant charges another.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 65 Verse 9. The onlookers, thrilled and eager to witness the fight, raised their arms, which were like thunderbolt (vajra)-like fingers, and uttered lion-like roars, as Adhirathi's son (Karṇa), with a fierce desire to kill, advanced towards Arjuna, just as one intoxicated elephant charges another.

sanskrit: अभ्यक्रोशन्सोमकास्तत्र पार्थं त्वरस्व याह्यर्जुन विध्य कर्णम् ।छिन्ध्यस्य मूर्धानमलं चिरेण श्रद्धां च राज्याद्धृतराष्ट्रसूनोः ॥१०॥
transliteration: abhyakrośansomakāstatra pārthaṁ; tvarasva yāhyarjuna vidhya karṇam ,chindhyasya mūrdhānamalaṁ cireṇa; śraddhāṁ ca rājyāddhṛtarāṣṭrasūnoḥ.
english: The Somakas then shouted to Partha, "Hurry, Arjuna! Go and strike Karna! Cut off his head! Enough of this delay! And destroy the hope (śraddhā) of Dhritarashtra's son for the kingdom."
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 65 Verse 10. The Somakas then shouted to Partha, "Hurry, Arjuna! Go and strike Karna! Cut off his head! Enough of this delay! And destroy the hope (śraddhā) of Dhritarashtra's son for the kingdom."

sanskrit: तथास्माकं बहवस्तत्र योधाः कर्णं तदा याहि याहीत्यवोचन् ।जह्यर्जुनं कर्ण ततः सचीराः पुनर्वनं यान्तु चिराय पार्थाः ॥११॥
transliteration: tathāsmākaṁ bahavastatra yodhāḥ; karṇaṁ tadā yāhi yāhītyavocan ,jahyarjunaṁ karṇa tataḥ sacīrāḥ; punarvanaṁ yāntu cirāya pārthāḥ.
english: Similarly, many of our warriors there then said to Karna, "Go, go! Karna, slay Arjuna! Then let the sons of Pritha, clad in bark garments, return to the forest (vana) for a long time again!"
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 65 Verse 11. Similarly, many of our warriors there then said to Karna, "Go, go! Karna, slay Arjuna! Then let the sons of Pritha, clad in bark garments, return to the forest (vana) for a long time again!"

sanskrit: ततः कर्णः प्रथमं तत्र पार्थं महेषुभिर्दशभिः पर्यविध्यत् ।तमर्जुनः प्रत्यविध्यच्छिताग्रैः कक्षान्तरे दशभिरतीव क्रुद्धः ॥१२॥
transliteration: tataḥ karṇaḥ prathamaṁ tatra pārthaṁ; maheṣubhirdaśabhiḥ paryavidhyat ,tamarjunaḥ pratyavidhyacchitāgraiḥ; kakṣāntare daśabhiratīva kruddhaḥ.
english: Then, Karna first assailed Partha there with ten great arrows. Exceedingly enraged, Arjuna retaliated by piercing him (Karna) with ten sharp-pointed arrows in his armpit region.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 65 Verse 12. Then, Karna first assailed Partha there with ten great arrows. Exceedingly enraged, Arjuna retaliated by piercing him (Karna) with ten sharp-pointed arrows in his armpit region.

sanskrit: परस्परं तौ विशिखैः सुतीक्ष्णैस्ततक्षतुः सूतपुत्रोऽर्जुनश्च ।परस्परस्यान्तरेप्सू विमर्दे सुभीममभ्याययतुः प्रहृष्टौ ॥१३॥
transliteration: parasparaṁ tau viśikhaiḥ sutīkṣṇai;statakṣatuḥ sūtaputro'rjunaśca ,parasparasyāntarepsū vimarde; subhīmamabhyāyayatuḥ prahṛṣṭau.
english: Karna, the charioteer's son, and Arjuna, struck each other with very sharp arrows. Seeking each other's vulnerable spots in the fierce combat, the two greatly delighted warriors advanced dreadfully.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 65 Verse 13. Karna, the charioteer's son, and Arjuna, struck each other with very sharp arrows. Seeking each other's vulnerable spots in the fierce combat, the two greatly delighted warriors advanced dreadfully.

sanskrit: अमृष्यमाणश्च महाविमर्दे तत्राक्रुध्यद्भीमसेनो महात्मा ।अथाब्रवीत्पाणिना पाणिमाघ्नन्संदष्टौष्ठो नृत्यति वादयन्निव ।कथं नु त्वां सूतपुत्रः किरीटिन्महेषुभिर्दशभिरविध्यदग्रे ॥१४॥
transliteration: amṛṣyamāṇaśca mahāvimarde; tatrākrudhyadbhīmaseno mahātmā ,athābravītpāṇinā pāṇimāghna;nsaṁdaṣṭauṣṭho nṛtyati vādayanniva ,kathaṁ nu tvāṁ sūtaputraḥ kirīṭi;nmaheṣubhirdaśabhiravidhyadagre.
english: Unable to tolerate the great conflict, the magnanimous Bhimasena became enraged there. Then, striking his hand with his hand, with bitten lips, and seeming to dance and play an instrument in his agitation, he said, "O crowned one, Arjuna, how then did the son of a charioteer (Karna) pierce you first with ten great arrows?"
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 65 Verse 14. Unable to tolerate the great conflict, the magnanimous Bhimasena became enraged there. Then, striking his hand with his hand, with bitten lips, and seeming to dance and play an instrument in his agitation, he said, "O crowned one, Arjuna, how then did the son of a charioteer (Karna) pierce you first with ten great arrows?"

sanskrit: यया धृत्या सर्वभूतान्यजैषीर्ग्रासं ददद्वह्नये खाण्डवे त्वम् ।तया धृत्या सूतपुत्रं जहि त्वमहं वैनं गदया पोथयिष्ये ॥१५॥
transliteration: yayā dhṛtyā sarvabhūtānyajaiṣī;rgrāsaṁ dadadvahnaye khāṇḍave tvam ,tayā dhṛtyā sūtaputraṁ jahi tva;mahaṁ vainaṁ gadayā pothayiṣye.
english: You, by which resolve you conquered all beings while offering oblation (food) to Agni in the Khandava forest, by that very same resolve, slay the son of a charioteer (Karna)! If you don't, I will surely crush him with my mace.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 65 Verse 15. You, by which resolve you conquered all beings while offering oblation (food) to Agni in the Khandava forest, by that very same resolve, slay the son of a charioteer (Karna)! If you don't, I will surely crush him with my mace.

sanskrit: अथाब्रवीद्वासुदेवोऽपि पार्थं दृष्ट्वा रथेषून्प्रतिहन्यमानान् ।अमीमृदत्सर्वथा तेऽद्य कर्णो ह्यस्त्रैरस्त्राणि किमिदं किरीटिन् ॥१६॥
transliteration: athābravīdvāsudevo'pi pārthaṁ; dṛṣṭvā ratheṣūnpratihanyamānān ,amīmṛdatsarvathā te'dya karṇo; hyastrairastrāṇi kimidaṁ kirīṭin.
english: Then Vasudeva (Krishna) also spoke to Partha (Arjuna) after seeing his arrows being repelled from the chariot. "O crowned one, Arjuna, Karna has utterly crushed your weapons with his own weapons today. What is this?"
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 65 Verse 16. Then Vasudeva (Krishna) also spoke to Partha (Arjuna) after seeing his arrows being repelled from the chariot. "O crowned one, Arjuna, Karna has utterly crushed your weapons with his own weapons today. What is this?"

sanskrit: स वीर किं मुह्यसि नावधीयसे नदन्त्येते कुरवः संप्रहृष्टाः ।कर्णं पुरस्कृत्य विदुर्हि सर्वे त्वदस्त्रमस्त्रैर्विनिपात्यमानम् ॥१७॥
transliteration: sa vīra kiṁ muhyasi nāvadhīyase; nadantyete kuravaḥ saṁprahṛṣṭāḥ ,karṇaṁ puraskṛtya vidurhi sarve; tvadastramastrairvinipātyamānam.
english: O hero, why are you bewildered? Why are you not being attentive (or not prevailing)? These Kauravas, greatly delighted, are shouting! All of them, with Karna at the forefront, indeed know that your weapon (astra) is being vanquished by (his) weapons.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 65 Verse 17. O hero, why are you bewildered? Why are you not being attentive (or not prevailing)? These Kauravas, greatly delighted, are shouting! All of them, with Karna at the forefront, indeed know that your weapon (astra) is being vanquished by (his) weapons.

sanskrit: यया धृत्या निहतं तामसास्त्रं युगे युगे राक्षसाश्चापि घोराः ।दम्भोद्भवाश्चासुराश्चाहवेषु तया धृत्या त्वं जहि सूतपुत्रम् ॥१८॥
transliteration: yayā dhṛtyā nihataṁ tāmasāstraṁ; yuge yuge rākṣasāścāpi ghorāḥ ,dambhodbhavāścāsurāścāhaveṣu; tayā dhṛtyā tvaṁ jahi sūtaputram.
english: With that very same steadfastness (dhṛti) by which the Tāmasa weapon was destroyed repeatedly in every age, and by which fierce Rākṣasas and Asuras born of pride were vanquished in battles, with that steadfastness (dhṛti) you must now kill the son of Sūta.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 65 Verse 18. With that very same steadfastness (dhṛti) by which the Tāmasa weapon was destroyed repeatedly in every age, and by which fierce Rākṣasas and Asuras born of pride were vanquished in battles, with that steadfastness (dhṛti) you must now kill the son of Sūta.

sanskrit: अनेन वास्य क्षुरनेमिनाद्य संछिन्द्धि मूर्धानमरेः प्रसह्य ।मया निसृष्टेन सुदर्शनेन वज्रेण शक्रो नमुचेरिवारेः ॥१९॥
transliteration: anena vāsya kṣuraneminādya; saṁchinddhi mūrdhānamareḥ prasahya ,mayā nisṛṣṭena sudarśanena; vajreṇa śakro namucerivāreḥ.
english: Today, with this razor-sharp (weapon), forcibly sever the head of your enemy, just as Śakra (Indra) defeated his foe Namuci with the thunderbolt (vajra), and as if (you were using) the Sudarśana (chakra) which I have bestowed.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 65 Verse 19. Today, with this razor-sharp (weapon), forcibly sever the head of your enemy, just as Śakra (Indra) defeated his foe Namuci with the thunderbolt (vajra), and as if (you were using) the Sudarśana (chakra) which I have bestowed.

sanskrit: किरातरूपी भगवान्यया च त्वया महत्या परितोषितोऽभूत् ।तां त्वं धृतिं वीर पुनर्गृहीत्वा सहानुबन्धं जहि सूतपुत्रम् ॥२०॥
transliteration: kirātarūpī bhagavānyayā ca; tvayā mahatyā paritoṣito'bhūt ,tāṁ tvaṁ dhṛtiṁ vīra punargṛhītvā; sahānubandhaṁ jahi sūtaputram.
english: O hero, take up again that great steadfastness (dhṛti) by which you pleased the Lord (Bhagavān) who was in the form of a Kirāta, and with it, kill the son of Sūta along with his associates.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 65 Verse 20. O hero, take up again that great steadfastness (dhṛti) by which you pleased the Lord (Bhagavān) who was in the form of a Kirāta, and with it, kill the son of Sūta along with his associates.

sanskrit: ततो महीं सागरमेखलां त्वं सपत्तनां ग्रामवतीं समृद्धाम् ।प्रयच्छ राज्ञे निहतारिसंघां यशश्च पार्थातुलमाप्नुहि त्वम् ॥२१॥
transliteration: tato mahīṁ sāgaramekhalāṁ tvaṁ; sapattanāṁ grāmavatīṁ samṛddhām ,prayaccha rājñe nihatārisaṁghāṁ; yaśaśca pārthātulamāpnuhi tvam.
english: Then, O Pārtha, you should bestow this prosperous earth, girdled by the oceans and filled with cities and villages, upon the king (Yudhiṣṭhira), having first destroyed the hosts of enemies. And you yourself will attain unequalled fame.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 65 Verse 21. Then, O Pārtha, you should bestow this prosperous earth, girdled by the oceans and filled with cities and villages, upon the king (Yudhiṣṭhira), having first destroyed the hosts of enemies. And you yourself will attain unequalled fame.

sanskrit: संचोदितो भीमजनार्दनाभ्यां स्मृत्वा तदात्मानमवेक्ष्य सत्त्वम् ।महात्मनश्चागमने विदित्वा प्रयोजनं केशवमित्युवाच ॥२२॥
transliteration: saṁcodito bhīmajanārdanābhyāṁ; smṛtvā tadātmānamavekṣya sattvam ,mahātmanaścāgamane viditvā; prayojanaṁ keśavamityuvāca.
english: Urged by Bhīma and Janārdana (Kṛṣṇa), and reflecting upon his own intrinsic nature (ātman) and courage (sattvam), and having understood the purpose of the great soul's (Kṛṣṇa's) arrival, he then spoke to Keśava (Kṛṣṇa) thus.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 65 Verse 22. Urged by Bhīma and Janārdana (Kṛṣṇa), and reflecting upon his own intrinsic nature (ātman) and courage (sattvam), and having understood the purpose of the great soul's (Kṛṣṇa's) arrival, he then spoke to Keśava (Kṛṣṇa) thus.

sanskrit: प्रादुष्करोम्येष महास्त्रमुग्रं शिवाय लोकस्य वधाय सौतेः ।तन्मेऽनुजानातु भवान्सुराश्च ब्रह्मा भवो ब्रह्मविदश्च सर्वे ॥२३॥
transliteration: prāduṣkaromyeṣa mahāstramugraṁ; śivāya lokasya vadhāya sauteḥ ,tanme'nujānātu bhavānsurāśca; brahmā bhavo brahmavidaśca sarve.
english: I shall now manifest this terrible, mighty weapon for the welfare of the world and for the destruction of Sauti (Aśvatthāman). Therefore, may you (revered one), the gods, Brahmā, Bhava (Śiva), and all the knowers of Brahman (brahman) permit me to do so.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 65 Verse 23. I shall now manifest this terrible, mighty weapon for the welfare of the world and for the destruction of Sauti (Aśvatthāman). Therefore, may you (revered one), the gods, Brahmā, Bhava (Śiva), and all the knowers of Brahman (brahman) permit me to do so.

sanskrit: इत्यूचिवान्ब्राह्ममसह्यमस्त्रं प्रादुश्चक्रे मनसा संविधेयम् ।ततो दिशश्च प्रदिशश्च सर्वाः समावृणोत्सायकैर्भूरितेजाः ।ससर्ज बाणान्भरतर्षभोऽपि शतंशतानेकवदाशुवेगान् ॥२४॥
transliteration: ityūcivānbrāhmamasahyamastraṁ; prāduścakre manasā saṁvidheyam ,tato diśaśca pradiśaśca sarvāḥ; samāvṛṇotsāyakairbhūritejāḥ ,sasarja bāṇānbharatarṣabho'pi; śataṁśatānekavadāśuvegān.
english: Having spoken thus, he manifested the unbearable Brahma weapon, which could be controlled by the mind. Then, he of great splendor (Arjuna) covered all the cardinal and intermediate directions with arrows. The best of the Bharatas (Arjuna) also released hundreds upon hundreds of arrows, swiftly moving as if they were a single torrent.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 65 Verse 24. Having spoken thus, he manifested the unbearable Brahma weapon, which could be controlled by the mind. Then, he of great splendor (Arjuna) covered all the cardinal and intermediate directions with arrows. The best of the Bharatas (Arjuna) also released hundreds upon hundreds of arrows, swiftly moving as if they were a single torrent.

sanskrit: वैकर्तनेनापि तथाजिमध्ये सहस्रशो बाणगणा विसृष्टाः ।ते घोषिणः पाण्डवमभ्युपेयुः पर्जन्यमुक्ता इव वारिधाराः ॥२५॥
transliteration: vaikartanenāpi tathājimadhye; sahasraśo bāṇagaṇā visṛṣṭāḥ ,te ghoṣiṇaḥ pāṇḍavamabhyupeyuḥ; parjanyamuktā iva vāridhārāḥ.
english: Similarly, by the son of Vikartana (Karṇa) also, thousands of hosts of arrows were released in the midst of battle. Those roaring arrows approached the Pāṇḍava (Arjuna) like torrents of rain released by a thundercloud.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 65 Verse 25. Similarly, by the son of Vikartana (Karṇa) also, thousands of hosts of arrows were released in the midst of battle. Those roaring arrows approached the Pāṇḍava (Arjuna) like torrents of rain released by a thundercloud.

sanskrit: स भीमसेनं च जनार्दनं च किरीटिनं चाप्यमनुष्यकर्मा ।त्रिभिस्त्रिभिर्भीमबलो निहत्य ननाद घोरं महता स्वरेण ॥२६॥
transliteration: sa bhīmasenaṁ ca janārdanaṁ ca; kirīṭinaṁ cāpyamanuṣyakarmā ,tribhistribhirbhīmabalo nihatya; nanāda ghoraṁ mahatā svareṇa.
english: He, possessing terrible strength and performing superhuman feats, having struck Bhimasena, Janardana (Krishna), and Kirīṭin (Arjuna) with three (arrows) each, roared fiercely with a mighty sound.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 65 Verse 26. He, possessing terrible strength and performing superhuman feats, having struck Bhimasena, Janardana (Krishna), and Kirīṭin (Arjuna) with three (arrows) each, roared fiercely with a mighty sound.

sanskrit: स कर्णबाणाभिहतः किरीटी भीमं तथा प्रेक्ष्य जनार्दनं च ।अमृष्यमाणः पुनरेव पार्थः शरान्दशाष्टौ च समुद्बबर्ह ॥२७॥
transliteration: sa karṇabāṇābhihataḥ kirīṭī; bhīmaṁ tathā prekṣya janārdanaṁ ca ,amṛṣyamāṇaḥ punareva pārthaḥ; śarāndaśāṣṭau ca samudbabarha.
english: Kirīṭin (Arjuna), struck by Karna's arrows, and seeing Bhima and Janardana (Krishna) in that state, Partha, unable to tolerate this, again shot forth eighteen arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 65 Verse 27. Kirīṭin (Arjuna), struck by Karna's arrows, and seeing Bhima and Janardana (Krishna) in that state, Partha, unable to tolerate this, again shot forth eighteen arrows.

sanskrit: सुषेणमेकेन शरेण विद्ध्वा शल्यं चतुर्भिस्त्रिभिरेव कर्णम् ।ततः सुमुक्तैर्दशभिर्जघान सभापतिं काञ्चनवर्मनद्धम् ॥२८॥
transliteration: suṣeṇamekena śareṇa viddhvā; śalyaṁ caturbhistribhireva karṇam ,tataḥ sumuktairdaśabhirjaghāna; sabhāpatiṁ kāñcanavarmanaddham.
english: Having pierced Sushena with one arrow, Shalya with four, and Karna with three, he then struck the assembly chief (sabhāpati), who was clad in golden armor, with ten well-shot arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 65 Verse 28. Having pierced Sushena with one arrow, Shalya with four, and Karna with three, he then struck the assembly chief (sabhāpati), who was clad in golden armor, with ten well-shot arrows.

sanskrit: स राजपुत्रो विशिरा विबाहुर्विवाजिसूतो विधनुर्विकेतुः ।ततो रथाग्रादपतत्प्रभग्नः परश्वधैः शाल इवाभिकृत्तः ॥२९॥
transliteration: sa rājaputro viśirā vibāhu;rvivājisūto vidhanurviketuḥ ,tato rathāgrādapatatprabhagnaḥ; paraśvadhaiḥ śāla ivābhikṛttaḥ.
english: That prince, headless, armless, without horses or a charioteer, without a bow, and without a standard, then fell from the front of the chariot, utterly broken (prabhagna), just like a Sala tree felled by battle-axes.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 65 Verse 29. That prince, headless, armless, without horses or a charioteer, without a bow, and without a standard, then fell from the front of the chariot, utterly broken (prabhagna), just like a Sala tree felled by battle-axes.

sanskrit: पुनश्च कर्णं त्रिभिरष्टभिश्च द्वाभ्यां चतुर्भिर्दशभिश्च विद्ध्वा ।चतुःशतान्द्विरदान्सायुधीयान्हत्वा रथानष्टशतं जघान ।सहस्रमश्वांश्च पुनश्च सादीनष्टौ सहस्राणि च पत्तिवीरान् ॥३०॥
transliteration: punaśca karṇaṁ tribhiraṣṭabhiśca; dvābhyāṁ caturbhirdaśabhiśca viddhvā ,catuḥśatāndviradānsāyudhīyā;nhatvā rathānaṣṭaśataṁ jaghāna ,sahasramaśvāṁśca punaśca sādī;naṣṭau sahasrāṇi ca pattivīrān.
english: Again, having pierced Karṇa with three, then eight, then two, then four, and then ten (arrows), he killed four hundred armed elephants, struck down eight hundred chariots, and again, a thousand horses with their riders, and eight thousand foot soldiers.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 65 Verse 30. Again, having pierced Karṇa with three, then eight, then two, then four, and then ten (arrows), he killed four hundred armed elephants, struck down eight hundred chariots, and again, a thousand horses with their riders, and eight thousand foot soldiers.

sanskrit: दृष्ट्वाजिमुख्यावथ युध्यमानौ दिदृक्षवः शूरवरावरिघ्नौ ।कर्णं च पार्थं च नियम्य वाहान्खस्था महीस्थाश्च जनावतस्थुः ॥३१॥
transliteration: dṛṣṭvājimukhyāvatha yudhyamānau; didṛkṣavaḥ śūravarāvarighnau ,karṇaṁ ca pārthaṁ ca niyamya vāhā;nkhasthā mahīsthāśca janāvatasthuḥ.
english: Then, seeing the two chief warriors, Karṇa and Pārtha (Arjuna), fighting, eager to behold these two heroes, destroyers of the best enemies, people, whether in the sky or on the ground, restrained their mounts and stood still.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 65 Verse 31. Then, seeing the two chief warriors, Karṇa and Pārtha (Arjuna), fighting, eager to behold these two heroes, destroyers of the best enemies, people, whether in the sky or on the ground, restrained their mounts and stood still.

sanskrit: ततो धनुर्ज्या सहसातिकृष्टा सुघोषमाच्छिद्यत पाण्डवस्य ।तस्मिन्क्षणे सूतपुत्रस्तु पार्थं समाचिनोत्क्षुद्रकाणां शतेन ॥३२॥
transliteration: tato dhanurjyā sahasātikṛṣṭā; sughoṣamācchidyata pāṇḍavasya ,tasminkṣaṇe sūtaputrastu pārthaṁ; samācinotkṣudrakāṇāṁ śatena.
english: Then, the extremely taut bowstring of the Pāṇḍava (Arjuna) suddenly snapped with a loud twang. At that precise moment, the son of the charioteer (Karṇa) assailed Pārtha (Arjuna) with a hundred small arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 65 Verse 32. Then, the extremely taut bowstring of the Pāṇḍava (Arjuna) suddenly snapped with a loud twang. At that precise moment, the son of the charioteer (Karṇa) assailed Pārtha (Arjuna) with a hundred small arrows.

sanskrit: निर्मुक्तसर्पप्रतिमैश्च तीक्ष्णैस्तैलप्रधौतैः खगपत्रवाजैः ।षष्ट्या नाराचैर्वासुदेवं बिभेद तदन्तरं सोमकाः प्राद्रवन्त ॥३३॥
transliteration: nirmuktasarpapratimaiśca tīkṣṇai;stailapradhautaiḥ khagapatravājaiḥ ,ṣaṣṭyā nārācairvāsudevaṁ bibheda; tadantaraṁ somakāḥ prādravanta.
english: (Karṇa) pierced Vāsudeva (Krishna) with sixty sharp nārāca arrows, which resembled shed snake-skins, were polished with oil, and feathered with bird wings. Immediately after this, the Somakas fled.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 65 Verse 33. (Karṇa) pierced Vāsudeva (Krishna) with sixty sharp nārāca arrows, which resembled shed snake-skins, were polished with oil, and feathered with bird wings. Immediately after this, the Somakas fled.

sanskrit: ततो धनुर्ज्यामवधम्य शीघ्रं शरानस्तानाधिरथेर्विधम्य ।सुसंरब्धः कर्णशरक्षताङ्गो रणे पार्थः सोमकान्प्रत्यगृह्णात् ।न पक्षिणः संपतन्त्यन्तरिक्षे क्षेपीयसास्त्रेण कृतेऽन्धकारे ॥३४॥
transliteration: tato dhanurjyāmavadhamya śīghraṁ; śarānastānādhirathervidhamya ,susaṁrabdhaḥ karṇaśarakṣatāṅgo; raṇe pārthaḥ somakānpratyagṛhṇāt ,na pakṣiṇaḥ saṁpatantyantarikṣe; kṣepīyasāstreṇa kṛte'ndhakāre.
english: Then, quickly drawing his bowstring and destroying the arrows shot by Adhiratha's son (Karna), Partha (Arjuna), his body wounded by Karna's arrows, became greatly enraged. In battle, he engaged with the Somakas. So intense was the fight that no birds could fly in the sky, due to the darkness created by exceptionally swift weapons.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 65 Verse 34. Then, quickly drawing his bowstring and destroying the arrows shot by Adhiratha's son (Karna), Partha (Arjuna), his body wounded by Karna's arrows, became greatly enraged. In battle, he engaged with the Somakas. So intense was the fight that no birds could fly in the sky, due to the darkness created by exceptionally swift weapons.

sanskrit: शल्यं च पार्थो दशभिः पृषत्कैर्भृशं तनुत्रे प्रहसन्नविध्यत् ।ततः कर्णं द्वादशभिः सुमुक्तैर्विद्ध्वा पुनः सप्तभिरभ्यविध्यत् ॥३५॥
transliteration: śalyaṁ ca pārtho daśabhiḥ pṛṣatkai;rbhṛśaṁ tanutre prahasannavidhyat ,tataḥ karṇaṁ dvādaśabhiḥ sumuktai;rviddhvā punaḥ saptabhirabhyavidhyat.
english: Partha (Arjuna), laughing, severely pierced Salya's armor with ten arrows. Then, having already pierced Karna with twelve well-shot arrows, he struck him again with seven more.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 65 Verse 35. Partha (Arjuna), laughing, severely pierced Salya's armor with ten arrows. Then, having already pierced Karna with twelve well-shot arrows, he struck him again with seven more.

sanskrit: स पार्थबाणासनवेगनुन्नैर्दृढाहतः पत्रिभिरुग्रवेगैः ।विभिन्नगात्रः क्षतजोक्षिताङ्गः कर्णो बभौ रुद्र इवाततेषुः ॥३६॥
transliteration: sa pārthabāṇāsanaveganunnai;rdṛḍhāhataḥ patribhirugravegaiḥ ,vibhinnagātraḥ kṣatajokṣitāṅgaḥ; karṇo babhau rudra ivātateṣuḥ.
english: Karna, whose body was torn and soaked in blood, severely struck by swift and terrible arrows impelled by the force of Partha's (Arjuna's) bow, shone like Rudra (Shiva) with his bow drawn.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 65 Verse 36. Karna, whose body was torn and soaked in blood, severely struck by swift and terrible arrows impelled by the force of Partha's (Arjuna's) bow, shone like Rudra (Shiva) with his bow drawn.

sanskrit: ततस्त्रिभिश्च त्रिदशाधिपोपमं शरैर्बिभेदाधिरथिर्धनंजयम् ।शरांस्तु पञ्च ज्वलितानिवोरगान्प्रवीरयामास जिघांसुरच्युते ॥३७॥
transliteration: tatastribhiśca tridaśādhipopamaṁ; śarairbibhedādhirathirdhanaṁjayam ,śarāṁstu pañca jvalitānivoragā;npravīrayāmāsa jighāṁsuracyute.
english: Then, Adhiratha's son (Karna) pierced Dhananjaya (Arjuna), who was comparable to the lord of the gods (Indra), with three arrows. And desiring to kill the immutable (acyuta) one (Krishna), he then launched five arrows that were like flaming snakes.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 65 Verse 37. Then, Adhiratha's son (Karna) pierced Dhananjaya (Arjuna), who was comparable to the lord of the gods (Indra), with three arrows. And desiring to kill the immutable (acyuta) one (Krishna), he then launched five arrows that were like flaming snakes.

sanskrit: ते वर्म भित्त्वा पुरुषोत्तमस्य सुवर्णचित्रं न्यपतन्सुमुक्ताः ।वेगेन गामाविविशुः सुवेगाः स्नात्वा च कर्णाभिमुखाः प्रतीयुः ॥३८॥
transliteration: te varma bhittvā puruṣottamasya; suvarṇacitraṁ nyapatansumuktāḥ ,vegena gāmāviviśuḥ suvegāḥ; snātvā ca karṇābhimukhāḥ pratīyuḥ.
english: Those well-released arrows, piercing the golden-adorned armor of the best of men (puruṣottama), fell down. Extremely swift, they penetrated the earth with great speed, and having plunged (into it), they seemed to return towards Karna.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 65 Verse 38. Those well-released arrows, piercing the golden-adorned armor of the best of men (puruṣottama), fell down. Extremely swift, they penetrated the earth with great speed, and having plunged (into it), they seemed to return towards Karna.

sanskrit: तान्पञ्चभल्लैस्त्वरितैः सुमुक्तैस्त्रिधा त्रिधैकैकमथोच्चकर्त ।धनंजयस्ते न्यपतन्पृथिव्यां महाहयस्तक्षकपुत्रपक्षाः ॥३९॥
transliteration: tānpañcabhallaistvaritaiḥ sumuktai;stridhā tridhaikaikamathoccakarta ,dhanaṁjayaste nyapatanpṛthivyāṁ; mahāhayastakṣakaputrapakṣāḥ.
english: With five swift, well-released bhalla-arrows, Arjuna (dhanañjaya) then cut each of them into three parts. And those great steeds, swift as the offspring of Takṣaka (takṣakaputrapakṣa), fell to the earth.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 65 Verse 39. With five swift, well-released bhalla-arrows, Arjuna (dhanañjaya) then cut each of them into three parts. And those great steeds, swift as the offspring of Takṣaka (takṣakaputrapakṣa), fell to the earth.

sanskrit: ततः प्रजज्वाल किरीटमाली क्रोधेन कक्षं प्रदहन्निवाग्निः ।स कर्णमाकर्णविकृष्टसृष्टैः शरैः शरीरान्तकरैर्ज्वलद्भिः ।मर्मस्वविध्यत्स चचाल दुःखाद्धैर्यात्तु तस्थावतिमात्रधैर्यः ॥४०॥
transliteration: tataḥ prajajvāla kirīṭamālī; krodhena kakṣaṁ pradahannivāgniḥ ,sa karṇamākarṇavikṛṣṭasṛṣṭaiḥ; śaraiḥ śarīrāntakarairjvaladbhiḥ ,marmasvavidhyatsa cacāla duḥkhā;ddhairyāttu tasthāvatimātradhairyaḥ.
english: Then Arjuna (kirīṭamālī), adorned with a crown, blazed forth with anger like a fire consuming a dry forest. He (Arjuna) pierced Karna in his vital spots with blazing arrows, drawn fully to the ear and capable of ending life. Karna trembled from the pain, but due to his immense fortitude, he stood firm.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 65 Verse 40. Then Arjuna (kirīṭamālī), adorned with a crown, blazed forth with anger like a fire consuming a dry forest. He (Arjuna) pierced Karna in his vital spots with blazing arrows, drawn fully to the ear and capable of ending life. Karna trembled from the pain, but due to his immense fortitude, he stood firm.

sanskrit: ततः शरौघैः प्रदिशो दिशश्च रविप्रभा कर्णरथश्च राजन् ।अदृश्य आसीत्कुपिते धनंजये तुषारनीहारवृतं यथा नभः ॥४१॥
transliteration: tataḥ śaraughaiḥ pradiśo diśaśca; raviprabhā karṇarathaśca rājan ,adṛśya āsītkupite dhanaṁjaye; tuṣāranīhāravṛtaṁ yathā nabhaḥ.
english: O King, then, when Arjuna (dhanañjaya) was enraged, the cardinal directions, the intermediate directions, the sun's brilliance, and even Karna's chariot became invisible due to the torrents of arrows, just as the sky is enveloped by frost and mist.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 65 Verse 41. O King, then, when Arjuna (dhanañjaya) was enraged, the cardinal directions, the intermediate directions, the sun's brilliance, and even Karna's chariot became invisible due to the torrents of arrows, just as the sky is enveloped by frost and mist.

sanskrit: स चक्ररक्षानथ पादरक्षान्पुरःसरान्पृष्ठगोपांश्च सर्वान् ।दुर्योधनेनानुमतानरिघ्नान्समुच्चितान्सुरथान्सारभूतान् ॥४२॥
transliteration: sa cakrarakṣānatha pādarakṣā;npuraḥsarānpṛṣṭhagopāṁśca sarvān ,duryodhanenānumatānarighnā;nsamuccitānsurathānsārabhūtān.
english: He [Arjuna] faced all those wheel-guards and foot-guards, the vanguard and the rear-guards, who were approved by Duryodhana, skilled in slaying enemies, well-assembled, possessing excellent chariots, and truly valiant.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 65 Verse 42. He [Arjuna] faced all those wheel-guards and foot-guards, the vanguard and the rear-guards, who were approved by Duryodhana, skilled in slaying enemies, well-assembled, possessing excellent chariots, and truly valiant.

sanskrit: द्विसाहस्रान्समरे सव्यसाची कुरुप्रवीरानृषभः कुरूणाम् ।क्षणेन सर्वान्सरथाश्वसूतान्निनाय राजन्क्षयमेकवीरः ॥४३॥
transliteration: dvisāhasrānsamare savyasācī; kurupravīrānṛṣabhaḥ kurūṇām ,kṣaṇena sarvānsarathāśvasūtā;nnināya rājankṣayamekavīraḥ.
english: O King, the ambidextrous Arjuna (savyasācī), the foremost hero (ṛṣabhaḥ) among the Kurus, the unrivaled warrior (ekavīraḥ), instantly led all two thousand valiant Kuru heroes, along with their chariots, horses, and charioteers, to destruction in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 65 Verse 43. O King, the ambidextrous Arjuna (savyasācī), the foremost hero (ṛṣabhaḥ) among the Kurus, the unrivaled warrior (ekavīraḥ), instantly led all two thousand valiant Kuru heroes, along with their chariots, horses, and charioteers, to destruction in battle.

sanskrit: अथापलायन्त विहाय कर्णं तवात्मजाः कुरवश्चावशिष्टाः ।हतानवाकीर्य शरक्षतांश्च लालप्यमानांस्तनयान्पितॄंश्च ॥४४॥
transliteration: athāpalāyanta vihāya karṇaṁ; tavātmajāḥ kuravaścāvaśiṣṭāḥ ,hatānavākīrya śarakṣatāṁśca; lālapyamānāṁstanayānpitṝṁśca.
english: Then, your [Dhṛtarāṣṭra's] remaining sons and the Kurus fled, abandoning Karṇa. Lamenting their own sons and fathers who were slain or wounded by arrows, they left them scattered [on the battlefield].
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 65 Verse 44. Then, your [Dhṛtarāṣṭra's] remaining sons and the Kurus fled, abandoning Karṇa. Lamenting their own sons and fathers who were slain or wounded by arrows, they left them scattered [on the battlefield].

sanskrit: स सर्वतः प्रेक्ष्य दिशो विशून्या भयावदीर्णैः कुरुभिर्विहीनः ।न विव्यथे भारत तत्र कर्णः प्रतीपमेवार्जुनमभ्यधावत् ॥४५॥
transliteration: sa sarvataḥ prekṣya diśo viśūnyā; bhayāvadīrṇaiḥ kurubhirvihīnaḥ ,na vivyathe bhārata tatra karṇaḥ; pratīpamevārjunamabhyadhāvat.
english: O Bhārata, having observed the directions utterly deserted everywhere, and abandoned by the Kurus who were torn apart by fear, Karṇa was not distressed. Instead, he charged directly towards Arjuna there.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 65 Verse 45. O Bhārata, having observed the directions utterly deserted everywhere, and abandoned by the Kurus who were torn apart by fear, Karṇa was not distressed. Instead, he charged directly towards Arjuna there.

sanskrit: संजय उवाच ।ततोऽपयाताः शरपातमात्रमवस्थिताः कुरवो भिन्नसेनाः ।विद्युत्प्रकाशं ददृशुः समन्ताद्धनंजयास्त्रं समुदीर्यमाणम् ॥१॥
transliteration: saṁjaya uvāca ,tato'payātāḥ śarapātamātra;mavasthitāḥ kuravo bhinnasenāḥ ,vidyutprakāśaṁ dadṛśuḥ samantā;ddhanaṁjayāstraṁ samudīryamāṇam.
english: Sanjaya said: Then, the Kurus, whose armies were shattered, stood at a distance of only an arrow's fall. From all sides, they saw Arjuna's (Dhanaṃjaya) weapon (astra) being discharged, shining like lightning.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 66 Verse 1. Sanjaya said: Then, the Kurus, whose armies were shattered, stood at a distance of only an arrow's fall. From all sides, they saw Arjuna's (Dhanaṃjaya) weapon (astra) being discharged, shining like lightning.

sanskrit: तदर्जुनास्त्रं ग्रसते स्म वीरान्वियत्तथाकाशमनन्तघोषम् ।क्रुद्धेन पार्थेन तदाशु सृष्टं वधाय कर्णस्य महाविमर्दे ॥२॥
transliteration: tadarjunāstraṁ grasate sma vīrā;nviyattathākāśamanantaghoṣam ,kruddhena pārthena tadāśu sṛṣṭaṁ; vadhāya karṇasya mahāvimarde.
english: That weapon (astra) of Arjuna was devouring the heroes, filling the atmosphere and the sky with endless roars. It was swiftly discharged by the enraged Arjuna (Pārtha) for Karna's slaying in the great battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 66 Verse 2. That weapon (astra) of Arjuna was devouring the heroes, filling the atmosphere and the sky with endless roars. It was swiftly discharged by the enraged Arjuna (Pārtha) for Karna's slaying in the great battle.

sanskrit: रामादुपात्तेन महामहिम्ना आथर्वणेनारिविनाशनेन ।तदर्जुनास्त्रं व्यधमद्दहन्तं पार्थं च बाणैर्निशितैर्निजघ्ने ॥३॥
transliteration: rāmādupāttena mahāmahimnā; ātharvaṇenārivināśanena ,tadarjunāstraṁ vyadhamaddahantaṁ; pārthaṁ ca bāṇairniśitairnijaghne.
english: (Karna's weapon,) obtained from Rama (Paraśurāma), possessing great power, Atharvanic (magical) in nature, and destructive to enemies, destroyed that weapon (astra) of Arjuna. It then struck Arjuna (Pārtha), who was burning, with sharp arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 66 Verse 3. (Karna's weapon,) obtained from Rama (Paraśurāma), possessing great power, Atharvanic (magical) in nature, and destructive to enemies, destroyed that weapon (astra) of Arjuna. It then struck Arjuna (Pārtha), who was burning, with sharp arrows.

sanskrit: ततो विमर्दः सुमहान्बभूव तस्यार्जुनस्याधिरथेश्च राजन् ।अन्योन्यमासादयतोः पृषत्कैर्विषाणघातैर्द्विपयोरिवोग्रैः ॥४॥
transliteration: tato vimardaḥ sumahānbabhūva; tasyārjunasyādhiratheśca rājan ,anyonyamāsādayatoḥ pṛṣatkai;rviṣāṇaghātairdvipayorivograiḥ.
english: O King, then a very great battle ensued between that Arjuna and Adhirathi (Karna), as they confronted each other with arrows, fierce like two elephants clashing with their tusks.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 66 Verse 4. O King, then a very great battle ensued between that Arjuna and Adhirathi (Karna), as they confronted each other with arrows, fierce like two elephants clashing with their tusks.

sanskrit: ततो रिपुघ्नं समधत्त कर्णः सुसंशितं सर्पमुखं ज्वलन्तम् ।रौद्रं शरं संयति सुप्रधौतं पार्थार्थमत्यर्थचिराय गुप्तम् ॥५॥
transliteration: tato ripughnaṁ samadhatta karṇaḥ; susaṁśitaṁ sarpamukhaṁ jvalantam ,raudraṁ śaraṁ saṁyati supradhautaṁ; pārthārthamatyarthacirāya guptam.
english: Then Karna fixed upon his bow a fierce, enemy-slaying, very sharp, serpent-mouthed, blazing, and well-polished arrow. This arrow had been kept secret and carefully guarded for a very long time, specifically for Arjuna (Pārtha) in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 66 Verse 5. Then Karna fixed upon his bow a fierce, enemy-slaying, very sharp, serpent-mouthed, blazing, and well-polished arrow. This arrow had been kept secret and carefully guarded for a very long time, specifically for Arjuna (Pārtha) in battle.

sanskrit: सदार्चितं चन्दनचूर्णशायिनं सुवर्णनालीशयनं महाविषम् ।प्रदीप्तमैरावतवंशसंभवं शिरो जिहीर्षुर्युधि फल्गुनस्य ॥६॥
transliteration: sadārcitaṁ candanacūrṇaśāyinaṁ; suvarṇanālīśayanaṁ mahāviṣam ,pradīptamairāvatavaṁśasaṁbhavaṁ; śiro jihīrṣuryudhi phalgunasya.
english: (Karna, with this arrow), desiring to cut off Arjuna's (Phalguna's) head in battle, (fixed an arrow that was) always revered, kept resting on sandalwood powder, lying in a golden casket, greatly venomous, blazing, and of the lineage of Airavata (a celestial elephant, implying divine origin or serpent-like nature).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 66 Verse 6. (Karna, with this arrow), desiring to cut off Arjuna's (Phalguna's) head in battle, (fixed an arrow that was) always revered, kept resting on sandalwood powder, lying in a golden casket, greatly venomous, blazing, and of the lineage of Airavata (a celestial elephant, implying divine origin or serpent-like nature).

sanskrit: तमब्रवीन्मद्रराजो महात्मा वैकर्तनं प्रेक्ष्य हि संहितेषुम् ।न कर्ण ग्रीवामिषुरेष प्राप्स्यते संलक्ष्य संधत्स्व शरं शिरोघ्नम् ॥७॥
transliteration: tamabravīnmadrarājo mahātmā; vaikartanaṁ prekṣya hi saṁhiteṣum ,na karṇa grīvāmiṣureṣa prāpsyate; saṁlakṣya saṁdhatsva śaraṁ śiroghnam.
english: Then the great-souled king of Madra, Shalya, having seen Karna (Vaikartana) with his arrow fixed on the bow, spoke to him: "O Karna, this arrow will surely not strike the neck. Aim carefully and fix the arrow meant to sever the head!"
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 66 Verse 7. Then the great-souled king of Madra, Shalya, having seen Karna (Vaikartana) with his arrow fixed on the bow, spoke to him: "O Karna, this arrow will surely not strike the neck. Aim carefully and fix the arrow meant to sever the head!"

sanskrit: अथाब्रवीत्क्रोधसंरक्तनेत्रः कर्णः शल्यं संधितेषुः प्रसह्य ।न संधत्ते द्विः शरं शल्य कर्णो न मादृशाः शाठ्ययुक्ता भवन्ति ॥८॥
transliteration: athābravītkrodhasaṁraktanetraḥ; karṇaḥ śalyaṁ saṁdhiteṣuḥ prasahya ,na saṁdhatte dviḥ śaraṁ śalya karṇo; na mādṛśāḥ śāṭhyayuktā bhavanti.
english: Then Karna, his eyes reddened with anger, and with the arrow already fixed, spoke to Shalya resolutely: "O Shalya, Karna does not fix an arrow twice! Men like me are not prone to deceit."
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 66 Verse 8. Then Karna, his eyes reddened with anger, and with the arrow already fixed, spoke to Shalya resolutely: "O Shalya, Karna does not fix an arrow twice! Men like me are not prone to deceit."

sanskrit: तथैवमुक्त्वा विससर्ज तं शरं बलाहकं वर्षघनाभिपूजितम् ।हतोऽसि वै फल्गुन इत्यवोचत्ततस्त्वरन्नूर्जितमुत्ससर्ज ॥९॥
transliteration: tathaivamuktvā visasarja taṁ śaraṁ; balāhakaṁ varṣaghanābhipūjitam ,hato'si vai phalguna ityavoca;ttatastvarannūrjitamutsasarja.
english: Having spoken thus, Karna released that arrow named Balāhaka, which was celebrated like a rain cloud. He then declared, 'Indeed, Arjuna (Phalguna), you are slain!' After saying this, he quickly released another powerful (missile).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 66 Verse 9. Having spoken thus, Karna released that arrow named Balāhaka, which was celebrated like a rain cloud. He then declared, 'Indeed, Arjuna (Phalguna), you are slain!' After saying this, he quickly released another powerful (missile).

sanskrit: संधीयमानं भुजगं दृष्ट्वा कर्णेन माधवः ।आक्रम्य स्यन्दनं पद्भ्यां बलेन बलिनां वरः ॥१०॥
transliteration: saṁdhīyamānaṁ bhujagaṁ dṛṣṭvā karṇena mādhavaḥ ,ākramya syandanaṁ padbhyāṁ balena balināṁ varaḥ.
english: Having seen the snake-missile (bhujagam) being aimed by Karna, Krishna (Mādhava), the best among the strong, forcefully pressed down on the chariot with his feet.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 66 Verse 10. Having seen the snake-missile (bhujagam) being aimed by Karna, Krishna (Mādhava), the best among the strong, forcefully pressed down on the chariot with his feet.

sanskrit: अवगाढे रथे भूमौ जानुभ्यामगमन्हयाः ।ततः शरः सोऽभ्यहनत्किरीटं तस्य धीमतः ॥११॥
transliteration: avagāḍhe rathe bhūmau jānubhyāmagamanhayāḥ ,tataḥ śaraḥ so'bhyahanatkirīṭaṁ tasya dhīmataḥ.
english: When the chariot had sunk into the earth up to the knees of the horses, then that arrow struck the diadem (kirīṭa) of the wise Arjuna.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 66 Verse 11. When the chariot had sunk into the earth up to the knees of the horses, then that arrow struck the diadem (kirīṭa) of the wise Arjuna.

sanskrit: अथार्जुनस्योत्तमगात्रभूषणं धरावियद्द्योसलिलेषु विश्रुतम् ।बलास्त्रसर्गोत्तमयत्नमन्युभिः शरेण मूर्ध्नः स जहार सूतजः ॥१२॥
transliteration: athārjunasyottamagātrabhūṣaṇaṁ; dharāviyaddyosalileṣu viśrutam ,balāstrasargottamayatnamanyubhiḥ; śareṇa mūrdhnaḥ sa jahāra sūtajaḥ.
english: Then, the son of Sūta (Karna), with his arrow and with forceful weapon-release, excellent skill, and wrath, took away from Arjuna's head that supreme ornament, which was renowned throughout the earth, sky, and waters.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 66 Verse 12. Then, the son of Sūta (Karna), with his arrow and with forceful weapon-release, excellent skill, and wrath, took away from Arjuna's head that supreme ornament, which was renowned throughout the earth, sky, and waters.

sanskrit: दिवाकरेन्दुज्वलनग्रहत्विषं सुवर्णमुक्तामणिजालभूषितम् ।पुरंदरार्थं तपसा प्रयत्नतः स्वयं कृतं यद्भुवनस्य सूनुना ॥१३॥
transliteration: divākarendujvalanagrahatviṣaṁ; suvarṇamuktāmaṇijālabhūṣitam ,puraṁdarārthaṁ tapasā prayatnataḥ; svayaṁ kṛtaṁ yadbhuvanasya sūnunā.
english: That [crown], which was made by the son of Bhuvana (Tvaṣṭṛ) himself with diligent effort and asceticism (tapas) for the sake of Indra, shone with the brilliance of the sun, moon, fire, and planets, and was embellished with a network of gold, pearls, and jewels.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 66 Verse 13. That [crown], which was made by the son of Bhuvana (Tvaṣṭṛ) himself with diligent effort and asceticism (tapas) for the sake of Indra, shone with the brilliance of the sun, moon, fire, and planets, and was embellished with a network of gold, pearls, and jewels.

sanskrit: महार्हरूपं द्विषतां भयंकरं विभाति चात्यर्थसुखं सुगन्धि तत् ।निजघ्नुषे देवरिपून्सुरेश्वरः स्वयं ददौ यत्सुमनाः किरीटिने ॥१४॥
transliteration: mahārharūpaṁ dviṣatāṁ bhayaṁkaraṁ; vibhāti cātyarthasukhaṁ sugandhi tat ,nijaghnuṣe devaripūnsureśvaraḥ; svayaṁ dadau yatsumanāḥ kirīṭine.
english: That [crown] was of magnificent form, terrifying to enemies, and shone, moreover, as exceedingly pleasant and fragrant. The lord of the gods (Indra), being pleased, personally gave that to Kirīṭin (Arjuna), who had slain the enemies of the gods.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 66 Verse 14. That [crown] was of magnificent form, terrifying to enemies, and shone, moreover, as exceedingly pleasant and fragrant. The lord of the gods (Indra), being pleased, personally gave that to Kirīṭin (Arjuna), who had slain the enemies of the gods.

sanskrit: हराम्बुपाखण्डलवित्तगोप्तृभिः पिनाकपाशाशनिसायकोत्तमैः ।सुरोत्तमैरप्यविषह्यमर्दितुं प्रसह्य नागेन जहार यद्वृषः ॥१५॥
transliteration: harāmbupākhaṇḍalavittagoptṛbhiḥ; pinākapāśāśanisāyakottamaiḥ ,surottamairapyaviṣahyamardituṁ; prasahya nāgena jahāra yadvṛṣaḥ.
english: That [crown], which was impossible for even the best of gods - Shiva, Varuṇa, Indra, and Kubera, with their supreme weapons Pināka, Pāśa, Aśani, and excellent arrows - to injure, the hero (Bhagadatta) forcibly struck away (jahāra) by means of a Naga-headed weapon.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 66 Verse 15. That [crown], which was impossible for even the best of gods - Shiva, Varuṇa, Indra, and Kubera, with their supreme weapons Pināka, Pāśa, Aśani, and excellent arrows - to injure, the hero (Bhagadatta) forcibly struck away (jahāra) by means of a Naga-headed weapon.

sanskrit: तदुत्तमेषून्मथितं विषाग्निना प्रदीप्तमर्चिष्मदभिक्षिति प्रियम् ।पपात पार्थस्य किरीटमुत्तमं दिवाकरोऽस्तादिव पर्वताज्ज्वलन् ॥१६॥
transliteration: taduttameṣūnmathitaṁ viṣāgninā; pradīptamarciṣmadabhikṣiti priyam ,papāta pārthasya kirīṭamuttamaṁ; divākaro'stādiva parvatājjvalan.
english: That excellent and beloved crown of Pārtha (Arjuna), shattered by the supreme arrow and blazing with venomous fire, fell to the ground, radiant and luminous, just as the blazing sun drops from the setting mountain.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 66 Verse 16. That excellent and beloved crown of Pārtha (Arjuna), shattered by the supreme arrow and blazing with venomous fire, fell to the ground, radiant and luminous, just as the blazing sun drops from the setting mountain.

sanskrit: ततः किरीटं बहुरत्नमण्डितं जहार नागोऽर्जुनमूर्धतो बलात् ।गिरेः सुजाताङ्कुरपुष्पितद्रुमं महेन्द्रवज्रः शिखरं यथोत्तमम् ॥१७॥
transliteration: tataḥ kirīṭaṁ bahuratnamaṇḍitaṁ; jahāra nāgo'rjunamūrdhato balāt ,gireḥ sujātāṅkurapuṣpitadrumaṁ; mahendravajraḥ śikharaṁ yathottamam.
english: Then, the Nāga forcefully snatched the crown, adorned with many jewels, from Arjuna's head, just as Indra's thunderbolt (mahendravajra) strikes down an excellent mountain peak with its flourishing sprouts and flowering trees.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 66 Verse 17. Then, the Nāga forcefully snatched the crown, adorned with many jewels, from Arjuna's head, just as Indra's thunderbolt (mahendravajra) strikes down an excellent mountain peak with its flourishing sprouts and flowering trees.

sanskrit: मही वियद्द्यौः सलिलानि वायुना यथा विभिन्नानि विभान्ति भारत ।तथैव शब्दो भुवनेष्वभूत्तदा जना व्यवस्यन्व्यथिताश्च चस्खलुः ॥१८॥
transliteration: mahī viyaddyauḥ salilāni vāyunā; yathā vibhinnāni vibhānti bhārata ,tathaiva śabdo bhuvaneṣvabhūttadā; janā vyavasyanvyathitāśca caskhaluḥ.
english: O Bhārata, just as the earth, sky, heaven, and waters appear distinct through the medium of air, so too did a loud sound arise throughout the worlds then, and people, though attempting to maintain composure, became agitated and stumbled.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 66 Verse 18. O Bhārata, just as the earth, sky, heaven, and waters appear distinct through the medium of air, so too did a loud sound arise throughout the worlds then, and people, though attempting to maintain composure, became agitated and stumbled.

sanskrit: ततः समुद्ग्रथ्य सितेन वाससा स्वमूर्धजानव्यथितः स्थितोऽर्जुनः ।विभाति संपूर्णमरीचिभास्वता शिरोगतेनोदयपर्वतो यथा ॥१९॥
transliteration: tataḥ samudgrathya sitena vāsasā; svamūrdhajānavyathitaḥ sthito'rjunaḥ ,vibhāti saṁpūrṇamarīcibhāsvatā; śirogatenodayaparvato yathā.
english: Then Arjuna, unperturbed, stood having tied up his own hair with a white cloth. He shone just like the Udaya mountain (udayaparvata) does with the full-rayed brilliant sun situated on its peak.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 66 Verse 19. Then Arjuna, unperturbed, stood having tied up his own hair with a white cloth. He shone just like the Udaya mountain (udayaparvata) does with the full-rayed brilliant sun situated on its peak.

sanskrit: बलाहकः कर्णभुजेरितस्ततो हुताशनार्कप्रतिमद्युतिर्महान् ।महोरगः कृतवैरोऽर्जुनेन किरीटमासाद्य समुत्पपात ॥२०॥
transliteration: balāhakaḥ karṇabhujeritastato; hutāśanārkapratimadyutirmahān ,mahoragaḥ kṛtavairo'rjunena; kirīṭamāsādya samutpapāta.
english: The great serpent (Nāga) Balāhaka, whose radiance resembled fire and the sun, and who had been hurled about by Karṇa's arm, having conceived enmity towards Arjuna, seized the crown and flew upwards.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 66 Verse 20. The great serpent (Nāga) Balāhaka, whose radiance resembled fire and the sun, and who had been hurled about by Karṇa's arm, having conceived enmity towards Arjuna, seized the crown and flew upwards.

sanskrit: तमब्रवीद्विद्धि कृतागसं मे कृष्णाद्य मातुर्वधजातवैरम् ।ततः कृष्णः पार्थमुवाच संख्ये महोरगं कृतवैरं जहि त्वम् ॥२१॥
transliteration: tamabravīdviddhi kṛtāgasaṁ me; kṛṣṇādya māturvadhajātavairam ,tataḥ kṛṣṇaḥ pārthamuvāca saṁkhye; mahoragaṁ kṛtavairaṁ jahi tvam.
english: Regarding him (Takṣaka), it was said: 'Understand that he has committed an offense against me; his enmity arose today from the killing of his mother, by Kṛṣṇa's (Vāsudeva's) actions.' Then, Kṛṣṇa said to Arjuna (Pārtha) in battle, 'You must kill this great serpent (mahoraga) who has made himself an enemy.'
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 66 Verse 21. Regarding him (Takṣaka), it was said: 'Understand that he has committed an offense against me; his enmity arose today from the killing of his mother, by Kṛṣṇa's (Vāsudeva's) actions.' Then, Kṛṣṇa said to Arjuna (Pārtha) in battle, 'You must kill this great serpent (mahoraga) who has made himself an enemy.'

sanskrit: स एवमुक्तो मधुसूदनेन गाण्डीवधन्वा रिपुषूग्रधन्वा ।उवाच को न्वेष ममाद्य नागः स्वयं य आगाद्गरुडस्य वक्त्रम् ॥२२॥
transliteration: sa evamukto madhusūdanena; gāṇḍīvadhanvā ripuṣūgradhanvā ,uvāca ko nveṣa mamādya nāgaḥ; svayaṁ ya āgādgaruḍasya vaktram.
english: Thus addressed by Madhusūdana, Arjuna (Gaṇḍīvadhānvā), who was formidable with his bow among enemies, replied, 'Who indeed is this serpent (nāga) for me today, who has himself come into the mouth of (Garuḍa)?'
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 66 Verse 22. Thus addressed by Madhusūdana, Arjuna (Gaṇḍīvadhānvā), who was formidable with his bow among enemies, replied, 'Who indeed is this serpent (nāga) for me today, who has himself come into the mouth of (Garuḍa)?'

sanskrit: कृष्ण उवाच ।योऽसौ त्वया खाण्डवे चित्रभानुं संतर्पयानेन धनुर्धरेण ।वियद्गतो बाणनिकृत्तदेहो ह्यनेकरूपो निहतास्य माता ॥२३॥
transliteration: kṛṣṇa uvāca ,yo'sau tvayā khāṇḍave citrabhānuṁ; saṁtarpayānena dhanurdhareṇa ,viyadgato bāṇanikṛttadeho; hyanekarūpo nihatāsya mātā.
english: Kṛṣṇa said, 'This is the very one (Takṣaka) whose mother was killed, and whose multi-formed body, having gone into the sky, was severed by arrows by you, the archer, when you were gratifying the fire-god (Citrabhanu) in the (Khāṇḍava) forest.'
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 66 Verse 23. Kṛṣṇa said, 'This is the very one (Takṣaka) whose mother was killed, and whose multi-formed body, having gone into the sky, was severed by arrows by you, the archer, when you were gratifying the fire-god (Citrabhanu) in the (Khāṇḍava) forest.'

sanskrit: ततस्तु जिष्णुः परिहृत्य शेषांश्चिच्छेद षड्भिर्निशितैः सुधारैः ।नागं वियत्तिर्यगिवोत्पतन्तं स छिन्नगात्रो निपपात भूमौ ॥२४॥
transliteration: tatastu jiṣṇuḥ parihṛtya śeṣāṁ;ściccheda ṣaḍbhirniśitaiḥ sudhāraiḥ ,nāgaṁ viyattiryagivotpatantaṁ; sa chinnagātro nipapāta bhūmau.
english: Then indeed, Arjuna (Jiṣṇu), avoiding the remaining (serpents), cut down the serpent (Takṣaka) with six exceedingly sharp arrows as it was flying obliquely through the sky. With its limbs severed, it fell to the ground.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 66 Verse 24. Then indeed, Arjuna (Jiṣṇu), avoiding the remaining (serpents), cut down the serpent (Takṣaka) with six exceedingly sharp arrows as it was flying obliquely through the sky. With its limbs severed, it fell to the ground.

sanskrit: तस्मिन्मुहूर्ते दशभिः पृषत्कैः शिलाशितैर्बर्हिणवाजितैश्च ।विव्याध कर्णः पुरुषप्रवीरं धनंजयं तिर्यगवेक्षमाणम् ॥२५॥
transliteration: tasminmuhūrte daśabhiḥ pṛṣatkaiḥ; śilāśitairbarhiṇavājitaiśca ,vivyādha karṇaḥ puruṣapravīraṁ; dhanaṁjayaṁ tiryagavekṣamāṇam.
english: At that moment, Karna pierced Dhananjaya (Arjuna), the foremost among men (puruṣa), with ten arrows that were sharpened on stone and adorned with peacock feathers, while Arjuna was looking askance.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 66 Verse 25. At that moment, Karna pierced Dhananjaya (Arjuna), the foremost among men (puruṣa), with ten arrows that were sharpened on stone and adorned with peacock feathers, while Arjuna was looking askance.

sanskrit: ततोऽर्जुनो द्वादशभिर्विमुक्तैराकर्णमुक्तैर्निशितैः समर्प्य ।नाराचमाशीविषतुल्यवेगमाकर्णपूर्णायतमुत्ससर्ज ॥२६॥
transliteration: tato'rjuno dvādaśabhirvimuktai;rākarṇamuktairniśitaiḥ samarpya ,nārācamāśīviṣatulyavega;mākarṇapūrṇāyatamutsasarja.
english: Then Arjuna, having first loosed twelve sharp arrows drawn back to his ear, then discharged a formidable iron arrow, drawn back to its full length up to his ear, whose speed was like that of a venomous serpent.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 66 Verse 26. Then Arjuna, having first loosed twelve sharp arrows drawn back to his ear, then discharged a formidable iron arrow, drawn back to its full length up to his ear, whose speed was like that of a venomous serpent.

sanskrit: स चित्रवर्मेषुवरो विदार्य प्राणान्निरस्यन्निव साधु मुक्तः ।कर्णस्य पीत्वा रुधिरं विवेश वसुंधरां शोणितवाजदिग्धः ॥२७॥
transliteration: sa citravarmeṣuvaro vidārya; prāṇānnirasyanniva sādhu muktaḥ ,karṇasya pītvā rudhiraṁ viveśa; vasuṁdharāṁ śoṇitavājadigdhaḥ.
english: That excellent arrow, well-discharged, tore apart Karna's armor, as if extracting his vital breaths (prāṇa). Having drunk Karna's blood, it then penetrated the earth (vasuṃdharā), smeared with blood and marrow.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 66 Verse 27. That excellent arrow, well-discharged, tore apart Karna's armor, as if extracting his vital breaths (prāṇa). Having drunk Karna's blood, it then penetrated the earth (vasuṃdharā), smeared with blood and marrow.

sanskrit: ततो वृषो बाणनिपातकोपितो महोरगो दण्डविघट्टितो यथा ।तथाशुकारी व्यसृजच्छरोत्तमान्महाविषः सर्प इवोत्तमं विषम् ॥२८॥
transliteration: tato vṛṣo bāṇanipātakopito; mahorago daṇḍavighaṭṭito yathā ,tathāśukārī vyasṛjaccharottamā;nmahāviṣaḥ sarpa ivottamaṁ viṣam.
english: Then Karna (Vṛṣa), enraged by the strike of the arrows, much like a great serpent provoked by a stick, quickly acted and discharged excellent arrows, just as a greatly venomous serpent emits its potent venom.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 66 Verse 28. Then Karna (Vṛṣa), enraged by the strike of the arrows, much like a great serpent provoked by a stick, quickly acted and discharged excellent arrows, just as a greatly venomous serpent emits its potent venom.

sanskrit: जनार्दनं द्वादशभिः पराभिनन्नवैर्नवत्या च शरैस्तथार्जुनम् ।शरेण घोरेण पुनश्च पाण्डवं विभिद्य कर्णोऽभ्यनदज्जहास च ॥२९॥
transliteration: janārdanaṁ dvādaśabhiḥ parābhina;nnavairnavatyā ca śaraistathārjunam ,śareṇa ghoreṇa punaśca pāṇḍavaṁ; vibhidya karṇo'bhyanadajjahāsa ca.
english: Karna first pierced Janardana (Krishna) with twelve arrows, and then Arjuna with ninety-nine arrows. Subsequently, having again pierced Arjuna, the son of Pandu, with a dreadful arrow, Karna roared and laughed.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 66 Verse 29. Karna first pierced Janardana (Krishna) with twelve arrows, and then Arjuna with ninety-nine arrows. Subsequently, having again pierced Arjuna, the son of Pandu, with a dreadful arrow, Karna roared and laughed.

sanskrit: तमस्य हर्षं ममृषे न पाण्डवो बिभेद मर्माणि ततोऽस्य मर्मवित् ।परं शरैः पत्रिभिरिन्द्रविक्रमस्तथा यथेन्द्रो बलमोजसाहनत् ॥३०॥
transliteration: tamasya harṣaṁ mamṛṣe na pāṇḍavo; bibheda marmāṇi tato'sya marmavit ,paraṁ śaraiḥ patribhirindravikrama;stathā yathendro balamojasāhanat.
english: The son of Pandu (Arjuna) did not endure Karna's joy. Then, Arjuna, who knew vital spots and possessed Indra's valor, severely pierced Karna's vital spots with feathered arrows, just as Indra, with his might, struck down the demon Bala.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 66 Verse 30. The son of Pandu (Arjuna) did not endure Karna's joy. Then, Arjuna, who knew vital spots and possessed Indra's valor, severely pierced Karna's vital spots with feathered arrows, just as Indra, with his might, struck down the demon Bala.

sanskrit: ततः शराणां नवतीर्नवार्जुनः ससर्ज कर्णेऽन्तकदण्डसंनिभाः ।शरैर्भृशायस्ततनुः प्रविव्यथे तथा यथा वज्रविदारितोऽचलः ॥३१॥
transliteration: tataḥ śarāṇāṁ navatīrnavārjunaḥ; sasarja karṇe'ntakadaṇḍasaṁnibhāḥ ,śarairbhṛśāyastatanuḥ pravivyathe; tathā yathā vajravidārito'calaḥ.
english: Then Arjuna shot ninety-nine arrows, resembling the rod of Yama (Antaka), at Karna. Karna, whose body was greatly agitated by these arrows, felt intense pain, just as a mountain is shattered by a thunderbolt.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 66 Verse 31. Then Arjuna shot ninety-nine arrows, resembling the rod of Yama (Antaka), at Karna. Karna, whose body was greatly agitated by these arrows, felt intense pain, just as a mountain is shattered by a thunderbolt.

sanskrit: मणिप्रवेकोत्तमवज्रहाटकैरलंकृतं चास्य वराङ्गभूषणम् ।प्रविद्धमुर्व्यां निपपात पत्रिभिर्धनंजयेनोत्तमकुण्डलेऽपि च ॥३२॥
transliteration: maṇipravekottamavajrahāṭakai;ralaṁkṛtaṁ cāsya varāṅgabhūṣaṇam ,praviddhamurvyāṁ nipapāta patribhi;rdhanaṁjayenottamakuṇḍale'pi ca.
english: And his magnificent body ornament, adorned with choice gems, supreme diamonds, and gold, pierced by Dhanañjaya's (Arjuna's) feathered arrows, fell to the ground. His excellent earrings also fell.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 66 Verse 32. And his magnificent body ornament, adorned with choice gems, supreme diamonds, and gold, pierced by Dhanañjaya's (Arjuna's) feathered arrows, fell to the ground. His excellent earrings also fell.

sanskrit: महाधनं शिल्पिवरैः प्रयत्नतः कृतं यदस्योत्तमवर्म भास्वरम् ।सुदीर्घकालेन तदस्य पाण्डवः क्षणेन बाणैर्बहुधा व्यशातयत् ॥३३॥
transliteration: mahādhanaṁ śilpivaraiḥ prayatnataḥ; kṛtaṁ yadasyottamavarma bhāsvaram ,sudīrghakālena tadasya pāṇḍavaḥ; kṣaṇena bāṇairbahudhā vyaśātayat.
english: Arjuna (the Pāṇḍava) shattered in a moment, with arrows, in many ways, that splendid, excellent armor of Karna's, which had been crafted with great effort by master artisans and was of immense value, having been prepared over a very long period.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 66 Verse 33. Arjuna (the Pāṇḍava) shattered in a moment, with arrows, in many ways, that splendid, excellent armor of Karna's, which had been crafted with great effort by master artisans and was of immense value, having been prepared over a very long period.

sanskrit: स तं विवर्माणमथोत्तमेषुभिः शरैश्चतुर्भिः कुपितः पराभिनत् ।स विव्यथेऽत्यर्थमरिप्रहारितो यथातुरः पित्तकफानिलव्रणैः ॥३४॥
transliteration: sa taṁ vivarmāṇamathottameṣubhiḥ; śaraiścaturbhiḥ kupitaḥ parābhinat ,sa vivyathe'tyarthamariprahārito; yathāturaḥ pittakaphānilavraṇaiḥ.
english: Then, enraged, Arjuna pierced him (Karna), now stripped of his armor, with four excellent arrows. Struck by the enemy, Karna suffered intensely, like a patient tormented by diseases caused by imbalances of bile, phlegm, and wind.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 66 Verse 34. Then, enraged, Arjuna pierced him (Karna), now stripped of his armor, with four excellent arrows. Struck by the enemy, Karna suffered intensely, like a patient tormented by diseases caused by imbalances of bile, phlegm, and wind.

sanskrit: महाधनुर्मण्डलनिःसृतैः शितैः क्रियाप्रयत्नप्रहितैर्बलेन च ।ततक्ष कर्णं बहुभिः शरोत्तमैर्बिभेद मर्मस्वपि चार्जुनस्त्वरन् ॥३५॥
transliteration: mahādhanurmaṇḍalaniḥsṛtaiḥ śitaiḥ; kriyāprayatnaprahitairbalena ca ,tatakṣa karṇaṁ bahubhiḥ śarottamai;rbibheda marmasvapi cārjunastvaran.
english: And Arjuna, swiftly, with many excellent, sharp arrows - discharged with skillful effort and great force from the arc of his mighty bow - struck Karna, piercing him even in his vital spots.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 66 Verse 35. And Arjuna, swiftly, with many excellent, sharp arrows - discharged with skillful effort and great force from the arc of his mighty bow - struck Karna, piercing him even in his vital spots.

sanskrit: दृढाहतः पत्रिभिरुग्रवेगैः पार्थेन कर्णो विविधैः शिताग्रैः ।बभौ गिरिर्गैरिकधातुरक्तः क्षरन्प्रपातैरिव रक्तमम्भः ॥३६॥
transliteration: dṛḍhāhataḥ patribhirugravegaiḥ; pārthena karṇo vividhaiḥ śitāgraiḥ ,babhau girirgairikadhāturaktaḥ; kṣaranprapātairiva raktamambhaḥ.
english: Severely struck by Arjuna's (Pārtha's) various, sharp-pointed, fiercely swift winged arrows, Karna appeared like a mountain stained red with ochre minerals, from which torrents of water resembling blood gush forth.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 66 Verse 36. Severely struck by Arjuna's (Pārtha's) various, sharp-pointed, fiercely swift winged arrows, Karna appeared like a mountain stained red with ochre minerals, from which torrents of water resembling blood gush forth.

sanskrit: साश्वं तु कर्णं सरथं किरीटी समाचिनोद्भारत वत्सदन्तैः ।प्रच्छादयामास दिशश्च बाणैः सर्वप्रयत्नात्तपनीयपुङ्खैः ॥३७॥
transliteration: sāśvaṁ tu karṇaṁ sarathaṁ kirīṭī; samācinodbhārata vatsadantaiḥ ,pracchādayāmāsa diśaśca bāṇaiḥ; sarvaprayatnāttapanīyapuṅkhaiḥ.
english: Arjuna (kirīṭī), O Bhārata, indeed showered Karṇa, along with his horses and chariot, with calf-tooth arrows. He also, with all his effort, completely covered the directions with arrows that had golden shafts.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 66 Verse 37. Arjuna (kirīṭī), O Bhārata, indeed showered Karṇa, along with his horses and chariot, with calf-tooth arrows. He also, with all his effort, completely covered the directions with arrows that had golden shafts.

sanskrit: स वत्सदन्तैः पृथुपीनवक्षाः समाचितः स्माधिरथिर्विभाति ।सुपुष्पिताशोकपलाशशाल्मलिर्यथाचलः स्पन्दनचन्दनायुतः ॥३८॥
transliteration: sa vatsadantaiḥ pṛthupīnavakṣāḥ; samācitaḥ smādhirathirvibhāti ,supuṣpitāśokapalāśaśālmali;ryathācalaḥ spandanacandanāyutaḥ.
english: Karṇa, the son of Adhiratha, with his broad and stout chest, appeared splendid, covered with calf-tooth arrows. He was like a mountain adorned with beautifully blooming Aśoka, Palāśa, and Śālmalī trees, and accompanied by Syandana and Candana trees.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 66 Verse 38. Karṇa, the son of Adhiratha, with his broad and stout chest, appeared splendid, covered with calf-tooth arrows. He was like a mountain adorned with beautifully blooming Aśoka, Palāśa, and Śālmalī trees, and accompanied by Syandana and Candana trees.

sanskrit: शरैः शरीरे बहुधा समर्पितैर्विभाति कर्णः समरे विशां पते ।महीरुहैराचितसानुकन्दरो यथा महेन्द्रः शुभकर्णिकारवान् ॥३९॥
transliteration: śaraiḥ śarīre bahudhā samarpitai;rvibhāti karṇaḥ samare viśāṁ pate ,mahīruhairācitasānukandaro; yathā mahendraḥ śubhakarṇikāravān.
english: O lord of the people, Karṇa shone brilliantly in battle, his body abundantly covered with arrows. He was like the great Mahendra mountain, whose slopes and caves are filled with trees, and which is adorned with beautiful Karṇikāra trees.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 66 Verse 39. O lord of the people, Karṇa shone brilliantly in battle, his body abundantly covered with arrows. He was like the great Mahendra mountain, whose slopes and caves are filled with trees, and which is adorned with beautiful Karṇikāra trees.

sanskrit: स बाणसंघान्धनुषा व्यवासृजन्विभाति कर्णः शरजालरश्मिवान् ।सलोहितो रक्तगभस्तिमण्डलो दिवाकरोऽस्ताभिमुखो यथा तथा ॥४०॥
transliteration: sa bāṇasaṁghāndhanuṣā vyavāsṛja;nvibhāti karṇaḥ śarajālaraśmivān ,salohito raktagabhastimaṇḍalo; divākaro'stābhimukho yathā tathā.
english: Karṇa, releasing volleys of arrows with his bow, shone forth, possessing rays like a network of arrows. He appeared just like the sun (divākara), reddish and having a disk of red rays, as it faces its setting.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 66 Verse 40. Karṇa, releasing volleys of arrows with his bow, shone forth, possessing rays like a network of arrows. He appeared just like the sun (divākara), reddish and having a disk of red rays, as it faces its setting.

sanskrit: बाह्वन्तरादाधिरथेर्विमुक्तान्बाणान्महाहीनिव दीप्यमानान् ।व्यध्वंसयन्नर्जुनबाहुमुक्ताः शराः समासाद्य दिशः शिताग्राः ॥४१॥
transliteration: bāhvantarādādhirathervimuktā;nbāṇānmahāhīniva dīpyamānān ,vyadhvaṁsayannarjunabāhumuktāḥ; śarāḥ samāsādya diśaḥ śitāgrāḥ.
english: Arjuna's sharp-pointed arrows, released from his arms, scattered the blazing arrows - which were like great serpents - that had been loosed by Karna (ādhirathi) from within his arms, immediately upon meeting them.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 66 Verse 41. Arjuna's sharp-pointed arrows, released from his arms, scattered the blazing arrows - which were like great serpents - that had been loosed by Karna (ādhirathi) from within his arms, immediately upon meeting them.

sanskrit: ततश्चक्रमपतत्तस्य भूमौ स विह्वलः समरे सूतपुत्रः ।घूर्णे रथे ब्राह्मणस्याभिशापाद्रामादुपात्तेऽप्रतिभाति चास्त्रे ॥४२॥
transliteration: tataścakramapatattasya bhūmau; sa vihvalaḥ samare sūtaputraḥ ,ghūrṇe rathe brāhmaṇasyābhiśāpā;drāmādupātte'pratibhāti cāstre.
english: Then, his chariot's wheel sank into the ground, and Karna (sūtaputra) became distraught in battle. This occurred because, due to a Brahmin's curse and the failure of the weapon (astra) he had received from Rama (Parashurama), his chariot was sinking.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 66 Verse 42. Then, his chariot's wheel sank into the ground, and Karna (sūtaputra) became distraught in battle. This occurred because, due to a Brahmin's curse and the failure of the weapon (astra) he had received from Rama (Parashurama), his chariot was sinking.

sanskrit: अमृष्यमाणो व्यसनानि तानि हस्तौ विधुन्वन्स विगर्हमाणः ।धर्मप्रधानानभिपाति धर्म इत्यब्रुवन्धर्मविदः सदैव ।ममापि निम्नोऽद्य न पाति भक्तान्मन्ये न नित्यं परिपाति धर्मः ॥४३॥
transliteration: amṛṣyamāṇo vyasanāni tāni; hastau vidhunvansa vigarhamāṇaḥ ,dharmapradhānānabhipāti dharma; ityabruvandharmavidaḥ sadaiva ,mamāpi nimno'dya na pāti bhaktā;nmanye na nityaṁ paripāti dharmaḥ.
english: Unable to bear those misfortunes, he shook his hands and lamented. Those who understood the natural law (dharma) always proclaimed, "The natural law (dharma) protects those who give it primary importance." (But Karna thought:) "Today, it does not even protect my devotees, who are in a low state. I believe the natural law (dharma) does not always provide complete protection."
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 66 Verse 43. Unable to bear those misfortunes, he shook his hands and lamented. Those who understood the natural law (dharma) always proclaimed, "The natural law (dharma) protects those who give it primary importance." (But Karna thought:) "Today, it does not even protect my devotees, who are in a low state. I believe the natural law (dharma) does not always provide complete protection."

sanskrit: एवं ब्रुवन्प्रस्खलिताश्वसूतो विचाल्यमानोऽर्जुनशस्त्रपातैः ।मर्माभिघाताच्छलितः क्रियासु पुनः पुनर्धर्ममगर्हदाजौ ॥४४॥
transliteration: evaṁ bruvanpraskhalitāśvasūto; vicālyamāno'rjunaśastrapātaiḥ ,marmābhighātācchalitaḥ kriyāsu; punaḥ punardharmamagarhadājau.
english: Speaking thus, with his horses and charioteer faltering, being shaken by the impact of Arjuna's weapons, and hindered in his actions by blows to vital points, he repeatedly blamed the natural law (dharma) in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 66 Verse 44. Speaking thus, with his horses and charioteer faltering, being shaken by the impact of Arjuna's weapons, and hindered in his actions by blows to vital points, he repeatedly blamed the natural law (dharma) in battle.

sanskrit: ततः शरैर्भीमतरैरविध्यत्त्रिभिराहवे ।हस्ते कर्णस्तदा पार्थमभ्यविध्यच्च सप्तभिः ॥४५॥
transliteration: tataḥ śarairbhīmatarairavidhyattribhirāhave ,haste karṇastadā pārthamabhyavidhyacca saptabhiḥ.
english: Then, in the battle, (Karna) pierced (him) with three extremely formidable arrows. And then Karna also pierced Partha (Arjuna) in the hand with seven (arrows).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 66 Verse 45. Then, in the battle, (Karna) pierced (him) with three extremely formidable arrows. And then Karna also pierced Partha (Arjuna) in the hand with seven (arrows).

sanskrit: ततोऽर्जुनः सप्तदश तिग्मतेजानजिह्मगान् ।इन्द्राशनिसमान्घोरानसृजत्पावकोपमान् ॥४६॥
transliteration: tato'rjunaḥ saptadaśa tigmatejānajihmagān ,indrāśanisamānghorānasṛjatpāvakopamān.
english: Then Arjuna discharged seventeen (arrows) that were sharp-pointed, straight-moving, dreadful, similar to Indra's thunderbolt, and comparable to fire.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 66 Verse 46. Then Arjuna discharged seventeen (arrows) that were sharp-pointed, straight-moving, dreadful, similar to Indra's thunderbolt, and comparable to fire.

sanskrit: निर्भिद्य ते भीमवेगा न्यपतन्पृथिवीतले ।कम्पितात्मा तथा कर्णः शक्त्या चेष्टामदर्शयत् ॥४७॥
transliteration: nirbhidya te bhīmavegā nyapatanpṛthivītale ,kampitātmā tathā karṇaḥ śaktyā ceṣṭāmadarśayat.
english: Having penetrated (their targets), those terribly swift (arrows) fell to the ground. Karna, with his inner self (ātman) shaken, then displayed his counter-effort with his divine missile (śakti).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 66 Verse 47. Having penetrated (their targets), those terribly swift (arrows) fell to the ground. Karna, with his inner self (ātman) shaken, then displayed his counter-effort with his divine missile (śakti).

sanskrit: बलेनाथ स संस्तभ्य ब्रह्मास्त्रं समुदैरयत् ।ऐन्द्रास्त्रमर्जुनश्चापि तद्दृष्ट्वाभिन्यमन्त्रयत् ॥४८॥
transliteration: balenātha sa saṁstabhya brahmāstraṁ samudairayat ,aindrāstramarjunaścāpi taddṛṣṭvābhinyamantrayat.
english: Then, having stabilized himself with (his) inner strength, he (Karna) discharged the Brahmastra (divine weapon). And Arjuna, having seen that (Brahmastra), also invoked the Aindrāstra (divine weapon).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 66 Verse 48. Then, having stabilized himself with (his) inner strength, he (Karna) discharged the Brahmastra (divine weapon). And Arjuna, having seen that (Brahmastra), also invoked the Aindrāstra (divine weapon).

sanskrit: गाण्डीवं ज्यां च बाणांश्च अनुमन्त्र्य धनंजयः ।असृजच्छरवर्षाणि वर्षाणीव पुरंदरः ॥४९॥
transliteration: gāṇḍīvaṁ jyāṁ ca bāṇāṁśca anumantrya dhanaṁjayaḥ ,asṛjaccharavarṣāṇi varṣāṇīva puraṁdaraḥ.
english: After consecrating his Gaṇḍīva bow, its string, and his arrows with a mantra, Dhananjaya (Arjuna) unleashed torrents of arrows, just as Indra pours down showers of rain.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 66 Verse 49. After consecrating his Gaṇḍīva bow, its string, and his arrows with a mantra, Dhananjaya (Arjuna) unleashed torrents of arrows, just as Indra pours down showers of rain.

sanskrit: ततस्तेजोमया बाणा रथात्पार्थस्य निःसृताः ।प्रादुरासन्महावीर्याः कर्णस्य रथमन्तिकात् ॥५०॥
transliteration: tatastejomayā bāṇā rathātpārthasya niḥsṛtāḥ ,prādurāsanmahāvīryāḥ karṇasya rathamantikāt.
english: Then, fiery arrows, brimming with great power, shot forth from Arjuna's (Partha's) chariot and materialized near Karna's chariot.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 66 Verse 50. Then, fiery arrows, brimming with great power, shot forth from Arjuna's (Partha's) chariot and materialized near Karna's chariot.

sanskrit: तान्कर्णस्त्वग्रतोऽभ्यस्तान्मोघांश्चक्रे महारथः ।ततोऽब्रवीद्वृष्णिवीरस्तस्मिन्नस्त्रे विनाशिते ॥५१॥
transliteration: tānkarṇastvagrato'bhyastānmoghāṁścakre mahārathaḥ ,tato'bravīdvṛṣṇivīrastasminnastre vināśite.
english: But Karna, the great warrior, made those arrows useless that were discharged directly at him. Then, with that weapon rendered ineffective, the hero of the Vṛṣṇis (Krishna) spoke.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 66 Verse 51. But Karna, the great warrior, made those arrows useless that were discharged directly at him. Then, with that weapon rendered ineffective, the hero of the Vṛṣṇis (Krishna) spoke.

sanskrit: विसृजास्त्रं परं पार्थ राधेयो ग्रसते शरान् ।ब्रह्मास्त्रमर्जुनश्चापि संमन्त्र्याथ प्रयोजयत् ॥५२॥
transliteration: visṛjāstraṁ paraṁ pārtha rādheyo grasate śarān ,brahmāstramarjunaścāpi saṁmantryātha prayojayat.
english: "O Partha, unleash a supreme weapon! Radheya (Karna) is neutralizing your arrows." And Arjuna, after invoking the Brahmastra (divine weapon) with a mantra, then deployed it.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 66 Verse 52. "O Partha, unleash a supreme weapon! Radheya (Karna) is neutralizing your arrows." And Arjuna, after invoking the Brahmastra (divine weapon) with a mantra, then deployed it.

sanskrit: छादयित्वा ततो बाणैः कर्णं प्रभ्राम्य चार्जुनः ।तस्य कर्णः शरैः क्रुद्धश्चिच्छेद ज्यां सुतेजनैः ॥५३॥
transliteration: chādayitvā tato bāṇaiḥ karṇaṁ prabhrāmya cārjunaḥ ,tasya karṇaḥ śaraiḥ kruddhaściccheda jyāṁ sutejanaiḥ.
english: Then Arjuna, having enveloped Karna with arrows and caused him to reel, Karna, enraged, cut Arjuna's bowstring with his extremely sharp arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 66 Verse 53. Then Arjuna, having enveloped Karna with arrows and caused him to reel, Karna, enraged, cut Arjuna's bowstring with his extremely sharp arrows.

sanskrit: ततो ज्यामवधायान्यामनुमृज्य च पाण्डवः ।शरैरवाकिरत्कर्णं दीप्यमानैः सहस्रशः ॥५४॥
transliteration: tato jyāmavadhāyānyāmanumṛjya ca pāṇḍavaḥ ,śarairavākiratkarṇaṁ dīpyamānaiḥ sahasraśaḥ.
english: Then the Pāṇḍava (Arjuna), having quickly strung another bowstring and adjusted it, showered Karna with thousands of blazing arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 66 Verse 54. Then the Pāṇḍava (Arjuna), having quickly strung another bowstring and adjusted it, showered Karna with thousands of blazing arrows.

sanskrit: तस्य ज्याच्छेदनं कर्णो ज्यावधानं च संयुगे ।नान्वबुध्यत शीघ्रत्वात्तदद्भुतमिवाभवत् ॥५५॥
transliteration: tasya jyācchedanaṁ karṇo jyāvadhānaṁ ca saṁyuge ,nānvabudhyata śīghratvāttadadbhutamivābhavat.
english: In the battle, Karna did not even perceive the cutting of the bowstring nor its re-stringing by Arjuna, due to the latter's swiftness. That was indeed like a miracle.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 66 Verse 55. In the battle, Karna did not even perceive the cutting of the bowstring nor its re-stringing by Arjuna, due to the latter's swiftness. That was indeed like a miracle.

sanskrit: अस्त्रैरस्त्राणि राधेयः प्रत्यहन्सव्यसाचिनः ।चक्रे चाभ्यधिकं पार्थात्स्ववीर्यं प्रतिदर्शयन् ॥५६॥
transliteration: astrairastrāṇi rādheyaḥ pratyahansavyasācinaḥ ,cakre cābhyadhikaṁ pārthātsvavīryaṁ pratidarśayan.
english: Rādheya (Karna) countered the weapons of Savyasācin (Arjuna) with his own missile-weapons. And, demonstrating his own prowess, he showed himself to be superior to Pārtha (Arjuna).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 66 Verse 56. Rādheya (Karna) countered the weapons of Savyasācin (Arjuna) with his own missile-weapons. And, demonstrating his own prowess, he showed himself to be superior to Pārtha (Arjuna).

sanskrit: ततः कृष्णोऽर्जुनं दृष्ट्वा कर्णास्त्रेणाभिपीडितम् ।अभ्यस्येत्यब्रवीत्पार्थमातिष्ठास्त्रमनुत्तमम् ॥५७॥
transliteration: tataḥ kṛṣṇo'rjunaṁ dṛṣṭvā karṇāstreṇābhipīḍitam ,abhyasyetyabravītpārthamātiṣṭhāstramanuttamam.
english: Then Krishna, seeing Arjuna distressed by Karna's weapon, said to Partha (Arjuna), 'Exert yourself! Wield the unsurpassed weapon!'
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 66 Verse 57. Then Krishna, seeing Arjuna distressed by Karna's weapon, said to Partha (Arjuna), 'Exert yourself! Wield the unsurpassed weapon!'

sanskrit: ततोऽन्यमग्निसदृशं शरं सर्पविषोपमम् ।अश्मसारमयं दिव्यमनुमन्त्र्य धनंजयः ॥५८॥
transliteration: tato'nyamagnisadṛśaṁ śaraṁ sarpaviṣopamam ,aśmasāramayaṁ divyamanumantrya dhanaṁjayaḥ.
english: Then Dhananjaya (Arjuna), after consecrating another divine arrow, one resembling fire, possessing venom like a serpent's, and made of steel...
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 66 Verse 58. Then Dhananjaya (Arjuna), after consecrating another divine arrow, one resembling fire, possessing venom like a serpent's, and made of steel...

sanskrit: रौद्रमस्त्रं समादाय क्षेप्तुकामः किरीटवान् ।ततोऽग्रसन्मही चक्रं राधेयस्य महामृधे ॥५९॥
transliteration: raudramastraṁ samādāya kṣeptukāmaḥ kirīṭavān ,tato'grasanmahī cakraṁ rādheyasya mahāmṛdhe.
english: The crowned (Arjuna), having taken up that dreadful weapon and intending to hurl it, (was interrupted when) then, in the great battle, the Earth swallowed Radheya's (Karna's) chariot wheel.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 66 Verse 59. The crowned (Arjuna), having taken up that dreadful weapon and intending to hurl it, (was interrupted when) then, in the great battle, the Earth swallowed Radheya's (Karna's) chariot wheel.

sanskrit: ग्रस्तचक्रस्तु राधेयः कोपादश्रूण्यवर्तयत् ।सोऽब्रवीदर्जुनं चापि मुहूर्तं क्षम पाण्डव ॥६०॥
transliteration: grastacakrastu rādheyaḥ kopādaśrūṇyavartayat ,so'bravīdarjunaṁ cāpi muhūrtaṁ kṣama pāṇḍava.
english: But Radheya (Karna), whose chariot wheel had been swallowed, shed tears out of anger. He then said to Arjuna, 'O Pandava (Arjuna), wait for a moment!'
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 66 Verse 60. But Radheya (Karna), whose chariot wheel had been swallowed, shed tears out of anger. He then said to Arjuna, 'O Pandava (Arjuna), wait for a moment!'

sanskrit: मध्ये चक्रमवग्रस्तं दृष्ट्वा दैवादिदं मम ।पार्थ कापुरुषाचीर्णमभिसंधिं विवर्जय ॥६१॥
transliteration: madhye cakramavagrastaṁ dṛṣṭvā daivādidaṁ mama ,pārtha kāpuruṣācīrṇamabhisaṁdhiṁ vivarjaya.
english: O Pārtha, seeing my chariot wheel stuck in the ground by fate, abandon this action (abhisaṃdhi) characteristic of a coward.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 66 Verse 61. O Pārtha, seeing my chariot wheel stuck in the ground by fate, abandon this action (abhisaṃdhi) characteristic of a coward.

sanskrit: प्रकीर्णकेशे विमुखे ब्राह्मणे च कृताञ्जलौ ।शरणागते न्यस्तशस्त्रे तथा व्यसनगेऽर्जुन ॥६२॥
transliteration: prakīrṇakeśe vimukhe brāhmaṇe ca kṛtāñjalau ,śaraṇāgate nyastaśastre tathā vyasanage'rjuna.
english: O Arjuna, (one should not attack) a brāhmaṇa who has dishevelled hair, whose face is averted, who has folded hands in supplication, who has sought refuge, who has laid down his weapons, or who is in distress.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 66 Verse 62. O Arjuna, (one should not attack) a brāhmaṇa who has dishevelled hair, whose face is averted, who has folded hands in supplication, who has sought refuge, who has laid down his weapons, or who is in distress.

sanskrit: अबाणे भ्रष्टकवचे भ्रष्टभग्नायुधे तथा ।न शूराः प्रहरन्त्याजौ न राज्ञे पार्थिवास्तथा ।त्वं च शूरोऽसि कौन्तेय तस्मात्क्षम मुहूर्तकम् ॥६३॥
