transliteration: abāṇe bhraṣṭakavace bhraṣṭabhagnāyudhe tathā ,na śūrāḥ praharantyājau na rājñe pārthivāstathā ,tvaṁ ca śūro'si kaunteya tasmātkṣama muhūrtakam.
english: Similarly, heroes (śūra) do not strike in battle one who is weaponless, whose armor has fallen off, and whose weapons are broken and shattered. Nor do kings (pārthiva) attack another king (rājan) in such a state. O son of Kuntī, you are indeed a hero (śūra); therefore, wait for a moment.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 66 Verse 63. Similarly, heroes (śūra) do not strike in battle one who is weaponless, whose armor has fallen off, and whose weapons are broken and shattered. Nor do kings (pārthiva) attack another king (rājan) in such a state. O son of Kuntī, you are indeed a hero (śūra); therefore, wait for a moment.

sanskrit: यावच्चक्रमिदं भूमेरुद्धरामि धनंजय ।न मां रथस्थो भूमिष्ठमसज्जं हन्तुमर्हसि ।न वासुदेवात्त्वत्तो वा पाण्डवेय बिभेम्यहम् ॥६४॥
transliteration: yāvaccakramidaṁ bhūmeruddharāmi dhanaṁjaya ,na māṁ rathastho bhūmiṣṭhamasajjaṁ hantumarhasi ,na vāsudevāttvatto vā pāṇḍaveya bibhemyaham.
english: O Dhanañjaya, (wait) until I lift this wheel from the earth. You, being on your chariot, should not kill me while I am standing on the ground and am unprepared (asajja). O son of Pāṇḍu, I do not fear Vāsudeva or you.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 66 Verse 64. O Dhanañjaya, (wait) until I lift this wheel from the earth. You, being on your chariot, should not kill me while I am standing on the ground and am unprepared (asajja). O son of Pāṇḍu, I do not fear Vāsudeva or you.

sanskrit: त्वं हि क्षत्रियदायादो महाकुलविवर्धनः ।स्मृत्वा धर्मोपदेशं त्वं मुहूर्तं क्षम पाण्डव ॥६५॥
transliteration: tvaṁ hi kṣatriyadāyādo mahākulavivardhanaḥ ,smṛtvā dharmopadeśaṁ tvaṁ muhūrtaṁ kṣama pāṇḍava.
english: You are indeed the heir of a warrior and the enhancer of a great lineage. Remembering the instruction regarding righteous conduct (dharma), you, O Pāṇḍava, should forgive for a moment.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 66 Verse 65. You are indeed the heir of a warrior and the enhancer of a great lineage. Remembering the instruction regarding righteous conduct (dharma), you, O Pāṇḍava, should forgive for a moment.

sanskrit: संजय उवाच ।अथाब्रवीद्वासुदेवो रथस्थो राधेय दिष्ट्या स्मरसीह धर्मम् ।प्रायेण नीचा व्यसनेषु मग्ना निन्दन्ति दैवं कुकृतं न तत्तत् ॥१॥
transliteration: saṁjaya uvāca ,athābravīdvāsudevo rathastho; rādheya diṣṭyā smarasīha dharmam ,prāyeṇa nīcā vyasaneṣu magnā; nindanti daivaṁ kukṛtaṁ na tattat.
english: Saṃjaya said: Then Vāsudeva, while in the chariot, spoke: 'O son of Rādhā, it is fortunate that you remember righteous conduct (dharma) here. Usually, ignoble people, when submerged in calamities, blame fate and not their own misdeeds.'
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 67 Verse 1. Saṃjaya said: Then Vāsudeva, while in the chariot, spoke: 'O son of Rādhā, it is fortunate that you remember righteous conduct (dharma) here. Usually, ignoble people, when submerged in calamities, blame fate and not their own misdeeds.'

sanskrit: यद्द्रौपदीमेकवस्त्रां सभायामानाय्य त्वं चैव सुयोधनश्च ।दुःशासनः शकुनिः सौबलश्च न ते कर्ण प्रत्यभात्तत्र धर्मः ॥२॥
transliteration: yaddraupadīmekavastrāṁ sabhāyā;mānāyya tvaṁ caiva suyodhanaśca ,duḥśāsanaḥ śakuniḥ saubalaśca; na te karṇa pratyabhāttatra dharmaḥ.
english: O Karṇa, when you, along with Suyodhana, Duḥśāsana, and Śakuni, son of Subala, had brought Draupadī, clad in a single garment, into the assembly, why did righteous conduct (dharma) not occur to you then?
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 67 Verse 2. O Karṇa, when you, along with Suyodhana, Duḥśāsana, and Śakuni, son of Subala, had brought Draupadī, clad in a single garment, into the assembly, why did righteous conduct (dharma) not occur to you then?

sanskrit: यदा सभायां कौन्तेयमनक्षज्ञं युधिष्ठिरम् ।अक्षज्ञः शकुनिर्जेता तदा धर्मः क्व ते गतः ॥३॥
transliteration: yadā sabhāyāṁ kaunteyamanakṣajñaṁ yudhiṣṭhiram ,akṣajñaḥ śakunirjetā tadā dharmaḥ kva te gataḥ.
english: When, in the assembly, Yudhiṣṭhira, the son of Kuntī, who was unskilled in dice, was defeated by Śakuni, who was skilled in dice, then where had righteous conduct (dharma) gone for you?
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 67 Verse 3. When, in the assembly, Yudhiṣṭhira, the son of Kuntī, who was unskilled in dice, was defeated by Śakuni, who was skilled in dice, then where had righteous conduct (dharma) gone for you?

sanskrit: यदा रजस्वलां कृष्णां दुःशासनवशे स्थिताम् ।सभायां प्राहसः कर्ण क्व ते धर्मस्तदा गतः ॥४॥
transliteration: yadā rajasvalāṁ kṛṣṇāṁ duḥśāsanavaśe sthitām ,sabhāyāṁ prāhasaḥ karṇa kva te dharmastadā gataḥ.
english: O Karṇa, when you laughed in the assembly at Kṛṣṇā (Draupadi), who was menstruating and under Duḥśāsana's control, where had your sense of natural law (dharma) gone then?
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 67 Verse 4. O Karṇa, when you laughed in the assembly at Kṛṣṇā (Draupadi), who was menstruating and under Duḥśāsana's control, where had your sense of natural law (dharma) gone then?

sanskrit: राज्यलुब्धः पुनः कर्ण समाह्वयसि पाण्डवम् ।गान्धारराजमाश्रित्य क्व ते धर्मस्तदा गतः ॥५॥
transliteration: rājyalubdhaḥ punaḥ karṇa samāhvayasi pāṇḍavam ,gāndhārarājamāśritya kva te dharmastadā gataḥ.
english: O Karṇa, greedy for the kingdom, you again summon the Pāṇḍava, having relied on the king of Gandhāra (Śakuni). Where had your sense of natural law (dharma) gone then?
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 67 Verse 5. O Karṇa, greedy for the kingdom, you again summon the Pāṇḍava, having relied on the king of Gandhāra (Śakuni). Where had your sense of natural law (dharma) gone then?

sanskrit: एवमुक्ते तु राधेये वासुदेवेन पाण्डवम् ।मन्युरभ्याविशत्तीव्रः स्मृत्वा तत्तद्धनंजयम् ॥६॥
transliteration: evamukte tu rādheye vāsudevena pāṇḍavam ,manyurabhyāviśattīvraḥ smṛtvā tattaddhanaṁjayam.
english: When Vāsudeva (Krishna) had thus spoken to the son of Rādhā (Karṇa), intense anger seized Dhanañjaya (Arjuna) as he remembered those specific incidents.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 67 Verse 6. When Vāsudeva (Krishna) had thus spoken to the son of Rādhā (Karṇa), intense anger seized Dhanañjaya (Arjuna) as he remembered those specific incidents.

sanskrit: तस्य क्रोधेन सर्वेभ्यः स्रोतोभ्यस्तेजसोऽर्चिषः ।प्रादुरासन्महाराज तदद्भुतमिवाभवत् ॥७॥
transliteration: tasya krodhena sarvebhyaḥ srotobhyastejaso'rciṣaḥ ,prādurāsanmahārāja tadadbhutamivābhavat.
english: O great king, due to his (Arjuna's) anger, rays of fiery energy manifested from all his bodily channels. That was truly a wondrous sight.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 67 Verse 7. O great king, due to his (Arjuna's) anger, rays of fiery energy manifested from all his bodily channels. That was truly a wondrous sight.

sanskrit: तं समीक्ष्य ततः कर्णो ब्रह्मास्त्रेण धनंजयम् ।अभ्यवर्षत्पुनर्यत्नमकरोद्रथसर्जने ।तदस्त्रमस्त्रेणावार्य प्रजहारास्य पाण्डवः ॥८॥
transliteration: taṁ samīkṣya tataḥ karṇo brahmāstreṇa dhanaṁjayam ,abhyavarṣatpunaryatnamakarodrathasarjane ,tadastramastreṇāvārya prajahārāsya pāṇḍavaḥ.
english: Upon seeing Arjuna (dhanañjayam), Karṇa then assailed him with the Brahmāstra. He then made another attempt at creating a chariot. However, the Pāṇḍava, repelling that weapon with his own, struck Karṇa.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 67 Verse 8. Upon seeing Arjuna (dhanañjayam), Karṇa then assailed him with the Brahmāstra. He then made another attempt at creating a chariot. However, the Pāṇḍava, repelling that weapon with his own, struck Karṇa.

sanskrit: ततोऽन्यदस्त्रं कौन्तेयो दयितं जातवेदसः ।मुमोच कर्णमुद्दिश्य तत्प्रजज्वाल वै भृशम् ॥९॥
transliteration: tato'nyadastraṁ kaunteyo dayitaṁ jātavedasaḥ ,mumoca karṇamuddiśya tatprajajvāla vai bhṛśam.
english: Then Arjuna (kaunteya), son of Kunti, discharged another weapon, one dear to the fire-god (jātavedas), aiming it at Karṇa. That weapon blazed intensely.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 67 Verse 9. Then Arjuna (kaunteya), son of Kunti, discharged another weapon, one dear to the fire-god (jātavedas), aiming it at Karṇa. That weapon blazed intensely.

sanskrit: वारुणेन ततः कर्णः शमयामास पावकम् ।जीमूतैश्च दिशः सर्वाश्चक्रे तिमिरदुर्दिनाः ॥१०॥
transliteration: vāruṇena tataḥ karṇaḥ śamayāmāsa pāvakam ,jīmūtaiśca diśaḥ sarvāścakre timiradurdināḥ.
english: Then Karṇa extinguished that fiery weapon with the Vāruṇa weapon. And with clouds, he made all directions dark and gloomy.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 67 Verse 10. Then Karṇa extinguished that fiery weapon with the Vāruṇa weapon. And with clouds, he made all directions dark and gloomy.

sanskrit: पाण्डवेयस्त्वसंभ्रान्तो वायव्यास्त्रेण वीर्यवान् ।अपोवाह तदाभ्राणि राधेयस्य प्रपश्यतः ॥११॥
transliteration: pāṇḍaveyastvasaṁbhrānto vāyavyāstreṇa vīryavān ,apovāha tadābhrāṇi rādheyasya prapaśyataḥ.
english: But the powerful son of Pāṇḍu (pāṇḍaveya), remaining undisturbed, then dispelled those clouds with the Vāyavya weapon, while Karṇa (rādheya) watched.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 67 Verse 11. But the powerful son of Pāṇḍu (pāṇḍaveya), remaining undisturbed, then dispelled those clouds with the Vāyavya weapon, while Karṇa (rādheya) watched.

sanskrit: तं हस्तिकक्ष्याप्रवरं च बाणैः सुवर्णमुक्तामणिवज्रमृष्टम् ।कालप्रयत्नोत्तमशिल्पियत्नैः कृतं सुरूपं वितमस्कमुच्चैः ॥१२॥
transliteration: taṁ hastikakṣyāpravaraṁ ca bāṇaiḥ; suvarṇamuktāmaṇivajramṛṣṭam ,kālaprayatnottamaśilpiyatnaiḥ; kṛtaṁ surūpaṁ vitamaskamuccaiḥ.
english: That excellent elephant-banner (hastikakṣyā), adorned with arrows, polished with gold, pearls, jewels, and diamonds, created with great effort by the best artisans over time, was very beautiful, flawless, and stood high.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 67 Verse 12. That excellent elephant-banner (hastikakṣyā), adorned with arrows, polished with gold, pearls, jewels, and diamonds, created with great effort by the best artisans over time, was very beautiful, flawless, and stood high.

sanskrit: ऊर्जस्करं तव सैन्यस्य नित्यममित्रवित्रासनमीड्यरूपम् ।विख्यातमादित्यसमस्य लोके त्विषा समं पावकभानुचन्द्रैः ॥१३॥
transliteration: ūrjaskaraṁ tava sainyasya nitya;mamitravitrāsanamīḍyarūpam ,vikhyātamādityasamasya loke; tviṣā samaṁ pāvakabhānucandraiḥ.
english: It was always a source of strength for your army, constantly terrifying enemies, and of a glorious form. Renowned in the world for being like the sun, its brilliance was equal to that of fire, the sun, and the moon.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 67 Verse 13. It was always a source of strength for your army, constantly terrifying enemies, and of a glorious form. Renowned in the world for being like the sun, its brilliance was equal to that of fire, the sun, and the moon.

sanskrit: ततः क्षुरेणाधिरथेः किरीटी सुवर्णपुङ्खेन शितेन यत्तः ।श्रिया ज्वलन्तं ध्वजमुन्ममाथ महारथस्याधिरथेर्महात्मा ॥१४॥
transliteration: tataḥ kṣureṇādhiratheḥ kirīṭī; suvarṇapuṅkhena śitena yattaḥ ,śriyā jvalantaṁ dhvajamunmamātha; mahārathasyādhirathermahātmā.
english: Then, the great-souled, crowned Arjuna (kirīṭī), exerting himself, felled that glorious and blazing banner of the great charioteer (mahāratha), Karna (adhirathi), with a sharp, gold-shafted razor-arrow.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 67 Verse 14. Then, the great-souled, crowned Arjuna (kirīṭī), exerting himself, felled that glorious and blazing banner of the great charioteer (mahāratha), Karna (adhirathi), with a sharp, gold-shafted razor-arrow.

sanskrit: यशश्च धर्मश्च जयश्च मारिष प्रियाणि सर्वाणि च तेन केतुना ।तदा कुरूणां हृदयानि चापतन्बभूव हाहेति च निस्वनो महान् ॥१५॥
transliteration: yaśaśca dharmaśca jayaśca māriṣa; priyāṇi sarvāṇi ca tena ketunā ,tadā kurūṇāṁ hṛdayāni cāpata;nbabhūva hāheti ca nisvano mahān.
english: O venerable one (māriṣa), Karna's fame, his adherence to natural law (dharma), his victory, and all his cherished things were linked to that banner (ketu). At that moment, the hearts of the Kurus sank, and a great cry of 'Alas! Alas!' arose.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 67 Verse 15. O venerable one (māriṣa), Karna's fame, his adherence to natural law (dharma), his victory, and all his cherished things were linked to that banner (ketu). At that moment, the hearts of the Kurus sank, and a great cry of 'Alas! Alas!' arose.

sanskrit: अथ त्वरन्कर्णवधाय पाण्डवो महेन्द्रवज्रानलदण्डसंनिभम् ।आदत्त पार्थोऽञ्जलिकं निषङ्गात्सहस्ररश्मेरिव रश्मिमुत्तमम् ॥१६॥
transliteration: atha tvarankarṇavadhāya pāṇḍavo; mahendravajrānaladaṇḍasaṁnibham ,ādatta pārtho'ñjalikaṁ niṣaṅgā;tsahasraraśmeriva raśmimuttamam.
english: Then, hastening to slay Karna, Partha (Arjuna) took from his quiver an excellent arrow called Añjalika. It resembled the thunderbolt of the great Indra and a fiery mace, shining like a supreme ray from the thousand-rayed sun.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 67 Verse 16. Then, hastening to slay Karna, Partha (Arjuna) took from his quiver an excellent arrow called Añjalika. It resembled the thunderbolt of the great Indra and a fiery mace, shining like a supreme ray from the thousand-rayed sun.

sanskrit: मर्मच्छिदं शोणितमांसदिग्धं वैश्वानरार्कप्रतिमं महार्हम् ।नराश्वनागासुहरं त्र्यरत्निं षड्वाजमञ्जोगतिमुग्रवेगम् ॥१७॥
transliteration: marmacchidaṁ śoṇitamāṁsadigdhaṁ; vaiśvānarārkapratimaṁ mahārham ,narāśvanāgāsuharaṁ tryaratniṁ; ṣaḍvājamañjogatimugravegam.
english: [He took an arrow that was] capable of piercing vital spots, smeared with blood and flesh, glowing like the universal fire (vaiśvānara) and the sun, highly precious, capable of taking the lives of men, horses, and elephants, three cubits long, fitted with six feathers, flying with a straight course, and possessing tremendous speed.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 67 Verse 17. [He took an arrow that was] capable of piercing vital spots, smeared with blood and flesh, glowing like the universal fire (vaiśvānara) and the sun, highly precious, capable of taking the lives of men, horses, and elephants, three cubits long, fitted with six feathers, flying with a straight course, and possessing tremendous speed.

sanskrit: सहस्रनेत्राशनितुल्यतेजसं समानक्रव्यादमिवातिदुःसहम् ।पिनाकनारायणचक्रसंनिभं भयंकरं प्राणभृतां विनाशनम् ॥१८॥
transliteration: sahasranetrāśanitulyatejasaṁ; samānakravyādamivātiduḥsaham ,pinākanārāyaṇacakrasaṁnibhaṁ; bhayaṁkaraṁ prāṇabhṛtāṁ vināśanam.
english: [The arrow possessed] a brilliance equal to the thunderbolt of the thousand-eyed Indra, and was exceedingly unbearable, like a flesh-eater. It resembled Śiva's Pinaka bow and Viṣṇu's discus (cakra), striking terror and causing the utter destruction of all living beings.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 67 Verse 18. [The arrow possessed] a brilliance equal to the thunderbolt of the thousand-eyed Indra, and was exceedingly unbearable, like a flesh-eater. It resembled Śiva's Pinaka bow and Viṣṇu's discus (cakra), striking terror and causing the utter destruction of all living beings.

sanskrit: युक्त्वा महास्त्रेण परेण मन्त्रविद्विकृष्य गाण्डीवमुवाच सस्वनम् ।अयं महास्त्रोऽप्रतिमो धृतः शरः शरीरभिच्चासुहरश्च दुर्हृदः ॥१९॥
transliteration: yuktvā mahāstreṇa pareṇa mantravi;dvikṛṣya gāṇḍīvamuvāca sasvanam ,ayaṁ mahāstro'pratimo dhṛtaḥ śaraḥ; śarīrabhiccāsuharaśca durhṛdaḥ.
english: The knower of sacred incantations (mantravit), Arjuna, having empowered the arrow with a supreme great divine weapon (mahāstra), and having drawn his Gāṇḍīva bow with a twang, declared: "This unparalleled great missile of an arrow, which I hold, is destined to pierce the bodies and take the lives of my enemies."
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 67 Verse 19. The knower of sacred incantations (mantravit), Arjuna, having empowered the arrow with a supreme great divine weapon (mahāstra), and having drawn his Gāṇḍīva bow with a twang, declared: "This unparalleled great missile of an arrow, which I hold, is destined to pierce the bodies and take the lives of my enemies."

sanskrit: तपोऽस्ति तप्तं गुरवश्च तोषिता मया यदिष्टं सुहृदां तथा श्रुतम् ।अनेन सत्येन निहन्त्वयं शरः सुदंशितः कर्णमरिं ममाजितः ॥२०॥
transliteration: tapo'sti taptaṁ guravaśca toṣitā; mayā yadiṣṭaṁ suhṛdāṁ tathā śrutam ,anena satyena nihantvayaṁ śaraḥ; sudaṁśitaḥ karṇamariṁ mamājitaḥ.
english: If asceticism (tapas) has been performed by me, and if my teachers (guru) have been pleased, and if what is desired by my friends has been fulfilled, then by this truth, may this well-sharpened arrow of mine slay my undefeated enemy, Karṇa.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 67 Verse 20. If asceticism (tapas) has been performed by me, and if my teachers (guru) have been pleased, and if what is desired by my friends has been fulfilled, then by this truth, may this well-sharpened arrow of mine slay my undefeated enemy, Karṇa.

sanskrit: इत्यूचिवांस्तं स मुमोच बाणं धनंजयः कर्णवधाय घोरम् ।कृत्यामथर्वाङ्गिरसीमिवोग्रां दीप्तामसह्यां युधि मृत्युनापि ॥२१॥
transliteration: ityūcivāṁstaṁ sa mumoca bāṇaṁ; dhanaṁjayaḥ karṇavadhāya ghoram ,kṛtyāmatharvāṅgirasīmivogrāṁ; dīptāmasahyāṁ yudhi mṛtyunāpi.
english: Having spoken thus, Dhanaṃjaya (Arjuna) released that terrible arrow for the slaying of Karṇa. It was dreadful, blazing, and unbearable, like an Atharvāṅgiras Kṛtyā (a malevolent magic spell), which even Death (mṛtyu) itself could not withstand in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 67 Verse 21. Having spoken thus, Dhanaṃjaya (Arjuna) released that terrible arrow for the slaying of Karṇa. It was dreadful, blazing, and unbearable, like an Atharvāṅgiras Kṛtyā (a malevolent magic spell), which even Death (mṛtyu) itself could not withstand in battle.

sanskrit: ब्रुवन्किरीटी तमतिप्रहृष्टो अयं शरो मे विजयावहोऽस्तु ।जिघांसुरर्केन्दुसमप्रभावः कर्णं समाप्तिं नयतां यमाय ॥२२॥
transliteration: bruvankirīṭī tamatiprahṛṣṭo; ayaṁ śaro me vijayāvaho'stu ,jighāṁsurarkendusamaprabhāvaḥ; karṇaṁ samāptiṁ nayatāṁ yamāya.
english: Speaking thus, the extremely delighted diadem-wearer (Arjuna) said: 'May this arrow of mine bring victory! Desirous of killing, and having the splendor of the sun and moon, may it lead Karṇa to his end, to Yama.'
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 67 Verse 22. Speaking thus, the extremely delighted diadem-wearer (Arjuna) said: 'May this arrow of mine bring victory! Desirous of killing, and having the splendor of the sun and moon, may it lead Karṇa to his end, to Yama.'

sanskrit: तेनेषुवर्येण किरीटमाली प्रहृष्टरूपो विजयावहेन ।जिघांसुरर्केन्दुसमप्रभेण चक्रे विषक्तं रिपुमाततायी ॥२३॥
transliteration: teneṣuvaryeṇa kirīṭamālī; prahṛṣṭarūpo vijayāvahena ,jighāṁsurarkendusamaprabheṇa; cakre viṣaktaṁ ripumātatāyī.
english: With that best of arrows, which brought victory, and which, desirous of killing, possessed the splendor of the sun and moon, the diadem-wearer (Arjuna), whose form was joyful, struck his aggressive enemy, piercing him.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 67 Verse 23. With that best of arrows, which brought victory, and which, desirous of killing, possessed the splendor of the sun and moon, the diadem-wearer (Arjuna), whose form was joyful, struck his aggressive enemy, piercing him.

sanskrit: तदुद्यतादित्यसमानवर्चसं शरन्नभोमध्यगभास्करोपमम् ।वराङ्गमुर्व्यामपतच्चमूपतेर्दिवाकरोऽस्तादिव रक्तमण्डलः ॥२४॥
transliteration: tadudyatādityasamānavarcasaṁ; śarannabhomadhyagabhāskaropamam ,varāṅgamurvyāmapataccamūpate;rdivākaro'stādiva raktamaṇḍalaḥ.
english: That glorious head of the commander (Karna), brilliant like the rising sun and resembling the sun positioned in the middle of an autumn sky, fell to the earth, just as the sun, with its reddish disc, descends from the setting point.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 67 Verse 24. That glorious head of the commander (Karna), brilliant like the rising sun and resembling the sun positioned in the middle of an autumn sky, fell to the earth, just as the sun, with its reddish disc, descends from the setting point.

sanskrit: तदस्य देही सततं सुखोदितं स्वरूपमत्यर्थमुदारकर्मणः ।परेण कृच्छ्रेण शरीरमत्यजद्गृहं महर्द्धीव ससङ्गमीश्वरः ॥२५॥
transliteration: tadasya dehī satataṁ sukhoditaṁ; svarūpamatyarthamudārakarmaṇaḥ ,pareṇa kṛcchreṇa śarīramatyaja;dgṛhaṁ maharddhīva sasaṅgamīśvaraḥ.
english: That embodied being (dehī) of exceedingly noble actions (karma), with great difficulty abandoned his body (śarīram) and his perpetually radiant form (svarūpam). He, the lord (īśvaraḥ), departed like a very wealthy man who, despite his attachment, abandons his house.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 67 Verse 25. That embodied being (dehī) of exceedingly noble actions (karma), with great difficulty abandoned his body (śarīram) and his perpetually radiant form (svarūpam). He, the lord (īśvaraḥ), departed like a very wealthy man who, despite his attachment, abandons his house.

sanskrit: शरैर्विभुग्नं व्यसु तद्विवर्मणः पपात कर्णस्य शरीरमुच्छ्रितम् ।स्रवद्व्रणं गैरिकतोयविस्रवं गिरेर्यथा वज्रहतं शिरस्तथा ॥२६॥
transliteration: śarairvibhugnaṁ vyasu tadvivarmaṇaḥ; papāta karṇasya śarīramucchritam ,sravadvraṇaṁ gairikatoyavisravaṁ; gireryathā vajrahataṁ śirastathā.
english: Karna's lofty body, shattered by arrows, lifeless, and without armor, fell. With flowing wounds that streamed red, mineral-like water (blood), it resembled a mountain peak struck by a thunderbolt (vajra).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 67 Verse 26. Karna's lofty body, shattered by arrows, lifeless, and without armor, fell. With flowing wounds that streamed red, mineral-like water (blood), it resembled a mountain peak struck by a thunderbolt (vajra).

sanskrit: देहात्तु कर्णस्य निपातितस्य तेजो दीप्तं खं विगाह्याचिरेण ।तदद्भुतं सर्वमनुष्ययोधाः पश्यन्ति राजन्निहते स्म कर्णे ॥२७॥
transliteration: dehāttu karṇasya nipātitasya; tejo dīptaṁ khaṁ vigāhyācireṇa ,tadadbhutaṁ sarvamanuṣyayodhāḥ; paśyanti rājannihate sma karṇe.
english: O King, from the fallen body of Karna, a blazing effulgence (tejas) swiftly ascended, penetrating the sky. All the human warriors witnessed that astonishing sight when Karna was slain.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 67 Verse 27. O King, from the fallen body of Karna, a blazing effulgence (tejas) swiftly ascended, penetrating the sky. All the human warriors witnessed that astonishing sight when Karna was slain.

sanskrit: तं सोमकाः प्रेक्ष्य हतं शयानं प्रीता नादं सह सैन्यैरकुर्वन् ।तूर्याणि चाजघ्नुरतीव हृष्टा वासांसि चैवादुधुवुर्भुजांश्च ।बलान्विताश्चाप्यपरे ह्यनृत्यन्नन्योन्यमाश्लिष्य नदन्त ऊचुः ॥२८॥
transliteration: taṁ somakāḥ prekṣya hataṁ śayānaṁ; prītā nādaṁ saha sainyairakurvan ,tūryāṇi cājaghnuratīva hṛṣṭā; vāsāṁsi caivādudhuvurbhujāṁśca ,balānvitāścāpyapare hyanṛtya;nnanyonyamāśliṣya nadanta ūcuḥ.
english: Seeing him (Karṇa) killed and lying on the ground, the Somakas, delighted, made a great noise with their armies. Exceedingly joyful, they beat their drums, waved their garments and arms, and others, filled with strength, danced, embracing each other, and shouted out loud.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 67 Verse 28. Seeing him (Karṇa) killed and lying on the ground, the Somakas, delighted, made a great noise with their armies. Exceedingly joyful, they beat their drums, waved their garments and arms, and others, filled with strength, danced, embracing each other, and shouted out loud.

sanskrit: दृष्ट्वा तु कर्णं भुवि निष्टनन्तं हतं रथात्सायकेनावभिन्नम् ।महानिलेनाग्निमिवापविद्धं यज्ञावसाने शयने निशान्ते ॥२९॥
transliteration: dṛṣṭvā tu karṇaṁ bhuvi niṣṭanantaṁ; hataṁ rathātsāyakenāvabhinnam ,mahānilenāgnimivāpaviddhaṁ; yajñāvasāne śayane niśānte.
english: Having seen Karṇa, pierced by an arrow from his chariot, slain and groaning on the ground - like a fire extinguished by a great wind at the end of a ritual (yajña), lying at dawn.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 67 Verse 29. Having seen Karṇa, pierced by an arrow from his chariot, slain and groaning on the ground - like a fire extinguished by a great wind at the end of a ritual (yajña), lying at dawn.

sanskrit: शरैराचितसर्वाङ्गः शोणितौघपरिप्लुतः ।विभाति देहः कर्णस्य स्वरश्मिभिरिवांशुमान् ॥३०॥
transliteration: śarairācitasarvāṅgaḥ śoṇitaughapariplutaḥ ,vibhāti dehaḥ karṇasya svaraśmibhirivāṁśumān.
english: His entire body (sarvāṅga) covered with arrows and drenched in a stream of blood, Karṇa's body (deha) shines like the sun (aṃśumān) with its own rays.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 67 Verse 30. His entire body (sarvāṅga) covered with arrows and drenched in a stream of blood, Karṇa's body (deha) shines like the sun (aṃśumān) with its own rays.

sanskrit: प्रताप्य सेनामामित्रीं दीप्तैः शरगभस्तिभिः ।बलिनार्जुनकालेन नीतोऽस्तं कर्णभास्करः ॥३१॥
transliteration: pratāpya senāmāmitrīṁ dīptaiḥ śaragabhastibhiḥ ,balinārjunakālena nīto'staṁ karṇabhāskaraḥ.
english: Having scorched the enemy army with his blazing arrow-rays, Karṇa, who was like the sun, was brought to setting (astaṃ nītaḥ) by mighty Arjuna, who was like time (kālena).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 67 Verse 31. Having scorched the enemy army with his blazing arrow-rays, Karṇa, who was like the sun, was brought to setting (astaṃ nītaḥ) by mighty Arjuna, who was like time (kālena).

sanskrit: अस्तं गच्छन्यथादित्यः प्रभामादाय गच्छति ।एवं जीवितमादाय कर्णस्येषुर्जगाम ह ॥३२॥
transliteration: astaṁ gacchanyathādityaḥ prabhāmādāya gacchati ,evaṁ jīvitamādāya karṇasyeṣurjagāma ha.
english: Just as the setting sun departs taking its radiance, similarly, the arrow (that struck) Karna went, taking away his life.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 67 Verse 32. Just as the setting sun departs taking its radiance, similarly, the arrow (that struck) Karna went, taking away his life.

sanskrit: अपराह्णे पराह्णस्य सूतपुत्रस्य मारिष ।छिन्नमञ्जलिकेनाजौ सोत्सेधमपतच्छिरः ॥३३॥
transliteration: aparāhṇe parāhṇasya sūtaputrasya māriṣa ,chinnamañjalikenājau sotsedhamapatacchiraḥ.
english: O venerable one, in the late afternoon, the head of the son of Suta (Karna) fell in battle with a surge, severed by the Anjalika arrow.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 67 Verse 33. O venerable one, in the late afternoon, the head of the son of Suta (Karna) fell in battle with a surge, severed by the Anjalika arrow.

sanskrit: उपर्युपरि सैन्यानां तस्य शत्रोस्तदञ्जसा ।शिरः कर्णस्य सोत्सेधमिषुः सोऽपाहरद्द्रुतम् ॥३४॥
transliteration: uparyupari sainyānāṁ tasya śatrostadañjasā ,śiraḥ karṇasya sotsedhamiṣuḥ so'pāharaddrutam.
english: That arrow of his enemy (Arjuna) swiftly carried off Karna's elevated head, high above the armies.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 67 Verse 34. That arrow of his enemy (Arjuna) swiftly carried off Karna's elevated head, high above the armies.

sanskrit: संजय उवाच ।कर्णं तु शूरं पतितं पृथिव्यां शराचितं शोणितदिग्धगात्रम् ।दृष्ट्वा शयानं भुवि मद्रराजश्छिन्नध्वजेनापययौ रथेन ॥३५॥
transliteration: saṁjaya uvāca ,karṇaṁ tu śūraṁ patitaṁ pṛthivyāṁ; śarācitaṁ śoṇitadigdhagātram ,dṛṣṭvā śayānaṁ bhuvi madrarāja;śchinnadhvajenāpayayau rathena.
english: Sanjaya said: But seeing the heroic Karna fallen on the earth, covered with arrows, his body smeared with blood, and lying on the ground, the king of Madra (Shalya) departed in his chariot, its banner having been severed.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 67 Verse 35. Sanjaya said: But seeing the heroic Karna fallen on the earth, covered with arrows, his body smeared with blood, and lying on the ground, the king of Madra (Shalya) departed in his chariot, its banner having been severed.

sanskrit: कर्णे हते कुरवः प्राद्रवन्त भयार्दिता गाढविद्धाश्च संख्ये ।अवेक्षमाणा मुहुरर्जुनस्य ध्वजं महान्तं वपुषा ज्वलन्तम् ॥३६॥
transliteration: karṇe hate kuravaḥ prādravanta; bhayārditā gāḍhaviddhāśca saṁkhye ,avekṣamāṇā muhurarjunasya; dhvajaṁ mahāntaṁ vapuṣā jvalantam.
english: After Karṇa was killed, the Kurus, deeply wounded in battle and tormented by fear, fled. They repeatedly glanced at Arjuna's magnificent banner, which blazed forth in its form.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 67 Verse 36. After Karṇa was killed, the Kurus, deeply wounded in battle and tormented by fear, fled. They repeatedly glanced at Arjuna's magnificent banner, which blazed forth in its form.

sanskrit: सहस्रनेत्रप्रतिमानकर्मणः सहस्रपत्रप्रतिमाननं शुभम् ।सहस्ररश्मिर्दिनसंक्षये यथा तथापतत्तस्य शिरो वसुंधराम् ॥३७॥
transliteration: sahasranetrapratimānakarmaṇaḥ; sahasrapatrapratimānanaṁ śubham ,sahasraraśmirdinasaṁkṣaye yathā; tathāpatattasya śiro vasuṁdharām.
english: The head of him whose deeds were like the thousand-eyed Indra, and whose auspicious face was like a thousand-petaled lotus, fell to the earth, just as the thousand-rayed sun descends at the end of the day.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 67 Verse 37. The head of him whose deeds were like the thousand-eyed Indra, and whose auspicious face was like a thousand-petaled lotus, fell to the earth, just as the thousand-rayed sun descends at the end of the day.

sanskrit: संजय उवाच ।शल्यस्तु कर्णार्जुनयोर्विमर्दे बलानि दृष्ट्वा मृदितानि बाणैः ।दुर्योधनं यान्तमवेक्षमाणो संदर्शयद्भारत युद्धभूमिम् ॥१॥
transliteration: saṁjaya uvāca ,śalyastu karṇārjunayorvimarde; balāni dṛṣṭvā mṛditāni bāṇaiḥ ,duryodhanaṁ yāntamavekṣamāṇo; saṁdarśayadbhārata yuddhabhūmim.
english: Sañjaya said: But Śalya, seeing the armies crushed by arrows in the fierce clash between Karṇa and Arjuna, and noticing Duryodhana departing, pointed out the battlefield, O Bhārata.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 68 Verse 1. Sañjaya said: But Śalya, seeing the armies crushed by arrows in the fierce clash between Karṇa and Arjuna, and noticing Duryodhana departing, pointed out the battlefield, O Bhārata.

sanskrit: निपातितस्यन्दनवाजिनागं दृष्ट्वा बलं तद्धतसूतपुत्रम् ।दुर्योधनोऽश्रुप्रतिपूर्णनेत्रो मुहुर्मुहुर्न्यश्वसदार्तरूपः ॥२॥
transliteration: nipātitasyandanavājināgaṁ; dṛṣṭvā balaṁ taddhatasūtaputram ,duryodhano'śrupratipūrṇanetro; muhurmuhurnyaśvasadārtarūpaḥ.
english: Upon seeing that army - whose chariots, horses, and elephants had been struck down, and whose leader, Karṇa, was slain - Duryodhana, with eyes full of tears, sighed repeatedly, appearing deeply distressed.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 68 Verse 2. Upon seeing that army - whose chariots, horses, and elephants had been struck down, and whose leader, Karṇa, was slain - Duryodhana, with eyes full of tears, sighed repeatedly, appearing deeply distressed.

sanskrit: कर्णं तु शूरं पतितं पृथिव्यां शराचितं शोणितदिग्धगात्रम् ।यदृच्छया सूर्यमिवावनिस्थं दिदृक्षवः संपरिवार्य तस्थुः ॥३॥
transliteration: karṇaṁ tu śūraṁ patitaṁ pṛthivyāṁ; śarācitaṁ śoṇitadigdhagātram ,yadṛcchayā sūryamivāvanisthaṁ; didṛkṣavaḥ saṁparivārya tasthuḥ.
english: But, the heroic Karṇa, fallen on the earth, whose body was smeared with blood and covered with arrows, lay there like the sun fallen upon the ground by chance. Desirous of seeing him, they stood surrounding him.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 68 Verse 3. But, the heroic Karṇa, fallen on the earth, whose body was smeared with blood and covered with arrows, lay there like the sun fallen upon the ground by chance. Desirous of seeing him, they stood surrounding him.

sanskrit: प्रहृष्टवित्रस्तविषण्णविस्मृतास्तथापरे शोकगता इवाभवन् ।परे त्वदीयाश्च परस्परेण यथा यथैषां प्रकृतिस्तथाभवन् ॥४॥
transliteration: prahṛṣṭavitrastaviṣaṇṇavismṛtā;stathāpare śokagatā ivābhavan ,pare tvadīyāśca paraspareṇa; yathā yathaiṣāṁ prakṛtistathābhavan.
english: Some were greatly delighted, others terrified, others dejected, and still others astonished. Likewise, some became as if overcome with grief. Your people and others [the Pāṇḍavas' allies], each reacted among themselves according to their intrinsic nature (prakṛti).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 68 Verse 4. Some were greatly delighted, others terrified, others dejected, and still others astonished. Likewise, some became as if overcome with grief. Your people and others [the Pāṇḍavas' allies], each reacted among themselves according to their intrinsic nature (prakṛti).

sanskrit: प्रविद्धवर्माभरणाम्बरायुधं धनंजयेनाभिहतं हतौजसम् ।निशम्य कर्णं कुरवः प्रदुद्रुवुर्हतर्षभा गाव इवाकुलाकुलाः ॥५॥
transliteration: praviddhavarmābharaṇāmbarāyudhaṁ; dhanaṁjayenābhihataṁ hataujasam ,niśamya karṇaṁ kuravaḥ pradudruvu;rhatarṣabhā gāva ivākulākulāḥ.
english: Having witnessed Karṇa, whose armor, ornaments, garments, and weapons were scattered, who was struck down by Dhanañjaya, and whose vitality was destroyed, the Kurus fled, utterly bewildered, like cows whose chief bull has been slain.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 68 Verse 5. Having witnessed Karṇa, whose armor, ornaments, garments, and weapons were scattered, who was struck down by Dhanañjaya, and whose vitality was destroyed, the Kurus fled, utterly bewildered, like cows whose chief bull has been slain.

sanskrit: कृत्वा विमर्दं भृशमर्जुनेन कर्णं हतं केसरिणेव नागम् ।दृष्ट्वा शयानं भुवि मद्रराजो भीतोऽपसर्पत्सरथः सुशीघ्रम् ॥६॥
transliteration: kṛtvā vimardaṁ bhṛśamarjunena; karṇaṁ hataṁ kesariṇeva nāgam ,dṛṣṭvā śayānaṁ bhuvi madrarājo; bhīto'pasarpatsarathaḥ suśīghram.
english: Having engaged in a fierce battle with Arjuna, and seeing Karṇa slain and lying on the ground like an elephant by a lion, the king of Madras, terrified, fled very swiftly with his chariot.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 68 Verse 6. Having engaged in a fierce battle with Arjuna, and seeing Karṇa slain and lying on the ground like an elephant by a lion, the king of Madras, terrified, fled very swiftly with his chariot.

sanskrit: मद्राधिपश्चापि विमूढचेतास्तूर्णं रथेनापहृतध्वजेन ।दुर्योधनस्यान्तिकमेत्य शीघ्रं संभाष्य दुःखार्तमुवाच वाक्यम् ॥७॥
transliteration: madrādhipaścāpi vimūḍhacetā;stūrṇaṁ rathenāpahṛtadhvajena ,duryodhanasyāntikametya śīghraṁ; saṁbhāṣya duḥkhārtamuvāca vākyam.
english: The King of Madras (Śalya), whose mind was bewildered, quickly approached Duryodhana with his chariot from which the banner had been torn down. Having arrived near him and conversed swiftly, he spoke these words, which were afflicted with sorrow.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 68 Verse 7. The King of Madras (Śalya), whose mind was bewildered, quickly approached Duryodhana with his chariot from which the banner had been torn down. Having arrived near him and conversed swiftly, he spoke these words, which were afflicted with sorrow.

sanskrit: विशीर्णनागाश्वरथप्रवीरं बलं त्वदियं यमराष्ट्रकल्पम् ।अन्योन्यमासाद्य हतं महद्भिर्नराश्वनागैर्गिरिकूटकल्पैः ॥८॥
transliteration: viśīrṇanāgāśvarathapravīraṁ; balaṁ tvadiyaṁ yamarāṣṭrakalpam ,anyonyamāsādya hataṁ mahadbhi;rnarāśvanāgairgirikūṭakalpaiḥ.
english: Your army, whose elephants, horses, chariots, and chief warriors are shattered, resembles the realm of Yama (the lord of death). Its mighty men, horses, and elephants, comparable to mountain peaks, have been destroyed by encountering one another.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 68 Verse 8. Your army, whose elephants, horses, chariots, and chief warriors are shattered, resembles the realm of Yama (the lord of death). Its mighty men, horses, and elephants, comparable to mountain peaks, have been destroyed by encountering one another.

sanskrit: नैतादृशं भारत युद्धमासीद्यथाद्य कर्णार्जुनयोर्बभूव ।ग्रस्तौ हि कर्णेन समेत्य कृष्णावन्ये च सर्वे तव शत्रवो ये ॥९॥
transliteration: naitādṛśaṁ bhārata yuddhamāsī;dyathādya karṇārjunayorbabhūva ,grastau hi karṇena sametya kṛṣṇā;vanye ca sarve tava śatravo ye.
english: O Bhārata (Duryodhana), such a battle as that which took place today between Karṇa and Arjuna has never occurred. Indeed, the two Kṛṣṇas (Arjuna and Vāsudeva), along with all your other enemies, have been overwhelmed by Karṇa after confronting him.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 68 Verse 9. O Bhārata (Duryodhana), such a battle as that which took place today between Karṇa and Arjuna has never occurred. Indeed, the two Kṛṣṇas (Arjuna and Vāsudeva), along with all your other enemies, have been overwhelmed by Karṇa after confronting him.

sanskrit: दैवं तु यत्तत्स्ववशं प्रवृत्तं तत्पाण्डवान्पाति हिनस्ति चास्मान् ।तवार्थसिद्ध्यर्थकरा हि सर्वे प्रसह्य वीरा निहता द्विषद्भिः ॥१०॥
transliteration: daivaṁ tu yattatsvavaśaṁ pravṛttaṁ; tatpāṇḍavānpāti hinasti cāsmān ,tavārthasiddhyarthakarā hi sarve; prasahya vīrā nihatā dviṣadbhiḥ.
english: Indeed, destiny (daivam), which is self-willed and has come into play, protects the Pāṇḍavas and destroys us. Truly, all the heroes who were meant to achieve your objectives have been forcibly slain by the enemies.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 68 Verse 10. Indeed, destiny (daivam), which is self-willed and has come into play, protects the Pāṇḍavas and destroys us. Truly, all the heroes who were meant to achieve your objectives have been forcibly slain by the enemies.

sanskrit: कुबेरवैवस्वतवासवानां तुल्यप्रभावाम्बुपतेश्च वीराः ।वीर्येण शौर्येण बलेन चैव तैस्तैश्च युक्ता विपुलैर्गुणौघैः ॥११॥
transliteration: kuberavaivasvatavāsavānāṁ; tulyaprabhāvāmbupateśca vīrāḥ ,vīryeṇa śauryeṇa balena caiva; taistaiśca yuktā vipulairguṇaughaiḥ.
english: These heroes, equal in splendor to Kubera, Yama (Vaivasvata), Indra (Vasava), and Varuna (Ambupati), were endowed with valor, heroism, strength, and various other vast multitudes of excellent qualities.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 68 Verse 11. These heroes, equal in splendor to Kubera, Yama (Vaivasvata), Indra (Vasava), and Varuna (Ambupati), were endowed with valor, heroism, strength, and various other vast multitudes of excellent qualities.

sanskrit: अवध्यकल्पा निहता नरेन्द्रास्तवार्थकामा युधि पाण्डवेयैः ।तन्मा शुचो भारत दिष्टमेतत्पर्यायसिद्धिर्न सदास्ति सिद्धिः ॥१२॥
transliteration: avadhyakalpā nihatā narendrā;stavārthakāmā yudhi pāṇḍaveyaiḥ ,tanmā śuco bhārata diṣṭameta;tparyāyasiddhirna sadāsti siddhiḥ.
english: Your kings, who were almost invulnerable and fought for your cause, have been slain in battle by the Pandavas. Therefore, O descendant of Bharata, do not grieve; this is destiny. Success is not always constant; it alternates.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 68 Verse 12. Your kings, who were almost invulnerable and fought for your cause, have been slain in battle by the Pandavas. Therefore, O descendant of Bharata, do not grieve; this is destiny. Success is not always constant; it alternates.

sanskrit: एतद्वचो मद्रपतेर्निशम्य स्वं चापनीतं मनसा निरीक्ष्य ।दुर्योधनो दीनमना विसंज्ञः पुनः पुनर्न्यश्वसदार्तरूपः ॥१३॥
transliteration: etadvaco madrapaterniśamya; svaṁ cāpanītaṁ manasā nirīkṣya ,duryodhano dīnamanā visaṁjñaḥ; punaḥ punarnyaśvasadārtarūpaḥ.
english: After hearing these words from the king of Madra (Salya), and mentally reflecting on his own misdeeds, Duryodhana became dejected and senseless, sighing repeatedly like one in distress.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 68 Verse 13. After hearing these words from the king of Madra (Salya), and mentally reflecting on his own misdeeds, Duryodhana became dejected and senseless, sighing repeatedly like one in distress.

sanskrit: तं ध्यानमूकं कृपणं भृशार्तमार्तायनिर्दीनमुवाच वाक्यम् ।पश्येदमुग्रं नरवाजिनागैरायोधनं वीरहतैः प्रपन्नम् ॥१४॥
transliteration: taṁ dhyānamūkaṁ kṛpaṇaṁ bhṛśārta;mārtāyanirdīnamuvāca vākyam ,paśyedamugraṁ naravājināgai;rāyodhanaṁ vīrahataiḥ prapannam.
english: Sanjaya (Artayani) then addressed Duryodhana, who was silent in deep thought (dhyāna), miserable, greatly distressed, and dejected, with these words: "Behold this terrible battlefield, now filled with men, horses, and elephants slain by valiant heroes."
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 68 Verse 14. Sanjaya (Artayani) then addressed Duryodhana, who was silent in deep thought (dhyāna), miserable, greatly distressed, and dejected, with these words: "Behold this terrible battlefield, now filled with men, horses, and elephants slain by valiant heroes."

sanskrit: महीधराभैः पतितैर्महागजैः सकृत्प्रविद्धैः शरविद्धमर्मभिः ।तैर्विह्वलद्भिश्च गतासुभिश्च प्रध्वस्तयन्त्रायुधवर्मयोधैः ॥१५॥
transliteration: mahīdharābhaiḥ patitairmahāgajaiḥ; sakṛtpraviddhaiḥ śaraviddhamarmabhiḥ ,tairvihvaladbhiśca gatāsubhiśca; pradhvastayantrāyudhavarmayodhaiḥ.
english: The great elephants, resembling mountains, had fallen, repeatedly pierced and with their vital parts struck by arrows. Among them were those that were still trembling and those that were already lifeless, their war machines, weapons, armor, and warriors utterly destroyed.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 68 Verse 15. The great elephants, resembling mountains, had fallen, repeatedly pierced and with their vital parts struck by arrows. Among them were those that were still trembling and those that were already lifeless, their war machines, weapons, armor, and warriors utterly destroyed.

sanskrit: वज्रापविद्धैरिव चाचलेन्द्रैर्विभिन्नपाषाणमृगद्रुमौषधैः ।प्रविद्धघण्टाङ्कुशतोमरध्वजैः सहेममालै रुधिरौघसंप्लुतैः ॥१६॥
transliteration: vajrāpaviddhairiva cācalendrai;rvibhinnapāṣāṇamṛgadrumauṣadhaiḥ ,praviddhaghaṇṭāṅkuśatomaradhvajaiḥ; sahemamālai rudhiraughasaṁplutaiḥ.
english: (They lay there) like great mountains struck down by a thunderbolt, their rocks, deer, trees, and herbs shattered. Their bells, goads, javelins, and banners were scattered, and they were adorned with golden garlands, completely drenched in torrents of blood.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 68 Verse 16. (They lay there) like great mountains struck down by a thunderbolt, their rocks, deer, trees, and herbs shattered. Their bells, goads, javelins, and banners were scattered, and they were adorned with golden garlands, completely drenched in torrents of blood.

sanskrit: शरावभिन्नैः पतितैश्च वाजिभिः श्वसद्भिरन्यैः क्षतजं वमद्भिः ।दीनैः स्तनद्भिः परिवृत्तनेत्रैर्महीं दशद्भिः कृपणं नदद्भिः ॥१७॥
transliteration: śarāvabhinnaiḥ patitaiśca vājibhiḥ; śvasadbhiranyaiḥ kṣatajaṁ vamadbhiḥ ,dīnaiḥ stanadbhiḥ parivṛttanetrai;rmahīṁ daśadbhiḥ kṛpaṇaṁ nadadbhiḥ.
english: And there were fallen horses, pierced by arrows; others were gasping, vomiting blood, and appearing pitiable. They were groaning, with their eyes rolling, biting the earth, and crying out miserably.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 68 Verse 17. And there were fallen horses, pierced by arrows; others were gasping, vomiting blood, and appearing pitiable. They were groaning, with their eyes rolling, biting the earth, and crying out miserably.

sanskrit: तथापविद्धैर्गजवाजियोधैर्मन्दासुभिश्चैव गतासुभिश्च ।नराश्वनागैश्च रथैश्च मर्दितैर्मही महावैतरणीव दुर्दृशा ॥१८॥
transliteration: tathāpaviddhairgajavājiyodhai;rmandāsubhiścaiva gatāsubhiśca ,narāśvanāgaiśca rathaiśca marditai;rmahī mahāvaitaraṇīva durdṛśā.
english: Similarly, with elephant-riders and cavalrymen thrown down - some barely clinging to life, and others already lifeless - and with men, horses, elephants, and chariots crushed, the earth became a terrifying sight, resembling a great (river) Vaitaraṇī.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 68 Verse 18. Similarly, with elephant-riders and cavalrymen thrown down - some barely clinging to life, and others already lifeless - and with men, horses, elephants, and chariots crushed, the earth became a terrifying sight, resembling a great (river) Vaitaraṇī.

sanskrit: गजैर्निकृत्तापरहस्तगात्रैरुद्वेपमानैः पतितैः पृथिव्याम् ।यशस्विभिर्नागरथाश्वयोधिभिः पदातिभिश्चाभिमुखैर्हतैः परैः ।विशीर्णवर्माभरणाम्बरायुधैर्वृता निशान्तैरिव पावकैर्मही ॥१९॥
transliteration: gajairnikṛttāparahastagātrai;rudvepamānaiḥ patitaiḥ pṛthivyām ,yaśasvibhirnāgarathāśvayodhibhiḥ; padātibhiścābhimukhairhataiḥ paraiḥ ,viśīrṇavarmābharaṇāmbarāyudhai;rvṛtā niśāntairiva pāvakairmahī.
english: The earth was covered with elephants whose other trunks and bodies had been severed, who were trembling and had fallen to the ground. It was also covered with glorious enemies, who fought from elephants, chariots, and horses, and with foot soldiers, all of whom had been slain while facing the front, their armor, ornaments, garments, and weapons shattered. The earth appeared as if covered by extinguished fires.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 68 Verse 19. The earth was covered with elephants whose other trunks and bodies had been severed, who were trembling and had fallen to the ground. It was also covered with glorious enemies, who fought from elephants, chariots, and horses, and with foot soldiers, all of whom had been slain while facing the front, their armor, ornaments, garments, and weapons shattered. The earth appeared as if covered by extinguished fires.

sanskrit: शरप्रहाराभिहतैर्महाबलैरवेक्ष्यमाणैः पतितैः सहस्रशः ।प्रनष्टसंज्ञैः पुनरुच्छ्वसद्भिर्मही बभूवानुगतैरिवाग्निभिः ।दिवश्च्युतैर्भूरतिदीप्तिमद्भिर्नक्तं ग्रहैर्द्यौरमलेव दीप्तैः ॥२०॥
transliteration: śaraprahārābhihatairmahābalai;ravekṣyamāṇaiḥ patitaiḥ sahasraśaḥ ,pranaṣṭasaṁjñaiḥ punarucchvasadbhi;rmahī babhūvānugatairivāgnibhiḥ ,divaścyutairbhūratidīptimadbhi;rnaktaṁ grahairdyauramaleva dīptaiḥ.
english: The earth became covered, as if by fires, with thousands of powerful warriors who had been struck by arrow blows, fallen, and were seen to have lost consciousness only to revive again. This was like the clear night sky shining with exceedingly radiant planets that had fallen from the heavens.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 68 Verse 20. The earth became covered, as if by fires, with thousands of powerful warriors who had been struck by arrow blows, fallen, and were seen to have lost consciousness only to revive again. This was like the clear night sky shining with exceedingly radiant planets that had fallen from the heavens.

sanskrit: शरास्तु कर्णार्जुनबाहुमुक्ता विदार्य नागाश्वमनुष्यदेहान् ।प्राणान्निरस्याशु महीमतीयुर्महोरगा वासमिवाभितोऽस्त्रैः ॥२१॥
transliteration: śarāstu karṇārjunabāhumuktā; vidārya nāgāśvamanuṣyadehān ,prāṇānnirasyāśu mahīmatīyu;rmahoragā vāsamivābhito'straiḥ.
english: But the arrows, released from the arms of Karṇa and Arjuna, tearing apart the bodies of elephants, horses, and men and quickly expelling their life (prāṇa), passed into the earth, just as great serpents swiftly enter their abodes to escape weapons all around.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 68 Verse 21. But the arrows, released from the arms of Karṇa and Arjuna, tearing apart the bodies of elephants, horses, and men and quickly expelling their life (prāṇa), passed into the earth, just as great serpents swiftly enter their abodes to escape weapons all around.

sanskrit: हतैर्मनुष्याश्वगजैश्च संख्ये शरावभिन्नैश्च रथैर्बभूव ।धनंजयस्याधिरथेश्च मार्गे गजैरगम्या वसुधातिदुर्गा ॥२२॥
transliteration: hatairmanuṣyāśvagajaiśca saṁkhye; śarāvabhinnaiśca rathairbabhūva ,dhanaṁjayasyādhiratheśca mārge; gajairagamyā vasudhātidurgā.
english: The earth, on the path traversed by Dhanañjaya and Adhirathi, became extremely difficult to traverse and impassable even for elephants, due to the men, horses, and elephants slain in battle and the chariots shattered by arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 68 Verse 22. The earth, on the path traversed by Dhanañjaya and Adhirathi, became extremely difficult to traverse and impassable even for elephants, due to the men, horses, and elephants slain in battle and the chariots shattered by arrows.

sanskrit: रथैर्वरेषून्मथितैश्च योधैः संस्यूतसूताश्ववरायुधध्वजैः ।विशीर्णशस्त्रैर्विनिकृत्तबन्धुरैर्निकृत्तचक्राक्षयुगत्रिवेणुभिः ॥२३॥
transliteration: rathairvareṣūnmathitaiśca yodhaiḥ; saṁsyūtasūtāśvavarāyudhadhvajaiḥ ,viśīrṇaśastrairvinikṛttabandhurai;rnikṛttacakrākṣayugatriveṇubhiḥ.
english: The earth was covered with excellent chariots and warriors, crushed and churned; with charioteers, horses, splendid weapons, and banners interwoven; with shattered weapons, with their frames cut off, and with their wheels, axles, yokes, and triple poles severed.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 68 Verse 23. The earth was covered with excellent chariots and warriors, crushed and churned; with charioteers, horses, splendid weapons, and banners interwoven; with shattered weapons, with their frames cut off, and with their wheels, axles, yokes, and triple poles severed.

sanskrit: विमुक्तयन्त्रैर्निहतैरयस्मयैर्हतानुषङ्गैर्विनिषङ्गबन्धुरैः ।प्रभग्ननीडैर्मणिहेममण्डितैः स्तृता मही द्यौरिव शारदैर्घनैः ॥२४॥
transliteration: vimuktayantrairnihatairayasmayai;rhatānuṣaṅgairviniṣaṅgabandhuraiḥ ,prabhagnanīḍairmaṇihemamaṇḍitaiḥ; stṛtā mahī dyauriva śāradairghanaiḥ.
english: The earth was strewn with those (chariots and warriors) whose mechanisms were shattered, with those struck down and made of iron, with broken attachments, with shattered quivers and frames, with broken seats, and those adorned with jewels and gold, just as the sky is strewn with autumnal clouds.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 68 Verse 24. The earth was strewn with those (chariots and warriors) whose mechanisms were shattered, with those struck down and made of iron, with broken attachments, with shattered quivers and frames, with broken seats, and those adorned with jewels and gold, just as the sky is strewn with autumnal clouds.

sanskrit: विकृष्यमणैर्जवनैरलंकृतैर्हतेश्वरैराजिरथैः सुकल्पितैः ।मनुष्यमातङ्गरथाश्वराशिभिर्द्रुतं व्रजन्तो बहुधा विचूर्णिताः ॥२५॥
transliteration: vikṛṣyamaṇairjavanairalaṁkṛtai;rhateśvarairājirathaiḥ sukalpitaiḥ ,manuṣyamātaṅgarathāśvarāśibhi;rdrutaṁ vrajanto bahudhā vicūrṇitāḥ.
english: Those moving swiftly were crushed in many ways: by swift horses being dragged, by decorated (chariots), by battle chariots whose masters were slain and which were well-prepared, and by heaps of men, elephants, chariots, and horses.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 68 Verse 25. Those moving swiftly were crushed in many ways: by swift horses being dragged, by decorated (chariots), by battle chariots whose masters were slain and which were well-prepared, and by heaps of men, elephants, chariots, and horses.

sanskrit: सहेमपट्टाः परिघाः परश्वधाः कडङ्गरायोमुसलानि पट्टिशाः ।पेतुश्च खड्गा विमला विकोशा गदाश्च जाम्बूनदपट्टबद्धाः ॥२६॥
transliteration: sahemapaṭṭāḥ parighāḥ paraśvadhāḥ; kaḍaṅgarāyomusalāni paṭṭiśāḥ ,petuśca khaḍgā vimalā vikośā; gadāśca jāmbūnadapaṭṭabaddhāḥ.
english: Iron bars (maces) with golden bands, axes, darts, iron pestles, and spears fell; and spotless, unsheathed swords, and maces fastened with excellent golden bands (also fell).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 68 Verse 26. Iron bars (maces) with golden bands, axes, darts, iron pestles, and spears fell; and spotless, unsheathed swords, and maces fastened with excellent golden bands (also fell).

sanskrit: चापानि रुक्माङ्गदभूषणानि शराश्च कार्तस्वरचित्रपुङ्खाः ।ऋष्ट्यश्च पीता विमला विकोशाः प्रासाः सखड्गाः कनकावभासाः ॥२७॥
transliteration: cāpāni rukmāṅgadabhūṣaṇāni; śarāśca kārtasvaracitrapuṅkhāḥ ,ṛṣṭyaśca pītā vimalā vikośāḥ; prāsāḥ sakhaḍgāḥ kanakāvabhāsāḥ.
english: Bows adorned with golden armlets, and arrows with variegated golden fletching; also, shining, unsheathed yellow spears, and javelins accompanied by swords, all having the luster of gold.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 68 Verse 27. Bows adorned with golden armlets, and arrows with variegated golden fletching; also, shining, unsheathed yellow spears, and javelins accompanied by swords, all having the luster of gold.

sanskrit: छत्राणि वालव्यजनानि शङ्खाः स्रजश्च पुष्पोत्तमहेमचित्राः ।कुथाः पताकाम्बरवेष्टिताश्च किरीटमाला मुकुटाश्च शुभ्राः ॥२८॥
transliteration: chatrāṇi vālavyajanāni śaṅkhāḥ; srajaśca puṣpottamahemacitrāḥ ,kuthāḥ patākāmbaraveṣṭitāśca; kirīṭamālā mukuṭāśca śubhrāḥ.
english: Umbrellas, yak-tail whisks, and conches; and garlands variegated with splendid flowers and gold; elephant housings adorned with banners and cloths; and bright rows of crowns and diadems.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 68 Verse 28. Umbrellas, yak-tail whisks, and conches; and garlands variegated with splendid flowers and gold; elephant housings adorned with banners and cloths; and bright rows of crowns and diadems.

sanskrit: प्रकीर्णका विप्रकीर्णाः कुथाश्च प्रधानमुक्तातरलाश्च हाराः ।आपीडकेयूरवराङ्गदानि ग्रैवेयनिष्काः ससुवर्णसूत्राः ॥२९॥
transliteration: prakīrṇakā viprakīrṇāḥ kuthāśca; pradhānamuktātaralāśca hārāḥ ,āpīḍakeyūravarāṅgadāni; graiveyaniṣkāḥ sasuvarṇasūtrāḥ.
english: Scattered and widely dispersed (various) elephant housings, and necklaces with prominent, shimmering pearls; also, head ornaments, armlets, excellent bracelets, and necklaces (graiveya) with golden threads.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 68 Verse 29. Scattered and widely dispersed (various) elephant housings, and necklaces with prominent, shimmering pearls; also, head ornaments, armlets, excellent bracelets, and necklaces (graiveya) with golden threads.

sanskrit: मण्युत्तमा वज्रसुवर्णमुक्ता रत्नानि चोच्चावचमङ्गलानि ।गात्राणि चात्यन्तसुखोचितानि शिरांसि चेन्दुप्रतिमाननानि ॥३०॥
transliteration: maṇyuttamā vajrasuvarṇamuktā; ratnāni coccāvacamaṅgalāni ,gātrāṇi cātyantasukhocitāni; śirāṁsi cendupratimānanāni.
english: Excellent jewels, diamonds, gold, pearls, and various other auspicious gems; bodies accustomed to the highest comfort; and heads with faces resembling the moon.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 68 Verse 30. Excellent jewels, diamonds, gold, pearls, and various other auspicious gems; bodies accustomed to the highest comfort; and heads with faces resembling the moon.

sanskrit: देहांश्च भोगांश्च परिच्छदांश्च त्यक्त्वा मनोज्ञानि सुखानि चापि ।स्वधर्मनिष्ठां महतीमवाप्य व्याप्तांश्च लोकान्यशसा समीयुः ॥३१॥
transliteration: dehāṁśca bhogāṁśca paricchadāṁśca; tyaktvā manojñāni sukhāni cāpi ,svadharmaniṣṭhāṁ mahatīmavāpya; vyāptāṁśca lokānyaśasā samīyuḥ.
english: Having abandoned bodies, enjoyments, and possessions, as well as pleasant pleasures, and having achieved great dedication to their own natural law (svadharma), they attained the worlds, pervaded by their fame.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 68 Verse 31. Having abandoned bodies, enjoyments, and possessions, as well as pleasant pleasures, and having achieved great dedication to their own natural law (svadharma), they attained the worlds, pervaded by their fame.

sanskrit: इत्येवमुक्त्वा विरराम शल्यो दुर्योधनः शोकपरीतचेताः ।हा कर्ण हा कर्ण इति ब्रुवाण आर्तो विसंज्ञो भृशमश्रुनेत्रः ॥३२॥
transliteration: ityevamuktvā virarāma śalyo; duryodhanaḥ śokaparītacetāḥ ,hā karṇa hā karṇa iti bruvāṇa; ārto visaṁjño bhṛśamaśrunetraḥ.
english: Having spoken thus, Shalya ceased. Duryodhana, whose mind was overwhelmed by sorrow, cried out, "Alas, Karna! Alas, Karna!" He was greatly distressed, senseless, and had tearful eyes.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 68 Verse 32. Having spoken thus, Shalya ceased. Duryodhana, whose mind was overwhelmed by sorrow, cried out, "Alas, Karna! Alas, Karna!" He was greatly distressed, senseless, and had tearful eyes.

sanskrit: तं द्रोणपुत्रप्रमुखा नरेन्द्राः सर्वे समाश्वास्य सह प्रयान्ति ।निरीक्षमाणा मुहुरर्जुनस्य ध्वजं महान्तं यशसा ज्वलन्तम् ॥३३॥
transliteration: taṁ droṇaputrapramukhā narendrāḥ; sarve samāśvāsya saha prayānti ,nirīkṣamāṇā muhurarjunasya; dhvajaṁ mahāntaṁ yaśasā jvalantam.
english: All the kings, led by Droṇa's son, having consoled him (Duryodhana), then proceed together. They repeatedly gaze at Arjuna's great banner, which is resplendent with glory.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 68 Verse 33. All the kings, led by Droṇa's son, having consoled him (Duryodhana), then proceed together. They repeatedly gaze at Arjuna's great banner, which is resplendent with glory.

sanskrit: नराश्वमातङ्गशरीरजेन रक्तेन सिक्ता रुधिरेण भूमिः ।रक्ताम्बरस्रक्तपनीययोगान्नारी प्रकाशा इव सर्वगम्या ॥३४॥
transliteration: narāśvamātaṅgaśarīrajena; raktena siktā rudhireṇa bhūmiḥ ,raktāmbarasraktapanīyayogā;nnārī prakāśā iva sarvagamyā.
english: Drenched with blood and gore originating from the bodies of men, horses, and elephants, the earth appeared like a woman accessible to all, resembling various collections of red garments and spilled drinks.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 68 Verse 34. Drenched with blood and gore originating from the bodies of men, horses, and elephants, the earth appeared like a woman accessible to all, resembling various collections of red garments and spilled drinks.

sanskrit: प्रच्छन्नरूपा रुधिरेण राजन्रौद्रे मुहूर्तेऽतिविराजमानाः ।नैवावतस्थुः कुरवः समीक्ष्य प्रव्राजिता देवलोकाश्च सर्वे ॥३५॥
transliteration: pracchannarūpā rudhireṇa rāja;nraudre muhūrte'tivirājamānāḥ ,naivāvatasthuḥ kuravaḥ samīkṣya; pravrājitā devalokāśca sarve.
english: O King, the Kuru warriors, whose forms were hidden by blood yet shone exceedingly brightly in that terrible moment, did not remain steady, having observed all the celestial beings who had departed.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 68 Verse 35. O King, the Kuru warriors, whose forms were hidden by blood yet shone exceedingly brightly in that terrible moment, did not remain steady, having observed all the celestial beings who had departed.

sanskrit: वधेन कर्णस्य सुदुःखितास्ते हा कर्ण हा कर्ण इति ब्रुवाणाः ।द्रुतं प्रयाताः शिबिराणि राजन्दिवाकरं रक्तमवेक्षमाणाः ॥३६॥
transliteration: vadhena karṇasya suduḥkhitāste; hā karṇa hā karṇa iti bruvāṇāḥ ,drutaṁ prayātāḥ śibirāṇi rāja;ndivākaraṁ raktamavekṣamāṇāḥ.
english: O King, greatly distressed by the killing of Karna, and crying 'Alas, Karna! Alas, Karna!', they quickly departed to their camps, looking at the blood-red sun.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 68 Verse 36. O King, greatly distressed by the killing of Karna, and crying 'Alas, Karna! Alas, Karna!', they quickly departed to their camps, looking at the blood-red sun.

sanskrit: गाण्डीवमुक्तैस्तु सुवर्णपुङ्खैः शितैः शरैः शोणितदिग्धवाजैः ।शरैश्चिताङ्गो भुवि भाति कर्णो हतोऽपि सन्सूर्य इवांशुमाली ॥३७॥
transliteration: gāṇḍīvamuktaistu suvarṇapuṅkhaiḥ; śitaiḥ śaraiḥ śoṇitadigdhavājaiḥ ,śaraiścitāṅgo bhuvi bhāti karṇo; hato'pi sansūrya ivāṁśumālī.
english: On the ground, Karna, his body pierced by sharp arrows released from the Gāṇḍīva bow, arrows with golden shafts and feathers smeared with blood, shone brilliantly even in death, like the sun (aṃśumālī) adorned with its rays.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 68 Verse 37. On the ground, Karna, his body pierced by sharp arrows released from the Gāṇḍīva bow, arrows with golden shafts and feathers smeared with blood, shone brilliantly even in death, like the sun (aṃśumālī) adorned with its rays.

sanskrit: कर्णस्य देहं रुधिरावसिक्तं भक्तानुकम्पी भगवान्विवस्वान् ।स्पृष्ट्वा करैर्लोहितरक्तरूपः सिष्णासुरभ्येति परं समुद्रम् ॥३८॥
transliteration: karṇasya dehaṁ rudhirāvasiktaṁ; bhaktānukampī bhagavānvivasvān ,spṛṣṭvā karairlohitaraktarūpaḥ; siṣṇāsurabhyeti paraṁ samudram.
english: The divine Sun (Vivasvān), who is compassionate to his devotees, having touched Karna's body, which was drenched in blood, with his rays, and having taken on a blood-red appearance himself, then approached the great ocean as if desiring to bathe.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 68 Verse 38. The divine Sun (Vivasvān), who is compassionate to his devotees, having touched Karna's body, which was drenched in blood, with his rays, and having taken on a blood-red appearance himself, then approached the great ocean as if desiring to bathe.

sanskrit: इतीव संचिन्त्य सुरर्षिसंघाः संप्रस्थिता यान्ति यथानिकेतम् ।संचिन्तयित्वा च जना विसस्रुर्यथासुखं खं च महीतलं च ॥३९॥
transliteration: itīva saṁcintya surarṣisaṁghāḥ; saṁprasthitā yānti yathāniketam ,saṁcintayitvā ca janā visasru;ryathāsukhaṁ khaṁ ca mahītalaṁ ca.
english: Having thus reflected, the assemblies of divine sages departed and went to their respective dwellings. And the people, having likewise pondered, dispersed at their ease, some moving into the sky and others onto the earth.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 68 Verse 39. Having thus reflected, the assemblies of divine sages departed and went to their respective dwellings. And the people, having likewise pondered, dispersed at their ease, some moving into the sky and others onto the earth.

sanskrit: तदद्भुतं प्राणभृतां भयंकरं निशम्य युद्धं कुरुवीरमुख्ययोः ।धनंजयस्याधिरथेश्च विस्मिताः प्रशंसमानाः प्रययुस्तदा जनाः ॥४०॥
transliteration: tadadbhutaṁ prāṇabhṛtāṁ bhayaṁkaraṁ; niśamya yuddhaṁ kuruvīramukhyayoḥ ,dhanaṁjayasyādhiratheśca vismitāḥ; praśaṁsamānāḥ prayayustadā janāḥ.
english: Having witnessed that astonishing and terrifying battle between the two foremost Kuru heroes, Arjuna (Dhanañjaya) and Adhirathi's son (Karṇa), the people were astonished. Then, praising them, they departed.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 68 Verse 40. Having witnessed that astonishing and terrifying battle between the two foremost Kuru heroes, Arjuna (Dhanañjaya) and Adhirathi's son (Karṇa), the people were astonished. Then, praising them, they departed.

sanskrit: शरैः संकृत्तवर्माणं वीरं विशसने हतम् ।गतासुमपि राधेयं नैव लक्ष्मीर्व्यमुञ्चत ॥४१॥
transliteration: śaraiḥ saṁkṛttavarmāṇaṁ vīraṁ viśasane hatam ,gatāsumapi rādheyaṁ naiva lakṣmīrvyamuñcata.
english: Even though his armor was completely shattered by arrows and he, the hero Radheya (Karṇa), was slain in that great slaughter, glory (lakṣmī) never abandoned him, even with his life departed.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 68 Verse 41. Even though his armor was completely shattered by arrows and he, the hero Radheya (Karṇa), was slain in that great slaughter, glory (lakṣmī) never abandoned him, even with his life departed.

sanskrit: नानाभरणवान्राजन्मृष्टजाम्बूनदाङ्गदः ।हतो वैकर्तनः शेते पादपोऽङ्कुरवानिव ॥४२॥
transliteration: nānābharaṇavānrājanmṛṣṭajāmbūnadāṅgadaḥ ,hato vaikartanaḥ śete pādapo'ṅkuravāniva.
english: O king, the slain Vaikartana (Karṇa), adorned with various ornaments and gleaming golden armlets, lies like a tree still retaining its sprouts.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 68 Verse 42. O king, the slain Vaikartana (Karṇa), adorned with various ornaments and gleaming golden armlets, lies like a tree still retaining its sprouts.

sanskrit: कनकोत्तमसंकाशः प्रदीप्त इव पावकः ।सपुत्रः पुरुषव्याघ्रः संशान्तः पार्थतेजसा ।प्रताप्य पाण्डवान्राजन्पाञ्चालांश्चास्त्रतेजसा ॥४३॥
transliteration: kanakottamasaṁkāśaḥ pradīpta iva pāvakaḥ ,saputraḥ puruṣavyāghraḥ saṁśāntaḥ pārthatejasā ,pratāpya pāṇḍavānrājanpāñcālāṁścāstratejasā.
english: O King, that tiger among men, who shone like the finest gold and blazed like fire, who, along with his son, had tormented the Pāṇḍavas and Pañcālas with the might of his weapons, was finally extinguished by the prowess of Arjuna (Pārtha).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 68 Verse 43. O King, that tiger among men, who shone like the finest gold and blazed like fire, who, along with his son, had tormented the Pāṇḍavas and Pañcālas with the might of his weapons, was finally extinguished by the prowess of Arjuna (Pārtha).

sanskrit: ददानीत्येव योऽवोचन्न नास्तीत्यर्थितोऽर्थिभिः ।सद्भिः सदा सत्पुरुषः स हतो द्वैरथे वृषः ॥४४॥
transliteration: dadānītyeva yo'vocanna nāstītyarthito'rthibhiḥ ,sadbhiḥ sadā satpuruṣaḥ sa hato dvairathe vṛṣaḥ.
english: He who, when entreated by those seeking help, would always declare, 'I will give!', never saying, 'There is nothing,' that truly virtuous man among the good, that bull-like warrior, was killed in a duel.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 68 Verse 44. He who, when entreated by those seeking help, would always declare, 'I will give!', never saying, 'There is nothing,' that truly virtuous man among the good, that bull-like warrior, was killed in a duel.

sanskrit: यस्य ब्राह्मणसात्सर्वमात्मार्थं न महात्मनः ।नादेयं ब्राह्मणेष्वासीद्यस्य स्वमपि जीवितम् ॥४५॥
transliteration: yasya brāhmaṇasātsarvamātmārthaṁ na mahātmanaḥ ,nādeyaṁ brāhmaṇeṣvāsīdyasya svamapi jīvitam.
english: For that great-souled one (mahātman), everything he possessed was for the Brahmins (brāhmaṇa), not for his own self (ātman). There was nothing that could not be given to Brahmins, not even his own life (jīvitam).
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 68 Verse 45. For that great-souled one (mahātman), everything he possessed was for the Brahmins (brāhmaṇa), not for his own self (ātman). There was nothing that could not be given to Brahmins, not even his own life (jīvitam).

sanskrit: सदा नॄणां प्रियो दाता प्रियदानो दिवं गतः ।आदाय तव पुत्राणां जयाशां शर्म वर्म च ॥४६॥
transliteration: sadā nṝṇāṁ priyo dātā priyadāno divaṁ gataḥ ,ādāya tava putrāṇāṁ jayāśāṁ śarma varma ca.
english: Always beloved by people, a generous giver whose gifts were cherished, he has departed to heaven, having carried away the hope of victory, well-being, and protection from your sons.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 68 Verse 46. Always beloved by people, a generous giver whose gifts were cherished, he has departed to heaven, having carried away the hope of victory, well-being, and protection from your sons.

sanskrit: हते स्म कर्णे सरितो न स्रवन्ति जगाम चास्तं कलुषो दिवाकरः ।ग्रहश्च तिर्यग्ज्वलितार्कवर्णो यमस्य पुत्रोऽभ्युदियाय राजन् ॥४७॥
transliteration: hate sma karṇe sarito na sravanti; jagāma cāstaṁ kaluṣo divākaraḥ ,grahaśca tiryagjvalitārkavarṇo; yamasya putro'bhyudiyāya rājan.
english: O King, when Karṇa was slain, the rivers ceased to flow, and the darkened sun (divākara) set. And a planet, burning obliquely with the color of the sun, the son of Yama (dharma), arose.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 68 Verse 47. O King, when Karṇa was slain, the rivers ceased to flow, and the darkened sun (divākara) set. And a planet, burning obliquely with the color of the sun, the son of Yama (dharma), arose.

sanskrit: नभः पफालाथ ननाद चोर्वी ववुश्च वाताः परुषातिवेलम् ।दिशः सधूमाश्च भृशं प्रजज्वलुर्महार्णवाश्चुक्षुभिरे च सस्वनाः ॥४८॥
transliteration: nabhaḥ paphālātha nanāda corvī; vavuśca vātāḥ paruṣātivelam ,diśaḥ sadhūmāśca bhṛśaṁ prajajvalu;rmahārṇavāścukṣubhire ca sasvanāḥ.
english: Then the sky split, and the earth (urvī) roared. The winds blew fiercely and excessively. The smoky directions blazed intensely, and the great oceans (mahārṇava) churned with mighty roars.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 68 Verse 48. Then the sky split, and the earth (urvī) roared. The winds blew fiercely and excessively. The smoky directions blazed intensely, and the great oceans (mahārṇava) churned with mighty roars.

sanskrit: सकाननाः साद्रिचयाश्चकम्पुः प्रविव्यथुर्भूतगणाश्च मारिष ।बृहस्पती रोहिणीं संप्रपीड्य बभूव चन्द्रार्कसमानवर्णः ॥४९॥
transliteration: sakānanāḥ sādricayāścakampuḥ; pravivyathurbhūtagaṇāśca māriṣa ,bṛhaspatī rohiṇīṁ saṁprapīḍya; babhūva candrārkasamānavarṇaḥ.
english: O respected one (māriṣa), (the lands) with forests (kānana) and mountain ranges trembled, and the multitudes of beings (bhūtaganāḥ) were distressed. Jupiter (Bṛhaspati), having severely afflicted (the constellation) Rohiṇī, became the color of the moon and the sun combined.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 68 Verse 49. O respected one (māriṣa), (the lands) with forests (kānana) and mountain ranges trembled, and the multitudes of beings (bhūtaganāḥ) were distressed. Jupiter (Bṛhaspati), having severely afflicted (the constellation) Rohiṇī, became the color of the moon and the sun combined.

sanskrit: हते कर्णे न दिशो विप्रजज्ञुस्तमोवृता द्यौर्विचचाल भूमिः ।पपात चोल्का ज्वलनप्रकाशा निशाचराश्चाप्यभवन्प्रहृष्टाः ॥५०॥
transliteration: hate karṇe na diśo viprajajñu;stamovṛtā dyaurvicacāla bhūmiḥ ,papāta colkā jvalanaprakāśā; niśācarāścāpyabhavanprahṛṣṭāḥ.
english: When Karṇa was slain, the directions (diś) became unclear, the sky, covered in darkness, trembled, and the earth (bhūmi) shook. A meteor (ulkā) bright like a flame fell, and the night-wanderers (niśācara) also became exceedingly joyful.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 68 Verse 50. When Karṇa was slain, the directions (diś) became unclear, the sky, covered in darkness, trembled, and the earth (bhūmi) shook. A meteor (ulkā) bright like a flame fell, and the night-wanderers (niśācara) also became exceedingly joyful.

sanskrit: शशिप्रकाशाननमर्जुनो यदा क्षुरेण कर्णस्य शिरो न्यपातयत् ।अथान्तरिक्षे दिवि चेह चासकृद्बभूव हाहेति जनस्य निस्वनः ॥५१॥
transliteration: śaśiprakāśānanamarjuno yadā; kṣureṇa karṇasya śiro nyapātayat ,athāntarikṣe divi ceha cāsakṛ;dbabhūva hāheti janasya nisvanaḥ.
english: When Arjuna struck down Karna's head, which had a radiant face like the moon, with an arrow, then repeatedly, both in the atmosphere, in heaven, and on earth, there arose a mournful cry of "alas! alas!" from the people.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 68 Verse 51. When Arjuna struck down Karna's head, which had a radiant face like the moon, with an arrow, then repeatedly, both in the atmosphere, in heaven, and on earth, there arose a mournful cry of "alas! alas!" from the people.

sanskrit: स देवगन्धर्वमनुष्यपूजितं निहत्य कर्णं रिपुमाहवेऽर्जुनः ।रराज पार्थः परमेण तेजसा वृत्रं निहत्येव सहस्रलोचनः ॥५२॥
transliteration: sa devagandharvamanuṣyapūjitaṁ; nihatya karṇaṁ ripumāhave'rjunaḥ ,rarāja pārthaḥ parameṇa tejasā; vṛtraṁ nihatyeva sahasralocanaḥ.
english: Having slain Karna, his enemy who was honored by gods, Gandharvas, and humans, in battle, Arjuna (Pārtha) shone with supreme splendor, just as the thousand-eyed Indra shone after slaying Vritra.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 68 Verse 52. Having slain Karna, his enemy who was honored by gods, Gandharvas, and humans, in battle, Arjuna (Pārtha) shone with supreme splendor, just as the thousand-eyed Indra shone after slaying Vritra.

sanskrit: ततो रथेनाम्बुदवृन्दनादिना शरन्नभोमध्यगभास्करत्विषा ।पताकिना भीमनिनादकेतुना हिमेन्दुशङ्खस्फटिकावभासिना ।सुवर्णमुक्तामणिवज्रविद्रुमैरलंकृतेनाप्रतिमानरंहसा ॥५३॥
transliteration: tato rathenāmbudavṛndanādinā; śarannabhomadhyagabhāskaratviṣā ,patākinā bhīmaninādaketunā; himenduśaṅkhasphaṭikāvabhāsinā ,suvarṇamuktāmaṇivajravidrumai;ralaṁkṛtenāpratimānaraṁhasā.
english: Then, with that chariot  -  which thundered like masses of clouds, shone with the brilliance of the sun in the autumn sky, bore many banners, had a standard that produced a dreadful sound, gleamed like snow, the moon, a conch, or crystal, was adorned with gold, pearls, gems, diamonds, and corals, and possessed unequalled speed...
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 68 Verse 53. Then, with that chariot  -  which thundered like masses of clouds, shone with the brilliance of the sun in the autumn sky, bore many banners, had a standard that produced a dreadful sound, gleamed like snow, the moon, a conch, or crystal, was adorned with gold, pearls, gems, diamonds, and corals, and possessed unequalled speed...

sanskrit: नरोत्तमौ पाण्डवकेशिमर्दनावुदाहितावग्निदिवाकरोपमौ ।रणाजिरे वीतभयौ विरेजतुः समानयानाविव विष्णुवासवौ ॥५४॥
transliteration: narottamau pāṇḍavakeśimardanā;vudāhitāvagnidivākaropamau ,raṇājire vītabhayau virejatuḥ; samānayānāviva viṣṇuvāsavau.
english: Then, the two best among men - Arjuna, the Pāṇḍava, and Krishna, the slayer of Keśi (Keśimardana) - likened to fire and the sun, shone fearlessly on the battlefield, just like Vishnu and Vasava (Indra) on their common vehicle.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 68 Verse 54. Then, the two best among men - Arjuna, the Pāṇḍava, and Krishna, the slayer of Keśi (Keśimardana) - likened to fire and the sun, shone fearlessly on the battlefield, just like Vishnu and Vasava (Indra) on their common vehicle.

sanskrit: ततो धनुर्ज्यातलनेमिनिस्वनैः प्रसह्य कृत्वा च रिपून्हतप्रभान् ।संसाधयित्वैव कुरूञ्शरौघैः कपिध्वजः पक्षिवरध्वजश्च ।प्रसह्य शङ्खौ धमतुः सुघोषौ मनांस्यरीणामवसादयन्तौ ॥५५॥
transliteration: tato dhanurjyātalaneminisvanaiḥ; prasahya kṛtvā ca ripūnhataprabhān ,saṁsādhayitvaiva kurūñśaraughaiḥ; kapidhvajaḥ pakṣivaradhvajaśca ,prasahya śaṅkhau dhamatuḥ sughoṣau; manāṁsyarīṇāmavasādayantau.
english: Then, having forcefully rendered the enemies powerless by the sounds of their bowstrings, palm-guards, and chariot wheels, and having completely subdued the Kurus with torrents of arrows, Arjuna (whose banner bears a monkey) and Krishna (whose banner bears the best of birds) mightily blew their two resounding conch shells, disheartening the minds of their adversaries.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 68 Verse 55. Then, having forcefully rendered the enemies powerless by the sounds of their bowstrings, palm-guards, and chariot wheels, and having completely subdued the Kurus with torrents of arrows, Arjuna (whose banner bears a monkey) and Krishna (whose banner bears the best of birds) mightily blew their two resounding conch shells, disheartening the minds of their adversaries.

sanskrit: सुवर्णजालावततौ महास्वनौ हिमावदातौ परिगृह्य पाणिभिः ।चुचुम्बतुः शङ्खवरौ नृणां वरौ वराननाभ्यां युगपच्च दध्मतुः ॥५६॥
transliteration: suvarṇajālāvatatau mahāsvanau; himāvadātau parigṛhya pāṇibhiḥ ,cucumbatuḥ śaṅkhavarau nṛṇāṁ varau; varānanābhyāṁ yugapacca dadhmatuḥ.
english: Those two best among men (Arjuna and Krishna), grasping with their hands the two supreme conch shells - which were adorned with golden nets, profoundly resonant, and pristine white like snow - then kissed them and simultaneously blew them with their beautiful faces.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 68 Verse 56. Those two best among men (Arjuna and Krishna), grasping with their hands the two supreme conch shells - which were adorned with golden nets, profoundly resonant, and pristine white like snow - then kissed them and simultaneously blew them with their beautiful faces.

sanskrit: पाञ्चजन्यस्य निर्घोषो देवदत्तस्य चोभयोः ।पृथिवीमन्तरिक्षं च द्यामपश्चाप्यपूरयत् ॥५७॥
transliteration: pāñcajanyasya nirghoṣo devadattasya cobhayoḥ ,pṛthivīmantarikṣaṁ ca dyāmapaścāpyapūrayat.
english: The formidable sound of Pāñcajanya (Krishna's conch) and Devadatta (Arjuna's conch) together filled the earth, the atmosphere, the sky, and the waters.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 68 Verse 57. The formidable sound of Pāñcajanya (Krishna's conch) and Devadatta (Arjuna's conch) together filled the earth, the atmosphere, the sky, and the waters.

sanskrit: तौ शङ्खशब्देन निनादयन्तौ वनानि शैलान्सरितो दिशश्च ।वित्रासयन्तौ तव पुत्रसेनां युधिष्ठिरं नन्दयतः स्म वीरौ ॥५८॥
transliteration: tau śaṅkhaśabdena ninādayantau; vanāni śailānsarito diśaśca ,vitrāsayantau tava putrasenāṁ; yudhiṣṭhiraṁ nandayataḥ sma vīrau.
english: Those two heroes (Arjuna and Krishna), causing the forests, mountains, rivers, and all directions to reverberate with the sound of their conch shells, and striking terror into your sons' army, brought delight to Yudhishthira.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 68 Verse 58. Those two heroes (Arjuna and Krishna), causing the forests, mountains, rivers, and all directions to reverberate with the sound of their conch shells, and striking terror into your sons' army, brought delight to Yudhishthira.

sanskrit: ततः प्रयाताः कुरवो जवेन श्रुत्वैव शङ्खस्वनमीर्यमाणम् ।विहाय मद्राधिपतिं पतिं च दुर्योधनं भारत भारतानाम् ॥५९॥
transliteration: tataḥ prayātāḥ kuravo javena; śrutvaiva śaṅkhasvanamīryamāṇam ,vihāya madrādhipatiṁ patiṁ ca; duryodhanaṁ bhārata bhāratānām.
english: Then, O descendant of Bhārata, as soon as they heard the conch sound being blown, the Kurus swiftly departed, abandoning the King of Madras and their leader Duryodhana, who was the lord of the Bhāratas.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 68 Verse 59. Then, O descendant of Bhārata, as soon as they heard the conch sound being blown, the Kurus swiftly departed, abandoning the King of Madras and their leader Duryodhana, who was the lord of the Bhāratas.

sanskrit: महाहवे तं बहु शोभमानं धनंजयं भूतगणाः समेताः ।तदान्वमोदन्त जनार्दनं च प्रभाकरावभ्युदितौ यथैव ॥६०॥
transliteration: mahāhave taṁ bahu śobhamānaṁ; dhanaṁjayaṁ bhūtagaṇāḥ sametāḥ ,tadānvamodanta janārdanaṁ ca; prabhākarāvabhyuditau yathaiva.
english: Then, the assembled hosts of beings greatly acclaimed Dhananjaya (Arjuna), who was shining splendidly in the great battle, and Janardana (Krishna), just as two suns rise gloriously.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 68 Verse 60. Then, the assembled hosts of beings greatly acclaimed Dhananjaya (Arjuna), who was shining splendidly in the great battle, and Janardana (Krishna), just as two suns rise gloriously.

sanskrit: समाचितौ कर्णशरैः परंतपावुभौ व्यभातां समरेऽच्युतार्जुनौ ।तमो निहत्याभ्युदितौ यथामलौ शशाङ्कसूर्याविव रश्मिमालिनौ ॥६१॥
transliteration: samācitau karṇaśaraiḥ paraṁtapā;vubhau vyabhātāṁ samare'cyutārjunau ,tamo nihatyābhyuditau yathāmalau; śaśāṅkasūryāviva raśmimālinau.
english: Although covered by Karna's arrows, both Achyuta (Krishna) and Arjuna, those destroyers of foes, shone brilliantly in battle. They appeared like the spotless moon and sun, radiant with their garlands of rays, having dispelled the darkness.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 68 Verse 61. Although covered by Karna's arrows, both Achyuta (Krishna) and Arjuna, those destroyers of foes, shone brilliantly in battle. They appeared like the spotless moon and sun, radiant with their garlands of rays, having dispelled the darkness.

sanskrit: विहाय तान्बाणगणानथागतौ सुहृद्वृतावप्रतिमानविक्रमौ ।सुखं प्रविष्टौ शिबिरं स्वमीश्वरौ सदस्यहूताविव वासवाच्युतौ ॥६२॥
transliteration: vihāya tānbāṇagaṇānathāgatau; suhṛdvṛtāvapratimānavikramau ,sukhaṁ praviṣṭau śibiraṁ svamīśvarau; sadasyahūtāviva vāsavācyutau.
english: Then, leaving behind those masses of arrows, the two lords (Krishna and Arjuna), who were surrounded by friends and possessed unequalled valor, comfortably entered their own camp, just as Indra and Krishna (Achyuta) are invited into an assembly.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 68 Verse 62. Then, leaving behind those masses of arrows, the two lords (Krishna and Arjuna), who were surrounded by friends and possessed unequalled valor, comfortably entered their own camp, just as Indra and Krishna (Achyuta) are invited into an assembly.

sanskrit: सदेवगन्धर्वमनुष्यचारणैर्महर्षिभिर्यक्षमहोरगैरपि ।जयाभिवृद्ध्या परयाभिपूजितौ निहत्य कर्णं परमाहवे तदा ॥६३॥
transliteration: sadevagandharvamanuṣyacāraṇai;rmaharṣibhiryakṣamahoragairapi ,jayābhivṛddhyā parayābhipūjitau; nihatya karṇaṁ paramāhave tadā.
english: Then, having slain Karna in the supreme battle, the two (Arjuna and Krishna) were greatly honored by gods, gandharvas, humans, charanas, great sages, yakshas, and even by great serpents, celebrating their great victory.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 68 Verse 63. Then, having slain Karna in the supreme battle, the two (Arjuna and Krishna) were greatly honored by gods, gandharvas, humans, charanas, great sages, yakshas, and even by great serpents, celebrating their great victory.

sanskrit: संजय उवाच ।तथा निपातिते कर्णे तव सैन्ये च विद्रुते ।आश्लिष्य पार्थं दाशार्हो हर्षाद्वचनमब्रवीत् ॥१॥
transliteration: saṁjaya uvāca ,tathā nipātite karṇe tava sainye ca vidrute ,āśliṣya pārthaṁ dāśārho harṣādvacanamabravīt.
english: Saṃjaya said: When Karna was thus struck down and your army had fled, Krishna, the descendant of Daśārha, embracing Pārtha (Arjuna), spoke these words joyfully.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 69 Verse 1. Saṃjaya said: When Karna was thus struck down and your army had fled, Krishna, the descendant of Daśārha, embracing Pārtha (Arjuna), spoke these words joyfully.

sanskrit: हतो बलभिदा वृत्रस्त्वया कर्णो धनंजय ।वधं वै कर्णवृत्राभ्यां कथयिष्यन्ति मानवाः ॥२॥
transliteration: hato balabhidā vṛtrastvayā karṇo dhanaṁjaya ,vadhaṁ vai karṇavṛtrābhyāṁ kathayiṣyanti mānavāḥ.
english: O Dhanaṃjaya (Arjuna), just as Vṛtra was slain by Balabhit (Indra), so too Karna has been slain by you. Indeed, people will speak of the killing of both Karna and Vṛtra.
search_text: Mahabharata Karṇa Parva Chapter 69 Verse 2. O Dhanaṃjaya (Arjuna), just as Vṛtra was slain by Balabhit (Indra), so too Karna has been slain by you. Indeed, people will speak of the killing of both Karna and Vṛtra.
